Malt. amu water; am-amre to water (as the mouth); amsro waterish; amye to bathe (oneself); amte to bathe (another). / ? Cf. Skt. ambu-. DED (S) 158.
Ta. ayam water, spring, tank, pond. Ma. ayam pool, tank.
Ta. ayal, acal neighbourhood, ad- ‘jacent place, being foreign or alien; acalar neighbours, strangers. Ma, ayal neighbour- hood. DED(S) 159.

Ta. ayir Cpp-, -tt-) to suspect; n. doubt, suspicion; ayirppu doubt, suspicion; aiyam doubt, uncertainty, scepticism. Ma. ayyam doubt. Ka, (Gul.) oyb id., suspicion; ? (Kitt.) naccu, nasa doubt. ? Malt. asr-asr ane to hesitate. Cf. 898 Ta. émam. DED(S, N) 778.
Ta. ayirai, acarai, acalai loach, sandy colour, Cobitis thermalis; ayilai a kind of fish. Ma, ayala a fish, mackerel, scomber; aila, ayila a fish; ayira a kind of small fish, loach. DED (S) 160.
Ta. ayil iron. Ma. ayir, ayiram any ore. Ka, aduru native metal. Tu. ajirda karba very hard iron. DED(S) 161.

Ta. ayil javelin, lance, surgical knife, lancet. Ma, ayil javelin, lance; ayiri surgical knife, lancet. 194 Kol. ayk- (ayakt-) to sweep away; ayak (obi. ayk-) rubbish. Nk. ayk- to sweep. Nk. (Ch,} ay- id. Pa. ayp- (ayt-) id. Ga, (Oll.) ays-, (S.) ayk- (ayt-) id.; (S.°) ayp- to clean, wipe. DED(S) 162.

Pa. (S.) ayk a kind of grain called in Halbi kang [i.e. Panicum italicum; cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 2605]. Ga, (S.”) aykil a kind of millet called in Te. korralu [i.e. Setaria italica]. [Setaria italica Beauv. = Panicum italicum Linn. ] DEDS 28. 196 (a) Ta. ayyan, aiyan father, sage, priest, teacher, brahman, superior person, master, king; ayya father, respectable man; aiyar men worthy of respect, sages, brahmans, title of smarta brahmans, etc.; tam-aiyan elder brother or parallel male cousin; numaiyan your elder brother; ai lord, master, husband, king, guru, priest, teacher, father. Ma. ayyan father, lord; tamayan elder ‘brother. Ko. ayn father, father’s brother or parallel male cousin, mother’s sister’s husband. To. in, eyi- (voc. eya-) id. Ka, ayya, aya father, grand- father, master, lord, teacher. Kod. ayyé father’s brother or parallel male “cousin, mother’s sister’s husband; tammayya-n younger brother (voc.). Tu. ayye priest, minister, teacher, master; tammaiya an affectionate form of addressing a younger brother. Te. ayya, aya father. Kol. (SR) ayya mother’s father; ayyak god. Go. (Koya Su.) eyal father. Cf. 920 Ta. aiyam. / ? Cf. Skt. arya-; Pali ayya-. DED (N) 163. (b) Ta. aiya excl. of wonder; excl. of pity, concern; aiyako excl. of pity, sorrow; ai wonder, astonishment; aiy-enal uttering ai expressive of wonder, of distress or mental suffering, of assent; aiyaiyO excl. of pity or grief; alyO excl. of wonder; excl. of pity, concern; excl. of poignant grief. Ma. ayya int

Go. (Koya Su.) @rg- to lay eggs (as a hen). Konda ar- (-t-) id. Pe, ar- (-t-) id. Mand. er- Ct-) id. DEDS(N) 29. : 198 Ta, araka a plough with bullocks, etc. complete. Malt, are a plough. DEDS 30. 1 99 Ta. arakku lac, sealing-wax, shellac, or resin melted with turpentine; arakkam shellac. Ma. arakku gumlac, sealing-wax. Ka. aragu id. Tu. ara, aragy id. | Cf, Skt. laksa- (Mbh.); raksa- (lex.) lac. Cf. also Skt. al: - rake le akta(ka)-. 200 Kol. aragdi, (Pat., p. 27) argli chest of body. Nk, aragli id. Nk. (Ch.) argil id. Ga. (Ol.) argil id. Go, (M.) arayul, (A.) aril, (G.) arti, (Ma. Ko.) ariyul id.; (Tr. W.) aril udder (Voc. 76). DED(S) 165.

Ta. aracan, araican, araiyan king, ‘sovereign, prince; araci queen; aracu, araicu Kingliness, king, kingdom, government; arai politics; ara-manai king’s palace (also aran- manai royal place, mansion, with aran defence, fortress, castle, < Skt. Sarana-). Ma. aracan, arasan, atayan king; aracam, aracu royalty, . constitution; ara-mana king’s house, palace, court. Ko. arc official of Mohammedan government of Mysore; arman palace. To. ars king; European man; arman palace. Ka. arasa (im cpds. ara-), arasu king, lord; arasi, arasati queen; ara-mane royal dwelling, palace, court, government. Kod. aramane palace. Tu. arasu, arase king, monarch; aramane, arantadé palace, royal residence. Te, arusu king. / < Skt. yajan-. A form in an unknown, but presumably SDr., language is arahanasha, gen. sg. of arahana- (cf. Ta. aracan), in a ‘Dravidian Prakrit’ legend on a Satavahana coin (2nd cent. A.D.); D. C. Sircar, Epigraphia Indica 35.247-52 (1961). DED 202 Ta, atacu, araca-maram pipal, Ficus religiosa; arai a tree, prob. pipal (Tolk.). Ma. arayal, aracu, aragal, arasu I’. religiosa. Ka. arase, arise, arali, arale, alali, alle id.; ravi id. (< Te.). Kod. arali-mara id. Te. ravi, ragi id. Kol. ra-vi id. Konda rayi maran id. DED(N)
Ka. arana a nuptial present. Te. arana- mu a gift of money to a daughter and son-in- law at the time of marriage. ? Cf. 227 Ta. arul (Kitt.). DED 169. :

Ta. aranai typical lizard, Lacertidae; smooth streaked lizard, Lacerta interpunctula. Ma, arana green house lizard, L. interpunctula. Ka, arane, rane, rini greenish kind of lizard which is said to poison by licking, L. inter- punctula. Tu. arane id. DED 170.
Te. aranfi, arfti, (coll), arati, (K.) ananti, anti, anti plaintain tree, banana tree. Go. (S.) arti, (Pat.) arti [ie. arti] banana (Voe. 80; < Te.). DEDS(N) 31.

Ta. arattai galangal, Alpinia. Ma. aratta Alpinia galanga. DEDS 32. : 207 Ta, aril interlacing as of bamboo stalks growing together, low jungle, thicket, dense bush, bamboo. Te. aralu (K.), allu to spread (as a creeper), creep. Ga. (S.) all- to spread as a creeper. DED(S) 171. arantal {20] aru
Ta. (DCV)} arantal tender wild jack [i.e. tree or fruit]. Ma. (DCV) 4rantal id. DEDS 33.

Ta. aralai stone broken for roads; ari pebbles or gems or pieces of metal put into women’s anklets to make them tinkle; aril gravel, small sharp pebble. To. arl jackstraws, pebbles used for counters. Cf. 3959 Ta. paral. DED(S) 172.
Ta. arali, alari, (PPT/) aralai oleander, Nerium odorum; arali fetid tree, Sterculia foetida, Ma. arali, alari Nerium odorum; Plumeria acuminata. DED 173.

Ta. ari (-v-, -nt-) to cut off, nip off; Gpp-, -tt-) to gnaw as white ants; n. cutting; ari vat billhook, sickle, garden knife; arivi reaped corn, handful of reaped grain; aruvi stubble of Indian millet; arakku (arakki-) to clip off, prune, cut, sever. Ma, ariyuka to reap corn, cut grass, cut very small, hack to pieces; ari val sickle, reaping hook; arikka to gnaw as vermin; arakkuka to cut, chop off, sever; aranhuka to cut or chop off (the branches of trees or plants), Ko, ayr small heap of grain stalks made of one handful in reaping. Jo. ark- (arky-) to chip, cut square (end of plank or post). Ka. ari (arid-) to cut or lop off; n, cutting off, gnawing as vermin, a handful or more of corn cut at one stroke; arisu to cause to cut off; arival, aruval sickle. Kol. ark- (arakt-) to harvest (or with 315 Ta. aru). Nk, ark- to cut, reap (as for Kol,). Nk. (CH.) ark- to cut paddy, harvest; arup-/arp- to cut. Pe. raz- (rast-) to cut (e.g. with saw). Kui raspa (rast-) to cut across, cut crosswise; n. a cross cut. Kuwi (Su.) rat- (-h-) to cut; (S.; BB 1963, p. 269) ratpinai to haggle (i.e. to hack; pl. action). DED(S) 175.

Ta. ari- (-pp-, -tt-) to sift, separate larger from smaller bodies, separate by washing; n. sifting, separating; arippu sift- ing, separating; arippan sifter, one who sifts. Ma, arikka to sift, cleanse tice by washing, filter; arippu sifting, gold wash. Ka, ari to clear of dregs and impurities, settle (tr.) as turbid water; araka the act of cleansing rice from dust and stones by washing; (Bark.) arpi- to filter; arpne a kind of filter. Kod. ari- (ariv, arifij-) (liquid) is strained; (Kar. also) (toddy) seeps from tree; (arip-, aric-) to strain (liquid). Tu. aripuni to sift, strain, filter; aripe sieve, colander, strainer. Cf. 219 Ta. ari. DED(N) 176.

Ta. ari (pp-, -tt-) to have an acute itching sensation; arippu itching. Ma. arikka to itch, Ko, ayr- (are-) (mouth) burns; ? arl fever (or with 276 Ta. agal). To. ary- (arc-) (woman) desires sexual intercourse. DED 177. 215 Ya, ari rice, paddy, ear of paddy; arici rice without husk, any husked grain. Ma. ari grain of rice freed from chaff, seed, grain; (Kaut.) arici rice, Ko, aky grain of any grain food when husked. To. aSky rice; nesiSky id. (cf, s.v. 3753 Ta. nel). Ka, akki rice deprived of its husk, grain that resembles rice. Kod akki husked rice. 7u. ari rice freed from husk, any small grain; akki rice, corn (only in cpds.). Bel. (LSB 2.2) argi rice. ? Te. (B, K) arise a sweetmeat made of rice, flour, and jJaggery. Cf. 3829 Ko. pace-k, To. poéifky. DED(S, N) 178. 216 Ta, ari tax, duty; appu loan, debt (< Te.). Ka. accu to pay unjustly, as rent for Jand on which the crop has failed; make a payment which is not properly due and on account of which one suffers loss; accike paying unjustly. Te. ari tax, tribute; aruvu to pay as taxes; 7. a loan of grain, etc., purchase or sale on credit; accu to be in debt, owe, incur or suffer loss; accu-konu to pay, pay what one is not bound to pay; accu-dala debt; accika deficiency, want, defect; appu loan. Kuwi (F.) arva, (S.) aruwu loan (< Te.). Cf. 223 Ta. arukku. DED(S) 179.
Ta. ari. DED 3168.

Ta. ari beauty; arivai woman between the age of 20 and 25, woman, lady. Ma, aruya fine woman. ? Tu. aragini a neat or tidy woman. DED 180. 218 Ta, ari lines in- the white of the eye; 2 alari id. Ka. ari a disease of the eye. DED 181. 219 Ta, ari fermented liquor, toddy; ariyal, arutti, aruppam toddy. Ma. ariyuka to tap a toddy palm. Cf. 213 Ta. ari. DED 182.
Ta. aricanam turmeric. Ko, arcn saffron (ie. turmeric). To, arsn saffron, yellow. Xa. arisina, arisina, arasina, arasina, arsina turmeric, Curcuma longa Lin. DED 183.

Ta. aru (arum, 4r) rare, difficult, etc.; aritu that which is difficult, rare, precious; ariya difficult; arumai rareness, smaliness; difficulty; arumanta rare, precious; aruku (aruki-) to become scarce, diminished, be reduced, happen rarely, disappear; arukku (arukki-) to make scarce, reduce, put a stop to, destroy; regard as precious; 7. scarceness, arukkam smallness, brevity; arukkapi rareness, preciousness; arufku, aruntal, aruvam rareness; aruppam difficulty, fort; (PPT/) aruppu the natural protection of a forest; arakku (arakki-) to cause to diminish; ari difficulty; ariya little, delicate. Ma. aru rare, unusual, impossible; ariya rare, uncommon; aruma, atima superiority, importance; difficulty; arippam difficulty; arutu what ought not to be, impossibility; arukuka to diminish. Ko. arg- (argy-) to be reduced (water by evapor- ation), (swelling) goes down, become reformed in character, be depressed (because of pov- erty); ark- (arky-) to use up extravagantly. To, arx- (arxy-) to be subdued; ark- (arky-) to subdue; arkm state of being subdued. Ka, see eterno aradu, aridu, arudu that is impossible, wonder- ful, great or important. Tu, arkuni to become less, be humbled, depressed. Te. arudu tare, scarce, excellent; », rarity, marvel, surprise; arudandu, arudu-padu to wonder; aridi rare, uncommon, difficult, impossible, wonder, surprise, astonishment. DED(S) 184.

Ta. aruku (aruki-) to approach; n, nearness, vicinity, neighbourhood, place, border, edge; arukar, arukal nearness; aruvu (aruvi-) to approach. Ma. aru, aruvu brim, edge, margin; ariku border, edge, brink, neighbourhood, side, adjacent place; arikattu, arike, arikil near; arika-vari way close by. Ka. aragu, arigu, arugu vicinity, border, edge, margin, Tu. aru, arugu brim, edge, margin, border. Te. arugu to gO, pass (of time), come, proceed, walk, (K. also) approach; aru nearness; aruta(n), aruta(n) near, close by; arudencu to come, go. Go. (Tr.) araluk waiind to follow persistently, closely (Voce. 75), Kur, arbar-parbar neighbours. DED(S, N) 185.

Ta. arukku (arukki-) to show dis- inclination in a bargain; arukkani reluctance to accommodate, closeness in a bargain. Ko, ark- (arky-) (father) refuses to repay daughter’s bride-price. Cf. 216 Ta. ari. DED 186.

Ta. arumpu (arumpi-) to bud, Sprout, spring forth; n. bud, soft sprouting hair, young beard or moustache; arumpi (-pp-, -tt-) to bud, sprout, rise; arumpar bud; aruppam germ of a grain of paddy, first sprouting of a moustache. Ma, arimpu flower bud, first appearance of fruit. Ka, arumbu flower bud. DED 187.

Ta. aruvi river’s mouth. Tu, aruve mouth of a river, seaport. ? Cf. 278 Ta. ari. DED 188. 226 Ta, aruvu (aruvi-) to spring out and gently flow down; aruvi waterfall, spring at the foot of a hill. Ma. aruvuka to Spring out and flow down gently; aruvi, arivi waterfall, cascade. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) ebbi waterfall. Ka, arbi a torrent that has ceased from flowing or is checked in its course; (PBh.) arvi constant descent of water, waterfall; Glav.) abbi a tiny waterfall. Ty. arbi water collected in an elevated place; arbi kituni water to fall in broken streams over a small height; (B-K.) arabi, arbi waterfall. Kor. (0.) arbili water particles drifting in when it rains. DED(N) 189.

Ta. arul (aruli-) to be gracious to, favour, speak gtaciously, command, grant, bestow; n. grace, mercy, favour, benevolence, good deeds, order, command: arulal liberality, generosity, munificence: arulén gracious person. Ma, arul grace, favour, deigning, command; aruluka to deign, vouchsafe, grant, command. Ko. arl voice of god spoken through diviner. Te. arulu tenderness, affec- tion, fondness, Cf. 381 Ta. arvam. ? Cf. 203 Ka. arana. DED 190. 228 (2) Ta. arai (-v-, -nt-) to become macerated, worn out; (-pp-, -ft-) to grind, pulverize, destroy by rubbing, mash, destroy, clear of seeds, gin; araippu grinding, pulver- izing; aravu {aravi-) to torment (cf. Tu.); arakku (arakki-) to rub with the palm of hand or the sole of foot, ruin, cause trouble to; aratku (aranhki-) to suffer, be destroyed; arantai affliction, trouble; aruvu (aruvi-) to wear away (as a flood erodes a river’s banks), afflict. Ma. arayuka to be bruised; aravu grinding; arekka to grind to powder; arappu powdering; ararinuka, arakkuka bruising gently to remove the husk by rubbing. /r, re- (ret-) to grind. Ko. arv- (art-) to grind (chilies). To, ar8- (ary-) to sharpen. Ka. are (arad-) to grind, pulverize, crush; n. state of being ground, etc.; arayisv, arisu to cause to grind, etc.; arata act of grinding; ari to grind on a slab, rub cotton with the feet in cleaning it; (Hav.) araccu to crush; (Hav.) arappu coconut paste. Kod. ara- (arap-, arat-) to grind with roller stone, mix up in a paste in preparing curry spic

Ta. arai waist, loins, stomach. Ma. ara middle of the body, loins, waist. To. ar nip silver waist-string. Kod. are waist. DED 193. 231 Kur. argna (argyas) to climb, mount an animal, rise (as sun, moon, stars), rise in pitch (as a drum), get puffed up, (eyes) are turned up before death; (argas) to make climb, lift, haul up, take upon one’s shoulders, lay a burden on, begin, raise, increase; argta’and, arga’ind to make climb, lift up, etc. Malt. arge to climb; argtre to lift, raise. 9 Tu, (B-K.) arumbu to climb. DED(N) 195. 232 Ko, arb extreme happiness (at getting a good meal, finding money, escaping death, etc.). To, arb good life (with nothing to do ‘but eat). Te. abbu to be acquired, gained, come into one’s enjoyment, possession, or reach. Kuwi (S.) abbinai to prosper. DED 196.

Te. ralu to fall or drop down (of leaves, tears, etc.); ral(u)cu, ral(u)pu to cause to fall or drop down, knock off or down; rai(u)pu falling or dropping down, causing to fall or drop down. Kol. (SR. Kin.) ril- (rat-) to fall; (SR) rap- to fell. Nk. ral- (rat-) to fall out; ralp- to make to fall out. Go. (A. G. Mu. Ma. S. Ko.) ar-, (Tr. W. Ph.) arrana to fall; caus. (Tr.) arhutténa, (Ph.) arahtana, arsah tana, (Mu.) arih-/arh-, (S.) arah-, (S-R.) arusana to cause to fall (Voce. 72); (Ko.) ral- to fall (Voc. 3027; <-Te.). Konda ar- id.; arap- to fell, make to fall. Pe. ar- (-t-) to fall; véra arbond setting of the sun, west; rat- (-t-} to let fall, drop; motion tratka-; infens, ratpa-. Mand, re- (-t-) to fall, descend, (sun) to set; ret- to Jet fall, drop. Kuwi (F.) riali, (S.) ri’nai, (Isr.) ri- to fal) DED(S, N) 197.

Ta. al- (1 sg. allén, 1 pl. allém, 2 sg. allai, 2 pl. allir, 3 sg. m, allan, 3 pl. m. allar, 3 sg. fi allal, 3 sg. neuf. anru, 3 pl. neut. alla; alla is used in mod. Ta. for all persons and numbers) to be not so-and-so; allatu evil, sin; or, if not, or else; allavai sin, evil, useless- ness; alldta which is not, different from; allamal, allal, anri, anriyil except, besides; anmaj negation of identity, reciprocal negation; evil. Ma. alla is not that, not thus; allaté else, otherwise, except; allaykil if not. Ko. ala- (for all persons and numbers) to be not so-and-so; ala-d which is not so-and-so; bad; except, besides, not only but also; ala-vo-r bad men; ala-tme-l if/since it is not so. To. alosy except (lit. not being); Ka. alla (for all persons and numbers; also 3 sg. neut. alladu, altu) to be not so-and-so, be not fit or proper; allada being not so or that, being not proper. Kod. alla (for all persons and numbers) to be not so-and-so; allate not only but also. Go. (Tr.) hal, hall, haillé not (see

Ta. al night, darkness, evening; alku night, afternoon; alkal night; el, elli id; ex.) yalam id. Ma, al, allu darkness, night. Kur. ellna to be night-blind, (Hahn also) to be very dark. ? Cf. 3613 Ta. nallam. DED(S) 199.

Ta. ala (-pp-, -nt-) to suffer, be in distress, suffer privation, be in want; alam distress, pain, misery; alappu confusion of mind; alantalai distress, vexation, confusion; alantai trouble; alanton one who is in distress; alati-kulati disorder, confusion; alafku (alanki-) to be agitated in mind, troubled; alacu (alaci-) to suffer, be distressed, be exhausted, become weary; alamaru (alamari-) to be agitated, distressed; alamala (-pp-, -nt-) to be confused, dazed, be anxious; alampal vexation, trouble; alavu (alavi-) to be troubled ‘ta mind; n. confusion, perturbation; alavalai hasty action, confusion of mind, distress; alu (-pp-, -tt-) to be weary, tired by overwork or care; aluppu weariness, exhaustion; aluval business, affair; alai (-v-, -nt-) to be harassed, wander in weariness (cf. 240 Ta. alanku), (-pp-, -tt-) to harass, vex, afflict, annoy; 7. oppression; alaicu (alaici-) to be lazy; alaiccal weariness, vexation; alaippu disturbance, distress, trouble; alaivu mental agitation, trouble, distress; allal affliction, distress; alla (pp-, -nt-) to suffer, be in distress; n. distress; allatu (allati-) to suffer. Ma, ala lamentation; alaftwiwuka, alukka to be worn out, grow lean; aluppu weariness; alampal having enough of it, vexation; alayuka to be wearied; alaccal vexation, mourning; alasal, alagal agitation, fatigue, disappointment; alasuka to be tired; alati troublesome; alappu confusion, stir, fright; alampu trouble; alam- puka to be agitated, tired; aluval bustle, busine

Ta. alaku blade of a weapon, or in- strument, head of an arrow, weaver’s stay or staff to adjust a warp; alakku roof laths, a pole with an iron hook to pluck fruits or leaves. Ma. alaku lath, splint, palm or bamboo leaf, weaver’s staff, blade of sword, knife, or spear. Ka. alagu, alugu blade of a knife, sword, etc. Te. altigu tip of an arrow, sword. DED 201.
Ta. alaku number, calculation, cowries (as signs of number in reckoning). ? Kod. (Mercara) alu cowrie. DED 202.
Ta. alaku, (Tinn.) alavu jaw, mandibles, bird’s beak, Ma. alaku, alavu upper portion of the jawbone. Ka, halaku the lower part of the cheek. DIDS 34.

Ta. alanku (alanki-) to move, shake, swing, dangle, be in motion; alankal wreath, waving ear of corn; alakku (alakki-) to cause to move, shake; alacu (alaci-) to shake, be agitated; alamaru to whirl; alampu (alampi-) to move, wabble (as water in a vessel not full), swerve from a proper line of conduct, fluctu- ate, wander; cause to wander on account of panic; alutku (alunki-) to shake (intr.) slightly; alukku (alukki-) to shake (¢r.) slightly; strut, swagger; alai (-v-, -nt-) to wave, shake, move (as a reflection in water), go to and fro for an object, roam, wander (cf. 236 Ta. ala), stagger, totter; (-pp- -tt-) to move (fr.), shake, cause to wander back and forth; m. wave, billow, ripple; alaiccal, alaical wandering; alaicu (alaici-) to shake (¢r.), agitate; alaippu moving, shaking; alaiyal wandering, waving; alaivu moving, shaking, waving; allatu (allafi-) to wander from place to place. Ma. ala wave; alukka, alafiuka to shake; alatwal com- motion; aluffial stir; alukkuka to agitate; alayuka to fluctuate, be tossed, roam; alekkuka to beat against (as waves on a shore); ano hhuka to move, shake; anakkuka to shake fan, excite; anakkam moving, shaking. 77 le»- (lend-) to roam, wander. Ko. alg- (algy to shake (intr.), swagger; alk- (alky-) to shak (tr.), shake down (e.g. fruit); alv- (ald-) t: wander aimlessly (cf. 236 Ta. ala). To. alx {alxy-) to shake (intr.); alk- (alky-) to shak (t.). Ka, alaku, alagu, aluku, alugu, alusgu alaku, aluku, alku to move about, shake, b shaky, tr
Ka. alabu, alubu name applied to different species of Alysicarpus and other leguminous plants; (UNR) alubu weeds. Te. alamu id. Go. (S.) allam id. (Voc. 98; < Te.). DED (S) 207.

Ta. allappan a chatterer; alappu (alappi-) to chatter, prattle, talk nonsense; alampu (alampi-) to sound, tinkle; alampal a loud noise; alavalai babbler; alavalaimai babbling; alavai woman that chatters cease- lessly; alar idle talk, gossip; alaru (alari-) to roar, bellow, bleat, weep aloud, sorrow; alaral loud cry, great sound; alarru (alarri-) to talk unceasingly and irregularly. Ma, alappan chatterer, prattler; alappu chattering, confused noise; alavan loquacious babbler; alaruka to roar, bellow, cry (as elephant, tiger, woman in labour); alarcca roaring, etc.; alavan boaster, exaggerator (or cf. 296 Ta. ala). Jr. Wisru to shout, roar, (woman) gossips. Ka, (PBh.) alaru to cry aloud. Te. alikidi noise, sound, disturbance; alabalamu outcry, noise, uproar. DED(S) 211.

Ta. alampu (alampi-) to wash, rinse; alacu (alaci-) to rinse; alaicu (alaici-) to wash, rinse; (RS, p. 128, item 47) alaittal to wash clothes by moving them about in water. Ma. alakkuka to wash clothes by beating; alakku washing; alampuka to shake clothes in water. To. asp- (aspy-) to clean. Ka, alambu, alumbu, alabu, alubu to rinse, wash; ale to wash, alasu to shake or agitate in water (as a cloth, vegetables, etc., for cleansing). Tu. alambuni to wash; alumbuni, lumbuni to plunge, wash, rinse. Te. alamu to smear, (B) wash; aladu to smear, daub, apply; aluku to smear the floor of a house or a mud wall, etc. with macerated cowdung. Kui. akali rinsing. DED(N) 208.

Ta. alar (-v-, -nt-) to blossom, open up, spread (as rays of the sun, water), increase in size, manifest itself; m. full-blown flower; alartti process of blooming; alarttu (alartti-) to cause to blossom; alari flower. Ma. alar a blossom, opening flower. alari a flower; alaruka to open (as flowers), be hot and dry; alarcca heat, lust. Ka, alar, aral, aral, arlu to spread, become distended, open, blossom; alar, aral, aral, alai a flower; alarike blossom- ing; alarisu, alarcu to cause to spread, blossom, etc.; aral-uni, alar-uni the large black humble bee, Bombinatrix glabra. Tu. alaruni, araluni, araluni to open, expand, bloom, blossom; aral-uni the black bee. Te. alaru to open as 2 flower, expand, shine, glitter, rejoice; m. a flower, blossom, (K. also) splendour, joy; splendid; (K.) alarcu to blossom. ? Cf. 248 Ta, alari. / Cf. Mar. aral a flower; OMar. (Master) arala id. DED(S) 209. 248 Ta, alari beauty. Ka. alampu, lampu beauty, ornament, pleasure, magnificence. Te. alararu to shine, glitter; suit well, be proper, fit; rejoice, be pleased; alarincu to please, gratify. ? Cf. 247 Ta. alar. DED(S) 210.

Ta. alavatgu custom, practice (< Te.). Te. ala-vadu to be accustomed, be practised in; become a habit; ala-varincu to practise, learn; habituate, teach; ala-varucu to habitu- ate, teach; ala-vatu habit, custom, practice; habitual. DED 212.

Ma. aliyuka to melt, dissolve (as salt, heart); alikka, aliyikka to melt (¢7.); aliccal, alivu melting, compassion; ali-ppunnu foul ulcer. Kod. ali- (aliv-, alifij-) to dissolve (intr.); (alip-, alic-) to dissolve (tr.). Tu. aliyuni to dissolve, decay; ? eliyuni, étuni to melt (intr.; as any soft substance, butter, lead, etc.); elipuni, elpuni, @lavuni id. (¢r.). DED 213. 251 Ka, ale interj. used in calling to woman. Pa. ale exclamation used as voc. (a form alor with the termination of the 2nd pl. is also recorded). Konda ale, ale ale term of endear- ment used in addressing a familiar person. Pe. ale particle used with vocative. Kui ale term of affection addressed to a child. Malt. ale O! employed to a plurality of persons. DEDS 35.

Ta. alku (alki-) to shrink, diminish, lessen; alkal deficiency, poverty; akku (akki-) to be reduced, shrink, be dejected, become closed (as a flower); nalkir (-v-, -nt-) to be poor, indigent, destitute; m. poverty. Ka. akkudisu to become small, wane. DED 2.

Ta. alkul side, waist, pudendum muliebre. Ma. alkitam vulva. Ko. algalv hindquarters (of person or animal), loins. To, agelf buttocks and thighs. /? Cf. Skt. alga- the groin (VS, MS). DED 214. 254 Kur. aikhnad to laugh, laugh at, mock, seduce; alkhanakhrna to laugh together, have unlawful intercourse; alkhaban’na joking relations. Malt. alge to laugh; alqro person addicted to laughter. DED 215.

Ta. alaku grains of paddy, ear of paddy or other grain. Kui algu rice obtained from paddy without boiling it. Kur. alkhya parched rice (H. cytlya; the paddy is first steeped in tepid water, then parched, finally unhusked by means of a wooden pedal and winnowed), DED (S) 216.

Ta. alli waterlily, Nymphaea lotus, inner flower petals as opposed to pulli fila- ment of stamen. Ma. alli pericarp of lotus with the surrounding filaments, anthers, stamen, lotus. Tu. alli, allige waterlily, flower of N. speciosum, Te. alli the water plant called N. alba. DED 217.

Ta. alli iron-wood tree, Memecylon edule. Ka. alamar, (Lush.) alle a sp. of Mem- ecylon. Kod. udit-ale M. edule. Tu. alimaru, alimary Memecylon. Te, (B., quoting Roxb.) alli M. edule. DED 218. alliyan [25] avvai
Ta. alliyan stray elephant separated from the fold; alliyam Krgna’s dance when he broke the tusk of the elephant that was set upon him by Kamsa. Ma. alliyan female elephant. DED(N) 219.
Te. alliya, alle bow-string. Pa, alka id. Cf. 260 Ta. allu. DED 220.

Ta. allu (alli-) to knit, build, weave; to interlace closely (intr.). Ka. allu to join, connect, knit nets, etc., plait, braid, wattle together. Te. allu to plait, braid, weave, wattle, intertwine, compose, fabricate; alludu plaiting, braiding, netting. Kol. al- (alt-) to weave; (SR) all- id., dress (hair). Nk. all- to weave, plait. Pa, alc- to wind round (as snake or creeper round tree); alt- to be twined around; altayp- (altayt-) to wind round (¢7.). Ga. (S) all- to twine. Go. (Ko.) all- to weave, plait (Voc. 97). Konda al- (af-, an-) to twine, twist, plait; alpa- (-t-) to twist or twine, plait or weave a cot, weave a garland; arpa- (-t-) to spread as a creeper; altay 4- to be interwoven, grow thickly and densely as a creeper; (BB) ar-, (af-, an-) to plait, weave. Kui alpa (alt-) to weave sticks together into fence, interlace, plait, intertwine. Kuwi (S) allinai to inter- tangle, plait; (Su.) al- (-it-) to plait (hair).

Ka. (Gowda) avdu ankle of an animal (sic) (avadu is given as ‘standard Ka.’). Tu. abadu, avadu hoof. 263 Ta, avanam, amanam a measure = 20,000 areca-nuts; ampanam a grain measure. Ma, avanam a weight or measure, a mason’s rule or level. /? Cf. Pali ambana-, ammana- a measure of capacity; Skt. armana- a measure of one drona; Turner, CDIAL, no. 688. DED 223.

Ta. avarai field-bean, Dolichus lablab. Ma. avara, avarakka, amara, amarakka country bean. Ko. avr bean. To. efir id. Ka, avare, avari, fvare, amare pulse called D. lablab. Tu. abaré, avaré a common country bean, Lablab vulgaris; abadé, avadé, avadé a kind of bean. DED 224.

Ta. avalam suffering, pain, distress, poverty, want, sorrowing, care, anxiety, fault, sickness, disease; avali (-pp-, -tt-) to suffer, be distressed, lament, weep, be flurried; avalai pain; avali (-pp-, -tt-) to weep, cry, lament, grieve; avalati complaint, grievance. Ma. aval sorrow, pain; avalati vexation, grumbling, complaint. Ke. havali trouble, annoyance. Tu. havali trouble, difficulty, evil. Te. avali trouble; avadi id., danger, calamity. Br. avalénging to become confused, feel embar- rassed. DED(S) 225.
Ta. (DCV) avali jungle-cork tree; {Ta. Lex, cowhage]. Ma. (DCV) aval jungle cork tree. DEDS 36.

Ta. avi (-v-, -nt-) to become repressed subdued, cease, desist from action, becomr extinguished, fail, diminish, perish; (-pp- -tt-) to suppress, repress, subdue, extinguish destroy; avivu extinction, annihilation; nav: Cpp-, -tt-), navig (-pp-, -tt-) to extinguish. Ma. avikka, amikka to destroy. To, ofy- (ofs- to calm down (intr.), cease talking; (ofc-) tc calm (f7.), make to cease talking. Ka. avi tc go out, be extinguished, perish. Te. aviyu tc be destroyed. Go. (SR.) aviyand to be tired (M.) avkanad to tire; (Ko.) ayv- to be tired exhausted (Voce. 101). ? Konda namb- (fire to cool down, (lamp) to be put out; nap- tc extinguish. Cf. 2341 Ta. avi and 2343 Ta . camai. DED(S) 226. 268 Ta, avi (-v-, -nt-) to be boiled, cookec by boiling or steaming, swelter; (-pp-, -tt-) t« boil in a liquid, cook by boiling or by steam ing; n. boiled rice, food; aviyal boiling cooking, food, swelter, sultriness; avai (-pp- -tt-) to cook, boil; avig boiled rice; (Tinn. aviyalu a kind of vegetable curry. Ma. aviyuk: to boil on fire, be digested; avikka, amikka t: boil, digest; aviyal a kind of vegetable curry boiling, cooking. DED 227.

Ta. aviri, avuri indigo plant, Indigoferc tinctoria, Ma, amari, avari id. Te. aviri id DED 228. 270 Ta, avit single grain of boiled rice. Ka avudu a grain of anything; agalu, agulu ; grain of boiled rice, a small lump of ric: kneaded into a ball. Kor. (O.) avri cooke: rice. DED 229.
Ta. avucu neatness, trimness. Ka, avus: properness, neatness. Cf. 2342 Ta. camai esp. Ka. savaru, savasu. DED 230.

Ta. avuliy2 pomfret, Stromatens. Ma avoli, avéli, #koli Stromatens. DED 231. 273 Ta, avvai mother, old woman, woma’ ascetic; tavvai mother, elder sister, goddes of misfortune as the older sister of Laksm’ evvai our younger sister. Ko. av mother mother’s sister or female parallel cousin. Tc af, afuf id. Ka. avve, avva mother (used as title of respect and love), grandmother, an elderly woman; abbe mother. Kod. avy mother, mother’s sister or female parallc cousin. Tu. abbé an elderly woman, matror Te, avva mother, grandmother, an old womar Pa. avva father’s mother. Ga. (S.2) avve ol woman. Go. (Ma.) avva, (L.) ava mothe: (Tr.) awwal (pl. awwahk) mother, dam c man or beast; (Ph.) avval, (Ma.) avval (p avvasku), (S.) aval @l avahku), (L.) avi mother; (Tr.) maiwal my mother (lit. ov mother); miwal your mother (lit. mother « you plural); (Tr.) awhari mother, dam of ma or beast; (W.) awhari, (Ph.) avhati, (G.) av: hari mother (for -hari, see 2357) (Voce. 102 araku : [26] azivu (Koya Su.) yavva mother, Konda ave (pl. avek) id. Mand. ava elder brother’s wife. Kui (K.) ava id. Kuwi (D.) ava id.; (F.) awa sister-in-law. / Cf. Pkt. avvad- mother, Mar. ava, avaji term of courtesy in addressing a Sudra woman; Turner, CDIAL, no. 904. DED(S, N) 232.
Ta. araku beauty, happiness, excellence; arakan, arakiyan, agakiyan handsome man; fem. agaki; anafiku beauty, form (or with

Tu. barvé a kind of fish. Te. varava- c&pa id. DED 4316. : §276 Ta. val strong, hard, forceful, skilful; vallam, vallamai, vallai strength; vallavan, vallan strong man, capable man; vallalan mighty man, hero; vallu (valli-) to be able; vallunar capable people; vala-kkai, valah-kai tight hand; valatu right side, victory; valam, valan strength, power, right side, victory, authority; valavai ability, powerful person; yalavan capable man; vali strength, power; (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to be strong, hard, com- pel; valitu that which is strong, force, com- pulsion; valippu firmness, stability; vali-patn to become strong; valimai strength, skill, hardness; valiya strong, big; valiyan strong, powerful, skilful man; valivu strength; valu id., skill, ability; (-pp-, -tt-) to be strong or hard; valuppu firmness, strength; valumai strength, force, violence; vallapam strength, power, ability; varpu strength, hardness; vanpu, vanmai strength, firmness; vanmam force; val greatness, abundance; va&limai greatness, strength. Ma. val, valu, valiya strong, powerful, great; valiya forcibly, suddenly; valant-kai right hand; valam the right or strong side; valippam, valima great- ness, bigness; valluka to be able, strong; vallabham power, capacity; vanpu greatness, strength; vampikka to grow large, grow arrogant, Ko, val powerful, very, right; val kay right hand; val(n) man who is clever at cheating; fem. valy, valc; val pat excessive; yalc- (valc-) (man) becomes stout, (heart) becomes bold, (grain)

Ta. ari (-v-, -nt-) to perish, be ruined, decay, be mutilated, fail, be defeated, suffer, be used up; (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy, spend, ruin, damage, efface, bring to a close; #. ruin, destruction; agipu, arippu destruction; agimpu evil deed; agimpan one who does evil; agivatu harm; arivu destruction, loss, immoral action, expenditure, defeat, distress; axan, aranam corpse (or with 284 Ta. agunku). Ma, agiyuka to be destroyed, spent, sold, become loose, untied; agikka to loose, waste, spend; ari, azivu, ariccal expense; agayuka to slacken (ntr.); agekka to loosen, slacken. Ko. alc- (alc-) to be erased; erase; alyv destruction. To, ody- (ods-) (money) is spent; (odc-) (stones of ruinous building) slip down; to spend, expend (property at funeral), over- come (in contest). Ka. ari to be ruined, be destroyed, perish, decay, disappear, die; to ruin, destroy, etc.; m. ruin, waste, damage, loss; aripu, agavu, agivu, arusu, ariyisu, apisu, azicu, arupu to destroy; azipu, agivu, arthu, arupu, aruvu, aguhu, agvu ruin, destruction, death; agika destroyer; agikisu to efface, erase; (PBh.) agkadu to be consumed, finished; alaru to be destroyed. Tu. arpuni to efface, waste, obliterate; alipuni, aliyuni, alipuni to perish, die, be destroyed, be ruined; aliyuni to perish, die, become extinct; alivu, alikattu, aliyantara extinction, ruin; areduni to spoil, corrupt, mar, destroy, ruin. Te. adiy-asa vain hopes; aduva, (coll.) adava old, damaged (as goods); dakka damage, harm, injiry, loss; dappi

Ta. arificil, arificu, anificil sage-leaved alangium, Alangium lamarckii. Ma. ariiinil A, decapetalum; araiiitil the buttress tree (2 = anififil). / Cf. Skt. atkota-, aitkotha-, ankola-, afkolla-, atikotaka- A. hexapetalum; Turner, CDIAL, no. 113. DED(S) 238. 281 Ma, arivu tenderness, love. Ko, akm affection. To. arkyn passionate woman. Ka. (K.?) azipu desire, affection; (Kitt.) agugu, arkar to love; arti, arti love, desire, a pleasant object; arkar, akkare, akkara, akkaru, akkasa love, affection. Tu. arkame strong desire, eagerness; (B-K.) akkare affection, fondness; (Manner) arti amusement, fun, curiosity, aru [27] a’ fondness (or with 381 Ta. drvam). Te. akkara desire. Cf. 276 Ta. aral. DED(S) 239.

Ta. aru (-v-, -t-) to cry, weep, lament; arai (-pp-, -tt-) to call, cry out, summon; nv. crying, weeping; arukuni tearful person, one who is always crying; agukai pathetic senti- ment; aguiku (arunki-) ‘to weep; arufkal loud noise, uproar. Ma, aruka to weep, cry; ayaykkuka to shout, cry out. Ko. ag- (art-) to weep, cry; agl, akl act of lamenting; arg- (argy-) (cattle) bellow in protest at ill-trea- ment. To. or- (ory-) (child) weeps. Ka. ag, aru (axt-, att-) to weep, cry; ar, aru, aga, arke, arke, akke weeping, lamenting; arasu, arisu ' to make to cry; aruvike crying; agukuli an easily crying or fretful person. Tu. arpini to weep, cry; arpdta lamentation; arpele one easily moved to tears, a fretful person; leppuni to call, invite; leppu, lavu a cry. ? Te, éducu to cry, weep, lament, shed tears; édupu weeping, crying, lamentation. Kol, ar- (art-) to weep, (crow) caws, (peacock) screams, (tiger) roars. Nk, ar- to weep, cry. NK. (Ch.) al- to weep; caus. alup-. Pa. ar- to weep, cry (of various animals); artip- (artit-) to cause to weep or cry. Ga. (Oll.) ar- to weep, cry (of various animals); arup- (arut-) to make to cry; (S.) ad- to weep; (S.3) ad- (af-) id., cry like an animal. Go. (A. Y. W. Ma. S.) ar-, (Ph.} ar-, (Tr.) ayana to weep, cry; caus. (S-R.) adusana, (S.) arah- (Voc. 84). Konda arba- to weep, cry. Pe. ar- (art-) to weep, cry (of animals); intens. arba-; caus, rat-; intens. of caus. yatpa-. Mand. yi- to cry. Kui riva (it-) to weep, cry, lament, howl, utter a sound (b

Ta. arukku dirt, stain; excrement, impurity of mind or soul, discharges after confinement. Ma. arukku, aruinu, arukkal dirt. filth. To. 6sk dirty. Tu. adaka, adka, adukajagu an unclean place, a burying place. DED 241.

Ta. argunku (arunki-) to be spoiled, injured, disfigured; arunkal ruin; aruku (aruki-) to rot, decompose, putrefy; arukal rotten fruit, putrefied matter, uncleanness; agan, ajanam corpse (or with 277 Ta. ari). a, agukuka to rot, decay; arukkuka to putrefy. ? To. olg far articles (par) in the outer room or outer section of the room of ti- dairy (i.e. more exposed to pollution than those of the inner section; cf. Ta. agukal uncleanness). Ka. agugu to decay, rot, spoil by being overboiled; arkame state of being decayed, dissolved, digested; arku to be worn out, decayed, digested; arkisu, akkisu to digest; argu to be dissolved, decay. DED(S, N) 242. 285 Ta, aguttu (arutti-) to press down, press hard, make firm, compact, insist on, plunge, immerse; #, pressure, imprint; aruttar hardness (as of wood), closeness of texture a< of cloth, durability, emphasis, profoundnes: in one’s mental application; aruntu (arunti-\ to become pressed, be impressed, become firm, compact, persist, be immersed. Ma. aruttu strong texture, pregnant speech, ful! of dregs; aguttuka to fix, set; aruntuka to be immersed, plunge, sink. Ko, aj- (aj-) to dir into liquid, dip rice into broth and remove at once before it is soaked, dunk. Ka, ardu. addu, ajju, eddu to immerse, dip, dye; sink in a fluid; addayisu to immerse, put into: addike, adduge immersion. Tu. arduni to wash or bleach clothes. Te. addu to dip, dye. print with colours; (B also) to press gently, blot (ink); addakamu chintz-printing, dying with col

Ta. ayuvam = greatness, abundance, excellence, expanse. Ka. alumba_ excess, greatness, excellence, further, etc. DED 246. 288 Pa, arj- (flavour of something) is perceived (particularly salt, cup ayjoto); arcip- (arcit-) to add as flavouring. Konda al- (at-, an-) (tongue) to get the taste of. Pe. and- (-t-) (taste) comes out, is perceived. Mand. and- (taste, e.g. of salt) to pervade food, be present, be perceived. Kur. andng to spread throughout, pervade, be felt, tasted in every portion of the mass prepared (of spices and ferments; e.g. salt, bek andca). DED(S, N) 247.

Ta. al handful, anything contained within the hollow of the hand; allu (alli-) to take up in the hollow of the hand; 7. handful of grain given on the threshing floor or in the bazaar as perquisite. Ma, alluka to take up with the hollow of the hand. Kur. (Hahn) lapna to measure with the hollow of the hand; lapp a handful; (Gr.) laph the quantity of grains, etc. that one can hold upon the flat of the hand, the fingers being very slightly curved upward. Malt. lape a measure, as much as is contained in two hands put together; ldpye to measure out with two hands put together. DEDS 38.

Ta. al strength, firmness. Ka. ala, alavi, alavu, alavu power, strength, force, ability, possibility, practicability. Te. alavi power, ability, possibility, practicability; alavu power, ability, strength, exertion; lavu strength, power, ability, bigness, fatness, corpulence, robustness; adj. big, large, stout, corpulent, robust. Kol. la-v fat. Pa. lav strength. Go. lav (Mu. Ko.) strength, (M.} force (Voe. al [28] ali 3112). Konda alvi energy, stamina; al id., endurance; la@vu much. Cf. 399 Ta. al. DED(S) 248.
Ta. al closeness, thickness; allal crowdedness, closeness; allaju (allati-) to be close; allu (alli-) to be dense, thickly interwoven; alam density, closeness. Ma. al narrowness. DED 249.

Ta. al, alam sharpness, keenness of edge. Ma. al sharpness; allay-adyi-poka to become very thin and sharp. DED 250. 294 Ta, allai side of the body. Ko, alk a-r- (a-c) to shake Gntr.), walk in tired fashion; alk a-{- (a-c-) to shake (¢7.), wag; al belly (al a-r- belly shakes, i.e. fear comes); alkat waist on either side of belly. Ka. al, allu shaking, rising, and falling with alternate motions, heaving of the alle’s; alle that portion of the abdomen of man which is immediately below the costal margin, is regarded as a very tender spot, and heaves in quick breathing; the weak spot, tenderness, weakness; the flank of an animal; (Hav.) alaficu to agitate (liquid). Tu. alantuni to shake, move; allé, allé the side, groin, hip. DED(S) 251.

Ta. ala (-pp-, -nt-) to measure, limit, define; alapu, alappam measurement; alappu id., bounds, limit; alavyu measure, extent, size, number; alavi limit; alavai measure, bounds, limit; alavan one who measures the grain. Ma, alakka to measure; alavu measure, capacity, measure of time; alattam measure- ment; alavan measurer. Ko, alv- (ald-) to measure; alv limit. To. al- (al@-) to measure; alt- (ob) measure. Ka. ale (alad-, aled-) to measure; 7. measure; alate, alte measure, extent, measurement; alavu, alabu measure. -Kod. ala- (alap-, aland-) to measure; alate act of measuring. Tu, ala measure, capacity; alaka large basket for measuring rice; alaké, alaté measurement; alapuni to measure; lakké measure, mark; lappu measuring, measurement; lappuni to measure; lappivuni to cause to measure. Te, alavi measure, extent; lavu dimensions, magnitude. Pe. lac- (-c-) to measure. Mand. lec- id. Kui dasa (dasi-) id.; m. act of measuring; (K.) dac- to measure. Kuwi (F.) lacali, (S.) lcinai, (Su.) lac- Cit-) id.; (isr.) lask- Cit-) id. (pre- sumably pl. action). DED(S) 252.

Ta. ala (-pp-, -nt-) to mingle, blend, talk together, hold converse, gossip; alappu chatter; alapp-ala to chatter; alavu (alavi-) to blend, mingle, mix; alav-alavu to hold intimate intercourse, live socially, converse freely; m. intimacy; aldvu (alavi-) to mingle, converse, hold social. intercourse, stu (é.); ali (-v-, -nt-) to mix, mingle; alai (-v-, -nt-) to be mixed, mingled; to mix up (tr.), mingle. Ma. alayuka to mix up, mingle. Ka, ala, alavu, alavi joining, contact, contiguity, nearness. Tu. alavuni to mix; alamuni to mix with the hand; alavuni to mix, temper; alavu steel; allavuni to mix; alejuni, alejjuni to mix, mingle, be united; alejivuni to cause to mingle. Te. (B., K.) ldyu to approach; alamu to seize, lay hold of, take; (K. also) mix with, be one with. Konda lap- (-t-) to join two pieces together, fit one into or on another. Pe, lab- (lapt-) to fit into (intr.); lap- (-t-) to insert, fit into (t7.). Br. hailing (present halé-, imper. halh, past halk-) to seize, hold, buy, overtake, detect, tack together (a tear); get caught, stick; ha- in hatining, etc., s.v. 3098 (MBE 1962, p. 63). DED (S) 253. 297 Go, (Elwin) lakdna, lakanj the sacrifice after a successful hunt (Voc. 3103). Pe. rik- Ct-) to offer worship, sacrifice; intens, paibga-; rikel jini priest who offers sacrifice. Mand. rak- to offer worship, sacrifice. Kui laka (laki-) id.; m. a sacrifice, worship; laka sdka a sacrifice, worship. Kuwi (S.) lak- to sacrifice; lakinai to praise; lakwinai to invoke; (Ma

Ta. alam saltpan, maritime tract, saline soil, sea; alakkar sea, ocean, saltpans; alatti woman of maritime tracts; alavar persons belonging to the caste of salt manu- facturers; alar saltishness, brackishness; upp- alam saltpan, saline land; ump-alam saltpan. Ma. alam salt marsh, saltpan; alakkar sea; upp-alam salt marsh; alavan a member of the caste of salt manufacturers. Te. upp-alamu saltpan, salt-works. DED 255.

Ta. ampi, (PPTT) ampi common mushroom. Ma. (Tiyya) alambu a variety of mushroom. Ka. anabe, anambe, alambe, alambe mushroom, Tu. limbu, nambu mushroom, toadstool, fungus; (B-K.) alambu, alambu mushroom, Kor. (T.) armbi id, / Cf. Mar. alabé, alabhé mushroom; Pkt. (DNM) alamba- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 1365. DED (N) 305. 301 Ta, ali (-v-, -nt-) to be attached; (pp-, -tt-) to protect, nourish, be gracious, show favour, create desire; m. love, grace, desire; alittu, alitu that which deserves pity; alintar loving persons; aliyan one who has great love, gracious benefactor; one who deserves pro- tection. Ma. ali love, affection; alikkuka to grant, bestow, protect; aliyan one who is kind and affectionate. Ko. ayl mog twin children. Ka, alipu, alupu to be addicted to, fong for; m.. hankering after, desire, love; alaviga friend. Te. (B., K) lacu to long for, desire. DED 256.

Ta. ali (-v-, -nt-) to become mellow, be over-boiled; alaivu becoming soft as rice by over-cooking. Ma, aliyuka to be over- ali [29] a tipe, decay; aliccal mellowness, decay. Tu. alufguni to be soft (as a ripe fruit)’ DED 57,
Ka. alige, alage a capacious earthen vessel to hold water or grain. Te, alaki an earthen pot. DEDS 39.

Ka. alaku, aluku to prick, throb (as a thorn in the flesh); ”. the pricking of a thorn, state of being pricked as with a thorn, State of being distressed or annoyed. Te. alugu, aluyu to be angry or displeased, take offence; aluka, alukuva anger, displeasure. DEDS 40.

Te. (K.) aluku to sprinkle, scatter (ice, etc., in a net to lure birds). Kol. (SR) alk- to sprinkle. Nk. alk- id. Pa. alk- id. Mand. ranj- to bail out (water) by sprinkling. Kui lanja (lanji-) to sprinkle, scatter around; n, sprinkling; (K.) lanj- to bail water with hands. Kuwi (Su.) ranj- to bail out (pool, by sprinkling action); (F.) ranjali to bail out; (S.) lanjinai to scatter, speckle. ? Ta. alavu (alavi-) to spill. Cf. note on 3790 Ta. nol. DED(S) 258. :

Ta. alukku (alukki-) to be perturbed, frightened. Ma. alukkuka to start, shrink, cramp of limbs; alukkam awe, fear; alarkka to lament, cry; alarcca bellowing; alappu shriek. Ka. alar, alaku, aliku, aluku, alku, alkar to fear; alarisu, alakisu, alikisu to frighten; alar, alaku, alku, alarpu fear, anguish; alasu sound im coitu. Tu. alkuni, alkuni to be afraid, start, shudder; alku timidity; timid, frightened. Te. aluku to be afraid; n. fear, dread; aluku = aluku 2.; alukari coward. DED 259.

Ka. alur to enclose, cover, overspread, prevail, fall upon; vb.n. alurke. Te. alamu to spread, extend, (K. also) overspread (of smoke over sky), prevail intensely (of passions); lancu to spread (intr.); n. lipu. Konda almi- (-t-) to level, spread out (as pieces of wood after the forest is felled, in order to fet them dry). DED(S, N) 260.

Ta. alai anthill, hole in the ground, hollow in tree, cave. Ma. ala hole in trees, in the ground; allappu hollow, hole. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) ale hole. Ko, al cave. To. olb animal’s den, cave. Te. liga hole, burrow. Kur, alap hollow place underground, cavern; lata hole, cavity, den. DED(S, N) 261. 309 Kol. alng- (alankt-) to kill. Nk. alay- id. Or both with 277 Ta. ari, where see possible etyma in Pa. Ga. DEDS 41.
Ta. arafi-kai palm of the hand. Te, ara-céyi id.; ara-kalu sole of the foot. Kol. (SR.) arankei (n, not n), (Hislop) arungkei Palm of the hand; (Kamaleswaran). DEDS(N)

Ta. aram moral or religious duty, virtue, dharma, Yama; aravan one who is virtuous, god, Buddha, ascetic, etc.; ara virtue, that which is holy, female asceti araviya virtuous; agaviyan virtuous ma’ aran sacrificer. Ma. aram Jaw, dharma. K. ara, aru virtue, charity, alms, law, dharm Yama. ? Cf. 405 Ta. dru. DED 262. 312 Ta, aral black sand found on tl seashore, water. Ma. aral black sand. K: ani aral, arulu, arla, arlu mud, clay. DF 313 Ta, aruva, aruva-natu [one of the 1. region[s}] where a vulgar dialect of Tamil w spoken, possibly a large portion of sou’ Arcot district; aruvar, aravar the Tamils. K arava, aravu, aruvu, arva Tamil; aravagitt araviti a Tamil woman. Tu. arava Tamil. 7 aravamu Tamil language; aravalu Tamiliar DED 264.

Ta. ari (-v-, -nt-) to know, understan perceive, ascertain; m. knowledge; arikk notice; arivi (pp-, -tt-) to make know announce; arivu knowledge, intelligenc perception; arivan wise man; arivai wisdo: aruku (aruki-) to know. Ma, ariyuka - know, understand; be known; arivu knov edge, understanding; ariyikka, arivikka make known; ariyippu notice; arivippik! to inform. Ko. ayr- (arc-) to know; arv sens wisdom; arkym information piven, roy proclamation. To. ary- (ars-) to know; ary bet- (°bety-), arybet- (°bety-) to be insan mad (see 5511 Ta. veri; ‘to be deranged understanding’). Ka. ari (arid-, arit-) to kno’ arike knowledge, information; arita, ariv aravu, aruvu knowledge, perception; arip arupu, arisu to make known, communica! reveal; arime, ariha knowledge; (K.”) ariy mike ignorance; arake searching, inquirir arasu to inquire after, seek, search; m. 1 search, inquiry. Kod. ari- (ariv-, arifij-) find out; arip- (aripi-) to inform, tell; ari knowledge. Tu. arupu, aruhu knowled: understanding, perception; arike conser confession, request; a. malpuni to reve confess, inform, represent, request. K- (T.) ade to search. Te, erfigu to know, und stand, perceive; eruka, erukuva knowled: understanding; eruku gypsy, fortune-tell- Kol, (Kin.) erka knowledge (< Te.). / harrifing to inquire, ask. DED(S, N) 265.

Ta. aru (aruv-, arr-/(mod.) arunt-) be severed, break as a rope, cease, becor extinct, perish, be decided, settled; (-p: -tt-) to break off, cut, part asunder, sev: cleave, exterminate, determine, resolve; a: becoming detached by cutting; aruti er close, termination, limit, destruction; arup harvest, reaping the crop, piece, secti sawn; arumpu dearth, famine; arumai stability, transience; arainan one who rea: aruvatai harvest; arram destruction, ru destitution. Ma, aruka to be severed, cut o break, cease; arukka to sever, cut off, deci: aru severing, cutting; aruti end, limit; arup aru [30] . anv severing, sawing, harvest; arumpu dearth, arram extremity, end. Ko. arv- (art-) to cut (meat) into small pieces for broth; armbar- (armbaf-) to Starve; ? arvanga-r place where no one has lived (for ka-r wilderness, see 1438); arvaty n. pr. Badaga village (the Badaga name is Aratti; probably < arv-paty ‘Badaga village in distant, lonely place’). To, arf- (art-) to cut, reap. Ka. aru to be severed or disjoined, be cut asunder, cease, end; araka state of being broken or injured as a tile, ball, etc.; arake fragment, piece. Kod. ara- (arap-, arat-) to cut. Tu. arpuni to efface, obliterate. Te. aru to be destroyed, decrease; ara defect, incompleteness; aragora defect, deficiency; rakku, (K. also) rakku to scratch; n. scratching. Kol, ark- (arakt-) to harvest grain by cutting (or with 175 Ta. ari). Nk. ark- to cut, reap (as for Kol.). Kuwi arna (Isr.) crop, harvest, (Su.) crop, culti- vati
Ta. aru (aruv-, arr-/arunt-) to abide, dwell; arai place of encampment, camp; (Annamalai, p. 875) aruppu fort; ? alku, arku to abide, dwell. Te. arupu to keep, preserve, secure, DEDS(N) 43.

Ta. aruvai cloth, garment; arai curtain. Ka. ara, arave, arive, aruve cloth. Tu. arve a cloth, old rag. Te. ara rag. Cf. 5163 Ta. (Dravikkai. DED (S) 268. 319 Ta, arai (-v-, -nt-) to speak, sound; 7. sound, word, reply. Ka. -aracu, aricu, arucu, arcu to cry out aloud, clamour, scream; racce crying aloud, noisy and abusive clamour. Tu. areduni to low. Kor. (T.) ajike speech. Te. ayacu to cry or cry out, shout, clamour, shriek, bark, bray, crow, bellow; arapu a ery, shriek, yell, shout, clamour; ranke roar, .cry; rantu noise; rajju useless talk; (B) vb, to be talkative, prattle; n. boast. Kol. raz- (rast-) to say, speak, talk nonsense. Nk. (Ch,) arap- to crow. Ga, (Ol.) arg- (cow) to bellow. 2Go, (SR. Tr. W. Y. Mu.) ran(g)- to abuse (Voce, 3015). Kur yaspa (ast-) to ring, make a reverberating sound. DED (S) 269.

Ta. arai (-v-, -nt-) to slap, beat (as a drum), hammer, cut in pieces, hack, beat upon (as wind, waves); n slap, blow, dashing as of waves, wave, cutting, chopping; (Koll.) areccal a blow. Ma. ara a blow; arayuka to beat hard, (rain) pelts, beat drums. Ka. are to strike, slap, beat; . a slap, stroke. Kur, assnd to beat, play on or sound (any stringed or percussion instrument). Cf. 2421 Te. caracu. DED 270.
Ta. arai rock, ledge, grinding stone. Ko. ar flat rock. To. ar id. Ka. are stone, rock, slab; rayi stone (< Te.). Te. rayi, rayi (stem 12-, ra-) stone, rock; rappa a smal! stone. DED(N) 271.

Ta. ayai room, apartment, chamber, cell, drawer, compartment, square ona chess- board. Ma, ara partition, room, magazine, treasury. Tu. adé inner room, small room, Te, ata, arra room, chamber, partition or division. Go. (SR.) arra part of the room; (Pat.) arra a room (Voc. 83); (Mu.) ahpi room of a house (Voe. 114). DEDG, N) 272.

Ta. aruvaru (-pp-, -tt) to loathe, abhor, feel disgusted with; aruvaruppu disgust, loathing, abhorrence. Ma. arekka to loathe, dislike; areppu qualm, aversion; areppikka to make to loathe. Tu. argiliyuni, argilyuni to loathe, be disgusted; argule loathsomeness, disgust, aversion. DED (S) 273.
Ta. arcixam, arcirai, acciram early dewy season, first half of the dewy season. Ka. aykil cold, cold dew, frost, snow, cold season. DED 274,

Go. (Mu.) arr- to be correct, be satis- factory; (Elwin) arro taboo, used chiefly of food (3sg.. neg. = it is not correct); (L.) aror enemy (Voc. 82). Kui abga (< ag-b-; agd-) to be seemly, be proper, be fitting, be suitable, suit, fit, agree, fulfil, ». suitability. DEDS 44. 326 Ta, anantar sleep, drowsiness, stupor, loss of consciousness, inebriety, confusion of mind; anantal sleep, drowsiness, stupor. Ma. anantal light sleep. DED 276.

Ta. anal fire, heat (as of fever), warmth, glow, thunderbolt; (analv-, ananr-) to burn, glow, blaze, be hot, cause heat (as the sun, fire, fever); anali fire, sun; anarkal flint; anarru (anarri-) to heat, make hot, burn, affect with colic pains, be angry with; groan with pain. Ma. anal, analca, anacca, anappu fire, heat; analuka to become hot; analkkuka to be hot, be warm; anattuka to make warm or hot. Ka. analu heat. / Cf. Skt. anala-. DED 277.

Ko. anv fertility of ground. Ka. anu, anuyu, anavu fitness, propriety, nicety, loveliness, that is pleasing, charming or beautiful, worth, merit, readiness, success, proper or correct way, scheme, device, anuppu [31] circumstance, opportunity. Ju. anutana fit- ness, opportunity. Te, anuvu fitness, con- venience, means, contrivance; suitable, fit, convenient. ? 7a. a, am beautiful pretty; ammai beauty, prettiness. Cf. 2423 Ka. cannu. DED 1999(d).

Ta. anuppu (anuppi-) to send, ac- company one a little way out of respect; ampakam leave, permission (< Te.) Ma. anuppuka to send. Ka. ampaka sending, dispatching, entertainment given to friends at their departure. Te, anucu, ancu, anupu, ampu to send; ampakamu sending away, dismissal, permission to go or leave. Ta. alai sugar-cane press, sugar-cane. Ma, Gla sugar-mili. Ka. ale a sugar-cane press or mill, Tu, ale a press, mill. DED 329.

Ma. atta bitter gourd. Ka, dvade a bitter acute-angled cucumber. Te. Avada, (VPK) amada-kaya, amada-kaya id. DED(S) 330. 390 Ta, avam quiver; bow-string; jfiva- nazi, dva-nazikai quiver. Ma, ava-nazi id. / 2 < Skt. capa-; Pkt. cava- bow. DED 331. 391 Ta, Avarai, avirai tanner’s senna, Cassia auriculata; Tinnevelly senna; avakai T. senna. Ma, aviram C. auriculata, Ko. a-vrm (obl. a-vrt)- id. Ka. dvarike gida id. DED 332.

Ta. avi (-pp-, -tt-) to gape, yawn, open the mouth so as to express loudly; 1, yawn. Ma, 4vi if- to yawn. Ko, a-vaj a yawn. To. o+piily- (0+ piile-) to yawn. Ka. akalisu, akulisu, agulisu to yawn, gape; dkalike, dakulike, agalike, a4gulike yawning, gaping. Kod. a-valic- (aevalici-) to yawn. Tu. avaly a yawn, av-id- to yawn. Kor, (M.) avalsu id. Te. dvalincu, avulincu to yawn, gape; dvulinta a yawn. Pa. am-, av- to yawn; amkud, avkud, akub a yawn. Ga. (Oll.) am- to yawn; (S.2) amk- id.; (8.3) amk- id.; dmkun a yawn. Go. (Ko.) avi a yawn (Voce. 145). Kur. aula’ana to yawn. Malt. 4wole id. Br. avaning id. DED(S) 333. 393 Ta, avi (-pp-, -tt-) to sigh, let out (as smoke); m. breath, sigh, soul, steam, vapour, smoke. Ma, dvi, dvati breath, life, vapour, steam; avi if- to sigh. Ko, a-yv soul, steam, vapour. To. ofy breath; o-foj, o-fy vapour, morning mist, visible breath. Ka, avi, avari, aviri steam, vapour, heat. Te. avi steam, vapour, heat; aviri vapour, exhalation, heat of the breath; avincu to cook by steam or vapour; 4virillu to sweat, steam, evaporate. Pa. akub steam, vapour. Ga. (P.) akum, (8.3) avir steam. Kuwi (Su.) aviri steam, vapour (< Te.). DED(S, N) 334. 394 Ta, avu (Avi-) to desire; aval great desire, craving, earnestness; (PR) dvalar lovers; avadvu (avavi-) to desire, crave for, covet; ava desire for a thing, covetousness; avavan avaricious person; avavu avidity; Ma, avikka to desire; dval desire; Av-@rram excessive desire; akavu gluttony, greediness, To, o-fil desir

Ta. agakku, agakku, arakku ollock, one-eighth of a measure. Ma, agakku handful, one-eighth of a nayi. Ko. ak the olk measure (in numeration: o-r ak one olk; for i-r o-k two olk, cf. 681 Ta. urakku), To. ak the atok measure for ghee or liquid (in numeration: wi-r ak one atok, i-r ak two atok, oy ak five atok, ma-k three atok; as milk measure = 1/4 ko-ny or 1/8 pin). Ka. ayakku one-eighth of a seer. /? Cf. Skt. ddhaka- (Turner, CDIAL, no. 1106). DED 339.

Ta. ai circle, ring, wheel, discus weapon, Ma, agi roundness (as of disk, ring, etc.); funeral pile. Ka. agi roundness, a circle, a discus; api roundness. Te. api spherical, round, globular (applied to pearls). ? Go. GR.) fre potter’s wheel (Voc. 138). DED(N) 340.

Ta. al man, husband, servant, labourer, adult; alan husband; (Tinn.) aliyan id.; an atti [37] aru male, manliness, courage, superiority, warrior; nan manly person; anmai manliness, virility, - courage; atavan man, youth. Ma. al a person, able person, servant, slave; an male; anma bravery. Ko, a-l man, servant, husband. To. o-l man, Toda; o-] coolie (< Ta.). Ka. al servant, soldier, messenger, a grown person in general; at, an male; manliness, bravery; alike state of being a person; alutana, altana service; prowess, valour; alma, anma, anba husband, anmu to be manly, vigorous; 7. manliness, vigour. Kod. a-li servant; a-ni, a-n_a-|i man, male; a-ynuiifii male child (< a-ni-kufifi). Tu. alu person, labourer, messen- ger; Almage servant; apy adj. male; anujovu, anjavu a male, a man (jOvu child); (Bright and Ramanujan) anu boy. Kur, al adult male, husband, servant, mankind; alas an adult male person, husband, friend, servant, soldier, Malt. al-urqe to grow up to maturity. Cf. 291 Ta. al strength. DED(S) 342(a).

Ta. atti woman, wife; ataval woman (lex.). Ka, adahgi a female. Tu. ade a coward, Te. alu, (inscr.) alu (pl. dndru) woman, wife; Gtadi, idadi, ddudi woman; ddangulu females, women; adahgi a man of womanish character and ways; du womanishness. Pa, adey wife. Ga. (S.*) ada payya female calf. Go. (Tr.) ar, (G.Mu.M.) ar (pL W.Mu.Ma. ask) woman (Voce. 139). Konda andu female (prefixed to certain animal names); alu wife; alsi id. (re- fated to 3rd person: ‘his w.’); a1 masir wife and husband; aru wife (N. and W. dialects). Pe. ar (pl, acku) woman, wife. Mand. ar (pl. acke) id.; Sco-mgar (pl. aco-mgahke) woman (for -mgat, see 4616). Kui asa (pl. -ska/-saka) id., female; (Letchmajee) ali woman, wife. Kuwi (S.) adi, (T.) aru wife; (Isr.) ari woman, wife; (D.) aca (pl aska) woman; (S.) aca female. ur, &li woman, wife. ? Ta, alaku hen of fowl, peacock, etc. / Cf. Skt. alf- a woman’s female friend (this is borrowed back as Ma. ali, Ka. ali, ali, Tc. ali, ali id). DED(S) 342(5), DEDS 47. 401 Ta, al-enal onom. expression signifying a dog’s howling. Ma. aluka to cry out, roar; alippu explosion. DED 343.
Ta. arattam agony of extreme sickness. Ka, arita the anxiety of a sick man. Te. aratamu grief, sorrow; ar¥tincu to grieve. DED 344.
Ta. aratdru slander, calumny. Tu. arsivuni to despise, disparage. Te. aradi slander, disgrace; arudfyu ridicule, scorn. Cf. 3397 Ta. tilru. DED(S) 345.

Ta. aru (ari-) to cool, grow cold, be appeased, alleviated, mitigated, be suppressed, heal (as a wound); arru (arri-) to cool (¢.), assuage, appease, alleviate, mitigate, comfort, console, soothe, dry (f., as the hair). Ma. aruka to be extinguished, grow cool, be allayed, calmed, dry up (as land, washed hair, wounds), heal; agruka to cool, allay, calm, dry (t%.). Ko. a-r- (a-ry-) to become cool (liquid or solid), (dew) dries, (wound) heals: a-rc- (a-re-) to make (liquid) cool, make to dry in heat; a-t- (a-ty-) to expose to fire to remove pollution (e.g. wet clothes or meat which might damage child in house, jewelry removed from corpse); arv- (art-) (water) is wanted; ni-r arp thirst; arp a-r- (a-ry-), arp e-gu-y- (e-gu-c-) to take a rest. To. o-r- (o-ry-) (hot water) cools, become dry by heating (e.g. cloth, head, body); o-t- (o-ty-) to dry in heat (#7.); a-l- (a-{-) (stream) goes dry, (pool, puddle) dries up; art- (first stem does not occur) to be thirsty; arp a rest; arp odg- (odgy-) to take a rest; arn (obl. arn-) dry dung; ? ar- (ar8-) to be fatigued; o-r- (o-ry) (dispute) is settled; o-re- (o-r€-) to settle (dispute) (7 < Badaga); o-re- (o-1%-} to reopen settled dispute because the settle- ment was unjust (ie. alleviate, etc.). Ka, ar. aru to be extinguished, go out, grow cool. be calmed, allayed, appeased, dry, become dry, heal (as a wound); aru state of going out, state of being dried, etc.; drisu to cause to go out, allay, dry (t.); ar (art-) to be dried, dry up, disa

Ta. aru (obl. arru-) way, road, path means, manner, method. Ma. dru way, manner edir {38] i Ko. o-yp-a-y (Obl. o-yn-a-t-) path; a-La-r (obi. a-l-a-t-) way, distance; a+r a-r- (a-c-) to settle upon a course of action (see 347 Ta. au). To. o-r (Obl. o.t-) way, entrance into thicket; o-l ar (0b! o-f a-t-) path (cf. 399); tir a-r (obl. a-t-) open space between front of enclosing wall and entrance of house (cf. 3259); po-s a-r (Obl. a-t-) entrance, doorway (ef, 5354); -a-r by way of, through; around, all over; up to (a time), until. Cf, 2417 Go. sarri; ? cf. 311 Ta. aram. DED(S) 347. 406 Pa, édir, (S) @yir hail. Ga, (P. S.*) adir, (S.?) adur id. Go. (G. Mu. Ma.) adur id. (Voc. 129), Konda Anriga, (B.) anrga (andrga) id. Pe, injga id. Mand. anjgi id. Kui aji id. Kuwi (F.) ajjt, (Su.) aji (pl -na) id.; (S.) ajinga hailstones. Cf. 355 Ka. ani, 378 Ka. ari, and

Ta. arru (arri-) to become strong, powerful, be possible, be sufficient, accumu- late (as wealth), sustain, carry; @rral strength, power, prowess, ability, abundance, endur- ance, courage; drra greatly, exceedingly, entirely; @nra excellent, grand, splendid; anral greatness, dignity, abundance, copious- ness; @nravar, anrar, anrdr erudite, wise persons; anrdl lady of exalted character. Ma, arruka to grow richly, thrive; drram much; arral growing; healthy, magnificent growth; adnra wide, excellent. Ko. a-r- (only in negative, following S?-ul) not to be willing to do so-and-so (Su. 1971, p. 342); (only in negative, following S'-l) cannot do so-and-so. To, (TS, song 12) o-xare- you will not be (said to be Badaga; -ar- belongs here). Ka. ar (art-) to be or become strong, be powerful, able, or competent, be possible, can, may, be adequate, be able for, be able to endure; 410 (a) Ta. i demonstr. base expr. the nearer or proximate person or thing; pref. to nouns, expr, nearness [i before consonant, ivv before vowel]; ivan, ival, ivarfivarkal, itu (before consonant)/iktu (before vowel), iv/ ivai this man, this woman, these persons/this person (hon), this thing, these things; adj. inta, inai; Thkan, ifkittu, ihku, itkuttai, inke, itdl, itoli here; impar here; this world; ivan, in this place, this world; infu, intai here, in this world; ittan, iifanam, iwku here; in this manner; itd, it0, inta, intd, ita behold!; intantai on this side; ippafi, ivvatu, innanam, fikanam in this way; i-pp

Ta. anam support; ani id., basis. Ma. anam support. Ka, an, inu to be upheld, rest on, lean against, recline on, lean, bend, support (as the head), bear, endure, suffer; anike, afike, ake leaning on, a staff to lean upon; anisu to bend or hold towards, make lean against, cause to recline on, cause to be up- held, protect; anu leaning on; Apu an object to lean on, refuge, protection. Tu. anipuni to make lean. Te. anu to lean, recline, be a support, lean or recline on; anincu to place or Jay so as to Jean against, lean (t7.), support; Snika support, prop, strength. Ga. (S.°) an-ér- to come to help. Konda anika support, help, prop. ? Kui (tlau) tnba (@{-) to lay one’s head upon; #. reclining. ? Kur. inna to cause pain by being pressed against the skin (as gravel on the road, a knot in a mat). Malt. anrete to lean or rest on pillows. DED(S) 350.

Ka. an(u) to lay hold of, hold, put on, take, seize; ainika, dfke laying hold of, seizing; anke-gol to seize. Te, (SA aci-konu_ to seize, take away; (Sank.} nfcu to snatch, seize, usurp; naci-kolu usurpation. Go. anjanad (Tr.) to catch the blood of a slain animal, (Ph.) to catch something falling (Voe, 124). Pe. anj- (anc-) to catch (some- thing thrown at one). Kui anjo a circular wicker trap for catching fish. DEDS 57. thus; (Asher-Radhakrishnan, p. 150) intarua- kal here you are! Ma, i, i this; ivan, ival, ivar, itu, iva this man, this woman, these persons, this thing, these things; infu in this direction, here; inane, ifnhiné thus; iité, iinita here, hither; ihhOttu hither, this way; it® behold here!; ittaram this kind, thus; ittiti so much; inna this, such; ippati thus; ippuram this side.; inna this, such; ippafi thus; i-ppuram_ this side.; i-ppdl now. Ko. i- near the speaker in ivl, ivr, id this man, this woman, these persons, this thing/these things; adj. i; i- this place; i-k to this place; i-tr from this place; io-n, to-l, io-r, ied this man, woman, etc.; ijn hence; it this direction; ita-k a little this way; itltr from this direction; itervi- in this neighbour- hood; ily now; ilyo-n, ilyo-l, ilyo-r, ilyd this man, woman, etc., now; ila. lo here!; inm (obl. int-) like this, this fashion, this amount; intk to this extent; ina-n/ino-n, ino-l, ino-r, ind such an important man, woman, etc., as this; ina-, inina- adj, such and such (as this); i [39] inta- so great, such an

Ta. ikanai a kind of tree. Ma, ikana(m) a kind of tree. 413 Ta, ikal (ikalv-, ikanr-; ikali-) to dis- agree, hate, be inimical, compete; n. enmity, hatred, battle, war; ikalan warrior, jackal; ikaliyar enemies; ikaloOn enemy; ical (icali-) to be in disagreement with one another, wrangle; icali quarrelsome woman; iyal ikar [41] it (iyalv-, iyanr-; iyali-) to compete, wager; n, rivalry, competition. Ma, ikaluka to hate, reject, vic, compete; ikal fight; igal resistance. DED 353.

Ta. ikar (-v-, -nt-) to slight, despise; be careless, negligent; forget; m. contempt, reproach; ikagcci disparagement, negligence; dislike; ikagvu contempt, scorn. Ma. ikaruka to despise, blame, condemn,
Ta. iku (-pp-, -tt-) to kill, destroy, fell, put to rout; ikul, ikuli thunderbolt, Ma. ikukka, ikaykkuka to conquer. DED 354.
Ta. iku (-pp-, -tt-), (PR) ikai (pp-, -tt-) to give. Ka. ikku to give (as money, alms, a name, medicine); ifgu to give. Cf.
Ta. ikulai woman’s confidante; kindred, friendship. Kuwi (Mah.) iguya mother’s younger sister; iguva father’s younger brother. DEN 7

Te. igul(u)cu, igilincu, ivul(u)cu to grin, show the teeth; (Merolu) iccu to grin. Pa, ikp- (ikt-) id. Kur. ijga’ana, ijigna to show the teeth; ijgd characterised by protruding teeth. Malt, igje to grin, reproach; igjro wry- mouthed. Tu. nijikatruni, nijikavuni, nijkz- vuni, nikavuni to grin; kali nikavuni to show the teeth, as dogs, monkeys; nikuni to appear, as the projecting teeth. Cf. 554 Ta. iru. DED (S) 357.

Te. inkuva a sigh. Go, (Ma.) inka a belch (Voc. 171); (LuS.) inkawata hiccough. Kur. itkhna to cough. Malt. inge id.; inqrése to force phlegm from the throat, hawk; ing-piice hiccough. Br. hikking to hiccough (or probably IA; see Turner, CDIAL, nos. 14074, 14075). DED(N) 358.

Ta. itku). Kui rinda (rindi-) to be stable, steady, stand firm, stand still; pl. action ritka (ritki-); rispa (rist-) to steady, set in position. Kuwi (S.) re’nai to abide. Cf. 823 Kol. er-; 2? cf. 467 Ka. imbu and 492 Ka. irku, DED (S, N) 407. 481 Ta, iru great, spacious, vast; irumai greatness, Jargeness, hugeness, eminence. Te. inu, in: inumikkili very much, very great (for mikkili, see 4838 Ta. miku); inu-maddi Terminalia tomentosa (a large tree; for maddi, see 4718 Ta. marutu). DEN 9.

Ta. icahku, cahkaii-ceti, uvar-ccahkam, mut-canku mistletoe berry thorn, Azima tetracantha, Ma. iyanhku, isahku id. Ka. egaci, uppegaci, uppagaci, uppacci Monetia bar- lerioides; (DCV) isagele mistletoe berry thorn. [A, tetracantha, Lamk. = M. barlerioides, L’Hérit.} DED(S) 359. 360 Kur. isung oil. Malt, isgnu id. DED 0. . 423 Kur. isigna (isgy%) to open in tong slits, chap, crack; isigka crack, slit, chink. Malt. isge to be well parched (as grain), be cracked (as the skin). DED 361.
Ka. cannu. /? Influenced by Skt. chanda-. DED 1921.

Ta. ici fie!, excl. expressive of disgust or loathing. To. #8 excl. of dislike. Ka. is, issi interj. of disgust. Tu. isu, isi interj. expr. disgust. Te. isixd, isi, issird, issi fie! (denotes contempt, dislike, disapprobation). Malt. is- pori fie! fie! DED 362. 425 Konda (BB) is- (-t-) to strike. Pe. ih- (ist-) id., beat (drum). Mand ih- (-t-) to beat. Kui ihpa (iht-) to thrust into or throug spear, spit, impale. Kuwi (F.) ih- (ist-), i: darata sile ihim ‘bolt the door!’ and pa 3pl. masc., isteri. DEDS 60.

Ta. ifici ginger. Ma, ifici. Ko. inj. Ko ifiji. / Cf. Pali sifgi-, sitgivera-; Skt. spigavers (Turner, CDIAL, no. £2588): for -vera-, s: 5535 *vér- root. (Ch.) dik-, dig- flood water to subsid digap- to make to subside (cf. 501). Ga. (I

Ta. ita (-pp-, -nt-) to be cracked, spl be torn off as skin; dig, scoop out, hollo force a way through as an elephant in batt’ root up as a hog, gore as a bull, throw | clods in a furrow; itappu large cleft, ga itakkar [42] itu clod of earth thrown out by ploughing; itavan clod, lump of earth; iti Cv-, -nt-) to crumble, fall to pieces, be in ruins (as a wall), become eroded, break in two, part; Cpp-, -tt-) to break to pieces, demolish; itical grain bruised in pounding; ifivu decay, destruction, crumbling down as of an undermined bank. Ma. itakka to dig, split; itika to crumbic, fall to pieces, be split, be powdered, fall, be reduced (as a price); itikka to demolish, humble; iticcal demolition, ruins, dejection; ifippu ruination; iftivu demolition, despon- dency, degradation. Jr. depisu to cause to break. Ko. iry- (ire-) to crush in a mortar, break, destroy in one movement (e.g. wall), be broken; iryv large ditch caused by erosion. To, iry- (irs-) to fall down (as a house); (ite-) to pull down (a ruined house), Ka, idi to be powdered, ground, crumble; idisu to cause to powder; idupu breach, crack, hole; inde crack, slit. Kod. idi- (idiv-, idifij-) (wall) falls, Gand) slips; (idip-, idic-) to knock over (wall, something piled or built up). Tu. idé split, chasm, Te. idiyu to split, crack, fall to pieces, crumble, fall in; idupu breach, crack, hole; inde crack, slit. 7 Cf. 2955 Pe. drin(g)- and 2974 Kol, depla. DED (S) 366. 433 Ta, itakkar indecent words, terms denoting things or

Ta. italai wild olive, Olea dioica. Ma. itala a wood, burning very rapidly, (WZ) lea dioica; (DCV) etala id. Ka, (DCV) edala wild olive. DED(S) 370. 437 Ta, ifaru (ifari-) to stumble, strike one’s foot against, kick, obstruct; 7. obstacle; ifaral stumbling, obstacle. Ma. itaruka to stumble, trip, falter, hesitate; itarcca stum- bling, hesitation; itampuka to stumble, knock against, oppose, walk with difficulty; itampal contrariety; ifantuka to walk with difficulty. To. id- (idy-) to limp. Ka. idaru, edaru to stumble, trip; edavu, edapu the foot to be struck at a thing so as to catise a person to fall or to be near to fall, strike with the foot against, stumble, trip; edacu, edagu difficulty, esp. in speaking; (Nanj.) edgu to stumble; edahu to stumble, commit a mistake; n, stumbling; edahuha stumbling, deviating from the right course; (llav.) dafku to stumble. Tu. ettuni id.; (B-K.) edanku, dahku, datku, donku id., hit against; dumpiyunt, (B-K.} dompu to stumble. Kor. (T.) dakki id. Te. dekku to hinder, prevent, arrest. Malt. iymbe, irpe to tumble, be dashed against. DED(S, N) 371.

Ta. iti Cpp-, -tt-) to sound loud, roar, thunder, throb, beat, ache (as the head); 7. thunder, roar, great noise, ache, throbbing pain; ifippu thunder, noise. Ma. iti thunder- bolt. Ko. iry- (ire-) (heart) beats fast, thunder. To. iry- (ire-) to thunder; iry thunder. Go. (Ko. M.) idr- to thunder (Voce. 163). DED(S) 372.

Ta. iti flour, esp. of rice or millet; light meal with flour as its chief ingredient; itiyal a loose confectionary made of flour; itiyappam steamed rice-cake. Ma. itiyappam, itiyunni a kind of cake. Te. idi, idikudaka, idikudumu different sorts of cakes. Cf. 455 Ta. iffali. DEDS 64.

Ka. idi to be joined or united, be close, be mixed together, crowd; 1. state of being close, joined, or together; the whole; (PBh.) iduvu heap, mass. Kod. idi the whole. Tu. idi entire, whole. Cf. 458 Ta. infai and

Ta. itu (ifuv-, itt-) to place, deposit, put in, keep, throw, cast away, discharge (as arrows), give, pour (as rain), put on (as a bangle), compare, bury, lay (as an egg); itu applying, putting on, delivering, handing over, appropriateness, fitness, equality, a pledge, security, mortgage. Ma. ituka to put, place, plant, wear, cast, throw, give, act in general; ituvikka, itikka, itukkuka to make to put, etc.; ifu placing, stability, durability, close texture, equivalent, what itu [43] it is equal, matches. Ko, id- (if-) to put, fix; i-t shot; equal, equality. To. id- (it-) to put, place, give, throw, (sun) shines, (wind) blows, wear (jewelry anywhere except around neck); auxiliary of perseverdtive aspect; ide- (idt-) to set fire to, smoke (tobacco). Ka, idu (iff-) to let go or send from the hand, put down, throw, put, place, apply, set, plant, put on (as ornaments), lay by, keep, produce, perform, effect, make; n. putting, throwing, putting on, an ornament; == idu; idisu to cause to put, etc.; iduge, idige putting down or on, that is put on, an orna- ment; iduvike putting, applying, planting, producing, etc.; idu putting, placing, that is put, an object or mark to aim at, throwing, fitness, proportion, equality, that is equal or matches, an equivalent, a pledge. Kod. id- (iduv-, iff{-) to drop (t.); (Kar, also) to wear (not used for sari). Tu. idpini to place, put down; idu a pledge, pawn, an equivalent; dipini to place, keep, put, reserve, lay by; dipavuni to cause to place,

Ta. itu (ituv-, itf-) to hit against; itacu (ifaci-) to strike, collide; iti (-pp-, -tt-) to butt, hit against, kill; m2. stroke, blow, push. Ma. itayuka to hit against, press against, quarrel; ifaccal a quarrel; itaccil conflict; iti stroke, blow, shock, bruising, pounding; ifikka to beat, bruise. Ko, id- (it-) to beat; i-t a blow; ityl act of striking a blow. To. id- (if-) to hit against. Ka, idaku to beat, cuff; idi to pound, beat; (PBh.) idu (iff-) to hit; dikka, dikki, di, diku a butt with the head. 7u. edahkuni to hit, kick; edapuni, edupuni, edpuni to thump, strike with the fist, pound in a mortar.
