Ka. idaku, ita} ifuku, idanku, idiku, iduka, iduku, idu; narrowness, closeness, straitness, difficult trouble; iftu state of being close, narro: pressed, thronged; ittanisu, ittalisu to | close, thronged, crowded, abundant, assemt in large numbers; ittana, iftala a crow throng, mass, abundance. Tu. iffide narro ness; (B-K.) edefkiluy corner, predicamer Te. ittalamu great, abundant. Ga, (S. idigeda a few. DED(S, N) 378.

Ta. ifai (-v-, -nt-) to make room, ¢ out of the way, retreat, fall back. itamy (itampi-) to keep aloof. Ma. ifayikka, itek! to disunite, separate; ifaccal family discor separation, Te. edayu to be separated disunited, remove; edayika separation; eday to break, separate, disunite; edagillu to sta’ aside, get out of the way, edagala, edagala further off, ata little distance; edamuga at distance, aloof; edalu to be broken, (K.) ° separated (as joints); edalincu to separa’ remove, loosen; (K.) edal(u)cu to remo. take off (as clothes); edavu distant, remo! davvu distance; distant. Kol, (SR.) daw itai [44] inai dautan distant. Nk. dhav distance, Ga. (8.3) id-ér- to get separated, Malt, erare to keep aloof, get away; eratre to separate, remove.

Ta. itai (-v-, -nt-) to grow weary (as with long waiting), be damped in spirits. Te. dayyu to be tired or fatigued, (K. also) be weakened, emaciated. Kol, day- (dayt-) to become thin (< Te.). Kuwi (Su.) dah- to become thin; (F.) daha thin (of body), lean; (S.) dahinai to wither; dahi kinai to im- becilitate (sic); Cisr.) dah- (-it-) to become weak or thin. From DED(S) 435.

Ta. itai middle in space or time, interval, gap, unfilled space, waist; ifuppu waist, side, loins, hips. Ma. ifa, efa interval, place between, interval of time, middle, waist; ituppu the hip. Ko. er place or time between, interval (esp. of time). To, ir kwa-y bamboo vessel into which butter and butter- milk are poured from vessels of the inner section of the ti- dairy, and from which they are poured into vessels of the outer section (to keep the ritual purity of objects in the inner section free from any outside con- tamination; lit. intermediate kwa-y vessel [for kwa-y, see 2225]). Ka. eda, ede place -or time between, interval, distance, middle, waist, inferiority; edati middle aged woman. Tu, idé interval, space between, interval of time; idetara middling, inferior; edavaftu, edevattu middling, indifferent; dekkaru the space between the legs. Te. eda space, interval, distance, difference; edamu intervening space, distance. DED(S, N) 380.

Ta. itai, itam left side; itan one on the left side; ifattai, ifavan left ox in the yoke. Ma. itam, etam left side. Ko. ey id. To. Or left. Ka. eda left, left side; edacu state of being left-handed. Kod. édate left. Tu. eda, yada id.; edafku on the left side; yadambu left side; damma, datta left. Te. edama left, left-hand, the left-hand side; da, da left. Kol. (Hislop) edamakei left (i.e. left hand: < Te.). Nk. (Ch.) dakiyan ki left hand. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 5539, *davva-, *diiva-, *devva-, etc. (e.g. H. dawa, Mar. dava, Or. debard), whence Nk. diva; Pa. debri; Ga. (Oll.) debri; Go. (SR. S.)} dema, (L.) demar (Voce, 1583), (M.) diva; Kui deba, debe, debo; Kuwi (F.) tebri, (S.) tébri, (Su. P.) tebri. DED(S) 381.

Ta. itai the herdsman caste; ifaiyar men of the herdsman caste inhabiting the mullai country; fem. ifaicci. Ma. itayan, etayan the middie caste of shepherds and cowherds, rather foreigners in Malabar; fem. itacci; itasséri herdsmen’s quarter. Cf. Ta. intax shepherds, / Cf. Sgh. efidéra herds- men, DED(S) 382. 451 Kur. ina to fry, roast, parch (as food grains), toast (as bread). Malt erye to parch grain. DEDS 65. 452 Kol. (Kin.) irp wall. Go. (Mu.) dipi mud-wali (Voce, 1566), (Kamaleswaran.) DEN 8.

Ma. ittara wooden block with a notch, used by carpenters to wedge in planks in order to work them. Tu. itfaré, iftire a mor- tice, cavity cut into a piece of timber to receive the end of a beam or plank; a stone placed under a beam. DED 384. 455 Ta, ittali a kind of cake prepared by steaming semi-solid dough made of rice mixed with black gram. Ma, itfali, iddali a kind of cake. Ka. iddali, iddalige, iddalige a kind of sour pudding made of rice flour, black gram, cummin seed, ginger, salt, pepper, etc., and boiled in steam, Te, iddena a sort of cake. Cf. 439 Ta. iti. / Cf. Skt. indari-, indali- a kind of cake (ex). DED(S) 385.
Ta. inar (ipari-) to be dense; mm a cluster of flowers, full-blown flower, pollen, bunch of fruit. Ma. inar fish-spawn. DED 386.

Ta. inai (-v-, -nt-) to join, unite @ntr.), agree, be suited, be like, resemble; (-pp-, -tt-) to join (t.), tie (as a garland); 7. union, likeness, pair, couple; inaippu union, equality; inatku (inafki-) to consent, comply with; n, union, friendship, fit mate; inafki girl’s companion; inatkan friend; inankal consent; inankar match, comparison; inakku (inakki-) to cause to agree, unite, connect, fit; 7. union, comparison, match; inakkam fitting well together, fitness, friendship, agreement; 2 ellu (elli-) to equal. Ma. ina pair, couple, mate, companion, union; inayuka to agree _ well, suit; inekka to unite, couple, be equal; inakkam concord, union, submission; inakku agreement; inafiiuka to agree, make peace, grow tame; inakkuka to pacify, tame; inathu relationship, those of the same caste; inanhan kinsman; fem. inatiatti. Ka. ene, ena a couple, pair, connexion, adjustment, equality, simi- larity, a match, Kod. éne double (éne makka twins; éne ba-le double plantain); éne a-d- (snakes) mate. Tu, iné, iné a couple, pair, companion, mate. Te. ena equal; equality, a match; enayincu to mix, mingle, join, unite; enayika fitness, mingling; enayu to resemble, be equal or similar to, mingle, unite, (K. also) fit, suit; eniyu to mingle, mix, join, unite; enucu, enupu to mix (fr.), mingle, stir with a stick as ingredients in a dish; enupudu act of mixing, mingling, or intai [45] i: stirring with a stick; enika, enike a thread composed of four single yarns united; (K.) nenayu to fit, suit

Ta. intai circlet of flowers, variety of garland. Ma, inta a kind of garland. Ka. inde wreath, garland. Te. (SAN, VN, DCV) inde garland. Cf. 440 Ka. idi. DED (S) 388. 459 Ta, itag, atag petal, palmyra leaf, eyelid, Ma, ital flower leaf, ovaria of fish; ita sprout, shoot, tender leaf; itaykkuka to sprout. Ka. esal, (PBh.) esag petal, small wis with leaves, Tu. esalu petal, leaf. DED

Ta. itti white fig, Ficus infectoria; stone fig, f. gibbosa parasitica; tailed oval- leaved fig, F. talboti; icci oval-leaved fig; jointed ovate-leaved fig, F. tstela; icciyal, iccil jointed ovate-leaved fig; iratti jointed ovate-leaved fig; subserrate rhomboid-leaved fig, F. gibbosa tuberculata; iratakam jointed ovate-leaved fig; irali white fig; irri tailed oval-leaved fig. Ma. itti, ittiy-al waved leaved fig-tree, F. venosa; a parasitical plant, Loran- thus coriaceus. Kod. itti F. (2 gibbosa). DED(S) 390. 461 Kur. ittna, in: xattna ittna to portion . out, distribute; (Hahn) ittna to dole out. Malt. ite to divide (as a share). DED(S) 391. 462 Kur.. idna to put in the ground and cover (as seed for growth), plant, transplant, introduce and establish (as a new religion); idm refl.-pass. Malt. ide to plant, erect, build. DED 392, 463 Kur. idrna@ to cease, stop (of rain), come to a close (said of proceedings of some importance); idhi cessation of rain, break in the rains. Malt. idre to cease (as rain), become sober from intoxication. DED(S) 393.
Ka. ibbudiu-balli the mefon plant, Cucumis melo Lin. Tu. ibbudlu a kind of cucumber. DED 394.
Ta. imil hump on bull. Ma. imi.hump. 466 Ta, imir (-v-, -nt-) to sound, hum; imig (-v-, -nt-) to sound, buzz; (-pp-, -tt-) to sound, hum; 2. sound, hum, roar. Ma. imaruka to groan.

Ka. imbu a halting or resting place, home, place, space, room; imba_ width, breadth. Tu. imbu place, room, space, refuge, opportunity; broad, wide. Te. immu a place, home, room, space; convenience, convenient, suitable. ? Cf. 480 Ta. iru. DED 395,

Ta. (Koli.) ukkaram sultriness. Ka. uga, ugi, uge steam, vapour or reek (as that which rises from a gtain-pit). Tu. ugé, ukki steam. Te. ukka closeness or oppressiveness of weather, sultriness, confined heat, want of breeze or ventilation; ukkiribikkiri close, suffocating, oppressive; (K.) ukku_ to be suffocated through excessive humidity and hot weather. Go. (Ch.) ukkur steam; (L.) ukur breath; (Ko.) ukur heat; (A.) ukkur, (SR.) ukkad sweat; (Tr.) ukur the boiling point (Voc. 219; or Tr. ukur with 666(@) Ka. urku, ukku). Cf. 656 Ta. uru. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) ukkola- heat, sultriness. DED(S) 486.

Ta. iyatku (iyaiki-) to move, stir, go, proceed, walk about; m. movement, act of going; iyakku (iyakki-) to cause to go, train or break in (as a bull or horse); 7. motio going, marching; iyakkam motion, movi: about, way; icahku (icanki-) to go or le: to (as a way); icai (-pp-, -tt-) to bring abou iyal (iyaly-, iyanz-; iyali-) to go on foc dance; nm. pace, gait; iyavu way; leadin proceeding; iyavul leadership, god, wa iyavai way, path. Ma. iyahfuka to mo steadily; iyakkuka to cause to move; iyakka motion, movement. Ko, i-y- (i-c-), i-c- (i-c to drive (cattle). Ka. (PBh.) esagu to driv DED (S) 397.

Ta. iyampu (iyampi-) to sound, sa utter; iyakku (iyakki-) to cause to soun iyakkam a musical composition, pitch ( three kinds); iyam sound, word, music instrument; iyantai an ancient melody tyr iyavan drummer; icai (-pp-, -tt-) to soun sound as a musical instrument, expre: signify, play as on a lute; n. sound, noi: word, fame, song, music; ? €e Samaveda ( with 879 Ta. enku). Ma. iyampuka to soun ica, iga song, music; iSampuka to say, spea utter. Ka, (PBh.) ese to sound. ? Kui ¢ (ési-) to sing, chant, recite, read aloud, relat tell, say; m. act of singing, relating, tellin Br. hit talk, conversation, promise, matt (talked about); hit kanning to talk, discu say things about one; hitmit conversatic gossip (BDCG, p. 16). DED(S) 398.

Ta. iyal (iyalv-, iyanr-; iyali-) to | possible, befall, be associated with; accey agree to, approach, resemble; vn. fitne good conduct, likeness, nature, qualit tyalpu nature, proper behaviour, goodne propriety; iyalvu nature, means of attainir iyarru (iyarri-) to do, effect, cause to a: control the’ movements of, create, compo: iyarri, iyarral effort; iyarkai nature, custo: iyai (-v-, -nt-) to be agreeable, agree, harmo ize, resemble; (-pp-, -tt-) to join, conne adapt; iyaipu union, harmony, appropria: ness; iyaivu union, joining together; icai (- -nt-) to fit in (as one plank with anothe harmonize, consent, agree; (-pp-, -tt-) bind, resemble; n. union, harmony; icaipy combination, joining so as to fit in, joir icaivu agreement, consent, suitability. A‘ iyaluka to agree, go fairly, be proper; jy what is proper; nature, condition; streng? power; iyarruka to cause, induce; ikarru to cause, effect;iyappu joint, joining togeth: iyayuka to be agreeable, harmonise; iyaykku to join (fr.); isayuka to agree, join togeth- iSaykkuka to join (t7.); isal style, what proper. Ka, ilu attachment, as that of anim to men, of children to parents; esagu engage in, undertake, commence, do, mal perform; (PBh.) esaka action, deed, wo: Tu, iyaruni, iyavuni to be sufficient. 7 iya-konu, iyya-konu to consent; iya-ko’ iyya-kolu consent; fluvu honour, chasti- Cf. 905 Ta. al. DED(S, N) 399. .

Ta. ira (-pp-, -nt-) to beg alms, besee: entreat; irappu begging; iraval begging, a: irafiku [46] irali thing borrowed to be returned after use (except money); iravu beggary; iravalan, iravOn beggar. Ma, irakkuka to beg, ask alms; irappu begging; irappan beggar; iravu thing lent. Ka. ere (erad-) to beg, ask, solicit; era, erayu, erapu, eraval that which is asked for, that which one desires to borrow, a thing borrowed for temporary use, state of borrow- ing from one another. Kod. era- (erap-, erand-} to beg; (Shanmugam) erapé beggar; erapaci a female beggar. Tu. eravu act of lending or borrowing for temporary use; randuni to beseech, entreat; randelu begging, entreating. Te. eravu a loan, thing borrowed for temporary use. Malt. irgre to borrow, take a loan. DED(S) 400. 473 Ta, irahku (iratki-) to feel pity, con- descend, show grace; be aggrieved, distressed in mind; repent; irakkam mercy, grace, pity, compassion, regret, sorrow. Ma. erakkam kindness, pity, compassion; wretchedness, affliction, miserable state. From DED 694.

Ta. irantu, (coll.) rentu two; iranfam, irantavatu second; iranai couple, pair; irattai pair, married couple, twins, even numbers; irattaiyar twins; iratti Cpp-, -tt-) to double (fr.), Tepeat; be doubled, return, disagree; n, double quantity; irattippu double quantity; irattu (iratfi-) to double (intr), sound alternately; wave alternately (t.); 1. double- ness; iru (before consonant), ir (before vowel) adj. two; iru-patu, iru-vatu twenty; iru-niiru 200; irumai twofold state; iruvar two persons; ivv-iranfu two by two. Ma. rantu two; ranf-aka to be divided, be doubled; ranfam second; ranfikka to be divided, disagree; double, multiply; iratfta double, even; iratti double, twice as much, irattikka to double, multiply; iru (before consonant), ir (before vowel) adj. two; iruvar two persons; iru-patu twenty; iru-niiru 200; i-rantu by twos. Jr. randu, rendu two. Ko. eyd two; ir va-d twenty; irrva-d by twenties; ir pa-ny two pany measures; i vat od twenty-one, etc.; i nu-r 200; i-r a-y two years; i-r o-k two olk measures; er calg two calg measures. To. e-d (obl. e-dn-/e-n-; e-t- when preceded by plural personal or reflexive pronoun) two; i fo8 twenty; i nu-r 200; i kwa-w two kwa-x measures; i-r 0-7 two years; ier ak two a€ok measures; i-ty double, even (of numbers); im double; imu twins (vl. imua-m twins on more than one occasion); i-Styu- twice. Ka. eradu, erdu, erag two; adj. iru, ir, ik, ic, etc., ir; irpattu, ippattu. twenty; innOru 200; irbar, ibbar, irvar two persons; irme, imme

Ta. iralai stag; kind of deer. Ka. erale, erale antelope, deer, incl., for example, the black and spotted antelope. Tu. erale ante- lope, deer. Te. (Inscr.) iri (pl. irulu) stag; irri antelope; ? léti, lédi id. Malt. ilaru the mouse deer. DED 403.
Ta. irali, irali cassia, Ka. r@la id. Te. réla-ceftu, (Inscr.) réla purging cassia, Cassia iri [47] ire (cathartocarpus) fistula. Go. (Ph.M.) rela, ie rérkad Cassia fistula (Voc. 3062). DED(S)
Ta. iri (-v-, -nt-) to be destroyed, retreat, flee away; (-pp-, -tt-) to defeat, destroy, drive away; irifian foe; iriyal running, speeding. Ko. irymury gey- (gec-) to kill in a battle. DED(S) 405,

Ta. irical break, crack. Ma. iriyuka to wrench off, twist off, pluck (as fruits, branches); iriccal plucking. Kol. iri- (irit-) to tear (intr.); irk- (irikt-) to tear (tr.). Nk. ir- to be torn; irk- to tear. Kui ira a splinter. Malt. ire to lop off, hew. DED(S) 406.

Ta. iru (-pp-, -nt-) to exist, remain, sit down, live, belong to (dative of person); auxiliary; iruttu (irutti-) to cause to sit, detain, fix permanently; iruvu (iruvi-) to cause to be or abide; irukkai sitting, seat, residence; iruppu seat, residence, condition in life, balance on hand, surplus, stores, merchandise, wares. Ma. irikkuka to. sit, remain, be in a place; auxiliary; irikka being; irippu_ sitting, residence, position; iruttuka to seat, place, detain, ‘settle; iruttikka to cause to seat; ixutti a seat; iruttam, iruttu sitting, staying. Ko. ig- (ir- in one form; it-) to exist, be in a place; irp collection of property; irpga-rn rich man; ? igc- (igc-) to ignite (tic fire). To, ir- (i6-) to sit, remain, live; perfective-continuative auxiliary; ist- (isty-) to seat, keep in a place; isk lifetime; i-gk dwelling, act of dwelling. Ka, ir, iru (irdu, iddu) to be, exist, remain, stay, delay, belong to (dative of person); irisu to cause to be or stay, place, put, deposit; iravu, irapu being, staying, resting, state, condition: iruvike, iruha being, existing, etc.; irisuha causing to be or to stay, etc.; irke, ikke being, abode, seat, place. Kod. ir- (-ipp-, ifij-) to be in a place. Ju. ippuni (itt-) to be, exist, live in; have, possess (dative of person). Te. iravu place, abode; firm; iravu- padu to become established, firm; iruvu neighbourhood, place, home; irugu neigh- bourhood; neighbouring; iravdru to become firm, fixed, settled; ikka place, abode, dwelling, seat; inkuv

Ta. irul ironwood of Ceylon, Mesua ferrea; (DCV) irumpukam ironwood. Ma, irimpakam, (DCV) irul id. Ka. (DCV) irul id. Tu. pM ayirdl id. Cf. 483 Ta. itp DEDS 6 483 Ta, ifti blackwood, Dalbergia latifolic Ma. iruvil, wtl D. sisu; vitfi Bombay black wood, D. latifolia. Ka. irugundi D. sissoc ibadi, ibbadi, bite D. latifolia Roxb. Tu biti blackwood; kari-biti ebony, D. latifolic« Te, irugudu, iruvudu D, latifolia; (B.) ibbade ibbeda a certain tree. Cf. 2552 Ta. iravu DED(S) 408.

Ta. irucu axle-tree. Ka. irasu, iracu iricu, irucu, irci, ircu, era-kolu an iron axlc tree. Te. irusu axle, axle-tree. Kol. (Br. i-rcu axle, Ga, (S.*) issu axle (< Te.). Go (Ma.) irs(u), (Ko.) irs id. (< Te.; Voe. 190: (S.) nirsu id. (< Te.; Voe. 1992). Kuwt (F. hirst id. (< Te.). DED(S) 409.

Ta. iruppai, irumpai, ippai, iluppe mahua tree, Bassia longifolia, Ma. irippe iruppa, ilippa, iluppa B, latifolia. Ka. ippe hippe B. latifolia Roxb. Tu. ippe, (B-K. also irippe the olive tree. Te. ippa B. longifolia Kol, (SR.) ippa, (Kin.) ippa id. Nk, (Ch. irpu mahua. Pa, irup, irpa id. Ga. (S.” ) itp id. Go. (Tr.) iri-mara, (W.) irt, (Ph.) in (pl. iruhk), (Ch) irup/irup, (Y.) iruk, (A. hiruk, (Mu.) irum/irum, (Ma. G.) irp(i), (M. irpi, {Ko.) fru mara id. (Voc. 183); (ASu. hirp id.; hirtk mahua flowers; (LuS.) irpo: mahua. "Konda ipa maran the mahua tree Kui irpi, (K) ripi mahua. Kuwi (Su. P.) irp id.; irpi kdyu mahua liquor. DED(S, N) 410

Ta. irumpu iron, instrument, weapon Ma. icumpu, irimpu iron. Ko. ib id. To. i needle. Kod. irimbi iron. Te. inumu id. Koi (Kin.) inum (pl. inmul) iron, sword. Ku (Friend-Pereira) rumba vadi ironstone (fo yah see 5285). Cf. 2552 Ta. iravu. DED(S 487 Ta, iruvi Nepal aconite. Ma. iruvi - drug, (ML) Nepal aconite. DED 412.

Ta. iruvéri, iruvéli cuscuss grass, Ma iruvéri, iruvéli Andropogon muricatus. Tu irveri id. /Cf. Skt. hrivera-, hrivela(ka). hirivera- a kind of Andropogon (acc. to some Pavonia odorata); Parpola 1977-78, pp. 252 4. DED 413.

Ta. irai (-v-, -nt-) to sound, roar (a the sea), rumble (as a crowd), wamble (a the bowels), whiz (as birds when flying) Gpp-, -tt-) to cry out (as in anger), hiss (as : snake), pant, breathe hard, wheeze, n. sound roar, splash (as of a running river): iraicca sound, noise, clamour; iraippu buzzing, din wheezing, asthma; iratku (iratki-) to weep cry, roar, sound (as yar); irattu (iratfi-) t sound; make to sound, beat (as drum); iriya weeping; irumu (irumi-) to cough; m, cough irumal cough. Ma, irekka to pant, snore bluster, roar as sea; iraccal, ireppu noise (a of sea, bowels), hum, buzz; irampuka t: bluster, be loud; irampal roar of the sea difficulty of breathing: irumal a cough irai [48] iri eraccil din, bustle; erappu buzzing, din, wheezing; erampam din, bustle, great noise, Ka, rampa clamour, vociferation; rambu clamour, noise; rambha sounding, roaring, bellowing, lowing, making a noise. Kod. rampa hubbub. 7u, ramb(h)artti, rambaroti, rambhata clamour, outcry. Te. rampu (probably ‘expressive’ 1), rampu_ noise, uproar, disturbance, squabble, wrangle, scandal; rampilQ@)u, rampilu to make an uproar. Kol, (Kin.) rampam uproar, com- motion. Pa, ran ban disorder. / Cf. Skt. rambh-, ramb- to sound; rambha- sounding, roaring, lowing; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10634. DED(S, N) 414, DED(N) 4237.

Ta. irai food of birds, beasts, and other inferior animals, prey; (Koll.) . erav prey. Ma, ixa food of birds, snakes, infants. Ko. er food brought by birds to young, prey of a wild animal. Ke. ere food for animals, as for snakes, birds, etc. Te. era food. Br, iragh bread, food. DED 415. 491 Ta, iraical flaw (as of a_ precious stone), roughness (as of a brick). Ma. iraccal the appearance of cracked, uneven wood. DED 416.

Ka. irku, ikku to lay or put down, put, place, beat, serve up (as food); be put; n. act of putting, etc.; ikkuva, ikkuha laying, putting, etc.; irkisu, ikkisu to cause to put. Tu, ikkuni to put, serve up (as food). ? Cf. 442 and 443 Ta. itu, 480 Ta. iru, 502 Ta. iri, 516 Ta. iratku. DED 418.

Ta. il house, home, place, wife; illam house, home; illaval, ilfal wife, mistress of house; illatan, illali householder. Ma. il house, place; illam house of Nambudiri. Kod. illavén man who is a relative. Tu. ilty house, dwelling, family. Te, illu (adj. inti) house, dwelling, habitation; illafamu, illantramu living in the house of one’s wife’s father, Kol. ella house; (SR.) iltama younger sister’s husband; (Kin.) iltam boy who serves for a wife in her father’s house (SR. and Kin. < Te.). Nk. ella house. Pa, it podiroof. Go. (SR.) il house (Voc. 196). Konda ilu (pl. ilku) house. Pe. il (obl. inj-, pl. ilku) house. Mand. il id. Kui idu house, dwelling, shed, hut. Kuwi (F.) illu (pl, itka), (S.) illu, (Su. P.) i @L ilka) house. DED(S) 420.

Ta. ila, ilavam, ilavu red-flowered silk-cotton tree, Bombax malabaricum. Ma. ilavam, ilavu silk-cotton tree, B. pentandra. Ka. elava the seemul or silk-cotton tree, B. heptaphyllum Roxb. Tu, ala silk-cotton tree. Pa, neluk id. Go, (Ma.) léki, (M.) leke B. malabaricum (Voe. 3126); (LuS.) lékee id. Konda léka maran id. Kuwi (Su.) leko, (Isr.} léko marnu, (Mah.) dakangi id. Cf.

Ta. ilai leaf, petal; Cpp-, -tt-) to become green. Ma, ila leaf; ilaccal foliage; ilakku, elakku a single leaflet of a palm. Ko. el leaf. To. es id. Ka, ele, ela id. Kod. elakanda id. Tu, elé, iré id. Go. (Mu.) kork-ila new leaf (Voce. 934; for kork-, cf. 2149). DED(S) 423. 498 Ta, illatai soot adhering to the inner side of the roof. Ma, illara-kkari, illara-kkari grime, soot. Ka, illana soot adhering to the inner side of the roof. DED 424.
Ka. illamalli a tell-tale, backbiting. Te. illaimalli a backbiter, tell-tale. DED 425. 500 Ta, ivuli horse. Br. (h)ulli-id. (Burrow 1972.) DEN 9A.

Ta. iza (-pp-, -nt-) to lose, forfeit; lose by death; irappu loss; iravu loss, deprivation, detriment; destruction, ruin, death; desti- tution. Ma, irakkam loss; irakkuka to lose, forfeit; igavu death. Cf. also ‘lose’ for Ta. irukku in 503. From DED 426.

Ta. igi (-v-, -nt-) to descend, dismount, fall, drop down, be reduced in circumstances, be inferior; (-pp-, -tt-) to lower, let down, degrade, despise; iriccu (iricci-) to lower, let down, remit {as taxes), pull down (as a building), disgrace; iricinan, igitian, igiyinan outcaste; irittu (iritti-) to lower, let down, remit (as taxes); igippu contemptuous treat- ment; izipu, igivu’ inferiority, disgrace, decrease, hollow, pit. Ma. irika to descend; igikka to lower, take down; izgivu going down, sinking; igruka to fall, sink; igal remissness, defect; iliyuka to descend, get down. To. i-x- (i-xy-) to descend; i-k- (i-ky-) to put down (burden). Ka. iri (igid-) to come down, descend, alight, go down, set (as the sun), become less, subside, be depressed or hum- bled, become lean; ia, izi, itu coming down, descending, being depressed, being humbled, declining, abating; ixaku, igiku, iguku to cause to go down, lower, go down, incline; irakisu to cause to lower, etc.; irata, igita descending, inclining; irike descent, humbled condition; iripu, izapu, igupu to cause to descend, let down, lower; iriyuvike descend- ing. Kod. ili- (iliv-, ilifj-) to descend, get ready; ilip- (ilipi-), (Mercara dialect) i-p- (i-pi-) to lower, get (bullocks) out of shed; 2 ala- (-p-, -t-) to sit down (< *iray-; MBE 1970, p. 147). Tu. iliyuni to fall, tumble, come down; iriyuni to fall, drop (as fruits, jeaves); iri abatement of intoxication. Te. digu to alight, descend, decrease; diggu, (K. also) diggu to d

Ta. igai yarn, single-twisted threac darning; (-v-, -nt-) to be reeled, as yarr (-pp-, -tt-) to spin. Ma. ira a single threac long hair; igekka to patch, mend; ilekkuk to wind up thread. Ko, el thread in cloth Ka, eye thread; egtu the string or cord of th warp of the texture of a cot. Tu, ile a hai: fibre; (B-K.) ile, ele a fibre, thread. Ku €spa (&st-) to spin thread; mn. act of spinnin; DED(S, N) 429. irai

Ta. irai (-v-, -nt-) to associate very intimately, have tender emotions (as in love), be together (as husband and wife), copulate (as snakes). Ko. ig- (ity-) to have sexual intercourse with. Ka, elasu to desire, long for, take pleasure in; ? defigu sexual union. Te. (K.) dégu to copulate with, ? Kol. deng- (denkt-) to have sexual intercourse with. ? Cf. 457 Ta. inai. DED(S) 430.

Ma. ixayuka to trail, creep, crawl (as infants); iga-jati reptiles; izaccal creeping; éyuka to creep. Te. déku, dékuru, (K. also) dé@ku to creep or slide along the ground on the posteriors. DED(S) 431. 509 Ta, ilaku (iJaki-) to shake (iz#r.), agitate; ilakku (ilakki-) to shake (tr.). Ma. ilakuka to move, shake, fluctuate, ilakkuka to move (f7.), shake, stir, disturb, interrupt; stir (intr.); ilakkikka to get into motion, set on (e.g. a dog); ilakkam shaking, fluctuation, irresolution. Ko. elg- (elgy-) (something) is slightly raised or imperfectly fixed in place, lift one’s body slightly from ground, (mind) is moved (by pity); elk- (elky-) to lift slightly from position, lever out, pull (trigger); ele- (elc-) (blister) rises. Ka. eladu, elidu moving about, unsteadiness, fickleness; (Hav.) elagu to be excited. Kod. él- (éli-) to leave one’s position, -leave abruptly, be removed from one’s position; élak- @laki-) to make to leave a position, uproot, (god) possesses (man who devil-dances); élaka uprooting from position, violent, shaking (as when possessed by a god). Tu. lakkuni to rise, get up, depart, start; lakkavuni to lift, raise, wake, rouse, steal. Go. (Mu.) lesk- to tremble, be possessed by gods; léske (pl. -+r) medium through whom gods speak (he always shakes his head); (Ma.) lés- to cast out evil spirits by means of spells; léske (pl. -r) priest, shaman (Voe. 3135). Br. hilh fever (MBE 1962, p. 63). DED(S) 432.

Ta. ilaku (itaki-) to become relaxed (as a bowstring), get loose (as screws), become pliable (as melted iron), get reduced in hardness owing to moisture, grow soft, liquefy, lessen in intensity or severity (as wind, rain, sunshine, fever), become tender, mild, yielding, placable; ilakku (ilakki-) to slacken (fr.), relax (as a spring), loosen (as a rope), moisten (as the throat with liquid), relieve (as the bowels by cathartics), cause to relent, make less firm (as a peg driven into the ground); ilakkam laxity, relaxation, tenderness, leniency; ilakkari (-pp-, -tt-) to relax; ilakkaram laxity, relaxation, indulgence; (Tinn.) ela loose. Ma. ilekkuka to slacken, subside, abate; remit, make to rest; ilavu leave, holiday, rest. Ka. (Hav.) elakku_to unstick. Go. (Ph.) rengand, renginad, (W.) rengina to be loose; (Tr.) réngana to be open; caus, réngstand; (Mu.) leh-, (Ma.) le?-, (Pat.) lehand to untie, loosen; (Ko.) léyg- to become loose; caus, 1eh- (Voce. 3047). DED(S) 433, and from DED(S) 4246. [50] ila

Ta. ili (-v-, -nt-) to become low-spirited because of being ridiculed by others; (pp-, -tt-) to laugh, scorn, ridicule, disgrace, grin, show the teeth (as in cringing or in craving servilely); 1. laughter, derisive laughter, disgrace, contempt, scorn, defect; ilivu ridicule, disgrace, disgust. ? ici (-pp-, -tt-) to laugh; 1. laughter. icippu laughter. Ma. ili contemptuous grin; ilika to fret, as children; ilikka to grin (as dogs, monkeys), show the teeth; iliccal grinning, bashfulness; ilippu grinning, neighing, nonsensical talk. DED 434.

Ta. ilai (pp-, -tt-) to grow weary, be fatigued, get exhausted, become worn out, be emaciated, fail before a foe, lag behind a rival, become impoverished, reach the stage of diminishing returns (as land), grow weak and lacking in fruitfulness (as trees), fade or lose vigour (as a plant); ilaittavan poor man, weak man; ilaippu weariness, fatigue, lassitude, affliction, distress; ilakkam weariness, faint- ness; ilaku (ilaki-) to grow weary, become tired, fade away (euphemism = die), perish; eli (-v-, -nt-) to become feeble, low-spirited; 2 cilai Cpp-, -tt-) to be wearied, tired. Ma. ilaccal weariness; ilappu id., lassitude; iay- kkuka to be fatigued, get exhausted, grow lean; ilikkuka to be emaciated, grow lean or thin; (Tiyya) ilusan a loafer. Ka. ilidu the state of wasting away, diminishing, decay. Te, anna weariness. DED (S, N) 435.

Ta. ila, ilam, ilai young, tender; ila-nir tender coconut milk, tender coconut; ifai youth, tender age; ilaimai youth; ilaicci younger sister; ilaifian younger brother, lad, young man; ilaitu that which is young and not fully developed; ilaiyar youths, young men, servants; ilaiyavar, ilaiyar young women; ilaiyavan younger person, lad, youth, younger brother; ilaiyan, ilaiyan, faiyOn younger brother; ilaku (ilaki-) to sprout afresh, send forth tender shoots; ifacu that which is young or tender; ilantai youth, tender years; ilamai childhood, youth, tenderness, immaturity of knowledge and intellect, amorousness; ilaval younger brother, lad, son. Ma, ila tender, young, weak; ila-nir unripe coconut; ilappam state of being young, juvenility, tenderness; ilama youth, tender age, junior raja; ilayavan young, younger. Ko. el time of youth; young. To. el young; tipcat (the small stick hit in the game); elk time of youth. Ka. el, ela, ele tenderness, youth, weakness, moderation; ela-nir water of an unripe coconut; elaku young age; elatu, eladu, eledu that is tender, young; eletana. tenderness, youth. Kod. éleé youth. Tu. elatu, ele tender, green, not fully grown; lattu young, tender, unripe, green, imperfect; ellydye a youth, junior, minor; lambu tender; lambé a tender fruit. Te. 18ta, 1 young, tender, light, delicate, soft; ‘J@ka servant; [éga a sucking or young calf; léma a woman; tender; ela young, tender, delicate, slight, gentle; ela-naga damsel (cf.

Ta. ira (-pp-, -nt-) to die, become obsolete, depart;, irakku (irakki-) to kill; irappu, iravi, iravu death; iru (iruv-, izr-) to die, end in; (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy, bring to an end; iruti, iru termination, end, death, limit; irr-ayal penultimate. Ma. irakkuka to die; irappu, iguti end; iru ruin. Ka, irku, ikku to kill, destroy; 1. killing, destroying; irkisu to cause to kill. Tu. irkavuni, ikkayuni to extinguish; irkuni to be quenched. Kui ejga (ejgi-) to die in childbirth (Su. 1973). DED 437.

Ta. ira (-pp-, -nt-) to go beyond, transcend, excel, be pre-eminent, pass by (of time), transgress; irappa much, exceed- ingly; irappu transgression, passing, excess, abundant. To. ert- (erty-) to overcome in contest, outstrip. Kur. esn@ (essas) to trans- gress. Or do Ta. and To. go with 2589 Ta. cira? DED 438.

Ta. iratku (irahki-) to descend, alight, fall (as rain), disembark, settle into place, halt, abate (as poison, small-pox, etc., which are supposed to pass away from the head downward), bow respectfully, fall from a high state; irahkal place of descent, of de- barkation; irakku (irakki-) to lower, let down, put down (load), unload; , unburden- ing, discharging; irai (-v-, -nt-) to bow before {as in salutation), worship; iraificu (iraifici-) id., fall down, hang low (as a cluster of coco- nuts), bow, bend. Ma. iratihuka to descend, go down, disembark, be swallowed; irakkuka to put down, unlade, expel, swallow; irakkam descending, slope, abatement, ebb; iraficuka to adore, make obeisance; irayuka to bow, salute; iravu a valley, descending slope. Jr. rafigu/ra-fga to descend. Ko. erg- (ergy-) to go down, (food) is swallowed; erk- (erky-) to put down (load), reduce (a fine), (priest) unties (front hair knot); erg down. To. ir (irky-) to swallow; erk down, the east; muc irk- (irky-) to. shave (beard and hair) in pa ment of vow (lit. to make the hair descen < Ta.; cf. 4921). Ka. eragu to bow, be ber crouch, come down, alight, fall upon, attac enter, join, accrue to; nm. a bow, obeisanc eragisu to cause to bow, etc.; eraka comi: down, etc.; eraguha bowing, coming dow perching, etc. Kod, érang- (érangi-) (foo liquid) slips down throat, goes down slow! érak- (€raki-) to let slip down throat slow! Tu, eraguni to bow, fall upon, seize, aligh ekkuni to ebb, decline; jappuni to descen alig

Ta. iyavu, ira, igal prawn, shrim iral bivalve shellfish, mussel. Ma, iravu praw: shrimp. ? Tu. etti prawn. Te. reyya, roy) prawn, shrimp. Pa, réda shrimp. Ga. (S. reyya shrimp, prawn. Go, (Ko.) réyi kit id. (Voce. 3060); (Koya Su.) reyya id. Kone reyo id. Kuwi (S.) r€ya id. DED(S) 440.

Ta. iravu, iral honeycomb; irattu i - (local dial.). Ka. (Hal.) &ri empty beehiv (Jénu Kuruba, LSB 4.12) irle honeycom' (Sholiga, LSB 6.18) rade id. Kod. te-n-eri i Tu. (Bhattacharya; Brahmin dial.) edi i Go, (Tr.) yéri the bees’ comb in which t! eges and grubs live; (Ma.) arey part of hiv where bees and grubs live (Voce, 392). Ki jéka honeycomb. DED(S, N) 441. . 519 Ta. iyavular hill tribes. Ma, irava’ travalar hill tribe of basket-makers. Ka. era: a hill tribe in Coorg. DED 442.

Ta. iru (iruv-, irr-) to break, snap ( a stick), become severed (as a limb); Cpp -tt-) to break off (as a branch), snap asunde smash, break in pieces. Ma. iruka to brea! snap, Te, (B.) itiyu to be broken, smashe: Kui renga (rengi-) to be broken in piec: (bread, cakes); repka (< rek-p-; rekt-) 1 break into pieces, Kur. esna (essas) to brea’ divide by force (a stick, a tooth, etc.), quel crush; esrna to get broken; esrna murcurr to break short, snap, part asunder sudden! DED 443. iru [52] in- §21 Ta. iru (-pp-, -tt-) to pay (as a tax, a debt); iruppu payment of debt, tax; irai tax on land, duty. Ma, ira taxes, tribute, a certain fee for land-tenure; irukkuka to pay as tax; iruppu paying a tax or repaying a debt. Kuwi (Iisr.) r@fg- (-it-) to pay off. Kur. erxna to be (so much) out of pocket, be fined, lose money; erxta’and to make one (so much) the poorer, fine, extort, make disgorge. Malt. ergtre to force another to pay his debts. DED(S) 444.

Ta. iru (pp-, -tt-) to strain, percolate (as a liquid); igru-ccotfu to exude and drop at intervals. Afa. iruka (irr-) to drip, drop; igrikka to dribble; (Tiyya) irru a drop; irruva to trickle. Ka. ibbani fog, mist, dew; (Tipt., LSB 18.16) ircilu fine drops of rain. Nk, (Ch.) erap- to strain off water from boiled rice. Go. (ASu.) ir- to ooze. DED(S, N) 445.
Ta. iru -pp-, -tt-) to tarry, stay; iruppu, irai abiding, tarrying. Ka. iru to stay, hesitate; irisu to cause to stay, put. DED 446.

Ta. iruku (iruki-) to become tight (as a knot), harden (as land dried by the sun, molten, metals when they are cooled), be- come dry (as mortar or clay), thicken (as phlegm), congeal, coagulate, solidify, become firm, be fixed or rooted in; irukku (irukki-) to tighten, tie close or hard, make compact, repress, restrain, drive in (as a nail); 7. pres- sure, coercion, reproof, hard knot; irukkam tightness, compactness, rigidness, niggardli- ness; irukkan miser; irukal contraction; irumpu thicket, shrub, bush; ikku danger, trouble; ikkatfu trouble, difficulty, strait- ened circumstances. Ma. irukuka to become tight, close; irukkuka to tie tight, catch as a crab, (river) to have a narrow bed; irukkam, irukku tightness, covetousness, ikku trouble; ikkaftu straits, difficulty; irra stinginess, a miser; irran miser; irrikka to keep tenaciously, lay up. Ko. ixg- (irgy-) to become tight, be compressed, be tightly wedged in; irk- (irky-) to tighten (clothes), pinch, press tightly in crowd; ikat obstruction, esp. on narrow path so that one cannot pass. To. ikot difficulty, narrowness of path. Ka. iraku, iriku, iruku, irku, irahku, iritku, irunku, iri to be closely confined, be compressed or squeezed; con- fine, compress, squeeze, press, pinch; iraku, ipiku, irku, ikku state of being close, con- fined, squeezed, or straitened; irkattu, ikk- attu state of having little room from side to side, narrowness as that of a door, state of being strait or difficult to pass; iruku state of being cl
Ta. irufku great millet (Sorghum vulgare); irati Italian millet (Sefaria italica), black Italian millet (Panicum indicum). Ma, irufmu a kind of maize. Cf. 812 Kod. eri. From DED (S) 695.
Ta. iruma (-pp-, -nt-) to be elated, feel exulted; be self-conceited; irumappu elation, conceit, arrogance. Ma, irumannu indifferently, arrogantly.

Ta. itai anyone who is great (as one’s father or guru or any renowned and illustrious person), master, chief, elder brother, husband, king, supreme god, height, head, eminence; iraimai kingly superiority, celebrity, govern- ment, divinity; iraiyavan chicf, god; traiyan iva; iraivan god, chief, master, husband, venerable person; iraivi mistress, queen, Parvati, Ma. iran, ran sire, used in addressing princes. Ka. ere state of being a master or husband; a master; ereya master, king, hus- band; erati a mistress. Te. era lord (Nellore inscr. [7th-8th_cent.]; so -Master, BSOAS 12. 351; Inscr.”); rédu king, lord, master, husband. DED 448.

Ta. irai, irappu inside of a sloping roof, eaves of a house; iyavaram eaves of a house. Ma. ira, irakali, irayarram eaves ofa house; irampu eaves, brow of a hill, edge; igavaram veranda. Ka. erakil, erake a roof, thatch. Kod. éraki eaves. ? Te, era, in: talliy- era the uppermost well-tube which forms a parapet round a well. Konda rékam roof. Pe, jégom id. ? Kui répa the lower slopes of a hill. Cf. $169 Ta. irappai. DED(S) 449.

Ta. iracci flesh; irri meat. Ma. iracci flesh, meat. Kod. eraci id. Te. eraci id. Konda re- (-t-) to eat meat, relish. Pe. jey flesh (or with 2549 Te. ciguru). DED(S) 450. 530 (a) Ta. in- sweet, pleasant, agreeable; sweetness, pleasantness; inpam delight, happi- ness, sweetness, pleasantness, sexual love, marriage; inpan husband; inpu, inimai, inippu sweetness, pleasure, delight; ini (-pp-, -tt-) to be sweet to the taste, be pleasant; initu barnes PMOL ec ROAR, AME NEEM inam [53] i that which is sweet, agreeable, good; iniyar agreeable persons; inndmai pain, distress, misfortune; innatku evil, pain; innal un- pleasantness, trouble; innar enemies. Ma. inpam, inippam, inuppam, inima pleasure, delight; iniya sweet; innafikam vexation, trouble; inikkuka to be sweet; initu, inutu a sweet or agreeable thing; inivu sweetness, love, | affection; (Tiyya) inippu sweetness. Ka, in (im, iff, etc.), ini, ine, impu, imbu, immu, irpu sweetness, pleasantness, charm, loveliness; inidu that which is sweet; imbane, immane in a sweet manner; irme loveliness, desire, charm, beauty. Tu. impu agreeableness, pleasantness. Te. incu to be agreeable; n. sugarcane; {K.) indu to be tasty, agreeable; impu (K., B. also imbu, immu) agreeable- ness, sweetness, beauty, pleasure, desire; adj. agreeable, pleasant; impitamu pleasin: agreeable. (b) Te. emme beauty, charm, amorov pastime; emmekadu a beau; emmekadi belle. Kur. emba a taste; pleasant to th taste. Malt. embe sweet, sweetness; emf embre to be sweetish

Ta. inam class, group, kind, specie: race, tribe, herd, associates. Ma. inam cla of animals, swarm. DED 452. $32 Ta. inai (-v-, -nt-) to Jament, crs grieve; Gpp- -tt-) to torment; inaivu cryin in distress, pain of mind. Ma. enayuka t moan, groan, lament, cry, sob; enaccal cryin: weeping. I

Ta. i fly, bee; tén-i honey bee; (dial., Andronov, p.65) iccai fly; iccdppi fly- flapper (ie icc-Oppi, cf. 2878). Ma. icca fly. Jr. i-ppi id. PalKu., AlKu. i-pi id. Ko, i-p id. To. i-py id. Tu. (B-K.) ippi eggs laid by a fly, usually in ulcers. Te. Iga id.; téti a large black humble-bee, Bombinatrix glabra (see 3268 (b) Ta. tén). Kol, ni-nga fly. Nk, ninga id. Go. phuki bee (phuk+i). Kui puki id. (puk +i). Kuwi (Isr.) ipu maggot. Kur. tini fly (tin+i); @xa gadfly, horsefly. Malt. téni bec. Br. hilh fly. Cf. 536 Ta. ical. DED (S, N) 453. 534 Ta, i, it wing; irkkil, irkku feather of an arrow. Te, tka, tke feather. Konda inka pubic hair. DED(N) 454. 535 Kol. (SR) ik- to pound. Mk, Tk- to pound grain. Nk. (Ch.) ik- to pound, (C.) to husk (rice). Kur. ixna to rough-husk, half- husk; ixma to be half-husked; (Hahn) grind, powder. Malt. igre to grind very fine. DED(S) _ 536 Ta, ical, iyal winged white ants. Ma, iyal winged termites; iyamparra_ moth, winged termite, grasshopper. Ka, ical the white ant when winged. Te. isullu winged white ants; usidi (pl usillu) winged white ant. Cf. 533 Ta. 7. DED 457.

Ta. intu (inti-) to gather, come to- gether, be close together, get to be a compact mass, abound; intu-nir sea; i¢tu (ifti-) to collect, hoard, accumulate, acquire; 1. a flock, swarm; iftam throng, group, abundance, acquiting, earning; iffal amassing wealt! Ma. iftam collection; iffuka to heap uy increase; ituka to join, increase; inta a swarn Ka. idu joining, uniting, increase, plents strength, wealth; idukdra, idugira a powerft man; (PBh.) inde heap, mass. Cf. 440 K: idi. DED 458.

Ta. infu (inf-) to gouge, extract, pu out. ? Ma, inuka to bend forward or bach ward so as to apply force. 540 Ta, im place for cremation of the dear burial ground; imam burning ground, funer: pyre; (Tinn.) funeral. Ma. imam cemeter: funeral pyre. funeral rites. , 541 Kur. ima white ant; imna to be deteri- rated or destroyed by white ants. Malt. 1m white ants. DED 460.

Ta. ir Cpp-, -tt-) to drag along, pul attract, carry away (as a current), excoriat (as a tiger), flay, draw, paint, write; (-v-, -nt- to be drawn out; saw; irppu pull, tu: attraction; ir-val a saw. Ma, fruka to sav split (or with 2491 Ma. cintuka); ir splittin; sawing; ir-val, ircca-val a saw; ircca sawin (Kaut.) irkkuka to draw, drag. To. i-€ (i-By-) to pull, drag; i-sf- (i-st-) id. (or wit 504 (a) Ta. iru). Ka. ir to pull, draw. Pe irp- (irt-) to pull. Ga. (S) ir- to pull, dra; Go. (Ma.) tic- to cut with saw (Voc. 3034 ? Pe. nir- (-t-) to pull. DED(S, N) 461.

Tu. fruni to rub off, be worn ow Pa, ire- to rub. DED 462. §44 Ta, ir, irkku, irkkil rib of palm lea! Ma, irkkil, ikkil, irkkal, i] stalk of coconu leaf, mid-rib of any palm Jeaf. ? Tu. (DCT cikuru rib of palm leaf. DED(S) 463. $45 Ta. ir minuteness; irmai subtlety minuteness. Te. ir slight (e.g. ir enda sligh sunshine; ir eltigu low voice). DED 464. iral
Ta. iral, irul internal organ of the body, as liver or spleen. Ma. iral liver. Jr. i-ralu, iervo id. A/Ku. (one dial.) i«ruvu id. Ko. i-suv id. To. ii-ruf id. Ka. (Ziegler) hiri id. DED 465.
To. in cicada. Tu. irinti, (BRR) iruptu cricket. Kui irri id. Malt, iro a chirping insect found chiefly on mango trees. DED(S) 466.

Te. Tlugu, nil(wgu, (K. also) nilugu to die, perish. Pa, il- to fall off (leaves, fruits, etc., from trees); itip- (ifit-) to cause (leaves, etc.) to fall off; (S) ilip- (ilit-) (hen) to shed its feathers. Ga. (OU.) ft to fali off, slip down; flup- (ilut-) to make to fall; (S.) il to fall down as fruit, etc. DED 467. §49 Ta, igam toddy, arrack; iravan, ipuvan one of the caste of toddy-drawers; iza-ccéri street inhabited by toddy-drawers. Ma. iravan = Ta. iravan. Ka. idi toddy; idiga man of the toddy-drawers caste; fem. idigitti. Te. idiga, indra the toddy-drawer caste; indravadu man of the toddy-drawer caste; fem, indradi. Cf. 550 Ta. igam; legend current in Malabar ascribes the origin of this caste to Ceylon. DED 468.
Ta. iram Ceylon. Ma, igam id. / ? < Skt. simhala-, Pkt. sihala-. DED 469.

Ta. ilai). Ka. ki to become pus, become putrid; kivu, kiva, kimu pus, matter of a sore; kini any impurity of the teeth. Kod, kiey- (kisyuv-. kiej-) to become rotten; cela pus. Tu. kivu pus. Te. ciku, civuku, (B. also) cimuku to rot; cimu pus; cimidi snot; cida any disease of animals or plants, rot, blight. Kol sivk- (sivikt-) to become rotten; siem pus; (Kin.) cimur, (SR.) simtd nasal discharge; (Kin.)} cirmur, (Pat., p. 31) simud ear-wax. Nk. sim pus. Go. (Koya Su.) sim id. (< Te.). Mand. hiven id. Kui sivendi, Sivenji, (K.) hivenji pus; sriva (srit-), (P.) stihpa (sriht-) to rot, decay. Kuwi (F.) siwessi, (Isr.) sivesi, (S.) simesi, (Su.) simesi, (P.) simu, (D.) sivendi pus; (F.) sirhali, (S.) srinai to rot; (Isr.) sir- (sirh-) to go bad. Kur. kittna to rot. Malt, kite id.; kitro rotten. Br. kish pus, snot. For the possibility of two bases, with k- and c- respectively, see Burro'v 1976, p. 41. DED(S) 1337.
Ka. ile orange. Te. ide a kind of orange. / Cf. Mar. id Citrus aurantium. DEDS 69.

Ta. iral grief (as of a broken heart), deep-seated sorrow. Ma, ira, iru anger, fury; [54] u iruka, irekka to be angry; irikka to rage, be in a huff; irippikka to provoke; irram dis- pleasure. Cf. 2639 Ta. ciru. DED(S, N) 471.

Ta. iru gums; eyiru gums, tooth, tusk of elephant or wild hog; (Koll.) evur, (SATD) ekur gums, Ma, éri gums; ekiru, akiru tooth. To. i gums. Ka. iru, igaru id. Te. iguru, ciguru the guins, the tender part under the nail. Go, (Tr.) hirar the gums (Voe. 3550). Cf. 418 Te. igul(ujcu. DED (S) 472.

Ta. in (inp-, inr-) to bear, bring forth, yean, produce, yield, bring into being; irram giving birth, bringing forth; irru bringing forth (applicd to animals), young one brought forth; i (-v-, -nt-) to create, bring into exist- ence, bring forth. Mfa. inuka to shoot into ears; to bear, bring forth, yean; iluka to shoot into ears; irram the womb of animals; irru bringing forth, the infant, young of animals. Ko. i-n (i-nd-) (animal) bears young; i-{ may (woman or buffalo) which having borne once or twice is barren (may) thereafter. To. i-n (i-d-) to bear (calf); i-t- G-ty-) (goddess) brings forth child, (god) makes to bear (calf); i-f o-y- to be about to calve;i-t e-p ir buffalo within a month of calving; i-¢ slime passed in dysentery or by buffalo during calving. Ka, in (id-) to bring forth young, yean, cub; iduvike bringing forth; i (id-), iyu to bring forth; fyisu, isu to cause to bring forth, help in bringing forth. Te. inu to yean, bring forth, bear, calve, foal, produce; inika calving, bearing; Tta yeaning, bringing forth young, Pa. in- (grain) produces head (vereil inomov). Konda ind- (-it-) (animals) to bring forth young. Br. hining to lamb, kid, foal, calf, etc. DED(S) 473.

Ta. inai vein of a leaf. Te. ine mid- rib of a leaf, or any one of the ribs running from it. Kuwi (Isr.) ina a sinew. DED 474. U 557 (a) Ta. u demonstr. base expressing a person, place, or thing occupying an inter- mediate position, neither far nor near, and meaning yonder or occupying a position near the person or persons spoken to; demonstr. part. before nouns, expr. intermediate position or position near the person or persons spoken to [u before consonant, uw before vowel]; uvan, uval, uvar, utu, uv/uvai that male person, that female person, those persons, that thing, those things; adj, unta; tiikan, taku yonder, where the person spoken to is; unnan, uffanam in the way you do, in the place where you are; u-ppal the side near the person addressed, that, (intermediate) side, upper or further region, back; umpar elevated spot, height, sky, celestial world, celestials, gods; adv. yonder, aloft; umparar celestials; ummai birth previous.to the present one; existence after the present life; uvan upper place, place above; uvanam height; uvanai the celestial region. Ma, u (L. V. Ramaswami Ayyar, The Evolution of Malaya- lam Morphology 32, finds no forms); umpar gods. Ko. u-n, u-l, u-r he, she, they (persons) (= nearer demonstr. stem i-, s.v. 410(a) Ta. i). Ka. u- base indicating intermediate placc, quantity, and time; adj. U; uva/iita, uval/tke, uvar, udu, uvu this male, this female, these persons, this thing, these things (intermediate); unitu so much as this (in the middle); untu

Ta. uka (-pp-, -nt-) to be glad, pleased, desire, hanker after; ukappu pleasure, joy, favourite resort, wish, choice; ukavai joy, happiness; uva (-pp-, -nt-) to be glad, Tejoice, be pleased with, like, be pleasing; uvakai joy, gladness, love, desire; uvappu joy, delight, desire, wish; uvavu preat pleasure, gladness; Okai (< uvakai) delight, joy. Ma. uvakka to love; to exult; uvappu gladness, love; uvavi inclination; upavi, Ovi, Spi love; upavikka to love. Ko. og- (ogy-) to be infatuated, desire strongly sexually; ? ok a-| lover; oka-c mis- tress. Ka. 6 (6t-) to be attached to, love, be pleased with, be kind; ? ose to be delighted or joyful, be delightful, be suitable; osage joy, joyful occasion, festival. Te. uvvilul-dru, uvvil-Oru desires spring up; to be eager or impatient, have a vehement desire, water (as the mouth at the sight or thought of); (B) uvvayi cheerfulness, joy; ? gomu love, affec- tion, fondness, cherishing. Cf. 1071 Ta. ©. DED (S) 476.

Ta. uka (-pp-, -nt-) to ascend, rise stately, soar upward; ukappu, uvappu height, elevation; ukalu (ukali-) to leap, bound, frisk, gambol, jump over, run about; ukali (-pp-, -tt-) to leap, jump, delight, exult; ukalu (ukali-) to billow, rise and roll in large waves; ukai (-pp-, -tt-) to rise, leap, jump up. Ma. uvakka to jump up; ukkuka to leap, spring upward or forward, start, rebound. Ka. oge to come forth, arise, be born. Tu. ubaru high; above. Te. ufikincu to spring, jump. ? Cf. 2828 Kol. sok-. DED(S) 477. 560 7a. uka, uva, uvay sandpaper t: Dillenia indica; wka, ukay, ukai, upa, 61 tooth-brush_ tree, Salvadora persica. ? uka-maram (D. speciosa), Careya arbor malay-uka Aleurites moluccana Rh. / ogani, uguni, goni, gOnu C. arborea Roxb., according to others, 8. persica Lin. Te. wv D. speciosa, DED(S) 478.

Ta. ukir finger-nail, toe-nail, claw. A ukir id. Ko, u-r finger-nail, claw, each half hoof; u-r-gan finger-nail. To. ti-r finger-n: toe-nail; ti-r-xon end of finger-nail. Ka. u; nail, claw; ugi to hurt, tear, rend with 1 nails; ugurisu to scratch asunder. Kod. nail. Tu. uguru. Te. gdru. Kol, go-r (pl. go-< Nk, gor (pl. gor). Nk. (Ch.) gor. Pa. ¢g (pl géril). Ga. (S.) gére. Go. (S.) gor (Ve 1233). Konda goru. Mand. gtr (pl. -k Kui godra, unguli, (K.) unjuli, Kuwi ( gort, (S.) gdru, (Su. P.) goru (pl. gork Kur, (Hahn, Tiga) or°x (Grignard’s or is erroneous; Pfeiffer). Malt. orgu. Br. finger. ? Cf. 926 Ma. okkuka and 2257 7 korai. DED (S, N) 479.

Ta. uku (ukuy-, ukk-) to be shed feathers or hair, be spilled, gush forth, f down, die, set; (-pp-, -tt-) to let fall, sp scatter, cast, shed as leaves or feathers, sh (tear), pour out; ukuvu spilling. Ma, tkka spill, shed. Ko. u-c- (u-c-) to be spilt; sr (f.), pour (water, grain), pour off (wat from grain). To. u-e- (u-&) to throw aw (dirty water); ux- (uk-) to leak, dribble; 1 (uf6-) to fall down (of flowers or frui (uft-) to shake off (water from head, du mud), empty (bag of grain), throw (spea’ shout (words of song, néw). Ka, ugu (ok} to become loose, burst forth, flow, rv trickle, be shed or spilt; let loose, etc.; vom ugisu to spill, shed, etc.; ogu, ogisu = ug ugisu. Tu, guppuni to pour, shed, spill; (B-k ugipu id. Xor. (T.) ogi to pour. Te. teu fall off as hair from sickness; guppu to thro’ fling, sprinkle (as something contained the closed hand), discharge (as an arrow). F uy-, (S) uv- (hair) falls out. Malt. ogore : tumble down, be rolled down; ogortre to rc down. DED(S, N) 480, and from DED 144

Ta. ukai (-v-, -nt-) to move as a boat, ¢ as a vehicle, walk as an animal; (-pp-, -tt-) 1 drive as a carriage, ride as a horse, row as boat, discharge as an arrow. Ma, ukayuka 1 go, reach, walk; ukaykkuka to drive. 564 Ta, ukkam waist; ukkal side; ukkal the hips; okkal, okkalai hip, side of the bod Ma, ukkam, ukkal middle, hip, side; okk hip, loins; okkil waist, hip; ? utukku loin Ko, uk part of waistcloth on each hip. 71 okka hip, waist. /? Cf. Skt. ukha- neut. particular part of the upper leg (RV+ DED (S) 481.

Ta. ukkalam night watch, advan guard, entrenchment around a camp. Ak ukkalam advanced guard, night patrol. K: ukkumam {56] uccu ukkada entrenchment about a camp, advanced guard, watch, guardhouse, end or outermost post of a town or village. Tu. ukkada, ukkuda lookout place between the bounds of two towns. Te. ukkalamu vanguard, pickets round acamp. DED 482.
Ta. ukkumam collusion, connivance. Ka, ukkiva, ukkeva cunning, fraud. Te. ukkivamu baseness, cunning, hypocrisy; ukki- vaidu a cunning man, hypocrite. DED(S) 484.
Ta. ukku (ukki-) to rot, decay, moulder, pine away, waste away; ukkal rottenness, that which is rotten or decayed. Ma. ukkuka to rot in water (as wood). ? Cf.

Ta. ukkaram). Konda urp- (-t-) to boil (as water); (BB) urpu steam. Pe, ur- (-t-) to boil up, effervesce. Mand. ur- (-t-) id. Kui urpa (urt-) to seethe, boil, bubble, emit froth; n. state of boiling, seething. Kuwt (F.) trhali to boil over; (S.) urh’nai to swell (boiled milk). Br, (h)tringing, (h)Urénging to swell up, break out (of boils, etc.), sprout. (b) Ta, uppu (uppi-) to become big (as a seed), bloat, puff up (as the abdomen), rise (as leaven); uppal flatulence; upukku (upukki-) to swell, overflow; upparam flatulent disten- tion of the abdomen (< Te.); (CTD) ubbu to become hot. Ko. ub- (uby-) (part of the body) swells; ubc- (ubc-) to coax child with pleasing words. To. ub- (uby-) to boil over; ?upum much; ? uporty pleasure, elation. Ka. urbu, urvu, ubbu to swell, increase, rise, be elated, be puffed up, become glad; n. state of being swollen, etc., height, elation, joy, pride; ubbisu to cause to swell, raise, rouse, puff up by flattering; ubbadiga, ubbudiga, ubbariga greatness; ubbafe, ubbatte swelling, increase, elation, greatness, boldness; ubbara, urbara swelling, increase, state of being swollen, risen, or full to overflowing, great- ness, abundance; ubbike, ubbuvike swelling, etc.; ubiku to swell, rise, overflow. Tu. ubbuni, ubbéruni to swell, be elated with joy or puffed up with pride; uberuni to become thick; ubbara high, swollen, turbid. Te. ubuku to swell, heave, rise, jut, bulge, project, overflow, burst out; 7. swelling, jutting, heaving, bulging, projection:

Ka. ugarisu to gasp, breathe hard, respire with difficulty. Je. ukkisa a dry cough; (B.) ogar(u)jcu to gasp, breathe hard; (B.) ogar(u)pu gasping, panting; vagar(u)cu to sigh; vagar(u)pu sign, asthma. / Te. ukkisa possibly < Skt, ut-kasate, Pali ukkasati coughs; Turner, CDIAL, no. 1722. DED 487.

Ta. ukkam rope or cord attached to anything, as to a handle (< Te.). Ka. ugga id.; ukkada piece of rope that is tied to the lower end of a well-rope for fastening the vessel, and is of a different texture, generally not so thick. Tu. (BRR) oggi a suspender or cord so attached to a pot that it may be carried about with its mouth untilted. Te. uggamu a suspender or cord so attached to a pot that it may be carried about with its mouth horizontal. Nk. (Ch.) ug(g)ur rope, bowstring; ogur rope. Kur. ugi the top-knots by which the strings which support a carrier’s loads are connected with the shoulder-stick. Cf. 708 Ta. uri. / Cf. Pkt. (PNM) ukka- = kupatula-. DED (S) 488. 571 Ka.-uggu repeatedly to utter unmean- ing sounds, stammer; 7. unmeaning sounds (such as one uses to amuse a baby), stammer- ing; uggadisu to utter repeatedly, cry out, repeat certain meaningless sounds so as to harmonize with dance or music; uggada, uggadane repeated sounds, noise, clamour, repetition of certain meaningless sounds uttered to harmonize with dance or music. Tu. ugguni to stammer. Te. uggadincu, (B also) uggalincu to utter; sound, produce sound. DED 489. 572 To, ugorm ring (in song; < Badaga). Ka. udgara, ufigura, ungra ring. Tu. ufgila id. Te. ung(a)ramu id. Kol. ongaram id. Nk. (Ch.}) ungriyam, uygra ring. DED(S) 490.

Ka. us, ts, hus, hds sound used in sighing when tired. Tu. usu, ussane a deep sigh. Te. us interj. expresses tiredness, pain, dejection, etc. DED 491. 574 Te, usirika emblic myrobalan. Kol. (SR.) usurka gooseberry. Go. (ASu.) usirka a tree. Konda usrika maran, (Sova dial.) strika mranu, (BB) sUrika maran aonla tree, emblic myrobalan. Pe. hiirka mar id. Mand. jUrka id. Kui (K.) jura id. Kuwi (Su. P.) jur’o id. DEDS 71. 575 Ka, usuku, usaku, usige, usabu, usubu, usuyu sand. Te. usuka, isuka, isumu_ id.,; (VPK) isaka (isike, iseka, iska), usike (useka, uske) id. Kol, (SR) uska, (Kin.) uska id. Go. (M.) uska, (A.) uske id. (Voce. 293). Konda iska id. DED(S) 492.

Te. usumu to scour, wash, cleanse; (K.) usugu to rub; be chafed. Kol. (SR.) usm- to wipe, apply. Vk. (Ch.) us- to remove dirt, clean. Pa, uyk- to rub, rub off; (S) ujip- (ujit-) to wipe, sweep. Ga, (Oll.) uyk- to rub, rub off. Go. (Tr.) ustimand to wipe, clean with a cloth; (W. Ph.) usmana to wipe; (G. M. Ko.) ums-, (Mu.) umis-, (Ma.) ume- id. (Voe. 295). Malt, nusge to rub, clean; nusgre to rub oneself. DED(S) 493. ; 577 Ko, ucg- (ucgy-) to move slightly horizontally (intr.); uck- (ucky-) id. (¢.). To. usx- (usxy-) id. (intr.); usk- (usky-) id. (tr.). DED 494. 578 Te, (K.) tyu (ts-) to spit. Kol. u-s- (u-st-) id. Nk. Us- id. Nk. (Ch.) Us- id.; sen spittle. Pa. uce- to spit; uckud spitting, spittle. Ga. (Ol) us-, (S.) Uc-, (S.2) Os- to spit. Go. (Tr. A. Ch.) ucc-, (Ko.) us- to spit; (A.) uc, (Ch.) dc, (Ph. Ma.) uccul, (Ko.) usul, (KoB.) liuccul spittle, saliva (Voe. 224); (A. Mu. §.) usk- to spit, spit out (Vl. action; MBE 1975, no. 110); (Ma. S.) uskul, (Mu.) uskle spittle (Voc. 291). DED(S) 495. $79 Ta. ucci crown of head, head, summit, zenith; uccani highest point, top, summit; ? ukkam head. Ma. ucci crown of head; uccani highest point, summit, top. To, uky top of tree, zenith. Ka. ucci top or crown of head. Te. ucci id. DED 496.

Ta. ucci dog; ucc-enal utterance of an imitative word used in calling a dog; uficu onom. expr. used in calling dogs; ucuppu (ucuppi-) to urge on, as dogs. Ka. ucci a particle used in calling dogs. DED (S) 497. 581 Ta, uccu (ucci-) to conquer in a game, get possession of another’s property by fraud. Ma. uccuka to win or secure by subtlety or artifice. uc- 57] ufa

Ty. ujjfru, (B-K.) ujveru a_ pestle; (B-K.) ujide, jidde a small mortar. Bel. (LSB 2.2) ijde mortar; ijgeri pestle. Pa. uyp- to husk (rice). Ga. (OIL) uyup- (uyut-) to pound (rice, etc.). Go. (Tr. SR. W. Ph.) ussana, (G. Mu. Ma. S. Ko.) us-, (M.) usina to pound with pestle, husk rice (Voc, 298); (Tr.) uskal a big pestle, rice-pounder; (Ch.) uskal, (A.) usval, (G. Mu. Ma.) usmal, (M. Ko.) uspal pestle (Voce. 292). Pe. uh- (ust-) to husk (rice). Mand. uhka- to pound. Kui uhpa (uht-) to strike against, impress, make a mark, write; n, act of striking against, marking, writing. Kuwi (F.) ussali, (Su.) uh- (ust-) to pound paddy; (S.) uh’nai to pestle. / Cf. Pkt. usuyala- mortar (see Turner, CDIAL, no. 2360). DED(S, N) 498. 583A Kui uje, ujene truth, accuracy, certainty; true, etc.; truly, etc. Kuwi (F.) Ujjoje’e falsely; Ujje’e falsehood; (S.) ucee veh’nasi liar. Kur. ujgd uprightness, moral rectitude; adj. straight, upright; (Hahn) straight, right, true, straightforward. Malt. ugjo true, real; truth. DEDS 72. 584 Ta, ufiarru (ufiarri-) to attempt vigorously, strive diligently, do, perform; urge, spur to action; m. zeal, effort. Tt. onary art, skill, means, attempt, device. Te. onaru to happen, occur; onarincu, onarucu to do, perform, make, effect; onangudu to happen, occur, be effected, be obtained, be fulfilled; onagtircu to effect, cause, ac- complish, obtain. DED 499.

Ta. utakku (utakki-) to be fitted to the string of the bow (as an arrow); shoot (as an arrow); n. thread of a screw; utu point where the arrow is pressed against the bowstring, feather of an arrow, arrowhead. Ma, utakku catch, latching, notch, obstruction, dispute; ufakkuka to catch hold of, resist, wrestle. DED (S) 500. 586 Ta, ufal body, birth, texture of cloth; utalam, ufampu, ufar body. Ma. utal, ufalam body, trunk, life, texture of cloth; ufampu body, anus. Ko. off belly, interior of body, trunk of body. To. wit chest of body, desire. Ka. odal body, belly, stomach; odambi, odambe body; doflu, dollu a proturberant, huge belly. Tu. udalu, udalu, odatu belly, stomach; dollu, follu, tollé fat, stout; a pot- belly. Te. odalu, olflu body. Konda orol (ob, oroR-) id. DED(S) 501. 587 Ta, utu (-pp-, -tt-) to put on (as clothes), surround, encircle; ufuttu (ufutti-) to dress one; ufukkai clothing; ufuppu cloth, unsewn garment, clothes; utai clothes, gar- ment, dress. Ma. utukka to dress, put on (chiefly the lower garment); ufuppu dressing, clothes; utuppikka, ufuttuka to dress another, marry; uta cloth, dancer’s pantaloons. To. udp dress of non-Todas. Ka. udu (uff-) to put round the waist and fasten there by tucking in or by a knot, wind or wrap roun¢ the waist; udisu to cause to put on in ¢ peculiar manner (as certain clothes); udi, ude udu, udike, udige, uduge act of putting or in a peculiar manner (as certain clothes) raiment put on in that manner, raiment ir general; uduka, udu

Te. uduku to boil, seethe, bubble with heat, simmer; . heat, boiling; udi kincu, udikilu, udikillu to boil (7), cook Go. (Koya Su.) udk é@ru hot water. Kuwi (S. udku heat. Kur. urturna to be agitated by the action of heat, boil, be boiled or cooked; b: tired up to excitement. Ta. (Keikadi dialect Hislop, Papers relating to the Aborigina: Tribes of the Central Provinces, Part It p. 19) udku (presumably udku) hot (< Te.) DED(N) 504.

Ta. utukkai small drum tapering ir the middle. Ma. utukka a tabor resemblin; an hour-glass. Tu. uduku a kind of smal drum. Te, uduka small drum of the shape o' an hour-glass. / Cf. Skt. hudukka- a kind o' tattle or small drum; hudukkad- a kind o° drum. DED 505.
Ka. udute squirrel. Te. uduta id. Cf

Tu. (B-K.) ulutigy a tick found on th: body of animals. Pa. udum (pl. udmut) tick Go. (Tr. Ph.) urtm (pl. urtthk), (D. Mu. urum, (Ma.) urm(i) id. (Voc, 277); (ASu. urlm id. Mand. rime id. Kui damba id. insect parasite. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) ru’ma tick (S.) Ima horsefly. DED(S, N) 508.

Ta. utumpu iguana, Varanus benga lensis; (Koll.) ofakkan a lizard. Ma, ufumpt iguana. Jr, uruga/iruga id. To. (Sak.) uduxi (probably udx) a big lizard. Ka. uda, udt iguana. Kod. udumbi id. Tu. udu, odu, oddt id. Te. udumu id. Kol. udug (pi. udgul) scaly pangolin (the meaning is probably wrong) (Kin.) urug iguana. Nk. hurug (pl hurgul id. Pa. udu (pl. udul) id. Go. (Tr. W. Ph. wrum (pl urruhk), (Mu. Ko.) urpal, (Ma. orpal(i) id.; (M.) urpal anteater (Voc. 270) (Koya Su.) urpal iguana. Konda urbu, (BB urbu id. DED(S) 509.

Ta. uftai wealth; ufaimai state o possessing, possession, wéalth, property ufaiyavan owner, possessor. of wealth ufai [58] un ufaiyan, ufaiyavar, utaiyar master, lord. Ma. ufama property; utaya possessing; utayavan, utayatu owner. Ko. oryn owner; oyrm jewelry, things in house. Jo. wirm things burned at funerals, vessels. Ka. odame a possession, wealth, ornament, jewel; ode, odetana, odeyatana, odatana possession, ownership, lordship; odeya owner, lord, master, ruler; odati female having ownership, mistress. Kod. odevé master, god; odeyé husband. Tu. odaye, odeye proprietor, master, Jord; odati mistress; odatana, odetana proprietor- ship; odave property. Te. odami, odame wealth; odayadu, odayfidu master, lord, owner, husband; odayuralu mistress, female owner. Kur. tndri, tiri, (Hahn) undri mistress of a house. / Cf. OMar. (Master) od&ra owner. DED(N) 510.
Ta. utai Acacia planifrons; A. latronum; A, eburnea, Ma. ota a kind of thorny tree, umbrella thorn, A, planifrons. Ka. (Lush.) odejali A. planifrons (for jali, see 2474). DEDS 73.

Ga. (S) uff- to be trapped, caught in a trap. Go, (Tr.) urrana to be jammed in a trap (of fish). (Voe. 267d). DED 511. 596 Kol. ut- (utt-) to fall down. Pa, uf tirr- to stumble. Malt. ure to fall, drop down. DED 512. 597 Kur. utna (uttas) to close an aperture by filling or obstructing; refl.-pass, ufUrna (uftras) to set, be hidden behind. Malt. ute to cover, fill up (as a hole); utgre to be covered up, be filled up (as a cavity or hole). DED $13. 598 Kur. urna to have one’s sufficiency, (stomach) fills; grow broad, be as thick as required; urda’aina, ur'dnd to give one his fill, quench (thirst). Malt. ure to be filled, be satisfied; urde to fill (as the belly), satisfy. DED 514. 599 Ka, uddi, oddi heap or pile, esp. a pile of three with a fourth set on top. Tu. uddi any pile of three with one on the top. Te. udda, udd& a parcel or pack of four. DED 515.

Ta. un (unp-/unnuv-, unf-) to eat or drink, suck (as a child), take food, swallow without biting, enjoy, experience; unfaru- to eat and digest, experience to the fullest extent; unti food, boiled rice, food of birds and beasts, experience; unni one who eats; unnir drinking water; una food; unavu, una food, boiled rice, foodstuffs; tftam food, refreshment; Wtti food of birds and beasts, food; tttu (tifti-) to put small quantity of food into the mouth (as of a child, sick person), coax and make one eat one’s food (as a mother her child), entertain (as a guest), instil, infuse, dye, cause to experience (as the fruits of one’s actions), suck (as a calf or kid); . feeding, food, morsel given to a child or sick person; Uf¢fuvan one who prepares food, cook; tin eating, food, experience of joys and sorrows by the soul, as the inevitable fruits of karma; Unan glutton; tpi one who eats. Ma. upnuka to eat (esp. rice), suck; unni suckling, infant; dttuka to feed, feast gods and brahmans, temper iron; Uftu giving rice, esp. to brahmans, welding iron; Uftukar cooks; tin food, boiled rice, meal. Ko. un- (ud-) to drink, suck; unke- (unke-) to make to drink, make to feast; u-{- (w-y{-) to suck (young of animals that stand while suckling); u-tm meal paid to god at god-making cere- mony; u-n food, food eaten after dry funeral. To. un- (ud-) to drink, (child) takes breast, (rain) rains; u-n food, feast. Ka, un (und-), unnu, umbu to eat what forms a person’s (or in poetry, certain animals’) real

Ka. unaci-minu porpoise. Te. ulaca- minu, uluca id. / Cf. Skt. ulupin- id. From DED 597. 603 Ta, unar (-v-, -nt-) to wake from sleep, get back to consciousness, recover from languor, be conscious of, know, consider, examine, experience, become reconciled (as husband to wife); upar, unarcci mind, knowl- edge, feeling; upartti id., recollection; uparttu (unartti-) to teach, enlighten, wake from sleep, pacify, remind; unarppu clearness of mind; uparyu consciousness, waking from sleep, reconciliation. Ma. unaruka to awake, watch, be conscious, perceive; unarcca watchfulness; unarvvu intelligence; unarttuka to rouse, inform; unarttikka to inform a superior. Ka. onar to feel, perceive, under- stand. DED 518.

Ta. upni tick on dogs, sheep, cattle. Ma, unni tick on cows, dogs. Ko. uny insect that infects the eyes of men or animals and sucks blood. Ka. unni, unme tick; (Hav.) [59} utar upuggu id. Tu, (OCV) unufgu id. Kor. (M. unga id. Cf. 600 Ta. un. DED(S) 519,

Ma. utakuka to prosper, thrive. Ke odagu, odugu, odavu to become endowe with power, prosper, thrive, increase. Tu odaguni to prosper. Te. (K.) odavu to flourish odalu to increase, flourish; odugu, oduvu t: increase, thrive; m. abundance. DED 520. 606 Ta, utatu lip, brim, edge of a wound utafan blubber-lipped man; fem. utati. Mc utafu lip. ? To, u@ur gums. Ka. odadu, odudu odaru lip. DED(S) 521.

Ta. utappi undigested food in th. stomach of a beast; ventricle of animal: Ma. utappi id.; otappa ventricle of animal: 608 Ta, utappu (utappi-) to scold, reprove utampu (utampi-) id.; to frighten. Ma. ota ppuka to scold, reprove. 609 Ta, utavu (utavi-) to give, contribut (as one’s mite), help, aid, assist, be of use utavi aid, help, assistance, gift, contributior ? Utiyam profit, gain,. benefit, advantage. Ma utakuka to serve, be at hand and of use, helr utakkam, utavi help. Ko. odg- (odgy-) t: become ready, help; odk- (odky-) to mak: ready; odyv aid, help; of- (oty-) to b friendly; ot state of being friendly, help nearness, proximity. To, wi@x- (wiOxy-) t: get ready (intr.), be of use; wiOk- (wi®ky- to get ready (¢r.); wi€fy help, aid; o-@y help usefulness. Ka, odagu, odugu, odavu to kee] Close to, come to hand, accrue to, be a hand, be of use; odagu that is at hand, tha has accrued to one or has come into one’ possession; odagisu, odavisu to cause (some thing) to come to or near, cause to b: obtained, give, produce. Tu, odaguni to b- opportune or ready; odagavuni to procure supply, prepare, assist. Te. odavu to b- serviceable, useful, suitable, fit, or proper serve, occur, arise; (K.) give. ? Cf. 972 Ta ottacai. DED (S) 522.
Ta. utal ram, he-goat. Ma, utal ram goat.

Ta utalai jungle mango, Cerben odollam, Ma, utalam, utal id. DED 523. 612 Tu, udalu white ant. Kor. (0.) odal id. Nk, (Ch.) udri id. Go. (Hislop) udeli (A,) udri, (Y.) udri id. (Voe. 245). DEDS 75 613 Ta, utaru (utari-) to shake off, shak: out (as a cloth), renounce (as the world friends, etc.); shake, as one’s hands, feet, o body, through cold, fear, or anger; uta Cpp-, -tt-) to tremble with fear, shiver witl cold; utaippu fright, alarm; utir (-v-, -nt-) t: be shaken with the wind; (-pp-, -tt-) to shak: off, Ma, utaruka to be in hurty or confusion shake off. Ka, odayu to shake, shake off; shaking, etc.; odarisu to cause to shake. Tu udévuni to throw out; the hands to shak as in sickness. Te. udaru, (K. also) udury udilu to tremble, shake, shiver, quake; uti {60] ubusu trembling, shaking. Ga. (Oll.) udurp- (udurt-) to shake. Cf. 615 Ta. utir. DED 524. 614 Ta, uti, oti Indian ash tree, Odina wodier; uti falcate trumpet flower, Doli- chandrone falcata. Ma, uti, oti O. pinnata. Ka, udi O. wodier Roxb. Te. oddi O. pinnata. Kol. (Wagh.) oddi id. (< Te.). [O. pinnata js not identified in Hooker.] DED(S) 525.

Ta. utir (v-, -nt-) to drop off (as leaves, fruits), fall out (as hair), be blasted, drop down (as tears), crumble (as cakes), be demolished; (-pp-, -tt-) to cast leaves or fruits (as trees), cause to drop or fall in numbers, strip, knock out (as teeth), shed (as tears), break in pieces; m, crumb, piece, fragment; utiri that which falls off or is separated, paddy that has fallen from the stalk. Ma. utiruka to fall, drop (as fruits, leaves), moult (as feathers), drip through a sieve; utirkka to cause to drop, shed (tears); utircca, utirmma falling, dropping, showering. To, wi8- (wi8y-) (waist-cloth) slips off. Ka, udir, udaru, udur to fall, fall down or off, drop (as fruits or leaves from a tree, hailstones from the sky, teeth, etc.); udirisu, udircu, udarisu, udarcu, udurisu to cause to fall or drop down, etc.; udaru falling or dropping down. Tu, uduruni, udaruni to fall, drop off, hang down. Cf. 613 Ta. utaru. DED 526. 616 Ta, utai (-v-, -nt-) to kick, plant the foot firmly against a post or a wall; (-pp-, -tt-) to kick, spurn, beat; m. kick, pressure of foot as in pushing, beating. Ma. uta a kick, re- bound; utayuka, utekka to kick, rebound, of- fend, cheat; utappu striking against an offence. Ko, od- (ot-), ov- (ovt-), o- (ot-) to kick; od a kick; od a-r- (a-c-) to struggle, try (to do, find, etc.), perform the movements of sexual intercourse; od a-rl act of struggling. Ka, ode (odad-/odd-), odi to kick, spur, fix the foot against or on; ode a kick; odaha kicking, spurring

Ko. ut- (uty-) to draw out from a bundle, draw from socket or hole. Kod. utt- (utti-) to untie (knot). ?Cf. 652 Ta. uri. DEDS 74. 618 Ta, uttanta-mani, uttantal woman’s necklace made of gold beads. Ka. uttanda, uttandalu, uttaragige gold necklace worn by women. Te. uttandamulu (pl.) gold necklace worn by women; (B. also) uttariga a kind of necklace. DED 528. 619 Ka, uttarani, uttarane, uttarini, utta- réni a common weed. Achyranthes aspera Lin. Tu. uttarane id. Te, uttarénu, (VPK) uttaréni, uttaréni id. DED(S) 529.

Ka. uttutte, uttatti, uttotte dried dates, the fruit of Phoenix dactylifera L. Tu, uttutti the dried date fruit. Kol. (Kin.) usti mak wild date (khajira). Go. (ASu.) ucctti date palm. (Kamaleswaran for Kol. and Go.) /Cf. Mar. utoti date. DED(S, N) 530.

Ir. uddya long. Ko. udm length. Ka. udda, uddi, uddu height, length, depth. Kod. udda length, height; uddaté long. Tu. udda length, distance; long. DED 532. 622 Ka, uddi, uddige low ridge in a field for retaining the water of irrigation. Te. uddari a steep bank; uddi bank or dam with a channel on it to convey water pumped into it to higher lands. ? Ma, utir little heaps of ground for planting rice in marshes. DED 533. 623 Ta, utti a player on the opposite side, corresponding to one on one’s-own side in a game in which the players are divided into two parties (< Te.). Tu. udri a match, pair. Te, uddi a match, an equal, a rival; equal; uddincu to pair, match, couple; uddikadu, uddidu companion, friend. DED(S) 534.

Ta. unti navel, belly. Ma. unti navel. ? Cf. Ta. tunti navel, belly. DED(S) 535. 625 Ta, untu (unti-) to push out, thrust forward, fling, discharge, cast away, drive, send; uttu (utti-) to discard, throw away; nuntu (nunti-) to propel, thrust forth, cast away; unnu (unni-) to propel. Ma. untuka to protrude; push, thrust, shove; untu a push, shove; untikka to cause to push. Ko. ud- (udy-) to push (person, small object with hand). To. ud- (udy-) to jostle. DED 536.

Ta. uppasavar a Telugu caste who are usually tank- and well-diggers and road- workers (< Te.). Te. uppara name of a caste whose occupation is digging tanks, etc.; uppari a digger, tank-digger; fem. upparadi. Cf. 628 Ta. upparakkaran. DED 537. 627 Ta, uppili name of several plants. Ma. uppila Macaranga indica R.W. Ka. uppalige a small tree, M. i. Tu. uppala, uppaligé M. 7.; (B-K.) iplige = uppaligé. DED 538. 628 Ta, upparakkaran one who plasters a wall, who rubs mortar (< Ka.). Ka, uppara plastering; uppara (< upparakara) brick- layer, stonemason, plasterer; fem. uppdratt; upparike the business of a bricklayer. Cf.

Ka. uppi thorny medicinal styptic shrub with a gall as large as a nutmeg, Volkameria capparis sepia. Te. uppi a kind of herb; (B) V. capparis sepia, Monetia barlerioides, Azima_ tetracantha, Capparis sepiaria; puyyi a thorny shrub, C. s. DED(N) 540.
Ta. uppi a game of women. Ma. uppi id, DED 541.
Te. ubusu, (K.) Usu leisure; chat, talk; (Visakha dialect) Osul-ddukonu to chat. Pa. Ub- to speak, converse; Ubal a saying; ibkud conversation, a saying. Cf. 937 Ka. osage, usir. DEDS(N) 76. uppacam

Ta. uppacam, uppucam asthma (< Te.). Ma. ummifttam difficult breathing, sobbing of children; umayuka to. breathe heavily; uma a cough. Ko. uba-cm panting. To. ubosm quickened breathing (as in fever). Ka, ubbasa, ubbusa, ubbusu, urabasa, urbasa, ummalu difficult breathing, asthma. Tu. ubbasa, ubbesa difficulty of breathing, asthma. Te. ubbasamu asthma. DED 543. 633 To, iif in- (id-) to blow, blow away (e.g. ashes). Ka. uph, uphi sound emitted when strongly blowing with the mouth to remove impurities. Br. huf puff of wind, blast, windy talk; huf kanning to blow upon. DED(S) 545. : 634 Ta, umari, umiri marsh samphire, Salicornia brachiata. Ma. umari Salicornia, DED 546. 635 Ta, umal long bag made of palmyra leaves. Ma, umal id.; omal a kind of basket used by fishermen.

Ta. umi (-v-, -nt-) to spit, gargle; umi- nir spittle, saliva; umivu spitting; umig (-v-, -nt-) to spit, gargle, emit, vomit; umir-nir spittle, saliva; umigvu spitting, spittle; tumi (-v-, -nt-) to spit (contamination of umi and tuppu). Ma, umiyuka, umikka to spit out; umi, umigu spittle; umigka to spit, emit. Ka. ugi to spit, spirt out, emit; ugur (ugurd-), ugar to emit (as saliva, etc.), spit out, spit, sputter;'m. spittle, saliva; ugurisu to cause to emit or spit; uguguvike spitting, etc.; ummalu, ummulu phlegm, mucus. Tu, ubbiyuni to spit; ubbi saliva, spittle; ubbina spittoon; ugipuni to spit out; (B-K.) ubi to spit. Kor. (O.) ubbi to spit; (T.) umi saliva. Te. umiyu pet spit out; ummi spittle, saliva. DED

Ta. umi husk; (-pp-, -tt-) to become chaff; (Koll.) ummi husk, Ma, umi husk, chaff, bran. Ko, u-ym flour. Ka. ummi, ubbalu, ubalu husk or chaff of paddy; uy, uyyu, hay husk of rice or corn. Kod. umi paddy husks. Tu. umi, ummi husk or chaff of paddy. Te, umaka (B), umuka, fika, ka husk, chaff. Ga. (S.°) tka id. Kur. unk id. Malt. umku id. DED(S) 548. 638 Ka, (Gowda) ummuni an insect. Tu. umily, (B-K.) umbli mosquito, gnat. Nk. (Ch. ummel mosquito: DEDS 77,

Ta. umpal descendant. To. ub, in song-units: en mox uba+, en mary ubae O my child! DEDS 78. 640 Kur. umpna to perforate, pierce through, cut a passage for (through some- thing). Malt, unrpe to bore through; unrpre to be bored through; unrpo bored, a hole. DED 550. 641 Kui umung uta (uti-) to fall prostrate. Kur. umb’t, humbu’t, umba’a, humba’a, humkurya, omba’a, homba’d with the face turned downward; umb’i manna to bow (61] profoundly, go down on one’s knees. Mc umgre to bend (as in respect); umgro be meek, humble. (Pfeiffer: Kur. < Mund DEDS(N) 79.

Ta. ummaccu, ummicam groove in ' framework of a jewel in which stones are be set. Ma, ummaccu a wire-drawer’s pla Te. ommaccu bezel which fastens a preci: stone in a jewel, cavity in which a precic stone is set in a jewel. DED 551.

Ta. umm-enal utterance of an int: um expressive of assent; um-koftu to respe by ejaculating um as in listening to a st that is told; Um-enal expression signify: assent, attentiveness. To.
