Skt. hum, hiim and M and NIA derivatives, espressive of attention a assent, Turner, CDZAL, nos. 14132, 1412 e.g. Lilasuka Bilvamafigala’s Krgnakammam: 11.72, in Frances Wilson, The Love of Krish (University of Pennsylvania Press, 197: pp. 166-7; Parpola 1977-78, pp. 246-! DED 552. 644 Ta, (Devanesan: Tinnevelly dial.) m: to urinate; (Tinn.) mdlu id.; (Koll.) mal: : Ma. molla, mollu urine; molluka top: urine. Zr, mallu to urinate; 1. (Bhattachar 1958) urine. Ko, mol id. Kol. umb (umbut-), (Kin.) umul-, (SR.) Oml- to urina umbulud urine. Mk, umbul- (umbut-) urinate. Vk, (Ch.) umbul- id.; umulta, um! urine. Pa. uml-, umbl- to urinate; umluke umbulkud urine. Ga. (P.) umbl- to urina umbulkur urine; (S.°) Ul to urinate. G (Ch. Driberg) dl urine (Voe. 325); (AS: ul id. Konda mil- (mut-) to urinate: mal urine. Pe, miinku (pl.) id. Mand. mtn (pl.) id. Kui milba (mot-) to urinate; mal urine, Kuwi (F.) mytkali to urinate; mro‘ urine; (S.) minkinai to urinate; munka urir murkinai to piddle, piss; (Su. P.)

Ta. uy (-v-, -nt-) to live, subsist, ha being, be saved, be relieved (from troubl: escape (as from danger); (-pp-, -tt-) to ensu salvation; uykai salvation, deliverance, esca- from hardship; uyti salvation, deliveranc remedy, expiation, ceasing; uyvu escapi: from danger, salvation, remedy; uyal livir being, escaping; uyavu means of saving li! uyir (-pp-, -tt-) to revive, regain consciot ness, be in vigorous functioning activit breathe hard, be wafted as fragrance, breat' one’s last; give birth to, smell, say, decla: emit; m. life (animal or vegetable), so: uyar [62] ural living being, wind, voice; uyirppu reanimation, revival, breath, wind, air, sighing, sweet fragrance; ucir life. Mfa. uyir, usir life, breath; uyirkka to live, revive, survive; uyirppu life, reanimation; uyirppikka to quicken, raise to life; virkkuka to sigh, breathe; virppu breath. Ko. ucr life, male genitals (polite word). To, ii-r, usir life; yu-k, tiy, in song units; yu-k fitad drawing longing sighs, tiy nar@ad walking with heavy breathing. Ka. usir, usur, usuru breath, life, taking breath, caesura; usalu breath. Kod. usiri breath. Tu. usuru, usulu, nu- sulu breath, life. Te, usuru, usuru id.; usuranu to sigh, be much vexed; tir(u)cu to breathe, sigh; tr(u)pu breath, sigh; Orata taking or recovering breath, being refreshed, conso- lation, comfort, rest; Gradil()u, dradu to be consoled, calmed, appeased; iiradincu to console, comfort, appease. Konda usutr life, breath. Kur, ujjna to have life, reside, beco

Ta. uyar (-y-, -nt-) to rise (as water), move toward the meridian (as a heavenly body), be high, elevated, lofty, grow, increase, be excellent, eminent; (-pp-, -tt-) caus.; n. greatness, renown; uyarcci height, excellence; uyartti excellence; uyarttu (uyartti-) to lift up, elevate, raise (as price), promote to higher office, honour, praise, raise the voice; uyamtor the great, celestials, persons born in the higher castes; uyarpu height, mound, greatness; uyarvu lofty height, greatness; uyaram, ucaram height, eminence; uyari that which is tall; (Koll.) ocar to be raised; ocatt to lift; ocatti height; ocakké above; ocaram height. Ma, uyaruka to rise (as birds), be high, eminent, tall; uyaram height, pride; uyarcca elevation; uyarttuka to raise, elevate. Kod. oyandé that which is high (of field in a valley); oyandadi the place higher up. Tu. Orige tallness, height. DED(S) 555.

Ta. uyar (-v-, -nt-) to vanish, disappear, be removed, Ma. uyaruka to be lost. To, u-r- (u-8-) to disappear, be lost; (u-d-) to disappear suddenly by magic; u-f- (u-t-) to cause to disappear of a sudden, be a terrible fellow and do damage; u-@, in: fr u-8, o-f u-8 nm. pr. places at the end of the road to the world of the dead ‘where the buffaloes, the people disappear’ (cf. 4376). DED(S) 556.

Ta. ura Cpp-, -tt-) to become loud (as the voice), become harsh (as a noise), become furious (as the wind), be boisterous (as the sea), become violent (as a controversy); urakka loudly, distinctly; urappu (urappi-) to whoop, shout so as to menace or intimi- date, bluster, roar, frighten, cause to sound loudly; urappal whoop, roaring sound; urappu shout, roar, intimidation, bluster, threat; uraru (urari-}, urarru (urarri-) to resound, roar; urai (-pp-, -tt-} to sound, speak, tell; n. roar, loud noise, speaking, utterance, word, fame; uraiyal narrating. Ma. urakka, urekka to speak, say; ura, uri word, famé; urammuka, urampuka to grumble, roar (cf. 718 Ta. urumu); uriy-atuka to utter, speak; uriy- attam talk; orappuka to vociferate in driving cattle. Ka. ore to sound, utter, speak, say, relate; m. word; ura, uru crying; uruvani crying, crying aloud. Kod. orad- (oraduv., orat-) to answer (or with 650 Ko. orp-). Kor, _(M.) ojji to say. Te. Uraka (neg. gerund of *Or-), uraka, uraka silently, quietly, not speaking; merely, simply, vainly; roda noise, outcry, uproar; ? r6ju to pant, gasp; 7. pant- ing; rdjudu prattle. Pa, ur- to groan. ? Go, (SR.) ronjana to grieve, cry (Voc. 3069). DED 557. 649 Ta, ura (pp-, -tt-) to become firm, hard ‘(as the soil), become thick, coarse (as paper); urappu hardness (as of rice that is not well boiled), coarseness or roughness (as of cloth or paper), resoluteness, strength; uram strength, hardness, compactness, resolution, heart of a tree, manure (as

Ko. orp- (orpy-) to act in overween- ing, desperate fashion, refuse to listen, (ill- ness) is dangerous. To, warp- (warpy-) to refuse to do something. Ka. ura, uru im- petuosity, etc.; uruvani, uruvane haste, rashness, passionate and overbearing behav- jour; uruvanisu, uravanisu to act hastily, overbearingly, etc.; urutu to be overbearing; urutu, orafu, ortu overbearing manner, gruffness; (PBh.) orantu overbearing manner; orantutanam the state of being overbearing. Kod. orad- (oraduv-, oraf-) to answer (or with 557 Ta. ura). Tu. oratuni, oradaduni to wrangle; oradifa mutual wrangling; oranfu, orantu hatred, ill-will, retaliation; orantuni to hate, retaliate. Kuwi (S.) Urinai to ignore; (Isr.) tr- (-it-) to disobey. DED 559. 651 Ta, ural mortar, mould for making vermicelli or the like. Ma. ural wooden mortar for beating rice. Ko, olka-{ mortar; olka-L kal a stone mortar; olkeyk, olki-k work of preparing grain by pounding. Ka. oral, oral, orlu, ollu mortar of stone or hard wood. Kod. ora mortar of stone or wood. Tu. oralu, (B-K.) uraly, oraly a large uri [63] ur mortar. Te. rolu, rolu mortar. Cf. 665 Ta. urai. DED(N) 560.

Ta. uri (-v-, -nt-) to peel (intr., as skin, bark); strip off (as clothes), deprive of, rob; Cpp-, -tt-) to slough off, flay, strip off (as bark); 2. rind, peel, skin stripped off, bark; urivai skin, hide, peel, rind, stripping, flaying, peeling off; uruvu (uruvi-) to strip (as beads from a string, as leaves from a twig), unsheath (as a sword). Ma. uri skin; uriyuka to be stripped, skinned; strip off; utikka to flay, skin a jackfruit, coco-nut; urippu, uriccal flaying, stripping; druka to strip off, flay, draw off or out, unsheathe. To, uSt- (uSty-) to take off (ring, bangle, shirt or coat, sari). Ka. uricu to flay, strip off covering or skin, cast off (as the slough); get loose; urcu to draw (as a sword); become loose; uccu to draw (as a sword), cast (as a slough), loosen (as a knot); become loose, be cast (as a slough); ore to draw, pull, draw out or off; (Hav.) urmbu to pluck leaves. Tu. rumbuni to strip off, pluck off, as leaves from a stalk; rumbu stripping, plucking; (B-K.) urumbu, rumbu to tear off (as leaves from a twig). Kor. (M.) urbu to pluck leaves. Nk. uyp- to take off (clothes wrapped round middle). Pa. uyk-, uykip- (uykit-) (serpent) to slough its skin. Go. uy~ (Mu.) to be flayed, (Ko.) to be sloughed (skin of snake); (Ma.) uy?- (snake) to slough its skin; (Ko.) uysp- to flay; (Mu.) uvile, (Ma. Ko.) uyka slough of snake (Voc, 257); (SR.) ucchana to strip (of hemp); (Tr.) UWcand to strip hemp, pluck (bird), (pigs) strip kodon field; (G.) ucc- to strip off (Voce.
Ta. uri a measure of capacity = 4 measure. Ma, uri half a nari, or two ugakku. DED 562.

Ta. urimai ownership, that which is owned, claim for right of possession, slave, services of a bondservant, duty, affection, liberty; urittu right, proprietorship, affection; uritu that which is related; uriyan, uriyavan, uriydn one who is worthy, qualified, one who has rights, heir. Ma. uriyan suitable, proper; Urppu claim, right. Kur. urb well- to-do, well-off, rich; urbas’ master, landlord, (pl.) parents; urbni mistress of the house, lady. DED(S, N) 563.

Ma. uri a running knot, loop, noose. Ko. urkl mo-r slip-knot (mo.-r id.). Ka. ural, uril, urul, ural, urlu, urlu a running knot, noose, snare. Tu. urlu a noose, snare. Te. uri noose or running knot, halter, snare, execution by a halter; uccu slip-knot, noose, halter; (K.) uralu, urulu to be caught in a snare. Konda (BB) Uri a trap for birds. Kui rusu, ruhu noose, snare, trap. Kuwi (F.) Orr (pl. Oyka) snare; Oru ogali to noose (fo ogali, see 934); (S.) uruta heth’nai id.; urr: hanging; (Isr.) uru, (Su.) huru (pl. -ka) snare (Possibly add 582; so K.} DED(S, N) 564.

Ta. uru (-pp-, -tt-) to burn, smart, b angry; uruppam, uruppu heat, anger; urumar heat (as of the sun, of the atmosphere: sultriness, noon; urumpu ire, exasperatior (Asher-Radhakrishnan, p. 155) uruttu (urutti- to make angry. Ma. uruppam anger. Jr. ubbe iiri-ni-ru sweat. A/Ku. uriid. Uru, UrapU, UripU, UrUpU, UruvU, UrpU burning flame; urugu passion, anger, wrath; ummal heat; grief, trouble; ummalike heat; grie/ distress; ummalisu to be hot; to grieve, b distressed. Kod. uri (-v-, -fij-) burning sensatio: is felt; uri burning sensation. Tu. uri blaze flame, heat, acute pain, wrath; uriyuni t- burn, blaze, feel a burning sensation, b angry, envious, (belly) is hungry; urkih prickly heat. Te. (K.) uriyu to burn (intr. be afflicted, grieve; (K.) uralu to burn (intr. be ablaze; ummalincu to grieve, sorrow ummalinta, ummalimpu, ummalika erie‘ uneasiness, disq

Ta. uru C-pp-, -tt-) to assume a forn issue forth, appear, come into existenc: n, body, shape, form, beauty, idol; urup form, shape, colour; uruvam = un 7.; uruy shape, form, Ma. uru, uruvu, uruvam forn Ko. urp shape, appearance, image of god i form of small silver plate; urv trunk of bods To, urp form, idol; uSt- (uSty-) to appea- come into sight, (star) rises; uf back of trun of body (also u-f in songs; uncertainty abou phonetics). Ta. uruku (uruki-) to dissolve (intr.) with heat, melt, liquefy, be fused, become tender, melt (as the heart), be kind, glow with love, be emaciated; urukku (urukki-) to melt (é.) with heat (as metals or congealed substances), dissolve, liquefy, fuse, soften (as feelings), reduce, emaciate (as the body), destroy; #. steel, anything melted, product of liquefaction; urukkam melting of heart, tenderness, compassion, love (as to a deity, friend, or child); urukkinam that which facilitates the fusion of metals (as borax). Kuwi (T.) rily- to be dissolved; (S.) rtkhnai to smelt; (Isr.) uku, (S.) ukku steel. Cf. 656 Ta. uru. DED(S) 569. 662 Go, (Tr., etc.) urunganad to be broken; uruhtand to break (t.) Ta. urai (-v-, -nt-) to be reduced into a powder or paste, wear away by attrition, be indented or effaced by rubbing; Cpp-, -tt-) torub into a paste, wear away by rubbing, grate, test on the touchstone, smear, polish; n, rubbing, friction, attrition; fineness of gold or silver as tested on the touchstone; urai-kal touchstone, small stone for rubbing pills into powder; uraical friction; uraicu (uraici-) to rub against (Gnir.), chafe, wear away by use; rub hard (#.), scour, waste away by rubbing; uraificu (uraifici-) to rub (t7.); uraippu rubbing, assaying; uracu (uraci-) to rub against; urificu (urifici-) to rub oneself, rub against; to wear away by rubbing (fr.), grind away, scrape, smear, anoint; urifiu (urifii-) to rub (éntz.); urifical rubbing, chafing; uray (-v-, -nt-), urayficu (urayfici-) to rub (intr, as an animal urai [65] urku against a tree, as two branches together); urdcu§ (urdci-), urdficu (urdfici-) to rub (intr.). Ma. urasuka to rub, come into contact, contend, form into a pill; urasal friction, contest; ura rubbing, a stroke; ura-kallu, uravu-kallu touchstone; urayuka to rub, wear by friction; uravu rubbing, touch; urekka to rub, grate, polish, grind, assay metal; uriyuka to be chafed; urufriuka, urammuka, urummuka, urattuka to rub against, graze, touch; urusuka to wear off, diminish. Ko. orv- (ort-) to rub into paste, rub with a stone in making pot; (ord-) to touch or stroke gently; orj- (orj-) to rub; uj- (uj-) to rub, file, sharpen. To. warf- (wart-) to rub

Ta. ula (-pp-, -nt-) to become dimin- ished, be wasted, be devoid of, die, terminate; ulakkai end, ruin, death; ulappu wasting, perishing, defect, death, limit; ulai (-v-, -nt-) to perish, be ruined (as houses, land, crops); (pp-, -tt-) to ruin; ulaivu ruin, destruction, defeat, poverty, trouble. Ma, ulakkuka to shrink up: ulayuka to be impoverished, tuined; ulaccal, ulavu ruin. DED(S) 579.

Ta. ulakkai pestle. Ma, ulakka pestle for pounding rice. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) alke pestle. Ko, elk pestle. To. wask grainpounder. Ka. olake, onake, onike wooden pestle for pounding rice and other things; (Gowda) onikE pestle. Kod. olake wooden pestle. / 2? Cf. Skt. ultikhala- mortar; Turner, CDIAL, no. 2360. DED(N) 580. 673 Ta, ulantu caseworm, larva of the caddis fly; silk produced by the ulantu. Ma. ulantu id. DEDS 83.

Ta. ular (-v-, -nt-) to become dry, wither, be parched up, pine away, droop; ularcci dryness, withered state, emaciated condition, weariness of the mind; ularttu (ulartti-) to dry (i7.), desiccate; wlaru (ulari-) to dry up, wither, be injured, worn out. Ma. ularuka to dry (ntr.); wlareca dryness; ularttuka, ularkkuka to dry (i7.), air. DED 581.

Ka. ulimiri the plant Crataeva tapia or Capparis trifoliata. Te. ulimidi, utimiri Crataeva tapia. DED 582. 676 Ta, ullam, ullam hilsa, silvery shot with gold and purple, Clupea ilisha; a sea- fish, silvery shot with yellow and purple, C, toli, Ma, ullam a kind of fish. Te, ullakd- jépa, (B) ullaku-cépa, ullam-cépa the bony hilsa or sable fish. DED 583. 677 Kol. ul (obi. uf-) day, in: mu-nd ul three days. Nk. (Ch.) Odil day. Kur. ulla day; ulléy two days; ullmiind three days; ullndx four days; odul, undul one day. Malt. ulle by day; ulli-mdqi night and day; ullond day before yesterday; ullte, ullti of the day; ullu daylight. DED(S) 584.

Ta. uvattu (uvatti-) to loathe, recoil from, satiate, surfeit; m. nausea; uvatti Cpp-, -tt-) to nauseate, loathe; m. nauseating, loath- ing; ukatfu (ukatti-) to nauseate (intr). Ma. uyattuka to loathe, recoil from; omattuka to have a vomiting sensation; ugekka to be inclined to vomit; urappu nausea; uyantuka, ugantuka to belch. Ka, ubbalisu, ubbulisu to nauseate, become squeamish; ubbala, ubbalike nausea, qualm; okkulisu to belch; ogadisu to vomit; ogadike vomiting. Tu. ukalu, ukkalu vomiting, an emetic. Kuwi (Ist.) ub- (-it-) to have indigestion. Kur. ullaxarna to feel nausea. Malt. unglare id.; unglatre to cause to feel nausea. ? Cf. 1029 Ta: Okkd@lam. DED(S) 547, DEDS 84.

Ta. uvi Cv-, -nt-) to boil away; Cpp-, -tt-) to boil (¢r.); uviyal a boiled dish; uvalam boiled rice. Te. (K.) uppu to boil (t7.); (K.) uppari that which is hot or heated on fire (as a pan). Go. (Mu.) uh-, (Ma.) u?- to cook by boiling; (Tr.) uhuttand to put dal into hot water (Voc. 301). Kuwi (f.) Upali to boil; sr.) up- Cit-) to boil food. DED(S) 585.

Ta. ura (-v-, -nt-) to experience sorrow, pain, trouble, ot fatigue, suffer; ugappu suffering; uravu bodily exertion; ugai (-pp-, -tt-) to labour hard, toil, suffer hardship; earn; uraippu toil, industry, effort, earnings; (inscr.}) ugappam work. Ma. urakka to be wearied, despair; ugayuka, ugekka to labour; urappu labour. Ka, urgi, uggi a kind of penance. DED(S) 586.

Ta. urakku measure of capacity (2 ollocks = 1/4 of a measure), dice-box. Ma. urakku 1/4 nazi. Ko, otk a small measure of capacity; i-r o-k two olk; (for o-r ak one olk, see 397 Ta. ayakku, arakku). To, wak small bamboo vessel. Tu. (D.N.S. Bhat, p. 12) oranka, olanka seer (measure). DED(N) 587.

Ta. uzampu (urampi-) to be disturbed, confused; urappu (urappi-) to confuse, disturb; wlampu (ulampi-) to stir up game. Ma. ugampuka to hasten, hurry; to be dis- concerted; ugappuka to hasten, hurry; to be dishevelled.

Ta. ugalai horizontal bar of wood in a doorway or across a road, crossbar. Ma. urala horizontal bar of wood placed in a doorway; ura-vatil a barred gate. Ko, u-gan uri [67] uruval pen-bars, planks used to close cowshed door. Ka. ubbana wooden beam for locking a door; a club; (Hav.) urvélu gate with round movable poles. Kod. ubba poles in slots forming a gate. Tu. uro, urolu, uruvolu, Urolu a bar across a passage; (B-K.) uruvelu, uruvolu movable bars across a gate. Kor. (O.) ireli a wooden latch. /? Cf. Skt. hudukka- the bar or bolt of a door (lex.), H. huguk, hurka (Kur. huyuk wooden bar or bolt of a door < NIA). DED(N) 544, DEDS 85. .

Ta. uri place, site, side; ugai place. Ma. uri circumstances; uga place, esp. about a king. DED 588. 685 Ta, uriiiai balloon vine, Cardiosper- mum halicacabum. Ma, utiiifia Cissus pedata, Cardiospermum halicacabum, Sapindus lauri- folius, used for Soma sacrifices. Tu. urunde- baru smooth-leaved heart-pea, Cardiosper- mum halicacabum (biiru creeper). DED(S) 686 (a) Ma. uriyuka to rub, stroke, em- brocate; ugiccil massaging, shampooing; ugiyal shampooing; ugivu wiping, polishing; (Tiyya) uyiccil massage. Ka. tdu to apply an unctuous substance, smear, anoint. Te. duvvu to stroke, tub gently or tenderly. Pe, rlic- (-c-}, rlcpa- to smear with cowdung; roh- (rost-) to rub, scrape (e.g. strings of violin); ,iz- (rUst-) to scrape, plane. Mand, yUhpa- to plaster. Kui Usa (sUsi-) to stroke, rub, scrape; 7. stroking, scraping; pl. action yUska (rUski-); rdsa (rdsi-) to scrape, draw one surface over another, play a violin; pl action yOska (qOski-). Kuwi (F., p. 119) rth-, (S.) lth’nai, luspinai, (T.) yUsp- to stroke; (Isr.) yUh- to pat; risa yUsa smoothly. (b) Go, (Tr.) Usana, (ASu.) Os- to smear cowdung; (M.) usina@ to smear. Konda Us- Gt-) to apply oil, anoint; caus, Usis-/Uspis-; Uspa- (-t-) to apply oil, etc. to one’s own body. Pe, dc- (-c-) to rub on (oil on hair, etc.). Kuwi (F.) Ussali to anoint oneself; Uspi kiali to anoint another; Uspali to plaster with cowdung; (Su.) th- (ist-) to anoint oneself (p.269); Usp- (-it-) to smear with cowdung. DED(S, N) 590, DEDS 839, and from DED

Ma. uziyuka to spit out (after rinsing the mouth). Ko. ulu-v spittle. Ka. (Hal.) ulugu, (HavS.) uligu to spit. Kor. (T.) uri, (M.) thu id. Pa. uy- to spit out (pips, etc., not saliva). Go. (A.) uyi- to spit out (e.g. stone of fruit) (Voce. 276). DED(N) 591. 688 Ta, uu (-v-, -t-) to plough, dig up, root up (as pigs), scratch, incise (as bees in a flower); urgavan, uravOn, uraan ploughman, agriculturalist; fem. uratti; uravu ploughing, agriculture; uzal ploughing, scratching, or probing (as bees the flowers); ugunar plough- men; ugakku (urakki-) to plough; tunnu (tunni-) id. (< Te.). Ma. uguka, urukuka, ugutuka to plough; ugavu, uzama_ tillage; ugavan ploughman, farmer. Ko. ug- (urt-) to plough, be ploughed; ukl act of ploughing; u-v one furrow in ploughing. To. usf- (ust-) to plough. Ka. ug (ugt, utt-) id.; urasu, urisu, urusu to cause to plough; uzata, uruta, ugame, ugime, ugume, ural, uzuvike, urike, uguke, urke, ukke ploughing. Kod. u-[- (upp-, utt-) to plough. Tu. dduni, hUduni id.; dappuni, adapuni (Brahman dialect adeppuni, ada- ppuni; L. V. Ramaswami Aiyar, BSOS 6.900) id.; ura, dappu ploughing; uraly, oralu, oralu a ploughman’s or herdsman’s song. Te. dunnu, dunu to plough, till; dukki ploughing, tillage. Kol. ur- (urt-) to harrow; (SR.) to plough. Nk. ur- to plough, harrow. Pa. uy- to plough; (S) dukki cultivated upland field called in Ha. maran (< Te.). Ga. (S) ad- to plough. Go. (W.) urind, (A. Ko.) ur-, (Pat.) ud- (written ud-), (SR.) urana, (M.) urdand id. (Voce.

Ta. uru (-v-, -t-) to arrange or adjust (as hair with the fingers); ? ular (-v-, -nt-; ulari-) to spread out and draw the fingers through (as in drying wet hair), adjust or smooth out as birds their feathers. Ko. ug a-{- (a-c-) to clean hair by untangling snarts, removing lice, etc, Kod. u-k- (u-ki-) to comb. Tu, dubina a comb; urvane an instrument for destroying nits; urané a kind of comb. Kor. (T.) uyyali to comb. Te. duvvu to comb; duvvena a comb. Nk, (Ch.) a- to comb. Pa. uy-, (S) urv- id. Ga. (S.) uduv-, (P. S.?) urv-, (S.°) udv- id. Go, (G. Mu.) ur-, (S.) Os-, (Pat.) uccana id. (Voc. 275, 225); (Koya Su.) dus- to comb hair. Konda dus- id.; dUsay 4 to comb one’s own hair. Kuwi (F.) rissali to dress (another’s hair); rica a comb; @.) lea, (S.) Nica id. DED (S, N) 593. 690 Ta, urguntu black gram, urad, Phaseolus mungo Linn. Ma, ugunnu P. radiatus, kidney- bean. Ka, urdu, uddu P, mungo, var. radiata, Linn. Tu. urdu P. mungo, kidney-bean. Te, uddulu P. radiatus, black gram. Kol. (Kin.) urunde black gram. Nk. urndal (pl.) urad. /Cf, Pkt. (DNM) udida-; H. urad, urad, urd, Mar. udid; Turner, CDIAL, no. 1693. DED 594, 691 Ta, uguval love. Ka, urugu, urigu, uggu to be attached to, be fond of, love; uyugu erotic sentiments; urge attachment, love. Kui orpa (ort-) to pine for, lust after; n. desire, lust (or with 1006 Ta. ollu). DED 595. uguvai [68) ul

Ta. uruvai tiger. Te. (K.) duvvu id. Kol. (SR) duva, (Kin.) dt panther. Pa. dt (pl. duvul) tiger. Go. (M.) du, dual, (L.) duval id.; (A) dual, (Pat.) duwal, duwwal panther (Voc. 1578); (Koya Su.) duv (pl. dak) id.; (ASu.) duvwval id.; (Koi [Cain, [4 8.35]}) duvvu tiger; ?(SR.) duhkyad leopard; (A.) duhkiak wolf (Voce. 1579). ? Ga. (S.) ducca wolf. DED 596.

Ta. uruvai a green sea-fish, Gobius ornatus; a brown freshwater fish, G. giuris; a yellowish fish, found in fresh- and back- waters. G. striatus; uluvai G. ornatus; G. giuris; a sea-fish, brownish grey, Saurida tumbil. Ma. uruva a kind of green sea-fish; a kind of yellow fish. Te. unuju a kind of fish. DED $97.

Ta. urai deer. Ma, uga-man, ugal-man porcine deer. Ka. duppi the spotted deer with branching horns, the axis. Tu. uré, ule, (B-K) ule deer. Te. duppi id. Kol. (Kin.) duppi id. Pa. urup (pl. urpul) spotted deer. Ga, (P.) duppi id.; (S.2) duppi stag. Go. (A.) duppal, (S.) duppi, (Ko.) luppi, (M.) ltpi spotted deer (Voc. 1570, 3117). Konda dupi axis deer. DED(S, N) 598.

Te. Ud(u)cu, tr(u)cu to sweep, collect with the hand or otherwise, as what is scattered on the ground or other ,surface; td(u)pu sweeping. Kol. (Wagh.) urp- to sweep up, collect (rubbish). Vk. urp- to collect together what is scattered. Pa. uycip- (urcit-) to heap up, put together. Ga. (S.3) trs- (drus-) to wipe with finger (like cream froma cup). Go. (Tr.) urpana (so Voc.; Gramm., p.67, urpana) to clean a threshing floor; urp- (Ma.) to scrape into a heap with hands, (Ko.) to collect (dust) in a heap (Voc. 281). Konda @rs- (t-) to sweep or clean (a threshing floor). Kui dupka (< duk-p-; dukt-) to scrape to- gether, sweep up, clear away rubbish, scavenge; m1. act of scraping together, sweep- ing up, scavenging; ? ritpa (rift-) to scrape into a heap. From DED(S) 506.

Ko. uc- (uc-) to have diarrhoea; ucl stool in diarrhoea; uc urine (only in a pro- verb). To, u+c- (u°é-) (buffalo) has diarrhoea. Ka. urcu, uccu to be purged; uccike, uccita diarrhoea; ucce urine. Kod. ucce id.; ucc-a-f- to urinate. Tu, urcuni, (D.N.S. Bhat, p. 12) ulc- to go to stool; urcafa looseness of bowels, Uliyuni to ooze; the bowels to evacuate frequently. Te. ucca urine. Go. (Tr.) urkand, (S-R.) urkana, (Mu. Ma. S.) urk- to urinate; (Tr.) urk, (Ph.) urkul, (Mu.) urk, urkle, (Ma.) urkul, (S.) urukulu urine (Voc. 278). DED(S, N) 576.

Ta. ul to be, have (1 sg. ulén, ullén, 3 sg. m. ulan, neuf. ulatu, 3 pl m. ular, neut, ula); untu is, are (existence); ulla who is, which is, true, actual; ullatu, ulatu that which is, truth, soul; ullavan he who has, rich person; ullar those who are present, those who possess; ulamai truth, reality; ulavu real nature; unmai existence, reality, state of being, nature, truth. Ma. ul to be there, exist; untu there is, exists; ulla existing, true, real, in or to which there is; ulavu coming into existence; ullavan rich; unma reality, truth; (K.M. Narayana Menon, p. 36) onfu has; olla having; (p. 258) olfama, onma existence, Ko. ol- (3 pers. neut. odo-) to exist, be in a place; od truth. To. wild- (3 pers. wid-i) to exist, be in a place. Ka. ul (3 pers. untu), ol to be, have; adj. that is, — that is true; ulla being, having, being able; ullatana state of being or having; unqu that is or exists, existence, existing; ol(u)pu, oluhu essence, possession, trueness; unmu to arise, come into existence (or in 4482, with Ka. punmu). Kod, ull- (3 pers. undi) to be, be in a place, have. Tu. ull- (3 sg. neut. undu; pres. tense) to be, exist, have. Te. undu to be, exist, live, dwell; un(u)cu to place, put, set, lay, keep, preserve, set apart for a purpose, appoint; uniki existence, being, remaining, stay, dwelling, home, place, residence, state, condition. Kol. an- (and-), Nk. and- to be in a place, be so-and-so, owe d to *untu. ? Kui lohpa (loht-) to abide, remain, reside. ? Kuwi (S.) loy-,

Ta. ul inside, interior of a place, mind, heart; a locative ending; ullam mind, heart, intention, thought, soul; ullakam mind, heart; ulla! intention, thought; ullu (ulli-) to think, think on, remember; ulku (ulki-) to think. Ma, ullu inside, whatever is inside; locative ending; uflam, ullakam inside, mind. Ko. ul the inside; in, into; olg-l, olk (< olg-k) within the time before (something happens); ulpar- (ulpat-) to obey. To. ul the inside; in, into; ulpor- (ulpot-) to agree (-k to). Ka. ul the inside; ol id.; in, into; ofa, olavu the inside; olagu that is inside, the inside; heart; in, into; olage in the inside, in, into. Kod. oli the inside; ulli inner thought, that which is in the mind. Tu. ula inside, inner part; uliyi in, into; ola inner, internal; (B-K.) olavu the mind, secret, thought. Te. ullamu the heart, mind; 10 in, inside; inner, internal, inside; léga, within, in, inside; I6gada interior, past time; légali, Idgili interior of a house, house; lénu the inside, heart, mind; lona, (Inscr.?) olana (7th cent.), léna (9-10th cent.) within, inside, inwardly; (K.) l6n-agu to come under one’s control; lopala within, in, inside; 716gu, lofgu, I6badu to yield, submit, be subdued, retreat; lékuva sub- ordination, subjection; subordinate, subject to; lécu to subdue; (K.) loggu to be humbled, subdue, Kol. lo+pal the inside; in, inside. Nk. \Opa ady. inside; l6pal the inside. Nk. {Ch.} lopun inside. Pa, ole(k) house, Ga. (OIL) ule, (S) ullen id. Go. (Tr. W. Ph. A.) ron (obi. r

Ta. ulu wood-worm; that which is rotten; (-pp-, -tt-) to be worm-eaten (as wood), be eaten out by insects (as grain, seeds); uluppu condition of being worm- eaten, worm-eaten tree; (Koll.) uluvey wood- worm. Ma. ulumpu grain-moth; ulu wood- worm; ulukkuka to be worm-eaten; uluppu state of being worm-eaten. ? Ka. (Gowda) ulhgu a nit. ? Te. lita ant. ? Kui lupenji a speties of ant. Cf. 3715 Ta. nulampu. DED 602. 701 Ya, ulai mane, hair of head. Ma, ula mane of horse or lion, man’s hair.
Ta. ulai mud. Ma, ula id.

Ta. ufku (utki-) to be afraid, feel shy; n. fear, shame, modesty. Ma. ulukka to start, be unnerved; uluppu shamefacedness, feeling. Ka, ulaku to start, make starting efforts so as to get out of a swamp, etc. Te. ul(u)ku to start suddenly as from alarm; 7. alarm, fright. DED 603. 704 Ta, ullal, ullan, ullu common snipe, Gallinago media. Ma. uli sandpiper. Ka. ullatgi snipe; lapwing. Te. ullamu_ snipe; ullanki, ullanigi sp. curlew; lapwing (acc. to some authorities), DED 604.

Ta. ulli onion, garlic. Ma. ulli id. Ko. uly onion. To. uly id.; pol u-ty garlic ( Ta. vell-ulli). Ka. ulli a bulb, an onion. Tu, ulli, ulli a generic name for garlic (B-K. ulli a generic name for onion or garlic); boll-ulfi garlic. Te. ulli onion, garlic. Kol, (Kin.) ulli onion. Nk. (Ch.) ullig id. Pa, ulli garlic. Ga. (P. S.7) ulli onion. Go. (Tr. W.) ulli, (A. Y. Mu. Ko.) ulli id. (Voc. 283). Konda uli id. Pe, tri id. Mand, uli id. Kuwi (F.) ulti, (S.) ulli gidda, (Su.) ulli id.; (P.) vella Olli garlic. Kur. (Mirdha dial., BB 1958) uli onion. / Cf. Skt. Oli- id. (Schmidt, Nachtrdge), Or. uli id. DED (S) 605. 706 Pa, ud- to crush (nits, lice; only re- corded in phrase: pénul udomo). Konda ur- (uRt-) to butt, gore (buffalo, etc. with horns), crush (nits). Pe. uz- (ust-) to butt, gore; to crush (lice). Mand, uy- (cow) to gore; to crush (lice). Kui ubga (< ug-b-; ugd-) to collide, strike against, butt; pl. action uska (uski-). Kuwi (Su.} ur- (h-) to butt, gore; (F.) Urhali to butt. DEDS 86.

Ta. urahku (uranki-) to sleep, feel drowsy, be weary; uraku (uraki-) to sleep; urakku (urakki-) to put to sleep (as a child), weigh down the eyelids as in sleep; urakkam, urakku sleep, drowsiness, weariness. Ma. uranfuka to sleep; urakkuka to put to sleep; urakkam, urakku sleep. Jr. rofgu to sleep. Ko. org- (orgy-) to sleep; ork sleep. To, warx- (warxy-), war- (wary-) to sleep; wark- (warky-) to make to sleep (by lullaby, etc.); wark sleep. Ka, urugu to be crooked, bent, distorted; oragu, oratigu id., incline, recline, lean upon, lie down, rest, sleep; urugu state of being crooked; oragu id., cushion to lean the back against. Kod. or- (ori-) to fall asleep; oraki sleep. Tu. oraguni to recline, lean the back against; oragu cushion to lean the back against; orduni to bend, bow, lean on one side; orda bent; orva crookedness. Te. oragu, oragu to bend, bow down, lean, incline, recline; n. slant, inclination; large pillow for reclining on; adj. slanting; orava crooked, bent; oragudu slant, incline; orag- incu to place in a slanting position; oggu to offer, present, give, lend, yield. Pa. org- to lean. Go, (Ko.) urng- to be bent; 7, urh-/uhr- (Voc. 267a); (SR.) variyana, (S.) varah- to bend (fr.) (Voce. 3183); (ASu.) vari- to bend (intr. ); varts- id. (tr.). DED(S, N) 606. 708 Ta, uri hoop or rope network for placing pots, and suspended by a cord from the roof of a house, from the hand, or from the end of a pole carried on the shoulder. Ma, uri network for suspending pots. Ko. ury s

Ta. uri (-v-, -nt-) to snuff up, sip up; urificu (urifici-) to sip, suck up, draw into the mouth as in tasting liquids, snuff up, sniff, draw in sharply through the nose, take in, absorb as a sponge. Ka. urubu violence (of wind), rapidity, fleetness, speed, force. DED(S, N) 617. 720 Ta, urai (-v-, -nt-) to thicken, curdle, coagulate, congeal, freeze; mn. reserve of curds for curdling milk; urai-mGr small quantity of sour diluted curd poured over milk to curdle it. (Tr.) urrand to coagulate (of ice, dahi, oil, etc.) (Voc. 267d). DED (S, N) 618.

Ta. urai (-pp-, -tt-) to become firm, steadfast, decided (as the mind); uraippu firmness, steadfastness; uruti firmness, strength, certainty, assurance. Te, orapu steadiness, firmness; steady, firm; uridi firm, strong; firmness, strength; uriya a brave man. Kur. ordna to support. Cf. 763 Ta. Onru. DED 619.

Ta. urai (-pp-, -tt-) to be biting, pun- gent, sharp; #. pungency, corrosiveness; uraippu sharpness, pungency. Ma. ura sharp- ness, Te, orra pungent, acrid; pungency, acridity; orridanamu, orrana pungency, acridity; varra pungent taste, pungency; pungent. DED(S) 620.

Ka. uni to be soaked, lie steeped, soak; unisu to soak; Te. (K.) unupu to dye clothes. Ka. tsaravalli, Usarulli, husarul({)i the Indian monitor, Varanus dracaena, Te. isaravelli sp. chameleon. Ga. (S.3) Usarelli chameleon. Ta. Otu (Uti-) to gnaw through and bore holes (as a beetle or moth). Kur, utUgna to sting, (water) to be piercingly cold, rouse one against a third person; (Hahn) utga sting; (Pfeiffer). Ta. tim dumbness; tman, uvaman dumb man; fem, Umacci, Umaicci; timai dumbness, dumb person; Umaiyan a dumb man. Ma. tman dumb, stupid. Ka. ume dumb man, taciturn man. DED 639. 747 Ta, Oman owl. Ma. Ta. ur (-v-, -nt-) to be unloosed, re- laxed. Ma, truka to slip off, fall off. Te. uriyu to shake, tremble, get loose. DEDS 90.

Ma. tUrkkuka to blow, fan or kindle. Ko. irp- (irpy-) to blow through (tube, wind instrument). To. iirp- (iirpy-) to play (flute). Ka, urubu to blow strongly with the mouth; (Nanj.) urbu to blow off; (Gowda) urgu to blow air to enkindle fire. Kod. urip- (uripi-) to play (flute). Tu. urpuni to blow a wind instrument; uripuni to sound, play upon. Kor, (O.) drpu to blow. Go. (Mu.) ur- (2 tr-) to biow with mouth, play on flute, kindle fire by blowing, exorcize; (Ma.) fir- to blow (fire, flute), (M.) urand to blow (Voc. 259). Kur, tina to blow, play upon a wind-instru- ment, set a fire to, put out (a light), draw (poison by magic insufflations). Malt. ure to blow (as the wind does), blow a fire or a trumpet, breathe upon (in sorcery); durtre to have exorcisms performed in case of illness. Cf. 645 Ta. uy and 741 Ta. Otu. DED(S, N) 578.

Ta. tr village, town, city. Ma. tr id. Ko. user village. To. usr village of Tamils or Badagas. Ka. tr village, town. Kod. weti village. Tu. Oru village, town. Te. tru id. Br. hifri mud receptacle [i.e. unbaked clay jar] for storing fodder, etc. DEDS 91.
Pa. thuvi (PL tluvul, tlukul) plantain. Ga, (P. 8.7) uluk id.; (S.2) ulg banana. DEDS 92. 755 Ma, Uval ituka, Oral ituka to whistle; Ovi whistle. Te. Wa a whistle; (B} dyu to whistle. DED 644.

Ta. Oy (-pp-, -tt-) to decay (as flesh, fruits), become putrid, be spoiled, rot, stink; Urttal stench; Ural dirt, mud, mire, that which is putrid, nasty (as excrement), decayed, spoiled; Ogali (-pp-, -tt-) to become spoiled, decayed (as meat, fruits); gil filthy mire; Dlai offensive smell; ticu (Uci-) to decay, become rotten, become stale; tcal that which is fetid, that which Has become stale; ty (-v-, -nt-) to be overripe, decay; uru (-pp-, -tt-) to decay, rot, putrefy; Uttai dirt, im- purity). Ta. Ula a carnivorous marine fish, Sphyraena acutipinnies. Ma, wlavu a large flat sea-fish, ray, skate.

Ta. lai how! of dog or jackal, cry of person in anguish (applied contemptuously); Dlaiy itu to howl as jackal or dog; Uli sound; alan jackal; ulampu (ulampi-) to make a noise, howl (like a jackal); ularu (ulari- ) to roar, shout, clamour without sense; ularal shouting, clamour; ulai (-v-, -nt-) to howl (as a jackal); (-pp-, -tt-) to sound, roar, howl (as a jackal), give forth a sound, call, invite; 2. sound, high tone of voice, weeping. Ta. ekku (ekki-) to pull with fingers (as cotton), search for (as true meaning of a- passage), scrutinize; ekkuru to be pulled thinly with the fingers (as cotton). Ka. eggu, heggu shame, feeling of disgrace, blame, harm; egguli a bashful person, a rustic or low person; egga a rude, rustic, stupid, or low man; eggulitana shame, bashful- ness. Ta. ecar water in a pot set over the fire for boiling rice (< Ka. or Te.). Ka. esar, esaru water in a cooking pot when in a state of boiling, water that is strained off from boiled vegetables or pulse, the water with the boiled vegetables, etc., pepper water. Te. esaru water boiled for the purpose of dressing food. Kol. esar water boiled for cooking grain. Nk. esar hot water in which meal, etc., is boiled or flour is kneaded. Nk. (Ch.) esar var- (water) to boil. Ga. (P.) esar water in een rice is boiled. Cf. 881 Kui @ju. DED(S) 778 Ka, ese to shine, be brilliant, be beautiful, appear; esaka shine, splendour, beauty, appearance, state of being, fate, delight. Te. esakamu joy, delight, (K.) splendour; esaihgu, es4gu to arise, appear, exist, happen, (K. also) shine; (K.) esalaru to shine, flourish. DED(S) 665.

Ta. eccam remainder, remains of food, spittle, whatever has come in contact with the mouth (as defiling), dung of birds, lizards, etc.; deficiency, defect; eccil saliva, spittle, anything defiled by contact with the mouth, anything defiled, refuse of food, Jeavings, excretions from the body (as faeces, urine, semen), leavings of sacrificial oblation made of pounded rice; eccilar persons defiled by contact with eccil, low-caste people, untouchables; eccu defect; eficu (efici-) to remain, be left behind, survive, lack, be deficient, be spoiled, marred, transgress; efical defect, blemish, extinction; eficalar strangers; eficali (-pp-, -tt-) to bring discredit upon, cause degradation, cause discomfiture. Ma, eccil, iccil remains and refuse of victuals; ecci remains of victuals; eccam excrements of lizards, flies; eficuka to become surplus. Ko. ecl water that has been used to wash hand after eating, remnants of food, food eaten as part of ceremony as leavings from offering. To, icil pollution of food by being left aS a remnant, pollution caused by death or birth. Ka, efijal that which in eating is left on the plates, sticks to the mouth or hands, or falls to the ground, that of which a piece is bitten off, ie. al food or drink which has somehow come in contact with the mouth or hands of the eater and there- fore is regarded as impure; efijalisu to defile; Cal.) nefijala spittle, food or drink which "has come in contact with the mouth and ecar {77} etna is regarded as impure. Kod.

Ta. etfi, atti strychnine tree, Strych- nos nux-vomica, Ma. etti id. Ka. itfi, ittangi, itte, itahgi id. DED 668.

Ta. eftan boor, fool (< Ka.). Ka. eddu, heddu stupidity, foolishness; a stupid thing or person; edda, hedda a dull, stupid man; fem, eddati, heddati; eddatana, eddu- tana, heddatana stupidity; edega a good-for- nothing fellow; fem, edati. Te. eddaya, edde, eddédu an ignorant man, boor, fool; eddetanamu stupidity. DED(S, N) 677.

Ta. en thought, intention, delibera- tion, esteem, calculation, mathematics, num- ber; ennu (enni-) to think, consider, deter- mine, esteem, conjecture, count, reckon, compute, set a price upon; ennam thought, idea, respect, deliberation, anxiety, mathe- matics; ennar, ennalar mathematicians; ennal intention, counting, deliberation; ennikkai numbering, esteem, reverence; €ni number; &tal meaning, intention, thought. Ka. entu arrogance. Tu, entu boasting, pride, craftiness, cunning. DED 679,

Ta. etir that which is opposite, over against, in front, before; obstacle, that which is contrary, adverse, hostile; adv. in front; (-v-, -nt-) to happen, befall, come to pass in future, precede, be opposed, be at variance; oppose, confront, meet; (-pp-, -tt-) to meet face to face, encounter, oppose, withstand, prevent, hinder; etirntdr adversaries, com- batants; etirmai happening in future; etirvu meeting, confronting, happening; etiri enemy. Pa. endr-. DED 823. 977 Kur. ottha burden; heavy, difficult, hard to suffer, important. Malt. ote heavy, important. DED 824. 978 Kur, odna (oddas) to kindle a fire by means of straw or shavings. Ta. eppiraci, Eppiyan simpleton, fool. Ka, &brasi, ebada a foolish, silly man; fem, ebadi. Tu. ebulante halfwitted, silly. Te. ebberasi, ebrasi a slovenly person; ebberikamu disgust, loathing; loathsomeness; ebbe interj. expr. a feeling of disgust; (B.) eb-bettu dislike. DED 689.

Ta. ey (-y-, -t-) to discharge arrows; n, arrow; eyin hunting tribe of the desert tract; eyinan hunter; € shooting, arrow; @vu (évi-) to hurl, discharge (as an arrow), throw (as a dart); n. arrow; evvu (evvi-) to discharge (as a missile or arrow); écu (&ci-) to hurl, dart. Ta. ey (-pp-, -tt-) to know, understand; eyyamai ignorance. To. iy- (is-) to know how to. Ga, (P.) etap- (etat-) to think. Konda nes- (-t-) to know, be capable of. DED(S) 692.

Ta. ey (-pp-, -tt-) to grow weary, fail in strength, flag as from want of food; exert oneself; eyppu weariness, languor, time of adversity. Kui epa (et-) to arrive, reach, overtake, be sufficient; eppa (ept-) to cause to reach, cause to arrive at, cause to suffice. Ka. eral, eral, elar, elal wind, air. Te. temm-era cool breeze, zephyr; temm-eral south wind (Kumarasambhava; inscr., p. 99) (cf. Te. temma wetness, moisture, in 2539); payy-ara cool wind, south wind (for Te. payy-, see 3821 Ta. paca, paital). Cf. Ta. nigali air, nizal wind. DEDS(N) 96.

Ta. eri (-v-, -nt-) to burn, blaze, shine, (sore or fever) burns, suffer a painful emotion; Cpp-, -tt-) to burn, consume by fire, scorch in flame; ». fire, flame, brightness; ericcal burning, heating, burning sensation, pungency, anger, jealousy; erippu burning, pungency, envy; eriyal burning, shining; that which is burnt, as charred food at the bottom of a vessel in cooking; (PR) eriyam phosphorus; erivu burning, heat, anger, jealousy. Ma, eri heat, burning, pungency; eriyuka to burn, be consumed; erivu pungency, zeal; ericcal burning, smarting of eyes, mouth, etc.; erikka to kindle fire, burn (f7.); erippu flame, torch; eruvu pungency. Kod. eri- (erip-, eric-) (mouth) has burning or astringent sensation; erivi burning or astrin- gent sensation in mouth. Tu. eriyuni to glare, be hot; caus, eriyavuni; eri the glare or heat of fire. Te, eriyu to burn, grieve; eriyincu to burn, set on fire, inflame, afflict, hurt; eragali, eravali wildfire; ércu to burn, set on fire; @rpu burning; (K.) talay-éru headache. Kol. erk- (erekt-) to light (fire). Nk, erk- id. Nk. Ch.) erk- id. Pa. erip- (erit-) to burn the mouth. Go. (Ma.) ers-, (Mu.) ars- to taste pungent (Voc. 356); (Mu.) arri- (boil) to throb with pain; (Ph.) eritana to hurt, pain; (Tr.) e@ritand to hurt or ache violently (Voc. 347). Konda er- (-t-) to kindle fire, light, burn; eris- to cause to burn, let someone light a fire. Kui

Ta. eruku (eruki-) to have loose motions (said of cattle); eru manure, excre- ment. Ma. eru manure; erukuka to discharge dung. Ta. erukku Calotropis gigantea. Ma. erikku id. Ka, ekke, ekka, erke, yakka gigantic swallow-wort or manure-leaf, C. gigantea R. Br. Tu. ekkamalé, ekkamalé, ekkamé swallow-wort, C. gigantea. / Cf. Skt. arka- C. gigantea. DED 697.
