Y.) hermi buffalo; (Tr.) armi, (L.) ermi female buffalo; (MuW.) arm, (MuE.) armi buffalo (Voce. 352). DED(S) 699.

Ta. eruvai blood, (?) copper. Ka. ere a dark-red or dark-brown colour, a dark or dusky colour; (Badaga) erande sp. fruit, red in colour, Te. récu, récu-kukka a sort of ounce or lynx said to climb trees and to destroy tigers; (B.) a hound or wild dog. Kol. resn aete wild dog (i.e. *res na-te; see 3650). Pa. iric netta id. Ga, (S.*) s&s nete hunting dog, hound. Go. (Ma.) erm ney, (D.) erom nay, (Mu.) atrm/arm nay wild dog (Voe. 353); (M.) rac nai, (Ko.) rasi ney id. (Voc. 3010). For ‘wild dog’, cf. 1931 Ta. ce- red, esp. the items for ‘red dog, wild dog’. DED 417, DED(N) 700.
Ta. eruvai a kind of kite whose head is white and whose body is brown; eagle. Ma. eruva eagle, kite.
Ta. eruvai European bamboo reed; a species of Cyperus; straight sedge tuber. Ma, eruva a kind of grass. 820 Ka, ere black soil. Te. ré-gada, ré-gadi clay (gadda clod; see 1148). From DED 700.

Ta. irai intestinal worm. Ma, ira bait, worms. Ka, ere a worm that lives in orts, a worm in general, bait. Kod. ere, ere pulu worm (earthworm, tapeworm). Tu. eru moth, worm. Te. era, erra earthworm. Go. (M) erad id.; (Ko.) ere puruk earthworms (Voe. 345). DED(S) 701. er- [81] elkhna 823 Kol. er- (e-, a-; edd-) to become, happen, (story) is finished. Vk. er- to become. Nk. (Ch,) er-/en- (ed-/edd-) to be. Pa. er- (eii-) to become, happen, be. Ga. (OIL) er- (ed-, en-) to be, become, happen, be born; (S.) é- (edd-, enn-) to become. Br, ar-é- ‘emphatic present’ stem of anning to be (see 333; BDCG, §3.24). Cf. 480 Ta. iru. DED(S) 702.

Ta. erukku (erukki-) to cut, hew, strike (as a bush), beat (as a drum), kill, destroy. Pa, erk- to cut down bushes, etc., in clearing land. Go. (Ko.) erk- to cut down (grass, bushes); erkem (pl, erke) billhook (Voc. 349). Kui erga (ergi-) to make a clear- ing, clear jungle or thick grass or scrub; 7. act of clearing a jungle. Kuwi (Isr.) erg- (-it-) to cut, slash. ? Malt. engde to clear away weeds, (Gramm., p.66) cut down a jungle. DED(S) 703. 825 Kur. erkhos husband’s younger brother; erkhd husband’s younger sister. Malt. erqo husband’s younger brother, DED 704. 826 Ko, itg kindling (dry plants with leaves still on), Ka. (Hav.) ege small branch, sprout; (Bark.) efkli thin branch. Tu, eggé a branch, “bough; eggely a small branch. Kor. (M,) egkili branch. ? Te. rivva, rivafa a twig. DED 705,

Ko. ed trunk from chest to navel; courage; ed maser chest of body (marr id.). To. eBy courage. Ka, ede, edde, erde chest, breast, courage; edegara bold man. Tu. ede heart, chest, bosom. Te. eda heart, mind, wish, breast, fear, alarm; eddda, edda the heart, mind, breast. Pa. edram, edrom chest. Konda eduram (pl. -ku) chest. DED(S) 683. 828 Kur. errma (irryas) to sweep; errta’and to make sweep. Malt, ere to sweep; erpo a broom. DED 706.

Ta. el lustre, splendour, light, sun, daytime; elli, ellai sun, daytime; iJaku (ilaki-), ilatku (ilahki-) to shine, glisten, glitter. Ma, ilakuka to shine, twinkle; ilaAkuka to shine; el lustre, splendour, light; ella light. Te. (K.) elamu to be shiny, splendid. Cf. 861 Ta. eri and 869 Ta. enru. / ? Cf. Pkt. (DNM) alla- day. DED(S) 707. 830 Kol. (Kin.) elava a wave. Go. (A.) helva id., flood (Voc. 3587). DED 708. 831 Ta, ela interj. used in addressing a person in a familiar or friendly manner; ella here, you!; a word used in addressing a woman friend; ellé here, you! used in address- ing a woman friend; an excl. of wonder or pity; ello excl. expressive of surprise or pity; ila excl. used in ancient times in addressing a woman in a familiar manner; eluva (always in vocative), eluval, eluvan man-friend; eluvai lady-companion; @14 excl. of familiar address to a companion, whether man or woman. To. es particle added to name by male in addressing a younger male in his own or a descending generation. Ka. ela, ele, el@, elo, eld excl. of a familiar and friendly character, used in calling or directly addressing any person; elage ho! used in calling to females. Tu, ela interj. of surprise, Kui la companion- ship; @la_gatanju male companion; fem. tla gatari. DED(S) 709.
Ka. elarcu to make vigorous, enliven. Te. elami joy, pleasure, delight; elayu to be pleased, rejoice; elayincu to please, gratify; elarucu to rejoice, delight; elarupu joy, pleasure. DEDS 97.

Ta. eli rat, bandicoot. Ma. eli rat. Ko. eyj id. To. isy id. Ka. eli, ili rat, mouse. Kod. eli rat. Tu. eli, ili id. Te. eluka, (B. also) elika rat; ciff-eluka mouse. Kol, elka rat. Nk, elka id. Nk. (Ch.) elli (ol. -g) id. Pa. el (pl. elkul) id. Ga. (OL) sirel mouse (i.e. small rat). Go. (A. Y. S.) elli rat; (Tr. Ph.) alli (pl. alk) id., (Tr. also) mouse; (W.) alli. mouse (Voce. 367); (Koya Su.) ellu rat. Konda elka id. Br. hal id. Cf. 2630 Pa. cir el and 2661 Ta, cun{an, cunt-eli. DED(S) 710.
Ta. eli, lu liquor. Ma. eli id.

Te. elfigu voice; elfgincu, elfigincu, elfigiccu to make a noise, cry, roar, sound, resound, Pa. ilung voice. Ga. (S.5) léng a tune. Go. (Tr. M. W. Ph.) lng voice, noise; (Ma.) léngi, (Ch. Mu. S.) l&y, (Ko.) léng(u) voice (Voe, 3127). Konda lin voice, tone. ? Cf. Kor. (O.) elkiri to weep. DED(S) 711.

Ta. elumiccai, elumiccaft kay sour lime, Citrus medica acida. Ma. elumicca lemon tree; itumpicci, irimpicci, irumpicci lime. Ko, elmic id. To. iigmié, tismiiZ, iismixky id. Ka, ilimifici lemon. DED(S) 712. 837 Pa, elub (pl elbul) white ant. Ga, (OL.) elub (pl, elbul), (S.) elltth (i elbul), (S.2) elb (elub) id. Go. (Ma. M.) elum, (Ko.) elu pete, (Tr. W. Mu.) allum white ant; (D.) ellum patteg white ants (Voc. 368) (for pete, patten, see 4169). Konda lim pirku white ant. Pe. lim dar id. Mand, lim id. Kui, dimbu (no pl.) id. Kuwi (S.) limpu, (Su.) limbu, (P. D.) lémpu id.; (F.) limbu ant’s earthwork. DED(S) 713.
Ta. elumpi hill olive. Ma. ilumpi id.

Ta. elumpu, enpu bone; erpu-ccatt - kam body (lit. cage made of bones). Ma. ellu, elumpu, elufiku, eluvu, elpu bone. Ko. ely id. To, elf id.; kit elf ankle (for kit- small, see 1594); pid-esp heel (pid- behind, 4205). Ka, elu, eluvu, elubu, iluvu, emike, emake, (PBh.) elvu bone; (Hav.) elugu id. Kod. elimbi id. Tu. elu id. Kor. (M.) elkali, (T.) elkade id. Te. emmu, emmuka, emuka id.; makkelu bones. /Cf. Skt. edifka-, BHS eluka- monument containing the ashes or bones of a dead person. DED(S) 714. 840 Kur, elkhna to pour liquid out (by tilting a vessel standing on the ground); elkhrna to be poured out. Malt. eqe to pour elkhing [82] eru out from a vessel. Cf. 866 Ta. erru (Pfeiffer). DEDS 98. 841 Kur, elkhrna to go down (sun, moon, stars), be well past the meridian. Malt. elqre to descend (as the sun). DED 715. 842 Kol. (SR.) elg- to embrace. Nk, elg- id. Pa. ol-, olv, oli- (< *olg-) id. DED 716.

Pa. elgg- to peel off (intr.); elkip- (elkit-) id. (t7.). Ga. (8) elug- to peel (as fruit); (P.) elinp- (elint-) to peel off (m%). Go. (Ko.) el- to be peeled off; elsp- to peel off (Voc. 365)..? Ka. (Tipt.} ebbu to peel. DED(S) 717.

Ta. ellam whole, all (personal as well as impersonal); ellavarum, ellarum all persons; ellemum, ellomum we all; ellirum you all. Ma. ellam, ellavum all; eflirum you all. Ko. el, elm all. To, elm (ce. elnm, dat. eltkm) all (only in songs: noun+o+est+elim); (rare in prose) el, elm id.; om elom all of us; nim elom all of you. Ka. ella all, everything, the whole; ellarum all persons. Kod. ella+ all. Tu. -la (2? -l@) = additive particle *-um (in all usages; MBE 1974a, pp. 107-8). Te. ella all, whole; ellavadu every man, everyone; ellaru all persons, all men. Kol. ittar el both. Kuwi (F.) ele’e whole. DED(S, N) 718.
Tu. elle tomorrow; ellafiji day after tomorrow. Te, elli tomorrow; ellundi day after tomorrow. DED 719.

Ta. ellai limit, border, boundary, measure, goal, extremity. Ma, ella limit; eluka limit, border, boundary. To. ely bound- ary. Ka. elle limit, boundary. Te. ella id. DED 720. 847 Kur. ebsna to lose, forfeit, wander from and not be able to find, cease to per- ceive (as from distance or darkness); ebsrna to be lost, estranged, strayed, disappear from sight. Malt, ewje to be lost, go astray; ewtre to lose. DED 721. 848 Ta, exil beauty, gracefulness; eriliya beautiful. Ma, eril beauty, brightness, splen- dour. ? To, ely beautiful (meaning uncertain; followed by buffalo name in vocative, in songs). Ka, edda beauty, charm, goodness. Tu. edde goodness; good, superior, fair; ady, well. Pa, edo, (S.) eda good; (S.) edka very. Malt. éru good, beautiful, in health. DED(S) 722,
Ta. exili cloud. Ma. erili id.

Ta. egini curtain. Ma. erini id. 851 (a) Ta. eru (-v-, -nt-) to rise (as from seat or bed), ascend (as heavenly body), rise by one’s own power (as a bird), appear, originate, be excited, aroused, increase, swell, grow (as a tree, breasts), return to life, awake, spread (as fame, rumour), begin, com- mence; egal enthusiasm, elation, originating; erucci rising, ascent, elevation, starting (as of an idol in procession), origin, birth, ap- pearance, beginning; eruppu (eruppi-) to cause or help to rise, erect (a building), awake, rouse, restore to life, excite, stimu- late, instigate, agitate, inflame (as the pas- sions), raise (as the voice in speaking or singing); nm. rousing, waking; eruppam rising, getting up, growth, height, elevation; erpumpu (egumpi-) = eru; empu (empi-) to rise, spring up; erumai height; eruvu (eruvi-) to cause to rise; evvu (evvi-) to rise; efu (-pp-, -tt-) to take up, raise, bear, carry, hold up, weigh in a balance, undertake, utter or sing in a loud voice, speak highly of, choose, build (as a wall), remove; be inflated (as a monkey’s cheeks), be high (as the forchead), be promi- nent (as nose or ear); etukkal lifting; etuttal weight, measurement by balance; efuttan pack-ox; etuppu elevation, superiority, under- taking, arrogance, superciliousness; etuppu (efuppi-) to awake, produce (as harmonious sounds from an instrument), dispel, drive away; efai weighing, weight, rousing from sleep, lengthening, increasing; itai weight; etci rising sign (astrology);
Ta. exu a kind of weapon. Ma. eru, eruku a club. DED 724. |

Ta. erutu (eruti-}) to write, paint, draw; become indented by pressure; eruttu letter, writing, painting, drawing, mem- branous intervals on the skull, fancied as written lines of destiny. Ma. erutuka to paint, write, learn; egutikka to cause to paint or write, teach, obtain a document; eruttu picture, letter, learning; eruttan writer; painted. Ko. eld- (eldy-) to write; elt writing: elp unjoined suture in top of child’s skull. Kod. élid- (@lidi-) to write; éliti writing, education. DED 725. 854 Ta, el, en Sesamum indicum; en ney gingily oil. Ma. el, elu sesamum; enna, ell enna gingelly oil. Ko. en oil. Ka, el, eflu S. indicum; enne oil. Kod. élli gingily seed; énne, éll énne gingily oil. Tu. enme gingily oil seed; enne oil. DED 726. 854A Ma. elanta, elunta plank. Ka. ele rafter, DEDS.99,

Ta. eli easy; elimai case, facility; eliyan man easy of access; elivu unaffected ease, approachableness; enmai easiness as of acquisition or access. Ma. elutu easy; elu- ppami facility; easy. Ko. el(i) sitting at ease, easy; elt! easy, easily. To. elal ease, easy. DED 727.

Ta. elimai, elumai despicablesness, lowness of rank, circumstances, or character, poverty, weakness, depression of spirit; eli Cpp-, -tt-) to cry down, disparage; elifiar the poor; elitaravu humility, poverty; eliyavan, eliyan poor man, man of low caste; ellu (elli-) to ignore, disregard, ridicule; elku (elki-) to despise, slight; enmai lowness of tank or condition; ilamai inferiority, baseness; ilappam id., meanness, flimsiness. Ma. eli to be light, slight; elima, eluppam lowliness; elutu = mean, slight; tappam _ slightness, worthlessness; ilama inferiority, lowliness. Ka. (PBh.) ili to become sapless or weak; ilisu to make less or low. Tu. elli, ellya small, inferior, mean. Te. ellidamu scorn, contempt, disregard; contemptible. Cf. 776 Ka. Te. egeu and 911 Ta. trai. DED(S, N) 728.

Ta. enku, elu, uliyam a bear; ilaj Indian black bear. Ma, ufiyam a bear. Te. elOgu, eluvu id. Pa. ili (pl. itil) id. Ga. (OIL) ilij (pl. ilji), (S.) Wij GL ijil; | = dz) id. Go. (A. Y.M.Ko.) erj, Ma.) er, (S.) erju, (D.) exjal, (W.) axjal, (Tr.) agjal (pe ayjahk/arjalor), (Ph.) arjal (pi. -or) id.; (Ph.) arje female bear (Voc. 361). Konda (S. and E.) olzu (pl. olsku), (N. and W.) orzu (pl. oysku) bear. Kui odi, oli id. Kuwi (S.) oudi, (Su.) o’ri id. Malt. eju id. DED(S) 729.

Ta. elku (elki-) to fear, be bashful, hesitate. Ka, elar fear. Kur, elena (ilcyas) to fear, have a reverential awe of, have mis- givings; el€gta’and to frighten, threaten. Malt. elce to fear; elttre to frighten. DED 859 Ta, eri (-v-, -nt-) to throw, discharge, hack, chop, smash, destroy, beat, pounce, kick; n. throw, fling, kick; eriva missiles; exru (erri-) to strike, kick, butt, cut, cleave, pierce, kill; #2. kicking, hitting, pushing, attacking; Gru throw, beating (as of a drum), stroke (as of a sword), pouncing upon, destroying, scar; iru (-pp-, -tt-) to throw as a spear, fling. Ma. eri a throw; eriyuka to throw, fling, hit; erikka to remove the bark from timber, rough hew for sawing; erru a blow (chiefly beating clothes); erruka to throw (as with sling), fillip, propel, spirt, beat hard; @ru a throw. Ko. eyr- (erc-) to cut, kill, slaughter (animal); (head) aches; eyrl a cut; et- (ety-) to fight; eerp a cut, blow; e+r(p) parr pain of wound. Jo. ery (ers-) to throw, cut, sting, charge with horns, sacrifice; iz- (ix8-) to hit with weapon; i+rk depression made by cutting; ir depression in anything; ? -et- (-efy-) mediative-causative suffix (e.g. tinet- to cause to eat); ox et to make broad (cf. 8 Ta. akal). Ka. iri (irid-) to throw, beat, strike, pierce, kill, butt: throb (as a sore); ira, iri, iru tossing, butting; iraku, irike, irata, irita, iripu striking, piercing, goring, etc.; @ru that with which one pierces or which one throws; a cut, wound; étu a blow, throw, shot; e

Te. (K.) remmu to snatch, pull out, pluck out (as hair). Go. (Tr.) aritand to drag along the ground; arri along with, taking; (W.) arritana, (Ch.) arri- to drag; (W.) arri tatdna to convey away; (Ch.) arri wara bring- ing come!; (Y.) ari sota she went off taking; (Ma.) ar vay- to come bringing (Voce, 73); (ASu.) eri- to pull, drag. Konda (BB) rel- (ren-, reR-) to pull. Pe, jel- (-t-) id. Kui jelba (jes-) to pull, draw; n. act of pulling, drawing; pl. action jelka (jelki-). Kuwi (F.) rejali (rec-) to drag, pull; (S.) rennai id.; rejja-koddinai to drag; (Su.) re’- (rece-) to pull. DED(S, N) 4243.
Ta. eri (-pp-, -tt-) to shine, glitter; erippu lustre, brightness, hot sun. Ma. erikka to shine (as sun); erippu sunshine. Cf. 829 Ta. el and 869 Ta. enru. DED 732.

Ta. eri (-pp-, -tt-) to take pity, have compassion; erru (erri-) to feel compassion. To. kon el- (et-) to long for (person, -k); muen el- (et-) to like (cf. 5031); mu-net affection, love (only recorded as woman’s- name; MBE 1974b, p. 90). Ka. eraka, eruka love, good will. DED(S) 733.
Ka. eru to be full, complete. Tu. erkuni to be full of milk (as breast or udder). Ta, erur strength, might [so DCV]. DEDS 100.
Ta. erumpu, erumpi, irumpi, uravi ant. Ma. erumpu, ifumpu, urumpu. Ko, irb. To. irb. Ka. irumpu, irumpe, irupe, irive, irave, iruvu, iruve, irvu, irve, (Hav.) erugu. Kod. urupi. DED 734.

Ta. erug a hill tree with red flowers; erugam a hill tree. Te, erupa, exta, errana, errani redness, red, scarlet, crimson; (K.) Exrana n. pr. 14th cent. author (erra + anna elder brother); Erama n. pr. man (8th cent.; inscr., p.355); modem names Errayya, Erramma (MBE 1978, p. 355). Kol. (SR.) errodi, (Kin.) erori red. Go. (S. Ko.) erra red (Voc. 355; < Te.). Konda era, erani red. DED(S) 700.

Ta. erru (erri-) to throw out (as water from a vessel); irai (-v-, -nt-) to scatter (intr.), disperse; (-pp-, -tt-) to splash (¢.), spatter, scatter, strew, draw and pour out water, irrigate, bale out, squander; iraivai receptacle for drawing water for irrigation; irat¢u (irafti-) to sprinkle, splash. Ma. irekka to bale out; irayuka id., scatter, disperse; irava basket for drawing water; ericcil rainwater blown in by the wind. To. er- (er®-) to scoop up {water with vessel). Ka. ere to pour any liquids, cast [84] enu (as metal); m. pouring; eracu, ercu to scoop, sprinkle, scatter, strew, sow; eraka, eraka _ any metal infusion; molten state, fusion. Tu. eraka molten, cast (as metal); eraguni to melt, Kur. ecchna to dash a liquid out or over (by scooping, splashing, besprinkting). Cf. 840 Kur. elkhna (Pfeiffer). DED(S)735.

Ta. en (enp-,enr-) to say, Utter, express; enkai saying; enpi (-pp-, -tt-) to make one establish or prove (as a statement). Ma. ennuka to sound, say, think (defective), to sound thus, appear thus, be such (aux. vb.). Ko. in- (id-), -n- (-d-) to say (so-and-so), be said to be (so-and-so). To, in- (id-) to say (so-and-so). Ka. en (end-), ennu, an (and-), annu to say, speak, call, name; enisu, ennisu, | enasu, anasu, anisu, annisu to cause to say, cause oneself to say, cause to be called, cause oneself to be called, be called, be spoken of; enuha, ennike, ennuvike, annuvike saying, calling, naming; embu, imbu a saying, a word. Kod. *enn- (emb-, end-) to say (so-and-so). Tu. anpini, inpini to say, speak; endruni to say fully. Te. anu to say, utter, speak; adj. named, called; anipincu to seem, look, appear, be considered; pretend, cause to believe, cause to be said. anukonu to think, believe, suppose, say to oneself; (7th cent.; inscr., p. 354) envanru he who is called. Kol. en- (ent-), (SR.) in- to say (so-and-so), be said to be (so-and-so). Nk. en- (ent-) to say. Nk. (Ch.) en- (end-) id. Pa. en- (end-, ett-) to say (so-and-so). Ga. (S.) in- id. Go. (Tr.) indiana (inj-) to say, call; (A. Y. Mu.) ind- (itt-), (Ma.) in- (-t-) to say (Voe. 173). Konda in- (iR-) id. Pe. in- (ic-) id. Mand. in- (ic-) id. Kui, inba (is-) to say, be articulate; n. saying, speaking, articulation; ispa (ist-) to cause to say, make articulate. Kuwi (F.) injali, (S) innai, (Su.) in- (icc-) to say; (Isr.) in

Ta. enru sun; enr Ug sunshine, sun, summer. ? To. ery sun, in song unit; er ot nelp ground cool in the sun [during the rainy season] (interpretation doubtful, but a parallel unit has né-r ‘sun’, for which see 2910). Te. enda sunshine, sun, heat of the sun; endu to be heated by exposure to the sun, dry up, evaporate; endugulu grain ex- posed to the sun for being dried. Nk. (Ch.) edde sunshine. Pa, nendi, (NE.) nendi heat of the sun. Go. (A.D. MuE. Ma. 8.) eddi, (G. M. Ko.) ed id.; (SR.) yaddi hot, sunlight; (Tr. Ph.) addi, (W.) adi, (Ch. MuW.) addi heat of the sun (Voc. 342). Cf. 829 Ta. el and 861 Ta. eri. DED(S) 738. .. = ; [85]

Ta. € increase, abundance, pile, look- ing upward, pride, arrogance; éntu (énti-) to rise high, be elevated, be eminent, excel- lent; Ental height, dignity; éttu (étti-) to praise, extol; mn. praising, extolling. Ma. éntuka to rise; ettakkal praisers, singers (2? < Ta.). Ka, (PBh.) esakam glory, magna- nimity. Te. €cu to increase, grow; €écina exceeding, great, extreme; &@pu bloom, increase, growth, prosperity; spirit, ardour; édu to bring up, nourish, cherish, protect; edugu to grow, increase in stature; es&gu to increase (intr.); esakamu increase, rise; esa excess, superiority; very, excessively; esaldru to increase; (K.) saru to increase, flourish, exceed, Kuwi (isr.) epa a- to increase. ? Malt. ite to spring up (as seed); itgre, itre to be hatched. Cf. 3730 Ta. niva. DED(S) 739. 871 Ta, ku (&ki-) to go, pass, walk. Te. égu, (K.) gu to go, proceed; (Inscr.) Egincu to carry the deity in procession through the village streets. Kur, @kna (ikyas) to walk, direct or conduct affairs. Malt, éke to go, move. DED 740. 872 Ma, &kuka to give, bestow. Br. ét- allomorph’ in pres. indefinite, imper., and optionally probable future, of tining to give (cf. 3098) (BDCG, p. 25, fn.6). DEDS 101. 873 Kur. €&x-Ema to give complacent looks at one’s own dress. Malt. éqto fop, coxcomb. DEDS 102. 874 Kui éga (@gi-) to be open, ajar, un- closed, gaping, open out (imtr.); n. state of being open; @gali ajar; tpka (< tk-p-; &kt-) to open, open up, prize open. Kuwi (F.) ekh-, in: ida’a ekhmi uncover th

Ta. @hku (éhki-}) to pine, languish, long for, yearn after; &kkam despondency, craving; ékkaru (tkkari-) to languish, long for; @caru (@caruv-, écarr-) to long for, desire; be troubled, feel sorry; Ecaravu desire, longing; regret, penitence. Ma. ékkam grief, anxiety. Ko, e-ng- (e-ngy-) to grieve; e-katm extreme sorrow. Tu. (B-K.) €vuro greediness; yévura excessive desire. Te. Ekaru, &karu to long; Skata strong desire, longing; @cu to harass, Sdikola E torment, annoy, trouble; &pu torment. Kol. (Pat., p. 107) @va jealous. Kur. €xnd to be dissatisfied, want more. Malt. ége to be dissatisfied. DED(S) 745, DED(S; N) 747, and from DED 741. 879 Ta, @hku (@hki-) to sound, scream as a peacock, weep, wail; éfkal shout, screaming as of a peacock, weeping; ? @e Samaveda (or with 470 Ta. iyampu). Ko. e+kalc- (e-kalc-) to shout to someone from a distance. To, 6«x- (G*xy-) to scream (peacock or diviner); 6exm (Obl. G+xt-) scream of a peacock or a diviner. Kod. e+ng- (e+ ngi-) to lengthen note (in singing, crowing). Te. (Merolu) égu to sound; éncu to make sound. Go. (Tr.) yécana, (Ph.) yécand to play a flute or any wind instrument (Voce. 375). DED(S) 746, and from DEDS 93.

Ta. &cu (Eci-) to abuse, reproach, rail at, insult; @cal reproaching, abusing, slander; écu fault, blemish; @ccu abuse, insult, re- proach. Ma, éccu reviling, abusing, back- biting; sani backbiting, tale-bearing; ésanikka to breed discord. Te. ku to expose or publish the faults of, defame. DED(N) 748. 881 Kur @ju cooked rice, rice-water, gruel: a meal. Kuwi (S.) yéu, in: haru yéuta ifttinai to marinate (for haru salt, see 2674b). (Burrow-Bhattacharya, IJ 5.135.) Cf. 777 Ta. ecar.

Ta. étakam a kind of cloth. Ma. étakam id. 883 Ta, ttakOtam perverseness, contradic- tion, confusion. Ma, é@fakt{am opposition, perverseness. Te. édagddamu, édakddamu contradiction, opposition. DED 749. 884 Ta, &tu petal, flower, eyelid, strip of palmyra leaf for writing, book; é@takam flower; coconut palm, palmyra palm. Ma. &tu palm leaf, leaf of book, book, flower leaf; Efakam a flower; coconut palm, palmyra palm. DED (S) 750. 885 Kur. &der parrot (Tiga, Bleses; Grignard nas erroneously éder). Malt. edru id. DED 55.

Ta. @n, éni boundary, limit. Ma. nu edge, chiefly the three edges of the coconut. Ko, e-+ner edge. Ka, n, tnu edge, border, point. Te. (VPK, intro. p. 123) nu one edge ofa blade of hoe or spade. DED(S) 751. 887 Ta, &, Enam, Enai, étci firmness, stability; en strength; (Tinn.) nu to support. Ma, nam steadfastness; énu energy, firmness, stability. DED(S) 752.
Te. Edikodla the pole or shaft of a plough. Konda Enta (pl énten) ploughshaft. Kuwi (F.) entikarra cart-shaft. DEDS 104. étam {86] al

Ta. étam suffering, affliction, distress; fault, defect, blemish; calamity, ruin; &tu fault, defect. Ma. ®tam suffering, affliction, distress; fault. Ju. €tu annoyance (from goblins). Te. @tari a villain, miscreant.

Ta. étalan foe, enemy; étilan stranger, neutral; étilar other, strangers, foes, enemies, prostitutes; etila] strange unfamiliar woman, co-wife; étilalan stranger; étinmai strangeness, foreignness, enmity. Ma. étil neighbourhood; ttalar enemies. DEDS 105.

Ta. entu (Enti-) to stretch out the hands, receive or hold in the hands, hold up, support; éntal stretching out the hands (as a beggar), holding up, supporting. Ma, tntuka to take up, wield, stretch arms or legs; €ttam praising, extolling; éttuka to praise, extol. DED 756.
Ka. epi Shorea robusta. Te. (B.) Spe S.r., the sal tree (for Sank. épe Hardwickia binata, see 5150 Ta. ya). DED 758.
Ta. éppam belch. Ma, éEmpal id.; émpakkam, é@mpalam belching; (Tiyya) ém- pila a belch. DED(N) 759.

Ta. émam bewilderment, perplexity; émal confusion of mind; émar (-pp-, -tt-} to be confused, bewildered; émaru (@mari-) to be beguiled, inveigled; €marru (€marri-) to hoodwink, deceive; Emarram state of being defrauded, deceit, confusion of mind; émali simpleton, fool; émuru (émuruv-, émurr-) to be perplexed, bewildered. Ma, émal confusion of mind, bewilderment; emari a cheat; marram deceit, fraud; ema- ruka to hoodwink, deceive; tmali beggar, silly man. Ko. e+masr- (e+ma-ry-) to gasp (from sudden fright, shock, while dying); e+ma-en- (eemaend-) to be cheated; be dazed by being caught suddenly in wrong- doing; e»ma+t- (e«ma-ty-) to cheat; e-ma-+tl tricks. To. e»moert- (e*mo-ty-) to deceive. ? Ka, vémaru to be careless, fall a prey to deceit. Te. @maru, émapu to be forgetful, be deceived, err; Emarincu, Emarucu, émarcu to make one forget, deceive, take by surprise. Cf. 190 Ta. ayir. DED 760.

Ta. &y (-v-, -nt-) to be suited, be fit, be similar to, meet; (-pp-, -tt-) to be similar to, tell a seeming truth, deceive; éyppu deceit; €yvu comparison, resemblance, Ma. écuka, suka to unite, fit, meet, take effect; €ccu tie, connexion; ékkuka to join so as to fit, patch, deceive; Eppu a joint, patch; éppikka to get fitted together; éyuka to be fit, be suited; éykkuka to unite, join; éypu joint. Ko. e+p joint of two pieces of iron; e-guet- (esguec-) to gather (intr.); e+guet- (esguec-) to gather (fuel, honey, grain, money). To. esy- (e*c-) to join (pieces or two ends) together; O+kusy (6ekusry-) (things) are gathered, (men) work together; Ovkuef- (Gekuety-) to gather (things). For Ko. and To., cf. 1882. DED 744, 761.
Ta. éral mussel or other bivalve, snail; eruntu bivalve shellfish (as mussels, oysters); erunti a kind of small shell. Ma. éral shell fish, snail; eruntu, erintu shellfish in rivers, DED 762.

Ta. eri large tank, reservoir for irri- gation, lake. Ma. &ri stakes to support banking work, bank. Ka. é@ri a raised bank, the bank of a tank, a tank. Kod. eri parapet of well, bund (in paddy-fields, of tank). Tu. tri a bed for planting vegetables, etc. DED 763.
Ta. Gri-valai a freshwater fish, Wallago attu, Ma, @ri a kind of fish. Tu. @ri id. DED 164.

Ko. er uk- (uky-) to play ‘peeping tom’. Kui @ra (€ri-) to spy, scout; m. spying, scouting; pl action @rka (@rki-). ? Kuwi (S.) hénai to scout; héri kiyali to see; (Su. P.) hénd- (hét-) id. Kur. @rn@ (iryas) to see, look, look at, look after, look for, wait for, exam- ine, try; rta’ana@ to let see, show; érdnakhrna to look at one another. Malt. ére to see, behold, observe; érye to peep, spy. Cf. 892 Kur. @thrna. / Cf. Skt. heraka- spy, Pkt. her- to look at or for, and many NIA verbs; Turner, CDIAL, no. 14165. DED(S) 765. 904 Ta, Gri hump on bull. Ma. éri id. 905 Ta, el (€rp-, err-; Elv- Enr-) to happen, occur, be suitable, convenient, just; to receive, a . [87] au welcome, admit, consent, beg, undertake, engage in; @1 suitability, appropriateness, fitness; @rpu id., acceptance, reception; érpon beggar; Grratu that which is fit, proper; (Asher-Radhakrishnan) @ram — acceptance; érpatu to come into existence, be produced, be engaged in, agree, consent; érpafuttu to create, establish, persuade, prepare, arrange, appoint; rpatu arrangement, rule, appoint- ment to an office. Ma, @l possibility, reach; preparation, responsibility; Gluka to suit, fit; élkka to receive, take in charge, admit, confess; élpetuka to engage in, be responsible for, vb.n. @lpatu; elpetukka, élpetuttuka, apikka to entrust with; érra admitted, agreed, undertaken. Ko. e+l- (e+t-) to catch in some- thing held up; e-rpa+y- (obl. e-rpa-f-) pre- paration, selection of a man to do something; eerpart- (e-rparty-) to

Ta. él (erp-, trr-; élv-, Enr-) to oppose in battle, encounter (as a foe); érral dispu- tation, polemics; érrar foes, enemies. Ma. akka (€rr-) to hit, meet in battle; apikka to wound, make (a dog) to attack or seize. Ka. tr to meet in battle, oppose; n. state of meeting and opposing, a wound. Tu. Grsavuni to beat, strike. DED 767. 907 Ta, @lam cardamom plant, Eletteria cardamomum; cardamom; @lav-arici carda- mom seed. Ma. @lam cardamoms; @latt-ari cardamom seed. Ka. @l-akki, yalakki, yalaki large cardamoms. Kod. e+lakki cardamom seeds; e+la male, estati male cardamom plantation. Tu. @-akki cardamoms. Te. éla, @laki cardamom plant. @lakulu cardamom seeds. /Cf. Skt. ela-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 2522. DED 768.

Ta. vam fault, blemish. Ka. @va disgust, dislike, hatred; ugliness; évaysu to be disgusted or vexed; héva disgust, repug- nance; hévarisu to feel. aversion. Te, &va, évamu dislike, disgust, hate; disgusting, hateful; @vagincu to hate, dislike, be dis- gusted. DED(S) 770. 909 Ta, évu (@vi-) to command, direct, incite, inspire (as god), speak; éval instigation, command; @valan, évarkdran a servant. Ma, ékuka to say, command; é@kal a command; ‘€val command, work; évalan a servant, attendant. Ko, e+lv matter, affair, errand; e-lvgaerm servant. To, 6-fil, e-fil a com- mand, order (< Ta.); e+fil ol, e-f o-f ser- vant. DED(S) 771.

Ta. @ru seven; egmai id.; ezu-patu seventy; eru-nOru 700; erumai seven success- ive births; eruvar seven persons; ewv-éru seven each, Ma, &ru seven; eru-patu seventy; eru- nntru 700; eguvar seven persons. Ko, e+y (Sholur dialect e+g) seven; el vat seventy; e calg seven calg measures; e pasny seven pa*ny measures. To. Ow seven; paew seven- teen; Ge kwa+x seven kwa+x measures. Ka, €ru seven; r-pattu, eppattu seventy; ér-nru, éru-niru, elniru 700; érbar, @rvaru seven persons. Kod. &+li seven; é+lane seventh; élu-vadi seventy; é+I-nueri 700; évvé seven persons (in songs). Zu. @ly seven; Slane seventh; élvery seven persons; elpa, #@ipa, erpa_ seventy. Te. @du, (7th cent.; inscr., p. 356) @mu seven; @duguru seven persons; én-noru 700; debbadi, debbhai, dabbhai seventy; debbandru seventy persons. Kol. (SR.) ed, (Kin.) & seven; (SR.) ergur, edgur seven persons. Go. (Y. Ch. Ma.) @run, (Tr. W.} yérung, (SR.) yedung, (S.) eri seven (usually non-masce.),; (Y.) €rvir, (Mand.) @rur, (S.) Grur, (Ma.) €rvur seven (masc.) (Voc. 396); (Tr.) yérk seven each. Konda Gru seven. Kui (Letchmajee) odgi seven; odi pattu seven times twelve dozen (= 1008); (Friend-Pereira; Gtmsar dialect) od seven; odgi seven things; (K.) ori seven; orin gdri 140 (cf. DBIA 122). DED (S) 772.
Ta. trai indigent person, poor wretch, helpless fellow; Graimai poverty. Ma. &ra a poor or innocent person, one who is helpless; (Tiyya) ya poor person. To. e+ly poor. Cf.
Ka. @e name of a metre. Te, éla a kind of song, (B.) a catch used by rowers of boats. Malt. éle to sing. DED(S) 773.

Ka. é@r to glean, gather, pick off, select, choose. Te. Gru to pick up, select, cull, glean, gather; Srincu to cause to be gleaned; @rcu to choose, select, pick out; érparincu, @rparacu to select, choose; Erpu selection. DED 774. 915 Ma, ekaruka to winnow, fan and clean (as paddy). Te. (K.) @ru to sift. Kol. (SR.) ers- id. Go. (Tr. Ph.) erand to be separated, be separate from; caus. (Tr.) érstana, (Mu.) @r- (cross-roads) to divide, spread out in different directions (Voc. 393). Konda erlis- (-t-) to separate (as one’s legs), keep away from; é@rp- (t-) to keep apart, keep separate; (BB) ér- to be separated, rés- (-t-) to winnow; ? néspa- (-t-) to clean .or winnow (rice, etc.). Pe. jéc- to winnow. Kui a (@ji-) to be separate from, detached, move aside, make way for; . act of moving aside; @spa (@st-) to separate; m. act of separating; (K.) @sp- to winnow. Kuwi (T.) érlali to be separated, (Isr.) erl- (-it-) to go by a different path; (F.) érsali to separate; (S.) @rsinai to sort; (Su.) érs- to winnow; (T.) tsp- to winnow with a sidewise motion; (P.) thp- to winnow; (Isr.) Grs- Cit-) to separate, keep separate. Cf. 2019 Ta. céffai, Ma. céruka. DED(S, N) 775.

Ta. Gru (@ri-) to rise, ascend (as the heavenly bodies), mount, climb, abound in number, weight, or measure, increase in price, quality, spread, be diffused (as poison), stow, be laden (as cargo); Era so as to exceed, more than, above; @rru (@rri-) to lift up, raise, increase (as price), load (as a cart or ship), eulogize, found, establish; érram mounting, ascending, raising up, rising (as the tide), eulogy, increase, excess, preemi- nence, well-sweep, picottah; @1 (@rp-, trr-; élv-, Enr-) to be excessive, abound; awake from sleep; bear, lift up, carry. Ma. truka to rise, increase, be much, arise, ascend; éra much, more, beyond; @rruka to raise, embark, multiply; @rram rising, ascent, in- crease, climbing, pulling up, what is too much, embarkation; @rru mounting palm trees, flow of tide; étkka (@rr-) to multiply; @ttam machine to draw water. Ko, eer- (e-ry-) (advice) is listened to; e+r, erresr, esi up; e+r big; e+t- (e+c) to place (burden) on (person or animal); e+ fl act of raising (e.g. a fine); e+t an edge of bank of stream or tank (or with 5159 Ta. yaru; cf. 122 Ta. anai). To. 6-r- (Sery-) to rise high, (poison, disease) is diffused, load (bullock, cart); G-re- (Geré-) to make high; 6+ (G»t-) to be fat. (boil) comes to a head; et- (ety-) to raise, in: poew ful et- to thatch the ti* dairy (ic. to raise the thatching grass to/of the dairy), a-s fon et- to burn the model house at a woman’s funeral (lit. to raise the house to the sky). Ka. @ru to rise, increase, ascend, moun

Ta. ru bull, male of certain animals (pig, deer, buffalo, sambar, tiger, lion); érrai, (lex.) ttai male of any animal remark- able for physical strength. Ma. @ru bullock; éran bull. Ko. e+r male buffalo; tir e-r buffalo calf at Toda calf sacrifice. To. e+r male buffalo, bull. ?Br. ar€ (pl. arisk) male individual, person, husband (cf. BDCG, p. 10). ?Cf. 2820 Konda séri. DED(S) 777.
Ta. nam palm-leaf vessel for drinking toddy; tool. Ma, nam id.
Ta. Enai other, the rest. Br. él6 the other, the other one, the second. (Kamales- waran, with opposition to derivation of Ta. énai from @n why?). DEN 13. Al

Ta. aiyam alms, beggar’s bowl. Ma. ayyam aims. Cf. 196(a) Ta. ayyan. DED 779. 921 Ta, aiyavi mustard; ? aintai id. Ma. aiyavi, ayyavi id. ? Te. ava id.; avalu mustard aivanam {89} okkal seeds. /For possible relationship with Skt. sarsapa-, Pkt. sasava- (> Ka. sasavi, sdsave, Te. sdsuvulu), see Burrow, The Sanskrit Language, p. 379, and Mayrhofer, s.v. 922 Ta, aivanam mountain paddy. Ma. aivanam wild paddy, Oryza mutica. O 923 Kui oanju, oaru, ori, Ovi that male, those males, that female or thing, those females or things (at the greatest distance); adj, 6; oko adj., adv, distant, far away; okoki to a distance away; okoti from a distance; omba there, yonder; one ady. that way, that side; Ose that size. Br. 6, Sd this, that (inter- mediate); pl ofk; shun thus; adj. such; oka(n) from here or thereabouts, by this or that way; hereafter, thereafter; 6xa so much, so many; Ong in this, that direction; oré(k) here, there; Oska(n) up to this, that time. (But the prima facie similarity is deceptive, since Br. 6 is a borrowing from Panj.-Lahnda 6 or Indian Persian 6 ‘that’; MBE 1980b.) From DED 475.

Ta. o (-pp-, -tt-) to be suited to, appropriate, be acceptable, be in happy con- cord, resemble, equal, appear as if it were; ottatu that which is commensurate with or fit, that which has the approval of the world; oppu (oppi-) to agree, accede to, assent; 7. that which is fit or proper, beauty, consent, approval, uniformity, likeness, analogy; oppantam agreement, contract, unanimity; oppam comparison, resemblance, ornamen- tation; signature; kaiy-oppam signature; oppanai comparison, likeness, adornment; oppi (-pp-, -tt-) to liken, compare, embellish; cause to agree, deliver; oppitam acceptability, propriety, fairness; oppumai likeness; oppuvi Gpp-, -tt-) to surrender, consign, illustrate; owu (ovvi-) to be like, be congruous, con- sistent; Opati particle of comparison. Ma. okkuka to be like, please, be acceptable, agree, compare; otta equal, consistent, agree- able, right; oppanam comparison; adornment; oppam equality, harmony; oppantam agree- ment, contract; opparam unity, agreement; oppati comparison, simile; oppayam some- thing agreeable; opparan a mediator; oppari parable, flattery; opparittam simile, com- parison; oppu conformity; signature; oppikka to equalize, compare, adjust, please, persuade; kayy-oppu signature; ovvuka to be in agree- ment. Ko, op- (opy-) to be acceptable; accept, agree with; op state of being in agreement; opc- (ope-) to hand over, entrust. To. up- (upy-) to be pleased, agree; upc- (upé-), ufe- (ufé-) to hand over; koy-upm signature. Ka. oppu, orp

Ta. okkal relations, kinsfolk; okkali (-pp-, -tt-) to hold friendly communion with one’s relations, maintain one’s relations, give them medical aid, etc.; okkiliyan member of a caste of cultivators from the Kannada provinces who have settled in the districts of Madura and Coimbatore. Ma. okkiliyan a cultivating class. Ko. okl family. To. wikil family within clan. Ka. okkal residing, residence, home, tenancy, tenant, husband- man, farmer, subject; okkala, okkaliga hus- bandman, farmer, Stidra; okkalagitti, okkali- giti, okkaliti a farmer woman; okkalatana husbandry, farming. Kod. okka patrilineal joint family. Tu. okkelu a tenant; okkelme okkuka [90] utan one of a cultivating class called the Bants, a Bant; fem. okkeldi, okkendi. / Cf. Pkt. okkia- a dwelling, residence. DED 782.
Ma. okkuka to indent. Ka. (Bark.) okki to scratch (as fowls); (Hav.) okku to dig. Tu. okkuni to scratch. Kor. (T.) ogi to cut. Go. (M.) uhcana to scratch (Voce, 304).

Ma. okkuka cattle to trample upon sheaves of corn; vb, noun okkal. Ko. ok- (oky-) to drive (cattle) round in threshing; okl act of threshing one lot of grain com- pletely. To. wik- (wiky-) (bullocks) go round in threshing. Ka. okku to tread out corn, remove grain from the ears by the treading of oxen, thresh with sticks; n. theshing; okkal, okkike, okkanike threshing; okkisu to cause corn to be trodden out. Kod. okk- (okki-) to thresh (paddy crop) by driving cattle round on heaps of grain; okki heap of paddy on threshing floor on which cattle are driven round. Cf. Ta, ukai (-v-, -nt-) to move (as a boat), go (as a vehicle), walk (as an animal); (-pp-, -tt-) to drive, ride, row (as a boat), discharge (as an arrow), Stir up (as dust); To. moBy wax- (waxt-) to stir up the ashes after burning of relics at second funeral, in order to see if anvthing is left (mo8y may mean ‘ashes’; cf. moBy x6-s the ké-s tree at No+s mund where these ashes are put). / ? Cf. Pkt. ukkhan- to thresh. DED (S) 784. 928 Kui okol, okoli, (7) ekoli backyard, rear of the house. Kuwi (F.) okkopara the space at the back of a house; (Isr.) okopara backyard of house. DEDS 107.-

Tu. kodé yesterday. Kol. okkod id. Nk. okkor id. Vk. (Ch.) ok(kjon id. Cf. 989 Pa. ori. DED(S) 785. 930 Kur. okkna (ukkyas) to sit down, seat oneself, alight, perch, hold a session, come to a standstill, acquire steadiness, reside; okkrna to sit down in order to breathe or recover oneself; okta’ana to invite to sit down, give firmness to, settle. Malt. oke to sit, be situated; okete to sit down, settle; oktre to place, put down, make to sit. DED 786. 931 Kol. (SSTW, p.83) panta okeng to reap (i.e. cart crops from field). Pa, okp- (okt-) to remove in loads, cart. Ga. (Oll.) oskap- (oskat-) to remove. Go. (L.) akana to take, bring, carry (Voe. 1). Konda ok- (¢t-) to carry on the head or shoulder in a container; caus. okis-. Pe. ok- (-t-) to carry harvest from field. Kui opka (< ok-p-; okt-) to remove in loads, cart; m. carting, cartage. Kuwi (S.) okh’nai to remove. Cf. 984 Ta. oy. DED (S) 787.
Ka. ogatu, ogate, Ogate riddle. Tu. ogatu riddle, mystic language. DED 788.
Ka. oge to wash. Kor. (M.) oge id.

Te. (K.) oggu to lay a trap, net, lay a wager. Go. (Ma.) og- to set (fishtrap); (Ph.) vaggand, vakkana to set snare (Voc. 399). Kui oga (ogi-) to trap, snare; n. act of trapping. Kuwi (F.) tra ogali to noose (for tru, see 639); (isr.) og- Cit-) to set a trap. DED(S) 790. 935 Kol. ong- (onkt-) to carry (child) on ‘hip; odyg- (odonkt-) to have (child) with oneself while sleeping. Nk. ongip- to carry (child) on hip. Vk. (Ch.) oy- to take child in lap. Pa. ong- id. Ga. (OlL.) ong: id. Go. (Ma.) ogng-. (Mu.) orun- id. (Voc. 425); (Mu.) urn-, (Ch.) urgg- to embrace, take in arms; (Tr.) unrgana to sleep with a child in arms (Voce. 267c). DED(S) 791. 936 Kur. ongkhna/ungkhna (ungkhyas) to get drunk, be intoxicated; ongkhta’ana to make drunk, produce loss of sensibility (as cold or chloroform), turn the head of. Malt. onge to be under the effect of drink or poison, be intoxicated; onq-male drunkard; onqtre to take effect (as poison), DED 792.

Ka. osage speech, report, news; usit, usur(u) to utter, speak, say, tell. Tu. osage, osaya, osaye news; the ceremony of sending a messenger to a girl’s husband or parents with the news of her attaining puberty. Kuwi, (Isr.) oc- Git) to pronounce, guess; oconi riddle. Cf. 631 Te. ubusu, tsu. DEDS 108. 938 Kui osan dba, osan tanji stepfather; osan aja, osan fadi stepmother. Kuwi (S.) Gca tanji stepfather; Oca talli stepmother; dsr.) Gea to one side; 6ca talli stepmother. DEDS 109.
Ta. oci (-v-, -nt-) to be coy, bashful; occi, ofici (pp-, -tt-) to feel abashed, be shy; occam bashfulness, shyness. Ma. occam id.

Ta. oci (-v-, -nt-) to be broken as a stick, bend under a weight; (-pp-, -tt-) to break as a stick; ociyal a tree, a branch of which has been broken. Te. (K.) osulu to be loose (as joints), slip out. DED (S) 793. 941 Kur. osga rat. Malt. osge mouse. DED 794,

Ta. occiyam amorous speech, con- tempt; occiyaii-col to speak libidinously. Ka. occiya, occi, occaya, occeya disgrace, opprobrium, impropriety, a lewd expression, contempt. Te. occemu disgrace, indignity, insult. DED 795.
Ta. occi, ofici woman’s breast (nursery). Ka, ongu teat, pap. DED 796.
Ka. odar, odarcu, odaricu, odarisu to join (intr., tr.), undertake, begin, do, perform, produce, make an effort; odarcisu to under- take, begin, Te. odamu to be produced, arise. DED 797.

Ta. ufan together with, altogether, immediately after, at once; ufan-pafu to agree, yield; ufan- patikkai, utam-patikkai contract, agreement, promise; ufam-patu to harmonize; ufan-patu consent, concord; utantai union, alliance, relationship; -ofu, oti [91] ofufku -Oftu with. Ma. utan together with, at once; utam-pefuka to agree; utam-pati agreement, contract; ufappam, ufanma, ufama friend- ship, relation; -otu, -6tu with. Ko. -oer id. To. -wit with, after; witk together, undivided. Ka, odan, odam, oda union, together, as soon as; with; odam-badu, oda-badu to agree, consent; oda-badike agreement, treaty. Tu. oda with; odam- -badipuni to consent, agree, be pleased; odam-badiké, odam- badigé apree- ment, consent, treaty; "dambadipuni, dam- badiyuni, d dambadisuni to please, reconcile, appease, persuade; dambadigé delight, pleasure, love, acquiescence, persuasion. Te. odam-badu, oda-badu to consent, agree; odam-badika, od4-badika agreement, consent, treaty; odam-baracu, od4-baracu to persuade; oda-batu consent, assent. DED(S) 798.

Ta. oti). Konda ruy- (it-) to be broken; ruk- to break, smash. DEDS 81. 663 Ta, uruvu (uruvi-) to pierce through, penetrate (as an arrow, a needle). Ma. uruvuka to pierce through, penetrate. Ka. ureu, uccu to enter into and go out on the other side, penetrate. Tu, urumbuni, rummuni to bore. Te. uccu to enter, penetrate, pierce. Pa. ucc- to transfix. Kuwi (Su.) uh- (ust-) to pierce; (Isr.) uh- to stab. Kur. hurna, hurna to strike at and penetrate without piercing, goad, thrust something pointed into cavity (e.g. pick teeth). DED(S) 570. 664 (a) Ta. urul (uruly-, urunt-) to roll, tumble over and over, revolve (as a wheel), spin, become round, globular; m. car wheel, wheeled vehicle; uruli wheel, circle, small vessel of bell-metal that is circular in shape; urulai wheel, anything that rolls or turns (as a ball); urutci revolving, rotundity; uruttu (urufti-) to roll (7.), revolve, whirl, impose and confound by high-sounding verbiage; n. rolling, wheel, roundness, fraud; urutai cart; urunfai ball, anything round, roundness, mouthful of food in the shape of a ball. Ma, urul circular, a wheel, rolling wave; uruluka (urunf{-) to roll (ntr.), toss, revolve; urunta round; urula a ball; urulca rolling, roundness; uruli cauldron to boil 4-5 measures of rice; urufttu what is round, fraud; uruttan deceiver; urutfuka to roll (¢.), cheat. Jr. rullu (rund-) to roll Gntr.); ruttu id (¢r.). Ko. urn- (urd-) to roll Gtr.) over and over in one direction; urf- (urty-) id. (¢.). To. u-l- (u-d-) to r
Ma. oti side, piece of ground, division or range of ricefields. Tu. odi border; division, plot, or range of ricefields. DED 801.
Ta. oticil brickbat, kavan [i.e. sling}. Ma. oticil, otisil sling. Te. odisela id. DEDS 110.

Ta. otu festering sore; ofu-kkatfi boil in the armpit. Ma. ofuvu bubo; ofi- kkuru bubo, rupture. Ka. odise, oducu bubo; udacu, uducu venereal boil in the iliac region; udi a kind of disease. Tu. odi bubo. Te. (B.) odise-gadda, vadise-gadda bubo. DED 802.
Ta. otu, ofuvan, otuvai, ofaicci, (Lush.) otuku, otuppai the tree Cleistanthus collinus. Ma. orks odukku id. Go. (M.) orcha id. (Voc. 419). DEDS 111.
Ma. otukkuka to pay taxes. Ka. offaja tribute. DED 803.

Ta. ofunku (ofufki-) to be restrained, become tranquil, become reduced, grow less, shrink, slide to one side (as when meeting a ott® {92} od- superior), be subservient, be concealed, hidden, cease (as noise), be weary; otukku (otukki-) to subjugate, reduce, restrain, subdue, rob; nm. contraction, compression, that which is narrow, dent or depression in a metal utensil; otukkam narrowness, close- ness, self-restraint, reduction, contraction, place of concealment, secrecy, gradual sinking (of circumstances, of the powers of the body); otunikal obstacle, impediment; otuttki tortoise (as that which contracts). Ma. ofu- nhnhuka to come to an end, die (esp. of small- pox); oftukkuka to finish, destroy; otukkam the end; finally; otuka to come to an end; ofuvu the end. Ko, org- (orgy-) to be de- stroyed; ork- (orky-) to reduce (iron) in breadth. To. widg- (widgy-) to be crushed; witk- (wirky-) to crush, beat black and blue. Ka. udugu, udagu to subdue, restrain; shrink, shrivel, contract, be bent, decrease, fail, fade, be finished, desist, leave, quit, abandon, stop, remove; udugisu, udagisu to cause to shrink, etc.; lessen (as strength); udukisu to restrain, keep in, tighten, com- press, straighten, vex, oppress. Tu. odungelu, odunkelu, odkelu, odka the end; odungeluni to cease to bear ‘fruit. Te. udugu, uduvu to give up, quit, leave, stop, cease, desist from; udupu to remove, efface, wipe away (or with 1009 Ta. ogi); (K.) dufiku to be lowered, diminish, be subdued; dofiku to dry up,
Ma. otti, orra a kind of cake. Kod. otti round wafer of rice flour eaten with rice and curry. Tu. odé, odetaddye a kind of cake. DED 805.

Ta. ottiyam kind of witchcraft. Ma. Ottiyam sorcery, witchcraft; oti sorcery; Ofiyan sorcerer, esp. of low castes; ofikka to use witchcraft; otikkuka to practice sorcery, use witchcraft. Ko. orc- (orce-) (Kurumba) kills by witchcraft. To. wiry witchcraft practised by Kurumbas; wiry- (wirc-) to bewitch, kill by magic. DED 806.

Ta. ottu (ofti-) to stick (as with paste or gum), glue on, betake oneself to, make friends with; n. patch, piece stuck or fastened on, birdlime, union, friendship; oftal adhesion, attachment; offi that which adheres to, coheres with; otfinar friends, adherents; ottalan foe; ot{ar foes; ontu (onfi-) to join (tr.). Ma, oftuka to adhere, stick; oftikka to paste, glue; offal adhesion; offam what holds together, stops a leak; ottu glue, union; otfalar enemies. Ko. of- (oty-) to stick (intr.); ot- (oc-) to fasten up (hair) on nape of neck. Ka. otfu to become joined, adhere to (as mud, etc.), come together, assemble, become a mass or heap; join (t7.), put together, pile up; n. close adhering or cohesion of a lump of clay, conjunction, union, total, heap, pile; ottaje heap, mass, company; oftaysu to collect, assemble, etc.; offalu a mass, heap; ottil a handful; ottike heaping; ottisu to join (#.), heap, cause to heap or pile up; oddu to heap up; m. a mass, heap; odda, oddana a pile, heap, army. Tu. ottumt to heap, pile up; oftu total, the whole. Te. (B.) ottu a total; (K.) ottu to arise (as fear), accumulate, be put together; to put (as fire). Pa, otp- (oft-) to apply whitewash, etc. Ga. (P.) orp- (ort-) to smear with cowdung. DED (S) 807.

Ta. oftu (ofti-) to wager, stake, bind oneself to do a thing; n. oath; ottam a wager, stake. Ma. ottam a wager, stake at play. Ka. oftu a vow, solemn promise, oath; (PBh.) to make a vow; oddu to put a stake at play; n. a Stake at play; odda a wager, bet. Te. oftu an oath; oddu to wager, stake, bet: #. a wager; oddanamu, oddimi a stake, wager. Kol. (Pat., p.91) od oath. Kui oda (odi-) to swear an oath; 2. swearing. Kuwi (S.) ottinai to swear; (F.) ‘Stali to take an oath; (Isr.) ofu proof. /Cf, Pkt. (NM) hudd#- a wager; hodda- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 14175. DED (S, N) 808.
Ta. ottu; ? cf. 2982 Ta. tonkan. DED (N) 804. 955 Kur. ott& joint in a bamboo stem, knot in a plank, bony protuberance of limbs; (Hahn) a joint of the limbs or fingers. Malt. ofge ankle. DEDS 114.

Ta. oftiyanam gold or silver girdle or belt worn by women. Ma. ufafifian gold chain round the loins. Ka. odyana, oddyana, oddana, oddavana, oddivana, odvana belt of gold or silver chiefly worn by women. Tu, odyane, odyana belt or girdle made of gold and generally worn by a devil-dancer. Te. oddanamu belt of gold or silver worn by women. Probably < *oti-fian waist-string (see aL 2908); cf. esp. Ma. ufafifian. DED (N) 8

Ko. od- (ody-) to set (net, spring- trap). Ka, oddu to place, put, lay, put towards, present, hold (the hands) so as to receive, ‘catch or support a thing, fix; oddike, odduvike putting, etc.; oddisu to cause to put, etc. Tu. odduni to hold or stretch out (as the hand); oddavuni id.,‘set (a snare), cast (a net). Te. oddu to stretch out, extend, present, offer, spread (as a net), throw, cast; (Inscr.} oduku to give, deposit. Go, (SR.) urrang to put a noose (Voc, 268). Malt. oddu [93] opnad adye to lay a snare. / Cf. Pali oddeti to throw” out (a net), lay (snare). DED(S) 811. 963 (a) Ka. oddu to become opposite, oppose, counteract, resist; (PBh.) oddana a shield. Te. oddu to interpose, prevent; n, an obstacle. Malt. odenre a shield. DED (S) 812. (b) Ta, ottaram obstinacy, stubbornness, perversity. Ma. otfaram id. Te. oddaramu enmity, opposition; oddarincu to be hostile. 964 Ka, oddu the original number whether multiplicand or dividend. Tu. odde multipli- cation table. Te. oddu the multiplicand. DED 833.

Ko. od slope of hill. Ka. oddu bank, causeway; ondi bank, shore. Te. oddu id. Go, (S. Ko.) odu raised bank of river (Voe. 403). Konda odu bank of a river or stream. Kuwi (Su. P. Isr.) odu bank of river: Kur. otta shore. DED(S) 814.
Ta. oftu stubble. Ma. ofu id. Tu. oddu id. DED 815.

Te. odikamu prettiness, beauty, el- egance. Kol. (Pat., p. 107) odav attractive. Kui oda (odi-) to be clear, pure, bright; n. purity, cleanness; pl action otka (ofki-). Kuwi (F.) odi(ti) clear (of water); (S.) oth’nai to purify, refine, heal; othpinai to heal; otpinai to cleanse; otpi képinai to clean; (Mah.) oddamne beautiful; (Isr.) od- (-it-) to grow well, recover; be filtered; of- (-h-) to strain, filter; otpi ki- to make clean, cause to be clean. DEDS(N) 112.
Ta. oftu-kkafukkan small ear-ring. Ma. ontya a kind of ear-ring. Ke. onti a sort of Jarge gold ear-ring. Tu. onti an ear-ring. Te. ontu a sort of ear-ring worn by men. DED 16.

Ta. ontu (onti-) to take shelter, con- ceal oneself (as a person to shoot game), lurk (as an animal for prey); ottu (ofti-) to play the eavesdropper, lurk, lie in ambush. Jr. ondal shed, shelter, hiding-place. Ko. od- (ody-) to crouch down behind shelter, eavesdrop. Ga, (S.2) onda den (of wild animal). Malt. ode shelter, refuge; odye to screen, shelter. ?Cf. Kur (Tiga) ordrna to take shelter. DED(S) 817.

Ka. onfu to agree (with one’s health), be salubrious. Te. ontu to be agreeable, wholesome, or salubrious, (K. also) be friend- ly; onfami disagreement, enmity; onfaramu enmity; ontimpu convenience, aid, help. DED(S) 818.
Ko. onfgyn boar. Te. ontikadu, onftigddu wild boar. DED 819.

Ta. ottacai aid, help, assistance. Ma. ottasa help. Ko, otaec id. To. wito® id. Ka. ottase id. ?Cf. 609 Ta. utavu. DED 820. 973 (a) Ta. ottu (otti-), orru (orri-) to make room for; orri-pp6- to go away from; orti- vai- to place out of the way, adjourn. Ma. orruka to step aside, retire, cringe. Ko. ot- (oty-) to precede on the way, go fast. Ka. ottu to give way, leave space, step aside. Te. ottu, ottilu, ofttillu, ottigillu to step aside, make way, move out of the way. (b) Ta. otufku (otufki-) to get out of the way, step aside (as a mark of respect, before a superior), retreat, be defeated, seek refuge; be finished, settled, adjusted, completed; otukku (otukki-) to put on one side (as the hair), cause to get out of the way (as cattle in the road), push into a corner, separate (as persons in a quarrel), gather on one side or tuck up (as one’s clothes while crossing a river), place out of reach, expel (as from caste), dispatch (as a business), settle; m. that which is apart, refuge, screen; otukkam privacy, retiring, hiding-place. Ma, otufifuka to give way, step aside, yield; be adjusted; otukkuka to subdue, settle: otukkam subjec- tion, being settled and compressed; otukku shelter. To. wi8x- GviOxy-) (horns of fighting buffaloes) slip apart. Te. odtigu to move or step aside, make room, sidle, shrink from, sneak, slink. DED 821.
Ta. ottu (otti-) to keep time with cymbals or with the hands; 7. time in music. Ma, ottuka to jump, skip, dance, indicate the time (of music by moving the eyebrows). DED 822.

Ka. ottu to cut or break betel nut. Te. ottu to cut or break with nippers. DEDS 115. 976 Kui ota (oti-) to fetch (persons). Kur. otthOrma (otthras) to take out, bring out, expel; ond°rna (ondras) to bring, take along, take for wife. Malt. otre to take out, bring out; ondre to bring. Cf. 984 Ta. oy and ? cf.

Ka. onali, ondari, ondri, vandari, vandare, vandri a sieve, strainer; one, one to winnow, sift; onasu, onisu to cause to shake to and fro; (Gowda) oficu to sift. Kod. onali a sieve. DED(S) 827. 981 Kur. opna to wet, soak (with a view to softening and preparing for work); refl.- pass, oprna. Malt, oprare to become soft (as earth by water). DED 828. oppati [94] oru

Ta. oppati harvest. Te. obbidi threshing of corn. DED 829. 983 Ka, obbatfu, obbiftu a kind of sweet cake. Te, obbattu id. DED 830. 984 Ta, oy (-v-, -nt-) to drag along (as a flood), launch (as a boat), send forth, give; oyyal forwarding, giving; uy (pp-, -tt-) to carry, take away, lead, guide, send, discharge (as arrow), give. To. iiy- (iis-) to chase. Ka. oy, uy (oyd-) to carry off, conduct, carry, convey; osage a gift, present; (Hav.) occu to row a boat. Kod. uyti force. Tu. oyipuni to draw, pull, drag; oyipavuni to cause to draw or pull; oyilu force or velocity of a stream; occiyuni to deliver, give in charge; occuni to row a boat. Bel. (LSB 2.1) ogi to pull. Te. osfgu, osavu to give; 7. a gift; uccu to send. Pa, uy- (ufi-) to carry, take, take away. Ga. (OUL) uy- (ud-, un-) to take, take away. Go. (A. Y. G. Mu.) oy-/6- to take, take away, carry; (SR.) oyana to take; (Tr.) woiand, QV.) woina, (Ph.) voitana, voiand to take away; caus. (Ph.) vosahtanad; (Ma. Ko.) oy-, (M.) oyanad to carry (Voe. 414). Konda o- (-t-) to carry, take away, take along with oneself; caus, opis-. Pe, 5- (-t-) to take. Mand. - (-t-) id. Kui opa (ot-) to lead away, take away (persons); 7. act of leading away; Ova (Ot-) to take away (things), take, receive; n, act of taking. Kuwi (F.) oiyali (6-) to take away, remove. Kur, ho’ona (occas/hoccas) to take, receive, transport, conduct; reff. ho’orna; pass. hotarnd; caus, hOota’and. Malt. oye (oc-) to take away. Cf. 931 Kol. ok-, 976 Kui ota, and 1051 Pa.
Ka. oykane orderly, properly, exactly, clearly. Tu. vaimé straightness, fitness, rectitude. Te, ogi order, a range or line; ogin in order, Kur, uira’ana to put in order, arrange. DEDS(N) 116.
Ka. (Hav.) orale, (Gowda) orlE, (Hal.) alle, valle white ant. Tu. orale id. Kor. (M. T.) orle id. From DEDS(N) 75.

Pa. ori, orin yesterday. Ga. (OL, S.) orgun id. Cf. 929 Kol, okkod. DED 833. 990 (a) Ta. oru (before consonant), or (before vowel) one; ori (-pp-, -tt-}) to be united in harmony; orukkam oneness, same- ness; orukka for each; orukku (orukki-) to bring together; orunku altogether, simul- taneously; (orunki-) to have a singleness of aim or purpose, join together; oruttan, oruvan a male person; fem. orutti; oruttu concentration of mind; orumi (-pp-, -tt-) to be in unison, unite; orumikka together; orumippu union, harmony, undivided atten- tion; orumai oneness, union, singleness, loneliness, uniqueness, concentration of mind; oruvar one person (fon.); oruvu (oruvi-) to resemble, equal; oré only, one; oru-talai one- sidedness; Ormai unity; Or-onru, ovv-onru each one, one at a time. Ma. oru, dr one, the same; oruvan, oruttan one man; fem. oruval, orutti; orukkuka to bring together; oruma union, harmony; orumikka to join, be recon- ciled; Grd, Stdrd, Gro6nnu cach one. Ko. or paeny one paeny measure; or karm one karm measure; or port half of the day; oer ak one olk measure; oer asy one year; oeraele co-wife; 0 caery one side; o budy of one mind; orrvis one at a time, once every day; obi+ same place, together; o-tal crookedness (e.g. of mouth), deviation from straight line or perfect shape or position, one side (off the path); otalg- (otalgy-) to go on one side, avoid by going around; ? ong- (ongy-) to bea half-breed, change (imtr.) magically into something else; onge- (ongce-) to bree

Ta. oruhku (orunfki-) to sink, decline, become reduced, be ruined, perish; n, suppression, restraint; orukku (orukki-) to subdue, control, kill, slay. Ma. orufifiuka to yield. Ka. oggu to bend, bow, become sub- missive. Te, oggu to bend, turn towards, become submissive, yield. DEDS 118.
Ma. orunfuka to be ready; orukkuka to prepare, get ready; orukkam preparation, Te, oggu to be prepared or ready, undertake. DEDS 119.

Ta. oruvu (oruvi-) to abandon, re- nounce, pass over, escape, be excepted; oruvu, ortiu, ortiutal leaving, separation, renunciation. Go. (Ma.) ori- to move aside; (SR.) vorke aside (Voce. 415). DEDS 120.

Ta. oli Cpp-, -tt-) to sound, roar; 7. sound, noise, roar, speech; olippu sonorous- ness, roar; Of sound, lullaby; slam sound, noise, roar, cry of lamentation; 61 Attu to sing oli [96] ollu a lullaby; ulampu (ulampi-) to make a noise, roar, thunder; ulappal, ulampal combined noise of many sounds; dlu (Oli-) to make a noise. Ma. oli a sound; olikka to sound, as running water, ring bell; ulampuka to make a noise; ulappam hubbub, clamour; slam cry for help; ol howling; a jackal. Ko. oj noise; o-l aef- (a+c-) to drive round bullocks when threshing. Ka. uli (ulid-) to sound, cry, utter, speak, say; m. sound, cry, word; ole a sound; (PBh.) to utter; ulipa, ulipu, ulivu, uluvu a sound, cry. Kod. oli noise of a metal bell. Te. uliyu to sound, resound; ulivu sound, voice, Kur. ol°xnad (ulxyas), in phrase cixna ol©xnd to bewail, lament. Malt. olge to cry, weep. ? Br. hdghing (also stem h6-) to weep (: Malt. olge). Cf. 5283 Ta. vali. DED(S) 837.

Ta. oli (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to shoot forth, be luxuriant, prosper, thrive; oliyal luxuriance, shoot, sprout; ulavai green twig with leaves upon it, branch of a tree, grove. Ka, ole to appear, come to light. Tu. ulevuni to shine. Te. olayu to appear, arise, happen, (K. also) occur, increase, shine. DED 838.

Ta. oli (-pp-, -tt-) to wash as clothes, remove as dirt. fa. olumpuka to wash clothes by shaking them in water, rub off, shake off, cleanse; ulampuka to rinse. Kod. oli- (olip-, olic-) to wash (clothes); olap- (olapi-) to wash (plates, pots); olamb- (olambi-) to gargle and spit out (water). Tu. olapy, olam- pu, olpe cleanliness, purity; lumbuni_ to plunge, wash, rinse. DED (S) 839.

Ta. oliyal river. Ma, oliyuka to flow; olikka to flow, run as water, blood from wounds; olippu flowing, looseness of bowels; olivu flowing; (Tiyya) olu water-current; dluka to flow, ooze out; Ola trickling; Oli a spring, temporary well; oldla falling in drops, trickling. Jr. uli waterfall. PalKu., AlKu. oli id. Ko. oyl waterfall in channel or river. To. wasy waterfall; wasf- Gvast-) to defecate (of adults); wa+} pit drainage hole in wall (cf. 4317, esp. Ta. pugal, To. pil). Te. oluku to be spilled, run, flow, overflow, gush out. Cf. 1068 Ka, dl-adu and 5367 Ta. vali. DED (S, N) 840.

Ta. oliyal skin, hide. Tu. olipu skin, skin of a snake, shape. Te. oliyu to be peeled, flayed; olucu to peel, flay, strip; olupu peeling, flaying. Kol. (Wagh.) ols- to skin. Nk. ols- to peel. Pa. olc- to flay. Cf. 2856 Ta. coli (Kol. Nk. and Pa. may be borrowed from Te.). DED(S) 841.
Te. oliki a funeral pile. Pa. olggam blaze of fire; olip- (olit-) to char, scorch. Kur, olna to be on fire, (crop) is scorched by excessive heat; olOdna to set fire to, scorch. DEDS 121.
Ta. oluhku big mosquito; ulahku gnat, mosquito. Ma. oluiiku a kind of mosquito.- Kod. olafijia fly. DED 842.

Ta. olku (olki-) to shake, move, wave; ulukku (ulukki-) to shake (#7, as a tree), tremble (as in an earthquake); uluppu (ulu- ppi-) to shake off, cause to shed (as fruit or leaves from a tree); ulai (-v-, -nt-) to be disordered (as one’s affairs), become un- settled, loose, degenerate in morals, be agitated in mind, be dispersed (as an as- sembly, army), wander about; (-pp-, -tt-) to throw into disorder, unsettle, ruin, harass (as a demon); n. flurry, excitement, agitation; ulaiccal trouble, harassment; ulaical disordered state of one’s circumstances, ruin as of a house, derangement; ulaippu troubling, putting to rout, destruction; ulaivu agitation, vexation, uneasiness, trembling; Ma, ulayuka to be agitated, shake, become loose, slack, tired; ulekkuka to agitate, crumple (paper, ‘clothes, etc.), destroy; ulaccal, ulavu agi- tation; ulakkam shake, shudder; ulahiuka to shake (intr.), be shocked; ulasuka to swing, shake, move from side to side; ulu- kkuka, uluttuka to shake; olayuka to shake, tremble, olasal agitation, confusion; olumpuka to move from side to side. Ka. ole to swing (intr.), wave, shake, tremble, move, move in a swinging manner, hang or bend to one side; shake (tr.), etc., bend; n. act of swing- ing, etc.; olapu moving the body in a foppish manner; olahu swinging to and fro, a swing; oleta swinging; uluku, ulku, uluku, uf{ku the body to shake, tremble, be agitated, etc. Kog._oli- (oliv-, olifij-) (fruit) drops from tree in large quantities; (olip-, olic-) to bea

Ta. olku (olki-) to grow weak or faint, pine, be disheartened, become reduced, slender, thin, be emaciated; orku (orki-) to be deficient, be wanting, fall short, droop; orkam poverty, indigence, destitution, feeble- ness, weakness, deficiency, dearth; offi thin person, thinness, slenderness. Ma, olfkuka to grow weak, contract; ollati a thin, slender person. Tu. olandala, olandale swoon, faint- ing. Te. olf3-bovu to faint, grow or turn pale, fade. ?Cf. 1025 Ta. onpatu. Cf. 5281 Ta. vali. DED (S) 844.

Ta. oliyal cloth, garment. Ma. olli sheet, cover five yards by three; olliyal, oliyal royal cloth. Ka. olli a small dotra; (K.?) ollanige a cloth, an upper cloth. Tu. olli, olle sheet used for covering the body in bed, a laced cloth for wearing; olanigé a cloth covering the privities. Te. olliya, ofle an upper garment; valuva, valuvu a fine cloth, a cloth. DED(S, N) 845.

Ta. ollu (prob. olli-) to be possible, practicable, be fit, suitable, agree, combine, consent; ollal being able, possible, recon- ciling after a love quarrel; ollamai inability, contempt, absence of desire; ollatavar, olla enemies; ollunar friends, associates; ol vari ollu [97] oli in a suitable place, af a suitable time. Ma. olluka to consent, love, be fit, possible; (eg. ) olla, valla must not, ought not; olfatta un- becoming, indecent, bad; ollal ability, possibility. Ko, olaed bad; olaspm bad return for good action, refusing to keep a bargain. To. wal- (neg. only) to be bad; walo(@) bad; iis- (iisy-) to long for. Ka. ol to be pleased, like, love, desire; olavu pleasure, kindness, favour; olavara, valavara pleasure, charm; oli, ole to be pleased, desire, like, be apt, fit, be agreeable; olime, olume pleasure, love, kindness; 6l-ddu to have affection for, love; Ol-ata affection, friendship. Tu. olapuni to love, fondle; olame, olume affection, love, kindness; oliyuni to favour, be kind; olipe present, gift. Te. valacu (neg. gerund ollaka) to love, wish, desire; ollami refusal, rejection, declining; valadu, vaddu is not required or wanted; must not, ought not; valapu love, desire, wish; (B.) dlimika friendship. Kui orpa (ort-) to pine for, lust after; m, desire, Just (or with 691 Ta. uguval). Cf. $276 Ta. val. DED(S) 846.

Ta. ollu (olli-) to mend (as a net), braid (as a basket). Ma. orral net of pack- thread, basket for holding pots, weel, fishing with weels. Go. (Ma.) olc-, (Ko.) ols-, (Tr. W.) walcand, (Ph. Ch.) valc- to sew; (L.) olcana to repair, mend (Voc. 431). DED(S) 847.
Ta. ollai, ollé, oll-ena rapidly, quickly. Ma. olla a short time, rapidity.

Ta. ori (-v-, -nt-) to cease, be finished, ended, die, be settled, decided, be empty, unoccupied; (-pp-, -tt-) to finish, bring to an end, destroy, expel, empty; oriccu (ogicci-) to put an end to, banish, vacate, destroy; ogippu exclusion, dismissal; orivu ceasing, end; oy (-v-, -nt-) to wipe out, efface; be off, escape. Ma, oriyuka to leave off, be free from, empty; ogiccal, ogivu vacancy; ogikka to quit, give way, escape, make to cease, drive away, free; orippikka to dispossess, make to retire; orippu discharge. To. widy- (wids-), ody- (ode-) to be separated from (-k) by pre- deceasing or not surviving or by others predeceasing, leave behind; widy- (widc-) to put by, save. Ka. ugi to leave, abandon, quit, except; be left, remain, be left out, excepted, or excluded, remain alive, be saved, remain behind; n. remaining, remnant; uriki, uraki, uzike, ugke, ukke remaining, remainder, remnant; urate remaining, remainder; urave remaining, being saved, refuge; uripu to cause or suffer to remain, spare, save; odi to separate from, leave. Ta. orukku (orukki-} to draw out, as gold thread. Ko. ork- (orky-) to embroider. To. wirk- (wirky-) id. Tu, ntlodu a spindle. Te. oduku, vaduku to spin (t7.). Nk. (Ch.) oc-/os- to sew. DEDS 122. 1015 Ta, oli (-v-, -nt-) to hide, steal away; (-pp-, -tt-) to hide, conceal, disguise; lie hid; n, hiding, lurking-place, screeen, cover for a fowler, decoy animal; olippu slinking away, hiding, concealing, keeping secret; olivu place of concealment; ulavu secrecy, spying. Te. oli light, brightness, splendour, sun, moon, star, fire, sunshine, lamp, beauty; oliyavan, oliyOn sun; olir (-v-, -nt-), oliru (oliri-) to shine; olirvu, oligu brightness; onmai brilliance, brightness, beauty. Ta. oli excellence, fame, wisdom; olliyan good man, wise man; onmai goodness, wisdom; ofpam intelligence, wisdom, Ko, oly, olyd good. To. wily good; wilt id., what is good. Ka. olle, holle, ulli a kind of amphis- baena. Kod. olle pa-»mbi water-snake. Tu. olle, olle id. DED 856.

Ta. oru (-pp-, -tt-) to dislike, be disgusted with, rebuke, punish, suppress (as the desires), mortify (as the body); oruppu dislike, aversion, rebuke, punishment, self- mortification; urai (-pp-, -tt-) to rebuke, disgrace. DED(S) 857.
Ta. orri mortgage with possession (as of land, trees, cattle, etc.). Ma. orri a pawn; Orra-ttali marriage badge (or with 1023). Ka. otte a pledge, pawn. Tu. otti id. DED 858.

Ta. orru (orri-) to press, hug close, stamp (as a seal), press down, press upon, attack, apply fomentation; 2. fomentation; ottu (otti-) to place in contact with, foment; orratam, ottatam, ottanam fomentation. Tu. oftuni to press; ottuni to press, shampoo, seal, stamp; be close, thickset; ottu closeness, thickness; ottaya force, compulsion; ottavuni to cause to press, be impressed or sealed; ottele oppressor. Te, ottu to press, squeeze, foment, urge, pro- duce a mark on a surface by pressure; 7m. pressure, denseness; close, thick, dense; ottadamu fomenting, pressing gently. Kuwi (Isr.) ottu thickness; ? (S.) ulvnai to press. DED(S) 859. ~

Te. orru (orri-) to spy out; 2. espion- age, a spy; orran, Orruvan, orral a spy: orru- mai qualities requisite in a spy; orruvi Cpp-, -tt-) to keep oneself informed of events through spies. Ma, orru private intelligence, secret information; orran, orrukaran a spy; orrikka to cause to spy. DED 860.

Ta. orru flat bracelet for a child; ottu a thin bangle. Ma, orra-ttali_ marriage badge (or with 1020). Te. ottulu gold brace- lets for children. DED 861. 1024 Kur. (Hahn) on’na to be sharp, cut. Malt. onke sharp; blade of a knife. DEDS 124.

Ta. onpatu, ompatu, ompdtu nine. Ma, ompatu id. Ko. orba+d, (Sholur dialect) onba+d id.; patrmba-d, potrmba-d nineteen; orba- calg nine calg measures. Ta. 6-nay Indian wolf, Canis pallipes. Ma, 6-nay wolf. DED 863.
Ta. 6 shutter or other means to stop the flow of water. Ma, Oku, Gvu floodgate, kali [99] otu sluice, drain; otti open gutter, drain. DED 864.
Ka. okali, Okufi red liquid sprinkled or squirted at the holi feast, at the close of a temple festival, or at marriage. Tu. okali, dkuli id. DED 865.

Ta. okkalam, Okkdlippu, Onkal retch- ing; Okkali (-pp-, -tt-), Ofku (GAki-), GAk- ari Cpp-, -tt-), Onkali (-pp-, -tt-) to retch, vomit. Ma. okkanam_ retching, nausea; Okkanikka to retch, vomit. Cf. 678 Ta. uvatfu. {Cf Mar. okn€ to vomit; Turner, CDIAL, no. 2538, *okk-. DED(S) 866.

Ta. drai boiled rice, rice that is mixed with other edibles such as tamarind, sesamum, etc. Ma. dra boiled rice. Ka. Ogara boiled rice. Te. Ogiramu, Oremu, (Inscr.) dviramu food, boiled rice. / Cf. OMar. (Master) ogara, vogara rice, food. DED(S) 867. 1031 Kur. ogna (Ugyas) to swim, float; OSgrnt to be swum across, (tank or stream) supports; Ogta’and to make swim, float (trees, etc.). Malt. ége to swim, float. DED 868.

Ko. o-+garv- (o+gard-) to listen with- out speaking, be silent when called, keep silent (*org- +1258 *karv- to lie hidden). To, o+x to hear (a song-word). Tu. Sngéruni, Shonuni to listen, overhear, eavesdrop; dkonuni to answer a call (cf. 643 Te. i-konu to hear, listen to; or with 643). DED(S) 869.

Ta. 6hku (Onki-) to grow, rise high (as a tree), ascend (as a flame), be lofty, spread, be exalted, dignified, increase in wealth or renown; lift up, raise (as arm, weapon, pestle); Ofkal height, rising, moun- tain, mound; okku (Okki-) to raise, lift up, cause to rise; 6kkam height, increase, bigness; dccam eminence; Sccal height, elevation; Occu (Occi-) to raise in order to strike; Sppu (Oppi-) to raise. Ta. Scaram for the sake of, on ac- count of. Ka, Osuga, Osugara, Oskara cause, reason, sake. ? Te, kdsaramu, kOsamu for the sake of, on account of. DED 871. 1035 Kur. 6sa mushroom. Malt, ésu id. DED 872.

Gja, djagatti, dji beautiful; oju beauty; Sjitasi a handsome man; Oja harku ornaments; Ojali turh’nai to rig; Ojapee that 1 may look nice. DEDS 125.

Ka. doje a row, line, heap, mass, regularity, order, propriety; ojayila a man of ° propriety; Gjisu to be properly or carefully performed. Te, dja a row, line, regularity, _ order, manner, way. Stuka to run (as men, animals, roots, etc.), ships to sail, flow easily, meet with no impediment; ofikka, Oftuka to drive, chase, steer, navigate; Ota water-course; Otfam a course, run; Otfi ship’s captain. Ko. o-r- (ore-) to run; o+f act of running. To. wi-d- (wiedy-) to run; wi+{ act of running; wi-{- (wi-ty-) to drive calf from udder by hitting it under th

Ta. otu (Obl. Stt-) shell (of egg, tortoise, crab), tile, potsherd, earthen vessel, skull. Ma. ofu shell, potsherd, tile; Ottikka shell. Ko. o+r (obl. o+f-) shell, tile, potsherd, skull. Ta. Stai longer internoded stout reed bamboo, Ochlandra travancorica. Ma. Sta a large reed; ottal a kind of small bamboo, Ochlandra rheedii. Ko. e«r (obl. e-t-), oF (obl, 0*{-) sp. Ka. ote the tree Garcinia pictoria Roxb., the Mysore gamboge tree; Ste-puli its fruit. Tu. Onte-puli, Ste-puli the sour fruit of G. cambogia. DED 880.

Ta. Otai metal plate or badge for the forehead, frontlet for elephants. Ma. Sta ornamented frontlet of an elephant. 1046 Ma, dni a footpath. Ka. oni lane, alley, street. Kod. o+ni lane leading to house. Tu, Oni lane, alley. Te. Gni-kattu a dark alley, (B.) a narrow pass between hills. DED 881.

Ta. Stam moisture, dampness, flood, sea, wave.’ Ma. Otam dampness in rainy season. Ka. odde wetness, dampness, moisture. Tu, odde id.; wet; veddé moist, wet. Nk. (Ch.) vad, vod dew. Cf. 743 Konda tid-. DED(S)
Ka. Oda, vada ditch, trench, pitfall for catching elephants. Te. Odamu pitfall for catching elephants. DED 883.
Ma. oti share, part. Kod. o+di share. DED 884.

Ta. tu (Gti-) to read, recite, utter mantras, repeat prayers, speak, declare; dtuvi (-pp-, -tt-) to teach the Vedas, instruct; Gtal reciting (as the Veda); Sti learning, learned person; Ottu reciting, uttering (as a mantra), the Veda. Ta. otti chameleon, Chamaeleo vul- garis; bloodsucker lizard, Calotes versicolor; Sti, Onti, Span, Oman bloodsucker lizard; ontin bloodsucker; (Devanesan; Coimbatore dial.) Otakkan id. Ma. Ontu chameleon; bloodsucker, Lacerta cristata. To, wis large jungle lizard. Ka. oti, (K.?) onti a kind of lizard or chameleon, bloodsucker, L. cristata. Kod. o-ndi, o+tike+té chameleon. Tu. Gnti bloodsucker, salamander. ? Malt, ute a tree lizard called also qése-6nu, lit, bloodsucker. DED (S) 887.

Ta. G6mam bishop’s weed, Carum copticum, Ma. dmam sison. Ka, 6ma, Smu, ovu, vima, voma, hdma bishop’s weed, Ligusticum (or Ptychotis) ajwaen, Tu. Oma the seed of bishop’s weed, common caraway, Sison amomum, Ptychotis. Te, Omamu the seed of bishop’s weed. S. ammi, P. ajowan. /Cf. Mar. Sva L. ajwaen; bishop’s weed, or the seed of it, S. ammi. [C. copticum Benth. = L, ajawain Fleming = L. ajouan Roxb. = P. ajowan DC. = S. ammi Sacq.] DED 890.
Te. Smal rumour. Ma, omal id.

Ta. Smpu (Smpi-) to protect, cherish, bring up. Ma. Ompuka to cherish, take care of; stroke (as in embracing, blessing); 6mana fondness, tenderness with children, darling; nicety, beauty; Omanikka to fondle, caress; omal fondness, darling; neatness, Ka, dvu to take care of, guard, protect, cherish. Tu. omana darling, dear; fondness, tenderness. Te. Smu to preserve, cherish. DED 891.

Ta. Sy (-v-, -nt-) to cease, come to an end, expire, perish, be diminished, reduced, become tired, weary, weak, rest; oyccal ceasing, weakness, debility; oyvu cessation, termination, weariness, rest; Ovu (Gvi-) to cease, terminate, become extinct; n. cessation; oci (-v-, -nt-) to suffer, become weary.
