Ko. kicv- (kict-) to make a grimace (of derision, etc.) by opening mouth and drawing back lips, open mouth at death; kicvasyn one who acts foolishly through ceyir- ; [140] gifiju temporary inadvertence; ginj- (ginj-) (dog) shows (teeth) with a snarl. Ka. kisi (kisid-/ kist-) to expand, open, withdraw the lips from the teeth so as to show them, grin, put astride; vb.n. kisa, kisi, kisu; kisikisi nagu to grin in laughter, grin; kicikil to neigh. Tu. kisu, kisuka, kisi grinning; kisi- kisi a titter, laugh.” /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 3889, *khiss- grin, snarl (probably only some items). DED(N) 1268.

Ta. ceyir- (-pp-, -tt-) to be angry with, show signs of anger, afflict, cause pain; n, anger, rage, battle, fight; ceyirppu anger. Ka, kisur to be intolerable or very disagree- able; kisur, kisur, kesaru an intolerable or very disagreeable state; strife, quarrel; the evil eye, evil, harm; kittada disagreeableness, disgustfulness. Tu. kedaruni to be excited, drritated. Te. kiduku to fret, be cross on waking from sleep (as children). Pe. kickica diseust. Pe. kickica filthy, disgusting; disgusted. Kuwi (S.) kijja kijja dnai to loathe. DED(S, N) 1269. 1512 Ta, kiccili Sylhet orange, Citrus aurantium; Seville orange, C. vulgaris, Ma. kiccili orange. Ka. kicili, kittale, kittile an orange; (Gowda) cittuli orange. Tu. kittali, kittili, kitlé, citturli an orange; citlipuli a kind of orange; (B-K.) citturli, cittupuli an orange. Te. kiccili, kittali the orange tree. DED(S, N) 1270.

Te. (K.B.) giccu to scratch, pinch. Kol. (SR.) kism-, (Pat., p.159) kiseng to pinch. Nk. (Ch.) kis-/kicc- id. Pa. kik- id.; kiec-. to pluck (strings of instrument with finger). Ga, (Oll.) kisk- to pinch; (S.) kice- to pluck. Go, (Tr.) kiskanad, (A. Mu. Ma.S.) kisk- to pinch (Voc. 700); (Ph.) kicmana to pinch, scratch; (Mu.) kism- to pinch (Voe. 672); (Tr.) kicc&nd to pick bhaji, i.e. green leaves for salad; (M.) kicana to pluck (Voc. 671). Konda kis- (-t-) to pinch, nip, squeeze with fingers; caus. kisis-, kispis-. Pe. kic- (-c-) to pinch. Mand. kic- id. Kui kisa (kisi-) to pinch, nip; m. a pinch, nip; pl. action kiska (kiski-); (K.) kicc- to pinch, nip. Kuwi (Su.) kicc- (it-), (F.) kicali to pinch; (S.) kicinai to nip (spelled with ch rather than z). Kur. kiccnd to break into very small pieces (soft things, esp. bread, greens) with the fingers; ? kissna to open and expand the flesh with a needle, etc., e.g. in order to extract a thorn; to goad. ? Malt. kise to dig out a thorn from the flesh. Br. kishking to pluck, break off. DED (S) 1271. 1514 Ta, kiccu fire. Kurub, (LSB 1.11) ticci id. Ko. tic id. To. tit id.; kic, ki€ id. (in songs; < Badaga). Ka. kiccu, kircu id. Kod. titti id. Tu. kitty lightning; kiccu, kicci, kicce fire, envy, indigestion. Kor. (O.) kicci jealousy. Te. ciccu fire, flame; ciccara, ciccura fiery; kittu fire. Kol kis id, Nk. kicc (c = ts) id. Nk. (Ch.) kie (¢ = ts) id. Pa. kic id. Ga. (Oll.) kis; (S.) kieccu id.; (P.) kismul fireplace. Go. (all dialects)

Ta. kiccukicc-enal onom. expr. signi- fying chirping (as lizards); kiccu chirp of birds, shrill crying sound; kicc-itu to chirp (as ‘birds, lizards), chatter (as monkeys), squeak, scream (as an infant); kiccukkice- enal onom. expr. signifying screaming, squeak- ing, chirping, twittering sound; kiccin infant. Ma. kiccukiccu chirp; kiccu sobbing; kiccuka to sob; kificuka, keficuka to squeak. Ka. kica-guttu, kici-guttu, kicakica annu, kicikici annu to chirp (as birds), chatter (as monkeys); kicu = kica; kicak-annu to chatter (as babies do); kicu kicu chirping of birds, squeaking of rats; giji biji the chirping and chattering of birds. Tu. kicikici chirping, prattling; kicu- kicu, kicukitu squeaking of rats, etc.; kicuni o to squeak; gijigiji a chirping noise. Te, kicakica chirping of birds, etc.; kica-koftu to chirp; kicu squeaking, shrill; kicum-anu to squeak, make a shrill noise. / Cf. Skt. (epic +) kicaka- a kind of hollow bamboo (whistling or rattling in the wind), Arundo karka; a kind of bird. DED(S, N) 1273. 1516 Ta, kiccukkiccu miftu/ppanpu to tickle (¢.). To. kiskwif- (kiskwity-) id.; kiskwil- (kiskwid-) tickling is (with dative). DED 1274. 1517 Kur. kiss pig. Malt. kisu id. DED 1275. 1518 Konda kizay- (-t-) to jeer at, ridicule. Kuwi (S8.) kijowi joke; kijowigatti witty, kitzowi kinai to jest, jeer, joke; (Isr.) kijovi joke; kijovi ki- to make fun, joke. ? Malt. qese to tease, disturb. DEDS 201.

Ka. gijati, giji giji a jelly-like, viscous, pulpous, sticky state. Tu. giji giji soft, pul- pous. Pa. gifiji, gififii pith. Go. (Ko.) ginji pulp of fruit (Voc. 1078). Kur. ginjnd to beat or press into pulp, Cf. 1880 Ta. kicci. /MBE 1969, p.290, no.5, for areal ety- mology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 4153. DEDS 202.

Ka. giji, gija a sound denoting con- fusion, etc.; giji giji, gija gija state of being very crowded. Ju. gijilu confusion. /MBE 1969, p.290, no.6, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 4153. DEDS 203.
Ka. gifiju state of being full of seeds and of a disagreeable taste, said of the heads of some vegetables. Te, ginja a seed, a grain. Konda ginza a seed. DED(S) 1276. kifa [141] kiffu

Ta. kita (-pp-, -nt-) to lie, lie down (as in sleep, in inactivity), dwell, sleep, rest, be bedridden; kifakkai recumbent posture, bed; kifattal recumbent posture; kifattu (kifatti-) to place in a recumbent posture, lay to rest, stretch oneself on the ground; kifappu (kifappi-) to cause to lie down; n, resting, sleeping; kifai lying down. Ma. kita layer, bank, mound, what lies or remains; kitakka to lie, rest, dwell, be left; n. bed; kitattuka to lay down, put to sleep; kitappu lying, situation, remainder. Ka. kede to fall down, drop, sink; let fall, drop thoughtlessly (as words); m, act of thoughtlessly dropping words; kedapu, kedavu, kedahu, kedivu to make fall down, fell, throw to the ground, pull down (as a house); kade to fall down, sink. Kod. kadake bed, including bedding. Tu. kedaguni to fall or slip down. Te. kedayu to fall or fall over, tumble over; kedapu to cause to fall, push or tumble over, throw; kedayika, kedavu falling, fall. Go. (SR. Tr.) karrina to fall (Tr. at a man’s feet); (Y.) ker- (-t-) to fall at the feet (Voc. 558). ? Malt. kéde (kodya) to lie down; kodete to lay oneself down. DED(S) 1277.

Ta. kitafku prison; storehouse; kittahki storehouse. Ma, kitaftuiu prison; storehouse (godown): Ka. gadanga, gadangu, giddangi storeroom, godown. Tu. gadatigy id. Te. giddanhgi gaol; storehouse, godown. / Cf. Hobson-Jobson, s.v. godown. DED 1278.

Ta. kitahku ditch, trench, moat, pond, tank; kitahkar moat, ocean; kitankil moat. Ma. kifatiiu trench, ditch. Ka. kadagu small channel issuing from a larger one and leading water e.g. to a plantain tree; kadan- gu channel, ditch, trench. Kod. kadangi deep ditch without water. DED(S) 1279. 1527 Ka, gidaga, gidiga, giduga, dége hawk, falcon, small kestrel, jugger falcon. Kod. kiduvé kite, hawk. Tu. gidi falcon, hawk, eagle. Te, giddu hawk; déga hawk, falcon. Pa. (S.) deya hawk. Go. (SR) dego eagle. Konda déga falcon. Kuwi (S.) déga hawk. DED 1280.
To. kiry a spark. Ka. kidi, (Hav.) kedi id. Kod. kédi, cedi id. Tu. kidi, (B-K.) kedi id. DED(N) 1281.

Ka. gidi to enter by mouthfuls, or devour mouthful after mouthful, or to cause mouthfuls to enter; to force into the mouth (of others, as food, cloth). Tu. gidku, gidaka mouthful, draught; (B-K.) gidi to stuff in, thrust in. DEDS 206.

Ta. kiftukitu Cpp-, -tt-) to tremble, shake, shiver, totter (as a loose wall), chatter (as the teeth with cold); ”. quaking, trembling; kifukif-enal expr. signifying tremor; kifu- kitay (-pp-, -tt-) to be amazed, tremble with fear, Ma, kitukita shivering; kitukitukka, kitukkuka, kitufmuka to shiver, tremble; kitilam tremor. DED 1282.

Ta. kitukitu (-pp-, -tt-) to sound (as the rolling of a carriage), rumble (as a thunder- cloud); m. a small drum; kitukit-enal onom. expr. signifying rumbling sound; kitukkatfi sound of something shaking in a hollow vessel; small drum as an accompaniment; kitukku small drum as an accompaniment; kitukku-kitukk-enal onom. expr. signifying hollow sound. Ma. kitukkuka, kitufifuka to sound (as vessels knocking against each other); kitukkam rattling sound; kifukki, kitukku sounding devices. DED 1283.

Ta. kituku braided coconut leaf for thatching, cadjan. Ma. kitil, kituku coconut leaves matted for screens, fans, thatches. Tu. gidiki, gidiké, gidké a blind or screen made of palm leaves. / Cf. Skt. (inser.; JEG, p. 159) kitika- matting, screen; Pali kitika- movable screen or curtain. DED(N) 1284.
Te. kituku a secret. Pa, kitk- to be silent; kitukre silently. Ga. (P.) kitke er- to be silent. DED(S) 1285. :

Ta. kifupiti tambourine of oval form; uproar, loud noise. Ma. kitupiti tabour. Ka, gidibidi, gidimidi small drum shaped like an hourglass. Tu. gidibidi a small drum. Te. gidiya a sort of drum. DED 1286. 1535 Ta, kitai sola pith; pith; kiféccu, kitéccai, kifaicci, kitaiccu, kifaiccai pith: catai, cafaicci sola pith, Aeschynomene aspera. Ma. kitacci, kafacci, kitésu sola, pithy-stemmed swamp plant. ? Kui krada pith. DEDS 207. 1536 Kui gidri knee-cap. Kuwi (Su.) gri’ma id. DEDS 209.

Ta. kittu (kifti-) to draw near (in time or place), be on friendly terms with, be attained, accomplished, be clenched (as the teeth in lockjaw); approach, attack, meet, tie, bind; kitfa near, close by; kittam nearness, vicinity; kitti clamps (used in torture, etc.); kittinar relations, friends, associates; kitai (-pp-, -tt-) to be obtained, found, come into one’s possession, join, come together; ap- proach, encounter, oppose; 7. comparison, likeness, equality. Ma. kita approach, match, equality; kitayuka to knock against, quarrel, be found or obtained; kitaccal meeting, quarrelling; kitekka to be obtained, engage in; kittuka to come to hand, be obtained, reach; kitfam vicinity, nearness; kifti torture by pressing the hands between two sticks. To. kit- (kity-) to be caught (in crowd, by buffalo’s horns, by promise that one must keep, etc.); kid- vicinity (kidk, kids). Ka. kittu to touch, reach, come to hand, be obtained; gittu, gitu to be obtained, etc., be pressed closely one to the other; gittisu, gitisu to cause oneself to be reached; kitti torture in which hands, ears or noses are gitte {142} citar pressed between two sticks; kidu touching, approach; gade touching, fitting, meeting; match, comparison, equality; gede touching; match; friendship, intimacy. Kod. kift- (kitti-) to be got, come into possession of. Tu. kitta proximity; near; gitfa proximate, near. Kor. (M.) kitti to touch. Te. kiftu to approach, draw near, agree, suit; gittu to be agreeable or favourable, friend

Ka. (Hav.) gitte lump of earth. Tu. gitte a clod or lump, usually of earth. 1540 Ta, kinfan a kind of coarse cotton cloth, striped or checkered gingham. Ma. kintari stout cloth, double-threaded stuff. Ka. ginta gingham, stout cloth, double- threaded stuff. Te. gintemu weaving with a double thread, a thick cloth. DED 1288.

Ta. kinti small drinking vessel with nozzle. Ma. kinti goblet, water-vessel with a snout. Ko. kindy metal drinking vessel (no spout). Ka. gindi, ginnal, ginnil small metal water-vessel (with or without spout). Kod. kindi small metal vessel with spout. Tu, gindi, gindya, gindyé goblet, small metal vessel; ginnalu goglet, cup of bell-metal. Te. gindi jug, drinking vessel with a spout. Cf. 1543 Ta. kinnam. / Cf. Mar. gindi small metal vessel. DED 1289.

Ta. kinfu (kinfi-) to poke, stir, scratch (as a fowl), dig up, burrow in (as rats, worms), dig, probe, examine, incite (as to a quarrel); kinfal setting one to do some mischief; kentu (kenfi-) to dig, cut and eat. Ma. kintuka to stir a pap, dig; kintal pumping a person. Ko. kej- (kej-) to flay, cut open or cut up (carcass, meat). To. kéd- (kédy-) to flay. Tu, cendaduni to destroy, ruin. Te, cendu to cut; cendadu to cut to pieces. ? Go. (Tr. Ph.) kiddi an axe-cut (Voc, 674). Malt. kinde to cut flesh or fish. DED 1290.

Ta. kinnam, kinni small metal cup, clepsydra. Ma. kinnam metal plate, gong. To. kinm (obl. kint-) metal eating vessel. Tu, kinnalu brass cup. Te. ginniya, ginne cup, bowl, goblet. Ga, (P.) gina metal pot, cup. Go. (LuS.) geené a metal vessel. Konda gina id., metal cup. Kuwi (Mah.) gina metal cup. Cf. 1541 Ta. kinti. /Cf. Or. gina metal cup. DED(S) 1291. 1544 Ta, kinni cover of the hilt of a sword. Ma. kinti swordblade. DED 1292.

To. kinn-enal, kinkin-enal, kipir-enal, kinil-enal, kinin-enal, kinukku-kkinukk-enal onom,. expr. signifying tinkling sound; kini cymbal; kinkini, kitkini tinkling ornament, girdle of small bells; rattlewort, Crotalaria puicherrima, kiniti rattlewort; kinai a small drum. Ma, kinukina tinkling; kinutfuka, kinukkuka to tinkle. To. kén/k6nil in- (id-) id.; kop gon in- (id-) (bell) to peal. Ka. kini- guttu, kinikini ennu to tinkle (as small bells, etc.); gini sound in imitation of chinking, light rattling; giniginike the plant Crotalaria in its various species, of which the seeds rattle. Tu. kinikini tinkling of small bells, a clinking sound; kinilu noise made by the fall of a small bell; ginigini, ginilu tinkling sound of a small bell. Te. giiiguru a ringing sound; (B.) kinakinaladu to tinkle. /MBE 1969, p. _ 291, no. 7, for areal etymology, with refer- ence to Turner, CDIAL, no. 3152, kifkini- small bell; added are: Pali kipi sound of a small bell, BHS kinikinpaya- vb. applied to [sound of] sandals, Pkt. kipikininta- making noise kin kin. DED(S, N) 1293.

Ta. citar (-v-, -nt-) to scatter, strew, be scattered, strewn, spread over; (-pp-, -tt-) to shed, scatter; 1. spilling, shedding, pollen, powder; citaral splashing, bespattering; citaru (citari-) to disperse, scatter (¢7., as grain); be shed, scattered (as a flock), be squandered; citai (-v-, -nt-) to be scattered, dispersed; Cpp-, -tt-) to scatter (¢7.); cetil dust; cintu (cinti-) to be strewn, spilled, trickle, be destroyed; scatter, strew, spill, sprinkle, destroy; cintu (cinti-) to cast, scatter. Ma. citar dust; citaguka to be scattered, spilt; citarikka‘to spill, scatter; cintuka, cinnuka to be spilt, scattered; cinnikka to scatter. Ko. kid- (kidy-) to sprinkle from ends of fingers. To. kid- (kidy-) to sprinkle. Ka. kedar, kedaru, cadaru, cedaru to be scattered about, be dishevelled, disperse; scatter or throw or toss about; cadarisu to scatter, disperse; kedar state of being scattered, etc.; kedari she whose hair is dishevelled, kedayike state of being dishévelled. Tu. kedaruni to break out, burst forth; cadaruni to be scattered, dispersed; cadaravuni, cadaripuni to disperse, scatter (tr,); caduru, cadaru, cadara dispersion, scattering. Te. cedaru to be scattered, dis- persed, or splashed; ciduku to spill or run out; cindu to be spilt or shed, run out of a vessel; spill (t7.), suffer to run over. Kuwi (F.) sedrali to be dispersed; sedyi kiali to disperse; (in both r = r). Kur, kidgnad to drizzle; (Hahn) kid’gna to rain, spray. DED(S) 1294.

Ta. citar (-pp-, -tt-) to separate, split, cut, hack; 4. rag, cloth; citarvai cloth worm out or reduced to a rag; citalai small piece of cloth, rag; citaval cutting off, cropping; strip of cloth, rag, torn piece of cloth; citar cloth, rag, thin bark of certain trees used as clothing; citai (-v-, -nt-) to be sundered; (-pp-, -tt-) to shear; 7. sail; cintu (cinti-) to cut off. Ma. cinnikka to cleave, split; cintuka to tear (as paper, leaves), strip (a plantain leaf); cintu a shred, strip, streak. Te. cindara fragment, piece; ruin. Go. (Tr.) kiditana to shred chillies, etc. with fingernails (Voc. 676). Malt. kithke to mince; kithre to split wood. Cf, 1953-Ta. cettu. DED(S) 1295. ew _ Mand, kitele id. cital {143} cirai

Ta. cital, citalai termite, white ant, flying white ant; cel white ant. Ma. cital, cital, cetal termite. Kurub. (LSB 1.12) kadlt id. To. kezal id. (< Ka.). Ka. gedal, geijalu,: geddali, geddalu id. Kod. kede white ant. Te. ceda, cedalu white ants, termites. Kol. (Kin.) ceda, (Haig) sédal white ant. Nk. gedal id. Konda seda white ants. DED(S, N) 1296. 1549 Konda kiteri maran castor oil plant. Rui (K.) keri id. Kuwi (Su.) kiepi marnu id.; (F.) kieri niyt oil for cooking; (S.) kieni niyu castor oil. DEDS 211.

Nk. (Ch.) git- to penetrate, pierce; gitup- to bury. Go. (Ko.) git- to prick, pierce, penetrate (thorn, etc.) (Voce. 1079). DEDS 1551 (a) Tu. kidykily, kid(y)kely, kidkyly armpit; tickling; k. apini to be tickled; k. maduni to tickle. Mand. kiti ki- id. Kui kitki lomberi, kiti kola armpit; kiti tickling; kiti ava to be tickled; kiti giva to tickle; (K.) kitkorodi armpit. Kuwi (F.) gidori kiali to tickle; (S.) gitori kinai to titillate. (b) Go. (Tr.) kutli armpit; (Ph.) kutli kiyana to tickle; (Tr.) kutke kiana id. (Voc. 747). Kur. gutu armpit. Cf. Ga. (Oll.) kusul armpit, Kol. (SR.) kusalipen to tickle. DEDS 13.

Ta. kittu (kitti-) to hop, leap about on one leg; kintu (kinti-) id., to limp, hobble, stand or walk on toes of one foot or both, go on tiptoe; kuntu (kunti-) to stand on tiptoe, hop on one leg; n. hopping; kuntuppu a boy’s game of hopping on one leg; kontu (konti-) to hop, as in a game; n. a hopping game. Ma. kittuka to hop about on one leg, stand or walk on the tiptoes; kuntuka, kun- tikka to walk on tiptoe, be lifted up. Cf.
Ka. gidda, girda, gira a fourth part; giddana, gidna the fourth part of a solige. Te. gidda, gidde one-fourth of the sola measure, DED 1298.

Ka. kimug state of being crushed; kimugcu to crush, bruise or squeeze by means of the hand; (Bark., LSB 11.8) gimcé to squeeze. Kor. (M.) gimci id. ? Te. cimudu to overboil (Gintr.); cimud(u)cu to overboil or cause to boil too much; cimidika state of being overboiled, overboiled rice; (com- municated by Bh. Krishnamurti). DEDS(N) 214,

Ta. kimpuri ornamental ring. ? Tu. kidumbré, kidumbré, kidamburé a brass ear-ring. DED 1300. 1558 Kur. (LSI 4.421) ximb-, (Hahn) khimcna to embrace; (Grignard) xe6cna to clasp in the arms, embrace, place under the arm, adopt; xeSndna to lie down with (a small child, in order to lull him to sleep, suckle him, etc.); xeOndrna reflex.-pass. of preceding; (Hahn) xemdna@ to sleep with a child in the bosom. Malt. qemde to carry on the side, support with the arm. Br. xumb an embrace, as much as can be carried in one’s two arms; xumb kanning to embrace. DED (S) 1301. 1559 Kur, (Hahn) kiya small wooden box for keeping red lead. Malt. kiya small wooden box. DEDS 215.

Ta. ciratku eruption, pimple, itch. Ma. ciratiiu itch, scab. Ka. keraku scab. Tu. kirmbuni, kirmbaruni to itch; rub, scratch; kirm bafa, kirmbelu itching, scratching; kerani, kerani a kind of scab; warts; kirani a kind of itch; krini the eruptive kind of rickets; kére- karu a scaly-skinned leg; kére-me@yi a scaly skin. Te. cirugudu a species of leprosy which causes Jarge round spots all over the body; ringworm. DED(S) 1302. 1561 Kol, (SR.) kirav- to search; (W.) kivar- (kivart-) to feel for in darkness. Nk. kiray- to seek. DED 1303.

Ta. ciri (-pp-, -tt-) to laugh, neigh (as a horse), blossom, ridicule; n. laughter, smile; cizippu laughter, ridicule, jest, neigh. Ma. citi laughter; cirikka to laugh; kirikkuka to show the teeth, grin. To. siry joy. Ka. kiri to grin, show the teeth; kiriku displaying or showing the teeth; kirisu to cause to display or show the teeth; ciricu to titter, laugh; (Coorg) siri to smile. Kuwi (F.) khrikhali (¢ = r) to neigh. DED(S) 1304.

Tu. (Eng.-Tulu Dict.) girige ankle. Pa, (S.) gita key wrist. Go. (Ko.) girri kal ankle, (Voce. 1088). Pe. kirnji gati ankle bone. Mand. kernji_ gati id. Kuwi (T.) kirli, (Su.) kirli dekka, (D.) gri’ni ankle. DEDS 216.

Ta. cirai (-pp-, -tt-) to shave, cut with a sickle; ciraiyan barber; ciray (-pp-, -tt-) to get scratched (as in the skin); curan{u {curanti-), curantu (curanfi-) to scratch, scrape with fingernail or instrument, erase; curanfi, curanfi scraper, scrapings. Ma. cira shaving; a grater, scraper for coco-nuts (also cirava); cirekka to shave, scrape; cireppu shaving, scraping; cirayan a shaved person; cirampan the god of barbers; cirakuka, cura- kuka to grate; cirantuka, curanfuka to scratch, scrape; cikkuka, cikayuka to scratch (as fowls). Ko, kekarv- (kekart-), kekrv- (kekrt-) to scratch lightly (to wake a person), (cattle) paw the ground before fighting, (animal) digs. To, kerf- (kert-) to scratch, egg on; kok- (k6ky-) (buffalo) scratches itself against rock. Ka. kere to shave, scrape, scratch; kerakalu scrapings of burnt rice from a pot; (Gowda) kE«rt to scratch, kiri to shave, keranfu to dig with the nails, scratch; gere a scratch as kirfici [144] with the fingernails. Tu. kerepuni to scrape, polish; kereficuni, keranfuni to scratch the ground (as a fowl); kirejuni to cut as straw, grass-stumps, etc.; to scrape or clear, as the ground; (B-K,) kiresy, keresu_to scrape. Kol. kerk- (kerekt-) to shave; (SR.) kerren to sharpen. Nk. (Ch.) ker- to shave. Pa. kir-, kirv-, kirc- to scratch; kirp- (kirt-) (fowl) scratches. Kur, xerena (xircyas) to rub off, scour. Malt, qerce to scrape; qére to shave; qértre to be shaved; qéru barber. ? Br. karghing to shear, crop down, mow down. DED(S,

Ka. (Hav.) kirfici slippery mud. Tu. kirafici, kirefici mud, mire; adj. miry, dirty, foul; (B-K. also) kirifici mire, mud. 1566 Kur. kirnad (kirryas) to come back, return, face about, come round in turn; kirta’anad to send or take back, return (ér.), give in return. Malt. kire to return; kirtre to give or take back, recover. DED 1306. 1567 Nk, (Ch.) khuriya bee. Pe. kirga a kind of bee. Mand. kerga id. Kui (K.) kirga vihanga a kind of bee. Kuwi (Su.) krlya viha bee; kriya niyu honey; (F.) kiringya bee; (S.) kriya honey. DEDS 217. 1568 Pa, girgira cold. Ga. (P.) girgira id. Go. (Mu.) kirin-, (Ko.) kirgg-, (M.) kidang- to be cold (Voce. 681); (Koya Su.) kirg- to become cool. Kur. kirna to be cold, feel’ cold. DEDS(N) 218. 1569 Konda (BB) girli, in: jéta girli cricket. Pe. girngel id. Mand. girngel id. Kuwi (T-) girngili, Isr.) krifgeli id. DEDS 219.

Ta. kil (kirp-, kirr-) to be able; kirpu, kerpu strength, power, ability, workmanship, action, act; kirgal being able to do. Ma. kelpu strength, power; kelpar the mighty. ? Ko. icg- (gicgy-) to be a fit or deserving person, aaa Po. kis- (kid-) to be able; ? kel o-k old man; kelo€é old woman (or these two with

Ta. cil some, few, small, slight; cilar some, a few persons; cila some, a few; cilku (cilki-) to become few, dwindle, cillai humble- ness, meanness; cirpam fewness, shortness (as of duration); cinmai smallness, fewness. Ma, cila some, several. Ko. kil gaf- (gac-) to be too small for one’s years. To. kisf a few. Ka. kela some, several, a few; kelabar some persons; kelavu some or a few things; a few. Tu. kela, kelavu some, few, several. Kor. (O.) killi small. Te. cilipi small, slight, despicable; cilupa slight, little. Kol. (Br.) cil-elka mouse. Cf. 1594 Ta. ciru. DED(N) 1308.
Ta. kili fear, fright. Ma. killu doubt. Ko. gily extreme fear. Ka. gili fear. Tu. gilku, gilki fear, trembling. Te, gili fear, terror. DED 1309.
Tu. kilefiji, (BRR) kelefiji a fly. Kor. (O.) kelengi, (T.) kelngi house-fly. DED(S) 1310. kil-kill-enal

Ta. cilai (-pp-, -tt-) to sound, resound, roar, twang; rage, be angry; n. sound, roar, bellow, twang; cil sound, noise; cill-enal onom. expr. of shrill sound; cilampu (cilampi-) to sound, make a tinkling noise, echo; 7. sound, noise, resonance; tinkling anklets; cilampal sound of a lute; a chatterer; cilucilu (-pp-, -tt-) to sound (as in frying), talk with- out restraint, make a hissing noise; cilumpu (cilumpi-) to sound; (Tinn.) celatke a tiny bell. Ma. cila, cilappu, cileppu ringing sound; . cilekka to rattle, tinkle, chatter, chirp, bark; cilampu foot-trinket filled with pebbles for tinkling, worn by dancers; cilampuka to tinkle, be out of tune; kal-cilahku anklet. Ko. kile- (kilc-) to utter shrill cry of joy; 2 gel id- (it-) (bullock) makes noise when it sees another bullock and prepares to fight; jelk anklet with bells (or with 2572). To. kis- (kisO-) to crow; it f6-r xis- to call out buffalo names to amuse oneself; kilk- (kilky-) to neigh; kiel ik- (iky-) (elephant) to trumpet (the verb is said to be < Badaga). Ka. kele to ery or shout with energy or for joy, voci- ferate (abusively); keleta abusive vociferation; cili an imit. sound; sele sound, noise, echo. Tu. kilevuni, kilévuni to whistle, resound; kelepuni, kilepuni to crow. Kor. (O.) kelappu to cry. Te. celégu to sound; (K.) kelayu to rage; kilarincu, kilarucu to make a noise, shout. Go. (SR.) kiliyand to shout; kilitana (Tr.) to weep loudly, (Ph.) to cry out, scream, (W.) killitind to chirp, cry out; (ChD,) kilita

Ta. kil-kill-enal onom. expr. signify- ing clinking sound; kilukku (kilukki-) to ring, rattle, clink; ». rattling, tinkling; rattling sticks of schoolboys, children’s rattle-box; kilukili, kilukituppu, kilukiluppai children’s rattle-box; kilukilu (-pp-, -tt-) to rattle, tinkle, resound with noise; kilukil-enal onom. expt. signifying rattling, tinkling, jingling sound; kilunku (kilufki-) to tinkle, ring, rattle, jingle. Ma. kilukilu tinkling, rattling, loud laughter, monkey’s babble; kilukilukka to rattle, ring; kilukku a rattle; kilukkuka to wear foot-trinkets; kilutiuka a ringing of bells. Ko. gil(n), gilgil(n) (to laugh) trium- phantly. Ka, kilakila-gutqu, kilikili-gu¢qu to laugh heartily, titter; kilakila-nagu, kilikili- nagu to titter or laugh from pleasure; kilibili the chatter of certain birds, children, etc.; gil, gila, gili, gilu sound in imit. of tinkling, jingling, rattling; gilaku, giluku sound emitted by a child’s rattle-box; gilake, gilike, gilke a child’s rattle-box. Tu. kilakila, kilikili chirp- gilgilat [145] cilumpu ing, screeching; (laughing) heartily. Te. kila- kila sound of laughter, chirping of birds, din, clamour; kilakilal-adu to chirp, make noise; gilaka, gifka a child’s rattle; giluku, gilku jingling sound of bracelets or bells on the ankles. Kui kila bila noise of chattering or of confused conversation. /MBE 1969, p.290, no.8, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDJAL, no. 3180, kilakilayati, no. 3185, kilakila-. DED 1312. 1576 Pa, gilgila

Ta. cil small piece (as of broken glass), potsherd, flat round stone; cillu broken piece (as of stone), a round piece used by children in play; cilli small broken pieces of stone, etc., shivers. Ma, cil, cillu a small round piece (as draughts}, small-shot, a flat stone for engraving. Ka, kelle a splinter, shiver; kellayisu to splinter Gntr.). Tu. kellu a splinter; cillu a small round piece, a piece for playing at draughts (< Ma.). Te. cilla- petiku, cillapencu potsherd (pefiku, pencu id.). DED 1313, 2107. 1578 Ta, kizahku esculent or bulbous root, as potato, yam, turnip, parsnip, palm- yra root. Ma. kiraniu bulb, yam Dioscorea aculeata. Ka, genasu, genisu, gerasu a bulbous root, the name of several species of Yam dioscorea and of Batatas ipomoea, generally meant for the common sweet potato, B. edulis Chois; (Hav.) genaygu sweet potato. Tu. kerehgu sweet potato, the root D. acu- leata; (B-K.) kirehgu, kerengu, kelenigu id.; bulbous root in general. Kor. (M.) genagi, (O.) gerengi, (T.) gerngi sweet potato. Te. genasu, genusu the root of D. aculeata. Kol. (SR.) kirre roots. Nk. kirre root, tuber. Go. (Mu.) kiginj mafi a kind of tuber (Voe. 691). 2? Cf. 1683 Konda kuni. DED(S) 1314.

Ta. kigam, kizatu old age; aged person, animal, or thing (contemptuous); kigamai, kigavu old age; kiravan, kigavon old man; fem. kiravi; kiratan old fellow (used in contempt): fem. kigati. Ma. kixavan old man; fem. kigavi, kigatti. ? To. kel ot old man; kelot old woman (or with 1570 Ta. kil). Ka. kegava, keriva old man. Tu. kiru ancient, old. DED(S) 1315. . 1580 7a. kigaan curd. Ka. kagal butter- milk, curds. Te. kinu, (B.; comm. by K.) kranu thick buttermilk. / Cf. Skt. kilafa-, kilala- (Turner, CDIAL, no. 3181). DEDS (N) 221. _ 4581 Ta. kigi Cv-, -nt-) to be torn, rent in shreds, be scratched, give way, split, be defeated, perish; (-pp-, -tt-) to tear, rend, split, cut, rip up, lacerate, uproot, scratch; n. piece of cloth torn off, gold or other valuables tied up in a piece of cloth as a prize; kigiyal, kirical rent in cloth, tattered garment; kigivu rent, tear; kir (-v-, -nt-) to tend, tear, split, destroy, dig, be uprooted. Ma. kiri piece of cloth containing a prize or present; kirikka to lacerate, rend. Ko. kig- (kirt-) to pluck up, pull (teeth), seize forcibly, go away in a hurry; kit- (kity-) to tear with the teeth; kiv- (kivd-) to become torn; (kivt-) to tear. To. kiff- (ki8t-) to pluck out by roots, rape. Ka. ki (kigt-) to draw or pull out, pluck up or out, uproot, pull off, rob; kittu to pluck out or up. Tu. (B-K.) giri, sili to loosen; ? (Manner) kilekuni, kelekuni to draw out, pull. Vk. (Ch.) ki- to pluck. Cf. 1622 Ta. kil and 1624 Ta. kiru. DED(S, N) 1316.

Go. (Mu.) kille upper part of comb (Voc. 697). Konda (BB) kira- to comb; kirpa a comb; (K.) kilpa a variety of comb. Pe. gir- (-t-) to comb. ? Kui kulpa (kult-) to separate, take out weeds, comb the hair. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) kin- (-h-), (P.) kif- Cit) to comb; (I.) kirha-kodali, (S.) kinha koddinai to comb (oneself). 7? Cf. 1588 Ta. kilai. DEDS 210.

Ta. kilar (-v-, -nt-) to rise, ascend, emerge, shoot up, increase, shine, be con- spicuous, be exalted, be roused; n. light; kilarcei rising, zeal, growth; kilartti rising in tumult; kilarttu (kilartti-) to raise up, fill; kilampu (kilampi-) to rise in air or water, shoot up, sprout, emerge from obscurity, prosper, be stirred, increase; kilappu (kilappi-) to raise, excite, rouse; #. raising, rousing, kilappam ising, swelling; kilai a sprout, shoot, bud, branch; (-pp-, -tt-) 0 grow thick or close, abound, appear. Ma. kilaruka to tise, grow high, burst (as a floor not smeared with cowdung); kilar splendour; kilarcca rising; kilarttuka to raise, make high; kilaruka to rise (as dust); kilampuka to rise, bear a grudge; kilukka to grow up, sprout; kilunnu a shoot; kilurkka to shoot, bud again; kila bud, sprout; kiliccal bud. Ka, kelar to gape, open, expand, extend, blossom, appear, rise; keralu to increase (as pain, etc.), spread (as a sore), Te. keralu to increase, rise, swell; (K.) genayu to increase, flourish. Pe. gil- Gt-) eaves) to sprout. Kui kl&ja (kiéji-), (P.) kelja (kelji-) to be distended (udder), be ready for milking; kripa (krit-) to swell; n. inflation, expansion. DED(S) 1317: (1584 Ta. kili, killai parrot. Ma. kili. Ko. kily vaky. To. kily. Ka. gili, gini. Kod. gini. Tu. gili, gini. Te. ciluka. Pa. kil (pl. kifkul). Ga. (S.) killin (pl. kilkil). DED(N) 1318.
Ta. kilificil bivalve, mussel, oyster- shell. Ma. kilifici a shellfish, (DCV) kitifnil oyster shell. DED(S) 1319. :

Ta. cilumpu, celimpu verdigris;, kal- impu id., rust, blemish. Ma. kilarkka to be corroded; kilarppu, kilavu, kalimpu verdigris: kilaccal corrosion. Ko, kilm(b) verdigris. Ka. kilubu to get corroded, be covered with verdigris; kilubu, kilabu, kilibu, kilumbu, kiluvu verdigris, rust. Tu. kilimbu, kilubu, kilbu, kilubu, kilumbu, kilumu, kilembi, kilembu rust, verdigris, corrosion; (B-K.) kanengu, kalefgu rust, verdigris. Kor. (O.) kiluvai 1146} kelbu green rust on copper. Te. cilumu, kilumu verdigris. Ga. (S., LSB 20.5) kilugu id. Konda kilin offensive smell; corrosion. Kuwi (F.) kilmt rust. DED(S, N) 1320.

Ta. kiluvai Indian balm of gilead, Balsamodendron Berryi: hill balsam tree, B. caudatum. Ma, (DCV)_ kiliva Madras balsam tree [i.e. B. Berryi]. DEDS 222. 1588 Ta, kilai (-pp- -tt-) to dig up, stir, scratch up (as fowls), root up (as pigs), burrow into (as roots), excavate; (-v-, -nt-) to pry out (as a thorn from the flesh with a needle); kilaippu digging, scratching, burrow- ing; kilaru (kilari-) to poke, ransack, dig up, disturb, stir (as with a ladle), probe, disclose; killu (killi-) to dig out, scoop. Ma, kila digging, dug place, mudwall; kilekka to dig up, work with spade, raise mud or earth; kilappikka to cause to dig; cilluka to scratch (as fowls). To. kily- (kilc-) to ladle out (food). Kod. kala- (kalap-, kalat-) to dig. Tu. kilekuni to stir, move, irritate, provoke. Te. kelaku, kelahku, kelanu to stir, mix; kellagincu to stir up, loosen, dibble, enrage, exasperate.

Ta. killu (killi-) to pinch with the thumb and fingers, nip, pluck; 1. pinching, nipping. Ma. killuka to nip, pinch, pluck; killal pinching, a pinch. To, kity- (kilc-) to pinch with fingernails. Ke. gindu to pinch with the nails. Tu. kinkuni, kinkuni (n, not fi, before k) to pinch. Te. gillu to pinch, nip with the nails; m. a pinch; gilludu pinch- ing, a pinch. Mand. krik- (-t-) to pluck (flower). DED(S) 1322.

Ta. kiravu (kiravi-) to cry (as a peacock); kirakirav-enal onom. expr. signify- ing calling repeatedly, crying sound as of a peacock. Ko, kiry- (kire-) to shout; kiry a shout. To. kire- (kiré-) to shout. Ka. kiracu, kiricu, kipucu, kircu to cry, shout, scream, kiracuvike crying, etc.; kiruguttu, kirguffu to cry, scream; kiru, cir, ciru to scream, cry out; giru, ciru, jiru screaming, shrilling, buzzing. Tu. kirottuni to roar, as an elephant. Te. ciru, (K.) ciru to call; cirudu calling. Kui kihpa (kiht-) to shout, cry out. Kuwi kiry- Cit-) (Ist.) to shriek, moan with pain, (T.) shout. Br. kiréng abuse; k. tining to abuse (or with 1597 Ta. cirukku). DED(S) 1323.
Ma. ciri, cira, cixi_ lip; kiri corner of the mouth. Kod. kiri lip. DED 1324.
Ka. kirige a small sire worn by girls. Tu. kirigi, kirige, girigi the garment worn by a girl. DEDS 223.

Ta. kiriccu creaking sound; kiriccan watchman’s rattle, child’s rattle. Ma, kirayuka to creak (as a door); kirikirukka creaking, noise of writing on olas; kirukira rustling, rattling noise; kirukirukka to rustle, rattle. Ko, gitk clapper for scaring birds. Ka. kira, kiraku, kiriki, kiru, giraku, giriki, giruku, giru, git creaking: kirrane with a rattling noise. Tu. kirikiri, girigiti creaking (as shoes, etc.); kirukiru kirky a creaking noise. Te. kirakira creaking, rattling; kirgu creaking, creak. DED 1325.

Ta. ciru (cir before vowel), cirru small; ciru (-pp-, -tt-), ciruku (ciruki-) to be small, short, stunted in growth, shrink, diminish, be impoverished, sink low; cirukku (cirukki-) to reduce in size or quantity, lessen; ciruka sparingly, a little; cirukkan boy; cirukki girl, wench, maidservant; cirumat smaliness, insignificance, youth, diminution, poverty, scarcity, baseness; ciruval little child, childhood; ciruvan boy, son, low person; ciruvi daughter; ciruvdr little boys, children; cirampu (cirampi-) to shrink, look small, grow lean; cirar children; ciricu that which is small, young boy or girl; ciritu that which is small; ciriya small, young, base; ciriyan insignificant person, boy; cirral boy, servant, boy or girl cooly. Ma. cirru, cerru small; cirral_ boy labourer; ceru small, little, young, mean; cerukuka to shrink; cerukkan lad, servant, bridegroom; cerukki girl, ceruppam, ceruma childhood; ceruman child, slave, Pulayan; cerumi girl, slave gitl, Pulachi. Ko. kir small. To. kir id.; kit elf ankle (lit. small bone; see 839); sitik, titik a little. Ka. kiri, kira, kiru, kiridu, kirdu, kittu that which is small, little, short, young, insignificant, inferior; (quoted by Shanmugam from Vadda- radhane) kiriyam young man; fem. kiriyal; epic. pl. kiriyar. Kod. kirké, ceryé small. Tu. kiri, kiru small, little, young, low, mean, kirdy inferior; kirtara an inferior sort; kiriye a junior, younger member; kigga little, small; tidu, tidi small; tidipafica dwarfish, short; tiddya jun

Ta. kirukiru (-pp-, -tt-) to be giddy, swim (as the head), be confounded, confused, disconcerted; kiyukiruppu giddiness; kiru-kir- enal expr. signifying being giddy, moving rapidly; kirukku (kirukki-) to feel giddy, be delirious; . giddiness, craziness. Ma, kiru- kirukka to become embroiled; kirukkam dizziness, giddiness. Ka. giri, gira, giru, girake, giraki, giriki, girike whirling, going round; gira gutfu to go round (as the head from biliousness); girrane with a whirl. Tu. giriyuni to suffer from giddiness; girigifti a child’s toy with wheels whirling round by means of a string between them. Te. giragira round and round, spinning; girruna circularly, in a circle; giraka, gilaka, (VPK also) girra, gira, gilla pulley over which to pull water from well; (VPK for all items) wheel of cart. Go. (Tr.) kirkand a whirligig, water-beetle (Voc. kirukku [147] kitu a 719). /Cf. Mar. girakn@ to whirl. DED(S) 1596 Ta, kirukku craziness, lunacy; passion, conceit, arrogance; kirukkan conceited, arro- gant fellow; crazy fellow, lunatic; (Koll.) kirucal obstinacy. Ma. kirukka to be insolent; kirutu, kiruvu insolence; (Shanmugam) kiru- kkan lunatic; keruvu pride; kerukka to ‘be proud, opposed, defy. Ko, kird act of disobedience, doing opposite of what one is told to do. To, kirk violent anger, madness (or with 1597 Ta. cirukku). Ka, keccu pride. DED 1328.

Ta. cirukku (citukki-) to be angry with; ceru (-v-, -nt-) to hate, dislike, detest, be angry with; (pp-, -tt-) to be angry at, detest; m. anger; cerunar, ceruvar, cerrar, cerrdr foes; cerumpu, ceral hatred; cerram hatred, aversion, irrepressible anger, love- quarrel; cirarru (ciragri-) to be angry with. Ko, cirv- (cirt-) id. To, kirk violent anger, madness (or with 1596 Ta. kirukku); kit tot-/kitk @Qof- to become mad with anger (lit. anger strikes him [dat.]); kern gwil- to murmur in protest (for kwil-, see 2152). Ka. kiru to sage, burn with rage or great desire; keral to become angry, begin to rage; keralcu to enrage (or keral and keralcu with
Te. kirufiku (kiyufki-) to move (intr.), stir, shake, stagger. Te. (K.) girupu to shake, move (intr., as the body, ears of an animal); (K.) gilupu to shake (¢.) as the pigtail. DED 1350.

Ko. kirbn leopard. To. kirme-p id. Ka. kiraba, kiruba id.; kiraba, kirabu, kuruba, kuruva, katte-kiraba, -giraba, — -kuruba, -guruba, -guruva hyaena (katte ass). Tu. katte- kurbe id. Cf. 2590 Ta. ciruttai. DED 1331. 1600 7a. cina (-pp-, -nt-) to be enraged, be very angry, become red with inflammation (as tumour); cinappu anger, fury, inflamed condition; cinam anger, fury, war; cinavu (cinavi-) to be angry, indignant, fight; cinaval battic; cinavar, cinavunar enemies. Ma. cinam wrath; cinikka to be touchy; cinippu anger; cinippikka to irritate; cinaftika to be touchy (? or with 2521 Ta. cinufku). To. sinm anger, grudge. Ka, kinisu to be or become angry; n. anger, wrath. Te. kiniyu to be angry, dis- pleased or irritated; kinuka, kinka anger, displeasure, wrath, rage; kinupu anger. DED 1601 Kol. (Kin.) kinani, (SR.) kinam cold. Go. (A.) kinan, (L.) kind id. (Voe. 678); ‘(ASu.) kinnan wet, cool; kinnita cold. DED 1333.

To. kin small. Ka. (Hav.) kinkini beralu little finger. Kod. kipné boy. Tu. kinni small, young; the young of an animal, smallness; kinyavu the young of an animal, a little thing; kinyappé mother’s younger Sister; kinyamme father’s younger brother; kinkana, kinkana a little; kinuru, kinaru, kinaly a bit, trifle. Kor. (T.) kinnige younger one; (O.) kinyo small. DED(S, N) 1335. 1604 Kod. kinn- (kinni-) to tear into Strips (rags, plantain or screwpine leaves). Kol. kini- (kinit-) to break into pieces (intr.); kink- (kinikt-) id. (¢7.). 2k. (Ch.) kinup- to break, crack knuckles. DED(S) 1336.

Ta. ci pus; mucous matter, as of the nose; cikkai phlegm; citam slime or mucus voided in dysentery; cittai rottenness: cittai- kkan blear eyes; cir pus. Ma, ci putrid matter, secretion of eyelids; cika (cinnu) to rot, be spoiled; tr. cikka; cikku rotten; ciccal, ciyal rottenness, caries. Ko. kiv pus, ear-wax; kit- (kity-) to rot, (wound, corpse) decays and stinks; gin snot, dirt, esp. excrement. To. kitil rotten; ?i-nm (odf ient-) snot (or with

Ta. kikkiy-enal onom. expr. signi- fying chirping, peeping sound (as of chickens), screeching, screaming sound (as of birds). Ka. ki ki the cry of certain birds, the squeak- ing of pigs. Te. gika an elephant’s roar; (B.) gigi shrilly. DED(N) 1338.

Ka. gijaga, gijuga the bottlebird, Ploceus baya. Kod, ki+je pakki weaver bird. Te. gijigadu, gijjigidu the weaver bird, Baya ploceus baya. DED(S) 1339. _ 1609 Ka, kifu disregard, scorn. Te. kitu id. DED 1340. cippu

Ta. cippu a comb, a small cluster or bunch of plantain fruits; civu (civi-) to comb or brush the hair. Ma. cika to comb; cintuka to comb the hair with the fingers; cippi a comb, a cluster or comb of a plantain bunch; cirppu a comb. To. ki-+c- (kieé-) to comb. Ka. (Jenu Kuruba, LSB 4.12) sipe comb; (Coorg) sippe comb of banana; sipe comb. Kod. cip-' (ciepi-) to comb; ciepi a comb. Tu. kipu a bunch [of plantains]; cipu, (B-K.) sipu a comb or cluster of plantains: cimpu id. Te. (B.) cipu a comb, part of a bunch of plantains. Pa. kipid a comb. DED (S, N) 1341.
Ka. gibu house, abode. Te. gimu house. /Cf. Krishnamurti, Language 39. 563, for possible derivation with suffix *-mpu from Pkt. giha- < Skt. grha-. DED(S) 1342.

Ta. ciy Cpp-, -tt-) to cut with adze, cut down. Ko. ki+c- (ki-+c-) to cut by chipping. To. kisc- (ki+é-) to scrape. Ka, kisu to make thin, scrape, furbish or polish; . scraping, scratching; kis-uli a small chisel; (Hav.) gisuli chisel. Tu. kisuli, gisuli a carpentet’s plane; gisuni to pare, strip off, shave, scrape. Konda kis- (-t-) to scrape, scratch. Kur, kisla adze. DED(S) 1343.
Ta. kiri, kiri-ppillai mongoose, Her- pestes mungo. Ma, kiti. Ka. kiri. Kod. kieri. Kui kirpeni. DED 1344.

Ta. kiri-ppaimpu, kirai-ppampu_ a species of intestinal worms, Lumbricus. Ma, kiri-ppampu worm bred in the body. DEDS 225. 1615 Kui gira the voice. Kuwi (S.) giya, gita, giga word, syllable (pL gianga); dsr.) giy& voice. DEDS 226.

Ko. kier (obl. ki-t-) loose bosom and waist of woman’s clothes when tied at waist for working. Tu. girpuni, gidypuni, giduruni, gid(u)runi to loosen, untie, break, pull down; to be loose, be pulled down; gidpu loosening. Kor. (O.) girpu, (M.) kippu to pluck. DEDS 227.

Ta. kirai greens, pot-herbs, vegetables; - kirai-ttantu spinach, Amarantus gangeticus. Ma. cira greens, eatable leaves, esp. Chenopo- dium album and various Amarantus species; kira greens; (Tiyya) kira, cira spinach. Ko. ki-r sp. Amarantus. To. kisry id. Ka. kire, kire various species of greens. Te. (B.) kira, kire herb, vegetable, greens. Cf. 1760 Ta. kuy. [Various edible greens, especially Amarant(h)us species. The botanical identifi- cation of the several A. species is apparently still a problem. Hooker’s possibilities include A, paniculatus Linn. = A. speciosus Sims, and A. caudatus Linn. = A. paniculatus Wall.) DED(N) 1345. 1618 Kur, kiba frost, ice. Malt. kiwe cold, cool; kiw-kiwre to feel cool or cold. ? Cf. Ta. kitir cold, cold wind; kittal sensation of cold, chilliness. DED(S) 1347. [148] kiru

Ta. ki place or space below, under- neath, bottom, east, former time, inferiority; adv, down; kirmai abasement, humility; kirvu place below; kiratu that which is under or below; kigdr persons inferior in status; kir-antai-ccikai arrears of former years (cf. 5153); kinar mean, low, vulgar people; kigakku east, bottom, low place, lowness. Ma. kir, kiru place below; under, down; what is former; kig-antu last year (cf. 5153); kigma inferiority; kigakku the lowland of the Tamil country, east; kirakkan man from the east; kigiyuka to descend, be low, degraded; kigikka to make to descend, humble. Ko. ki+- lower, east; kierm (Obl. ki+rt-) monsoon clouds which lie on thé hills as mist. To. ki-- lower; ki-koy the underneath place; ko-d last year (cf. 5153). Ka. kip, kiga, kig state of being low, below, under, down, base, meanness; a low, inferior, base, or mean man; kizatana lowness, inferiority; kizgu, kirgu to be low, be dwarfish; kelagu state of being under, etc.; lower side, bottom; under, down; state of being previous; (PBh.) kerage below, under. Kod. ki+- lower, below; kisli id.; kieda place below, down; ki-l- aendi last year; kippupi lower level in a field (cf. 5086 meppuni and 4269 Ka. huni); kikkiré junior member of a house; ké-ki the east; ké-kié eastern. Tu. kilu base, low, mean; kidu inferior; kitake, kifage a base or rude fellow; kilméfu inferior and superior; upside-down. Te. kri- lower, below; k(r)inda below, down; k(r)indu the part or region below, past time; krindiki do
Ta. kig bridle, rein, bit. Ka. kig bit of a bridle. DED 1349. 1621 Kur. kiya hunger, poverty, desire. Malt. kiye hunger, thirst, appetite, famine; kirwa hungry, thirsty. DED 1350.

Ta. kil (kilv-, kinf-) to rend (¢r.), tear, split; burst (imtr., as bund of an over- full tank); n. part, portion, section. Ma. cil splinter, piece of wood, wedge. Ko. ci+l pieces (of pot, paper, etc.), division of land. Ka. gilu to rend (ir.); sil, silu to split @tr.), divide, be rent; split (tr.), etc.; m. splitting, a split, fragment, part. Tu. cilu a split, splinter; silu a crack, crash, split. Te. cilu to break (tr.), split, crack, be divided, broken, torn, rent, split; cilucu, cilcu_ to split (t.), break, divide, tear; cilika slice, sliver. Cf. 1581 Ta. kigi and 1624 Ta. kiru. DED(N) 1351.

Ta. kirv (kiri-) to scratch, draw lines, scribble, write; . scratch, mark, stroke, scrawl, writing; kiral scratching, marking, drawing lines, writing; kirru stroke, line, por eset ce ge re tee GEICO OOM TOPE RRO Rn se SA EP ATA TR EEE merge ser sanoy kiru {149] kusi mark, stripe; kirukku (kirukki-) to scribble, write illegibly, cancel, score out; 2. scribble, scoring out anything written; kirukkal scribbling, scoring out; (Tinn.) kicu to scratch. Ma, kiru a stripe; kiruka to scratch, draw lines; kirukka to erase, strike out; kicukka to scratch (as fowls). Ko. ki-er a mark, line. Ka, giru to scratch, scrape, draw lines or streaks; m. a line, streak, stripe; kiru to scratch, scrape: gitu a line, stroke; giku to scratch, scrape, etc.; gicu id., to scrawl, scribble, draw a line or lines over with a pen; m. scratch, line made with a pen, scrawling; giju to draw a line or lines over with a pen; kiruku to scratch out, erase. Kod. gi+c- (gi-ci-) to make a mark by scratch- ing. Tu. kiruni, giruni to scratch, wound slightly by the nails; kiry, gira, giru scratch, superficial wound; gifu line, stroke, mark; gicuni to scratch over with a pen, scrawl, draw lines; geré a line, row, stroke. Kor. (T.) kit- to scrape. Te. giru to scratch, scrape, (K. also) comb with fingers; girudu scratch- ing, scraping; (K.) girupu to pass fingers through (as feathers of a bird, etc.), pat, stroke; gira, gita stroke, line; giku to scratch, scrape, erase; gicu, giyu to scratch, scrape, draw (as a line on a s

Ta. kiru (kiri-) to hint at; ? kiri Cpp-, -tt-) to play pranks; 7. stratagem, artifice, practical joke. Te. girupu, (K. also) girpu, giruvu, girvu, gilupu, gifu to wink, as in making a sign or conveying a hint. Ga. (S.°) gitap- to wink (< Te.). Go, (Tr.) gir-gir (hurana) to wink jocularly at anyone as a signal. Kuwi (F.} kannii gitali to blink; (S.) kanka gitkinai to sleer (sic). DED(S) 1354. 1626 Pa, kukondi, (S.) kukandi leopard. Go. (Koya Su.) kukkal id. DEN 18.
Ka. kukkati fork-tailed shrike. Te. kikatimaga id. DED 1355.

Ta. kukku (kukki-) to sit on one's legs, squat. Ko. kuki-r- (kukre-) to sit down. Ka, kukkarisu to squat. Tu. kufikuru going on hands and legs, as an infant; (B-K.) kokkaru-kullu. to sit on the half-bended knees. Te. (Merolu) kukundu to sit. Kui kopka (< kok-p-; kokt-) to sit, sit down, sit on, settle down, subside; m. act of sitting, subsidence. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) kug- (-it-), (P.) kog-, (F.) ktigali, (S.) kugginai, (D.) gok- to ety (Mah) kupki ki- to make to sit. DED(S) 1629 Ko, kik small round basket. Ka. kukke, kukki smail basket made of bamboo. Tu, kukke basket; kurkilu a kind of basket. Kor. (T.) cotta kurkeli a small basket. ? Cf.

Ka. guggari, gugguri, gugri grain boiled whole or half dressed, and seasoned. Tu. gugguri soaked grain. Te.guggillu (pl) any grain boiled but not reduced to a pulp. Go. (ASu.) gugri any grain’ boiled and seasoned, DED(N) 1360.

Ka. gugguri, guggari curling, a curl; guhguru, gujuru state of being curled or tangled. Tu, gujjiru curled. DED 1361. 1634 Te, kunkati, kifka¢i a lock or tuft of hair, (SAN also) crest of peacock. Go, (Ma.) kukur(@i), (Ko.) kukur cock’s comb (Voc. 712); (Koya Su.) kukod, (ASu.) kokkor id.; (LuS.) kookooree crest on a bird’s head. Konda (BB) kukufi hair. DED(S) 1361, DEN 19. 1634A Ka, gungada, gungadi, gufgani mosquito; gunguru eye-fly; mosquito, gnat. Tu. gugguru small insect infesting grain. Kui gungu a large wood-boring insect. Kuw? (Su.) gongara viha a kind of mosquito. /Cf. Mar. guguradé mosquito. DEDS 228. 1635 Konda (BB) kus- (-t-) to urinate: kuski (usually pl. kuskin) urine. Mand. kuh- (-t-) to urinate. Cf. 2128 Ka. korcu. DED 1362, DEDS 229.

Ka. kusi to bend, stoop, be lowered, hang down from heaviness, sink, give way, settle, tumble in, wear away, etc.; nm. state of being bent, sunk, etc.; kusuku state of being lowered or low, as a door, etc. Tu. kusiyuni, kusipuni, kusipuni to contract, kusuku {150} gufiju shrink in; kusuruni to shrink in; kuskuni to shrink, give way, sink in. Kor. (M.) kusi below. ?7e. kudiyu to sink, go down, decrease; kudincu to suppress, abate, lessen. Cf. 1907 Ka. kitlu. DED(N) 1363.

Ka. kusuku, kusaku, kusiku, kusufiku to strike, beat, wash by slightly beating; kusubu, kusumu to wash clothes by beating them on a flat stone, knock down; kukku, kuduku to wash clothes by beating. Tu. kurkavuni to wash well, as clothes. Te. gunju to beat (as clothes in washing). DED 1364.

Ta. kucukucu (-pp-, -tt-) to whisper; kucukucuppu whispering; kucukuc-enal onom. expr. signifying whispering; kacu-kuc-enal, kacu-muc-enal whispering into the ear. Ma. kusukusukka, kucukucukka to whisper; kusa- ’ likka id., to mumble; kasukusu imit. sound of whispering. Ko, gue gue in- (id-) to whisper; gucgucn in a whisper, secretly. Ka. kucu, kusa, kusu, guja, guju, gusa, gusu, kisu, gisu a sound imitating whispering (frequently reduplicated). Tu. guji, guju, gujji, guiju, gusu, kusukusu whispering. Te. gusagusa whisper; in a whisper or low voice. Go. (Mu.) kuskusa vark- to whisper (Voc. 809; for vatk-, see 5310): (SR.) kusai silently, noise- lessly (Voe. 807); (ASu.) kusay in a low voice, secretly; kusmfis man- to be silent. Kur. (Hahn) kusmusa’ana to whisper. /Cf. Pkt. (Avahattha; Saraha 3.5) khusukhusa- to whisper; H. khuskhus, Nep. khuskhus whispering. Areal etymology to be added to MBE. 1969 (the IA items are not in Turner, CDIAL): Dr. *k/gucVk/gucV-:IA *khusu- khusV-. DED(S, N) 1365.

Ta. kuccu cluster, tuft, tassel, weaver’s long brush; koccu tassel; kuficam bunch of flowers, tassel, cluster of grass, bushy tail of the yak, weaver’s brush; kuiici tuft of hair (esp. of man), crest of peacock, tassels (as insignia of royalty), membrum virile; kuficu membrum virile. Ma. kuccu, kuficam, kufici tassel, brush (esp. of toddy- drawers); kucci penis; koficu mane of animals. To. kwisy /kwik fringe (on garment). Ka. kuccu, koccu bunch, bundle, cluster, tassel, brush; kufica id., a kind of fan or chowry; gofical cluster, bunch; gofici a mass; gofice mass, cluster; ? kunne genitals (quoted by Katre 1968.95). Tu. kucci, gofiju tassel; kufica id., flybrush; kufifia genitals; gojici, goficilu bunch, cluster. Te. kuccu_ tassel, tuft. Go. (SR.) kucc(y)a bunch (Voce. 718); (Ch.) kunjar, (Mu.) kunjar hair-knot; (Tr.) kunja the knob in the bun of hair tied on the top of the head; (Ko.) kunja kelk plaited hair (Voc. 723). Kui gusa short hair at the nape of the neck. Cf. 2092 Ta. kottu. / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 3408, ktirca- bunch, tuft (of grass, etc., often used as a seat), brush; no. 4172, guccha- bundle, bunch, clump, bush; guluccha-, gulufic(h)a-, gutsa- bundle, cluster, nosegay, etc.; no. 4174, guiija- bunch, bundle, cluster. DED(S) 1368.

Ta. kuccu splinter, bit of stick, stalk; kucci splinter, peg, stick. Ma. kuccu chip, piece of straw. Ka. (Hav.) kutti stump. Tu. kucci, kutti the stump of a shrub. Pa, kucci stump of a tree. Ga. (P.) kusun (pl. kuskul) id. Konda gusil id. Pe. gruc id. Mand. gruc id. DED(S, N) 1369. 1641 Kur. (Hahn) kuji shadow, phantom. Malt. kuji shadow. DEDS 230.
Ma. kififiu the hump on a bull’s shoulders. Go. (Ma.) kunjayi hump of bullock (Voe. 723); (LuS.) koonjaree id. Konda gunzam, guzam shoulder.

Tu. kofijelu a kind of weel for catch- ing fish. Pa. kufia small bamboo basket. Ga. (S.2) kunje small basket. Go. (Ko.) kucca, (M.) kilca large basket (Voc. 719). ? Cf. 1629 Ko. kik, Ka. kukke, and 1779 Ma. kuriyan. DEDS(N) 231.

Ta. (Koll.) kokkey neck. Ma, kuiici nape of neck; kona throat. Ka, kunke nape of neck, shoulder; gotike throat; gon, gor neck. Te. konkulu the bones on either side of the nape of the neck. Nk. (Ch.) gurunga neck.. Go. (Tr.) gurnga oesophagus; (Ch. G. Ma. Ko.) gurnga throat, (G. also) Adam’s apple; (M.) gurnga neck, throat: (S.) guruna Adam’s apple (Voc. 1162); (ASu.) gurna id. DED (S) 4370.

Ta. kuiici anything small; young bird, chicken; kuficu young of birds and various animals. Ma. kufifiu, kufici young, small, infant; kuiifian boy; also endearingly of girls; kufifii = kufifiu, kufifian; girl. Ko. kunj children as given by god, men as children of god; kun small. Ka. kunni young of an animal, esp. a young dog; gunna smallness; (Hav,) kuffii a young one. Kod, kufifii child; ma-qguifi 2, pr. bull in mythology (see 4780); (Shanmugam) kufifiappé uncle, father’s younger brother; kufifiavvé aunt, father’s younger sister. Tu. kundu a child of Pariahs; (B-K.) kufifii, kunni small. Te. gunna young of an animal; kunna, kina infant, young of an animal; kunnadu boy, lad; (Inscr.) kunn-, ulus (pi) sucklings, children. Kol. ku+na puppy. Kur. curkunju, (Hahn) curxunjil the last-born of a family (for cur/cur, cf. cudda end, last). Cf. 1873 To. kuex. DED(S) 1371.

Fa. guiifii owl; uma gufii a kind of owl. Go. (Ma. Ko.) kunji large owl (Voe. 724). Konda (BB) uma gunji owl. Kui gunji id. Kuwi (S.) gunji id. DED (S) 1372. 1648 Ka, guiiju to pull (mér.), contract, move convulsively (as muscles, hands or legs). Te. gunju to pull (¢.), drag; ginju- konu to writhe, move the limbs convulsively; gijagija violently, convulsively; . violent struggle, convulsion; gijagija-gonu, gijagijal- kutakam [151] kuti adu to be in convulsions. Nk. kuy- to pull, drag. Go. (M.) gunjana to drag (Voc, 1105); (Koya Su.) gunj- to pull. Kuwi (sr.) gunjeri a- to be giddy, weak. ? Br. xunzing, xinzing, xizzing, xuzzing to move in a sitting or crouching position (Bray: < Pers. khaz-). DED (8) 1373. 1649 Ta, kutakam, kutaku Coorg; kutakku west; adj. kuta. Ma. kutakam, kufaku, kotaku Coorg; kufakan, kufavan Coorg man. Ka. kodagu Coorg; kodaga Coorg man. Kod. kodavi Coorg; kodavé Coorg man; fem. kodavati. Tu. kodagu Coorg; kodage Coorg man. ? Kur. kiy4x adj. Oraon; kfirxas an Oraon man; fem. kiy'xni;, (Hahn) kurux Orag; (Pfeiffer). Cf. 2203 Ta. kofai. DED(N) 374,

Ta. kutacam, kutaca-ppalai Conessi bark, Holarrhena antidysenterica. Ma. kutaka- ppala Echites pubescens. Ka. kodasige, kodisigu, korisigu, kurutiga Wrightia anti- dysenterica R. Br. Tu. kudeiiji-mara id. Te. kodise, kodise-pala W. tinctoria. / Cf. Skt. kufaja-, kutaca- W. antidysenterica; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3229. [Holarrhena = Echites = Wrightia.| DED(S) 1375.

Ta. kutam waterpot, hub of a wheel; kutafikar waterpot; kufantam pot; kutantai Kumbakonam (old name); kufukkai coco- nut or other hard shell used as vessel, pitcher; kutikai ascetic’s pitcher; kufuvai vessel with a small narrow mouth, pitcher of an asectic. Ma, kufam waterpot; kutukka shells (as of gourds) used as vessels, small cooking vessel with narrow mouth; kutuka, kutuva small vessel. Ko. korm (obi. kort-) waterpot with small mouth; ? kuck small clay pot used to drink from (2? < *kuftikkay). To. kurky small pot. Ka, koda earthen pitcher or pot; kudike small earthen, metal, or wood vessel; gudu- vana, gudana large water-vessel, used also for storing grain; earthen pot used for churn- ing. Kod. kudike pot in which food (esp. rice) is cooked. Tu. kudki, kudké, gudke small earthen vessel. Te. kudaka, kuduka cup, bowl, scoop, any cup-like thing; gudaka a coconut or other similar shell; (B) gudaka, kudaka shell of a fruit prepared to serve as a snuff-box, etc., small metal box; (Inscr.) kudalu small earthen vessels. Kuwi (Su.) doka, (S.) doka, (F.) dékka pot (Te. kudaka > *kddka > doka). /Cf. Skt. kita- waterpot; Turner, CDJAL, no. 3227. DED(N) 1376.

Ta. kufar, kutal, kufalai bowels, intestines, entrails. Ma, kutar, kutal bowels, placenta, prolapsus ani, etc. Ko, korn small intestines. To. kwir id. Go, (Tr.) kundali a stomach of ruminants (Voce. 740). DED 77.

Ta. kufalai long cylindrical basket of palmleaf, ring of wickerwork for a well, cover of palmleaf carried as protection against rain. Ka. (Gowda, p.102) kudpu small basket. Tu. kudupu a strainer made of wicker- work. Pa. (S.) kudp- (kudt-) to weave baskets, etc. out of bamboo. Ga. (P.) kurp- (kurt-) to plait split bamboos to make baskets; ? (Oll.) kurutp- (kuruft-), (S.) kurutk- (kuruft-), (S.3) kurudp- to spin thread. Kur. (Hahn) kudna to plait, braid, twist. DEDS (N) 232, DED 1488.

Ta. kuti (-pp-, -tt-) to drink, inhale; n. drinking, beverage, drunkenness; kufiyan drunkard. Ma, kuti drinking, water drunk after meals, soaking; kutikka to drink, swallow; kufippikka to give to drink, soak; kuftiyan drunkard. Ko. kury- (kure-) to drink (only in: uc kury- to drink urine, ie. to be humbled). To. kudt- (only 2nd stem) to drink (in song; < Badaga or Ta.). Ka. kudi to drink, inhale; 2. drinking; kudisu to cause to drink; kudika, kudaka drinker, drunkard; kudita, kudata drinking, a draught; kudu, kudiyuvike drinking. Kod. kudi- (kudip-, kudic-) to drink. Tu. kudcuni to drink excessively, swallow liquor; kudcelu, kudi- celu drunkenness; kudcele, kudicele drunkard. Te, kuducu to eat, suck, drink, enjoy, suffer: kudupu to feed, suckle, cause to eat, enjoy, or suffer; 7. eating, food, enjoying, suffering; kudupari one who eats, enjoys, or suffers; kudi right, right-hand; kuditi the washings of rice, split pulse, etc., used as a drink for cattle. Cf. 1658 Ko. gurakn. / Cf. Skt. kuti- intoxicating liquor. DED(S) 1378.

Ta. kuti house, abode, home, family, lineage, town, tenants; kufikai hut made of leaves, temple; kufical hut; kuficai; kufifiai small hut, cottage; kutimai family, lineage, allegiance (as of subjects to their sovereign), servitude; kutiy-al tenant; kufiyilar tenants; kufil hut, shed, abode; kutafkar hut, cottage; katumpu_ relations. Ma, kufi house, hut, family, wife, tribe; kutima the body of landholders, tenantry; kufiyan slaves (e.g. in Coorg); kutiyan inhabitant, subject, tenant; kufififil hut, thatch; kutil hut, outhouse near palace for menials. Ko. kurjl shed, bathroom of Kota house; kurm family; kudl front room of house; kurl hut; gury temple. To. kwis shed for small calves; kug room (in dairy or house); kuds outer room of dairy, in: kuds was fireplace in outer room of lowest grade of dairies (cf. 2857), kudg mony bell(s) in outer section of tis dairy, used on non- sacred buffaloes (cf. 4672); kury Hindu temple; ?kwidy a family of children. Ka. kudiya, kudu ‘dra, farmer; gudi house, temple; gudil, gudalu, gudisalu, gudasalu, gudasala, etc. hut with a thatched roof. Kod. kudi family of servants living in one hut; kudié man of toddy-tapper caste. Tu. gudi small pagoda or shrine; gudisaly, gudisilu, gudsilu, gudicilu hut, shed. Te. kotika hamlet; gudi temple; gudise hut, cottage, hovel. Kol. (SR) gudi temple. Pa. gudi temple, village resthouse. Ga. (Ofl.) gudi temple. Go, (Ko.) kurma hut, outhouse: (Ma.) kurma menstruation; (Grigson) kurma lon menstruation hut (Voce. 782,
Ta. kutifiai rock horned-owl, Bubo bengalensis (cf. 2200 Ta. kottan id). Te, gudla-gttba an owl. / Cf. Mar. ghudgé owl. DED 1380.

Ko. gurakn noise of drinking so that — water plops inside. To. kurk one swallow of water. Ka. kutuku, gufaku, gutuku a gulp, draught, morsel; food; kutukisu, gufukisu to gulp; gutukkane with a single gulp; gutti a mixture of medicine of such a quantity as can easily be swallowed by babies. Tu. gutuka, gutuku a single gulp, draught; gudumbu gulp- ing. Te. gutaka a single swallow or gulp; gutuku, gutukku, gudukku the sound of swallowing; gutagufa the sound made in drinking water rapidly. Ga. (S.*) gutuka an act of swallowing (< Te.). Cf. 1654 Ta. kufi. {MBE 1969, pp. 296-7, no. 43, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 4481, *ghutt- (Pkt. ghu/ot¢- to drink, and NIA forms). DED(S) 1381.

Ta. kutukutu (-pp-, -tt-) to rumble, rattle; kutukuta, kujukuti hookah (as pro- ducing a gurgle); kufukutuppai anything making a rattling sound, small tambourine, wooden clapper; kutukutukkai ripe coconut in which the kernel rattles; kutukuf-enal expr. signifying gurgling, rattling sound. Ma. kuftukufa onomatop. descriptive of guggling, rumbling noise. Ko. gurg- (gurgy-) to thunder; gurg thunder; guy gur in- (id-) (water) runs with a guigle: gor gor in- (id-) to make noise (stone rattled in pot, bee buzzing about in pot, any swift movement, as of flying, of movement in a narrow place, of train in tunnel). Yo, kudx- (kudxy-) (stomach, thunder) rumbles, (buffalo, man) makes stamping noise in running, (flies) buzz, (fire) crackles; kuds noise of walking; kudir--- (in-) noise of god Koentéw opening and shutting his dwelling. Ka. gudu, gudi, guru a sound used in imitating rumbling, growling, grumbling, thundering, or roaring; gudugu to thunder; run when playing at tipcat or ball, bawling and keeping one’s breath at the same time; gudugu, gudigu thunder, roar, etc.; gudugudu gurgling sound of running water; gudugudisu to grumble, roar, etc.; gudugudi a hubble-bubble. Tu. gudugudu a rumbling noise (as of thunder); a noise made in smoking tobacco in a hubble-bubble, a noise made in shaking a coconut whose kernel is dried within; gudugudi, gudigudi a hubble- bubble; gudumbu the noise of anything falling into a well, etc. Kol. (SR.) gudm- to make noise; (Kin.) gurmcad it thunders. Pa.

Ta. kutai (-v-, -nt-) to work through as bees gathering honey, scoop, hollow out, bore, perforate, penetrate;. anything hollow, ola basket for eating and drinking from; kufaivu hollow, cavity; kufa cavity, cavern; kutavu cave. To. kudy pit, mortar; kudy- (kuds-) to have a shallow hollow (as back of thumb by pressure of index finger); (kudc-) to make into a shallow hollow (e.g. pile of rice, back of thumb by pressure of index finger); (for d, MBE 1974b, p. 44, n. 20). Ka. kode to hollow, excavate, scoop, scrape out, remove with the finger ot a pick (as ear- wax); kodacu, kodasu to remove the car-wax with the finger or with a pick, remove the impurities from out of a vessel by rubbing its inside with the hand; kudite, kudate, kudute palm of the hand, esp. hollowed or held as a cup; godagu, godugu hollow, hole; got(a)ru hole, hollow in a wall, tree, etc. Tu. kudé, gudé a rat’s hole. Kor. (O.) gode id. Kur. khodra, khodrd, khodor hollow (of a tree-trunk), full of holes; cavity inside a tree, hole. Cf. 1818 Ta. kural. /Cf. Skt. kotara- hollow of a tree; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3496, including H. khodar, khorra (whence the Kur, items). DED(N) 1383.
Ta. kufai (-v- -nt-) to pain (as the ear, the leg); kufaiccal neuralgia, gnawing pain. Ka. kode to ache, itch very much; kodata, kodeta the pain of a sore, severe itching. DED 1384.

Me. kufayuka to throw out, fling away, shake extremities; kufaccal shaking off. Ko. kerv- (kerd-) to shake (7). To. kwiry- (kwire-) to move violently, thrashing about (snake when attacked, person in convulsion). Ka. kodapu, kodavu to scatter or throw in different directions with the ku fai [153] kuttu hand, shake or toss about. Kod. koda- (kodap-, kodand-) to shake (body; ¢7.); kodak- (kodaki-) to shake (tr.), wag (tail); kudi- (kudiv-, kudind-) (earth) shakes. Tu. kudpuni to shiver (as from ague); dust, shake off (as the dust ofa cloth, etc.). DED(S) 1385.

Ta. kutai umbrella, parasol, canopy. Ma. kufa umbrella. Ko. kor umbrella made of leaves (only in a proverb); ker umbrella. To. kway id. Ka. kode id., parasol. Kod. kode umbrella. Tu. kodé id. Te. godugu id., parasol. Kuwi (F.) girgt, (S.) gudugu, (Su. P.) gurgu umbrella (< Te.). /Cf. Skt. (lex.) utkifa- umbrella, parasol. DED(S) 1386.

To. kutur sitie++ cooing of pigeons (for siti---, see 2763). Ka. kufru, gutru id. Tu. vie id. Kuwi Csr.) gudr- (-it-) to coo. DED 1387. 1668 Ma, kuftakam, kuttukam cauldron, large vessel with narrow mouth (esp. for treasure). Kod, Ku ftuva big copper pot for heating water. DED 1388.

Ta. kuttam depth, pond; kuftai pool, small pond; kuntam deep cavity, pit, pool; kuptu depth, hollow, pond, manure-pit. Ma. kuptam, kunfu what is hollow and deep, hole, pit. Ka, kunda, konda, kunte pit, pool, pond; gunda hollowness and deepness; gundi hole, pit, hollow, pit of the stomach; gundige pit of the stomach; gunditu, gundittu that is deep; gunpu, gumpu, gumbu depth, pro- fundity, solemnity, secrecy. Kod. kundi pit; kunditere manure-pit. Tu. kunda a pit; konda pit, hole; gundi abyss, gulf, great depth; gumpu secret, concealed. Te. kunta, gunta pond, pit; kundu cistern; gundamu fire-pit; (Inscr.) a hollow or pit in the dry bed of a stream; gunta pit, hollow, depression. Kol. (Pat., p. 115) gundi deep. Nk. ghundik id. Pa. gutta pool. Go. (A.) kunta id. (Voc. 737). Konda gufa pit, hollow in the ground. Kui kutt a large pit (Chandrasekhar, Trans. Linguistic Circle Delhi 1958, p. 2). Kuwi (S.) guntomi pit; (Isr.) kundi pond. Cf. 1818 Ta. kugal and 2082 Kur. xondxa. /Cf. Skt. kunda- round hole in ground (for water or sacred fire), pit, well, spring. DED(S) 1389.

Ta. kuttam smallness; young of a monkey; kuftan laddie, lassie, as a term of endearment; kid or lamb; kutti young of a dog, pig, tiger, etc.; little girl; smallness; kuttai shortness, dwarfishness; kuft-éru a small bull; kuntai shortness, that which is short and stout. Ma, kuftfan boy, lamb, calf; kutti young of any animal, child (chiefly girl), pupil of eye; kufu small, narrow. To. kut short, small (ku¢ xwar small shola, kut- xify short-ears, i.e. tiger); kwitn (oc. kwita+) husband, man younger than a woman speak- ing, man (suffix in proper names) (for restricted use in all meanings, MBE 1974b, p.39, §5.67); kwity wife (very restricted use, ibid. §§5.66, 67). Ke. giddu, guddu shortness, smallness; gidda dwarf; fem. giddi; gudda dwarf, a boy; smallness, shortness; (Hav.) kunfu short. Kod. kutti child of any caste except Coorgs; young of animals (except dog, cat, pig). Tu. gidda small, short; gidde, giddele, gidye, gidyele dwarf, short man; fem. giddi, giddu; kunte a short man, dwarf; kunfi a female dwarf; short; (B-K.) guddu short. Te. sida Beka short, dwarfish; gidasa id., stunted; (SAN) gudda child. Go. (Ko.) gufur mane dwarf (Voce. 1109); (Ko.) ~gundal short (Voc. 1118). Kui gtfa short, dwarfish; gati stumpy, short, shortened. Kur. gudril, gurril dwarfish (of persons and animals only). Br. ghuddi, guddt small; urchin. / Cf. Mar. gidda@ short and thick. DED(S) 1390.

Ta. kuttu (kutti-) to cuff, strike with the knuckles on the head or temples; 2, a blow with the knuckles or the fist on the head, cuff. Ma. kuftuka to pound, cuff. Ko. kuf- (kuc-) to pound. To, kuf- (kufy-) to knock, pound. Ka, kuffu to beat, strike, pound, bruise; 2. a blow, a pulverized sub- stance; kuttuvike, kuftuha beating, etc. Kod. kutt- (kutti-) to pound. Tu. kuftuni to thump, give a blow, strike with the fist, pound, bruise; kuttu a blow given with the fist. Kol. kudk- (kudukt-) to pound (grain); (Pat., p. 159) kudkeng to knock on door. Nk. kurk- to pound, knock. Pa. kutip- (kufit-) to punch, knock (door); (S.) kuft- to strike foot against stone. Konda (BB) guf- to knock with the fist. Kui guta fist. Malt. kutye to nail, drive in a peg (< IA). Br. kutting to pound; kutta bruised (< IA). Cf. 2063 Ta. kottu. / Cf. Skt. kuftayati to bruise, crush, pound, strike lightly; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3241(1). DED(S) 1391.

Ka. kuttu to prick, ache;n. a pricking or piercing pain in the bowels arising from constipation, etc. Te. kuftu to prick, pierce, bore, sting, stitch, sew; 4. stitching, sewing, seam, stitch, hole or bore made in ear or nose for wearing an ornament, a sting, a sharp sudden pain; kuffincu to cause to be sewn or stitched. Kol. kut- (kuft-) to sew. Nk. kuft- id. Pa. kutf- to pierce, sew. Ga. (Oll.) kut- id.; (S.) kuft- to stitch, sew. Go. (S.) kuf- to stitch, sew. (Voc. 727). ? Kui kuta (kuti-) to incite, instigate, urge, rouse, stir to action (usually in pl. action form kufka [kufki-]}. Cf. 1677 Kur. kudnad and 2064 Ta. kottu. DED(S) 1392. kufftu
Ta. kuftu secret. To. ku a secret; reserve. Ka. guttu a secret, one’s private affairs. Kod. gutti secret. Tu. guttu a secret, one’s private affairs; secret. Te. guttu a secret, secrecy. DED 1393.
Ta. kuttai, kuffai-maram stocks; kai- kkuttai handcuffs. To. kuty, koy-kuty id. DED 1394.
Ka. kutte state of being pulverized by woodworms; kufte-hugu woodworm. Tu, kutfé decay, rottenness; kufté-puri a borer that destroys trees. DED 1395.

Ma. kutta a knotty log. Ko. gut stake to which animal is tied, any large wooden peg. To. kuty a stump. Ka, (Coorg) kuttu stem of a tree which remains after cutting it. Kod. kutte log. Tu. kutti stake, peg, stump. Go. (Mu.) kutta, gutta, (G. Ma.) gutta, (Ko.) guta stump of tree; (S.) kutta id., stubble; (FH.) kuta jowari stubble (Voc, 731). Pe. kuta stump of tree. Kui gia, (K.) guta id. Kuwi (Su.) guttu (pl gutka) id., stubble of paddy; (Isr.) kuduli tog. / The items here, those in DBIA 104 (add: Go. [SR.] gutfam, (M.} gutta, [L.] guta peg [Voe. 1112}), and those in Turner, CDIAL, no. 3893 *khutta-, *khupta- and no. 3748 ks6da-, exhibit - considerable convergence and present many problems of immediate relationship. DED(S) 1396. 1677 Kur. kudna/kurma (kuddas) to thread, string, fix on any pointed instrument (eg. a spit), throw the woof-thread in weaving. Malt. kude to pierce as with a needle. Cf.

Go. (W. Ph.) gudi navel (Voce, 1127). Kur, kudd@ umbilical cord. Malt, kude navel. Cf. 2050 Ta. koti. DED(S) 1398. 1679 Kol. guddi a bear. Nk. guddi id. Pa. (S.) guddi black. Go. (M.) gud, (Ko.) gudi id. (Voe, 1115). DED(S) 1399. 1680 Ka, guddu, guddi eyeball, egg; gudasu anything round; gudi a circle, halo. Tu, kudu testicles of dogs, cats, etc. Te. g(rjuddu eye- ball, egg; gudusu a circle, round; godda cylindrical stone; gudi halo round the sun or moon. Kol. gud (pl. gudi) testicle. Nk. (Ch.) gud (pl 4) egg; kanta gudda eyeball; ? gadda stone, pestle made of stone. Go. (Ko.) gudi variety of small bead (Voc. 1114). Konda gudu eyeball, egg, testicle (< Te.); gurdu, (Sova dial.) gurzu pupil, eyeball. Kuwi (Su.) gudu egg (< Te.). Cf. 1695 Ta. kuntu. /Cf. Skt. gutika-, guda- globe, ball, pill; Turner, CDZAL, no. His, DED(S, N) 1400,

Ta. kutfai kerchief, towel, small strip of cloth. Ka, gudde a piece of cloth, used by men. Tu. kuntu cloth, garment, dress. Kor, (O.) kuntu cloth. Te. gudda cloth, a bit or piece of cloth. Konda gunder(i) saree, a loincloth for women. DED(S) 1401. [154] kuntan 1682 Ta, (CTD) kunfu a small hill, Ka. gudda, guddu mountain, hill; gudde heap, pile; gutta, guffe hillock. Kod. gudde heap. Tu. guddé hill, (B-K. also) heap. Te. gutta hill, hillock, mountain, heap; guddamu, guddamu a plot of land, field; (VPK) gudde, guddi field on mountain slopes, a high field. Ga. (S.3) gudiya a small field for cultivation of vegetables. Go. (A. S.) gutta hill, mountain (Voc. $111); (Mu.) gudrsi, gudra hillock (Voc. 1116). Konda gude hill field; gudri hillock. Pe. guti bank of river. Mand. guti id. Kuwi (Su.) gudia field of dry cultivation; (S.) gudika dry land; (F.) kddia paddy field; (Isr.) gudiya field, esp. a dry one suitable for ploughing. DED(S) 1402. 1683 Konda kuni tuber. Pe. kini id., root. Mand. kune tuber. Kui kuna tuberous root, sweet potato, yam. Kuwi (F) pdrdo kina sweet potato; (S) kunna yam, potatoes; (Su. P.) kuna tuber; (Isr.) kuna kuca radish.

Ta. kunil and 2210 Konda kona / Cf. Skt. kunapa- spear. DED(S) 1729. 2077 Kol. gonda man of Gond tribe; fem. gondortad. Nk. (Ch.) gond man of Gond tribe; fem. gondia. Pe. gdnd Gond. Kui gonda the Gond tribe; géndenju (pl. gondenga) a Gond man or boy; fem, gondali (pl. gondaliska, gondasaka). / Cf. Skt. gonda- a man of low tribe (in the Vindhya mountains), a mountaineer, Pkt. (DNM) fonda a forest, ingles Turner, CDIAL, no. 4276. DEDS(N)

Ta. kunukunu (-pp-, -tt-) to whine, murmur, grumble, speak through the nose; kunukun-enal onom. expr. signifying whimper- ing, whining; kunukkan one who talks through his nose; kunuku (kunuki-) to prattle, talk pleasantly; kunattu (kunatti-) to wheedle, whine. Ka, guna, gunu, gona a sound in imitation of grumbling; gunugunu murmuring, buzzing, whispering among the people; gonagu to nasalize;n. nasalization, grumbling, murmuring; gonaga man who _ nasalizes; (Coorg) gunugu to murmur. Tu. gunugunu, gunuguttu, gunugunu murmuring, muttering, grumbling; gunuguftuni to mutter, etc. Te. gondgu, gontigu, (K. also) god4gu to murmur, mutter, grumble; gonagona murmuringly; guniyv to grumble, murmur. Go. (Koya T.) gunang- to grumble. Kur. gun’na to fret. Malt. kunkunare to murmur. /MBE 1969, p. 291, no.11, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 4013(2), H. gungun murmur, etc.; also BHS ghuna- ghunadyate buzzes (of a bee), etc. DED(S, N) 1405. 1686 Ta, kunukku an eat-ornament. Ma. kunukku a lotus-like ear-ring; nose-ring.

Ma. kuntan cripple. Ko. kupt- (kunty-) to be Jame, limp; kun¢ lame; kunfn man with two legs useless or amputated; fem. kunfy; kuf lameness; kufn man with kunte {155} kutalai slight limp; fem. kuty. To. kut lameness. Ka. kuntu, kinfu to limp, halt, hop; 7. lameness, hopping, a hop; kunfisu to cause to hop, etce.; kunta lame man; fem. kunti; kuntanike hopping; kunfitana lameness. Kod. kunt- (kunti-) to be lame, limp; kunti lame- ness; kunfté lame man; fem, kunti. Tu. kuntuni, kupfavuni, kontuni, konfavuni to halt, limp; kunfa lame, crippled; konfafgelu hobbling, lame. Te. kuntu to limp, wal lamely; kuntugincu to limp; kupfi Jame, lameness, lame man; kuntitanamu lameness. Kol. (SR. ) kunt- to limp. Pa, (S.) kiltal lame. Go. (Tr.) katana to go lame, usually of animals, rarely of men; (Mu.) kur- to hop on one leg, limp; (M.) kata lame; kufahana to limp; (SR.) kufal, (Ma.) katal lame; (Ko.) kilfal a lame man (Voc. 824); (S.) kunti lame (Voce. 736). /Cf. the ‘lame’ words in Turner, CDIAL, nos. 3261, 3262, 3941. DED(S) 1408. 1689 Ka, kunte a harrow, the web-beam in a loom. Tu. kunfé the web beam of a loom. Ye. guntaka, (VPK) guntika, gunfiki, guntike, guntava, giifava a harrow. DED (S)
Ma. kunta slave; dirty woman. Ka. gunda servant. Te. gundddu id. DED 1410.

Te. gunda powder. Pa. gunda dust. Go, (M.) gundam powder; (Ko.) gundal sawdust (Voc. 1117). Konda gunda powder, dust. Pe, gunda flour. Mand. gunda powder. Kuwi (Su.) gunda, (F.) giinda flour. Kur. gundna to reduce to powder; gunda powder, flour, fragments, (Hahn) flour made of rice (also gundi). Malt, kundo anything reduced to powder; gundi dust. /Cf. Skt. gundaka- dust, powder; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4193. DED (S) 1411.

Ta. kunti heart, kidney, roe of fish; kunfi-kkay kidneys, heart. Ma. kunti-kkayi cashew fruit without the nut, and other analogies (kidneys). Ka. gundige the heart, coutage; gundi-kayi the heart (in a physical sense only). Tu. gundigé the breast, heart; bravery, courage; gunde courage; gunde-kayi the heart. Te. gunde, gundelu, gundiya the chest, heart, courage, boldness; gunde-kaya the heart; gunda brave, bold; rude, rough. Nk. gundur-kaya kidney. Pa, (S.) gunder- kaya heart. Go, (Tr.) gundur-kaia kidney; (Ma.) gunde, gundru kaya, (S.) gunde kaya, (Ko.) gunder kaya heart; (S.) gu gunge gizzard; (Ko.) gunde chest (Voc. 1119). Konda (BB) gunda id.; gunda kaya heart. Malt. konda id. DED(S) 1412. 1693A Ta. kupti buttocks, rump; bottom (as of a vessel), end of a fruit or nut opposite to the stalk. Ma. kunfti posteriors, anus; bottom of a vessel. Ka. kunde buttocks, anus; bottom of a vessel. Te. kuffe anus. Ga, (P.) kung id. Kuwi (Mah.) ktina buttock. DED(S) 1413.
Ka. (Hav.) kundige plantain flower bud. Tu. (B-K.) kupdige the plantain flower.

Ta. kunfu ball, anything globular and heavy, bullet, testicle of beasts. Ko. gund gal, kal gund a huge, round stone; gund gat a way of tying up a person in a ball. Ka, gunda roundness; gundu anything globular, round stone for grinding, boulder, plummet, testicle of beasts, bullet; gundage, gunduge of a round, globular form. Tu, gundu anything round, ball, bullet; round, globular; gunde round. Te. gundu bullet, rock, bead, anything spherical; gundrani, gundramu round, spheri- cal, cylindrical; rubbu-gundu stone pestle or roller used in grinding things in a mortar. Kol. gund stone; (SR.) rubgund pounding stone, Nk, ghund stone. Ga. (8.7) kundran round. Go. (S.) gundkula péru bead necklace (Voc. 1121); (M.) gondra round (Voc. 1195); (ASu.) gundat id. Cf. 1680 Ka. guddu. / Cf. Mar. d, gid@ stone, esp. round stone. DED(S) 1414.
Ma. kunna penis. Ka. (Nanj.) kunne mari id. Kod. kunne id. Tu. kunné id. DED 1415.
Ma. kutanam remainder, refuse, grated coconut from which the milk is expressed. Te, koda, kodava remainder. DED 1416.
Ta. kutappu (kutappi-), kuta¢tu (kutaffi-) to turn about food in the mouth, munch. Te, (K.) kodupu id. DED 1417.

Ka. kudaru, kudiru, kuduru low ground, a hollow, bed of a stream, stream. Tu. kuduru small island, sandbank. Go. (Mu.) kudur sand (Voce, 752). Kur. kudar low- situated ricefields; (Hahn) kudur nai wet or low fields. DEDS(N) 238.

Ta. kutalai lisp, prattle of children, soft talk (as of young girls); simpleton; kutalaimai indistinctness as in child’s prattle; kutattu (kutatti-) to babble, prattle. To. kwi@il stammerer. Ka, kodalu, kodlu to hesitate in speaking, stammer, mutter with the omission of a syllable; n. (also kodal) act of stammering; kodala stammerer. Tu, koddé, goddé stammering, lisping. Te. kotuku, koduku to speak with some syllables sup- pressed, mumble, hesitate in speaking; n. low mumbling speech, mutter; (K.) kodalu stam- kutaru [156] kutti mering, mumbling; (K.) kodalincu to lisp as children do, speak softly. DED 1418.

Ta. kutaru (kutari-) to become loose, dishevelled. Ma, kutaruka to shake off. Konda kut- (-t-) to strip off, remove (cloth- ing), untie (knot), open (doors). Pe. kut- (-t-) to loosen, unfasten, take off (hat). Kuwi (Isr.) kud- Cit-) to shift, move (intr.); kut- (-h-) to shift (¢7.). DEDS 240.
Ta. kutaru (kutari-) to dig up and scatter. Te. kodaru to be scattered. DEDS 241.

Ta. kuti (pp-, -tt-) to jump, leap, bound, frolic, leap over, escape from, splash (as water), spurt out; 7. jump, leap; kutippu leaping. Ma. kuti leap, gallop; kutikka to jump, skip, boil, bubble up; kutukkuka to take a spring in order to leap. Ka. gudi to . jump, stamp, make a noise with the feet; kuduku to trot; #. trotting; (Hav. S.) gudiku to jump. Tu. guttu a leap, jump; a stride. Te. kudupu to shake (ér.), agitate, jolt; n. shaking, jolting; kudulu to be shaken, jolt. (K. also) shake while walking, flutter in agony; kudilincu to shake (f7.); kudilika shaking, agitation, jolting. Konda gudlis- (-t-) to shake violently. Kur. kuddna to move about; kudaba’ana to make run; kudakudi in hot haste; kuduy-kudur at a trot. Cf. 1711(@) Ta. kutirai. DED(S) 1419.
Ka. (Hav.) kudi turn. Tu. kudi id., instalment.
Ta. kuti, kutih-kal, kuti-kkal, kuti-kal heel of the foot. Ma, kuti, kuti-kal id. Te. gudi-kalu id. DED 1420. 1708 Kui (K.) guti mouth. Kuwi (Su. P.) guti, (F. S. Isr.) giiti id. DEDS 242.

Ta. kutir (-yv- -nt-) to be settled, determined, fixed up. Ma. kutir small mounds . of earth in ricefields, on which the rice is sown and after it is grown sufficiently, is transplanted with a hoe. Ko, kud-id- (it-) to fix firmly in ground, tamp earth around (post), hammer end of iron bar to shorten it, apply force. Ka. kuduru to settle, recover from illness, be set, arranged, fixed, settled, set tight, be reformed, become firm, resolute, prosper, succeed; n. (also kudri) settlement, symmetry, orderliness, health, a garden bed, a rim preventing grain flying out of a mortar, a ring of cord to prevent a pot from rolling over.
