Tu. kuduruni to recover from sickness; kudi fixed, settled. Te. kuduru to become settled, established, be adjusted or arranged, suit, fit, become calm, become firm, resolute, fixed in opinion, be cured, be set right, terminate successfully; m. recovery of health, adjustment, fitness, beauty, symmetry, steadi- ness, a ring of straw, rope, etc., placed under a pot to prevent its rolling over, a support, a rim of stone or other material placed under a mortar to prevent spilling of rice, a garden bed; well-formed, beautiful; kudurcu, kudi- tincu to arrange, adjust, settle, cure, rectify; kudirika state of being well adjusted, steadi- ness; (K.) kodaru to increase, abound; kuduta- badu to become settled, well-grounded, arranged, quieted, pacified. Konda guduti a long pial in the house to keep waterpots, etc. on. Kuwi (Ist.) kudru top of fireplace. DED(S) 1421.

Ta. kutai loop, running knot, button or clasp of a bracelet. Ma, kuta loop (as of bowstring); kutam tightness, elasticity. Ka. kude a fetter; gutta closeness, tightness; gudi to tie the feet or legs, gudi, gudige, guduge, gudupu rope for the feet used in climbing palm trees; guddi a clog tied to the neck of cattle. Te. kudiyu to become tight or close (as a knot); guttamu fitting, tight (as a brace- let); (K.) kuducu to hold tight, tie tight; gudi-garra a clog tied to the neck of cattle. Pa, gudam button. Go. (Mu.) gudam id. (Voce. 1125). DED(S) 1425.

Ta. kutai notch at end of bow to secure the loop of a bowstring, notch at the feather end of an arrow; (-pp-, -tt-) to fasten the bowstring at the notch. Ma, kuta notch of bow or arrow, notch or step ina log of wood or rough ladder used for climbing, steps of well or ladder; kutekka to make a notch; kutam notch. Kuwi (Su.) kudi tip of bow. DED (S) 1426. 1715 Mand. gudga- to blaze; gudva flame. Kuwi (T.) gudva, (F.) gitdtivwa, (S.) guduwa id. DEDS 243.
Ka. (Hav.) kutta straight up. Tu. (B-K.) kutta vertical, steep, straight.

Ta. kutti bottle, vial for oi! or oint- ment. Kod. kutti tall, narrow container made of bamboo or bell-metal; pa+ kutti milking pot. Tu, kutti a liquid measure as of oil, etc. DED 1427. kuttige {157} gunduri

Ta. kuttu. (kutti-}) to puncture, pierce, bore, perforate, stab, sew, gore, insert punctuation marks, dig; pain, ache (as the head); m. thrust, stab, puncture, prick, incision, goading, dot, punctuation mark, acute pain, ache; kuttal internal pain, aching, wounding; kutti iron probe to test the con- tents of a sack, coulter of a plough; kutar (-v-, -nt-) to peck at. Ma, kuttuka to pierce, stab, sting, embroider, dig, prick in an ola, write, vb. intr. of piercing pain; kuttikka to get made by a tailor; kuttu a stab, prick, dot, pain; kutaruka to stab, pierce. Ko, kut- (kuty-) to pierce, prick, sew, butt with horn, gore; (belly) aches with pricking sensation; kut pierced hole, half of cloak; kutl act of pricking, goring, butting. To. kut- (kuty-) to pierce, stitch, sew, dip (something into something), thrust (hand into hole, pocket, water); kut sewing, seam, half of cloak. Kod. kutt- (kutti-) to thrust (with end of stick), gore. Tu. kuttuni to bore, pierce; kuttu a prick, puncture, very small hole; kuttaruni to feel a biting or itching sensation, ache; kuttata, kuttata pain. Konda gut- (-t-) to stitch (as a leaf-plate, leaf-cup, ete., flowers into a garland), pierce, butt; caus. gutis-/ gutpis-, Pe. kund- (kunt-) to punch with fist, pierce with spear; kutkahad- to strike one another with fists. Mand. kut- to punch with fist; kund- (kunt-) to pierce, prod. Kui kuta (kuti-) to prick; 2. act of pricking. Kuwi (S.) kuttinai to stab, stitch; (Su.) kut- Cit-) to prick. Br. xutting to

Ta. kuttu (kutti-) to plant, set, fix in the ground, set on edge (as bricks in arching, terracing); kuttaf-kal, kuttu-kkal stone or brick laid upright on edge. Ko. kut- (kuty-) to build up stones into wall. ? To, kuS&t- (kuSty-) to build (wall of pen, etc.) with stones. Go, (Elwin) kutukal, (Grigson) koto- memorial menhir (Voc. 743). DED(S)
To. kwit open place surrounded by thickets. Ka. kuttur, kuttar bush, thicket; gutti bush. DED(S) 1431.

Ta. kuntali, kuntali pickaxe. Ma. kuntali, kintali id. Kurub. (LSB LIL) kidli a spade. Ko. kuda+y hoe. Ka. guddali, gudli a kind of pickaxe, hoe. Kod. guddali hoe with spade-like blade. Tu. guddali, guddoli, (B-K.) guddoli a kind of pickaxe; guddolipuni to dig with a pickaxe. Te, guddali, (VPK) guddili, guddela, guddeli, guddélu a hoe; guddalincu to hoe. Wk, kudal spade. Go. (G. Mu.) kuday spade, axe; (Ma.M.Ko.) guddar spade, hoe (Voc, 749); (LuS.) goodar hoe. Konda gudeli hoe-like instrument for digging. Malt. qodali a spade. Cf. 1719 Ta. kuttu. /Cf. Skt. kuddala- spade, hoe; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3286. DED(S, N) 1432.
Ka. kunda a pillar of bricks, etc. Tu. kunda pillar, post. Te. kunda id. Malt, kunda block, Idg. ? Cf. Ta. kantu pillar, post. DED (S) 1433.

Ta. kuntam haystack. Ka. kuttari a stack, rick. DED 1434. 1725 Ta, kuntanam interspace for setting gems in a jewel; fine gold (< Te.). Ka. kun- dana setting a precious stone in fine gold; fine gold; kundana fine gold. Tu. kundana pure gold. Te. kundanamu fine gold used in very thin foils in setting precious stones; setting precious stones with fine gold. DED 1435.

Ta. kuntani large mortar, ‘protective ting placed over a mortar to prevent the grain from scattering. Ma. kuntani, kintani mortar for beating paddy. Ka. kundanige, kundani, kundalige wooden rim of a mortar so placed as to keep in the contents while beating. Te. kundi, kundene rim of stone or other material placed upon a mortar to prevent spilling of rice, etc. DED 1436, 1727 Kol. (Kin.) gunti bow. Go. (A,) gunti, (S.) gunti id.; (Ma.) guncili pellet-bow (Voe. 1132). Pe. gunci, gunca id. Kur. gurtha, guntha id. Malt. gunrta id. DED(S, N) 1437.

Ta. kuntu (kunti-) to sit on the heels with legs folded upright, squat; n. sitting on the heels, squatting. Ma, kuttuka to squat, sit on one’s heels. Ka, kuntu, kiitu having sat down. Tu. (B-K.) kutonu to sit. Te. gontu-glrcundu to squat, sit with the soles of the feet fully on the ground and the buttocks touching it or close to it; kudikilu, kudikilabadu to squat down; kundikaflu, kundikundikallu a boys’ game like leapfrog; kund@lu hare. Go; (Ko.) kud- to sit (Voc. 748); caus. (KoyaT.) kup-, (KoyaSu.) kuppis-; (many dialects) kuttul a stool to sit on (Voc. 745). DED(S, N) 1438. 1729 Kui gunda (gundi-) to sprout, bud, shoot forth into bud or ear; 2, a sprouting, budding. ? Kuwi (Isr.) kunda a very small plot of ground (e.g. for seed-bed). Kur. kundna to germinate, bud, shoot out; kundrna to be born; kundrka birth: kundrta’- ana to generate, beget, produce. Malt, kunde to be born, be created. DED 1439. 1730 Kui gunduri (pl. gunduraka) a round thing, circle; round, circular, surrounding; gunjeri round, circular, surrounding, all around. Kuwi (F.) kiindrémi grayu a round hole. DEDS 245. kuppam 1158] kumi 1731 (a) Te. kuppam multitude, heap; kuppal heap, as of manure, mound, multi- tude; kuppai heap, mound, sweepings, rubbish, excrement, dung. Ma. kuppa heap of dirt or refuse. Ko. kip heap of weeds, rubbish; ki(p) parc rubbish heap near house where people defecate, defecation. To. kip rubbish. Ka. kuppe, guppe heap, pile, dung- hill; kuppalisu to heap, amass; kuppu to heap up; ko

Ka. (Nanj.) gumpu forest, thick growth. Pa. (S.) guppa scrub. Go. guppa (Grigson) undergrowth, (Ko.) thick forest; (Mu.) gupsal full of leaves, bushy (Voce. 1136); (Tr.) gubia dense tangle of tall grass and thorn at the foot of a thorny bush (Voe. 1138). Kui guba a bush. Kur. xopp@ shrub, bush. Cf. 1741 Ta. kumi. DEDS(N) 246. 1734 Konda kupi crab. Pe. kupi id. Mand. kupe id. Kuwi (f.) kopi, (S.) kuppi, (Su. P.) kupi id. ?Cf. Ta. (TATD) koppin female crab. DED(S) 1442.
Ka. (Hav.) kuppulu a bird. Tu. kupulu, kupulu, (B-K.) kuplu the crow- pheasant.

Ta. kuppuru (kuppuruv-, kuppurr-) to traverse, cross, leap or spring across; fall headlong. Ko. kup broad jump with even feet; kup aer- to make a standing broad jump. To, kup leap; kup-o-ry standing broad jump. Ka, kuppu to leap, jump, leap over, join the feet together and jump; kupparisu, kuppalisu to fix the feet together and jump, jump over; lift up and throw to the ground; kuppisu to cause (an animal) to jump with joined feet; kippari to jump. Te. kuppincu to bring the feet together or take firm footing, holding in the breath, when about to jump; kuppigantu a leap, jump; gumpena a leap. ? Ga. (S.°) kovk- (kovuk-) to jump. Pe. gum- id.; gup- to climb. Mand. gum- to jump. Kuwi (Isr.) gumb- (-it-) id. DED(S) 1444.
Ta. kuppai-meni Acalypha indica (meni id.). Ma. kuppa-méni id. Ka. kuppe, kuppe-gida id. Te. kuppenta, (acc. to some authorities also) kuppi id. DED 1445.

Ka. gubu gubu égu to stand up in a tumultuous or noisy manner; gubaru noise of a crowd. Te. gubagubalu hubbub, tumult; gubagubal-adu to be in great excitement or uproar. Cf. 1744 Ta. kumuru. / Cf. H. gubar outcry. DED 1446.

Ta. kumattu (kumatti-) to retch, have vomiting sensation, keck, vomit, eject, detest; n. vomiting with loathing, kecking. Ka, kumufu loathsomeness, nauseousness, mustiness, damp smell, decay. / Cf. Mar. kubat rank, musty, mouldy. DED 1447. 1740 Ta, kumari cultivation in hills. Ka. kumari, (PBh.) kummari a piece of ground in a jungle, on which the trees are cut down and burned, whereafter it is cultivated for one or two years only; (Hav.) kumméri forest culti- vation. Tu. kuméru a waste land cleared for cultivation. DED 1448.

Ta. kumi (-v-, -nt-) to be heaped up, accumulate, crowd; (-pp-, -tt-) to heap up, ‘accumulate, gather; (Koll.) kumiyal pile; kuvi (-v-, -nt-) to crowd, press up (as peopte), be piled up (as sand, grain), be accumulated, stored up; (pp-, -tt-) to heap up, pile up conically, accumulate, hoard up; kuvavu {kuvavi-) to be piled up; heap up, gather; kumig [159] kumpu n. heap, pile, assemblage, mound; kuval, kuvai heap, pile, collection, mound; kuviyal, kuvivu heap, pile; kumpu crowd, collection, group; kumpal id., heap, clump, cluster; kumpam, kumpi heap; kilppu (ktppi-) to heap up (as sand or grain); kilvai crowd, gathering. Ma, kumi heap (as of rice), stack,. pile; kumiyuka to be heaped together; kum- ikka, kumekka to heap up; kuvika to be assembled; kuvikka to heap up; kOmpal a heap; kampikka to heap. Ko. gub a division or group of people; gubl crowd, herd; gum a plant. To. kubil herd; kupy crowd, herd, bunch (of coins on neck-chain, or hanging from armlet). Ka. gumi, gummi, gummu, gumme, gumpu heap, crowd, multitude;. gubaru, guburu thickness, crowdedness, thick foliage of a tree or shrub. Tu, gumugumu noise of a multitude; gumpu flock, crowd, multitude, heap. Te. gumi crowd, multitude; gumpu id., group; guburu, gumuru thickness {as of foliage), a cluster; kuva, kuvva heap, pile. Kol. gum assembly; gum er- to assemble. Nk, ghuma er- id. Pa. kum- to heap on to. Go, (Ma.) gum ki- to assemble, collect (t7.} (Voc. 1142). Konda kumba a small heap conical in shape; (BB) kuma a heap.

Ta. kumir coomb teak, small cash- mere tree; kiimpal coonib teak, Gmelina arborea. Ma, kumirz, kumpil G. asiatica. Ka. (Lush.) kumule, kumbudi, ktle G. arborea. Kod. kummi, kumbili id. Te. (Lush.) gum- mudu, gumudu G. arborea and _asiatica; (inscr.) kumadu G. arborea. Kol. (Kin.) kumre G. arborea. Pa. gumri id. Go. (Tr.) gummur mara the kumin tree, Careya arborea (Voce. 1149); (Koya Lush.) gumudu G, arborea; (LuS.) koomooree the koombhee tree. /Cf. Skt. (lex.) kumuda- G. arborea. DED 1450.

Ta. kumix). Kui kupa hillock; kopa hump, cow’s hump. Kuwi (F.) kitpa mound; (Su.) kupli hillock: (P.) kuppa stack, (S.) kuppu anai to overfill; (Su.) gwu hump of ox. Kur. xoppna to form into a pile, heap up; xoprna to be heaped up; xop°rxola, (Bleses also) xoporka abundant, swarming. Malt. qope to heap, pile up; 7. heap, pile; qopre to come together, assemble. Cf. 1741 Ta. kumi. DED(S, N) 1440, DEDS 247. (b) To. kofoy top of a hill, horizon (where sun is at rising). Ka. (Hav.) kobe top of a coconut tree; kobalu top of a roof. Tu. kubalu top of the roof; kubé top of a coconut tree. Kor. (O.) kobali top of the roof; kobe top of a coconut tree. Te. kopparamu, kopramu the top, summit, a turret; koppu the crest or ridge of a roof. 1732 :Te. kuppam village, small village of fishermen and other low-caste people; kum- pai settlement (esp. of Paficamas). Ka, koppa, koppal small village; koppala n. of villages; kompe small village or hamlet, hut; (Badaga; Hock.) kombe Kurumba village. Tu. koppa hut of the lowest grade of pariahs called MarimanSery. Te. kuppamu small village or hamlet; kompa house (used only in con- tempt). Kol. (SR.) kuppi hut. Kui kumbu house (balance word of idu id.). Kur. kumba log-hut. /Cf. Turner, CDJAL, no. 3938, *khoppa-, *khompa-. DED(S) 1441.

Ta. kumuru (kumuri-) to resound, trumpet, bellow, crash (as thunder), have confused uproar; kumural roaring, resound- ing; kumicu (kumiri-) to resound, roar. Ma. kumuruka to make thundering sound. Cf.

Ta. kumuru (kumuri-) to gush out, as milk from the breast. Te. kummarincu to pour out (f.); kummaril(f)u to flow out, gush out; gumma one Spirt, jet or gush (of milk from the teat of a cow, etc.). From DED(S) 1443.

Ta. kumai (-v-, -nt-) to be overboiled, be boiled soft to a mash, be destroyed, be distressed; (-pp-, -tt-) to over-boil, reduce to a mash by boiling, tread out into a mash, destroy, annoy; a. destruction, distress; kumunku (kumunki-) to mash (nt). ? Tu. kumevuni to decay, putrefy (as meat); kume- tely putrid meat or fish. Te. (Merolu) kum{u nauseating smell. Cf. 1753 Ka. kumbu. DED (N) 1453.
Ta. kumpam upper part of the back between the shoulders. Ma. (Tiyya) kumbi buttocks. ? Ko. kipc id. To. kub back; behind. DED(N) 1454.

Ta. kumpi mud, mire, or slough emitting stinking smell. To, kub, in: ku(b) baf- (two calves} take on the same smell (from a mixture of scented plants in mud) (for pat-, see 4034). Tu. gumma stench, stinking. Te. gummi mire, deep mud. Cf.
Ma. kumpi penis. Tu. kumbi id. DED(S) 1457.

Ta. kump-itu (ifuv-, iff-) to join hands in worship, make obeisance with the hands joined and raised, beg, entreat; 7. worship. Ma. kump-ituka, kumm-ituka to bow down, prostrate oneself, worship. Ko. kub-ir- (if-) to bow down, pray; kumite- salutation used by Kota to Badaga or Kur- umba. To, kub-id- (if-) to salute (not used of religious salutation); ? kued- (ku-d@-) to bow, bend down. Ka. kumbu bending, bowing down, obeisance; kumbidu to bow down, do obeisance. DED 1458.

Ta. kumpitu-catti chafing-dish, port- able furnace, potsherd in which fire is kept by goldsmiths; kumutam oven, stove; kummafti chafing-dish. Ka. kuppadige, kuppate, kum- pate, kummata, kummate id. Te. kumpati id. Cf. 1752 Ta. kumpu. DED(S) 1459.

Ta. kumpu (kumpi-) to become charred (as food when boiled with insufficient water); kumpal smell of charred rice; kumpi hot ashes; kumuru (kumuri-) to burst with distress; kumai (-v-, -nt-) to be hot, sultry. Ma. kumpi, kumpiri mirage; kumpal inward heat; kummu_ expr. descriptive of heat; kummal_ sultriness, mustiness; kumuruka, kumiruka to be hot, close; kumural oppressive heat; ? kukkuka to be hot; ? kuppu heat. Ka. kome to begin to burn, as fire or anger. Tu. kumbi mirage; gumulu fire burning in embers; gumuluni to be hot, feel hot as in a fit of fever. Te. kummu smouldering ashes; kumulu kumbu [160] kurafku to smoulder, burn stowly underneath without flame, be consumed inwardly, grieve, pine. Go. (Hislop) kum smoke (Voc. 763); (Tr.) gubri fine ashes of burnt-out fire (Voc. 1141); (Koya Su.) kumpod smoke. Cf. 1751 Ta. kumpitu-cafti. / Cf. Pkt. (ONM) kumuli- fire- place. DED(S, N) 1460.
Ka. kumbu decay (of dry trees or bamboos). Kod. kumbi dry rot, rust. Tu. kumbu rottenness; rotten, decayed. Cf.
Ta. kummayam lime, mortar. Ma. kummayam lime, chunam. Tu. kumaya, kummaya lime, mortar. DED 1463. ‘

Ta. kummi, kommi dance with clapping of hands and singing, esp. among girls; kommai clapping of hands, as in danc- ing; kuman{ai a merry dance. Ma. kummi clapping hands, a play of women. DED(S) 1464. 1757 Kod. kumme stomach. Go. (Ma.} kummi id. (Voc. 770). Kuf kimba lower part of the abdomen. DEDS(N) 248.
Ka. gumma obugbear, devil. Tu. gummé id. /? Cf. Skt. ksma-, kagma- n. of an imp or goblin. DED 1465.
Te. kommire-pamu a sort of eel. Nk, kumre an eel-like fish. Pa. kumra min kind of fish called in Hi. bim [eel]. DED 1466.

Ta. kuy seasoning with spices, spicy seasoned curry. Te, kira a vegetable, garden- stuff or curry-stuff. Kol. (Br.) kucce curry. Nk, kucce cooked vegetables. Nk. (Ch.} kucce id. Pa, kucca vegetables, greens. Ga. (OIL) kuse, (S.) kucce vegetables. Go, (Tr.) kusri dal, ie. cooked pulse; (W.) kusri vegetable; (Mu.) kusir, kusri, (Ma.) kusir(i) cooked vegetable; (L.) kusor herbs, vegetables (Voc. 812). Konda kusa edible greens and vegetables. Pe, kuca vegetables, curry. Mand. kuca id. Kui kisa edible leaves, greens, vegetables, herbs, addition to curry. Kuwi (S.) kuca curry; (Su. P.) kucca cooked vegetable. Cf.

Ta. kuyam (kucam- first member of cpd.) potter caste; kuyavan, kucavan potter; fem. kuyatti, kucatti; ko potter. Ma, kuyavan, kugavan potter; fern. kuyatti, kuyavi, kusavi. Ko. ko+ev Kota man; ko»ty Kota woman; ko» mog Kota child; ko- ka-l Kota village (for kael, see 1484). To. kwi-f Kota man: kwi+ty' Kota woman; kwie ko-l Kota village. Ka, k6va, kuvara potter. Tu. kusave id. DED (S) 1468.

Ta. kuyil (kuyilv-, kuyinr-) to bore, perforate, tunnel; n. hole, perforation. Kod. kuy- (kuyyuv-, kuyfij-) (hole) is bored; (kuyp-, kuyc-) to bore (hole), (fly, ant) bites; kuy pain felt from a fly’s or bee’s sting, a bee’s stinging organ; (Mercara dial.) kuyli bee’s sting. DED(S) 1469.

Ta. kuyil koel, Indian cuckoo, Eudynamis honorata, (kuyilv-, kuyinr-) to call, whoop, halloo; utter, tell; kuyirru (kuyirri-) to tell, utter. Ma. kuyil, kuril Indian cuckoo, Cuculus or E. orientalis, Ka. kukil cuckoo; yb. to cry asa cuckoo; (Kumt.; UP.U.) kigula cuckoo. Tu. kogilé, kojilé, (B-K.) kuyilu id. Kuwi (Mah.) kuhu pata id. /Cf. Skt. kokila- Indian cuckoo; cf. Pkt. kuhila- id. DED(S, N) 1470. 1765 Ta, kuyil, kuyin cloud. Go. (Tr.) kuhkar fog, mist (Voc. 817); (Mu.) guhra sky, cloud (Voc, 1169). Kui kutu kueri mist, fog. / Cf. Skt. kuhi-, kuhedi-, etc. id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3386. DEN 23.

Ta. kurakam myna, starling, Acrido- theres tristis, Ka. goravaika, goravahke the common maina, A. ftristis, or the pastor. Te. goruvanka, gora, gOrahka, goriika, goruvahka myna. Go, (Koya Su.) gérdd id. DED(N) 1471.

Ta. kurafiku (kurafki-) to bend, incline, droop, wither, hang down, dangle, repose, diminish; nm. bending, inclining; kunku (kunki-) to decrease, diminish, sink, be humbled; ktr (-v-, -nt-) to bend (nzz.), contract with cold. Ma. kufikuka to sink low; kuficuka to stoop, bow. Ko. kueg im / kueg et buffalo / bullock with horns curving under chin. To. ku+x ir buffalo with horns curving under chin. Ka. kuggu, kungu, kujficu, kur, to sink, become low, be bent down, be depressed, crouch, decrease, cease or stop (as a voice or speech); kuggisu, kurgisu to cause to be depressed, etc.; kurgu state of being bent, etc., a hump; kuggu to contort, shrink. Tu. kugguni to be humbled, depressed, sink. Te. k(unhgu, krungilu to sink, fall down, fall in a heap; kuncu to depress (tr.). Pa, gurcip- (gurcit-) to bend. Go. (Ko.) kung- to be wrinkled or contracted (of the skin) (Voc. 717). 2? Mand. gritc- to bend. Kui gronga (grdngi-) to crouch over the fire; krésu bending, bowed; krdsu inba to be bent, bowed, bending over, hanging over; 7(K.) krG- to bend (intr.); krop- id. (tr). DED(S, N) 1472, DED 1555.
Ta. (DCV) kurattkam shingle tree (= Darjeeling red cedar, Artocarpus fraxini- folius); kurafkan id. Ma. (DCV) kuraftam id. DEDS 250.

Ta. kuratku monkey, ape; kurasi- kan mischievous fellow, as a monkey; konti ape (< Te.). Ma. kurafitu, (2on.) kurafhan monkey, chiefly macaco. Ko. korg black monkey. To. kwarg monkey. Ka. korangi, koti id. Tu. kuratga, koti id. Te. k()Sti td. kuracu {161} kuruju Kol. ko+ti red-faced monkey. Vk. koti id. Ga, (S) konti monkey; (S.?) koti red-faced monkey. Konda konti monkey. DED(S) 1473. 1770 Ta, kuracu, kuraccat horse’s hoof. Ka, gorasu, gorase, gorise, gorusu hoof. Te, gorija, gorise, (B. also) gorije, korije id. / Cf. Skt. khura- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3906. DED 1474.
Ta. kurappam currycomb. Ma. kurappam, kurappan id. Ka, korapa, gorapa id. Te. kurapamu, korapamu, gorapamu id. /7Cf. Tumer, CDIAL, no. 3730, ksurapra- (‘scraper’-meanings). DED(S, N) 1475.
Ta. kurampu artificial bank, dam, causeway, bund, ridge in a ricefield or garden, boundary, limit. Te. kurimidi, kurimidi-gattu a bank for retaining water in a field. DEDS 51,

Ta. kurampai bird’s nest. Ma, kuram- py nest made by a sow before littering. DED 1476. 1774 Ta, kural voice, word, throat, wind- pipe; kuravai chorus of shrill sound made by women on festive occasions. Ma, kural, kural sound, voice, throat, neck of palm tree; kurava shouting (esp. of women). Ka. koral, koral, korlu, kol, kollu sound, voice, throat, neck; koralcu to call or cry out; kural to cry, shout. Kod. kora gullet, windpipe. Tu, kurelu nape of the neck; (B-K.) kuraly, koralu humming a tune, responding to a call. Te. (K.) krol(u)cu to sing, chirp, read out, recite. DED) 1477.

Ta. kural corn-ear, spike, flower- cluster, link, tie, band, stalk, sheath of millet or plantain. Tu. koraly, koraly an ear of corn. DED(N) 1478. 1776 Ta, kural dim, tawny colour. Ma, kural, kiral brown. DED 1479. 1777 Ta, kural a kind of owl. Te, koradu owl. Go. (Mu.) kurval id.; (Ma.) kurval(i) sp. small owl (Voce. 790). Pe, kurva owl. Mand. kurva id. DEDS 252, 260.

Ka. koragu to become deprived of juice or sapless, shrivel, be emaciated, wane, sorrow; n. sorrow; korafu, koranfu the state of being checked in growth or stunted. Tu. kuruntuni, kurantuni, kurluni to contract, shrivel, sink in; kuruntu, kurlu contraction; (B-K.) koraguni to wane, sorrow. Te, (K., B.) kruyyu to grow lean, diminish, droop, shrink (or with 1851 Ta. kuru). Go, kurumne ayana (SR.) to wither, (Ph.) to dry up, perish; (Tr.) kurrumne aiana to dry up com- pletely, of crops (Voc. 772). Kui krusu withered, sere. /Cf. Apabhramsa (Maha- purana) kurumina- faded, withered.
Ma. kuriyan and 1644 Tu. kiiijely, Pa. kuiia. DED(S) 1357. . -1630 Kur. kukk head, extremity; mother- tuber, Malt. kuku head. DED 1358.

Ta. kuru brilliancy, lustre, effulgence; -Cpp-, -tt-) to glisten; kuruku, kuruttu white- ness; kurumai lustre, brightness; ? kuricil illustrious person. Ko. kurj beautiful or handsome person. Kol. (Hislop) kuro silver. Nk. (Hislop) kuro id. Nk, (Ch.) khura id. Go. (Hislop, L.) kuro id. (Voc. 777); ? (Hislop) kural king (Voc. 771). DEDS(N) 253.

Ta. kuruku pith as of a tree or elephant’s tusk; kuruttu pith as of elephant’s tusk, brain matter; kuruti brain. Te. kottemu the pulp of palmyra fruit. Kuwi (Kasipur) gurju pith. Kur. gudda the pulp of fruits or of coconut, kernel (< IA). / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 4197, *gudda- pith, core, and no. 4314, gorda- brain. DEDS 254. 1784 Ka, gurugulu sunflower, Heliotropium indicum. Te. é) gurugu, gurigi, (VPK) guraga, gunugu, gunaka, gunuku id. DED(S) 1785 Kol. (Hislop) kori antelope. Pa. kuri id. Ga, (Oll.) kuruy deer. Go, (Tr. etc.) kurs (pl. -k) deer, antelope (Voc. 792). Kui kruhu (pl kruhka), (P.) krusu (pl. kruska) barking deer, jungle sheep. Kuwi (S.) kluhu antelope; (Su.) kruhu (pl kruska), (P.) kurhu antelope. ? Ma, kitran hog-deer. / Cf. Skt. Kurafiga- a species of antelope, antelope or deer in general. DED(S) 1485.

Ka. kuruju framework of bamboo slats covered with paper or cloth or leaves, used to put idols in, or by the bride and bridegroom to sit in. Tu. kuruji, gurji a temporary halting place for idols, decorated with leaves, flags, etc.; a temporary shed covered with awning for offering oblations to the manes of deceased Sudras. ? Ko. kurutu [162] gurykat funeral car (-kaf prob. with 1145, the bed on which the corpse is placed under the tall, storied framework). DED(S) 1486.

Ta. kurutu Gn cpds. kusutfu-) blind- ness, dimness in gems, wrong side of a cloth; kurutan blind man; fem. kurufti. Ma, kurutu blindness; kurufan blind man, fem, kuruti, kuruticci. Ko. kurd, kuer blindness; kurdn, ku-rn blind man; fem. kurdy, kuery. Ka. kurudu, kuradu, kuddu, kurulu blindness; kuruda, kurada, kudda blind man; fem kurudi, kuradi, kuddi. Kod. (Shanmugam) kurid blind; kuridé a blind man; fem. kuridi. Tu, kurudu, kuruda, kurdu blind; kurude, kurde, kurute blind man. Te. g(r)uddi blind- ness, blind man, blind; gruddu, gudditanamu blindness. Kol, guddi id. Nk. guddi blind. Go. (SR. Y.S.) guddi blind (Voe. 1115). Malt. qotti a blind person; qotre to become blind. DED(S) 1487. 1788 Ta, kuruti blood, red colour. Ma, kuruti blood. Ka. (Hav.) kurudi coloured red water. Tu. (BRR) kurdi, (Bhattacharya) kurudi red liquid prepared by mixing turmeric and lime, used for auspicious purposes. DED(S) 1489.
Ta. kuruntu wild lime, Atalantia; A, racemosa, A, missionis. Ma, kurunnu Trichilia spinosa [= A. monophylla}. DED 1490. .

Ta. kurumpi comb of white ant’s nest. ?Ka. (Nanj.) gugge an insect in the snake hole. Pa. gurma comb of white ant’s nest. ? Go. (Mu.) garem, (Ma.) garum(i) nest inside anthill (Voc. 1050). Konda ktru white ant’s nest. DEDS(N) 255.

Ta. kuruku young of an animal; kuruman the young of certain animals and birds; kuruttu, kuruntu sprout, white tender leaves of a tree, shoots of grain; kurulai young of certain animals, young of a snake, child, kurumpai immature coconuts or palmyra nuts, fruit buds; (NTD) kurumpal immature coconut or palmyra nut. Ma, kurukka to sprout, shoot; kurikka breaking out like efflorescence; kurikku, kurukku young fruit; kuruttu, kurunnu sprout, shoot; kuruppu sprouting; kurumpa tender young coconut. Ko. kun young cow (up to three years). Ka. kurune tenderness; kurube, kurumbe a tender young coconut; (Bark., LSB 11.8; Hav.) kurule plantain sprout. Tu. kurulé, kurlé a tender plantain tree, a young pig, the fresh shoots of grain, (B-K.) a baby. Pe, kurnja young male child; fem. kurnji. Kur. kurii colt. / Cf. Skt. (Hem. Un. 326) kurumbo ‘nkurah. DED(S, N) 1491.
Ta. kurumpai bow-string hemp, Sansevieria zeylanica, Ma. kurumpa id. DED 1492.

Ta. kuruvi, kurii small bird. “Aga, kuruvi, kurikil, kiri sparrow. Kurub. (LSB 1.12) kibbi id. Ka. gubbi, gubbil, gubbacci grucci a small bird, esp. a sparrow. Tu. gurubi, gurbi, gubbi sparrow. DED(N) 1493. 1794 Ta, kurul (kurulv-, kurunf-) to curl (intr.); n. curl, lock of hair, woman’s hair; kural, kGral woman’s hair, feathers. Ma. kurul, kurul curls. Ka. kurul, kurlu. curl, lock of hair, hair. Tu. kurumbily curled hair. Te. kurulu hair, curls or ringlets of hair. Cf.

Ka. gurule, gulli, gulfe bubble, blister, pustule, small round shell, Tu. gullé, gullé bubble, blister, pustule; guila a kind of small mollusk. Te. gulla a shell, a white pustule; gulaka shell, pebble. ? Kol. (Kin.) gurgela snail. Pa. guili shell, cowrie; gula snail. Kui gola, goli shell (said to be < Or.). 27a. kurulai tortoise. /Cf. Halbi ghula snail. DED(S, N) 1495.

Ta. kurai (-pp-, -tt-) to bark, jubilate, shout; 2. noise, roar, shout; Kuraippu noise; kukkal, kukkan dog. Ma. kura disagreeable sound, cough, barking; kurekka to cough, bark, hem; kitrkka, kdrkkam, kirkku snoring, war-cry, roar. Ko. kurv- (kurt-) to snore; kerv- (kert-) to bark. To. kwarf- (kwart-) to snore, bark. Ka, kure an imitative sound; kure kure a sound used in calling a dog. Kod. kora- (korap-, korat-) to bark; ku+ke a snore; k, bali- to snore (cf. 5282 Ta. vali). Tu, korapuni, korepini, korepuni to bark, roar; korejuni to make a noise. Te, kukka dog. Pa, ktr- to groan; (S.) kuri- (owl) hoots. Go, (Tr.) kuhascina@ to bark, growl, groan (as in lifting a heavy weight) (Voe. 815). Malt. kér-kur call to a dog. Br. xurrukav a snore. Cf. 1901 Ta. kitran and 2122 Ma. kora. / Cf. Skt. kurkura-, kukkura-, kukura- dog; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3329. DED(S, N) 1496 (and from 1538).

Te. gurigi a very small earthen pot. Go, (A. Y.Ma.) kurvi earthen cooking pot; (W.) kurvi earthen jar; kurvi pitcher (black, for cooking) (Voc. 791). Kui (K.) kuri pot. Kuwi (Gramophone Records of the Languages and Dialects of the Madras Presidency, Text of Passages, p.72, Vizagapatam Kondh) kurri, (Mah.) kur'i pot. DEDS 256. 1798 Pa, kurtubi leech. Ga. (P.S.7) kur- tum id. DEDS 257.

Pa. kurda root, tuber. Ga. (S.?) kure id.; ? kurud a kind of vegetable. DEDS 258. 1800 Mand. gurma spider. Kui kurma, gurma id. Kuwi (D.) kurma id. DEDS 259. 1801 Kol. (Kin.) kurra male calf. Nk. (Ch.) kurra id. Pa. kurra id. Go. (A. Mu. Ma. Ko.) © kurra, (Tr. W. Ph.) kurra, (Y.M.) kura id., bull calf (Voce. 785). Konda (BB) kura male calf. Kuwr (S.) kurra dalu id. DED(S) 1497.

Te. (K.) grucci katigilincu to embrace tightly. Go. (Tr. L-H.) guhtana to seize, grasp (Voc. 1168). DED 1498. kulavu [163] 1803 7a. kulavu (kulavi-) to walk, move about. To, kwal- (kwad-) to go round and round (millet in mortar pit, buffaloes in pen), frisk about, run about wasting time. DEDS (N) 261.
Ta. kulavu (kulavi-) to be on intimate terms, be friends. Ma, kulavuka to be bent on, converse with. DED 1499.

Ta. kulukkai, oc.) kuluppai, kulu- mai circular earthen bin for storing grain. Ma. kulukka receptacle of rice, made of bamboo mats or twigs. Konda kolki a big basket to store grain, kept ona terrace below the roof. Kuwi (T.) kolki receptacle for storing paddy. DED(N) 1500. _ 1806 Ta. kulutku (kulutki-) to be shaken, agitated, tremble, shudder, quake with fear; kulukku (kulukki-) to shake up and down (tr.), agitate, shake together in a mass, mix by shaking together; walk with affected gestures, put on airs; 7. shake, jolt, affected gestures, foppish airs; kulukki gay, dressy, mincing woman; kulai (-v-, -nt-) to tremble, shudder, shake, be deranged, upset, thrown into confusion, be dishevelled, become loose, be destroyed, lose one’s heart, become softened; (-pp-, -tt-) to shake, agitate, disturb, disorganize, scatter, untie, loosen, destroy; kulai-kulai- to tremble, be agitated greatly; kulaiccal destruction, ruin; kulaippan, kulai- ppu shivering fits. Ma. kulufnuka to shake, quake, be agitated; kulukkuka to shake, agitate; kulukkam a shock; kulukku a shaking; kulayuka to jolt, shake, be agitated (as a branch under a monkey); kulavu hesitation; kulekka to shake (fr.). Ko. kulg- (kulgy-) to shake (intr.; diviner, angry man, man with fever), jog up and down (like horse); kulk- (kulky-) to shake (t7,); kolv- (kold-) to suffer because of disease; (kolt-) to make to suffer by beating or wounding, mash up (berries) with plunger in hollow bamboo. ? To, kwal- (kwal8-) to become emaciat

Ta. kulai Cpp-, -tt-) to shoot forth in a bunch (as a plantain); n. cluster, bunch (as of fruits, flowers); kuluhku (kulunki-) to be full, abundant (Asher-Radhakrishnan, p. 65). Ma, kula bunch (esp. of coconuts and plantains, also of flowers): kulekka to bear fruit. To. kwin bunch of fruit, esp. plantains. Ka, gole, gone cluster or bunch of fruits (plantains, mangoes, grapes, coconuts, etc.); kKonar(u) to get shoots, sprout; m. shoot, sprout, new branch. Kod. kola- (kolap-, kolat-) (plant) shoots against (one who planted it; in a proverb); kole bunch of plantains. Tu. goné a bunch of fruits (as plantains, coconuts); (B-K.) kile, kile a bunch. Te. gola a bunch, a cluster; gela a bunch. Kuwi (F.) gella bunch (of plantains). DED (S, N) 1504. 1811 Ta, kulai (-pp-, -tt-) to bark (as a dog), talk incoherently; kulaippu barking, snarling; kulavai chorus of shrill sounds. Ma, kulakuld imit. of barking. DED 1505.
Ta. kulai notch in a bow to keep the string in check; bow-string. Ma. kula noose of bow-string, end of bow or arrow; kulekka to draw the bow. Ka. gole notched extremity or horn of a bow. DED 1506.

Ka. gullu loud noise, hubbub. Tu. guilu a great noise, shout, uproar. Te. gollu noise, hubbub, uproar; kolakola noise, tumult; golagola a confused noise; gdlu loud noise or outcry; (Merolu) gulgu to grumble. Cf. 2252 Ko. goel. DED(S) 1507. 1814 Go, (Tr.) kullum, (other dialects) kulum pus in a boil (Voc. 804). Konda kal- Cit-) to rot (as an ulcer), fester. Pe. ktl- (-t-) to suppurate; kilpenj pus. Kuwi (S.) kuth’nai to ulcerate. ? Pa, culj- (pus) to form; culjukud pus. ? Ga, (P.) suluskur id. DED(S) 1508.
Ta. kullai wild basil, sacred basil, scarlet ixora. ? Tu. kula, kulavu a kind of creeper. DEDS 263.
Ta. kuvai, kukai crucible. Ma. kuva id. Ka. kove id. Tu. kové id., mould. DED 1509.

Ta. kugampu (kurampi-) to become mixed, stirred up, be troubled, confused, be agitated, boisterous (as the sea); 7. mixture, liquid of thick consistency, mud; kurappu (kuzappi-) to mix, stir, confuse, disturb, bewilder, disconcert; m. act of mixing, agitat- kural [164] kurai ing; kurappam confusion, agitation, disturb- ance, storm, hurricane; kurappan quarrelsome fellow; kugaru (kugari-) to be mingled, be mixed up; kugampal any liquid of thick consistency; kugai (-v-, -nt-) to be troubled; kurai-kurai- to be confused. Ma, kurampu thickened fluid, ointment; kurampikka to grow thick; kugappam intricacy, danger, misery; kugappuka to be hurried; kurakku intricacy (as of thread), confusion; kuranuka to be troubled, be entangled; kugakkuka to disable, trouble, perplex; kugayuka to be perplexed; kugekka to confuse, perplex by meddling. Ke. kura confusion, disorder, perplexity; koramba, koxe hindrance; (Nanj.) kolku to stir with the hand. Pe. grok- (-t-) to mix and stir déra meal with water; groka turbid. Kui glonga (glongi-) to be muddy, turbid, impure; n. turbidity; glopka (< * glok-p-; glokt-) to make muddy or turbid, adulterate, use bad language against; n. act of making turbid, adulteration. Kuwi (S.) gona éyu muddy water; gono puddle; (F.) goffo/gorro/gorro muddy; (Isr.) gnok- to stir things in hot water. DED(S, N) 1510, DEDS 347.

Te. kural any tube-shaped thing, flute, pipe, tubularity, curling hair, woman’s hair dressed by coiling and tying up behind in a rofl; (kugalv-, kuganr-) to curl (nzr.); kugarci curling (as of a woman’s hair); kugai, kuray tube, pipe; kugi pit, hole, hollow, cavity, depression, excavation, pond, well; (-v-, -nt-) to be hollowed out (as a hole, pit, cavity), sink hollow; (-pp-, -tt-) to form pits, hollows, cavities, sink, excavate, inscribe, engrave; kurici pot, hub of a wheel; kuzif- kai palm of the hand; kugippu forming pits, a hollow, depression; kugivu hollowness; kugumpu pit. Ma, kural tube, flute, women’s hair tied in a knot; kugali woman with fine hair; kuga what is hollow, eye of a needle, aperture in the head of an axe or hoe, bone in forearm or foreleg; kugi hollow, hole, excavation, pit, grave; kuriyuka to become hollow, deepened; kugikka to dig a hole, dig out. Ko. korl, kolasy tube; kol clarinet; kuy pit; kup pit, dimple; kupn man with dimples; fem. kupy; go-b deep hole in ground; gorl hollow between two sloping hills. To. kwe+t tube, Kota clarinet, tune of clarinet, curl; kus small valley, ditch; kue{ ravine, dry pit. Ka, kogal flute, fife; kogavi, kogave, korayi, kogivi tube, blow-pipe (esp. used to blow fires with), tubular stalk; kuri, guri, kuni, guni hollow, hole, pit, grave; kuri to make a hole, dig; kunike a hollow, cavity; (K.?) kulumpe a hollow place where water accumulates; (Bark.) gumme a deep pond; (Gul.) gumma depth; kovi tube, flute, gun (< Te.). Ko

Ta. kuravi grinding pestle. Ma, kuravi small rolling stone to grind with. DED 1512. 1820 Kol. kuri- (kurit-) to start, protrude from hole, come forth, (sun) rises; (SR.) kurtly- to start; (W) kurs- (kurust-) to escape; kursip- (kursipt-) to make to escape. Vk. kur- to come out, emerge, (sun) rises. Vk. (Ch.) kuy- to come out, appear (star), rise (sun) (< *kuzi-). DED(S) 1480. in 1821 Ta, kuru assembly, flock, herd, heap; kurumpu, kuzam_ herd, crowd; kugumu (kugumi-), kuguvu (kuguvi-) to collect in large numbers (men, animals), gather together; kurumal, kuguval, kugtlu, kugial assembling, crowding, crowd. Ka. gundu, gunpu assem- blage, crowd, heap. Pa. kusayp- (kurayt-) to heap up. Ga, (Oll.) kurap- (kurat-) id. DED 1513.

Ta. kugai (-v-, -nt-) to become soft, mashy, pulpy (as well-cooked), be over- boiled (as rice), melt, become tender (as the mind), fade, languish; (-pp-, -tt-) to macerate, mash, reduce to pulp, make soft by mixing with water, mix (as powder with a liquid), cause to melt; n. soft mud, mire; kuraiyal that which is mashed or softened, as over- boiled rice; kugaivu mashy condition, tender- ness of mind, fading, languishing; ktgai mud. Ma, kugakuza slimy, soft; kurayuka to be macerated, kneaded, mixed as pap; kurekka to mix, macerate, knead; ? kutumpuka to rot, putrefy; ?kuyumpu, kuyimpu, kusumpu, kusumpal mustiness, damp smell, decay; kig what is rotten; kfga rottenness. Ko. kolv- (kold-) (flesh) putrefies so as to fall apart; kuev- (kued-) (grain) gets soft and spoils because it got wet; (ku-t-) to cause (grain) to get, etc.; ?ko-b feverish place, grazing lands in feverish river valley. To. kwad- (kwad@-) to rot; kwa+w decayed dead body. Ka. kore to decay (as a corpse), rot {as fruit, fibres, wood), become putrid, grow pap-like, wear out (as cloth), pine away on account of grief; m. anything worn out, rotten, putrid, corrupt, excretion of the body, dirt, mud; kog state of being rotten or pap- like; kogaku state of being worn out, decayed, rotten, putrid, stinking, state of being lan- guished, pined away; that is putrid, etc.; kogakutana putrescence; koraci, korace state of being putrid, impure, pap-like, soft, or muddy; soft mud, koece mud, mire, bath- . drainage. Tu. kureyuni,

Ye. krunku, (K.; modern) gunku, guku to sink, plunge, set (as the sun), die; n, a plunge; ? (K.) kiru to sink, be drowned (or with 1767 Ta. kuranku). Pe. krun(g)- (krunt-) to sink, submerge (intr.); kruk- (-t-) id. (#7); kri- (-t-) to sink into (mud). Mand. krung- to sink; kruk- to submerge, make to drown. Kui krumba (krumbi-) to sink beneath, dive, plunge into; n, diving; krva (krit-) to sink down, sink into, (sun) sets; n. subsidence, sinking into, setting of the sun. Kuwi (F.) kruiyali (krOt-) to sink; verra krilpf sunset; (P.?) krugg- (-it-) to be submerged; (Su.) kril- to be deep; (S.) kldpu depth. From DED(S) 1472. 1827 (a) Ta, kulakam a dry or liquid measure = + nari. Ma, kolakam a measure of 50 Idangaris in Wayanddu. ?7o. kwa+x (when numerated, kwa-w) a large measure. Ka, kolaga, kula a measure of capacity = 4 bollas. Kod. (Shanumgam) kolaka a measure of capacity. Tu, kolaga a measure of grain. Te. (Unscr.) kolagamu water-vessel. / Cf. Skt. kudava-, kugapa, kufapa- a measure of grain or of wood or of iron, ete.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3246. (b) Te. kola measurement; kol&di limit, extent, measure, count; kolagadu one who measures grain; kolfta measurement, measur- ing; kolucu to measure. Kol. kul- (kult-) to count; (Pat., p. 163) kulleng to measure. Nk. kull- id. Nk. (Ch) kul- id. Go, (M.) kolsana, (Ko.) kols- id. (< Te.) (Voc. 949). Konda koli- (-t-) id. Kuwi (F.) kdla a measure (< Te.). Since all occurrences of Te. (kolucu, etc.) have I in early inscriptions, there is do

Ta. kulam tank, reservoir, lake. Ma. kulam tank. Ka. kola, kolahe, kona pond. Tu. kula tank, pond. Te. kolanu, kolaku, kola- kuvu id.; (VPK) kollu deep pond dug or built near the outlet of a tank, in which water is collected before supplying it to fields; kollu gunfa pond into which water from irrigation wells is baled out. Go. (Mu.) kol tank.(Voe. 946). Kui glinju a small pool, puddle. /Cf. Skt. killa- pond, pool. DED(S) 1518.

Ta. kulampu hoof. Ma, kulampu id. Ko. kolk id. To. kwikx lower joints of calf’s leg down to hoof, wrist. Ka. kolaga, kolagu, konagu hoof; (eastern dialects; LSB 1.3) kolbu id. Kor. (O.) kolanci, (T.) kolnci. (M.) kolci id. DED(N) 1519.

Ta. kulavi wasp (Sphegidae), car- penter bee (Xylocopa tenisocapa). Ma. kulavi wasp, hornet. Cf. 1833 Ta. kuli. DED 1520. kularu [166] kuram 1831 Ta, kularu (kulari-) to stammer (through fear, anger, confusion), howl, yell; kuligu (kuliri-) to sound, rattle: 1. sound, rattling noise; kulir kettle drum; contrivance to scare away parrots; kugaku (kuraki-) to prattle, wheedle; kugaru (kurari-) to babble (as an infant), talk indistinctly, crow; kur- arru (kugagri-) to make incoherent or jin- distinct sounds as when affected by strong emotions). Ma. kularuka to stammer. ? To. ku-y the cries or twittering of birds, buzzing of bees, tinkling of silver chain (7S: ir fil xuer the cry of a buffalo bird, ku-y kee fily tinkling silver chain). Ka. kilir, kilir to sound, neigh; kelar to cry out, roar (or with 2017(b), esp. Ta. kilavi). DED (S) 1521.

Ta. kuli (-pp-, -tt-) to bathe, wash one’s body up to the neck, take purificatory bath after menstruation, dive for pearls; 7. bath, ablution, diving; kulippu, kuliyal washing, bathing. Ma, kuli bathing, ablution; kulikka to wash, bathe, plunge into water. Kod. kuli- (kulip-, kulic-) to take bath, kulipéki menstruation (lit. need to bathe); kuli mane menstrual hut; (Shanmugam) kulip bathing; kuliyame pregnancy (lit. need not to bathe). Zu. kulunkuni to wet, moisten; Kolambe bath (comm. by K.). DED(S, N) 1522. .

Ta. kuli (-pp-, -tt-) to pierce (as an arrow). Tu, kulipuni to sting (as an ant). Br. xulling to pierce, stab. Cf. 1830 Ta. kulavi. DEDS 266. 1834 Ta, kulir (-v-, -nt-) to feel cool (as breeze), be cool, refreshing, get numbed (as in death); 7. coldness, chilliness, ague, shiver- ing; kulircci, kulirtti, kulutti coolness, cold, act of cooling or refreshing, numbness (as in death); kulirppu, kulirmai, kulumai_ coolness, kindness: kuliri a fan; kulla-kkulir- to be intensely cool and refreshing. Ma. kulir, kulur coldness, cool, refreshing; kuliruka to be chilly, refreshed; kulirma freshness; kulic- ppu, kuluppam chilliness; kulirppikka to chill, quiet, refresh, comfort; kulukulu intense cold. Ko. kulak in-, kulkul in- (hands, feet, body) feel cool, (mind) feels calm and peaceful. Ka. kulir to be cool or cold; ». coldness, coolness, cold, snow, frost. Kod. kufi- (kulip-, kulit-) cold feeling is; kuliri kala cold season. DED 1523. 1835 Ta, kulir (v-, -nt-) to sit, rest; m2 sitting, resting. To. kwil- (kwild-) to hatch (< Badaga). Ka. kulir, kullir, kullu, kundm, kundaru, kidru, kiidaru to bend, stoop, squat, sit down. Tu, kulluni to sit, sit down, lodge, dwell; (B-K.) kulupu to sit. Kor, (O.) kulali, (T.) kulli, (M.) kollani id. Go. (Ph.) kuls@ stool (Voc. 805). DED(N) 1524.
Ta. kuluvan fowler’s attendant, (DCV) hillman; kulivai fowler’s attendant. Ma, (DCV) kuluvan hillman. DEDS 267.

Ma. gultigulu sound of fruits falling into water. Ka, gulugulu murmuring or gurgling sound produced by tfunning or rippling water. Tu. gulugulu a noise made as in filling a pitcher with water. /MBE 1969, p. 291, no. 10, for areal etymology; the IA items are not in Turner, CDIAL: Skt. gulu- gulita- roaring of an clephant, gulagula- thunder, gulagulayate thunders, and Pali and Pkt. items. DED 1525.
Ka. guilaa stout herb, Solanum ferox. Tu. gulla a Kind of round brinjal, S. Jacquini; gulla-badané S. ferox. DEDS 268.

Ta. kultam, kulfal shortness in stature, dwarfishness; kulla-nart jackal; kullan short, undersized man; fem. kulli; kili dwarfishness, shortness. Ma. kultan short man, dwarf; fem. kulli; kolan young, short, stunted. Ko. kul shortness and broadness of figure; kul(n) short man; fem. kuly. Ka. kullu shortness; kulla a short man. Kod. (Shanmugam) kullé a short man; fem. kulli. Cf. 1851 Ta. kuru; prob, contracted from *kural-. DED(S) 1526.

Ta. kurafku thigh. Ma. kuraku, kuravu quarter of animals, ham, thigh, loins. Ko. korg thigh. Te. kuyuvu id. Kol. kudug (obl. kudg-) id. Pa. kudu (pl kudul), (NE.) kudu id. Ga. (OU) kuyug (pl. kuygul) id. Go. (Ys. Ph.) kurki, (SR. Mu.S.) kurki, (M.) kurk, kurki, (G. Ko.) kurku, (Ma.) kohki id. (Voce. 779). Konda kurgu (pl. kuRku) id. Kui kaju (pl. kuska), kuj(u)gu id. Kuwi (.) kudgi lap, thigh; (S.) kudugu calf of leg (sic); (Su. Isr.) kudgu thigh. Kur. xosga leg, thigh. Malt. qosge thigh. DED 1527. .
Ta. kurahku a branch channel. To. kwar small thicket (with stream running through). Ka. korakalu a cut, excavation made by running water, bed of a stream. DEDS 269.

Ta. kuratu small block or clump of wood, plank. Ma. kuratu, kuratu piece of wood, kurana log, stump, gnarled wood; kuranfi board used as seat, stump; kuranfu log. Ko. kud club, bar of door, log. To. kud large stick, club. Ka, koradu trunk of a lopped tree, stump, piece of wood; (Hav.) kodanfi log of wood. Kod. kutfi small piece of wood. Tu. korady log, stump; kodadu chopping-block; (B-K.) kudanti, kodanfi a small log of wood. Te. koradu the trunk of a tree of which the branches are lopped off, a stump. DED(S) 1528.
Ta. kuratu pincers, forceps, crab. Te. koradu a kind of pincers. DED 1529.

Ta. kuram Kurava tribe, palmistry as practised by Kurava women; kuravan man of a caste of fowlers, snake-catchers, basket- makers, and fortune-tellers; fem. kuratti, kuravi, kuravaiici; kuravanar the Kurava tribe of the mountain; kurifici hilly tract; kuricci village in the hilly tract; village; kurumpar petty chieftains; an aboriginal tribe; hunters; a caste of shepherds who kurayai (167] kuru weave blankets; kurumpu petty chieftains; a class of savages supposed to form a part of the aborigines of south India; village. Ma. kuravan wandering tribe of basket-makers, snake-catchers, and gipsies; kuricci hill country; kuricciyan a hill tribe; kurumpan shepherd; caste of mountaineers in Waya- nadu; kuruppu a chief. To. kurb (r, not 1) man of Kurumba tribe living in the Nilgiri jungles; fem, kurt, kurbé. Ka. korava, korama man ofa now settled tribe who make blankets, mats, etc., are musicians, ete.; fem koravaiiji, koravati, koraviti (commonly fortune-tellers); koraca a thievish wandering hill tribe that sell wooden combs, ete.; kuruba man of the shepherd caste; kurumba a caste of moun- taineers; kurike village. Kod. kurubé shepherd; (Shanmugam) Kuruba caste man; fem. kuru- bati. Tu. korage man of a tribe that feeds on carrion and whose women wear leaves; fem. korapalu; koraji, koraju a serpent charmer; kurube shepherd. Te. korava name of a tribe of mountaineers; fem, koravata; korayanji a gipsy; kurupa, kuruma a caste of shepherds who weave blankets. Go. (Pat.) kurmal shepherd (V
Ta. kuravai black murrel, Ophio- cephalus striatus; O. punctatus. Te. korra a kind of fish, (B.) Silurus boalis; (B.) korra- muttu Gobius lagocephalus. DED 1531.

Ta. kuralai calumny, backbiting. Ma. kurala, kurala_talebearing, slander, backbiting. ? Go. (LuS.) kurcha backbiting. Ma. kuricci a kind of fish with many | sharp bones. Tu, kurci a kind of fish. DEDS 1849 (a) Ta. kurifici conehead. (b) Ta, kuraiici, kurifici, kurantam, kuran- fakam, kdrantam henna, Lawsonia alba; common yellow nail dye, thorny nail dye, Barleria prionitis. Ma, kurifiiti various Barleria and Justicia species. Te. goranta, gorita L, alba. ict Skt. kurunta(ka)-, kuranfaka-, kurandaka-, kuruntha- yellow Barleria; kuraba(ka)- a ted kind of Barleria; Pkt. Kori ta. Barleria; Turner, CDIAL, nos. 3322, 3326. DED 1535. 1850 (a) Ta. kuru (kuruv-, kurr-) to pound in a mortar, husk; kurru (kurri-) to pound, strike, hit, crush (as lice). Ko. kur- (kut-) to pound (clay in preparation for making pots). Ga. (S.*) kurk- (kuruk-) to beat like a carpet. Go. (A.) kurkal stone pestle (Voc. 778). Kur. kussna@ to strike by thrusting the head against, butt (Pfeiffer). (b) Ta, kuttu (kutti-) to strike with the fist, cuff, pound (as in a mortar}, peck, afflict, injure; mn. blow with the fist, cuff, pounding (as in a mortar); kuttal hitting. Ma, kuttuka to cuff, beat in a mortar; kuttu a blow. Ko, kut blow with fist; gud- (gudy-) to strike with fist, nudge with elbow; gud fist, blow with fist, nudge,

Ta. kuru short, dwarfish, defective; (-pp-, -tt-) to become short, contract, shrink; kurumai shortness, dwarfishness, defective- ness; kuruku (kuruki-) to grow short, stumpy, dwarfish, shrink, be reduced, decrease; kurukku = (kurukki-) to shorten, reduce, abbreviate; n, shortness of distance, con- traction; kurukkam shortness, abbreviation; kuyukkal reduction, contraction; kurukal that which is short; kurai C-v-, -nt-) to dim- inish, dwindle, be reduced, prove insufficient, be defective, droop in affliction, languish from worries, lose courage, suffer defeat; (-pp-, -tt-) to lessen, shorten; 7. deficiency, defect, poverty; kuraiccal deficiency, scarcity, disparagement; kuraivu lack, deficiency, defect, small quantity, poverty; kurag (-v-, -nt-) to stoop, bend flow; kural, kuril short- ness, dwarfishness, smallness; kuralan dwarf; fem, kurali; kuralai dwarfishness, poverty, kuriyOn person of short stature; kurram fault, defect, reproach; kurri stump; kurr- uyir state of being half-dead; kunru (kunri-) to decrease, become reduced, droop, languish, become stunted; n. deficiency; kunral dim- inishing; kuficam, kufican a dwarf. Ma. kuru short, little, brief; kurutu, kuriyatu what is short; kuru nari, kurukkan jackal; kuruka to grow short, be abridged, thicken by boiling; kurukku what is short; kugukkuka to shorten, diminish, boil down, contract, pull in; kuru- ppam shortness, kurumpu infant; kura want, defect, blemish, disgrace; kurayuka to dwindle, sink in price, be deficient, sh

Ka. kuruje, kujje an unripe fruit of the jack tree. Tu. gujje a tender or half- ripe jack fruit. Cf. 1851 Ta. kuru. DEDS 271. 1855 Ta, kurumpi ear-wax; excretion of the poeys a urine and faeces. Ka. kurugani, ani, kilgani, kiigate, koggu, e€ eal- wax. Tu. kirmbily, iderabille id, Te gubili, gulibi, gulimi id. Pa. girub id.;? korii- to scrape wax out of ear. Go. (W. Ph.) koranged, (Ch.) korngel, (Ma.) kornguli, (A.) korekal, (Mand.) korveli ear-wax (Voc, 924); 7(S.) gtinju id. (Voe. 1170); (Koya Su.) kornjil id. DED(S, N) 1540.

Ta. kurumpu wickedness, mischief, battle, war; kurumpan mischief-maker. Ma. kurumpu haughtiness, insolence; kurumpan insolent, stubborn. Ka. kurumbu, kurumbi stubbornness, foolishness; kuruba a stubborn foolish man. DED 1541. :
Ya. kurumpu stronghold, fort. Ka. kupumba a man belonging to a fort. DED
Ta. kuruvai a dark species of paddy maturing in two months; an inferior reddish paddy maturing in three months. Ma, kuruva a sort of black paddy. DED 1543.

Ta. kurai (-pp-, -tt-) to cut, reap; n. piece, section; kuru (kuruv, kurr-) to pluck. Ma. kurekka to cut off. Ko, korv- (kort-) to make falfen branch into club. To. kwarf- (kwart-) to cut; kwic- (kwit-) to chop up (wood, etc.). Ka. kore, kori to cut (as wood with a saw, the throat with a sword, stalks of millet), break (as a hole in a wall), bore, excavate (as running water the soil), pierce (as cold; or with 2168 Ko. korv-); kori a large branch cut off from a thorn- bush; kore cutting, a cut-off piece (as of sugar-cane); koreyuvike cutting, etc.; koreta, korata act of cutting, etc., the piercing of cold; korcu, koccu to cut-away, up, or to pieces (as thorn-bushes); koccu act of or an instrument for cutting to pieces, state of being cut into pieces; koccike act of cutting or cutting up. Tu. kudupuni to cut, reap; kudé piece of wood; kujimbu, kujumbu a chip, fragment; kojapuni, kojepuni, kojafuni, urai {170] ktikai kojiluni, kojeluni, kojelpuni to break, cut in pieces; (B-K.) koccu to cut in pieces. Kor. (T.) kudi to saw. Te. (Inscr.) kOya a cut-off portion. Nk. (Ch,) kutuk-/kutk- to cut (meat); kutup- to cut with axe. Pa, kud- (kutt-), (NE.) kud- (kuft-) to cut; kud-, (NE.) kud- to kindle fire by bamboo stick. Ga. (S.3) kuy- to cut. Kui krapa (krat-) to cut, saw; n. act of sawing. DED(S, N) 1544.
Ta. kurai small island, Ka. kuruva, kurava, kurva island. Go. (LuS.) koorta an island. DED(N) 1545.

Ta. kuru (kurzi-) to puncture, prick, pierce. Te. g(rjuccu to thrust, push, insert (as a stick, needle or the like into something or through a hole), pierce, prick, thread together (as beads), thread a needle. Go. (Mu.) guhce- to pierce, poke; (Ma.) gu’c- to poke with finger, punch with elbow (Voc. 1167). From DED 1392.

Ta. kuni (-pp-, -tt-) to dance; kunippu dance. Ma. kunikka to dance, jump. Ko. kony- (konc-), kone- (konc-) (calf or other animal) frisks. To. kwidz- (kwidj}) (calf) frisks. Ka. kuni to move in a hopping, skip- ping, or jumping manner, dance; n. dancing; kuniyuvike, kuniha dancing; (K.?) konaku to jump; leap; 1. a leap, jump; (Hav.) kopi to dance; konippe dancing child (small girl). Kor. (T.) koni to dance. Te. guniyu, gunucu to dangle, dance; gunupu a dance. DED(N) 1547.

Ta. kunram, kunzu hill, mountain; kunruvar mountaineers. Ma, kunnam moun- tain; kunnu hill, mountain; conical heap, hill-fort; kunnan mountaineer; kunnikka to pile up, heap up; kuru hill. To. kiid-xas, kiids large rock standing by itself. Ka. konda hill, mountain (< Te.). Kod. kundi moun- tain. Te. konda, (inscr.) konra mountain, hill, rock; kondavadu a mountaineer, kuruva, (Inscr.) kuruva a taised ground, footpath on a hill. Nk. (Ch.) kod hill. Pa, kondi (pL kondkul) mountain. Ga. (S.2) kondekor the Gadbas near Salur; (S.?) kondavan a hill-man, Go. (W.) kuru hill; (Ph.) kuro mountain, forest (Voc. 795). Konda goron pl. goroku/ goronku) hill, mountain, forest on a hill. Cf. 1844 Ta. kuram. DED(S) 1548.

Ta. kunri crab’s eye, Abrus pre- catorius. Ma, kunni id. Ka. kunni, kunne, guru-guiji, guru-guiije, guru-gafiji id.; nasu- gunni Carpopogon pruriens Roxb. = Mucuna pruriens DC. [a near relative of A. precator- ius]. Tu. gurgufiji, gurgofiji seed of A. preca- torius, Te. gurija, gurivenda A. precatorius, guri-ginja, (B. also) guru-ginja seed _of A, precatorius. | Cf. Skt. kuficika-, gufija- A. precatorius (Turner, CDJAL, no. 4176); cf. H. ghaghci, ghfighci id. DED 1549.

Ta. kit cooing, as of a dove; ki-ka clamour, outcry; kU-kitv-enal, kU-kav-enal bawling, screeching, complaining, lamenting; kiccal outcry, clamous, hue and cry; kUval crying aloud, bawling, crowing; kdvu (ktvi-) to crow, scream (as a peacock), cry (as a cuckoo or birds in general), call out, whoop, trumpet, cry out for help, call; summon; kavuvan cock; kukku (kukki-) to whoop; kukkal whooping cough. Ma, kttkuka, kavuka to cry aloud, crow, call; kikkuka to cry, bawl, esp. of men; kfkki a bawler, (Tiyya) a whistle; kakkiri a bawler, coward; ktkal, Koval, kovu, kiippu a cry. To. ku- (i)n- (()d-) (woman) shouts to a distance; ku+x- (kuexy-) to crow; kub- (kuby-) to shout, bawl out. Ka. kf a cry, call, clamour; kigu to cry aloud, cry out; kdgu, kiku a cry, clamour, shout; kikalu crying, shouting; kuguvike crying aloud; kuvi to cry out; kububu, kubibi a kind of outery. Kod. kue- (kuev-, kuend-) to crow; ku-ti a shout, noise; (Shanmugam) ktipade a roar. Tu, kaguni to cry out; kiigu a cry, exclamation, outcry. Te. klka a cry, shout; kfita id., the cry of any animal; kkarincu to scold, reprove; kilyu to cry out, shout, clamour, howl; kayi, kuyi, kuyyi a cry, outcry, shout. Kol. kueg- (ku-kt-) to call; kuy- (kuyt-) (cock) crows. Nk. ktig- to call. Pa, kiy- to crow, call; kGp- (kGt-) to whistle. Ga. (Oll.) kdy-, guy- to crow, call. Go. (Ph. Y. Ch. Mu. Ma. Ko.) kils- to crow; (Tr.) kor-kusana, korr- kussand cock to crow (Voe. 837); (ASu.) kUs- to coo. Konda ktk- (+-) to call, invite, sho

Ta. kiikai rock horned owl, Bubo bengalensis; ktn owl. Ma, ktman id. Ko. gumn id. To. ki+x id. Ka. gilge, gigi, gibe id.; (Gowda) gumma id. Kod. gu-mi id. Tu, gig, gummé id.; gngu the cry of an owl. Te. giba owl. Kol. (Br.) gu-pa id. Go. (Ch.} gugva owl (Voc. 1101); (S.) gupa id. (Voc. 1135). Kui muska gumberi a large owl. / Cf. Skt. ghaka- owl, Mar. ghubad id., etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4494. DED(S) 1552. kitkai {171} kutu

Ta. kikai, kitvai East Indian arrow- root, Curcuma angustifolia. Ma. ktva. Ka. kadve, kuwve. Tu. kdvé, kuvé. DED 1553.4 | +. 1873 To, ku-x girl, daughter. Ka. kisu infant, babe; maiden, young virgin. Tu. kkru young, small. Kor. (O.T.) kije small boy; kuji girl; (M.) koji boy. Te. kdsu low, base, trifling, insignificant; kfituru, (B. also) katu, (inscr.) kuntur daugher; (Inscr.) ktvita id.

Ta. kicu (kici-) to be shy, coy, be ticklish, be tender (as an eye), recoil, shrink back; k0cam, kcal shyness; kiiccam shyness, ticklishness, hesitating, timidity; kUccu horri- pilation. Ma. kicuka to be shy, dread; kdsal, (Kaut.) kiiccam timidity, shyness; mayir- ktccu horripilation. ? Ko. koyed timidity, terror (or with 2250 Ta. kogai). Tu. kusu- runi to be afraid; kUruni to hesitate. Te. (K.) kosaru to hesitate, shrink, fear. Kui kja shame, bashfulness, shyness. DED (S, N) 1557, DED 1367.

Ko. gu+c- (gu-c-) to lie or fall in a mass, (rain) pours down; put down in a mass; guecl heap of fuel. To, ku-s- (ku-sy-) to be heaped up; ku-sil a heap. Tu. gilcuni, giisuni, ssuni to pour, shed, spill; gijuni to ooze. DED(S) 1558.

Ta. klccam small-sized post used in building. Ka, kilcu, kitca post or pillar in a wall for the support of beams; gujju queen- post, strut. Tu. gujji, gujju pole or post fixed in water. (Ko.) gunje post, pillar (Voce. 1107); (Koya T.) ginji post. Pe. gitj (pl. gilcku) pillar, post. DED (S, N) 1559. 1879 Pa, ktci, kdcu (pf kilckul) crowbar. Ga. (P.) gusi id. Go. kinj @l kisk) (Mu.) hoe, (Elwin) shouldered digging stick; (Ko.) kinj crowbar (Voc. 822); (ASu.) kilysa id., ploughshare. DEDS(N) 276. 1880 Ta, ktcci pulp of wood-apple. Ma. kUfifiu, kUfifii, kilfifial centre of a fruit, stalk in the midst of a jackfruit. Ka, kusuri, kusuru the pulp of some vegetables and fruits; (Hav.) «-gifiji stalk inside a jackfruit or pineapple. Tu. kiliji, gitfiji the stalk in the midst of a jackfruit, the germ-matter of a boil; kusri pulp of a fruit. Te. gujuru, guiju, gunju id.; (Merolu) gojju pulp. Ga. (S.3) gurju juice, pulp. Go. (Tr.) gunit sweet inner fibre of sugar-cane (Voc. 1106). Kuwi (D.) gurju pith. Cf. 1519 Ka. gijafi and 2179 Ko. kovg. DED (S) 1366, 1560. 1881 Ta, kUfaram tent. Ma. k0{aram tent, camp. Ka, gudara, gudare, gundara, gUdara tent. Tu. gudara id. Te, gudaramu, gudaru, gidaramu id. / Cf. Skt. @ex.) kufaru-, Pkt. guddara- id., Mar. gudhar id., howdah. DED 1561.

Ta. ktitu (kifi-) to come together, join, meet, assemble, combine, become con- ciliated, be stored up, happen, associate, cohabit, arrive at, be possible, be fit, proper, be achieved; kita together with, in addition to; kilfal joining, sexual union; kdtutal, kUfutalai success; kilfju (kuffi-) to unite (tr.), join, combine, mingle, add, convoke {an assembly); m. fellowship, assistance, relationship, throng, illicit intercourse, mix- ture; kif{{am union, meeting, crowd, group, association, kindred, companions, battle, sexual intercourse; ki¢tar companions, mem- bers of same clan or tribe; kiftal uniting, seeking the alliance of powerful kings; kuf- talan, kutfali associate, partner in trade. To, kusr- (ku+ry-) to join Qntr.), have i

Ta. kUfai basket made of rattan, ola, or bamboo, palm-leaf rain-covering; kifu basket for catching fish. Ma, kttfa id. Ko. kuer winnowing basket, a winnowing-basket of grain. To. kuer contribution of a measure (kwa+x) of grain, given by each Badaga house to Toda. Ka. glide, gttda basket, basket used as a bucket; kile, ktili basket for fishing. (M) gula basket; gulla (S.) big basket, (Ko.)} basket (Voc. 1166). DED (S) 1564. 1885 Tu, kutu hip. Pe. kuta thigh. Mand. kufe id. Kui kui hip. ? Kuwi (Isr.) krundu id. DEDS 233, 277. 1886 Kol. ku-fe cow. Nk. khofe id. Nk. (Ch.) kate id. DED (S) 1565. 1887 Ta, kittu (kUffi-) to gather up with a broom. Ka. gudasu, gudisu, gudsu, gudiyisu, gldisu to sweep; gudisalu sweeping. Tu. gudsu a kind of broom. DED 1567.
Ta. kati pudendum muliebre. Ma. kiti posteriors, membrum muliebre. To. ku-@y anus, region of buttocks in general. Tu. kildi anus, posteriors, membrum mulie- bre. DED 1568.
Ka. kituru, Bryonia scabra Rottl. Te. kifturu-budamu 8B. scabrella (budamu B. callosa). DED(S) 1569.

Ta. kilttu dance, dramatic perform- ance, strange event; kulttan dancer, actor; kitti female actor, dancer, prostitute. Ma. kittu dance, drama; kitti, kitticci harlot; (Kaut.) kittan dancer. To. ku+t Tamil dance, unusual thing. Ka, kitu dance, play. DED(S) 1570.
Ka. gilde prolapsus of the anus. Tu. gide id. Te. gilda, (low) gudda id. DED 1571.

Ja. kuntal long flowing tresses of a woman. Ma, kilntal woman’s hair. Ka. kudal hair (of the head or body). Tu. kujalu the hair of the head. Kor. (M.) kildlu hair. - / Cf. Skt. kuntala-. Ko.) kthk (sg. and pi.) mushroom(s) (Voce. 839). Konda (B.) kind mushroom. Pe, kind (pl kitku) id. Mand. gttnd (pl. giltke) id. Kui ktndu (pl, katka) id. Kuwi (Su. P. Isr.) ktndu (pl kUtka) id. Cf. 1875 Kol. ktku. DED(S) 1573. ktmpu [173] koli 1894 Ta, kiimpu (kilmpi-) to close, shut (as a flower), contract, shrink, lose courage; kappu (kdppi-) to close, contract, shut in (as an umbrella), draw in (as the sun its rays), join hands as in worship; n. joining hands as in worship; kuvi (-v-, -nt-) to close (as flowers by night); Gpp-, -tt-) id., draw in (as the sun its rays in setting); kuvalai blue nelumbo (which closes by day]. Ta. kr (-v-, -nt-) to covet, hanker after. Ma. kiruka to love, mind; kir, kittru love; kiran friend, lover, protector. Ka. kur to mind, be attached to, love; kUrpa, kipa loving, beloved; lover, husband; kdrpu, ktirme love. Ta. kUr (-pp-, -tt-) to be sharp (as the edge or point of an instrument), be keen (as the intellect); m. sharpness, pointed edge, cutting or sarcastic speech, pivot on which door swings; kircci, kUrppu, kirmai, kiriyam sharpness, keenness, pointedness; kflrccu id., pointed stick; kiriyan sharp, clever person; kiccu sharp point.
