Ka, kir sharpness, acuteness; ktrike sharpness, pointedness; kUritu, kirittu that which is sharp; kitrahu sword; kurida a sharp, acute, or brave man; kUrpu sharpness, valour, a keen, penetrating look; (Bark.) ktpi a barber’s knife. Kod. ku+f- (ku fi-) to sharpen. Tu, kO¢uni id.; kurpu sharpness as of a cutting instrument. Te. kriru sharp; kici sharp, pointed, tapering; kUcamu a peg. DED(S) 1578, DED 1566.
Ka. kur to sit down; kirisu to cause to sit. Te. kir(u)cundu to sit, be seated. Pe, kuc- (-c-) to sit. Mand. kuh- (-t-) id. DED (S) 1580.
Ta. karan dog. Tu. (B-K.) ktra id.; Kori Ditch. Cf. 1796 Ta. kurai. DED(S, N)

Tu. kOruni to be drowsy, sleepy; kirangelu drowsiness, sleepiness; kirele a sleepy fellow, sluggard. Te. kUr(u)ku to sleep; n. sleep; (K.) kiru to sleep, doze; kunuku to nod with sleep, doze, slumber. Nk. gungi sleep. Pa. ktrk- to nod in sleepiness; ktrukud, kurkukud drowsiness; (S.) gurgal sleep, night. Ga, (P.) kark- to doze; (S.3) kork- (kiruk-) to sleep briefly, take a nap; (OIL) kurkal sleep. Go. (SR. Tr. Ph.) karkana, (W.) kurkina, (Ch. Mu. Ma. Ko.) kark- to doze, nod in sleepiness; (Y. 8.) kirka sleepi- ness; (Ph.) kirki id., drowsiness; (W.) kurki, (S.) kimmur sleep (Voc. 831). Kuwi (F.) kdrnkali to nod with sleep. Kur. kigna to doze, slumber (out of time). Malt. kuge to slumber. ? To, kwixy- (kwixc-) to take a nap. DED(S, N) 1582. 1903 Ka, gitru phlegmatic and asthmatic disease. Tu. giiru asthma, cough, DED 1583.
Ta. kirai sloping roof (commonly thatched with grass or palm leaf), small hut; kurampai small hut, hovel, shed. Ma. kdra hut, thatch. ? Kol. (Pat., p.59) kurodi hog- house. DED 1584.

Ta. kOli wages, pay, freight; kalik- karan, kdliy-al hired labourer, cooly; kai- kkilli daily wages; bribe, cash payment, money paid by parents of bride to bride- groom. Ma, kuli hire, wages (esp. daily): kilikkaran, kiliy-al hired labourer, coolie; kai-kkali_bribe, fine upon a lease and its renewal. Ko. ku+j daily wages paid in money; kusjgaern, ku-j al day-labourer, coolie; kuslyka«rn cooly (only applied to Tamil and other coolies). To. kékwily bribe, pay- ment made by bridegroom to bride’s father. Ka, kili hire, wages (esp. daily); day- labourer, coolie; kilikara, kiligtra day- labourer; kay-kUli, key-gili working as a day- labourer. Kod. ku-li wages. Tu. kuli hire. Te, kuli wages, hire, working for wages; kali hired labourer, coolie. Pa, kOli labourer’s wages. Go, (Ko.) kili wages (Voc. 836). Suu koli (S.) payment, (F.) present. DED(S) 1906 Konda kili paddy. Pe. kuli id. Mand. kali id. Kui kidi grain, paddy, seed. Kuwi (Su. P. F. S.) ktli paddy. ? Ta, ktlam grains, esp. of 18 kinds, viz. nel, pul, etc. ? Br, xolum wheat (or with DBIA 123, < Skt. godhima-). (ra. Br., comm. by Kamaleswaran.) DEDS(N) 78. kalu {174] kiru 1907 (a) Ka. kil to fall down, be over- turned, be destroyed, ruined; kilisu to cause to fall, destroy. Te. kau to fall down, sink, drop, die, fall dead; kul(u)cu to cause to fall, fell, bring to the ground, knock down, kill; kilabadu to fall down suddenly, sink, drop. (b) Go. (Tr. koyand to topple over (of a vessel, boulder on hillside), founder (of a bulloc

Ta. ktval a well, hollow, hole, pit. Kod. ku-+va shallow well (where water can be dipped by hand). Tw. guvely a well. Kor. (T.) kuyyeli id. / Prob. < Pkt. kilva- < Skt. kilpa-; cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 3400, DED 1590. 1910 Ta, kivilam, kivilai, ktiviram bael, Aegle marmelos. Ma. kivalam A. marmelos; Crataeva religiosa. Ka. kumbala-mara A. marmelos. DED(S) 1591.

Ta. kg thick gruel, porridge, semi- liquid food, food; ki a to be overboiled (as tice). Ma, ktir boiled rice; kOgam rice, as eaten in temples. Ko. ku+ cooked rice. To. ku- food (in song-unit: of@ k/xu+ e-d two lots of cooked food); ku» Owi-er cooked rice from which water has been drained. Ka. ktty boiled rice, food. Kod. ku-li cooked rice; ku Kala, kue kudike rice pot; kue padi food carried in a plantain leaf as a lunch. Tu. kOru, kilu, kilu boiled rice. Te. kiidu boiled rice, food. Mand, kiir gruel. Kuwi (S.) kiidu rice. /Cf. Skt. kira-, kuru- boiled rice; Pali Pkt. kifra- id. DED(S) 1592. 1912 Konda kiiram (BB) knife, (K.) small knife. Kui kitre knife. DEDS 279. 1913 Ya, kirai dullness of intellect, stupidity; kigan one who is devoid of clear understanding; ktxaiyan dolt, booby. ? Ko. kuekn foolish man; fem, kueyk. Ka, kira a vulgar, rude, stupid, useless, mean, or vile man; vulgar, etc.; fem. kiire; kiir, ktira fierce, cruel; kilza-nayi a ferocious, mean dog. Tu, kala, khilla bad, mean, cruel; ktir-nayi a fierce dog. Te. killa, kdlGdu a wicked man, a cruel man. DED(S) 1593. 1914 Ta, kigai that which is short; dwarf snake, Calamaridae; ktizai-kka{a, kOrai-kkita tailless he-buffalo; kgai-kkai, kian-kai maimed hand, kizai-kkompan ox with blunt horns; ktiai-nari short-tailed fox; kiraiyan short, stunted person. Ka. kire, kile stump of jola left in the ground to push out new shoots, stubble in general. ? Br, k0f{i hornless. _ DED(N) 1594. dete Bert

Ta. kil (kulv-, kiipt-) to crowd to- gether, assemble, muster; kili company, multitude, family. Ka. gtle, gilevu, gtlya, gule, gulya people leaving a place en masse from invasion or famine. Tu. gilé id. DED 1595. 1916 Ta, kilam broken pieces of straw or hemp, chaff, sediment. Ma, ktlam chaff of corn, etc. ? Go. kurmi (Tr.) the harder part of rice, kodon, etc. which remains after grinding, (W. Ph. LSJ [Betul, p. 499]) chaff (Voe. 784); (ASu.) kurma id. DED(N) 1596.

Ta. kOli ox, covering bull. Ko. gu-ly bull belonging to a dead man, that is released from work until its death. To. ku-ly stud bull. Ka, gutli bull, esp. a bull allowed to roam at liberty and dedicated to a deity. Kod. guelia bull. DED 1597.

Ta. kili devil, demon. Ma, kiili demon, ghost. Ka, (Hav.) kole ghost. Tu. kulé id., apparition. Kor, (T.) kolnari devil. Cf. Ka. péhkuli, péikuni, s.v. 4438 Ta. pey. DED(S) 1598. 1919 Kol, ku-l- (ku-f-) (water) run from punctured vessel or tap; ku-Ip- (ku-lupt-) to puncture (vessel) so that water runs out. Nk. kul- to leak. DED 1599.
Ta. ktli large species of eagle. Te. gili vulture. DEDS 280.

Ta. ktru (kiri-) to speak, assert, cry out the price, cry aloud, proclaim; kUrram word; kiirru utterance, proclamation, word. Ma, ktiruka to speak, proclaim; kUrru call, cry of men, noise; ktirram cry (as for help). Ka, girpisu, giirmisu to murmur or roar (as water of a river or the sea), sound (as a trumpet), roar or bellow, cry aloud, Tu. gUruni to hoot. Te. ghimillu to sound, resound (gh- from Skt. ghiirn- to move to and fro > Te. ghirniflu to whirl, turn round). DED 1600.
Ka. glru to turn or uproot the earth with horns or tusks. Te. gufi a hog. Cf. 2257 Ta. k6rai. DEDS 281.

Ta. kUru-kol to stuff, cram. Ma, kurummuka to eat greedily, cram in. ? Ka, kuluku to fill with force, cram into, stuff. Te. kiru to stow into an insufficient space, cram, stuff, thrust; k(dukku to stuff, cram, pack or stow too closcly, push, thrust; kruk- kudu stuffing, cramming. Ga. (S.2) kukkap- 1 stuff in (< Te.); kitrap- id. (< Te.). DED 1601.

Ta. kUru section, division, part, share; kirram species, class; Yama; that which ruins or destroys; kOrran, kfrru, kUrruvan Yama. Ma. ktru, ktir part, share, division of time, party, partnership; kUrran partner; kOrran, pereerenenarrs ‘koyai [175] ce- kOrram enemy, destroyer. Ko. ku-r (obL ku+t-) share. To, kur share, share inherited from father. Ka. kdru a part, portion, share in cultivation, etc. Te, (B.) kGru a share, the king’s or government portion. DED 1602.
Ta. kOrai cloth, clothes. Ma, kura a set of cloths, thick cloth. Ka. kore clothes, Cf. 2255 Ma. kora. DED(S) 1603.
Ma. kta, ktirdn insect, moth, cock- yack Ka. kare a kind of cloth-louse. DED 04.

Ta. kin bend, curve, hump on the back, humpback, snail; ktnu (ktini-) to curve, become crooked, bend down, become hunchbacked; kipal bend, curve, hump; ktnan humpback; fem, ktni; kuni (-v-, -nt-) to bend (as a bow), bow, stoop; (-pp-, -tt-) to bend (#7), curve; m. curvature, bow (weapon); kunippu bending. Ma. kunuka to stoop, be crookbacked; kin a humpback; kanal, ktintal bending; kdnan humpbacked; fem. kUni, kUnicci; kuni semicircle, curve; kuniyuka to bow, stoop, bend; kunikka to make a curve, cause to stand stooping. Ko. kuen- (kuend-) to be in bowed position (looking down, bent with pain, tiger crouch- ing), become bent with age. To. kuen hénch- back. Ka, ktin (kiint-), kiinu to be bent or bowed, bend, stoop; 1, a hump; kina, gtina a humpbacked man; fem. kini, gtini; ktntu bending, bent state; ginu a hump; kuni to bend, bow, stoop, shrink; 7. a bent or curved ground; kunungu to bend, stoop, crouch, contract oneself, shrivel up. Kod. (Shanmu- gam) kin hunchback; gitne hunchbacked man; gtini hunchbacked woman. Tu, gunu a hump; gitné a hunchback. Te. gtnu a hump, a crooked back; gttni humpbacked; gimivadu a humpback; fem. gtnidi; (B.) kuni angle, bit of land. Go, (S.) gun- to bend (Voe. 1128). Kuwi (Su.) gu’u hump of cow. DED (S, N) 1605.

Ta. kin cauldron; kUnai large earthen boiler, baling bucket. Ma. (DCV) ktina earthen vessel. Ka, kiini earthen basin used by oilmen. Te, gina large earthen pot. Kuwi (Isr.) gtina a large pot. DED(S) 1606.

Ta. (Devanesan, p.3) kumuri a kind of pigeon. Ka, gtlva dove. Te. kuki id.; guvva dove, pigeon. Go. (Tr.) gummal, gummul the grey dove with a ring round its neck (Voc. 1148). Konda govanda pigeon. Kui guguti dove. Kuwi (Su. P. Isr.) kuguri, (F.) kigdri, . (S.) kugguri id. Cf. Ko. gugr, gugrgre+s Pigeon’s noise; gugreety pigeon (for -cety, see 2763). Cf. 1868 Ta. ka. DED(S) 1589.

Ta. ce-, cem-, cevv-, ceyya, cétakam, céttu red; cekkam, cekkar, cekkal, cekii, cemmai, cey, cevvu, cevval, cevér-enal, cé, ctkkai, ctku, c&kai, céntu, céppu, céy, civv- enal, civappu, civér-enal redness; ceccai id., dxora_ coccinea (with scarlet blossoms); ceppal red colour (as of dawn); ceyyavan, ceyyan, ceyyan, ceyyOn, céyan, céyan, civappan person of red or brown complexion; ceval, cevalai, civalai ruddy person or animal: cival Indian partridge; cé (-pp-, -nt-) to redden, get angry; cékil tawny-coloured bull; cét-a tawny-coloured cow; cé@yttu, c@ytu that which is red; civa (-pp-, -nt-) to redden, blush, become angry; cekka-cciva (-v-, -nt-), cekkafi-ceku to be deep red; cekka-cciver- enal, cekka-ccevér-enal deep redness; cen-nay brown-coloured dog, Canis dukhunensis: kempu ruby (< Ka.). Ma. ce-, cem-, céya red; cemma, cevva, cona, covva redness; cekkal dawn; cekki-ppi, cetti-ppt, cecci-ppt I. coccinea; cehiuka, cempikka, conekka, cuvakka to be red; (Tiyya) cemappu redness: cemakkuva to redden; cuvappu, cdppu red; red colour; ruby; cecca a kind of ruby; cen- nayi wolf or rather C. primaevus; kempu tuby (< Ka.). Ko, ken, ke-t red; kep red, red- ness; kep avy, ken a-v red cow; ke na+y wild dog; keky red clay; keb gal flint; ke- katy clinkers (i.e. red tumps) from smithy fireplace; kepn m.pr. red bullock or male dog; fem. kepy; kempn ”.pr. man; kempy n.pr, woman. To, ké- red (e.g. ké mon red soil; k6mo-s npr, reddish buffalo); ke no-y wild dog. Ka. ke-, kem- red; kefka, kefig

Ta. kekkattam loud laughter; kekkali (-pp-, -tt-) to laugh violently; 7. loud laughter. To. (all in songs) kek smile, laughter; ke(k) kisad laughing; koks/kekS in- to make noise of laughter (uncertain which is correct). Te. (B.) geggili, geggalu derision. DED(S) 1608. 1934 Konda (BB) key a kind of pulse, Cajanus indicus (= Te. kandulu; see 1213). Pe, ken id. Mand. kan id. Kui (Mah.) ka’anga id. (arhar). Kuwi (isr.) kayn (lL kariga) beans; (F.) khana raher (dhal); (Mah.) kanga, kangu id. ?Cf. Tu. kamangayi a wild species of green gram, Phaseolus rostratus. DEDS 284.
Ka. kesavu mushroom. Malt. qejo a kind of mushroom. /Cf. Skt. kacaka- id. DEDS 285.

Ka. key (Hav.) crop, (Bark.) paddy plant; (Hal.) kéyyu id. Tu. (B-K.) keyi the crop of paddy. Go. (Mu.) keja paddy (Voe. 841). Kur. xess paddy (rice in the husk or standing in the field). Malt. qesu paddy. DED(S, N) 1609. 1937 Konda kezeri elka mouse, muskrat (7) Pe, kejra orli a kind of rat. Kuwi (D.) kecca ori id. DEDS 286.
Ko. ked belly from navel to groin. To. ke® lower belly (of man). Ka. geije the groin between the belly and the thigh. Te. gajja id. DED(S) 1611.

Ta. keficu (kefici-) to beg humbly, entreat, crave, beseech with supplicating gestures. Ma, keficuka to beg, supplicate, emulate in vain; kificuka to beg. Ko. kenj- (kenj-) to beg. Go, (Ko.) kesuy- to ask, request (Voc. 849). Malt. kéwejare to entreat, Cf. Go. kénj-, s.v. 2017(a) Ta. ket. DED(S) 1612. 1940 Konda kinz- (-it-) to be torn, as a cloth, etc.; kis- (-t-) to tear. Pe. kenj- (kenc-) to be torn; kec- (-c-} to tear. Mand. kenj- (kenc-) to be torn; kec- (-c-) to tear, Kui genja (genji-) to be separated from, divided from, depart from secede; m, separation, secession, schism; pl. action geska (geski-); gespa (gest-) to separate, make a division between; mn. act of separating; geji frayed, split; geji inba to be frayed, worn out, split. Kuwi (Su.) genj- (it-), (F.) genjali to be torn; (Su.) geh- (gest-), (F.) gessali (gest-) to tear; (S.) gespinai to laniate [i.e. tear to pieces]. DEDS 287.

Ta. ceti shrub, bush; Cpp-, -tt-) to grow bushy, shoot out (as sprays, foliage). Ma, ceti shrub, small tree. Ko. girv plant; gevd thick bushy tree; gevd va-lm bushy tail (of peacock, wild dog). To. kidf shrub. Ka, gida, gidu, cetfu plant as of chili, brinjal, pulse, shrub, small tree, tree in general; sidumbu, sidumbe a (thorny) tree, bush, thicket. Kod. gida plant. Tu. gida shrub. Te. cetfu tree, plant, bush, creeper. Kol. (Kin.) sett, ceft bush, smalf tree. Nk. sett tree. Nk. (Ch.) set(t), saff tree. Go, (Ko.) katta, (Mu.) gatta shrub, small tree (Voc. 475). Malt. kitu a young plant. DED(S) 1613.

Ta. kefu (ketuv-, kef{-) to perish, be destroyed, decay, rot, become damaged, spoiled, fall on evil days, degenerate, be reduced, run away defeated; (pp-, -tt-) to destroy, squander, extinguish, damage, spoil, corrupt, defeat, lose; . peril, poverty; keffa bad, spoiled, ruined; kettavan a bad, immoral person; fem. kettaval; ketal dr evil fate, ketutal ruin, damage, danger, degeneracy; kefuti ruin, loss, damage, thing lost, danger, affliction, evil; kefumpu ruin, evil; kéfu ruin, loss, damage, adversity, death, evil; kétan ruined, miserable man, he who ruins; fem. keti. Ma. ketu ruin; kefuka to be extinguished, be ruined, spoilt, damaged; kefuti ruin, danger, weakness, misery; kefumpu depravity, rottenness; kefumpikka to be spoiled by drying up; ke¢ta lost, bad; ketukka to quench, do away with, damage, ruin; kéfu destruction, loss, damage, hurt; kéfan a rogue. Ko, ker- (ket-) to die, be ruined, be lost; kere- (kerc-) to ruin, destroy, lose; keer (Obl. ke+{-) ruin, harm, danger, loss, funeral, corpse. To, kér- (k6t-) to be spoiled, become bad in conduct, be extinguished, die (others than Todas); kérc- (koré-) to kill by witchcraft, extinguish, make to go wrong way and lose property; kodOil misfortune, evil; kédc- (k6dé-) to destroy (< Badaga); ké-d (Obl. ké-f-) dead person (corpse at first funeral, relics at second funeral), funeral; ké-¢ o-x- to die (used of Todas). Ka. kedu, kidu (keff-) to be destroyed, be ruined, be spoiled, become bad, become vicious, be extinguished, ceas

Te. gitta, (VPK) gitte, gette, getike, gotte hoof; gédekallu long slender legs. Kol. getta foot, leg, hoof. Wk. getfa foot. Pa. géta leg from knee to ankle; claw of crab. Go. (Ko.) géta hoof (Voc. 1182). Konda (BB) gita id. Kur, xedd foot, leg. Malt. qedu the legs, the feet. DED(S) 1615.

Ma. kinayuka to quarrel, become angry. Ka. kepaku to irritate, provoke, by words or deeds. Tu. kenakuni, kenkuni, kenakuni to provoke, irritate, kindle. Te. cenaku, cenuku to touch, stir up, provoke; n, a touch; cenaku, cenayu to touch, op- pose, rush against, (K. also for cenaku) have sexual intercourse with another woman. Kur. xenna (xinnyas) to have intercourse with (a female). DED 1616.
Ka. gentu remoteness, distance, re- moval; (PBh.) to remove oneself to a distance. Te. genfu to push, turn out, drive out, expel; gentincu to cause to be pushed or turned out. DED 1618.

Ta. kentai ankle. Ma. kenippu joint, articulation. Ka. ginnu, gennu knot, joint, as of sugar-cane, finger, etc.; ganfu knot of cord, joint of reed, bamboo, cane, joint or articulation of body. Kod, gippi joint in wiist or fingers, knot in sugat-cane; ka-li- gipni ankle. Tu. ganfu, ganfu knot in string, ankle, knot or joint of reed or cane. Te. gantu, (VPK) ganta a knot. Nk. kande joint in bamboo. Cf. 1160 Ta. kan joint. / Cf. Skt. ganda- joint; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3998 (also génda-). DED(S) 1619.

Ta. kenfai a freshwater fish, Barbus. Ma. kenta a carp, Cyprinus, Ka. gepde-min a sort of fish. Te. gande, gende a fish; (B.) gendi, gendiya, gende the carp, C. fimbriatus. Kur. kindd a species of fish; (Hahn) kindd injd carp fish. DED(S) 1620.
Ta. kentai ridicule, mockery, banter. To. kidy abuse. DED 1621.

To. kédm (obl. kédt-) live coal. Ka. kenda id.; kendavisu to put live coals on (for blasting rocks). Tu. kenda, genda live coal. DED 1623. 1951 Konda ked- (kett-) (BB) (water) to be heated, (K.) to boil; (BB) ket- (-t-) to heat (water). Pe. ked- (kett-} to become hot; kedi ki- to heat (water). DEDS 288. 1952 Kui gredi, greji soft, pulpy, festering; gredi inba to be soft, pulpy; gredna in a manner determined by pulpiness or softness; gredna duda to step into soft mud. Kur. gedla (of fruits) overripe, having a soft and decaying appearance, falling to pieces; having lost all consistency (of over-cooked food). Malt. gidi pulp of fruit. DEDS 289.

Ta. cettu (cetti-) to cut with adze, chisel, pare off; ». cutting, chiselling; cetukku (cetukki-) to cut off a surface, pare, shave off, plane, hew with an adze, chisel; n. paring, chiselling; kettu (ketti-) to split asunder by driving in and turning the hatchet, chop, mince, cut off, make an incision; strike the stick in the game of tipcat. Ma. cettuka to chip, cut off, pare, plane, clear ground, dig slightly; cettal chipping, planing, etc.; cettu cutting, parings, rind or peel. Ko. ket- (kety-) to dig with hoe or other digging tool. To, k6t- (kéty-) to smooth (plank with adze), dig (earth with hoe). Ka. kettu to pare the surface, make thin, chip, scrape or chip off (as grass), dig slightly, engrave, carve; kettike act of chipping, etc.; kettisu to cause to carve or engrave, cause to chip, etc.; kette a chip, paring; kedaku to stir, scratch (as fowls), dig up or loosen earth. Kod. kett- (ketti-) to cut (tree, or with large knife or axe); ketti a cut, blow. Tu. kettuni to carve, chisel, engrave, dig slightly; ketté a piece, slice, chip; kettigé carving, engraving. Kor. (O.) kederpu to dig. Cf. 1547 Ta. citar. DED(N) 1624.

Ta. kettu-kkett-enal, ketath-ketam- enal palpitation of the heart through fright. Ka. kettu to quiver, shiver, tremble. Kod. kett- (ketti-) to gore with horns (said of buffaloes and bison, which sway the horns from side to side). Tu. kittuni to flutter, be in agitation. Kur. xettnd (xittyas) to shake off or out by imparting jerks, clean (viz. by shaking, beating, or similar forcible action); xetrna to be thrown off or out by knocks or by a jerk. Malt. qete to dust, beat the jungles when hunting. DED(S) 1625.

Ta. ceppu (ceppi-) to say, speak, declare, tell; n. speech, word, answer, reply; ceppal saying, replying, declaring. Ma. ceppuka to say, (dog) growls when snatching at some- xeppas thing. Te. ceppu to say, tell, relate, narrate, recite, (K. also) scold, reprove. Kur. kebna (keppas) to scold, lecture. DED 1626. 1956 Kur. xeppas a visitor or friend enter- tained in the house, guest; fellow-villager, neighbour; (Hahn) xeppa inhabitant of a village; xeppar inhabitants, population. Malt. qepu village; qepo inhabitant of a village, neighbour. Cf. 1990 Ta. cé. DED(S, N) 1627.

Ta. cey (-v-, -t-) to do, make, create, cause; n, deed, act, action; ceykai deed, act, action, work, conduct, that which is manu- factured; ceyti deed, act, action, occupation, conduct; ceyyal conduct, work; ceyyul, ceyarkai action; ceyvatu that which ought to be done; ceyal act, action, work, conduct. Ma, ceyka to do, act; ceyal action; ceyti doings, news; ceyyikka to cause to do. Ko. gey- (gec-), key- (kee-; ina few constructions) to do, make; kek, keky work. To. kiy- (kis-) to do, make; kok, kek an art. Ka. key, kai, , gey to perform, do, make, work; be service- able, fit; keyta, geyta doing, an act; keyme, geyme, géme doing, work, achievement. Kod. key- (keyyuv-, kejj-) to work. Tu. gaipini, geyipini to do, fulfil. Te, céyu to do, perform, make, create, (K. also) occur, happen, (body of child) grows; (K. B.) céta doing, act, action, (inscr., Nellore, 6th cent. A.D.) kesiri they did, késina that he did; (Kumarasambhava 10.198) s€gi action. Kol. kak- (kakt-; also ka- instead of kak- in the present) to do, make; have sexual inter- course (on this meaning, Kuiper, IZ 2.239) (2 for the form, contamination with a form like Nk. akk-, s.v, 333 Ta. 3). Nk. (Cit.) kak- to do. Ga. (Oll) key- (ked-, ken-), (S.) key- to do. Go. (Tr.) kiana (neg. kéw-), (W.) kiyana, (Ph. SR. M. L.) kiyana, (D. Mu. Ma. S.) ki- to do, make, prepare, caus. (Ph.) kisahtana, (Mu.) kih-; (S.. kimuy doing (Voc. 703). Konda ki- (-t-) to do, make. Pe. ki Ct-) id. Mand. ki- Ct) id. Kui kiva (kit-), giva (git-) t

Ta. cey field, esp. wet field; kaitai paddy field. Ma. ceyi rice-field. Ka. key, key(y)i, kay field. Kod. kette wet mud, mire. Te. cénu (pl. cétu) field; (Inscr.) kayya a rice-field; kaili field. Kol. (Kin.) kén, (SR) ken id. Nk. kén id. Pa, (S) ken field for shifting cultivation. Kur. xajj earth, mould, clay, mud, corpse. Malt. qaju earth, mud. Cf. 2020 Ta. céru; cf. 3630 Ta. nanai, esp. {178} cerumu Ta. nancey, Te. nanja; ? cf. 1993 Kur. xéxel. DED(S) 1629. 1959 Konda ker- (-t-) to take handfuls or small quantities out of a mass (of grain, etc.), take into a ladle before serving, collect into a heap and pick up. Pe. gre- (-t-) to scoop up with the hand. Mand. grepa- to scoop up. Kui gripa (grat-), (P.) grépa (grét-) to scoop up, shovel into with the hands, scrape to- gether. Kuwi (F.) grecali (gret-) to gather up, take handful. DEDS 290.

Ta. ceru). Ta. cerumu (cerumi-) to hem, cough; cerumal clearing the throat; cerumu (cerumi-) to hem, grunt, sob agitatedly. ? Ma, cuma cough; cumekka to cough. Ka. kemmu to kec {179] cevi cough; #. coughing. Ka. kela side, vicinity, the right or left side; keladan a man who is on one side, or on both sides. Kod. kela belly. Tu. kila id., kelly brink, edge, vicinity. Ta. keli (-pp-, -tt-) to conquer, over- come; kelippu winning, success, victory. Kol, (SR) gell- to win. Nk. ghel- id. Konda gels- (-t-) to win, escape from danger; gelpis- (-t-) to save, rescue, cause to win or become eminent. Kuwi (S.) gelhinai to win; (F.) gelpali to save. ?Cf. 5493 Te. vel. DED (S, N) 1641. 1973 Go, (many dialects) kel (pl. -k) hair, feather (Voc. 847). Konda kelu (pl. kelku) feather. (Voc. 848), Konda gibi, (Guri dial.) kibi, (Sova dial.; BB) gitoni id. Pe. kitul id. Mand. giy (pl. -ke) id. Kur. xebda id. Malt. qethwu id. Br. xaf id. (hb) Tu. ceradu ear. For separation from (a), see Krishnamurti, Language 39.562. (c) Ta. cevitu deafness, deaf person or animal; cevitan, ceviti deaf man, woman. Ma. cevitu deafne
Ka. kevvane with the hissing sound of a stone, etc., thrown away with great force. Te. kevvuna resoundingly, loudly. DED 1646.

Ta. keguvu (keruvi-) to unite, em- brace; n. friendship; kegumu (kegumi-) to attain, join, unite; kegt friendship; kigamai friendship, alliance, relationship; day of the week, as related to each of the seven planets; kirin owner; kigavan, kiravOn owner, master, husband; kigavi wife, mistress; kigal, kigatti proprietress. Ta. ceri Cv-, -nt-) to be thick (as foliage, hair), crowded, be in close union, be tight (as bangles), be controlled, keep within bounds; (pp-, -tt-) to join together, tighten, shut, close, block up, secure, store up, pack closely; ceri, cerivu denseness, abundance, union, self-restraint; ceru (ceruv-, cerr-) to control (as the senses), hinder, prevent, overcome, Cpp-, -tt-) to suppress, subdue, resist, prevent, prevent the passage {as of water), overcome; 7. tank, field, garden plot; ceruppu restriction; ceruvu field; cirai Cpp-, -tt-) to restrain, imprison, dam up; n. guard, confinement, prison, captivity; dam, bank, tank, boundary; ciraivan captive, slave; cerrai thicket, crowd. Ma. ceriyuka to be thronged, cerukkuka to dam. up, enclose, oppose, prevent; cira enclosure, dam, limit, tank, reservoir. To. kerf- (kert-) (water) is dammed, (crowd) gathers; to dam; ker party, side; ser imprisonment; ked- (kedy-) to be stuck in (spear in tree, stick in hand or foot); to fix in position (waistcloth, sticks at end of tidgepole to hold thatch); ter paf- (paty-) to arrest, seize, and confine (in song phrases). Ka. kig (kett-) to confine, close, shut, block up, make a fence, cover; kere tank; giraku state of being strait (as a gate), of being firmly fixed (as a door), of being close, pressing (as a crowd), sere to contract, con- fine, harass; n. confinement, bondage, prison. Ta. ceru (ceruv-, -cerr-; -pp-, -tt-), ceri (-pp-, -tt-), cerru (cerri-) to kill, destroy; ceruppu, ceral, cerral killing; ceru (pp-, -tt-) to destroy, ruin. Ko. kerngl and 3424 To. tergy. Ta. cerru (cerri-) to set (as a jewel); ceyal setting work in jewelry. Ka. kettu, keccu to enclose, set (as precious stones); kettane, ketne, kettike, kettige act of setting, state of being set. To. ken moz,, (in song) moze xen, ez, xen thick buttermilk, thick mass of butter- mitk (see 430 and 4630). Ka. kene cream of milk. Cf. 1404 Ta. kana. DEDS 292.

Ta. cenni head, summit, elephant’s temple; cenni, cennai cheek. Ma. cenni kenni temples; konni cheek; cennam jaw, cheek; ceppi cheek; ? cella id.; ? cekila celukka, céla gills. Ka. kégu to cry as a peacock; n, the cry of a peacock; kéku, kéke the cry of a peacock; kekku to shout in training cattle. ? Kod. ka+k- (ka+ki-) to call, invite, summon, take (person) with oneself. Pa. kéc- to shave; kéma razor. Ga. (OIL) kés- to shave; k@sal razor. DEDS 294. Min’ Kur, x@s®r (xesr-) neck, shoulders. ‘alt. qasru neck. ?Cf. 1366 Ta. . DED 1662. a Kaguttu

Ta. kéni small tank, well, ditch, trench; kinaru well. Ma. keni temporary well or tank, hole dug in a tiver’s bed, a mine; kinaru well. Ka. kéni temporary well or tank, hole dug in a river’s bed. DED 1663. 1999 Ka, géni rent, contract. Tu. géni id. DED 1664, mae “ee

Pa. kéndid (pl kendil) feather. Ga. (P.) kendif, (S.2) kendut @L kendukul) id.; S., Krishnamurti) kendi (i. kendikil, ken- dikal), (S.3) kendit hair. DEDS 296. 2003 Ma, kémam strength, solidity. Ka. kéma callosity, as that of a wart. DED 1666.

Ta. cémpu, cémpai Indian kales, Colocasia antiquorum, a garden plant, C. indica. Ma. cémpu, cémpa Caladium esculen- tum, Ka. késave, késu, kesa, kesavu taro, Colocasia antiquorum, Arum colocasia L. Tu, cévu, tévu a kind of yam, A. colocasia: Caladium esculentum, Te. céma Colocasia antiquorum, Pa, kibi (pl. kibul) A. colocasia (so correct voc.). Ga, (P.) kiyub Colocasia antiquorum. Ga. (P.) keral story, tale. Pe, kér- (-t) to sing; kérkond song. Mand. ker- to sing. Kui kérondi story, tale, fable. Kuut i i i i cori [182] ket (Su. Isr.) kér- Ch-), (S.) kerh’nai, (D.) kré- (t-) to sing. Kur. khiri tale, fable, legend, riddle. Malt. qéri tale. Cf. 1960 Ta. cirarru and 2009 Ta. kéru. DEDS 297.

Ta. céri town, village, hamlet; street, quarters of the Pariahs. Ma, céri assemblage, village street. Ko. ke+ry, keer strect, cx- ogamous division in Kota village. To. ke+ry street of Badaga village. Te. kréva a side; kré-, as in krégannu outer corner of the eye. Pa, keri side. Kui kéri the side, flank. ? Br. xér behind (rep., ady.); x@r kanning to show one’s back, run away (BDCG, §2.16). DED(S) 1670. 2009 Ta, kéru (kéri-) to cackle (as a hen), speak in a low and tremulous voice, breathe with effort (as with phlegm in the throat). Te, kéru to chuckle with joy, (B. also) warble, cluck (as alaying hen), make a low inarticulate sobbing sound of joy or grief. Kol. ke-ti noise, shout; ke+ri kak- to shout. Cf. 1960 Ta. cirarru and 2006 Ga. keral. ? Cf. 2013 Kur. xér. DED(S) 1671.

Ta. keru. DED 1675. 2014 Kur, x@l tomtom. Malt. qéle a small drum; qélwa a drummer. DED 1676. 2015 Ka, kel a large earthen waterjar. Kor. (O.) kéli rice-pot. Konda kéelu (pl. kétku) earthen pot. Te. kéru to deride, ridicule; kKéradamu ridicule, derision; krédincu to slight; krénincu to jeer, ridicule. Go. (W) kisi derision (Voc. 708). Ta. cérai, carai rat snake, Ptyas mucosus, Ma, c®ra rat snake, Amphisbaena or Coryphodon. Ko. ke+r va+b sp. harmless snake. To. ke+t, keer foeb sp. snake. Ka. kere rat snake, whip-snake, P. mucosus. Ko. ke-d- (ke-dy-) to lean one’s back against support. To, ké-r- (k6-8), sd-r- (sd-d-) to lean against (intr); k6- St- (k6-8ty-) id. (ir). Ta. céru (in cpds. ctrru-) mud, mire, slush, liquid of thick consistency; ceyyal mire, slush; céval mud, mire. Ma, céru (in epds. cérru-) mire, wet soil (for transplanting rice}. Ta. kai (-pp-, -tt-) to adorn, decorate; n, decoration, dressing. Ka. kai, kay, key trim, decoration; keydi state of being dressed or decorated. Te. kai-séyu to adorn, ornament; kai-séta adornment, ornamentation. ? Kui kiva (kit-) to put on (clothes, shoes, ring); pL action kipka (kipki-). DED(S) 1684. 2025 Kui kai rust. Kur. (Hahn) kaiy@ id., moss. DEDS 300.

Ta. kaital, kaitai fragment screw- pine, Pandanus odoratissimus. Ma, kaita id. Ka, kédage, kédige id. Tu. kédai, kedayi, kédayi flower of the tree P. odoratissimus. Te. gédagi P. odoratissimus, {Cf. Skt. ketaka-, ketaki-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3462. DED 1685.
Ka. kaidu, keydu weapon; keydu- kara soldier. Tu. kaidu, kaidu sword. Te. kaiduvu weapon. DED 1686.

Ta. kaintalai, kayini, kaini widow; kaimmai_ widowhood, widow, lovelorn con- dition, kai-kkilai unreciprocated love. Tu. kai-ponjavu a single woman. Pa. kétal, (NE.) kétal widow; kétub widower; kétub cind orphan. Ga, (Ol) kéfal widow. DED(S)
Ta. kaivdram encomium; kaivari panegyrist. Ka. (PBh.) kaivaram praise. Te. kaivaramu praise, encomium, flattery. DEN

Ta. kokkari Cpp-, -tt-) to shout in triumph; cluck, cackle (as goose), chuckle (as crow); 1. shouting. Ma. kokku cackling, chuckling; kokkuka, kokkikka to cackle (as a hen), cluck, pipe, cry asa deer. Ka, kokkok ennu to cackle or cluck as a hen; kokkarisu to threaten, menace, abuse, deride, shout; chuckle, giggle. Te. (B.) kokkarincu to mock, ridicule; shout, cluck. ?8r. kakaring to cackle. DED(S, N) 1688.

Ta. kokki hook, clasp (as of a neck- lace or ear-ring); hooked knife attached to a long bamboo; kokkarai crookedness, deform- ity, rake; kokkuvay the hook of a clasp in an ornament; ? kurafku hook, clasp, link in ‘jewelry; ? kuranfu (kuranti-) to be crooked or bent (as horns, fingers, limbs, fruits), be convulsed, have spasms, coil up (as a small reptile), Ma. kokka clasp, hook, crook (as for plucking fruits), neck-clasp; kokkara crooked, bent backwards. Ko, kok- (koky-) to become very bent with age; koky crook, hook; konk crooked. To. kwiky crook, hook. Ka. kokki, kokke crookedness, perverseness, a crook, bend, hook; kogga, kokkari, konga, kongari crookedness; kofki a hook, fish-hook, angle; konku to be bent, get crooked, curved, dis- torted, deformed, or curled, become perverse, untrue, etc.; 7. (also kofgu) state of being bent, crooked, etc.; kofikisu to make crooked, distort. Kod. kokke crook, hook, anything bent; kokk- (kokki-) to be bent. Tu. kokkeé a hook, clasp; konké, kunké a hook, crooked- ness; crooked, curved, bent; kofgu irregularity, crookedness; bent, curved, arched, irregular. Te. kokki, kofki a hook; kokkemu_ buckle or hook in a jewel, etc.; kofkara crookedness; goggi uneven, irregular, (K.) not straight; (K.) goggipannu/pallu a crooked tooth. Kol. gog- (gokt-) to bend over; gogip- (gogipt-) to make to bend over, bend (¢r.) at a sharp angle; (SR.) konkdi, kokdi crookedly. Nk. ghogg- to bend; konki curved hoe. Nk. (Ch.) konga, kohonga elbow. Pa. kokor- to be b
Ta. kokku mango tree (recorded as a Tulu word). Tu. kukku a mango. Cf. DCV, no. 269. DEDS 201.
Ma. kokku long beak, bill. Ka. kokku beak, bill. Kod. kokki beak. Tu. kokku, kokkayi, kokayi bill, beak. DED 1690.
Ka. kokke-gida Tabernaemontana coronaria Br. Tu. kokke-kayi fruit of the tree T. coronaria. DED 1691.
Tu. gonga a large cowrie, shell. Kui gongo prawn. Kuwi (Su.) goskori shrimp (or with 2044). 2. Cf. 2044 Ta. koficu. / Cf. Skt. gafgata- etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4109. DEDS 303.

Ta. konku, kohkam the Tamil country comprising the districts of Coimbatore, Salem, and a portion of Mysore; kofkan inhabitant of the Konku country. Ma. konfhu n.pr. of the Chéra or Kerala country, esp. the country about Coimbatore; kofinan the king of Kofigu, a king of Kerala. Ko. kong the plains south and east of the Nilgiris; kong may the NE. monsoon. To. kwig the plains south and east of the Nilgiris. Ka. kofigu the Kongu country (Céra or Kerala, esp. the country about Coimbatore); kofga an inhabitant of the Kongu country. 7. kotga the Ctra or Kérala country. DED 1692.
Ta. kotkai woman’s breast, pro- tuberance of a tree. Ma. kohka woman’s breast; kofikacci, kotkicci woman with full breasts. Kui kanguri nipple, teat. DED 1693.
Ka. (Hav.) kojanti refuse (as of fruits). Tu. kujanti, kojanti the refuse of vegetables from which the juice has been expressed,

Ta. koccu thick mess of boiled brinjals, mangoes, etc., seasoned with tama- rind, chilies, salt, etc. Ka. gojju id. Tu. gojji boiled juice or pulp of any fruit, mixed with coconut, salt, chillies, etc., and eaten with boiled rice. DED 1694.

Ta. koccai meanness, despicableness; mean, despicable person; koccu small, young; xossna [185] kotu koccan young boy; koficam little, small quantity; kofican mean person. Ma. koccu short, small, young, mean; (also koccan) little boy, stunted fruit; kocci girl; koficam a little; kofifiu a young stunted coconut. To. kwit humbled, low (comm. by PSS). Ka. kojica, kofice a little, littleness, inferiority. Te. koncemu a little, a few; deficiency, contempt; little, few, slight, mean, deficient. Pa, koyyal lean. Kuwi (S.) kocceka, (Isr) koceka a little. DED(S) 1695. 2042 Kur. xossna (xussya) to burn without flame, catch fire, (fire) catches; xossta’and to ignite, cause (fire) to catch. Malt. qose to be burnt, burn; qostre to burn (fr), in- flame. DED 1696.

Ta. kojficu (kojfici-) to prattle (as children), talk amorously (as young women), fondle; kofical childish prattle, amorous talk, fondling; konnu (konni-) to stammer, stutter, babble; konnai stammering, babbling. Ma. koficuka to prattle, fondle, caress (as a child), flirt; kofifiuka id., stammer, hesitate in speaking; kofical fondling, coquetry; kofifia id., prattle, inarticulate speech; kofifian stammerer. To. kwiz- (kwij-) to ridicule; kwizil ridicule. Ka. koccu, korcu to speak much or braggingly, utter in ostentatious language; konfie (Gowda) babbic, (Hav.) prattle. Tu. koccuni to prate, brag; koeca, kdcé boasting; (B-K.) kofifia prattling; (BRR) kofifié stammering. Kur. (Hahn) kurca’and to stammer, speak like a child. DED(S, N) 1697.
Ta. koficu a kind of fish. Ma. kofican, koficu prawn, lobster. Konda konto shrimp, prawn. Kuwi (Su.) goskori shrimp (or with

Ka. kodata a stick tied to a dog’s neck, by which it is fastened at home or led about. Te. (B.) konata, konatamu a stick to tie a dog to; (VPK) kodatam, kontam a hooked stick on which are hung ropes and other accessories of the plough; a stick 2 feet long, at right angles to the harrow, to which the animals are attached by a rope. DED (S) 1699.

Ma. kofanna, kutanna both handsful. Kod. kodande double handful (with two hands together). Te. konidili a handful. Go. (Tr.) kofop a double handful; (Ma.) kork mendu a full double handful; korov double handful; folded hands; (Mu.) korv ar- to worship with folded hands; (Tr.) karwe kiana to beseech, pray to a god; (SR.) karve kiyana to worship (Voc. 938). Kui groda the scoop or receptacle made by holding both hands together. Kuwi (Su.) gro’li, gro’leka, (S.) glo’oli, glo’oleka, (Isr.) groleka, (T.) gorli a double handful; Csr.) gro%ti cupped hands. DED(S, N) 1700.
Ka. Kogame, kodambe a double basket for catching fish. Te. (B.) kodama a basket in which fish are snared. DED 1701.
Ka. godave connexion, concern with. Tu. godavé id. Te. (K.) godava bother, worry, fuss. DED(S) 1703.

Ta. kofti banner, flag, streamer; kStu summit of a hill, peak, mountain; k6tai mountain; kofar peak, summit of a tower; kuvatu mountain, hill, peak; kufumi summit of a mountain, top of a building, crown of the head, bird’s crest, tuft of hair (esp. of men), crown, projecting corners on which a door swings. Ma. kofi top, extremity, flag, banner, sprout; kOtu end; kuvatu hill, mountain-top; kufuma, kuftumma narrow point, bird’s crest, pivot of door used as hinge, lock of hair worn as caste distinction: kottu head of a bone. Ko. kory flag on temple; kof top tuft of hair (of Kota boy, brahman), crest of bird; kuf clitoris. To. kwit tip, nipple, child’s back lock of hair. Ka. kudi pointed end, point, extreme tip of a creeper, sprout, end, top, flag, banner; gudi point, flag, banner; kudilu sprout, shoot; kédu a point, the peak or top of a hill; koftu a point, nipple, crest, gold orna- ment wor by women in their plaited hair; kotta state of being extreme; kofta-kone the extreme point; (Hav.) kodi sprout; Kod. kodi top (of mountain, tree, rock, table), rim of pit or tank, flag. Tu. kodi point, end, extremity, sprout, flag; kodipuni to bud, germinate; (B-K.) kodipu, kodipelu a sprout; kodiré the top-leaf; koftu cock’s comb, peacock’s tuft. Te. kodi tip, top, end or point of a flame; kotta-kona the very end or extremity. Kol. (Kin.) kori point. Pa. kiltor cock’s comb. Go. (Tr.) koddi tender tip or shoot of a plant or tree; koddi (S.) end, tip, (Mu.) tip of bow; (A.) kodi point (Voc. 8

Ta. koti creeper, umbilical cord. Ma. koti creeper, what is long and thin, umbilical cord, ete. Ko. kory creeper; koc binding (for firewood, etc.) made from plant. To, kwiry creeper. Kod. kodi ele betel leaf. Pe. godi creeper. Mand. kuri id. Cf. 1678, esp. Kur. kudda. DED (S) 1705.
Ta. koticci jaws; kofiru, kotuppu cheek, jaw. Ma. kofififia temples. Tu. kodefiji the inside of the cheeks. Kuwi (F.) kiidra, (isr.) kidru jaw (of human beings). DED 1706.
Ta. kotiru pincers. Ma. kotil tongs. Ko. kor hook of tongs. /Cf. Skt. (P. 4.4.18) kutilika- smith’s tongs. DED(S) 1707.

Ta. kotu (-pp-, -tt-) to give (to 3rd person; Tolk. Coll, 30), bring forth, allow: kotuppu giving; kotai giving away as a gift; kotaimai munificence; kotatyon munificent person. Ma, kotukka to give (to 3rd person): kotuppikka to cause to give. Ko. kor- (kof-) kotu [186] to give to 3rd person. To. kwir- (kwirt-) id.; kwar fine levied by assembly. Ke, kodu, kudu (koft-) to give, allow, emit (as a sound); kodu, kodu, koduvike, koduha giving; kodage, kodige gift, grant; kodisu, kudisu to cause to give; kodisuvike causing to give. Kod. kodi- (kodip-, kodit-) to give to 3rd person. Tu. korpini to give; korpavuni to cause to give, give through another; korpata giving; (B-K.) koru, kolu to give, grant. ? Kur. kurpi what is given to a servant above his yearly pay. DED(N) 1708. 2054 (a) Ta. kotu curved, bent, crooked; kotumai_ crookedness, obliquity; kofukki hooked bar for fastening doors, clasp of an ornament; kofun-kay cucumber; kofus-kai folded arm; kotu-maram bow; kotu-vay curved or bent edge (as of billhook); kotu- val pruning knife, billhook, sickle, battle- axe; kuta curved, bent; kufakkam bend, curve, crookedness; kutakki that which is crooked; kutakkiyan humpback; kuftahku (kutanki-) to bend (@tr.); kutatikai palm of hand; kufantai curve; kutavu (kutavi-) to be crooked, bent, curved; m. bend, curve; kut@ bend, curve; kdfu (k6ti-) to bend, be crooked, go astray, be biased; . crookedness, obliquity; k6tal bending, curving; koti bend, curve; kodttam bend, curve, warp, partiality,

Ta. kotu cruel, severe; koftumai cruelty, tyranny, severity, roughness, un- couthness, vileness; kotuku (kofuki-) to be cruel, ruthless; kotuakai, kofumpu severity, harshness, oppression; kotuvai wickedness, mischievousness; kofiiram cruelty, severity; kdtu hardship, oppression. Ma. kotu extreme, steep; severe, intense, cruel; kofufkai op- pression; kofuppam — severity, intensity; kotuma tyranny; (Kaut.) kofiyan a fierce, angry man. Ka. kududu steepness. Te. (B.) godiga id. .Kuwi (Isr.) kof- Cit-) to be hot, fierce (of sun). DED(S) 1710.

Ta. kofuku (koftuki-) to shrink or shiver with cold. Ma. kofuka to feel very cold; kdttam coldness, stiffness. Ka. kodu to be cool or cold; 7. coldness; kofa, kodata coldness. Cf. 2203 Ta. kdfai. DED 1711.
Ta. kofu-véeli, kotu-véri, kofi-véli Ceylon leadwort, Plumbago zeylanica. Ma. kofu-véli, kofu-véri id. DED 1712.

Ta. kottakai shed with sloping roofs, cow-stall; marriage pandal; Koffam cattle- shed; koffil cow-stall, shed, hut; (STD) kotambe feeding place for cattle. Ma. kotfil cowhouse, shed, workshop, house. a. kottage, kotige, kottige stall or outhouse (esp. for cattle), barn, room. Kod. kotti shed. Tu. kotta hut or dwelling of Koragars; kotya shed, stall. Te. kot{imu stable for cattle or horses; kottayi thatched shed. Kol. (Kin.) korka, (SR.) korka cowshed; (Pat., p. 59) kontodi henhouse. Nk. khota cowshed. Nk. (Ch.}) korka id. Go. (Y.) kota, (Ko.) kotam (pl. kotak) id. (Voc. 880); (SR.) kotka shed; (W. G. Mu. Ma.) korka, (Ph.) korka, kurka cowshed (Voc. 886); (Mu.) koftorla, kotorli shed for goats (Voc. 884). Malt. kota hamlet. / Influenced by Skt. gostha-. DED(S) 1713.

Ta. kottam hollow piece of bamboo for giving medicine to cattle. Kea. kotta, gotta bamboo tube for administering food or medicine to cattle. Tu. kondé a hollow piece of bamboo. Te. (B.) gottamu tube, cylinder. Go. (SR.) ghotang (pl) pipe (Voc. 1257); (Mu.) gotta small wooden case for carrying tobacco (Voce. 1188); (Ph.) ghonta a section of bamboo used as a receptacle for oil or salt (Voc. 1258); (ASu.) gotta quiver. Konda gofem a bamboo pipe to keep a cigar in. Cf.
Ta. koftfam small ola basket; kottu basket made of rattan. Ma. kotta basket. DED 1715.
Ta. kotti an aquatic plant, Apono- geton monostachyum. Ma. kotti id. Te. (DCYV) kotfi an aquatic plant. DED(S) 1716.
Ta. (DCV) Kottil kokra laurel. Ma. (DCV) kofttili id. DEDS 304.

Ta. koffu (koffi-) to beat (as a drum, tambourine), hammer, beat (as a brazier), clap, strike with the palms, pound (as paddy); n. beat, stroke, drumbeat, time-measure; kot{an, kotfan mallet; kof¢i time-measure; koftfanam pounding or husking paddy; kofu Cpp-, -tt-) to thrash, abuse roundly; kofai blows, round abuse. Ma. kottuka to beat so as to produce a sound (as drum, metals, bells), clap hands; kotfu beating a drum, clapping hands, buffet, knocking of knees against each other; kotti mallet; koftanam beating the husk from paddy in a slovenly manner; kojukka to flog. Ko. -kotk- (kotky-) to strike (with small hammer), knock on (door), strike tipcat in hole in ground. To. kwifk- (kwifky-) to tap (on door, something with stick); kwit fil} woodpecker. Ka. kodati, kodganti a wooden hammer; koft{ana beating the husk from paddy; kofftuha beating; kudu to beat. Kod. kotf- (kotfi-) to tap, beat (drum). Tu. kodapuni to forge, hammer; kodapavuni to weld, forge together; (B-K.) kudapu to hammer metallic objects; korpini to beat. Te. kottu to beat, strike, knock; strike (as a clock); ”. a blow, stroke. Pa. koff- to strike with axe. Ga. (Oll.) kof- id. Go. kot- (Mu.) to cut with axe, (Ko.) strike with horn (Voc. 888); kofela (A.) mallet, (Mu. Ma.) drumstick (Voc. 882); (SR. Tr. W. Ph.) kohkanad to crush, pound, butt, gore; kohk- (Mu.) to gore, (G.) thresh with flail; (Ma.) ko?k- to butt (Voc. 959); (ASu.) kohk- to grind into a paste, pound (in a mortar), - beat staiks of millet, wheat, et

Ta. koftu (koffi-) to sting (as a scorpion, wasp); . stinging; hoe with short handle, weeding-hoe, spade; kofukku sting of a wasp, hornet, scorpion, claws of a crab, lobster; té{-kotfan a green insect whose touch produces the same sensation as a scorpion-sting; téf-kuticci a black bee (for tét-, see 3470 Ta. tel). Ma. kottuka to sting (of scorpion); kefukka scorpion’s sting. Ko. kotk- (kofky-) (snake) strikes, bites; ? kako-t hoe with sharp, broad blade (for ka-, see 1265). Ka. kutuku to sting (as a scorpion); kuduku to peck; kukku to peck, strike something with a stone, etc., in a pecking manner, dig up the ground slightly with a hoe; (Hav.) kodappu to peck. Ti. kodapuni, (B-K.) kudapu to bite (as a serpent), peck, strike with the beak; kukkuli pecking; kukku- liyuni to peck; koffu, kofré spade; (B-K.) kudpolu a hornet. Nk. kork- to peck. Pa. kodk- id.; kotf- id., dig; kotal hoe. Ga. (OlL.) kof- to dig, (fowl) to peck; kotal hoe, spade; (S.} kof- to bite (as a snake). Go. koftaina (SR. Tr.) to peck, pierce leaves and sew them for platters, (Ph.) to pierce, thrust; (A.) koft- to hoe; (M.) kofana to sew; (Tr.) gottana to poke or thrust with a stick or finger; kOfstand to have one’s ears pierced (Voc. 888): (Sr. Tr. W. Ph.) kohkana to prick, puncture, tattoo; (Mu. Ko.) kohk-, (Ma.) ke?k- to peck (Voc. 959). Pe, kof- (-t-) to dig, hoe, (snake) to bite: kodgi hoe. Mand. kuf- (-t-) (snake) to bite, (hen) to peck. Kui (K.) kodi hoe. Kuwi (Su. Mah. Isr.) korgi, (F.) kargi, (S.) korg

Ta. kottu (koffi-) to pour forth, shower down, empty the contents of a basket or sack, throw into a vessel; drop (as leaves), fall off (as hair); 1. pouring, empty- ing; kotfam flowing, pouring. Ma. kottuka to shoot out, empty a sack. ? Te. kotfukoni- povu to be carried along by stream or air current. DED(N) 1719.

Ta. ko{fam arrogance, mischievous- ness, petulance. Ka. gotfu state of being hard to get or very scarce; obstinacy. Je. gotfu hard, difficult; stubborn; godda stub- born. Cf. 2079 Ta. konti. DED(S) 1721.

Pa. kordel el, (S.) kddel bandicoot. Go. (Mu.) koftél, (Ma.) kofteli, (Ko.) kofel uppe sp. rat (Voce. 883). DEDS 306. 2069 7a. kottai seed of any kind not enclosed in chaff or husk, nut, stone, kernel; testicles; (RS, p. 142, items 200, 201) koftan- kacci, koftacci coconut shell. Ma. kofta kernel of fruit, particularly of coconut, castor-oil seed; kurafta, kuratfa kernel; kuranfi stone of palmfruit. Ko. ket testes; scrotum. Ka. kotte, gorate stone or kernel of fruit, esp. of mangoes; gotta mango stone. Kod. korandi id. Tu. kotté kernel of a nut, testicles; kottafiji a fruit without flesh; kotfayi a dried areca-nut; korantu kernel or stone of fruit, cashew-nut; gotfu kernel of a nut as coconut, almond, castor-oil seed. Te. kuridi dried whole kernel of coconut. Kol. (Kin.) gorva stone of fruit. Vk. gorage stone of fruit. Kur. gofa any seed which forms inside a fruit or shell. Malt. gota a seed or berry. /Cf. words meaning ‘fruit, kernel, kofttaiy -ilantai [188] koti seed’ in Turner, CDIAL, no. 4271 (so noted by Turner). DED(S, N) 1722.

Ta. kottaiy-ilantai woody-fruited jujube, Zizyphus xvlopyrus; kontai jujube tree. Ka. kotfa, kodaci-gida, godaci-gida Z. xylopy- rus Willd. Tu. kotfe-mullu id. Te. gotti, (VPK) gofike id. Go. (M.) ghotia id.; (Tr.) ghatol mara id., the ghont tree [Z. x.] (Voe. £260), /Cf. Skt. ghonfa- a kind of jujube; (Schmidt, Nach irage) gota- Zizyphus jujuba. DED(S) 1723. 2071 Kol. kotte small stick, match, (Kin.) arrow. Nk. kotte small stick. Go. (ASu.) kottéyla stick. DED 1724. 2072 Kur. xofta the bel fruit, Aegle marmelos. Malt. qote id. DED 1725.
Ka. godda peril, jeopardy. Te. god- damu_ peril, evil; godaba, godava danger, calamity. DED 1726.

Ta. koftu barren woman. Ko. godn man who has no children; gody barren woman. Ka. goddu state of being barren, sapless; a barren cow, etc.; goddi barren woman. Tu. goddu barren. Te. goddu barren, empty; barrenness, a barren woman or animal; any beast; (B.) goddu, a beast; godlu (p/.) kine, horned cattle: (Satk.) godduratu, godralu barren woman. Go. (S.) god (pl gorku), (Ma.) godu, (Ko.) god(u) (pl godk) cow; (M.) god(u) cattle, cow (Voc. 1189); (Koya Su.) goddu (pl godku) cow. Konda (BB, 1972) godu barren; (K.) godu godra cattle, etc. DED 1727 (Go. from DED(S) 1823).
Tu. konajé a fly infesting the eyes. Te. konuju, konusu a tick. Ga. (S.3) konsul a tick (? pl). DEDS 307.
Ka. konapi a flail. Te. gunapamu iron crowbar. Konda gunpam spade. Cf.
Ta. kopfal cloud, rain. Ma. kontal cloud, sky. Te. (VPK) beda-gondalu big massive rainy clouds. DED(S) 1730.

Ta. konfi insubordinate, naughty person or animal; prostitute, concubine. Ma. konti a stray cow, unruly woman. Ka. kKoftu a shameless man. Tu. kopta viciousness, stupidity; vicious, stupid. Te. konte a cunning, mischievous, or prankish person; cunning, etc.; gonfu a wicked man; fem. gonti; gonfari a_crucl man. Kur, kotto fornication. Cf.
Ka. kondi the sting of a scorpion. Tu. kondi_a sting. Te. kondi the sting of a scorpion. Cf. 2064 Ta. kottu. DED 1732.

Ta. kontai tuft, dressing of hair in large coil on the head, crest of a bird, head {as of a nail), knob (as of a cane), round top. Ma. konfa tuft of hair. Ko. gond knob on end of walking-stick, head of pin; kond knot of hair at back of head. To. kwidy Badaga woman’s knot of hair at back of head (< Badaga konde). Ka. konde, gonde tuft, tassel, cluster. Kod. konde tassels of sash, knob-like foot of canestem. Tu. gondé topknot, tassel, cluster. Te. konde, (K. also) Kondi knot of hair on the crown of the head. Cf. 2049 Ta. kofi. /Cf. Skt. kunda- clump (e.g. darbha-kunda-), Pkt. (DVM) gondi- = mafijari-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3266; cf. also Mar. géda cluster, tuft. DED (S) 1733. 2082 Kur. xondxa, xOrx¥ deep; a pit, abyss. Malt. qonde deep, low lands. Cf.
Ka. gonnevuru an insect found in timber (cf. 4312 Ta. pugu). Kui gungu a large wood-boring insect. het Skt. ghuna- wood- worm; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4482. DEDS 309.

Ta. koti (-pp-, -tt-) to boil, bubble up from heat, effervesce, be heated (as the body, ground, etc.), be enraged, be offended, burn with desire; ». bubbling up (as of boiling water or oil), heat (as of fire, weather, etc.), fever, rage, grief, desire; koti-koti- to bubble up (as boiling rice); kotippu, kotiyal boiling, bubbling up, heat, fever, rage, grief; kotiyan one who hankers after food: kutampu (kutampi-) to boil up, bubble up (as boiling water), get angry; kutukutu (-pp-, -tt-) to desire eagerly; kutukutuppu eagerness, desire. Ma. koti eagerness, greediness; kotikka to be greedy, envious, covet; kotiyan glutton. Ko. kode- (kode-) to quiver (of water about to boil, flesh of animal just killed, eyelid). To. kwi8y- (...) (water) approaches boiling- point; kwi@yet-, kwiOkyef- to make (water) boil; ? kwiy- (kwic-} to bubble, boil. Ka. kudi to boil, bubble up, boil up, suffer pain or vexation in the mind: n, boiling, etc., grief; kudisu, kudiyisu, kudasu_ to boil (t.); kudige, kudiha boiling, ete.; kutakuta, kotakota the noise of boiling water; kudakal, kudapal state of being (partially) boited; (K.7) kuduguli glutton, greedy person; (Hav.) kodi to boil; desire to eat; n. boiling; kodippatu greedy. Kod. kodi- (kodip-, kodic-) to boil Gntr.) with bubbling noise, feel love for, kiss, feel concerned for; kodi love, desire. Tu. kodipuni, kodiyuni to boil (intr.), seethe; kodipavuni to cause to boil; kodipelu, kodupelu act of boiling; kodi greediness, eagerness; kudipuni, kudyun
Ka. (Hav.) kodilu curry. Tu. koddelu, koddyelu a highly seasoned soup or pulse.
Ta. kotuku, kocu, kocuku mosquito, gnat, fly. Ma. kotu, kotuku mosquito, gnat, ir. kucune mosquito. To. kwi@f sp. biting insect. DED 1736.

Ta. kotumpu coconut fibre; kutam- pai rolf of palmyra leaves or cloth worn in the ear-lobe to widen the perforation; katam- pai coconut husk or fibre that covers the nut, ends and bits of palmyra leaves cut for writing; kdtu refuse, waste, empty kernels, etc.; covering, capsule, pod; fibrous structure of flowers. Ma, kotumpu, kotumpil strong fibrous fruit-stalk of coconut, used for strings; husk of corn before the ears burst, covering of coconut blossoms; kotampa fibres used as string; katampa husk of coco- nut. Tu. kodumbu, kudumbu the cymbiform sheath of a bunch of coconuts. Cf. 2224 Ta. kompai. DED 1737.
Ta. kotuvai a pledge, pawn, mortgage. Te, kuduva, koduva id. DED 1738.
Ka. (Hav.) kottalige stalk of a coco- nut branch. 7u. kottalige, (B-K.) kottalige a palm branch stripped of its leaves. Kor. (M.) kontalgi stalk of a coconut branch.

Ta. kottalam bastion. Ma, kottalam, kontalam bulwark, bastion; trough, stone pavement. Ka. kottala, kottala bulwark, bastion. Tu. kottala, kottala id.; trough, stone pavement. Te. krottalamu, k(rotta- damu bastion. DED 1739.

Ta. kottu (kotti-) to grub up, hoe, peck (as a bird), bite (as a snake), chop, hack, mince, carve; ». grubbing, mincing, pecking, biting (as a serpent), gashing, a small hoe for rooting out weeds; kottal pecking; kottan a mason; kontu (konti-) to peck, mince, gnaw, gore, pierce; pick up shells, etc., from the floor before a thrown- up shell comes down (as in game played by girls); kontal picking, nibbling. Ma. kottuka to dig, carve, peck, pick up, bite (as snakes), strike, cut; kottu stinging, digging, pecking, picking up; kotti pick-axe, stone-digger, carver. Ko. kot- (koty-) to peck, bite, hook (small stick) in playing tipcat; kot a peck. To. kwi€k- (kwi8ky-) to peck, (snake) bites. Ka. kottu to chop, mince. Kod. kott- (kotti-) to pick up with single sharp motion, peck; kotti para game of jackstones. Tu. godduni to pick or devour, as a fowl. Te. kondu to mince, cut or chop into small pieces. Wk. gondip- to tattoo. Go. (Mu.) gond- to cut with axe (Voc. 1199); gonda (Mu.) piece, (Ma.) fog of wood (Voc. 1230); (LuS.) gonda bit, piece. Konda kot- (-t-) (BB) to peck, (K.) hammer, hit hard on the top. Pe. gonda piece, piece of wood, log. Kui kospa (kost-) to beat, strike with stick or mallet; peck, bite. sting; n. a beating, bite, sting. Kuwi kot- (-h-) (Su.) to dig with hoe, (Isr.) peck, hoe: (F.) kéthali to hoe; (S.) kothnai to pick (with pickaxe); kotpinai to haggle, hash (ie. mince: pl. action). Kur. xotna/xosna (xottas) to cut by striking, slash, inflict a gash, wound by a b

Ta. kottu bunch, cluster, assembly. family; kontu cluster of flowers, gathering. multitude. Ma, kottu bunch of leaves or flowers, cluster. Ka. kottu bunch, cluster: kottani, kottari multitude, flock, assemblage: gondani, gondana, gondala mass, assemblage. concourse of people, crowd; gondanisu to assemble; gutti, gudi bunch or cluster of fruits or flowers. Te. gutti, gudi, (B.) gottu bunch, cluster. Cf. 1639 Ta. kuccu. / Cf. Pkt. (DNM) kunti- = majijari-; (ONM, Norman} gutti- garland. DED(S, N) 1741.
Ta. kottukkaran head of a company of labourers. Ko. gotga-rn treasurer of the village. Ka. gottu appointment, rule, regu- lation; gottugara headman. DED 1742.

Ta. kottai rottenness, blot, blemish. fibrous rising on cloth, scab, scale. Ma. kotta dust, dirt as on the clothes of a traveller. Tu. kotté trash, dirt; gotté sweepings, rubbish DED(S) 1743. 2095 Kur. (Hahn) xotor injo a kind of fish. Malt. qotro minu id. DEDS 310.

Ka. godda a kind of black ant, the bite of which is painful; ? korale a kind of ant. Te. (B.) gorre-cedalu (Kitt. gorre-cedalu) soldier ants (cf. 1548 Ta. cital). ? Kol, (Kin.) kanda a big ant (with Ga., Kamaleswaran). ? Ga. (S.) kondki, (S.2) kondke large black ant. ?Go. (Mu. Ma.) gdde, (Ko.) godo id. (Voc. 1226). DED(S, N) 1744.

Ta. kontalam confusion, perturbation. ‘turmoil; kontali (-pp-, -tt-) to be rough or boisterous, swell (as the sea), be stormy. tumultuous. Ka. gondala confusion, bustle. Te. kondalamu sorrow, perturbation. /Cf. Apabhramsa (Mahapurana) gondala- a battle kontalam [190] kompu gathering, clamour or tumult and confusion (cf. Shriyan, pp. 220-2). DED 1745.
Ta. kontalam a kind of dance; konti mask, masquerade dance. Te. gondili, gondli dancing. / Cf. Skt. gaundali- a kind of dancing (Mandakranta Bose, Classical Indian Dancing, p.9, etc.). DED 1746.
Ka. gondi, gondu alley, lane, narrow assage in the ocean, strait. Te. gondi corner, ane. DED 1747.

Tu. kondra smail, little. Te. koddi a little, a few; mean, vile, low, base; a small quantity, a trifle; koddiga a little, slightly; konta some, a little, a part; kondaru some, a few persons; konni some, a few; konndallu a few days. Kui gonde, gondeka some. DEDS 312.
Ta. koppam keddah for elephants. Ma. koppam pitfall for catching elephants. DED 1749.

Ta. kopparai brass or copper boiler with rings for. handles, cauldron. Ma. koppara a boiler, chiefly of copper. Ka. koppara, kopparige a metal boiler, used also for keeping oil or ghee in. 7u. kopparigé cauldron, boiler with a wide mouth. Te. koppera a metal cauldron or boiler. /Cf. Mar. kopra small iron pot; Turner, CDJAL, no. 3519. DED(S) 1750.

Ta. kopparai, koppari dried coconut kernel, copra. Ma. koppara id. Ka. kobbari, kobari id. Tu. koppara, koppari, kobbari, kobari id. Te. kobbari, kobbera kernel of the coconut: pertaining to the coconut; kobbari- kaya, kobbera-kaya a coconut. Kuwi (Su.} kobri id. ? Cf. 2146 Ta. kogu. / Cf. Skt. (lex, ) kilrpasa- the inner part of a coconut. Cf. H. khopa, khopra, Mar. khobré; Turner, CD/AL, no. 3936. DED(S) 1751.

Ta. koppalam, koppul, koppulam bubble; pustule, blister; koppali (-pp-, -tt-), koppuli Gpp- -tt-) to blister, rise in bubbles (as water), puff out the cheeks and blow; kopptgr pustule, blister; kumig (-pp-, -tt-) to be spherical or conical, form into bubble; kumigppu bubbling up; kumigi © bubble; Cpp-, -tt-) to bubble, rise in bubbles or pustules. Ma. koppul bubble, pustule; kumala, kumila, kuvala bubble; kumalekka to bubble up. Ka. kuppalisu to blister; (Tipt.) gobbe bubble. Tu. gobbuli id. Cf. 4455 Ta. pokuftu. DED(S, N) 1752.

Ta. koppali (Cpp-, -tt-), koppuli Gpp-, -tt-) to gargle. Ma. koppulikka id. Tu. koppalipuni to gargle, rinse the mouth. Pa, kupulp- (kupult-) id. Cf. 2114 Kol. kopli. DED 1753. 2108 (a) Ta. koppi a game of young girls attended with clapping of hands, singing, and dancing (< Te.). Tu. gobbu a play, dance, game; gobbuni to play, trifle with, mock; gobbavuni to make one play; gobbafa amuse- ment. Te. gobbi a dance of young women, accompanied with clapping of hands to a song by all; gobbillu dancing, capering. (>) Ta. kuravai dance in a circle prevalent among the women of sylvan or hill tracts. Pa. kurral, kurval a kind of dance. (In (a) -bb- is probably assimilated from -rv-.) DED (S) 1754.

Ta. koppu chignon, coil of hair. Ka, koppu female’s hair tied in a tuft. Te. koppu chignon. Go. (SR. Ch. G. Ko.) kupar, (Ma.) kupari chignon; (Tr.) kilpar topknot of hair (Voc. 759); (LuS.) koopagai the tuft of hair left on the head. Konda kopu (pl. kopku) hair (of men or women). Kui kopa coil of a woman’s hair; kupuli crest of a bird, tuft of feathers. Kuwi (i.) kiibbt@ coiffure (female); (Mah. Isr.) kubbu chignon, woman’s hair-bun. Kur. (Grignard, Bleses, Tiga) khop® young people's back hair when raised and folded up, chignon; (Hahn) xoppa& chignon. /Cf. H. khopa, etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3939. DED(S) 1755,

Ta. kapp-enal onom. expr. of moving quickly, swiftly or suddenly; kupp-enal expr. signifying suddenness. Te. gobbuna quickly, swiftly, at once. Kui gupna, gopna fitfully, suddenly, hastily. DED 1757. 2113 (a) Go. (Mu.) kope, marka kope a kind of insect (marka mango; Voce. 977). Kui kopatora, (P.) kopolosi grasshopper, locust. Kuwi (T.) gop’iri, (Kasipur) gop’eri, (S.) gdperi, sr.) gop%eri grasshopper. Malt. qopo id., locust. (b) Ta. k6xi grasshopper. Go. (ASu.) khora id. DEDS(N) 314. 2114 Kol. kopli mouth. Nk. kopli id. Pa, kuplong cheek. Cf. 2107 Ta. koppali. DED 1758.

Ta. kompu branch, horn of an animal, musical horn, tusk; kompan tusked or horned animal, clever man (used ironically); koppu branch. Ma. kompu horn, tusk, musical horn, branch, pole, mast, spear; kompan horned, male of cattle; a valiant, arrogant person; kompikka to grow arrogant, angry. Ko. kob branch, horn of an animal, musical horn, tusk; kombn man who thinks well of himself, boastful man; fem. komby. To. kub horn blown by Kota musician, Ka. kombay [194] kokl- kombu branch, horn of an animal, musical horn, tusk, root; kombe, (Nanj.) kome branch. Kod. kombi branch, horn of an animal. Tu. kombu id., musical horn, tusk; (B-K.) kombe a buck deer; kombelu a horn used by a toddy-tapper. Te. kommu horn, musical horn, tusk; komma branch. Kol. kom branch of tree, horn. Nk. komm horn, Ga. (S.2) kommu_ horn. Go. (Ko.) koma branch of tree (Voc. 912). Konda koma (pl. komey) branch of tree: komu (pl. komku) horn, Pe, koma, komo id. Mand. kumu id. Kui (Mah., p. 103) gopka branches. Kuwi (Su.) komma (pl. -na), (F.) koma (pl. koOmanga), (S.) komma branch; (Su. P.) kommu (pl. komka), (F.) komt (pl. k6mka), (S.) kommu horn. DED (S, N) 1759. :

Ta. kommai_ clothes-basket. Ma. komma a little bag of straw or cloth, purse. Ka. komme corn-bin. Kod. komme basket for storing things. Te. kummi, gummi a high basket for holding corn. Nk. ghummi large bamboo receptacle for storing grain. DED(S) 1761.
Ta. kommai rampart, bulwark. Ka. kumbe wall on a flat roof that serves for a balustrade. Te. komma, k6dfta-komma_ the upper part or coping of a fort-wall. DED 1762.

Ta. koy (-v., -t-) to pluck (as flowers), cut, reap, shear (as hair), snip off, choose, select; kuyam sickle, reaping-hook, curved knife, razor; kuvil reaping, cutting. Ma. koyka to cut, reap, crop, mow; koyyal, koyttu reaping; koyil the hire of a fruit- gatherer. Ko, koy- (koc-) to cut; koyl harvest, reaping. To. kwiy- (kwis-) to pluck (fruits), (bee) sucks. Ka. koy (koyd-, kod-), kuy to cut, saw, crop, reap, pluck (as fruit); x. cutting, etc.; koyilu, kuyilu cutting, reaping, plucking; koyita, koyikatana, kuyyuvike cutting; koyika man who cuts; fem. koyke; koyki woman whose (ears, etc.) have been cut. Kod. koy- (koyyuv-, kojj-) to pluck, harvest. Tu. koipini, koyipini, koyyuni to cut, reap, mow; koyilu, koilu reaping, harvest, a small paddy field; koyyelu harvest. Te. koyu to cut, sever, divide, reap, pluck; k6ta cutting, reaping. ? Kol. (SR.) kayipen to cut (tir hair; Kamaleswaran). Pa. koy- (kofi-) to reap, harvest; koval stubble. Ga. (Oli.) koy- (kod-, kon-) to reap; (S) koy- to cut. Go. (A. Y. Mu. Ma. Ko. etc.) koy- to reap, cut, pluck (fruit); (Tr.) koiana, kdiyana to gather berries, mangoes, etc., harvest wheat or crops; (M.) koidana to reap; (Ko.) koyal stubble (Voce. 914). Konda koy- (-t-) to reap, harvest, cut, pluck (flowers, etc.); (Sova dial.) koer, (BB) ko%er (0b1 ko%eR-) sickle. Pe. koy- (-t-) to cut (grass, weeds, etc.): koyes, kaves sickle. Kui kodva (kot-) to reap, cut off; . reaping, harvesting; kderi harvest, reaping. Kuwi (F.) koiyali (kot-) to pluck. Ku

Te. koyya stick, rod, staff, ship’s mast, wood; wooden; Nk. koyya stalk of jowar. Pa. koyya stick, drum-stick, thorn, sting. Ga. (S.2) koyya quill of porcupine. Go. (Ko.) koyye id., thorn (Voc. 916). fonda koya a wooden piece, log. DED(S) 2121 Ko, koyk Rhamnus Wightii. To. kakakoy id. DEN 28.

Ma. (Kanikkar dial.; Bhattacharya) kora asthma. Ko. kor kor in- to make the sound of a death-rattle. Ka. kora, gora sound produced in the throat by hoarseness, the purr of a cat; kora kora ennu that sound to be produced in the throat; kore to snore. Tu. korape, korapele one who snores; korapelu snoring. Cf. 1796 Ta. kurai. DED 1765 (and from 1496, 1538).
Ma. kurajifii mire, mud, fine clay. Ko. kor manure, filth, mud. Jo. kwar mud. DEDS(N) 315. .

Ka. koradu, kodda a kind of rainbow appearing in a straight form. Te. koradu a red streak in the clouds, (B.) a gleam or streak of a rainbow in the clouds; (VPK) koradu, korru rainbow. Konda (BB) kurveli id. DED(S) 1766.

Ta. kokku common crane, Grus cinerea; stork, paddy bird; kuruku heron, stork, crane, bird, gallinaceous fowl, anril bird. Ma. kokku, kokkan, kocca, kuriyan paddy bird, heron; kuru heron. To. ko&k heron. Ka. kokku, kokkare crane; kukku heron, crane. 7u. korfigu crane, stork. Te. konga, kokkera, kokkarayi crane; pegguru, begguru (< peru-kuru) adjutant crane. Kol. (Kin.) konga crane. Pa. kokkal (pl. kokkacil) id. Ga. (S) kokkale (pl kokkasif) heron; (8.2) konalin (pl konasil), (S.3) kokalin crane. Go. (L.) koruku id. (Voc, 921); (Mu.) kokodal heron, duck (Voc. 870); (Ma. Ko.) konga crane (Voc. 874). Kui kohko paddy bird. Kuwi (S.) kongi, (T.) kokora crane. Br. xaxiir demoiselle crane. /Cf. Skt. katka- er Turner, CDIAL, no. 2595. DED(S)
Pa. gorka spear. Go. (SR. Mu. M. S. Ko.) gorka, (Ma.) gohka id. (Voc. 1205); (Lus.) gohka id. Cf. 2064 Ta. kotfu. DED
Ka. korcu, koccu stench of urine. Tu. kujary id., any bad smell. Kor. (O.) kotte urine. Cf. 1635 Konda kus-. DED(N) 1362.

Ga. (S.2) gorpa shell of tortoise. Kuwi (Su.) gorpo id., shell of egg; (sr.) gorpa shell of tortoise; skull. DEDS 317. 2130 Kur. xor°p pus; xor°pna (xurpy4) to ripen as a boil, suppurate. Malt. qorpu pus. Cf. 1780 Ta. kuru (Krishnamurti, Language 39.561). DED(S) 1771.

Ta. kol (kolv-, kong-) to kill, murder, destroy, ruin, fell, reap (as the heads of grain), afflict, tease; n. act of killing, affliction; kolli that which kills; kolai killing, murder, vexation, teasing; kolaifian, kolainan, kolai- van murderer, hunter. Ma. koliuka to kill, murder; kollikka to make to kill; kolli killing; kula killing, murder. Ko. kol act of killing. kol gal thief; kolga»rn murderer. To. kwaly murder; murderous (in song, of buffalo, kite, dry season); who has died or is near death (in song, old man or woman): kwalyxo-yn mur- derer; kogn the messenger of the god of death (lit. the killer). Ka. kol, kolu, kolfu (kond-) to kill, murder; kolisu, kollisu, kolsu to cause to kill; kole killing, murder, slaughter; kolega murderer; kolluvike killing; kuli a killer. Kod. koll- (kolluy-, kond-) to kill. Tu. kolé murder. Kor. (M.) koru, (T.) kori, (O.) korru to kill. Te. (B.) kollu id.; (Safk.) kola sin, (K. also) murder, holocaust, enmity. Br. xalling to strike, kill, fire (gun), throw (stone); ?xath pain, labour pains, colic (cf. Ta. kol affliction). DED(S) 1772.

Ta. kol working in iron, blacksmith; kollan blacksmith. Ma. kollan blacksmith, artificer, Ko. kole-l smithy, temple in Kota village. To. kwala+! Kota smithy. Ka. kolime, kolume, kulame, kulime, kulume, kulme fire- pit, furnace; (Bell.; U.P.U.) konimi black- smith; (Gowda) kolla id. Kod. kollé black- smith. Te. kolimi furnace. Go. (SR.) kollusana to mend implements; (Ph.) kolstana, kulsana to forge; (Ir.) kolstanad to repair (of plough- shares); (SR.) kolmi smithy (Voc. 948). Kuwt (F.) kolhali to forge. DED(S, N) 1773, 1774.

Ta. kolucu gold or silver chain for arm or ankle. Ka. golasu, gonasu a link of chain. Tu. gonasu, ganasu id. Te. golusu a chain. Kol. (SR.) golst id: (< Te.; Kamales- waran). DED(N) 1775. 2135 Kur. xola tail. Malt. qoli id. DED 1776.

Ta. kulavu (kulavi-) to bend, curve; n. bend, curve. Kui klonga (kldngi-) to be contracted, drawn in, bent up; klopka (< klok-p-; kI6kt-) to contract, draw up, depress. Kur. xolkhna, xol°xna to cause one to bend the head; xolkhrnd, xolxrna to bend the head, bow, stoop. Malt. gqolgru below, beneath, underneath; kolge to curve, bend; kolgro bent, curved. DEN 29 (Pfeiffer for Kur, Malt.) -
Ma. kolli valley, corner. Ka. kolli, kolle a bend, corner, gulf, bay. Kod. kolli small stream with rocky bed. Tu. kolli a bay. DED 1777.
Ka. golli pudendum muliebre. Tu. koyilé id. Te. (B.) golli id. DED 1778.

Ma. kolli a [kind of] fish. Tu. koleji id. DEDS 319. 2140 Kur. xollni to serve (rice, meat, potatoes, etc.) out of the cooking vessel, transfer by means of a ladle from the cooking pot into the plates or bowls ready for a meal. Malt. qole to take up liquids (as with a spoon). DED 1779. 2141 Kur. xoll® razor, Malt. qole id. / For a similar word, cf. Santali hola’d razor (Pinnow, p. 174). DED(N) 1780.
Ta. kuvalai socket. Ma. (DCV) kuvala id. Ka. (OCV) gile id. Tu. goté hollow- ness; hollow; (DCV) socket; kolé, kolé hollow, sunken. Te. (DCV) géle socket. DEDS 264.

Ta. kori (-pp-, -tt-) to sift in a win- nowing fan; koyippu sifting. Ma. korikka to sift, winnow; kogi sifting. Ko. kod- (kody-) to separate broken from whole grains (by winnowing with several side-to-side move- ments followed by sharp upward movement, the process being repeatedly done). To. kwic- (kwié-) to separate grain from stones, etc., with winnowing basket; ? kwaSt bran. Ka. koceu to shake flour, as it comes from the mill, on a small fan so as to separate any impurities. Te. kdu to separate the broken from unbroken grian, or the smaller particles from dhall or split pulse, etc. Kol. gonjip- (gonjipt-) to winnow with side-to- side motion. Nk. gonjip- id. Go. (Tr.) kossana (Gramm.,; Voc. k6ssana) to sift in a supa with a sideways motion; (Ch. Mu. Ma.) koss- to sift with sideways motion; (Ph.) kussitana, kossina to winnow (Voc. 953); (Tr.) goenjé kiand to swing grain in a flat basket with a sideways sweep (Voc. 1220); (Tr.) kurhuttana to shake roasted mahua in a basket to get rid of the fibrous part (Voc. 802). Konda kors- (-t-) to winnow. Kui krohpa (kroht-) id., sift. Kuwi (F.) korssali to winnow; (S.) Kloh’nai to sift; (Su. P.) kroh- (krost-), (Isr.) kroh- to winnow. DED(S) 1782. korifici 1193]

Ta. korifici, kogufici purple wild indigo, Tephrosta. purpurea. Ma. koxififidl Galega Colonila. Ka. koggi, koggidi, koggili, Kogge a very common undershrub, Tephrosia Purpurea. [T. purpurea Pers. = G. Colonila Ham.] DED(S) 1783.

Ta. kogu fat, flourishing, prosperous; n. fat; -pp-, -tt-) to prosper, flourish, be rich or fertile as soil, grow fat, be plump, be of thick consistency as sandalpaste, be saucy, insolent; koruppu richness, fat, grease, plump- hess, thickness in consistency, sauciness, impudence; korumai plumpness, luxuriance, richness, fertility; kdg slippery, oily, fat, thriving, luxurious, rich. Ma. koru fat, thick, solid; kogukka to grow thick, solid, stiff by boiling, grow fat, stout, arrogant; koruppu solidity as of broth or curry, fatness, stout- ness, pride. ? Ko. kol- (kod-) to be well in health. To. kwalp fat, in good circumstances; kéw fat, stoutness, broadness, honeycomb; kwa+w proudness; kog ir old buffalo; kog foly big dairy at To+ro+r village. Ka, korvu, korbu, kobbu to grow fat, thick, stout, increase, grow, be rank in growth, become proud, presumptuous, insolent; 7. fat, fatness, rankness, pride, arrogance; kobbisu to fatten; kobbiga a proud man. Tu. kommé corpulence, | fatness; corpulent, fat. Te. k(r)ovvu to be- come fat, fatten, (K. also) become proud; n. fat, grease, lust, pride, arrogance; kroy- vina fat, plump, headstrong, ungovernable. Kol. (Kin.) koru fat (1.). Nk. koru id. Pa. kor, (S.) kory- to be fat; korukud fatness, fat. Ga (P.) korkut, (S.2) kodukuft, (5.3) kodukud fat. Go. (A.) korvinj, (W.) kurwinj, (Mu.) kKorvenj, (Ma.) korvonj, (Ko.) korvos fat; (Mand.) kor@- to become fat (Voc. 944); (L.) koduvasaka, kosela fat (Voc. 951). Konda korvu fat of animals; (BB) k

Ta. koru bar of metal, ploughshare. Ma. koru ploughshare. Ko. koy iton point of plough. To. ku- ploughshare (< Badaga gits, Language 15.47; the word occurs only in one passage and the meaning is arrived at by etymology). Ka. kura, kuru, gura, guru ploughshare, iron used in cauterizing. Tu. koru a bar of metal. DED(N) 1785,

Ta. korutu (koruti-) to peck, drill through, hollow out (as beetles in wood or flowers), pluck, cull out, rend, tear; kotu (koti-) to peck and adjust with the beak (as feathers), disentangle (as the hair) with the fingers, pick (as food in eating), tear in strips (as tender leaves), hollow, excavate, scoop out; kotuvi (-pp-, -tt-) to remove, root out, exterminte; k6tai woman’s hair. Ma. kotu dressing hair, cutting a fence; kotuka to dress chair, feathers, trees), cut planks so as to fit into each other. DED (S) 1786. koruntu

Ta. koruntu_ tender twig, tendril, tender leaf, shoot, anything young, tender- ness; korumai freshness (as of shoots), beauty; koruntan husband, husband’s younger brother; korunti wife’s sister, brother's wife; korunan husband; kuga young, tender; kurakan youth, beautiful person, Skanda; kugaku youthful- ness, beauty, infant; kurantai infant, child- hood; kugavi infant, young of certain animals, young of the vegetable kingdom; kuravu tender age, juvenility; kurai (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to sprout or shoot forth; n. tender leaf, sprout, shoot. Ma. kogunnu, koruntu tender twig, young shoot, new-grown hair. To. kwiz, twig. Ka. koda tenderness, tender age, youth; konasu young one of wild beasts. Tu. koré weak, small. Kor. (O. T.) korayi, (M.) kori husband; (O. M. T.) korti wife. Te. krotta (in cpds. kro-) new, fresh; koduku son; Kondika child; kodama the young of any animal; young; komma maiden, female; kogalu daughter-inlaw; koframu, kodantra- mu, kodantrikamu, kédarikamu the position and duties of a daughter-in-law, daughter-in- lawship. Kol. kovve young of bird or animal; koral younger brother’s wife; kommal (pl. kommasil) daughter. Vk. kovve young of bird or animal; koral daughter-in-law, bride; kommal (pl. kommagil) daughter. Nk. (Ch.} komma daughter; kola bride, son’s wife, younger brother’s wife. Pa. kor very young; korung new shoot, sprout; kore- to sprout; korol bride. Ga. (Ol) koral son’s wife, younger brother’s wife; (S) kodus-, Koge- to sprout; (P.) koruy young shoot. G

Ta. kol (kolv-, konf-) to seize, receive, buy, acquire, marry, abduct, con- tain, learn, think, regard, esteem; aux. with refl. meaning; kolkai taking, accepting, opinion, doctrine; kolvGn buyer, student; kollunar buyers, learners; kollai robbery, plunder; koluttu (kolutti-) to cause to hold, apply, explain, teach; 1. clasp of a jewel, joint of the body; koluvu (koluvi-) to cause to hold, clasp, buckle up, hook on; koluvi, kolukki hook, clasp; kolai hold as of a string in a bow, determination; kofpu intention, idea; kontal receiving, taking; konfi getting possession of, theft, plunder; corner pin of a door on which it swings, clamp, cleat of a doorlock, the pin that holds the share to the plough; konf{-atu to enjoy a person’s society, praise, appreciate, celebrate (as a festival), solemnize; kopa- to bring, take, fetch, con- duct; kdl taking, receiving, accepting, holding, opinion, tenet, decision; koli receiver; kotal taking, buying, taking lessons, thinking; kolu royal presence, durbar, presence of the deity in a temple; decorations in a Hindu house at the Navaratri festival (<< Te.). Ma. kolka (kont-; imper. kd) to hold, contain, receive, acquire, marry; aux. with refl. mean- ing; kollikka to make to hold or receive; koluttuka to make to hold, hook, clasp, fasten a rope to a load; koluttu what holds, hook, link, stitch; kolla, konti plunder; kon{-atuka to be interested, celebrate, praise; kol holding, taking, purchase; kolu service, king’s presence; magnificence, prosperit

Ta. kol (kolv-, konf-) to strike, hurt; kol killing, murder. Ma. kolka (konf{-) to hit, take effect, come in contact; kollikka to hit; kol hitting, wound, damage. Ko. kol-/kon- (kod-) to pain, trouble. To. kwil- (kwid-) to quarrel, (urine) troubles (i.e. one wishes to urinate). Tu. konpini to hit; kolpuni, kolpuni to come into collision. Te. (B.) konu to be pierced as by an arrow. Kol. gosl- (godd-) to beat, shoot with bow; (P.) god- to cut with axe. Nk. gor- (gof-) to strike, beat, kill. DED 1789. kol [195} korram

Ta. kol horse gram, Dolichos uni- florus, Ma. koltu horse gram, Glycine tomen- tosa. Tu, kudu horse gram. Kor. (M.) konne dried plants of horse gram. Pa. kol (pl kolkul) D. uniflorus, Ga. (S.2) kolut, (S.3) kolup horse gram. Go. (Mu.) kor, (Ma.) kori, (Ko.) kore id: (Voc. 937). Kui (K.) koraka (pl.) id. Kuwi (Su.) kora (pl. -na), (S) kdda id. / Cf. Skt. kulattha- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3335. [D. biflorus Linn. = D. uniflorus Lam. = G. uniflora Dalz. G. tomentosa not in Hooker.] DED(S) 1790.
Ka. kolake, kolke the third crop of tice. Tu. kolaké, kolaké id. DED 1791.
Ta. kolai melody, song. Ko, kol tune, song. ? To. kon words of songs, words used in dance-song. DED 1792.
Ka. kolpu, koppu, koppafta the notched extremity or horn of a bow. Te. koppu tip or end of a bow, ridge or crest of a roof. DED 1793.
Ka. kolla a deep place, a depth, the cleft in a rock, a cave, etc. Tu. kolamé a very deep pit, abyss, hell. DEN 30.

Ta. kolli firebrand, fire, quick- tongued person; koluttu (kolutti-) to kindle, set on fire, ignite; burn (intr); koluntu (kolunti-), koruntu (korunti-) to burn, be kindled; koluvu (koluvi-) to kindle (as fire). Ma. kolli firebrand, firewood; koluttuka to set on fire, light, kindle. Ko. koyl burning firewood, faggot; koylt a+1 man who carries new fire round at ceremonies. To. kwily firebrand, glowing ember. Ka. kolli, kolle firebrand. Tu. kolli, kolli id. DED(8) 1794.

Ka. golle the anus of males. Kod. golle anus. DED 1795. 2160 Kol. kor (pl. kodl) hen, (SR.) cock, fowl. Nk. kor (pl. kodl) hen. Pa. korr (pl. -ul) cock, hen, fowl. Ga. (Oll.) kor, (S.) korru (pl. korg) cock. Go. (Tr.) korr fowl; (A. Y. G.) kor, (Mu. Ko.) korr, (Ma.) kor id., hen (Voc. 917). Konda koru (pl koRku) hen. Pe. kozu (pil. kosku), kuzu (pl. kusku) fowl. Mand. kuy id. Kui koju (ol koska) id. Kuwi (F.) koiyii (pl. kdska), (Su. P.) koyu (pl. koska) id., hen. DED (S) 1768.
Ka. kora-muffu tool, instrument (muftu id). Te. kora use, profit; useful, promable; kora-muftu tool, instrument. DED 1796.
Tu. kojapu sour milk, curds; (B-K.) kujely, in: nirkujelu to curdle, coagulate. Go. (A. Y. Mu.) korop, (Ch. Tr.) korrop, (W. Ph.) kurrop, (S.) korrup buttermilk (Voc. 926). DEDS 322.

Ta. kural Italian millet. Ko. koyl Setaria italica; korly id. (< Badaga). Ka. korale, korle a kind of millet, Panicum italicum Lin. Te. korralu (pl) id.; korra the cereal yielding korralu. Pa. koyla P. italicum, Go, (Tr.) kohala, (W. Ph.) kohala, (S.) kohala, (Ma.) ko%la Panicum miliare (Voc. 957); (Mu.) gorran (pl), (Ma.) gora (pl. -y), (Ko.) korra mandeya corn, Eleusine coracana (Voc. 1207). Konda (BB) korey (pl) a grain (= Or. kafhgu). Kui kueri millet. [S. italica Beauv. = P. italicum Linn.] DED(S) 1797.

Ta. kori (pp-, -tt-) to nip off the husks of grains, nibble grain, graze, pick up food here and there (as cattle). Ma. korikka to nibble (as a mouse), eat grains, nipping off the husk. Ka koruku, koraku, kuruku, kurunku to bite, gnaw, nibble. Kod. kori- (korip-, koric-) (snake) strikes, (rat) eats grain by cracking husk; (Kar.) kurik- (kuriki-) to munch. Tu, (B-K.) kojaly to gnaw as rats do with grains and fruits. Te. koruku to bite, gnaw; n. a bite. Kol. kork- (korokt-) to bite, sting, (Kin.) nibble. Nk. kork- to bite. Pa. kork- to.cut with the teeth or scissors, gnaw, nibble. Ga. (5.3) kork- (koruk-) to gnaw. Go. (many dialects) kork-, (Tr.) korkana id.; (Ma.) kohk- id., bite, nibble (Voc. 927). Konda koRk- (-t-) to crush with the teeth, bite. DED(S, N) 1798. 2465 (a) Ta. kori sheep. Ma. kori a small kind of sheep. Ko. kory a+_ sheep (a+r goat). To. kury sheep. Ka. kuri, kori sheep, ram; kuritana a sheepish, foolish disposition. Kod. kori sheep. Tu. kuri id. Te. gore, gorre, gorriya, (Inscr.) goriya, (B.) gorre, gor(r)iya id. Kol. (Kin. SR.) gorre id. Nk. (Ch.) gorre id. Go. (A. Ma.) gorre id.; (SR.) gore goat (Voc. 1208). Konda gore id. Kuwi (F.) gori, (isr.) gorri, (P.) gore id.; (S.) gorri id., sheep. (b) Te. gorre, in: (B.) kukka-gorre barking deer, Cervulus muntjac [also ‘jungle sheep’]; (Sank. SAN) konda-gorre, (B.) konda-gorre deer, antelope. Kol. goria deer, (Kin) ante- lope. Go. (Ko.) gorre four-horned antelope; (L.) gore deer (Voc. 1209). DED(S) 1799, and from DED

Ta. korukoru (-pp-, -tt-) to bubble with anger; Koru-koruv-enal onom. expr. signifying anger. Te. korakora angrily; anger, angry look; korakoral-ddu to be angry, have an angry look; (K.) koralu to grow angry. DED 1800.

Ta. korukku, korukku © syphilis, chancre. Tu. korkalu a kind of sore. Te. koruku chancre. DED 1801. 2168 Ko, korv- (kord-) to be cold; kor, korv coldness. To. kwar- (kwarO-) to feel cold; kwar cold; kwar- (kwar@-} to be cold (in songs; ? < Badaga or Ko.) Ka. kore, kori to pierce (as cold); koreta, korata the piercing of cold; (or with 1859 Ta. kurai).
