Tt (B-K.) korale cold. Kol, (Pat., p. 23) karvun id. Go. (Ph.) kharra frost (Voc. 995); (L.) karing, koring cold. DED(S, N) 1802.

Ta. korram victory, success, bravery, power, sovereignty; korravan king, victor; korravi queen; korravai goddess of war and victory (George L. Hart IJ, The Poems of Ancient Tamil, pp. 23-4), Durga; korri korran [196] xOxt Durga. Ma, korram victory, royalty; korravan king, headman. DED 1803. 2170 Ma: korran tam, boar, tomcat; korri ewe, female cat, bandicoot; kurififii, kuruffi she-cat. To. kwaty cat. Ka. kotti male or female cat. Tu. kuttiri a civet cat. DED(S) 1804.

Ta. korru food, means of subsistence; (inscr.) kottu cooked rice. Ma. korru food, tice. ?Kuwi (D.) kohpe pej of mandeya. DED(S) 1805. 2172 Konda koRo- (-t-) to doze in sleep, sit and doze. Kui kofrongi ava to be drowsy, sleepy. Kuwi (Su.) koftoni, (S.) kotoni dozing, sleepiness; (F.) kotori-hdcali, (S.) kothoni-honai to nod with sleep. ? Cf. Ta. kottavi, (Koll.) kotfay, kottayug yawn. Ma. koftavi yawn, gaping. DEDS 321.
Ta. kon fear. Te. konku to be timid or shy, fear, hesitate; m. timidity, fear, shy- ness, hesitation; koncu, konju, koncincu to hesitate, draw back, be afraid; k6ca a coward. DED 1806.

Ta. konai tip, end (as of a needle); -kunai sharp point or tip of a thing. Ko. kot side (a kotl on that side, i kot on this side); koty trigger of gun. Ka. kone extremity, point, tip, end, corner, sprout. Kod. kotti tip, nipple, joint where flower and stem are joined. 7u. koné point, end, extremity. Te. kona end, extremity, point, tip, summit; konaka, in: cevi-konaka tip of the ear. Konda kona pointed end (as of a leaf, etc.). DED (S) 1807.
Ta. konzai Indian laburnum, Cassia fistula; red Indian laburnum, C. marginata; Siamese tree senna, C. siamea. Ma. konna C. fistula, Ka. konde id. Tu. kondé pudding- pipe tree, C. fistula. DED 1808.

Ta. ko (-pp-, -tt-), kor Cpp-, -tt-) to string (as beads, flowers), insert, thread (as a needle), arrange, reduce to order; koppu stringing, threading, inserting, arrangement, order, decoration; kodvai, korvai stinging, filing, arranging, string of ornamental beads; kotai garland. Ma. kokka, korkka to string together (as a garland, pearls, beads), thread a needle; koccil what appears like a string, pod, legume; képpu order, arrangement; kova, korva what is strung together; kérmpal, kOmpu string of pearls, bunch of things. Ko. koev- (koet-} to string, thread; koed long strip of dried meat. Ka, kG (k6d-, k6t-) to string (pearls, beads, etc.) upon a thread, thread; be threaded, be entered. Kod. koya- (koyap-, koyat-) to string. Tu. kopé a string of fruits, fish, etc.; gopuni to hold fast, join; gdpu a thin girdle; gompé a string of fruits, fish, etc. Te. k(r)Ova a string, as of pearls, etc. Kol. ko-nz- (koenst-) to string, thread; (Pat., p. 171) gGnzeng to thread. Vk. k6nj- (j = dz) to string, thread. Pa, kdp- (k6t-) to string. ? Go. kac- (Ch. Tr. W. SR.) to thread needle, (Mu. Ma.) to string (garland, beads); (Ko.) kas- to string beads (Voc. 622). ? Malt. kunye (kunc-) to string (as beads). DED(S, N) 1809.

Ta. ko, kon, kOman emperor, king, great man, leadership; koyil palace, temple; koyinmai, kdvinmai, kdnmai royal dignity, arrogance; kovil temple; konafu a division of the Chola country; kdcar name of certain chieftains mentioned in the Sangam literature and connected with the Tulu country. Ma. kd, kon, kOman king; koyil, kGvil palace, temple; kodyilakam palace; konma, koyma royal authority. Ko. ko-nasy the plains; ko-na+to-n, ko-na+fo-r man, men of the plains. Te. koyila, kovela temple. Pa. koc king. Ga. (S) k6su id. ? Kur, koha great, big, haughty, important, eminent in rank, etc.; kohar elders, grandees, chiefs; (Hahn) kogha great one, elder relative; koghar elders. DED 1810.

Ta. kd mountain. Te, kOdu, koduvaidu a Khond, man of a certain hilltribe; koya name of a certain tribe of mountaineers. Go. (Tr.) koitur (pl. koditdrk) a Gond male; fem. koitar; (Y.G.) koy, (SR.Ch.) koya Gond man; (Ko.) koytand (i. koytar) Koya man (Voc. 913). Konda ktibi the Konda Dora (the speakers of this language). Kui kui the Kond tribe or language; kttenju a Kond man or boy; fem. kiali; kui above, aloft, over; atop, upon; kuiki to the place above; kuiti from the place above. Kuwi (F.) kitvina the Patja Khonds; (S.) kui up, above, west; kilita in the west. DED(S) 1811. :

Ko. ko+g stone of olive. To. kwi-g seed inside fruit. Ka. (Hav.) kdgile seed inside mango stone. Go. (Ma.) go?i stone of mango (Voc. 1183); (Mu.) gohi pulp of fruit (Voce. 1215). Kuwi (Isr.) goh?6 cashew nut tree. Kur, kogd mango stone; kuhti kernel of the mango stone. Cf. 1880 Ta. kitcci. / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 3525, *kdya- inside of fruit (H. koya pulp of jackfruit, Mar. koy mango stone, etc.). From DED(S) 1560. 2180 Konda kok- (-t-) to raise and project the head (while seeing a distant object). Kui gopka (< gok-p-; godkt-) to stretch forth, stretch the neck in order to observe some- thing, crane the head out, put forth the ear (corn), Kuwi (Isr.) gok- to look up, await; (F.) gokhmii look up! DEDS 324.

Te. koka a woman’s garment or cloth, a cloth. Nk. (Ch.) kok(k)e cloth (for women). DEDS 323. 2182 Kur. xdxa behind (of time or place), afterwards; xOxantd last in a series, following .in time, future, unfavourable; xOxna to throw behind one’s back, cast aside, turn one’s back upon, arrive before another. Malt. qéq behind, at the back; qéqe the back; qéqeye to turn one’s back; qoqte, qéqwa the latter or last one. DED 1812. sdgi [197] gofu

Ka. godgi Deckanee hemp, Hibiscus cannabinus. Te. gdgu, gonu id.; gofgilra leaves of H. cannabinus. DED(S) 1813. 2184 Ko, keygl Rumex nepalensis (the long oblong leaves aré used by girls to make dolls); keyg van girvy Polygonum rude and chinense (closely related to R. n.; for pan, sce 4004). To. kwa-gil R. 1. and P. r. and e. Ka, kogilé gida a plant of which the leaves are used instead of plates, etc.; kodgiley ele = kuvwvey ele leaf of k. (the leaves are dried to make raincapes; kuvve is not identified botanically —it is not the same as kuvve in 1872). 2185 Ta, konku common caung. Hopea wightiana, ironwood of Malabar, H. parvi- flora. Ma, konku, in: (Lush.) neduvar gdfgu Hi, parviflora. DEDS 325.

Ka. kGsu, kdcu state of being crooked or curved, deviation from squareness (as that of torn cloth, of a wall, etc.). Tu. kodsu oblique, squint (of eye). Te. kdsu obliqueness; oblique, not at right angles. DED 1814. 1287 Ma, kodccuka to be contracted, shudder; k&ccal contraction, cramp. Ko. ko+j- (ko-j-) (leg or arm) becomes asleep. DED 1815. . 1288 Kur. x@col bone. Malt. qoclu id. DED 1816. 2189 Kur. x6sna (xfisyas) to pulverize by pounding with a heavy instrument which beats vertically, harass. Malt. qdse to pound, smash. Br. xdshking, Voc. var.) xdshing to rub; tene xdshking to consort with, interfere with. DED 1817.

Ka. godju to entangle, be entangled (as thread); . state of being entangled, of being embarrassed; gufiju to entangle, be entangled; nm. state of being entangled. Te. géju, gdjidu to pester, harass, trouble. DED 1818. 2191 Kol. (SR.) koja cup. Go. (ASu.) khodja bowl. (Kamaleswaran.) DEN 31.

Ko. ko+j spider. Te. kodca, in: puli- koca a large venomous spider. Ga. (P.) kovise spider. Konda gonzru, gonzur(i), (BB also) gondru id. Pe. kéndru id: Kuwi (F.) kdchuti, (S.) kocudi, (Isr.) kocoti id. DEDS 328. 2196 Ta, kofaram monkey. /r. kdda (small) monkey; kiidag monkey. Ko. ko» small monkey. To. kwiern monkey. Ka. kddaga monkey, ape. Kod. ko-dé monkey. Ju. kodafiji, kodafja, kodafgy baboon. DED 1820.
Ta. koti weir of a tank, outlet for surplus water. Ka. kodi a passage to carry off excess of water, outlet of a tank. Te. (VPR) kodi outlet of tank. DED(S) 1821.
Ta. k6dti . newly purchased cloth; kétikam cloth; k6tikar weaver. Ma. koti new unbleached cloth, shroud for burying. Kuwi (Isr.) k6ri loincloth. DED (S) 1822.

Te. kddiya, kode young bull: adj. male (e.g. kéde diida bull calf), young, youthful; kddekidu a young man. Kol. (Haig) kode bull. Vk. khore male calf. Konda kodi cow; kdre young bullock. Pe. kodi cow. Mand. kiidi id. Kui kodi id., ox. Kuwi (F.) kédi cow; (S.) kajja kddi bull; (Su. P.) kddi cow. DED(S) 1823. 2200 7a. kotu (in cpds. kdtf{u-) horn, tusk, branch of tree, cluster, bunch, coil of hair, line, diagram, bank of stream or pool; kuvafu branch of a tree; kO¢fan, kOftuvan rock horned-owl (cf. 1657 Ta. kufifiai). Ko. ko-r (obl. ko+f-) horns (one horn is kob), half of hair on each side of parting, side in game, log, section of bamboo used as fuel, line marked out. To. kwi-er (O61. kwi-f-) horn, branch, path across stream in thicket. Ka. kodu horn, tusk, branch of a tree; kdg horn. Tu, kodu, kodu horn. Te. kodu rivulet, branch of a river. Pa. kod (pl. kOdul) horn. Ga. (OU) kor (pl. kGrgul) id. Go. (Tr.) kor (obl. k6t-, pl. koOhk) horn of cattle or wild animals, branch of a tree; (W. Ph. A. Ch.) kor (pl. kohk), (S.) kor (Pi kohku), (Ma.) koqu (pl. kohku) horn; (M.) kohk branch (Voc. 980); (LuS.) kogoo a hom. Kui koju (pl kdska) horn, antler. Cf. 2049 Ta. koti. DED (N) 1824.

Ko. ko-ntl pocket in outside edge of cloak. To. kwies id.; kwi- fy id. (in songs). Ka. gdtu border or hem of a garment; fringe, edging, trimming. Tu. gofu embroidery, lace. Te. g6tu an ornamental appendage to the border of a cloth, fringe, hem, edging. /Cf. Mar. got hem of garment; H. gota edging of gold lace. DED 1825.

Ka. gofu state of being full-grown, but hard; (also got-adike) a hard, inferior kind kotai [198] of areca-nut. Kod. go-t-adake full grown, tough areca-nut. Tu. gonfu dried (as the kernel of a coconut), (BRR, also gofu) state of being full-grown, dried and hard. Te. gon{u-pOka, (B. also) gotu-poka an inferior kind of areca-nut. DED (S) 1826.

Ta. kdtai west wind; summer, intense heat of summer. Ma. kota west wind, cool wind, west. Ko. keer SW. monsoon. To. kwa+rp monsoon, year. Ka. kdde west wind, cool wind (ie. the hot land-wind for the Mysore, Telugu, and Tamil countries), the hot season [‘cool wind’ is a quotation from Gundert]. Cf. 1649 Ta. kutakam and 2056 Ta. kotuku. DED 1827.

Ta. kotfalai distress, torment; k6tfi trouble, vexation, annoyance. ? Ma, koga violence, Ka. kofale, kdtle trouble, affliction, pain; kotaliga man who causes trouble. Tu. kotalé pain, sickness, hardship; ? kdra measles. ?Te. (B.) kodadu to suffer pain. DED(S) 1828.
Ta. kdtti larger stick in the game of tipcat. Ma. koftti a stick for play. Ko. ko-+ty gal big marble used with a lot of small pebpies in one form of jackstraws. DED(S) 1829.

Ta. kOdftti pleasantry, joke, mimicry, grotesque gestures; kOnanki clown in a play. Me. kotti, gosfhi grimaces, pranks; kotafki buffoon, harlequin. Ka. kodangi id. Tu. kodangye id. Te. konanhgi buffoon; kodigamu, kodi ridicule; kédigidu one who ridicules. | ane k6tfi forms may be from Skt. DED 1830. 2207 (a) Ta. kOttai fort, castle; kdfu stronghold. Ma. k6dtta fort, residence; kotu fort. Ko. ko+{ castle, palatial mansion. To. kwa-t bungalow. Ka. kote fort, rampart; (BRS konte fort. Kod. ko+te palace. Tu. ko{é fort. Te. kdta, (Inscr.) koftamu id. Kuwi (S.) k6fa palace, fort. / Cf. Skt. kotta-, kota- fort, stronghold. DED 1831. (b) Ko. go-y (obi. go-t-) wall. Ka. gode id. Tu. gode id. Te. goda id. Kol, (SR.) goda id. Kuwi (S.) koda wall, prison, (isr.) koda wall, DED 1833.
Ta. kOftai measure of capacity = 21 marakkal; abundance, plenty. Ma. kotta much, plenty. DED 1832.

Ta. kon crookedness, angle, cross- ness of disposition; kdnu (k6ni-) to be bent, curved, be crooked, deviate, be perverse; kdpam curve, curvature, scimitar, angle, corner; konal obliquity, hump, crookedness (as of mind); kdnan humpback; konai curvature, crookedness, cruelty; kunaku (kunaki-) to become bent, crooked; kunakku (kunakki-) to bend (t7.); ™. crookedness, curvature, crossness; kunalai bending of the body through bashfulness; kunukku (kunu- kki-) to bend (tr.). Ma. kon corner, angle; kOnuka to bend (intr.); kOnam corner; koni corner of a piazza. Ko. goen corner of room. kOmarxad Ka. kon, kona, kone, kona angle, corner. Tu. kona, koné id.; kofigannu a squint eye. Te. kdpamu angle, corner; k6na corner. Ga. P.) kOne corner. Konda (BB) kona id. Cf. 2054 (bd) Ta. koti. /Cf. Skt. kona- corner, angle, point of the compass; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3504, DED(S, N) 1834. 2210 Konda kona a big long stick. Pe. kona carrying-stick (for two people). Kui koro rod, club. Cf. 1684 Ta. kupil and 2076 Ka. konapi. DEDS 329.
Ka. kone an inner apartment or chamber, a kitchen. Tr. k6né a room, apatt- ment. Go. (Mu.) konar (pi. koinalhk) shed for hens (Voe. 971).
Jr. (Bhattacharya 1958) ko+ne male buffalo. Ka. kona id. Tu, gone id. DEDS 330.

Ta. k6tantam a rope for punishment, suspended in schools, on which a boy is tied up with his hands clasped and which he is not permitted to loose. Ma. kétantam id. Ka, kédanda id. Tu. kédanda id. Te. kédan- damu id. DED 1836. 2214 Ta, kdtai woman (beautiful as a garland). Te, gdti woman. DEDS 331.
Ka. konéri, konéru a_ stone-faced tank with steps on all sides. Te. konéru, kOneru a square tank or pond with steps constructed on all sides.

Ka. gonde bull, ox. Te. godda ox. Kol. (SR.) konda bull; (Kin.) konda bullock. Nk. (Ch.) konda id. Pa. konda bison. Ga. (Oll.) kGnde cow; (S.) kondé bullock. Go. (Tr.) konda, (other dialects) kénda bullock, ox (Voc. 972). DED(S) 1837. 2217 Pa, kddi marking-nut tree. Ga. (S.?) kondu maren, (S.3) kontin id. Go. (SR.) kohka mara, (Tr.) kohkd mara, (W. Ph.) kohka, (CY. Ch. Mu.) kohka, (Ma.) ko7ka, (M.) kohoka id. (Voc. 960). DEDS 332.

Te. kdvuramu ambush, lying in ambush. Pa, kop- (kot-) to tend (cattle). Ga, (Oll.) k5p- (k6t-) id. Go. (Ch. D. Mu. Ma.) kopal, GV. Ph.) kopal cowherd; (Tr.) kopat term of abuse applied to Gaiki graziers; (Ph. Mu.) kdpe (pi. -hk) cowherdess (Voc. 975); (Mu. Pat.) koh- to tend cattle; (Mu.) kohval cowherd (Voc. 955). Kui kopa (kopi-) to tend, herd (cattle, sheep); 7. tending, herding.
Ma. koppu a feast. Kod. ko-pi festivity. DED 1839.

Ta. komafti Telugu-speaking mer- chant caste. Ma. komatti a tribe of Lingaite merchants, e.g. in Wayanadu. Ka. kémafi, komatiga a Vaisya shopkeeper. Tu. komati, komatige a class of merchants. Te. komafi a Vaisya. Nk. (Ch.) kémfi man of the trader caste; fem, kOmtigra. DED 1840. 2221 Kur. komarxad mann a tree, the leaves of which are edible, Bauhinia purpurea ee wat komalam [199] kol (Sad. koinar); (Hahn) kom arxa a vegetable, the leaves of the ko’enar tree (for arxd, see 59). Malt. komo a delicate vegetable obtained from the kachnar tree. DEDS 333.

Ta. komalam romping as of fat bullocks; komali buffoon, jester; kummalam jumping, romping, moving sportively (as children, calves, etc.); kummatfi romping, jumping. Ma. komalam jesting; kOm4li jester. Ko, ko-ma+ely buffoon in drama. ? Tu. (B-K.} kop4ro jesting, buffoonery. DED(S, N) 1841.
Ta. kompi chameleon, Chameeles vulgaris, bloodsucker, Lacerta cristata. Ka. gosumbe chameleon, gecko. DEDS 334.
Ta. kompai shell of coconut or areca nut with the husk, young palmyra fruit after the edible kernel is removed. Ma, kGmpu a very small stunted coconut. Cf. 2087 Ta. kotumpu.

Ta. kody vessel for taking out toddy.. To. kwaey bamboo pot used at ti+-dairy. Br. x6 cooking-pot. DED(S) 1842. 2226 Konda goyi smoke (of kitchen). Pe. koy smoke; kéd- (k6tt-) to smoke (intr.); kot- (kott-) id. (t7.), burn incense. ? Pa. gilii- to smoke; glfii, gifijkud smoke. ?Go. (W. Ph.) kusso smoke; (SR. Ch.-Mu.) kosso, (Mu. Ma.) kosoy, (G.) kosoyi soot; (Tr.) koss3 soot on bottom of cooking-pot (Voe. 954). ? Kui kuhula smoke. DEDS(N) 335. 2227 Kur. xodyn® (xojjas) to measure, ascertain the extent, height, quantity or capacity of; xdytarma to be measured. Malt. qoye to weigh, measure; qoytre to have any- thing weighed or measured. ? Go. kahtana (Tr.) to measure, count, (W.) to number, (Ch.) to count; (A. SR. Mu.) kah- to count; (Ma.) ka?- to measure; (Ph.) kahcdnd, kahtana id. (Voc. 612). DED(N) 1843.

Ta. koram, korakam metallic dish or plate; kérakai begging-bowl of the Buddhist ascetics. Te. kdra cup, tray. /Cf. BHS khora- almsbowl, khoraka- pot; Pkt. (Ardhamagadhi) khoraya- a kind of round pot. DED(S) 1844.

Ta. koran torch tree, xora parviflora. Ka. goravi, goravu an evergreen tree which makes good torches, J. parviflora Vahl. Te. koravi, (B. also) korivi a firebrand, (B. also) touchwood tree, Sapindus tetraphylla or Guarea molinoea Roxb.; korakancu, kora- kaccu firebrand, burning piece of wood. Konda koroy a burning torch. DED(S) 1845.
Ka. gori-kayi Dolichos fabaeformis, Te, gdru-cikkudu id. DED 1846.

Ta. kor (kori-), kdlu (kdli-) to bale, draw up (as with an ola bucket), gather with a sweep of the arm; k6rikai, kOrakai ladle. Ma. koruka to draw water, gather up, ladle out, take in heaps; kdral vb.n.; a fishing basket; kdru-vala_ fishing-net; kodrika iron ladle. Ka. godr to catch fish; goru to draw, gather or sweep together, remove the im- purities of a heap of grain by repeated piling, fish with a net to which a handle is attached, draw a plank to which a handle is attached with the hand over ploughed ground to level it, plunder; m. drawing, etc.; gdri drawing, drawing in, raking, a kind of rake, attracting, decoying; gore a shovel for cleaning a boat. 7 Kod. ko-t- (ko-ti-) to fill by scooping. Tu. gdruni, koruni to gather, pile up, catch fish by a hand-net, level a ploughed field with a plank; gdralé small hand-net; gora- balé large hand-net; (B-K.) kori a kind of ladle to serve rice. Te. (B.) gdra a drill plough; gora-cekka a drag with which rivers are cleared; (VPK) gora-cekka, gdra-palaka a hand instrument for levelling a ploughed field. Go, (Ma.) kor- to cast net (Voc. 981). DED (S, N) 1847.

Ta. koru (kori-) to request, wish; kdrikkai request, wish; kocuru, kucar any- thing extra obtained from a shopkeeper as a bargain. Ma, kdruka to wish, hope, purpose, think; kdru a wish. Ko. ko-ryk vow paid to god. Ka. kdru, kdru to purpose, think, wish, desire; korike wishing, desiring; kosaru to demand an article gratis or into the bargain; be greedy, desire, hanker; n. anything asked for and given to boot; eagerness, cupidity; kosarike hankering after, desire. Tu. kodruni to desire, hope, expect; k6riké, kdrigé hope, desire, wish; kusuri, kusri, kosaru anything given to boot when a person buys com- modities. Te. koru to desire, request, want, choose; korika, koriki desire, wish, longing, selection; (K.) koralu to desire, wish; kosaru to ask for a little extra quantity after pur- chasing any article, (K. B. also) be greedy, eager for; m. a little quantity of anything given by a trader over and above what has been paid for, (K. B. also) eagerness, greedi- ness, lust. Ga. (S.) kdr- to desire. Konda kosor anything demanded gratis after pur- chasing vegetables, etc. Kuwi (S.) k6rinai to vote; (Isr.) kdésori bonus. DED(S) 1848.
Ma. koruka to eat greedily. Ka. (Hav. 8., p. 78) kollu to drink. Te. (K.) krdlu to drink, eat. Kuwi (Isr.) gronj- (-it-) to drink, guzzle. DED 1849.
Ka. kdrinde a kind of fragrant plant. Te. kodrinda a prickly shrub, the bramble. DEDS 336.

Ta. korai sedges and bulrushes, Cyperus. Ma. kora C. juncifolius. Ka. koranari- gadde a kind of sedge, C. hexastachyus Rottb. DED 1850. 2236 Kur. koma to enter, go in, (fever) seizes, be readmitted among. Malt. kore to enter, go in, enlist; kortre to let enter, thrust in. DED 1851.

Ta. kot stick, staff, branch, arrow. Ma. kol staff, rod, stick, arrow. Ko. ko-l stick, story of funeral car. To. kwies stick. Ka. kdl, kodlu stick, staff, arrow. Kod. ko-li stick, Tu. kolu, kolu stick, staff. Te. kdla kal [200] koli id., arrow; long, oblong; kolana elongatedness, elongation; k6dlani elongated. Kol. (SR) kola, (Kin.) kola stick. Nk. (Ch.) kol pestle. Pa. kol shaft of arrow. Go. (A.) kéla id.; kola (Tr.) a thin twig or stick, esp. for kindling a fire, (W. Ph.) stick, rod, a blade of grass, straw; (G. Mu. Ma. Ko.) kola handle of plough, sickle, knife, etc. (Voc. 988); (ASu.) kola stick, arrow, slate-pencil; (LuS.) kola the handle of an implement. Konda kol big wooden pestle. Pe. kot pestle. Mand. kul id. Kui kodu (pl. kotka) id. Kuwi (F.) kola (pl. kolka), (S. Su.) kdlu (pl. kolka) id. Cf. 2240 Ta. kdlam (Tu. Te. Go.). / Cf. OMar. (Master) kola stick. DED(S) 1852.
Ta. kl, kolam raft, float. Ma. kolam raft. Ka. kol raft, float. Te. (B.) kolamu id. /Cf. Skt., BHS kola- boat, raft, Pali kulla- id. DED 1853.
Ka. kol, kul length, largeness. Te. kolu big, huge; much, very. DED 1854.

Te. kolam beauty, colour, form, shape, costume, attire as worn by actors, ornament. Ma. kélam form, figure (chiefly of masks, dresses); idol, body, beauty. Ka. kola ornament, decoration, form, figure (chiefly of masks, dresses, etc.). Tu. kdla a devil-dance. Te. (B.) kdlamu a dance, dancing. Go. (Mu.) kola the dandar dance [i.e. stick dance]; kola pata kind of song associated with the dandar dance (Voc. 986). Or the Tu. Te. Go. words with 2237 Ta. kdl. DED (S) 1855.
Ta. kola flying fish, Exocaetus; garfish, Belone. Ma. kolan, kola-min, koli needle-fish. DED 1856.
Ka. koli a stubble of jdla. Te. kdle a stub or stump of corn. DED 1857.

Ta. kolu (kéli-) to commence, effect, accomplish, consider, deliberate, ponder. Ma. koluka to have to do with, use, care for. DED 1858. 2244 Kur. kul belly, stomach, womb; kilas offspring, descendant. Malt. kdli abdomen. Br. xl womb, offspring, entrails, woof, weft; xGlaxil, xOxi entrails, woof and warp. /?<IA. Cf. H. kol breast, bosom; kaula, kola, kauli id., lap; Turner, CDIAL, no. 3607. DED(S) 1859.
Ta. k6li sp. privet, Ligustrum perro- tettii. Ka. (Lush.) koli id., ZL. neilgherrense. ? To. kwiesy sp. plant. DEN 32.
Te. gdlincu to fry. Go. (S.) golis- id. (Voc. 1236). DEDS 337.

Ta. k6vai a climbing shrub, Bryonia epigaea; a common creeper of the hedges, Coccinia indica (also kovvai). Ma. kova B. grandis. Ka. kove the climbing plant B. grandis with beautiful red fruit. Te. krovi, (B. also) kovi, g(r)6vi Galedupa indica Lam. [Corallocarpus epigaea Hook. = Bryonia epigaea Rottler; Cephalandra indica Naud. = B. grandis Linn. = Coccinia indica W. & A.; Pongamia glabra Vent. = G. indica Lain. = G. arborea Roxb.] DED 1861.

Ta. kori gallinaceous fowl. Ma. koxi fowl. Ko. ko-y id. To. kwi-dy id.; kwi-y (in songs; either from Ko. or from Badaga k6- i) id. Ka, kori a cock, a hen, a fowl in general. Kod. ko-li fowl. Tu. kori, (B-K. also) koli id. Te. k6di id. Nk. (Ch.) gogodi, gogori cock (< Go.). Go. (Tr.) gogort, (Ph.) gugort, (Y.) ghogri, (Mu. Ma. S. Ko.) gogor id. (Voe. 1184). Cf. Apabhraméa (VJasaharacarit) kodi- id., fowl. DED(S) 1862.

Ta. koxai phlegm, mucus, saliva. Ka. kore thick phlegm. Kui (K.) groho phlegm. Kuwi (D.) krahu ki- to spit out phlegm. Kur. -x6, in: tuppalxd saliva, spittle (see 3323; Busrow 1968, p.67). DED(N) 1863, DEDS

Ta. kdgai bashfulness, timidity; bash- ful person. Ma, kora bashfulness. ? Ko. koyed timidity, terror (or with 1876 Ta. kiicu). Ka. kodu to shrink, fear; 1. shrinking, fear; a wonderful thing, wonder. Te. (B.) kod-adu to be amazed or confounded. DED(S) 1864.

Ta. kdl calumny, aspersion, tale- bearing, falsehood; kolan tale-bearer; koluttu (kolutti-) to slander, calumniate; konfi, kontiyam tale-bearing, backbiting; kuntani, kuntuni slander, calumny. Ma, kunfani backbiting. Ko. ko-l laying information about something heard. Ka. kof calumny, false imputation; kondeya, konde, kondega backbiting, calumny, slander, abuse, an informer, slanderer, defamer; kondisu_ to slander, defame. Te. kondemu_ backbiting, accusation, slander, calumny; kondek@du tale-bearer, slanderer, informer; fem. konde- katte. Ga. (OIL) goler- (gdlen-) to abuse; (P.) gole abuse. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM; Norman) kop- diya- one who creates dissension among villagers. DED(S, N) 1865.

Ko. gol, go-lm sorrow; go-lo- excl. used in songs; go++s in- (id-) to lament. Ka, gol, gor sound of howling lamenting, wailing, or roaring; gold the sound produced in lamentation and ‘weeping. Kod. go-li struggling, agony. Tu. golu, golu grief, lamen- tation. Te. godu grief, affliction, a long and sad story or account; gola wailing, loud out- cry, howl; golu loud noise or outery; golu- golu onom, crying or lamentation. Cf. 1813 Ka. gullu. DED(S, N) 1866.

Ta. koli banyan, fig, tree bearing fruit without outwardly blossoming; kopi fig; korifici trees or plants, as the figs, which bear without blossoming. ? Me. koli an epidendron, grasping plant (some figs are of this nature). Ka. goli all kinds of fig trees which bear no apparent flowers; banyan; Ficus elastica Roxb.; goni F. elastica; F. religiosa. Tu. golida mara banyan tree, FP. indica. DED 1867. kora : {201] akkul

Ma. (Tiyya) k6ra rough cloth. To, kwier rag. Ka. kori id., worn-out blanket. Tu. (B-K.) k6ro rough cloth. Cf. 1925 Ta. kirai. DED(S, N) 1868. _2256 Konda (BB) kori buffalo. Pe. kudru id. Mand. kudru id. Kui koru (pl. korka) id. Kuwi (Su. P.) kodru, (F.) kodri, (S.) kodru, godru id. DEDS 340.

Ta. korai a scratch, as on the body. Ma. koruka to tear the flesh by thorns; koru cutting, tearing. Ka. kdye cutting, sharpness, pointedness; a tusk, fang; gdru to scratch. Kod. ko-re tusk of elephant or boar. ? Tu. kdruni, goruni to dig out or up. Te. kora tusk, fang, tooth; koru to scrape with a grater, (K. also) cut, scratch; m. scrapings of coconut; gdku to scratch with nails or the like, scrape. Kol. (SR.) kok- to itch, scratch; (Kin.) kdk- to itch. Vk. kokk- to itch, scratch. Go. (Koya T.) koru tusk. Kui grOpa (grot-) to claw, scratch; . act of clawing, scratching, a scratch. ? Cf. Te. goru, s.v. 561 Ta. ukir; cf. 1922 Ka. giru. DED(S, N) 1869.

Te. kSgu, gorugu to shave. Go. (A. Ch. G. S. Ko.) kori-, (Ph.) koritana, korritana, koritind, (Mu.) kori-, korri-, (Ma.) kori-, (M.) korana id.; (Tr.) koritand to cut the hair, shave (Voc. 918). DED 1870. C, 2264 Kur. ca’(a)ni (cacas) to stink, give forth an offensive smell; to smell in general. Malt. cenye (cenca) to emit odour; cenyro scented, smelling. DEDS 348.

To. oxy- (oxs-) to chew. Ka. agi to champ, bite, chew, eat; avudu to chew with the teeth, champ, chew the cud; jagi, jegi, jigi, (Bark.. Hav.) jagi, (Hal.) jegi.to chew. Tu. aggiyuni, agyuni id, bite. DED(N) 13.
Ta. cakati mud, mire, bog, puddle; (RS, p.144, item 219, recorded from an early dictionary) cavati, (Tinn.) cavaSi mud. Ma. cakati clay, mire. DEDS(N) 349.
Ko. cak lean (of meat); cakn lean man; fem. caky. Tu. cakku, (B-K.) cekku stunted, short, lean; cakké a weakling, lean person. DEDS 351.

Ta. cakkattam, cakkantam scoff, mockery, sport, censure. Ka. cakkanda sportful, idle talk; happiness, pleasure, con- tentedness; jakkulisu, jakkulisu to amuse, divert, rejoice, play about, jeer at, make sport of, deride. Tu. cakkanda plausibleness, speciousness. Te. (B.) jakkalimpu jeering, quizzing. Go. (Ph.) cakka kiyadnd to deride, 2259 Kui (K.) krandu mongoose. Kuwi (Su.) krandu, (S.) krandu, (Isr.) gandra id. DEDS 341. 2260 Mand. gri- to be bitter. Kui gripka (< grik-p; grikt-) to irritate the throat, as when something briny or pungent is eaten; ? gretenji sour, unpleasant to the taste. Kuwi (Su.) grik- (-h-) to be bitter. DEDS 342.
Ka. cakkala, cekkala a small cot or oblong low couch of cane-work. Tu. (B-K.) cakkano a snfall mat.
Ta. cakkali (-v-, -nt-) to become oblate, flattened, compressed. Ka. cakkari that has become flat by pressure. Tu. cakku flat. DED(S) 1872. °

Ta. cakkili caste of shoemakers; cakkiliyan chuckler, worker in Jeather; cakkilicci woman of the Cakkili caste; cekkili cobbler. Ma. cakkiliyan a Tamil shoemaker; fem. cakkilicci. Ka. cakkala skin, leather. Kuwi (S.) jaggelaasi, (Isr.) jakera shoemaker. / Cf. Mar. sagal goat’s skin leather. DED 1873.
Ka. cakli the Manilla tamarind, Pithecolobium dulce Benth. Tu. cakkulimara the Manilla tamarind tree, Inga dulcis. {P. dulce Benth. = I. dulcis Willd.} DED 1874.

Ta. akkul, cafkam armpit; akkulu (-pp- -tt-) to tickle. Ma. akkulam armpit, tickling; ikkili, kikkili tickling; kikkilikka to tickle. Ka. akala a term used in tickling: akala-cakala, akala-sakala excessive tickling: akkalike tickling; cakkalaguli, cakalaguli, cakkalaguli tickling another; jakkulisu to tickle, touch; ? jangudi the region of the cakkai [202] saficu arm. Kod. kakkuli tickling. Te. cakkili arm- pit; cakkiligili, cakkiliginta tickling; cafka, cankili armpit. Kol (SR.) sankad, (Kin.) sanka, (P.) sakka id. Wk, sakka id. Ga, (S.7) canka id. Kur. cangrna to itch, be the seat of a titillation; cangrta’ana to tickle, titillate, please (as a musical sound). Malt. céql- kiinde to tickle. DED(S) 1875.

Ta. cakkai jackfruit, jungle jack. Ma, cakka big round fruit as of Artocarpus lintegrifolia), Ananas (kaita-- [Pandanus odoratissimus]), bread-fruit [A. incisa}, wild Artocarpus {A. hirsuta]. Ka. (Lush.) jaka A. integrifolia. Kod. cakke jackfruit. DED 1876.

Ta. cakkai refuse as of sugar-cane after pressing, rind of fibrous parts of fruits, anything wanting in solidity or strength, anything useless, bark; cakkal rotten straw, muck, that which is withered, shrivelled, chaffy. Afa. cakka skin or rind of a fruit thrown away after pressing it out. Te. (K.) cekka oil-cake (refuse after pressing oil). Cf 2751 Ta. cekil. DED(S) 1877. 2277 Kur, cakna to sharpen an edge instru- ment, whet. Malt. cake to sharpen, whet. DED 1878. 2278 Kur. cakkhna (cakkhyas/cakkos) to pierce with a prick, prick, penetrate into, puncture, cause a prickly sensation, experi- ence a prickly sensation; reff. cakkhrna (to get tattooed, etc.); cakkhta’and to cause to be pierced, tattooed. Malt. cage to sting, pierce, stab; also applied to the sowing of certain grains for which holes are made in the earth; caqro worm-eaten roots; caqtre to have the ears bored; caqu shooting pains in the stomach. Br. jaxxing to run into, pierce. DED 1879.

Ka. jagati, jagali, jagali, jaguli an artificially raised place, a kind of sacrificial altar, the pedestal of an idol, a seat of mud, stone, etc. Tu. jagali an open veranda. Te. jagati, jagile a pyal. DED 1880.

Te. jaggu shining, brilliancy. Pa. jagjaga clean (of clothes), bright. Go. (S.) cakk- to dazzle (Voc. 1273). /MBE 1969, p.291, no. 13, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 5076, Pkt. jagajaganta- shining, etc., and no. 5318, Skt. jhagajhagayate flashes, sparkles, etc. DED(N) 1881.

Ta. canku (canki-) to be dispirited. Ko. jag- Gagy-) to be afraid. Ka. jahkisu to scold, chide; jaikane, janke scolding, chiding. Tu. hafgusavuni, hafgipuni to upbraid, rebuke. Te. jafku to fear, be afraid, hesitate, shake; 7. fear, hesitation; jafikincu to frighten, intimidate; jake, jafkena threat; januku to fear. DED(S) 1882.

Ta. cahku throat (loc.); caitku- kataital rattling in the throat, as of dying persons. Ma. cahku throat; cafiku karayuka to rattle in the throat, as of dying persons. Pe, hak neck; hak-nara throat. DEDS(N) 353.

Ko. jank slackness (of rope); jang- (jangy-) (rope or waistcloth) becomes slack or loose, (handle of tool) is loose. Ka. jahgala slackness, looseness; jagur to move off, slip, drop down. Te. (K.; classical) jahgalam a loose garment. DED(S, N) 1883.

Ka. jahgali, jahguli, jangala, jahguri mass, assemblage, herd, etc. Te. (B.) jangili a herd of cattle. DED 1884. 2285 Ka, cafgu, cefigu to jump, skip, frisk about, caper; 7. a jump, étc.; cafigane in bounds, friskily, with agility; cigi, jigi to jump. Tu. catga, cahgané, cafgamafga, cahgaimangai a frolic, gambol; hoax, humbug, deceit. Te. cefiguna nimbly, agilely, quickly, suddenly, applied to leaping; cenganalu leap- ing, frisking, gambol, capers; (B.) cefigu, jangu agility. Nk. (Ch.) cangay- to climb; caggap- to make to climb. DED(S, N) 1885.

Ka. jahge a stride. Te. jahga, anga id. Kol. zanga pace. Pa. janga step, stride. /Cf. Skt. jahgha- lower leg (Turner, CDIAL, no. 5082), from which these words are probably derived (Krishnamurti, Language 39.563). DED(S) 1886.
Ka. cajje, cejje, jejjie, sajie, seijje Holcus spicatus. Te. sajje, (VPK) sajja, sadda (sodda) id. DED(S) 1888.
Ka. cafica, caficu a man of wild tribe that resides in forests, a Sabara; fem. caiiciti. Te. cencu, canciidu a certain savage tribe; a man of this tribe; fem, cenceta. DED 1889.
Ka. caiicali, caricali a small tree commonly cultivated, Flacourtia cataphracta Roxb, Je. cencali Digera muricata. DED 1890.

Ka. saficu to be full of artifices or act cunningly; n. expedient, means, artifice, strategem, trick, intrigue; a trace. Tu. saficu intrigue, plotting, artifice, stratagem, spying. Te. sancu manner, way, artifice, stratagem; a trace: (B.) cancu trim, delicacy, fashion, way, mode. ? Ta. camucu seditious or riotous assembly; evil counsel, plot. DED 1891. canju {203] cattam 2294 Kur. (Mirdha dial., BB 1958) canju antelope. Malt. canju a deer. / Cf. Skt. (lex.) caficu- deer. DEDS 356.

Ta. catakku speed, rapidity; catakk- enal onom. expr. signifying haste, rush; catta speedily; caft-enal onom. expr. signify- ing quickness, suddenness. Ka. catakkane all at once, suddenly, used of rising or sneezing; cattane suddenly, all at once, quickly, also of sneezing. Tu. cattane suddenly. Te. cattana soon, without delay, promptly. Cf. 3022 Ko. dadn. / An areal etymology: Turner, CDIAL, no.. 4570, Beng. H. Guj. Mar. cat quickly, suddenly; no. 4969, Nep. chaffai quickly; no. 5327(2) *jhatt-, e.g. H. jhat quickly, Mar. jhat-jhat smartly, Beng. jhaf-, jhat-pat quickly. DED(S) 1892. ;

Ta. cata-cat-enal onom. expr. signify- ing the sound of falling trees, report of a gun, rattling of stones thrown; cata-catav-enal onom. expr. signifying crash, peal of repeated sound; cata-puf-enal, cafar-enal onom. expr. of crackling noise. Ma. cata-cata crash, peal, descriptive of battle noises. Ko. cata+r in- to make noise like whipcrack; cafa-r potasr in-, caftr pofr in- to make noise like whipcrack or loud crackling of fire. Ka. cat sound of wood when suddenly broken or that of an earthen pot when cracking on the fire, a term in imitation of the smacking noise of a whip or of the noise of a smart blow with a cane; cafa-cafa, cafa-pata the snapping sound of com in being parched; catal, catil imitation of the loud crack of a whip; cafirane, cafuku, cataku with the noise of a smart blow witha cane or whip. Kod. cat noise of cracking fruit or nut. Tu. catakka a slapping or cracking sound; catacata, catapata noise produced as in slapping or cracking a whip; catila a crack- ing sound; catyka a snapping sound. Kol. saf- (satt-) to shoot with gun. Kur. catka’and, catga’ana to slap in the face (also Bleses and Tiga; Hahn’s catga’and has t mistakenly); cafcatrna to crackle, give forth a series of small explosions (as fire or loud slaps). Malt. cay-catre to crackle (as wood in the fire). /MBE 1969, pp. 291-2, no. 14, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 4570, “*cata-, cafacata-, cafacafayate. DED (S) 1893.

Ka. jadi to use threatening language, menace, frighten, blame, urge on by cries. Tu. jadipini, jadipuni, jadiyuni to drive catile; jadu fright, shyness; dadipuni to drive away. Te. jadiyu to fear, be afraid; jadipincu to frighten; jadupu fear, fright, dread, alarm. Cf. 2407 Ta. calai. DED 1895.

Ka. jadi to wave, brandish as a sword, etc.; to move to and fro. Tu. jadipuni to shake off. Te. jadincu to flap or toss about, shake; (K.) jadiyu to fly, brandish as a weapon. Cf. 2403 Go. jarhutténa. DEDS 357. 2299 Ka, jadipa the sound or cry of birds. Te, sadi noise. Kur. saddna to emit a sound, ting, clank; sarz sound, noise. Malt. sadi a sound, voice. DED(S) 1896.

Ta. cafai (-v-, -nt-) to flatten, as the head or point of a nail by repeated blows, clinch, rivet; cafu (cati-) to beat, trample, gore, kill, destroy; cattu (caffi-) to beat, strike; caffam beating. Ka. jadi to beat, pound, crush, beat into (as mud into a hole), force in, ram as a cartridge, drive in as a nail; caus, jadisu; jadata, jadita beating, ramming, forcing in as a cartridge, driving in as a nail. Tu. jadipini, jadipuni, jadiyuni to ram, stuff, load as firearms; cadayisuni to beat, strike, flog; cadi a whip, stripe. Te. sadincu to pound, beat; sadimpu pounding, beating; sadimpulu rice beaten and cleaned; (K.) jadiyu to beat, hit. Pa. cadp- (cadt-) to strike, beat, hammer. Kui jarsa a whip, scourge. Malt. jare to shake down, beat down as fruits. DED(S) 1894.

Ta. catai (-v-, -nt-) to become weary, dispirited, be stunted in growth (as trees, plants); cataivu wearisomeness, depression of spirits, dejection. Ma. catekka to grow thin, lean, weak. Tu. cadpu leanness, thin- ness. Kur. carmna (Tiga carmna) to become flaccid, flabby (as a plant cut off from the stem), feel or look weak, enervated (through heat, exertion, illness, cares); carmta’and to weaken, debilitate, unnerve. Malt. cayme to wither, be scorched. DEDS 358, and from DED (S) 1930.

Pa. cadug (pl. -gul), (S.) cadum footprint, trace. Ga. (P.) sarin (pl. -il} foot- print. From DED(S) 63. 2303 Kol. satta shoulder-blade, (SR.) shoulder. Nk, satfa back. Go. (W. Ph.) satta, (Tr.) satta, (G. Mu. S.} hafta, (M. Ma.) afta, (Ko.) ata jaba shoulder (Voc. 3326); (ASu.) setta shoulder-blade. ? Ma. caftuvam shoulder. bone (or with 2309 Ta. cattukam). Cf. 2764 Ta. cettai. DED(N) 1898.

Ta. catftam wooden frame, socket, plan, model, rule, order, regulation, exactness, precision; caffa properly, rightly; caftakam frame, framework; bed, couch; shape, figure, image, body. Ma. cattam frame as of a door, window, cot; enclosure, mould, plan, order, proportion, nature, disposition, regulation, tule, law. Ko. cat rack, poles on roof parallel to roof-tree to hold tiles or thatch; cafm rule, tribal custom. Ka. catta frame of a cart, bedstead, chair, or picture, bottom or plat- form of a cart, bier; plan, order, regulation, neatness, fineness; caftu the bottom or platform of a cart. Tu. catta a litter, sort of palanquin, bier, window frame. Te. caftamu a frame, a plan, system, arrangement, law, regulation, rule. /Cf. Skt. (lex.) safta- two pieces of timber at the side of a door; Turner, CDIAL, no. 13102. DED(S) 1899.
Ta. caffam sac or gland in the anal pouch of the civet cat. Ma, catfam id. DED 1900. catti : [204] cati

Ta. catfi earthen vessel, pan. Ma. catti pot, pan. Ka. cafti, cetti earthen pot or pan; catige, cattige small earthen pot with a broad mouth. Ju. catti broad-mouthed spittoon, chamber pot. Te. caf{i earthen pot with a wide mouth. Konda sati cooking pot. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, nos. 4736, 4738, 4739; the items in DBIA 158 are re-borrowings from CDIAL 4736. DED(S, N) 1901.
Ta. cattu destruction, injury, waste; catti (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy, ruin, kill. Ka. cattu destruction. Tu. cattu end, dissolution, death, ruin, extinction. DED 1902.

Ka. cattu, caffa, catte flatness, levelness. Tu. caffé flat, level; catté-kallu a flat stone. Te. cattu rock. Pa. cat (pl. catful) id. Konda satu, safu-panku flat rock (panku . stone). DED{S) 1903, 1904.

Ta. cattukam, caffuvam ladle, metal spatula with a long handle for turning and removing a cooked cake. Ma, caffukam ladle, metal spoon; ? catjuvam shoulder-bone (or with 2303 Kol. satta). Ko. caty goel iron ladle with flat, round blade, for taking rice from pot. Ka. satuka, saftu, sattuga, sotaka ladle, spoon. Kod. cattuva wooden spoon used for stirring. /u. satti a kind of wooden ladle; satfuga, taftuga a flat kind of trough for serving boiled rice. Te. caffuvame a sort of spoon with a shallow bowl having holes in it. Ga. (P.) safve ladle. Konda satva ladle made of wood for serving soup or curry. Pe, hajva ladle; oar. Kuwi (Su.) hatva ladle. / Cf. Skt. catuka- a wooden vessel for taking up fluid; Pkt. (ONM; Norman) catpe wooden spoon; Turner, CDJAL, no. 4575. DEDG, N) 1905. 2310 Ta, cattai jacket, coat, gown, cloak, slough of a snake. Ma, catfa jacket, bodice, .mailcoat, armour. Ka. caffe a garment adapted to the body after the European fashion. Tu. cattegare a native who assumes European dress. Te. cafta a small sack, jacket, coat. DED 1906. 2311 Kol. (Pat., p. 59) satte mat. Nk. satte id. Pa. catta id. Ga. (S.3) satte id. / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 4574, *catta- bamboo matting; (Burrow 1967, p. 41). DED(N) 1907.

Pa. catf- to roast, set fire; cadip- (cadit-) to boil (t7.). Ga. sat- (OLL.) to roast, (S.3) burn. DED 1908. ; 2313 Ka, jadda nearness, union; jadd- akkara one consonant written below another; dadda closeness, union; daddakkara a com- pound or double consonant; dande state of being adjoining or close by, vicinity. Te. jadda a compound consonant; (B. also) near, close by, connected with; danda nearness, proximity, side. DED(S) 1909.

Ka. jaddu, jiddu, daddu callosity, a calious spot, wart, scar; dadda a blockhead, a ‘stupid, doltish, ignorant man. Kod. daddé a dull man; fem. dadde; (Shanmugam) daddi a dull woman. Ju. jaddu callosity, hardness as of the skin of the hand, etc., from much use; daddu id.; dull, stupid, idle; dadde a stupid fellow; fem. daddi. Kor. (M.) jeddi mole (on skin). Te. jaddu drowsiness, sluggish- ness. DED 1910.

Ta. anil, apilam, (Ag., p. 175) aniyal, (Koll.) annattin squirrel. Ma. anil, annal, apnan id.; enuttku a variety of mountain squirrel. Ko, eendl squirrel. To. anil id. Ka. anal, anil, alale, alil, ajul, allima, a{indaki, inaci, (Bark.) capila id. Kod. anekotfi id. Tu. capilu, canily, tanilu, (B-K. also) anilu id. DED(S) 1911.

Ta. cantu chaff, broken chips of spoilt straw; ? an insect damaging growing crops. Ma. canfu chaff; canti sediment, husks, pepper amenta, dry leaves, ? Ka. carafa, catta extraneous matter separated by straining, sifting, or filtering. DED 1912.
Ta. canfai conflict, quarrel, fight, war. Ma, canta quarrel. DEDS 360.
Ma. canna buttocks of animals, ham. Kod. canne buttocks. ? 7a. cannam penis; cannu (canni-) to copulate. DED 1914.

Te. canpakam, ceppakam Michelia champaca. Ma, cempakam, campakam. Ka, sampage, sampige, campaka, Kod. cappayé- mara campaka tree; cappayém-pu+ campaka flower. Tu. sampigé, sampayi. Te. samp€ga, campakamu (c, not ts), (B. also) canupakamu (c= ts). /Cf. Skt. campaka-; BHS canpaka- (Brough, BSOAS 16. 366); Turner, CD/JAL, no. 4678. DED 1915.

Ta. cati Cpp-, -tt-) to destroy, kill; catavu (catavi-) to be shattered or broken, be rotten, decayed; catai (-v-, -nt-) to be bruised, crushed; (-pp-, -tt-) to crush, bruise, mash; tatai (-v-, -nt-) to be shattered, made fruitless; tatar (-v-, -nt-) to be crushed. Ma, cata a bruise; catayuka to be bruised, squashed; catekka to squash, crush, pound, bray; catuhiuka to be crushed, compressed, beaten into another shape; catukkuka to bruise, macerate; catukku a bruise. Ko. cady a murder; pair word with cudy news (cudy cady); cac- (cac-) to hammer with heavy blows, pat, stroke, kill (animal); cac a blow. To. toOx- (toOxy-) to be squeezed or smashed flat, (person) gets into difficulties; toOk- (toOky-) to squeeze or smash flat; toc- (tot-) , to beat (or with 3089 Ma. taykkuka). Ka. sadaku to beat; sade, sadi to pound to some extent, bruise, squash, crush; caccu, ceccu, jajju to strike, bruise, crush; tadaku, taduku, tade to strike, beat. Tu. cade severe fighting, destruction of life; sadi striking, beating; cati [205] sadevuni to get hardened, wear away as an earthen vessel; jajjuni to bruise, squash, pound. Te, cadiyu to be crushed or broken; cadupu to crush, kill; cadumu to strike: cadunu even, level. Pa. catip- (catit-), (S.) cacip- (cacit-) to trample, kick. DED(S)

Ta. cati (-pp-, -tt-) to deceive; n. treachery, perfidy, wiles. Ma. cati deccit, treachery; catikka to cheat, circumvent, betray. Ka. (Hav.) cadi deceit. Kod. cadi- (cadip-, cadic-) to cheat. Tu. (B-K.) cadi deceit. / 2? < Skt. chad- (cf. chadman- deceit). DED 1917.
Ta. catir cheapness, low price, frugality; catur cheapness. Ma. catir cheap. DED 1918.
Ta. catir boundary, limit. Ma. atir id. Ka. (Gowda) adiri border. Tu. adiru, adury a limit, boundary wall. DED 118, 1915.

Ta. catuppu-nilam bog, marshy ground; catuval swampy ground; cetukku mud, mire; cetumpu (cetumpi-) to become damp and moist, be soaked; n. mud, mire, thin current of water as of rivers in summer. Ma, (Tiyya) catuppu marsh. Te. caduku mud. DEN 33. :

Ta. catur ability, skill, dexterity; means, contrivance; catir ability, skill, dexterity; greatness, excellence; beauty, loveliness; catiri skilful woman. Ma. caturam dexterous, clever, lovely. Ka. caduru, ceduru cleverness. Tu, cadupu skill, ingenuity, cunning. Te, cadurfidu a clever, able, or capable man; fem. caduralu; ? caduvu to read, study; 7. reading, learning, education; caduvari scholar. / Cf. Skt. catura- dexterous, clever; charming, agreeable. DED 1920.

Ta. cantam beauty, colour, shape, form, pleasure, happiness, manners, habits; antam beauty, comeliness. Ma. cantam beauty, elegance; antam beauty. Ka, canda, cenda pleasing, beautiful, lovely, charming, propriety, fitness, niceness, beauty; appear- ance, shape, form, kind, manner; anda fitness, beauty, pleasantness; suitable, nice or good form or manner, array, manner, form, nature, faculty. Tu. canda beauty, elegance; anda form, shape, beauty, elegance, handsomeness, symmetry; purport; beautiful, pretty; anda- canda very handsome, pleasant. Te. candamu manner, way, state, condition; andamu beauty; andagidu a handsome man; fem, andakatte; andagincu to look beautiful or nice, appear adorned, be beautiful; anda- impu adorning, ornamenting, beauty. Cf.

Ta. capam, cavam bamboo, Bambusa arundinacea (< Ka.). ?To, tef sp. bamboo used in building. Ke, capa bamboo cane. DED(S) 1923. 2331 Ta, cappatfai flatness, anything flat; cappafi anything flat; cappali (-v-, -nt-) to flatten, be crushed, pressed out of form; cappu (cappi-) to be bent, pressed in; cappai that which is flattened. Ma, cappafa flat; cippu what is smoothed, flat. Ka, cappate, capafe, appafe flatness, flat; cappadi a large flat stone; cappe that which is flattened or pressed down. Te. cappi flat, not projecting; cappidi flat, not projecting, snub. Kol. (Pat., p. 115) sapat flat. Konda sapajam ki- to crush. Kuwi (Isr.) sapta flat. / Cf. Skt. carpafa., etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4696. DED 1924.
Ta. cappal refuse of food, leavings. Ma, campu leavings, refuse. Kol. sap- (sapt-) to pour away water from hand-washing. Pa, capp- to pour. DED(S) 1925.
Ka. sappala, sappul, soppal, soppul, soppula a sound, a noise. Te. cappudu id.; cappudincu to make a noise. DED 1926.

Ta. cappu (cappi-) to masticate, chew (as betel or tobacco), mumble in eating, munch, sip, suck; cavattu (cavatfi-) to chew, masticate, swallow down; cavai (-pp-, -tt-) to chew, munch, suck mother’s milk. Ma. cappuka to smack the lips, suck, sip, eat whilst working; cava chewing, the sound of it; cavekka to chew; cavaccal chewing. Ko. cap- (capy-) to suck repeatedly (as when child sucks breast); ayv- (avc-) to chew. To, sop- (sopy-) to suck. Ka, capparisu to chuckle or cluck to an animal to urge it on, smack the lips in sipping or supping, produce the peculiar sound of chewing, chew with a noise; cappa- rane the peculiar smacking of the lips by which peasants stop their cattle, chuckling; jabbu, jabbisu to suck. Kod. cavel- (cave"mp-, caveMnt-) to chew; (Nalknad dialect) caye"- (caye"mp-, caya™nt-) id.; (Shanmugam) caymp chewing. Tu. capparipuni to smack the lips; capacapa a noise made with the mouth as a pig in eating. Te. capparincu to smack, suck with a noise; (K.) cappu to smack, suck, smack with noise. Kol (Pat., p. 171) savseng to chew. Nk. savs- id. Pa. cavl-, cal-, (S.) calv- id. Ga, (S.3) savl- (salv-, saluv-) id. Go, (Ko.) alv- id. (< *avl-, with metathesis of -vl- to -lv- as in Pa. Ga.; Voc. 91); (ASu.)} jalu- id. Malt. cap-captre to smack, chomp; copye to chew (as sugarcane). Br, cap-cap kanning to smack the tongue against the roof of the mouth in eating. /Cf. Turner, CD/AL, no. 4675, *cappayati, Pali cappeti chews, capucapukara- making the sound of chewing; MBE 1

Ta. cappani clapping hands. Ko. capaft noise of hand-clap. Ka. capparisu, cappalisu to slap, pat; cappate, cappali, cappale clapping the hands; tappalisu to clap cappuna [206] the hands. Tu. cappali clapping the hands; cappalipuni to clap the hands. Te, cappata a slap of the hand, the hand held with fingers fully extended, the flat of the hand; cappatincu to clap, slap; cappatlu (p/.) clapping of the hands; camaru, camuru to slap with the open hand; (K.) capparincu to pat, stroke with the hand. Nk. capat a slap. Go. (Ma.) capur, (A.) capta, (Ko.} capoy a blow with the hand, slap (Voc. 1284). Mand. hapay a slap. Kui japka japka vépa to slap, beat with open hand. Kuwi (Su.) sapor a slap. Cf. 157 Ta. appu. / Cf. Skt. capefa- a slap with the open hand; Turner, CDIAL, nos. 4673, 4696. DED(S, N) 1928(a).

Te. cappuna quickly, at once, promptly. Go. CW.) japne, (Ph.) jhap, (Ko.) jappe, japi quickly (Voc. 1390). Kur, capna (cappyas) to hurry. _ 2337 Ta, cappai that which is insipid or tasteless; capp-enal onom. expr. signifying insipidity; cappattai flatness, emptiness, hol- lowness. Ma. cappafta vapid, flat, insipid. Ko. cap, capiely tastelessness of broth with- out salt. Ka. cappage, sappage, sappane flat, vapid, insipid, dull, inert; insipidity, etc.; cappe, sappe flatness, insipidness, state of being spiritless, dull, flat, inert, etc. Kod. cappe tasteless, insipid. Tu. cappatu tasteless, insipid, bad, worthless; cappé tasteless, vapid, unsavoury; jaule, jembé tasteless, insipid. Te, cappa tasteless, insipid, vapid, flat, dull; capp4ga tastelessly, insipidly; cappana taste- lessness, insipidity; cappani tasteless, insipid, flat, dull; cappidi insipid, tasteless, saltless. Nk. sappe tasteless, insipid. Pa. capre id. Ga, (Oll.) sapre id.; (S) sapréti, sapra, (S.3) sappan id. Konda (BB) sapra insipid. Kuwi (Su.) hapili, (Isr.) hapli id., saltless. /Cf. Skt. capata- arasah (Hem. Un. 142), Mar. sapak insipid, unsavory, wanting the requisite saltness or sweetness, Konk. cappo flat, insipid. DED(S) 1929.

Ta. cappai that which is weak, lean, emaciated, useless, despicable, mean; cavu (-pp-, -tt-) to become tired, become weak, emaciated, fail (as a crop), fall flat (as a crop), lose crispness; cavutam fatigue (as of mind), weakness, emaciation. Ka, jabbala, jabbalu state of being weak, infirm or frail from old age, that of being soft or pulpy as of a ripe fruit, that of being relaxed or slack as of female’s breast; jabbu weakness, frailty, softness, pulpiness, relaxed state, slackness. Tu, jabbu hollow, empty within, not solid, old, infirm; jabbe an old man; jabbi, jabbu an old woman, a hag; jabulu slack. Te. jabbu illness, sickness, indisposition, weakness, backwardness, poorness, slowness, sluggish- ness; weak, poor, bad, no good, inferior, defective, meagre, shabby, slow, sluggish, flat, dull. Go. (ASu.) sapptr lean, thin. Konda sapur being lean, skinny; sapurti Jean, skinny. DED (S, N) 1930. camai

Ta. cappai hips, haunch, shoulder blade; ceppu hip; appu thigh. Ka. cappe the hip bone; (UNR) jabbe the outer side of the thigh. Te. jabba shoulder, outer side of the thigh. Pa. (S.) jabba shoulder. Go. (Ko.) jaba, in: ata jaba shoulder (Voc. 3326). Konda zeba 1d. DED(S) 1931.

Ta. cappai a spar of wood placed for the wheels of a car to run smoothly in a sandy road, rafter, a chip of wood. Te. (B.) cappa a wedge, a spar of wood placed for the wheels of a car to pass over in a sandy road. DED 1932.

Ta. avi (-v-, -nt-) to ferment (as decayed fruit, vegetable matter, manure heaps); avical that which is decayed. Ma. aviyuka to rot, spoil (as fruits laid on a heap); avikka, amikka to produce rotting (as of fruits for distillation). Ka. avi to rot, be spoiled, damaged. Te. (K.) aviyu to rot. Nk. (Ch.) sam- to be rotten. Pa. cam- to go bad, become rotten; camip- (camit-) to make to go rotten. Ga. (OU.) sam- to become rotten; (S.) cammi cen- to rot (as fruit); (8.3) ? savur rheum of the eyes. Go. (Ch.) sav-, (Tr.) sowwana, (Ph.) savvand, sauvana, (SR.) sovana, (Mu.) hav-, (Ma.) av- to be rotten, go bad (food, eggs) (Voc, 3357). Konda sab- (-it-) to rot and produce an offensive smell (as cooked tice, etc.). Pe. hab- ¢hapt-) to go bad. Manda hab- to decay. Kuwi (isr.) hap- to be rotten; (Mah.) jabbar ganda bad smell. Kur. cltwarna to get an unpleasant taste. Cf. 267 Ta. avi, 2343 Ta. camai, and 2424 Kur. canxna,. DED(S) 1933. 2342 Ta, camai (-v-, -nt-) to be made, constructed, formed, get ready, prepare (oneself), be suitable, commence, mature, attain puberty;, Cpp-, -tt-) to create, do, perform, get ready, prepare, cook; camaippu action, effort; camaiyal cooking, cooked food; camaivu state, situation; cavatari (-pp-, -tt-) to acquire, get ready, behave agreeably; cavaranai preparedness. Ma, cama- yam getting ready for a grand occasion, equipment, dress and ornaments, preparation, mien and manner, stocking a garden; camayuka to get ready, dress well, assume a shape, be matured,

Ta. camai (-v-, -nt-) to be consumed, destroyed, finish (¢r.); (-pp-, -tt-) to kill, destroy; camaivu destruction. Ma. cavati consumption. Ko. cam- (camd-) to be finished, spent; (camt-) to finish. Ka. same, save, savi to be destroyed, be consumed, wear away, be abraded, pass away (as time), decline in strength; samisu, samayisu, savisu, savayisu to make to wear away, etc.; savar to perish. Tu. savipuni to abrade. Te, samayu to die, perish, be destroyed; samayincu to kill, destroy; camaru to kill. Go. (SR.) javu- kana, (M. 8.) havk-, (Mu.) havk-, hovk-, (Ma. Ko.) avk- to kill (Voc. 1407); (LuS.) houkto as 267 Ta. avi and 234} Ta. avi. DED 5. 2344 Ta, campatam cloth. Ma, campatam very dirty cloth. DED 1936.
Ka. jambara affair, business. Tu. jambiitra id. DED(S) 1937.
Ta. camp a superior kind of paddy, Ma, campa id. Ke, sambe-nellu id. Te, sam- bavu, (VPK) samba (samba, simbam), sim- baru (sambaru-, sambhava-, sambhavu-) a fine sort of rice. DED(S) 1938.

Ta. campu elephant grass, Typha elephantina; sola pith; campah-korai elephant grass; canpu elephant grass; a species of sedge grass. Ka. jambu a kind of reed or sedge, T. angustifolia L.; ? 8pu elephant grass, T. elephantina Roxb.; ?jondu elephant grass. Te. jambu a bulrush, sedge. DED(S) 1939.

Ta. campai fish. Ma. campa a fish, boat-load of fishes. DED 1940. 2349 Ta, cammafti smith’s large hammer, sledge; cammaffi-kktam hammer for beating metal into plates or thin leaves. Ma, cammatti- kUfam id. To. somofy hammer, Ka. cammat- (pige sledge hammer. Tu. camatige id. Te. sammeta id. DED 1941.
Ta. cammanam, cappapam sitting cross-legged. Ma, cammanam id. DED 1942,

Ka. say to cease, be quieted, be stilled; saytu cessation, ceasing from action, rest, ease, quiet, stillness, silence; saypu cessation, quiet, stillness; saddu (sg.), saddi (pl) cease! stop! (verb used in the imperative only); sasane, sasine still, uttering no sound, motionless; savagisu to cease, become quiet, still. Tu. (B-K.) sairu, hairy, tairy, airu to wait, stop, be patient. Cf. 2458 Kol. say-. - cara-car-enal 2352 (a) Ta. carakka quickly, speedily. Ka. sarakkane swiftly; sarasarane quickly, hastily. Te. saraga quickly, rapidly, soon; saragu quickness, swiftness, speed. Go, (Mu.) carkne immediately (Voc. 1289). /Cf. Skt. srak quickly, speedily, instantly. MBE 1969, p. 292, no. 17, for areal etymology for Ka. sarasarane, with reference, to some items in Turner, CDIAL, no. 13257, sarasara- moving hither and thither. (b) Ka. caceara haste, with haste; ceccara quickness, quickly; caccariga, ceccariga a zealously active, restless, quick man. Tu. caccara haste, speed, hurry. Te. ceccera quickly, speedily. (c) Ta. caraff-enal onom. expr. of moving quickly; caraffu-caraff-enal onom. expr. of swishing, rushing sound as of air in motion. Ko, carat pair-word with cadm sound, noise (cadm carat). (d) Ka. jarrane quickly, rushingly. Tu. jarra suddenly, quickly. Je. jarruna quickly. DED (S) 1943. 2353 Ta, carakku goods, articles of mer- chandise, gold, solid worth, ability, curry- stuffs, spices, medicinal substances. Ma. carakku merchandise, cargo, different mov- ables or valuable ar

Ta. carafu twisted thread, cord, twine, a necklet of plaited gold thread, gold, Silver, or cotton thread, nose-ring of bullocks, chain as of mountains, row; stratagem, trick. Ma. carafu cord, string (esp. nuptial), bow- string, measuring line. Ko. card flat neck- band closely fitting. Te. tradu cord, rope, twine, string, thread. Kol, ta+d rope. Nk. tar id. DED 1947.

Nk. jaran, in: tak-jaran father (cf. 3152). Go. (Mu. G.) haral a term affixed to names of relationship when referred to 3rd person, to denote respect; fem, hari (Voc. 3524; cf. 273 Ta. avvai, Go. avhari, etc.). DEDS 361. 2358 Ta, carappani gold neck-chain inlaid with diamonds; carappali id., gold necklet. Ma. sarappali a gold chain of several rows round the neck. Ko, carpeylg woman’s neck- chain. Ka. sarapani, sarapali, sarpali a chain, a kind of collar or necklace. Tu. sarapali, sarapani, sarpali, sarapoli a chain of any metal. Te. (B.) sarapani, sarapini, sarapeni, saraphani an ornament of gold chains of two or more folds. DED 1948.

Ta. aravu, aravam, ara, ara snake. Ma, aravu, aravam serpent. Te. tricu id. Ga, (8.3) tisu krait. Go. (Tr.) tarash, (W.) taras, (Ch.) taranj (pl tarask) (M.) taras, tars, (A. Mu.) taras, (Ma.) tars, (S.) taras(u) snake (Voc. 1665); (LuS.) turashee cobra. Konda saras(u) snake. Pe. rac id. Mand. trehe id. Kui srasu (pl sraska), (Mah.) sracu id. Kuwi (F.) rict (pl. riska) id.; (Su.) racu id., in: nagargcu cobra; (S.) racu snake, snail. Cf. 2360 Ta. cari. / Cf. Pkt. (DNM) sarahaya- snake. DED(S) 1949.

Ta. cari (-v-, -nt-) to slip away, slide down, roll, tumble, stumble down, give way, yield, lean, incline, be aslant, slope; (pp-, -tt-) to cause to slip or roll, topple, pour down, make slant, incline; n, declivity, slope of a mountain; carivu sliding, rolling, slipping down, slope, declivity; carukku (carukki-) to slip; caruyu (caruvi-) to slip away, slide down; n, declivity, steep side of a rock; caruval sloping, slope. Ma. cariyuka to slide, slip, roll down, lean, bend; carikka to bend, make to lean sidewards, lower a vessel, pour; carippikka to cause to lean; cariccal a low shed, side- room; carivu, caru inclination, slope, bending; carkuka to glide, slide; arikka to creep as snakes, worms. Ko. jarv- (jard-) to slip and fall, slide down a slope; (jart-) to cause to slip and fall; jag- Gagy-) (solid thing) budges from place. Ka. sari to move, go, go or move to one side, move out of place, slide, go to the right or left, slip, fall down, run off, run [208] calacala away; put on one side; 71. sliding, etc., flight, state of being aside, precipice, deep ravine; sari steep precipice; jari to slip or fall, slide, collapse, slip away; 4. ravine; jaragu, jarigu, jarugu to slip, slide, roll down, move aside, elapse (time); saraku, saruku to slip, slide, move aside, give place, yield; jaggu to bend down (as a tree with fruit), bend in (as a roof with weight), sink (as a wall); sink, be diminished, grow less (as the water of a river); n, bending. ? Kod. tari- (tariv-, tarifij-) to

Ta. calakai a grain measure = 4 poti or a bullock load = 60 to 80 measures; land sufficient in extent for sowing one calakai of _paddy. Ko. calg a grain measure. Ka. solage, solige, sollage, sollige a measure of capacity = 4 of a kudava or of a balla. Te. salaka a small sack; 2£ times the measure called tumu. DED 1954. 2366 Ta, calacala onom. expr. of purling as of water; cala-cal-enal onom. expr. of rustling as of dried leaves, sounding as of drizzling rain; calacala (-pp-, -tt-) to rustle, be talking incessantly. Ma. calacala the jingle of bells. Tu. calacala a noise in wading through water, as children make while playing. Te. calacala the rippling of a current or streamlet; the noise of falling leaves, etc. / MBE 1969, ERAS Toner merge On MRRP OESER RRA ost !— RSE neem cee UREN ARO Tee calame p. 292, no. 18, for areal etymology, including also 2405 Ta. cala-cal-enal, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 5002, *chala-, e.g. H. chalchalana to move with a rustling sound, make a rippling sound, etc. DED 1955.
Ta. calacala. DED 1987.

Ka. calame, calime, calume, calme, cilume an orifice, a bore, small pit, hole dug in the dry bed of a river ora dried-up tank, Spring of water or a fountain head. Tu, cilimbi, cilimé, cilmé a small tank. Te. celama hole or pit dug for water in the dry bed of a Tiver or rivulet, etc. Kuwi (S) salma well, ? Br. kal place where water collects, water- hold (or < H. khal creek, inlet, canal, river, trench; cf. MBE 1961b, pp. 377-8). Cf.
Ta. calavai bleaching or washing of cloth, washed cloth. Ka. salave washing and Dleaching bee, cotton cloth. Te. caluva washing, bleaching, a washed bl cloth. DED 1957. . or Bleached
Ta. calapam, cilapam pearl-fishery. Ma, salapam, silapam id. Te. salapamu id. DED 1958.

Ta. cali (pp-, -tt-), callu (calli-) to sift; callafai sieve. Ma. calikka to sift; callata a large sieve. Ka, jallisu to sift; jalladi, jallade, jalade, jarade sieve. Te. jallincu to sift; jalleda ‘sieve. Go. (S,.) jalor(a), (Ko.) jator, (G.) saliri, (Mu.) colir, sdlit, (Ma.) joli sieve (Voc. 1370, 1404). Pe. hanel id. Kui sdleri id. Kuwi (ist.) hani ki- to sift. / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, nos. 4771, 4772. DED(S) 1959.
Ta. cali (pp-, -tt-) to be weary, proome exhausted; calippu weariness, languor. a. ©. a to become tired; calippu fatigue. DED 1960. pp ‘ene
Te. cali cima a sort of black ant. Kol, salli sima white ant. DEDS 366.

Ka. jalugu place where water drops or oozes. Pa, jalug, jatub place where water oozes. Kuwi (S) jalla spring. Kur. jalka’ana (of the rains) to percolate and soak the ground thoroughly, so that in low-lying spots water oozes out or opens new springs; refl.- ree jalkarna. Cf. 2367 Ka. calame, DED(S) 2374 Ta, alai (-pp-, -tt-) to beat, slap. Go. (Mu.) hal-, (L.) halasna to beat; (Mu.) halhi- halha a to exchange blows (Voc. 3531); (LuS.) halsna to ‘beat. DEDS 367, 2375 Kur. calki a special kind of grass from which brooms are made;-a_ broom. att celaki a broom made of grass. DEDS

Tu. selé chink, crack, flaw as in a stone. Te. selagu, selayu, (K. also) sel4gu, celagu, celavu to cut; sela hole. Kur. calxnz to open, uncover; calxrna to open (intr.). Malt. calge to split or break open; calgro [209] cavati torn asunder. Br. caling, calénging to become cracked, split. DED 1962.

Ka. calla, cella great mirth, fun, jest, smiling, laughter; callavadu to frolic, sport, gambol; callata, cell@ta sport, fun. Te. cella- famu sport, play, toying; celladu to Sport, play, frolic; ceral-adu to sport, play, roam about for pleasure; ceral-dtamu sport, play, roaming for pleasure. DED 63.

Ta. callatam short drawers. Ma. callatam id. Ko. calalm, calm, (Kurgoej and Meena-r dialects) colalm, co-lm peri- neal cloth. Ka. caddi, canaka, canna, cenna breeches which reach only to the middle of the thighs; callana, cal(a)na, collana, colina drawers or breeches of different length. Tu. callana, callana short breeches reachin; only to the middle of the thighs. Te. callidamu, ceddi short trousers reaching only to the knee or the middle of the thigh. / Cf. Skt. canda- taka-, calanaka- a short petticoat, Pkt. (DNM) candataka- id., Mar. caddi short underwear, sone short breeches and drawers. DED(S)
Ma. callam boatman’s pole. Ka. jall id. Tu. jalla id. DED 1965. P “

Ta. calli small pieces of stone or glass, potsherd, small chips as of stone, rubble, small flat shells used for lime, small copper coin. Ma. calli chip, potsherds, copper cash. Ka, jalli broken stone, metal. Tu. calli chip, potsherd; jalli broken stones. Te. jalli road metal, broken stone. Pa, jalub small stone chips. DED 1966.
Ma. calli a kind of grass or reed. Tu. calli a reed, a kind of grass. DED 1967.

Ka. jalle, jarave a bamboo pole; a iene Kod. jalle cane of sugar. DED 2384 Ta, callu (calli-) to sprinkle water. Ka. cailu, cel, cellu, celahku to scatter, pour out, shed, spill; callisu, cellisu to cause to scatter, etc.; calaku, calafku, calangu to let go from the hand, discharge, throw away; Jellane with a violent gush (of water): cale, caleya, calaka, calaya, caliya sprinkling. Kod. cell- (celli-) to throw away diquid), scatter (grain). Tu. calluni to spill, shed Gntr.); celluni to sprinkle, spill, shed; spill (intr.), drop; cellata scattering, spilling. Te. callu to sprinkle, scatter, strew, spread, cast or throw loosely, sow; callakamu sprinkling, watering, irrigation; jallu rain, a shower, spray of rainwater, etc.; v.i = callu. Nk. sall- to sprinkle. Pa, cal- (rain) falls in a shower. Ga. (S.) jall- to sow seed, scatter. Go. (S.) jal- to strain water from boiled tice; (Ko.) jall- to swill with water (Voc. 1403); (Koya Su.) all- to sprinkle (water). Kuwi (S.) jallinai to scatter; (Isr.) jal- (-it-) to throw out liquids. DED(S, N) 1969.

Ta. cavati ornament for the neck consisting of three or more gold cords, ear cavatu ornament worn by women; cavali a kind of necklace for women. Ma. cavafi a peculiar neck ornament, a kind of ear-rings. DED(S) 1971.

Ta. cavatu. /Cf. Nahali copo salt. DED(S) 2201. 2675 Ta, cuvavu musk-shrew. Kol. supke young rat. Pa. cumari, cumbri musk-rat. Go. (Tr.) stipe house-mouse; (SR.) supe small mouse; huppe (M.) rat, mouse, (D.) rat, (Mu.) field-rat; (Ko.) uppe rat (Voc. 3440). Pe. cupel musk-rat. Kui supa, supenji, superi shrew-mouse, musk-rat. Kuwi (S.) suppesi musk-mice; (Mah.) supri musk-rat. DED(S) 2202. 2676 Kur. cubb® peacock. Malt. cuwe id. DED 2203.

Ta. cavattu (cavaffi-) to destroy, ruin (as a town), kill, beat, tread upon, trample; (NTD) camuff- to tread on. Ma. caviftuka to kick, tread; caviftikka to cause to tread on; cavitfu, cavafi a kick. Kod. cavt- (cavti-) to step on; cavfi footprint. Cf. 2695 Ta. cuvatu. DED 1973.

Ka. savatu, savufu, sautu, sd{u ladle, spoon. Ji. saunfu, saufu id. ? Go. (SR. Ch. Ph.) sukkur, (Tr. Ch.) sukkur, (W.) sukur, (Mu.) hukkur, (M.) hukur, (Ma.) ukkuri id. (Voc. 3433; or with 2728 Tu. suri). DEDS(N) 370.

Ka. savaru to rub in or apply to (as water, oil, medicine, polish or ashes), convey with a stick, knife, etc., any sticky substance to a vessel by rubbing the substance off on its brim: savarisu to have applied to, etc. Te. camuru oil, any oily or unctuous substance; vb. (also carumu) to smear, daub, rub as with an oil, etc., apply; (B.) javaru to apply, put on (medicine, etc.); {Inscr.) samaru ghee. DED 1974.

Ko. cavr- (cavry-}) to cut down a whole collection of standing plants, bushes, etc. Ka. savaru to cut off the side branches of a branch of a tree, the branches of a bush or bamboo, or the bark of a tree; diminish prominence of the soil by cutting away a little at a time, chip, cut, cut down, caus. savarisu. Tu. tavuruni, tauruni to strip off as the leaves of a tree, cut away any leaves or brushwood, clear a bush; (B-K.) taberu, saberu to chop off, trim. DED 1975.

Ta. aval rice obtained from fried paddy by pestling it; avai (-pp-, -tt-) to pound in a mortar, crush, cuff, prod; avaiyal well- husked rice. Ma. avil rice bruised and dried, avekka to beat rice; aval flattened rice ob- tained from paddy by pestling it. Ko. kac av- (avt-) to pestle (millet) second time; aky av- (avt-) to pestle (millet) third time. To. af- (aft-) to pound with light strokes; ofil puffed tice. Ka. aval pound, beat; 7. pounding, beating in a mortar; (also aval-akki) rice bruised and crushed. Kod. av!-akki rice fried and each grain pounded flat. Tv. abepuni, abeyuni, abeccuni to beat or pound rice. Kol, (Kin.) cavli mortar. Nk. savli id. Nk, {210} cavi (Ch.) savii id. Pa, cavil id.; cavkol (pl. cav- kocil) pestle. Ga. (OIL) savul mortar; savkol pestle; (S.) savvul mortar; savkol pestle. 9Go. (W. Ph.) sahki, (Tr.) sahki, (Ch.) sahki, (A. Y.) cahki, (G. Mu.) hahki, (Ma.) ahki, a?ki, (Ko.) ahk id. (Voc. 3363; or with 2799 Konda sonki, Pc. henki). /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 4749, *camala-, *cavala- husked tice (Burrow 1967, p. 41). DED(S, N) 1976.

Ta. cavalai leanness of an infant not fed on mother’s milk, tenderness, immaturity; cavanku (cavaiki-) to become lean, emaciated, shrink, subside, faint, languish; cavu (pp-, -tt-) to become weak, be emaciated; cavi withered crop, blighted or empty grain; cavattai withered grain, chaff, emaciated person, dried betel leaves. Ma, cavala empty corn, hollowness, leanness; cayi, caxi empty grain, blighted corn; cankerworm, palmer- worm; cata seedless, empty as husk. Ko. jag- (jagy-) to become Ican; jalv- (jald-) to become lean and stringy with old age (jalv- < *javl-). Tu. (B-K.) cavuli old. Te. (B.) cavile leanness, thinness; cavile-poyina lean, thin as an infant, sivi a blasted stalk of withered corn. DEDS 1977.
Ta. caval (cavaly-, cavant-) to bend (intr.), be supple (as the arms of a fencer); cavattu (cavatti-) to bend (¢r.), twist. Ma, cavaluka to be flexible. DED 1978.
Ta. cavali cloth, piece-goods. Ma. cavali any cloth. Ka. javali cloth of any kind. Tu. javali, jauli cloth. Te. javali cloths, drapery. DED 1879.

Ta. cavaru rubbish, sweepings. Ma. cavaru, cavar green leaves and rubbish used © as manute, sweepings; cavarr-ila dried leaves. To. tofir afterbirth (of goddess or buffalo) (or with 2400 Ta. cavvu). DED 1980. 2396 (a) Ta. cavi taste. Ko. cayv taste left in mouth for food just eaten. Ka. savi, samvi that has taste, that is palatable, savoury, sweet or nice; taste, sweetness; savi, save to test by eating, taste, eat; savigara a man who creates pleasant feelings, a jocose or witty man, etc.; savisu to cause to taste or eat; saviha taste, flavour. Tu. sabi, savi flavour, taste; palatable, sweet; sabipuni to taste, try the flavour; savipuni to be tasteful, savoury, sweet; tabi flavour, taste, savoury, delicious; tabipuni to appease (as the appetite); tabafuni to remain as an aftertaste (cf. 2386). Te. cavi taste; cavi-gonu to taste. Kol. savvi sweet. Nk. savad id. (b) Ta. cuvatu sweetness, taste; cuvafan person of refined taste; cuvai Cpp-, -tt-) to taste, eat, chew, kiss, experience, enjoy; be palatable, agreeable, pleasing; n. taste, flavour, deliciousness, sweetness, that which is pleas- ing or gratifying to the senses, the sense of taste. Ma. cuva taste, flavour, unpleasant taste (as in fever), slight after-effects of former troubles; cuvekka to taste, produce a taste, remain as after-taste. DED(S) 1981. Ka. javi, jave, jame the hair of a horse’s tail. Te. (B.) javvi id. DED 1982. 2398 Ka, javugu, jogu, javalu, javulu swampy ground. Te, javuku, jauku to be shaky, unsteady, loose, (K. also) (wall) becomes weak before falling, lose courage, be marshy; javajava onom. denoting trembling, (K. also) softness, weakness; joku to tremble, totter, stagger. DED 1983.
Ka. ‘savute, saute, savate, savati, savunte a kind of cucumber, Cucumis utilissi- mus Roxb. Kod. cavte cucumber. Tu. sauté, savuté, tauté, tavuté id. Kor. (M.) cavu, (T.) tavnte id. DED 1984.

Ta. cavvu membrane as of the dia- phragm or the eye, thin scales on a healed wound, proud flesh in ulcers, scirrhus forma- tion in cancer, envelope round the pulp of fruit, of a bulbous root, pellicle. Ma. cavvu the omentum. To. tofir afterbirth (of goddess eRe alo) (or with 2395 Ta. cavaru). DED 2401 Ta, agunku, aluaku, nalufku pangolin, Indian scaly ant-eater. Ma. alunku, allan pangolin; alluvan armadillo. Kod. cellavé scaly anteater. Tu. alafiku ant-eater; alahku armadillo. Te. aluga, atuva, (B) atuga, aluva, alava an animal resembling an armadillo. Vk. (Ch.) sarei scaly ant-eater. Go. (Mu. Ma.) jarum scaly pangolin (Voc. 1400). Kui jalenji armadillo. DED(S) 243. 2402 (a) Ga. (P.S.2) sarit (pl. sakkil) bullock. Konda ranu (pl. raku) ox; (Pulgura dial.) sara bull. Kuwi srahnu kodi bullock (kodi cow, ox; see 2199). (b) Nk. (Ch.) sanap cowdung. Pa, carpi (prob. rather carpi) id. Ga. (OlL.) sarpi, (S) sadpi id. Go. (Tr.) sarapi, (W.) sarapt, (SR.) sadapi, (G.) sarap, harap, (Mu.M.) harap, (Ma.) arapi, (S.) harpi, arpi, (Ko.} arap id. (Voc. 3352). Konda rapi id. Pe. rapi id. Mand. yapi id. Kuwi (Su. P.) yapi, (F.) rapi id. Cf. 4210 Ta. pi. DED(S) 1986.

Go. (Tr.) jarhuttana to shake violently (tr.) (Voc. 1402). Konda sar- (-t-) to remove dust by shaking, shake off a hand; (BB) (hen) to flap wings. Kui sarpa (sart-) to flick off, dust off, shake out, flap wings; jarpa (jart-) to shake, shake dust off, flap, flick, beat the wings. Kuwi (Su.) han- (h-) (hen) to flap. wings; (S.) hanpinai to thresh (with hand). Cf. 2298 Ka. jadi. / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 5328, *jhatayati; there has been borrowing in one direction or the other, as Kol. zadp- to flick away (< Mar.). DEDS 372.

Ta. cala-cal-enal onom. expr. of (a babbling, (b) splashing, pattering, bs of han calappu (calappi-) to babble, prate; calacala (-pp-, -tt-) id.; patter (as rain). Ka. calamala, calapala noise of bubbling water. Tu. calacala a bubbling noise. / For areal etymology, see
Ta. cali (-pp-, -tt-) to become stale and sour, grow mouldy as food, as liquors in incipient fermentation, rot. Ma, calikka to grow mouldy, rotten. Ka. caliya state of growing putrid. DED 1988.

Ta. calai (-pp-, -tt-} to grow tired, become weary (Annamalai, p.875). Ma. calikka, calufiiuka to shake, be frightened. Ka. cali to fear, succumb, be deprived of one’s power or ~high spirit, become tired. Tu. calipuni to shake. Te. jaluka trembling, tong. fear. Cf. 2297 Ka. jadi. DED(N) 2408 Ta, cali cold, chilliness; ali, tali cool- ness. Ka. cali, cali, sali coldness, cold, cool- ness, chili, frost, snow, etc. Tu. cali, cali, sali cold, chilliness, shivering as in ague; cold, chilly; cavuli, cavuli cold. Kor. (O.) talli id. Te. cali cold, low temperature, sensation of cold, chilliness; calimiri, caluva, calfana, calladamu coolness, cold; calfa cool, cold; calliga in a cool or cold manner, calmly, gently; calidi rice cooked in the evening and kept for use in the following morning, stale food, breakfast. Pa. (S.) talla coolness. Ga. (P.) calandi cold rice serving for breakfast; saldi early morning. Konda salan cold, cool; salva breakfast; saldi id., (B.) early morning. Kuwi (Isr.) salma cool; saldi mid-morning. Cf. 3045 Ta. tan. DED(S) 1990.
Ta. cali catarrh, mucus blown out of the nose, phlegm; (-pp-, -tt-) to catch cold; calippu catarrh. Ma. cali mucus; semen. Te. jalubu a cold, catarrh, influenza. Ga. (S.?) jalbu id. DED 1991.

Ma. aliyan brother-in-law, wife’s brother or sister’s husband. Ko. ayl brother- in-law, male cross-cousin (male speaking); ayl baevn brothers-in-law, male cross-cousins (collectively); mal ayl son-in-law who lives in father-in-law’s house. Ka. aliya son-in-law; aliyatana state of being a son-inlaw. 7u. aliya son-in-law, nephew. Te. alltdu, alluv3du son-in-law; alliyamu feast or entertainment given to son-in-law by father-in-law immedi- ately after the marriage. Kol. saenzin (pl sa-Inikul) younger sister’s husband; sanma mother’s younger sister; (SR.) sanma id., sister’s daughter. Nk, sanikul, sanjin son-in- law, younger sister’s husband. Nk, (Ch.} sanjil bridegroom. Pa. caliiid (pl -kul) bride- groom. Ga, (OL) salfiid (pl -kil) son-in-law, younger sister’s husband. Go. (Tr.) sanné son-in-law; sanne-midr sister’s daughter; sanné- marri sister’s son; stlar-sann@ younger sister’s alai (212] ca husband; (SR.) sanai son-in-law; sane younger sister’s husband; (Y.) sare, sade, (Mu.) hare, (Ma.) anne, ame daughter’s husband (Voc. 3327); (ASu.) saré, (LuS.) hunné son-in-law. Konda sanin, (with reference to 3rd person) sanisi id. Kuwi (P.) hone, (F.) honesi id. Br. stlum_ son-intaw; brother-in-law (or from IA; Turner, CDIAL, no. 13871, syala-). DED (S) 1970, and from DED(S) 256. 2411 Ta, alai curds, curdled milk, butter- milk, butter; alli butter. Ka. ale buttermilk. Tu, alé, alé id. Te. calla curds or curdled milk, buttermilk. Ta. koppu women’s ear-ornament wor at top of helix. Ma. koppu upper ear- ting of women. Ka, koppu ornament for the upper part of the ear, worn by women. Kod. koppumbi long straight ornament worn through top of ear. Tu. koppu a female’s upper ear-ring. Te. kuppe an ornament worn by women on the plaited hair. DED 1756.

Ta. callal mud, stush; catacala Cpp-, -tt-) to be wet, muddy, sloppy; allal, alakkar, alaru mud, mire. Ma. calla bog, sludge; cali, celi, cella mud, mire; calumpu bog, slush. Ka. caliya state of growing muddy; alla-nela bog, morass. DED(S) 1993.
Ta. callu (calli-) to slacken, abate. Ma. calijuka to slacken. DED 1994,

Ta. callu, callai trouble, annoyance; callu-ppullu quarrel, trouble, worry. Ma, callu bad, rude; callakkan a very passionate man; callattam meanness. Ko, cal act of troubling or causing trouble. To. sol hin- drance, inconvenience. DED 1995.

Ta. callu-kkay tender and undevel- oped green fruit. Ma. callu unsubstantial, unripe. Ka, jallu unsubstantiality, emptiness, hollowness, worthlessness, defect; jalluga a weak, feeble man; jalu worthlessness, empti- ness, hollowness; ? jaradu unsubstantiality, uselessness; (Coorg) salke lean. Tu. cailu, (B-K.) callu stupid, dull, timorous, imbecile, weak, useless; callapini to get weak, become useless; calle a weak or timid man. Kui sadu (pl. satka) an empty ear of corn; an unformed grain, husk with no grain inside. DED(S) 1996.

Go. (Tr.) sarri road, walk; (AY) sari, (G.) harri, (Mu.) harr, (Ma.) ay(i), (M.) har, (S.) hari, (Ko.) ar road (Voce, 3339). Konda sari (pl. saRku) way, path. Pe. hazi id. ? Kuwt (S. Su.) jiyu, (P. F.) jiru id, road. ?Tu, sadi id. ?Ka, (Tipt.) adi track, way (< Tu.). Cf. 405 Ta. aru. DED(S) 1953.
Ka. sari paste, gum, glue. Te, (B.) eari, sari starch; paste or gruel used as paste. ? Kui sadali gum, resin. DEDS 374.
Te. aru, arru neck. Pa, car (obl. cari-, pl cartil) id. ? Ta, eruttu id.; eruttam id., nape of neck. ? Ma. erattu back of the neck; eruttam neck, back of the neck. DED 1997.

Te. caracu to slap or strike with the open hand or with something broad, clap, pat; carapu a slap or clap with the open hand. Go. (Mu.) jah- to beat, fight, strike with hammer (Voc, 1409). Konda saR- (t-) to slap with hand, clap hands, thrash (as clothes against a rock in washing). Pe. hah- (hast-) to beat. Kui sahpa (saht-) to beat, thrash, belabour, slap; n. beating, thrashing. Kuwi (Su.) hah- (hast-) to thresh with flail; (F.) hassali (hast-), (S.) hah’nai id. Cf. 320 Ta. arai. DEDS 376.

Ka. jare, jari, jere, jeri to rebuke, abuse, jeer at; m. an imprecation or term of abuse; jaraha rebuke, censure; jarvu, jabbu, jabarisu, jabbarisu to chide, scold, abuse, rebuke. Tu. jaripuni, jarepuni, jarpuni to insult, jeer, despise. DED 1998.

Ka. cannu, cennu straightness, beauty, grace, niceness, properness, elegance; canna, cenna a man of beauty, a handsome man; fem. canni, canne, cenne; _cannage hand- somely, nicely, properly; canniga a handsome, fine man. Tu. canna handsome, well. Te. cennu beauty, grace, elegance, manner, way. Cf. 328 Ko. anv and 2328 Ta. cantam. DED 1999(a). 2424 Kur. canxna to turn stale (of cooked things, meat or vegetables), turn mouldy (as bread). Malt. cange to be or become rotten (of cooked food); cangro rotten. Cf. 2341 Ta. avi. DED 2000. °
Ta. cannai timber used as a lever to help the movement of temple car. Ka, sanne, sonne a lever. Tu. sanne-kolu id. DED 2001.

Ta. ca (cav-/cakuv-, cett-) to die, be spoiled or blighted (as crops), be ex- hausted; cavu death, ghost; cakkatu death; cettal dying; ?ceku (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy, kill. Tu, saipini, taipini to die, be starved; sailé ghost; sdvu, tavu death, mourning feast. Te. caccu (ca-, cav-) to die, fade, wither, cease, disappear; adj. dead, vigourless, in- sipid; caivu death; campu to kill, put to death; campudu killing.
