Pe. ha- (-t-) to die, (fire) to go out; hatpa- to extinguish (fire). Mand. ha- to die, (fire) to go out; hat. to extinguish (fire). Kui sava (sat-) to die, be ill, suffer; 7. death, illness, suffering; sappa (sapt-) to kill, ex- tinguish; n. act of killing, extinguishing. Kuwi (F.) haiali (hat-), (S.) hanai, (Su.) ha- (-t-) to dic; (Isr.) haki death. Kur. kh@ ena (keceas) to dic, fall out of use {as a law); (Hahn) khiv, (Grign

Ko. cask (caryk-) to make to grow, fear, support; ?ca-kn tameness (but not really tractable or domesticated). To. so-k (so-ky)- to rear (in songs). Ka. saku to bring up, foster, rear, nurse, keep, protect, preserve; n, bringing up, fostering, etc.; sakisu to cause to bring up, etc. Kod. ca-k- (ca-ki-) to rear (child, young animal). Tu. satikuni to foster, nourish, nurse, bring up, take care of, protect, shelter; caus. sankavuni; sahkané fostering, nursing, nourishing, bringing up, adoption. Te. siku to rear, bring up, foster, educate, train up; s&kudu rearing, bringing up. Cf.

Ta. cay (v-, -nt-) to march in crowds, happen, succeed. Ko. Ca-g- (ca+yg-) (man) becomes old, (span of time) is past, (disease) is cured, (possessions) are moved from one house to another; ca+ge- (ca-ge-) to live, shift (possessions) from one house to another. Ka, sagu to go, move forward, proceed, advance, continue in time, last, come upon with grievous effect (as trouble), be brought about, achieved, or accomplished; sagu, saga advance, improvement, cultivation, tillage; sagavali, sdguvali cultivation; Sagisu to cause to go or go on, Carry on, cause to proceed or advance, perform or do, conduct, carry on, manage, convey, sustain, maintain, foster, nourish, cultivate. Tu, Saguni to proceed, Progress (as work), be dispatched (as goods), move forward, advance; sagu prosecution, progress, as of work; sagavuni to expedite (as work), send, dispatch (as goods), do a thing perfunctorily; saguvali, sagoli, sdgvali farming, tillage, cultivation of land. Te. cagu, sagu to go on, proceed, get on, advance, continue, last, be successful, be effective, take effect; n. cultivation, tillage; sagincu to conduct, carry on, proceed with, continue; sagubadi cultivation, tillage. Kuwi (S.) saginai to manage; (Ist.) sag- Cit-) to grow, spread out, file out, proceed. DED (N) 2006. 2431 Kur. cixna to Sow, scatter seed. Malt, cage to divide, scatter, sow. DEDS 377.

Ta. cakkaiyan member of a caste whose profession in ancient times was to sing and dance in temples and palaces; cakkai- kkOttu dance performed by Cakkiyan. Ma. cakki, pl. hon. cakkiyar, cakyar a caste of half-brahmans who sing and dance before gods and brahmans. DEDS 378.

Ma. cimpu length, stretch. Kz. cacu to stretch out, hold out, extend, stretch forth, put out as the hand, etc.; capu stretch, length, extent; jambu length; jagisu to stretch or raise (the body to its full extent); japu the measure of a long stride; sagu to lengthen, extend; dapu stretch, etc.; the measure of a stride. Kod. ca+c- (ca+ci-) to stretch out (hand), straighten; Ca+fijé (past verbal adj. of *casy-, cariij- to be straight) straight. Tw. cacuni to stretch out, hold out. Te. cagu to be stretched or extended; extend, lengthen; fall prostrate; cAgilu to prostrate oneself, lie down, recline; cagilinta prostrating oneself before an idol; c&cu to extend (tr.), stretch, lengthen; capu id.; n. extending, stretch, extent, length, a full piece of cloth; jampu long; delay, procrastination; jagu delay; sigu to extend, lengthen, expand, spread out, prostrate oneself; sigbadu, sagilabadu to fall down at full length. Kol (Pat., p. 131) Sageng to stretch (intr). Go. (Tr.) sthatang (sahc-) to stretch out (one’s hands or feet as women do when fighting); (Tr. SR.) sangana, (Ch.) sang- to spread as a creeper; (Ph.) sahcina, sahtand to make to grow oT spread: (SR.) cihane to stretch; (Mu.) hah- to spread, extend; (Ma.) an- to stretch, be elongated; caus. 47-; (Ko.) ang- to be stretched out; tr. ah- (Voc. 3365). Konda sag- (-it-) (body or limbs) to be stretched ‘out to full length; sak- to stretch out (fingers, limbs); sap- to stretch out (tongue, hands, legs). Pe. jac- (jacc-) to stretch

Ta. caici mother’s milk (nursery). Ka. caci, taci a childish word for the female breast. Te. caci woman’s breast (B. a child’s word). DED 2009. 2437 Ka, jaju, jadu colour of red ochre, red colour. Je. jaju, jadu redness. (R. Rama- chandra Rao, Descriptive Grammar of Pampa Bharata, p. 356: derived from Skt. dhatu-.) DED 2010.
Ka. sati likeness, similarity; like, equal. Te. safi, satika equal, like, similar; equality, likeness, similarity, an equal, a parallel. DED 2011.
Ma. catuka, cintuka to throw; caftuka to throw darts, hurl; caftu a hurl ? Ka. jadisu to throw. Tu. canduni to fling a spear. DED 2012.

Ta. (NTD) cattam jumping. Ma. catuka to leap; catikka to make to leap; cattam a leap; cattu jumping. Aur. cataxna (catxyas) to stride, stride across, jump over, Malt. cagte to jump over. DED(S) 2013. 2441 Ka, catu a refuge, shelter, anything that screens from rain, wind or heat of the sun. Te, cafu concealment, cover,, shelter, screen; cafu-padu to be concealed or hidden. Konda satu hiding, secret place. Kuwi (Isr.) saf- Cit-) to block out light, cover up. DED (S) 2014. 2442 Ta, c@tai appearance, feature, simi- larity, inclination, tendency, temperament, hint, significant gesture, trifle, slightness. Ma, cata faint notice of something, a trace. Ko, jar sign (made by hands, eyes, etc.). Ka, jadu, jade mark of a footstep or of a carriage wheel, track, trace, a slight or faint notice, wink, hint. Te. jada a sign, trace, track: hint, intimation, way, manner. DED 2015.
Ta. catti, cXttai whip, string to spin top. Ma. catta whipcord, whip. Ko. ja-t string fo spin top. Ka. cati whip. Te. jafi, (VPR) cati id. Kol. sa-¢ id. DED(S) 2016,

Ta. can span (measurement). Ma. can. Ko, gan. To. kien, Ka, gén, géna, genu. Kod. ca+ni. Tu. genu, géna. Te. jéna, Kol. ze-na. DED 2017. 2445 Ka, cana, cana, cna a small chisel. Tu. c®nu, cénu awl, chisel. Te. snamu id. DED 20f8.
Ta. canin member of the Shanar caste whose occupation is toddy drawing; canrar Shanars. Ma. cinan, cannan a caste of palmyra cultivators in Travancore. DED 2019
Ya. cintu menstrual discharge. Ma. cantu semen. Je. sidu id. Konda sand(u) the fluid ejection from the female or the male genital organ. DED (S) 2020.

Ta. cittu (catti-) to daub, smear, anoint; cantam sandal; cintu sandal tree, sandal paste, black pigment made of burnt rice or ragi used as tilaka, sacred ashes, paste, mortar, plaster; cantu. sandalwood; catur Cpp-, -tt-) to daub, besmear. Ma. cantu compound ointment of sandal, camphor, musk and saffron; cement; tar. Ka, sadu, sindu a fragrant substance, perfume, the black colour made of burnt rice or ragi with which females and children mark their fore- heads. Kod. ca-ndi sandalwood. Tu. sadiké smearing. Te. cadu to rub into a paste, (K. also) sharpen blade on stone; 7, a black spot on the forehead, beauty spot, a black prep- aration of burnt rice, etc., used for beauty spot on the forehead; (Inscr.) catu-padi sandal paste. /Cf. Skt. candana- sandal (tree, wood, paste) (whence borrowed forms, e.g. Te. candanamu, To. todonm), cf. (Kaut.) satana- a kind of sandal. DED 2021.

Ta. cattu); carttikka to adorn as an image with flowers. Ko. cary near; ar ca+ry left side; val caery, val ka casry right side; 0 ca*ry one side; val casrym all around. To. So-ry near (in songs; < Badaga). Ka. sar to come or go near or to, approach, be or become near, join, associate oneself to, come to hand, be obtained, come about, come or go, be applied or used; saran nearness, proximity; sare being near, nearness, prox- imity, union; sarke approach, nearness, proximity; sarcu to make oneself come or go near or to, go near, approach; make go or come near or to, apply to, put to, on, or in, put; ar to be united. DED(N) 030.
Ta. cattu (catti-) to beat, thrash; n. beating, thrashing. Ko. ca-t- (ca+ty-) to give a blow to, beat. DED 2023.

Ka. jani-gida a small tree, Grewia abutilifolia; (Lush.) jana G. asiatica; tada- © jana G. tiliaefolia; kari-jina G. orbiculata. Te. (SAN) jana a kind of tree; (Lush.) jana G. orbiculata; nalla-jana, pedda-jana_ G. asiatica. DEDS 380,

Ta. caippai mat made of grass or rushes. Ma. cappa hut, shed of leaves. To. so-py sleeping mat (< Badaga). Ka. capa, cape, sape mat. Te. cipa id. Nk. capa id. Pa. cap id. Go. (Y.) capre, (G.) capi, (Mu.) cap, (Ma.) sapi, (Ko.) capa id. (Voc. 1299). Konda sapa id. Kuwi (Su.) sipe, (S.) cape id. DED(S) 2024.

Ta. campu (campi-) to wither, droop, perish, pine away, close up as flowers, decline, shrink, lose consciousness, grow dim as the eyes; campal, cAimpar ashes, withered flower, old age; cay (-v-, -nt-) to grow thin; cayvu . deficiency. Ma. campuka to contract itself, shut, blink as eyes; cimpal contracting, closing; ashes. Tu. cambuni to close, shut, shrivel as a flower. DED 2025, and from DED 307.
Ta. cimpu (campi-) to pull in by jerks, haul, draw in, pump. Ju, cambuni to lift with a lever; cHpané a lever. DEDS 381.
Ta. cay sedge; ? Tu. c& Andropogon schoenanthus, DEDS 382. .

Ta. cay (-v-, -nt-) to incline, hang down, decline as a heavenly body, bend, turn down as the ear, recline, lie down, give cay [215] * saru way, break, flee, be partial, biased, deviate, lean; (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to incline, bend or stoop, turn in a new direction, drive, steer, prejudice, spoil, defeat; cayvu slope, declivity, etc.; c#yppu slope, slant, side or declivity of a mountain, sloping roof, etc.; cayal inclining, etc. Ma, cayka to incline, lean to one side, rest or lie on, settle down, disappear; cayal, caccal bending sideways as a falling tree, side, inclination; caykka to lean against, bend, put one on the other, lower, level the ground by beating, lie down to sleep; cdyvu, cayippu inclination; ayka to bend as for an exertion; aficuka to bend forward, incline, bow. Kod. taye- (-p-, -t-) to level field and break up clods with a plank drawn by bullocks; x. plank thus used (cf. Ma. caykka). Tu. cacuni to bow. Te, caya side, direction, quarter, vicinity, neighbourhood. Mand. he-, j& to descend; caus, htpa-, jtpa-; (for Mand. and its relationship with Kui in this entry, see Burrow 1976, pp.40~1). Kui japa (jat-) to descend; n. descent; jappa (japt-) to cause to descend. DED (S) 2026.

Ta. cay brilliance, light, beauty, colour, fame, reputation; cayam colour, tinge, tint, dye, true colour, real nature; cayal beauty, gracefulness, colour, likeness, resemblance, reflected image, shadow; ay (-v-, -nt-) to be or become beautiful; 7. beauty; ai beauty; aitu that which is beauti- ful, beauty; aiya beautiful. Ma. cay beauty; cayam colour, dye, paint; cayal shape, figure, resemblance, beauty, colour. Ko. ca:yv colour. Kod. casy beauty; ca-yli well @dyv.); caryka+ré handsome man; fem. ca-ykarati. ? Kui sanja (sanji-) to be proper, good, right, beautiful, bright; #. goodness, beauty; adv. Seautitully; pl. action saska (saski-). DED (S) 2458 Kol. say- (sayt-) to leave, let go, release; sasi- (sasit-) to leave (say- + sie- to give); (SR.) sdy- to leave. Nk. s@y- leave, abandon. Nk, (Ch.) say- to leave. Pa. cay- id.; (S.) caypip- (caypit-) to cause to leave, wean. Ga. (Oll.) say- to leave, abandon; (S.) say key- to leave, let loose; (S.3) say- to leave, heal (@ntr.); sayupp- to heal a wound. ? To. toy- (toc-) to give the slip to. Cf. 2351 Ka. say. DED(S, N) 2028.
Ka. jayila dog. Tu. javala-nayi wolf- dog. Te. jagiamu hound, hunting dog. DED 2029.

Ta. car (-v-, -nt-) to reach, approach, depend upon, take shelter in, be near to, be associated or connected with, unite, be related to, resemble, lean on, recline against; n. joining, uniting, place, situation, side; carpu place, side, help, support, refuge, shelter, attachment, birth, bias, partiality, friendship, approximation, nearness; carvu place, residence, pial, refuge, basis, help, sup- port, means, attachment, vicinity, partiality; carntor relatives, friends; carttu (cartti-) to cause to lean, support, join, unite, connect; carcci leaning, uniting, connexion, approach, support; carppu sloping roof; caral drawing near, side, slope of mountain; cari side, wing, tow, series; cattu (catti-) to close as a door (= carttu); ar (Cv-, -nt-) to combine with, belong to. Ma. caruka to lean against, rely upon, be attached to, be shut, place against, put on; cara bending sideways, nigh, close; caral leaning against, inclination, side, declivity of hill, support; carikka to lay against in order to support, shut the door; carnnavar kinsmen; carcea relation by blood; carttu joining, assemblage; carttuka to join as wood, put on dress, adorn, throw on (see
Ta. caranai purslane-leaved trianth- ema, Trianthema decandra. Ma. carana T. popogyna: (B.) Boerhavia diffusa. DED 031.

Ta. caram scaffolding, sticks tied to the smaller branches of a flower-tree as a scaffold for picking flowers. Ko. ca+mm (obl. ca+rt-) beams on which floor of attic is laid. Ka, sira, sirave scaffolding. Te. siruva scaffolding, a small bank, a bridge. Pe. hara bridge. Kuwi (S.) sara id. DED 2032.
Ta. cari time, turn. Ma. sari id. Ka. sari, sare id. Tu. sari, sarti id. Te. sari id., repetition, occasion. DED 2033.
Te. sarincu to extend, spread. Konda (BB) sar- Cit-) to spread (e.g. sore). DEDS 384.

Ka. jarige-huli-mara Mysore gamboge tree, Garcinia morella Desr. Tu. jarige G, pictoria. [G. morella Desrouss. = G. pictoria Roxb.] DED 2034. "2468 Kol. sa-r (pl. sa-dl), (Kin.) car (pl. cad!) thorn. Vk, sar (L_ sad) id. Nk. (Ch.) sar id. Pa. caka id. Ga, (P.) sap (pl. sul), (S,) cappu id.; (S.3) sappu id., spine, spike. Go. (Tr.) sap, (G.S.) hap, (Mu.) hap (pl. hahk),. (Ma.) apG@) (pl. aku) thorn (Voc. 3369). Konda sambu (pl. sapku) id. Kui sapu, (K.) hapu id. Kuwi (Su.P.) hapu (pl. hapka), (F.) hapt, (S.) hapu id. DED (S) 2035. 2469 Kur. car a tree of the reed kind, which grows to a height of 7 or 8 feet (its wood is very hard and serves to make pen- holders and arrow-shafts); arrow-shaft, arrow. Malt. caru arrow. DED 2036. 2470 (a) Ta. cal (calv-, canr-) to be abun- dant, full, be suitable, fitting, be great, noble; be sufficient; 7. fullness, abundance; calpu excellence, nobility; cdnrdr the great, the noble. Ma. cala richly, fully. Ko. ta-rna-fo-n a Toda of the division that includes the clans of To-ro+r and Niry (tasrnaer= To. to-ro-1); ta-d nay ghee brought to the Kota village ceremony by the Todas (of the olan related in the jajmani economy). To. torr important man; clan(s), people of the clan, esp. of the clans of the higher-ranking moiety; the higher- ranking moiety (usually called to-rOas); the Todas (in contrast to the Tamilians); to-ro-r- n. pr. of the chief hamlet of one of the clans (lit. place of the to-r). Ka. sal, salu to be sufficient or enough, suffice. Te

Ta. cal furrow in ploughing, track of a sower while passing and repassing in sowing; cilai street, avenue, road. Ma. cal furrow, channel, track, line, direction. Ko. ca-l furrow. To. so-l id. Ka, sala continuous line, a furrow. Kod. ca-lii line, furrow, one com- plete ploughing of a field, people related in any way by descent. Tw. salu line, row, furrow. Te. calu id., groove, track; calupu line, row, series. Ga, (S.3) salu furrow made by plough. Go. (Ko.) ai furrow (< Te.; Voe. caru 140). Konda (BB, 1972) sal id. DED(S) 2038. 2472 Ko, ca-lm (obl. ca-It-) debt, loan, account of all debts and credits, account of one’s life, piece of advice. To. so-lm assets and liabilities. Ka. sal, salu to be bound to, become indebted, be placed under a debt, be obliged or under obligation, be required, fit or proper; sala debt; saliga debtor, creditor; silahguli person who is wont to contract debt. Kod. sa-la a loan. Tu. sala debt. DED(S) 2039. 2473 Ka, jalari Shorea talura Roxb., S. laccifera Wall. Te. jalari Vatica laccifera, {S. Tabura Roxb. = S. laccifera Heyne = V. lacci- fera W. & A.| DED 2040.

Ta. cali umbrella-thorn babul, Acacia planifrons; elephant thorn, A. tomentosa, buffalo-thorn cutch, A. latronum. Ka. jali thorny babool tree, A. arabica Willd.; A. Farnesiana. Te. (VPK) jali, jala A. arabica (branches are cut and used for fencing). DED(S) 2041.

Ta. cailikan, caliyan a caste of weavers. Ma. caliyan a weaver. Ka, saliga, saliya id, Tu. talye weaver; spider; (Shanmugam) salye caste of weavers; fem. satyetti. Te. sale caste of weavers; salidu, salevadu a weaver. Ga. (S.2) sale pindake spider; sale patfu spidet- web. Kuwi (S.) saliesi weaver. Cf. 2809 Ta. céntiravar. DED(S) 2042. ©.) 2476 Kur. cali the 3 or 4 feet of free ground immediately in front of a house. Malt. cali a threshing floor. DED 2043.
Ta. calikai money-bag. Ma. calika id. Ka. jalige id., purse. Te. jaliya, jale, jaleya a sort of purse. / ? < Skt. jalaka-. DED 2044.
Ka. sailuva, salva a kind of bird, a hawk used in hunting. Te. saluva, saluvamu, salvamu a specics of hawk. / Cf. Skt. jaliva-, éaliva- a kind of hawk (M@nasollasa). DED 2045. ,
Ta. calai oil sardine, sp. of Clupea. Ma. cala-min sardine. DED 2046. 2480 Kol. (Haig) saté, (Kin.) salle, (Pusad, BB 1957) sale quail. Nk. sale (sare) id. DEDS 386.
To. carai stripe. Ka. cara line, streak. Te. cara, carika line, streak, stripe. DED(S) 2047.

Ta. caru (cati-) to slip off, slip down as from a tree, slant, incline as a post, deviate, flow, issue; cdral drizzling rain; carukku (carukki-) to slip .or slide, go astray; 7. slipping, sliding; caruku (caruki-) to slip, slide, fail: carukkal slipperiness, slipping, slippery place. Ma, caruka to run off or out, drizzle; carruka to drizzle; caral, carral, maga carral, carru mara drizzling rain. Ka. jaru to slip, slide, slip away, steal away, withdraw, retire, shrink, go off or start swiftly, run, drop or ooze out, flow, (knot) slips or becomes loose; } care [217] imir n. slipping, etc.; jarisu to make to slip, go away, etc.; jira, jarike, jaruvike slipping, sliding, slipperiness, flowing, trickling. Kod. ja+r- (jaeri-) to slip, be slippery; ja+rike slip- petiness, slippery. Tu. jaruni to slip, slide down, be slippery or smooth, tumble, fall down, be dislocated, shrink, hesitate, back- slide, flee, flay, lop off as branches of a tree; jarangely slipperiness, glibness, smoothness; jarundye an unsteady man, a backslider,; jarele a runaway, an unsteady man, a back- slider, coward; jarupadi, jarpadi running away, decampment, retreat; ili-jaru a slope, slipperiness (cf. 503 for ili-). Te. jaru to slide, glide, slip, become loose, ooze; n. slipperiness; slippery; jarucu, jarcu to let slip or drop, let down, loosen; jarudu slipperiness, sliding, slipping; jarumidi a slipknot. Go. (Ko.) jarjar 4- to slip (Voc. 1399). Konda zar- (-it-) ae slide. Cf. 2360 Ta. cari. DED(S. N)

Ta. ciru (cari-) to sweep the threshing- floor and gather scattered grain. Te. (K.) caru to sweep the threshing-floor with a broom. DED 2049. 2484 Ta, caru juice, sap, water in which aromatic substances are infused, pepper water; ctu sap, juice, sweetness, tastiness, toddy, honey, treacle. Ma. c@ru sap as of a palm tree, broth or soup, infusion, decoction. Ka, caru, saru sap, juice, broth, relish in a liquid state, weill-seasoned sauce, pepper water. Tu. caru a kind of pepper water; sdru sap, soup, broth. Te, céru tamarind soup or broth; (Merolu) caru a kind of curry. Pa. kéd broth. Kui (P.) jau dhal, gravy, soup (from DBIA 166). Kuwi (F.) jaiyt curry; (S.) jayu sauce, curry; (Su.) jayu cooked pulse; (Isr.) jayu kuca vegetables. /Cf. Mar. sar a dilute mixture of tamarind, etc. DED(S)

Ta. aru six; aru-patu sixty; aru- nfru. 600; arumai six; aruvar six persons; avv-aru by sixes. Ma. aru six; aru-patu sixty; aru-nnOru 600; aruvar six persons. Ko, a-r six; ar vat sixty; a+r nu-r 600; ar va-ny six pany measures. To. o+r six; pa+r sixteen; aro@ sixty; o-r nusr 600; ar xwa-w six kwa+x measures. Ka, aru six; ara-vattu, aru- vattu, ar-vattu sixty; aru-niru, dru-n0ru 600; aruvar, @rvaru six persons. Kod. a-ri six, a-rane sixth; aru-vadi sixty; aer-nu-ri 600. Tu. aji six; ajane sixth; ajipa, ajippa, ajipa, ajpa sixty. Te. aru six; druguru, Aruvuru six persons; aru-vadi, aruvai, aravai sixty; aru- vandru sixty persons. Kol. (SR. Kin., Haig) ar six; (SR.) argur six persons. Nk. (Ch,) sadi six. Go. (Tr.) sdrung six; sark six each; (W.) sirlfig, (Pat.) harung, (M.) ar, haram, (L.) hartiig six; (Y.) sarvir, (G.) sarvur, (Mu.) harvur, hadruy, (Ma.) arvur six (masc.) (Voe. 3372); sarne (W.) fourth day after tomorrow, (Ph.) sixth day (Voc. 3344); Kui (Letch- majee) sajgi six; saja pattu six times twelve dozen (= 864); (Friend-Pereira; GUmsar dialect) saj six; sajgi six things; (K.) haja six. DED(S) 2051. 2486 Ta, carru (carri-) to publish, announce, explain in detail, speak, mention, praise, beat as a drum; . proclaiming, declaring, sound. Ma, carruka to speak loud, call on gods and sing (as astrologers); kurram caryuka to declare guilty, impute a fault to. Ko. ca+r- (caery-) to tell news of in all places. Ka. siru to cry out, proclaim aloud, publish; sarisu to cause to pro

Ta. cikkanavu hardness, firmness. Ka. jigatu, jigate, jibatu stickiness, gumminess, gum, birdlime; jigi, jibi thickness, viscidity, glutinousness, gum; cigil, jigil to be sticky, gummy, viscid, glutinous, adhesive, adhere, stick fast; jibbu stickiness, sap that issues from the stalk of the mango after it is cut from the tree; cikani hard, substantial, thick, rich, fat. Te. jigata gum, paste, slime, bird- lime, stickiness, gumminess, viscosity; jigi viscidity, glutinousness; jiguru gum, birdlime; cikk4-badu, cikka-vadu to become thick or inspissated; cikkana thickness as of a liquid, density, closeness of texture, etc.; adj. cikkani. Kol. (Pat. p.107) sikot sticky. Go. (Y.) sever, (A.) sever, (SR.) saver, (Tr.) sowwr, (M.) hever, (L.) hevor, (Ma.) ever, (Ko.) ever gum; (Mu.) haver birdlime (Voc, 3476). Konda _ sika thickness of fluid; sikani thick. / Cf. Skt. cikka- gum, birdlime (Ma@nasollasa): cikkana- unctuous; any smooth liquid, gum; Turner, CDIAL, nos. 4780, 4782. DED(S) 53.

Ta. imix C-v-, -nt-) to sprout, shoot forth. Ka. igaru, cigur, cigaru id., 1. a sprout, shoot, young leaf; cigi to sprout, shoot. Tu. igaruni, iguruni to bud, germinate, shoot up; iguru bud, germ, tender shoot; cigyru sprout, bud, germ; ciguruni, ciguruni, siguruni, tigu- runi, tiguruni, tigruni, tiguluni to sprout, shoot, germinate; tegyluni to sprout, shoot, bud; tegulu a sprout, bud; (B-K.) egulu, ege a tender shoot. Te. igirincu, igur(u)cu, ivirincu, ivur(u)cu to bud or put forth buds, become red; iguru, ivuru a bud, sprout, shoot, red young leaf; cigirincu, cigurucu to put forth leaves, leaf; ciguru, civuru tender young leaf, sprout, shoot, sprig, young foliage, small twigs; civaka, civva twig, branchlet. Ga. (S.3) cigali (218] cikku sigur tender leaf. Kur. cigd, cigi young plant just piercing the soil, small seedling: cigyarna to sprout, shoot, come out. / Cf. Mar. sigri a tender sprout or shoot. DED(S) 2054.

Ka. cigali, ciguli a ball made of fried singilli-oil seed mixed with jaggery. Te. (B.) cimili oilcake on which cattle are fed; (B.) cimmiri, cimmili (also Safk.), cimmiruntalu, cimmilunfalu a sweetmeat made of fried sesamum seed with dry coco-nut kernels and sugar (eaten during pregnancy). /? Cf. Skt. Sagkuli- a large round cake composed of ground rice, sesamum, and sugar, and cooked in oil; cf. DBJA 126. DED(S) 2055.

Ma. cintuka to tear (as paper, leaves); cittu a shred, strip; iruka to saw, split (or with 542 Ta. ir). To. sixb sharp end of piece of wood (< Badaga). Ka. sigi to split (as wood), tear or rend with the teeth as sugar- cane; sigasu, sigisu to have split; sigulu to be split, cleft; split (t.); sigur, siguru, sibaru, sivaru, sivuru a splinter, a shiver (cf. s.v.
Te. cikilu to cry tenderly or fondly, as a child. Kur. cixna to weep, cry, sob; caus cixta’ana, c¥xcTxrna to have a mind to weep, have tears in the eyes. DEDS 391.

Ta. cikka in brief, in a nutshell. Ma, cikkini young, small (said of girls). In cikkedu mosquito. Ko. cikn mouse. Ka. cikka, ciga little, small, young; ciku smallness, littleness of size, used of grain and pulse; cikkatana childhood, youth. Kod. cikk-adake very young, tender areca-nut. Tu. cikka little, young, small, short; cikini tender, young, small; cikké a dwarf; (B-K.) cikkeli a small variety of mouse. Te. cikiciki small, little. Go. (Tr.) cikkal, (Ph.) cikkal muskrat (Voc. 1304); (ASu.) cikkal mouse. / Cf. Skt. cikka- gaja- = gaja-bilaka- (Yasastilaka); Pkt. cikka- small; cikka- a small or trifling matter; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4781, *cikka- small. DED(S, N) 2057.

Ta. cikkati field-bean. Te. cikkudu the bean called Dolichos lablab. Ga. (S.7) cikur bean(s) (< Te.) Go. (Ma. Ko.) cikur (kaya) a kind of beans (< Te.; Voc. 1303). Konda sikuti beans, D, 1 DED(S) 2058.

Ta. cikkam comb; cikkaru (cikkari-) to disentangle; cikkarukki a kind of comb. Ma. cikkuka to disentangle, dry and dress the hair after bathing; cikkarukkuka to dis- entangle, comb out; cikkarukki a comb. Ka. sikkati, sikkatige, sikkanige id. Te. cikkantu, cikkanfe a sort of long comb with a few big teeth. DED(S) 2059.

Ta. cikku (cikki-) to become en- tangled, complicated, be caught, ensnared, be obtained; 7. tangle, twist, intricacy, snare, entanglement; cikk-ena firmly, tenaciously, tightly, closely; cifiku (cifki-) to be caught; ikal intricacy, obscurity, involvedness; ? citukku a tangle. Ma. cikku being entangled, intricacy; cikkuka to be entangled. Ko. cik- (ciky-) to be caught, get in trouble, be got, obtained; cikc- (cike-) to make to be caught, get, obtain; tik tangle in the hair. To. tik- (tiky-) to be trapped (physically or figurat- ively); sik knot or tangle in hair. Ka. sikku, sigu (sikk-), sirku, siliku, siluku, silku to become entangled, be caught, fall into the hands of, be got; sikku, sirku, silku state of being entangled, entanglement, intricacy, impediment, obstacle, trouble. Kod. cikk- (cikki-) to become entangled. Ji. sikkuni, tikkuni to be ensnared, caught, seized, ob- tained; sikkavuni, tikkavuni to ensnare, entangle, button; sikku, tikku entanglement, intricacy, perplexity, distress? sikkattu, tikk- aftu labyrinth, intricacy; tikkatuni to be matted, be entangled, intricate; silkuni to be entangled, be perplexed; salkuni to be entangled; salkavuni to entangle; (B-K.) tirku, sirku entanglement, difficulty. Te. cikku to be entangled, ensnared, be obtained, got, come to hand; n. a tangle, intricacy, difficulty, trouble. Kol, sik- (sikt-) to hang (intr.); sikip- (sikipt-) id. (tr.); (Pat., p. 159) sikkeng to get stuck. Pa. cirng- to get stuck, entangled, trapped: cirkip- (

Ta. cikk-ena quickly, promptly. Ma. cikk-ennu, cikkan@ resolutely, quickly. To. tigsn unexpectedly; tig ary- (ars-) to come to know suddenly. Ka. cekkane, jaggane quickly, speedily; cakkane quickly, fast, suddenly: caga, ctga, ctgi, jiga, jegga, jégi quickness; quickly. Tu. cakka quickly, at once. DED 2500 7a. cikku modesty, shame (loc.). Ko. cig shame, self-respect. To. sig shame (in songs). Ka, siggu shame, decency, modesty, bashfulness, timidity, disgust; siggali a person who is ashamed, a bashful person. Te. siggu shame, disgrace, dishonour, bashfulness, shy- ness, coyness, modesty; siggari one who is ashamed, bashful or shy (masc. or fem.); fem. siggarikattiya (tte); siggaritanamu bash- fulness, shyness, shamefacedness; siggaru to have no shame; siggidi a shameless or brazen- faced person. Go, (M.) sig shame (< Te.; Voc. 3382). Konda sigu id. Kuwi (Mah.) siku, sigu id. DED(S) 2062. 2501 Ta, citka-vagai, citkan-varai a variety of plantain introduced from Ceylon (said to be < Simhala- Ceylon), Ma. cifihan a sort of plantain; cifiha-ppazam, cifha-vara its fruit. Kod. cing’ ba-le'sp. plantain. Tu, sitga-baré id. DED 2063. 2502 Ka, singanika, siftgalika a black monkey. Tu. cifiglike a large kind of ape. Te. (B.) sitgilikamu the great black monkey. DED(S) 2064.

Ta. citku (cifki-) to diminish, wane, decrease, faint, fail, droop, decay, perish, elapse, pass away (as time); cikku (cikki-) to become lean or emaciated (< Te.); cikal (cikalv-, cikanr-) to diminish, decrease, perish; n. want, ruin. Kod. cikk- (cikki-) to become tired; cikki numb = sensation of mouth produced by areca-nut. Tu. tirkuni to be lean, weak; sirkuni to fade, shrivel, become weak, lean, fall off; tirku, sirku fading, weakness, faintness; tirkogé a lean of weak man. Te. cikku to become lean or thin. Pa. cing- (sun) declines after midday. Go. (Tr. Ph.) sirana, (SR.) sirind to grow thin; (W.) sirtor weak; (Hislop) sir thin (Voc. 3400). Konda sik- Ct-) to be reduced (as body), fail (in flesh), pine in fear, etc. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 13387, *sikka-, *singa-, Pkt. sifiga- lean, thih. DED(S, N) 2065. 2504 Ka, jitke antelope; cigari, cigare black buck or antelope; (Bellary; U.P-U.) jimke deer. Tu. jihké antelope. Te. jifka id. DED 2066. 2505 Kur. cingnd to pinch slightly be- tween the finger-ends, give a slight scratch (e.g. to a fruit to see if it is ripe): reff.-pass. cingma, Malt, cinge to pinch. ? Kor. (O.) eunku to pinch. DED 2067.
Ka. (Tipt.) cigla a fly. Mand. higne mosquito; henne a fly.
Ka. citike, cifaki, cufike a pinch, as: of snuff. Tu. cif ku, citiki, citka id. Te, (K.) cifike id. DED boa
Ka. citi cifi hode to throb, as the head from ache. Tu. citicifi the throbbing pain of a boil when suppurating or forming. DED 2069.

Ta. citucituppu onom. expr. of hissing noise, as of a burning wick when it contains particles of water. Ka. cita cita the sound produced when water touches burning oil; the snapping sound of corn in being parched; citicifi the sound of sparks or flames bursting forth and crackling; cifil the crackling of flames. Tu. citicifi a crackling noise. Te, citacifa the crackling noise of burning; crackingly. Cf. 2511 Ta. cittikkai. /MBE 1969, pp. 292-3, no. 19, for areal etymology; cf. BHS cificitayati/e sputters (of the noise made by hot things, possibly when in contact with water), Pali citicifayati id. (esp. in contact with water), H. citci{ana to make a cracking or crackling noise: no entry in Turner, CDJAL. DED 2070.
Ka. ciflisu to break, snap, crack, burst, etc. Te. citlu, (K. also) cifilu id.; ciflincu id. (7). Pa. citf- to cut, split. Ga, (Ol.) sif- id. DED 2071.

Ta. cittikkai snap of the finger (< Te.). Ka. citaku, cituku, cifufiku a sound in imitation of the cracking or snapping of the fingers; cifike, citaki, cituke a snap with the finger and the thumb; citufikisu to snap with the finger and the thumb. ? Kod. cedi- (cediv-, cedifij-) to break of itself with’ a cracking noise. Tu. cituku, citiki, citka a snap of the fingers; citiciti a crashing noise. Te. cifika a snap of the fingers; cifikarincu to produce a sound by a snap of the fingers; cifikarinta a snap of the fingers; (K.) cituku to snap the fingers; n. a snapping sound: (K.) ciffa a snap of the fingers. Go. (G.) cifkuli, (Mu.) citkul, (S.) citkan snapping of fingers; (Mu.) cifkul-nék- to snap fingers (Voc. 1311). Konda (BB, 1972) sitim crack- ing of the knuckles. ? Malt. code to crack (as the fingers do); codtre to crack the fingers. Cf. 2509 Ta. citucituppu. /Cfh Skt. chofikd- a snap with the thumb and fingers, Mar. citki, cufki id.; Turner, Nep. Dict., s.v. curki, cutki. DED(S) 2072. 2512 Ta, citti a small measure, Ka. cifti, ciffe a measure of grain equal to four séru, one equal to four solige. Te. citti one fourth of the measure called sla. DEDS 392,

Ta. citfu anything small; cittu- kkuruvi house sparrow; (Tinn.) cittu a small bird. Ka. citfu, cifa, cifi smallness, shortness, etc.; ciff-ili mouse. Tu. citté small, little. ciftai (220] Te. citti id., young; citf-eluka mouse. Ga. (S.3) cittu fine husk. Kui situri small, dwarf- ish. Kuwi (F.) sita kadda a stream; sitki a mouse; (sr.) sif?eri orli a kind of rat. Cf.

Go. (fr.) siddi a mousetrap (Voe. 3389). Kui sindu (pl. sitka) a trap for small birds, a snare. 2518 (a) Kol. sidde squitrel. Nk. sidde id. Go. (W. Ph.) cidrat, (Mu.) cidral (pl. cidrahk) id. (Voce, 1314). Kur. cigra, cidr® id’ / For similar words in Munda languages, see Pinnow, p. 157, Emeneau, JAOS 82.109, and Pfeiffer, p. 170. (b) Konda sirkuli squirrel. Pe, hirkoli id. Kui sicuni, siruri, (K.) heruri id. Kuwi (Mah.) her’uri, (D.) hir’uni id. / Cf. Skt. cikroda- id. (Vaijayanti; > Te. cikrddamu id.), H. cikhur id. DED(S, N) 2077.
Ka. jinagu, jinugu, jinagu, jinugu fineness or thinness (as of texture, thread, powder, written letters, etc.). Te. jiltigu, jiluvu fine cloth; small, slight, fine, delicate. DED 2078.

Ta. cipukku (cinukki-) to yield in small quantities, ooze, issue in drops, drizzle; cinukku-ccinukk-enal onom. expr. of issuing in drops; cinuttku (cinunki-) to drizzle. Ma. cinafiuka to rain slightly, drizzle. Ka, jinugu, jinutigu id.; . drizzling rain; jinigisu to liquefy; (Nanj.) sinku (n, not g) to drizzle. Te. cinuku a drop, a thin or drizzling rain, drizzle, sprinkle of rain; vb. to fall in drops. Pa, (S.) cinkip- (cinkit-) to drip. Ga. (8.3) sinkul raindrops. DED(N) 2079.

Ta. cinuhku (cinufki-) to whine, whimper; cinukkam whining, whimpering; cinukkan worthless person, as always whining, cinukku-ccinukk-enal onom. expr. of worry- ing with frequent crying. Ma. cinafifhuka to be touchy (? or with 1600 Ta. cina), to repine, whine, whimper; cinakkuka to mutter, neigh. Ka, jinugu to mutter, murmur, hum. DED 2080. 2522 Konda sinpa- (-t-) to shave (beard, head, etc.); sinpis- (-t-) to cause to shave; cintam sirim razor. Pe. hen- (-t-) to shave. Kui silpa (silt-) to shave (beard, head); n. a shave; sine, (K.) heni razor. Kuwi (Su.) hin- (-h-), (F.) hirhali, (S.) hinh’nai to shave; (Su. Isr.) hinpa, (F.) hirpa, (S.) hinipa, (Mah.) hinipa razor. ? Ta. inuiku (inufki-) fo pull off as a leaf from a twig, pluck as a flower, DEDS 393.

Ta. cintu stench, as of rancid curd. Ka. sindu a disagreeable, nauseous, fetid smell (as of sheep, tigers, rancid butter, etc.); jaddu the disagreeable smell of sheep’s milk, etc.; jiddu rancidity, etc. Kod. cindi scent (found by dog in hunting, etc.). Tu. jiddu, jidda_rancidity, mustiness; rancid, musty. DED(S) 2081. 2524 Ta, citar, citaram raindrop; citari rain. Ma. citar a drop as of ghee. Go. (Mu.) ‘hidihk-, (Ma.) idk-, irk- to sprinkle; (M.) hidkana to spray (Voc. 3546). Malt. cithge to begin to rain drop by drop. DED(S) 2082.

Ta. citampu (citampi-) to be spoiled by too much moisture; 7. spoilt condition, excessive maceration; citampal being softened by soaking or spoiled by too much moisture. Te. citacita dampness, moisture; cittadi damp, moisture, wetness; damp, wet, moist; cittari the rainy season. Kui sindali moist, damp. DEDS 394.

Ta. citai (-v-, -nt-) to be injured, spoiled, be broken, become corrupt, prove untrue; (-pp-, -tt-) to injure, waste, destroy, ruin, demolish, kill, uproot; citai, citaivu injury, degeneracy, ruin, defeat, fault, defect; citaku (citaki-) to strip off, erase. Ka. cidaku, ciduku to squeeze the soaked pulse called avare with the fingers and thus remove the skin; sindu, sendu to rub out, extinguish, destroy. Te, cituku, ciduku to be crushed, broken or smashed; break, burst; cidiyu to be broken, smashed or crushed, break; cidupa a bit, fragment, piece; cidupu to crush, smash, squeeze, break; cidumu to pinch, nip; cidura, cidurupa, cidrupa a piece, bit, fragment. Konda sidli- (-t-) to be crushed (as an overripe fruit). Malt. cithge to squeeze out the seed or stone from a fruit. DED(S) 2083. 2527 Kol. sit- (sitt-) to thrust into hole, gore. Nk. Ssitt- to pierce with horns. Pa, citt- to butt with horns, gore. Ga, sit- (P.) to gore with horns; (S.3) to pierce; (S.) citt- to insert (as flowers into a braid). DED(S) 2084. 2528 Kol. sittenas {lun in the evening; (SR.) cintevela evening. Nk, Sitte id. Pa. citta night. DED 2085.

Ta. cintam, intam tamarind tree. Te. cinta tamarind. Kol. (SR.) sinta id.; (P.) sitta mak tamarind tree. Nk. sitta tamarind. Go. (Tr.) sitta mara, (G. Mu. S.) hitta, (Ma. Ko.) itta, (M.) ita id. (Voc. 3381). Konda sita maran tamarind tree. Kur. citi a tamarind seed with one of its sides rubbed until it has become white; ciyA a tamarind seed. Malt. cintu ‘ {221} imai site sour. /Cf. Skt. cifica- tamarind; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4792. DED(S) 2086.

Ta. cintu a kind of musical com- position, masquerade dance, a musical note; centu a primary melody type. Ma. cintu a poetical measure. Te, cindu dancing; cindilu to shake, move; cindu-drokku, cindulu- drokku to dance (for trokku, sce 3522; M. Kandappa Chetty). DEDS(N) 395.
Ta. cippam parcel, bundle, a man’s load of tobacco leaves. Ma. cippam bundle, bale, chiefly of tobacco. Te. cippamu a bundle or parcel. DED 2088.

Ta. ippi pearl-oyster, shell; cippi shell, shellfish, coconut shell for measuring out curds. Ma. ippi, cippi oyster shell. Ka. cippu, sippu, cimpi, cimpe, simpi, simpu, simpe oyster shell, mussel, cockle, a portion of the shell of a coconut, skull, a pearl oyster; (Gowda) cippi coconut shell. Tu. cippi coconut shell, oyster shell, pearl; tippi, sippi_ coconut shell. 7e. cippa a shell; (kobbari c®) coconut shell; (mokali c®) knee-pan, patella; (tala c°) skull; (muttepu c°) mother-of-pearl. Go. (Ma.) ipi shell, conch (Voc. 174). /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 13417, *sippi-; Pali sippi- pearl oyster, Pkt. sippi- id., etc. DED(S) 2089.

Ko. ceb sores on the mouth in syphilis. Ka. cibba, cibbu, sibba, sibbu, sibbe a whitish, reddish or blackish spot on the body. Tu. cibbu white spots on the body; Sibba, ibbu, subba, subbu a blotch, dis- colouring of the skin; sidibu, gidubu the pustule of smallpox. Te. sibbiyamu, sibbemu, sdbiya, sdbe a sort of discoloration and spots formed on the skin; cidumu itch, scabies. Go. (SR.) subba white patches on a man’s skin (Voc. 3441); 2 (Tr.) sipowa id. (Voce. 3421). {Said to be from Skt. sidhma- blotch, leprous spot, leprosy. Cf. Mar. sib€ a mild form of leprosy, a blotch or scab of it; Turner, CDIAL, no. 13406. DED(S) 2090.
Ka. cibbala, cibbalu, cibbil, cibbul a bamboo lid. Te. sibbi lid, dish. DED 2091.

Ka. jibb-adike, jibal-adike a young areca-nut cut into two pieces and boiled. Kod. jibbi, jibb-adake a young and tender grade of areca-nut. Tu. jibalu, jibulu, jibulu boiled, tender areca-nuts. DED 2092. 2539 Ta, cemmal water. Tu. sima moist- ness, cold; sima cold (n.), chill; simma cold (adj.); temma a cough, cold. Te. cemma moisture, damp; moist, damp; cemmagincu, cemmagilu, cemmagillu to become damp or moist, sweat; cemmafa, cemafa, cemaru sweat; (K.) cemaru to melt, become wet in sweat; cemar(ujcu id.; temma wetness, moisture; temm-era cool breeze, zephyr (for era, sce 810 Ka. eral); temada phlegm, mucus. Kuwi (S. 7.) jimbri piyu drizzle. ?Kur. simdharna to become damp. DED(S) 2093.

Ta. cimintu (ciminfi-) to tickle, pluck, pinch, prod; cintu (cinfi-) to tease, vex. Ma, cinfuka children to nettle, prick, pull one another. Ka. cimutu to press to- gether, squeeze, pinch; (Gowda) jiginti to pinch. Tu. cimukuni id.; caunfuni to pinch, nip. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 4822, *cimb- pinch; Nep. cimfanu, cimotanu to pinch, Beng. cimfa id., Mar. cimafné, cibatné, cibalpé id., and related forms. DED(S) 2094.

Ta. cimili cricket. Ma. cimilia certain vanfu fie. black bee, wasp, beetle]. Ka. cimmandi, cimmande, jimmande cricket. Te. - (B.) cimata, cimita, cimmata, cimmeta the grave cricket that destroys clothes; moth, bookworm, cockroach. ? Kol. simri, in: ka-kasimri dragonfly (Kamaleswaran). Cf. Ta. cil-vantu cricket, etc., s.v. 2588 Ta. cill- enal. DED (N) 2095.

Ta. imix (-v-, -nt-) to bind as by order, restrain; (-pp-, -tt-) to fasten, tie; m bond, cord; cimix (-pp-, -tt-) to bind, entrap; 1. small round jewel box, small casket; cimizppu bondage, tie. Ma. cimig small box. Te. imudu to be contained within; imud(u)cu to put in, thrust in. DED 2096, DEDS 66.

Ma. cimiftu young, little; cimitfan child. Pe. himna child, young of animal. Mand. himna id.; hipra son. ? Kui mida, mila child, infant, young of animal (< *smil- < *siml-), ? Kuwi (D.) mila child. DEDS 399.

Ta. cimai summit of a mountain, hair-tuft; cimaiyam top, summit of a moun- tain, peak, mountain, hill. Te. civara end, point, tip, extremity. Kur. cum’a, cup’’ projecting point on a hill or mountain, peak, crag. DEDS 398.

Ta. imai (-pp-, -tt-) to wink, glitter, twinkle, shine; n. eyelid, winking of the eye, time spent in winking; cimig (-pp-, -tt-) to wink, blink; cimiftu (cimiftti-) id., to make a signal with the eyes; 1. blinking of the eyes, significant wink; cimili (-pp-, -tt-) to wink; cimilippu winking of the eyes; cimpuli (-pp-, -{t-) to close or shut the eyes as in joy, terror, etc.; nimai (-pp-, -tt-) to wink; n. eyelid. Ma. ima eyelash; imekka to blink, twinkle; cima eyelash; cimpuka, cimmuka, cimpuka to twinkle with the eyes; cimpal twinkling of the eyes; cintuka to give a secret sign. Ka. ime, eme, eve eyelash, eyelid; (Hal.) kanjeme (n, not fi) eyelid; cimufu, civafu, civufu to twinkle or wink with the eyes. Kod. (Shanmu- gam) cimm- to twinkle; cimm blinking of the cimpu [222] il eyes. Tu. imé, simé, simmé eyelash. Te. cimutu to wink. /Cf. Nep. cim closing the eyes; cimlanu to blink; Bashkarik i¢cimik blink (Turner, CDIAL, no. 4822; different from the items in that entry related to 2540; Burrow 1967, p. 41). ? Cf. Skt. smil- to wink, twinkle. DED(N) 2097.

Ta. cimpu small splinter or fibre rising on a smooth surface of wood or metal, chip, flake from beaten tron, frayed ends of a worm cord, bamboo splits, twig: cimpal splinter, rough nap. Ma. cimpu_ splinter, _ bamboo slip. DED 2098. 2547 Kui (Mah. p.87) homburu cloth. Kuwi (Su.) himbori, humbori, (F.) himb6ri, (S.) him bori id. DEDS 400.

Ka. cimmu to discharge by means of two fingers, shoot with the finger, push forward by means of the foot or a stick, cast, fling, push away, butt or toss with the horns, squirt, sputter; break forth, flow forth, burst forth, gush out; brandish a weapon, shake, swing; cimmisu to cause oneself to spring out, gush forth; come forth suddenly; shake (¢.), swing; cimaku, cimiku, cimuku, civuku, cimakisu, cimikisu, cimukisu, civukisu. to sprinkle; simpipi sprinkling; simpisu, simpa- disu, simbadisu, simmadisu to sprinkle, besprinkle; ? cigi to discharge with or from the fingers. Kor. (O.) cimki to splash: cimci’ to sprinkle. Te. cimmu to throw, cast, fling, toss, spurt, squirt, thrust, butt as with the hors; #. a spurt, thrust, butt; cimmudu throwing, flinging, casting; cippil@)u to gush, flow, overflow, well forth, rise; cimmanamu spurting, squirting. /Cf. Pkt. simpai, sippai to sprinkle, Mar. sipné id. DED(S, N) 2099.
Te. ciyya flesh, muscle; jigili plump, fleshy. Ga. (Oll.) seg muscle, flesh. Go. (Mand.) sikahk flesh (Voc. 3378). Pe. jey id. (or with 529 Ta. iraicci). DED(S) 2100.

Ta. cirattai coconut shell, begging bowl; carattai coconut shell (loc.). Ma. ciratta coconut shell, chiefly the lower half; ciratti the upper half of a coconut shell. Ka. (DCV) gerate, (Hal.) gerate, (Bark.) garifa coconut shell. Kod. cerate id. Kor. geyte id. DED(S, N) 2101. 2551 Ta, ciral kingfisher. ?Go. (ASu.) ciral a kind of bird. DEN 34.

Ta. iravu, ita, ira, ra night; iru black; irutci, iruttu, irunmai, irumai darkness; irul darkness, dark colour, ignorance; (irulv-, irunt-) to become dark, dim, obscure, be black in colour, be darkened (as the mind); irulan Iruta (member of a tribe in the Nilgiris); iruntai, iruntu, iruntil charcoal. Ma. iravu, ira, 14 night; iru to be dark; iruffu, iri¢fu, irul darkness; irulka to grow dark; irular a caste of jungle dwellers; irunnal charcoal; ikkari soot, grime. Jr. ra+vu, raepodu, ra+ve- podu night. Ko. irl night; ilma-mm night, the whole night. To. i+f night; isQa+s night-time; erl Irula; fem. eré (Sak. ert, erl¢); ? ermoln sand (obi. ermolt-; cf. 4666 (b) Ta. manal sand). Ka. irul, iralu, iratu, irlu night; iddal, ijjalu charcoal; (eastern dialects) iglu, (western dialects) ijlu coal (LSB 1.3). Kod. iri night; irili night-time; irif? darkness. Tu. irku night; irlu, itty dusk, darkness, night. Te. irulu darkness, shades, shadows; irulu konu to become dark or obscure; réyi (in cpds. ré-) night. Kol. (Kin.) cirum very dark; sindi soot. Pa. cirun charcoal. Ga. (OML.) siring black; sirgat black, rusty; (S.) sirggafti black; (P.) sirig (pL sirgil) charcoal, cinders: (S.°) sitrin soot, ashes; (Oll.) sirtal cvening. Go. (Mu.) hirk, (S.) hirki, (M.) hirki,-irki, (Ma. Ko.) irk charcoal (Voc. 3551). Konda siruki coal; ?reyu night; (BB) sirik charcoal. Pe. rika, ringa id. Mand. riggay (pl) id. Kui sriva soot; singa charcoal. Kuwi (Su. P.) ringa, (S.) ringla id. Cf. 483 Ta. itti, 486

Ta. il non-existence, death; illai it is not (in Old Tamil with a complete neg. paradigm); no; ilamai absence, non-existence, poverty, want; illitavan one utterly destitute; ian poor man; ill-enal death, negating existence; flam, ilampai poverty; ili one who is without (at end of cpd.); inmai total il {223} jila negation of existence, poverty; inriya that which is not. Ma. illa does not exist, there is not; no, not; adj. ila; illayma nat existence, poverty, want. Ko, il- not to exist, not to be in a place (complete negative paradigm). To. il- id.; 108 not being; idy which is not (in song). Ka. illa, illam, illayu is or are not; no, not; ila a man who has not, a place that is free from; illade gerund not being; illada adj. not being; illave state of not being. Kod. ille be not (for all persons and numbers). “Tu, ijji, (B-K.) iddi not to exist, not to be. Te. \é- (complete neg. paradigm; 3 sg. non-m., lédu) be not; léka without, not having; or, else; Iédu no, none; léni absent, wanting, not having, having nothing, poor, needy; lébaramu nothing; lemi, lémidi absence, non-existence, not being, want, poverty, neediness; -idi he who has not. Pa. cila does not exist (also inflected for gender and number; 3 sg. m. cilad, neut. cila, pl m. cilar, neut. cilav); cilaka without. Go. (A. Y.) sile, (SR.) sille, (W. Ph. Pat.) hille, (M.) hille, hile, (Mu.) hille, (Ma.) ille not, no; (Pat.) hill- to be not (complete neg. paradigm; SR. also gives conjugated forms of sil()-) (Voce. 3414); (Koy
Ta. il, iflam, ii, itavu, cillam clearing- nut tree, Strychnos potatorum Linn. Ka. cilla, cillu, cittu, cali id. Te. cilla id. DED 2108.

Ka. cilaka, ciluka a hasp or small chain for fastening a door; (Tipt.) cilka a door staple; (Coorg) silku iron bolt. Tu. ciluku, cilky the catch or bolt of a window. Te. cilaka, ciluka, ciluku a padlock, the bolt ot hasp of a door; (B.) cifakata, (also Sank.) cilukada a saddle buckle, a ring at the end of a rope used as a girth through which the other end is passed to fasten the saddle. Go. (G.) silka a leaf-cup pin (Voc, 3412). DED 2109.
Ta. cilanti, cilampi spider. Ma. cilanni. ? To. twa-lozn. Ka. seladi, selandi. Te. celadi, celagamu. /Cf. Skt. Salaka- id. DED(S) 2110.

Ta. cilanti pimple, small boil, abscess, ulcer, venereal boil. Ma. cilannia kind of ulcer or bubo. Te. celidia kind of skin-disease; (K.) a skin rash with small boils (also celdi). DED (S) 2111, and from DED 1617.
Ta. cilampam, cilamam practice of the art of using quarterstaff, fencing. Ma. cilampam fencing; cilampi fencer. DED 2112.

Ta. ilir (-pp-, -tt-) to stand erect as the hair stands on end from fright, etc.; sprout, germinate; cilir (-pp-, -tt-) to sprout, shoot, bristle as the hair on the body; cilir- cilir- to get goose-skin from intense emotion; ciluppu (ciluppi-) to bristle; cilumpu (cilumpi-) id., have an uneven surface by the rise of splinters, fibres, etc.; cilumpu, cilukku splinter or fibre rising on a smooth surface of wood. Kui slinga (slingi-) to stand on end (hair). DED 2113.
Ta. cilu Cpp-, -tt-) to open, split dntr.), cilukku chipping, cutting; ciluval tags, tatters. Ko. cilv- (cild-) to break along the length of the grain (intr.); (cilt-) id. (t7.). DED (S) 2114.

Ta. ciluku trouble, affliction, per- plexity, quarrel, obstacle; cilukku quarrel, trouble; cilukan, cilukkan troublesome or quarrelsome man; cillarai annoying business, trouble from thieves. Ma. ciluku trouble; cilukan quarrelsome. Ka. siluku, sifku trouble- someness, viciousness; cillatana trouble. Te. silugu, siluvu mischief, trouble. DED 2115.

Ta. cilukku iron staple, tooth of a saw, barb. Ma. cilukku spike, iron barb, javelin. Tu. cilléli a sharp stick to dart {sic} a fish with. Te. cillak6la lance, javelin; ciluku, sela arrow; selagdla goad; selapandi porcupine (for pandi pig, see 4039), / Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 12466, Skt. sili-. DED 2116.

Ta. cilucilu (-pp-, -tt-) to rain gently; cilu-nir raindrop dripping from leaves; cilum- pu (cilumpi-) to flow out, gush out. Ko. cilk ir- (i{-) to drizzle. Ka. (Tipt.) cilkuniru water in fine drops; jilipu to ooze (as water from a new pot). Kor. (O.) cilbi to splash water with the finger. Te. ciluku to sprinkle (¢.), spirt, shed; be spilled; cilikincu, cilukarincu to sprinkle; (K.) cilacila imit. of flowing; cilupu a pond; cifka a small water-channel. Kol. (.) silka river. Vk. Silka brook, river. Pa, cilva brook, rivulet. Go. (A.) silka small river; (G.) hilka, (Ma. Ko.) ilka rivulet (Voc. 3411). Kuwi (Isr.) silk- (-it-) to splash (intr.). Cf. 2785 Ka. sele. / Cf. Pkt. cilicilia- a shower: cilicilla- wet (MBE 1969, p. 293, no. 20). DED(S, N) 2117.

Ta. ciluppu (ciluppi-) to churn, stir, agitate; cilumpu (cilumpi-) to be agitated, shaken; (Devanesan, p.3) ciluppi a churning staff. Ka. cilku cilku noise of milk in churn- ing; (Nanj.) silbu to churn. Je. ciluku to churn. DED(S, N) 2118.
Ta. cilai bow. Ma. cila id. DED 2119.

Ko. jelk. DED(S, N) 2075. 2516 Ka, jiddu a substance that is smeary, greasy or oily,as ghee or oil. Te. jiddu oiliness, grease or greasiness, unctuousness. Nk, jitkip- to stick to (t7.). ? Pa, cin- to stick, adhere (< cin-/cind-); caus. cinip- (cinit-). Kui sitpa, (sitt-), sirpa (sirt-) to be sticky, tacky; sindu, sindru, jindru gum, resin. Kuwi (Su.) hindru resin. DED(S) 2076.

Te. jila itching, itch; jilajila tingling, tickling, or titillation of the skin; (B.) jila- jilam-anu to itch, be prurient, tingle, tickle. Kui sili sali va to itch, tingle. Kur. cilgaz itching; a caterpillar whose touch causes the hand to itch severely; cilgalagnad to feel an cillarai [224] cilla itch. Malt, cilge itching; cilgtre to cause to itch. /MBE 1969, p.293, no.21, for a possible areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDJAL, no. 4874. DED(S) 2121.

Ta. cillarai things scattered here and there in small quantities, sundries, fractional quantities, change (as of a rupee); trifling, insignificant matter; cilvinam odd; savings effected from the sum allotted for household expenses. Ma. cillara small, trifling matter; cilvanam, cillanam odd; remaining fraction, small articles of daily use bought in the bazaar. Ko. cilr odd (over a round number; ca-vim cilr kaec a thousand-odd rupees), cilr de-va-dy a small-change god (contrasted with coeym the great god). To. silosr, silfo+- mm silver, smali change; sily silver (in songs). Ka. cillara, cillare, cilre smallness, pettiness, fractional quantities, money under the value of a rupee; cilavan the odd money over a round sum. 7u. cillaré small money, change, trifle; little, trifling; cilvana, ciluvana the odd money over a round sum; little, small, trifling, mean, low. Te. cillara small, unimportant, insignificant, trifling, of no note or conse- quence, minor, non-essential, sundry, mis- cellaneous, odd, extra, additional; sundries, odds and ends, miscellaneous articles, change, small coins. Kuwi (S.) cillera prancu to retail; (Isr.) sirlu change (coins). /Cf. Mar. cillar petty, trifling article, change (of money). DED(S) 2122.

Ta. ili small hole (as in a pitcher), orifice as of the teat; cilli leak, hole, crack. Ma. cilli-kkuttu a little hole. Ka. jilli_ small hole in an earthen vessel. Te. cilli small hole as ina pot, paper, etc. DED 2123.

Ta. cilucitu (-pp-, -tt-), jilujilu to feel chill, be cool: cilucil-enal, jilujil-enal onom. expr. of being cool; cill-itu, jill-ifu to become chill; cill-enal, jill-enal onom. expr. of being very chill; jillu extreme cold (< Te.); jilu- jiluppu coolness, Ka. jiltu the sensation pro- duced by touching cold water. Te. jillum-anu to feel or be very cold or chill. Pe. jila cold. Mand. jilin (pL) cold (water). Kui jili cold, chill; jili inba to be cold, cool; jilna coldly, coolly. Kuwi (S.) julla innai to be very cold. DED (S, N) 2124.
Ta. cillai unruly mischievous dispo- sition (as of a bull), wicked and libidinous woman. Ka. cille a worthless, mischievous, misbehaving woman. DEDS 405.

Ko. jiv jiv in- (id-) (limbs) become numb from cobra’s bite, from sitting in a cramped position, or from extreme exertion, Ka, jum, jumy, juma, jumu, jummu imit. sound used in the sensation of coldness or chill, of the taking effect of poison, of horrip- ilation, of the pain of a wound, (also jommu, jomu; cf. 2882 Ta. cOmpu) of the numbness of a limb kept long in one position; juvvu a sound to express the sensation of fatigue and pain in the feet after walking. Te. jumjum numbness, ‘sleeping’ of the limb; a throbbing pain; horripilation; jumma a sudden stunning or smarting sensation; (K.) jiva jiva numbness of limb. Pe. junjuni numbness, pins and needles, DED(N) 2125.

Ta. civinhki Indian lynx, Felis caracal; hunting leopard, F. jubate. Ma. civindi a hunting leopard. Ka, sivangi the tiger-wolf, the hyzena; a leopard used in hunting. Te. civagi, civvaigi, civvigi, sivagi, sivatigi, siv- vanigi, sivvigi hyena. DED 2126.
Ta. civiri a fan. Ka. siguri, (K.?) sigudi a kind of chowrie. Te. sivirt a chowrie. Kui jiperi a fan. DED(S) 2127.
Ko. civk (repeated any number of times) onom. of young birds twittering. Ka. civu civu the twittering of small birds; the peculiar cry of rats or mice. DED 2128.
Go. (Mu.) hirka, (Ma. M.) irka, (L.) hirrka cucumber (Voc. 3555). Konda ryika id. Pe. rila id. ? Tu. cilli, cillé a kind of cucumber. DEDS 407.

Tu. tirtu, hirtu, (B-K. also) sirtu down, beneath, below, under; adj. lower, subordinate; tirtattu lower side or level; tirtané lower part of a slope; tiryara low; below, downward, under. Go. (Tr.) sir under, beneath; (W. Ph.) siri, (S.) hir(u), (L.) hidu below; (Ma.) ir beneath, below; (Ma. S.) irta lower (Voc. 3408); (ASu.) sir down, below; sipayih east. Kui (K.) sigta lower. Kuwi (Isr.) ri’i low, lower; ri?ika lower part (of village, etc.), DEDS(N) 401.

Ta. ili (-v-, -nt-) to strip off, pluck. Ma. cilikka (the rind of a fruit) to open from ripeness. Tu. cilkuni to flay. Pa. cilgg- to peel off, flake off Gntr.); cilkip- (cilkit-) to peel off, scale off (¢.). Kui slinga (stingi-) to be plucked, untied, loosened; slipka (< slik-p-; slikt-) to loose, untie, pluck off. Br. sil skin, slough of snake, fruit-skin, rind (prob. < Panj.-Lahnda chill skin, peel; Turner, CDIAL, no. $052). DED(S) 2120.

Ka. cilike stick and other small pieces of wood. Pa. cilpa piece of wood (Halbi si®); cilurka (pl. cilurkel; S.) chips of wood. Go. (Mu.) hirpey (pl) bark of tree; (M.) higpe fuel; (L.) hirpe firewood (Voc. 3556); (LuS.) hirpé a chip or shaving. Konda silpa chip of wood. Pe, cilpa id., bark.

Ma. (Tiyya, Calicut) cilla small branch of a tree; (Tiyya, Cannanore) illi twig. Ko. cil branch of a tree, tine of antlers, tributary of rtver; cil kav pole with a fork at the end. Ka. cille bifurcation, a fork (as that formed by two branches); ? (Hav.) geflu branch. ? Tu. (B-K.) gellu id., twig. Te. (B.) cilla bifurcated; cillu a fork (as that formed by two branches). Pa. cella, jella branch, bough. DED(S, N) 2129. cill-enal (225] ci 2588 7a. cill-enal onom. expr. of being noisy, boisterous; cilucilu (-pp-, -tt-), cilu- pulu (-pp-, -tt-) to be chattering; cilu-cil-enal, cilu-pul-enal, cillu-ppill-enal onom. expr. of bubbling, as boiling water; cil-vanfu, cil-vitu, cillitu cricket, Ma. cil-vitu, civitu, cifu id. Cf. 2541 Ta. cimifi for ‘cricket’. DED 2130. _ 2589 Ta. cita (-pp-, -tt-) to be eminent, illustrious, surpass, be abundant, be aus- picious, be graceful, . rejoice; cirantor the great, the illustrious, gods, relatives, ascetics: Cirappu_ pre-eminence, pomp, abundance, wealth, happiness, honours, esteem, periodical festival in a temple; ciravu meritorious deed. Ma, cirakka (cirannu) to be glorious. Ka. (K.2) serapu hospitality, honour, festival.

Ta. ciruttai panther, Felis pardus, Ka, cirate, ciraca, circa, circu a small kind of tiger, the cheeta or hunting leopard, F. jubata, Kod. cirate leopard. Tu. cirtepili id. Te. ciruta leopard, cheetah; ciru puli leopard. Cf. 1599 Ko. kirbn and 1326 Ta. ciru. DED (S.) 2132. _ 2591 Ta, cirai, cigaku, cigakar wing; irai, iraku, irakar, irakkai wing, feather. Ma. iraku, ciraku wing. Ko. rek wing, feather. Ka, erake, erafke, rakke, rekke wing; ratte, rette wing, upper arm. Kod. rekke wing: ratte upper arm. Ju. edifke, refiké wing. Te, eraka, rekka, rekka, neraka, neri id. Kol. redapa, (SR.) reppa id.; (P.) rerapa id., feather. Vk. rekka, reppa wing. Pa. (S.) rekka id. Go. (S.) rekka wing-feather; reka (M.) feather, (Ko.) wing (Voc. 3045). Konda reka wing, upper arm. Kuwi (Su.) rekka wing. Cf.

Ta. cinai. DED (S) 2300. 2799 Konda sonki (n, not 9) mortar for pounding fixed in the veranda floor. Pe. henki mortar. Mand. heni id. Kui seni a bamboo or wooden mortar for pounding rice. Kuwi (Su. F.S.) he’ni, (P.) henni, (Mah.) heni mortar for pounding rice. ? Go. (W. Ph.) sahki, (Tr.) sahki, (Ch.) sahki, (A. Y.) cahki, (G. Mu.) hahki, (Ma.) ahki, a’ki, (Ko.) ahk mortar (yee. 3363) (or with 2391 Ta. aval). DEDS 2800 Konda (BB) stk- (-t-) to itch. Pe. h®k- (t-) id.; h@pkor cow itch, Mucuna pruriens. Mand. hék- to itch; to scratch. Kui sépka (< sek-p-; s®kt-) to itch. Kuwi (Su. P.S.) henguli the itch; (F.) hengali aiyali, (D.) hépk-, (Isr.) s®k- (-h-) to itch. DEDS 442. _ 2801 Ta. ctkaram acquisition, that which is secured, savings, provision, preparation, readiness, collection, assemblage, gathering, family, tribe, district, station; cékari (-pp-, -tt-) to acquire, get, secure, procure, make ready, collect, gather, assemble. Ma. é&kharam assemblage, heap; s¢kharikka to pile up (these are Sanskritized in form, though there is no Skt. Sekhara- in this meaning). Te. s®karamu saving, collecting, collection, amassing; stka- rincu to save, collect, amass, obtain, procure. DED 2301.

Ta. cinna small, little, inferior, mean, low, young; cinnam smallness, minuteness, anything small; cinni small child, little thing, a small measure. Me. cinna small: cinnan a short man; cinampu a little; cinakku a bit. Ka, cipi, cinni, cinne smallness, shortness; cinna a little one, a boy; cina smallness, small pieces; cinna, jinna smallness, etc. Kod. cinni small; cenna a little. Te. cinna small, little, young, younger, petty, mean, trifling, trivial; cinnddanamu smallness, child- hood, youth; cinnadi girl, lass; cinnavadu boy, young man, one who is younger; cinni small, little, pretty. Kol. (W. SR.) sinnam small, young, younger; (Kin.) cinni small. Nk. sinna(m) little. Pa, cin little (in a text; cin cin dira ney just a little castor oil); cind, (NE) cind son. Ga. (Oll.) sini vande little finger; sind son; (S) cindu, sindu (pl cindil, sindil) id.; (S.2) cin makil children. Go. (Mu.) cini andki little finger (Voc. 1315). Br, cunak small; cund child. / Cf. Mar. cinka grat, tiny (n, not fh; also cimka). DED(S) 2595 Ta, (Lush.) cinnafici Lagerstroemia parviflora, Ma. (Lush.) cimajici id. Ke. can- nafgi, cennangi id. Te. (Lush., Kitt.) cannafgi, (Lush.) cimafici id. DEDS 409,

Ta. cinnam coin, gold coin. Ko. cinm gold. To. sin gold; ear-ring; tingony bag in which old coins are put for use af funerals (for -gony cf. App. 33 Ta. koni). Ka. cinna gold. Tu. cinna id. Te. cinnidamu, cinamu id.; cinnamu a weight (of gold) equal to 1/30 of a tola. DED(S) 2136.
Ta. cinpi Indian shrubby copper- leaf, Acalypha fruticosa. Ka. cinni birch- leaved Acalypha, A. betulina Retz. {A. fruticosa Forsk. = A. betulina Retz.| DED

Ta. i (-v-, -nt-) to give to inferiors, agree, consent; ikai gift; fkaiyan liberal person; ivu_ giving, gift, distribution; ivi Cpp-, -tt-) to distribute; ivn donor. Ka. i (itt-) to give, allow, permit; isu, iyisu to cause to give. Te. iecu (7- iyy-, ivv-) to give, allow, permit; igi, ivi giving, donation, charity, a gift; igikadu, ivikadu, ivari a liberal, bountiful person. Kol, sie- (si-t-) to give. Nk. $i- id. Nk. (Ch) si- id. Pa. ci- (cifi-) id. Ga. (OIL) si- (sid-, siy-, sin-), (S) si- id. Go. (Tr.) siana to give, allow; (M) hiyana (hit-), (G. Mu. S.) hi-, (Ma. Ko.) i- to give (Voc. 3418). Konda si-(-t-) id. Pe. hi- (-t-), (in songs) si- id. Mand, hi- id. Kui siva (sit-), jiva Qit-) id., allow; n. act of giving, bestowal; (K.) hi- to give. Kuwi (P.) hiali, (S.) hinai, (Su.) hi- (-t-) id. Kur. efing (ciccas) id., allow. Malt. ciye (cic-) to give away. Cf. 416 Ta. iku. DED(S) 2138.

Ta. ci (-pp-, -tt-) to sweep off, brush away, wipe off, cleanse; ciy (-pp-, -tt-) to sweep; civu (civi-) to sweep clean (as floor), clean (as teeth); cikku rubbish; cikam-pul, ciku, cirku broom-grass; (RS, p. 147, item 258) cipra a broom. Ma. ctl broom, besom. To. kisp broom, Ka. cipari id. Kod. ci-pe broom made of grass, palm-leaves, etc. Kor. (O.) tippi to sweep. Te. cikili, cTpuru a broom. Kol. sabdi id. Nk. (Ch.) sabdi id. Pa. c&pid id. Ga. (Oll.) s®pet, (S.) ctpéd, ci [226] ical sépe, (S.°) stpét id. Go. (Tr.) saiyana, saittana, (Pat.) héyana, (G.) hey-, (Mu.) hay-, (S.) heyy-, ey-, (Ma.) ey-, (Ko.) éy- to sweep (Voc. 3471); (S.) h&pur, (M.) hepur, (Ma.) Spor, (Ko,.) tpur broom; (M.) hepa Tiy- sanolaena agrostis {a grass} (Voe. 3590). Konda sipa (-t-) to sweep; siper broom, Pe. hip- Ct-) to sweep; hipos broom. Mand. hép- to sweep; hépur broom. Kui sépa (sépi-) to sweep; . sweeping; séperi broom, brush. Kuwi (F.) hepali, (S.) hépinai, (Su.) hép- (-it-) to sweep; (F.) hepori, (S.) hépori, herpori (7), (Su.) hépori (pl héporka), (P.) hapuri broom. Kur, c¥cna to rub with some- thing soft for cleaning or drying, wipe away, dry; cicrna to wipe off. Malt. cice to wipe, clean. DED(S, N) 2139.

Ta. ci); sibu bamboo-slit. Tu. tigipuni, tigupuni, (B-K.) sigipu to split, slit, cut, saw, tear; tiguruni, tigruni, tiguluni, siguruni to split: tiguté, tigté, tigité a split, splinter, slip, piece; (B-K.) tiburu a crack, split. Te. citu to gash, rend, tear, cut in slices; cirudu tearing; cirfigu, cinfigu to be torn or rent; n. a tear or rent; cincu, cimpu to tear, rend; cimpi, cifki ragged, tattered; a rent, tear. Pa, cik-, (S.) cikip- (cikit-) to tear; (S.) cing- to be torn. Go. (Tr.) sinder a splinter of wood, bamboo, etc. (Voc. 3396); (M.) hikang to tear; (G. Mu. L.) hik-, (Ma.) ik- to tear (tr.); CL.) hikaval carpenter. (Voc. 3560); (Koya Su.) ift- to be torn; ik- to tear. Kui siki inba to be torn, frayed, split, snapped, broken; siri torn, split (or < IA). Kuwi (F.) sipla a splinter, chip; (F.) hissali, (S.) hih’nai to split. Kur. cirna to divide (by rending, splitting, sawing) (or < IA). Br. citing to slit, slice, tear open (or < TA). /Cf. Skt. cira- rag (Turner, CDIAL, no. 4843), and ibid. no. 4844 *cirayati. DED(S, N) 2056. 2492 Konda sik- (-t-) to laugh, smile: sikpis- (-t-) to cause to laugh. Mand. hik- (-t-) to laugh. Kuwi (F.) sikhali to grin; (T.) patka sik- id.; (Isr.) sik- to bare the teeth; sikla one who laughs a lot and for a long time. DEDS 389.

Ta. ci an excl. of contempt, disgust, repudiation; n. disdain; ci-cci, ci-ci an excl. of contempt, abhorrence; ciy-enal onom. expr. signifying disgust; cicci fie!. Ma. ci id. Ka. ci fie! shame! Tu. ci, cici, chi, chichi fie! Te. ci fie! nonsense! DED 2143.

Te. cikati darkness, obscurity, gloom, dark; cijfkati twilight; ciku a blind person; blind. Kol. (Kin.) cikati darkness; (SR.) cikat dark. Pa, cikod (p1 cikocil) darkness, night. Ga. (Oll.) sikat, (S.2) cikat darkness. Go, (A.) sikati id.; (Tr.) sikati pitch darkness; QW.) sikati, (M.) ikay, (Ko.) ikar darkness; (Mu.) ikar, (S.) hikad darkness, night (Voc. 3379). Konda sikat(i) darkness. Cf. 2552 Ta. iravu. DED (S) 2144.
Ka. sigadi, sigudi a very smail kind of fish that is sold in a dried state. Tu. tikodé a kind of fish. DED 2145.

Ka. cikuru-vayicockroach. Te. cikuru- vayi, cikurayi bat, cricket; civuka-pitta a bat. DED(S) 2146. 2607 (2) Ta. ci-kkdy, ciya-kkay, cikai- kkay soap-pod wattle, Acacia concinna. Ma. cikka-kkayi, ciya-kkayi Mimosa ahstergens (the fruit serves for cleansing the hair after bathing). To. siex A. dealbata and melano- xylon [two Australian species, much. planted in the Nilgiris]. Ka. sige a climbing prickly shrub, the pods of which are used like soap for washing the hair, etc., A. concinna (the soap-nut tree is often called sige, but not correctly; it is antavala, etc.). Tu. sige, sige- kayi pods of M. abstergens reduced to fine powder and used as soap for cleansing, soap- nut. Te. si-kaya A. concinna; cikirénu, (B. also) cikirtni a certain medicinal tree, the leaves of which are ground and used as a soap. Konda siko soap-nut tree; (Sova dial.) sili mranu id. (b) Ta. ikai sp. of mimosa; tiger-stopper (Mysore thorn, Caesalpinia sepiaria}; ikkai tiger-stopper; sp. of stinking swallow-wort [Pentatropis microphylla]; itku sp. of sensi- tive-tree; tiger-stopper; inkai sp. of sensitive- tree, M. rubicaulis; sp. of stinking swallow- wort; fyai tiger-stopper; sp. of sensitive-tree. Ma, inna M. intsia, the bark of which is used in bathing; ifica, ifica, ififia A. intsia. (c) Ta. intu tiger-stopper; intu cight-pinnate soap-pod, A. intsia caesia; sensitive-plant, mimosa; M. rubicaulis; tiger-stopper; intai eight-pinnate soap-pod; trichotomous-flower- ing smooth jasmine; tiger-stopper; sp. of

Ta. ical whistle; iccu-kkofttu to whistle. Tu. isuly whistling. Te. ila, Ula a whistle; fla koffu to whistle. Kol, i-sa pod- id. Go. (Tr.) isri a whistle (Voe. 217); (W. Ph.) sisari id. (Voc. 3429); (ASu.) sisri id. Konda (BB) sisuri whistle. Pe. sisiri whistling. citu (227] cippu Kui sisori id.; sisori vika to whistle; (Mah., p. 129) hucuri whistle. Kuwi (I°.) sisori kiali, (S.) cicodi kinai to whistle; (Mah., p. 129) cicari whistle; (S.) cicinai to pipe. Cf. 2638 Ta. cir. DED(S, N) 456. ;

Ta. citu skein of thread = eight kufi- cam (k. = 120 threads of the warp). Ka. citaku a skein of thread. Te. cidu a skein which is seven times the quantity called punjamu (p. = a skein of sixty threads). DED 2148.
Ta. cifai small ball cakes of rice flour. Ma. cita a round cake or omelet. Ka. cide a sweetmeat in the form of a marble fried in ghee or oil. Te. jidiya, jide a sort of sweetmeat. DED 2149.

Ka. side, sede to become stiff as a limb, rope, etc., become erect from cold as the hair on the body. Kol si+d- (si-dt-) (part of body), gets numb. Pa. cid- (imb) becomes numb. 7? Go. sirum saiana to have pins and needles after a limb has gone to sleep. ? Kui (K.) dima numbness, loss of sensation in the limbs. DED(N) 2150.

Ka. sitar anger, wrath. Te. cidara disgust, dislike, abhorrence, trouble, vexation, annoyance. DED 2151. 2616 Ta, cittai want of character, lowness, badness; low, base person. Ma. citta lowness, badness. DED 2152,

Ta. intu date-palm, Phoenix dactyli- fera; dwarf wild date-palm, P. farinifera; ificu id.; wild date-palm, P. sylvestris; iccam- panai wild date-palm. Ma. intal P. farinifera (cirr-), Elate sylvestris (katt-); intu a cycas [i.e. sago-palm]; itta P. dactylifera. Ka, ical, icala, icale, icil wild date tree, P. sylvestris Lin. Tu. fcilu, ificilu the wild date; indu sago-palm, Caryota urens, Te. Tta, (SAN also) du the wild date tree; (B.) idadi, (SAN) fdadu the marshy date tree, P. or Elate paludosa. Pa. cind date-palm. Ga. (S.?) sindi id. Go. (Y.) sindi wild date-palm; (W.) chatli-sindi date tree; (Mu. Ma. §.) hindi wild date-palm; (M.) indi id., Phoenix acaulis; (Ko.) ite kata wild date-palm (Voce, 3395); (Ko.) @tel date-paim (Voc. 381); (ASu.) sindi id., P. dactylifera. Konda sitel dubu, sintel maranu date tree, P. sylvestris. Pe. hindi wild date-palm. Kui sita a small date bush. Kuwi (Su.) sindi, (Isr.) sindi marnu wild date-palm, Kur, kinda palm tree, date tree (= H. khajur) /Cf. Skt. hintala- the marshy date tree, Phoenix or Elate paludosa (Turner, CDIAL, no. 14093), saindhi- spirituous liquor; Pkt. sindi- date-palm, khajttrf. Cf. also Savara sindi-n a variety of date or toddy tree. [P. sylvestris Roxb. = EF. cs sylvestris Linn. P. dactylifera is an introduced species. The classification of the various Phoenix species in Hooker is only tentative.] DED(S) 459.

Ta. cintu (cinti-) to blow, as the nose; cintal mucous matter of the nose; cintu (cinti-) to blow the nose. Ma. cin- tuka, cirruka id. Ka, sin (sint-, sind-), sinu (sit-) to sneeze; #, sneezing, a sneeze. Tu. hintruni, himpuni, (B-K.) simpy to. blow the nose. Kor. (O.) cimpu to clear the nose. Te. cidu to blow the nose. Kol. (Pat., p. 135) sindeng id. Pa, tid- to clear the nose. Ga. (S.) cind- (c= ts) to sneeze; (P.S.3) sind- to blow the nose. Go. (Mu.) hind-, (Ma. Ko.) ind- id, (Voc. 3563). Konda sind- (it-) id. Kui srinda (srindi-), (K.) hind- id. Kuwi (F.) sindali, (T.) hind-, Csr.) sind- Cit-) id. DED(S, N) 2153. 2619 Pa, cipri leaf-cup. Go. (S.) hipi spoon (Voc, 3564). Pe. hipri leaf-cup. Kui sipori a leaf ladle or spoon. Kuwi (Isr.) sipri container made from leaf. DEDS(N) 412.

Go. (Tr.) sipi the part over the liver; (Ch.) sipi upper belly (Voc. 3419). Kuwi (Su.) hipa-daki, (P.) sipa-daki chest (cf. 2976 Kui daki breastbone, chest). ? Cf. 2532 Nk. (Ch.) ciparta dokka. DEDS 413. 2621 (a) Ta. cippu (cippi-) to suck. Ma. impuka to suck, lick, sip. Ko. ci-p- (ci-py-) to suck out at one draught; 7. ci-p. Ka. cipu, sipu to suck the substance out of anything, suck. Te. ciku, (K.) ciku to smack, suck, place (as fruit or the like) partly in the mouth and draw in the juice; (B.) cipu to make a sound with the lips; (Sank.) cibuku, (K. also) civuku to smack, suck. Kur. cipna (cippyas) to suck. Malt. cipe id. (6) Ta. cppu to suck, sip; cippi artificial nipple for a child to suck; cflmpu (cOmpi-) to suck, fondle with the lips; Gmpu (ampi-) to suck; umi (-v-, -nt-) id. Ma. Ompuka to suck, eat; umma a kiss; umikkuka to sip, suck. Ko. ub- (uby-) to suck penis. Ka, ummu a kiss. Kod, uet- (ueti-) to suck. Tu. jumbuni to suck, sip, lick; (B-K.) jumbu, jimbu to suck. Kor. (O.) jumbu id. ? Kol, (Pat., p. 155) Umburseng id. (for urs-, see 709). Pa, citk-, cltv- to suck. Ga, (S) cupk- (cupt-) to suck, smack. Go. (Tr. Ph.) suhkana, (Ma.) u?k- to suck (Voc. 3449); (Ph. W.) ut- id. (Voe, 312; cf. Kod. uet-). Konda silb- (-it-) id., kiss. Mand. jUb- to suck. Kui jimba (jambi-), japa (jat-) to suck, absorb; 7. suction, absorption. Kuwi (F.) jupali to suck, kiss. Kur. conkhna (cunkhyas), (Hahn) cuntkna to kiss. Malt. cumge to kiss; cumqrése to sound with the lips in calling anim

Ta. cippu bolt, wooden brace to a door driven into the ground when bolting, shutter of a sluice. Ma. cippu bolt pushed up from below; cirppu a bolt, bar. Ka. (Hav.) cima {228] ciru cipu wooden latch. Tu. cimpi, cimpu, cimpu a bolt, bar, latch; cipu a wedge, wooden peg, (B-K. also) bolt, latch. DED 2155.
Te. cima ant. Kol, siema, (SR.) sime id. Nk, Sima id. Konda sima id. Kuwi (F. Su.) sima, (P.) hima id. DED(S) 2156.

Ta. cir (-pp-, -tt-) to be excellent, be superior, be suitable, fitting (as an oppor- tunity), fall into rhythmic movement; 7. prosperity, wealth, beauty, gracefulness, goodness, greatness, excellence, superiority, paramount importance, esteem, regard, repu- tation, fame, nature, good normal condition, literal meaning, equilibrium, evenness, balance, measure, quantity, heaviness, time-measure, song, metrical foot, sound of musical instru- ments; cirmai greatness, excellence, eminence, reputation, renown, weight, moderateness, . decorum, smoothness, evenness, polish;cirppu excellence. Ma. cir good condition, stoutness, increase; cirkka to swell, become stout; cirppikka to increase; ciru good condition, luck. DED 2157.

Ta. ir, irppi nit. Ma. ir id., iruka to comb hairs. Ko. ci-r nit. To, tier id. Ka. ir, ipi, sir id.; ir to comb out nits; ir-anige, sir- anige comb for nits or Jice; (Hav.) ciguru nit. Kod. ci-ri id. Tu, tiru, (BRR) ciry, siry id.; tiruvana nit-pick. Te. iru, ipi nit; ir(u)cu to comb out nits; ir(u) pena comb for removing nits. Kol. six nit. Nk. Sir id. NK. (Ch.) sirku (pl) nits. Ga. (S.3) irs- (iris-) to comb. Go, (Ch. Ph. W.) sir (pl -k), (Mu.) hi, (S.) hir, ir nit; (Tr.) sir louse (sic) (Voe. 3422); (W. Ph.) cirni comb for removing nits (Voc. 1325); (Koya Su.) irs- to comb out nits. Pe. hir (i -ku) nit. Mand. hir (pl. -ke) id. Kui sireni, sireri comb. Kuwi (S.) hiru, (Su. P.) hiru (pl hirka) nit. Kur. cir id. DED(S, N) 2158. . 2626 Go. (Tr.) siz, (A.) sir, (Y. Ch. Ph.) sir, (D. G.) hir, (Mu.) hir, (S.) hir, ir, (Ma.) “ir root (Voc. 3423). Pe. cira id. Kui siru (pl. sirka) id. Kuwi (F.) hird, (S.) hiru, (Su. P.) hiru (pl. hirka) id. / Cf. Maithili sir fibrous roots of tree or plant, in Turner, CDIAL, no. 13421. DED(S) 2162.

Ka. cirana, cirna, jirna a small chisel, esp. used in cutting metals. Te. ciranamu a small chisel. / Cf. Mar. cirn® id. DED 2159. 2628 Kol. (SR.) sire, (Kin.) cire chironji tree. Nk. Sire id. Pa. cir chironji nut. Go. (Tr.) saréka chironji tree, Buchanania angustifolia Roxb. [better, B. latifolia Roxb.]; (Ch.) sagéka, (G. Mu.) réka chironji (Voc. 3342); (SR.) sitka mara id. (Voc. 3403); (ASu.) serka id. Pe. rtka mar B. latifolia. Mand. réko id. Kui sréko a species of forest tree. Kuwi (T.) r8ko B. latifolia. DED(S) 2160.

Ta. cirai cloth; cilai cloth, garment, woman’s cloth. Ma. cila, sila cloth. 7o. si-ly a cloak (in songs). Ka. sire cloth, garment in general (not sewed), female’s garment (not sewed), Tu. siré, siré female’s garment. Te. cira id., any cloth in general. Go. (Ko.) cila cloak (Voc. 1336); (Ko.) sile cloth (Voe. 3428). Kuwi (Isr.) sila paciya dhoti. / Ka. sire is said to be from Skt. cira-, but the sense is rather that of Skt. cela- clothes, garment; there has prob. been contamination between the two words. DED(S) 2161.

Pa. cit el bandicoot. Ga. (S.) cirel rat. Konda (BB, 1972) sirli a kind of rat. Pe. hirel orli bandicoot. Kuwi (Su.) hireli orli a kind of rat; (S.) dandi hireli bandicoot. Cf. 833 Ta. eli. DED(S) 2163. 2631 Konda sirba- to cry (pair-word with 282 arba-); arbadey sirbadey crying con- stantly. Pe. hirba- to call (intensive base only). Malt. cirqe to shout, make a noise. DEN 36A.
Ma. cila, sila bag, purse. Ko, ci+rm (obl. ci-rt-) bag. To, tierm (bl. ti-rt-) gunny sack, Ka, cira, cila, cilu bag, sack. Kod. ci-le bag. Tu. ciravu bag, purse, pocket. DED 2164.
Ka. cilamande ankle. Te. cilamanda id. DED 2165.

Ka. cila a word of calling to a cat, like pussy. Te. cili a pet name for a cat, puss, pussy. DED 2166. 2635 Kui sidi raw, unripe, green; sober; sidari something that is raw, unripe, green; a sober woman; sidanju a sober man. Kuwi (Su.) hili unripe; (F.) hili raw; (S.) hiili green. DEDS 415.
Ta. cival that which is loosely woven. Ma. cival thinness of cloth. DED 2167.

Ta. cir, cirkkai, cikkal whistle, whistling; cifti whistling, toy whistle. Ma. cil, cil a sound. Ka. sillu whistling (of men or birds); cil, sil a scream, a cry; (Tipt.) silpu whistling. Cf. 2610 Ta. ical. / Cf. Mar. Sil a whistle. DED(S, N) 2168.

Ta. ciru (ciri-) to hiss as a serpent, snort as a horse, sniff, be infuriated, swell with rage, roar and blaze forth as a flame, get angry with; 2. anger; cirram anger, fury; ciru-cir-enal onom. expr. signifying violent anger; cintu (cinti-) to be angry with, hiss (as a serpent). Ma. ciruka to hiss, puff as a ‘snake, foam, rage; cirikka to make to hiss, enrage; cirram rage; cirru a hiss, puffing; cirruka to hiss, rage. Ka. siru to grow en- raged, become angry, be very contentious or quarrelsome; 7. raging, fierceness; sivarisu, sivarisu to hiss, puff, pass the proper temper, show disgust or disdain; s@ru to rail at, treat with derision; ? sittu anger, rage. Kod. ci-r- cierl {229] cufu (ci+ri-) to grumble, answer back grumblingly. Tu, siruni to hiss; sitfu anger, displeasure. Te. (B.) ciru to hiss like a serpent. ? Pe. hic- (hice-) to threaten. Cf. 553 Ta. iral. DED (S, N) 2169. :

Ko. ci-rl (to rain) in a drizzle. To. si-r spray. Ka. siru to be scattered, be sprinkled, fly about; sir-pani spray or fine drops of water; sirumbul being scattered, sprinkled, etc. ? Te. (K.) cirumu to fling, throw. Go. (ChD.) jirit fountain (Voc. 1423; so correct Voe.); (ASu.) sigk- to sprinkle water. Kui sirpa (sirt-) to shake out, sprinkle; n. act of sprinkling, shaking out; ? sirsi giva to sprinkle. DED(S, N) 2170.
Ta. cini false hemp tree, Tetrameles nudiflora. Ma, (DCV) cini id. DEDS 416.

Ko. ciif+++ onom. of hot iron sizzling in water, of a burning corpse. Ka, cumyi, cuy, comyi the hissing of water on heated iron or fire. Tu. cufiyicufiyi hissing noise of a heated metal dipped into water. Te. cfiyi the hissing noise produced by water coming into contact with hot metal or other substance. Kur. choéchoérna to fizz, hiss (said of a fire or red-hot iron upon which water is thrown); cho&mo&® onom. imitating the sound of something roasting. DED 2171.
Ta. cukiyan a kind of sweet pastry ball. Ka. suggu rice parched, ground and mixed with jaggery and coconut. Te. sukiya a kind of sweet cake or bun. DED 2172.

Ka. sugi to tear off, strip off, plunder, peel; ugi to pull, draw away, rob, draw or tear off, draw (a sword), pull out. Kur coxnad (cokkhas) to pluck, cull (fruit, flower, useful leaves); coxrn@ (Jahn) to fall off as fruits, (Grignard) be plucked. Malt. coge (coq-) to pluck off, set free; cogre to drdép off, get free, be ransomed. DED(N) 2173.
Ta. cukir (-v-, -nt-) to card as cotton, part, separate (as hair, fibres), rub clean and smooth as a lute-string; clr (-v-, -nt-) to pick, separate. Ma, citruka to pick cotton. DED 2174.

Ta. cukkai star (< Te.) Ka. cukke small mark, dot; cikki, cikke spot, speck, dot (as of cat, leopard), star. Te. cukka star, spot, dot, drop. Kol sukka star. Nk. cukka (c= ts) id. Nk. (Ch.) cukkin id. Pa. cukka id. Ga. (S.2) cukka drop of rain; (S.3) sukka star. Go. (Tr.) sukkum, (G.) huko, (Mu.) hukka, hukkom, (S.) hukka, (Ma.) ukkum, ukka, (Ko.) ukam id. (Voc. 3432). Konda suka id. Pe. huka (pl. -q) id.; hukeran, hukerin (pl. only recorded) stars. Mand. hukerin id. Kui suka star. Kuwi (F.) hitka, (S. Su. P.) hukka id.; (D.) suk’erika stars. DED(S) 2175.

Ka. suggi the season of spring (from the middle of March to that of May), the season of gathering a crop of any kind (esp. the time of reaping the corn and grain), harvest, plenty, a feast. Tu. suggi the twelfth JTulu month (March-April), the second rice- crop, harvest. Go. (Hislop) sugge harvest (Voce. 3436). / Cf. Mar. sugi the period of coming to maturity or perfection, or of the abounding or prevailing (of the products of the earth, of articles of merchandise, etc.), season. DED(S) 2176.

Ta. cufku end of cloth left hanging out in dressing, pleat or fold of garment, silken tassel used in plaiting girls’ hair (< Te.). Ka. cutgu, jutigu end of a turban sticking out, a small part torn and hanging to the thing, a dangling tatter..Te. cufgulu the end(s) of a garment; (B.) cungu a skirt, the end of a cloth. Kol. (SR.) june cloth; (Kin.) jugne dhoti. DED 2177.

Ta. cufkutu trivial balance, trifling amount in arrears (< Te.). Ka. cufgadi pettiness, small money, change, a trifling or petty sum above the large one, odd (as in one hundred and odd). Tu. cungudi a trifling sum, odds, small coins, change, anything of little value. Te. cungudu small balance of money due. Cf. Ta. cukku small bit, fragment, piece, particle, worthless thing, cukkal small piece, bit. DED 2178.
Ka. cuficu a projecting ledge on a house. Te. cuncu-midde an overhanging tower or turret. DED 2179.

Ka. cuficu the hair curling round the forehead. Te. cuncu a small lock of hair left upon the crown of the head. Kol. (Kin.) cuncur crest of cock (Kamaleswaran). Pa. cucurka topknot. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) cuficua- Sekhara-. DED(S, N) 2180.

Ka. jufijuru state of being curled or tangled. Te. junjuru dishevelled hair.
