DED 2181. 2653 Kur. cunjnd to pound rice, beat with repeated vertical knocks; refl.-pass. cunjrna; cunjkd a clay-coated hole in the ground, used as a mortar for pounding rice in. Malt. cunje to clean rice by pounding. DED 2182.

Ta. cutu (cufuv-, cutf-) to be hot, burn; warm (¢r.), heat, burn up, roast, toast, bake, fry, cook in steam, burn as bricks in a kiln, cauterize, brand; 1, burning, heating, scalding; cufar light, brilliance, lustre, sun, sunshine, moon, planet, fire, burning lamp, flame, spark; cufal drops of burning oil falling from a lamp, charred end of a burning stick; cufalai, cufu-kafu burning-ground; ctu (cUti-) to brand (as cattle), cauterize; n. that which is heated, burnt, roasted, heat, warmth, feverishness, fomentation, hot temper, anger, brand, clffu that which is burnt or cooked, a kind of ola torch. Ma. cutuka to burn, be hot, feel hot; burn (¢#.), make hot, toast, roast, bake, boil; cufuvikka to get one to burn; cufar fire, brightness; cufala the burning- place in the southern corner of the com- pound, burning or burying place; cfu heat, burning; ciftu torch: cllfta top of a coconut cutfi [230] branch used asa torch. Ko. tur- (tuf-) to roast, bake (pots), burn (corpses); cur ga+r funesal burning-place; cu-r (obi. cu-f-) heat, a burn, spark thrown off by hammered iron; pair- word with pa-r hardship (pa-r cu+r). To. tur- (tuf-) to burn (#r.). Ka. sudu (suff-) to burn, roast, bake. fire (a gun); be consumed with fire, burn, feel hot, be roasted; 7. burn- ing, etc.; suduka who has burned; suduvike, - suduha burning; sudu-gadu cemetery; stidu burning, cauterization; site a torch of wisps, etc.; soda burning; sodar, sodaru, sodalu lamp; supfage, suntige roast meat, act of burning o

Ta. cutti tuft of hair. Ma. (Tiyya) juttu tuft of hair on shaven head. Ka. juffa, juttu, juflu the tuft of hair left on the crown of the head at the ceremony of tonsure, crest, tuft growing on an animal’s head; (Hav.) jottu hair-knot. Kod. jitti tuft of hair as worn by brahmans, etc., woman’s backhair. Tu. juttu tuft of hair on the head, cue. 7e. juffu long lock or tuft of hair, crest of a bird such as a peacock. Kol. (Br.) jufti tuft of hair. Go. (¥.) cutti, (Tr. Ph.) cutti, (V. Ch.) cutfig Ql.) hair (Voc, 1346); (S.) jutfi topknot, cock’s comb (Voc. 1434), (Su.) jutti tuft of hair. Kur. cufti hair (of man or animals), bristles, mane. / Cf. Skt. jti{a- twisted hair of ascetics and Siva (Turner, CDIAL, no. 5258), and Turner, CDIAL, no. 4883, esp. 3 *cdffa-; some of the items recorded here may be < IA. DED(S, N) 2184. cunanku 2656 Ta, cutti intelligent person; ctltfikkai ingenuity, sharpness, smartness; cllttukkaran witty person. Ma, cutti smartness, quickness. To. sutm cleverness, skill (pait-word with potm). Ka. sufi clever, able, powerful; stti smartness, quickness. Tu. cufi, sufi smart, clever. Te. slfi clearheadedness, method, orderliness. DED (N) 2185.

Ta. cutti small ornament worn by women and children on the forehead, white curt on the forehead of bull or cow, white spot on the head of beast or serpent, plate of gold worn on the forehead as a mark of distinction. Ma. cutti mark or jewel on the forehead; cutfu natural mark on the skin, e.g. of cows, children. Ka. cufti a kind of frontlet. Tu. citti a small spot or speck, spotted, speckled. DED 2186. +:

Ta. cuttu (cutfi-) to point out, show, designate, indicate, have in view, aim at, desire, think, consider, honour; n. indication, reference, that which is intended or desig- nated, honour. Ma. cuttu pointing; ctipfuka to point at, aim; cilnfal pointing out; cunfan- viral forefinger. Ka. suttu to point at with the finger; suftumbe the index finger; cUfi aim. Tu. cUfi id.; tinduni to aim, point at, ttndelu pointing, directing. Te. sUti aim; jutta-vrélu forefinger. ? Pa. futip- (iit) to show (or with 3566 Ta. tomru), Kui silfa (siti-) to point with the finger; sija vanju the index finger. Kuwi (f) hitali, (sr.) hif- (it-) to point out; (F.) hita vwanja forefinger. DED (S) 2187. 2659 (a) Ta. cunahku yellow spreading spots on the body of women (regarded as beautiful), sallow complexion as of a love- lorn woman, a spreading skin disease esp. of animals, pollen dust; cunam yellow spreading spots on the body of women; cunai (-pp-, -tt-) to itch, be inflamed, twinge, tingle; 7. pustules, prickle (as in leaves, stalks, etc.), down on fruits, itching, smarting, keenness, sharpness, shame, sense. Ma, cupanfu vari- egated surface, soft down as on young palm leaves, scales of fish, blotch, scab, spots on the skin, yellow or dark (considered as a beauty); cuna down on a fruit, sensitiveness; cunekka to have the skin irritated, be touchy. Kod. culli spot, mark; culli payyu spotted cow. Tu. cupnpai scab, scurf; tunnahgu, tunahgu, tunpai the scurf or mealy crust of the body, coniiké, conké, so

Ta. cupafiku (cunahki-) to be jaded, loiter, linger. Ka. junugu to shrink, contract, withdraw, retire; 1. state of being shrunk, a shrunk, crippled body. Te. judiigu, juntgu, (K. also) jundigu to lurk, slink, (K. also) shrink, withdraw. Cf. 2687 Ta. curunku. DED (S) 2189. cuntan 2661 Ta, cunfan grey musk shrew; cunt- eli, (Koll.) cunfin mouse, Mus urbanus; cUran grey musk shrew; mUfi-cOru musk-rat, Sorex indicus. Ma. cupt-eli mouse, musk-rat. Ka. sunda, sund-ili, sundil-ili, sond-ili, sondil- ili, cuiic-ili musk-rat. Kod. cing house- mouse, field-mouse. Tu. sund-eli musk-rat. Te. cuncu mouse, musk-rat; cunc-eluka, cund-eluka mouse; ctr-eluka species of mouse. Kol, (Kin.) cindrag musk-rat. Go. (Mu, Ma.) clict musk-rat (Voce, 1353); (Ma.) cutti-eli, (Mu.) cufi, cutiyal small rat (Voc. 1344); (Ph.) cote mouse (Voc. 1368). Konda (BB) susuki musk-rat. Kur. colto mouse. For eli, etc., see 833 Ta. eli. Cf. 2664 Ta. cuntu; the shrews seem to be differentiated from rats and mice by the length of the snout. /cf. Skt. sundi-musika-, gandha-sundini- (Burrow, Kratylos 15.56), cuficu-mfgika-, ehucchtndura-, cucundari-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5053. Cf. also Skt. tufuma- mouse, rat. For similar words in Munda languages, see Pinnow, p.95 (Santali cfind musk-rat, etc.), ang bmeneau, JAOS 82.109. DED(S, N)

Ta. cuntu (cunti-) to dry up, be evaporated by heat; boil (¢7.), stew, simmer; n, sediment, that which adheres to a pot when boiling. Ko. cund- (cundy-) to boil away (infr.); ?to starve oneself for one’s children’s sake. Ka. sundu to evaporate; sundisu to make evaporate, reduce in boiling. Te. cundu to be evaporated or dried up. / Cf. Skt. sunth- to become dry. DED(S) 2191.

Ta. cuntu (cunti-) to shoot with the thumb and finger (as a marble), flip a coin, tap with the thumb or finger, jerk (as reins), draw out an clastic body and let it recoil with a jerk; n. jerking, flipping; cuntu- vil toy bow for shooting stones or pellets. Ma. cuntu-villu a crossbow. Ko. cund- (cundy-) to test (coin) by flicking it up in the air and listening to the noise made by the thumbnail in flicking. Te. (B.) cundu to be flicked, as water from the finger. DED(S) 2192. 2664 Ta, cuntu, conju bill, beak, lower lip. Ma. cunfu a short beak or bill, lips, snout; cunfan pointed, pick-axe, blubber- lipped; tonna, tolla mouth. Ka. cundu a bird’s beak. Kor. (M.) julli beak. Nk. (Ch.) salla mouth, face. Pa. cond mouth, beak. Ga. (S.7) sollu, (6.3) sofdu) mouth; (P.) sondi beak. Go. (Ko.) sonde lip (Voc. 3494). Pe. condi beak of bird. Kui suda mouth, beak, brim. Kuwi (D.) hondi mouth. Kur. cold pointed (said of the mouth of animals). Cf. 2661 Ta. cuntan, 3296 Ta. tuti, and 3311 Ta. tunfam. DED(S) 2193.

Ta. cunfu and 3296 Ta. tuti. / Cf. Turner, CDJAL, no. 5853, tunda- beak, snout, trunk of an elephant; ibid., no. 12516, sunda- elephant’s trunk. DED(S) 2713, _ 3312 Ta. tuptan murderer. Ma, tuntan insolent. Ka. tuntu wickedness: tunta a mischievous, wicked, rascally, insolent, lewd man, a rogue; tunfi a wicked female; tunta- tana, tundutana wickedness, etc.; tundu mischief, rascality, etc.; tondu wild or over- bearing behaviour. Tw. tunta mischievous, impudent, wicked; tunftatana impudence, wickedness; tunfe a wicked or impudent man; tunfi, tuntedi a mischievous or im- pudent woman. Te. tunta, tuntari a wicked, dundigu mischievous, impudent fellow; wicked, mis- chievous, impudent; tuntatanamu wickedness and impudence; dundagamu wickedness, mischievousness, shamclessness; dundagidu a wicked, mischievous man, scamp. DED(S) 2714. 3313 Kea. dundigu Jatropha curcas Lin. Te. dundigamu J. glandulifera. DED 2715.

Ta. cuntai Solanum torvum; S. verbascifolium; Indian tree potato. Ma. cunta S. pubescens. Ko. cid mul S. indicum. Ka. sunde, sonde S. pubescens. Kod. cunde sp. Solanum. Tu. sormté a kind of solanum; oy sunde Indian currant tomato. DED(S) {231} uppu
Ta. cunni membrum virile. Ma. cunni penis. Ka. tuppi, tupne id. 7e. culla, cculli id. Kur. condd id. Cf. 2681 Pe. cuyi. DED 2195.

Te. utuku, uduku to wash (as clothes), drub, thrash. Kol, sutk- (sutukt-) to wash (clothes). Nk. sutk- id. Nk. (Ch.) sut-, sut(u)k- id. Go. (SR. Tr. Ph.) sukkand, (G. Mu.) huk-, (Ma. Ko.) ukk- id.; (M.) hukana to wash (Voc. 3431). DED(S) 2196.

Ta. cutti, cuttiyal small hammer. Ma. ‘cutti, cuttika, tutti hammer. Ka. suttige id. Tu. sutti, suttigé, suttiyé, sutté, suttyé id. Te, sutte id. Go. (A.) sutte id. (Voce. 3438). Kuwi (S.) suthi id. /Cf. Mar. sutki an instru- ment of stone-splitters. DED(S) 2197.

Ta. cuppal, cuppi twigs; uval, uvalai twigs and sprays, dried leaves. Ma. cappu leaf, esp. plantain leaf; tal green leaves, small branches, shrubs. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) tovli leaves. Ko. cop broth made of plants, plants used in making broth. To, tub leaves of tree; sup leaves (in song; < Badaga sappu). Ka. sappu, soppu, tappala, toppala, toppalu, toval all green leaves of plants in general, foliage, a herb, greens, covering, bark; tovaltu having put forth new leaves; sabbige, sabbe a slender branch of a tree or shrub; jabbalu a grassy ‘place. Kod. cappala leaf; toppi small leaves. Tu. coppu, soppu, tappu leaf, foliage, greens; tappoliyuni to be covered with foliage. DED (S, N) 2200. 2674 (a) Ta. uppuw salt, alkali, saltness; uppar salt manufacturers; uppalam salt- pans, saline land (cf. 299 alam salt-pan); uppalavan salt-maker; uvalakam, umpalam salt-pan; umanan salt-maker, dealer in salt; fem. umanatti. Ma. uppu salt, saltness, condi- ment; uppikka to be salty; uppalam salt marsh, Ko, up salt. To. up id. Ka. uppu id.; uppaliga man who belongs to a division of the salt-maker caste, the members of which are also fishermen; uppada salted and dried vegetables. Kod. uppi salt. Tu. uppu id.; uppadu pickles; uppuda salted fish; upparu brackishness. Te. uppu salt; uppana saltness, salt taste; uppani saltish, brackish; uppalamu salt-pan, salt-works. Kol sup salt. Nk. supp id. Nk. (Ch.} sup id. Pa. cup id. Ga. (Oll.) sup, (S.) cuppu id. (b) Ta. uvar (-pp-, -tt-) to taste saltish, be brac

Ta. cuma (-pp-, -nt-) to become heavy as debt or interest, be burdened; bear, support, carry a burden, take upon oneself, bear in the womb; (Tinn.) cema to carry; cummafu burden, load, load pad for head; cumatalai, cumaitalai burden, responsibility; cumati load, burden, abundance; cumattu (cumatti-) to burden, load, impose; cumai bearing, burden, load; cumaiyan porter; cumméatu load pad for head; cummai burden, charge; cimpuri coil of straw used for keeping steady a vessel, load, etc., as on the ground or head. Ma. cuma burden, load; cumakka to carry a burden; cumafu, cdtu burden, load; cumatala charge, duty; cumattuka to load, charge; cummal load; cummafu pad for head to carry burdens; cummuka to bear, carry. Ko, tam{ carrying pad. Ke. simbi, simbe ring of cloth to be put under a vessel, etc., upon the head; a coil for setting a vessel on the ground; the coil of a snake; sivadu, sivadi, sivudi, sivudu pad to be put under a load on the head; ? (Hav.) t6mpata carrying on the head (or with 3540 Go. tdc-). Tu. tumbuni, (B-K.) tumbu, sumbu, humbu to bear, carry as a load; tumbavuni to load; tumbonuni, tummonuni to bear or suffer, be patient. Bel. (LSB 2.1) simbi to carry. Te. cumma, cumpata carrying pad (pata cloth). Kol. (SR. Kin.) kunt- to lift; (W.) kut- (kutt-) to place burden on head. Vk. cumma (c = ts) pad on head for carrying. Pa. kumt- to carry on head; cumfal carrying pad on head. Ga. (Ol.) kumt- to carry on head; (P.) sumfal head-pad for carrying loads. Go. (Ph.) sum- m

Ta. cumma leisurely, without any occupation or work, in normal condition, in health, silently, quietly, in perfect peace and test, bare, without any reason, uselessly, vaguely, unintentionally, at random, as a joke, gratuitously, gratis, freely, unceremoniously, continuously, repeatedly. Ma. cumma without cause or emotion, gratis, quietly. Ka. summa, summage, summane quietly, silently, cause- lessly, without proper or just cause, without a particular cause, in an objectless manner, for nothing, uselessly, without the intention of getting labour done, without the intention of exacting remuneration; (PBh.) ummane silently. Kol. surmi silent; (Pat., p. 191) surum aneng to be silent. VA. surum silent; surmak a secret. Go. (Tr. SR.) cammake silent (Voc. 1287). DED(S) 2205. 2679 Kol sum- (sumt-) to catch, seize, buy. Nk. sum- id., lay. Vk. (Ch.) sum- to buy, catch, hold, Pa, cumm- to seize, catch hold of. DED(S) 2206.

Ko. ci-! life; air, blast of air from bellows; noise, small noise. Ka. suy, suyi, sly to breathe, sigh; m. breath, a sigh; suyil, suyilu, suylu, stly, stlu breath, a sigh. Tu. suyilu, suyyu, tuyily breath, respiration; sighing, a sigh;suyimpuni, suyimbuni, suyy uni to blow the nose, hiss, snort, bellow, fizz, grunt, etc. ? Pa, cuyk- to sob. Cf. 3512 Ta. toy. DED (S) 2207.
Pe. cuy (child’s) penis. Mand. hiy penis. ?Go. (ASu.) ctlcd id. Cf. 2666 Ta. eunni. DEDS(N) 419.
Ta. curam narrow and difficult path. Ma. curam difficult road, pass, ghaut. DED 2210.

Ta. curi (-v-, -nt-) to be spiral as conch, whirl round, eddy (as water), curl; Cpp-, -tt-) to wind spirally, whirl, curl, lie in a circle; ». whirling, spiral, curve, screw, white curl on the forehead of bulls; curiyal curling, curly hair, lock of hair, woman’s curi [233] hair; curul (curulv-, curunt-) to become coiled, roll, curl (as hair); n. rolling, roll, coil, curl, woman’s hair curled and tied up in dressing; curulal ringlet, coil; curulai roll; curuffu (curuffi-) to roll up, coil, curl, fold, twist; 1. curling, coiling, anything rolled up, cheroot; curuttai curly hair, curly-haired boy or girl; curunai anything rolled up; cur (-pp-, -tt-) to revolve, whirl round; ctrppu whirling, revolving, bracelet; ctral whirling as of wind. Ma, curiyal a round rattan. basket; curuftu a roll, cheroot, a sheaf; curul scroll, roll; curuluka to be rolled up, be curled; curutfuka to roll up (t7.). Ko. curp- (curd-) to Hie in coils (snake, rope); curf- (curty-) to coil, roll (t.). To, tu-| (tu-d-) to be rolled up (curl, leaf); tu-{- (tu-ty-) to roll up (¢7.), curl chair), tie up (hair); tusk hair curl; tu-r storm; su-t Cigar (< Ta. curutfu). Ka. suruli, surule, surali a coil, roll; surufu, suruntu to coil, roll up (intr.); surku, sukku to curl; surku, sukku, sufiku, sokku a curl. Kod. turid- (turind-) to be rolled up; turif- (turifi-) to roll up (¢7. ); tore a string that goes round; (torev-, torand-) (string) is wound round and round; tora- (torap-, torat-) to wind (string) round and ro

Ta. curi (-pp-, -tt-) to bore, perforate as in an ola leaf or book; xv. hole, aperture, perforation through the leaves of an ola book, instrument for boring ola leaf or book; curai hollowness, hollow interior, tubularity, cavity; a kind of sharp crowbar; clr (-v-, -nt-) to bore, pierce, scoop out; cllrvu piercing, boring. Ma. curi round hole pierced through olas to thread them, the instrument which makes the hole. Xa. (Bark.) suri to string, as flowers. Tu. (B-K.) suri to string, bore. DED (S, N) 2212.

Te. (TVB, Guntur dial.; comm. by K.) cllru, in: poga cttru smoke to coat walls, roof, etc. with soot (for poga, see 4240). Pa. curuk deposit of soot on the ceiling or on any object that has been near the fire. Ga, (P.) suygur soot; (S.2) pogasur id. (< Te.). Kui srdbi, (K.) sro’ id. Kuwi (Su.) curu-cur-enal ro’va, (F.) rd’ya, (S.) roowa id. DEDS(N) 420.

Ta. curufku (curuiki-) to shrink, contract, shrivel, wrinkle, lessen, dwindle, be reduced, curtailed, be puckered, creased, be furled, closed as flower, etc.; curunkal anything shrunk, wrinkled, wrinkle, crease; curukku (curukki-) to curtail, reduce, lessen, compress, contract, furl, close, epitomize; n. contraction, reduction, wrinkle, fold, crease, slipknot, noose, epitome; curukkam brevity, conciseness, abbreviation, epitome, shortness, contraction, decrease; cukku (cukki-) to get dry; curul (curulv-, curunt-) to shrivel, shrink (as leaf), droop (as from heat, hunger) be reduced to severe straits; curi (-v-, -nt-) to wrinkle; (-pp-, -tt-) to get dried, contract, shrink, wrinkle; draw in (as a tortoise its head). Ma. curunnuka to shrivel, shrink, be contracted, wrinkled, decrease; curukkam contraction, shortness; curukku sliding string, noose; curukkuka to contract (fr.), furl (sails), wrap, fold, reduce, shorten; cukkuka to grow dry, shrivel; cukku dry; cunnuka to grow dry or lean, be reduced; clluka to shrink, contract; clal shrinking; cofiku withered. Ko. curg- (curgy-) to stint oneself to support family; curk- (curky-) to make small, draw up (legs, fingers), shrink; cug- (cugy-) to be creased; corfy state of a hoe being worn short by much use. To. tuer- (tu+ty-) to shrink (itr); tu-L- (tu+d-) to pull legs close to body (e.g. while sleeping). Ka. surku, sukku to contract, shrivel, shrink, fade; surku, sukku, sufku, sokku contracting, shrivelling, shrinking, a wrinkle; so

Ta. curu-cur-enal expr. signifying twitching with pain, feeling hot in the bady; curu-cur-enal onom, expr. signifying stinging (as thorn). Ko. cur in-, cur cur in- (belly) curumpu [234] teels hungry, (heart) burns with guilt. Ka. curu a sound in imitation of the burning caused by the bite of an ant, the sting of a scorpion, the touch of fire, etc.; curu curu with violent smarting. Te. curram anu to feel a smarting, burning, or sharp pain or sensation. DED 2214. 2689 Ta, curumpu bee, drone; curumpi (-pp-, -tt-) to hum (as bees). Kol. surund (obi. surnd-) honeycomb; surunt potte bee; (Kin.) curund sp. bee; curund tatta honeycomb. Nk. surund bee, honey. Pa. curud, curdi (stem curd-) sp. bee. Go. sirwé-phuki bee which builds in hotlow boughs; (SR.) surve medium-sized beehive; (L.) hurrve honey; (Ma.) urve talla honeycomb; orve kind of bee; orve neyi honey; (Ko.) urve, uruve kind of bee (Voc. 3448), Kuwi (F.) hort! swarm of bees. DED(S) 2215.

Ta. curai calabash climber, Lagenaria vulgaris. Ma, cura pumpkin, water gourd. Ka. sore, sdre the bottle gourd, L. vulgaris, Kod. tore gourd. Tu. turé, suré water gourd, pumpkin. Te. (B.) sora, sorra, sdra bottle gourd, Cucurbita lagenaria, [L. vulgaris Seringe = C. lagenaria Linn.} DED 2216.

Go. (G.) curki, (Ph.) curki a small basket (Voc. 1351). Konda surki id. Pe. curki id. Kuwi (sr.) surki id. ? Ma. curiyal a round rattan basket. DEDS 421. 2692 Kur, jurjur’rna to feel heavy (as from bad digestion), feel out of sorts (as under the approach of fever), Malt. jirjura slowly, dimly, drowsily. /Cf. Mar. sursuri dullness and drowsiness which arise from eating plentifully of certain fruits or after a full and heavy meal (MBE 1969, p. 293, no. 22). DED 2217.

Ta. ulavu (ulavi-), ulavu (ulavi-/ulay-) to move about, walk or ride for recreation, take a jaunt, stroll or ramble, go in procession; ul procession; ulattu (ulitti-) to ride about, pass in procession; take out for a walk as a child, lead about as a horse; ». walking up and down for amusement, display or exercise, moving to and fro; culavu (culavi-) to revolve, move round, hover about; lantu to walk about, wander about; lattu to walk about, ride about; take out for a walk. Ma. ulavuka, ilavuka to take a walk; ulattuka to take for a walk (children, etc.). Pa. cul- to go round, wander about. DED(S) 2218.
Ta. cuvatu track, footstep, sign. Ma. cuvatu, cumafu, cdtu footmark, vestige, foot, base, step; cuvattuka to track, search after. Cf. 2387 Ta. cavattu. DED 2220.

Ta. cuval nape of the neck, upper part of the neck, back, mane; cekil upper part of curanku the shoulders. Ma. cumal shoulder. Ka, tegal shoulder. Kui sukori, sukoli shoulder-blade. Br. cugh nape of the neck. (cf. Bal. ug id.; Elfenbein, p. 26). DED(S, N) 2221.

Ta. cuvalai pipal; cuvi white fig, Ficus infectoria; stone fig, F. gibbosa parasitica. Ma. (Lush.) cuvann-al F. infectoria. Ka, juvvi mara wavy-leaved fig tree, F. infectoria, Te. juwi F. tsiela. Kol (Kin.) juvi pipal, F. religiosa. Pa. j meri sp. Ficus (prob. religiosa). Go. (Ko.) j® mara Ficus sp. (Voce. 1441). DED (S) 2222. 2698 (a) Ta. cugatku (cugahki-) to whirl, be tossed about; cugal (cugalv-, cuganr-) to whirl, spin, rotate, roll, turn on axis (as wheel), revolve in an orbit, roam, wander, be tossed about, driven to and fro, be agitated, troubled, distressed in mind, droop, faint, languish, be dizzy, swim as the eyes; 7. whirling, revolving, curl, eddy, etc.; curarci whirling, spinning, rotating, wandering to and fro, trouble, agitation; cugarral giddiness; cusarri handle as of spinning-wheel, fan; cugarru (curarri-) to whirl (¢r.), spin, swing round, turn, roll, brandish, flourish, wave, cause dizziness, make giddy; n. whirling, wheeling, spinning; curi (-v-, -nt-) to become curved, curled, involved, form eddies as on the surface of water, screw up one’s face in disgust, be cunning, guileful, be distracted, agitated; (-pp-, -tt-) to whirl as a whirlwind, form whirlpools or eddies, form curls; curl (fr.), curve, cause to roam, screw up one’s face in disgust; #1. whirling, vortex, eddy, curl, curved marks on head or body indicating one’s luck; curippu agitation; curiyal curling as of hair; cuziyan deceitful person; cugivu circumvention, anxiety; clr (v-, -nt-) to encom

Ta. cul, cullu pungency; cull-itu to prick, smart (as from pungency on the tongue), scorch (as the piercing heat of the sun), be stung to the quick; cull-enal expression of sternness, severity, or pungency. cullu-ccull- enal expr. signifying sharp pricking sensation. Ma, culuculé with sharp pain; culuculukka to feel hot, irritated by the touch of nettles, piercing pain. Ka. (Coorg) jullu to prick. DED (S) 2224.

Ta. culi (-v-, -nt-) to be angry; (-pp-, -tt-) to be angry with, show displeasure toward, dislike, feel pain; culivu anger; culukku (culukki-) to screw one’s face up in displeasure, frown. Ma. culiyuka to be wrinkled; culikka to be wrinkled, frown; culippu a frown, displeasure; culivu a wrinkle; culukku a frown, impatience; (Tiyya) culic- cilu wrinkle. DED(N) 2225.

Ta. culikku pikestaff, sharp-pointed stick carried by travellers. Ka. culike a stout stick to beat cotton with. DED 2226. 2702 ‘Ta. ulukku (ulukki-) to be sprained, dislocated; n. sprain, dislocation; culukku (culukki-) to be sprained, dislocated; ? ulai (-v-, -nt-) to ache or suffer pain in the limbs as from weariness, cold, rheumatism, suffer gtiping pain as with dysentery, suffer in mind, perish; (-pp-, -tt-) to afflict, torment, make sorrowful, dislike; ulaivu, ulaippu griping pain, intense pain. Ma. ulukkal, ulukku, ulukkam a sprain; ulukkuka to be dislocated; culukku sprain, wrench, cramp; culufhuka to be distorted, shrink, wrinkle; culuruka to distort, wrench; ulayuka to suffer a drag- ging pain. To. tik- (tiky-) to be sprained. Ka. ulaku to be sprained; ulaku, iluku cramp, sprain; uluku, ulku, uluku, ulku a sprain. Tu. ulku, ulku a sprain or cramp; ulkuni to be sprained or cramped. Te. iluku a sprain; (K. also) to be sprained. Go. (Koya Su.) ilk- to be sprained. Konda ilk- (-t-) (part of the body) to be sprained. DED(S, N) 2227.

Ta. culuvu ease, facility, lightness, cheapness. Ko. culv easy work, easy. Ka. suluvu facility, lightness; culaka, culuku lightness of temper or conduct, want of seriousness, levity. Tu. culaka light (not heavy), Te. culuka(n) lightly, with slight or disregard, easily; culukana lightness, easiness, disregard, slight, contempt; culukani light, easy, unimportant, insignificant; suluvu casy; easiness, facility. Kuwi (F.) sUlkara light (weight). /Ka. Te. suluvu (> Ta., Ko.) is said by Kitt. to be from Skt. sulabha-. DED (N) 2228. 2704 Ta, culai pulp as of jack-fruit. Ma. cula id., a layer. Ko, toyl seed with fleshy covering inside jack-fruit. Ka. tole a separate piece or division, one of the divisions in orange, jack-fruit, cotton-pod, etc.; (Hav.) sole pulp of jackfruit. Kor. (T.) sule, (M.) sola id. Te. tona a division in an orange or other similar fruit, a carpel; tola a carpel as of jack-fruit, etc. Kuwi (F.) tdla, (Isr.) to%la jack-fruit (edible parts). DED 2229,
Ta. cullan a kind of mosquito. Ka. solle mosquito. DEDS 424. culli [236] cuyru

Ta. culli dry twigs, esp. for fuel, small stick, branch. Ma. culli a dry spray, sprig, brushwood; cullal a chip, fuel stick, nulli small sticks for firewood. Ko, culk long pliable stick, stalk of plant. DED 2230.
Ta. (Koll.) culli a thorny plant. Ma. culli a thorny plant: Randia dumetorum, Tribulus lanuginosus or Barleria, Ruellia obovata. Tu. culli, tulli the plant of B. obo- vata. DED 2231.
Ka. sullu falsehood, untruth, fiction, a lie. Tu. sullu.a lie, falsehood, fib; false, untrue, DED 2232.

Ta. cullai potter’s kiln, furnace; ctlai kiln, furnace, funeral pile. Ma. culla potter’s furnace; cUla brick-kiln. /Cf. Skt. culli- fireplace, Pkt. (DNM) culli-, ulli- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4879. DED 2233.

Ka. sur, sura the sound produced when supping liquids; suri to drink with a supping noise; suruku the sound produced by supping, a sup; juru juru the sound pro- duced when a child sucks with force; nu Kuruba, LSB 4.12) juri to sip. Tu. surasurané drinking liquids with a sipping noise. Te. jurra a sup of liquid or semiliquid food; jurru to drink, sip or sup with a noise, asa liquid or semi-liquid food. Nk. curpip- to suck, Konda zuri (-t-) to suck up (any liquid food with a noise). Kuwi (Ist.) surpu re?- (rec-) to suck in, slurp, eat food noisily; (Isr. etc.) juru gruel. Kur. surUpna (surpyas) to drink with a noisy sucking of the lips; sur@khsur'khma to draw one’s breath con- vulsively, sob. / Cf. Pali suru-suru-karakam after the manner of making hissing sounds when eating (MBE 1969, p. 293, no. 23). DED(S, N) 2235.

Ta. curukku quickness, rapidity, diligence, briskness, haste, hastiness, irritable- ness, severity, sharpness, keenness, pungency, poignancy, high price, demand as in market; curukkan active, diligent person; hasty, irritable person; curucuru (-pp-, -tt-) to be in a hurry, bevery active; curucuruppu diligence, bustling, hurrying; curuti swiftness, alacrity. Ma. curukku sharpness, quickness, zeal; curukkan a keen fellow; curucuruppu alacrity and dispatch. Ko. curk fleetness of foot, diligence, hardworking; active, strong (of chillies). Ka. curuku, curaku, curku excessive heat of fire, great bodily heat, quickness, speed, pain, sharpness of a knife, etc., keen- ness, smartness, dearness in price. Kod. curiki speed. Tu. curuku quickness, haste, speed, sharpness, zeal, activity; quick, vigor- ous, zealous, vivid. Te. curuku activity, briskness, agility, alacrity, quickness, intelli- gence, smartness, hotness, sharpness, sharp pain, smart, burning sensation or pain; sharp, hot; curukkuna sharply, so as to cause a. burning or sharp pain. DED 2236.

Ta. curu-cur-enal, curir-enal, curir- enal onom. expr. signifying hissing (as water when in contact with fire); curu-curu (-pp-, -tt-) to hiss (as dry combustibles when ignited); curu-cur-enal expr. signifying rapid burning (as of dry combustibles); cukru, cukr-enal, cukkr-enal onom, expr. of rustling, as of palmyra leaves, spreading fire, etc. Ko. curcurn boiling hot (of water or bath); cur cur in- (meat) makes noise when roasting; cur in- (fire) makes noise of burning. Ka. curu curu the hissing or sputtering of boiling water, the spitting and sputtering of heated oil or ghee or of a thing frying; curcu, cuccu to burn, consume with fire; curcu burning. Tu. curucuru a hissing noise caused by frying, burning etc. Te. curacura, curruna with the crackling noise of fire. Kuwi (S.) sorro sorro in- to hiss (snake). /MBE 1969, p. 293, no. 24 (misprinted 28), for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 13498, Pkt. surasura- whizz, etc. DED(S) 2237.

Ta. curru for Ta. curru-murrum, To. tiit mutm, Ka. sutta mutta, Tu. suttamutta. DED(S) 4118. 5019 Nk, murmuri parched rice. Go, (SR.) murre fried gram; (F-H.) mureng puffed rice; (Tr.) murré roasted gram (Voc. 2917); (ASu.) muré parched gram. Kui muri parched rice. DEDS 804. 5020(a) Ta. mun in front, previous, prior; antiquity, eminence; sign of the locative; munpu former time, front, antiquity; bodily strength, greatness; before, in front of, formerly; munpan powerful man, leader, master; munr-il front of a house, space; munnam in front; munnar before, in advance, in front of, in former times; munnavan the first being, Siva, elder brother; munnaval elder sister; goddess of misfortune; munnu (munpi-) to meet, reach, join, precede; munné before, in the presence of; munnai former times, antiquity; elder sister, elder brother; munnor predecessors, ancestors, the ancients, chief ministers; munndn Ganesa, god, predecessor, ancestor, father, elder brother; munatu that which is in front, that which is earlier; munai front, face, appear- ance, superiority, eminence, point, sharpened end, edge, cape, headland; munaifiar comman- der of an army; munaivan God, as the first being, saint, chief, Arhat, the Buddha; murrai formerly, before; muttai front; munti front, outer edge of cloth; some time before; muntu (munti-) to come in front, advance, meet, be prior in time or place, take precedence, take mun [453] mua the lead, be first, surpass, excel, be old, of long standing; . antiquity, pri

Ta. cunai mountain pool or spring, tank, reservoir, pasture ground with tanks and shady trees; cunaivu rock water. ? To. sonp swampy place where water stands. Ka. done, done a small natural pond in rocks, a hollow, hole. Te. (B.) dona a pool on a hill. /? Cf. Skt. (Hem. Un.) cundhi- small pond, Pkt. cundhi- natural pool; Pali sondi- a natural tank in a rock; Skt. cunfi- well (see Mayrhofer s.v.). DED(S) 2239.

Ma. cuna, cina juice as of mango stalk after the fruit is broken off; cunekka to ooze out, be acrid. Ka. sone the watery juice that exudes from the stalk of the mango fruit and of the fruit of the marking-nut tree. Tu. tuné, (B-K. also) tune, sune gummy juice exuding from the stalk, etc. of certain fruits. Te. sona juice that exudes from plants. Cf. 2711 Kur. curra. DED(S, N) 2240.

Ta. c sound uttered in setting on dogs. Ka, ct! id. Tu. cu, cil id. Kol cue id. Br. chuh (= cu?] call to a dog. /Cf. H. Mar. cn sound uttered in setting on dogs. DED 2719 Kur. clignad to harrow. Malt. clige to poke, stir; cigure to poke, pick (as one’s teeth or ear). DED 2243, 2720 Kur. ctgul forefinger. Malt. cigréde, cugreye to point out with the finger (ede to show). DED 2244.

Ta. ciitu (cUfi-) to wear, esp. on the head, bear, sustain; be invested as with a title, be crowned, spread, cover, become bent or warped, surround, envelop; #. hair tuft, crest, comb; cltfu (cffi-) to adorn as with garlands, crown, invest with honours, spread over (as army), extend one’s sway; #. invest- ing, adorning as with crown, garland, crest, cock’s comb, snake’s head, felloe of wheel. Ma, cittuka to wear on the head, wear over the head as an umbrella; cOfal putting on the head, carrying an umbrella; cltikka to make to wear, crown; cltfuka to put on the head, tie a bundle like a hairlock; cflttu cock’s comb, mark on the forehead of cattle. To. tu-¢ front tuft of hair. Ka. stdu to put on the head as flowers, etc., receive, obtain. Kod. cu-d (cu-di) to stick (flower) in hair. Pa. ctind- to tie hair in knot; cfta woman’s hair tied in knot. Ga, (P.) sund- to rol! up and tie hair in bunch; (S.3) sife a type of men’s hair style. / Cf. Skt. ctida- hair tuft, cock’s sores Turner, CDIAL, no. 4883. DED(S)

Ta. Ogu (ifi-) to sulk, show dis- pleasure; ital sulking; ufal (ufalv-, ufanr-) to be enraged, quarrel, fight; ufaru (ufari-) to be enraged at; ufarru (ufarri-) to provoke, infuriate. Te. stdu enmity, pique, spite; an enemy; sUdukadu an enemy; uduku to be annoyed, vexed, or pained, feel jealous or envious; udikil()u to feel hurt or annoyed; udikincu to annoy or vex (one) for amuse- ment. DEDS(N) 425.

Ta. ctitu bundle of sheaves. Ma. cUttu bundle, sheaf as of transplanted rice. Ka, stdu a bundle as of grass, etc.; sidi a small bundle of grass. Tu. cdi a coil of rope, bundle of faggots; sUdi sheaf, bundle. Kuwi (Isr.) hii barga stick for carrying sheaves. DED 2248.

Ta. cUttu anus, buttocks, private parts, pudendum muliebre; cOru anus. Ma. cUttu testicles, penis. Kui suti female urinal passage. /Cf. Skt. ciita-, cifti-, cyuti- anus; Turner, CDJAL, no. 4860. DED 2249.

Ta. citr Cpp-, -tt-) to frighten, be cruel; ”. fear, suffering, affliction, sorrow, disease, cruelty, malignant deity, celestial maidens; cUrppu a cruel, ferocious deed. Ma. cut fiend, affliction, disgust. DED(S} 50.
Ta. clr pungency. Ma. clr bad smell. DEDS 426.
Ta. cttral common rattan, Calamus rotang. Ma. ciral rattan. Kod. tu+ra a cane. Tu. strolu, tOrolu a kind of rattan, C. rotang. DED 2251.
Tu. (B-K.) siri ladle-like spoon. ? Go, (SR. Ch. Ph.) sukkur, (Tr. Ch.) sukkur, QW.) sukur, (Mu.) hukkuy, (M.) hukur, (Ma.) ukkuri ladle, spoon (Voc. 3433; or with 2388 Ka. savatu). DEN 37.

Ka. cilru, sir, siru the part of a sloping roof that projects a little beyond the clrai [238] cUrai walls, eaves. Tu. (B-K.) stiri eave line (the border of a roof). Te. citru eaves, the edge of a roof, cornice; munjilru eaves. Pa. mufiir id. Konda (BB) munzum id. Cf. 5338 Ko. va-cr. DED 2252.

Ta. cirai, cttral oblique-leaved jujube, Zizyphus oenoplia. Ka. (Lush.) suri-mullu id. Tu. tiri-mullu id. Pa. citra Z. rugosa. Go. (Tr.) slre-mara the churni tree, Z. rugosa; (M) tiretonde id. (Voc. 3460); (ASu.) stiré oblique-leaved jujube, Z. oenoplia, DED 2253. 2731 Kui sUruri, siirori the shin. Kuwi (Su.) hisanani, (F.) hdsalari, (Isr.) httsanari shin- bone; (Mah. D.) sttsnaddi shin. DEDS 427.

Ta. ctl (cillv-, ciinr-) to become pregnant; . conception, pregnancy, egg, wateriness of clouds; ctili pregnant woman, cavul pregnancy of cows, mares, etc, Ma. cll pregnancy. Ka. cil, stil id.; -cal, in: coccal first pregnancy (for coc-, see 3516). Tu. sulagitti midwife. Te. cilu pregnancy, child, offspring; cili child, offspring; cUlatu a pregnant woman; cildi pregnancy. Kol. su-la, pair-word for ba-la: ba-la su-+la children (Kamaleswaran). Ga. (S.°) tal pregnancy (of animals). Kuwi (F.) stl pregnant (animal). DED(S, N) 2255. 2734 Ta, cil (cUlv-, cllnr-) to scoop, dig out, pierce, cut off; ciiral, ctirral, ctinral scoop- ing, digging out. Ma. clluka to make a deep and boring cut as into melons and jackfruits (cimnu nokkuka to taste such); pluck out. DED(N) 2256.

Ta. cig (-v-, -nt-) to deliberate, con- sider, conspire, select, know; , deliberation, counsel, investigation; ctigeci deliberation, consultation, wisdom; clfci counsel, detiber- ation; (-pp-, -tt-) to understand thoroughly, investigate carefully; cilftiyan minister; tg (-pp-, -tt-) to think; Urku (drki-) to meditate. Ka, ctpu seeing, sight, look, vision. Tu. tUpini, hUpini to see, look, observe, test, examine, visit, seek, consider, regard; tUpa- vuni to show; stke perception, cognition, apprehension; cUti, ciitu perception; (B-K.) tila, stla, hla to see. Kor. (M.) cll- id. Te. clcu (cld-; imperative cl, cummu) to see, observe, behold, look at, view; ctidiki vision; cpu, cOpincu to show, point at; cilpu sight, vision, look, glance; cumi, ctidi take care! Pa. cily- to see. Ga. (OU) sitr-, (S.) clid-, (S.3) stid- id. Go. (A. Y.) stir-, (Tr. M.) hurana, (Ph.) hurana, (W.) hirana, (G. Mu. S.) har-, (Ma. Ko.) dr- to see; caus. (ChD.) huysutana to show: (W.) hirsahtanad to reveal; (Ph. W.) sural blind (Voc. 3462): (Pat.) hupsanad to show (Voc. 3571). Konda sur- to see. Pe. hur- (-t-) id. Mand. hur- id. Kui stra (s@ri-) id.; n. act of seeing, sight; (pl. action) strka (srki-). DED(S) 2257.

Ta. Ug (-v-, -nt-) to grow old; (pp-, -tt-) to grow old, pass the prime of life; n. that which is of long date, karma, fruit of karma, established usage, time, turn, occasion, maturity, end, sun (cf. enr-Ur summer, sun- shine, sun, s.v. 869 Ta. enru sun); Uzttal opportunity, seasonable time; Ugmai estab- lished rule; gi end of the world, very long time, lifetime, world, fate, regular order; Uziyan one who lives to a great age; God, who will live through the final destruction of the world; ketal Or evil fate. Ma, tukkaran undertaker of a lottery; tram turn of duty, turn; Uri earth, world; Ufu time, turn. Ka. sir time, turn, season. DED 2258.
Ta. cUri, cOgiyal supporting stakes in wall or the eaves of a house. Ma. cilrika the wall-plate that supports the cross-beams of a roof. DED 2259.

Ta. cil (ctlp-, 7) to take an oath, vow; 1. vow, oath, malediction, curse; cill- uru to swear, take oath, take a vow. Ma. cugaruka to swear; curarcca ascertaining the truth by oath or ordeal; (Kaut.) ctf oath. Ka, sttrul (siirult-) to swear, utter a solemn declaration; #. an oath. Tu. stthu swearing. DED (N) 2260.
Ta. ciilam a whistle. Ma. cllla id. ? To. titly- (tiilc-) to twitter. DED(S) 2261.
Ma. cUli scales of fish, skin of a jackfruit kernel; tli id., husk. Tu. cuguli rind of a fruit or vegetable; coli, sti, (B-K. also) cilli skin, bark, rind. Cf. 2751 Ta. cekil. DEDS 428.

Ta. cilai prostitute. Ma. cilla, (Tiyya) cUlacci harlot; ctlan whoremonger. Ko, cu-] woman who has sexual intercourse with men of all castes, prostitute. Ka. stle harlot, prostitute. Kod. cu-le dancing-girl. Tu. stile whore, harlot. /Cf. Skt. $014 harlot, prosti- tute. DED(N) 2262. 2742 Kol. su-l- (su-It-) to get up, wake up; su-Ip- (su-lupt-) to,make to get up; (SR.) sull-, sul- to get up; sulp- to lift. VA. sill- to tise. Nk. (Ch.) suy- to be raised; sup- to raisc, lift. Pa. culp- (cult-) to rise, get up; cuntip- (cunfit-) to raise. Ga. (OllL.) silp- (silt-) to rise; sindup- (sindut-) to make to rise, lift; (S.) cil-, cind- to get up, rise (as pain); silk- to wake up, rise (as pain). DED (S) 2263.

Ta. cUsavali, cUyavari, clgai whirl- wind. Te. surakaruvali, (B.) suragali id. Kol, (Kin.) curagali id. Vk. suraghali id. Go. (Mu.) hura matya id. (mafya id.); (Ma.) ura-vari, (Ko.) urgal id. (Voce. 3572); (Koya Su.) stirgati id. Konda sura-gali id. Pe. huza-vani id. Kui sUjambatuli id. Kuwi (T., Kasipur) rua-gali, (S.) ruwa gali id. Cf. 2715 Ta. curru. For gali, ghali, sec 1499 Ka. gal(i). DED(S, N) 2209, and from DED 2238. 2744 Ta, cUrai robbery, dacoity, pillage. To. su-ry loot. Ka. sUre plundering, pillaging, ransacking, plunder, spoil. Ju. sttre plunder- sii [239] sidi ing, ransacking, pillaging. Te. cla plunder, loot. Kuwi (Mah.) juria dacoit. DED(S) 2264. 2745 Kol. (Kin.) sttdi stale (of cooked tice). Konda (BB) sUR- (food) to become stale, sour. Kuwi (Mah., p. 164) suhnadi stale. DEDS 429. 2746 Konda stRi leech. Pe. htci id. Kuwi (Su. P.) hutta, (F.) hta, (S.) hutta, hdci id. DEDS 430.

Ta. ce-, cem- straight; ceh-kol scepter; ceficam, ceficu correctness, directness; cep- pam straightness, correctness, exactness, smoothness, uprightness, impartiality, even- ness, equity, repair, renewal, preparedness, fitness, straight path, road; cemmal greatness, excellence, superiority, power, haughtiness, great person as king, god, hero; cemm-a to be right, proper, be haughty, superior, be over- joyed, be majestic in bearing; cemmappu exultation, pride, majestic bearing; cemmai goodness, soundness, good condition, spot- lessness, uprightness, directness, rectitude, fairness, impartiality, agreement, excellence, greatness, fineness, neatness, beauty; ceyya correct, perfect, sound; ceyyan just, impartial person; cevvanam rightly, properly, correctly; cevyi season, occasion, juncture, mature condition, newness, beauty, gracefulness, state, condition, propriety; cevvu straightness, correctness, rectitude, soundness, direction; cevvé tightly, correctly, directly, straightly, perpendicularly, straight up; cefi-cev@ pro- perly, directly; cevvai correctness, fitness, accuracy, straightness, evenness, smoothness, sound condition as of mind, body. Ma cemmu _ straightness, advance, prosperity; cemmé straightly, well; cevva correctness; cevvu, covvu propricty, straightness. Ka. say, sey, cey straightness, downrightness, rectitude, propricty, merit, beauty; sayta man of rectitude; saytu straightness, rectitude, propriety; sayte straightness, rectitude; sayda man of sectitude, honest

Ta. cakkai chips, small wooden peg; (Tinn.) cake rind of fruits. Ko. cek chip. Ka. cakke, cekke, sakke, sekke id. Tu. cakke, cekké, cekki chip, split, splinter; ? ceppé splinter, chip, slice, paring. Te. cekku to pare, cut the side or rind of, sharpen (pencil), engtave, carve; n. the rind or parings of fruits, etc.; cekka piece, chip, slice; cakku-c@yu to chop, cut to pieces, mince. Kol, sek- (sekt-) to make pointed (piece of wood); (Kin.) cekka, (P.) sekka bark of tree; ? (Br.) cergu to chip off (tr.). Nk. Ssekk- to chip, scrape; Sekka bark of tree. Nk. (Ch.) sek- to plough. Pa, cekk- to chip, scrape, plane; cekka piece, slice, chip of wood. Go, (Pat.) cekkadna to cut (Voc. 1357); (SR.) cekka piece (Voc. 1356); (Ko.) eke piece, peel, rind (Voc. 329); (S.) ceppa split wood (Voe. 1360); (A.) cakka bark (Voc. 1274). Konda sek (-t-) to plane, fashion things out of wood. ? Kui sespa (sest-) to scrape, plane, shave a stick or piece of wood; n. planing, scraping; sesa shavings. Kuwi (F.) sekali to scrape (with a hoe); (Isr.) seka piece. Kur. cakta, cakti a slice: cakta’ana to cut in slices; cegla chip, splinter. Malt, caka a slice, cagje to chop up (as meat). / ? Cf. Skt. Sakala- piece, fragment. DED (S) 2266.

Ka. (Bell.; U.P.U.) sekke country wine. Kui saka pith of sago palm, heart of sago palm. Kuwi (F.) hekka mrant palm tree (toddy); (S.) hekka sago; (Isr.) héka marnu toddy palm; h@ka tuka palm fibre. DEDS 350. 2750 Pe, jeki, jiki the small stalk on which a grain of paddy hangs. Mand. jakehin (p/.) id. ? Kui réga (pl. -nga) id. Kuwi (P.) jeka head of rice; (F.) jengt (pf. jekana) ear of paddy; (Isr.) jéfigu (pl jéka) head of rice. DEDS 431. 2751 Ta, cekil skin or rind of fruit, fish- scales. Kur, (Mirdha dial., BB 1958) cegalo bark of tree. Malt. ceglo shell of fruit. Cf.

Te. cehgata(n) near, close by; cefgali nearness, neighbourhood. Pa. cekkal near. Kur, cax@ around, near, in the vicinity of. DEDS 434. 2754 Ta, ceccai resting-place with roof of foliage. Ma. cecca a house of leaves. DED 2267. 2755 Ka, sedaku, sedavu pride, haughtiness, arrogance; sede to grow puffed up or proud. Tu. sedavu pride, haughtiness, affection, coquetry. DEDS 435.
Ma. setikka to be angry, frown, disrelish, be tired; ceftippu loathing. Kod. cedi, (Mercara dialect) codi anger. DED 2269.

Ka. sidi to be scattered, fly about, be spattered as mud, etc., burst forth spon- taneously as seed from an overripe fruit, move ceti [240] cempu quickly from sidé to side as muscles, as the head from pride, start, bounce; n. state of being scattered, etc.; an iron spring trap; sidil to be scattered in different directions, become loose; sidisu to cause to fly about, Tu, sedi, tedi sprinkling; sedipini, Seddyuni to sprinkle with fingers, as water, etc. DED 2270.

Ta. cefi light, splendour. Ka. sidil shaft of lightning, thunderbolt; (Hav.) sedilu thunder; (Kumta; U-P.U.) cedlu id. Kod. tédi thunder; tédi minn- to lighten. Tu. tedilu, sedilu thunder, thunderbolt, thunder- clap; *tedily-mefici flash of lighning. Kor, (T.) cedli thunder. ?Ga. (P.) jitki lightning. Malt. canrke id.; canrkeye to flash (as lightning). / Cf. Skt. tadit- lightning. DED(S) 2271.

Ta. cefi bad odour, stench; that which is decayed, faded, spoiled. Ma. ceti dirty, as ground, dung; cetikka to be dirty. Ko. ceyy, cet, cet ceyy bad smell. To. sidy id. Tu, settuni to decay, be spoiled; Settavuni to spoil, destroy, ruin; Ssetti, Seffé spoiled, ruined; tettuni to rot, decay, be damaged, fail; tef{@vuni to cause to be damaged, cor- rupted, or failed; teftu, settu, heftu bad, rotten; sedi strong, affecting the smell power- fully. Te. cedu to be rotten; cedda, cedu rotten, Konda (BB) send- to decay. From DED(S) 1614.

Ta. cetil a mechanism consisting of a standing post with a long sweep at its top on one end of which a person under a vow is suspended by a hook fastened into the integuments of his back and, raised high in the air, is swung round. Ka. sidi an iron hook; the hook machine (as in Ta.). Tu. sedi (as in Ta.). Te. sidi a hook or goad; (B. also) the hook machine (as in Ta.). DED 2272. 2762 Kur. cerna to raise a burden to one’s shoulders, undertake, suffer. Malt. cede to convey, carry. 2763 Ko, gugrcety pigeon (for gugr-, sce 1930; lit. the bird that says gugr). Jo. kutur siti--» cooing of pigeons (for kufur, see 1667; lit. O bird that says kutur). Aa. cetti hen-sparrow. Te. jitta the generic name of several birds [B. includes many warblers, babblers, wagtails, etc.]. /? Cf. Skt. cafaka- sparrow; Turner, CDIAL, no. 4571. DED 2273.
Ta. cettai wing, feather, plumage, fin, shoulderblade. Te. cettupa, cattupa wing. Cf. 2303 Kol. satfa. DED(N) 2274.

Ta. centu bali used in game, ball of thread, etc. Ma. cenfu a cotton ball, tassel. Ko. cend ball used in games, ball on man’s hair-cord. To. sed ball. Ka. cendu, candu, sendu a play ball. Kod. cendi ball. Tu. cendu id. Te. cendu ball to play with, cluster o flowers, bouquet. Kol. (Pat., p. 75) zendu a ball. Go. (Y.) cendu ball (< Te. or Mar.; Voe. 1358). Konda sendu id. / Cf. Mar. cédt id. DED (S) 2275.
Ta. cenfai a kind of large drum. Ma. centa id., kettledrum. Ka, (Bark.) cande a kind of drum. Kor. (M.) candi id. Tu. cendé a large drum. DED 2276.
Ta. cetil, cetil fish-scale, outer bark of trees; cital fish-scales. Ma, cetumpu, cetum- pal, cettal scales of fish. DED(S) 2277.
Ta. cetu Cpp-, -tt-) to lose lustre, shrink, be weak; cetukku that which is faded, dried as flowers; cetuval withering. Ma. cetukku rottenness; cetukkikka to be rotten. DED 2278.

Ta. cettai dry rubbish, dried vegetable matter as grass, leaves, etc., hay, straw; cettal dry, over-ripe coconut on the tree, shrivelled palmyra or other fruit, dried chillies, plantain trees, vegetables or grass; cetuku rubbish, chaff, dried leaves. Ma. cetta, cerra little sticks or leaves for kindling a fire or covering a hut, what is made of it, screen or wicket of wicker work. Ka. satte, sade, sedage rubbish, dirt, stuff, trash. Tu. ‘sed’ sweeping, rubbish, Kor. (M.) sada rubbish. Te. cetta id., dirt, trash, refuse; cedaramu trash. DED 2279.

Ta. ceppu casket, little box of metal, ivory or wood, a kind of water-vessel, toy utensil. Ma. ceppu a small round box of any material to hold jewelry, metal cover of the breasts, treasure. Kod. ceppi small round metal box with lid. DED 2280.

Ta. cempu copper, gold, metal vessel, liquid measure; cempan brown-coloured cow or bull; tampikai a kind of small water-pot. Ma. cempu copper, copper vessel. ? Ko. keby, keb-gindy globular metal drinking-vessel with spout. To. teb copper; t6(b) bity copper ring (in songs); ?k6b small brass vessel. Ka. cambu, cembu, combu copper, globular copper or glass vessel used for drinking water; cambige, tambige, tambuge globular copper or glass vessel. Kod. cembi copper, small metal pot. Tu. cembu brass, copper, small copper or brass pot; tambigée small, round, metal vessel. Te. cembu goblet, pitcher, ewer; tambuga a sort of drinking-vessel. / Cf. Mar. cab a metal vessel with a belly and a ey (241] cel tapering neck, a goglet. Are the Dr. words influenced by Skt. tamra-, Pkt. tamba- copper? DED(S) 2282.

Ta. ey, ey-ppanri porcupine. Ma. eyyan, eyyam-panni id., hedgehog. Ka. ey, eyyi, eyyu, du porcupine. Kod. eppandi id. Ju, eyi pafji id. Te, edu, &du-pandi id., hedgehog. Kol. (Kin.) sd porcupine. Nk $d id. Pa. c&dir id. Ga. (P.) stdel id. Go. (S.) &d padi id. (ed < Te.; Voc. 383). ? Malt. citru id. Cf. 2852 Tu. oyikkane. /Cf. Skt. sedha- hedgehog, porcupine; Turner, CDIAL, no. 12766(3). DED(S) 2283. 2777 Ka, seragu calamity, evil, mischief, sin, crime. Te. seragu calamity, misfortune. DED 2284.

Ta. ceruku (ceruki-) to insert, slide into; cerumu (cerumi-) to sink, pierce through; coruku (coruki-) to put in, insert, tuck in; coruvu a sheath. Ma. cenifuka to shove in, put in; corukuka to tuck in, shove in; teruk: kuka to tuck, gather up the cloth. To. teg- (tegy-) to fasten loin-cloth. Ka. serku, sekku to shove in, put in, insert, tuck (the "end of a garment) into another (part of the garment); sekke insertion; cuccu, curcu to cause to enter into, put upon as upon a roasting spit, insert. 7, cuccuni to pierce, bore. Te. cekku to set (as a precious stone), thrust, tuck up; (K. B.) ceruvu to insert, stick in; coccu (cor-) to enter, penetrate, pierce; begin (or cf, 2876(a) Kol. so-ng-); coccillu, cottencu to enter; con(u)pu to insert, introduce, thrust, put or send in; coppincu to insert; cordbadu to enter or rush into, intrude, trespass, en- croach; coraébaru to enter or rush into; corava entrance, introduction, commencement, be- ginning. "Go. (Mu.) harj- to fix, fit in (tr.) (Voc. 3527). Kur. xerma (xirryas) to intro- duce lengthwise by gradual pushing, insert, stick into or behind. Malt. qere to thrust in, tuck in. DED(S, N) 2285. — 2779 Kur. cerd yesterday; cerontd, certt of yesterday. Malt. cewr yesterday; cewti yesterday’s. Kuwi (sr.) héro?’ni_ two days ago. ? Konda sir, in: sir narye just early in the morning (or with 2553 Kol. sid: for narge, see 3621). DEDS(N) 438. 2780 Ka, (Hav.) jeppu to beat. Kor. (T.) jerpi to thrash; (M.) jappu to beat.

Ta. cel (celv-, cenr-) to go, flow, pass, occur, pass (as coin), be suitable, acceptable, pass away as time, disappear as anger, perish, die; m. career, course, payment of debt, period that has elapsed; celavu going, passing, running, flowing, manner or mode of walk, pace (of horse), journey, expedition, way, passage, route, street, conduct, behaviour, expense, charges, provisions needed for consumption, separation, departure, death, permission, leave; celuttu (celutti-) to cause to go or proceed, dispatch, circulate, deliver, discharge (as a missile), drive, impel, push forward, execute (as orders), administer (as justice), perform, observe, pay as tribute or debt; calikai excessive familiarity, intimacy, indulgent treatment. Ma. celka to pass through, enter upon, pass on, pass, be current, valid, cost, be required; celavu income, expense; celavan spendthrift, expensive per- son; fem. celavi; celuttuka to put in, drive in, make to pass on, pay, perform (as prom- ise); cellikka to make to pass in or on. Ko. cal- (cad-) (prayer) is acceptable to god, (coin) is genuine, (wife) is suitable mate; calc- {calc-) to pay (debt, vow), inform; calv, cely money for expenses, articles for sale, articles given by Kotas for Toda funeral. To. sal- (sad-) to belong to (e.g. a person’s property, a person to a clan, inherited property, etc.); salf expenses, necessities (for family, funeral, etc.); ? talc- (tal€-) to cause to be well known (to a person), inform, Ka. sal (sand-) to enter, engage

Te. celavi corner of the mouth. Go. (Tr.) silwi, (W.) silvi, (Ch.) silvi, (A. Y.) sivli, (G. Mu.) hilvi, (Mu.) hivil, (Ma.) ilvi, (MaS.) (h)ilvi, irvi, (KoB.) iruy lip (Voce. 3415); (ASu.) sivli id. DED (S) 2287.

Ta. cilatan male companion, associate, servant; cilati confidante, female servant or companion. Te. celi female friend or com- panion, woman; celiya woman; celimi friend- ship; celimari male or female friend or com- panion; celikadu friend, companion; celikatte female companion; celli, celliya, cel()iyalu, celle, cellelu younger sister. Kol. (Syed Khaja Mahboob Husain, Social Service and Tribal Welfare in Hyderabad, 1949) sellay (? = sefle) id. Pa. catal (pl. calacil) sister. Ga. (Oll.) selal (pi. setasil), (S.) cellal (pl. cellasil) id. Go. (Tr.) s@lar/s@lag (pl selahk), (W. Ph.) selar sister, younger sister; (M.) helar sister; (Mu.) haar (pl. hélahk), (Ma.) Glar (pl Giask) younger sister (Voc. 3486); (ASu.) sélar (i. sélak) id.; (LuS.) eldé a sister. DED(S) 2288.
Ma. celucela with a frizzling, tinkling noise. Kod. jelis onom, of jingle of money. DED 2289.
Ka. sele spring, fountain-head. Te. sela, selayéru waterfall, cascade (for éru, ef. 5159); jela a spring of water. Cf. 2569 Ta. cilucilu. DED (S) 2290.

Ta. celvam, celvu wealth, prosperity, beauty, enjoyment, pleasure, happiness; celvan wealthy man, lord; celvi wealthy woman, lady of rank, Laksmi; celvikkai luxury, affluence; cellam opulence, prosperity, private treasury (as of a king); cellan wealthy person. Ma. cellam wealth, Ka, calu, caluve, caluvu, calva, calvu, caluvike, calvike, celuva, celuvu, celva, celvu, celuvike, celvike beauty, hand- someness, elegance, prace, propriety, niceness; caluyatana beauty, charming, coquettish be- haviour; calumi, caluvi, caluvati, caluve, calvi, celvi, celve handsome woman, Tu. celuviké beauty, comeliness; celuve handsome man. Te. celuvamu, celuvu beauty, loveliness; celuva a beautiful woman, a woman. DED 2291.

Ta. cellam, cella-ppetti metallic box for keeping betel leaves and areca nut. Ma. callam, calla-ppetti small brass box containing money and betel; cellam copper vessel, as ‘for keeping or burying treasure. DEDS 439.
Ta. cellal freshwater fish, Etrophus maculatus. Te, jella a fish resembling the dragonet. DED 2292.

Ta. cezi (-pp-, -tt-) to thrive, flourish, grow well as vegetation, prosper (as kingdom, family, country), be fertile, be supcrabundant, be cheerful as countenance; (-v-, -nt-) to grow, increase; cegicci flourishing; cerippu, cerimpu, ceguttu, cerumpal fertility, flourishing con- dition, prosperity, plenteousness, abundance, fullness; cerumai flourishing condition, great- ness, excellence, splendour, beauty, graceful- ness, verdure. Ma, ceru fine, stout; cerikka to be fertile; cerippu fertility. ? Kui si bride. ? Kur, arxa-céxel the vegetable kingdom, plants in general (for arxd, see 59); (Hahn) céxél greens. ? Malt. ceglu small branches. (For Kur. and Malt., Pfeiffer.) DED(N) 2293. 2790 Ko, cel long, round stick of fairly large size. Ka, sele twig, small branch, stick, tod, also one for training or punishing chil- dren; vb. to cane with a stick; callu, celu, cellu, sellu a long flexible twig or rod. Kod. jale long thin pliable stick; (Gjalev-, jaland-) to sway like a long pliable stick; jala- (jalap-, jalat-) to make sway. Tu, cilé, sé a fishing- rod. Te. (B.) sela twig. DED (S) 2294. 2791 Ka, sele to draw, pull, pull off, rob, pull about, (also sale) rush forward with impetuosity (as the water of a stream or river); 7. pulling, pulling off, robbing, force of a stream. Tu. selé force. Te. (K.) celuku to pull out (as the eyes). DED 2295.

Ta. cellu flea, tick; tel, tellu, telku flea. Ma. cellu flea, tick, a large beetle on coconut trees, palm-worm, wood-worm. Ko. cek flea. To. tot id. Kod. celli id. DED 2296. 2793 Pe, hez- (hest-) to be caught, en- tangled (e.g. in snare). Kui sehpa (seht-) to be entangled, caught and held fast, trapped. Kuwi (S.) herh’nai to implicate, ensnare; (T.) her- (t-) to be caught in snare; (Isr.) hervi- (-t-) to be caught, be trapped. DEDS 440.
Te. ceraku, in: vantaceraku firewood for cooking (for vanta, see 5329). Go. (Mu.) herk a bundle of firewood (Voc. 3586). DEN 38.
Ka. ceruku sugarcane. Te. ceraku id. Kol. saragurak id. Nk. serak id. Konda seroki id. Kuwi (P.) srk id. DED(S) 2297.

Ko. cerngl corner of a cloak or piece of cloth. To. tergy corner of a garment. Ka. serafigu, seragu, sarafgu, saragu either end of a piece of cotton or silk cloth used as a garment, the loose end of a garment, that end which is put over the shoulder or head, the ornamental coloured cross stripes near each of the ends; cover, protection, refuge. gn eaten tet a ERS Tere eens tm ns ta cératta [243] stde Tu. serahgu the end or edge of a female’s garment. Te. cerdgu the skirt, hem, end or corner of a garment or cloth; direction, quarter; cehgu skirt, border, edge (of a cloth). DED 2298,

Ma. cératta, tératfa centipede, milli- pede. Ka. jari centipede, scolopendra; jirale id.; the worm Julus cornifex, a kind of earwig; cockroach; jirili an insect that is hurtful to cloth; jirle a kind of earwig; cock- roach; (Hav.) jerale cockroach; (Bell.; U.P.U.) girle id.; (Kumt.; U.P.U.) girale id. Tu. téran{é, (B-K.) c@rafe, cdrate, strate, strante centipede, millipede. Te. jeri, jerri centipede. Pa, jerri, (S.) cerri id. Go. (Ko.) jer (pl -i) id. (Voce. 1444); (Koya Su.) jerru id.; ? (Tr.) tiril the large orange-coloured centipede; a caterpillar which crawls swiftly and bites; (A.) tiril, (Ch.) tiril centipede (Voc. 1732). Konda seri id. DED(S, N) 2299. _ 2798 Ta. Enal ear of corn. Te. ennu, vennu id, Kol. (Kin.) cen head of paddy. Nk. gen (pl. Senkul) head of jowar. Pa. cen head of ‘paddy. Ga. (S.) cennu, (P.) sen ear of corn or paddy. Go. (A.) Sen head of paddy; (SR.) sefik corn (pi); (Tr.) san head of jowar; (Mu.) han head of paddy; (Pat.) hennu ear of wheat or jowari; (Ma.) enn(i) head of grain; (Ko.) en ear of corn (Voc. 3469). Konda seren (pl. sereku) id. Kur, (Mirdha dial., BB 1958) hessu cannu head of rice. Malt. canu pod. Cf.

Ta. céku solidity, hardness; c&kam hard core of a tree, central part of exogenous plant hardened by age, heartwood. Ka. ctgu, cége heart or core of a tree, essence, strength. Te. c&éga, ctva essence, core, pith, substance, strength, courage. DED 2302. 2803 7a. cékku breast milk; c&kkai woman’s breast. Ka. c&pu gush or spurt as of milk. Kod. (K. from Kod. informant) ce-pu (milk) to secrete. Te. c&pu, (K. also) cépu the breasts to become full with milk, the milk to be secreted, flow, rush or spring forth; let the milk flow; 1. the becoming full with milk (of breasts), the secretion or flowing forth of milk. Ga, (S.3) s@mpap- to become ready to be milked. DED 2303.

Ta. @hkal asthma in children. Ma. Siinuka to breathe with difficulty, breathe audibly, sigh; inal breathing with difficulty; ékkal asthma; &kkam id., hard breathing. Tu. sénikuni to pant, breathe quickly or heavily; stike, stfikelu panting, breathing quickly. ? Pe. h@bga (< hég-b-) to whisper, Kui stke panting, gasping for breath; s&ke s®ke inba to be out of breath, pant; stke vahpa to pant. Kuwi (S.) stke stke nénjinai to pant (for nénjinai see 3765). DEDS(N) 443, and from DED 3120.
Ka. (Hav.) jégku to hesitate. Tu. jefikuni to delay, be deferred; hesitate, waver; jeéhkavuni to detain, stop; delay, put off.

Ma. c®tia glutinous earth put on walls to keep off the rain, esp. red. Ka. j@di a sort of pipeclay. Tu. jédi, s®di glutinous clay, chalk, a kind of white earth. / Cf. Mar. sedi, Sedu, sidt pipeclay. DED 2305.

Ta. cén distance, height, width, length, long time; c®fci distance, remoteness; céy, c®ymai distance, remoteness, length; céyttu that which is distant, long; c@yan, céyOn one who is at a distance. Ma, cén breadth, height, strength. Go. (Mu.) c@m palat far away (Voc. 1364); (Mu. Ma. M.) j@k, (Ko.) jtka, jéke far, distant (Voc. 1445). Kui seko distant, far, far away. Kuwi.(Su.) hekko distant; (F.) hego far; (S.) heggo, heo id.; heotasi a man who is distant; (Mah.) hek, heko far. ? Kur. geccha distant, far off. ? Malt. gece, geci far. DED(S) 2306.
Ta. c®ntiravar weavers. Ka. jada, jéda a weaver belonging to the Lifgavanta sect; spider. Tu. jade, jadye weaver; spider. Te. jéndra, déndra a caste of weavers. Cf. 2475 ‘Ta. catiyan. DED 2307.
Ka. sede fatigue, weariness, distress. Te, stda, (B. also) sada fatigue, weariness. / 2? < Pali seda- sweat; Krishnamurti, Language 39. 563. DED(S) 2308. sédya [244] cr
Ka. s@dya cultivation, farming. Te. sédyamu id, DED 2309.

Ta. céntu (cénti-) to draw, as a rope running over a pulley; éntal irrigation tank. Ka, s@du, séndu to draw up (water from a well), pull in (as string of kite), draw in with the mouth, draw in with the breath (snuff, etc.), draw or smoke (pipe or cheroot, etc.), pull along, drag; sédu drawing, etc. Kod. se-d- (se-di-) to drink a deep draught. Tu. séduni to draw water. Te. c@du, (K.) c@du to draw up as water from a well, draw or pull as a string; céda, (K.) c@da bucket; c&-tradu rope used in drawing up water from a well with a small bucket or vessel. Malt. cénde to remove in small quantities from a heap. DED (S) 2310. *2813 Ta. céy son, child; juvenility, youth. Ma. cévala child at the breast (2). Tu. jévu child, lad, youth; jovu id., baby, female child; (Bright and Ramanujan, Brahmin dial. jévu, jovu girl); jokulu children; (B-K.) jéru pariah child. Pa. c&pal (pl. cépkul) boy, lad. Ga. (OU.) sépal (pl s@pakif) id.; (S.2) sapal boy. ? Kur. jSxas lad, youth; servant. DED (S,N) 2311.

Ta. cér (-v-, -nt-) to become united, incorporated, joined together, become mixed, blended, have connexion with, be in close friendship or union, fit, suit, be collected, aggregated, join, associate with, be in contact with, belong to; (-pp-, -tt-) to join, attach, admix, admit to one’s society, add, insert, gather, assemble; cérkkai collecting, gather- ing, combining, mixing, compound, fellow- ship, company, union; cértti combining, union, fellowship, suitability, fitness, resem- blance, equality; c®rntar dependants, persons under one’s protection, partisans, relatives; cérppu place, residence, abode, seashore, mixture, supplement; cérpu residence, house; cérvu arriving, joining, residence, town, village, union, junction, connexion, collection; cérvai fellowship, association, union, mixture, compound, collection, assemblage; cfra alto- gether, wholly; along with, in company with. Ma. c®ruka to approach, come close, join, belong to, fit, suit; céra close, over against; cérikka to have collected; c®rumanam assem- blage as for a riot, party; cérkka to make to arrive or join, collect as men, money; c@reca adherence, union, harmony, fitness; cérppu joining, mortising, assemblage; cérppikka to have assembled or collected; cérvva mixture. Ko. ceer- (ce+d-), ce+d- (ce-dy-) to arrive, join, gather (intr.); ce-t- (ce+ty-) to make to gather or enter a place; ce-rek a-l, fem. cesreka«c people between whom verbal taboos do not operate (including people of opposite sex who may matry). To.

Ta. @ plough, plough and team of oxen, yoke of oxen; cér id. (Jaffna). Ma. &r a yoke of oxen, plough with draught oxen. Ko, e+r pair of bullocks used in ploughing. To. e+x plough. Ka, @ru, ar pair of oxen yoked to a plough. Te. @ru plough with draught oxen made ready for ploughing. Kol. (Kin.) c&r plough and team of bullocks. Pa, cereyakul (pl.) pair of bullocks; irer two pairs of bullocks; muccer three pairs of bullocks; nel cer four pairs of bullocks; c8jer five pairs of bullocks. Go. (Tr. Ph. SR.) str, (Mu.) hér a plough; (MuE.) hér one pair of bullocks; (Ma.) @r plough and team (Voc. 3481). Konda séru yoke of oxen. Pe. hér set of plough and bullocks. Kui séru a yoke of oxen, a pair, two of cattle for ploughing; streka two, a pair, a yoke of oxen. Kuivi (F.) hert (pL hérka) plough; (Su.) h@ru TN CC LC AL TT Rn elt nt RR SIE "OT TT” rarreencrcinmeenpettiin nant We rene onerous risarenuitnsiticsenn, serene penjera (pl. hérka) pair of ploughing bullocks. DED (S) 2313.

Te. pen-jera a species of rock-snake; jerri-potu whipsnake; (quoted in [Gondi] DGG, p. 233) jerti goddu a kind of snake. Kol, (Kin.) jérigag (presumably jeérigad) sp. snake (H. dhaman). Pa. jéri id. Go. (A.) séri, (Tr.) sargoda, (Ch.) sargodal, (Mu.) hergodal the rat-snake, dhaman; (Ma.) er(e)godali a kind of snake (Voc. 3484; cf. Mu. godal dhaman snake, Voc. 1191). Cf. 2011 Ta. c&rai. DED (S, N) 2314. 2817 Kui strki the back of the neck. Kuwi (F.) hecki, (Isr. Su. P.) herki neck; (S.) herki shoulder. DED 2316.

Ta. c@tval male of birds and fowls (excepting peacock), cock, male swan, kite; stallion; c@, cé-ma bull; (Tinn.) cavalu a cock. Ma, céval, caval cock, male of birds. Malt. cawge cock-bird. DED(S) 2317, 2819 Te, eralu husband’s brother’s wife. Nk. (Ch.} serutra husband’s younger brother’s wife. Go. strand, serndu, harndu, ervond, erond (ctc., various dial.) spouse’s younger brother (or spouse’s younger sister’s husband); fem, serandal, serandar, serndar, harndar (etc.,; various dial.) spouses’ younger sister (Voc, 3472); (Tr.) striyar, striyat elder brother’s wife; (Y.) sereyar husband’s brother’s wife (Voe. 3485). Konda séron husband’s younger brother. Mand. h@jun wife’s younger brother. Kui sejenju. husband’s younger brother. DEDS 444. 2820 Konda stri koru cock. Kuwi (T.) sehi male; (T. Mah.) sehi neh’uri male dog.
Pa. ctna frost, ice. Kuwi (Mah.) henna hoar-frost. DEDS 445. ‘ 2824 Konda stna much. Pe. h@ni many. ? Te. (modern; K.) sana a lot. DEDS(N) 446.
Pe. jen back. Kuwi (Su.) jénu, (P.) jeo, (S.) jénu (loc. jéco) id. (Possibly < *pjtn < piran; cf. 4205 Ta. pin, esp. forms with -r-, Ta. pirakku, etc.) DEDS 447, [245] cokucu
