Tu. tabbilu, tabbuli an orphan; low, vulgar, weak, infirm, cow- ardly, timid, DED 2502. 3102 Ka, talapu, talupu to reach, come to hand, be received, arrive; talapisu to cause to reach, Tu, talapuni to arrive, reach. DED 2528.

Ta. talai head, top, end, tip, hair; talaippu beginning, ending, end or edge of cloth; talaimai leadership, pre-eminence; talaivan chief, headman, lord; fem. talaivi. Ma. tala head, top, point, extremity; talakkam, talappu top of tree; talati, talatu top of tree, point, tip. Ko. tal head, top, above, superior; talp end (of stick, branch, rope, etc.). To. tal head, end, edge; tas superior division of ti- dairy, tasm top; tast which is on top; talp tip (of stick, horn, penis); ta8-tin platform (tin) against inner wall of house, where vessels, etc., are kept; tesamf- (tesamt-) to perform child’s hair-cutting and naming ceremony (lit. to prepare head; for -amf-, see 162). Ka, tale, tala head, being uppermost or principal. Kod. tale end; ? talami hair of head or body; t. kale a single hair (kale a weed; see 1373). Tu. tare head, top, hair of head. Te. tala head, hair of the head; top, end, front; place, side, quarter. Kol. tal head. Nk. tal (pl -kul) id.; (pL -Sil) honeycomb. Nk. (Ch.} tal head. Pa, tel id.; honeycomb. Ga, (OIL) tal, (S.) tallu (pl talkil) head. Go. (A. Y. G. Mu.) talla (pl -y), (Tr.) tald (pi. -hk), (W.) tala, (S.) talla, (M. Ko.) tala id. (Voc. 1688); (Tr.) talla (pl tallang) the part of the comb in which honey is stored; (Ma.)} talla honeycomb (Voc. 1689). Konda tala tallana head. Kui tlau (pl. tlaka) head, hair of head; tlamberi, (K.) tlameri hair of head (cf. 4707). Kuwi (F.) thrayt, (S.) trayu, (Su, P. Isr.) trayu (pl, traka) head; (D.) tala hair. Malt. tali

Ta. tallu (talli-) to beat, crush. Ma. tallu a blow, stroke, beating; talluka to strike, beat; tallikka to cause to beat. Tu. dalliyuni to slap, beat. Te. (Merolu) talgu to strike. Go. (Ko.) talg- to strike, hit, hit the mark (Voc. 1686). Cf. 3130 Ka. talisu; the Tu. Te. Go. items with 1 in the two entries are uncertain of assignment as between *1 and *1; meaning and stem formation have been followed, but such items as Te. Go. talg- still present diffi- culty. DED(S) 2532. 3106 7a. tava much, intensely. To. tof In- (id-) to be perfect. Ka. tave abundantly, greatly, wholly, completely, exceedingly. DEDS 508.

To. tavahku (tavafki-) to be hin- dered, impeded, be in distress, be dejected; tavakkam impediment, hindrance, destitution, delay; tavakku sense of shame; tavatikam sorrow, grief. Tu. tamaké delay, procrasti- nation. Te. tamaku to hesitate, draw back; (K. B. also) fail, recede. DED 2533. 3108 Ta, tavanai limited time, fixed term for payment of a due or instalment, period of revenue collection, esp. of land tax. Ma. tavana a fixed time or term, an instalment. DED 2534.

Ta. tavay {-v-, -nt-) to creep, crawl (as infants, lizards, snakes), leap and flow (as waves), extend, traverse, spread on all sides; tival (tivalv-, tivant-) to move as on the ground. Ma. taviruka to creep along. To, tof- (tof8-) to go on all fours. Ka. tevalu to creep along on the ground. Te, temalu to set out, stir, get ready to go, go away, (K. also) slip, slide, DED(S) 2535.
Ta. tavalai animals of the frog and toad variety; tappalai, (loc.) tavakkalai frog; (Tinn.) tavaka, (Ag.) tavakkey id. Ma. tavala frog, chiefly in the grass. DED(N) 2536.

Ta. tavittu-kkoyya, — tavittu-ccefi, tavittu-pparam hill guava, Rhodomyrtus tom- entosa, Ko. tavt id. To. tatit id. Ka, tavute- gida gooseberry bush of the Nilgiris, red myrtle, R. tomentosa. DED 2538.

Ta. tavir (-v-, -nt-) to abstain, refrain, cease, become extinct, stay, abide, subside, abate, leave, separate from, forsake, shun, avoid, omit, renounce, give up, cease from; (-pp-, -tt-) to put away, remove, dispel, chase away, expel, exclude, discontinue, hinder, restrain; tavircci abiding, staying, interruption, cessation, break; tavira except. Ma. taviruka to be put aside; tavirkka to exclude, omit. Ka, (HavS.) tevru to drive away. Tu. tauruni to remove. DED 2539. 3114 Ma, tazayuka to be worn out, rubbed (as a rope), ground (as a knife), be habituated, practised; tarakkam practice, use; tarakkuka to exercise, habituate, break in; tarekka to rub down, grind (as sandal). Ka. (Hav.) tale to be worn out, rubbed; to rub (fr.). Te tarepuni to grind, tub, try, assay (metal); tareyuni, tarevuni to be rubbed off, abrade, wear away, become thin, wasted; tarelu worn out; (D.N.S. Bhat, p. 13) talepuna to rub. DED(N) 2541. - 3115 Ta, tayal (taralv-, taranr-) to glow, be very hot, burn, shine; vn. fire, live coals, embers; tagali fire; tagarci heat, glow; tanal live coals, embers, fire. Ka. tanalu glowing coals. Te. t(r)ampi fire pit, heap of cowdung cakes used as fuel; tanuku to burn (intr). Kol. tari- (tarit-) (fire) burns; tarp- (tarapt-) to light (fire). Wk. tar- to catch fire; tarp- to set fire to, kindle. Pa. tar-, tarv- to be hot; tarkip- (tarkit-) to heat; taruran hot. Ga. (P.) kis tarkamul burning coals. Go. (Tr.) tarmi a glowing piece of wood ash; (G. Ma. S.) tarmi, (Ko.) ta

Ta. tazu embracing; taguval a handful of ears of grain; taruvu (taguvi-) to clasp, embrace, hug, entwine; adopt as an opinion or course of life, treat kindly, make friend- ship, surround, compress, contain, keep within oneself, mix with, join, copulate; n. embracing, clasping, armful; tarfu em- bracing, uniting, women’s dance with clasped hands; tagal embrace, union. Ma, tarukuka to embrace, also of sexual embrace, hold fast. Ko. dab- (daby-) to put arms around; take- (takc-) to carry in arms under the cloak; tak lap, act of sitting on lap under cloak; ka-l tek id- (if-) to sit with feet crossed, throw in wrestling by hooking opponent’s leg with one’s own. To. teskwil- (tesvkwid-) to hug (cf. 2151 Ta. kol); telk, selk crook or (ne nario enamerergurnntena mint rte tent ANNONA etn eee tn perpen unt snettteesiesnE ISR am arn nt er nwa et eaten nae menmnenpe tre tarutanal (271] circle of arms. Ka. tarke, takke an embrace, the amount of wood, etc., that can be grasped with the arms at once; tagkeysu, takkeysu to embrace, etc.; tarbu, tabbu to embrace, clasp in the arms as a quantity of wood, étc.; Hn. an embrace, the amount of wood, etc., that can be taken in the arms at once; tabbisu to cause -to embrace; tekke embracing, an embrace, etc. Kod. tabb- (tabbi-) to embrace. Tu, tarkonuni to hold up in one’s arms as a sick person; tarvelu holding up in the arms, DED (S) 2543,
Ta. tarutanal ringworm. Ma. taruta- pans taritanam id., herpetic eruptions. DEDS

Ta. tarumpu (tagumpi-) to be scarred, bruised, marked, become practised, addicted; n, scar, cicatrice, bruise, weal, mark, im- pression, dent made in the skin, injury, blemish, stigma, defect in character. Ma, fagampu scar, callous spot as from a writing style, wart; tagampikka to grow callous: tarampiccavan callous, unfeeling. Ko, talm (obI. talt-) swelling raised by a blow, weal, Kui dali an inflamed patch of skin, blotch; dali inba to be blotchy, spread in patches, Malt. tada scar, spot. DED(S) 2544. 3119 Ta, tarai (-v-, -nt-) to sprout, shoot forth, thrive, grow luxuriant (as plants); (-pp-, -tt-) to flourish, thrive, grow luxuriantly (as plants), overflow with joy, be abundant (as a flood), multiply, grow, prosper (as a family, people, state); m. sprouting, sprout, shoot, spray, twig, bough with leaves, peacock’s tail, fan, a kind of garland; taraivu sprouting, shooting, germinating, foliage; taraippu flourishing, thriving. Ma, taza shoot, green twig with leaves, fan, royal umbrella; tarekka to shoot, sprout, thrive; tarappu_ thriving condition; tagappikka to make to thrive or prosper; teruku new leaves; terukka (trees) bud, sprout, thrive (as a house after mis- fortunes). Ka. dale, dali to become abundant as fruits, leaves, etc., on a tree, spread as smallpox or itch over the body; dallisu to spread widely, increase; tage parasol, um- brella. Kui dalga (dalgi-) to spread over a space, increase in extent, overspread, over- cast; 4. extension, a spreading over. ? Br. trik

Tu. tafka tungs, liver; safka liver. Kol. tarngud id. Nk. tarngur id. Nk. (Ch.) tang id. Pa. taruyg id. Ga. (Ol) tarin id, (S.*) tananil (pL) id. Go. (A.) taraki, (Tr.) tanaki, tanéki, (Ch.) tanéki, (Ph.) tanaki, (W.) taraki, (G.Mu.) taraki, tagak, (Ma.) tand’ki, (S.) tarki, (Ko.) tark id. (Voe. 1678); (LuS.) tudakee the heart; (Koya Su.) tanike liver. Konda tarki, (BB also) tanaki id. Pe, trakig (pl) id. Mand. traken id. Kui tlida, trida, (K.) tradanga id. Kuwi (F.) thra’na, (Su.) tra’na, (Isr.) tra’na, (P.) tatla id. /Cf. Skt. (@ex.) tadya- = kloman- lung. DED(S, N) 2546. talatala

Te. t(r)avvu to dig, excavate, scoop, unearth, stir up, bring to recollection: trav- vatamu digging; travvatincu to dig. Kol. (SR) tavy- id. Nk. tavy- id. Ga. (P.) tals- (rat, etc.) to scratch up earth. Go. (Tr.) tarana to dig or scratch up, as pigs (Voc. 1680a); (W. Mu. G. Ko.) tark-, (A. Y.) tark-, (Ch.) tark- to scratch; (Tr. Ph.) tarkana to scratch a place where it itches (Voc. 1711); (ASu,) tark- to scratch with fingernails; tar- to dig; 7 (Ko.) dév- to scratch up earth (of rats) (Voc. 1593); (Koya Su.) dév- to dig (well). Konda (BB) tar- (rat, etc.) to scratch up earth; ? (BB 1972) reb- to dig. Pe. rav- (-t-), rov- (-t:) to excavate earth, scoop out (pulp from gourd); rava, rova heap of excavated earth. Mand. tar- to scrape off (bark); rav- to excavate; yava heap of excavated earth. Kui tara (tari-) to dig out, excavate, scratch out: 7. excavation, scratching out; raja (raji-) to scratch up earth or dust; #, scratching up; (K.) gaj-, rab- to dig. Kuwi (isr.) tarj- (-it-), traj- (-it-) to scratch up, as dog, fowl, etc.; (S.) tarj- to scratch; yev- (-it-) (Su.) to dig, excavate, ist.) to take out soil from hole. DED(S, N) 2547, DEDS(N) 837 (Su. 1973, p. 141; *tar- aoe > ra-; 2? cf. 3178), and from DED

Ta. tal (tatp-, taff-) to hinder, ob- struct, stop, dam up; talampu floodgate, sluice. Ka. tal impeding, impediment, ob- stacle, delay; tal-poy to obstruct, impede, hinder, oppose; ‘taluvu to stop, delay; n. delay; (Hav.) talpu to obstruct, Tu. talepunit - to hinder, detain; (B-K.) talpu to interrupt, cause to stop. Te, talupu door, Cf. 3031 Ta. tatu. DED(S, N) 2548. 3124 Mand. tar- (-t-) to strip off (bark). Kur, tal@khna to pull off, strip, peel, decorti- cate; (Hahn) talkhna to sever, separate. Malt. tale to cut off. DEDS 511.

Ta. talatala (-pp-, -tt-) to be brilliant, transparent, be sleek (as the body), plump; talatalappu brilliance; tala-tal-enal expr. of being brilliant; talukku (talukki-) to be bright, glitter, shine; 7. shining, glittering, splendour. 7.Ko, tan tan in- (id-) (surface, cattle’s coat) shines; tantank in- (id-) (surface) feels smooth and satiny. Ka, talatala brightness, gleam, glitter, sunrise; talatalane, taratagane gleam- ingly, flashingly; talatalisu to glitter, flash, shine; talaku, talku shine, glitter, lustre, flash; talakkane flashingly, brightly; talapa, talapu, talupu splendour, lustre, shine; (PBh.) talarpu tal-tal-enal {272] tallu diffusion of splendour. Tu. talatala glitter, lustre. Te. talatala glitter, lustre, brilliancy, shining; talatalaladu to glitter, glisten; taluku glitter, shining, lustre. /MBE 1969, p. 294, no. 30, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDJAL, no. 5549, *fal-, those items that mean ‘glitter’ or the like; the IA items show ¢ by assimilation to *1. Cf. also Mar. talapné to shine, sparkle. DED (S, N) 2549.

Ta. tal-tal-enal expr. of bubbling, as boiling water. Ka. talatalane, talapalane with a briskly bubbling noise in boiling; talapala the noise of bubbling water or the brisk bubbling up of water in boiling; dal sound in imitation of that of boiling. Tu. talapala a bubbling noise. DED 2550.

Ta. talar (-v-, -nt-) to droop, faint, grow weary, enfeebled, infirm, or decrepit, grow slack, become relaxed as a tie or grasp, become flabby from age, suffer, lose one’s vitality; n. slackening; talarcci, talartti slack- ness, looseness, flexibility, weakness, in- firmity, faintness, langour, depression, lazi- ness, remissness; talarvu growing © slack, relaxing, faintness, weakness, depression, sorrow; talarttu (talartti-) to loosen (¢r.); talatala (-pp, -tt-) to become loose, as a cloth worn upon the person. Ma. talaruka to relax, slacken, be allayed, grow faint, weary; talarcca slackness, weariness, faintness; talarkka, talarttuka to moderate, abate. Ko. tala-r- (tala-ry-) to take rest. Kod, tale- (talev-, taland-) to become weak; talat- (talati-) to make weak, exhaust. 7u. talabala, falamala exhaustion, weariness; dalabala, datank’. loose. Malt. talgqro tender, delicate, weak, DED 2551.

Ta. talampu (talampi-) to stagger, totter; tall@fu (tallati-) id. Ka. talar to move, tremble, totter, move on or forward, start off, set out, depart; 7. moving, trembling, totter- ing; talaccu to go away from a place, start, depart; teral to move, stir, tremble, shake, quiver, move forward, go, set out, depart; teralike, teralke moving, etc.; teralicu, teralcu to cause to move or tremble, cause to go away, etc. Tu. -talabala, talamala alarm, agitation. Te. taralu to set out ‘or start; taralu to move, shake; talaru id., (K. also) be agitated, disturbed; teralu to start, set out, boil; terlu to boil, bubble up as water; (K.) teral(ujcu’ to remove, stir, cause to go; talaku to move, fear; n. motion, fear. Konda terli (-t-) (water) to boil excessively. Mand. dalka- to tremble. Cf. 3253 Nk. (Ch.) tirg-. / Cf. Skt. tarala-. DED(S) 2552.

Ka. talaviira, talara, talaci watchman, beadle. Tu. talavare village watchman. Te. talari watchman. / Cf. Skt. taldra(ka)-, tala- varga- city guard (Brhatkathakosa; Udaya- sundartkatha 75); Pkt. talara- town watch- man; Mar, talvar an officer of a village; taral a man of low caste whose duty it is to protect a village. DED(S) 2553. a

Ka. talisu to pound, beat, deprive rice of its bran by pounding. Tu. talpuni to thresh as paddy; talu threshed, beaten; (B-K.) talpu to knock, shake off. Go. (Tr.) dalsan® to pound (with fists or club), thresh with a flail; (Ph.) dalsina, dolsana, (SR.) dalsinad to pound (Voc. 1851), Cf. 3105 Ta. tallu, and see note there. / Cf. Apabhramsa (Mahapurana) talappa- a stroke, slap with the palm, DED(S, N) 2554.

Ta. talir (-pp-, -tt-) to shoot forth, Sprout, put forth leaves, flourish, prosper, rejoice; ”, sprout, tender shoot; talirppu sprouting. Ma. talir bud, new leaf, shoot; talirkka to bud, sprout, get fresh leaves; talirppu, talirmma budding. Ko, tayl breed of cattle. Ka. talal to bud, sprout, shoot; tali to shoot, sprout, put forth leaves; 7. race, family, stock, breed; talir to shoot, sprout, put forth leaves; nm. young shoot, sprout, new leaf or new leaves, Tu. taliry sprout, bud. Te, talaru to bloom, shine, thrive; taliru a sprout, shoot; talirucu to sprout, shoot, bloom, Kuwi (Isr.) dal- (-it-) to blossom. Cf. 3362 Ta. tulir, DED 2555. 3132 Ta, talukku (talukki-) to smear, rub in. Ka. talku to-smear or anoint the body with an unguent; 7. state of being anointed, DED 2556.

Ta. talai (-v-, -nt-) to fasten, bind, chain; (-pp-, -tt-) id., to entangle, confine, restrain, n. fastening, cord, rope; talaiyam bonds, fetters; tatku (tatki-) to bind, enchain, Ma, tala fetters, foot-rope for climbing palm trees; ‘talekka to fetter, shackle; taleppu fettering. To. tole sp. plant from whose bark string is made. Ka. tal to be joined, inter- twined, yoked; tale a tie, tether; talli con- nexion, association, company; dale to join by sewing together, seam. Kod, tale, dale loop of rope, noose. Tu. talé rope for climb- ing palm trees; talli intercourse, association; salé a sling, snare. Te. talugu, (B.) talaga tether or halter for cattle: (VPK) tal(u)gu, tagulu, tolugu, taluvu, tanugu id.; ? tai(ujcu to twist, twine. ? Br. tafing to tie up, bind, bewitch, build, construct (embankment); become congealed, gather (of clouds) (? < *talp- or *talv-, with clision of 4-). DED (S, N) 2557.
Ka. talli calumny, slander; (Hav.) tarale slander. Tu. talli id., defamation; taralé id., backbiting, tale-bearing. DED 2558.

Ta. tallu (talli-) to push, force for- ward, shove away, expel, reject, dismiss; be removed, be lost, fail; #. pushing, rejecting. Ma. talluka to push, thrust, reject, cast off; tallal pushing, rejection; tallu thrust, push. Ko. tal- (tayl-) to push, outcaste. To. tol- (toly-) to push. Ka, tallu to push, shove away, thrust, drive, throw, reject, dismiss, heave; 7. pushing, etc. Tu. talluni, talluni to push in, press through. Te. talagu (allomorph tala-), talgu, (K. also) talfigu to be lost or removed; tallai (273] remove; (K. also) way to be cleared of crowds. DED 2559.

Ya. tallai mother. Ma, talla id.; talla- viral thumb, great toe. Te. tal vi mother; original, first, principal, chief, main. Pa. tal mother; tal vanda thumb. Go. (Mu.) tallur mother of animals or birds, hen which has laid eggs more than once; tallur pén a female deity; (Ma.) tallor mother, female of animals; (M.) talur female of animals; talur kor hen (Voc. 1690); ? (Tr. W. Ph.) ali, (Ch. D. Ma.) tai, (G.) fatal cow; (G.) tai id., female of animal (Voc. 1489). Konda tali mother (invocatory; eg..bUmi tali mother earth!); adj. female (prefixed to certain animal names; e.g. tali kodi she-buffalo); (BB) tari female of animals. Pe. tari mother, Kui tadi mother, woman, female; tali female bird or animal, hen. Kuwi (Su.) tali female of animals; (S.) talli id.; mother; talli wansu thumb; (F.) tali vwanjo id. DED(S) 2560. 3137 Ka, (Hav.) talle wooden handle, as of an axe, Tu. (B-K.) talle id.

To. tot- (toty-) to slap. Te. (K.) taratu, taratu to whip; x. whip, whipping. Mand. teh- (-t-) to strike. Kuwi (F.) tassali to slap. DED(S) 2561. 3139 Go, (Ma.) taral(i) bark coat; (Grig- son, p. 336) t&ghali a raincoat made of strips of retted bark of the tree Kydia calycina (Voc. 1679). Konda tarla big container made of ada (Bauhinia racemosa) leaves and conical in shape. Kui faduri a rain hat made of bam- boo and leaves. Kuwi (T.) tarla leaf coat covering the back; (Isr.) terla rain cap made of leaves.

To. tari (-v-, -nt-) to be cut off, broken; (pp-, -tt-) to lop, chop off, cut off; n, cutting off, wooden post, stake, weaver’s loom, a kind of axe; tarikai.a kind of axe, chisel. Ma. tarikka to cut down; tari post, hedge-stake, stick, cutting, weaver’s loom. Ko. tayr- (tarc-) to cut, using implement with one hand, cut path through jungle; kati-r- (< katy-tayr-; katre- < katy- tarc-) to cut; tary, tayr fence-rails between which and the posts brush is filled in; tayrgo+r wattle-and-daub house-wall. Ka. tari, tare to strip off, cut off, cut; tari cutting, slaughter; stake, post, sharp knife or sword. Kod. tari- (tarip-, taric-) to chop into small bits; (Shanmugam) tarip cutting. Tu. taripuni to lop off, clear (jungle); tajapuni, (B-K. also) sajapu, hajapu to chop, cut in pieces. Te. tarugu, targu, tayuvu, tarvu to Slice, chop; tarimena, taruvadi, (B. also) tarimidi, tarivini lathe. Kol, targ- ‘(tarakt-) to cut, cut off. Nk, targ- to cut. Go. (Ch.) tarhutana, (Tr.) taruhtana, (W.) tarahtana, (Hislop) taruh siana to sacrifice (Voce, 1677). Mand, de to cut (eg. string), Kuwi (Su.) da’- ‘(dat-) (S.) da’nai, (D.) dre- (-t-) to cut; (PF ) dacali to cut with knife. Kur. tarna (taryas) to fell (tree), lop off (bough). Malt, tare to cut tarcind down, fell; tare to break (as a stick), injure. Cf. 3437 Ta. teri. DED(S, N) 2562.
Ko. tarv- (tard-) to become abraded by moving over rough surface or by having something rubbed over it; (tart-) id. (tr). Ka. tari to be chafed, abraded or grazed. DED 2563.

Ta. taru (taruv-, tarr-) to wear tightly as a cloth, fasten; taruvay occasion, particular juncture, crisis, rare opportunity, stage (as in life); tarai (-v-, -nt-) to rivet, drive in (nails); taru putting on a cloth in the fashion of a divided skirt; taram opportune moment, time, turn; tarunam right time, proper season. Ma. tara foundation, nailing, riveting; taruka to be tucked in before and behind; tarayuka to be fixed; tarekka to drive in, hammer, fasten; enter, be fixed; taru wearing clothes tucked in; taram time, opportunity. To. tar- (tar8-) to get stuck Gn mud, difficulties): tarf- (tart-) to make poor; tef- ‘(tet-) to wrap (garment) tightly round waist, (cloth) binds (legs); tesor- (tesot-) to place cover on oneself (or someone else) for sleeping (for or-, see 79 Ta. affu to put). Ka, tari state of being joined, of being put in or down, fixed or settled; taru (tatt-) to join, approach, engage in; 7. state of being joined or connected, of being fit, of being settled; taru-vay order, succession: taruna fit or proper time. Tu. tarapuni, tarpuni to rivet, fasten firmly; be riveted, fixed. Te. tari opportunity, proper time, season, occasion; tari-gonu to endeavour, attempt; tariyu, tariyd-badu to approach; (K.) tarupu to join together, amass (wealth); taruvayi subsequent time, sequence, sequel, what follows; taruvata afterwards, subse. quently, next; tarunamu opportunity, time, season. Kuwi (F.) dari next (adv.). DED 2564.

Ta. taruku (taruki-) to be hindered, checked, frustrated, stammer; tarumpu dam to stop a stream and turn it in a different direction. Ka. tarubu, tarabu to stay, stop: tarumbu id.; to stop (tr.), restrain. Kuwi (P.) tamb- (-it-) to stop; (F.) tambali to be silent; taphali to interrupt; tapkali to quiet: (S.) tambinai to be silent; (Isr.) tamb- (-it-) to be quiet, stop; tap- (-h-) to stop (tr.). DED 2565. 3144 Ta, taruppu, tarippu a white stone of inferior value. Ka, tarapu, tarabu, tarupu an inferior stone like a diamond. Te. tarupu an artificial diamond. DED(S) 2527.

Ta. tarai (-v-, -nt-) to be or become bald; tattai baldness. Ko. tarv- (tard-) (head) becomes bald. To. tar- (tar8-) to become bald; tarf- (tart-) to make bald, pluck (hair, fowl); tar mad bald head. Ka. tarafa, tarafa, taratu baldness; bald. / Cf, Mar. tarté baldness. DED (S) 2566.

Go. (Tr.) tarcana, (Mu.) tarc- to scrape; (Ma.) tarsk- id., plane; (D.) task-, (Mu.) tarsk-/tarisk- to level, scrape (Voc, 1670). Konda (BB) tarh- (i.c. taR-) to scrape. Pe, treh- (trest-) id., plane, cut with adze. tanai {274] tanfu Mand. teh- (-t-) to shave. Kui tahpa (taht-) to smooth off, level down, chip, scrape; 7. act of smoothing off. Kuwi (Su.) tah- (tast-) to scrape, plane; (S.) tah’nai to engrave. DEDS 512. 3147 Ta, tanai a particle denoting quan- tity and time-limit, as ittanai (see 410). Ma. tana measure. Ka. tanaka until, as far as. Te. danuka, daka until, up to, as far as, to. DEDS 513. 3148 7a. tannu (tanni-) to approach. Te. (K.) tanuku to touch, reach, occur, befall, (fear) arises in mind; (Sank.) danuku to touch, affect; (K.) daniyu to join, copulate; danivu copulation. DED(S) 2568. 3149 Kur. taka air, wind, breeze; tagma to blow (of the wind). Malt, take wind, air; tdgare to wave, undulate, fly with a waving motion as a sherd when thrown. Br. tahd wind. DED(S) 2569.

Ta. takku (takki-) to come in contact, collide, strike against as a vessel on a rock, be severe in reproof, attack, assault, strike, beat, dash, pounce upon, charge; #. attack, assault, dash, blow, clash; takkam attack, assault, hit; takkal striking, attacking, charging; tanku to hit against, strike. Ma, takkuka to hit, touch, beat. Ko. ta+k- (faeyk-) to touch with hand. To. to+k- (to-ky-) to touch (sacred bell in diary, of one who should not do so); shout with anger. Ka. taku, tagu, tafigu to come in contact with, touch, hit, strike or dash against, collide with, close with, attack; 7. joining, touching; (PBh.) t&pu to hit, strike, attack. Tu. takuni to hit, touch; taguni id., to come in collision or contact with; tigavuni to cause to touch; tacuni, taficuni to come into collision. Te. taku to touch, hit, attack, encounter, oppose in battle; ,. combat, attack; tikudu touch, contact; tikudala en- counter, meeting, coming together, collision, impact, shock; ta&cu to kick; t#pu a_ kick. DED 2570. 3151 Pa, tak- to walk; takip- (takit-) to make to walk. Ga, (OIL, S.) tak- to walk. Go. (A. Y. Tr. W. Mu. Ma. S. Ko.) tak- id.; caus. (Tr.) takstana, (W.) taksahtana; (Y.) takmar walking (Voc. 1695). Pe. tan(g)- (tant-) to walk. Mand. tay- id. Kui taka (ta@ki-) id.; 7. act of walking; ? (K.) tangu (pl. takaka) hoof. Kuwi (f.) takali, (S.) takinai, (Isr.) tak- (-it-) to walk. ? Cf. 3177 Ta. tavu. DED(S) 2571. 3152 Kol. ta+k father (always with pre- ceding possessor); ta*k ammaner parent

Ta. tahku (taiki-) to uphold, bear up, support, protect, guard, give shelter, endure, bear, assume, wear (as crown), delay, hinder, prevent, resist, ward off; 4. bearing, support- ing, support; tahkal supporting, enduring, bearing, delaying: tanki support, prop, de- fence, one who supports. Ma. tatu support, a vault, staff of a spear; titihuka to support, keep, sustain; tafifal support, reservoir; taffikka to make to support. Ko. ta+qg- (tasngy-) to support (burden), endure, make to escape from death or ill-treatment. To. toeg- (to-gy-) to support (burden); be stuck in branch; delay. Ka, taigu to support, keep, sustain, bear up; {aku a support, a vault. Kod. ta-+ng- (ta-ngi-) to fean (intr.); ta+k- (ta-ki-) id. (f7.); (Shanmugam) tag, tak leaning. Tu. tafgu a support, prop; tafiguni to assist, help, prop, support, sustain; t#A- golipuni to support; tankuni to take care of, nourish, foster. Cf. 2427 Ko. cae k-. DED(S) 73. : 3154 Kol. (SR.) tad- to spread bedding; tarekad bedding. Vk. tar- to spread. Ga. (S.3) taf- to spread like a mat. Go. (Tr.) tarana to spread out clothes, etc., build a nest; (Ph. A. G.) tar-, (M.) taran¥ to spread; (Mu.) tar- to spread (cloth, mat, etc.), build nest; (Ma.) tay to spread, (bird) to build nest (Voce. 1706); (ASu.) tar- to spread the bed. Kur, tira to lengthen, elongate, draw or stretch out. Malt. tanrye to spread out, stretch out; tdnrgre to be spread out. DED 2574,

Ta. yim (obl. em-), nafikal (odl enikal-) we (excl). Ma, fiainal (ob/. fannal., ennal-) id. Ko. a-m we (obi. em- excl; am- incl.). To. em (obl. em-) we (excl); om (obl. om-) we (incl). Ka. am (obi em-) we (excl) GG. Ss. Gai, Bull. Deccan College Research Institute, 1. 411 £.). Kod. enga (obl. enga-), nanga (dl. nayga-) we. Tu. enkuly (ob erikule-) we (excl). Te. emu, nému, mému (obl, man-/ma-) id. Kol. aem (obl. am-) id. Nk, am (obl. am-) id. Nk. (Ch.) am(e) (obl. am-) we. Pa. am (obl, am-) id. Ga. (OIL) am yamai 468) yanai (obl, am-), (S.) am (ob. amm-) id. Go. (Tr.) ammat, (W.) mammat, (Ph.) ammaf, ammot, mammat, mammar, (M.) mamaf, maf, (L.) mimat, (SR.) mamof, mamo, marat, (Mu.) mamma, mammot (obl ma- passim) we (Tr. excl) (Voc. 2707); (Koya Su.) mamma we. Konda mat(u) (obl. ma-) we (inel.); map(u) (obl. ma&-) we (excl). Pe. ap, apen (excl), As, aseg, hen (incl) (gen. ma, acc.-dat. man/mangen) We; mapo in our region, mabe by us, with us. Mand. am (obl. ma-) we. Kui amu, (K.) mamu we (exel.), aju we (incl) (obl for all m&-), Kuwi (F.) mambu, (S. Su. P. Isr.) mambu we (exel.); (F.) marro, (S. Isr.) maro we (incl.) (obl. for both ma-), (S.) mapo on our side. Kur. &m (obi, em-) we (excl). Malt. ém (obl. em-) id. Cf. 3647 Ta, nam and 5160 Ta. yan. DED(S) 4231.
Ka. tati skin, bark. Te, t3fa bark. DED 2575.

Ka. tatu to strike against, touch, come in contact with, etc.; strike; (Hav.) 1adu to butt with horns. Tu. taduni to gore, butt; tadu goring; tadelu act of goring or butting; tanfuni to touch, hit, come into collision or contact with, quarrel, fight; tainfavuni to make collide, etc.; (B-K. also) hantu to collide. Kor, (O.) tainti to hit. Te. tatincu to pat, slap. Cf. 3039 Ta. tattu. DED(S) 2576.

Ta. tattottam, tattottu fraud, decep- tion; perplexity, confusion. Ka. tatavata fraud, untruth. Te. tatotu deceit, knavery; deceitful, knavish; tatavata, tatamata deceit; tatamatincu to deceive. DED(S) 2577.

Ta. tintu (tanti-) to dance, skip, jump, leap across, jump over, cross, step over, transgress, surpass, excel; a. a leap, jump; tantavam leaping, jumping. Ma, tantuka to jump across; put into another place; tattuka to get over or through. Ko. da+{- (da«ty-) to cross (mark, stream, mountain, road). To. to-{- (to-ty-) to cross (boundary, etc.). Ka. tanfu to jump, dance, leap, skip over, cross; dau, dintu to jump, pass or step over, cross, ford, go beyond, exceed, transgress, pass away, expire; #. passing over, jump across, etc.; datisu, dantisu to cause to pass over. Kod. (Kar.) da+t- Gi-) to cross. Tu. dantuni to cross, ford, pass by. Te. d&tu to leap, jump, cross over, pass over, go beyond, transgress; 7. a leap, jump, crossing or passing over. Kol, dast- (da-ft-) to cross; da+tip- (da fipt-) to make to cross. Konda dat- (-t-) to hop, jump, hop in dance, jump over, walk fast. Br. tradding to skip, prance, dance about. /Cf. Skt. tandava- Siva’s dance. DED(S, N) 2578. PA ET Re NA AD ROA MEME RE OTS, EO ts aceanelaticted mitt aa taat acer men eearteneeaemeencien aaheibiiminaiaite nmameeemmennas ta nenerammnet readme Te tatu [275] tavu
Ta. tatu powder, dust, pollen. To. to-@ powdery, soft (of flour or powdered chillies). DED 2579.

Ya. tattd grandfather; tata id., father. | Ma, tatan father. Ka. tata grandfather, father. Kod. taeté grandfather (< Ka.). Tu. tate id. Te. tata id., father. Nk. (Ch.) tata grandfather. Pa, tata father. Go, (S. Ko.) tata() mother’s father (Voc. 1697); (Y.) tado father’s father (Voe. 1702); (LuS.) tado grandfather. / Cf. Skt. tata- father. DED(S) 2580. 3160A 7e. tapa a ladder, rung of a ladder, steps, stairs. Go. (Ko.) tapa ladder (Voce. 1704). Konda (BB) dapa ladder, steps, stairs,
Ta. tappu expected moment, ap- pointed time, convenience. Ma. tappu proper time, opportunity. To. top time, chance. DED 2581.

Ta. tam (ob. tam; before vowels tamm-) they themselves; you (hon. pl); tafkal you (fon. pl.); tamar one’s own people, relatives, kindred, friends, servants; tamarmai friendship; taman a male relative or friend; fem. tamal; tami solitude, loneliness, destitution; (-pp-, -tt-) to be alone, lonely; tamiyan solitary, lonely man, destitute person; fem. tamiyal; tam-appan father. Ma, tam (obl. tam-, tamm-) themselves; tahkal, tafihal they, themsclves; you (hon.); tamar one’s own people; tam-appan father. Ko. taem (obl tam-) themselves. To. tam (obi. tam-) id.; tadam each his own, separate, different (< tam-dam; *Nd > d); tada(m) marr separately (for ma-r, see 4717). Ka. tam (obi. tam-), tavu (obi, tav-) they, them- selves; you (on. ); tamatu, tamattu, tammatu, tammutu, tammadu theirs; tamar, tavar those who are his, hers or theirs, one’s own people. Kod. tanga (obl tanga-) themselves. Te. tamu (obi. tam-, tamm-), tamaru, tara they, themselves; you (hon.). Kol. *ta-m (oAl. tam-) they, themselves; tamne their own; tam ba-n his, her, their own father. Nk. tam they, themselves, Pa. tam (obi. tam-) id. Ga. (Oll.) tam (Obl tam-) id, Go, (Tr) tamma, tammaft id. (Voc. 1661). Konda (BB) tam id. Kui taru (mase.), tai (neut.) (ol, taran-) id. Kuwi (U.) tambt (ob tam-), -(S.) tambu (obl tam-) id. Kur. tam (obl tam-) id. Malt. tam, tami (obl tam-) id. Cf. 3196 Ta. tan. - DED (8) 2582, 3163 Ta, tamarai lotus, Nelumbium specio- sunt, tammi lotus. Ma. tamara id. Ka, tamare, tavare id. Kod. ta-vare

Pe. tar (lL -ku), dar (pl -ku) ant. Mand. tar (pl. -ke) id. Kui t@ru (pl tarka) a small black ant. ? Cf. Go. (Tr.) tard the queen white ant (Voc. 1708). DEDS 514, 3167 Ta, tar, tiram cord, rope. To. to-mm (obl. to-rt-) one string of bead necklace with several strands, one thread of skein hanging from woman’s armlet. Ka. dara string, thread. Tu, dara id. Te. daramu id., cord, twine. / ? < JA. Cf. Pkt. (DNM; Norman) dara- waist- band, girdle; cf. also Skt. davara(ka)- string, Turner, CDIAL, no. 6225, DED(N) 2586. 3168 Ma, taram a copper coin, 4 pice or 4 fanam. Ka. t@ra a copper coin of two kasu. Tu, tira two pie. DED 2587. 3169 7a. tard duck, heron. Ma. taravu duck. DED 2588,

Ta. tari way, road, path, right mode; tarai way, path (< Te.). Ko. aeda-ry road, path (cf, 4087 Ta. pay). Ka. dari way, road, path. Tu, dari id. Te. dari id., manner, mode. ?Cf. Ta. atar way, path, public road, rule; (communicated by Bh. Krishnamurti). DED (S) 2589. 3171 Ka, d&re-huli mara Averrhoea caram- bola, Tu, darepuli fruit of A. carambola. Cf.
Ta. tarai large brass trumpet, long reed instrument. Ma. tara a large trumpet. DED 2591. 3173 Kol. ta+r- (ta-rt-), (Pat., p. 119) fareng (hen) cackles. Pa. tarp- to cackte. DED

Te. tragu, travu to drink, (K. also) swallow, eat, smoke; trigudu, travudu drink- ing. Pa. tar- to swallow. Ga. (OlL) targ- id.; 3 &) sark- to drink (as ox in tank). DED 593. 3175 Ta, tali central piece of a neck orna- ment solemnly tied by the bridegroom around the bride’s neck as marriage badge, a child’s necklace, amulet tied on a child’s neck. Ma, tali the centre piece of a neck ornament tied as the marriage badge. Ko. ta-ly a heavy necklace. To. to-ly woman’s gold neck ornament. Ka. tali, tili a small round plate of gold worn at the neck as a marriage badge, that plate used as an orna- ment. Tu. tali, tali the marriage badge worn by a female. Te. tali small piece of gold tied by the bridegroom at the marriage ceremony to the neck of the bride. /Cf. Skt. tali- (Mayrhofer, s.v.). DED 2594. 3176 Ma, (Lush.)} tavi bracken, Pteris seeilina. Ko, taryv id. To. taf fern. DED 3177 Ta, tavu (tavi-) to jump up, leap, skip over, leap over, cross, spring upon, attack, fly, spread, be luxuriant; n. jumping, tr [276] tar leaping, moving, going, galloping; tavvu (tavvi-) to leap, jump, spring, tread gently, boast, be arrogant; ”. hopping, jumping, leaping; t@ attacking, rushing, jumping. Ma. tavuka to rush in upon, spread. Ka. tagu to jump, skip, leap over. ? Cf. 3151 Pa. tak-. DED(S) 2596.

Ta. tar (-v-, -nt-) to fall low, be lowered, be low (as a roof), flow down, descend, decline as the sun, sink in water, sink in circumstances or repute, decrease, decay, degenerate, despond, be dejected, prove inferior, fail, stay, rest, halt, hang down, be suspended (as the arms, as locks of hair), be deep, bend, droop, be bowed down, become low, subdued as sound; (-pp-, -tt-) to bow down (f,), let down, deepen, depress, degrade, wait, stay, delay; tarcci bending, humility, submissiveness, inferiority, meanness, baseness, vileness, deficiency, want, scarcity, arrears, deterioration, degradation, loss, decay (of wealth, power, etc.), delay, dishonour, disgrace; tartti lowness in rank, inferiority, dishonour; taxttu (tartti-) to bring low, lower, let down, deepen, reduce, diminish, keep under control, compel obedience, degrade, disgrace, delay, waste time, make a person or object stay, plant (as seeds), bury, sink, submerge, immerse; tagntar humble persons, low, mean persons; taxppam depth (as of water); tirppu lowering, burying, inserting, planting; tarmai humility, in- feriority (as in rank), degradation, baseness; tar-varam sloping roof, lean-to, penthouse; tarvu depth (as of a pit) shortness, degradation, fault, defect, poverty, foot (as of a mountain), resting-place, abode, self-control, modesty, distress, prostration in worship; taram lowness (as of the pitch of a tune), calmness, delay; t8tci degradation, disgrace, dilatoriness, tardi- ness; tarai (-v-, -nt-) to hang do

Ta. tag bolt, bar, latch, blocks in a wall to support beams; tag-kkol, t@za-kkol, tat-kol, tar-ppal bolt, bar; tal bolt, bar, latch, wooden catch turning on a central screw that fastens a pair of shutters, pin that holds a tenon in a mortise, key. Ma. tag, tagutu, tavu, takkura, (Kaut.) tigkuga bolt, bar, lock; takkol key. Ko, ta-korl, tas ko-lg European lock, padlock. To. togt bar at gate of pen; ? to-w plank. Ka. tag bar or bolt of a door; tapal bar, bolt. Kod. ta-li bolt of door. Tu. tarkoly, tarkolu, tarkoly key. Te. talamu lock, key. /Cf. Skt. tada(ka)-, tala-, Pali tala-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5749, DED(S, N) 2598.

Ka. tar palmyra or toddy palm, Borassus flabelliformis. Tu, tari, tali id. Te. tadu, (inscr., Inscr.?)} t&u id.; tafi of or belonging to the palmyra tree; tafti ceftu palmyra tree; tat-aku palmleaf. Kol. (Kin.) tati mak palmyra tree. Nk. tar mak/Sett j 3 tari [277] tarru toddy palm. Nk. (Ch.) tar id. Pa. tar id. Ga. (S.3) titi palmyra palm. Go. (G. Ma. Ko.) tar, (S.) tari, (A.) tadi toddy palm; (SR.) tadi kal palm liquor (Voc, 1709). Konda {ar maran, titi maran palmyra tree. Pe. tar mar toddy palm. Kuwi (Su.) tafi marnu, (S.) tati id. Kur. tar palm tree. Malt. télmi Borassus flabelliformis. / Cf. Skt, tila-, Pkt. tada-, tala-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5750 (some of the Dr. items may be < 1A). DED(S, N) 2599. 3181 Konda tari mranu, tari maran plantain tree, Kui tani plantain. Kuwi (Su. P.) tari, (F.) tarri, (S.) tadi, tadi id. DEDS 515.
Ta. tari large pan, pot, or vessel with a wide mouth, jar, burial urn; takagi, takali bowl of a lamp, plate from which food is eaten. Ma. tari an earthen washhand basin. /2< Skt. sthali-. DED 2600.

Ta. tirai fragrant screw-pine, coco- nut tree, spathe of the coconut tree. Ka. tare fragrant screw-pine, Pandanus odoratissi- mus. Tu. taré, caré, haré, talé, (B-K. also) sare coconut tree; tarayi, (B-K. also) carage, tarage, sarage, hadrage a coconut. DED 2601. 3184 Ta, taxvatam necklace of pearls or beads, string of rudraksa beads. Ma, tarvatam, tivatam necklace of gold, pearls, etc., hanging very low. Ka. tavada a kind of necklace formed of lotus beads hanging as low as the navel, worn by men. Te. tavadamu, tavalamu string of beads, rosary. Cf. 3178 Ta. tar and §220 Ta. vatam. DED 2602.
Ta. tagvatam. /Cf. Skt. vata- string, rope, tie; vataraka-, vatakara-, varataka- cord, string; Turner, CDJAL, no. 11212. DED(S) 4268.

Ta. tal log, foot, foot of a tree or mountain, stem, pedicle, stalk. Ma. tal stalk, stem (chiefly of edible plants), the foot. Ko. ta-1 stem, trunk, slope of hill. To. to-t thigh of animal’s hind leg, trunk of tree, slope of hill. Ka. tay stem or stalk of corn, flowers, etc. Kod. ta-li stem. ? Te. tatu empty ears of corn {i-e. straw after threshing; so K.]. DED 2603.

Ta. tali (-pp-, -tt-) to season and flavour curry, etc., with spices fried in ghee or oil, flavour medicine as with ghee, oil; talikkai, tailippu seasoning and flavouring curry. Ma, talikka to season food. Ka. talisu to season food, temper, sharpen as weapons; tilida, talada, talda, tatlu boiled and scasoned vegetables. Te. talincu to scason; talimpu seasoning, a seasoned curry; (K.) talabdyu to season food by pouring in fried spices; tlupu. Konda tilep scasoning of meats, etc. with spices. DED(S) 2604.

Ta. tali hedge bindweed, [pomaea sepiaria. Ma, tali a creeper, the leaves of which are used in cleansing the head before bathing, or in washing off the oil rubbed on the body; (Lush.) tirutali /pomaea sepiaria. DEDS 516.

Ta. taju (tali-) to bear, suffer, tolerate, be worth, be possible, practicable. Ko. ta+l- (taely:) to stop and wait. Ka. tal, talu (tald-) to hold, take, assume, get, obtain, receive, have or possess, undergo, experience, suffer patiently or quietly, be patient, endure, wait, last, continue unimpaired, wear well, bear with; talike undergoing, suffering, patience, patient endurance, perseverance; taldu to hold, etc.; talime, talume, talme patient endurance, patience, forbearance; tale to hold, bear, carry, etc.; put on (as clothes). Tu. taluni to bear, endure, suffer, forbear, have patience; talmé patience, forbearance, endurance. Te. talimi, talimi, talika patience, endurance; talimikidu one who has patience or endurance; fem, talimikatte; tal(u)cu to wear, put on; tatu to bear, suffer, endure, be patient, refrain, forbear, pause, wait, last, wear, be durable. DED 2605. 3189 7a. taru bunch, cluster as of plantains, dates, areca-nuts; (Koll.) tatt banana bunch. ye taru bunch or cluster of areca-nuts. DED 606.

Ta. tamu weaver’s bobbin, reel. Ma, t@ru the arrow of weavers which holds the yarn, DED 2607. 3191 Ma, taruka to sink, decline; tarikka tdrruka to let sink or fall. Kod. ta+r- (ta-v-} ta-ruv-, ta-nd-) to descend, (sun) sets; taet- (ta-ti-) to lower, close (window, door). Te. (K.) tara, (B.) taru to.descend, alight. From DED 2597, DED(N) 2608.

Ma. taruka to become thin, droop. Ko. targ ar- (at-) to become lean (for ar-, see 79). To, to-x- (to-xy-) to become lean, slender. Ka. tar, tau to become dry, dry up, wither, wane, become emaciated; taradi state of being or becoming dry; tariga a dry, sapless man; taragu that which is dried or to be dried, dry, fallen or dead leaves, a cake fried in oil and dried; tarale state of being dry, useless, vain; taralu, tarlu, tallu, taral a ripe fruit that has become dry, esp. a coconut. 7u. tarnfuni, (B-K.) tarunfu to shrivel; targodé leanness; sarhgode a slender man. Kor. (M.) darla dried leaves. Te. (K.) taru, (B.) taru to fall away in flesh, become lean, diminish, be reduced; triduvadu to become lean. DED(S, N) 2608. 3193 Ta, taruku (taruki-) to linger, loiter. Kor, (Q.) tari to wait. Te. taru, tiru to lurk, lic in wait. DED 2609.

Ta. t&rumaru confusion, disorder, perverseness, contrariety, impropriety, in- solence; t@ru-maray-ppécu to speak incoher- ently or inconsistently, talk nonsense, use insuiting language. Me, tarumaru confusion, disorder, Ka. tarumaru id. Tu. tarumary id. Te. tarumara id., irregularity; disorderly, disordered, confused, irregular, DED 2610.

Ta. tarru (t8rri-) to sift, winnow; n. winnowing; tattu (fatti-) to winnow; tari (-pp-, -tt-) to sift by a winnowing fan. ? Tu. saruni, sariyuni to sift. Te. tal(u)cu to sift or separate larger particles from flour in a winnowing basket. Pe. dalngi ki- to winnow with a sideways motion. ?8r, dranzing, drazing to throw up in the air, winnow. DED (S, N) 2611. tr [278] tikai

Te. tan (obi. tan-; before vowels tann-) oneself; tan@ himself, only, just; tanatu that which is one’s own, friendship, amity, intimacy; tanatal one’s own permanent servant, associate, mate; tani singleness, seclusion, solitude, independence; (-pp-, -tt-) to be alone, single, solitary, be separate, detached from company, have no equal or match, be deserted; tanicci woman separated from her husband; tanitti lonely woman forsaken and left helpless; tanittal single man or woman as a bachelor or widow; tanitar persons in solitude; tanimai singleness, seclu- sion, solitude, retirement, incomparableness, matchlessness, forlorn condition, helpless- ness; tannan-tani quite alone; tanmai nature, essence, property, inherent or abstract quality, character, temper, disposition, state, con- dition, position, circumstances; tannai lord, chief, elder brother; tanndr one’s kith and kin, relatives or dependants. Ma. tan (obi. tan-) self, oneself; tinum notwithstanding; tané by himself; tani, taniccu by itself, alone; tanima loneliness; tanikku to himself, to oneself, Ko. ta-n (obi. tan-/ta-) oneself; -dan also, even (concessive), ever (indefinite). To. ton (obl, tan-) oneself; -ton (-Bon, -don) only, just (= Skt. eva; cf. Ta. tan); atton just that much, in that same way (for at, see 1). Ka. tan (obl. tan-) he, she, it, in the refl. or reciprocal sense; tanatu, tanattu, tannadu, tandu his, hers, its. Kod. ta-ni (obi. tan-) oneself. Tu. tanu self, oneself; (hon. pl.) tanukulu; tannavu one’s own

Ta. tinri, tani belleric myrobalan, Terminalia belerica Roxb. Ma, tanni id. Ka. tari, tGre, tari id. Tu. dandi mara id. Te. tdi, tandra id.; (inscr.) tanri id. (in place names). Kol. (Wagh,) tandi id. Pa. déndi, déni (stems dénd-, dén-) id. Go. (Tr.) tahka, (Y.) tahka, (M.) tahka id. (Voc. 1490). Konda tandi mranu id. Kuwi (Mah. T.) tandi id. DED(S) 2614. 3199 Ka, tigade, tigudu, tigude, tegade Indian jalap, Ipomoea turpethum. Tu. tigadi id. Te. tegada id. / Cf. Skt. tripufa-, trivrta-, tripusa-, tribhandi-; Turner, CD/AL, no. 6055. DED 2615.

To. tikaz (-v-, -nt-) to shine (as diamonds), glimmer (as stars), be brilliant; tikageci, tikarvu brightness, lustre, splendour, tikagttu (tikagtti-) to explain clearly, make cleat, show clearly, beautify, adorn; tekir (-v-, -nt-) to be manifest, shine. Ma. tikaguka to shine. Malt. téqe to shine, glow; teg-tegre to be or become glossy. DED(S) 2616.

Ta. tikiri circle, circular form, wheel, potter’s wheel, the discus weapon, chariot, car. ? Ko, tayrgan potter’s wheel. Ka, tiguri, tigari, tiguru a wheel, esp. a potter’s wheel. Tu, tagori the potter’s wheel. DED 2617.

Ta. tikil, tikir fright, terror. Ko. digi-1 in- (id-) to be thunderstruck or as- tounded because found out in wrongdoing. Ka. digil, digilu, digalu, digulu, dhigit con- sternation, horror, alarm, fear. Tu. digilu fear, alarm, cowardice. Te, digulu fear, alarm; digulu-padu to fear, be alarmed. Konda tiyel fear; (BB) tiyel- to be afraid; tiyeli ki- to frighten. Cf. 3207 Ta. tikai. DED(S) 2618.
Ta. tiku-tik-enal expr. signifying bubbling of water. Ma. tikakka to boil, bubble up; tikattuka to boil (fr.). DED 2619.

Ta. tiku-tik-enal expr. signifying smarting of a sore. Ko. dig dig in- (id-) to feel slight burning pain (as of hunger after several days of fasting, or after a very severe pain has stopped). DED 2620. 3205 Konda tig- (-it-) to press down hard, lay pressure on. Pe. tig- (tikt-) to push. Mand. tig- id. 7. Cf. 3243 Ma. tirakkuka. DEDS 517.
Ka. (PBh,) tegale chest. Tu. tigalé . breast, chest.

Ta. tikai (-pp-, -tt-) to be taken aback, confused, perplexed, bewildered, be astonished, amazed; n. amazement; tikaippu amazement. To. tix- (tix6-) to take to heels, bolt away; tixf- (tixt-) to make (buffaloes) stand in swamp (i.e. confused and unable to run away) before they are caught and killed at funeral (word used only in narratives). Ka, tikkalu state of being confused or deranged in the mind. Je. tikamaka intricacy, con- fusion, perplexity; tikamaka-gonu, tikamakal- adu to be puzzled, perplexed, confused; tikka madness, craziness; mad, crazy; tikka-gonu to become mad; tikkafamakkafa confusion, perplexity. Cf. 3202 Ta. tikil. DED 2621. tikai [279] tia

Ta. tikai, tikaippu asthma; tikkumu- kku, tikkumukkal choking, suffocating; tikku- mukk-afu to be choked, smothered. Ma. tikkal quick, heaving respiration (as of a dying person); tikkumuffu being choked, stifled. To, tixp breathing that causes pain. Kur. tekhtekhrna, textexrna to breathe with a series of short quick expirations as a child about to cry, grow short of breath (as a child with overfilled stomach) (or the second meaning in 3453), DED(S) 2622.
Ma. tikkane pressing on, swiftly. Ka. dikkane, diggane suddenly, all at once, quickly. Tu, dikka quickly, speedily. Te. diggana, digguna suddenly, abruptly. DED(S) 2623.

Ta. tikku (tikki-) to stutter, stammer, err or hesitate as in recitation, reading, etc.; n, stuttering, halting in speech; tikku-vayan stammerer, stutterer. Ma. tikku stuttering; tikkuka to stammer. Ko, tekva-yn stammerer, stutterer. Ke. tikkalu stuttering. DED 2624.
Ka. tikku to rub, scour, treat harshly, annoy; fikkisu to cause to rub, etc. Tu. tikkuni to rub, brush, polish. DED 2625.

Tu. dikké, dikkely, (B-K. also) digelu hearth, fireplace. Kor. (T.) dunkeli oven. 3213 Ta, titkal moon, month. Ma. tifkal moon; tiftal month. Ko. tigl moon; ting! month. To. tigil moon; tief ‘month. Ka.- tingal moon, month; bel difgal moonlight. Kod. tinga month. Tu. titgolu moon, month. Kut (friend-Percira) tingal danju crescent moon. DED 2626. 3214 Kur. tingli fly. Malt. tilngru’ the common fly. DEDS 518. 3215 Kur. tiss? sour; tissna to turn sour, acid. Malt. tise sour. DED 2627. 3216 Ta, titukk-enal, titir-enal expr. signi- fying suddenness, unexpectedness; titukku, tifukkam sudden fear, shudder from fright, terror; titukku-ttitukk-enal expr. signifying starting repeatedly through fear or weak nerves, palpitating of the heart through fear; titukk-ifu to be startled, shocked, start with fear or surprise. Ma. titukku, titukkam being scared, hurried; titutifé hurriedly; titar fright, grief. Ko. didakn in a flash, swiftly. Ka. tiffane, tirrane whirlingly, hurriedly, con- fusedly. Tu. diduka, didika, didka_ quickly, suddenly; didudida quickly. DED 26 28.
Ta. tifutitu Cpp-, -tt-) to make a reiterated noise, as by hasty steps, to thump constantly. Ko, did did in- (id-) to make trampling noise. DED 2629.

Ta. titti window. Ka. diddi, didde, jiddi a wicket, a hole expressly made for egress or ingress. Tu. (B-K.) diddi, jiddi back, postern. Te. diddi a small door or gate, wicket, postern or back door, sallyport. Go. (SR.) diddi lane (Voc. 1864). / Cf. Mar. did, didi a wicket. DED(S, N) 2631.

Ta. tittu (tiffi-) to abuse, revile, curse, utter imprecations; m. reviling, scold- ing, vulgar abuse. Ka. tiftu to abuse, scold: n. abuse, scolding. Te. tiffu to curse, swear at, abuse, revile, rail at, rebuke, scold, chide; n, a curse, abuse, abusive language; tiftadu to revile, abuse, quarrel; titgafa reviling, abusing, a quarrel; tiffibdtu a scold, person who habitually uses obscene or abusive language. DED 2632.

Ta. tifgu rising ground, bank, eleva- tion, hillock, sandbank, wall separating elephant stables; tifti raised ground; tittai tising ground, bank, elevation, raised floor; tifar, tifal rising ground, bank, elevation, island, rubbish heap, prominence, pro- tuberance; tifara mound; tatal high land. Ma. titfa raised ground, hillock, shoal, raised seat as in a veranda; titfu mound, shoal; tinfu earthen wall, bank, shoal. Ko. tit hill. To. tit mountain. Ka. tifta mass, quantity, number; titfu, titfe rising ground, hillock; didda, diddu eminence, elevation, hillock; dintu rising ground, hillock; (Hav.) citte earthen seat. Tu. diddu elevated ground, mound; cifté, (B-K.) titfe the foundation platform of a house. Te. tiffa heap, mound, DED(S) 2633.

Ta. tin strong, hard, firm, close, compact; tinfi size, bulk; tingu stoutness, thickness; tinam, tinnam certainty, vigour, strength, solidity, robustness, power, tight- ness; tinnan strong, robust, powerful man; tinnimai firmness of mind; tinniyan strong man, brave man; tinmai strength, power, robustness, hardness, compactness, firmness, truth, certainty, constancy, heaviness, bulki- ness; tinar (-pp-, -tt-) to form a thick layer, be crowded, dense, close; m. denseness, thickness (as of a cloud); tini (-v-, -nt-) to be crowded, dense, close; (-pp-, -tt-) to cram, stuff; n. solidity; tintppu strength; tinimpu denseness; tiniyan fat man, clumsy beast or thing; tinivu Hardness, denseness; tinukkam closeness, compactness, solidity; tinufiku (tinuhki-) to become close, dense, thick, crowded; tifpam solidity, strength, firmness of mind. Ma, tin firm, strong, solid; tinnam strength; stiff, tight; tinma solidity; tinar swelling; tinarkka to swell, rise as the skin from a blow; tinfikka (the edges of a wound) become swollen; titfuka to be thronged, crowded, tight; tikkuka to press, throng; tikkal pressing, thronging. Ka. tini to be united or mixed together, be crowded or closely packed, be full or filled; tinuku, tinaku, tiniku to use pressure or strain as in childbirth or in easing nature, press, make violent efforts, undergo trouble; tinna, tinpu thickness, stoutness, greatness, weight, gravity, excess; dindu thickness, stoutness, strength, pride; tintini mass, crowd, multitude; tin
Ka. dinda Clitoria ternatea Lin. Te. dinfena id. DED 2635.

Ta. tintu semicircular cushion, any small construction of brick built as a support. Ma. tintu a bale of cloth. Ka. dindu a heap, roundish mass or piece, roundish big stone, bundle of wood or grass, bale of cloth; dinde a roundish big stone. Te. dipdu pillow, cushion, bolster, bundle, cylinder. / Cf. Mar. did a bale of cloth. DED 2636.
Ka. dindu the thread-like centre of an orange, ete., the inside stalk or heart of a plantain tree. Kod. dindi stem of plantain. Tu. dindy, dindu the tender stalk inside a plantain tree. DED 2637.
Ko. tidg flesh. To. tidx piece of flesh. ? Cf. Ta. tafi flesh. DED (S) 2638.

Ta. tinnai pial, raised platform or veranda in a house, mound. Ma. tinna, tina terrace, raised bank, open veranda. Ko, tin raised seat; tinva-l veranda (for va-l, see 5354). To. tin sleeping platform; ? tinp raised edge of path, sod. Aa. dinne, dinne arising ground, eminence, bank, islet; (Gowda) tinE veranda. Tu. tiné, tiné (BRR also) ciné smat! open veranda; dinné hillock, eminence. Kor. (O.) tene outer veranda, Te, tinniya, tinne, tiniya, tine pial; (B.) dinne, dinniya, tinne pial; high raised embankment. DED(S) 2639,
Ta. tippali long pepper, Piper longum. Ma, tippali id. Ka. tippali id. Tu. tirpali, (B-K.) tippili, ippili id. DED (N) 2640.

To. tippai mound, elevated ground; that which is bulky. Ko. tip rubbish heap. Ka. tippe heap, hillock, dunghill; dibba, dibbu eminence, hillock; tevar(u), tevari rising ground, hillock. Tu. tippé heap, pile, hill; tuppé stack, heap of corn or rice. Te. tippa hill, hillock, rock, mountain, heap, mound, small island; dibba hillock, mound, heap. Pa. dippa heap; dibba mound. Ga, (S.°) dibbe hillock, mound. Go. (Mu.) dippa high- land for cultivation, forest field (Voc. 1865): (M.) dibe heap (Voc. 1869). Kui depa rising ground, high land, lower slopes of a_ hill, shore, earth platform, veranda, dais. Kuwi (S.) debbe hill; ba’ali dibba sandhill. Kur. dippd mound, hillock. Malt. tube a heap of dhimirdhimir filth or sweepings. Cf. 3239 Ka. dimmi. DED(S) 2641.

Ka. dibbana a nuptial procession, esp. of the friends and relatives of the bride- groom; nibbana a marriage festival with all its display, esp. with its processions and gifts, the display of the gifts or the gifts themscives; nibbaniga the friend or attendant or com- panion of a bridegroom. Tu. dibbana bridal party, marriage procession.

Ka. dibbana wooden stopple or cork. Kod. dimmi stopper. Tu. dimmé wedge, stopple, plug. Te. dibbadamu lid or earthen cover for a jar; dimme wooden cork or stopple; dimmu wooden stopple for a leathern bottle. DED 2643.

Ta. timi-timi syllables sung to keep time in dancing; timi-timiy-enal onom. expr. of (a) keeping time in dancing or music, (0) repeated sounds in rapid succession. Ka. dim a sound in imitation of tingling or ringing; dimi sound produced by the quick motion of the feet in dancing. Tu. dimidimi dancing nimbly, agility; dimma the sound of a small finger-drum. Te. (B.) dimidimi, dimdim, dhimindhimin dingdong. DED(S) 2644.

Ta. timir Cv-, -nt-) to grow, increase, become more intense; (-pp-, -tt-) to grow stout from obesity; n. obesity; wantonness; timiru (timiri-) to grow tall and big. Ma. timiruka to swell, grow, be angry; timirkka to be mad with joy or rage; timirppu triumph, arrogance. Te, timuru to be proud, (K. also) abound, increase, bubble with desires, hasten; n. pride, arrogance, excessive or superabun- dant strength, vigour or spirits, pruricncy, (K. also) haste; tivuru to hasten, (K. also) increase, abound, swell (as the sound of war drums, etc.), rage. DED(S) 2645.

Ta. timir (-v-, -nt-) to smear as sandal paste, rub, apply to (as a flower to the skin), throw or scatter as on one’s body; (-pp-, -tt-) to rub, besmear: beat; tuvar (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to smear. Ka. timir to rub and clean the skin; tiguru to: rub, annoy, rub on as an unguent; tiguru, timir, tivir an unguent, perfume; temar to rub, rub out, destroy; tevaru to rub, tease, thwart. DED 2646.

Ta. timir numbness, stiffness from cold, dullness, sluggishness, anaesthesia; (-pp-, -tt-) to be numbed as a limb, be paralysed, be stupified; timircci, timirppu numbness, etc.; timiran dull, slow, inactive person. Ma. timir numbness. Te. timuru, timiri, timmiri numb- ness, palsy. Malt. tame to tingle (as a limb when asleep). DED(S) 2647. 3236 Kur. dhimitdhimit tkna, dhimirdhi- mired to walk with sonorous steps, step noisily, walk heavily (as an old man or a man carrying a burden) (for &kna, see 871). Malt. dimdimre to sound (as footsteps); dim-qerge to sound as when larger objects fall; dip-qerge to sound as when smaller objects fall (for gerge, see 1960). timilai [281] tiral
Ta. timilai a kind of drum, Ma. timila id. /Cf. Skt. timila- a musical instrument; Pkt. timila-, timila- id. DED(S) 2648.
Te. timman male of a species of monkey. Te. timmddu, timmanna, tippa- trimmari a monkey. DED 2649.

Ka. dimmi an eminence, elevated spot; temar rising ground, hillock; (Havs.) dimba bank of a river. Te. dimma any elev- ation or eminence, mound. Pa. demma elevated ground; nir demma island. Kuwi (Mah.) damaka flat ground on top of a moun- tain. ? Ta. timil hump of bullock. Cf. 3229 Ta. tippai. DED(S) 2650.

Ka. dimmu giddiness, confusedness, Te. dimmu, dimma giddiness; dimmari, dim- maridu a giddy-headed person. DED 2651. 3241 Kur. timmna to bungle at husking paddy, break the grains in the process of pounding; timmrmna (paddy grains) to be brittle. Malt. teme to prove false (as an arrow not going off). DEDS 519. _ 3242 Ta. tirakku (tirakki-) to search, inquire. Ma, tirayuka, tirakkuka to seek; tiraccal a search; tirayikka to cause a search to be made. DED 2652,

Ma. tirakkuka to press; tirakku thronging, pressing.
