Ga, (OIL) tufi-, (P.) tuyy- to go to sleep; (S.) tun sleep. Go. (W. Ph.) sunj-, (G. L. M) hunjana to sleep; (Mu.) hunj- id., lie down; caus. hunjih-; (Ma. Ko.) unj- to sleep; (Mu.) huskar sleep (Voc. 3437); (Elwin) huskar, (Ma.) uskay, uskand id. (Voc. 3578); (LuS.) hunchkaree id. Konda (Sova dial.) sus- (-t-) to sleep; (BB) sunz- (-it-) id.; (BB) stskanku sleepiness, sleepy. eyes. Pe. hunj- (hunc-) to sleep; jtc- (jlec-) to close the eyes, Mand. hunj- to sleep; hiskanke sleepiness, sleepy eyes. Kui sunja (sunji-) to sleep; m. sleep: stlsa (sUsi-) to close the eyes; pl. action sttska (sski-); silsa Kang
Ta. tutam, tufavu a liquid measure. Ma. tufam id., 4 of an Idangari; tufaku a small earthen vessel holding two nari, chiefly for toddy. DED 2694.

Ta. tufari, tofari sp. jujube, Zizyphus rugosa; a thorny straggling shrub, Scutia indica, Ma, tutari a thorn with edible fruit, Rhammus circumscissus; Z. jujuba, ete. [S. indica Brongn. = R. circumscissus Linn.] DED 2695.

Ta. tuti (-pp-, -tt-) to quiver, tremble, throb, palpitate, be in great flurry or agitation, be eager, suffer acutely (hunger), be rude, mischievous, roguish, be fidgety, giddy, glitter; om quivering, trepidation, speed, quickness, acuteness of intellect, cleverness, industry, superiority, strength; tutituti (-pp-, -tt-) to be in a great flurry, fret and fume; tufippu flurry, diligence, trembling, palpi- tation, pride, arrogance, anger, whirl (as of a whip); tufiyan man of irritable temper, wicked mischievous person; tufukku insolence, surliness, wickedness, mischief, quickness, expedition, activity. Ma. tufikka to throb, quiver, splash violently. Ka, dudi to throb and pain as a boil; dudita throbbing (of boil); duduku to act sashly, without deliberation, impudently, violently, profligately or wick- ediy; . rashness, insolence. Tu, duduku, dudukutana hurry, rashness, violence, in- justice, pride; dudukuni to be rash or hasty, be violent, haughty; dunipuni to struggle, strive, flutter, flap the wings in agitation as a bird, bustle, be in a hurry; dunipely strug- gling, striving, fluttering, bustling; dunipele bustler, busybody. Te. duduku = rashness, inconsiderateness, precipitancy or precipi tation; todibadu to be perplexed, confused. DED(S) 2696.

Ta. tutiyan industrious, busy person. Ka, dudi to labour, acquire by one’s labour or efforts; dudita acquisition, gain; dudime acquiring, requisition, gain. Tu. dudiyuni, dudipini to acquire, gain, earn, obtain; dudita, dudité gain, acquisition, property. ? Cf. 3524 Ta. toril. DED 2697.

Ta. tuti lip. Ma. cofi id. Ko. tuc id. Ka, tuti, (HavS. Hal.) todi id. Tu. dudi id., (B-K.) snout of an animal. Kor. (M.) tondi lip. Go. (M.) fote id. (Voce. 1523); (Tr) toddi (pl tork) mouth, face; (L.) toddi, foddi id; (A. Ch.) toddi, (SR. Y.) toddi mouth; (Ph.) tuddi, fudi mouth, face; (W.) tudi mouth; (G. M.) fodi, (Mu. Ma.) toddi, (S.) foddi, toddi mouth, face (Voc. 1527). Kui toda lip. Malt. toro mouth; toto beak, bill, Cf. 2664 Ta. cuntu, contu, and 3311 Ta. tuntam. / Connections among the NIA items in Turner, CDIAL, no. 5853. DED (S, N) 2698.

Ta. tuti a small drum shaped like an hour-glass; drummer; tufiyan drummer; tufu- mai a kind of drum. Ma. tufi a small drum shaped like an hour-glass. Ka. tudubu a kind of drum; (Hav.) dudi drum. Kod. dudi id. Tu. didumbu a big drum; (B-K.) dudi Adi Dravi- das’ long drum. Te. tudumu a kind of drum, tomtom. Go. (SR) tudum drum; (Grigson) turam kettle drum (Voce. 1740). Konda tirmi a small drum. Kui (K.) tudumi a kind of drum. /Cf. Mar. tudtim, tudaib a sort of kettle-drum. DED(S) 2699.
Ta. tufi cardamom plant. Ma. tufi cardamoms. /Cf. Skt. trufi- small carda- moms. DED(S) 2700.

Ta. tutuppu spatula, ladle, oar; tufuvai wooden ladle for taking ghee; (Koll.) tufippu boating stick (?). Ma. tutuppu stirrer, spatula, paddle. Ka. tudupu oar, flat wooden spoon. ? Cf. 3356 Ta. tura. DED 2701. 3300 7a. tufum-enal onom. expr. signi- fying jumping sound, as into water. Ka. dudum, dudhum imitation of the sound of a body suddenly falling or plunging into water; dudhum igi to plunge. Kol. dudm- (dudumt-) to swim. DED 2702.

Ta. tufai (-pp-, -tt-) to wipe, wipe off, scour, scrub, sweep, brush, dry by wiping as wet hair, polish, rub, apply, wipe out, ruin, destroy; tutaippam broom, besom; tofai (-pp-, -tt-) to wipe, wipe off, dust. Ma. tutekka to wipe, rub off, clean, extinguish; tufeppam, tufeppu broom; tuteppikka to get something wiped. Ka. tode to smear, besmear, daub, anoint, rub on (as oil on the hand), apply (as whitewash), wipe, remove by rubbing (as tears), wipe off, remove, cfface, obliterate, destroy; todasu, todayisu, todisu to wipe, etc., cause to smear or apply to (as chunam, cowdung, etc.}; todaka smearing. Tu, todepuni, tudepuni to anoint, paint, smear, rub. Te. tud(ujcu to wipe, rub, sweep, clean as any surface, wipe off or away, rub off or out, sweep or sweep away as dust, ete., from a surface; tud(u)pu wiping, rubbing out, blotting out, scoring out or correction in writing; todayu to wipe, wash. Ga. (Oll.) turs- to rub. Go. (Koya Su.) turs- to dry the body with a cloth after bathing. DED(N) 2703.

Ta. tufai, tofai thigh. Ma. tuta id. To. twar id. Ka, tode id. Kod. tode id. Tu. tudé thigh, shank, side piece. Te. toda thigh; tunfi hip, haunch. Kol. dut (pl duft) hip. Kui (K.) dondo thigh. Kuwi (S.) tuntu hip. Cf, 2840 Ka. sonfa. DED (S) 2704.

Ta. tutfu money of the value of 2 or 4 pies, money. Ma. tuffu a copper coin = 20 cash or $ pice. Ka, duddu a copper coin, 4 of an anna; money. Kod. duddi 4 pice e pies); money. Tu. duddu a copper coin worth 4 pies, copper coin in general, money. Te, duddu a coin of the value of 2 pies, and in some places of 4 pies; money, wealth. DED 2705. 3304 7a. tuttu-kkaffai, tuf¢u-ttafi short club, Ka. dodde the bat used at tipcat. Te. duddu a stout stick, club, cudgel; dudda- garra a stout stick or staff. Go. (S. M.) dudu tuni [289] stick; (M.) diidi id., cane (Voc. 1568). Konda dudu stout stick. Kui dita stick, staff, rod. sd (Isr.) dudu carrying-stick. DED(S)

Ta. tuni (-v-, -nt-) to be sundered, cut, severed, be removed, be torn, become clear, resolve, determine, ascertain, conclude; com- mence; (-pp-, -tt-) to cut, sever, cut off; 7, Piece, slice, chop, fragment, bit, morsel; tunikkai small piece, slice; tuniyal, tunukku, tupukkai small piece as of flesh; tunivu ascertainment, certainty, determination, de- cision, conclusion, opinion; piece. Ma. tuni piece; tuniyuka to cut short, decide. Ka. tunaka, tunaku, tunuku, tulaku fragment, piece, bit. Te. tuniya piece, bit, fragment; funiyu, tundgu to be cut or broken to pieces; tun(u)ka piece, bit, fragment; tun(u)mu to cut; tuttuniyalu small pieces, bits or frag- ments; ? trufgu to break, fall in pieces, perish, die; ? t(ryuncu to cut to pieces, tear, break, kill (or trufgu, truncu with 3339 Ta. tura). Vk. tunke half portion (of bread). Go, (L.) tunki a piece (Voe. 1744), Konda tuns- (-t-) to be broken in pieces (as a stick), become worn out (as a cloth); tunp- to make holes (in clothes), break into small pieces (as a stick). Pe, {Un- (fUf-) to cut, slaughter, sacrifice. Mand. tin- id. Kui tunu (pl. ftinga) log of wood, large beam or block of wood. Kuwi (Su.) tun- (-h-) to cut (with axe); (F.) ttrhali to behead; (S.) tun- to slay; tlnh- to slaughter; tOnpu the slaughter; (Mah.) tunpu a cut; (Isr.) {Un- (-h-) to cut, kill. ? Cf. 3310 Ta. tuntam. DED(S) 2707. 3306 Ta, tuni Gv-, -nt-) to dare, venture; tunivu confidence, boldness, daring, bravery; tuniccal, tunikaram daring; tunikari (-pp

Ta. tuni cloth for wear, hangings, pendants, decorations as of cloth, flag of a cat, bark-cloth. Ma. tuni cloth. Ko, tuny priest’s cloak (worn only at the milk-placing ceremony); Toda priest’s garment. To. tiny dairyman’s black garment of a single cloth. 5085 tuni cloth. Kor, (M.) tundu cloth. DED

Ta. tunai association, company, help, assistance, support, protection, companion, escort, friend, pair, couple, husband, wife, mate, brother or. sister, comparison, simili- tude, conjugal union; (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to resemble, be like; tunaimai union, help, ability, power; tunaivan husband, friend, companion, minister, helper, assistant, brother, cousin; tunaivi wife, sister, heroine’s confidante, lady’s maid; tunar (-v-, -nt-; “pp-, -tt-) to cluster as flowers: 7. flower, bunch of flowers, pollen of a flower, bunch of fruit; tunari bunch of flowers; tunai (-pp-, -tt-) to string as a garland; tunaiyal garland, wreath of flowers. Ma, tuna match, com- panion, help, assistance, guard, convoy; tungan tunavan friend; tunekka to help, succour, protect, accompany. Ka. tone likeness, patity, equality. Kog. tone a person’s support (physical or another person). Malt. tunge to collect; tungre to be collected, assemble. ? Cf. 3563 Ta. toran. DED 2710,

Ka. dundage, dundige, dunduge roundly, round, roundness, state of being without corners, well-proportioned, regular, etc.; dundu id., a female’s holiow, plain bracelet; dundane, dundanna, dundane round; dundisu to become round, form round masses, move circularly. Tu, dundu round, globular. DED 2711. -

Ta. tunfam piece, fragment, bit, slice, small piece of cloth, section, division, compartment, small plot of field, piece of fish-meat; tunfi (-pp-, -tt-) to cut, sever, tear up, divide, separate, cut short one’s words, speak in few words, dispute, disprove, rebuke; be cut off, detached, broken; , detached piece of high land left waste; tuntu piece, bit, fragment, slice, section, division, strip, chit, ticket, smail piece of cloth, towel. Ma. tup- fam piece, bit, slice; tunfikka to cut to pieces, cut off as the throat. Ko. tund picce. To, tud id.; cloth worn from armpits to knees by Badaga woman (< Badaga tundu). Ka, tundisu to cut or break into pieces, make piecemeal; tundu fragment, piece, bit, a little. Kod. tund- (tundi-) to break (intr; thread, rope, flower-stem, tip); tundi piece. Tu. tundu, sundu picce, slice. 7e. tunta, tundamu piece, fragment; tundincu to cut, sever; tundu, tunde, tundemu piece. Py. funda strip of cloth used as bandage. Malt. turge to break (as a stick); turgre to be broken; tufgro broken. 7? Br, turinging - to become undone (of stitches), clear (of clouds), separate (of milk). ? Cf. 3305 Ta. tuni. DED (N) 2712. ,

Ta. tuntam beak, bill, nose, ele- phant’s trunk;” tonfai, tonfalam elephant’s trunk. Ko. dund ‘id. Ka, tondalu, tondlu, tondla, sundalu, sundil, sundila, sondalu, sondilu, sondlu id.; (Coorg) dundu face of a cow, beak. Kod. dundi snout, face (insult- ing). Tu. sundily, sondily elephant’s probos- cis. Te. tondamu id. Nk. sondam elephant’s trunk. Go, (L.) ondi id. (Voc. 406). Cf.

Ta. tuti point, sharp edge. Ka. tudi extremity, end, point, top, tip; tuftfatudi, tuttatudi the very point or end. Tu. tudi point, end, extremity, top. Te. tuda end, extremity, tip; tudi end, termination; tuffa- tuda the very end or extremity; suda end, tip, point. Malt. tota point, poited. DED(S) 2716.

Ta. tutai (-v-, -nt-) to be crowded thick, close, intense, abound, be copious; (-pp-, -tt-) to press together; 1. closeness, crowded state; ? tatai Gv-, -nt-) to be thick- set, crowded, densely packed; (-pp-, -tt-) to be full, abundant; ? tatumpu (tatumpi-) to become full. Ko. tod- (tody-) to cook (grain or flour) into solid mass by compressing it with a stick. DED(S) 2717.

Ta. tutt¥ri a kind of bugle-horn. Ma. tuttari horn, trumpet. Ka. tutiri, tuttari, tuttitri a long trumpet. Tu. tuttari, tuttiri trumpet, horn, pipe. Te. tutar@ a kind of trumpet. /Cf. Mar. tutari a wind instrument, a sort of horn. DED 2718.

Ta. tutti wrinkled-leaved evening mallow, Abutilon asiaticum; country mallow. Te. tutturu-benda, (SAN) tutti, tuttiri A. indicum, Indian matlow. DED(S) 2719. 3318 Ta, tutturu ornament like the peri- carp of a lotus; tuttu-kkammal ear ornament worn by women. ? Ke duddu the inner pulpy mass of a cucumber or pumpkin that contains the seeds. Te. duddu pericarp of a lotus: a sort of ear ornament worn by women. DED 2720.

Tu. tuttuni to dress, wear, clothe; tuttu dressing; tuttavuni to cause to dress, clothe; (B-K.) tuttu, suttu, huttu to wear clothes by tying them round the waist (or suttu, huttu with 2715 Ta. curru). Kor. (T.) ttiindi to wear. Pa. tund- to wear (jacket, etc.); caus. tundip- (tundit-). DED 2721.

Ka. dundu excessive expenditure, waste, prodigality; dundugara spendthrift, prodigal. Tu. dundu prodigal, wasteful; dundugare a prodigal, squanderer. DED 2722. 3321 Kur. tundnad to be poured out, spill, pour into; tundrna to be poured out, be spilt. Afalt. tunde to spill, shed, throw out as water; tundgre to be spilt, be shed. DED 2723.

To. topy shrub, bush. Te. tuppa small bush; dubbu bunch or tuft of grass, small shrub. Kol. tuppa bird’s nest. Pa. tuppa tuft of reeds or similar plants. Ga. (5.3) tuppa (290) tumpi shrub. Go. (Ko.) tuppa nest (Voc. 1745). Konda tupa, dubu bush, shrub; (BB) dupa shrub. Kui duba bush, tuft, cluster, clump of trees. Kuwi (Isr.) dupa small thick bushes; (S.) relli tuppa bulrush (see 5171). Malt. dopeli a smali bunch. DED(S) 2724.

Ta. tuppu (tuppi-) to spit; nm. spittie; tuppal saliva, spittle. Ma. tuppuka to spit; tuppu, tuppal spittle. To. tif in- (id-) to spit. Ka, t& imit. sound of spitting and puffing away with the breath; tilntiri to spit; tiipu to spit, blow, puff away. Kod. tupp- (tuppi-) to spit. Te. tupukku, tuppu the sound made in spitting suddenly; (K.) tuppuna with the sound tuppu; tipoducu (i.e. t-poducu) to spit. Go. (Mand.) tuhkul spit, saliva (Voc. 1765). Pe. cup- (-t-) to spit. Kui silpa (stpi-) id.; m=. expectoration. Kuwi (IF. htipali to spit; htpki aiyali to vomit; (S.) htpinai to spew; htpkinai to vomit; htlpki vomiting; (Su.) hip- (it-) to spit; htpka (pl) saliva, spittle; (isr.) htp- (-it-) to spit; htpki- to vomit; hipka vomit, spittle; (Mah.) dopoki spittle. Kur. tuppna to spit; tuppalxd saliva, spittle (for -xd, see 2249 Ta. kOgai). Malt. tupe to spit; tupgle, tulgpe splittle (or with

Ta. tumi (-v-, -nt-) to be cut off, severed, perish, be crushed; (-pp-, -tf-) to cut off, saw, keep off, obstruct; cut, severance. Te. tumuru a small piece or bit; tuttumuru small bits or fragments, powder, dust. DED (S) 2727.

Ka. dumuku, dumiku to leap or jump down from above; dum, dhum sound in imitation of that produced by a body going or coming down suddenly from above; dumikku, dummikku, dhummikku to leap or jump down with the sound dum, etc.; dhum- mene with the sound dhum. Tu. dumukuni to jump down, alight quickly. Te. dumuku, dfiku to leap, jump, leap over, leap down from; #. a leap, jump; dumukul-ddu to leap or skip about, caper, frisk. Pe. dum- (-t-) to ascend, climb; caus. dup- (-t-). Mand. dum- to ascend, climb, DED(S) 2728. 3326A Ka, dumpa a root. Te. dumpa a bulbous root, bulb. Ga. (P.) dumpa root, tuber. DED 2729,

Ta. tumpi bee, male bee, dragon-fly. Ma, tumpi a black beetle flying at night, dragon-fly. Ko. tib beetle. To. tiiby sp. honey- making insect. Ka. tumbi, tumbe, dumibi, dumbe a large black bee resembling the humble-bec; (Bell.; U.P.U.) dummi a kind of bee. Kod. tumbi sp. flying beetle. Tu. tumbi gna tumpi , [291] tura the black bee. Te. tummeda a large black humble-bee. Pa. dumdi beetle. Go. (Ph.) tuhmeli_ sp. hornet (Voc. 1766). Konda (Sova dial.) tumberi bee. Kur, dumba wasp, hornet; dumbhard sp. of very long hornet; (Hahn) tumba black wasp; tumbil wasp. Malt, tumbe wasp; tumbrare black wasp. / Cf. Pkt. tum billi- honeycomb. DED(S) 2731.

Ta. tumpi Diospyros tomentosa: Ceylon ebony, D. ebenum: tumpili Coro- mandel ebony, D. melanoxylon; D, tupru; tumpai D. tomentosa. Ka. tumaki, tumati, tumbara, tumbari, tumburu, tUpare, titbare wild mangosteen tree, D. embryopteris Pers. (= Embryopteris glutenifera Roxb.) Te. tubiki, tumiki D. embryopteris; tumida D. melanoxylon. Kol. (Kin.) tumki id. Nk tumki id. Nk. (Ch.). tumik, turmig tendu tree. Pa. tumbri (stem tumbr-) D. melan- oxylon, Ga. (S.*) tumur ebony. Go. (Tr.) tumri-marya, (A. Y. Ch.) tumri, (W. Ph.) tumri, (G. Mu.) tumir, (Ma.) tumeri, (M.) tumer id., tendu tree, D. melanoxylon (Voe. 1751). Kui duri, dureni, (K.) dtri Coro- mandel cbony. Kuwi (P. 8.) dari ebony. /Cf. Skt. tumburu-, tumburi-, tubari- [cori- ander or] the fruit.of D. embryopteris; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5872. [Five species of Diospyros: D, tomentosa Roxb.; D. ebenum Koenig; D. melanoxylon Roxb.; D. tupru puch-Ham.; D, embryopteris Pers.} DED(S)

Ta. tumpi-kkai, tumpiccan-kai, tum- picci-kkai clephant’s trunk; tumpi elephant. Ma, tumpi-kkai elephant’s trunk. Kod. tumbi- kay id.; ba+-tumbi bird’s tail (ba-lf tail), Tu, oy tumbi elephant’s trunk. DED(N) 3331 7a. tumpai assembly, crowd. Jr. thumba much. Ko. tub- (tuby-) to be filled full; tube- (tube-) to fill (#.); tumn full. Ka. tumbu to become full, filled up, complete, abound, be amassed, become plump and strong; ”. becoming full; tumbisu to fill; tumbike repletion; tombe multitude, assem- blage, host, cluster, etc.; tombara mass, heap (of grain); timbu to fil (intr., tr.). Kod. dum b- (dumbi-) to become full; (Shanmugam) dumba much. Ju. tumbuni to be filled; tumbavuni to cause to fill; tumukuni, timu- kuni, timuruni, (B-K. also) dimuku to throng, crowd; tombara abundant, much. Kor, (M.) tumdi to fill. Cf. 3509 Ma. toppan. DED(S) 2735, 2739.

Ta. tumpu dust. Ka. dumbu id. Te. dummu, dumarumu, duvva id.; (VPK) tva, tuva, tuvva, dubba fine sand; (B.) turumu powder; vb, to reduce to powder. Kol. dubba small dust cloud; (Kin.) dust. Go. OL.) duba dust; (SR.) duba id., earth; (S.) dumu dust (Voc. 1571); (Ko.) dumul id. (Voc. 1876); (ASu.) dubba dirt. Kuwi (S.) dtmbru id. DED(S) 2736.
Ta. tumpu rope; frayed ends, as of a gut; border, fringe. Ma. tumpu rope; tumpu, tunpu extremity, end of a rope or string. DED 2737,

Ta. tumpai white dead nettle, Leucas; bitter toombay, L. aspera. Ma. tumpa Phlomis or L. indica. To. tib various Leucas species (zeylanica, aspera, linifolia) and Anaphilis species (neelgerriana, Bournei). Ka. tumbe, tumbi P. indica Lin.; the very common weed L. linifolia. Tu. també, tumbi P. indica. Te. tummi L. cephalotes. [Leucas = Phlomis.| DED 2738.
Te. tumma babool tree, Acacia arabica. Kol. (SR.) tumba id. (Kamaleswaran). Ga. (S.7) tumba maran, (S.3) tumba id. Go, (Koya Su.) tumma id. (< Te.). DEDS(N) 526.

Ta. tummu (tummi-), tumpu (tumpi-) to sneeze; tummu, tummal sneeze, sneezing, Ma, tummuka, tumpuka to sneeze; tumekka (cattle) snort; tuvekka to sneeze, snort. Ko, tub- (tuby-) to sneeze. To, tiib- (tiiby-) id, Kod. timm- (timmi-), (Mercara dialect) tumm- (tummi-) id. Tu. tumbily sneezing, sneeze. Te, tummu to sneeze; n. sneeze. Kol tum- (tumt-) to sneeze. Nk. tum a sneeze. Nk. (Ch.) tam- to sneeze. Pa, tumm- id.; tumkud a sneeze, sneezing. Ga. (Oll.) tum-, (S.) tumm- to sneeze. Go. (Ko.) tum a sneeze (Voce, 1746); (Ph.) tuhkana, (Mu.) tuh-, (S,) tuhk-, (L.) tukhina to sneeze; (Tr.) tuhkang to cough (of cattle in the rainy season); (Ph.) tuhk a sneeze (Voc, 1763). Konda tup- (-t-) fo sneeze; tumbu (pl. tupku) a sneeze. Pe. tum- (-t-) to sneeze. Mand. tum- id. Kui (K.) tumb- (-it-) id. Kuwi (Su.) tumm-, (F.) tth- mali, (S.) ttminai id.; (S.) tamu a sneeze. Kur. tum’nd (tummyas) to sneeze; tum’ta’ana to cause one to sneeze. Malt. tume to sneeze. DED (S) 2740.

Ko. tusn- (tu-nd-) to pull along (something or someone), rape. Ka. tuyi (tuyid-, t0d-) to pull, draw, stretch, ? Te. duyyu, duyyu, dtyu, dfyu to draw as a sword out of the scabbard, remove or strip of leaves, etc., as a twig by drawing it through the hand or between the fingers. Go. (Ko.) duy- to strip (leaves of bough) (Voc. 1575; < Te.), DED(S) 2741. 3338 Ta, tuyya pure, holy; conclusive, certain; tuppu cleanness; f purity, cleanliness, immaculateness, that which is pure, bright- ness, whiteness; ticu cleanness; tlytu that which is pure, clean, immaculate, holy; tdymai, timai purity, cleanness, immaculate- ness, holiness, truth, salvation, goodness; tiya clean, pure, holy; tdyavan, tfyan pure and holy man, sage, ascetic; tttyal pure, spotless woman. Ma. tuyya fair, spotless; tf pure, bright; tuyma, ttma purity, perfec- tion. ? Br, tUbé moon (cf. sv. 5496(a) Ta. vel). DED 2742.

Ta. tuyya). Cf. 5504 Ta. vellari. / Cf. Pkt. (DNM) villa- bright, clear; vilha- white. DED(S, N) 4524. (6) Ta. ven-ney, vennai butter. Ma, venna. Ko. ven. To. pen. Ka. benne, benni. Kod. bonne ney. Ju. benpé, bonneyi. Te. venna. Cf. 3746 Ta. ney. DED 4511.

Ta. tura (-pp-, -nt-) to tunnel, bore: turappu tunnel; turappanam auger, drill, tura {292} tuvar tool for boring holes; turuvu (turuvi-) to bore, drill, perforate, scrape out as the pulp of a coconut; #. hole, scraping, scooping; turuval scrapings as of coconut pulp, boring, drilling. Ma. turakka to bury, undermine; turappanam carpenter’s drill, gimlet; turappan a bandicoot rat; turavu burrowing, mine, hole; tura hole, burrow. Ka. turi, turuvu to holiow, bore, drill, make a hole, grate, scrape as fruits, scrape out asa kernel out of its shell; n, grating, scraping out, etc. Tu, turipini, turipuni, turupuni to bore, perforate, string as beads; turiyuni, turuvuni to be bored, perforated, be strung. Te. turumu to scrape with a toothed instrument as the kernel of a coconut; ? trufigu to break, fall in pieces, perish, die; ? t(r)uncu to cut to pieces, tear, break, kill (or trufgu, truncu with 3305 Ta. tuni). Pa, turu soil dug out in a heap by rats. Konda (BB, 1972) truk- (-t-) (pig) to root up earth with snout. Kui trupka (< truk-p-; . trukt-) to bore, pierce; truspa (trust-), tuspa (tust-), to pierce a hole, breach; triva (trat-) to be pierced, holed; trunga (trungi-) to become a hole, be pierced. Kur. trn% to pierce through, perforate. Malt. ture to scratch out; turge to bury the ashes of the dead; ? tunga, tungra hollow of a bamboo or bridge, tube, tunnel. DED(S) 2743.

Ta. tura (-pp-, -nt-) to drive as an elephant, beat away as flies, shoot as an arrow, propel, disperse, scatter, direct, urge, encourage, drive in a nail, hammer; turattu (turatti-) to drive away, chase out, scare off as beasts, birds, remove, reject, expel as a servant, pursue as a thief, drive, cause to move fast as bullocks; turappu driving, hammering, discharging, dispelling, chasing, scaring away. Ma, turattuka to drive away. Ka. dobbu, dobbu, dabbu, dabbu to shove, push, thrust, throw down from above, put. Tu, dobbu pushing, shoving; dobbuni to push, shove, put off. Te, trdcu to push, shove, thrust; trd-padu to be pushed or driven; trd-patu. being pushed or driven; trd-padu to push; trdpu, tropudu a push, disregarding, discarding, prohibition, sub- traction, deduction; troyu to push, propel, thrust, shove, drive, reject, dismiss, eject, throw out, subtract, deduct; t(r)dlu to drive, drive away or out, exorcise; d(robbu to push, shove, thrust; #2. a push, thrust, shove. Nk. dhobb- to push. Pa, turkip- (turkit-) to push, shove. Ga, (S.) turus key- to push in, shove; (S.2) turuyp- to push into something. Go. (Ma.) ro?-, (Pat.) roppana, (L.) rosnd to drive (Voc. 3067). Kui tropa (trdt-) to press something forward with the fingers, massage. Kuwi (D.) trd- (-t-) to poke (fire). DED(S, N) 2744, DED(N) 2891.

Ta. turavu sultan-well, large well for irrigation purposes. Ma. turavu a large well. Te. doravi, doravu id.; doruvu id., a tank, a dock for ships. DED 2745. 3342 Ma, turala, torala cold, catarrh. Tu. toralé, (B-K.)} sorale muscus of the nose. DED(N) 2746.

To. turu rust, verdigris, flaw; ¢urucu, turuci blue vitriol, spot, dirt, blemish, stain, defect, rust; turicu. fault, crime, sorrow, affliction, perversity, blue vitriol; tukku, tuppu rust. Ma. turisu blue vitriol; turumpu, turuvu rust. Ka, tukku rust of iron; tutta, tuttu, tutte blue vitriol, Tu. tukky rust; mair(y)suttu, (Eng.-Tu. Dict.) mairytuttu blue vitriol. Te. t(ruppu rust; (SAN) trukku id., verdigris. /Cf. Skt. tuttha- blue vitriol; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5855. DED(S) 2747.
Ta. turutti islet in a river. Ma. turutti island, DED 2748.
Ta. turuppu-kktftu, turumpufan un- winnowed heap of grain. Ma. turumpu heap of threshed grain. Tu. tuppé stack, heap of corn or rice. DED 2749.

Ta. turumpu bits of straw, refuse stalks as. of sugar-cane; tural rubbish of dry leaves; tukku meanness, worthlessness, useless person or thing. Ma. turumpu straw, awn, Tie turumbu chaff, (B-K.) chaff and waste. Te. tukku, tukkudu rubbish, trash; trashy, worth- less; ? duggu spoiled or musty straw, Pa. turti rubbish, refuse. Ga. (Oll.) tur weed, grass. Go. (Ko.) tog dust, dirt, rubbish (Voc. 1798). Konda turi rubbish, sweepings, trash. Pe. tuker dirt. Kui turki refuse heap, manure. Kuwi (Su.) truki, (Mah.) turki refuse; (Isr.) turki/traki place for village rubbish. DED(S, N) 2750, DED(S) 2688.

Ta. turuvu (turuvi-) to scek, inquire into, search out, trace, pursue; 7. searching; turuval searching; turu-ppifi to detect, trace, search; turappu (turappi-) to seek; turavu spying; tuppu spying, investigation, sign, trace, evidence as of a crime; tumpu-pofu _to probe, spy out (Goc.); turuntu (turunti-) to explore, examine. Ka, tubbu to point out a thief or spy out a thief and make him known; be found out or discovered; 7. state of being found out or detected. 7x. tubbu dis- covery as of stolen property, detection as of an offence. DED 2751. 3348 Kol. turre pig. Nk. turre id. Nk, (Ch.) tur, turre id. Pa. turra sp. animal (not pig). Go, (Mu.) turre, (Ma.) toge animal called in Halbi kebri (Voc, 1756). Mand. turne animal called in local Oriya kebra. DED (S) 2752.

Ta. tuvahku (tuvanki-), tuvakku (tuv- akki-) to begin, enter upon; tuvakkam begin- ning, commencement. Ko, tovk- (tovky-) to begin. ? To. twi®x- (twiOxy-) to be at the point of, be ready to; twiOk- (twi®ky-) to start, begin. DED 2754,

Ta. tuvar (-v-, -nt-) to dry, wipe off moisture; become dry; n. firewood, dry tuvar {293] tuli leaves; tuvarttu (tuvartti-) to wipe off moist- ure; tuvaftu (tuvafti-) to wipe off moisture as after bathing; 2. wiping off moisture. Ma. tuvaruka, tukaruka to grow dry; tuvarttuka to make dry, wipe clean, remove superfluous water out of a rice-field; toruka to air, dry; tuvarcca, tukareca, tOrcca fair weather, cessation of rain (in the monsoon). DED(N) 2755.

Ta. tuvar astringency, astringent sub- stance; (-pp-, -tt-) to be astringent; tuvarppu astringency; tuka (-pp-, -tt-) to be bitter. Ka. tuvara, tovara, tovaru, togari, togaru astrin- gent. Tu. cogary id. Te. togara astringent taste. Kui torpa (tort-) to be astringent, have a lingering and clinging taste. / Cf. Skt. tubara-, tuvara- astringent, astringent taste; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5890. DED 2756.

Ta. tuvarai Cajanus indicus, dhal. Ma. tuvara id. To. tifiry Atylosia Candollei, jungle dhal. Ka, togari, tovati C. indicus. Kod. to-+ti be-le id. (for be-le, see 4444). Tu. togari, togaré id. Te. (SAN) togari, (VPK) togaru (togari) (pl. togallu) id. Kol, (Kin.) togar id. Nk. togari id. Go. (A.) tdri, (Y.) tOrin (pl), (Ma.) tori id. (Voc. 1770). / Cf. Skt. tubari-, tubarika- Cajanus indicus; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5866. DED(S, N) 2757.

Ta. tuval (tuvaly-, tuvan{-) to quiver, tremble. To, tu-l power of a god (in songs). Ka, t0lu, ttlaga, tina violent agitation, frenzy, demoniac possession. Tu. tila inspi- ration, enthusiasm, ecstasy. DED(S) 2758.

Ta. tuvai (-pp-, -tt-) to dip in, soak, temper (steel); (-v-, -nt-) to be dipped (as cloth in dye), be moistened with starch, be tempered (as steel). Ma. tuvekka to stcep, soak in water, temper (iron), Tu. tuva over- flowing, running over. ? Cf. 3555 Ta. toy. DED 2759. 3356 7a, tura (-pp-, -nt-) to stir as with a ladle; tugavai porridge as stirred with a ladle; tugavu (turavi-), tugavu (turdvi-), turd to stir with the hand, stir with a ladle, turn over as paddy spread in the sun, paddle or row a boat; tugai (-y-, -nt-) to propel by oars. Ma. tuga paddle, rudder: tugayuka to paddle, steer. Ka. togasu to turn round as with a ladle, row. Tu. sulayi rudder; sulavuni to row a boat; tulapini, tulavuni to row, paddle, steer. ? Cf. 3299 Ta. tutuppu. DED 2760. 3357 Ta, turay, tulaci, tulavam, tulavu sacred basil, Ocimum .sanctum Lin. Ma. tulasi. Ka. tolaci, tolace, tolafice, tolasi, tulasi. Kod. tolasi, (Shanmugam) _ tulasi. Tu. tulaci, tulasi, tulasi, tolaci. Te. tulasi. Pa, tulca. / Cf. Skt. tulasi- (Turner, CDIAL, no, 5885), from which some of the forms have been reborrowed, DED 2761. 3358 Kur. tur'xna, turxa’ana to take the skin off, shell, strip; refl.-pass. turxUrna. Br. trukking to pluck off, pluck, strip. DEDS $28. 3359 Ta, tulahku (tulanki-) to move, sway from side to side as an elephant, shake, be perturbed, be uprooted, droop; tulakku (tulakki-) to move, shake, bow, nod; 1. shaking, grief, sorrow; tulakkam_ shaking, waving, motion, agitation of mind, fear, dread, dwindling,

Ta. tulanku (tulafki-)-to shine, be bright, luminous, radiate; tulakkam_ bright- ness, splendour; tulakku (tulakki-) to polish; tulumpu (tulumpi-) to sparkle, glitter, shine; tulanku (tulanki-) to shine, glitter, be bright, be polished, be clear; tulakku (tulakki-) to polish, burnish, cause to shine, ilumine. make clear, sharpen; 7. lustre, polish; tulakkam lustre, brightness, splendour, polish, gloss, clearness. Ma. tulafifuka to glitter; tulakkuka to burnish; tulakkam splendour; tilafihuka, telainuka to shine, glitter; tilakkam, telak- kam splendour. Ka. tolagu to shine, be full of splendour; , shine, splendour; tolapu shine, lustre. Te. tulakincu to shine, rejoice; tulakimpu shining, rejoicing; (K.) tolaku to shine, be splendid. DED 2763.

Ta. tuli (-pp-, -tt-) to drip, fall in drops as rain, tears, trickle down, rain; sprinkle, let fall in drops; . raining, dripping, raindrop, globule of water, rain, small quantity; tulum- pu (tulumpi-) to brim over, overflow as tears in the eyes, melt; tulli a drop: tullam little drop of water. Ma. tuli a drop; tulikka to drop, flow freely (as toddy); tulli, (Tiyya) tolli.a drop. Ko. tolg- (tolgy-) to wash onc’s hands with tears. To. toty- (tole-) to sprinkle (intr, tr.); tolb- (tolby-) (liquid) slops over; tily a drop, Ka. tulaku to be scattered in drops, spill, run over; tuluku to sprinkle, scatter in drops, shed, throw as arrows; tulukuvike running over or out. Kod. tuli a drop of liquid. Tu. suliyuni to overflow, Kor. (T.) tolnki id. Te. toluku to rain. (K. tufir- [294] turu also) be spilled, overflow; tolakari the begin- ning of the rainy season, the rainy season; tolakarincu to tain for the first time in the season; tolukaru the rainy season (or the items tolakari to tolukaru in 3516); (K. B.) tulucu to shed, as fruit, powder, etc.; (K.) tulumu to cause leaves to fall by cutting or plucking; todaku, tondku, toléku to be slightly shaken or agitated as a liquid, be spilled; 7. slight agitation or motion in a liquid (or with 3359 Ta. tulaiku). Pa tolk- to spill (intr.); tolkip- (tolkit-), tolcip- (tolcit-) id. (t7.); tole- to spill (water out of a pot). Ga. (S.2) tolker kalam rainy season (< Te.); (S.3) tonk-ér- to trickle out. Konda tory- (-it-) (water) to be spilt; tork-

Ta. tulir- (-pp-, -tt-) to bud, sprout, shoot, put forth leaves, prosper, thrive; 7. bud, sprout, young leaf, tender foliage. Ma. tulir a bud; tulirkka to bud. Ka. suri tender sprout; (Bark.) suli, (Hal.) culi sprout; (Raba- Kavi, ESB 5.19) tolelu mango shoot. Kod. culi leaf shoot. Tu. suli a tender shoot, germ or bud. Malt, ciile to sprout; cio blade of grass or corn. Cf. 3131 Ta. talir. DED(S, N) 2765.

Ta. tulu, tulavam the Tulu country; the Tulu language. Ma. tulu Northern Kérala from Gokarna to Perumpura; tuluvan a Tulu man, Ka. tulu the Tulu country; tulava, tuluva a Tulu man, the Tulu language. Tu. tulu Tulu; of or belonging to Tulu; tuluve, tulutaye a Tulu man. DED 2766.

Ta. tullu (tulli-) to leap, frisk, spring up, jump up, be restive, trip along ina frolic- some manner, be haughty, arrogant, lead a happy-go-lucky life, tremble, quiver; . leap, jump, spring, arrogance; tullal frisking, leap- ing, dance, dancing, dancer. Ma. tulluka to frisk, jump, leap: tulfal jumping, tremor as of ague, demoniac possession; tullikka to cause to jump; tullan grasshopper; devil- dancer. Ka, tullu to roll. frisk, jump, leap. Kod. tull- (tulli-) to make small jumps. 71. tulluni, tulluni to roll, turn, leap, jump; tullely a marriage amongst Pariahs. Te. t)ullu to jump, leap, spring, caper, be arrogant or conceited: 2, jump, leap, spring, caper; arrogance, conceit; trullib6tu an arrogant or conceited person; trullinta a jump; trullagincu to jump up; truflaginta jumping up. Go. (Ko.) tul- to jump (Voc. 1761). DED(S, N) 2767.

Ta. tura (-v-, -nt-) to leave, relinquish, forsake, quit, abandon, desert, reject, discard, neglect, dispense with, omit, avoid, renounce worldly pleasures, become an ascetic; turan- tir, turantGr ascetics; turappu separation, parting, relinquishment, rejection; turavi renunciation; turavu. relinquishment, rejec- tion, renunciation; ? tuvvu (tuvvi-) to leave. ? Ko. tot- (toty-) to escape. To. twar- (twar6-) to become on bad terms with, abandon (friendship); (twart-) to make to be on bad terms (With oneself or with one another). Ka, tore to put away, abandon, quit, give up, tenounce, repudiate, reject; toravi leaving, abandoning. 7. torevuni to abandon, give up; torely abandonment. Te. (K.) tordgu, (Sank) toragu to abandon, quit, renounce; n, separation. Konda tuR- (t-) to remove (weeds, ete.). Pe. tuhi-, tussi- to throw away (combination of tuh- and hi- < si- to give, which is used as an auxiliary; tuh- appears not to be used by itself). Mand. tuh- id., (cow) to calve. Auf tuhpa (tuht-) to cast away, throw off, abandon, leave, except, relinquish, let alone, let be; #. abandon- ment, relinquishing. Kuwi (F) tssali (ttst-) to throw away; (Su.) tuh- (tust-) to throw away; also used as an auxiliary. ? Malt, tuwe to throw away, disregard. DED(S, N) 2768.
Ta. turatti. DED(S) 2925. 3548 Ta, tonfan wolf. Ka, tola id. Tu. toly, (B-K) tole id. Te. t6délu id. Br. tola jackal. DED 2926.

Ta. turu (turuv-, turr-) to be thick, crowded, full, be closed; (-pp-, -tt-) to cram as food into the mouth, stuff, press or crowd into a bag or box; m. thickness, closeness, crowdedness, eating; turumpu (turumpi-), | turumu (turumi-) to be close, crowded; turumal closcness; turupavam id., thickness, crowdedness; turuval thronging, crowding, eating; turru (turri-) to eat, seize with the mouth, lie close; #. boiled rice, food, ball of boiled rice as a mouthful, crowd, multitude; tunru (tunri-) to be close, thick, crowded together, get near, approximate, get attached to; tunrunar friends (as being near); tunnu (tunni-) to be fitted, joined, attached, be thick, crowded, press close, approach, ap- proximate, adhere to, join; tunnal being near to or close together; tunn-alar foes, enemies; tunniyar, tunninar friends, relations, ad- herents; tuvanru (tuvanri-) to fill up, be thick, close, crowded, be in company, join, be heaped up; 7. fullness. Ma. turuka to be thronged, stuffed, close, cram, push in; ¢uru, turuku a heap, a thicket overgrown with grass; turuturé throngingly, pressingly; turu- ttuka to force in, cram, stuff. Ko. turg- (turgy-} to enter into hole, sink into swampy ground; turk- (turky-) to push through a hole, cram into mouth. Ka. turuku, turaku to force or crowd things into, cram, stuff, cause to enter; turuga, turagu a throng, crowd; turugal, turufigal id., mass, thicket; turugu to be crammed, thronged, crowded, closely packed, close, amassed or plentiful, appear i

Ta. turu-turuy-enal, turu-turuy-enal, turu-tur-enal expr. signifying restlessness, impatience, state of being always in motion; tututuru (-pp-, -tt-) to be fidgety, restless, quiver (as the lips or tongue through desire to speak), be in great haste; turuturuppu restlessness, impatience, haste. Tu. turuturu swiftly, quickly; turuta, turita, turta, turtu swiftness, haste; quick, swift. Te. turutu haste, quickness; turru haste; tora-padu to hasten. DED 2771. 3369 Ta, turuttu (turutti-) to bulge, pro- trude; thrust out; turuntu (turunti-) to en- large, as a hole or cavity. Ma. turikka to project, protrude; turippikka to press out. DED 2772. 3370 Ta, turai place, location, situation, way, path (as of justice), branch, section, category, method, means, seaport, harbour, roadstead, sea, river, place where washermen wash clothes, bathing ghat, frequented place, place of meeting, branch of knowledge, sub- ject or theme, proper arrangement, codifi- cation. Ma. tura frequented place, rendezvous, harbour, place for washing clothes, natural pond or cavern, Ka, tore a stream, river. Tu. tudé id. Kor, (O.) tode river. Te. (inser.) tore (in place names). DED(S) 2773.

Ta. tunpam, tunpu affliction, sorrow, distress, trouble, pain, disease, misfortune, calamity, penury; tunpap person in distress; . tunmai harm, evil; tuni (-pp-, -tt-) to loathe, abhor, be angry at, displeased with, be sulky, as in a love-quarrel; n, disgust, dissatisfaction, loathing, anger, displeasure, affliction, sorrow, distress, discase, sin, trouble, poverty; tunippu aversion. Ma, tunpam affliction. DED 2774. (295] : tifku

Ta. til, tvu, tuvai, tuvvai flesh, meat. Ma, (DCYV) tuva raw flesh. ? Go. (A.) savi, (Y. Ch.) savvi, (Tr.) sawwi, (W.) sawi, (Ph.) savvi, (Ma.) avin, havi, (M.) having, (Ko.) aviy flesh, meat; (Mu. S.) havi id., gum of tooth (Voc, 3359). Br, st flesh, meat. ? Cf. 728 Ta. o. DED(S) 2775, 3374 Konda da- (-t-) to be well grown (as fruit, child, etc.), be well-developed; caus. dup- (t-). Pe. da- (-t-) to prow ofd; duten old man, Kuwi (Mah.) dftanju old man; dutali old woman; (Isr.) dt- (-t-) to grow old; diti old. Perhaps from 4954 *mutu- > *mdt- > dt-. DEDS 530. 3375 Ko, du+ hollow in ground at burning- place where pyre is built. Ka, (Badaga; Emen- eau, Language 15.47 [1939]) du-e burial ground. Ga. (S.) dukke obsequies. Konda dtki graveyard, crematory. Pe. duger burial place. DEDS 531. 3376 (a) Ta. tUfku (tifki-) to hang, be suspended, swing, sway from side to side as an elephant, dance, sleep, slumber, die, spin as a top, droop as a plant, be sluggish, slow; delay, procrastinate, walk or trudge slowly, test profoundly; tifkal pendant, anything suspended, drowsiness, light sleep, dullness, depression, balance, scales, dancing, a tune of slow measure; ttkku (ttkki-) to lift up, raise, hoist, weigh, balance, consider, com- pare, hang, suspend, shake, agitate; n, pen- dant, anything suspended, balance, weight. plumb-line, height, perpendicularity, steep- ness, comparison, similitude, deliberation, pondering, dancing; tikkam sleep, drowsi- ness, fatigue, weariness, laziness, droopi
Te. dilda a calf. Go. (ASu.) dudde female young of buffalo. Konda dira calf (< Te.), DEN 44.
Ta. tinfil fish-hook, fishing tackle, hook. Ma. ciintal, ciinta fishing hook. ? Cf.

Ta. tinfu (tinti-) to shoot, dis- charge, propel an arrow, command, direct, incite, goad. remind, suggest, bring to notice as by word or signal, trim a lamp; 7. exciting, rousing. Ma. clntuka to shoot with a cross- bow, catch fish, trim a lamp. Ko. tu-nd- (tuendy-) to force to an action. Ka, dtdu to push, thrust, shove away or aside, throw out of, as out of a village, caste; dildisu to cause to push, etc., cause oneself to be pushed, etc. Kod. du-d- (du-di-) to push away. Tu, diduni to thrust, push, reject. Te. dtigu to butt, push, thrust. Malt. etife to cast, throw; cufare to spirt. ? Cf. 3379 Ta. tintil. DED 2782.

Ka. diintu, diifu to walk on one leg, hop, rock; duntu having uneven legs, rocking, unevenness, Te. (K.) dufu to jump over, run away. DED 2783. 3382 Konda (K., p.123) mudi tuttad turned the backside to. Pe. tit- (-t-) to crouch down; hendru tit- to lower the buttocks, Kui tispa (tist-) to bend down and turn the back upon a person. Kuwi (Isr.) tit- (-h-) to stoop, bend down. DEDS $32.

Ko. du-dy silk-cotton tree. Ka. didi cotton after being cleancd; silk of the silk- cotton tree used as tinder. Te, dtidi cotton. Go. (Ko.) dudli down (of birds) (Voc. 1875); (ASu.) lid silk-cotton tree. DEDS(N) 533.
Ta. titu, ttitu-valai, tituni, titunai climbing brinjal, Solanum trilobatum, Ma. tUtavalam id. ? Cf. Tu. kudané, kudané the fruit of S. pubescens, DED(N) 2784.

Ta. titai small vessel made of earth, a small measure of capacity. Ma, tita milkpot, cup. Te. dutta smajl earthen pot. Kol, (SR.) dutta earthen pot. DED 2785. 3386 Konda tipi rat-trap. Kuwi (Isr.) tapi trap for fish, rats, etc.

Tu. dupé food laid out for the dead. Konda diiba spirit of the dead. Pe. duben a sacrifice for the ancestors (akor duben). DEN 45. 3388 Konda tib- (-it-) to blow with the mouth, puff, blow out (lamp). Pe. tab- (tapt-) to blow with the mouth, DEDS 534,

Ta. timpu tube, tubutarity, sluice, outlet, vent in sluice, channel for irrigation, bamboo, bamboo tube, bamboo flute, a measure of capacity for grain, leathern bucket for baling water, gateway, doorway, path, way, narrow or difficult path, defile, pass; tlm a dry measure of capacity (< Te.). Ma. ttimpu sluice, floodgate, drain, spout, perforation. Jr, tumba measure (of paddy). Ka, titbu nave of a wheel, sluice, etc., of a tank, hole or eye of an axe, hoe, etc., into which the handle goes, tube of an ear-orna- (ur [297] tOru ment; tumbu a bore, tube, nave of the wheel through which the axle passes, outlet, sluice, watercourse; timbu (Hav.) outlet for a tank, (Gowda) sluice. Tu, tlmbara eye of a spade; timbary big hole, sluice, gutter; t&bé hole; tambu, tiimbé, simbu, sumbu sluice, gutter; sumbé small hole in a wall. Te. tdparamu hole; tlhmu a measure varying in capacity for different localities; a sluice, floodgate, drain, watercourse, outlet, bore, hole. Pa. tum botta, tum bukka hollow trunk of tree used for draining water off fields (Halbi tum); ttmu a measure, Go. (Mu.) tum hollowed trunk of tree for draining water (Voc. 1747). Konda tum a measure of grain equal to four seers. Malt. tumbra tube, tunnel; tombra tube, / Cf. Mar. tttb the nave of a wheel (Turner, CDIAL, no. 5869), whence this meaning in Ka. DED (S) 2786.

Ta. tiir (-v-, -nt-) to be filled up, be closed, choked up; (-pp-, -tt-) to fill up, close up as a well, choke up as a pit, hide, cover; n. rubbish at the bottom of a well, dregs, mud; tUrvu filling up, closing up as of a weil with rubbish; tUrvai accumulation of rubbish in a well, loosened earth from digging or ploughing, rubbish such as dry sticks, straws, dry leaves, etc.; tral filling up. Ma. ttruka to be filled up as a well; ttirkka to fill up; tuvaruka to be filled up as wells, etc. Kur. clrna to get obstructed, blocked up as a rat- hole, pipe, etc.; cUrta’4na to obstruct, block up. DED 2787.
Ka. dille itching, lust. Te. dtila itching, itch. Pa, dulkarfiid, duladama_ cow-itch (karfiid a kind of creeper). Go. (LuS.) doorwa oan cowhage. Konda dila itch. DED(S)

Ta. tival feather, feather of an arrow, quill pen, painter’s brush of cat’s or squirrel’s hair, sprout, shoot; tivi feather or down of birds, peacock’s tail, swan’s down, swan, quill pen; ti feather, plumage; tuy cotton. Ma. tival feather, pen, quill, an arrow’s feather, a painter’s brush; toppa wool, animal’s hair; toppal feather. To.. tu-fy feather, bird’s tail. Ka. tippur a bird’s wing or feather; tuppaga the plumage on some pigeons’ legs; tuppur a feather, the soft plumage or down of birds; the fine soft hair of rabbits; tupparu wool, fine soft hair as of cats; tuppafa, tubata wool. Kod. toppita feather. Tu. tuyi, (B-K. also) suyi feather, quill, the plume of birds; (Bhattacharya) slvi feather. Kor. (M.) cippudu id. Te. tiniga, tUniga dragon-fly; (SAN) truppudu feather, hair, down. Go. (Ma.) tog) (vi. tohku) large feather (Voc. 1823); (Mu.) tokenj, (Ma.) tokonji feather (Voc. 1797). Mand. tiku id. ‘/ Cf. Skt. ttila- cotton; Turner, CDIAL, no. $904. DED(S, N) 2790.

Ta. tilvu (tfvi-) to sprinkle, strew, scatter, spread out as grain for fowls, shower forth as arrows, put loosely in a measure as flour while measuring, strew or offer flowers in worship, rain; tilval sprinkling, spilling, drizzling, little drops of water, raindrops, rain, drizzle; tUvanam drizzie, rain driven in or scattered about in fine drops by the wind, place where a cascade falls, as the place of spray. Ma. ttikuka to strew, spill, shower; tiivuka to be spilled; scatter (tr); tavadnam rain driven by the wind; tikkuka to spill, scatter. To. tu-f- (tu-fy-) to spread (grain in sun to dry, husks for buffalo to eat). Tu. disuni to sprinkle. DED 2791.

Ka. tl to go, move or praceed off, drive away, pursue; tilu to go off or away, rush forward, attack, chase, recoil; tildu to drive away, remove, scatter; tilisu to cause to proceed, spur on. Te. (K.) titlu to be scattered as army in battle, be chased, run away; (K.) til(wjcu to chase. Kol. tu-l- (tu-¢-) to run; tu-Ip- (tu-lupt-) to make to run. Nk, til- (tO{-) to run, flow. Nk. (Ch) {U- (tf-) to run; ti. to drive away. Pa, tdl- to run, run away. ? Go. tUr- (Ch.) to fly away, (Y. G.) rise (dust, etc.), (Mu.) arise, be scattered (sparks); tiiran@ (Tr.) to fly away, of dust, clothes, in the wind, (Ph.) fly up; caus. (Ch.) titruh- to frighten away (birds); (Mu.) ttirh-/ttrih- to separate dirt from grain with the help of wind; (W.) turehtanad to winnow; (Ph.) tttrahtand to cause to fly up, squander (Voc. 1771). DED(S) 2792.
Ta. tUr (-pp-, -tt-) to sweep; tOrri a_ sweeper. Ma. titkkuka (tOrr-) to sweep; (Tiyya) tilppu sweeping. To. twa-nf- (twa-nt-) to sweep. DED(S, N) 2788.

Ta. tUru (tiri-) to traduce, slander; n, calumny, slander, ill-report; tira! slander, abuse; trru (tirri-) to publish abroad evil reports, slander, defame; tUrri tale-bearer. Ma, ttrruka to abuse, blame: diru blame, slander, ? To. tudy- (tudc-) to telf a lie. Ka. dtru to bear tales, report evil of others, blame, reproach, abuse, revile, calumniate, slander, asperse; 7, aspersion, blame, slander, calumny; dOrisu to cause to abuse. Kod. du-ri information laid against a person. Tu. dtruni to accuse, complain, blame, reproach, censure; dirata, dUru aspersion, blame, reproach; dirunaye, difrele an accuser, censurer, complainant; tUpuni to blame, abuse. Te. dru to reproach, blame, censure, abuse; m. reproach, blame, censure, abuse. Kui dohpa (doht-) to mention the. name of a person, cite, accuse, blame; praise, honour; n. Citation, accusation, praise. Cf. 403 Ta. arattira. DED(S) 2793.

Ta. tUru (tiri-) to drizzle: ttral, ttrral drizzling; tuvarru (tuvarri-) to scatter drops, sprinkle; tuvaral raining, drizzling, sprinkling; tuval (tuvalv-, tuvanr-) to drip as water, sprinkle, drizzle; tuvalai water particle. drop, spray, drizzle; tivalai small drop, spray, tir [298] tege raindrop, rain; ? tumi (-pp-, -tt-) to drizzle, sprinkle; 7. raindrops, light drizzling rain,. drop of water, spray; tumitam raindrops. Ma. ttrral drizzling rain. Ka, tiyu to fall in fine particles, drizzle, cause to drizzle or drop; n. falling in fine particles, drizzling, tiralu to drizzle; tuntur, tunturi drizzling, spray, a drop; tlparu to drizzle; jlpara, jUparu thin, drizzling rain, spray. Kor. (T.) durmbu to drizzle. Te. ttiru id.; tira, tuvvara raindrop, drizzling rain; tuppara drop of water, thin or drizzling rain, drizzte; tumpara a particle or drop of water, etc., a particle of spray, esp. spittle accidentally ejected from the mouth in speaking: tumpillu thin or drizzling rain, drizzle, spray, rain driven by wind, Konda tul- (tuR-) (water, etc.) to be splashed, scatter away in particles. Kuwi (S.) tiith’nai to speckle, intersperse, powder. DED(S, N) 2794. 3399 (a) Ta. (Devanesan, N. Arcot dial.) tilr (-v-, -nt-) to enter. Ka, tu to enter, enter a hole as a mouse, go through a hole or eye as a thread, etc., penetrate, pierce, n. penetrating, etc.; tilrisu to cause to enter, make go through as a thread through the eye of a needle. Te. tUru to enter, penetrate; tUrucu, trcu to inser

Ta. tekul (tekulv-, tekupf), tevil (tevilv-, teving-), tevul (tevulv-, tevunf-) to be full, increase, overflow; tekiz (-v-, -nt-) to be full; tekulam fullness, abundance, tekuffu (tekutfi-) to cloy, glut; tekifqu surfeit, teviffu (tevifti-) to become full, be sated, glutted, cloyed; #. loathing (as of food from satiety); tevvu (tevvi-) to fill; tikai (-v-, -nt-) to com- plete (intr.), come to an end. Ma. _tikayuka to become full, complete, be fulfilled, fin- ished; tika fullness; tikaccal, tikavu com- pletion, satiety; tikekka to complete, fill up, fulfil; tivirruka to force into a vessel, cram. Ka. tivu to become full, abound, spread; fill (tr.). Te. (K.) tegu (work) to be finished, ended; (Sank. K.) tegudala end, termination, completion. DED(S) 2801.

Ka. tege, tegi, tegu to put out or extinguish. Tu. tekkuni to be extinguished; (B-K.) tekkavu, tokkavu, tokkdvu to extin- guish, as fire. Kor, (O. T.) tekki to be extin- guished; (M.) tekdi to extinguish. Te. tegu to die; tegulu, tevulu disease, sickness; tega- faru to die, perish, come to an end, Kol. tik- (tikt-) to die. Nk, tikk- id. Vk. (Ch.) tik- to die. Go. (Mu.) deg- to break off, come to an end; (M.) deg- to burst (intr.); dehana to break (¢7.); (Ko.) deg- to break (intr.), (L.) degand id.; dekhana to tear, rend (Voc. 1886). tekku [299] teri Konda tegis- (-t-) to abandon (as one’s life) in recklessness, put an end to. DED(S) 2802. 3407 Ta, tekku (tekki-) to receive, take; tevvu (tevvi-) to get, take, obtain, seize, grasp; steal; tev, fevvu, tevu seizing, taking; té acquiring. Ko. tev- (tevd-) (river) subsides; (woman) thins walls of pot in preparation for closing the bottom; (tevt-) to pull along or ‘out of, (woman) makes clay rise in cylinder in throwing -pot. Ka, tege, tegu, tegi to pull, draw towards oneself, take, take away, remove; be taken away, be removed, become less or diminish, disappear; tege taking, etc. Tu. teguni to take; ? deppuni to take, receive. Te. tigiyu, tigucu, tiviyu, tivucu, (K. also) tivvu, tivu, tiyyu to pull, draw, drag, attract, take; tiyu, (K. also) tiyyu to take, remove, draw, pull, draw or pull out, take away, subtract; withdraw, diminish, be reduced, grow lean or thin, (K. also) subside as a tide. Kol. tiv- (tivt-) to pull; tivva force

Ta. teftiku, tefikam, ten, tennai coco- nut tree; temkay coconut. Ma. tefhiu coco- nut tree; tentah-kayi, téfsa, téiha coconut. Ko, ten kasy coconut; tegy coconut shell used as ladle. To. t6(g) go-y coconut; tég né-n coir rope; tegy half coconut shell used as ladle. Ka. tehgu, fehgu coconut palm, Cocos nucifera Lin.; teigay coconut. Kod. tengi mara coconut tree; teyge coconut. Tu. tefigu coconut tree. Te. tefkdya, fefkaya coconut; téjetfu, t@-mranu coconut tree. DED 2806. 3409 Kur, teygna (tingyas) to tell, narrate, explain; teggrna to confess, profess; tigngaba’- ana to moralize, preach, impress upon, in- culcate; refl.-pass. tingarna. Malt. tenge to tell, point out, relate. DED 2807.
Ta. tecci scarlet jungle geranium. Ma. tecci Chrysanthemum indicum, DED 2808.

Te. t(r)eddu wooden ladle or spoon, oar or paddle. Kol, (Kin.) tedd ladle. Nk. fedd id. Mand. tadge gourd spoon. Malt. tadu a wooden spoon. /Cf. Skt. tardt- wooden ladle, Pkt. taddu- id.; Turner, CDJAL, no. 5723. DED(S) 2809.

Ta. tentu (tenti-) to beg; tenfi beggar. Ma. tentuka to wander about as for alms; tental going about as for begging or obtaining something; tenti beggar. DED 2810, 3413 Ka, tetti, tatti, (Hav.) ketti egg. Tu. tetti, (B-K. also) ketti id. DED(N) 2811.

Ta. teppam, teppal raft, float; teppai taft. Ka. teppa raft, float. Tu, teppa id. Te. teppa, tépa id., catamaran. Go. (Ko.) tepe float (of rod and line) (Voc, 1774). / Cf. Skt. tarpa-, talpa- raft, float; Pkt. tappa- id.; Periplus tpdnmaya; Turner, CDIAL, no. 5726. DED(S) 2812. 3415 Kui (Mah. p.71) tepesa thatched roof. Kuwi (Mah.) tepori, temberi id. DEDS 536.

Ta. tempu, tenpu physical strength, daring, bravery, arrogance, energy. Te. tempu- boldness, daring, enterprise; tempi daring, boldness; tempari a brave, etc., man; tegu, tegd-badu, tegincu to dare, venture; teguva daring, boldness; t&kuva courage. DED(S)

Ta. temmiafi, temmanti senseless person, fool, incompetent person. Ma. tem- mati vagabond, debauchee. DEDS 537. 3418 Kur. taina (taiyyas), t@yna (téyyas) to send, carry newly married girl out of village. Malt. teye to send. For possible relationship with 3098 Ta. taru, see K., p. 389, BDCG, chap. 3, esp. §3.21, and Pfeiffer, p. 18. DED 2814.

Ta. teri (-v-, -nt-) to be seen, per- ceived, ascertained, become evident, be understood, intelligible, clear, possess the power of sight, be conscious (as of one’s guilt); investigate, test, ascertain, inquire, know, understand, select, choose, learn through listening, sift; (-pp-, -tt-) to make evident, bring to view, tell, declare, inform, explain specifically, write, inscribe, sift, choose, select, divide; terikkal narration in detail; terippu informing, acquainting, com- municating, investigation, saying, mentioning, writing, inscription, sifting, dividing; teriyal selection; terivi (-pp-, -tt-) to explain, point out, show, teach, bring to light, manifest, reveal, display; fterivu knowledge, under- standing, choosing, picking, selecting, any- thing selected or chosen, appearance, visibility, that which is known or ascertained; terul (terulv-, terunf-) to know, gain true knowl- edge, perceive, ascertain, understand clearly, be renowned, be clear, Jucid; n. knowledge, intelligence, clear perception, comprehension, wisdom; terufci knowledge, wisdom, under- standing; terutfu (terutfi-) to inform, make known, convince, persuade, enlighten the mind, confirm, assure, rub and test the tone of a lute-string; m informing, convincing; teruffal rubbing a Jute-string to test the tone; tér (-y-, -nt-) to examine, investigate, inquire into, understand, know, consider, deliberate, ponder well, elect, seek, ascertain, form a conclusion, doubt, question, be well versed in; t@rcci examinat

Ma. terika pad to put under vessels or for the head to carry burdens. Tu. teriya circular pad of wicker or straw placed under a vessel to make it steady; (B-K.) terve circular ae for carrying loads on the head. DED

Ta. terivai woman between 25 and 31 years of age, woman. Te. terava woman. ? Ka. taruvali boy, girl; taruvalitana childish- ness; (cf. tarala, tarula child, boy, tarale girl, which are, however, considered to be tatsa- mas of Skt. taruna-). DED 2817.

Ta. teru, teruvam, teruvu street, highway, public road. Ma, teru street, bazaar Street, a weaver village. ? Ko. tervie neigh- bourhood (in: i tervi-, a tervi-; al tervie in various caves). Bel, (LSB 2.2) teru way. Te. feruvu way, road, path; ? tennu id. DED (S, N) 2818.

Ta. terumarcu to be confused in mind; terumaral confusion, distress, fear. Tu. eyeduni to be confounded, confused. DEDS 38. 3424 Kurub, (LSB 1.12) tarkili wing. To. tergy id, Kod. terake id. Cf. 2591 Ta. cirai. DED(N) 2819. 3425 Konda terp- (-t-) to put to sleep, cause to lie down, lay. Pe. trép- (-t-) to lay down, put (child) to sleep. Mand. tér- (-t-) to lie, lie down; trép- (-t-) to lay down. Kui tréppa (trépt-) to cause to lie down, lay out, stretch out full length. Kuwi (Isr.) trip- (-h-) to lay down, make one sleep. Cf, 3460 Kol. te-r, DEDS 539,

Ta. telugu, teluiiku, teluikam, telin- kam Telugu country, Telugu language; telun- kan Telugu man. Ma. telufku Telugu country, language, and people. Ka. telugu, telagu, telufgu Telugu language; teluga, telaga, telufga Telugu man. Ju. telungu, telugu Telugu; telufige Telugu man. Te. telungu, tenungu Telugu language. Kol. telgi Telugu man, / Cf. Mar. teligz Telugu man; adj. taligi. DED 2820.

Te. (K.) telucu to praise, worship, Tequest, pray. Go. (Tr.) talehkang, (W.) talahkana to beg, ask for anything, esp. a bride (Voe. 1684); (ASu.) talk- to ask; (KoyaSu.) talp- id., beg. DED(N) 2821. 3428 Kur. tel€gna (telgcas) to tuck up (e.g. garment before sitting). Malt. téle to lift (as the corner of a curtain or hem of a dress). ? Ma. terukka to pull up the clothes, as when wading. DED(S) 2822,

Ka. tili a drinking vessel. Te. teliya, tele plate or dish. Go. (Mu.) tellay a frying- pan (Voc. 1787). DEDS 542. 3430 Kui tlépka (< tltk-p-; tlekt-) to put out the tongue, thrust forth from a cavity. Kuwi (F.) tekh-, in; venddri tekhmt put out your tongue! DEDS 540.

Ka. tevalu, tevulu an itching desire, an inordinate addiction. 7u. tevaly desire, wish, attachment. Te. tivuru to desire; tivufa desire. DED(S) 2823. 3431A Ta. tevvu (tevvi-) to beg hard, importune. Ma. téra beggar. Te. dé@vurincu to beg humbly, importune. Kur. tembna to beg for alms; temb@rus mendicant, beggar. DEDS 541.

Ta. teri (-pp-, -tt-) to drive or control by shouting, bluster, cause to sound (as a drum); resound, roar; n. sound, noise; terippu sound, noise, noisy rage. Ma. telikka to drive cattle with shouts. DED 2824.

Ta. tel (tetp-, tet{-) to become clear, clear-minded; te¢{a clear, plain; teftavar clear- sighted persons; tetpam clearness, ripe wis- dom; tenmai clearness, lucidity, clearness of intellect; telku (telki-) to become clear; tellu (telli-) to be clear, lucid, refined (as language), shine clearly, gleam, be mature in knowledge or experience; tellimai clearness, obviousness, intelligence, penetration, cleverness; telliyar | the learned, the wise; teli (-v-, -nt-) to become clear, limpid (as water by the settling of sediment), become serene (as the mind), be bright (as the countenance), become white, disappear (as famine, epidemic), become obvious, evident; consider, investigate, know, understand; (-pp-, -tt-) to clear, free (as from turbid matter), clarify, make known, pacify, reveal, dispel (as fear, sorrow); n. clearness, essence, light; telifian learned, wise man; teliccal healthy appearance; telippu clearing, refinement, purification, straining off; teliya clearly, evidently; telir (-v-, -nt-) to shine, sparkle; telivu clarity, brightness, perspicuity, essence, water strained from cooked rice, knowledge, waking state, placidity, serenity; tali (-v- -nt-) to comprehend clearly. Ma. teli clearness, brightness; teliyuka to become clear, brighten up, please, (matter) is decided; telivu clearness, brightness, perspicuity, proof; teliyikka to clear, clarify, make bright, clear away (as jungle), exhilarate, explain, prove; teliyippikka to filter, clarify, make bright, joyful. Ko. t

Ka. tel, telu thinness, fineness, delicateness, smaliness; telupu, telpu, teluvu thinness, delicateness, fineness, diluted, watery state; telulu fineness; tellage, tellane, tellanna, tellina, tellane thin, delicate, etc.; thinly, etc.; thinness, diluted state; teflitu, tellittu that is thin; tellida thin or delicate man. Kod. téllane thin (of a person or thing). Tu, telpu thinness; thin, lean; few, a little; tellavu, tellavu thin flat cake; tellena thinnish; telyn- tuni, telunfuni, telyntuni to contract, shrivel, wither, grow thin. ?Kol (SR.) tette thin (Kamaleswaran). / Cf. Skt. talina- thin, fine, slender, meagre; Pkt. talina- smail, slender, weak; tadina- = virala-. DED(S, N) 2826.

Ta. teli (-pp-, -tt-) to strew, scatter, sprinkle, sow as seed, cast up in sifting, winnow; n. sowing as of seeds in a field; telippu winnowing, sprinkling, scattering, sowing; tellu (telli-) to winnow, waft (as the sea), cast upon the shore; tali (-pp-, -tt-) to drip (as rain); sprinkle (¢.); 1. drop of water, raindrop, first shower of rain. Ma, telluka to sift or winnow by casting up gently in a fan; tellal winnowing; telli sifted powder; tali sprinkling water; ftalikka to sprinkle. Ko. tel- (teyl-) to winnow (flour) gently; teyt-/ telc- (telc-) to sprinkle (¢7.). Ka. tali to spread by scattering, strew, sprinkle; be scattered about; #. scattering, sprinkling; talisu to sprinkle, cause -to sprinkle; tentu to winnow corm. Kod, tali- (talip-, talic-) to sprinkle (liquid). Tu. talipu sprinkling; talipuni, (B-K. also) talipu to sprinkle; telluni to winnow, sift. Kor. (O.) talpi to sprinkle. Go. (A. Y. Ch.) tehc-, (Ph.) tahcana, tahcitana to winnow; (Tr.) tehcdnd to sift in a spa with a tossing motion, not sidewise (Voc. 1788); (Tr.) tirppana to sift earth from grain in a stipa; (Ph.) tirpana to sift (Voc, 1733). Kur, telna to winnow flour so as to separate it from stones or unground grain. Malt. téle to sift. DED(S, N) 2827. 3436 Kod. téli- (télip-, télic-), (Mercara dialect) toli- (tolip-, tolic-) to laugh; téli, (Mercara dialect) toli laughter; (Shanmugam) tolip id. Tu. telipuni, telpuni to laugh, smile, deride; telipavuni to make laugh; teliké a laugh, smile, ridicule. Kor.

Ta. teri (-pp-, -tt-) to burst asunder, snap in twain as a rope, split; break (fr.), cut. Ma. terikka to cut off. Ka. tiri to cut, cut off; n, cutting. Te. t(regu, treyyu to be cut, divided, or severed, snap (as a rope), be breached (as a dike); trevvu to be cut, (K. also) be snapped (as a rope); t(r)encu to snap, break, tear, cut, divide, sever (as a thread or string, etc.); t(empu id.; 2. state of being cut, divided, etc., discontinuity, pause. Go. (ASu.) tivy- to be broken (as a rope). Konda tev- (-it-) to be broken or snapped (as thread), be disjointed; tep- (-t-) to break, snap, nip off (as flowers), finish, redeem {a vow), discharge (a religious obligation). Pe. tréz- (trést-) to cut (e.g. crops). Mand. trey- to cut (paddy). Cf. 3140 Ta. tari; DED(S, N) 2829.

Ta. teri (-pp-, -tt-) to strike and fly off, start as the eyes, splash as water, fly off as sparks, be scattered as an army, spring, leap, bound, give throbbing pain, twang as bowstring with finger and thumb, thrum as the strings of a lute; #. spattering, splashing; tenru (tenri-) to be scattered, split to pieces, stray away as from a group. Ma. teri snappish, dashing, clashing; terikka to rebound, recoil, fly in pieces, make to fly off, splash, sputter; terippikka to make to bounce or splash; ue teru [302] tennu terru throwing stones, filliping marbles; terruka to shoot with a crossbow; terrikka to shoot or throw with a bow. Ko, tet- (tety-) (cattle) stray from a herd. ? To. tes gun (or with 3245 Ta. tiral). Ka. tiri throw- ing off a thing by quick motion, tossing. DED 2830.

Ta. teru (teruv-, terr-) to tarry. Ko. tet- (tefy-) opportunity or time is, be poss- ible (with dative of person); tepn well, in good circumstances, not widowed (said of the priest). To. tet- (tety-) leisure is; tern opportunity, right moment. Ka. terapu, terahu, terpu leisure, opportunity; teravu leisure; teppage, teppane at leisure, at ease, comfortable, well. Te. t@pa a time (eg. rendu tépalu twice). Kui tepka (<_tek-p-; tekt-) to have time for, overtake. DED(S) 2831,

Ta. teru (teruv-, terr-) to burn, scorch, be angry, sting (as wasp), punish, destroy; teral anger, heat, affliction; téru sting (as of a wasp). Vk. (Ci.) tirup sun’s tay. Pa. ted- (tett-), (NE.) ted- (tet{-) to be fierce (of sun’s heat). Go. (A. Y.) ter- to be fierce (heat of the sun); (Tr.) tariténa to be hot (of sun); taristina to heat bread over a flame after it has been cooked on the iron; (Ph.) tarrand, (Ma.) tag-, (Ko.) tar- to be fierce (of sun); (Mu.) tars-/taris- to heat (Voc. 1778); (Ma.) terk- to warm oneself by fire, (2) recover from illness (Voc. 1783). Konda (BB) ter- to be fierce (heat of sun); tervel sunshine; (K.; Sova dial.) terveli id. /Cf. Mar. tirip gleam of sunshine, hot blaze. DED(S, N) 2832. 3441 Ta, teru-porul indemnity, tribute: tirai tribute; (inscr.) tirappu assessed lands. Ma. tira tribute, taxes; an offering, an inferior feast. Ko. ter- (tet-) to pay (debt). To. tet- (tef-) to pay (fine, debt); ter a fine, compen- sation paid in buffaloes to man whose wife is taken by another. Ka. teru (tett-) to pay; teruvike paying; terisu, tettisu to cause to pay (as taxes, fines, etc.); tere, terage, terige, terege tribute, tax; tir(u) to exchange, barter, pay, offer, present, give; tera price paid for a wife; teravu id., (PBh.) tribute, tax; teranfu to make a gift of raiment to the bride and bridegroom at a wedding. Kod. ter- (teruv-, tett-) to pay (penalty). Tu. terigé taxes; tirmuru exchange. Te. (inscr.) tere a kind of tax. Go. (Pat.) terina to repay (Voc
Ta. terri raised veranda. Kod. tetti narrow walk built on all round house, DED 2834.

Ta. terru (terri-) to stumble, be obstructed, hindered, mistake, commit a fault, do wrong; stammer, stutter; 7. tripping, mistake, wrong. Ma. terru slip, stumble, mistake, something aside or out of order; terruka to slip, fail, mistake, err, be asunder or aside; terra aside, not in array; terral slippery place, mistake, etc.; terrikka to make to slip, err. Kod. teri- (terip-, teric-) to totter about (as a child or through weakness). ? ‘Ka. dattu stumbling, tripping. ? Tu. danguni, sentuni to stumble. Kor. (O.) danfi id. DED (S) 2835.

Ta. terru (terri-), tettu (tetti-) to become intertwined; braid, plait, entwine, weave, string up, tie together, tighten; 1. entwining, denseness. ? To. te-t- (te+ty-) to fold (leaf for drinking-cup). Ka. tettu to inter- twine, interweave, twist, be twisted, be con- nected or befriended; tettiga man who is entwined or closely bound to, a servant, connexion, friend; tettisu to bring into close connexion, cause to enter (as nails), insert, etc. Kur, tessna (tissyas) to plait, intertwine so as to form a Jong narrow strip, (Hahn) tie a knot (Pfeiffer). DED(S, N) 2836.

Ta. terru hedge of thorns protecting a passage; tettu hedge of bamboo or thorns, Ka. tadalu, tadlu a frame of thorns, etc., used as a gate in a hedge. Te. tefgu hedge round a city or fort; (VPK) tefte fencing; tette-gdda a wall fence. DED(S) 2837.
Ta. terru-ppal snagged tooth, super- numerary tooth. Ma, térra tusk of a boar or young elephant. DED 2838.

Ta. ten south, southern region; right side; tenral, tennal south wind, balmy breeze from the south, south-west monsoon; tenri, tenral south; tennar south; people of the south; tennan, tennavan southerner; tenatu that which is in the south; terku, tekku south. Ma, ten south; tennal southern breeze, zephyr; tekku south; tekkan southern. Ka. tef-gali south wind; tefika, tefkal, tehku, tembu, fenkalu, tefku the south. Kod. tekki, tékki south; tekkié southern. Tu. tenykayi, tenkayi, tehkayi the south, southern. ? Go, (ASu.) tela south. DED (N) 2839.

Ta. tennu (tenni-) to rise, (Tinn.) lift with a lever. Pa. (S.) tend- to risc, be taised: tetip- (tetit-) to raise. Ga. (OIL) tetp- (tett-) to raise, lift; (S.2) tepp- (tégup-) to lift. ee een ee enmeearyeangverpycasivin °° Am: emengentin we + matonnaeme gn Lee LORD ARE BE tékkam [303] ter Go, (most dialects) téd-, (Tr.) t@dana@ to rise (Voc, 1789); (Tr.) tehtind to cause to rise, build a house; (Mu. S.) tth- to lift, rouse; (Ko.) té(th)- to make to rise; (L.) tehana, (SR.) tahainag to lift (Voc. 1796); (ASu.) teh- to rouse from sleep, revive to life. Kur. tétna (tétcas/tettas) to assist a woman in raising a load to her head. DED(S, N) 2840. 3451 (a) Ta. tekkam eructation; tékku (t@kki-) to belch; n. belching, eructation; tékk-itu, tékk-eri to belch; tekiftu vomiting sensation; teviftu (teviffi-) to chew the cud. Ma, ttkkuka to belch; tékkam nausea, un- subdued anger; téitiuka to feel nausea, sob; tekittu belching; tikattuka to belch, feel nausea; té@ffuka to belch, ruminate. Ko. te-kl a belch. To. té-k- (té-ky-) to belch. Ka. tégu, téku id.; 1, a belch; téguvike belch- ing; (Bell; U.P.U.) dégu, (Gulb.; U.P.U.) déku to belch. Kod. té-kili a belch. Tu. t®gy id.; t®guni to belch. Te. Ta. t&kku teak, Tectona grandis, Ma. tekku id. Ka, tégu, tga, téhgu id. Tu. (B-K.) tekku teak wood. Te. t&ku, t@ku teak tree. Kol. (SR.) tek teak timber. Pa. ték meri teak tree. Go, (Tr.) téka mara, (W.) téka, (Ph.) ttka, (Mu. Md. etc.) tka id. (Voce. 1516). Kuwi (8u.) ttk teak. DIED (S) 2842,

Ta. teiku (ténki-) to fill, become full, rise. to the brim, be crowded, abundant, copious; stagnate; ttkku (tékki-) to drink to the fill, be full, copious, abundant, replete, be sated, glutted; stop the flow as of water, dam up; #. fullness, repletion, satiety; tkkam being brimful, fullness, satiety; obstruction of the flow of water; tekkar abundance. ‘Ka. téhki mass, multitude. Ta. t&tu (téfi-), téntu (ténfi-) to seck, search for, inquire after, acquire, earn, pro- cure, take care of, cherish, provide for, seck, try (as to do a thing); téttam, tétqu acquiring, earning, accumulation, seeking, search, pur- suit, acquisition, that which is earned or hoarded, anxiety, solicitude, earnest desire, appetite, longing. Tu. tépuni to rub, polish; tépu rubbing, whetting, polishing as a precious stone; tépa-kalilu a polishing stone, whetstone: t@pané rubbing, polishing as a precious stone; teémina loss in assaying ntetals, waste by polishing; téduni to grind, macerate; stjuni to sharpen, whet, polish. Ta. t®r car, chariot, vehicle. Ma, tér chariot, temple car. Ta. téri sandhill. Ma. t®ri hillock, swelling on the ground. DED 2849, 3462 7a. térai frog, toad. Ma. t@ra a lean frog. DED 2850. 3463 Kur. tela ebony tree. Malt. télo a sort of ebony tree and its fruit (Diospyros glut- inosa). DED 2851.

Ta. télu (téli-) to get rid of, escape danger (as a ship); télikkai lightness. Ka. tal(u) to be afloat, float; slip, slide or glide off, go backwards or away, become loose, sink into a fainting fit, faint; télisu to make float, open the eyes wide and stare upwards, as at the point of death, cause to fail as a promise; téku to float; tekku to cause to float; faint: tefku to be afloat, swim, float; (PBh.) téaku to float. Kod. (Kar.) te-t- (-i-) id. Tu. téluni id. Te. t@lu to float, swim, (K. also) stare upwards (as at death); télucu to cause to float: tétagiflu to float, turn up; teladu to float; télika lightness, levity, easiness; light, easy. Kol. (Pat., p. 151) téleng to float. Pa, tal- id. Ga, té-ér- id.; (S.3) télupp-, télsap- to float something. Go. té- (S.) to. float, (Ko.) (dust) to rise (Voc. 1794). Konda dél- (it-) to float on water, ? Kui tlépa (tlet-) (liquid) to stand in a depression. Kuwi (F.) télali to float; (Isr.) tél- Cit-) id., overflow. DED(S, N) 2852.
Ta. tévaiku lemur, Indian sloth, Loris gracilis. Ma. tévaitkam, tévanku sloth, bradypus. DEDS 549.

Ma. tévuka, tékuka to draw water, empty a well, bale out for irrigation; spatter. Tu. t@puni to throw water with a bucket or trough, irrigate; t@palé a kind of trough or bucket used for watering trees, etc.; (Bhatta- charya; brahmin dial.) sépuni to bale out. Te. dévu to search or grope for anything in water, take anything out of water with the hand. DED(S) 2853.
Ta. t@vai affairs, business, com- pelling need or necessity. Ma. téva need. DED 2854.

Ta. tel scorpion; téli scorpion-fish; ? tét-kuticci a black bec; té{-koffan a green insect whose touch produces the same sen- sation as a scorpion-sting (for kuficci and kottan, cf. 2064 Ta. koffu to sting as a scorpion, wasp). Br. télh id. DED(S) 2855.

Ta. téru (téri-) to be accepted as trite, be clarified, made clear as water, be strengthened, recover from swooning or intoxication, be thorough, accomplished, mature (as the mind), reach perfection, thrive as vegetation, be comforted, consoled, cheer up, be successful in examination, prove, result, amount to (as profit), trust, confide, believe in, decide; nm. clearness, certainty; t@runar the learned, trustworthy persons; téra thoroughly; téral clearness, pure, clarified toddy, honey, clarified juice; téri- nar tested or tried friends; téririavan person of experience, one well-versed in an art, an adept; térru (térri-) to make clear, convince, assure, relieve from doubt, know, under- stand, swear, take an oath, clear, clarify, refine, comfort, console, cure, give relief, communicate strength, nourish, © cherish, invigorate, encourage; nm. making clear, be- coming clear; t@rram certainty, assurance, determination, clearness, presence of mind, comfort, consolation, thriving, luxuriant growth; térran person of true knowledge; térra, térru, téru clearing-nut tree; térraf- kofttai clearing nut; terral clear-sighted person; terr-ena clearly; terr-enavu certainty, clearness; tét¢¢ai clearness, transparency, clear water. Ma, téruka to be strengthened, thrive, mend, recover, believe; téral clearness, thriv- ing, certainty, thought, nectar; térram firm- ness, faith, trust; térruka to make strong; térramaram Strychnos potatorum, Ko, te+r- (te-ry-) to be found satisfactory in one’s act
Ma. téruka to attack, pursue. Kod. do: t- (do- fi-), (Mercara dialect) de-t- (de-ti-), dé-t- (dé-ti-) to drive away (cattle, persons). sesqerum to drive, drive away. DED(S)

Ta. tai (-pp-, -tt-) to sew, stitch, nail, fasten beams with nails, spikes, or pegs, pin, plait or stitch as leaves into a plate, join, pierce, penetrate, prick as a thorn or arrow, tie, weave as a wreath, string as beads, adorn, decorate, set, enchase, wear, put on, paint; n, sewing, decoration, embellishment; taiyal sewing, stitching, needlework, embroidery, lace, that which is adorned or decorated. Ma. taikka to sew, seam; taital split bamboos joined for doors, mats, ceilings; taiyal sewing; taippu needlework. Ka. tépe, tyape a patch. Kuwi (F,) tacali, (S.) tacinai, (Su.) cac- (-it-), (Isr.) cac-/t#c- (-it-) to sew. DED(S) 2858.

Ta. tai young plant or tree. Ma. tai shoot, any young tree. Ka. sasi a young plant; (Gowda) deyi plant. Tu. dai a plant, young tree; sasi a young plant. Te. téga the tender palmyra shoot. DED(S) 2859.
Ta. taiyal beauty, woman; tayal woman. Ma. tatyal a beautiful woman. Te. toyyali, téyali woman. DED(S) 2860.

Ta. toku (tokuv-, tokk-) to assemble, collect, accumulate, form asa whole or lump, - aggregate, be summed up, totalled; (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to assemble, bring together, gather, sum up, total, add, summarize; tokuti assembly, collection, aggregation, society, company, association, class, flock, herd, aggregate, total; tokuppu sum total, multitude, crowd; tokai assembly, collection, association, flock, herd, swarm, school, bunch, sum, total, addition, summary; tuku (tukuv-, tukk-) to be gathered in a mass, as the hair; (-pp-, -tt-) to bring together, gather in a mass as hair.
