(many dialects) toy-, (Tr.) toiana, (W.) toiydnad to swell (Voc. 1819). DEDQ) 2861.
Ta. tokku, tokaiyal, tuvaiyal chutney. Ka. tokku Icaves or fruits bruised or pounded with a small portion of water and mixed with salt, chillies, etc. Te. tokku a kind of chutney or pickle. DED 2862.

Ta. tonku (tonki-) to hang, be sus- pended, hang down, dangle, be dependent, servile; tofkal hanging, anything pendent, hangings, pendent part of an ornament, ear- ormament, end of a garment hanging down, decorative hangings as of cloth, festoons, garland, peacock’s tail. Ma. tonkuka to hang, be pendent, depend on; tofnal hangings, drapery, tassel. Ko. tong- (tongy-) to hang (intr.), hang oneself; tonge- (tonge-) to hang (tr.). Ka. tohgu to hang, be pendent, hang down, swing, stoop; tofigal, tofige a cluster, bundie, bunch, tassel, tuft, mass; tonku to stoop. Tu. tongé, tongely cluster of blossoms, bunch of fruit. Te. tongu to lie down, recline, bend, incline, sleep; tofigundu to sleep, lie down, recline; tongi clicu to peep; (Inser.) tongodiu (pl) bundle, bunch. Kui dongu suspended, dangling, dependent from, hang- ing; dongu inba to be suspended. Kur. tangna to suspend, hang; refl-pass. tanga. Malt. tonge to bend, bend the knees slightly while dancing; tonqro bent. Cf. 3376(a@) Ta. tinku. / Cf. Mar. tdgal a head or ear of corn, pendent portion of an ear-ornament. DED(S) 2863. 3479 Kur. togkhna to break any part of a plant with the fingers, cull or pluck a leaf or flower, prune a tree. Malt. toge to nip off (as herbs), cut off (as bamboos); end, finish (as a narrative); toqre to be at an end, be finished, aeeat off, die; toqro broken, ending. DED

Ta. tofu (tofuy-, toff-) to touch, come in contact with, be connected, united with or joined to, feel, handle, take hold of, use, have illicit intercourse (as with another’s wife); (-pp-, -tt-) to connect, join, bind, fasten, fix (as arrow on bow), enchain, tie, surround; toffal touching, tying, binding; toffu touching, concerning, in reference to; tofakku (tofakki-), tufakku (tufakki-) to catch hold of, tie; get entangled, obstructed; n. tying, binding, entanglement, bondage, connexion; tufafku shackles; tofuppu con- tinuity, fastening, linking, tic, chain, illicit connexion, paramour, concubine; tofuvai that which is joined or appended, man and woman living in concubinage; tofai fastening, tying, series, succession, joints of the body, surrounding wall; tin-tofai string of a lute, lute (for tin:, see 3269); tofaiyal succession, fastening, tying, garland; tofar (-v-, -nt-) to follow uninterruptedly, be linked, pursue, persist in with energy, tie, bind, attack: n, following, succession, chain, fetters, friend- ship, connexion; tofarcci, totarpu, tofarvu pursuit, connexion, touch, rélationship, friendship, series, chain; tofarntdr_ friends; tofal, tufar chain; tufar (-v-, -nt-) to follow one after another; t&fu collection, assem- blage. Ma. toftuka to touch, feel, handle, come into contact; tofal touching, being close, a chain; tofuvikka to make to touch; tofu [306] toffi toffu concerning, about, tofukka to bring into contact, put together, put the arrow on the bow; tuta conne

Ta. tofu (tofuy-, toff-) to begin (tr.), occur, happen, come into being; (-pp-, -tt-) to commence, undertake; toffu beginning with, from, since; tofakku (totakki-), tufakku (tufakki-) to begin; tofakkam, tufakkam beginning, origin; totahku (tofanki-), tufahku (tufanki-) to begin, originate, undertake, engage in; tofaikal beginning, first creation, attempt; tofuppu commencement. Ma. tofuka to commence; totukka to commence a work; totfu beginning from; tufahfuka to begin, commence, undertake, do; tufanhikka to cause to commence or undertake; tutakkam beginning; tutaruka to commence, undertake, do. Ko. tofl te-I first menses. Ka. todaku, todanku, todagu to engage (as in work), begin, commence, undertake; todagisu_ to cause to commence or undertake; todaguha beginning, undertaking; todavu the beginning, the bottom; (K.2) todu to begin, start with. Tu. todaguni to begin, commence, undertake; todagely beginning. Te. toffu to commence; todagu, (K. also) tonagu to begin, commence; (K.) todaru to undertake. Kui fonda (tondi-) to begin, initiate, attempt; 7 beginning, attempt; pl. action totka (fofki-). Malt. torge to be in readiness for action. Br. durring, durrénging to dare, venture. DED 2866.

Ta. tofu (tofuv-, toff-) to put on (as a ring, clothes), wear (as shoes); (-pp-, -tt-) to wear (as clothes); toffatu shoes; tofakku (tofakki-) to wear, put on; tofuppu sandals; toti bracelet; (PR) tofalai leaf-garment; jewelled girdle. Ka. tudu/todu (toff-) to put on (as ornaments or clothes adapted to the body); tudisu, tudiyisu, todasu, todisu to put on another; tudige, tudage, tuduge, todage, todige, toduge, toda, todavu, toda certain clothing or dress, an ornament. Tu. todige clothes, dress, ornaments. Te. tod(u)gu, tod(u)vu to clothe, invest; todugu konu to put on, wear; todigincu to cause to wear Or put on; todavu ornament, jewel; todupu armour; (K.) toffu to put on; (K.) todu id., wear. Kol. tod- (totf-) to put on (shirt); (Kin.) tox- to put on (shoes, etc.); CW.) toda anklet; (Pat., p. 71) tordyal anklet. Vx. tor- (totf-) to wear (bodice or shirt). Ga. (S.) todg- to wear (clothes); tuff- to wear, put on. Konda toR- (-t-) to wear, put on (as saree); toRay 3- to get dressed. Pe. tuz- (tust-) to wear, put on. Mand. tuy- to wear. Kui tubga (< tug-b-; tugd-) to fasten a neck- lace; m. act of fastening. Kuwi (Su.) tur (-h-) to put on, wear (shoes); (sr.) turh-/ truh- (Git-) id. (shoes, clothing, etc.); (S) turh’nai to wear. Cf. 3545 Ta. cfu. / CE. Jaina Skt. UL 20.193) todaka- an anklet. DED(S, N) 2867.
Ka. tudugu, tudi theft; tuduga thief; (K.?) tonda id. Go. (Ko.) tohk- to steal (Voe. 1812). Kui (K.) torkenji thief; (Letchmajee, p.39) torka id. From DED(S) 2417, 2658.

Ta. totti water-trough, tub, cistern, | reservoir, manger, crib, refuse bin. Ma. toffi trough of stone or wood, manger, font. Ka, totti trough, font, tub. Kod. (Shanmugam) totfi bucket. Tu. totti bucket, trough, tub. Te. totti tub, trough, manger. DED 2868. totfi [307] don

Ta. totti fence, yard, pound, en- closure for selling timber or firewood, small village. Ma. totti long narrow garden, loft in a house; dofti cattle-pound. ? To. twidy pair- word with twi-tm (see 3549). Ka totti building with a square open court at the centre, the court itself; doddi, daddi cow-pen, cattle-pound, fold, stable. Kod. todia small garden adjoining a house. Tu. (DCV) duddi cattle-pound. Te. doddi backyard, stable for cattle. Go. (F-1.) dodi, (Grigson) dodi cow- pen (Voc. 1609). Konda dodi backyard of house (for plants, etc. to grow). Kuwi (Isr.) dodi backyard of house, used for kitchen garden. Cf. 3526 Ta. toru. DED(S, N) 2869.
Ta. tofttil cradle, cot for an infant. Ma. totil, totfi cradle. Ka. toftil, toffalu, totla, toflu id. Kod. totti id. Tu. toftily cradle, cot. Te. toftla, tofliya, tofle cradle. DED 2870.

Ma. tottu pedicle of a leaf, bunch of flowers. Ka. toftu, todambe foot-stalk of a fruit, flower or leaf. Tu. tofgu stem or stalk of a leaf or fruit. Kor, (M.) cotta stem of the jackfruit. Te. todima footstalk of a leaf, fruit or flower; (Merolu) todka stalk. Pa. (S.) dodoma handle of a vessel. Go. (Mu.) tod- doma, (Ma.) torma, (Ko.) forme stalk of leaf (Voc. 1526). DED(S) 2871.
Ma. tottu nipple. Ka. tottu id., point. Tu, tottu nipple of a breast. DED 2872.
Ka. totte basket made of coconut leaves. Tu. totte id. Go, (Ma.) doti a kind of basket (Voc. 1608). DED(N) 2873.

Ta. tin-totai beehive (for tin-, see 3268(b)); tofaiyal honeycomb. To. tot wax in honeycomb. Ka. tofte a bee’s empty cell. Tu. totté, tatfi honeycomb; todduvé beehive, honeycomb, a large bee; toddé a large kind of bee; its hive; toddelu a large bee. Te. teftiya, tette bechive. Kol, (Kin.) tatya id. Go. 3.3 tette, (S.) fette honeycomb (Voc. 1513). Konda téne-fata beehive. Kuwi (Su.) fatfi, (P.) fatto honeycomb; (F.) tatti wasps’ nest; (S.) tati beehive. DED(S) 2874.

Ta. totta big. Ma. tutam stoutness, plumpness. Ko. dod big, great; dod- (dody-) fo grow big. Ka. dodda big, large, stout, thick, great, extensive, spacious, respectable, eminent, important, chief, principal, loud; doddatana greatness, etc.; dodditu, doddittu that is large, etc. Tu, dodda big; doddastigé greatness, loftiness, riches, power, ostentation, pride. Te. dodda, doddu big. Kol. doo big, great, elder; dood big thing; (SR.) dodand big. Nk. dora big, elder; dohon big. Pa. dora big, stout (in: dra pofteta pregnant [potta belly]). Ga. (S.3) tofo vande thumb, big toe. Go. (SR.) dhoddhal, (Tr.) dhddal, (L-H.) dhodal stout; (Tr.) dddral urtim the very largest cattle tick (Voc. 1636). Kui dodori distended, swollen, pregnant. Kuwi (T.) dota vanju thumb. Kur. (Hahn) donda potta large-bellied. Malt. dudo id. DED(S) 2875.
Ta. totti-kkal bowleg. Te. doddi- kallu bandy legs. DEDS 551.
Ka. dodli a large shrub or small tree, Atalantia monophylla, Tu. doddalé, dodlé wild orange [lime], A. #1. DEDS $52.

Ta. tonatona (-pp-, -tt-) to vex with ceaseless talk; tcnatonappu, tonutonuppu chattering, babbling; tonuppu (tonuppi-) to chatter, babble; tonuppan vain talker; tola- tola (-pp-, -tt-) to babble, rattle away. Ka. tondu to chatter. DED 2876.
Ta. (STD) tonasi a fly on dogs. Ka. tonaci, tonace, tonaci, tonace, tonase a gadfly, a dog-fly, a horse fly.
Ta. tontu block of wood suspended from the neck of an animal to prevent it passing through hedges. Ma. tontu log of wood. Kuwi (F.) torpa wooden log (tied to eens of refractory cattle). DED(S)
Ta. tontu gap, narrow passage. Ma. oe passage between two mud-walls. DED

Ta. tontai throat, gullet, windpipe, larynx, voice, singing voice; (STD) donte throat. Ma. tonta windpipe, throat, gullet. Ko. ted throat (inside and outside). Ka. (Gowda) dondE, (Bark.) donde throat. Kod. tonde id. Tu. dondé id. ?Go. (Ma.) torsa, tosa, (G. Mu.) tsa, (L.) tosu, (Mu.) sdsa id. (Voe. 1830), Malt. tonre id. Cf. 2984 Go. fodra. DED(S, N) 2879.

Ta. tonfai a common creeper of the hedges (= kivai Bryonia epigaea). Ma. tonti B. grandis. Ka. tonde, tondi, donde, konde the gourd Momordica monadelpha Roxb. or B. grandis Lin, Te. donda Coccinia indica, (B.) Bryonia, M, monadelpha, etc. Pa. tunda creeper. Go, (PD, Mu. Ko.) fonda id. (Voc. 1529); (SR.) tondri tondla vegetable (Voc. 1531); (Tr.) fonddri Coccinia indica (Voc. 1538). Konda donda C. indica. Kui dondi pumpkin. /Cf. Skt. tundika-, tundikeri-, tundikesi- M. monadelpha: Mar. todli id.: Turner, CDIAL, no. 5854. Also Skt, tundi- a kind of gourd. [Cephalandra indica Naud. = M. monadelpha Roxb. = B. grandis Lin. = Coccinia indica W. & A.J] DED(S) 2880. 3500 Ta, tonfai thorny caper (Capparis horrida); atontai id.; Ceylon caper, C. zey- lanica, Te. @rudonda, (B. also) dredonda, adonda, (VPK) aradonda C. horrida, (B. also) Cc. zeylanica; (B.) donda Capparis. {C. horrida Linn. = C. zeylanica Roxb.] DED(S) 2881.

Ta. velku (velki-) to be ashamed, be coy, bashful, fear, shudder, be perplexed; vetku (vefki-) to be ashamed, be bashful, be afraid; vetkam, vekkam shame, modesty, bashfulness, coyness; vatku (vatki-) to be ashamed, be shy, bashful; 7, shame, shyness, modesty; vili- (-v-, -nt-) to be overcome with shame; vilivua shame. Ma, velkka to be ashamed, afraid; vekkam shame. Ko, vekm (obl. vekt-) feeling of modesty, shame. Ka. belkar to become afraid, fear; (K.2) belkane to turn pale through fear. Tz. bolcuni to shy, start aside suddenly as an animal; bolci, bolcely shyness, starting aside, suddenly; bolcavuni to make shy; bolaccely shying. DED(N) 4527.
Ka. tonde, tonne, donne chameleon. Te. tonda id.; blood-sucker. Konda fondo chameleon. DED (S) 2882. ,
Ko. don big club. Ka. donne, donne, doni cudgel, club. Kod. dogne club. Tu. donne, donne cudgel, club; donely club. DED 2883. ° todal(u) [308]

Ka. todal(u), todal, todul, toval faltering, stammering, speaking inarticulately or indistinctly, lisping; untruth; dodde in- distinct or inarticulate speech connected with stuttering. Tu. todale stammerer; todalé stammering, indistinct pronunciation, untruth; doddé lisping, stammering; dojjuni to stam- mer; dojje stammerer. Pa. totr- to stammer. Kuwi (F.) tothali to mumble, stammer; (S.) toth’nai to stammer. Malt. todlo stammeret, todlqote to stammer, lisp. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 5965, *totta-. DED(S) 2884.

Ko. ton Toda man (< *tod-n); tody Toda woman. To. toZ%-mox Toda woman. Ka. toda, todava a Toda. (Ta. tufavar, tofar Todas of the Nilgiris; the words are made on the basis of English pronunciation). DED 2885.
Ka. dondadi, dondana, dondani, don- duli mass, crowd, throng, squeeze, tumult, fray; dodde mass, heap, crowd. Te. dondadi crowd, throng; dontara, donti pile, heap. DED(S) 2886.

Ta. tontaravu, tontarai trouble, vex- ation, difficulty; tontari (-pp-, -tt-) to trouble, vex, annoy. Ma. tontaram intricacy, vexation. Ko. tondarv (obi. tondart-) act of troubling or disturbing a person; tonda-rm (obl. tonda-rt-) persistent trouble or pressure put upon a person. Ka, tondare, tondaru, tondre intricacy, embarrassment, entanglement, diffi- culty, trouble, vexation, molestation, impedi- ment, drawback. Kod. (Shanmugam) tondare trouble. Tu, tondaré entanglement, con- fusion, perplexity, annoyance, trouble, vex- ation, misfortune, distress. Te. tondara trouble, annoyance, vexation, haste, hurry, confusion, perplexity, embarrassment. DED 2887.

Ta. tonti large belly, abdomen, fold or collop of fat, obesity; (CTD, STD) dondi big belly. Ma. tonti pot-belly. Tu, tondé big, distended; tondé bafiji a big belly. Te. doddu one who is pot-bellied. /Cf. Skt. tunda-, tundila-; Turner, CDIAL, nos. 5858, 5862. DED(S) 2888.
Ka. dondi, donde bundle of sticks, gtass, rags, etc., used as a torch. Tu. dondi small torch made of a rag. DED 2889.
Ma. toppan much, plenty; ? tOna much, greatly. Ka. toppane greatly, much, thoroughly; (HavS.) tolu much, plenty. Kor. (f.) tappena much. Cf. 3331 Ta. tumpat. DED(S) 2890.

Ka. dombi, dombe, dombi, gombu crowd, mob, disorderly, riotous mob, fray, quarrel; dombigara man that belongs to a tictous mob, rioter. Tu. dombi, dombi, dombe mob, tabble, riot, uproar, dombigare tioter, turbulent man. Te. dommi mingled or confused noise, crowd, throng, mob, rabble, mélée, affray; dommul-adu to squabble, scuffle. DED 2892. tol

Go, (LuS.) toposhee a basket- work receptacle for grain. DED(N) 2893. 3512 Ta: toy. (-v-, -nt-) to breathe short and hard (as while suffering from asthma); toyvu difficulty of breathing, asthma. Ta. veru (-pp-, -tt-) to detest, loathe, hate, be angry at, dislike, renounce, be afflicted; verukkai, veruppu aversion, loath- ing, dislike. Ma. verukka to avoid, abstain from, loathe, hate, renounce; veruppu aversion, dislike, abomination; veruppikka to cause anger; virakkam repugnance. Ko. verv- (vert-) to dislike. Kur. birkha’anad to disgust with, make sick of, disaffect from; birkharna to grow disaffected, conceive a disgust for, be sick of. DED(S, N) 4537.

Ta. toy (-v-, -nt-) to languish, pine, grow weak, be weary, fatigued, fail in energy, dtoop, faint, flag, become slack, be loose, supple, yielding, bend through weakness or want of support; toyyal fainting, languishing. despondency, afflication, toyvu laxity, loose- ness as of arope, faintness; tuyanku (tuyanki-) to be exhausted, faint; tuyakku (tuyakki-) to slacken, relax; n. fatigue, loss of strength or courage, misconception, confusion, sorrow, distraction; tuyakkam fatigue, loss of strength or courage; tuyavu mental distraction, per- turbation; tuyar (-v-, -nt-) to grieve, sorrow, lament; 7. affliction, grief, sorrow; tuyarafi fatigue, fainting, drooping, grief; tuyaram sorrow, gricf, calamity, trouble, pity. Ma. tuyaram, tuyar calamity, gricf, pity; tuya- ruka to grieve; tuyarkka to afflict. Te. dosigu, dosavu calamity. 87. tusing, tustnging to faint, become unconscious. DED(S) 2895.

Tu. ddguni to craw! on hands and knees, or in a sitting position; doga crawling; dogguni to kneel, Te. dor(a)lu to roll Gintr.), ~ turn over; dor(a)lincu, (K. also) doral(u)cu id. (tr.); dOgu, ddgu, dgadu to crawl or move on the hands and knees, go on all fours as an infant, Go. (Ma.) dorg-, (Mu.) dorg- (buffalo) to wallow (Voc. 1898). P.) torg- to fall; (S.) torginai to fall down, (Isr.) torg-/ trog- Cit-) to fall; trok- (-h-) to drop, demol- ish, fell. DED(S) 2422, and from DED(S) 2211

Ka. (Nanj.) domme lungs of cow. Kog. (Shanmugam) tombe lungs. ? Te. dobba piece of flesh, clot of blood. Kol. (Kin.) dobba lung. Pa. dorba (pl. dorbel) id. Ga. (S.2) dobba (pl 1) id. Go. (G. Ma. Ko.) dorba id. (Voc. 1900); (ASu.) dobba id. Konda dobo id. DED(S, N) 2898. 3516 -Ta. tol old, ancient; tollai, tolfai antiquity, ancientness; tonru oldness, an- tiquity; old, ancient; tonmat oldness, aml- tiquity; tongu antiquity, old times. Ma. tontan old man; tonfi old woman. Ko. tol vier first pregnancy. Ka. coc-, In: coccal first pregnancy (for -cal < -cll, see 2733). Tu, tofe old man, tofi old woman; (B-K.) dolu {309} toru tondé old man; tondi old woman. Te. toli beginning, commencement; first, former, previous, old, ancient; formerly, previously; tolukaru the rainy season; tolakari the begin- ning of the rainy season, the rainy season; tolakarincu to rain for the first time in the season (for items tolukaru to tolakarincu, ‘first/commencing + [rainy] scason’, see 1278(c) karu season; communicated by K.; or in 3361); tonfi former, earlier, previous; ancient, old. Kol. tolli tomorrow; (Kin.) early in the ‘morning. Nk. tolli_ tomorrow. Pa. tolli id. Ge. (Oll.) tondug, tondunun, (S.) tondun id. Go. (SR.) tolle first (Voe. 1808); (ASu.) tottay old; tottdr ancestors; toll previous, in ancient times. Kuwi (S.) tolli, tollie, (Su.) toli before, formerly. DED(S, N) 2899. 3517 Konda dolu a creeper plant, shrub, plant of shoot height. Pe. dol plant; stem, trunk. Mand. dul plant; sapling.

Ta. tolai (-v-, -nt-) to become ex- tinct, perish, die, be exhausted, terminated, liquidated, end (as a way), expire (as time), be over, finished, be weary, be defeated, fail, be lost, leave, depart; (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy, kill, exterminate, spend, pass (as time), remove, wipe off, bring to an end, scttle, lose, surpass, defeat; n. ruin, distance, great distance; tolaiccu (tolaicci-) to kill, destroy, exhaust, pay, discharge (a debt); tolaipu perishing, destruction; tolaiva completion, end, extinction, destruction, defeat, failure, fatigue, weariness, dwindling, decrease, dis- tance; tulai (-v-, -nt-) to perish; caus. (-pp-, -tt-). Ma. tulayuka to be at an end, die; tulaccal end, difficulty; tulekka to consume, finish. Ko, tolg- (tolgy-) to be released from debt; tolk- (tolky-) to release from debt; ? telg- (telgy-) (hair, bushes) are divided; telk- (telky-) to divide (hair, cloak, bushes) so as to examine or go through. To. twal- (twal6-) to be settled and finished with; twalf- (twalt-) to settle (debt); twalx- (twalxy-) (plank) is out of plumb, (coagulant agent) is polluted; twalk- (twalky-) to make to be out of plumb. Ka, tolagu, tolahgu to go away, depart, go aside or out of the way, retire, separate (intr.), be separated, be excluded, fail; tola- gisu to cause to go away, etc.; (Hav.) tole to stand away; get rid of. Tu. tolaguni to depart, move away, leave, forsake, desert; telavuni, telivuni to go aside, make way, avoid, shun. Te. tolagu (K., imper. stem tola- is used
Ma. tokku armpit. Kur. tol9kh side of the body, flank; (Hahn) tolokh armpit. DEDS 550.
Ta. tollai trouble, perplexity, diffi- culty, work. Me. tolla trouble, vexation, danger. DED 2901.

Ma. torikka to beat the breast from grief, kick, whip with twigs, cudgel; toriyuka to droop, (leaves, fruits) to fall, patter. Ko. tol- (toly-) to disparage a man’s good qualities. Ka, tuzi to tread, tread on, trample, tread down, crush to pieces with the feet, tread out, annoy, harass, pain, be trodden down, become poor, be trodden under foot, be abject or cast out; m. treading, trampling, that which has been trodden; turisu to cause to tread; tugiha treading, etc.; turikal that is faded or withered; toxturi, tottara state of being perfectly trodden down, poor, miser- able, dejected or faded; (Hav.) toli to kick. Tu. torpuni to Kick, tread; torpu a kick: donkuni to tread, trample; (B-K.) toripu, tolipu, corpu to kick. Kor. (M.) toli, (T.) tori id. Te. trokku to tread, trample, tread or trample upon, press or crush under the foot; tread, place the foot; trokkudu treading, trampling, stamp of the foot. ? Kui duda (dudi-) to tread, trample, step upon; 7. tread, step. Kuwi (Isr.) turb- (-it-) to trample, tread out grain; ?(S.) tulpinai to tramp. DED(S, N) 2902, DEDS 527.

Ta. tori (-v-, -nt-) to be spilt. Tu. doriyuni to flow, etc. (or with 2883 Ta. cor). Go. (Tr.) tdra blood which precedes the birth of a child (Voc. 1825); (ASu.) tdrg- (water) to be spilt. Kui tora (tdri-) to be liquid, flow, trickle. DEN 46.

Ta. toil act, action, deed, work, office, calling, profession, order, command. workmanship, verb; togii working woman, maidservant, female slave; torukkan slave: toguttai slave-woman, immoral woman, maid- servant; torumpan slave, base person: togum- pi slave-woman; torumpu slavery, servitude, servile work, drudgery, .devotion to the service of god; toruvar servants, agricultural- ists, ploughmen; toruni servant woman; tontu slavery, devoted service, slave, devoted servant; tottan slave; tottu slave, dependent, menial, concubine. Ma. toril business. occu- pation. Ka. turil work, servitude, slavery; togtu, tottu (a male, but esp. also a female) servant, a strumpet. Tu. tolily trade, business; tonduni, dunduni to work hard; tottu maid servant; sonduni to exert, labour, work hard; sondu exertion, effort. Te. tottu female servant or slave, wench, prostitute, mistress or concubine. ? Cf. 3295 Ta. tutiyan. DED (S) 2903.

Ta. toru (-v-, -t-) to worship, adore, pay homage to; torukai worshipping, ador- ation, prayer; toruvu worshipping, adoration. toru {310} dohtina Ma. toryuka to salute by joining the hands, acknowledge superiority. Ka. turil salutation, obeisance, bow. Kod. to- (topp-, tott-) to salute. Tu. turli obeisance; solma, solmé salutation. Go. (Mu.) dori- to bow (Vee. 1902). Pe. tod- (toft-) id. Mand. tud- to bow head. ? Konda tuRpa- to invoke gods, fulfila religious vow, adore, worship, DED(S, N) 2904.

Ta. toru cattle-stall, manger, pound, stocks, married life, cage for wild animals; toruti multitude, crowd, herd, flock of birds; toruvam, toruku. cattle-stall, manger; tr, tdram cattle-stall; tontu cattle pound. Ma, tor stable; toruttu stable, sheepfold, pen for goats. Ko. to-y buffalo pen, To. twi+ corral, pen; tue fo-s entrance of pen. Pa, -tol in: cakur-tol cattle-shed (for cakur, see 2267). Cf. 3485 Ta. totfi. DED(S) 2905.
Ta. toxgu, toru-ndy, torunai a kind of leprosy. Ka. tonnu, tonnu leprosy, white leprosy, vitiligo. DED 2906.

Ta. tol (tofp-, totf-) to perforate, bore with an instrument; tolkal perforating; tolku excavation, pit; tollal hole; tollai hole, perforation, pit, anything tubular, fault, defect; tolai (-pp-, -tt-) to perforate, bore; n. hole; tulai (-pp-, -tt-) to make a hole, bore, drill, punch, pierce as with an arrow; #. hole, orifice, aperture, perforation, hollow as of a tube, bamboo, gateway, passage, flaw in a diamond; tulavai hole; tl (toftp-, tdft-) to perforate, bore through, dig out. scoop: nm. hole; tonti hole. Ko, toyl- (tolc-) to pierce: toyl hole in pen- post: tol hole, vagina: teli-(g) hole in wall between two houses (for handing through fire, ete... To. ttily gate-post of pen with holes for bars; tiity- (tiitc-) to make hole in stone or tree. Ka. tole hole, bored hole; tolle hollow, hole, cavity, deficit, debt; tolle hollow, cavity; follu, tollu state of being hollow, void, or empty within; toli hole, socket. (Tr.) tullane to be bored, pierced; caus. tufhuttana; (Mu.) tullih- to scrape out or bore out the pulp of a gourd (Voc. 1762); (A. Y.) doddo pit (ASu. dhoddo); (Tr.) dhddhur hole in a tree (Voc. 1611); (Tr) foti t

Ta. tolukku (tolukki-) to tie loosely, wear or attach loosely (as clothes); tolukku- kkontai loosened coil of a woman’s hatr. Tu. dolahkuni to relax, loosen; doluhkuni to be loose, Jax; dolafiky, dolufku loose, lax; looseness. DEDS 554.

Ta. tulai (-v-, -nt-) to disport in water, dive, be immersed; tulaiyam disporting in water. Ka. tole, toli to wash, remove by washing; tolasu, tolayisu to cause to wash, get washed, have washed. Tu. torapuni to wash superficially. Te. tolaécu to wash. Go. (Tr.) turlipstand to rinse out a cup or vessel which has some food at the bottom; (Ko.) so to rinse (mouth) (Voce. 1759). DED(S) 08.

Ta. tolku net for trapping. Ma. tolla snare, trap. DED 2909. 3532 Ta, toll-ayiram 900; ton-nfru ninety; tontu nine. Ma. toll-ayiram 900; ton-ntru ninety. Ko, tombat ninety. Xa. tom-battu, tom-bhattu ninety. Kod, tom-badi id. Tu. sonpa id. Te. tommidi nine; tommandu- guru, tommandru nine persons; tombadi, tombhai ninety; tombandru. ninety persons; tommantru, tommanniru 900. Kol. (SR.) tomdi, (Kin.) tomdi nine; (SR) tomater nine persons. Go. (Pat.) tomidi, (L.) tumidi nine; (Pat.) tombai ninety. Cf. 1025 Ta. onpatu and ? cf. 3528 Ta. tol. DED 2910, 3533 Ka, toralu, torale hole; tore, dore hollow, hole. Te. tora, torata, torra hole, cavity in a tree. Go. (Ko.) dora hole in tree (Voe. 1894). DED(S) 2911.
Ta. toll-dyiram; or ? cf. 1004 Ta. olku. DED 862. O

Ta. toru, toruvu herd of cows, cattle- stall, shepherd caste, crowd, multitude, host; toruavan shepherd; fem. toruvi, torutti, Ka. tur cow, kine; ‘toru, turupu, turuhu = turu (Gai, Historical Grammar of Old Kannada, index); turukara cowherd, owner of cows; turuvala, turuvala cowherd; fem. turuvalti. Te. torru cow. DED 2912. - 3535 Ka, doraku, dore to come or draw near, be equal to, resemble, suit, fit, come about, appear, be obtained, gained or found, accrue; dorakisu to cause to obtain, acquire for oneself, obtain; dore coming near, resem- blance, similarity, likeness, equality, adequacy, propriety. Tu. dorakuni, dorahkuni to be got, obtained, procured, found; dorakavuni to cause to obtain, procure. Kor. (T.) darké to obtain. Te. doraku to be found, got, pro- cured, obtained, gained; dorayu to occur, happen, be an equal or match, resembie; dorayu to obtain; (K.) doralu to occur, happen, accrue, join with; toralu to happen, occur, arise, (K.) meet, join, meet in battle. Go. (Ko.) dork- to be found (Voce. 1896); (Maria of Bastar; LSZ 4.535) doroktdr he was found (also in other Go. dialects in LSJ). Konda doRk- to be got or acquired, be available, be caught or defeated; doRkis- to make available, bring out. DED(S) 2896. 3536 Go, (Tr.) dohtana to bind, tie, build a house, weave a fish-trap; (M.) tohtana, (Ma,) to?-, do?-, doh-, (Ko.) toh-, (A. Y. Ch. G. Mu. S.) doh- to tie (Voc. 1811). Konda toR- (-t-) to tie, bind, build. Kui tohpa toy {311] tonfu (toht-) to tie, bind, fasten;

Ta. tukai (-pp-, -tt-) to tread down, trample on, bruise or destroy by treading, pound in a mortar, mash, vex; roam about, walk; tuvai (-pp-, -tt-) to tread down, pound as in a mortar; 7. treading, pounding. ? Ko. to+t- (to-ty-) to beat slightly. Ka. toku to beat, strike. Tu. toipuni id.; tdku collision. Pe, tog- (tokt-) to tread, step on. Mand. tug- (tukt-) to trample. Kui téga (tdgi-) to kick; n, a kick. Kuwi (F.) toiyali, (Su.) toy- to kick; (S.) toyinai to spurn; doy- (-it-) (D.) to tread on, trample; (Ist.) to kick, step on. Go. (Tr.) tdcan®# to put (a load) on one’s head; (L.) tocan@ to bear, carry; (A. Mu. Ma.) tdec-, (Ko.) tds- to carry on the head (Voc. 1815). Konda dds- (-t-) to carry (a load on the head); caus. ddsis- to cause somebody to carry on head. Pe. joc- (joce-) to carry on the head. Kui dlsa (dtisi-) id. Kuwi (Su.) juce- (-it-), (P.) ducc- (-it-), (F.) jacali, (S.) jUcinai, (Isr) juc- (-it-) id. ? Ka. (Hav.) tom- pata carrying on the head (or with 2677 Ta. cuma). DED(S) 2918. 3541 Ka, ddcu to plunder, rob. Te. ddguru theft; d&cu to plunder, pillage, sack, rob, loot; dSpari thief, robber, plunderer; dépidi plunder, pillage, sack; d6dpu to plunder, pillage, sack, loot. Konda d6s- (-t-) to pillage, plunder. Cf. 2982 Ta. tonkan. DED(S) 2919.

Ta. tdcai a kind of rice-cake. Ma. dosa a cake baked on an iron plate. Ka. dose a holed, i.e. spongy, cake of rice-flour, uddu, etc., baked on a potsherd or iron plate. Kod. dose a flat rice-cake. Tu. ddsé a cake of rice- flour. Te, d&se a kind of cake. /Cf. Skt. dhosaka-, dosa id. (P.K. Gode, Indian Linguistics 16. 226~31). DED 2920.

Ma. tofu water-course, natural and artificial. Ka. tédu water-course; (Hav.) c&di rivulet. Kod. to+di small stream, drain in paddy fields. Ju. toédu drain, sewer, gutter, brook, canal. Kor. (M.) toda stream. Go. (Tr. Ch.) dhad, (Ph.) dora, (D. G. Mu. Ma.) ddda, (Mu.) dhorgi river, nullah (Voc. 1638); (LuS.) dora running brook. DED(S) 2921.

Ta. tdfu shell of a fruit, as of a wood- apple; sheath of grain. Ma. tdqu shell (of various fruits), husk, pomegranate rind. Ka. togatu, togate, tte bark, yind, peel, pod. Go. (Tr.} t0t@ outer skin of the mahua fruit (Voc, 1817); (Koya Su.)' tofte peel. Cf.
Ta. tofu ear-jewel. Ma. tdta ear- ring of women. Cf. 3482 Ta. tofu. DED 2923,

Ta. totti menial servant of a village, scavenger; fem. tofticci; toftimai occupation of village messenger or scavenger. Ma. totti sweeper, fem. tdtticci. Ka. toti an inferior village servant, esp. one who performs the lowest offices, a sweeper, scavenger. Kod. (Shanmugam) to+ti scavenger; fem. tostici. Tu. toti scavenger, sweeper. Te. tOf{i a pariah. Pa. tOtal, tofa labourer. DED 2924. 3547 Ta, tdtti elephant hook or goad, hook, clasp, sharp weapon planted in the ground to keep off enemies. Ma. tdtti hook for driving an elephant, hook for plucking fruit; totfuka to pluck fruit with a totti. Ka. doti, loti pole with a hook for plucking fruit, gathering flowers. Tu. donfi long pole with hook to pluck fruit. Te. dO¢i long pole with hook for cutting off fruit from high trees. Pa, {otal bamboo rake. Kur. t8rni to hook in; {016 a pole with an iron hook or branch curved down at one extremity; tarsi . a variety of the native rake or #18; (Bleses) toro a long stick with an iron hook. Cf.

Ta. tonfu (tonfi-) to dig, hollow, excavate, scoop out, bore, draw or fetch water from a well, draw out details of, un- load (as a ship); tonfi pot of earth or metal used for drawing water; tdffam garden, orchard, plantation; tdttakkaran gardener, husbandman, owner of plantation; tofu (tofuv- toft-) to dig, scoop out, excavate, pierce through, n. field, garden; ttfavai garden, cultivated field. Ma. tonfuka to ‘ burrow, scoop out, remove (ear-wax), clean out (a well), dig; tonfi ‘small earthen vessel for drawing water, tdffam garden; toffak- karan gardener; tofi garden. Ko. to-d- (to-j-) to dig a deep hole; to»tm (Obl. to- ft-) fenced field. To. twi+y- (twfsry-) to dig with fingers: twistm (twi-dt-) garden; to-¢ = twi-tm (in song unit: to-¢ig 8o+r man on an estate, i.e. European). Ka. t0du-to take water out of a tdtu [312] tol large vessel with a small one, bail out water, put rice from one vessel into another one or another place, wind thread from one spool upon another one, remove as ear-wax, etc., dig, excavate a hole, burrow, bury (as an arrow in a victim), penetrate (as the breast); tOfa, tdnfa garden; tdtagara, tdtiga, tdntiga gardener. Kod. to-d- (to-di-) to dig (a well); to-ta garden. Tu. toduni to dig, burrow, mine; todafgely scooped, burrowed; tuda bored, scooped out; tOdfa garden, orchard, plantation; tofige a class of brahmans work- ing in plantations. Te. tédu to draw up, scoop or scoop out, ladle out, dip out; tota garden, grove, tope; t0favidu, tofidu gardener.
Ta. todtu device, means, Ko. to-d trick. To. twi-® cunning, trickery. Ka. todu an expedient. DED 2928.
Ta. toppu clump of trees, tope. Ma. tOppu grove, clump of trees, Ka. tdpu id., wood. Tu. tdpu id..Te. tépu garden, grove, tope. DED 2929.
Te. tomu to rub, clean. Ga. (S) dom- to wash and clean by rubbing. DED 2930.
Ta. tompu redness, red dye. Tu. topu red; a red-coloured cloth. Te. t8pu id. Kui {oper red, rosy. Cf. 3284 Ta. tukir. DED 2931.

Te. ddpu to put or thrust in, insert, introduce; (K.) dégu to enter into, make a passage in the wall. Kui ddpa (dodt-) to put within, insert, introduce, plunge into. Kuwi (Usr.) do?- (-t-) to put hand into something (water, etc.). ? Cf. 2868 Pe. jo-. DED(S) 2932,

Ta. toy (-v-, -nt-) to bathe, com- monly in cold water, become wet, soaked, temper by dipping in water (as heated metal); (-pp-, -tt-) to dip, soak, temper as iron, dye, tinge, stain, imbue, wash, cleanse; tdyccal, tdyppu tempering heated metal; toyyil cultivated moist land; toyyal mud. Ma. toyuka to dip, soak; toykka to temper metal. Ko, to-y-/to+{- (to-c-) to wash clean (cloth). To, twisry- (twi-+c-) to wash (clothes). Ka, tuy, toy, toyyu to be or become wet or moist; toyisu, toyyisu, tdyisu to cause to become wet, steep in water; toyyuvike becoming wet; td, toyu to become wet or moist, be dipped in; wet, moisten, dip, soak, steep; tokuli act of dipping, steeping. Tu. toyali, toyili washerwoman; toipuni, toyi- puni, tdyipuni to wash (as clothes). Te. tdgu, (B. also) ddgu, (K. also) ddgu to become wet or moist, (K. also) bathe, sink in water; (K.) técu to sink (fr.), bathe, dip in. Go. (Mu.) tdnj- to dive, submerge head under water while bathing (Voc. 1816). ? Cf.
Ta. toy (-v-, -nt-) to come in contact with, reach, unite, embrace, associate with, resemble; tdyntar friends. Ma, toyuka to unite. DED 2934.

Ka. tora bigness, largeness, plump- ness, stoutness, greatness, dignity, a stout man, a great man; tOritu that which is big; torida a big man. Tu. tra stoutness, thick- ness; thick, stout, large. Te. tGramu thick, stout, large. Kur trdja (trdji-) to grow in body, become mature and well-developed; pi. action trdska (trdski-). DED(N) 2935.

Ta. tol (torp-, tdrr-), tor (-pp-, -tt-) to be defeated, discomfited, vanquished (as in battle, game, dispute, etc.), fail in com- parison, yield, flinch, give way voluntarily, lose; n. defeat, discomfiture, misfortune, ill-luck, loss; tolvi, tolvu, torral, tdrpu dis- comfiture, loss, defeat; tdrvai defeat. Ma. tdlka to be defeated, lose a game, battle, or suit, be cheated in a bargain; toli defeat, loss; tolpikka to defeat, beat, baffle, excel. Ko, corl- (cost-) to be defeated, be inferior, fail; corev- (co-t-) to be defeated. To. twi-t- (twi-t-) to be defeated, become old; twi-Ie- (twi-lé-) to defeat; swi-l- (swi+t-) to be defeated (< Badaga). Ka. sol(u) to be de- feated or overcome, not to win; sdlisu to defeat, overcome, vanquish; sdla, sdlu defeat, Joss; soluvike, sdluve, sdlme id., losing. Kod. co-l- (co-p-, co-t-) to be defeated. Tu. tolpuni, sdpuni, sdluni to Jose at a game, be defeated, overcome; sdpaivuni, sdlavuni to make one lose at a game, defeat, overcome; sopu, sdlpu defeat, discomfiture; defeated, overcome; sdla defeat, failure, disappoint- ment. Kor. (T.) sdtu, sGti to be defeated. DED 2936.

Ta. tol skin, hide; toli (-pp-, -tt-) to strip off (bark), flay, husk, huli; n. skin, rind, husk; tokku skin, bark, rind. Me. tol skin, hide; tolikka to skin, pecl; toli skin, bark, peel, rind; tukal skin as of fruit; tokku skin. Ko. to+l skin, hide; to-kri small peg used in pegging down hide to dry (to-1+ karl [see 1389]). To. twi+s skin, hide. Ka. tol(u), togal, toval id., leather, skin of fruit. Kod. to-li skin, hide; toli- (tolip-, tolic-) to peel, hatch; (Shanmugam) tolip peeling. Tu, tugaly, toliké skin, bark, rind. Kor. (M.) toru skin. Te. tokka skin, bark, rind; tolu skin, hide, leather. Kol. (Kin., Haig, Hislop) tol skin, hide. Nk. tol skin. Nk. (Ch.) tol id. Pa, tol skin, bark. Ga. (OIL) tot id.; (8S. tol skin; tokka rind of fruit; (S.2) tokka bark. Go, (Tr. A. Y. Mu. W. Ph.) tol skin, hide; (D. G. M.) tdla skin, bark of tree; (S.) tol(u) (pl tolku), (Ko.) tolka id., skin of fruit (Voc. 1828); (Koya Su.) tolu. skin. {ola (313] Konda (BB) tol, (K.) tdlu skin (of animals); (K.) toko bark, rind. Pe. tol skin. Kui todu (pl. {6tka) buffalo hide (from which bellows or shiclds are made). Kuwi (F.) tolu, (S.) tolu, (Su. P. Isr.) tolu (pl totka) skin, hide. Ct. 3544 Ta. tofu. DED (S) 2937. 3560 Kui {ola lump, excrescence, egg. Kuwi (P. T. D.) tola egg. Br. -tol in: zartol tortoise egg (zar- < Ar. zahr). DEDS 557.

Pa. tdla seed of mahua. Pe. tora fruit of mahua. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) tolamba- mahua tree, dola- fruit of mahua tree. DEDS (N) 558. 3562 Ka, tive, tovve, tove, toge, toye a dish prepared of boiled split pulse. Tu. tave boiled pigeon peas. DED 2938.

Ta. tdran friend; tozi, tdzicci heroine’s confidante, maidservant; togamai friendship. Ma. toxan companion, friend; tari confidante, bridesmaid; toram, tdgama friendship. Te. toda, tddi, (inscr.) tdra with; todu help, aid, assistance; companion, associate; todupadu to assist; todivadu companion. Kol, to-ren (pL to-xenl) younger brother; to-rndal (pl. to-rndasil) younger sister. Nk. tdren (pl. torensil) brother; tdrndal (pl. tdrndasil, torndasikul) sister. Nk. (Ch.) tolen brother, mother’s sister’s son; t6nd sister, mother’s sister’s daughter. Pa. toled (pi, toler, tolenkul), (NE.) toled brother, younger brother. Ga. (Ol) toron (pl toronkul) younger brother; tond kinsman; (S.) tonddnd brother; tondud sister; (S.3) tOnek a relative. Go. (M.) tor, (L.) tor, (ASu.) tor with, (L. also) help; (L.) torata m@naval partner, fellow. Konda fonda intimate, friend; wife’s brother; friend- ship; tdru help, company, assistance; toran (Sembay dial.) brother (younger or elder), (BB) younger brother. Pe. tonden brother; torndel sister. Kui tdre friend, friendship; tdru friendship; tdrenja, toyenju male friend; fem. toreli, tozali. Kuwi (F.) tonesi friend (amongst men); (Isr.) tone, tone’esi, tOnesi friend; tépu companion; toga 4- to accompany. ? Cf. 3308 Ta. tunai. DED(S, N) 2939.
Ta. tol shoulder, arm. Ma. tal shoulder. Ko. to-l upper arm, elbow to shoulder. To. twi-lfody bangle worn on upper arm by women. Ka, tdl(u) the arm. Tu. wy id.; tolahgy shaking of the arm. DED 2940.
Ta. tdnri, totti, tonti Malabar glory lily, Gloriosa superba, Ma. méttonni id. Ka. totil id. DED 2941.

Ta. tonru (tonri-) to be visible, come to mind, appear, seem, spring up, come into existence, be born, exist; tOrru (torri-) id.; cause to appear, show, produce, create; n. appearance; tdnral appearance, splendour, superiority, great person, chief; torram appearance, vision, sight, conspicuousness, prominence, splendour, brightness, origin, birth, creation, fame; tdrral appearance; tOrraravu appearance, coming into existence, tlomba conspicuousness, incarnation; t6rum each, every, whenever (i.e. as much as appears). Ma. t6nnuka to spring up, occur, appear to the sight, come into the mind, please; tonnal ’ imagination, suggestion, instinct; tonnikka to produce an appearance, reveal, inspire; tOrruka to produce, restore to. life; torram appearance, rise (of the sun), show, spectacle, festivity; torum all, each. Ko. to-r- (to-ry-) to be visible; to-g- (to-yg-) intuition comes (to person). To. twi-l- (twi-d-), twi-t- (twi-ty-) to be foreseen; twier- (twi-ry-) id., be visible; ne-twi+r- (ne-twi-ry-) to be reflected (lit. to be visible as a reflection; for ne-s, see 3679). Ka. tor (tord-), toru (tri-) to appear, be visible, seem, be conspicuous, come into existence, occur, show, exhibit, evince; tdrisu to make appear, show, evince, cause oneself to appear, appear, occur; torike appearance, sight, seeming, conjecture, exhibiting, exhibition; torisuvike showing, etc.; tOru appearing, showing; toruvike, torke, tdrpu, tdrpe appearing; tO0cu to appear, seem, occur to the mind; ddvil, dd

Ta. nakar house, abode, mansion, temple, palace, town, city. Ma, nakar town, city. Tu. nagacy id. Te, nagaru palace; (Inscr.) nakaramu temple; nagari-adhikari temple manager; navaru temple. /Cf. Skt. nagara- town, city. DED 2943.

Ta. naku (nakuv-, nakk-) to laugh, smile, rejoice, bloom as a flower, open, expand, shine, glitter; nakkal laughing, mockery, brightness, splendour; nakal smiling, laughing, rejoicing, gladness, friendship, ridi- cule, brilliance; nakdar tooth, as appearing in laughter; nakaal laughter; nakai laughter, smile, cheerfulness, delight, joy, contemptuous laughter, sneer, derision, grinning, pleasantry, friendship, pleasant word, play, sport, flower, blossoming of flowers, brightness, splendour, teeth, pearl; (-pp-, -tt-) to laugh, smile; nahku (nafki-) to deride, mock at; n. derision, mockery. Ma, nakekka to laugh. Ka. magu (nakk-), nage to laugh, smile, laugh at, deride, open, blossom; 7. a laugh, smile, laughter, derision, blooming, blossoming; nagegara a man who causes to laugh, a ridiculer, joker. Tu. wnakali ridicule, satire, caricature, imi- tation; nakeruni to titter, giggle. Te. nagavu a laugh, smile; nagu to laugh, smile; n. a laugh, smile; nagibafu ridicule, derision, mockery; navvu to laugh, smile, joke, ridicule, 7. laughter, smile, jest, joke, fun, ridicule, derision; navvincu to cause to laugh, excite mirth in, amuse. Pa. nay- to laugh; nava jeri joke, joking; navip- (mavit-) to cause to laugh. Ga. (OL) nag-, (S.) nagg-, (S.*) nag- (nak-) to laugh. DED 2944.

Ta. nakku (nakki-) to lick, lap; 7. licking; nakkal food taken by licking; nakki a destitute person, as one who licks scrapings. Ma. nakkuka to lick; nakki a licker, a beggar. Ko. nak- (naky-) to lick. To. nok- (noky-) id. Ka. nakku, nekku id.; nekkisu to cause to lick. Kod. nakk- (nakki-) to lick; (Shanmugam) nakk licking. Tu, nakkuni, (B-K. also) nekkuni to lick, lap; makkavuni to cause to lick; nakkele a man who licks, esp. the plate in which food has been served; a greedy man; fem, nakkeldi. Te, naku to lick. Kol. na+k- (na-kt-) id. Nk. nak- id. Pa, nék- id. Ga. (Ol, S.) nak- id. Go. (Tr. Ph.) nakana, (W.) nakina, (Mu. Ma. S. Ko.) nak- id, (Voc. 1952). Konda nak- (nakt-) id. Pe. nak- Ct), (B.). nan(g)- (nant-) id. Mand. nék- id.; intens. nébga-. Kui naka (naki-) to lick, lap. Kuwi (F.) nakali, (Su. Isr.) nak- (-it-) to lick, (Isr. also) lap. DED(S) 2945. 3574 Kur. nakhrnd (nakkhras) aux. vb. with idea of reciprocity, dispersion or sever- ance. Malt. nage to act or be one to another. DED 2946.
Ma. nafku a fish. Tu, nahgu a kind of fish. DED 2947.

Ta. nafkai species of milkwort, Polygala glabra; large milkwott, P. telephoides. Ma. nafa a herb, Polygala. DED 2948. 3574 Ta, naci (-v-, -nt-) to be crushed, bruised, mashed, crumpled; (-pp-, -tt-) to crush, bruise, mash, press, squeeze, demolish, destroy; naciyal anything crushed or mashed; nacivu bruise, contusion, destruction, loss, injury; nacukku (nacukki-) to press, squeeze, crush, subdue; ”. bruise; nacufku (nacunki-) to be mashed, crushed; nai (-v-, -nt-) to be crushed, destroyed, perish; (-pp-, -ft-) to crush, destroy. ? Ko. nacak in- (id-) to make noise in cutting through flesh. Ka. najugu to squash, crush, bruise (as dry ginger); be squashed or bruised; najju a squashed state. Tu. nasiyuni to submit, subdue; nesipuni to press down, make tight. Te. najju crushed, cut up into small bits, minced; a bit, frag- ment. Kui nasa (nasi-) to press, crush; a. act , of crushing; nahi, nahiki destroyed, ruined, . demolished; (K.) nace- to press. DED(S, N) 2949.

Ta. naci (-v-, -mt-) to be worn, frayed (as cloth), be reduced in circumstances, nai (-v-, -nt-) to waste away, be frayed (as cloth); (Koll.) nacey disease. Ma. naital decay. Ka. nasi, nasiku to wear out, wear away, waste away, wither, wane, decay, spoil, be faint, become poor or poorly; #. wasting, waning, faint twilight (of the morning); nasu minute- ness, smallness, fineness, a little, a trifle. Tu. nasky twilight, dawn, early morning; neyiluni to fade, as a bunch of coconuts. Kur, nasna to cause men or cattle to sicken, languish, or die; nasrna to be ill or pine away. DED(S) 2950.

Ta. naccu (nacci-) to desire, long for, like, love; #. desire, hankering, liking; nacai desire, eagerness, avarice, love, affection, hope, expectation; (-v-, -nt-) to love, desire; nattu (natti-) to desire, long for, hanker after, love. Ko, nac- (nac-) to have great liking for (with dat.), feel desire; nacl desire for a person. Ka. naccu, narcu, neccu to confide, trust, confide in, rely on, belicve, desire, love; #. confidence, trast, faith, belief, reliance, longing, desire, love. Te. naccu to trust, (K. also) be agrceable, be lovable, be trusted, like, be attached to; naccika, nacciga trust, faith, belief; neccili a friend. Cf. 3600 Ta. nampu and 3602 Ta, naya. DED 2951.
Ta. naccu (nacci-) to tease, vex, trouble, harass; 1. trouble, worry; nacuval one who is always teasing. Te. sx naccu to tease, trouble; . troubling; ( pettu to annoy, tease, fret. DED 2952.
Ta. naccu delay, procrastination. Te. naceu delay, slowness; wearisome, tiresome; naccuk#du one who is slow or dilatory. DED 2953. ank.) naccu- | k i E naccu [315] nafu

Ta. naccu (nacci-) to babble, prate; n, babble. ? Ka. nakta a saying or proverb of the country. Te. nasuku, (K.} nasugu to murmur, utter indistinctly; . murmuring. DED 2954. 3580 7a. naficu (in cpds, naccu-) poison, venom, that which is malignant, baneful, pernicious, fatal; afterbirth, placenta; naficam poison, venoin; najican venomous person. Ma. nafjicu (in cpds. naccu-), nafifiu poison, esp. stupefying vegetable poison; nafifian a ma- licious man. To. noz, poison, afterbirth (of human beings), gall-bladder. Ka. nafiju poison, impurity of the bload, blemish, sin, paralysis of the brain. Tu. nafiji, nafiju poison, puerperal convulsions, impurity in the blood, scrofulous tendency, afterbirth, envy, jealousy; poison- ous, impure, unwholesome. Pa, nenj poison; nefij benda toad (benda frog). Go, (Tr. Ph.) nas panné, (SR.) nas panne toad; (Tr. Ph.) nas véli a creeper used to poison fish (véli < IA) (Voc. 1951). ? Kur. manj any vegetal poison used for intoxicating and catching fish. ? Malt, manjraha benumbed; manjrare to be benumbed, be sickly. (Mor Kur. Malt., Pfeiffer, p.114; he also compares Mundari najom ‘prepared poison; to poison’, which he takes to be a loanword from Dr.), DED (S) 2955.

Ko. nanjrk a small amount of food which will blunt the pangs. of hunger. Ka, nafiju, naficu to take a little lick, eat a little of anything like pickles and as a relish to the principal food. Tu. nesary, nesaly seasoning. Te, nanju, (K. also) nancu to eat in small bits or quantities, cat as a relish to the principal food, (K. also) take a lick; nanjidu flesh; (K.) nanjudu_ relish, pickle. Kol. nenjey meat, ‘vegetable; (SR.) nanjad, (Haig) nafid- zul flesh. Nk. nenjur (j = dz) id.; nenjey broth. ? Kur, mehjan tidbit (meat, fish, etc.) cooked together with meal to impart relish or flavour to it. DED(S) 2956.

Ta. nata (-pp-, -nt-) to walk, go, pass, proceed, happen; natakkai walking, proceed- ing, conduct, behaviour; natattu (nafatti-) to cause to walk as a child, lead, transact, administer; natattai walking, conduct, be- haviour; nafappu going and coming, con- duct, behaviour; nafam@ftu to go, move about, circulate as report, frequent, haunt; nafamatfam going about dancing; nafavu (natavi-) to cause to go, drive; manage, administer, direct; nafavai path, road, way; nafavafikkai conduct, deed; natai walk, walking, motion, journey, way, route, con- duct, behaviour. Ma. nafakka to walk, pro- ceed, behave, be carried out, succeed; nafatta walk,- custom; nafattuka to drive, direct, arrange, manage; natappu walking, demean- our; nata walk, pace, procession, deportment; naftamatu to dance, skin; natuka to walk. Ko. narv- (nard-) to walk, proceed, happen; nagt- (narty-) to teach to walk, make to proceed, manage affair; nark gait, course of movement; natty behaviour; narvarky conduct, happen- ings. To. nar- (nar@-) to walk, happen; nart- (narty-) to make to walk, string beads; nar step, style of walking; winar at the same time, together (< wid nay one step); widinar at once, immediately re widi- mar one step only; cf. 990(d)); nad- (nad8-) to happen; nadt- (nadty-) to conduct, organize; nadc- (nadé-) to organize, conduct (feast, ceremony). Ka. nade (nadad-) to walk, march, proceed, pass, be current; 7. walk, course, pace, deport- ment; nadi = nade vb.; nadage walking, pace, walk; nadate w

Ta. nafu (nafuy-, naff-) to set up (as a pillar, pole, mast), plant, set, place, estab- lish (as fame); nafal planting; natukal mem- orial stone (for eminent person); nafavu, nafavai transplantation of seedlings; nattu (natti-) to set up, fix, plant, place in the ground (as a pole), erect, insert, establish (as Jaws, etc.), establish one in life, create, write (with style on palm leaf); n. position, posture; naftam establishing. Ma. natuka to fix, be fixed, plant, enter, pierce; natuvikka, nafiyikka to get planted or transplanted; nafu, nafuval, nafal planting, transplanting; naticcal, nafil transplanting; nafti a planted bed; naftuka to fix in the ground, plant; natta, natti post. Ko. nar- (naf-) to fix up- right in ground, transplant; na+fy young paddy and ragi plants ready for transplanting. Ka. nadu (natt-), nedu (neff-) to fix firmly, stick or fix in the ground, plant, enter, pierce; nadisu, nedisu to cause to fix in the ground, plant, etc., cause to enter, plant, implant; nade fixedly, firmly; nedige a post or pillar fixed in the ground; nede to be fixed or planted; natu, nnqu to get within, enter, be pierced or stuck into (as a thorn}, fix in the ground, plant, pierce into; natu nafu {316] nannu planting; depth; natisu to cause to enter, make plant; nafi, natti young plants fit for transplanting. Kod. nadi- (nadip-, natf-) to. plant (post, seeds); na+fi act of transplanting. Tu. nadiyuni to be planted or transplanted; nadpini, nedpuni, nedipini, nedipuni to plant; naff

Ta. natu, nafuvu middle, centre, waist, equity; nafuvan in the centre; natu- van judge, arbiter, Yama; nafta-nafu the very middie. Ma. natu middle, centre, waist, equity. Ko. narv middle, centre; nafa- nary very centre; nary ke-r the centre one of the three exogamous divisions of the Kota village. To. norf (obi. nort-) middle, centre. Ka, nadu, naduvu, mada, nadavu, nadi middle, centre, waist; natta, natfe belonging to the middie, central; naduve, nadave, nadive the very middle, the middle, between, an intervening space, difference; nafta-nadu the very middle; naddi back of the waist, hollow above the loins. Kod. nadu middle, centre. Tu, nadu id., waist, loins; nadutara middling, moder- ate: nadupulu waist, loins; naffa central, middie. Te. nadu, nadumu, nadi, nadimi middle, waist; natt- middle, central; nafta- _nadumu the very middle; naddi small of the back, back of the waist. Kol, (SR.) nadum middle. Nk. narum id.; narmut in the middle. Nk. (Ch.} addumta middle (adj.); admun in the middle. Pa. narub middle; narbul in the middle. Ga. (S.7) narpi middle, second (child); (S.2) nadum waist (< Te. ). Go. (SR.) nadum middle; (Tr. Ch. Ph.) naddum id., among, (W.} nadum among; (Mu.) naddum middle; nadum narka mid- night; (Ma. 8.) nadum middle; (M.) nadum id., between; (Ko.) narmita internal; ‘nad narka midnight (Voce. 1919); (M.) nari- mursil waist (cf. 4955 Ta. mutuku); mursul- nari loins; (ASu.) nafp® middle (¢dj.); naddi waist; naddum centre, middle. Konda narmu waist, middle.

Ka. naftu the wide-spreading roots of several kinds of grass, and this grass itself. Te. (B.) nattu the bent grass that infests cornfields (having widely spreading perennial roots or underground stems). ? To. no-fy OBe-dt the sweeping ceremony (performed with kakarx grass, Eragrostis nigra Nees.; ey 1397(a), 3471, and TGT, text 56). DED 961.

Ta. nannu (nanni-) to draw near, approach, reach, be attached to, united with, adhere; nannunar friends, adherents; nanpu, nafpu love, attachment, affection, amity, friendship, relationship; nanpan friend, com- panion, associate; nanmal, nani nearness, proximity; naniyan one who is near; nanufiku (nanuhki-), nanuku (nanuki-) to approach, draw nigh, arrive at, become attached to or united with; nal (nafp-, naff-), nallu (nalli-) to approach, join, associate with, contract friendship, befriend, like, accept; nallunar friends, adherents, associates; naffar, naftor friends, relations. Ma. nannuka, nennuka to remember with love and gratitude. Ko. nany gratitude for help given by another (or pethaps ‘friendly help’). To. not friendship. Ka. nantu, nentu relationship, friendship; nanfa, nenfa relative, kinsman, friend; nanfa- tana, nenfatana, nanfartana, nantastike, nanftike relationship; nanpu, nenpu_ friend- ship, affection, love, favour, confidential relationship, familiarity, intimacy, relation- ship, delightfulness, charm, pleasantness, agreeability. Tu. nanty, nantu, nanfastigé, nenfastigé kin, relationship; nenfe a relation. Te. nantu friendship, a friend; nanguk@du- friend, companion;. fem. nantukatte; nan(u)- pu amiability, agreeability; (K.) nanucu to like, love, be fond of; (K.) nanuvu agreeable- ness. Ga. (Oll.) nan-, (S.) nann- to enter. Cf.

Ta. natu (-pp-, -tt-) to extinguish, quench, eclipse (as rays), destroy; nantu (nanti-). to be extinguished, put out (as a lamp), set, disappear, perish, dic, become spoiled, decay, waste; nutu (-pp-, -tt-) to put out, quench, extinguish, destroy, remove; nutuppu quenching, suppressing; ntftal being extinguished; notu (-pp-, -tt-) to put out, quench, extinguish; nontu (nonti-) to perish; (inscr.) nuntu (nunti-) to be extinguished. Ka. nandu to fose one’s splendour, be ex- tinguished, be quenched, go out, be ruined, destroyed, perish, disappear, vanish, destroy; caus, nadipu, nandisu; nundu to be extin- guished, go out (as fire); nundisu to extin- guish; nondu to be extinguished, go out, be quenched; extinguish; nondisu to extinguish, quench; (Hal.) nendu to go out (as light). DED(S, N) 2964.

Ta. nattam, nattu, mnantu — snail, conch; nattai, nantanam snail; nantam conch. Ma. natta an eatable snail, a shellfish. 7u. narté a bivalved shellfish, mollusc. Te. natta snail. Go. (Ko.) netka id. (Voc. 2026). DED (S) 2965,
Ta. nattu a kind of owl. Ma. nattu Malabar owlct. Tu. nattihge an owlet. /Cf. Skt. (Vaijayanti) naktaka- owl. DED 2966.
Ma. nattuka to stammer. Ka. nattu, nettu to stutter, stammer. Tu. nattihge a man partially dumb. Te. nattu to stammer, stutter; natti stammering or stammer, stutter- ing, stutter. DED 2967.
Ta. nama (-pp-, -tt-), namar (-pp-, -tt-), namu (-pp-, -tt-) to become damp, moist. Te. nemmu moisture, damp; nemmu- konu to become damp. Cf. 3617 Ta. navu. DED(S) 2969.

Ka. namalu, nevaru to chew, masti- cate, munch, chew the cud, ruminate; namalu, nemaru chewing the cud. Tu. nauntuni, naumpuni to chew; nauntu chewing; mauruni to masticate, chew; mauravuni to mumble, chew softly. Te. namalu to thew, masticate; (B.) navulu, naulu to chew; nemaru, (B.) nevuru rumination, chewing the cud, cud; nemarucu to chew the cud. Go. (Koya Su.) nalm- to chew (< Te. namalu). Konda namli- (-t-) to munch with noise, ruminate. DED (S, N) 2970.

Ta. namufu, namiftu lower lip. Ma. ammiftam id. Ka. avudu, avundu id., jaw. Te. avudu, audu under lip. Pa, navfon. chin; (8S) navurka lip. Go. (Ma.) laonda chin (Voe. 3081). Kur. laugo lips, chin. /Cf. Skt. Wex.) narmaftha- chin. DED(S) 2971.

Ta. namai (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-), nam- uftu (namuffi-) to itch; namai, namaippu, namaiccal itching, itch, irritation; namattu cori itch, scabies. Ka, nave itching, the itch; nase itching. Tu. navunta itching, irritation in the throat; nauluni to itch, be irritated. Te. fava, nasa, nasimiri itching. Kol (Kin.) Nasam-, fat, p. 135) nesam tineng to itch. DED 29

Ta. namai (-pp-, -tt-) to vex, trouble; navai (-pp-, -tt-) to kill; 2. blemish, defect, fault, evil, disgrace, meanness, punishment. Ka. name, nave to become insignificant in bulk or importance, fall away in flesh, become lean or thin, wear away, become less, become poor; navasiga man who harasses, troubles, or torments. Tu. nameyuni to wear away, become poor; nappu injury, degradation. Te. navayu, (B.) naviyu to languish, sink, perish, fall away {n flesh; navdta languishing, grief, (B.) illness, decline. ? Malt. name to se roach. Cf. 3648 Ta. naimpu. DED(S)

Ta. namai, vellai-namai (also Lush.), vellai-nakai button-tree, Anogeissus latifolia; fiemai a kind of tree (= namai A. 1, acc. to P.L. Samy, Proce, Second International Conference Seminar of Tamil Studies, 2.46). Ma. (Lush.) vella-nava A. latifolia, Pa. nam- mi (stem nam(m)-) id. Kur. (Mirdha dial., BB 1958) nauka dhaura tree, A. 1. Cf. 5510 Ta. vellai-namai. DED(S) 2974. .

Ta. nampu (nampi-) to long for, desire intensely, trust, confide in, rely on, believe, have faith in, hope, expect, accept; n, desire, hope; nampikkai hope, trust, faith, vow; nampal desire, trusting, believing; nam- pakam confidence, trust; navvu (navvi-) to trust unreservedly, expect eagerly. Ma. nampuka to confide, desire; verbal n. nam- pikka. Ko. namb- (namby-) to believe, trust; nambyk belief, trust. To. nob- (noby-) to believe, trust; nobky belief, promise; noby promise (in songs). Ka. nambu, (K.?) nemmu to confide, trust, believe; nambike, nambige, nembuge confidence. Kod. namb- (nambi-) to trust; (Shanmugam) nambike belief. Tu. nambuni, nammuni to believe, trust, rely on; nambigé fidelity. Te. nammu_ to believe, trust; nammakamu, nammika, nammiga trust, faith, belief, confidence, reliance. Konda (BB) nami- (-t-) to believe. Kui (K.) nam- id. Kuwi (F.) namali id.; (S.) nimminai, (Su.) nam- (-it-) id., trust. Cf. 3576 Ta. naccu and

Ta. nampitiri, namptri a class of brahmans in Malabar. Ma. nampiltiri, nam-- plri a high class of brahmans. Ka. nambOri a class of Smarta Brahmanas in the Malayala country. Tu. nambtri a brahman of Malabar. DED 2976.

Ta. naya (-pp-, -nt-) to desire greatly, long for, appreciate, respect, esteem, please, beseech, implore, love, woo, show affection for, be glad, rejoice, be ‘sweet, pleasing, naya [318] nal agreeable, be cheap; nayappi (-pp-, -tt-) to induce to love or desire, persuade, win one’s compliance; nayappu affection, love, desire, delight, pleasure, cheapness; nayam, nayan grace, favour, desire, happiness, joy, gladness, goodness, civility, courtesy, love, affection, tenderness, piety, devotion, cheapness, fine- ness; nayavar lovers, friends; né love, com- passion, mercy, grace; nécam love, affection, piety, desire as for learning, suitability; nécan friend, ally, votary, devotee; fem. néci; néci (-pp-, -tt-) to love; néyam love, affection, piety, devotion, good, benefit; nimmati tranquility (< Te.). Ma. nayam gentleness; cheapness; né@San friend. Ka. Naya softness, smoothness, fineness, mild- ness, gentleness, pleasantness, cheapness. Kod. (Shanmugam) nayal gentle, smooth. Tu. naya gentleness, meekness, kindness, fineness, polish, softness, delicacy, cheapness; fine, soft, smooth, glossy, gentle, meek, kind, cheap; naiya soft; nasé soft, gentle; softness; neyimé fine; neyi-muli soft grass; neyi-panti a smooth kind of grass, soft straw; neyyoli a tender palm-leaf. Te. naya beauty; nayamu softness, smoothness, cheapness, (K.) liking, excellence; necceli friend; nemmadi_ tran- quility; tranquil; nemmi love, affection, attachment, happiness, delight, peace of mind; nemmika love, affec

Ta. narantai, nattai, nantai parasitic leafless plant, Cassytha filiformis. Ma. naranta a creeper used medicinally against asthma and obstruction. DED(S) 2979. 3605 Ta, aral (aralv-, aranf-), arul (arulv-, arunt-) to be terrified; arattu (aratti-), aruttu (arutti-) to terrify, frighten; m. fear; arafti fear; arufti quaking, trembling, agitation; aratci, arufci confusion of mind, bewilder- ment; aruku (aruki-), arukku (arukki-) to be afraid. Ma. araluka to shrink; araftuka to frighten, alarm. Ko, arkl fear because dis- covery of one’s misdeeds is expected, fecling of anxiety about getting a living, sorrow because of bereavement. Ka. aral, arala, arulu, arulu, arlu, aralu bewilderment, terror, fear; are, aremare, aremarike hesitation, doubt. Tu, narahguni to waver, hesitate, be reluctant; naraguri a sheepish man, coward; narehguni to hesitate; narefgele a dull, tardy, slow, hesitating man. Te. aragali hesitation, doubt; aramara, aramarika id., consciousness of being a stranger; aravayi diffidence, hesitation. Kol. ari fear; ars- (arast-) to fear; arp- (arapt-), arsip- (arsipt-), (Kin.) arplip- to terrify. Mk. ari fear; ars- to fear; artip- to terrify. Nk. (Ch) ai fear; arus-/ars- to fear; arup-/arp- to frighten. Pa. nar fear; narc- to fear; narpip- (narpit-) to frighten. Ga. (Oll.) nar fear; nars- to fear; narupp- (narupt-) to terrify; (S.) nar fear; narc- to fear, Malt. arkare to be terrified; arkatre to terrify. Br. narring to flee, run away. DED(S) 2980.

Ta. nari jackal; tiger (cant); (-pp-, -tt-) to be foxy; narippu fox-like nature; nariyan crafty person; nakkan fox (< Te.). Ma. nari jackal, Canis aureus (gencrally kuru-nari); tiger, esp. female tiger. Ko. nayr jackal. To. mary id. Ka. nari jackal, fox; nakke jackal; wild (when prefixed to names of plants). Kod. navi tiger. Tu. nari jackal; fox. Te. nakka, nariyadu jackal. Kol. (Br.) nakka fox. Go. (Ma.) narkal (pi. narkasku), (Ko.) nakka jackal (Voc. 1930); (L.) darka fox (Voe. 1842). Konda naka jackal, fox. Kuwi (Su. P. F. S.) nakka, (Isr.) naka jackal. DED(S) 2981.

Ta. nariccal a kind of bat. Ma. nariccil, nariccir a bat. DED 2982, 3608 7a. narufku (narufki-) to be deficient in growth, decay, grow lean as a child, fail as a business or harvest; narufkal stunted growth; narafku (narafki-) to be stunted in growth. Ka. narafu to become deficient or stunted in growth; ». state of being stunted in growth. Ty. naggu stunted, not fully developed; naradu stuntedness: — stunted; fiakkaty, fiagapaty lean, thin, stunted, faded; nandy shrunken, shrivelled. Kur, narma’and to soften down, render less acute or ve- hement, render thinner, reduce in point of stoutness; narmamda to decrease, diminish; abate; (Hahn) narma’ana to become less, give way, waste. DED(S) 2983. 3609 Ta, narai grey hairs, whiteness, a white bull, etc.; (-pp-, -tt-) to become grey- haired, grow grey, fade as crops. Ma. nara greyness, hoary age; narekka to grow grey, be whitish; narappikka to bring on old age; (Tiyya) nera greyness; nerakkuva to become grey. Ko, nar grey hairs. To. nar grey. Ka. nare greyness of the hair, grey hair, hoary age; vb, (naret-, narat-) to become grey (hair); narati woman whose hair has become grey; nere greyness of the hair; vb, to become grey. Kod. nere grey. Tu. naré greyness, hoariness; grey, hoary; naraye grey-haired man, white- spotted ox; grey, white-spotted; nareti white- spotted cow; narevuni (hair) becomes grey. Te. nara greyness of the hair, grey hair; narayu, nariyu to grow grey (of the hair); narapu greyness of the hair; (VPK) neriyu to beco

Ta. nalahku (nalafki-) to grow faint, wilt, suffer, pine, lose stiffness, become crumpled, bend as a bow; nalakku (nalakki-) to crumple as cloth or paper, soil slightly, sully, tarnish; nali (-v-, -nt-) to waste, pine away, suffer, be in distress, yield before a foe, fail, slide, fall down; afflict, distress; C-pp-, -tt-) to afflict, cause distress; 1, suffer- ing, pain, thinness, leanness; nalivu trouble, distress, affliction, ruin, destruction; nalutku (nalufki-) to beome lean, be distressed; nalkGr (-v-, -nt-) to suffer, be wearied. Ka. nalagu, nalugu to become rumpled or ruffled (as cloth or paper), become rumpled or flabby by the heat of the sun, etc., droop as vegetables, etc., grow weakened, faint or feeble from sickness, trouble, grief; nalagisu, nalugisu to rumple, etc.; nalavu, naluyu weakness. Tu. nalaguni to fade, wither, be reduced by sickness. Te. naligu, naltigu, naliyu to be crushed, bruised, broken or reduced to powder, be crumpled or rumpled, (K. also) fade, wither, diminish (as brightness); nalagudu, naligudu state of being crushed, bruised or troubled; nalfcu to crush, bruise, rub; nalaékuva, nalukuva illness, indisposition; nalata disease; nali_ illness, indisposition; dust, powder; nalikamu small- ness, littleness, thinness; naliguli greatly crushed or broken; nal({u)cu, nal(u)pu to crush, rub; (K.) nalapu to crumple (flowers, etc.), harass. Ga. (8.3) nalpap- to squeeze. Konda nalni- (-t-) to be squeezed in handling; nalp- to squeeze, crush; nalnis- to c

Ko. nayl- (nalc-) to play; naylk play. To, naes- (na+sy-) to play (a game); ne-sk play. Ka. (Nanj. Hav.) nali to dance; (PBh.) naliyutt(e) dancing with delight. Kod. nali- (naliv-, nalifij-) to play (in songs). Tu. nalipuni to dance, leap; nalike dancing, jumping. Kur. nalna to dance, skip or jump about, bound up (as running water amidst boulders); caus. nalta’and, nalda’ana, nal@dna. Mair. late (lada) to dance; lalde to make to dance, lead about. DED(S, N) 2988.

Ta. nallam charcoal; blackness, dark- ness. Te. nalupu black, blackness; nalla id., charcoal; nallana blackness; nallani black. Ga. (S.3) nalam black, dirty (< Te.); (OU. S.7) narkam, (S.) narka, narkim night; (S.?) nisa narkam midnight. Go. (Tr. W. Ph. M.) narkd, (A. Y. Ch. D. G. Ko.) narka, (Ma.) nahka night (Voc. 1932). (An old Dr. internal sandhi in Ga. Go.: *nalk- > *nark-.) Cf. 235 Ta. al. DED(S) 2985, 2990.

Ta. navarai a kind of paddy; nakarai a kind of rice. Ma. navira, naviri, nakara a rice that ripens within two or three months; navara id.; Paspalum frumentaceum (2). Tu. navara a kind of grain; navare a kind of rice. Te. nivari, nivvari Oryza. /? Cf. Skt. nivara- wild rice; Turner, CD[AL, no. 7571. DED(S) 2991.
Ta. navir man’s hair; naviram man’s tuft of hair, crown of head, head. Ka. navir(u) hair. DED 2992.

Ta. navil (navilv-, navinr-) to say, tell, learn, utter, sound loudly, sing, perform (as a dance); navirru (navirri-) to say, utter, declare with authority; nuval (nuvaly-, nuvanr-) to say, declare, utter; m. word, saying; nuvarci saying, utterance. ? To. néw voice; song. DED 993.

Ta. navu (-v-, -nt-) to become soft, be soddened by boiling, rot, decay (as clothes, books, wood), be weary (as the limbs by labour). Ko. nav- (navd-) (cloth) becomes damp and chilly; (navt-) to make (cloth) to become damp and chilly. Cf. 3594 Ta. nama. DED(S) 2994. 3618 Ka, navuru, navaru, naviru that is tender, soft, fine, thin, as grass, hair, cloth, paper and other articles. Te. navuru, nauru soft, delicate. DED(S) 2995. navvi [320] nanai 3619 Ta, navvi female deer, young of a deer, youth, beauty; nauvi deer; (PP7J) navi a kind of deer. Te. navali woman. ? Cf.
Ta. navvi. DED(S, N) 3010. 3635 Ga, (S.7) nagu earring. Konda nagu id. (of women). Kuwi (F.) nagt earring, brass wire (worn by females only). DEDS 561.

Ta. nagukku (narukki-) to evade, slip away; nagunaru (-pp-, -tt-) to speak evasively, shuffle; naguppu (naruppi-) to shrink from, shirk; naruvu (naguvi-) to steal or skuik away, evade, shift, shuffle; naruval evading, skulking; nazuvi one who gives indirect answers, slippery person. Te. nakku to hide or conceal oneself, crouch, lie in wait. ? Kol. nadi- (nadit-) to hide (intr), (tiger) crouches; (SR.) nadas-, nars- (= nars-) to hide. Go. (Ko.) ark- to lurk, crouch, bend down (Voc. 86). Kuwi (Isr.) nand- Git) to go underneath, hide. DED(S) 2996.

Ta. nal night. Kol. a-le id.; a-lini in the morning; a-lintanat from morning. Nk. ale night; areni early in the morning. Nk. (Ch.} Men night; arda alen midnight (arda < TA). Pa, nendu nal midnight; ? and- evening to come on; andek evening; in the evening; andpip- (andpit-) to make to become evening (i.e. stay until evening). Konda (BB and K. Sova dial.) nara night; (K. Sova dial.) sir narne just early in the morning (for sir, see 2553 and 2779; communicated by K.). Pe. nana night; nanagge, narihig early in the morning. Mang. nalan darkness, night; natihiy early in the morning. Kui nadangi night; nadisi morning; ddangi, (K.) da’ana night; daisi early morning. Kuwi (Su.) la’ana, (P.) la’a, (F.) la’anga, (S.) _izanga, (D.) laV’aga, (Isr. 12a night; (Su.) Ifisi’e, (P.) la’asi early morning; (F.) lai, (S.) lai, (Isr. ) 17°i morning. Br. nan night. DED(S, N) 2997, and from DED(S) 199, 3621A Tu. nallu, nallu a kind of flea which infests poultry, cats, etc. Te. nalli bedbug. Ga. (P.) nandke, (S. 5.3) nanke bug. Go. (Tr. D. Mu.) nar, (Ma.) nar(i), (W. Ph.) nar id. (Voc, 1941); (LuS.) naré id. Konda (K.) nalu (pi. nalku), (N. and W. dial.) naru (pi narku), (BB) nag id. Pe. nanka, narka‘id. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) nakta, (F.) nukta (i.e. nakta), (S.) natka, (D.) naf’a id. (nakta metathesized from natka, originally a plural form). Kur, narga id. Malt, narge id. DED(S) 2998.

Ta. nal dense; nalam width, breadth, extent; nali (-v-, -nt-) to be close together, crowded, be vast in extent; n. closeness, density, vastness, width, multitude; nalippu overcrowdedness. Ka. (K.?) nati thickening of the skin, callosity. Konda nando fat, stout, bulky; many; a flock. DEN 48.

Ta. nara-nay-enal, naru-nar-enal onom. expr. of grinding the teeth; neruneru (-pp-, -tt-) to gnash one’s teeth, snap (as a stick in breaking), sound (as the biting of a hard, brittle, or crispy substance); neru-ner-enal onom. expr. signifying (@) crashing sound, (b) snapping or breaking sound; nerukk-enal onom, expr. signifying snapping sound; nera- ner-enal onom. expr. of grinding or gnashing the teeth; nerumu (nerumi-) to gnash. Ko, narkn with noise of crunching up bones; nark nark in- (id-) (gristle) makes noise when chewed. Ka. nara, naranarane, narak imit. sound of the noise of the cracking of the fingers and other joints or of the breaking of wood. 7u. naruguru, narunuru a crack, crash; sound produced while eating anything crisp. DED 2999.
Ka. naravalu, naruval, narval the tree Premna spinosa ot longifolia. Tu. naruvotu a kind of aromatic tree, the leaves of which are used for curry or‘ medicine, Saccharum munja {sic}. DED 3000.

Ta. narukku (narukki-) to cut off, mince, chop, smash; n. a piece cut off. Ma. narukkuka to cut off, clip, cut in pieces as paper; narukku a bit of palm-leaf. Te. naraku, naruku, (K. also) narku to cut, hew, fell, sever, chop; nm. a cut, wound; narakudu, narukudu cutting, a cut. Go. (Tr.) narkanad to cut (but not of meat nor of crops, but generally of wood); (L.) narkana, (M.) narkana to cut; (Ch.) nark- to cut (wood); (W. Ph.) narkand to chop; (Ch. Mu.) nark- to cut with axe; (Ma.) nark- to cut (firewood); (S.) nark- to cut (fuel) (Voc. 1931). Konda naRk- to chop, cut. Kuwi (Isr.) nafhg- (it) to lop off branches. Malt. narke to claw, scratch. Cf.
Ta. narumu = (narumi-) to crush between the teeth. Ka. Nazuju, noraju, nuruju gravel, grit. Te. naruju a small quantity; nayumadu to powder; narumu id.; 7. powder. DED(S) 3002.

Ta. naruvili, naruli sebesten plum, Cordia myxa. Ma. naruvari id. Te. nakkera id. Cf. 5408 Ta. viracu. DED(S) 3003. 3628 7u. nana, nanala yet, still, once more, farther on; (B-K.) nana, nana next, further on; nanala, nanala yet, still, Kur. nanna other; nantard elsewhere, in another direction. Malt. nan other; nane another; nandu again. DED 3004.
Ta. nanavu, nana wakefulness (op- posite to kanavu), certainty, truth, reality, daylight. Ka. nanasu, nanesu, nenasu truth; truly, certainly. DED 3005.
Ta. nanai (-v-, -nt-) to become wet, be moistened, soaked; (-pp-, -tt-) to wet, moisten, soak; nanaivu wet, moisture, humidity; nan- cey wet lands, wet cultivation (for -cey, cf.

Ta. nanai (-v-, -nt-) to bud, to appear; n. flower-bud. Ko. nan a bud, a wick. To. nan leaf-shoot. Ka. nane flower-bud, unblown flower, opening bud; vb. flower-buds to come forth, to bud. Te. nana flower, bud, sprout; nanayu to blossom; nanucu id., sprout; (K.) nan({u)pu to cause to blossom. DED 3007.

Ta. nanni that which is small, short. Te. nanna short. DED 3008. 3633 Ta, na, nakku, naivu tongue; navu (navi-) to thrust out the tongue and move it sideways, mock by thrusting out the tongue. Ma, na, nakku, navu tongue. Ko. na-lg id.; na-y tongue, esp. of a slaughtered cow or buffalo. To. no-f tongue, eddy; no- taf roof of mouth (tat pot). Ka. nalage, nalige tongue; (Hal.; Kumt., U.P.U.) fialige id. Kod. na-vu id. Tu. nal¥yi id. Te. nalika, naluka, nalka id.; (Merolu) nélka id. Kol na-lka id. "NK. natka id. Pa. nevid id. Ga. (Ol) nan, (S.) nangu id. Konda nalika id. DED(S, N) 3009.

Ta. naku); eda tender, young; eda- niru tender coconut water, a tender coconut; ira [S51] ir nelfta, nelatuka woman. Kol. le-nga calf. Nk, lenga id.; léta young (of infants). Pa. led (pl. ilenkul) young man, youth; ile (pl. ilecil) young woman, girl. Ga. (Oll.) ile bride; iled grown-up girl, woman; ilend (pl. iler) bridegroom. Go. (Tr.) raiydl (pl. raiydr) adult boy; fem. raiya; (Ph. ChD.) raiyor (pl. -k) boy; fem. raiyd; (M.) layor young; {L.) leyor young (nasc.); ley® id. (fem.); (Ma.) leyor young man; fem. leya (pl. -sk); (Ko.) leyond (pl leyor) young man; fem. léydar (Voce, 3066); (ASu.) diyydr young man; diyya young woman, (KoT.) leyondu young man; ledadi young woman; (SR.) Iehmi youth (Vec, 3125). Konda léya calf; léta tender (< Te.). Kui lavenju grown-up boy, young man, youth; laa grown-up girl, young woman; leti soft, yielding. Kuwi (F.) yaa young woman, virgin; (S.) laa virgin; (D.) ra’a (pl. -sika) young woman, (lIsr.) ya?a young unmarried girl; (F.S.) liddi soft. Kur. lidum soft. ? Br. ilum brother; jr sister. (For Kui livenju, laa, Kuwi ra’a, etc., see Krishnamurti 1980.) /Cf. Pkt. Lliva- child, boy, DED(S, N) 436, and from DED(S) 248.

Ta. natu (niati-) to seek, inquire after, pursue, examine, investigate, desire earnestly, know, understand, resemble, measure, reach, approach, think, consider, scent as dogs, be measured; nattam eye, sight, examination, investigation, astrology, beauty, desire, intention, pursuit, aim, quest, sus- picion, movement. Ma. natuka to follow with the eyes, covet, seek; nafttam investigation, desire. Tu, naduni to search, seek; nadavuni to cause to search. Go. (Ph. W.) nag- to see, look at; (W.) nad- to gaze (Voc. 1971). Cf. 3766. Ta. néfu, 3794 Ta. nd¢ttam, and 3642 Pa. nad-. DED(S) 3011.

Ta. natu (Obl and adj. nattu-) coun- try, district, province, locality, situation, earth, land, world, kingdom, state, rural tracts (opposite to nakaram), open place, side, agricultural tract; n&tap inhabitant, countryman, ruler, lord of a country; nati woman of a country, queen of a country; naiftavar countrymen, people of the country; natfam chiefship of'a district; nattar people of a country; natfan inhabitant of a country. Ma. natu cultivated land (opposite to kafu), the country (opposite to town), kingdom, province, smaller district; naffar the people. Ko. na-r (Obl na-f-) country, settled area (opposite to jungle), place where dead go. To. no-y (obl. no-f-) sacred place, dairy complex which is a god. Ka. nadu (culti- vated, planted) country (in opposition to kadu), province, district, country (in op- position to the town); nadadi countryman, tustic, an ordinary common person; nadiga village superintendent in the service of a smarta guru. ‘Kod. na-di district. Tu. nadu, nady district, village; nadige one of the cultivating classes in northern part of Canara. Te. nadu, nadika, (inscr.) napdu a country. Go. (Tr. W. Ph. Mu. A. etc.) nar (obl. nat-, pl. nahk), (Ma.) nag (bl. naf-, pl nahku) village (Voc. 1964); (LuS.) nagoo id. Konda niu (Obl. nat-, pl. naRku) id.; ndfan villager. Pe. naz/nas (obl. nat-, pi. nasku) village; nafakan villager. Mand. nay (obl. nat-) village. Kui naju (pl. naska) village, town, hamlet; nato village as contrasted with the fields or forest, home;

Ta. nan, nanmai sense of shame, bashfulness, modesty; nanam shyness, coy- ness (as a feminine quality), bashfulness in certain relationships (as the mother-in-law towards her son-in-law, as a man in a gather- nani [322] nar ing of women), embarrassment, delicate regard, esteem, respect, shame, sensitive dread of evil, shrinking as a sensitive plant to the touch, shying as a startled animal; nanu (nani-) to be shy, feel bashful, em- barrassed, be abashed, shrink back, feel repugnance or dislike; nanal feeling bashful, modest; nani (-pp-, -tt-) to be shy, bashful; n. bashful person. Ma. ninam shame, feeling of honour, modesty, bashfulness, disgrace; nanikka to be ashamed, bashful; nanippikka to make ashamed; ninuka to be ashamed. Ko. na+nd- (na-ndy-) to feel modesty before elder person; kana-n(d)- (kana-ndy-) to be modest, abashed, feel self-effacing (kan eye + na-nd-); kapaenm (< kan + na+nm; obl. kana-nt-) sense of shame; na-ckm (obl. na-ckt-) shame. To. no-n- (no-ny-) to be ashamed, be shy; no-n, no-sky shame. Ka. nan bashfulness, sense of shame, embarrass- _ment, shame, modesty; inherent excellence, grace; that is a cause of shame,-an organ of generation; nancu, naiicu, nicu to become abashed, ashamed or embarrassed; caus. nancisu, etc.; ndficike, nacike, nacige bash- fulness, sense of shame, shame, modesty, decorum, inherent excellence, grace. Kod. fiaena shame. Tu. ndnu, nana nakedness, nudity, the buttocks; nacuni to blush, be ashamed; naciké, nacig8 shame, modesty, blus
Ta. nanuvam common myna, Gracula tristis. Ma. nanuvam id. DED 3015. .

Ta. nattanar, natti, nattiin husband’s sister. Ma. natttin id., brother’s wife. Ko. na-tueny sister-in-law, female cross-cousin (woman speaking in all cases). Ka. nadani, nadini, naduni husband’s sister, brother’s wife; (Spencer) husband’s younger sister, wife’s sister, younger brother’s wife. Pa. andil, tandil elder brother’s wife. Konda nanra wife’s younger sister. Mand. nanjar id. Kui nanja younger sister-in-law. Kuwi (F.) nanjo sister-in-law; (Isr.) nanjo wife’s younger sister. Kur. nasgo elder brother's wife. /Cf. Skt. nanandy-, nandini-, nanda- husband’s sister; Turner, CDIAL, no. 6946. DED(S) 3017. 3645 Kur. nad devil, evil spirit; idol; nadas devil-worshipper; rascal, rogue, knave; nad-xall any field where an idol has been set up. Malt. nade a stone set up in the name of a deity; nado relating to nade, or a deity; nado-maku the intestines of a slaughtered animal, such as the liver, heart, etc., which are supposed to belong to the gods. DED 3018. 3646 Kui napa tuber, edible bulb. Kuwi (Isr.) napa forest food; edible tuber.

Ta. nim (obi, nam(m)-) we; nankal we (exclusive). Ma, nam (obl. nam(m)-) we, I and you. Ka, navu (od/. nam-) we (inclusive) (in Old Ka.; G. S. Gai, Bull, Deccan College Research Institute 1.411 f.); we (in mod. Ka.). Tu. nama we (inclusive). Te. manamu (obl. mana-) id. Kol. ne-nd id. Nk. nénd, n@m id. Go. namot we. Kur, nam (ob/. nam-, nang-) we (inclusive). Malt. nam (obl, nam-) id. Br. nan we. Cf, 5154 Ta. yam. DED 3019.

Ta. nampu (nampi-) to become meagre, thin, emaciated; 2. anything lean; nampal emaciation. Ka, nambu to be slack, devoid of energy, slothful or idle; nimba an jdle or slothful man. Cf. 3598 Ta. namai. DED 3020. :
Ta. nampu a small climber. Ma. nimpu a sprout; nampu shoot, sprout; the scion of a family. Te. namu new sprout growing from the stumps of the great millet. DED 3021.

Ta. nay, nai dog. Ma. nay id. Ko. nary id. To. no-y id. Ka. nay, nayi id.; nayitana doggishness. Kod. na-y dog. Tu. nayi id. Kol. a+te id.; resn a+te wild dog (ie. *res na-te, with original initial n- pre- served in this combination; see 817). Nk. ate dog. Nk. (Ch.) ate id. Pa. netta (pl. nettel) id.; iric netta wild dog. Ga. (Oll.) néte, (S.) enette dog: (P.) kope nete wild dog. Go. (Tr.} nai, (W.) nai, (M.) nai, (A. Ma. Ko.) ney dog (Voc. 2030); (M.) rac nai, (Ko.) rasi ney wild dog (Voc. 3010). Konda (BB; K., p. 181) nukuri dog. Pe. nekur, nikur id. Kui nakugi (pl. nakuraka, nahurka), nahori (pi. nahorka, nahka) id. Kuwi (F.) neh’tiri (pl. neska), (S.) nehudi (pl. neska), (Su.) nih’uri (v/, neska), (Isr.) neh?uri (7. -ka/neska) id. The element -kuri in Konda, etc. is probably to be con- nected with 2149 Ta. kurantai, etc., i.e. originally ‘young of dog’. ? Cf. 2916 Ta. fai. / Cf. Nahali nay dog. DED(S) 3022.

Ta. nar fibre, string, cord, rope; nari bow-string, fibrous covering at the bottom of a leaf-stalk, as of a coconut palm; naram cord. Ma. nar fibres of bark, strings and ropes from fibre.
