Ko, ma-r thin rope. Jo. no+r stting made from bark. Ka. nar fibre, hemp of plants, cloth made of fibres or hemp; nari bow-string, the web which surrounds the stipes of a palm-tree branch. Kod. na-ri fibre of plant. Tu. nary, naru fibrous bark or hemp of plants. Te. nara fibrous bark of trees; nari bow-string. Kol. (SR.) nara rope; (Kin.) nara id., hanging nar- {323] nikar rope-shelf. Go. (S.) nar @pa anjan tree (from which rope is prepared) (Voc. 1965). Konda nari vilu bow with bowstring. DED(S) 3023.

Ta. narattai, naram orange; narantam flower of bitter orange. Ma, (Tiyya) naram lemon plant. Ka. (K.?) nadrivana, naruli the lime fruit (2). Tu. narahgayi, ndrengi a kind of orange. /Cf. Skt. narahga- orange; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7073 (Su. 1973, p. 139). DEN 49.

Ta. nal, natu, nanku, nalku four; nankam fourth; nari fourfold; narpatu, nappatu forty; nantru 400; nal-ayiram 4,000; natvar four persons, narkali chair; nannanku by fours; four times four. Ma. nal, nalu, nafku, nan (before nasals) four; nalar, nalvar, naluvar four persons; nalam fourth; nalkkali quadruped; chair; mnatk- kalikka to creep on all fours; nalpatu forty; nanfru 400; nannalu by fours; nannanhku four times four. Ko. na-ng (n, not ) four; naeng nu-r 400; naslvat, na-lat forty; nasr pasny four pa-ny measures; na-r pa+d forty days; na-n digl four months; na-n mayn four o’clock; na+n janm four castes; naen mu-l four directions; na- calg four calg measures; na+ naef four days; na+ nu-r 400; Na+ na-ng by fours. To. no-+ng (n, not g; obl. no-engn-) four; pasng fourteen; no« xwa-w four kwa-x measures; no+ nusr 400; nat po® forty. Ka. naf(u), nal(w)ku, naku, ni four; nalvar four persons; nalvattu, nalvattu) forty; naindyu, nanfru, nannfru 400. Kod, na-li four; na» padi forty; na- nueri 400; na-vé four persons (in songs); nanna-li by fours. 7u. naly four; nalane fourth; nalvery four persons; nalpa forty; nalyntdu, naintidu, nantdu 400; natkolu, nalkolu four times, navola four seers. Te. nalugu, naluvu four; naluguru, naluvuru four persons; naluvadi, nalubadi, nalubhai, nala- bhai forty; naluvandru forty persons; nan- ntru 400. Kol. na-lig four things; nalgur four men; nallav four women; na-f udul four days; (SR.) nalgur four persons, Nk, nalin four things; nalgur four men; na

Ta. nal day, early dawn, forenoon; nalai, ndlaikku tomorrow; nanalum daily. Ma, nai day; nala tomorrow; (the n is lost in marral after tomorrow and atra{ so many days; so Gt.). Ko. na-| day; tomorrow; na-la-l -any day after tomorrow; ? nanky day after tomorrow. To, no-t day; ? no-fo- kosym daily, every day. Ka. nal day, time; nale the very next day, tomorrow; nadadu, nadidu, nadiddu, naliddu, ndlirdu the day after tomorrow. Kod. na-le tomorrow; na-le-ki in the near future. Te, n&@du (pl. natlu) day, time, that day, that time; nandu, nanatiki day by day, by degrees, gradually. Kol. nat (obi. naf-) (in nal two days; in natugi for two days only). Nk. marner the day after tomorrow (see 4766). Go. (Tr.) nari, (M.) naz, nari, (A. Y. G.) nari, (Mu. Ma.) nar(i), (Ko.) nar tomorrow; (W.) nari yesterday (?; but cf. H. kal ‘yesterday’ and ‘tomorrow’). Konda nandiy on that day; nandiRi of that day; nandiRand from that day onwards. Kur, nela, (Hahn, Bleses) n@la tomorrow. Malt. léle id. DED(S) 3025.

Ta. nal youth, juvenility, freshness. Ka. nali,.nalanalake softness, smoothness, tenderness, beauty, brightness, DEN 50. 3658 Ma, nikakka, nikaluka, nikaruka to fill up as a hole; nikappu filling up, levelling; nikattuka, nikarttuka to fill up, level, mend, perfect; nikagttuka to fulfil. Ka. (Hav.) nigi to fill a hole. Tu. neggi, neggya, ni ’ niggya full, complete; nigiyuni to be levelled, be filled up as a pit; nigipuni to level; nigipa- vuni to cause to level; nugiyuni, nuggiyuni to be filled up; nugipuni, nugupuni to fill up, as a pit. DED 3026.

Ta. nikar (-pp-, -tt-; nikari-) to shine, be visible; nm. lustre, brightness, splendour; nikar (-pp-, -tt-) to shine; niku-nikuy-enal expr. signifying the glittering of an object. Ka. niga, nigi glow; niga niga, nigi nigi very glowingly or brightly; negar to become manifest, notorious, well-known, famous, shine, appear; negarcu to make well-known, praise, make oneself renowned, cause oneself to appear, shine; negarte fame, notoriety, accomplishment; nege to be purified, become clear, shine. Tu. nigisy glitter, shining. Te. niganiga brilliancy, glitter; nigarincu to shine, glitter; nigarimpu shining, brilliancy, glitter; niggu brilliancy; negadu to be published, be well-known or famous; (K.) negad(i)ta fame. DED 3027. nikar (324] nira

Ta. nikar (-v-, -nt-) to resemble; (-pp-, -tt-; nikari-) to be similar, alike, rival; n, comparison, likeness, simile, equal, parallel, match, battle, fight; nikarttal battle, fight; nikara a term of comparison; nikarar enemies. Ma. nikar equality. DED 3028.

Ta. nikar (-v-, -nt-) to happen, occur, be current, passing (as time; nikar kalam present time, tense), enter, pass, abide, con- tinue, be performed, transacted, carried out; nikarcci occurrence, incident, event, situation, business, present moment; nikarpu occur- rence, event; nikarvu id., present time; nikarttu (nikartti-) to effect, perform, transact, set on foot, bring to pass; speak, say, mention, narrate, declare. Ka. megar to undertake, engage in, prepare, perform, do, make, cause to act, come, be used, be finished; negarcu to cause to undertake or do, cause oneself to undertake, begin, perform, compose, produce, cause; negarte action, work, practice, rites, conduct. Te. negadu to increase, spread, extend, improve, progress; neggu to be successful, succeed, thrive, prosper; neggincu to cause to succeed, effect, fulfil; niggu to manage, be able, cope; niggincu to manage, carry through, accomplish. DED 3029. 3662 Ma, nikalam, nigalam haughtiness; nikalikka, nigalikka to swell, strut. Ka nikkulisu to bend oneself from coquetry or in coquettish manner; nilku to stretch one- self upwards, rise up to one’s full height, stretch oneself; nil(u)ku to stretch oneself, stretch one’s limbs from fatigue, etc., stand on tiptoe, rise up to; (Gowda) nilki to peep. Tu, nilkuni, (B-K. also) ninky id.; to reach; nikaliyuni to peep. Kor. (M.) niki id. Te, nikku to become or stand erect, rise; be proud, conceited or presumptuous; 7. erect- ness, rising: pride, conceit; nikkavoducu to becom
Ka. nikkuva truth, certainty; truly, certainly. Je. nikkamu, nikkuvamu, (B.) nikkemu truth, fact, reality, certainty; true, real, certain; (B.) nik-kala a true dream. DED 031.

Ka. niggedi a shameless (or foolish) person. Tu. niggu, niggira inelegance, filthi- ness, nastiness, disgust; niggisy an unclean man. Te, niggadi cruel. DED 3665 Konda nin- (-it-) to rise up from a sitting position, wake up (from sleep); nik- to lift up, raise, rouse’ from sleep. Pe. nin(g)- (nigt-) to rise, get up; nik- (-t-) to raise. Mand. nin(g)- to rise; nik- to raise. Kui ninga (ningi-) to rise, arise, stand up; 7. act of rising, standing up; nipka (< nik-p-; nikt-) to raise, cause to stand up. Kuwi (F.) ningali to arise; nikhali to arouse; (S.) ninginai to rise; nikkh’nai (?) to rouse; (Su. P.) ning- (-it-) to rise; (Su.) nik- (-h-) to lift up, raise; (Isr) ning- (-it-) to get up, wake up; nik- Ch-) to lift up; to awaken. ?Cf. 3730 Ta. niva. DED(S) 3033. 3666 Kur. nisigna (nisgyas) to overlay with a coating of earth carefully smoothed down, stop a hole with earth, besmear with any adhesive substance; refl.-pass. nis'grnd. Mait. nisge to smooth; nisgro smooth; nisye to shampoo. DED 3034.
Tu. nitta pleasure; pleasant. Konda nit- to become sweet; nitni, (so7- masc.) nitnat sweet. DED 3035.

Ta. nina (-pp-, -nt-/-tt-) to tic up, fasten, braid; ninar (-v-, -nt-) to tie, fasten; ninavai tying, bondage, that which is plaited. To. nin twisted string; ar nin silver waist- string (ax waist). Ka. (Hal.) nene, (Gowda) nEnE wick. Tu. niné, nené, niné a wick. DED(N) 3036.
Ta. ninaru affection, love, benefit, good (< Te.). Te. nenaru affection, love, tenderness, pity. DED 3038. :
Te. nippati, nippatfu a sort of cake or biscuit. Kol ipate, (SR.) nipate, (Kin.) nipafe bread. Nk. ipat (pi. ipadl) id. DED 3039.

Ta. nira (-pp-, -nt-) to be full, be thick, crowded, spread, expand (as air or water); nirattu (niratti-) to level up; nirappu (nirappi-) to fill, replenish, cause to abound, complete, spread; 2. fullness, completeness, levelness; nirappam fullness, repletion, per- fection, excellence; nirampu (nirampi-) to become full, complete, replete, be abundant, end, terminate, attain puberty, mature (as grain); nirampa fully, abundantly; niravu (niravi-) to be filled, become level, full, covered, spread, expand; level, fillup, equalize (as threads for weaving). Ma. nirattuka to lay prostrate, level; niravuka to level ground; nirattu road, highway. /r. rombu to become full (cf. coll. Ta. romba fully). Kod. (Shan- mugam) nerap levelling. Te. nerayu, nerayu to spread (intr.), extend, be filled; nerayika becoming full; nerapu, nerapu to spread (tr.), fill; n. spreading; adj. much; nera, nera full; neravu, neravu fullness, spreading, full, much, well-spread, broad; reppamu fullness. Kol. neray- (nerayt-) to spread (intr.); nerap- (nerapt-) to spread (grain, chillies). Cf. 3682 Ta. nirai (in Te. there has been convergence so that separation is impossible); cf. 3770 Ta. nér. DED(N) 3041.

Ta. nira (-pp-, -nt-) to arrange in order, divide equally; nirappam symmetry, uniformity; niral (niralv-, niranr-) to be eo nirudu [325] nil placed in a row, arranged in order; n. order, arrangement, equality, similarity; niravu (niravi-) to lic in rows; nirai (-v-, -nt-) to place in a row; be in a row, form a column, be regular, orderly; (-pp-, -tt-) to arrange in order, classify, string together; follow in succession; #. row, column, line, series, order, regularity, arrangement, collection, herd; niraiccal, nitaical screen, hedge with stakes covered ,with palm leaves in regular order. Ma, nira line, row; nirakka to stand in a line, agree; nirattuka to put in a straight line, adjust, divide equally; nirappu evenness, agreement; niravu a straight line. Ko. nerv- (nerd-) to stand or be in a line (e.g. in dancing in a circle, in going along in company); nert- (nerty-) to make to stand in line. To. ner- (ner8-) to go in a line (people, ants), be in a line (trees), sit in a line; ner row, line; nerm (obi. nert-) companion on journey; er- (er6-) to sit in rows, walk in single file. Ka. nerake, nerike fence or wall of bamboos, palm branches, etc. Cf. 3772 Ta. nér. DED(S) 42.

Te. nirudu last year; nirufi of the last year (communicated by M, Kandappa Chetty). Nk. (Ch.) ernd last year. Pa, nirdi id.; next year. Ga, (OIL) nirdin last year. Go, (Tr.) niré, (W. Ph. Mu. Ma. Ch.) nire, (Ch.) nire id. (Yoo, 2006). Konda nirund id. DEDS(N) 567

Ta. nil (nirp-, ninr-) to stand, stop, halt, be steadfast, stay, continue, cease, be stopped, remain, wait, delay; nilavu (nilavi-) to be permanent, fixed, stay, exist, be in use, be extant; nilavu (nildvi-) to be permanent, fixed; niluvai standing, staying, balance, arrears; nilai standing, staying, firmness, stability, permanence, condition, state, place, stopping place, residence, depth of water allowing one to stand in, usage, custom; (prob. -v-, -nt-) to remain permanent, stay; (-pp- -tt-) to obtain a footing, be settled, lasting, delay, be just deep enough to allow a man to stand (as a river); nilaippu perma- nence, continuance, durability, perseverance, persistence; nilaimai condition, state (as of affairs), standing posture, firmness, truth, probity; nirpatu the immovables, as the vegetable kingdom; ninru always, perma- nently; nirral standing, staying; niruttu (nirutti-), niruvu (niruvi-) to set up, raise, erect, fix on a firm basis, determine, stop (as a person), defer, put an end to; nirai bringing to a stand, stopping, fixed position, strength of mind, self-control; niru (-pp-, -tt-) to put, set, place, create, construct, weigh, balance, decide; niguppan a balance. Ma. nilkka to stand, remain, last, stop, cease; nilpu standing, arrears, balance; nilpikka to make to stand, appoint; nila standing, place, position, a stop, station in life, custom; nilama quality, state; nilekka to come to a stand, cease, get a footing, remain; nilavu balance, arrears; niruttuka to ma

Ta. nilam ground, earth, land, soil, field, the earth, world, place, region. Ma. nilam ground, soil, the earth, a ricefield. Ko. nelm (obi. nelt-), neta-l ground. To. neln (od/. nelt-) ground, earth; nesn ground (in songs); nesof- (nesofy-) to put forehead to something (e.g. ground) ceremonially, put to forehead in salutation (for *of-, see 79). Ka. nela ground, soil, land, floor. Kod. nela ground. Tu. nela earth, soil, floor, ground. ? Pa. nendil, nédil earth, ground, floor. Cf. 2913 Ta. fialam. DED 3044. 3677 Ta, nivaru (nivari-) to swarm, gather thick. Ma, nivaru a throng. DED 3045.

Ka. (K.?) nibberagu forgetting one- self as a result of long or continuous amaze- ment. Te. nivvera, nivveragu great surprise, wonder, astonishment; nivvera-padu to be astonished or frightened; nivvera-patu aston- ishment, great fear, alarm,

Ta. nial, niral shade, shadow, reflec- tion, lustre; nigal (nigalv-, niganr-) to cast shadow, give shelter, shine, be reflected; nirali lustre, light; nigarru (nigarri-) to shed radiance, shade, protect; niraru (nigari-) to shine; nirai light, lustre. Ma, nigal shadow, likeness; niralikka to be reflected, appear faintly (as a tooth through the gums). Ko. ner shade. To. ne-s shade, reflection, relics preserved for second funeral; ne-! poly house in which relics are kept, hut at male funeral place; ne-I kal flint; ne-twier- (ne- twi-ry-) to be reflected (lit. to be visible as a reflection; for twier-, see 3566); ne-twi+k- (nee twi-ky-) to toll bell while mourning over relics between funerals (lit. to lift bell for/ over the relics: for twi-k-, see 3376(@)). Ka. neral, neral, nellu shade, shadow, reflection. Kod. néla shade, shadow. Tu. nirely, irely id. Te. nida id., reflection. Kol. ni-nda shade. Nk. ninda id. Pa. niga id. Ga. (OIL) niki id. Go. (L.) nira shadow; (S.) nira, nirka, (Ko.) nirka shade (Voc. 2009). Konda nira shadow, shade, reflection; (BB) nitga shadow. Pe. tiga id. (< *nrig-). Mand. rige id. Kut rilga id. shade, reflection, picture; (P.) rilgedi shade, shadow. Kuwi (P.) tia id. DED(S) 3046.

Ta. nira (-pp-, -tt-) to take on colour as fruits or leaves, deepen in colour, be distinguished, brilliant, be bright and fresh in| appearance; niram colour, complexion, dye, tincture, quality, property, temper, nature, light, lustre, fame, reputation. Ma, niram colour, light, splendour; nirakka to shine. Ko. nerm (obl nert-) colour. DED 3047. 3681 7a. niram bosom, breast, middle place, vital spot, body, skin. To. nerm (odl. nert-) vital spot. Ka. nera vital point, member or organ, a secret; neru vital organ, ctc. Tw. neravu the privitics of the male sex: niravu anus. Je, neranu a secret. ? Go. naram penis of animals. DED 3048.

Ta. nirai (-v-, -nt-) to become full, be replete, abound, be copious, pervade, be satisfied, contented; (-pp-, -tt-) to fill, make full, diffuse, cause to pervade, stuff, cram; n, completion, fullness, copiousness, excel- lence, splendour; niraiya abundantly, plenti- fully; niraivu fullness, completeness, per- fection, abundance, filling, satisfaction, excellence; niraivéru to be fulfilled, accom- plished; niraivérru to fulfil, complete, per- form, effect; niraivérram fulfilment, accom- plishment, performance. Ma. nira_ fullness; ceremonies for bringing wealth and blessing (beginning by taking home a handful of cars of corn as the first fruits); illan-nira the annual cleansing of the house-door with offerings of rice and sticking over it new ears of corn (and 6 or 10 or 16 different kinds of leaves) with cowdung; nirayuka to become full, be full; nirekka to fill; niravu fullness; nirappu filling. Ko. nerv- (nerd-) (girl) reaches puberty, (affair) comes to fruition; nert- (nerty-) to bring to fruition; nervest- (nerve-ty-) to make ready, finish. To. ner- (ner6-) to become full, (girl) reaches puberty, (beard) begins to grow, (moon) becomes full; nerf- (nert-) to fill (t7.); nerf full moon; ner no-f day of full moon; nerily full moon (in song). Ka, nere (nered-, nerad-) to become entire, full, complete, accom- plished, ready, perfect, mature, arrive at the age of menstruating, be realized, occur, suffice; 2. completeness, maturity, etc.; adv. completely, perfectly; nerate fulln

Ta. ninai (-v-, -nt-) to think, consider, reflect, ponder, remember, meditate, know, understand, intend, design, have in view, imagine, fancy, suppose; (-pp-, -tt-) id., resolve, determine; ninaital reflection; ninai- ppu thought; ninaivu thought, idea, reflec- tion, consideration,’ recollection, remem- brance, imagination, conception, notion, object, design, purpose, meditation, care. Ma. ninayuka to think; ninekka to think, remember, consider, wish; ninavu thought, a memorandum, notice, imagination; nineppa- var the thoughtful. Ko. neny-/nenc- (nenc-) to think. To. nenf- (nent-) to think of; nen- (nen8-) (Kurumba) thinks of in order to kill by witchcraft; nenp a thought; nep a sign to recognize something by. Ka, nenasu to think, imagine, think of, remember; nenapu, nenavu, nenavi, nenaha, nenahu thought, reflection, recollection, remembrance; nene to think, think on, bear in mind, be mindful of, wish, remember, recollect; neneyisu to think, etc.; neppu_ recollection, acquaintance; (Hav.) nempu memory. Kod. nena- (nenap-, nenat-) to think, reflect upon; (Shanmugam) nenap thought. Tu, nenepuni to think, imagine, mind, reflect, recollect, remember; ninepu, nenepu, nepa thought, intention, remem- brance, recollection, memory; (B-K.) nempu memory; nenevarige remembrance, recollec- tion, recognition. Te. neppu skill, cleverness; nelavu acquaintance, friendship, familiarity; nelavari an acquaintance, a person well known, one who knows a secret, a well- informed, experienced or cle

Ta. ni (obi. Old Ta. nin(n)-, mod. Ta. un(n)-) thou, you (2 sg.);/ton. nir; ninava yours (nt. pl.). Ma. ni (obi. nin(n)-) you (sg.). Ko. ni+ (obl. nin-/ni-/di-). To. ni+ (obl. nin-). Ka, nim, nin(u) (@dL nin(n)-). Kod. ni-ni/ni- (obi, nin-/ni--), Tu. i (obl nin-). Te. nivu, ivu (obi. ni-, nin-). Kol. niev (obl. in-). Nk. niv. Nk. (Ch.} iv, niv (obl. in-). Pa. in (obl. in-). Ga. (Oli.) in (obi, in-), (S.) in (OAL inn-). Go, (Tr. W. Ph.) imma, (Ch.) ima, (SR.) nime, (G. Mu.) nim(m)a, (Ma. M.) nima (0d/. ni-) (Voce, 177). Konda. nin (obl ni-). Pe. &n, énen (acc.-dat. ningey, gen. ni). Mand. in. Kui inu, (K.) ninu (obL ni-). Kuwi (F.) ning, (S. Su. P. Isr.) ninu (ob1 ni-. Kur. nin (041. ning-). Malt. nin (obl ning-). Br. ni (bl. ne@, n-; enclitic gen., dat., acc. -ne). Cf. 3688 Ta. nim. DED(S) 3051.

Ta. ni (-pp-, -tt-) to separate from, renounce as the world, put away, reject, put to disgrace, despise, loathe, abandon, leave, be removed; nifiku (nifki-) to leave, go, depart, separate from, give up, abandon, pass over, turn away, be warded off, be liberated, released, be dismissed, discharged, be ex- cepted, excluded, go, proceed, be spread out (as fingers); nifkal removing, separating; nikku (nikki-) to remove, exclude, put aside, dismiss, extricate, liberate, exempt, deduct, turn, draw aside as a curtain, kill, dispatch, destroy, open, force apart, spread out (as the fingers), give up, abandon, change; 1. separ- ation, removal, opening, cleft, crack, deduc- tion, remainder, balance; nikkam, nikku separation, removal, disengagement, liberation, gap, chink, crack; nikkal destroying, oppo- sition, disagreement, opening, hole; nikki leakage; nippu relinquishment, renunciation, separation, parting; nimpal interstice; nintu (ninti-) to relinquish, give up; nivu (nivi-) to cease, discontinue, pass beyond, transgress, break asunder as a chain, destroy; spread. Ma. nifihuka to go aside, go off, go farther, retire, vanish; nifitikka to make to retire; nikkam removal, departure, retirement, variation, exception; nikkuka to put away, aside, remove, abolish; nikki except; nikkikka to cause to remove, withdraw a complaint. Ko. ni-g- (ni-gy-) to cover a large expanse, be broad; ni-gc- (ni+gc-) to make to cover a large expanse, (feline) walks making its body long. To. ni-k- (ni+ky-) to
Tu. nicuni to go through, pierce. ? Te. Tgu to enter, penetrate. ? Kol. (Pat., pp. 155, 175) ikeng to pierce, thrust in (knife). DED 3053.

Ta. nintu (ninti-) to swim in water, overflow, swim across, cross over, escape from, get over, overcome; #. Ocean; nittam flood, depth, sea, abundance; nittu swimming, water of swimming depth, flood; niccu swimming, flood, swimming depth as of water; niccan, niccal swimmer; nificu = nintu vb.; to be actively engaged as in a stupendous work, drink to excess, esp. toddy. Ma. nintuka to swim, sprawl on the ground or in the water; nintam, nintal swimming; nintikka to make to swim. Ko, ni-nj- (nienj-), ni-cid- (ni-cit-) to swim. To. niez,- (ni-j-), ni-d- (niedy-), ni-s- (ni-sy-) id. Ka. isu, iju, hiju id; n. (also isa) swimming; ijisu to make swim (as a horse). Kod. mi-nd- (mi-ndi-) to swim. Tu. icuni, ijuni id.; ninduni id., float; nindavuni to set afloat, cause to swim,’ nindata swimming, floating. Kor. (O.) mindi to swim. Te. Tdu id.; ¥duladu id., hang; Tta swimming; Ttakddu swimmer. Kol, (SR.) ind- to swim. Go. (S.) itar- id.; (Ko.) inta swim- ming (Voce. 209); (Koya Su.) ind- to swim. Konda iyba- (-t-) to bathe (intens.). Pe. iba- id.; caus. itpa-. DED(S, N) 3054.

Ta. nim, nir, niyit, nivir, nikal (obl. Old Ta. num-, mod. Ta. um-, ustkal-) you (pl); nir you (sg. hon.); numag your relation, party or associate. Ma. nifnal (obl. ninnal-) you (pl). Ko. niem (Obl. nim-). To. nim (obi, nim-). Ka. nim, nivu, ningal (bl. nim(m)-). Kod. nigga (ob! ninga-). Tu. iry, nikulu (also obL). Te. iru, miru (obl. mi-, mim-). Kol, nier (obi. im-), Nk. nit. Nk. (Ch.) im (obl. im-). Pa. im (obl. im-). Ga. (Oll) im (obl, im-), (S.) im (0dl. imm-). Go. (Tr.) immat, (W. Ph.) immat, (Y.) imetf, (SR. S.) nimef, (Mu.) nimat, (L.) nimat, (Ma.) mit (obl. mi-) (Voe. 178). Konda mir(u), (BB) nim (obl, mi-). Pe. @p, pen (ace,-dat. miggen, gen, mi). Mand. im. Kui iru, (K.) miru (obi. mi-). Kuwi (F.) mimbt, (S. P. Isr.) mimbu, (Su.) miru (obL mi-). Kur. nim (ob), nim-). Malt. nim (obl. nim-). Br. num (obi, num-). Cf. 3684 Ta. ni. DED(S) 3055. 3689 Kui nimba (nimbi-) to live, survive, revive; #. life, survival; nippa (nipt-) to cause to live, give life to; nize living, alive. Kuwi (F.) nidali, (S.) nidinai, (Su. Isr.) nid- Cit-) to live, be alive; (Isr.) nif- (-h-) to make alive; (Mah.) nireyyu alive. ? Cf. 3675 Ta. nil. DED(S) 3056. 3690(a) Ta. nir water, sea, juice, liquor, urine, dampness, moisture; nature, disposi- tion, state, condition; (-pp-, -tt-) to become thin or watery (as liquid food in cooking), be wet, moist; nirmai property of water, as coolness; ir moisture, wetness, freshness, greenness; fram wet, moisture, humidity, freshness, greenness, coolness; irali

Ta. nivu (nivi-) to stroke, rub gently, handle softly, smooth by passing the fingers over, wipe off, daub, smear; nivi wiping. Ko. niv- (nivd-) to stroke, mas- sage, comb (hair). To. ni-f- (ni-fy-) to stroke. Ka. nivu to rub softly, use gentle friction, stroke down any member of the body, make straight or smooth by stroking, as the hair, beard, cloth, etc.; nivisu to cause to use gentle friction; nivaru to pass the hand gently over, stroke or rub gently; caus. nivarisu; névarisu to make straight, smooth, or proper by gently rubbing with the hand or fingering, rub gently with the hand or stroke. Tu. névaripuni to fondle, caress. Te. nivuru, nimuru to stroke, pass the hand over gently and caressingly. Kol. (Pat., p. 139) nigeng to massage. Kui néra (n@ri-) to rub, stroke, polish, massage; 7. act of rubbing. ? Kur. nina to rub down, powder, scrape into bits (as medicinal tock, kitchen nuts, etc.); nirigna to besmear, soil with dirt, rub; (Hahn) nindna to mark with chalk, DED(S) 3058.

Ta. nil (nilv-, ninf-) to be long, be great; n. length, extension, clongation, long time, duration, height, tallness, loftiness, depth, order, series, row; nila to a great length or distance, all along, at a great distance; nilam extension, length, distance, remoteness, delay, procrastination; nili (-pp-, -tt-) to be lengthened, extended, be long as time or life, last long, endure, be protracted, delayed; n. that which is long, lofty, tall person; nilippu prolongation; niliyatu that which is long, that which is omnipresent; niru {329] nusi ninu (nini-) to go a long distance; nitu (nifi-) to grow long, be lengthened, be ex- tended through space or time, spread, extend, abound, be copious, thrive, grow well, rise high, last long, endure, be permanent, delay; n. long time, permanence; nifti (-pp-, -tt-) to lengthen (time, distance, etc.), endure, last, be permanent; niftu (niffi-) to lengthen, extend, stretch out, straighten, offer as oblations, give, insert, drive into, speak at length or too much, prolong as a note, delay, procrastinate, retard, defer; niftu tcngth as of time or space, distance; nifttam length, elongation of sound, strengthening, lengthen- ing, procrastination; niftal lengthening, ex- tending, stretching; nifti (-pp-, -tt-) to lengthen, delay; be prolonged, endure long; nikalam length. Ma. niluka to extend oneself, grow long, be delayed, protracted; nila, nile, nilavé far; nilam length, distance; nilal growing longer; nil, nix length (in epds.); nitu length, l

Ta. nukam yoke, burden, power, strength, protecting bar of a door. Ma, nukam yoke, harness which joins the necks of two oxen. Ko. nuen a yoke (? < *nukan). Ka. noga, naga yoke. Kod. noga id. Tu. nuga, noga id. Te. noga pole or poles which connect the carriage with the yoke. /? < Skt. yuga-. DED(S) 3061.

Ta. nukar (-v-, -nt-) to enjoy, experi- ence as the fruit of actions, eat, drink, do, perform; nukareci, nukarvu enjoyment, ex- perience of pleasure or pain, as from former deeds; eating, feeding; sensation. Ma. nuka- ruka to swallow; nukarcca sipping, imbibing. Te. ndru mouth. Cf. 3697 Ta. nufiku. DED 3062. 3696 Kur. nukna to shake, cause to oscil- late, esp. up and down; refl.-pass. nukurng; nukra shaky, tottering; nukta’and to cause another to shake something. Malt. nuke to shake; nukre to swing, rock, be shaken. DED 3063.

Ta. nufku (nunki-) to swallow, devour, drink in large draughts, take posses- sion of, capture; nohku (nonki-) to swallow. Ko. nugg- (nuggy-) to gulp down without chewing. To. nug- (nugy-) to gulp down. Ka, nungu to swallow, devour; nufguvike, nunguha swallowing. Tu. nifguni, ninguduni, difguni, ditguduni, (B-K. also) nufgy to swallow. Kor. (M.) nunudu, (O.) nunu id. Kur. nunvxnd to swallow without chewing. gulp hastily, devour; pass, nunUxrna. Malt. nunge to swallow. Br. nughushing to swallow, devour, gulp down. Cf. 3695 Ta. nukar;

Ta. nufiku, (Tinn.) nonku pulpy kernel of a tender palmyra fruit, tender palmyra fruit; nukumpu unexpanded tender leaf of palmyra, plantain, etc., palmyra leaf. Mea. nofhu unripe pulp of a palmyra fruit: a coconut the kernel of which swells out into a sweet spongy substance through lying for a long time. Te. (B.) nudgu palm-fruit kernel while unripe. DED 3065.

Ka. nusi a minute insect that destroys wool, any cloth, and paper; one that destroys grain; eye-fly, gnat. Te. nusuma eye-fly, gnat. midge. Go, (D. Ma.) nusme mosquito (Voe. nun 2016); ntisi (Tr.) flour-weevil, (W. Ph.) weevil; (SR.) nusi crop rust (Voc. 2021). ? Ta. ucu woodworm. Cf. 3715 Ta. nulampu and 3779 Ta. no. From DED(S) 3077.

Te. nun minute, fine, etc.; nunpu, nunmai, nunuku, nunpimai minuteness, small- ness, slenderness, sharpness, refinement, nicety, exquisiteness (as in workmanship), acuteness, subtlety, discrimination, precision, accuracy, delicacies, dainties, mystery; nun- niyan person of acute intellect, quick parts or subtle discrimination, minister; nunakkam sharpness, minuteness, subtlety, fineness; nunankiyodr persons of subtle or sharp intel lect; nunafiku (nunafki-) to be thin, minute, attenuated, be subtle, fine, refined; 7. minute- ness, subtlety, fineness; nuni (-v-, -nt-) to waste away, be thinned down; (-pp-, -tt-) to sharpen toa point, powder, examine carefully; n, a point; nunukkam fineness, minuteness, sharpness, acuteness, acumen, subtlety, ex- quisiteness (as of work), niggardliness; nunukku (nunukki-) to make very small (as beads), powder, pulverize, pound, grind, shatter to pieces, write a small hand, be niggardly, stint, sharpen to a point, sharpen the wits, execute minutely or finely (as a work); m. smaliness, fineness, subtlety, any small or minute thing, small handwriting; (Ramnad dial., Annamalai, p. 875) unukku to powder: (= nunukku); nunuku (nunuki-) to be slender, delicate (as a woman’s waist), be minute, become thin, be sharp, keen, acute (as one’s intellect); nunuhku (nun- unki-) id., be powdered, pulverized, sing softly, as a tune; 7. powder: nunavai flour of rice and other grains, a ball of sesame confection; nuvanai minuteness, fineness, flour; nufpam minutene
Ta. nunafiku yellow spreading spots on skin; unni wart. Ma, nunil scurf, itch; unal eruption (on skin); unil id., vesicle as of itch; unni pimple on the face of infants. DEDS 568.

Ta. nundvu (nupavi-), nundcu (nup- aci-), nunai (-pp-, -tt-) to rub and feel with the fingers, feel with the tip of the tongue as the gums or palate. Ka. none to clean with the tongue the teeth of substances lodged between them. DED 3067.
Ta. nunai backbiting. Ma. nuna calumny, flattery, lie; nupayan_ talebearer, spy, calumniator; nunekka to backbite; nunattam backbiting. DED 3068.
Ta. nutampu boiled rice. Tu. nuppu id. (or with 600 Ta. un). DEDS 569.

Ta. nutal forehead, eyebrow, head, skull, top, upper part. Ma, nutal forehead, Ka, nosal(u) id. Tu. nesaly, nasudi id. Te. nuduru, nosalu id. Ga. (S,*) nudud id. Konda nud id. /Cf. Skt. nifata-, etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10970(2). DED(S) 3069.
Te. nuyi, nuyyi, nflyi a well. Kol. nuvvi id. Vk. nuvi id. Nk. (CA.) uy, uvvi id. Go. (Ko.) nuy id. (Voc. 2012). Konda (BB) nuy id. Kuwi (S.) nfyi id. DED(S) 3070.
Ta. nurampu mud, mire. Tu, nurum- bu alluvium. DED 3071.

Ma. nuri a bunch of rice plants and the space required to plant them, what three fingers can hold; nurikka to put some grains, take and put with three fingers; nurippikka to transplant. Kod. nuli cluster of paddy plants growing in one hole; ? ni-ri bundle of several paddy seedlings. ? Ta, nuru tender shoots in paddy stubble; noru after- shoot from the roots of rice, millet, etc. DED(S) 3072, 3709 Ma, nurumpu rot, wood-dust, iron rust, etc.; nurumpuka to rot, decay. Tu. nurumbu wood-dust, iron rust. DED 3073.

Ta. nurai froth, foam, scum, spume, lather, bubble; (-pp-, -tt-) to froth, foam, effervesce. Ma. nura foam, froth; nurayuka to froth; nurekka to foam, emit scum, fer- ment as toddy; nuri small bubbles of water; nurikka to rise in small bubbles. Ko. nor foam. Ka. nore foam, froth, scum. Kod. nere froth. Tu. nuré foam, froth, scum. Te. nurtigu, nuruvu foam, froth; nuccillu (pl) foam, DED 3074. 3711 Kur. nurugn® to push back into the fire unburnt ends of logs protruding; (Hahn) nurgna to shove in, insert, put fuel or half- burnt sticks into the fire. Malt. nurge to drag or draw (as a net); nurgre to move onward or slide. ? Ka. (K.7) niiru to force one’s way through. DEDS 570.
Ta. nuvanai black Italian millet. Ka. navane, navani a small grain, the Italian La peter cere eT ‘emer ett een _ nulayuka {331} millet or panic seed, Panicum italicum. DED 3075.
Ma. nulayuka to rot, moulder. Tu. nubbu mouldy, musty. Go. (Tr.) nilk mould on leather (Voc. 1998), DEDS 571.

Ta. nuyai (-v-, -nt-) to creep through a Narrow passage, penetrate, be impressed into one’s mind, get into, take up as an office, be interpolated; (-pp-, -tt-) to put, insert, inter- polate; narrow way, window, Opening, aperture, cave; nuguntu (nuyunti-), nugutu (nuguti-) to insert, stick in, tuck in, tie (as a coil of hair), keep ina place not easily found, carry away by stealth; skulk, slink or sneak away, slip out of sight as one among many, crawl, creep as reptiles; nurunti one who shirks responsibility; nuguvu (nuguvi-) to creep along (as reptiles); nugaval anything slippery; ngai postern, chink, hole, bore, a kind of lattice window, cave; noruntu (nogunti-) to insert, thrust. Ma. nugayuka to creep in, squeeze through; nura-vatil a small door; nifguka, niluka to creep in, Squeeze through, enter slily or with diffi culty; ? nga gap in a fence; ? nokkuka to pass through, pierce, bore through. Ka. Roxe to creep in, etc.; nurgu, nuggu to pass by pressing, squeeze through, force oneself into, enter without permission (as a door, a house), creep into (as a hole); nuggisu to cause to creep or squeeze through; nugicu to get out of (the hand, etc.) by wriggling (as a bird), slip (from the hand, as a rope); nugul to enter a door or a small narrow Passage or opening, go in with difficulty, squeeze through, creep in, enter; nup(u)cu, nunacu to slip, slide as out of the hand, slip away, glide or move off, escape; nusi to enter a door, etc.; nusul to enter a door or narrow Opening, re

Ta. nulampu, nullal, nollal gnat, eye-fly, mosquito; nilampi gnat; nullan small biting ant. Ma. nugampu a gnat, chiefly an eye-fly. Ka, nola, nolavu, nona, Nonavu a fly; noraju, norafju a (mosquito or) gnat, an eye-fly; (Hal.) nela a fly; (Gowda) nela house-fly; (Hav.) nelavu id. Kod. mu+li gnat, midge. Kor, (T.) nurigi mosquito. Kol. (Br.) nulle fly. Pa. nurfii, (S.) ugiii mosquito. Go. (Tr.) nullé a flea, esp. one which damages kodon and kutki flowers; nulle (Mu. Ma.) a small insect which bites in the tainy season, (W.) gnat; (L.) nulé, (M.) nile mosquito (Voc. 2015). Malt. nuto a kind of very small gnat. Cf. 700 Ta. ulu and 3699 Ka. nusi. DED(S, N) 3077.

Ka. nulgu to reduce to small frag- ments; be powdered. Tu. nuliyuni to be broken, shattered; nuli a bit, fragment. Te. LOR nuliyu to be broken, shattered. DED 3717 Ta, nullu (nu li-) to pinch, nip; 1. a pinch, nip. Ma reas to pinch, “luck: nulla a pinch, bit; nullal a pinch, harvest of grains that are picked. Ko. nuly- (nulc-) to pinch. Ka, (Gowda; Dr. Ling., p. 104) nulfu to pinch. Te. (K.) nul(ujcu to pinch, nip off, squeeze; nulumu to pinch. DED(N) 3079.
Ka. nuruku to wane away, become devoid of growth or increase. Tu. nurguni to decay. DEDS 572.
Ta. nuni point, tip, minuteness, fineness, smallness; (-pp-, -tt-) to sharpen to a point, whet, examine carefully, look intently; nunai point, tip, end. Ma. nuni very thin and meagre. DED 3080.

Ta. nt, nvu sesamum; ndlai a sesame ball, a preparation of sesame seed. Te. nifvu, nuvvu gingily seed; nfne, niniya oil; nii-bindi flour of gingili seeds; (VPK) nOgulu = nuyvulu. Kol. (SR.) nuvv sesamum, til; (W.) nu-ne oil. Nk. nuvv (pl. nuvvul) sesamum; ntine oil. Nk. (Ch.) W sesamum, Pa, nuvul (pl) sesamum; n@ ney sesamum oil. Ga. (8.3) nuvul (pl) Sesamum indicum. Go. (most dialects) nag(g), (Tr. A.) mung sesamum (Voe. 2018); (ASu.) nuf, (Koya Su.) noiiku (pl) id. Konda (BB 1972) ni id. DED(S) 3081.
Ta. nitkkam, nikku sissoo wood, Dalbergia sissoo. Ka. (DCV) nitke blackwood. Te, nitka a kind of tree; (OCV) ntka-manu blackwood, DED(S) 3082. nikku (332] niu

Ta. nikku (nikki-) to shove, push, thrust aside, impel, incite, discharge (weapon). Ka, nttku, ntigu, nitiku to shove, push, thrust aside, impel, urge on. Kod. nu-k- (nu-ki-) to push. Tu, ntkuni to thrust, push, reject; nurkuni to press on, elbow, push, make way. Te. ntiku to shove, push violently, thrust out; n. a thrust; ntikudu shoving, a shove. Kol. nu-k- (nu-ki-) to push so as to move. DED (N) 3083. 3723 Kur. niixna, nifkhna to bow (the head), keep down (the eyes); silence, defeat, put down. Malt, nige to droop, walk or behave without energy. DED 3084. 3724 Kur. nitna to plunge the hand into, touch, defile. Malt. nite to touch, meddle. DED 3085. . 3725 Kur, nitUmad to stretch out one’s legs in sitting or lying down; nltur okkna to stretch out one’s legs while sitting. Malt. nitre to stretch out the legs when lying down. DED 3086.

Ta. nul yarn, cotton thread, string, systematic treatise, science; (nlirp-, nUrr-) to spin, compose (as a poem), make a plot; nular, nldr learned persons; (Tinn.) nUpu spinning. Ma, niil thread, yarn, measuring line; nilkka to spin. Ko, nu-l thread; ? nurb- (nurby-) to twist, wring (neck). To. nuss thread; nu«sf- (nu-st-) to join ends of thread by rolling. Ka. nil yarn, thread (of cotton); (nilt-) to make thread, spin; ndlige spinning; nilisu to cause to spin; nuli to twist (as a rope. etc.), wring, curl (whiskers), roll (as cotton) between the hands, turn round (as the back), twist off (as a twig); (the intestines) to gripe; n. (also nulike, nulige) state of being twisted, a twist, cord, thread, twine; griping pain in the intestines; (Hal.) nugulu thread. Kod. nueli thread. Tu. nilu thread, yarn, nilodu spindle; ntipuni to spin, twist, string; nuppuni to twist. Kor. (M.) nuglu thread. Te. natu cotton thread; nulaka a rough kind of rope or string; nuli entanglement in a thread; nuliyu to be twisted; nulincu, nul(u)cu, nul(wpu, nulumu to twist; nulivu a twist. Kol, nuv, (Kin.) nil thread. Pa, nth id. Ga. (Oll.) ni id. Go, (many dialects) nul thread, string (Voc. 2020). Konda ntlu thread; (BB) nuls- to twist. Pe. nuf thread; nodn- (ndf-) to spin, twine. Mand, nul thread. Kui ntidu (pl niitka) cotton yarn, thread; nolba (ndt-) to twist strands together, spin thread; #. spinning. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) latu, (F.) tale, (S) 1olu thread. ? Kur. ndEna to wind or twist anything flexible, tw

Ta. nitgu (nfri-) to crush, pulverize, reduce to powder, demolish, destroy; 7. powder, dust, flour; nuvaru (nuvari-) to file; nurukku = (nurukki-) to crush, pulverize, reduce to powder; nuyufku (nurufki-) to be powdered, crushed, squeezed; . grit; norukku (norukki-) to break, crush, smash to pieces; n. crushing; norunku (norunki-) to be broken, crushed, smashed; . broken grains, grits, powder; norunoru (-pp-, -tt-) to be brittle, fragile; nukku (nukki-) to break in pieces, grind, powder, crush. Ma. nlru powder, esp. powdered lime, starch; ntiyuka to be pulverized; nurufiuka to be broken into small pieces, shattered, pulverized; nugufnu bit, chip, atom; nurukkuka to crush, break in pieces, pound; nuyukku bit, fragment, broken rice; nuccu broken rice. Ko. nuc grains broken in pestling; nug- (nugy-) to be hard-pressed (to support large family); nuge- (nuge-) to pound (chillies) with pestle. To. ntiic broken rice, chips; nuer lime. Ka. niu state of being crushed; nifku, nitgu fragments, grit; nusi to be reduced to small bits or powder, be crushed; n. state of being crushed; nuyuku, nurugu, nurgu, nuggu to crush;. be crushed; nurgu, nuggu, nurcu state of being crushed; nurcu, nuccu fragments, bits, grits; nurcu, nuyisu to crush to fragments. Kod. nuri very small bits of broken rice; nucci broken grains of rice. Tu. nuriyuni to break, go to pieces, decay; nuripuni, nurpuni to break, powder, granulate, pulverize; nuritely bruising, chips; nurkally kind of pebbles found in rice; nukk

Ta. nUru (ob. and in cpds. nUgru-) 100; niirruvar 100 persons. Ma. nOru (nOrru-) 100; nirr-intu 100 years; ntyruvar, nUrravar rte mentee tent niva [333] nefu 100 persons. Ko. nuer 100. To. nuer id. Ka. nUru id.; nilrar, ntrvar, (Shanmugam) nurvar 100 persons. Kod. nusri (obi nu-if-) 100. Tu, niidu (obi. nuta-) id. Te. niu (obi. ntfa-) id.; n@ruguru, niruvuru 100 persons. Go. (Tr.) nlr (pl nuhk), (L. SR.) nir, (Pat. Hislop) nur 100 (Voc. 2019); (ASu.) niir (pl. nuhk) id. DED(S) 3090.

Ta. niva (-pp-, -nt-) to rise, be elev- ated, grow, spread, overflow; nivar (-v-, -nt-) to rise high; nivappu elevation, height; ivar (-v-, -nt-) to rise on high, ascend, spring, leap, rush out, climb over, mount; ika (-pp-, -nt-) to leap over, cross over, transgress, overflow; iya (-pp-, -nt-) to pass beyond, excel, tran- scend; iyakkam greatness, excellence. Ma. nikakka to rise; ivaram height; ivaruka to rise up; ivarttuka to lift, raise; ekaruka to ascend, rise; ekarttuka, ekattuka to lift, raise; ekaram height, uphill; ikaykkuka to transgress. Ka. nege, nese to rise, ascend, go upward, jump; Negapu, negavu to lift up, hold uplifted; Negasu to cause to jump or jump over; egaru to rise, fly, jump; egarisu to cause to rise, cause to fly, cause to jump, shoplift; egarike jumping; egu rising, embarkation; ega diga up and down; (Hav.) neggu to lift. Kod. nekkura cliff. Tu. negiyuni to rise, come up; negipuni to leap, jump, spring up; negapuni to overflow; negatty overflowing. Kor. (M.) negi to lift. Te. negayu, egayu, nevayu to fly, go up, rise up, jump; negavu, egavu flying, flight; eguru, (K. also) evuru to fly, jump up; n, flying, flight, jump; eguva the top; eguvu flying, flight of bird; egupu flight, increase; egu, egudu high, raised; egayincu to toss up; egacu to cause to fly; egirincu to toss up, cause to fly; ega upwards; ega diga up and down; (K.) eydu to fly; egumati embarkation, putting goods on board of a ship, exportation; egudala the upper or higher part or region
Ka. negadi, nigadi, nagadi a cold, catarrh. Tu. negadi, negadé id. DED 3092.

Ka. negar, mnegage alligator. Tu. negaly id.; megary a sea-animal, the vehicle of Varuna. Te. (B.) negadu a polypus or marine animal supposed to entangle swim- mers. / Cf. Skt. nakra- crocodile; nikra- a kind of aquatic animal; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7038. DED 3093. 3733 Ta, neku (nekuv-, nekk-) to suffer, be distressed; evvu (evvi-) to cause pain; evvai care, anxiety; evvam affliction, distress, pestilence, dislike; @val poverty, want. Ko. neg- (negy-) to suffer from reverse of fortune; caus, nege- (nege-). Ka. (Hav.) negaru to suffer in sickbed. Tu. neggi, negi shyness, shame; (B-K.) nigary, negary to linger as a sick person. Te. nevva poverty, calamity, mis- fortune, distress, peril; (K.) nevulu to grieve, be distressed; n. (also negulu) regret, disquiet. DED (S) 3094.
Ka. nekkare, dodda nekkare Indian rhododendron, Melastoma malabathricum. Tu. nekkaré, nekkaru id. DEDS 573.

Tu. néga beauty, nicety; beautiful, nice, fair. Ga. (OUL.) niya, (S.) niyat good. Go, (M.) nehnd good, handsome; (L.) nihna, (Mu.) nehna, (Ma.) ne?na good; (W.) nahna well (of health); (Ph.} nahnal excellent, good (Voc. 2041). Konda negi good; negikan a good man; negend adv. good, well; negrenda adv. well, fine (of living, flourishing). Pe. nekri good; nekrakan a good man. Mand. nekran (masc.), nekdel (fem.), nekde (neut.) good. Kui negi good, well, fine, noble, splendid; (K.) nehi good. Kuwi (Su. S.) nehi, (Isr.) nehi, nehi, (F.) nihi id. Malt. neke to get well. / There has been convergence with IA (Turner, CDIAL, no, 7150, e.g. Mar. nik& good, pure), whence by borrowing forms like Pa. niko, Go. (W.) nikkd good. Pa. negget < Halbi nangat. DED(S, N) 3096.

Ta. neficu, neficam mind, conscience, heart, breast, bosom, chest, centre or heart of a thing, bravery, courage; neficul mind; neficulu (-pp-, -tt-) to be broken-hearted, dispirited. Ma. neficu, nefifiu heart, breast; neficar bold men. Ko. nanj heart. To. niz, id., dewlap. Kod. nefifii, (Mercara dialect) fiefifii chest of body. Te. nenjili uneasiness, distress, trouble of mind. Pa. dififii pith. Go. (LuS.) nénjomee mango stone. Konda (BB) ninjam chest. Pe. nenjom, nenja daki id; nenja pith (of a coconut). Mand, nenja- daki chest. Kui ninja heart of a tree, pith; solid, sound, uncracked, strong. Kuwi linja (F.) kernel, yolk of cgg, (Su.) pulp of fruit, (Isr.) yolk of egg. ? Kur. nisand core or hard wood of a tree. DED(S, N) 3097.

Ta. neti pungent odour causing a choking sensation, as of fried chillies; strong pungent smell, stench. Ka. nidi a strong, pungent, stifling smell, as that of tobacco or tobacco smoke, of cayenne pepper or its vapour when being roasted. DED 3098.

Ta. nefu long; (-pp-, -tt-) to be long (as time), continue (as rain), become tall; nefumai Jength, extension, tallness, height as of a person or tree, continuation, protrac- tion, greatness, boundlessness, excessiveness, depth; neti (-pp-, -tt-) to make delay; nefitu after a long delay; netippu long time; nefiyon tall person, great person, Visnu; nefil length, long vowel, that which is great or excessive; nefukku length, as of a wall; nefuka length- wise, longitudinally, straight on, continuously; netuku (nefuki-) to extend, be lengthened, grow tall, high or long, be protracted, de- layed; netuppam length, height; nefuman anything jong; netumi tall woman, tall tree netfam [334] ney (as palm-tree); net{a-netumai great length, excessive tallness or height; neftam tallness, length; neftaiku lengthiness; neffayam stretchers, piling up bricks perpendicularly or upright; netfu length, tailness, long dis- tance, extent, as of the breadth of a pial; netfai tallness. Ma, nefu long; nefukkam tallness; nefufkan tall; netuppu, netuppam length, height; nefumpu pride; netumpan arrogant; netuman tall man; nefuma, nefu- manam tallness; neffan tall man; nettayam a straight part in a river; a stretcher (brick or stone); nitu long, tall, straight; nitiydn tall person; nituppam tallness; niftal, niffah-kal shin. Ko. nernern flat on back (of lying or falling). To. n6r- (néry-), n6rx- (ndrxy-) to be or become tall; nérk- (nérky-) to lengthen (tr.), let (hair) grow. Ka. nidu, niftu state of being d

Ta. nettam perpendicularity; nattam erectness. Ma, nit{-ena in a standing position, perpendicularly. Ko. nefe-n, nete+! directly (of motion or of communication). Ka. neffage, netf3na, neffine, netfanna straightly, straight, erect, regular, proper, orderly, tightly, properly, nicely, neatly, plainly, clearly, distinctly; netfi, natti niceness, beauty, charm; nita, nigu straightness, state of standing upright; properness, neatness, elegance, beauty, tidiness; nijugara a tidy man; fem, nifugati; nétu straightness, direct- ness, truth. Ji. neffage straight, upright; straightly, properly; nidpa, nidpa, nitta straight, upright, erect; straightly, erectly; nifa upright, straight, direct; nifanifi straightly. Te. nitta erect; nifra, niftramu, neframu erectness, steepness; erect, vertical, steep, precipitous; nifrincu to keep or place erect; nifu elegance, beauty, neatness, prettiness, foppishness; nitukadu a beau; nifukattiya, nifukatte a belle; (K.) nettana ady. straight, in an orderly way, clearly. Pa. fitta straight. Go. (SR.) nig right, proper; (L.) nium, nitum indeed, truly (Voc, 2002). Konda nita koRi a strai ht pole on which the roof of a house rests. / Cf. Mar. nig straight; fit, right, proper. DED(S) 3100.
Ta. netfil, netih a bamboo. ? Tu. nedily a kind of bush, DED 3101.
Ka. (Hav.) nedi° gums (of teeth); (Gowda, Dr. Ling., p.94) niji id. Tu. nijji id. Kor. (O.) necci id. DEDS(N) 575.

Ka. netta, letta dice, gambling. Te. nettamu gambling; nettam-adu to play at dice, gamble; netta-palaka dice-board. / < Skt. netra- according to Kitt., but this mean- ing is not found in Skt., Pkt., or NIA (acc. to Turner). DEDS 576.

Ta. nempu joining dowel or nail, pins for jewels, rung or round of a ladder, ribs joining together the two beams of a well-sweep, serving also as steps for the man who treads it, ribs; nenpu rung. of a ladder, wooden pin. ? Ma. numpu a nail with two points to join planks. Te, nemmu an iron nail pointed at both ends, used in joining boards. DED 3102.

Ka. nemmi Dalbergia oujeinensis. Te. nemmi id. / Cf. Skt. (lex.) nemi- id. DEDS 577. 3745 Ta, ney (-v-, -t-) to weave as clothes, string, link together; neyvar the caste of weavers; neyvu weaving; necavu weaving, act of weaving, texture, intertexture, web; ? néyavai curtain. Ma. neyka to weave, plait mats; neyttu weaving; neypu a large mat for treading out corn in sandy districts; neyyal weaving. Ko. nec- (nec-) to weave. ? neg- ‘(negy-) to be close-woven; negc- (nege-) to make close-woven. To. nic- (nié-) to darn; nes- (nes@-), nis 0- (ni-@-) to weave. Ka. ny, néyi, neyyu, né, néyu to weave, entwine; neyi, né, néyu weaving, a web; n&vige, nByge, nége id., entwining or being entwined: neysu, néyisu to cause to weave; néyikara, ney ge- kara, nékara weaver. Kod. ne-y- (ne-yuy-, nejj-) to spin (thread); (Shanmugam) neyv braiding, weaving. Tu. neyuni to weave (as a spider); neyipini, népini, néyuni to weave, plait, braid; neyigé, n@pgé texture; neyigare weaver. Te. néyu to weave; néyincu to cause to be woven, get woven; n&ta _ weaving, texture; n®takidu, n€tari weaver; n&ta-purugu spider (see 4313), Go. (Koya Su) néce- to weave. Konda ney- (-t-) to weave or thatch the roof with leaves; cats. neyis-/neypis-. Kui nehpa (neht-) to build a fence. Kuwi (S.) neh’nai to interweave. Kur. essna (issyas) to weave, entwine into a fabric, furnish or adorn any article with net-work or plait-work. Malt. ese to plait, do mat-work. DED(S, N) 3103.

Ta. ney butter, ghee, oil, grease, fat, honey; (-pp-, -tt-) to be glossy, polished, be fat, plump, become greasy, unctuous, or sticky; neyppu unctuousness, oiliness, gloss, polish; néyam ghee, oil. Ma. ney any unctuous substance, grease, fat, oil, lard, ghee. Ko. nay ghee. To. niy id. Ka. ney, ney id. Kod. ney id. Tu. n&yi ghee, grease, fat. Te. neyyl, neyi ghee, oil; nitine, nifniya oil; gingily oil (< *nfl + ney; see 3720). Kol. ney butter, ghee; nu-ne oil; (SR.) pal nine ghee. Nk. ney ghee; nine sesamum oil. Vk, (Ch,) ey oil. Pa, ney oil, fat; nf! ney sesamum oil. Ga. (Oll.) ney, (S.) neyya (pi. neygil), (S.7) ney(yu) oil. Go, (A. Tr. W. Ph.) ni, (SR.) neyi, (M.) nei, (Mu.) niy, ney, (Ma.) niy(i), (S.) niy(y)u id.; ae eae CT OLN EA COR ARETE Yo panacea MTOM enema neytal {335] neyri (Ko.) niy ghee (Voc. 2001); (Tr.) pal ni ghee; phuki ni honey (phuki bee). Konda niyu oil. Mand. ney id. Kui niju id.; ptki niju honey. Kuwi (I) niyt, (Su. P.) niyu, (isr.) niyé oil. Kur, nét@ grease, fat, lard. Malt. nenya fat of an animal. Cf. 5496(b) Ta. ven-ney. DED(S) 3104.

Ta. neytal white Indian water-lily, Nymphaea lotus alba; blue nelumbo, tuber of red Indian water-lily, maritime tract, sorrow of lovers due to separation (assigned by convention to the maritime tract). Ma neytal a nymphaea; Menyanthes indica. Ka. neydal(u), neydil(u), neydale, neydile a water- lily. Tu. naidilé id. ? Te. (VPK, p. 13) néduru- néla low ground near the Godavari. DED(S, N) 3105. 3748 Ta, neyttdr blood. Ko. netr id. To. nét3/nets id. (in song). Ka. nettar(u), nettara, nettra id. Kod, netta ca-fici sp. sandalwood which is red. Tu. nettery blood. Te. netturu, netru id. Kol, netur, (Pat., p. 15) nettur id. Nk. nettur id. Nk. (Ch.) ettur id. Pa, netir id.; netro red. Ga. (S.) netttr, (P.) netir blood; (P.) netrat red. Go. (A. Y. S. Ko.) nettur, (M.) netur, (Tr. Ch. W. Ph. Mu.) nattur blood; (Ch.) nattur ran red; (Mu.) natral blackish red; (Hislop) netral red (Voce. 2027). Konda neter blood. Pe. neter id.; red. Mand. neter blood. Kui nederi balance word with taka blood (which is Or.). Kuwi (F.) netori, (Su.) neteri blood; (isr.) neteri/netori id.; red, Br, ditar blood. For -tdr in Ta., see 2883 Ta, cdr. DED(S) 3106.

Ma. nirahhuka to drag the tail or feet along the ground, creep, crawl; nirakkuka to push, shove; nirakkam, nirahhal crawling on the: ground. Te, (K.) nerumu to move on posteriors. DED 3107. 3750 Ta, neratu (nerati-) to be rough, hard; be halting; nerafu, nerufu that which is rough (as a road), mgged (as style), knotty or abstruse (as a passage); nirafu coarseness, roughness; neri roughness. Ka, naradu rough- ness. 7. narady id., wart, protuberance; rough, craggy. Te. neradu uneven, DED(S) 3108.

Ta. nel rice, paddy, grain of paddy. Ma, nel rice (as growing), rice in the husk, paddy. Ko. nel paddy, unhusked rice; nel aky husked rice. To. neg rice (in songs); negidky rice (see adky, s.v. 215 Ta. ari). Ka, nel, nellu paddy, rice in its husk, rice as growing, a grain of paddy. Kod. nell rice, paddy. Te. nelitru 7. pr. a town. DED(S) 3754 Ta, nilavu, nila moonlight, moon. Ma. nila, nilavu id.; (Tiyya) tavu moonlight. To. nesof (obi. nesot-) id. Kod. nelaci moon; nelaci boli moonlight. Te. nela moon, month, day of full moon; ven nela moonlight. Kol. nela moon. Nk. nela id. Pa. nelifi (pl, nelitil) moon, month. Ga. (Oll.) nelig id.; (S.) nelling moon; nela& (pi nelnil, nelingil) month. Go, (Tr.) nalénjf moon, new moon; (W. Ph.) nalenj, (SR. G. M) nelenj, (A.) lelenj, (Ma. Ko.) lénj moon; (Mu.) nalenj (i nalesk), lalenj, lelenj id., month; (M,) lenj month (Voce. 2036). Konda nela moon; nelen (pi.) months; (BB) lénz(u) moon, month, Pe. lénj (obi. léc-, pl. lécku) id. Mand. lénj id. Kut danju (pl. daska) id., season, period of time. Kuwi (F.) lenjll (pl leska), (S.) lénju, (Su. P. Isr.) fénju @ léska) moon, month. DED(S, N) 3113.

Ta. nelli emblic myrobalan, Phyl- lanthus emblica. Ma. nelli id. Ko. nel ban Fragaria nilgerrensis. To. nig ko+y sp. berry (very sour, but when water is drunk im- mediately, tastes sweet); emblic myrobalan. Ka, nelli P. emblica. Kod. nellike id. Tu. nelli id. Te. nelli id.; Premna esculenta. Pa. nella Phyllanthus emblica. Go. (A. Y. G. M.) nelli, (Tr. W. Ph.) nallf id. (Voc. 2037). Konda neli tamarind, Kui nedi id. Kuwi (F. 8.) lelli, (Isr.) leli id. All the fruits have the same taste effect (see To.; communicated by several Indian scholars). DED(S) 3114, 3145. 3756 Kol. evur spittle. Nk. evur id. Pa. nevur, nevud id. Ga. (OIL) nevur id. Go. (Ma.) evur, (Mu.) ervo, (Ko.) ervu, (S.) ervunj, ervonj id. (Voc. 364). Pe. evri id. Mand. evrig (pl.) id. DED(S) 3116.
Ta. neri (-pp-, -tt-) to press firmly with hand. Tu. nesipuni to press down, make tight. DED 3117.

Ta. .nerunal, nerunarru, nerunai, nennal yesterday; nérru id.; lately, recently. Ma. innalé yesterday. /r. nagattu, nd-ttu id. Ko. nesr (obi. ne-t-) id.; amne-r two days before yesterday. To. ine+r yesterday. Ka. ninne id., time lately passed. Kod. ninna+ndi yesterday (see 2920 Ta. fianru). Kor. (O. M. T.) eru id. Te. ninna id. Go. (Tr.) ninné (ob1. ninnéf-), (W. Ph.) ninné, (A. Y. Ma. Ko.) ninne id. (Voc 1980). Konda i’en (obi. ieR-), (Gori dial.) inen id, Mand. ineliy id. Kui riisi, risi id. Kuwi (P.) re’e, (T.) re'la, (F.) ini, (S.) re’eni, (Isr.) reeni, (Su) re’ni id. Br. dard id.; dare nan night ‘before last (cf. 3621) (Zvelebil, [JJ 7.328). Cf. 5020(b) for Ko. and To. DED(S, N) 3109, DEDS 827 (Su. 1973, p. 141: Kui-Kuwi *ner- > *nre- > re-).

Ta. nerri forehead, front, top, sum- mit; neri temples. Ma. nerri forehead; neruka crown of the head. Ko. nec forehead. To. nity id. Ka. netti id., head, crown of head. Kod. netti forehead. Tu. netti id., crown of head, front, (B-K.) the peak of a mountain neyru [336] nér or hill. Te. netti the head; nettamu high land or elevated ground, such as the crest or terrace of a hill. Kol. netti forehead. Vk. netti id. Ga. (S32) nedide scalp. Go. (Tr.) néc (tala) the crown of the head (Voc. 2045); ? (Tr.) nizil, (Ph.) nirit pulse in child’s head (Voc, 1984). Konda neti top of the head; neda vertex. Kur. mitil, mitil, (Hahn) nitil fontanelle. Malt. nitlu crown of the head; ? nepe forehead, brow. DED(S) 3118, and from DFD(S) 3069. 3760 Ta, nerru (nerri-) to thrust, dash, strike a mark (as with balls); netfu (neffi-) to thrust, push, strike as a stone or ball; 2. pushing, thrusting, striking, skipping as a stone or ball. Kod. nétt- (nétti-) to give a sharp shake (intr.), (sleep) is suddenly broken. Te. nettu to push, shove; n. push, shove; (K.) neppu to push out, thrust. Go, nattana (Tr.) to dash or throw down, discard, throw away, abandon (esp. a habit), (W. Ph.) to give birth to, calve; (Ch.) natt- to throw down; (W.) naccina, (Ph.) naccana, nacchanad to cast, fling (Voc. 1921). DED(S) 3119.

Tu. nésa asthma. Pa. neii(j)- to breathe; néfial, (S.) néfijkal breath; nefi(j)kud panting. Ga. (P.) n&nj- to breathe. Go. (Ma.) nénjkar(i) breath, pulse which throbs in a child’s head; (Ko.) n@- to breathe; nés breath; (A. G. Mu.) nésk- to breathe; néskana (SR.) to pant, (Ir. Ph.) to breathe heavily, pant; (M.) néskana to breathe (Voc, 2046). Konda nénz- Cit.) to sigh, respire when exhausted or tired, have hiccups while weeping; (BB) to breathe; nénzu breath. Pe. nénj- (nénc-) to breathe; intens. nécka-. Mand. nénj- id.;intens. nécka-. Kui nénja (nénji-) to breathe; n. breathing. Kuwi (F.) nenjali, (S.) nénjinai to breathe, gasp; (Isr.) nénj- (-it-) to breathe, moan with pain; (F. P.) nesteri, (Isr.) nésteri breath. Kur. naxni to breathe, rest, recover oneself; nixcarna to experience a convulsive and somewhat prolonged heaving of the breast, sigh, sob, draw in a long breath. Malt. negye to breathe; nége breath. DED(S) 3120.

Ta. nétu (n@ti-) to seek, look out for, desire, consider, aim at, earn; néf{am acquired property. Ma, n&tuka to obtain, get; nesfam acquisition, gain. ? Ka. niffisu to look at, ook at much, inquisitively or sternly, stare. Kod. né-d- (né-di-) to earn; (Shanmugam) ne fa earning. ?Br. dea borrowing of milch- animals for their milk, animal borrowed for its milk (Zvelebil, JJ 7.328). Cf. 3637 Ta. natu and 3794 Ta. ndttam. DED(N) 3121.
Ta. néntiram a kind of Malabar plantain. Ma, néntra-vara, naintra-vaga a large kind of plantain. 7u, néndra-bare the Malabar plantain. DED 3122.

Ta. némpu (némpi-) to winnow; ndmpu (ndmpi-) to separate by winnowing (as stones from rice); navu (navi-) to winnow and clear grain from stones. Ma. némpuka to winnow rice grain; navuka to cleanse tice from stones. Te. nému to winnow, sift. Go. (Tr.) @mand to sift out husks of rice, pulse, etc.; (G. Mu. Ma. S.) &m- to sift, winnow sideways. Kui mba (@mbi-) to separate tice grains from straw by shaking. Kur. nabna (Hahn) to thresh rice, winnow; (Grignard) tread as paddy, trample, throw down, beat. DED(S) 3123.

Ta. nér (-v-, -nt-) to meet, approach, come near to, obtain, agree, consent, resemble, equal, be fit, appropriate, seize, take hold of, grant, bestow, happen, occur, transpire, appear, come to view, oppose, resist, en- counter, attack, consecrate, dedicate, resolve, take a vow, entreat, pray; (-pp-, -tt-) to resemble, equal, oppose, resist, encounter, attack; m. resemblance, similarity, com- parison, agreement, consent, settlement, opposition; nércei adaptation, fitness, ap- propriateness, consent, agreement, harmony, vow, friendliness, amity, love; nértti vow; nér-paju to meet, be in conjunction with (as planets), occur, be appropriate, worthy, fall in one’s way, appear, come in front, meet, be in harmony or on good terms, learn well; nér-patu chance occutrence, means, method, consent, compliance; n@rpu_ meeting, occur- rence, happening; nérmai harmony, agree- ment; nérvu happening, consent, giving, soliciting, opposing, fighting, néra a particle of comparison; neri (-v-, -nt-) to be crowded together; (-pp-, -tt-) to approach; nerufiku (nerunki-) to: be near, approximate, be close together, crowd, be close as a relationship or connexion, approach, go near; nerukkam nearness, closeness, nerukku (nerukki-) to net [337] set thick; nirai (-v-, -nt-) to crowd, swam; make full, crowd; (-pp-, -tt-) to crowd, cluster, swarm, crowd together, form an assembly; n. collection, pack, herd. Ma, nér what is even or like, agreement; néralar enemies; nér-ituka to face, meet, oppose; néruka t

Ta. nér (-v-, -nt-) to grow thin, lean, be emaciated, be soft, yield to the touch; 7. minuteness, smallness, fineness, slenderness; nérmai fineness, thinness, minuteness. Ma. nér thin, delicate; n@ruka to be fine; nérkka to become thin, fine; n@rcca fineness; nérppu fineness, thinness, liquefaction; nérppikka to make thin, fine, attenuate; nérmma fineness, softness, delicacy; nériya fine, thin (as cloth, flour). Ju. néré thin, delicate; nérmé, nervé fine, thin, delicate. Go. (Ma.) nirpal thin (man or woman) (Voce. 1989). DED(S) 3125.

Ta. née straightness, directness, right- ness, justice, impartiality, morality, virtue, honesty, length, extension, row, series, regularity, verticality; nértti, n@rri excellence, elegance, that which is correct, equitable or just; nérmai straightness, directness, fidelity, honesty, impartiality, justice, propriety, morality, virtue. Ma. nér straightness, direc- tion, truth, justice; né@ré adv, straight; nérri straightness, what is right, common way, custom. To. né-sf- (nd-st-) to measure the length of; ?né+b direction. Ka. nér straight- ness, rightness, propriety, fitness, order; n@ra straightness, propriety, etc.; néritu, nérittu that which is straight, fit, proper or right; néridu propriety, rectitude, religious loyalty; nérida a brahman; fem. néridal; nérpu straight- ness, propriety, rectitude. Kod. né-ri truth, fact; neere direct, straight. 7u. né@ré straight- ness; justice, truth; straight, just, true; nerta straight, true; nertama straight ploughing. ? Pe. nén, népo, népond direction. Cf. 3673 Ta. nira to arrange in order. DED(S) 3126. A okkuka

Ta. nér (-v-, -nt-) to cut off, sever. Ka. nér to cut, cut off; ? nire to kill. Br. danning (neg. da-, present dé-, imper. da, dar, past daré-) to cut (of a knife), blight (as frost the crop), usurp (property), carry off (a prize), win, take away, remove (MBE 1962, pp. 60-61). DED(S) 3127.

Ta. néram time, season, opportunity. Ma. néram sun, day, light, time, season, hour, turn; né@rattu early, seasonably. Kod, ne-+ra sun, time; ne-rate early in the morning. Tu. nérdé id. Br. d& sun, sunshine, day, time; (communicated by Bh. Krishnamurti). DED (S) 3128.
Ta. né@ram guilt, fault, fine. Te. néramu fault, offence, crime, delinquency, misdemeanour, misdeed. Kuwi néromi (S.) fault, (Isr.) id., guilt (< Te.). DED(S) 3129.

Ta. nérvalam, népalam croton-oil plant, Croton tiglium, Ma. nérvalam, nirvalam id. Ka. n&paja, népata purgative nut plant. Te. népalamu C. tigltum. DED 3130. 3777 Kur, n&ma (niryas) to get dry, lose moisture; (Hahn) nimd to dry up. Malt. nére to get somewhat dry. DED(S) 3131.

Ka. névana, névala, lavita a necklace of silver or gold,a silver or gold girdle or zone; (Hal.) fidvala a girdle. Tu. névala, nevala a silver or gold girdle worn round the loins. Te. névadamu, névalamu a kind of necklace. DED\(N) 3132.

Ta. no small, tiny, minute, thin, slender; novvu (noyvi-) to become thin, slender; n. thinness; noci (-v-, -nt-) to be thin, slender, minute; nocivu slenderness, fineness; noy grits, groats, smallness, minuteness, softness, lightness; noytal minuteness; noytu that which is thin, poor, light; noyppam delicateness, tenderness, skill, ability; noym- mai, noyvu lightness, softness, minuteness; noyya small, minute, soft, weak, poor; noyy- enal expr. signifying lightness, - thinness, insignificance; nucuppu waist of a woman. Ma. noccu minute, light; nusu smail, minute, young; noy(i) grits, groats, anything minute. Ko. noca+k, onca-k a little. Ka, nusi state of being crushed, strengthless, etc., powder, dust. Tu. noccu, noccé minute, light, paltry; nuggu small, little; nuggely smaliness. Te. nusi small, little, slight; the dust into which wood is reduced by insects; nusitgu to slight, scorn; nfigu down, downiness, hairiness. Kol. (Pat., p. 115) nuyung smooth. Kui nisu soft, smooth, fleecy; nisu nisu inba to be soft, smooth, fleecy. Cf 3699 Ka. nusi. DED(S, N) 3133. :

Ma. okkuka to indent. Tu. (B-K.) okku to scratch, make furrows. Te. nokku to press, squeeze, pinch, indent; n. dent, impression, pressure, squeeze; (K.) nogulu to be pressed, squeezed, suffer under physical pressure; (K.) nogul(u)cu to crush. DED 3134. nocci [338] nol

Ta. nocci five-leaved chaste tree, Vitex negundo (ven-nocci); three-leaved chaste tree, V. frifolia (karu-nocei). Ma. nocci V. trifolia or negundo. Ka. nekki, Jakki, lakkili, lekki, lokki id. Tu. nekki V. trifolia. Te. (Lush.) noccili V. trifolia; lokki V. leucoxylon. ? Go. (A.) nirguli Vitex negundo (Voc. 2007). /Cf. Skt. nirgundi-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7308. DED(S) 3135.
Tu. nocci rice cooked with ghee, coconut, etc. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) nicci cooked rice. Te, neccu rice flour or small rice grits (or with 2927). DEN SI.

Ta. nutakku (nufakki-) to wash, wipe off moisture, destroy, dissolve. Tu. nidiyuni, nidipini, (B-K.) nedi to wash. Bel. (LSB 2.1) nodi id. Kol. od- (off-) to wash (body or part of body); o-dp- (o-dopt-) to bath (tr.). Nk. or- (otf-) to wash, bathe. Vk. (Ch.) or (ott-) to wash (hands, feet, etc.). Pa. nod- (nott-) to wash; nodip- (nodit-) to wash (another). Ga. (Oll.) nor- to. wash; (S.) norr- (noff-) to wash (clothes, -etc.). Go. (Tr.) norrand to wash (hands or feet); (W. Ph.) nurrand to wash; (G. S.) nor-, (Mu. Ko.) norr- to wash (hands, etc.); (Ma.) nog- (noht-) to wash (hands) (Voc. 2061). Konda nor- (noRt-) to wash, clean utensils, etc.; reff. norba- to wash (one’s own hands, face, etc.).. Pe. noz- (nost-}, nuz- (nust-) to wash. Mand. nuy- id. Kui nobga (<_nog-b-; nogd-) id.; 7. act of washing. Kuwi (S) norh’nai, (Su.) nor- Ch-) to wash. Kur. nOma {ntiryas) id., nérhorna to wash oneself, one’s own hands, feet, etc. Malt. nore to wash anything; nddre to wash one’s hands and feet. DED(S, N) 3136.

Ta. nofi (-v-, -nt-) to say, tell, speak, declare; (-pp-, -tt-) id., insinuate so as to stir up ill-will, make unfavourable allusions, use sarcasm; 1. word, phrase, ‘ language, speech, wise saying, riddle, enigma, stanza, noise. Ma. notikka to speak hastily or superciliously; notiyuka to murmur, lisp as fools. Ka. nudi to sound, utter, speak, say; 7. sounding, speaking, uttering a voice, speech, word, term, promise, language; nudisu, nudiyisu to cause or induce to utter, speak, etc.; perform music upon, play; nudiha uttering, telling; nudisuha making speak, addressing. Tu. nudi word, speech, saying, nudiyuni to pronounce, utter; nudipuni to speak, talk; nudipavuni to cause to speak, speak through another, play on a musical instrument. Te. nodi, nudi, noduvu word, expression; nodikfdu talkative man; nodikaramu, nudikdramu mode, style of speech; nodugu, nudugu to say, speak; n. word or expression, line in verse; noducu to find fault with; nodupu finding fault; nuduvari speaker, talker; nuduvu word, expression; vb. (K. also noduvu) to say, speak; nanudi rumour, report; saying, proverb. Nk. (Ch.) uduk-/ufk- to speak, suggest. DED(S) 3137.
Ka. (Sholiga, LSB 6.18) nofti but- tocks. Tu. niffely hip, loin, waist. DEN 52.

Ta. nonfu (nonti-) to walk lamely, limp, hobble from lameness or weariness; nonfal limping; nonti lame person or creature; crippled condition as of leg or arm; persons who dance on stilts; nentu (nenfi-) to limp. Ma. nontuka to limp, halt; noni cripple; nonfikka to maim. Ko. nond- (nondy-) to limp dragging one leg uselessly; nondy lame person, person who has arm or leg amputated. Cf. 5114 Ta. moffai. DED(S) 3138.

Te. nollu to level, scrape, gather, collect; nolla a harrow. Kuwi (Isr.) tol- Cit-) to level soil in new paddy field. From DED (S) 3140. 3789 Kol. nolgg- (nolonkt-) to thrash about on ground. Vk. norag- to roll on the ground. Go. (Tr. Ph.) nokkana@ to writhe in death, or in a fit; (SR.) nohkana to writhe (Voc. 2058); (ASu.) nork- to crawl on stomach; norkits- to make (child) crawl on stomach. DED(S) 3139.

Ta. nol (nolv-, nonf-), nolku (nolki-) to bale out as water, measure out as grain; ndntu (ndnqi-) to bale, scoop out; nulai a caste of fishermen; nulaiyan fisherman, inhabitant of the maritime tract; fem. nulaicci. Pa. ole- to bale out. Go. (Ph. W. A. Y.) roskana, (Tr.) roskana, (G. Mu. Ma. Ko.) losk-, (S.) loce- to bail out, (Tr.) bail out a pool in order to catch fish (Voce. 3074). Konda lo- (-t-) to fetch (water), dip and take out (water); lonz- to sprinkle or scatter (water, rice, etc.). Pe. ronj- (ronc-) to scatter, sow broadcast, bail out (by sprinkling). Kui nolpa (nolt-/ nof-) to ladle out. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) non- (-h-) to draw water. Kur, nollna to draw out (with a semicircular motion), scoop out as paddy with a winnowing basket, soup with a ladle; nolid fishing net, much like the English butterfly net. Malt. lole to take out with a spoon. Cf. 305 Te. aluku, from which Go., Konda (lonz-), Pe. have been transferred here, with which cf. esp. Pa. and Konda (lo-) (Su. 1969, p. 745); there has been conver- gence in meaning between the two entries in some languages. Cf. 5124 Ta. mol. DED(S) 3140, and from DED(S) 258.

Ta. nol (nolv-, nont-) to swallow, devour, Ma. nuna smack, slaver, greediness; nunayuka to eat greedily; nupaccal smacking the lips, greediness; nunpayan voracious; fer, nunacci; nunekka to smack the lips, have a ravenous appetite. Ka. none to swallow. Te. nena re LAH NCLCA He Renna eget ented RECA nN La merece se RSE Eg fa ee 52pm te EER Mtr MER HORNA ERTBAY EL TORIC smeoreenewnrsernnemtnra ane eet e peccoen sername cette = RWI SP ret fe nody [339] nol (K.) nollu (children) to suck fingers. Kol. (Pat., p. 143) nolayeng to swallow. Go. (Tr. W.) répana, (Mu. Ma. Ko.) lop- to swallow, gulp down (Voc, 3078). Kur, nuixnd to swallow without chewing, gulp hastily, devour: Cf. 3697 Ta. nunku (Pfeiffer). DED (S) 3141.

Ta. nd (-v-, nont-) to feel pain, ache, suffer, be grieved, distressed in mind, be injured, spoiled; 2. pain, iliness, weakness, infirmity; ndy Cpp-, -tt-; -v-, -nt-) to be ill, diseased, be debilitated, wither (as crops); n. malady, disease, grief, affliction, pain; ndvu pain, disease, grief; novvu (novvi-) to ache, pain; n. pain, illness; novval mental anguish, pain; noci (-v-, -nt-) to be in pain, suffer; nocivu pain, suffering; nompalam distress; no to suffer; m. pain, suffering; noyvu distress of mind; ndcal sickness, pain; nofical emaciation; nOficin, ndfici feeble, invalid person; uyafku (uyafiki-) to suffer, be in distress, pain (of body or mind), grow thin, become emaciated, be flexible, slender; uyakkam suffering, distress; uyavu (uyavi-) to suffer; n. distress, suffering; uyaval pain, suffering; uy® distress, suffering. Ma. ndka to pain, smart, be in labour; noyi, noyi weakness, pain; ndvu pain, ache, grief, sorrow; novuka to suffer pain, ache; ndvikka to pain, torment, afflict, offend; nomparam, nompal- am pain, sickness. Ko. nosy disease, pain. To. nue sickness. Ka. nd (nond-), noyyu, noyu, noy to suffer, pain, ache, (pain) is felt, grieve; ndyisu to cause to suffer pain, n&ta ache, pain; novu id., affliction. Kod. no- (novwv-, nond-) to pain (intr.); nombala pain. Tu. ndpuni, ndpini, ndyipini to ache, pain; ndvu pain, ache, distress. Te. noccu (novv-/ ndv-/nd-) to ache, pain, smart, be grieved; noncu to pain, wound, hurt (fr); nogulu, (K. also) novulu to grieve,

Ta. ndttam examination of coins, assaying, scrutiny of gems, precious metals, etc., value, criticism as by a rival artist or workman, attempt at wit, endeavour to excel, hinder or baffle another in speech; nodkku (ndkki-) to see, look at, behold, view, con- sider, reflect, regard, pay attention to, arrange, put in order, keep, protect, save, do, perform, resemble, compare, read, desire; 1. eye, sight, look, beauty, meaning, intention, knowledge, greatness, mode, style; nOkkam eye, eyesight, look, gaze, glance, view, aspects of a planet, appearance, expression, cast of countenance, beauty, attractiveness, watch, design, object, intention, aim, sense, intelligence, attention, observation, desire, indication, sign. Ma. ndfftam viewing, examination, experienced eye, sharp sight, shroffage, fortune-telling; nokkuka to look at, view, observe, look after, examine, be in a certain direction, seek, consider, regard, intend; nokkam_ view, beauty, eye; ndkkal a look; ndkku look, watchfulness, sight, beauty. Ko. no-f- (no+c-) to look at, look for, examinc, be looked at; no+t, no-fm (obi. no-ft-) a sight. To. nwi-t- (nwi-ty-) to look at; nwi-¢ a sight. Ka nddu to look, look on, look at, view, behold, look after, examine, consider, take care, see; ndduvike ‘ooking, etc.; nodisu to cause to look; nota looking at or on, viewing, behold- ing, seeing, examination, a sight, spectacle, sight, the power of seeing, a look, appearance; notaka a looker-on, spectator, eyewitness. Kod. no-t- (nosfi-)

Ta. ndnfu (monti-) to stir, dig up, grub up, root out, pick off as the scab of an ulcer, pick out as wax from the ear, pilfer, pluck as an ear of grain. Ma. nontuka to stir, dig, tease. Tu. nddadruni, nddavuni to rake up, stir. DED 3145. 3796 Konda (BB) ndmbu (Gi. nodpku), rom (pl. -ku) flea, tick. Pe. nom (pl. ndmku/ nopku) id. DEDS 584.

Ka. (K.?) nOrpu striking (7). Tu. ndpuni to beat, strike, flog. Kol. (Kin.) norp- to thresh (or with 3728). Kuwi (Su. Isr.) norp- (-it-) id. (or with 3728). 3798 Kur. not pumpkin. Malt. t6lu id., gourd. DEDS 585.

Ka. nol to precede, take the lead. Pe, n&k- (-t-) to go first, precede. Kui ndka (ndki-) to precede, go ahead of; ndki ahead, nol {340] paku in front of, in the van, in advance; nokiti ahead, in front of. Kuwi (F.) nodkali to advance; (T.) nok-, (S.) ndkhee hannai to precede; (F.) noki ahead; nokiti in front; nokigattasi the first one; (S.) nokita in front of, before; (Isr.} ndk- (-it-) to go ahead, precede; ndkita, noke’e in front of; (S.) Skhee hannai to precede. DED(S, N) 3146.

Ta. nol (ndrp-, ndrr-) to endure, suffer patiently as hunger, practise, do penance, practise austerities: 1. a means of attaining salvation; ndrpar ascetics, those who practise religious austerities; ndrpu bearing, endurance, performing penance; non (ndnp-, ndng-) to endure, bear, renounce as secular things, practise austerities; ndnpi ascetic; ndnpu, ndmpu ceremonial fasting, abstinence, penance; ndnmai bearing, en- durance, tolerance, power, greatness; ndnral patience, endurance. Ma. nolkka (nGzrr-) to fast, lead an austere life; nOnpu, ndmpu penance, fasting; ndlpikka to cause to fast. Ko. no«m(b) period when cloak is covered over head of relative of dead person (three days at green funeral, eight days at dry funeral). Ka. non (ndnt-) to observe a vow or religious obligation, perform anything as a meritorious act of devotion or austerity; caus, nonisu; ndmpi, ndmpu any religious act or obligation enjoined by the gods, any meritorious act of devotion or austerity. ? Kod. namme_ festival. Tu. nompu any meritorious act of devotion; a festival, esp. the one observed on the 14th day of the 6th lunar month in honour of Ananta; ndmbu fasting among the Mohammedans, fast, penance; ndmbuni to fast; ndhi a meritorious act or vow. Te. ndcu to perform an act of religious merit; nOta performing a meritorious act; ndmu to perform an act of religious merit; 2. vow, meritorious act. DED(S) 3147. P.B

Ta. paka-pakav-enal onom. expr. of crackling of fire; expr. signifying burning or smarting sensation of hunger. Ko, bagn, bagbagn blazing with sudden flame. Ka. baga, baga baga sound used to express suddenly blazing up, the crackling of flames, shining. brightly, and also burning of the body; bagabagane, baggane with the sound of bagabaga. Tu. bagabaga the crackling noise of conflagration. Te. bagguna suddenly (of burning or flaming); baggumanu to burn, flame, catch fire suddenly. DED 3149.

Ta. pakar (-v-, -nt-) to shift, move (intr.); pakarttu. (pakartti-) to transcribe, copy; pakarppu a copy; pakaram instead, in exchange. Ma. pakaruka to change in place, colour, etc., be exchanged; exchange (t.), barter; pakarcca change as of place, kind, weather; exchange; pakarkka to transcribe, copy; pakarppu, pérppu a copy; pakaram exchange, equivalent; in exchange. Tu, pagaruni to shift, change place; pagapuni to change, exchange, substitute; pagarcé, pagaté exchange, revenge; pagara exchange. DED 3150.
Ta. pakar (-v-, -nt-) to tell, utter, declare, say, announce, pronounce, publish; pakarcci speech, utterance, word, Te. pagatu to announce. DEDS 587.

Ta. pakal day, daytime, the morning sun, sun; pakalavan, pakaldn sun; pakal-maru in the daytime; ? pakar (-v-, -nt-) to emit lustre; #. radiance; ? pakaram brightness, splendour. Ma. pakal morning, daytime; pakalvan, pakalon sun. Ko. po-l daytime; time (od po-+lk at one and the same time); palmasrm daytime; ?vo-l day, daytime. To. poxol daytime, day (as opposed to night). Ka. pagal daytime, a day. Kod. po«li daytime. Tu, pagely id.; ? pagary time, tum. Te. pagalu, pavalu day, daytime. Pa. pakta day; (S.) payga dawn, morning; pokka, pokkai morning; pokkal day. Go. (Tr.) pial by day; (M.) piyal, (A.) piyal, (L.) peyal, (Ko.) payal a day; (Pat.} piyal noon; (Mu.) payyal daytime (Voc. 2122); (Mu.) pag, in: pag vinahk the whole night (Voc, 2072). Kur, pahpahma to dawn; pairi morning. Malt. paigre to dawn; pohpohre dawn. Cf.
Ta. pakagi arrow. Ma. pakari id. Tu. pagari, (B-K. also) pagali arrow, dart. DED (N) 3152.
Ta. pakanrai Indian jalap (a purgative root). Ma. pakunna a purgative root. DED 3153.

Ta. paku (pakuv-, pakk-) to be split, divided, be at variance, separate; divide (tr.); (pp- -tt-) to distribute, apportion, classify, give, divide, cut into pieces, tear off, root out; pakal dividing, separating; pakavu slice, share; pakir (-v-, -nt-) to divide into shares, distribute, break, split; separate (intr.); n. share, section, piece; pakuti, pakti portion, allotment, tribute, rent; pakuppu pagela [341] pafgu division, classification; pakai disagreement, hatred, enemy; (-pp-, -tt-) to hate, oppose; pakaifian, pakaivan enemy; pakaimai enmity; pakku fracture, duplicity; pa (-pp-, -tt-), pattu (patti-) to divide, distribute; pattu dividing, sharing, share, half; patti division, section, part, share; patu portion, share; panku share, portion, part, half; pahkam portion, division; pankan sharer. Ma. pakuka to be separate; pakuti division, share; pakukka to divide (¢r.); pakuppu distribution, portion or section; paka separation, enmity, incom- patibility; pakayan enemy; pakayuka to divide; pakekka to oppose, hate; pakappu distance, variance, hostility; pakafu a small bit; pafttku part, share; pankan partner, husband. ? Ko. pati«r- (patre-) to divide (< pat-ayr-, pat-arc-, or pat-tayr, pat-tarc-). To, pax- (pax@-) to be divided, be estranged; (paxt-) to distribute, divide; paxy division; ax hostility; axo-rn, (in songs) axexo-m enemy. Ka, pagadi tribute, tax; page, hage hatred, enmity, enemy; pagekira enemy; hagar(u), hanjara a split of bamboo. Kod. (Shanmugam) page enmity;

Ta. pakku scab ofa sore, dried mucus of the nose. Ka. hakku crusted or dry mucus or rheum, scab; hakkale an incrustation. Te. pakku scab. Ga. (S.4) pakku dried portion of any bodily secretion, scab. DED(S) 3155.

Ka. pakke the tamarisk tree, Tamarix indica. Te. pakkepakki cetfu tamarisk, T. gallica; (B) pakke, pakkiya tamarisk tree. If. gallica Linn. = T. gallica var. indica Willd. == T. indica Wight.] DED 3156.

Ta. pakk-enal onom. expr. of (a) being sudden, (b) bursting as with sudden laughter. Ka. paka imitation of the sound of laughter; paka paka(ne) nagu to laugh very loudly, giggle; pakkane suddenly; pakkane nagu to burst out laughing. Tu. pakapaka quickly; pakka, pakkané suddenly, unex- pectedly. Te. pakapaka, pakapaka a loud burst of laughter, the noise of laughter; pakkuna suddenly (applied to laughter). Ga, (S.3) pakal the sound of sudden laughter. DED 3157. 3814 Kur. bakka claws of a crab, (Hahn) forceps, shears. Malt. bake forceps. DEDS 590.
Ka. baggari thorax. Kod. baggare tib. DED 3158. 3816 Kur. (Hahn) bagra shank of the leg. Malt. bagde id.; bagd-poti calf of the leg. DEDS 588.

Ka. banke, bottka, bofike gum, glue, resin. Tu, pakara sap, juice. Te. banka gum, glue, matter or mucus discharged from the bowels; stinginess; viscous, glutinous, sticky, stingy; bafketa a stingy woman. Kol. (Kin.) banka gum; (BB 1957) bakka resin; (Pat., p. 43) bakka sap. Nk. bakka gum. Pa. bak, bakar gum of tree. Ga. (5.3) banka gum, stickiness. Go. (Mu.) boker gum (Voc. 2613). ? Cf. 3827 Ta. pacai. DED(S) 3159.

Ka. patiga state of being astride or forked, forked branch of a tree; pangada astride, apart, distinct; bagatu to disjoin the legs, open the legs, straddle. 7u. patgada distinct, apart; pafigadipuni to straddle, place apart; bagatuni to straddle, stand astride. Te. patiga forked branch of a tree; parting the legs wide; pafigati forked, pronged, placed wide apart; pafgafi-kallu bandy legs; pangafincu to part wide, straddle. Go.
