(Ko.) panga branch of tree (Voe. 2073). Konda panla fork; pagla sari a road that separates in two directions. DED(S) 3160. 3819 Kui pangal dina the plains country. Kuwi (D.) paggenga (pl) plains people; (F.) panga outside; pangati external; (S.) pangata Suside, away; panga raji the plains. DEDS
Ka. pangu, hahgu, angu the state of being obliged or indebted; pangiga one who paca is indebted or obliged. Tw. hangy, ahgy obligation; the state of being in the power of another; duty.

Ta. paca (-pp-, -nt-) to be green, turn sallow or pale, become golden (as the sky in the evening); pacanfai verdure, moisture, pacappu green colour, sallow complexion (due to Jovesickness), moisture; pacalai gold colour, sallowness; pacitu that which is green; pacu green, greenish yellow, yellow; (-pp-, -tt-) to be green, pacuppu greenness, greenish yellow; pacumai greenness, verdure, coolness, moistness, youth, newness, fresh- ness, greenish yellow; pacai moisture, sap, juice; paccai greenness, paleness (as of a maid separated from her lover}, unripeness, fresh- ness, coolness; paya (-pp-, -nt-) to change in complexion (as the skin through lovesickness), turn sallow through affliction; payappU change of hue, turning sallow, gold colour; payir growing grain, herbage, tender sprout; pai greenness, freshness, youth, | beauty; (-pp-, -tt-) to become green; paital cold, chilliness; paitu, paittu greenness, freshness, moisture; paimmai, paimai greenness, pacavar dealers in betel leaves; pacaval green field; paci that which is green; moss, lichen, sea- weed, mouldiness; pacu greenness, verdure; picai, pacam moss, pinam duckweed, a kind of moss. Ma. pacu, pai, paim tender, fresh, moist, green; paca, pasa, paya moisture, pasima freshness, softness, moisture; pacca greenness, freshness; payir green corn; payitha unripe fruit, young areca nut; pasi, pasar, payal, payil, payi green stuff on stagnant pools, mouldiness. Ko, pac green, raw (of meat), unboiled (of water), unbaked (of pots);

Ta. pacalai spinach, Spinacia oleracea; purslane, Portulaca quadrifida, Malabar night- shade, Basella, and some other plants; pacirt, payiri creeping purslane, P. quadrifida; vacalai, vacalai, vayalai, vayalai purslane, P. quadrifida, Ma. pacala, pasala Basella; vasala, vayara P. oleracea. Ka, basale Malabar night- shade, B. alba; basale B. cordifolia, B. lucida or rubra; baccali, baccale spinach; baccale- balli P. quadrifolia. Tu. basale Malabar night- shade, B. alba. Te. tiga-baccali B. cordifolia; dumpa-baccali, maffu-baccali S. tetranda. [B. rubra Linn. = B. alba Linn, = B. cordifolia Lamk. = B. lucida L. S. oleracea Linn. = S. . tetranda Roxb.] DED 3164. 3825 (a) Ta. paci (-pp-, -tt-) to be hungry; n, hunger; paciyan a hungry man. Ma, payi, pai, payikkam, paikkam, payippu hunger; payikka, paikka to be hungry; payyavan the hungry, Agni. To. osf- (ost-) hunger is (toa person); osf hunger. Ka, past to hunger, 7. hunger; pasivu hunger. Te. pastu fast, fasting; nippastu starvation (nip- < niru; s.v. 3682 Ta. nirai). ; (b) Ta. paficam scarcity, famine, dearth; paficai famine, poverty, poor person, weak, hasuku {343] emaciated person. Ma. paficam, paiifiam famine, scarcity, poverty. Ko. pajm (obl. pajt-) famine. To. pozm, ozm (< Badaga hafija) id. Ke. pafije poor, helpless, weak person; pafijetana poverty. Tu. pafija scarcity, famine. Te. nippaccaramu poverty, scarcity (see nippastu). DED 3165.
Ka. hasuku sharp, disagreeable smell. Te. pasi smell, scent. DEDS 593.

Ta. pacai stickiness, paste, glue, glutinous substance in fruits, roots, etc.; (-v-, -nt-) to become glutinous or viscous; stick ‘together (tr.); payirppu resin; payan juice; payin gum, glue. Ma. paca, pasa, paya gum, glue; payala, pakala the pulp inside the shell forming the coconut. Ko. pac flour- paste. Ka. pagil to be sticky, viscid, glutinous, adhesive, adhere, join; paginu, pagina gum, resin, exudation of trees. 7u, paya gum, resin; pasé, pasé greasiness, oiliness. ? Te. p&ésemu juice or sap as of mango or palm fruit. Go. (W.) pacihtana to stick (Voc. 2074). Kur. . - pai starch made of rice diluted in water; padxna to thicken, acquire half consistence (as glue, paste), become soft, half-melt; render half-fluid. Malt, pathge to be con- gealed. ? Cf. 3817 Ka. batke. DED(S) 3166.

Ta. pacai devotion, love, affection, desire; (-v-, -nt-) to be kind, affectionate; pacaintar friends; pacaivu compassion, kind- ness, affection. Ka) pasa wish, great desire, eagerness; (PBh.) pasavu desire, eagerness; ? pade to desire, wish. DED 3167.
Pe. bajek much; bajoy (p/.) many. Mand. bejek much. Cf. 4411 Ta. peru. DEDS 594,
Ta. paccati a kind of relish made of minced vegetables (Annamalai, p. 875). Ka. pac(c)adi a kind of pickle. Te. paccadi chut- ney. DEDS(N) 595.

Ta. paccilai Mysore gamboge, Garcinia xanthochymus. Ma. paccila Xanthochymus pictorius, Ka. paccari name of a tree [= Ta. paccilai, according to Ta. Lex.]. [G. xantho- chymus Hook. = X. pictorius Roxb.}] DED 170.
Ta. paccai skin, hide; covering as of the body of a yap. Tu. (B-K.) paca skin of the leg. Br. pacx natural outer sheath or covering, bark; pacirok, pacironk outer layer or crust. DED 3171.
Ma. pafica ricefield. Tu. pafica id. DEDS 596.
Ta. paiicari (-pp-, -tt-) to press, im- portune, talk at length; paficali (-pp-, -tt-) to upset mentally. Ma. paficarikka to im- portune. DED 3172.

Ta. pafici, paficu cotton, cloth, cotton cushion. Ma. pafifii cotton. To. poj Pafapata cotton blossom. Ka, pafiji, pafijike ball of cotton from which thread is spun. /Cf. Skt. pafiji-, pafijika- id.; Turner, CD/AL, no. 7688. DED 3173.

Ta. pataku small boat; dhoney, large boat; pafavu small boat; pafavan boatman; patuvai raft, float. Ma. pafavu, pafaku ship, large boat. Ka. padagu, padatigu, padahu, hadaga, hadagu id. Tu. pada, padavu boat; hadaga, adagy ship. Te. padava boat. / Cf. Mar. padav a kind of boat carrying from five to twenty. DED 3175.
Ta. patahku tent, awning, curtain; pataikam tent. Ma. patanhu tent, ‘awning. DED 3176.

Ta. (lex.) patti son; (SATD) patuci a young one. Ka. padati woman; (Sholiga, LSB 6.18) padde young boy. Te. padati, paddtuka woman; paducu girl; young, youth- ful; paducidu boy; patti a child. Kol. (SR.) padas boy, son. Pa. padic boy. Cf. 3881 Ka. padde. DED 3177, DEDS 604.

Ta. patapata (-pp-, -tt-) to rattle (as things falling, rolling or breaking); pata-pat- enal onom. expr. signifying bursting, break- ing, falling with a rattling noise; pafar-enal, patar-pafar-enal onom. expr. signifying crack- ing, bursting with a sudden noise, report or explosion; patir-enal onom. expr. signifying a sudden crash or explosion. Ma. patapata rattling noise, the report of a gun, etc.; pafakkam a cracker. Ko. pat in- (id-) to make noise of bursting of skin when burnt, of sharp blow, crack, etc.; pafa+rm with noise of a blow, or of breaking (stick, knife, rope). Ka, pada, pada pada sound imitating that of a cracker or squib; pata, pata pafa, pafarane, pattane sound imitating the flapping of wings or suspended clothes, of a dog shaking his body, of applying blows or kicks, of bursting; patir, pathil bang! Tu. padapada, pafapajane a flapping or rattling sound. Te. patapata noise made in breaking or bursting, gnashing the teeth, crackling; pafitumanu to break (intr.) with a crack. Kur. patpat any rapid succession of sounds (made e.g. by abjects falling in number); patpatrna, pat- pofma to crackle several times in succession, make a series of rapidly repeated noises. Cf.

Ta. patapata (-pp-, -tt-) to be over- hasty (as in speech); patapatappu precipitancy, agitation, overhastiness; pata-pat-enal onom. expr. signifying speaking in haste, hurry (as in preparation for a journey). Ko. patakn suddenly, with a jerk; completely, all at once. Ka. patakkane with the sound of patak, ie. suddenly and with a noise. Tu. patakka, patala, patfala smartly, sharply (as to strike thus); patta suddenly. DED(S) 3179.
Ta. patam instep. Ma. patam flat part of the hand or foot. Pe. pafa key palm of hand. Mand. pata kiy id.; pafa kal sole of foot. Kuwi (Su.) pata naki palm of hand. Cf. 3878 Ta. pattai. DED(S) 3181.

Ta. patar (-v-, -nt-) to run, spread (as ‘a creeper), overspread (as eruptions on the skin), spread (as light, fire, rumour, epidemic), be diffused, pervade, expand; m. spreading spots on skin, thick bush (esp. of creepers); patarcci, patartti creeping (as a vine), spread- ing (as darkness, light, fire, spots, eruptions); patar low bush, low thicket of creepers; patalai spreading, expanding, expanse. Ma. pataruka to spread (as fire, plants, odour), creep, climb; pafarcca climbing of vine, diffusion; patarttuka to train, support plants; patarppu, patappu bush, thicket; patal clump of bushes; patalam a spreading over, cover, mass. Tu. padaruni, padapuni to creep. DED 3182. 3845 Ta, patal, patalai small shutter of braided palm leaves or thorns, hurdle or wattled frame for sheltering cattle, sunshade. Ma. patal, patala a rough harrow. To. ody door, in song-unit; kabin ody iron door. Ka. padi leaf or panel of a door, a door. Kod. padi door. Tu. padi a thrashing frame, a door or shutter made of slips of bamboos tied together. Te. padi-kadpu, padi-gapu a waiting at the gate of one, one who waits at the gate. Konda pata an instrument to level the field after ploughing. DED(S) 3183.
Ta. patalai a bunch of fruits, pagu cluster, bunch of flowers or fruits. Ma. patala a cluster or comb of plantains. DED (S) 3184.
Ta. patarri a kind of plantain. Ma. patarri plantain tree. DED 3185.

Ta. pati (-v-, -nt-) to settle (as dust or sediment), rest (as clouds upon a moun- tain), roost, be subjugated, trained, tamed, become orderly, obey, sink in water, be immersed, subside (as water); (-pp-, -tt-) to practise, habituate oneself to. Ma. pafiyuka to settle, sink; become habitual by learning or exercise; patikka to plunge; pativu subjec- tion; habitual. Tu. padipuni to plunge. Pa. parva badi dovecote. Kuwi (T.) parva padi id. DED 3186.

Ta. pati a weight (= 100 palam), the ordinary measure of capacity (= 8 ollocks); fixed daily allowance for food; patiy-al hired servant, one whose wages are paid in grain. Ma pati measure (esp. of rice), regular atlow- ance; at the rate of. Ko. pary a measure (ofk) of grain given to a Toda when he comes to a village; payy bazaar measure of grain (= olk); quantity of food that one person eats at a meal. To. pory rice given at funeral, contri- bution given to Badagas at a two-day funeral. Ka, padi measure of capacity (= one-half seer); an allowance in food to servants, a gift to friends or poor people. Tu. padi daily allowance of food, extra pay to servants when travelling; alms in kind; a small weight. Te. padi a measure of capacity (= 14 seer). DED 3187.

Ta. pati step, stair, rung of a ladder, stirrup, grade, rank, sill; patikkattu flight of steps (kaftu building, structure). Ma. pati step, stair, threshold, bench, sill. Ko, parykat steps leading up .to veranda, steps of ladder. Ka. padi stirrup. Te. padi-kaffu a stair or step. DED 3188.
Ta. pafi manner, mode. Ka. padi manner, method, way. Te. (K.) badi, vadi manner. DEDS 597.

Ta. patu (patuv-, patf-) to perish, die, set (as a heavenly body), rain; (-pp-, -tt-) to lay horizontally, pave, spread out (as bedding), kill, cast down, fell; lie down to sleep or otherwise, roost (as birds); adj. base, low; patu-fidyiru setting sun; patukkai lying down, bed; pafai bed; layer, stratum; pafu lying prostrate, fall, sleep, death, ruin, loss, disaster; patai bier; paftam shower of rain. Ma. patuka (patt-) to fall, sink; patukka to lay stones, build (chiefly a well, tank); piss; lie down; n. bed; pata layer in mud walls, course of bricks; pafavu laying stones, pavement, patuppu, patappu bed, mat; patififiaru west; pafu falling, falling into one’s power; petuka (pett-) to fall, get into or under; petukka to piss. Ko. par- (paf-) to lie down, sleep; payc- (parc-) to make to lie, lay. To. por- (pof-) to lie down; lose (teeth; of children); pory sleeping-place (in song). Ka. padu (patf-) to lie down, set (as the sun), be spent (as the day), have sexual intercourse, die; n. setting, the west, dying; padu-n@sar the setting sun; paduva, padaval, paduval, paduvu the west, padal lying or falling down; pafu falling, a fall, a declining state, ebb of the tide; pati downfall, ruin. Kod. pad- (paduv-, paff-) to lic fallow; padit- (paditi-) to let lie fallow, (Kar. also) send (child) to sleep; padi-bu-mi fallow land; padi-fia-ri west. Tu. pada placing or laying; paddayi the west. Te. padu to fall, lie, recline, slecp; padamara the west; paduka bed; bedding; parucu, paracu, (K

Ta. pau (pafuv-, paff-) to occur, happen, come into existence, rise (as a heav- enly body), occur to mind, hit or strike against, touch, be caught (as fishes, birds, or other game), suffer, endure, dash against (tr.), agree to, be connected with, resemble; Cpp-, -tt-) to do, make, effect, catch, establish, entrust, cause to grow; pafuttu (pafutti-) to cause to suffer, put to trouble, cause to get, effect, bring into existence; pattafku natural state, truth; patai (-pp-, -tt-) to create, form, produce; acquire, get; pataippu creation, acquiring, possessing; pafu coming into being, happening, experience, condition, nature, industry, business, concern or affair, afflic- tion, suffering, place, situation; paffam a turn in the game of tipcat; pattal, pattali an industrious person. Ma. patu what happens, is common; paftuka (patf-) to be obtained, caught, happen, be in a state; paf¢afmu truth; pafukka to catch, obtain; pafu suffer- ing pain or damage, possibility, place, situation, nature; pafekka to make, create; patappu creation; people; pefuka (peff-) to happen, be in, belong to; auxiliary verb; petukka to enclose, ensnare; peffu getting into a direction, towards. Ko, pay- (pat-) to experience (emotion), be caught, seem good; par (@bl. pa+f-) work, concern. To. por- (pof-) to suffer, experience; po-ed suffering, affair, concern; ? pary- (pars-) to be possible; to endure. Ka. padu (pat{-) to get, obtain, catch, incur, undergo, expericnce, feel, suffer; m. getting, etc.; paduvike g
Ka. padu, pade place of refuge (for wild animals) between stones or in rocks. Te. paddku cave, hole, crevice. Nk. pharka den, lair (e.g. of tiger). Cf. 3892 Ta. panai. DED(S) 3193. ‘
Ta. patu toddy; patuvan toddy- seller; fem. patuvi. Malt. patka liquor dis- tilled from the flowers of the mahua tree. DEDS 600.
Ta. patuvan boil, abscess. Ma. patu- van small ulcer. DED 3194.

Ta. patai army, crowd, weapons, battle. Ma. pafa battle, army. To. par crowd; fight (in songs). Ka pade multitude, host, force, army; padeyila soldier; padevala, padevalla a general. Tu. padé multitude, mob, army. Te. padava fight, battle; pada- valamu van of an army; padavalu commander of an army. DED 3195. 3861 Malt. parge to stir up, incite. Br. pay@fing to instigate, provoke. DEDS 601.
Ka. patakaru tongs, pincers. Te.° pafakaru, pafukaru pair of tongs, large pincers. DEDS 603.
To. paftatai, patfarai anvil, smithy, forge. Ka. patfade, pattadi anvil, workshop. Te. pattika, patfeda anvil, paffada workshop. Cf. 86 Ta. atai. DED 3196.
Ta. pattatai neck-ornament (< Te.). Tu. pattadi a kind of necklace. Te. pafteda a sort of ornament worn by women round the neck, DED 3197.

Ta. patfa-ppakal open day, broad daylight. Ma. patta-pakal broad daylight, noon. Ko. pata-r in- (id-) (country, na-r) becomes fully light at dawn. To. of oxol broad daylight (in song). Te, patfa-pagalu broad day, midday. Cf, 3805 Ta. pakal. DED(S) 3198. ‘

Ta. patti cow-stall, sheepfold, hamlet, village; patfam sleeping place for animals; pattu hamlet, small town or village; paffinam maritime town, smnall town; pafappu enclosed garden; patappai id., backyard, cowstall. Ma. patti fold for cattle or sheep. Ko. paty Badaga village. To. ofy id. (< Badaga hafti). Ka. patti pen or fold, abode, hamlet; pata city, town, village. Tu. pafty nest. Te. patfu abode, dwelling place. /Cf. Turner, CDIAL, no. 7705, pattana-. DED 3199. .

Ta. patti lawless, unbridled person; theft; harlot, prostitute; pattikan thief, defrauder, pattimai deceit; pafu base, low; cruel; patiru lying, deceit, unruliness, lawless- ness, mischief, cruelty; patiran liar, cheat, rake, cruel person, mischievous; fer. patiri. Ma. patti miserable. vicious. Ka. padapu adultery (PBh., p. 417); baddi, boddi strum- pet, fornicatress; hadaka, haduka one who has or has had sexual intercourse; hadiki a woman who has been embraced sexually; hadabe, adabe perverseness, wickedness (used of stray cattle). Tu, padiké wickedness, depravity; bad, corrupt; padikenti bad, corrupt, padapé whore, whoremonger; hadabé, adabé bastard, illegitimately born, (B-K. also padpe) adultress; (B-K.) patike, patke wickedness, patinge 4 rogue. Te, paducu to prostitute oneself; padupu prostitution; padupudi, padapigom- ma prostitute. DED(S, N) 3200, and from DED 58.
Ta. patti dog; patti female of hog, dog or fox. Ma. patti dog, bitch; pafficci bitch. DED(S) 3201.

Ta. pattini fasting, abstinence, star- vation. Ma. pattini, pattini privation of food, starvation. Ko. patu-ny hunger. Kod. patfani starvation. /Cf. Skt. (Paramanandtyanamamala 1816) pettika- = adyana-. DED 3202. padde
Ko. pat scar, esp. made by burning. Ka. pattu a callous spot. DED 3203.

Ta. pattai palmyra timber, rafter, pattiyal lath, reeper. Ma. pafta areca bough. Ka, patfe palmyra timber, rafter, areca bough; patti piece of timber of door-frame, rafter, joist; patfika board. Tu. pafi rafter. Te. patte bar or spar of wood, piece of timber of door-frame; patti plank; paftika plank, board, bar of wood. Kol. patte plank. Nk. pati id. Pa. petti (pl. petkul) beam, post. Ga. (P.) patiya beam. Kui pati beam; pata board. Kur. patta beam in oilmill, DED(S) 3204.
Ta. pattai bark of tree. Ma, patta id. Ka. patte rind or bark of trees. Te, paffa, pattamu bark. Cf. 3897 Ga. panday. DED(S) 3205.

Ta. paftai painted stripe (as on a temple wall), piebald colour, dapple. Ma. patta stripe. Ka. paffe, pafti id. Kod. paffe striped or spotted (as tiger or leopard); pattati apr. of dappled cow. Tu. paffeé stripe. Te. patte stripe or streak of paint; padita stripe, streak, wale. DED 3206.

Ta. pattai flatness; pattam flat or level surface of anything, flat piece (as of bamboo). Ko. pat flatness (of piece of iron, of head); patm (ob/, patt-) ground for house. To. pof site of dairy or house. ? Kod. patti space before house, spreading space; mana-paffi sandbank. Nk. par place. Pa. pad place, site. Pe. pat kapra top of the head. Mand. pat kapra id. Malt. pata numeral classifier of flat objects. Cf, 3843 Ta. patam. DED(N) 3207. 3879 Kur. (lahn) pat&é worms in the stomach. Malt, pato id. DEDS 608. 3880 Kur. batri loose rings worn on the tocs by women. Malt. batt id. DEDS 609.

Ka. padde matutity, fitness for breeding. Te. padda female buffalo or cow fit for breeding, (VPK, communicated by K.) female buffalo calf; barre female buffalo. Kol. (Br.) padda-le-na female calf; (SR. Kin.) barre buffalo. Nk. padda heifer. Pa. (S.) pedda female calf. Ga. (Ol) bere, (P.) bede/ bere female calf; (S.3) bedde peyye calf. Go. (Tr. M.) padda cow-calf; (A.) padda piya, (W. Ph.) padda, (Y. Ch.) padda, (D.) pada peyya, (Mu.) padda, pedda, (Ma.) pada piya female calf, heifer, (Ko.) pada pila calf of buffalo (Voc. 2091); (LuS.) puda_ heifer. Konda pada young cow, heifer. Cf. 3840 Ta. patti. /Cf. Skt. (Brhatkathakosa) paddika- calf; Pkt. paddiya- young female buffalo, female calf, cow that has calved for the first time; (DNM) paddi- cow that has calved for the first time; (DNM) pedda- buffalo; fem. pedda-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8042. DED (S, N) 3208, DED 3321. baddu [347] panai
Te. baddu a thick or strong creeper or rope, cable. Ga. (S.2) baddu creeper. DEDS 610.
Ka. baddu bluntness, dullness. Tu. baddy blunt, edgeless; dull, stupid, obtuse.

Ta. pan service, work, . business, employment, decoration; pani act, action, performance, work, service, decoration; panati workmanship, action, creation, orna- ment; paniti work, structure, ornament; panikkan master-builder, carpenter; panik- kam, panikku accuracy of design, elabora- tion in a work; paninar servants; panpu action, deed; pannu (panni-) to make, effect, produce, adorn; pannu (panni-) to do any- thing with consideration and skill. Ma. pani work, labour, service, building, exertion; panikkan workman, artificer; paniyuka to build; paniyan a caste of cultivators in hilly districts; game-trackers, living chiefly in Wynad; pannuka, pannikka vb. denoting coitus (obscene). Ko. panyn man of a caste at Gudalur in Wynad; fem. pane; ? pan anvil. To. pony work. Ka. pannu to make ready, prepare, equip, decorate; pannika, pannige, pannuge arranging, making ready, equipping. Kod. pani work. Tu. panipuni to give a shape (e.g. to a vessel). Te. pani work, labour, act, deed, workmanship, art; (inscr.) pani work; pannu to contrive, plan, design, invent; (K. also) be ready, make ready; (K. also) x. suitability. Kol (SR.)} panni work, labour. Nk. pani work. Pa. pandp- (pandt-) to make, do. Ga. (Oll.) pand- to be able. Go. (LSI, Koi) pani, (Grigson) pari, (Ko.) pari work (Voce. 2092); pand- (G.) to build (house), (Mu.) to make, build, repair, (Ma.) to make, construct; (L.) pandand to make, repair (Voe. 2093). Konda pand- (-it-) to prepare, construct, devise, plan; pani work. Cf. 389
Ka. banapu a Sarge timber-tree, Terminalia tomentosa, vat. coriacea. Tu. banympu, banampu 7. coriacca. / Cf. Kon- kani banapu (Kt.) [7. tomentosa Bedd. var. coriacea = T, coriacea W. & A.] DED 3210.
Ka. banambe, banabe, banave, banive, balabe stack, rick. Tu. banabé stack. ? Ta. pafappu hayrick. DED 3211.

Ta. pani (-pp-, -tt-) to speak, say, declare (used of a superior), order, com- mand; n. saying, word, command; panippu command, order; palukku (palukki-) to be pronounced clearly, boast, speak (< Te.); pannu (panni-) to speak, say, talk, sing; panuval word, discourse. Ma. panikka to speak. To. pon®- (ponOy-) to talk in as- sembly; pont speech, words (in songs). Ka. panavu place appointed for meeting; (Hal.) halupu to speak like a madman while asleep; halebu to talk foolishly; halevaru to speak while asleep. Tu. panpini to say, tell, inform, narrate, teach. Bel. (LSB 2.3) hani to say. Te. pan(ujcu to send, command, commis- sion; (inscr.) pancu to send, commission; pani commission, mission, errand; panupu order, command, errand; pampu to send, dispatch, dismiss; . sending, order, mission, errand; paluku to sound, answer, utter, say; n, speaking, word, sound, voice; palikincu to cause to speak, utter, sound, or ring; palu- karincu to accost, speak to; ? palavu, palavin- cu, palavarincu, paluvarincu to talk in sleep; palavaramu, palavarinta, palavarimpu talking in sleep; ? palumu to prattle; 7, prattle (or with 4304 Ta. pulampu). Kol, pank- (panakt-) to send; pasna language. Nk. pank- to send. Konda palkis- to cause to sound, play on (a muscial instrument). Kui panda (pandi-) to send, commission; n. act of sending; plapa (plat-) to inquire, question, ask, address, say; n. inquiry, remark, Kuwi (F.) pandali, (S.) pantinai, (Isr.) pand- (-it-) to send. Br. pining (pa-, par-) to say

Ta. pani (-y-, -nt-) to be low in height, be short, be lowered, be humble, become inferior, fall (as prices, wages), bow, make obeisance to; (-pp-, -tt-) to lower or lead down, degrade, reduce (as price); x. bowing, reverencing; panippu lowness (as of tone); paniyal worship, obeisance; panivu sub- mission, humility, veneration, defect, low place, depression. Ma. paniyuka to worship, salute, acknowledge superiority. Ka, (Hal.) hanuku to bend, bow. Cf. 3900 Tu. pandu. DED(N) 3213. .
Ta. paniyaram cakes, pastry, fritters; panniyam cakes, pastry, confectionery; pan- fam cake. Ka. paniyana, panivadra sweet cake, fritter. Te, panyadramu, (SAN) panneramu cake. DEDS(N) 611.
Te. paduku a stone. Konda panku id. DEDS 612.

Ta. panai, pannai agricultural tract, paddy field, garden; (inscr.) pan field. Ma, pana gound which is worked (including stone- quarry). Ko. pan level grassy ground. Ka. pane ground that is worked, tillage, quarry; pan- neya, panya farm, landed estate. Tu. (B-K.) pane quarry. Te. (K.) pan(n)asa agricultural land given to brahmans (inscr. 6th cent. onwards; Epigraphia Indica 27. 225-8). Cf.
Ta. panai lair of an animal; pannai lair, sleeping place of beast. Ka. pane haunt or lair of wild beast. Cf. 3857 Ka. padu. DED(S) 3215.
Ta. panai drum, large drum, Ka. pane, pana small drum or tabor. / Cf. Skt. panava- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7716, DED 3216.

Ta. pamai thickness, bigness; panai branch of a tree; (-pp-, -tt-) to extend, ramify, grow thick or close as branches; panar, panaru branches, thick branches. Ka. pane upper part of the stem of a tree; ? pani stout stick or bat; hani the upper part of the panai [348] patam stem of a tree; hani-gombu the forked branch which issues from that part of the stem. Tu. pané the forked stem of a water lift. Pe. pan- (-t-) to grow, grow up, grow big or tall. DED (S) 3217.
Ta. panai pipal. Ka. pani id. DED 218.
Ka. pane, hane, ane, hani forehead. Tu. han, ané id. DEDS 613.

Ta. panti, panfam belly, paunch, body; vanfi belly, stomach. Ma. panti stomach; pantam crop of birds, craw. Kuwi (Su. P.) bandi belly; (Isr.) bandi stomach, bandi 4: to have diarrhoea; bandita A- to be pregnant. Cf, 5232 Ma. vatfi. / Cf. Skt. ex.) phinda- belly; Sgh. bada id. DED(S) 3220.

Ta. pantikai festival. Ma. pantika id. Te. panduga id. Go. (L.) pandom holiday; bandum (Elwin) festival, (Grigson) a_first- fruit or new-eating ceremony (Voc. 2097). Konda pandoy festival. DED(S) 3221.

Tu. panty, panfu fatigue, weariness. Te. pandu to lie down; pangukonu. id., sleep, be confined to one’s bed as with jliness; parundu to lie down, recline, repose, sleep. Pa. pand- to be tired, be defeated, lose. Ga. (Oll. S.) pand- to become tired; (S.*) pandi tiredness. Cf. 3888 Ta. pani. DED(S, N) 3222. : 3901 Pe, pandra- (ic. pandra 4) to fear. Mand. pandra- id. 2? Te. bendu-padu to be alarmed, dejected; bendu-parucu to frighten, intimidate. DEDS(N) 615. 3902 Ta, vantu low, mean conduct; vantan wicked person; vanfattanam wickedness, lewdness; vanfai that which is corrupt or vulgar. Ko. vad woman’s lewd conduct which results in a bastard child; vad mog bastard child; bandv lewdness (of a woman who has intercourse with many men); (sense- less) cattle, domestic animals. To. pod mox child which is illegitimate because bow was not given to mother. Ka. bandu shameless- ness, lewdness, disgrace; banda a shameless, dishonourable, lewd, profligate man; bandaga, bandatana, bandutana shamelessness, pro- fligacy. Kod. bandé shameless man; fem. bandati; bandatana shamelessness. Tu, bandu, bhandy, (B-K. only bandy) obscenity, rude- ness; bhanda obscene, impudent; bande, bhande a lewd man, an indecent man. fe. banda a rude, unfeeling man; obscene, shame- less; bandadu, bandagidu a rude, unfeeling man; bandafta wickedness; bandu indecent, obscene, gross. /Cf. Mar. bhand exposure, public disgrace, subjection to general ridicule; Turner, CDIAL, no. 9371, bhanda- jester, mime. DED 3223.

Ka. bande rock, block or slab of stone. Te. banda id., pestle without a ferrule, a much-used or worn-out block of stone. Ga. (S.2) banda boulder. Go. banda (A. Ma. M. S.) stone, rock, (A. S.) hail (Voc. 2484). /Cf. Mar. badya the top rock on a hill. DED (S) 3224.

Ta. panpu, patpu quality, nature, good quality, beauty. ? Ko. pan pan in- (id-) - (woman) looks very beautiful. DED(S) 3225. 3905 Ta, pata (-pp-, -tt-) to become soft; patamai softness, tenderness, mellowness, gentleness, mildness, pliancy, smoothness; patavu mildness, gentleness; pataviyatu that which is soft or smooth, mild or gentle; patavicu mildness, humbleness; patam gentle- ness, sweetness; pati (-v-, -nt-) to be mild, gentle, submissive, quail, flinch; pativu submission. Ma. patam softness, moderation, elasticity, yielding temper; patama, patuma pliancy; patarmma rottenness of rice through damp, softness of mind; patukka to be soft, tender, moderate, pliable; patuppu softness; patuppikka to soften. Kog. pada soft. Tu. padanuni, padanuni to become soft; pasifkara yielding, softness. Malt. pothge to be soft; pothqlare to become soft; pothqlatre to soften; pothqlo, pothqro soft, gentle, mild, meek, DED(S) 3226.

Ta. pata (-pp-, -tt-) to become moist, marshy; patam water, dampness, moisture. Ko. padm (obl. padt-) wetness of land just after rain, suitable for sowing. Tu. padampuni to become wet; padanuni, padanuni to get | wet, be chilly; padampu moisture; moist, wet; padampuni to moisten, wet; pasifkara dampness, wetness; paspu wet. Te. padanu, padunu moisture, dampness, wetness, a quantity of rain sufficient to render the earth fit for ploughing. Kui pete soft and damp; pete pete inba to be soft and damp; bede, bedi damp, moist, soft, wet; bede bede inba to be damp, moist, soft, wet. Br. paddam inflation, distension. Cf. 4505 Ma. potirkka, esp. for the meaning development of Br. DED(S, N) 3227.

Ta. patam proper consistency, re- quired degree of hardness or softness, quality of fitness, cooked rice, sharpness (as of the edge of a knife), tender grass. Ma, patam the right degree of ripeness, temperature, etc. Ko. padm (obi, padt-) temper of iron. To. pod, po@il green (not dead; of tree, grass); 2? po@ foes pure milk. Ka. pada proper or good state or condition, proper degree or temperature, the seasoning of any food, the right degree of ripeness, keenness of edge or sharpness; padanu (K.”), hadanu proper- ness, etc.; padula well-being, prosperity, happiness, tranquillity, security, safety; padu- liga a happy man; padulir to cheer up, become tranquil; padulisu to become well or happy, be calmed or soothed, feel refreshed, recover one’s strength; hasa niceness, comeliness, charm, beauty, good manner, state, or order, purity; hhasana, hasanu beauty, etc. Tw. patafigely harmony, fitness; pada abatement, patar [349] pattu subsidence, tranquillity; padana, padanappa well, nicely; hada the proper condition of things, sharpness (as of a knife), forming (as metals) to proper degree of hardness, boiling or cooking to the proper point. Te. padanu, padunu sharpness, ripeness, maturity, temper. Go. (ASu.) padna@ sharpness. Konda padnu being ready for use (as oilseed being preparéd for pressing), sharpening (of knife by heating and hammering). Kuw? (Isr.) padnu middle- aged (neither too much or too little); padnu 4- to have drunk (wine), not too much or too little, padnu met- to fla

Ta. patar chaff, husk, empty ears of grain; emptiness, worthlessness, worthless person; (-pp-, -tt-) to become useless; patati chaff, blighted grain, husk, futility. Ma. patir empty corn-husk, chaff; patirikka rice to be without grain. Ka, hadir a very young, quite unripe fruit. DED 3229.
Ta. patalai large-mouthed pot. To. pa@s large, broad-mouthed clay pot. Go. (Mu.) patli cooking pot (Voc. 2104). Malt. patli cooking-pot. DED(S) 3230.

Ta. pataru (patari-) to be flurried, confused, be impatient, overhasty, hurry; patarram rashness, hurry; patattam trembling, agitation; patai (-pp-, -tt-) to throb (as in sympathy), flutter, quiver, be in agony, shake, be anxious; pata-pat-enal expr. signi- fying the trembling of the heart. Ma. pataruka to be precipitate, over-hasty, be confused; patarikka to cause confusion; pata boiling, throbbing, foam, froth; patekka to palpitate, boil up; agitate; patappu throbbing; patapata boiling hot, effervescing; patupata bubbling up. Ka. padaru to be overhasty, speak un- advisedly, talk nonsense; pade to desire, wish, be eager; padap(p)u eagerness, zeal, pleasur- able excitement. Te. padaru, paduru, (K, also) padaru, padur(u)cu, padrucu to be overhasty or precipitate, be angry, move, be shaken; n. a hasty word; padafamu, padafu precipi- tation, hurry. Malt. padrare, padkare to prattle. DED(S) 3231.

Ta. pati (-v-, -nt-) to be imprinted, indented, be depressed, sunk, be low-lying (as land), sink in, penetrate, be absorbed (as the mind), perch, occupy, settle, alight (as a bird), be low (as price); insert, inlay, pave; Cpp-, -tt) to imprint, stamp, infix, insert, inlay, excavate, plant; n. penctration, sapling for transplantation, abode; town, city, village; patippu imprinting, indentation; patiyam sapling planted, graft; pativu impression, depression, permanence, custom, sapling. Ma. pati being fixed in, pressed down; patikka to impress, fasten on; patiyuka to be im- pressed, be pressed down; pativu settlement, osm. Malt. pature to take root. DED(S) . 3912 Ta. patuiku (patunki-) to hide, lie in’ ambush, crouch, disappear; patukku (patukki-) to hide (t7.), secrete; patukkam skulking, crouching; pativu stooping, crouch- ing. Ma. patuffuka, patiyuka to sneak, crouch; patukkuka to conceal; patukkam concealment, skulking; pati an ambush. Ko. padgg- (padngy-) to lie crouching for a spring. Ka. padugu to bend (intr.), bow. Tu. padeyuni to sink down, become invisible; padafkara in a stooping posture; padduni to sneak, crouch, hide, lurk. Malt. patgre to take refuge or shelter. Cf. 3810 Pa. pakk-. DED (S) 3233.
Ta. patutku. DEDS 589.

Ko. padn Celtis tetranda. To. poOn id. DED 3234. 3914 Kur. badnd to obey, defer to, refer the case to. Malt, badye to submit, be obedient. DED 3235. 3915 Ka, hadi a layer of stone or brick in the ground; (Gul.) pedra a layer. Tu. padury id., stratum. DEN 53. ° 3916 Kur. patta’anad to believe, admit. Malt. patyare to believe in. DEDS 616. 3917 Kur. bata bamboo laths, bamboo slips. Malt. bate thin slips of bamboo. Cf.

Ta. pattu, paktu ten; pak-pattu 100 (10 X 10); patt onpatu nineteen; patin- ten (in 11, 13, 15-18; patinalu, patindnku four- teen; patin ayiram 10,000); pann ifranfu, pannan{u twelve; -patu ten (irupatu, iruvatu twenty, muppatu thirty, narpatu, nappatu forty; aimpatu, ampatu fifty, arupatu sixty, erupatu seventy, enpatu eighty); patinmar ten persons; pappattu by tens, group of tens. Ma. pattu (obi. patin-) ten; -patu id. (in 20, etc.); patuppattu by tens; (Shanmugam) patinmar ten persons. Ko. pat ten; pad- id. (in 13, 14); padn- id. (in 15-18); pan- id. (in 11-18); patrmba-d, potrmba-d nineteen; pand eleven; -pon- ten (conservative speech; in 11, 12, 14-18); pod- id. (conservative speech; in 13); po calg ten calg measures; -vat ten (ivat twenty, na+lvat forty, ayvat fifty, arvat sixty elvat seventy); -bat id. (embat eighty, tombat ninety); -at id. (muat thirty, na-lat forty); patat by tens; irva-d twenty; orba+d nine; mu pa-+d thirty days; na-r pa-d forty days. To. pot ten; pon- id. (in 11-13); pa-ng fourteen; pusj fifteen; pa-r sixteen; paew seventeen; pu-f eighteen; pu-nbo@ nineteen; -foO ten (ifo8 twenty, 6wfo8 seventy, OSffo8 eighty); -po@ id. (mupo® thirty, natpoO forty); -bo6 id. (ebod fifty, e»nbo@ ninety); -o@ id. (aro@ sixty). Ka, pattu ten; patt ombattu nineteen; padin- ten (in 15-18); padi- id. (in 13, 14); pann- id. (in 11, 2); padimbar ten persons; (PBh.) payin-, in: payin-chasirvar 10,000 persons; -vattu. ten (mivattu thirty, nalvattu forty, ayvattu fifty, aruva
Te. paddu item, entry in an account. Pa, poddu thing, item, DED 3237.
Ta. pantam torch, lamp. Ma. pantam torch. Tu, pantyé small lamp. DED 3238.

Ta. pantayam contest for prize, stake, wager, prize. Ma, pantayam a stake. Ko, pandiegm (ob! pandi+gt-) race, contest. To. padym (obi. padyt-, padym-, pady-) race. Ka. panta, pandya bet, wager. Tu. panta id. Te. pantamu id.; pandemu id., race; panta- gincu to bet. Kuwi (F.) pandem koiyl game- cock; pandemi dossali to cock-fight. /B. Ramachandra Rao (PBh., p. 423) derives these words from Skt. panita- a wager. DED (S) 3239.

Ta. pantar, pantal pandal, store- house, arbour. Ma. pantal temporary shed of leaves or wickerwork erected esp. for marriages or feasts; pantalikka to spread like an arbour, be shady. Ka. pandar, pandara, pandal temporary shed of leaves, cloth, etc., erected for marriages, etc.; trellis for vines or creepers. Kod. panda temporary structure built for wedding or festival, pandal. Te. pandiri, pandili shed made of leaves or wickerwork, trellis for vines or creepers to run or spread over, a frame; padali a shed. Kol. pandri pandal, bath-house. Konda pandri pandal. Kuwi (S.) pandiri id.; sr.) pandri_ roof structure with thatch, small hut. DED(S) 3240.
Tu. pandi reserved, cautious. Te. padilamu care, caution. DEDS 618.
Ta. pantu ball used in play, roll as of string or thread; paintu ball. Ma. pantu ball of wood or pith, clew of thread. Te. banti a ball to play with, a ball in general. DED(S) 3241.
Ka. handu, hendu to move, shake, draw back (intr.). Tu. panduni to shake, be loose, oscillate, vibrate. DEDS 619.
Ka. pande a coward. Te. panda id.; cowardly, base. DEDS 620.

Ta. pappatam, appalam wafer of flour of black gram with bicarbonate of soda. Ma. pappatam id. Ka. pappadi, pappala, appada, appala id. Tu. pappada, pappala, appada, appala a very thin cake made of pulse, salt, etc. Te. appadamu, appalamu a sort of thin cake. /Cf. Skt. parpata- thin cake made of rice or pease-meal and baked in grease; Turner, CDJAL, no. 7934. DED 3243.
Ka. happu a piece of flesh. Kod. pappi id- to share out game (or with 3936). Tu, pappu carcass, carrion. DEDS 621.
Ta. pamparam top. Ma. pamparam top for play. /?< Skt. bhramaraka- hum- ming-top. DED 3244.
Ka. pambal ardent desire, solicitous or grievous thought; pambalisu to desire ardently, be full of solicitous or painful thought. Tu. hambalu solicitous thought. DEDS 622.
Ta. pampu (pampi-), pammu (pam- mi-) to sound; pampal sound; pampai a kind of drum or tabor. Te, pamba a kind of drum. DED 3245.

Ka. hambe the core or interior stalk of the plantain tree, of which curries and other things are made, (Hav.) skin of banana stem. Tu. bambu layers of the fibrous cover- ings of a plantain tree; bambé the core or inside stalk of a plantain tree; (B-K.} pambe, bambu, bambe layer of fibrous covering of a plantain tree.

Ta. pammu (pammi-) to baste, pin a seam to be sewed; pammal basting. Ka. hammu to make ropes. DEDS 623, 3935 Kol. pamme breast. Nk. pamme id. Nk. (Ch.) pomme breast, teat. Go, (Ma.) bomi (pl. bo?ku), (M.) boma, (Ko.) boma, (L.) bomo, (S.) bommu (pl. bomku) breast; (A.) bom (ol bomk) udder (Voc. 2631); (LuS.) bommoo breast. DED (S) 3246.

Ta. payal half, share. Ka. paficu, pasu (pacc-) to divide, separate, part, dis- tribute, share; be divided, etc.; pacci, paccu part, portion; pasuge dividing, separation, division; pampa equal share or division. Kod. pappi ig- to share out game (or with 3929), Tu. pasaly the share of the fisherman; haficuni to distribute, divide. Te. pancu to distribute, divide; pampu share;pampakamu distribution, division. Kol (Kin.) pay-, (SR.). paiy- to divide. Nk. payk- to distribute. Pa. payp- (payt-) to share. Ga. (S.) pay- to divide into shares; (S.>) payp- (payup-) to distribute. Pe. paspa (-t-) to divide, distribute. Kui pahpa paya [351] para (paht-) to share, apportion; n. apportionment; pahari part, share, portion. Cf. 3808 Ta. paku and #089 Tu. papafé, Te. payu. DED(S, N)

Ta. paya (-pp-, -nt-) to yield, produce, put forth fruit, be productive; payappu profit, advantage; payam id., fruit; payantdr parents. Tu. paya, payi an ear of rice, etc.; payakely, payac(c)ely the time of shooting of the ears of corn; payat&ye a thriving man; pay¥vuni to shoot, as an car of corn; paya gain, profit; coming into existence, being delivered of a child (as among pariahs); = paya. DEDS 624.
Ta. payam tank; payampu depression, hollow, pit, pit to ensnare elephants, kheda, tank, pond. Tu. bayambu a hole, hollow; adj. hollow. DEDS 625.

Ta. payal, paiyal, paital, pacal boy; paiyan boy, son; paical small boy, urchin; pacalai infancy, tenderness; (PN) payalai young one. Ma. paital, pasakan child; pagun- fal children. Ko. pay] young grain plant (not paddy), child. Ka. pasula, pasule child; pasuletana childhood; haykalu.. male or female children; hayda a boy; (K.”) peyya call Kod. pajja Holeya girl. Tu. pasi boy, child; paiyya child; paiyyé, paiyery, paiyye child, boy; Pariah’s child. Ror (Te PSB 9.45 payali child. Te. paida boy, child; paidali woman; peyya, péya calf, female calf, heifer. Nk. (Ch.} paya, piya calf. Pa. peyya id. Ga. (Oll.) pépap young calf;.(S.) bedda-peyya young cow (bedda female); '(S.3) peyye calf. Go. (L.) pedi girl; peda id., child; pedal child, children; (Y.) pédal son, boy; (Ma.) pédi girl; (S.) pédal boy, son, etc. (Voc. 2360); (many dialect forms, e.g.) pékur, pékor, pékal boy; poki, pikir girl (Voc 2357); (Tr.) paiyd, (A.) piya, (Pat.) peyya calf of cither sex, etc. (Voc. 2338). Konda peya calf (of cow). Cf. 3821 Ta. paca. DED N) 3248. ’

Ko. baga+l conspicuous (of a view from a high place), wide-open (of a door). Ka. bayal, bayil, baylu conspicuousness, celebrity. Tu. bayily, baily adj. public. Te. bayalu outside, exterior, sky, publicity; clear, evident, public, known. Go. (Ko.) bayul open space of ground; (M.) bail plain (Voce. 2502). Konda baylu open space. Kuwi (Su.) baylu maidan; (Isr.) bayalu field. Cf.

Ta. payaru green gram, Phaseolus mungo. Ma. payar(u) leguminous plants. Ka. pesar green gram. Tu. padengi, padefiji id. Te. pesalu id.,; pesara the green gram plant; belonging to or made of green gram. Kol (SR.) pesal, (Kin.) pesal green gram. Nk. pesal id. Pa. payari (pl. payarul), (S.) payal id. Go. (A.) pesel, (Y. G. Ma. S.) pesel, (Mu.) pasel, (Ko.) pesli id. (Voc. 2354). Kuwi (Su.) pes (pl. -ya) id. Cf. 3821 Ta. paca. DED(S)

Ta. payil (payilv-, payiny-) to become trained, accustomed, practise, learn by practice (as an art); 1. practice, habit; payirci practice, training, learning, familiarity; payirru (payirri-) to train, habituate, teach. Ma. payiluka to learn; payarruka to exercise in arms, practise; payarrikka to teach the use of arms, outwit; payarru fencing exercise, a trick. DED 3251,

Ta. payil (payily-, payinr-) to speak, utter, tell, talk, utter indistinct sound (as bird), call; n. word, secret language, cant; payirru (payirri-) to speak, utter, tell; payir (-v-, -nt-) to cry as beasts or birds, summon, call, sound as instruments; n. cry of animals, etc., sound, musical instrument, cant. Ma. payiluka to speak. DEDS 626, and from DED(S) 3251.
Ta. payini a kind of tree peculiar to hilly tracts. Ka, payne, bayne, bagini the bastard sago palm, Caryota urens. Tu. baina, baini id. DEDS 627.

Ta. paiya by degrees, slowly, gently; paya-ppaya slowly; (PV) pai gently; paipaya be gentle. Ma. payyagently, secretly. Ko. parym, paym slowly, silently, doing nothing; pa-ym ba-ym, pasyma+ym very slowly, very gradu- ally. Tu. payyana gently, softly, quietly. Te. pai pai, paipaiga, paipaina, paipaini easily, lightly, superficially. DED (S) 3252.
Ma. payyatti a fish. Tu, paiyyé a kind of fish. DED 3253. payy
Ma. payyana, payyani Bignonia longi- folia. Tu. paiyyané id. DED 3254.

Ta. para (-pp-, -nt-) to spread, be diffused, be flattened (as by hammering), be broad (as a plane surface); parakku (parakki-) to roam about; parafftai spreading (as of shrubs); parattu (paratti-) to spread (tr); parappu (parappi-) to spread (fr.; as grain), diffuse (as odour), disseminate (news), expand, establish, give lavishly; n. expanse, extension, space, diffused or extended state, diffusion, mass, sea, bed; parampu (parampi-) to spread (intr.), expand, pervade, be spread out or become flattened; n. roller for smooth- ing land newly ploughed; bamboo mat; pampu (pampi-) to be close, thick, crowded, be full, spread (as vegetation, water, darkness); pampal expanse, spaciousness, luxuriance, abundance, harvest; pammu (pammi-) to be close, thick, crowded; paravu (paravi-) to spread (intr.); lay open to view (as goods in a bazaar); paravai expanse, breadth, sea, that which is spread (as grain on floor); par ex- panse, earth, rock, rocky stratum, shelf of rock; param, pari earth; parar people of the earth. Ma. parakka to spread, be diffused, extended, become large, become public; -paranna broad, extended; parattuka to park [352] spread (tr.), flatten, level, divulge, proclaim; parappu spreading (of sound, disease, branches), extent, breadth, publicity, sea; parampu bamboo mat; paraya sea; paravuka to spread; par the earth, world. Ko. pard- (pardy-) (small objects) spread over large space, (news) spreads; part- (party-) to spread (grain, chillies) in sun to dry. To. par- (parO
Ko. park poverty; parkn poor man; fem. parky, parkc. Tu. paraki, parki, parkisy, parkely destitute, wretched. DED 3256.

Ta. paracu (paraci-) to praise, extol). paravu (paravi-) id., worship, reverence, adore, sing; paraval praising, worshipping. Ma. parikka a vow. Ko. parc- (parc-) to pray; parkym (obl. parkyt-) vow to a god, prayer. paratar To. paxt- (party-) to pray; arkym (obi. arkyt-) vow. Ka. parasu to utter a benediction, bless; parake, harake, harike benediction, vow. Kod. parake vow. Tu. parasy benediction; paraké vow made in trouble, beseeching; harasuni to bless, wish well. DED(S) 3257.
Ta. paratu, parantai, (Ramnad dial., Annamalai, p. 875) perante ankle. Ka. paradu id.; haradi id., wrist. DED(S, N) 3258.

Ta. paran, parani watch-tower, upper story, (also paranai) rack over a fireplace, loft under the roof of a house. Ma. paran, parana loft under the roof, rude ceiling, frame for drying something over the fireplace. Tu. parefiji, pareji scaffold, temporary platform. DED 3259.

Ta. parani jewel casket, small box, a kind of jar. Ma. parani vase, jar. Ka. barani small box, casket, China jar or vase. Kod. barani large clay pot with fairly narrow mouth,, for storing ghee, pickle, etc. Tu. barani, barané china jar. Te. barine small box, case or casket. /Cf. Sgh. baraniya pot (Turner, CDIAL, no. 9395). DED 3260.

Tu. paranty, poranty bull-frog. Kol. pande, (SR. also) pande frog, toad; biceps. Nk. pande frog; muscle, biceps. Nk. (Ch.) pande frog. Pa. benda id. Ga. (OH.) pandake id. Go. (Tr. SR. W. Ph.) panné, (Mu.) panne, (Ph.) pangal (n, not a), (Ma. Ko.) pande id. (Voc. 2118); (Ch. Ma.) panne, (A. Y.) penne muscle (Voc. 2119); (ASu.) penné frog. Pe. pana id. Kui pana frog, toad. Kuwi (S. Su.) panna, (Isr.) pana, (D.) betka frog. Kur. bharnda bull-frog. Cf. 1224 for ‘frog; biceps’. DED (S) 3242, 3261.

Ma. paratuka to seek groping, chiefly at night. Ka. paradu to scrape together with the hand or a hoe, (PBh.) to grope about, (Hav.) id. Kod. baraj- (baraji-) to feel for (something), grope. Tu. paraduni to grope, paranf{uni to grasp, seize, lay hold of; arantuni to grope for, grasp, seek, look for; aranfele a greedy person, miser; arafuni to scramble, fumble; arantuni to feel, grope, grasp at; (? forms with initial a- from Ka. dial.). Kor. (O.) barati to grope; (M.) haji to search. Te. (K.) pakul-ddu to grope with hands, as in darkness. Go. (Tr.) parkana, parkana, pirkana, (ChD.) parkand, (Mu. Ma.) park-, (SR.) parrand, (L.) parkanad, parahkana, (Pat.) pahakana to search (Voc. 2142). Konda parbi- (-t-) to feel with hands, grope, search in darkness by groping. Pe. prek- (-t-) to seek. Kui parmba (parmbi-) to grope, search blindly; n. groping. Kuwi (F.) parali, (S.) parinai, (Isr.) par- (-it-) to search; (F.) prakali to grope; (Isr.) park- (-it-) to feel one’s way in the dark; prek- (he) to search for lice in the hair. ? Kur. panjna to trace, track. ? Br. patting to search for. DED(S, N) 3262.

Ta. paratar, paratavar, paravar_ in- habitants of a maritime tract, fishing tribes. Ma. paravan dwellers on the seacoast, a caste param [353] of fishermen; fem, paratti. Tu. parave a man belonging to a low class of devil-dancers. / ? Cf. Skt. bharata- barbarian, mountaineer (= Sabara-). DED(S) 3263.

Ta. param armour for the body; a kind of shield; paricai shield, buckler. Ma. param shield; parica a round shield. Ka, parige, harige shield. / ? < Skt. phara-, pharaka-, phalaka- shield; Turner, CDJAL, no. 9054. DED 3264.

Ta. paral pebble, seed, stone of fruit; parukkai, parukkai-kkal small pebbles; paru- manal grit, sandstone. Ma. paral grit, coarse grain, gravel, cowry shell. Ko. parl pebble, one grain (of any grain). Ka. paral, paral pebble, stone; haralu castor oil plant; haral- epne, (Hal.) hallenne castor oil. Kod. para pebble. Tu. parely grain of sand, grit, gravel; grain of corn, etc.; castor seed; pareldenné, (B-K.) parelenne castor oil; par(a)pu a sand- bed. Kol. (Kin.) parca gravel. For Ka. Tu. enne, etc., see 854. Cf. 209 Ta. aralai. / Cf. Sgh. boralu gravel, pebbles. DED(S) 3265, DEN 55:
Ta. (Ag.) paravey a kind of fish. Ma. parava id.

Ko. payrv big storage pot for grain; kopayrv, kopi-rv id. (but bigger than payrv; does ko- belong with 2146 Ta. kogu?). To. parfy large earthen pot for carrying or boiling water. Ka, paravi carthen water-vessel of the form of the koda but larger. DED 3266. 3962 (a) Ta. pari (-v-, -nt-) to separate, be sundered, break off, be destroyed, cut asunder, destroy; (-pp-, -tt-) to cut asunder; parufiku (parunki-) to pluck (as fruit), tear off. ‘Ma, parikku, parukku scratch, wound, scar; paritnuka to pluck grass; pariffial scratched surface, hurt skin. Ko. p bay (bi-) house with roof and walls broken from disuse; pary gik broken basket used for carrying rubbish. Tu. paripuni to tear, rend; pariyuni to rend; parté, partely rent, tear (as in clothes); torn, tattered, old; partele man in rags; parluni to cut sharply, as a knife. Te. B.) pariyu to wear away, be spoiled, (plastering) falls away in shreds. Kol. part- (paratt-) to cut up. Pa, parng- to be split; parkip- (parkit-) to split, plough for the first time; parka piece, portion, split piece of wood. Ga. (P.) -parinp- (parint-) to split (firewood with axe). Konda (BB 1972) para crumb, fragment. Kui paja (paji-) to hew, cut down; parpa (part-) to cut through the bank of a wet field, irrigate; n. irrigation; pripka (< pyrak-p-; prakt-) to split open, cut open, tear open. Kuwi (Isr.) par- (-h-) to dig a ditch, (also parl- [-it-]} make channel for water, place for well; par(a)za split (in wood, wall, etc.), gap, hole. Kur. paraxna to sep

Ta. pari (-v-, -nt-) to run, go out, escape; (-pp-, -tt-) to run, proceed; 7, motion, speed, rapidity, pace of a horse, a horse; parippu motion; pariyal going fast. Ma. pari horse. To, pary- (parc-) (horse) gallops; ride at a gallop. Ka. pari, hari to run, flow, pro- ceed (as work), go away, disappear, be dis- charged (as a debt); m. moving, running, flowing, stream; parike, pariya running; parivu id., flowing, current. Tu. pariyuni, hariyuni to run, flow; aryuni to flow, run (as water); parapuni to flow (as water); parapu flowing, running; ? pari path, way; ) payyery horse; payyoly, payyoli id., riding, galloping; bayyely running, riding; bayyoli a walk. Te. parugu, paruvu, parvu running, a run; paruvidu to run; (K.) parapu to cause to flow; paru to run, run away, have a motion of the bowels, purge, (K. also) fly (or with

Ta. pari (-v-, -nt-) to be affectionate; n. love, affection; parivu affection, love, devotion, piety,. delight, pleasure. Ma. parivu love. Ka, paraliga paramour. Te. perima love, affection, DED(S) 3269.
Ta. pari (-v-, -nt-) to be troubled, distressed, suffer; 1. trouble, distress; parippu distress, sorrow; (PN) paravaral distress. Ma. parifinal griet, distraction. DED 3270.

Ta. pari delusion, deception. Ma. pariffhuka to be perplexed; parifthikka to confound. DED 3271. 3967 Kol. pari- (parit-), (SR.) pariy- to grind (flour); (Kin.) paray- to pound. Nk. paray- to grind. Vk. (Ch.) paray- id.; padgare a stone mill for grinding. DED(S) 3272.

Ta. paricu quality, nature; manner, way, method. Ma. paricu nature, manner; proper manner; pari way, manner. Ka. pariju, (K.?) parije manner; form, shape; pari way, manner, kind; (K.?) parice quality, nature. Te. parusu way, manner; pari manner. / Cf. Mar. pari kind, type, manner, style, etc. DED 3273. 3969 Ta, parificu hilt of a sword. Ma. parififiu id. Te. parfju id. DED 3274, pariyam

Ta. pariyam, paricam brideprice, hire of a prostitute; paricil gift, donation, present; paricilar solicitors of gifts; paricu gift, do- nation, present, boon. Ma. pariyam token given by bridegroom to bride. /J. Filliozat, Journal asiatique 251.269, would derive these items from Skt. sparsga-, Pkt. pharisa- touch. DED 3275.

Ta. aruvanpam copper tray. Ka. pariyana, pariyala, parivana, parydna, hari- yana, harivina, arivana a plate-like vessel of metal or wood. Ju. hariyana, harivana, arivana metal dish. Te. arivinamu a kind of dish, bowl, or plate. /Cf. Mar. pariel plate. DED 3276.

Ta. paru (-pp-, -tt-) to become large, bulky, plump, swell; adj. large, bulky (in: parun-kufal large intestine, etc.; cf. 1652); parukk-en- to be thick, bulky; parukkai becoming bulky; paruppu thickness, large- ness; parumam thickness, bulkiness, largeness; parumai thickness, bulkiness, corpulence, greatness; seriousness, importance, gravity; paruval anything large or thick; pararai large trunk of a tree; pari greatness, height; pariya thick, large, big; pariydn a great per- son, a being of great importance; par bulk, size; pari that which is heavy or big, that which is important; man of consequence, weight or importance; (-pp-, -tt-) to be bulky, huge; increase, expand. Ma. paru gross, big: parukka to grow bulky, stout; paruma gross- ness. To. par meen tree with huge trunk. Ke. hari, hariba a mass, multitude; bardu increase, greatness; barda a great person, a virtuous person. Tu, pariya plenty, exceed- ing, much. Te. pambu to increase, grow; pammu to increase; prabbu to increase, extend, flourish, thrive. Konda pabli- (-t-) to hatch (chickens as a fowl); grow in num- bers (as chickens hatched). Kuwi pada 4- (Su.) to grow up, become big, (Isr.) grow, increase; (Su. Isr.) padi ki- to make to grow; (F.) pada-aiyali, (S.) padda Anai to grow. Kur, pardna to grow in number, increase in quantity, grow in size or age, prosper, succeed, thrive. Mait. pathre to grow. DED (S, N) 3277.

Ta. paru coarse, rough; parukkan, parumai roughness, coarseness, grossness. Ma. parukku rough surface; (Tiyya) paruk- kuva to make rough; parupara roughly, harshly; paruparukka to be rough, harsh; paruparuppu roughness, harshness. Ka. patige, parparike, papparike roughness (of surface), harshness, rough manners. Te. baraka rough; barusu rough, coarse, rude, brutal; roughness, rudeness; parusamu, parusana harshness, cruelty; parusapu hard, harsh; berasu cruel, rough, not fine. Kol (Pat., p. 115) bargafe rough. Konda baRka roughness; rough. /? Cf. Skt. parusa- rough, hard, harsh. DED (S, N) 3278. _ 3974 Ta. paru pimple, pustule, boil; parukk- en- to blister. Ma. paru boil, ulcer. To. pop [354] parukkai boil. Kui parngoli sore on lip or tongue. Kuwi (Isr.) baresi pimple. DEDS 629, from DED 3277.

Ta. paruku (paruki-) to drink, eat, devour, enjoy; parukal drinking, liquid food; parukku (parukki-) to cause to drink. Ma. parukuka to drink, sip, nibbic; kiss, enjoy. Tu. parpini to drink, sip; take food (as a Pariah); parpavuni to cause to drink; par- pata drinking; parpele drunkard; paruvely food, meal (as of a Pariah). / 2? Cf. Mar. parguné a meal, feast. DED(S) 3279.

Ta. parutti Indian cotton plant, Gossypium herbaceum; cotton; param cotton plant. Ma, parutti cotton. To. pasty wick. Ka, parti, parti, patti cotton in the pod, cotton in general. Kod. parati cotton cloth. Tt. parti cotton. Te. p(rjatti id. Go. (Ko.) part id. (Voc. 2138). Kui parti id. Kuwi (Su.) pratti, (Isr.) parti, (F.) parti id) DED (S, N) 3280.

Ta. paruntu common kite; (Tinn.) perintu, (CTD) perandu vulture. Ma. paruntu, parantu kite, Accipiter nists. Ko. pad kite. To. paQ bird of prey (including kite, eagle, vulture). Ka. pardu, paddu kite, hawk, falcon, vulture. Kod. paddi eagle, vulture, kite. Te. panta-gadda_ vulture, cagle; (B.) bandu vulture; ra-bandu eagle, (K.) vulture (? ra- < raja king, ie. big). Go. (SR.) riya! vulture (Voce. 3025; ? borrowing from Te. r&-bandu). DED(S, N) 3281.

Ta. paruppu soft or inner Kernel of beans, almonds, etc., Kernel of Cajanus indicus (dal). Ma, patippu peas, pulse skinned in water, halved and dried; kernel of corn. Ka. pappu split pulse of any kind, split almonds, etc. Te. pappu split pulse, dal, kernels of. any kind freed from the shell.
Ta. parumal yardarm. Ma. parima- ram yard of a ship. DED 3283.

Ko. par par in- (id-) (country, na-+r) becomes a little light before dawn. Kea. pare to dawn. Te. pardgu to shine. Kui pari inba to dawn, break (dawn); 1. early dawn. Malt. parce to shine brightly, be seen clearly, DED 3284.

Ka. pare coat of an onion, film on the eye, slough of a snake, web of a spider, lamina, dry scab, dandruff; pari spider’s web. Tu. paré cataract or web on the eye. Go. (Ko.) barka membrane (Voc. 2505). Cf.

Ta. parukkai, porukku single grain of boiled rice; perukkal rice; poruku boiled rice. Te. pralu rice. Nk. /Ch.) perku (pl.) husked rice. Pa. peruk (pl. perkul) id. Go. (Tr.) par@k husked rice, kutki, etc.; (Ch.) parek husked (of rice); (Ph.) paraik, (W.) paraik, (A. Y.) perek husked rice; (SR.) pere, (S.) pariku rice; (Mu.) parem (pl. parek) grain baraxnd [355] para (of rice, etc.) (Voce. 2135). Konda per(u) (pl. perku) husked rice. Pe. preyi id: Mand. preyi id. Kui prau rice, husked paddy; prama a grain of boiled rice; koruvrau, kdruvau flaked rice (-p- > -y- in cpd.). DED(S) 3286. _ 3983 Kur. baraxnid to snore. Malt. barge id. DED 3287.

To. par par side by side; par pair (in songs; e.g. pax aes foes a pair of house en- trances). Kod. para side (e.g. appara that side, ké-kié para castern side). ? Cf. 4541 Ta. poru, DEDS 630. 3985 Kui parpi_ hornet. Kuwi (F.) prari {ie prati) wasp; (Isr.) parri a wild bee. DEDS 3986 (a) Ta. pal tooth; pallan man with long or large teeth; palli woman with long or large teeth; a kind of harrow; palaki a kind of harrow; paluku harrowing. Ma. pal, pallu tooth; pailan one who has large or peculiar teeth. Ko. pal tooth. To. pas id. Ka. pal id; palla elephant; halive, halaku, halike, halube, haluve a toothed harrow. Kod. palli tooth. Tu. paru animal’s tooth. Te. palu, pallu (pl. pandlu), pannu (back-formation from pl.) tooth; (VPK) palugu blade of pickaxe, crow- bar, etc.; (Sank.) palu-gorru a kind of harrow (gorru harrow, drill-plough). Kol. pal tooth, esp. front tooth. Vk. pal tooth. Vk. (Ch.} pal (p/. -ku/-gu) id. Pa, pel id. Ga. (Olt) pal id., sharp edge (usually pl.); (S.) pall (pL palkil) tooth. Go. (Tr. W. Mu. etc.) pal id.; (Ph.) pal, val id. (Voc. 2159). Konda pal id. Mand. pal (pl. -ke) id. Kui padu (pl. patka), palu (pl. palka) id. Kuwi (E.) palla (pl palka), (S.) pallu, (Su. P.) pallu (pl. palka), (Isr.) palu (pl. palka) id. Kur. pall id. Malt. palu the teeth. DED(S) 3288. (5) Tu. parkad(d)i toothpick. Te. (K.) palugarra toothstick. Pa, perkal, perkela twig used as a toothbrush. Ga. (8.3) palkara toothbrush. Go. (G. Mu. Ko.) parkal, (Ph.) palkar, (Ma.) pelkiag toothtwig (

Ta. pala many, several, diverse; palar many or several persons, assembly, society; pallar Many persons; panmai plurality, multitude; pal many (OTa., only in cpds., e.g. pal-kalum many times, often, pan-malai many hills; Zvelebil 1977, p.63). Ma. pala many, several, various. To. pes a large number, many. Ka, pala, palavu much, many, several, various; palar, palambar, palavar several persons; palarme several times. Te. palu many, several, various, different ;paluvuru, paluvundru many persons. Malt. palware to be multiplied, be bred; palwatre to breed, rear. DED 3289.

Ta. palavu, pala, pila jack-tree, Artocarpus integrifolia. Ma. pilavu, plavu id.; pla-kkayi jack-fruit. Ko. palasv jack- fruit. To. pasof id. Ka. panasa, palasa, palasu id. Kod. palaci id. Tu. pila, pela A. integri- folia; ? peja A. pubescens, wild jack. Kor. (T.) halta mara jackfruit tree. Te. panasa jack-fruit. Pa. penac id. Ga. (OML) panis id. Kuwi (F.) parha, (S) panha, (Isr.) panha id. /Cf. Skt. panasa-, palasa-, phanasa-, phalasa- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7781. DED 3989 Ka, (Hal.) halu thinned. Te. paluca thin, not thick (applied to a solid or a liquid), tare, not dense, not close, sparse; light, slight, contemptible; palucana thinness, etc. Konda alsa fin fof jiquid); palsay thinly, sparsely. ur. pelpel@ very thin, transparent. D (N) 634. P P EDS

Te. palupu halter for cattle. Konda (BB) palpu rope attaching bullock to plough. Kuwi (F.) palpt straw rope. /Cf. Skt. (lex.) palapa- halter, rope. DEDS 635. 3991 Kur. palkna to cut lengthwise, split, crack (the earth, a wall), chap (the hands, etc.). Malt. palke to cut up (as fruit or vegetable). ? Pa. palva split piece of wood. DED 3291. 3992 Kur. palxanja, (Hahn also) parxanja cucumber. Malt. palangje id. DED 3292.

Ta. palli wall-lizard, Lacerta gecko, Ma. palli house-lizard, L. gecko. Ko. e-paj small wall-lizard. To. pasy lizard. Ka. palli small house-lizard, L. gecko, Kod. palli lizard. Tu, palli house-lizard. Te. palli, balli lizard. Go. (Y.) pal lizard; (Ko.) bal dok(k)e house lizard (Voe. 2160). Konda bali tondo house-lizard (fondo chameleon). /ce. Skt. palli-, pallika- lizard, Mar. palli, pal id; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7973. DED(S) 3294,
Tu. pallé spleen. Te. balla enlarge- ment of the spleen. Pa. bella spleen. Kuwt (P.) baila, (F.) bella, (T.) bela id. DEDS 636.

Pa. palla, pala seedlings. Ga. (S.7) palle rice seedling. Konda pala (pl. palen) Seedlings for transplantation. Pe. para seedling. Kui plaha id. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) pala, (T.) pala rice seedling. /Cf. Halbi pala seedling, and Tumer, CDJAL, no. 7969, pallava-. DEDS (N) 640. eo Ka. bavara battle, war. Te. bavaramu id.

Ta. pavayam, pavalam, palakam red coral. Ma. pavagam, paviram coral. Ka. pavala id. Kod. paluva, pavva id. Tu. pakala, pakala, pavala, pavala id. Te. pagadamu, pavadamu id. Kuwt (S.) pagnelu id. / Cf. Skt. pravala-, pravada- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8794. DED 3999 Ta, paga old; paracu that which is old or damaged by time; param very aged person; paramai, pagaimai oldness, antiquity, that which is ancient, that which is old- fashioned, what is obsolete, old saying, long- established intimacy, décay from age, ancient paraku history, long-established custom; pagaitu, paraiyatu that which is old; paraiya old; paraiyar the ancients; pazaku (pagaki-) to be old; parutu anything rotten, state of being a corpse; pantu, panfai antiquity, former time. Ma. para old; parama oldness, old age, old times, acquaintance with customs, history; pagaya ancient, stale; pagavan old man, ancestor; pagakuka to grow old, be worn out, delay; parakkam oldness, delay; pantu antiquity. Ko. pay- (before vowel), pa- (before consonant) old; payd that which is old, which is of old, that was here always up to now. To. pa-w, pa-- (before con- sonants) old; paefn old (of things); pory stink of a corpse (or with 4312 Ta. pugu). Ka. paga, pare old, worn-out, ancient; pagatu that is old, worn out, faded or decayed; parasu to become old or worn out, decay, go to ruin, be spoiled; pageyam old man. Kod. pale old, old-fashioned (of a person); pale-di an old thing; palafijolli old saying; pandi antiquity, years past, for

Ta. paraku (paraki-) to practise, become initiated; become acquainted, be familiar; become fitted, tempered, whole- some (as a utensil or tool), become broken or trained (as an animal); pagakku (pagakki-) to train, domesticate; pagakkam training, exercise, use, habit, practice, custom; tame- ness, domesticity; pugafiku (pugafiki-) to- practise, exercise, -put to use; be conversant or acquainted, associate with; pugakkam familiarity, practice, use. Ma. pagakuka to be accustomed, acquainted; pagakkam long habit, acquaintance, experience; pagakkuka to practise anything, inure oneself to. Ko. palg- (palgy-) to become tame, become [356] : pagu used to a person’s ways, become amenable to suggestion for sexual intercourse; palk- (palky-) to tame; palkm (od/. palkt-) being on intimate terms; polgg- (polggy-) to be on intimate terms with, have interdining inter- course with. To. pa+g- (pa-gy-) to use (things, path); pa-k path, track. Ka. pagaka use, practice, habit, custom; pagagu to become used to, be trained; be in use; pagakisu, paragisu to accustom, make familiar by use, train; practise; use. From DED(N) 3296.

Ko. patpatn (tears are shed) in abundance. -? Ka. para, pagapaga sound in imitation of the rattling or pattering of the fall of loose or single things, as of hail-stones; paraparane with the repeated sound para; pala, palapala sound of rain or tears falling in big drops, of fruits falling in numbers when shaking a tree, of leaves agitated by wind; palapalane with the repeated sound pala. DEN 56. 4002 Ta, pari (-pp-, -tt-) to blame, censure, ridicule, revile, slander; #. blame, ridicule, slander, fault, sin, revenge; pagicai scorn; parippu scorn, blame, guilt, defect; pagutu defect, fault. Ma. pari fault, false accusation, deadly vengeance; pagikka to scold, blame, abuse. Ko. palc- (palc-) to abuse; paly retribu- tion; apr/lyk ipr/lyk onom. of scolding (as in; apryke- ipryke- (id)r mancuko- he scolded vigorously); ? payas¢ woman who has casual sexual intercourse. To. pa-k- (pa-ky-) to abuse. Ka. pagi to revile, scold, rebuke, scorn, upbraid, deride; 7. (also parivu) blame, rebuke, fault, guilt. Kod. pali (paltv-, paliiij-), pale- (palev-, paland-) to speak ill of; ? palame talk, gossip. Te. prayi blame; (K.) prabbu to abuse, blame, censure; 7. blame. Kur. payagnd (pargyas) to talk against one, detract, slander, tun one down, ? Cf. 4060 Go. pask-. DED(S, N) 3297.

Ta. pagiccu (paricci-) to praise, worship, bless, announce, tell; 7. praise, adoration. ?Ko. pard- (payd-/pardy-), ard- (ayd-/ardy-) to tell; paykm (obi. paykt-) a story; pasc- (pa+c-) (pair word with pare- to pray; see 3951). ? To. 68t- (G5ty-) to say, tell (story, lament) (or with 4430 Ta. p&cu); pa-ym (obi. pa-yt-) story of actual event. DED(S) 3298. 4004 7a. paxu (-pp-, -tt-) to ripen (as fruits, grain), grow mature, arrive at perfection (as in knowledge, piety), become old, come to a head (as a boil), change colour by age, become pate or yellowish (as the body by disease), become flexible, pliant; 7. (also paruppu) ripeness, yellowness (of fruits), leaf turned yellow with age; pagunu (paguni-), parunu (paguni-) to grow ripe, become mellow, mature, be full or perfect; pagam ripe fruit; pantam fruit. Ma. pagukka to grow ripe, become well-tempered, suppurate, decay; n. fruit put to ripen; paguppu ripening of fruit; paguppikka to ripen artificially; pagam ripe fruit, ripe plantains. Jr. pammu, paemu paru [357] ripe fruit. Ko. parv- (pard-) (fruits) become ripe, (boil, sore) opens; pan fruit. To. poSf- (post-) to ripen; pum, (in song) pan fruit; po-sf- (posst-) to yield fruit, (fruit) ripens. Ka. pan to be produced (ripe fruit); ». (also pannu) ripe fruit, ripeness. Kod. panni fruit. Tu. palkuni, palkuni to be very soft (as an overripe fruit), be pliant, flexible; parnduni to be ripe, mature, (hair) turns grey; parnda- vuni to ripen (¢r.); parndy ripeness, ripe fruit, ripe pl

Ta. pagu rib, side of the body. Ka. (PCV) paxke rib. Te. prakka side, part, quarter, direction. Konda parka side; parka dumu rib (dumu bone). Pe. parka side (as of river). Kuwi (Su. F.) parka, ([sr.) parka/praka armpit. /There has been convergence with loans from MIA pakkha- (DBJA 233a); cf. esp. Te. DEDS 637.
Ta. paguvam forest. Ma. paruvam id. Ka. paru, paruva forest, jungle. Te. (DCV) paduva forest. DED(S) 3300.

Ko. pak act of lying down, act of lying down and chewing cud; pak var- to lie down and chew cud (par- to lie); pak me+ki-- to rise up from chewing cud. To. oky sleeping- place, bedding (< Badaga). Ka. parke, pakke lying down, reposing; place for reposing, dormitory; cow-pen or herd of kine; hakke place for reposing, bed..7u. parké bedding; Pallam sleep, rest. Te. pakka bed, bedding. Cf. 3852 Ta. patu. DED(S) 3301.
Ka. palaiku, paluniku, palaficu to touch with force, strike or dash against, push. Tu. palenkuni to upset, overturn. DED 3302.

Ta. palapala (-pp-, -tt-) to glitter, shine (as hair well-oiled, a surface well- polished); palapalappu glittering, lustre; pala- palav-enal expr. signifying glittering; palicc- enal expr. signifying flashing, shining; palir- enal expr. signifying gleaming, flashing. Ma. palapala gleaming (with minnuka to shine); palunfuka to glitter. Ka. palakane, palacane, palaccane, palafice with a glitter, with pure brightness, with a flash; brightness, pureness, etc.; halacige flash, glitter, shine; (Gowda) pallange completely dawning. Tu. pallena light, shine; (B-K.} palapala term to indicate glittering. Te. palapala glitteringly; ? paluka, palukana paleness, whiteness. DED(S) 3303.

Ta. pala-palav-enal expr. signifying bursting sound; palir-enal expr. signifying cracking, crashing, clanging. Ma. palapala cracking, popping. Ka. palak sound produced when an earthen vessel with water breaks. Te. palapala cracklingly. DED 3304.

Ta. palappam, valappam a kind of soft stone used to write on wooden boards. Ma. valappam a sort of chalk. Ka, balipa, balaha a whitish pot-stone, Lapis allaris, frequently used (as a substitute for slate pencil, etc.) in writing. Tu. balapu, balavu potstone, glimmer, slate. Te. balapamu slate; balapapu pulla slate pencil. DED 3305.
Ta. patku (palki-), parku (parki-) to cower, crouch. Ma. palufinuka to cower, stoop. Konda (BB) parg- (-it-) (tiger) to crouch. Kui palga (palgi-) to bend forwards, stoop down. DED(S) 3306.

Ta. pallam lowness, low land, valley, ditch, dimple. Ma. pallam pit, hole, low ground, low shore; palla cavity, pit, hole. ir, palla river. Ko, pal im buffalo from the plains. To. pat valley. Ka. palla depth, pit, low ground, stream. Tu. palla, palla, palla shallow stream, pond, ditch, hollow, low spot, hole, pit: paliké valley, declivity, pond, ditch; (B-K.) palli, balli, pali a long ricefield. Te. pallamu pit, low ground, dale, (B. also) wet land, wet crop. Kol. (Haig) pallam east (cf. Kol. in 4567; Kamaleswaran). Ga. (S.3) palam downslope. Go. (Ma.) palla plain, pallayam maidan, (L.) meadow, plain (Voce. 2164). /Cf. Pkt. (DNA) pallavaya- field. DED(N) 3307, and from DEN 58.

Ta. pallayam, pallaiyam dish. Ma. pallayam basin, dish. Tu. pallayi earthen dish. Te. pallemu, palyamu, palleramu plate. /Cf. Mar. palé wooden bow!. DED 3308. 4018 Ta, palli hamlet, herdsman’s village, hermitage, temple (esp. of Buddhists and Jains), palace, workshop, sleeping place, school, room. Ma. palli hut, small settlement of jungle tribes, public building, place of worship for Buddhists or foreigners, mosque, royal couch. To. poly sacred dairy, matri-’ lineal sib, Badaga house; -ofy in: wisoly dairy of a particular grade, whose dairyman is wis of. Ka. palli, halli settlement, abode, hamlet, village; (K.?) palliru to rest, inhabit. Kod. palli hut of a Poleyé or Me-dé (both low castes). Tu. palli mosque; halli hamlet, small village. Te. palli village, hut; pailiya, palle small village; (inscr.) palli = palli. Pa. palli village (only in place names). Kur. palli, in: erpa-palli household, family establishment (erpa house). / Cf. Skt. pallT-, pallika- small village, esp. a settlement of wild tribes, hut, house; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7972. DED(S, N) 3309, 4019 7a. pallai that which is short and stocky (person, animal). ? Ko. pad very short (child, man). DED 3310.

Ta. para (parapp-, parant-) to fly, hover, flutter, move with celerity, hasten, be in a hurry, be greatly agitated, be scattered, dispersed, disappear; parapara (-pp-, -tt-) to hasten, hurry; parati over-hastiness, over- anxiety; parappan hasty person; parappu flying. haste, anxiety; parappai, paral bird: paravai bird, wing, feather, bee; pari (-v-, -nt-) to run away (as a horse), flow out quickly, be displaced suddenly, give way, fly off (as steam), be discharged (as an arrow); parai flying, wing, feather, bird; paru (pari-) to min, flee, be scattered, give way, be in disorder, cross over; m. hawk, kite, falcon, eagle; ruin; paral heavy shower; bull (cf. meaning of Ka. vb. paru). Ma. parakka to fly, flee; para, parava bird; parappu, paru flight; paruka to fly, flutter, drizzle, spread, unfold (as wings); paral flight, drizzling rain; parruka to let fly, scatter about. Ko, parn- (parnd-) to fly, run fast without stopping; become helpless because of sudden fright; paran birds (pair-word in: paky paran). To. porr- (porry-) to fly; port (po-ty-) to teach (young) to fly; o-re- (o+ré-) to throw in air and catch (Gackstraws, coins). Ka. pari, paru flying, running swiftly; parisu to cause oneself to run; parame the large black bee; par (pard-), paru (pari-) to leap up, run, jump, fly, palpitate, cross by jumping (as a stream), copulate (as a bull); parisu, harisu, frisu to cause to jump, etc.; paru running, flying; parum-bani flying drop, drizzling rain. Kod. pa+r- (pa-ri-) to fly,

Ta. parattai tangled locks, shaggy bushy hair; paratfai-ttalai head with shaggy, untidy hair; parattaiyan person with shaggy hair; fem, parattaicci; parattai-kkirai wild colewort. Ma. paru rough, harsh; paru-tala, payan-tala curly hair; parafta-ccira wild cole, Justicia madurensis, Ka. parate state of being rough, harsh, bristled, bushy, or curly. [Calo- Dhanes littoralis T. Anders. = J. m. Burm. (nail dye).} DED(S) 3312.

Ta. parantu (paranti-), parantu (par- anfi-), piranqu (piranfi-), purantu (puranti-) to scratch as with nails. Ma. parantuka to scratch, scrape. Ka. paradu, paracu to scratch with the finger or nails, claw; (Hav.) hargku to scratch with nails. Tu. parafikuni, parnkuni, parkuni id. Kol. pars- (parast-) to scratch (part of body). Nk, (Ch.) pars- to scratch. Pa. parc- to scratch; partid the itch: paytil urticaria. Ga. (S.) parc, (P.) pars- to scratch (body, etc.); (S.°) pars- (paris-) to scratch with fingernails or claws; parru prickly heat. Konda pas- (-t-) to scratch with the nails; pasay @- to get scratched. Pe. pac- (pacc-) to scratch. Mand. pac- id., comb hair; pecer a comb. Kuwi (F.) pacali, (S.) paccinai, (Su. Isr.) pac- (-it-) to scratch; (S.) pahinai to scrawl. Cf. $322 Ta. varantu. DED(S) 3313.

Ta. para-par-enal onom. expr. of sound made in tearing cloth, scratching. Ma. paruparé rough sound as of cloth tearing, bad cough. Ka. para, parapara sound imitating that of rending or tearing cloth; parane with the sound of para. 7. parapara noise of rending cloth. Te. parapara noise made in parampi [359] parru tearing anything; parru noise produced in tearing a cloth or the like. DED 3314.
Ta. parampi deceitful, cunning woman. Tu: parame clever man, cunning, way fellow; pamma fraud, trick. DED(S) 15.

Ta. parampu hill, mountain; paran- talai desert, village in a desert tract. Ma. parampu higher or dry ground laid out in terraces, all fields too high for rice cultivation, orchard, garden. To. pem small hill (or with

Ta. pari (-pp-, -tt-) to pluck, crop, pick off with twist, weed, eradicate, pull out (as an arrow), take by force, rob, destroy; (-v-, -nt-) to be loosened (as bonds), be cut off, torn apart (as roots); #. plucking, picking off, seizure, plunder; paru (pari-) to be torn in pieces; (inscr.) parippu hollow dug out (in bank of tank). Ma. pari pulling, tearing off; pariyuka to get loose, come off, tear, be scratched; parikka to pluck off, gather plants or fruits, pull out, rob; parippu plucking, gathering fruits, robbery. Ko. payr- (pare-) to break by pulling both ends (intr., tr.; as rope, flower-stem), pull out (as hair); artm, artym sharpness; payt rag. To. pary- (patc-) to pluck forcibly, (string) breaks; pary rag. Ka. pari to break off (as fruits, etc. from a tree), tear asunder, tear, rend, cut asunder, cut off, sever, cut; be torn asunder, be torn, etc.; 7. tearing, etc.; parivu tearing, tending, etc.; harita, harata, harta cutting, sharpness; paraku, paruku rag; (Hav.) harmbu to pluck leaves; hari to be sharp; haritta sharpness. Kod. _pari- (parip- paric-) to’ pluck.. Tu. parafkuni, parikuni, parkuni to pluck out; parpuni to pluck, pull out (as roots, grass, etc.); parpavuni to have pulled out, cause to pluck or pull out; pari opening, gap, (B-K.) sharpness; parté sharpness, edge; sharp; (also partely) a rent, tear, as in clothes; torn,. tattered. Kor. (T.) hajji to be torn. Te. pariya piece, bit, fragment, slice; pariga gleanings of corn. Ga. (Oll.) pay- to weed; payk- t
To. pary- (parc-) to beautify (bamboo pot with black dots, teeth by polishing). Ka. part to adorn, decorate; n. ornament, decor- ation, embellishment. DEDS 640A.
Ta. parivai n. of various plants. Ka. harive, harve a common potherb, Amarantus oleraceus. Tu. (B-K.) padipe, padpe the common herb used as a vegetable dish.

Ta. parai (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to speak, say; n. word, saying, statement; paraic- cal talk, speech; paraivu speaking, talking, chattering, proclaiming. Ma. parayuka to say, speak, tell; paraccal speech. To. parg ary- to know how to talk (or with 5204). Ka. pare abuse, censure; parcu, paccu to whisper; parisu to speak, chat; harafu to talk idly, prate; harate idle talk, prate, jabber; (Hav.) paraficu to mutter. Kod. pare- (parey-, parand-) to utter. Tu. pareficuni to prate, prattle, find fault with; paraficena, paraficely prattling, grumbling, murmuring; paraficele prattler, babbler, grumbler; paranfele prattler, grum- bler; paranda murmuring, grumbling; haratuni to prate, blabber; (h)arafé blabbing, prate, jabber. Ga. (S.) park- to say, speak; (S.3) park- (parik-) to speak, talk. Kui bargi order, command. DED(S) 3318.

Ta. parai drum,a measure of capacity, Paraiya caste; paraiyan member of the Paraiya caste; fem. paraicci, Ma. para drum, a rice measure, disk, circle; parayan a Pariah; fem. parayi, (Shanmugam) paracci. Ko. par drum; parn Pariah; fem. parc. To. par drum. Ka. pare id. Kod. pare drum (large, double- headed barrel drum beaten by Me-dé). Te. parra a certain measure of capacity. Go. (A.) para, (G.) parra, (Mu.) par(r)ay drum; (SR.) phard small drum (Voce. 2125). DED 3319,
Ta. ‘Pparai (-v-, -nt-) to be wasted, worn out, impaired, (-pp-, -tt-) to remove, destroy. Ka. parakalu, pare leanness, thinness, weakness, DEDS 641.

Ta. parru (parri-) to grasp, scize, catch, hold, receive, adhere to, touch, com- prehend; hold (as colour), be kindled, have effect (as drugs), stick, become joined to or welded together (as metals soldered), be fitting, be sufficient; n. grasp, seizure, accept- ance, adherence, affection, friendship, affinity, solder, paste; parram grasping; parri con- cerning, referring to; parrutal love, attach- ment, devotion. Ma. parru adhesion, close relation, friendship; parruka to stick to, adhere, catch, suit, fit, take effect (as fire), get, seize; parri concerning; parrikka to fix, join, paste, cause to take effect, fix in the heart or memory. Ko. pat- (pac-) to catch, seize, hold, hold out, be obstinate, resolve, (fire) catches; suit, please; Pat, pafl act of seizing; etc.; af- (ac-) to seize firmly, persist (in doing); pat- (paty-) to become stuck fast; at- (aty-) to climb; fight; paty concerning. To. pat- (paty-) to seize, catch, touch, marry (woman), (bull) covers cow; 6t- (6ty-) to stick to; climb. Ka. pattu to seize, catch, hold, take hold of; be held or contained; stick to; m. hold, seizure, firm grasp, per- sistence, resolution, obstinacy, habit, coher- ence; paffage obtaining, catching; pattu, partu to stick to, adhere, be united, join, take effect (as fire, dye); ascend, climb; n. adhesion, hold, a fight, friendship, trust; pattisu to cause to adhere or join, affix, etc.; pani pattige joining, a hold; pattuge state of being joined, etc., connexion; haccu to apply, put to,

Ta. pani (-pp-, -tt-) to be bedewed, flow out, be shed, rain incessantly, become cool, shiver with cold, tremble, fear, spring forth (as tears); cause to tremble; x. dew, chill, cold, tears, rain, mist, fog, haze, trembling, fever; panittal incessant rain; panippu agitation, trembling; panirru (pan- irri-) to shed; panukku (panukki-) to sprinkle, moisten by sprinkling; pannir rosewater or other fragrant extract. Ma. pani dew, fever; panikka to be feverish, shiver; panekka to ooze; pani-nir rosewater. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) penp? fever. Ko. payn dew, frost; ? pany- (panc-) only in phrase: unya-d panya-d (to act) without thought (cf. Ta. pani to fear). To. pony dew; pony- (pons-) (place) is full of fever; onk- (onky-) (rain) drizzles.
