Ka. pani, hani to drop; ». drop (of water, dew, etc.); haniku to fall in drops; hanisu, hanisu to pour (as water); pannir perfumed water, rose- water, etc. Kog. pann- (panni-) to drizzle, panni-male drizzle; pant fever; panni-ii erfume. Tu. pani drizzling- rain; pani dew, og, mist, snow; panipuni, panipuni to drizzle, shower; panniry rosewater. Ye. panniru, panniramu rosewater, perfumed water. Kol. (SR.) pani cold. Nk. pani coldness. Nk. (Ch.} panni winter, cold. Pa. pafiil cold. Ga. (OIL) pangil id.; (S.) paygil chill. Go. (Tr. W. Ph.) pini, (SR. Y. Mu. S.) pini, (G.) pin cold (Voe. 2270). Konda pini id. Pe. pini id. Mang. peni id. Kui péni cold weather, low temperature; cold, chilly. Kuwi (E.) penni, (S. Su. Isr.) peni cold. Kur, panna n. cold. Malt,
Ta. paniccai, panicci-kkay, paniccan- kay gaub fie. sp. Diospyros]. Ma. panicci Diospyros embryopteris, etc. DED 3323.

Ta. panai, (in cpds.) panam- palmyra palm, Borassus flabellifer. Ma. pana a palm- tree, esp. palmyra, B. flabelliformis, Ka. pane the bastard sago tree, Caryota urens Lin., from which toddy is made. Kod. pane-mara toddy palm. Tu. pane-kayi the fruit of palmyra; panoli a kind of palmyra. [B. flab- ellifer Linn. = B. flabelliformis Murr.} DED (S, N) 3324.
Ta. panaiyan, panai-viriyan krait, Bungarus caeruleus. Ma. panayan a large snake. / Cf. Skt. (Susr.) panasa- a species of serpent. DED 3325.

Ta. panri hog, swine, pig; vanri id. Ma. panni hog, pig. Ko. paj id. To. pody, (once in a story) pody id. Ka. pandi id. Kod. pandi id. Tu. pamiji id. Te. pandi id. Pa, pend, (NE.) pend id. Ge. (Ol!) pand, (S.} pandu (pl. pandkil) id. Go. (A. Y. Mu. S.) paddi, (Tr. W. Ph.) paddi id.; (Ma.) paddal swineherd (Voc, 2111). Konda panri pig. Pe. panji id. Mand. panji id. Kui paji id. Kuwi (F. S. Su.) pajji, Usr.) paji id. DED (S, N) 3326.
Ta. pannakam, pannanku top of a palanquin ot carriage. Ma. pannakam leaf- cover of a palanquin, awning, boat cover. Ka. pannafiga canopy over an open palanquin. Tu. pannagamu id. DED 3327.
Ta. panndaftai fibrous cloth-like web about the bottom of the leaf-stalk of a palmyra or coconut tree. Ma. pannafa id. Ka. pannade id. DED 3328.
Ka. panni bragging, self-conceit; panne pride, haughtiness. Tu. panni, panna bragging, self-conceit. DED 3329.
Ta. pannai camphor. Ka. panne id. DEDS 643.

Ka. pay to butt, gore, knock against. Tu, hakuni to beat, strike. Vk. (Ch.) pak-/pag- to beat, shoot. Go. (A. Ma.) pay-, (SR.) payana, (L.) pana, pain& to beat, strike; (Y. G.) pa-/pay- id., shoot (Voc. 2190); (Mu.) pthc- to strike, play on a drum, clap (hands) (Voc. 2371). Pe. pag- (pakt-) to strike, kill. Mand. pig- to kill. Kui paga (pagi-) to attack, fight, etc. (or with 4087 Ta. pay). Kuwi (P.) pay- Cit-), (S.) painai to strike, kill; (F.) paiyali to hit, kill; (Isr.) pay- (-it-) to beat, kill. DEDS 644.
Ta. pakal, paval balsam pear, Momor- dica charantia. Ma. paval id. Ka, hagal, hagala id. DED 3330.
Ta. pakan elephant driver, charioteer, horseman, rider; paku id., art, ability. Ma. pakan, pavan an elephant instructor; pavu all that an elephant has to learn in training him. DED 3331.

Ta. pakkam seaside village, town, village. Ma. pakkanar n. of a famous low- caste sage. Ka. padka-nadu jogi a kind of Sudra beggars. Te. paka hut, hovel; (K.) -paka pakku (361) pau (village name suffix); pika-nadu one of the Telugu districts under the Reddi kings. Konda paka hut. /Cf. Skt. pakkana- hut of a Candala, village inhabited by barbarians; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7620. DED(S, N) 3332.

Ta. pakku areca nut, areca palm; paku areca nut; pakku-veffi crackers for slicing areca nuts. Ma. pakku a raw areca nut; pakku-veffi betel-knife. Tu. pakutti knife for cutting betel nuts. Te. pSka Areca catechu, the areca tree; an areca nut. Kol. (Pat., p. 47) poe betel. Kuwi (S.) poka mrdnu areca tree. Cf. Skt. pga- A. catechu: Turner, CDIAL, no. 8313. DED 3333.

Ta. pakku bag. Ma. pakku id. Kod. pa+kki id. (in songs). Cf. 4450 Ta. pai. DED (S) 3334. 4050 Kur. pakna (pakkyas) to take up into one’s arms, on one’s lap: refl.-pass. pakarna (pakkras). Malt. pike to take in the lap. DED 35. 4051 Kur, bakna to throw in, put in (a basket, sack, vessel, any dry goods, corn, money, etc. with the hands). Malt. bake to take up (as earth). ? Ka. bikalu gleaning. Cf.
Te. pikudu moss, mossiness, scum or greenness on putrid water. Konda pékola fungus, moss on water; (BB) pékor duckweed. ? To. pasn moss. DEDS 646.

Ta. paikar side, neighbourhood, place, location; panku side, neighbourhood, place, companionship, partisanship; equality, like- ness, beauty, agreeableness, fashion, manners, politeness, means; pankan friend, companion, husband; panki, female’ companion of a heroine, lady’s maid; pankodr friends. Ma. pahku, pafiiu side, party, propriety, means; pahhan companion, friend. ? To. waex o+x- to side with, connive with. Ka. panhgu manner, form, shape, likeness. Konda (BB) pani, in: gitoni pai temple of head. DED(S) 3337. 4054 [see App. 46].
Ta. pacam eye of a needle, sewing, thread. Ko. pa-c eye of a needle; vulva (as a tiddling word). DED 3339.

Ta. pacanam diarrhoea. Ka. basu to void excrement. Te. pacanamu, pdsanamu evacuation of the bowels. DED 3340. 4057 Ka, hasuku a disagreeable smell of certain leaves or unripe fruits, the smell of raw meat, etc. Te, payu, (K. also) pacu, pasu to be rotten; p(raci foulness, staleness, nastiness. Kol, (SR.) pas- to get addled, get sour (of food). Nk. pas- to become stale. Ga. (S.3) pise left-over food from the previous day, spoiled food. Konda pis- (-t-) to rot and smell offensively (of any food, tice, fruit, or a dead animal). ? 7a. panfal rancidity, musti- ness, fetidness, mouldiness, staleness; panfai bad smell, as of rotten fish. Cf. 3999 “Ta. paga and 4110 Ta. pag. DED(S, N) 3341.
Ta. pacci milk,- mother’s milk (nursery word). Ma. pacci id. Ka. Paci id. DED 3342.

Go. (A.) pask- to dislike; (Tr. LH.) paskanad id., hate (Voc. 2214). Kui paska (paski-) to disparage, despise; n. disparage- ment. ? Ta. payir (-pp-, -tt-) to show aversion or disgust at, be sulky or ill-humoured about; Payirppu disgust, abhorrence; disagreement, syangement. 2 Cf. 4002 Ta. pari. DED(S)
Ma. pafam oil-dish. Tu. pada an oil vessel. DED 3345,

Ta. pattam garden. Ma. pafam range (esp. of ricefields), rice-land. Ko. pa+rmm (obl. pasrt-) level ground. To. o-d level ground (< Badaga ha-da open, grassy pround in village). Tu. padpu a plain. Te. paduva a low ground. DED(S, N) 3346.
Ka. (Bark.) hadi a small grove. Tu. padi an underwood, a small forest.

Ta. pati town, city, hamlet, pastoral village; pafam street, street of herdsmen. Ma. pati (in n.pr. of villages). Ka. padi settlement, hamlet, village. Kod. pa+di hut of a Kurumba. Te. pidu village (at the end of names of places). /Cf. Skt. pafaka- a kind of village, half a village (from which are borrowed Ta. pafakam street, section of a village, Ma. pafakam part of a village); Turner, CDIAL, no. 8031, to which add Mar. pada hamlet or cluster of houses of agriculturalists (also Guj., aye etc.); MBE 1974a, p. 132, n.17. DED

Ta. patu (pati-) to sing, chant, warble, hum; pafal versifying, song; pati singer; tune; paffu singing, song, music; pa verse, stanza, poem, pavalar pocts. Ma. patuka to sing; pai tune; paffu singing, song, poem. Ko. pa-ry- (pa-rd-) to make noise (crow, bees, gun); Pa-{ song. To. po+r (po-ry-) to sing (the song called post); po-t a particular type of Song; porf- (party-) to shout. Ka. padu to sing; padu, pata singing, song. Kod. pa-d- (pa-di-) to sing; pa-fi song. Te. padu to sing, chant, warble; pafa singing, song. Kol. pa-d- (pa-dt-) to sing; pa-fa song. Vk. par- to sing; pafa song. Nk. (Ch.) par- (pat-) to sing; pafa song. Pa. pad- to sing; pita song, story, word, language. Ga. (OIL) par- to sing; pate song; (S.) par- to sing; pafe word; (S.3) pate word, speech, pronunciation. Go. (M.) parana, (G. 5. Ko.) par-, (Ma.) pag-, (SR.) pafana to sing (Voc. 2194); (many dialects) var-, (Ma.) vag-, (Tr.) warana id. (Voc. 3226); (many dialects) paf¥ ‘song (Voc. 2179). Konda p&ar- to sing; pata song. Kuwi (F.) pacali, (T. Mah.) pac- to sing: (F.) paca, (T. Mah.) pacu song; (Isr.) para stanza, verse. Kur. parma to sing. Malt, pare id., bewail. DED(S) 3348. pafian [362] pimpu
Ta. pattin grandfather, ancestor; patti grandmother, aged woman. Ma, paftan grandfather (among Pulayars); patti wife of a tailor, midwife. DED 3349.
Ko. party size, strengtl’ equal to what must be done. Ka. pati extent, size. Te. pati extent, size, magnitude. DED 3350.

Ta. pan song, melody; Panar caste; praise, flattery; panan an ancient class of Tamil bards and minstrels; pani song, melody, music; panu song; pan music; papnu (panni-) to sing in an instrument (as a tune), tune musical instruments; pannal tuning the lute strings according to the required melody; papnumai quality of a melody; panniyam musical instrument; pannavan bard; pantar bards. Ma. panan a caste of musicians, actors and players. ? To. paen festival (any except funeral). Ka. panbe (DCV, no. 1218) dancing girl, (Kitt.) whore, adultress; (K.?) pana an expert in singing and dancing. Tw. (pada) panpini to recite a verse, sing a song. Go. (Ch. W. Ph. Mand.) panal a Pardhan man; fem. pane (Voc. 2184). Mand. penmi song. Kuwi (Mah.) panbu, (Isr.) panbu id. / Cf. BHS pana- a canddla, untouchable; Pkt. pana- id.; Or. pano name of the Domb caste in Ganjam. DED(S) 3351.

Ta. panali, vanali, vanay frying pan (< Te.). Ka. banali, banale, bindli, balali, balle frying pan of stone or metal. 7u. banalé, banalé a kind of frying pan. Te. banali frying pan. DED(S) 3352. 4070 Ka, banal state of becoming or being dim or discoloured. Ju. banaly darkness, blindness, dimness; banale a dim-sighted man. DED 3353.

Ta. panal agricultural tract, rice-field. Ka. (Coorg) bani a meadow. Kod. ba-ne open treeless meadow where cattle graze. Tu. bane, bané a hill or jungle near a rice- field. DED(N) 3354. 4072 Kui panba (pat-; future pa-), (P.) panpa (pant-) to obtain, get, receive, find; n. obtaining, getting, finding, wealth. Kuwi (Su.) pa’- (pat-; ppl. panbi) to find, get; (P.?) pa’- (paf-; infinitive padeli, neg. pra-, imper. 2sg, primu) to obtain; (F.) patali (furure par-, imper, pamt) to find; (S.) parinai id.; pa’nai to get, have; panpu the receipt; (Isr.) pan-/pna?- (-h-/-t-) to receive, get. Cf. 4034 Ta. parru. DEDS 648.
Ta. pantil sirissa; Batavian orange; bamboo. Tu. pandily, pandely a kind of tree. DEDS 649.
Ta. pati half. Ma. pati half, a share. Ko. paedy half. To. po-@y id. Cf. 3808 Ta. paku. DED 3355.

Ta. patiri trumpet-flower tree, Stereo- spermum chelonoides, suaveolens, and xylo- carpum. Ma. patiri trumpet-flower, Bignonia suaveolens. Ka. padari S. chelonoides DC. Tu. padari B. chelonoides. Te. padiri id. / Cf. Skt. pafald-, patali- B. suaveolens. [S. chelonoides DC. = B. chelonoides Lin. S, suaveolens DC. = B. suaveolens Roxb.}] DED 3356.

Ka. pidara, hadara adultery; padari, hadaragitti, hadarigiti adultcress; padariga, hadariga adultcrer. Tu. hadara adultery. Te. (SAN) padari base, inferior man; padali low man. DEDS 650. 4077 Kur. pada root. Malt, pathe root ofa tree. DEDS 651.
Ta. patti small field. Ma. patti garden bed. Ka. pati id. Tu. pati nursery for plants. Te. padu, padi garden bed or plot. DED 3357.

Ta. patti bathing tub, watering trough or basin, spout, drain; pattal wooden bucket; pattar id., wooden trough for feeding animals. Ka. pati basin for water round the foot of a tree. Tu. pati trough or bathing tub, spout, drain. Te. padi, padu basin for water round the foot of a tree. DED (S) 3358.

Ta. pantu cavity, hollow, deep hole; pattar, pattal ditch, depression. Fe. patu to bury, inhume, fix in the ground, plant; 7. burial, that which is buried or concealed underground; pit-ara grain-pit or cellar, patampi a pitfall. Kol pa-tar grain storage pit (< Te.). Pa. pend- to bury. Ga (P.) pendup- (pendut-) id. Konda panter grain-pit, cellar. DED(S, N) 3359. 4081 Ma, pantam fibres of a coconut branch. Tu. pandava, pandavu a thin strip of coconut branch or of other fibrous trees, generally used as a band or tie. DEDS 652.

Ta. pantal snake, mountain snake. Ma. (DCV) pantal snake. DEDS 653. 4083 (a) Ta. pappatti butterfly. Ma. pappatti id. Kod. pa-pili id., moth. Go. pape (A. Y¥. Ch. Ph. S.) butterfly, (Ma.) grass- hopper; (Tr.) papé butterfly; (W.) phape id., grasshopper; (Ph.) phaphe locust (Voc. 2189). Kur. papa butterfly. Cf. 4084 Pe. pami. (b) Ma. parra moth. Ka. (Bark.) hante id. Tu. panté butterfly; (B-K.) pate, pante id., moth. (c) Nk. (Ch.) pipuli butterfly. Pa. pilpili id. Go. (SR.) pipri, (Mu.) pipli id. (Voe. 2231). Kui pipili moth. /Cf. Halbi pilpili butterfly. (d) Kuwi (Su. S. Isr.) pubuli, (F.) pabili butterfly. DED(S, N) 3360.

Ta. pampu snake; pappu, in: pappu- ppakai Garuda, the enemy of serpents (cf. 3808); (lex.) pa snake. Ma, pampu id. Ko. pa+b id. To. posb id.; o-f id. (in songs; < Badaga haevu). Ka. pavu id. Kod. pasmbi id. Tu. havu id. (< Ka.); pamboly a kind of flat, long fish; pamboly-kéré rat-snake (cf. 2011). Te. pamu snake. Kol. pa»m id. Nk. pam id. Nk. (Ch.} pam id. Pa. bam id. Ga. coment pampu [363] parai (OIL) bam, (S.) bamu, (P.) bamb id. / Cf. Pkt. (DNM) pava- snake. DED(S) 3361.

Ja. pampu bank of a river or tank; pampuri moat; a girdle-like structure edged round a fort wail; a flight of steps leading from a fort wall into the moat surrounding it. Ma. pimpuvari, paimptiri steps or ledges inside a well; the projecting lower part of a wall, a low mud border round a house. DEDS 55.

Ta. pay (-v-, -nt-) to spring, leap, gallop, flow or gush out (blood or stream), plunge, dive, attack, pounce on, run, fly, flit, hurry, flee; plunge into, penetrate, rush against; payccal, paccal bounding, jump, torrent, butting, piercing; payccu (paycci-), payttu (paytti-) to lead or conduct water, irrigate, push over, upset, plunge into, cause to enter; n. throw (as of dice), plunging, spring, leap; (Tinn.) pacanam_ irrigation; payttul spring, leap; pavu (pavi-) to Jeap or jump over. Ma, payuka to run against (as bulls), leap, rush out, flee; paykkuka to cause to run, drive off; piccuka to cause to flow, thrust in; paccal running, leap, assault, flight. Ko. asdasry road, path (cf. 3170 Ta. tari). To. po+x- (po+xy-) to swoop, (god) travels, walks; paef threshold. Ka. pay to jump over, step over, cross, jump, leap, leap against, assault, step, advance, go; m. going, course, manner; padi, hadi road. Kod. pa-y- (pasyuv-, pasiij') to dash into; (Shanmugam) pasyp, pasyv dashing. Tu. hayuni to cross, ford. Kui panja (pinji-) to fly, leap; ». act of flying, flight; pl. action paska (paski-); paga (pagi-) to attack, pounce upon, swoop down on, spring at, wrestle with, fight; 7. attack, fight (or with 4044 Ka. pay). DED(S) 3362.

Ta. pay (-v-, -nt-) to spread (as water, darkness, light), extend; m. spreading, exten- sion, mat, sail; payal bedding; pavu (pavi-) to extend, be diffused, spread (as creepers); lay in order, pave, spread, seed closely for transplanting, transplant; ”. warp; p¥ expanse, warp; pimam extension, expanse; pdcarai encampment. Ma, pay mat, sail; pakuka, pakkuka to lay things regularly. on the ground, fix in the ground regularly, sow thickly for transplantation; be fixed; pakku sowing thickly; pakku, pavu ceiling; pavu, pa weaver’s warp. Ko. pa+t- (pa+ty-) to spread (cloth, mat; intr., tr.); pasy mat used as seat; pas tveyr sleeping mat; paec id.; level ground. To. poet- (po+ty-) to spread (bedding, mat). Ka. pasu to spread (as blanket, carpet, mat, leaves, etc.), lay; mn. bed; pasage, pasike, pasuge bed; (K.?) pas-are a flat and spacious stone; hasu bed, warp; 4su warp; hay a sail; pase layer, bed, beautiful seat. Tu. payi mat, sail; pasy, asy warp; pajé mat; paseruni to extend, diffuse, spread; payuni to be diffused as molten metal, etc. in a mould. Te. Ast-grovi piece of hollow bamboo used by a weaver in preparing the warp; asu, Asu-mranu a frame having a number of sticks in several rows, used by a weaver in preparing the warp (asu < Ka.). Konda pasuri stones arranged on one another in the form of a wall. Pe. pah- (past-) to spread out (mat, etc.).. Mand. pah- to spread (mat). Kui paspa (p4st-) to spread out, lay a cloth; n. act of spreading out something. Kuwi (F.) passali to s

Tu. papafé parting of the hair on a female’s forehead. Te. payu to separate (intr.), leave, quit, be disentangled; pacu to remove; (K. also) be disentangled (as hair); papu to separate (fr.), divide, part, remove, efface; paya branch, division, clove or division of garlic, etc.; papata the parting of the hair. Kol, pa-p- (pa-pt-) to comb. Nk. pap- id. Go. (Grigson) paya parting of the hair (Voc. 2191). Konda pay- (-t-) to leave, be gone. ? Kui (K.) parp- to comb. Kuwi (F.) papetti parting of the hair. ? To. pa»p hoof. Cf. 33e8 Ta. paku and 3936 Ta. payal. DED(S) 4090 Kur. bayna (baycas) to respect, keep on a footing of reserve with (elder brothers with younger brothers’ wives, elder sisters with younger sisters’ husbands, man with wife’s elder sisters, woman with husband’s elder brothers); (Hahn) biaend xoend to shun. Malt. bac-naqe to employ the plural form when relatives by law address one another; bac-naqpo a relative by law. DED 65. 4091 (a) Ta. par (-pp-, -tt-) to see, look at, examine, inspect, know, look for, desire, search, look after; parval looking; parvai id., eye, appearance, etc. Ma, parkka to regard, behold, wait for; parppu considering, expec- tation. Ka, par to look for, wait for, look after, consider, regard, desire; par(u) looking to or after; paraysu to desire; harayisu, haraysu to look for, get a longing after, desire; harayke desire. Tu. para guard, custody, keeping. Te. paruva sight, glance. (b) Ta, par, parppin, parppanan brahman; fem. parppani, parppin

Ta. parai crowbar, smal! hoe for cutting grass. Ma. para iron crowbar, lever, parai [364] bive bar used for digging. Ko. pasr crowbar. To. pa-r id. Ka. pare id., short hoe. Tu. parengi iron crowbar, lever. Te. para, para spade. Kol. (Kin.) gadda para spade (gadda clod). Nk. (Ch.) pahar crowbar. Ga. (S.3) para spade. Go. (A.) para id. (Voce. 2195). Kuwi (S.) para id. DED(S) 3367.
Ta. parai, parai horse mackerel, Caranx. Ma. para a fish, sp. Scomber. Tu, paré scomber. Te, (B.) pata-minu a_ fish called ‘old wife’ (it looks like a pomfret). DED 3368.

Ta. parppu fledgling, young of birds, young of tortoise, frog, toad, lizard, etc., young of quadrupeds; parval fledgling, young of deer and other animals. Ma. parppu shoal of young fish, small fry. Ka. papa small child; (Gul) para boy. Te. papa infant, babe, child; papadu boy. Pa. pap (pl. papkul; voc. papa) child, baby, young of animals. Ga. (OIL) pap child, young one, small one (of articles); papondi young, small, green (of fruit); pé-pap young calf. DED(S, N) 3369.

Ta. pal milk, milky juice of plants, fruits, etc. Ma. pal milk, vegetable milk. Ko. pa-l milk. To. po+s id.; pos! fier stomach full of milk (pi+r stomach); po«l fe-t middle finger (pe-t finger). Ka. pat milk, white juice of some plants, juice of sugar-cane or of coco- nut. Kod. pa+li milk; pa-kutti milking pot.- Tu. peru milk, coconut juice, milky juice of plants, Te. palu, pidi milk. Kol. pael id. Nk. pal id. Nk. (Ch.) pal id. Pa. pél id., breast. Ga. (Ol. S.) pal milk. Go. (many dialects) pal, (Tr.) pal (ob], pad-) id.; (Mu.) pal id., breast (Voc. 2206). Konda ‘pal id. Pe, pal milk. Kui padu, palu id., sap. Kuwi (F.) paiu, (S. Su. P. Isr.) palu milk. Br. path id., milky juice or sap of plants. /? Cf. Skt. (lex,) patana- milk of cow newly calved (cf. Burrow, BSOAS 40.176). DED(S) 3370.

Ta. pal part, portion, share, section, dividing; panmai portion, share; nature. Ma. pal part. Ko. pa-im (obi. pa-lt-) portion, division. To. po-lm (od/. polt-) share; subdivision of patrilineal sib. Ka. pal division, part, portion, share. Kod. pa-li ma+d- to divide, distribute. Tu. paly share, portion, part, division. Te, palu id., lot, fraction, Pa. péla portion. Cf. 3808 Ta. paku. DED(S) 3371.

Ta. palam bridge, jetty, dam; (Tinn. also) palam bridge. Ma. palam bridge over rivers or to connect the walls of compounds. Ko. palm (obl. paslt-) bridge. To. po-lm (obl. porlt-) id. Ka. pila id. Kod. pacla bridge made of tree, étc, DED 3372.

Ta. palai silvery-leaved ape-flower, Mimusops kauki; seven-leaved milk-plant, Alstonia scholaris. Ma. pala Echites scholaris, and various trees, including M. kauki. Ko. pa+lmarm anunidentified tree, prob. identical with To. pass. To. pass a timber tree (Rivers: ? Sideroxylon), Ka. pale M. kauki Lin.; A. scholaris R. Br. Tu. palé, palembu A. scholaris, Te. pMlagaruda id.; pala M. hexan- dra. [A. scholaris Brown = =E. scholaris Lin. Lush. gives Ta. pilai, Ka. hale for M. kauki, A. scholaris, and S. tomentosum, and some other trees.] DED 3373.

Ta. palai-kkuruvi a species of bird. Ka. pale the blue jay. Te. pala a kind of small bird, (B.} the Indian roller, Boracias indica, erroneously called a blue jay. DED 3374. 4102 Kur. balka turmeric; yellow; (Hahn) baikd yellow. Malt. balke tumeric; balko yellow. ? Cf. Ta. vallikam, valliyam turmeric. DEDS 658.
Ta. p§vattai pavetta, Pavetta indica. Ma, pavatta id. To. po-wfet Pavetta sp. Ka, pavate, papafe-gida P. indica Lin. Tu. pavafe id. Te. papata id. DED 3375.
Ta. paviri creeping purslane, Portulaca quadrifida. Te, pavila-ktra, pavili-ktra P. meridiana, [{P. quadrifida Linn. = P. meridiana Linn.] DED 3376.

Ta. pavili a kind of ear ornament (< Te.). Ka. bavali, bavuli a kind of ornament of gold, pearls, precious stones, etc., worn on the tip of the ear; bali a kind of earring. Tu, bavali, bavoli an ornament worn by women at the tip of the ear. Te. bavili an ornament or jewel hanging from the ear, an ear-pendant; bavira a kind of ear ornament. Konda baveli ear ormament. DED(S) 3377.

Ta. pakkan cat; wild cat. Ma, pokkan wild cat. Ka. bavuga male cat. Te. bavuré- billi (see 4180), bavuru-gidu tomcat, wild cat; bavurumanu to cry out, echo or resound (as an empty or desolate house). Nk. (Ch,} bagale (pl -I) cat. Pa. bavki wild cat. Go. (Mu.) bakoval male cat (Voc. 2469); (Ko.) bayok wild cat (Voc. 2503); Sc.) bhongal cat (Voc, 2670). Pe. boyka wild cat. Kui baogi, baoli id. Kuwi (Su.) bavli, (Mah.) baduli, (Isr.) bavuli id. DED(S) 3378, DEDS 591. 4107 Ta, pavai puppet, doll, image, picture, portrait, pupil of the eye, woman, damsel. Ma. pava doll, puppet. Ka. pape, hahe figure, ornamental form, puppet, doll, badge, ensign, pupil of the eye. Tu. papé image, statue, puppet, doll. Te. papa pupil of the eye, (K. also) image. For meanings, cf. 4365 Ta. plvai. DED 3379. 4108 Kol pa+v way, path; pasv o-l- to wait for (o+l- to see, look at). Nk. pav way, road. Nk. (Ch.) pav ‘path. Pa, pav road. Ga. (OWL) pav, (S.) pavu id. Kur, pab road, path, journey; pab @rna& to wait for (@rn% to see). Malt. pawu way, path, road. DED(S) 3380. 4109 Ka, (Hav.) bave layer on boiling rice. Tu. bavé the cream of milk, the pulpy Kernel of a tender coconut, a film on the eye. ; par [365] panai 4110 7a. pir Cpp-, -tt-) to go to ruin, be laid waste, become useless, be accursed (as a place or house); #, desolation, ruin, damage, loss, corruption, baseness, evil, emptiness, barrenness, barren or waste land; payi deso- lation; pagutu unprofitableness; damage, ruin. Ma, pag an empty place, vo
Ka. bayaka, baruka vegetables (or fruits) that have been split, dried, and thus preserved. Tu! balaka, balyka thin slices of plantains fried in oil, called sovereigns. DED 3382

Ta. pagi temple; town, city, town of an agricultural tract, hermitage; pani town, village, district; ?paganam paddy field, agricultural land, tank. Ko. e+r it va-+y fields near village which are ploughed in the sowing ceremony (vary < pa-y; lit. field to which they put the team of plough bullocks); vasyv Toda ti- dairy; karpo-y pen and cowshed attached (i.e. milking place; see 1385; is the second member po-+y or o+y?). To. po»w one variety of dairy (viz. all those belonging to the tis grade, the conical dairies, and a few others of high sanctity); pol o-k the priest of the ti+ dairy (i.e. the man [o- | ‘of the pow); -alf an element in many dairy names (e.g. mossalf, beside the pen mo-twi+ < mo-s + twi-); ? poe’, in: po-¥ tiny garment of priest of tis dairy, when worn around waist, and po-§ tu-{ front lock of hair as tied up by priest of tie dairy (MBE 1974b, pp. 13f.); oy field nearest Badaga village, garden (< Badaga h4-i). Ka. (Badaga) haei field near village (Emeneau, Language 15.45). ? Cf.
Ka. pari row, line, regularity, regular order or way, method, tule. Te. padi justice, propriety; nature, quality. / Cf. Skt. pali- row, line, range; Turner, CD/AL, no. 8041. DED 3384.

Ta. palam metal cast in moulds. Ma. palam ingot; valam id., bar of gold or iron; a hammer for the chisel. Ka. pala ingot of gold or silver. Te. palamu, pala ingot. DED 3385. 4115 Ma, pali rag, shred. Tu. pali id. DED 3386.

Ta. palai spathe of palms, Ma. pata id., bark or film of an areca branch (used as vessel for gathering toddy, as hat); padnu spatha of a coconut bunch. Ka, hale, hali broad spathe at the bottom of an areca: -palm branch, used as a vessel, etc.; (K.?)} pale the outer covering of the areca nut flowers. Tu. palé, palé, palembu spatha of palm blossoms or of an areca brarich, cap made of areca spatha. Kor. (M.) hale spatite of areca nut tree. Te. pala husk of a coconut. DED 3387. 4117 Ta, palaiyam army, war-camp, village surrounded by hillocks. Ma, palayam camp, army. Ka. paleya, palya, palye camp, settle- ment, hamlet. Tu. palya, palyé army, halting piace. Te. palemu guard, camp, army. DED

Ko. pa+| buffalo calf between one and two years old. To. po+t female buffalo calf between one and two years old. Tu. parolu a young she-buffalo. DED(S) 3389. 4119 Ka, paru to grow, become (in several cpds.) Te. paru to grow, become (in cpds,), (K, also) occur, happen. DED 3390.
Ta. paru ship, sailing ship; pakri coracle, boat, ship, vessel. Ma, paru small boat, catamaran; paral float, raft. Ka. paru a kind of boat or ship. ? Tu. pati small boat. DED (S) 3391.

Ta. parai rock, crag, straturn, hillock; par rock, rocky stratum, shelf of rock. Ma. para rock, large stone, firmness. Kod. pa-re flat stone, stone slab, place where bath is taken on paved floor in bath-house. Tu, padé rock. Kor. (T.) hade id. Pa. para slab of stone. Go. (Ma.) parum (pi. paru’ku) rock, boulder (Voe. 2197). DED(S) 3392.
Ko. past, in: kay paer upper arm (Kota Texts 25.57). To. po+r (obl. po-t-) id., (?) upper limb (shoulder to fingers). Tu. paré hip, shoulder. Pa, péru shoulder, side.
Ta. paccai cockroach, cricket. Ma. parra cockroach. To. pa+t id. Ka. hate, ate, Gowda) hantE id. Kod. pa-te id. DED(S)

Ta. panai large earthen pot; a measure of capacity; pand large rounded pot. Ma. pana, pani water pot. Ko.. pasny a measure (= eight olk). To. po-ny Badaga contribution to Toda (four olk a house). Ka. pane, hane a pot of metal or earth; bane, bana, bani large earthen pot with a broad mouth. Kod. parni a measure (= two seeri). Tu. pani, pané a large pot; pané a kind of brass vessel; bana, pio [366] pih- bana water-vessel. Te. bana a large earthen pot, boiler, kettle. Cf. 5327 Ta. vanai. DED (S) 3394. 4125 Kui pio golden oriole. Kuwi (Mah.,) pioti id. Kur. pid oriole. DEDS 659.
Ka. pikalakki Madras bulbul, Pycno- notus haemorhous; pikkulike n. of a bird. Te. pikili, pigili bulbul. /? Cf. Skt. pika- Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus. DED 3395.
Te. pikka calf of leg. Kol. (Kin.) pikka id. Ga. (OL) pika id.; (S.3) pika kal foreleg. Go. (L.) pih® shin (Voc. 2278). Konda pika calf of leg. Kuwi (Su.) pikka muscle of calf of leg. DED(S) 3397.

Ma. pikkuka (picc-) to break in pieces. Te. pigulu, (K. also) pikulu, pivulu to burst. Kui pinja (pinji-) id.; cs. pispa (pist-); pl. action piska (piski-). Kuwi (Su.) pinj- (-it-) (fire) to explode, spark out; pl. action pisk- (-it-). DED(S) 3398.
Ka. binku crookedness, dishonesty, guile. Tu. pik(Dlata cheating. Te. pikku to cheat; n. deceit, trick. Cf. 4166 Ta. pittalag- tam. DED(S) 3399.

Ta. picaku (picaki-) to fail, err, blunder, make a false step, trip, be dislo- cated (as a joint): picaku, picakku failure, mistake, blunder, deviation; pici falschood. Ma, (Tiyya) peSaku error. Ko. peck mistake, mishap, wrong-doing. Ju. bisaruni to slip, tumble. Te. besuku to miss the mark, glance aside, slip through the fingers. Go, (SR.) bhisurkana, (Tr.) bhisurkana to slip, slide; (Driberg) bisurkta slippery (Voc. 2551). Cf. 4187 Ta. pigai. / Cf. H. phisalna to slip; Turner, CDIAL, no. 9080, *phiss-. DED (S, N) 3400.

Ta. picir (-v-, -nt-) to drizzle, sprinkle; n. rain drop, spray; picupicu Cpp-, -tt-) to drizzle, sprinkle; picu-pic- -enal expr. signifying drizzling slightly; pitir drop of water; (-v-, -nt-) to be separated into small particles, fall to powder, become scattered; (-pp-, -tt-) to scatter, spread. Ma, pitar rain. To. pisk urine; pisk in- (id-) to urinate. ? Tu. picipici oozing, trickling; bijuni to ooze. Te. pisaru a bit, particle, small quantity. Pa. pitur (pi. -kul) crumbs, scattering of food. Cf. 4199 Go. pirr and 4407 Ta. pey. DED(S, N) 3401.
Ta. picir fibre. Te. picu the fibrous parts of plants, etc. Ga. (S.2) pisu fibrous matter of fruits (< Te.). Cf. 4216 Ma. pili. DED 3402.

Ta. picupicu (-pp-, -tt-) to be moist, sticky, be viscous, adhesive or oily; picu-pic- enal expr. signifying (a) moistness (as of a cloth), (Bb) stickiness; picin gum, stickiness, viscousness; picukku sticky substance; picukk- enal expr. signifying stickiness or viscosity. Ka. (Hav.) hisku oyster. Tu. picipici miry, pisapisal-aidu to be clammy or viscous; pisaka greasiness or dirt in the hair; pisunu gum, resin. /Cf. Skt. piccha- gum, slimy saliva; picchala-, picchila- slimy, smeary (Turner, CDIAL, nos. 8154, 8156); Skt. vijjala-, vijjana-, vijina-, vij(j)ila- slimy, smeary (May- thofer 3, pp. 205, 794). DED(S, N) 3403.

Ta. picai (-v-, -nt-) to work with the thumb and fingers in mixing, knead, squeeze or mash between the palms, crush and separ- ate as kernels of grain from the ear, rub or apply on the skin, strike against one another as branches in the wind; picakku (picakki-) to press between the fingers, squeeze, crush; picaru (picari-) to mingle, mix with the hand. Ma, pistju husks of fruits, oilcake; pigukku the remains of expressed coconuts. Ko. pi of (picgy-) to be soft so that it can be squeeze pick- (picky-) to squeeze, pinch; picpicn beaten to a pulp, cooked to a pulp (of grain). Ka. pisuku to squeeze, press asa fruit, knead, shampoo; hisi to squeeze (a ripe fruit) so as nearly to separate it into two pieces; (Hav.) hificu to squeeze out pus from a boil. Tu. piskuni, pisuni to squeeze, press; poje(A)kuni to knead, rub, squeeze, strangle; (B-K.) pijak- ky, pijanky, pejanky to squeeze, strangle. Kor. (O.) pijagki to crush. Te. pisuku to squeeze, press, knead, shampoo, handle. Nk. pijg- to knead. Pa. pik- to crush; ? pic- to grind. Ga. (Ol) piskolp- (piskolt-) to squeeze; (S.) pic- to milk. Go. (fr) piskana to knead flour; (many other dialects) pisk- to squeeze, press, knead; (SR.) puskana to knead (Voc. 2258). Pe. pic- (piec-) to squeeze, milk. Mand. pic- to milk. Kui (K.) pic- to press, squeeze, milk. Kuwi (Su.) pic- to press out, wring, milk; (Isr.) pic- Cit-) to milk, squeeze; (I'.) picali to milk, wring, press out -(oil); (S.) picinai to quash, wring. Kur. picka’ana to press and b

Ka. pisaru. /?Cf. Skt. pitta- bile (whence Ta. pittu, pittam, pitti bile, mad- ness; Ka. pitta id.; Kod. pitta giddiness; Tu. pitty bile, fickleness; pitta bilious, choleric; etc.). DED(S) 3407. 4143 (a) Ta. picanku (picanhki-) to become dirty, be soiled. Ka. piccu slimy impurities of the eyes, rheum; pisaru, pisuru filth of the body, rheum of the eye, etc.; (Hav.) hikku rheum. Tu. ? p@iici filth, dirt; ? purku, (B-K,. also) pulku rheum of the eye. Te. pusi id. Kur. bejje blearedness, watery rheum of the eyes; bejga blear-eyed. Br. pic rheum of the eye. /Cf. Skt. pifijata-, pifijtisa-, pefijiisa-, paifijlsa- id.; Turner, CDJAL, no. 8150. (b) Ta. pilai rheum, secretion from the eye: Ma. pila rheum of the eyes. DED(S, N) 3408.
Tu. pijiny ant. Bel. (LSB 2.3) hijini id. DEN 59,

Pe. pih- (pist-) to leave, abandon. Mand. pih- (-t-) id. Kui pihpa (piht-) to picci [367] Piti release, relinquish, let loose, set free, absolve, forgive, excuse, cast off; n. release, relinquish- ment, absolution. Kuwi (Su. P.) pih- (pist-) to leave, abandon; (Isr.) pih- (pist-) to leave, forsake; (F.) pissali to forsake; (S.) pih’nai to release, relinquish; pispi kinai to rescue, ransom; pispinai to release. ? Te. puccu to send, (K. also} abandon, give up, let go, release (as a sigh); puttencu to send, dis- patch, forward; puppincu to send. DED(S) 3405.

Ta. picci, pitti, pittikam, pittikai large-flowered jasmine. Ma. picci, piccakam Jasiminum grandiflorum, To. ka-pic Jasimi- num bignoniaceum (dark stems and leaves; for ka- see 1278). -[J. humilis Linn. = J. bignoniaceum Wall.] DED(S) 3406.
Tu. picci the testicles of animals. Te. picca testicle. Cf. 4151 Ta. pitukku. DEDS 662.
Tu. picci a nut, esp. the cashew nut. Te, pikka a nut. DEDS 663.

Ta. piccu bile, madness; piccan mad- man; fem. picci. Ma, piccu madness; piccan madman. Ko, puc madness. To, piic anger. _ Ka. peccu, paccu, puccu madness; pecca madman. Te, picci, picca madness, silliness, folly; mad, silly, foolish; pisa ignorance, foolishness. Nk. (Ch.) pisak mad. Ga. (S.*) picete mad woman. Go. (SR. Hislop) pisal, (L.) pisa, (SR.) pisol id. (Voc. 2256). Cf.

Ta. picinari, picinéri miser; picini niggard; miserliness, niggardliness; picuku (picuki-) to be niggardly; picukku miserliness, niggardliness; picukkar mean-minded persons; picupicuttavan niggard. Ma. pisukku niggardli- ness; pisukkan niggard, miser. Ju. picci Mammon; pifici parsimony, stinginess; stingy, miserly. Te. piccapattu stinginess, miserliness; pisidi, pisindri, pisini miserly, stingy; pisini- gottu miser; (K.) pikku to be miserly; (K.; modern) pinasi stingy person. Pa. pitey miser. DED(S, N) 3409. 4145 7a. pificu young tender fruit. Ma. pificu young fruit just set. Ka. pifiju, hicu, fcu fruit newly come forth from the blossom. Te. pindiya, pinde young berry, fruit just formed after the blossom. Ga. (S.*) pinje smail young berry. Konda pinza (pl. pinzen) tender fruit, young berry. Kuwi (Kasipur) pijari fruit (irpi p. mahua fruit). DED(S, N) 3410. A4l45A Kui pinja (pinji-) to rebound, leap; n. a leap; pl. action piska (piski-); pispa (pist-) to cause to rebound. Kuwi (Su.) pinj- (-it-) to spring, jump; pl. action pisk- (-it-); (S.) pinj- to spring. DEDS 666

Ta. pitar, pifari, pitar-ttalai, pufali nape of the neck; pitatku back of a blade or weapon, bottom (as of a basket); (Tinn.) poraéi nape. Ma. pifari, pirati, pitali nape of the neck. Ko. pertal id. Ka. peda state of being behind or after, the back, backwards; pedadale, pedandale back of the head; hedaku back or nape of the neck; the hind part of a fort. Kod. pada mande back of the head; padanefti nape of the neck. Tu. pedatgy behind the back. Te. peda hinder, back; pedatala back of the head; pedamu the face turned back; pedalu backyard of a house. Pa, pidtel behind, after. DED(S) 3411.
Ta. pifavam, pitavu, pita Bedaly emetic nut, Randia malabarica. Te. bedali Griffithia fragrans. [R. m. Lamk. = G. ff W. & A.| DEDS 667.

Ta. piti (-pp-, -tt-) to catch, grasp, seize, clutch, capture, cling to, contain, carry, keep back, understand, make a handful; cling (intr.), adhere, be pleasing, suitable; n. hold, clutch, pinch, seizure by the hand, comprehension, fist, closed hand, handle, handful, small quantity; pifittam withholding a part of payment, frugality; pifittu handful, pitippu grasping, holding, seizure, catching, sticking, money amassed, handle. Ma. Piti grasp, hold, closed hand, handful, handle, hilt, memory; pitikka to seize, catch, hold (as a vessel), stick to, understand, take effect; piticcu. beginning from; pifittam grasp, handle, knowledge, obstinacy; pifippikka to cause to seize or grasp; pifippu sticking, cleaving, capacity. Ko. pirc~ (pire-) to clench (hand); piry handful of anything but cooked tice; pic handful of boiled rice. To. idy- (idt-) to seize, cling to (< Badaga). Ka. pidi to seize, catch, grasp, deduct from wages, hold, begin (as rain), comprise, be able to receive (as sack, cart), be so spacious as to let (a thing) pass through; n. seizing, hold, grasping, clenching the fist, handful, fist, handle, hilt; pidite seizure; pidipu state of seizing or being seized; pidiyisu, pidisu to cause to seize; pidiha seizing, etc. Kod. pudi- (pudip-, pudic-) to catch, hold, be small enough to go into or through; pudi handful, morsel; (Shanmugam) pudip holding. Tu. pidita, piditta, pidta discrimination, judgement; hidi a hold, grasp, handful, handle, hilt; pudi handle, haft, hilt; puda,

Ta. piti female of elephant. Ma. piti id., the female of camels and pigs. Ko. piry ma-y female deer. Ka. pidi female elephant; (PBh.) pidi-vandi female hog. Te. pidi the female of an animal. DED 3413. ;
Ta. pifuku thunderbolt. Ma. pinar id. Ka, pidugu id. Te. pidugu id., lightning. Konda pirgu thunderbolt. DED 3414.
Ta. pitukku testicle. Ma. pitukku id. To. pixid testicles. Ka. hidiku, hidigu testicle. Cf. 4140 Tu. picci. DED(S) 3415.

Ta. pituhku (pituki-) to pull out or off, pluck up, extort, break through an obstruction, vex, give trouble; piftutkal pulling out, extortion, annoyance. Ma, Pituhfuka to pull out, extort, vex; pifaruka to be plucked up; pifarttuka to root up, open a boil. To. piry- (pirs-) (boil) opens; pire- (piré-) to open (a boil). Ka. pidaga, pidagu, pidugu trouble, affliction, disease. Te. (K.) puduku to pluck off, nip off, squeeze, press. Kui pyunga (pyungi-) to be snapped, broken off, plucked; prupka (< pruk-p-; prukt-) to snap, break off, pluck; pruku inba to be snapped, broken off; pruku ispa to snap, break off; brunga (brungi-) to be plucked out; brupka (< bruk-p-; brukt-) to pluck, pluck out, pull out; prisi prisi kova (kot-) to reap by stripping off ears of corn. DED(S) 3416.

Te. pitta bird. Kol. (Kin.) pifte young bird, chick; (P.) pitta bird. Nk. pitta id. Ga. (S34) pitode sp. nightingale. Go. (many dialects) wie (D. Mu. M. Ko.) Pife, (Tr.) pitte bird (Voc, 2218). DED(S, N) 3418.
Ka. bittala hugeness. Te. biftu greatly, much; excess. DED 3419.
Ka. (Hav.) pittu yellow on the teeth. Tu. pitty, pitta tartar on the teeth.

Ta. pinam, pinan corpse, carcass, disembodied soul, devil, spirit; pipi (-v-, -nt-) to die. Ma. pinam corpse, dead body of animals. Ko. penm (obl. pent-) corpse. To, in the dead (ino-r the world of the dead [nosr ‘place]; in f6w the god of the dead {= 6-n]). Ka. pena, hena corpse, carcass. Tu. puna corpse, dead body; dead, inactive; ena corpse. Kor. (T.) hina id. Te. pindgu, pingu corpse, dead body, carcass. Ga. (S) pinige corpse. Konda pingu (n, not 9) id. Kuwi (F.) piligil, (S.) pinugu, (Isr.) pinugu id. DED(S) 3420.

Ta. pinar roughness, unevenness, coarseness. Ma. pinar grossness, what is thick or stiff; a bruise; piparkka to grow thick, coagulated, swell (as a bruise); pinarppu coagulation, tumour; pinupiné thickly, stiffly. Ka. pinil hump. DED(S) 3421.

Ta. pini disease, sickness, suffering; pinittdr diseased persons; piniyan diseased man. Ma, pini ailment, affliction (esp. by demons). DED 3422. 4160 (a) Ta. pinai (-v-, -nt-) to entwine (intr.), unite, copulate; tie, fasten, clasp each other’s hands as in dancing; (-pp-, -tt-) to link, unite, tie, fasten, clasp hands; n. being knit together, joint in planks, tic, flower garland; bail, security, pledge, consent; pipaiccu copulation; pinaiyal joining together, flower garland, hinge, copulation; pini Cpp-, -tt-) to tie, fetter, link, win over; 1. fastening, bond, attachment, plait; pinippu binding, tic, attachment; pipafku (pinanki-) to be linked together, intertwined, be at variance; pinakku (pinakki-) to fasten, intertwine; pinakkam, pinakku twisting, interlacing, disagreement. Ma. pina tying, yoke, being involved, bail, surety, coupling; pinayuka to be entangled, sufferings to alight on one; pinaccal yoking, entanglement; pinekka to tie together, yoke, ensnare; pinafinuka to be entangled, quarrel, fight; pinakkuka to entangle, set at variance; pinakkam, pinakku confusion, quarrel, fight. Ko. pien- (piend-) to become entangled (of ropes, wrestlers’ legs); piepd- (piendy-) to entwine (ropes), tangle up (someone's legs); 2? pen ga+l ov- (child) waves arms and legs. To. pin surety. Ka, pepe to unite or tie different things together, intertwine, twist, plait, braid; be joined, unite, be intertwined, get entangled; #. an entwined state, union, company; penagu, penasu to wrangle, q

Ta. pinti flour, meal, powder. Te. pindi flour, powder. Kol. (SR.) pindi flour. Ga. (S.*) pindi id. Go. (A. Y. Ch. D. Ma. M.) pindi, (G, Ko.) pind, (Tr. W.) pindi id.; (Mu.) pindi id., powder (Voc. 2220). Konda (BB) pindi flour. DED(S) 3424.

Ta. pitir (-v-, -nt-) to be bewildered; n. conundrum, puzzle; putir riddle; pici enigma, riddle. Ka. padir, puduru speech that admits of a double meaning. Tu. padary ambiguity, vagueness; ambiguous, vague. ? Go. (SR.) pendo riddle (Voc. 2336). 2 Cf. 4364 Kol. puend-. DED(S) 3425. 4164 Kui pitila temple (of the head). Kuwi (Su.) pitela, (S.) pittela id. DEDS 670.

Ta. pituhku (pitufki-) to protrude, bulge, gush out, be expressed; pitukku (pit- ukki-) to press out, squeeze out as pus or pulp, express, blow up as a bladder, puff out; n. squeezing out, pressing out; pitukkam protruding, protuberance, hernia; pitakku (pitakki-) to be ‘squeezed or crushed. Ma. pitufiinuka to be squeezed, pressed out; pitukkuka, pitarruka, putukkuka to crush, press matter out of a boil. To. pi@x- (piOxy-) to be squeezed; piOk- (pi®ky-) to squeeze; pit ir milch buffalo (usage of ti+ dairy; or with 4183(a2) Ta. piri). Ka. hiduku to squeeze, press as a fruit, knead, shampoo, squeeze soaked pulse (avare) with the fingers and thus remove its- skin; m. state of being freed from one’s skin by squeezing. Te. pituku, piduku to draw (as milk), milk (as a cow), express, squeeze, press out; (K. also) (cows, etc.) give milk. Kol. (Kin.) pidk- to press. Go. (Tr.) pidtkana to strain at stool, exert oneself violently in vain (as in lifting a burden) (Voc. 2225). Kuwi (Isr.) pid- Cit-) to open up (e.g. boil, sore); burst open (intr.); pit- (h-) to poke to release blood, cause to burst, open up. Kur. pedexn’ (pedxas), pedxa’ind to pinch, squeeze, strangle. Malt. pethge to break open {as a large fruit); pethgre to burst or break (as a fruit). Cf. 4135 Te picai. DED(N) 3426.

Ta. pittalagtam deception, fraud. Ma. pittalattam lying, tricks. Ko. pitla+ fm failure to keep promise. Ka. pittalata trickery, lying, deceit, defrauding. Tu. (B-K.) pittri intrigue, conspiracy. Te. pitalafakamu id. Cf. 4130 Ka. bihku. DED 3427.

Te. pittu to break wind; 7. fart. Go. (Tr.) pitt@na, (Ph.) pitana to break wind; (Ph.) pit fart (Voc. 2223); (Mu.) pthk-, pik- to break wind (Voc. 2280); (Koya Su.) pitt- to fart; (ASu.) pihk- id.; pihka fart. Konda pit- (-t-) to fart; pitu fart. Pe. pit- (-t) to break wind. Mand. pit- id. Kui pita (piti-) id. Kuwi (Isr.) pit- (-it-) id.; pitu fart. Kur. pitna (pittyas) to break wind. Malt. pite id. DED (S, N) 3428.
Ta. pittai a tuft or bundle of hair. Tu. puccé braided or plaited hair. DED 3429.

Nk. (Ch.) pinda/pinde (pl pindel) ant. Pa. pinda fly. Ga. (OIL) pinde insect, fly; (S. P.) pindake (pi. -r) fly;(S.?) timuk pindake a kind of bee. ? Go. (A. Y. Ma. Ko.) pette, (Tr. W. Ph. Ch. Mu.) patte ant (Voc. 2332). 5a pindri locust, grasshopper. DED(S)
Ka. hippi, hippe the refuse or residue of what has been squeezed out. Te. pippi id. Cf. 4183(a) Ta. piri. DEDS 671.

Ta. piy (-v-, -nt-) to be tattered, torn off, torn into bits, be loosened, be carded (as cotton), be put to rout; (-pp-, -tt-) to rip, tear, rend, card (cotton), tear into small bits. Ma. piccuka, piccuka to tug, tear in pieces, prepare wool for carding. Ka. pifiju, hicu, icu to card cotton. Te. pinju to card cotton with the fingers; penjari the cotton-cleaner caste (< IA; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8163). / Cf. Skt. pifijana- bow for carding cotton; *pifijati cards cotton; Turner, CDIAL, nos. 8160, 8159. DED(S) 3431.

Ta. piyal nape of the neck; pical id., shoulder, hump as of an ox. Ko. peyl, peyl shoulder; ? pekartl nape of neck (kartl neck). Ka, pegal shoulder. Tu. pugely, puggely id. Bel. (LSB 2.3) hugeli id. Kur. pikhirpa the back of the body behind the shoulders. DED(N) 3432.
Tu. piyavu small chicken. Nk. (Ch.) piyofe chick. Go. (Ma. L.) pise, (Mu.) ponj pise, (M.) kor pise chicken (Voc. 2257). DEDS 664. pirantai [370] piri

Ta. piran{ai, pirattai, puranfai square- stalked vine, Vitis quadrangularis. Ma. piranta, piranti, paranta Cissus quadrangularis. Ka. (DCV) perande square-stalked vine. Tu. (DCV) perande id. [V. quadrangularis Wall. = C. quadrangularis Lin.] DED(S) 3433.

Ta. pirampu (in cpds. .pirappam-) rattan, Calamus rotang, switchy rattan, C vimanalis, Ma. perampu, purampu, punampu rattan. Ka. prabbali, habbe id. Te. p(dému, prabba, prabbili id., C. rotang. DED(S) 3434.

Ta. piri (-v-, -nt-; also piri) to become disjoined or parted, unfastened, be untwisted, be ripped or loosened (as a seam or texture), disagree (as persons); part (¢7.), separate, diverge, quit, depart; (-pp-, -tt-) to separate (tr.), disunite, sever, divide, untwist, untie, disentangle, solve (as a riddle), dismantle (as the thatch of a roof), distribute; pirical division, partition, threadbare or tattered condition; pirippu separation, division, es- trangement; piripu, pitivu separation, sever- ance, division, disunion, disagreement, loosen- ing, secession, gap. Ma. pirikka to sever, dismiss, divorce; piriccal separation, dismissal; piriyuka to become disjoined, separate, part with. Ko. piry- (pirc-) to separate part from part (intr, ftr.), demolish (building); pirc- (pire-) to separate (persons; fr.); pirnj- (pirnj-) to separate oneself from: piryv a place separate from another’s place. To. piry- (pirs-) to be demolished; (pirc-) to demolish; ? ptiry- (ptirs-) to disappear from sight (man over hill, etc., god dying and body is not found, sun setting); ? ptirc- (piiré-) to make (daytime, sun) to disappear (i.e. spend the day from morning until evening). Ka. hiri to separate into portions, break up, pull to pieces, demolish, pull out of, unsheathe, take (pearl) from (a string); be broken up or demolished, fall from (a bundle, a string); higgu to separate (intr), be disconnected, higgisu to separate, disjoin; higgalisu to separate or disjoin (as the legs), open wide (as an ey

Ta. piri twist, strand, wisp: puri (-v-, -nt-) to be twisted, curl, turn; n. cord, twine, rope, strand, twist (as of straw), curl, © spiral, conch. Ma. piri a twist, twining; piri- yuka to be twisted, warped; pirikka to twist; pirivu contortion, twisting; piriccal twisting; piriyan twisted; puri twisting, string; puriyuka to curl, twist. Ko. piry- (pirc-), pury- (purc-) to make (rope) by twisting. Ka. puri twisting, twist, twine, string. Kod. piri- (pirip-, piric-), biri- (birip-, biric-) to make (rope) by twisting; (Shanmugam) pirip twisting a rope. Tu. piri twist, spiral thread (as of a screw); piripuni to twist (as a rope); piripavuni to turn, twist; piriké twisting, winding, state of being shaped like the spiral thread of a screw; puri twining, twisting (as threads), coir yarn; puripu the twist as of a rope or cord; pijipuni to twist (as a rope, yarn) (suggested by BRR). Te. piri, puri twist, strand, twisting; pirigonu to be twisted; twist (tr.). Kui pripa (prit-) to roll up (tr.), roll into a roll, cylinder, tube, coil, or cone; n. act of rolling up; priu (pl. pringa) roll of string or fibre. Br. pirghing to twist (esp. a rope). Cf. 5279 Ta. valam-puri. DED(S) 3436. 4178 Konda piruri flute. Kui piroyri id. Kuwi (F.) piritri, (S.) pirudi, (Ist.) piruyi id. Kur. (Hahn) péoé id., pipe, whistle. Cf. 4197 Ta. pillai-kugal. / Cf. Pkt. pirilli- a kind of musical instrument. DEDS 672.
Te. pilaka a tuft or knot of hair. Konda _ piika, pilika pigtail, dangling ends of hair, Ktewi (F.) pilka lovelock (worn curled under the ear by males). DEDS 673.
Ta. pilli cat. Ka. pilli id. Te. pilli id. Kol. pilli id. Nk. pilli id. Pa, biley id. Konda bilay id. Kuwi (Su. P. Isr.) bileyi id. / CE. Turner, CDIAL, no. 9237(2), *billa-. DED (S) 3438.

Ta. pilli sorcery, magic. Ma. (Kanik- kar dial.; Bhattacharya) pilatti, platti sotccrer. Ko. pily, puly Toda witchcraft put on buffaloes; Kurumba witchcraft. To. _pily sorcery practised by Todas; pilyxo-rn Toda sorcerer. DED(N) 3439. 4182 Konda (BB) pir- (pif-) to peel. Pe. pir- (-t-) id. Mand. pir- (snake) to slough skin. DEDS 674. 4183 (a) Ta. pigi (-v-, -nt-) to squeeze, express, press out with hands, drip, exude, shed or pour (as rain); pinfi oilcake made of the residue of oil seeds. Ma. pizgiyuka to wring out, squecze out; pingi what is squeezed, wrung out, dregs, residue, sediment. Ko. ind- (indy-), indc- (indc-) to twist (something wet), oppress by uninterrupted action upon. To. pit ir milch buffalo (usage of ti+ dairy; or with 4165 Ta. pituftku). Ka. pigi to wring or squeeze out, press out juice, make sapless or dry, become sapless or dry; pindu, hindu to squeeze out, wring, pinch, milk, give milk, harass. Kod. pund- (pundi-) to squeeze (wet cloth, fruit, anything with liquid in it); punde wetness (as of wet clothes). Tu. pitkavuni to press out (as tamarind seeds pirika (371] pil or the testicles of animals); pinfuni to twist, turn, wring; pundiyuni, puntuni to wring (as a wet garment), press or express (as juice from fruits, etc.); purficuni, pureficuni, purecuni to squeeze (as a lemon); pindi, pundi oilcake, refuse of expressed coconut, etc. Kor. (T.) hircadi to squeeze. Te. piducu to squeeze, wring, press out; pindu id., press, milk; (K.) pindi oilcake. Kol. pi-nd-

Ta. pigukkai, purukkai dung of sheep, goats, rats, etc. Ma. pigukka excrements of rats, snakes. Ka. pirke, pikke dung of goats, sheep, deer, rats, etc.; ? (Hav.) pitte excre- ment of goat or sheep (or with 4394). Te. pidaka, piduka cake of cowdung dried for fuel. DED(S) 3441. :

Ta. pigai (-pp-, -tt-) to be emancipated from sin and births, obtain salvation, escape from evil or danger, live, get on in life; pixaippu escape, life, livelihood. Ma. pirekka to remain alive; pigeppu, picappu livelihood; pireppikka to restore, revive. Pa. pi- (pifi-) to live. Ga. (OlL.) pi-, (S.2) piy- (pif-) id. Go. pissana (Tr.) to be saved, live, earn one’s living, (Ph. W.) live, escape; pis- (Mu.) to live, be saved, be left over, (A. Ma.) to live; caus. (SR.) pisusana | to save; (W.) pisehtana to rescue; (Mu.) pish- to save (life, money, etc.); (W.) pistal alive (Voc. 2254). DED 3442.

Ta. pixai (-pp-, -tt-) to do wrong, fail, die, be missing, lost; n. fault, crime, defect, mistake; pigaippu mistake, failure; piyakku wrong, injustice; pigampu wickedness. Ma. piga fault, oversight, a fine; pigekka to err, fail, transgress; pizeppu, pizappu casualty; pigeppikka to cause to err; pirukuka to slip off, fall out of caste; pirukkuka to excom- municate. To. pe-f penalty paid to one who has been injured in a minor way (cf. Ma. pira a fine). Tu. pitkuduni, pinkuni, pifgu- luni to escape, slip out; piftkadruni, pinkavuni to cause to slip out, get on the sly; puluku, (B-K.) pulku slipping out, anything soft or greasy. Kol. pirs- (pirist-) to slip, slide; pirsip- (pirsipt-) to slide (¢r.). Pa. birj- to be slippery. Go. (Mu. Ma.) birsk-, (M.) birskand to slip, slide; (Ko.) birs- to be slippery (Voc. 2551). Kuwi (S.) pidho anai to trip. Cf. 4131 Ta. picaku. / Cf. Pkt. phillus-, phellus- to slip. ED(S, N) 3443. 4188 Konda pirgi- (-t-) to be completely covered by a cloth or upper garment; pirk- (-t-) to cover or envelop completely (as a dead body with a cloth), cover, mask. Kuwi (Su.) prik- (-h-) to cover; (F.) prigali to cover oneself; prikhali to cover; (S.) plikh’nai to close, cover; (Isr.). prik- (-h-) id. DEDS 678.

Te. piccuka sparrow. Ga. (S.?) piske id. Go. (Koya Su.) piske id. Mand. prih puti a small bird. Kuwi (T.) pirska sparrow; (Isr.) priska pota a kind of bird. DEDS(N) 675. _ 4191 Pe. priski spark. Mand. prihe, prihki id. Kuwi (Kasipur) priska, (Isr.) pyisi id. Br. prishk id. DEDS(N) 679. : 4192 Kui prihpa (priht-) to be tall, high, lofty, grow tall; pripa (prit-) to be tall; prisa tall, high, lofty. Kuwi (S.) pli- to be big; plinai to grow. DEDS(N) 680.

Te. p(régu entrail, gut, bowel. Kal. pe-gul (pl.) intestines; (SR.) pegt (pi. pegil) id. Nk. pégul id. Vk. (Ch.) pig(g)u intestine. Pa. pirul, piruvul (p/.) smaller intestines. Ga. (Oll.) purug (pl. purgul) stomach, intestines; (S.) puddug (pl pudgul) stomach; (S.?) pudg (pudug) stomach; pudgul intestines. Go. (Tr. SR. Y. W. Ph.) pir belly, stomach; (Ch.) pir (obl. pif-) belly; (Ph.) pital ayand to be pregnant (Voc. 2774). 2? Br. pid belly, stomach. DED(S) 3445.

Ta. pil (pilv-, pint-; pilp-, pift-) to burst open, be rent or cut, be broken to pieces, disagree; cleave asunder, divide, crush; pila (-v-, -nt-) to be split, cleaved, rent, cracked, be disunited, gape; split (tr.), cleave, rend, tear open, part asunder, pierce; pilappu crevice, cleft, splitting; pilavu cleft, crevice, gap, division, piece, disunion, splitting; pilavai piece; pilaccu lath; pilal pudendum muliebre. Ma. pilaruka to burst asunder (intr.), split; split (tr.), cleave; pilarcca splitting, a cleft; pilarkka, pilakka to split (tr), cleave, rend (also intr.); pilarppu, pilappu cleft, rent, crack, bit of a nut; pilukkuka to open (the lips); pelika to burst (as boils); pelikka to burst (tr.), split, disembowel (fish); spirt forth; peli a chip. ? Ko. pi-f- (pi-ty-), pi-nf- (pi-nty-) to separate joints of carcass. Ka. piligu, hilgu, hili to break, crack, burst; pelavu a flaw; ?pedasu brittleness. Tw. pulevu a crack. ? Te. p(r)@lu to explode, detonate, break, crack, burst, be fried or parched, break out in prickly heat or any eruption, have pimples; p(r)élucu, p(r)étcu piliru [372] . pidukannu to cause to break, burst or explode; p(r)élitu fried or parched grain. ? Kol. pe-l- (pe-It-) (grain) pops when parched over fire; pe-lip- (pe-lipt-) to make (grain) to pop. Pa. pil- to crack (intr.); pid- (pitf-) (boil) bursts, (fire) splutters, explodes; pidip- (pidit-) to make to burst, (hen) hatches eggs, pop rice; pindp- (pindt-) to break, extract (tooth). Ga. Ss.) pili cen- to

Ta. piliru (piliri-) to trumpet, roar (as an elephant); 7. (also piliral) great noise; pilirru (pilizri-) to make a roaring noise. Te. pilucu to call, invite, (K. also) name, shout; pilipincu to send for, invite, call; pilupu, pilupudu calling, a call, invitation. Ga. (P.) pilup- (pilut-) to thunder (subject mogul cloud). Kui pri cry of agony, scream; pri inba, pri pri inba to cry aloud in agony, scream in terror or pain. ? Malt. pinde to sound (as an instrument, cries of animals or birds). Cf. 5433 Ta. vil. DED(S) 3447.

Ta. pillah-kuzal, pull@f-kugal flute. Ka. pilla sound in imitation of that of a pipe or flute; pillan-govi pipe or flute. Te. pillat- govi, pilla-grovi, pilla-grolu, _pillana-grolu, pillana-grovi flute, flageolet. Cf. 4178 Konda piruzi. DED(S) 3448.

Ta. pil tender beauty of a child, pillai child, son, youth, daughter, young of many animals; title of Vellala caste; pil embryo, tender ears of corn, tenderness. Ma. pilla child, infant, young of animals, small fruit. To. pil, in: moet fil® son’s wife (not being one’s sister’s daughter; for most, see 4616); pel xo+f- (xo-t-) to be married to a man (ko-f- [woman] marries [man]; see 1416); towfily the lower-ranking moiety of the Todas (who are priests of the sacred dairies, ie. servants [*pilli] of the gods [téw, DBIA no. 219]). Ka. pille, pila, pilla child, young of any animal, that which is smail or petty. Kod. palle female of various wild animals (also dog, horse). Tu. pillé child, baby; pulli grandchild. Te. pilla child, baby, young of any animal, girl; small, little, young; pilaka, piluka a young one, young shoot; pilladi girl, lass, young woman. Kol. pilla baby, daughter, woman; pilla pedda females and males. Vk. pilla girl, daughter. Go. (A.) pilla, (Y.) pila, (Driberg) pilal young of animal; (Mu.) pila girl, young one of animals; (Ma.) pila, (Ko.) pilla child; (L.) pila id., young of an animal (Voce. 2253). Konda pila child, a small one. Pe. pilka young shoot of tree. Kur. pello female child, female not arrived at puberty, bride or young woman (before she has had children), maidservant; | pell maidservant. Malt. peli woman; pelo female (of plants). Br. pilldta poor child, small child, orphan, miserable. / Cf. Skt. pillika- chicken (Manasollasa); Pali_pillaka-, pillika-

Go. (Tr. W. Ph. Mu.) pirr, (A. G. S.) pir, (SR.) pir, (Ma.) peg, par, (L.) pegh rain (Voc. 2239). Konda piru (pl. piRku) id. Mand. piy id. Kui piju id. Kuwi (F.) piyd (pl piska), (S.) piyu, (Su. P.) piyu, (Isr.) piyu (pl. piska) id. Br. pir id.; pir dassing to rain. Cf. 4132 Ta. picir and 4407 Ta. pey. From DED(S) 3610. 4200 (a) Ta. pirag (-v-, -nt-) to tremble; pirarcci, piragvu shivering, trembling; pirak- kam awe, fear; pirappu fear, alarm. Ka. piriki coward. Te. pisiki id.; timid, cowardly. (b) Ka. pukkalu, pukku fear, timidity; pukkala, pukka timid man, coward. Kod. pukkilé coward. Tu. pukky, pukku fear, timidity; pukke, pukkele coward. Pa, (S.) purki timid. DED 3450.

Ta. pirag (-v-, -nt-) to be irregular, misplaced, out of order, be dislodged, dis- located; pirageci disarrangement, disorder. Ko. pern- (perd-) (joint) is dislocated; perf- (perfy-) to dislocate (joint) by force; (from DED 3516).
Ka. piri, piru flesh. Te. perasu id. DEDS 681.

Ma. pirukku gnat, mosquito. Kod. puriki mosquito. 7? Go. (Ma.) porki, (Ko.) pork (pl. -i), (A. Y. S.) purki, (D.) porki, (Ch. Tr. W. Ph.) parki louse (Voc. 2419); (Koya Su.) porki (pl) id. Kur. putUngi, putuggi midge. Malt. putgi sand-fly. / Cf. Skt. puttika- gnat. DED(N) 3451.

Tu. pidukannu blinking of the eyes. Go. (Ph.) pirani eyes to lose sight; (Mu.) pir- (eyes) to be blind; (M.) konda_ pirta blind (Voc. 2232); (Tr. Ph.) pihtana to shut the eyes, wink; (Ma.) pi?- to close the eyes (Voc. 2260). ? To. piry- (pits-) (eye) is blind. ? Br. pirring to twitch involuntarily (of eye, lip, heart) regarded as foretelling future pin {373] pi events (Bray: < IA, e.g. Lahnda, Panj. phur-). DEDS(N) 682.

Ta. pin back, rear part, end (as in place or time), that which is subsequent in time, younger brother; afterwards, subse- quently; pinpu that which is posterior in place or time; afterwards; pinnam, pinnar, pinné afterwards; pinnar those who come after, followers; successor, younger brother, the dras; pinnavan younger brother, youngest son; fem. pinnaval; pinnan younger brother; pinni younger sister, mother’s younger sister; pinnum moreover, again; pinnai that which is subsequent in time, younger brother, younger sister; moreover, afterwards; pinru (pinri-) to retreat, fall behind, fall below (in rank or quality), turn back, go astray, change; adv. afterwards; pinrai, pirrai next day, tomorrow; afterwards; pinri again, besides; pirakku back, rear; piraku id.; afterwards, presently, backward; pirku posteriority in space or time; pirral retreating; pirri that which goes behind; pitti back, hind part; pintu (pinti-) to go behind, be behind, happen subsequently, be subse- quent (in time, birth, or origin), be inferior, be tardy, delay; puram, puran back; purampu back of a person. Ma. pin backside, behind, after; other; pinné behind, after, yet, then, consecutively; pimpu the backside, rear; back, behind, after; piraku the backside; after; pirra next day; puram the back. Ko. pin again, other; pind- (pindy-) to walk behind; pibal afterwards, behind; piba+r (Obl piba-t-) behind, backwards; pitl, pite-+l the back; piga-r (obl piga-t-, piga-t-) place behind, time after; pebi+ followin

Ta. pinnu (pinni-) to plait, braid, lace, knit, weave, entwine, bind, embrace; become united; pinnal braiding, etc., web, entangle- ment, matted hair; pinnakam braided hair; pinnai hair; pannu (panni-) to weave. Ma. pinnuka to plait, twist, wreath; pinnal em- broilment. To. pin- (piny-) (hair) is matted; weave (basket), plait (hair). Ga. (S.*) pannap- to weave. Kur. pandna to roll and twist together filaments into threads. Br. pinning to be twisted (or with 4160(a) Ta. pipai). DED(S) 3454. 4208 Kur. (Hahn) pinn stick. Malt. pinu stick, staff. DEDS 683. 4209 Kur. pinni aunt (wife of father’s younger brother), niece (elder brother’s daughter). Malt. peni mother’s elder sister. Cf. 4205 Ta. pin (esp. Ta. pinnavan, pinnaval, etc.). DEDS(N) 684.

Ta. pi excrement, faeces; 4-ppi cowdung; piccu (picci-) to purge, have loose pikam [374] pili motions; piccal looseness of the bowels; pi-kkai, piccaf-kai, piccai-kkai left hand. Ma. pi excrements of man, birds, etc.; wax of the ear, mucus. Ko. pis excrement. Ka. pi, pé man’s dung. Tu. pi ordure, excrements of human beings, dogs, pigs, etc., ambi cowdung; picca, piccé left (hand). Te. piyi, piyyi excrement. Kol, (SR.) piya dirt, night soil. Nk, pi excrement. Pa. pi id. Ga, (OIL) pi, (S.4) piyu id. Go, (Tr.) pin human ordure (Voce, 2228); (Ch.) ping, (W. Ph.) pifg, (G. Ma.) piggu, (Mu. S. Ko.) pin excrement (Voe. 2262); (Koya T.) piyyu id. Konda pigqu (pl.) id. Pe. pin id. Mand. pig id. Kui piu (pl pinga) id. Kuwi (F.) piga, (Su. P. Isr.) pinga id. Kur. pik excrements, earwax. Malt. piku excrements. Br, pi excreta, esp. of human beings and birds. Cf. 2402(b) Pa. carpi. DED(S) 3455.

Ta. pikam, vikam padlock; ring. Ma. vikam padlock. Ka. biga, biyyaga id., lock. Kod. bi-+ga lock. Tu. biga id. Te. bigamu id., key. DED 3456. 4212 Ka, piku to pull out, pluck up, tear, excommunicate. Te. piku to pull out, root up, pluck out. Kol, (Pat., p. 147) pikeng to uproot. Vk, pik- to pull out. Ga. (S.3) pikap- to pull, pluck. Go. (Ma.) pihk- to pluck (Voc. 2279). 2? Ta. pig (-v-, -nt-) to uproot. ? Ma. piruka to root up, pluck off. DED(S) 3457.

Ko. pieg- (pi-gy-) to part (intr.; hair, bushes); pi-k- (pi-ky-) id. (¢7.). Ka. pikku to separate, disentangle, comb; (Hav.) pinku to be dislocated, slip off. Kor. (O.) pigki to slip out. DED 3459. 4214 (a) Ma. picca, piccan what is small, dwarfish, immature. Ka. picu shortness, smallness; picce shortness or deficiency in measure or weight. Tu. pificily thin, slender. Te. picca low, base, mean; short, deficient, false. (6) Ta. picca-kkatti, piccat-katti com- bined pocket knife and iron style, long country knife. Ma. piccat-katti a common knife. Kod. pi-ce-katti dagger worn in sash. ? Tu. bisatti knife. DED (S) 3461. 4215 Ta, piccu (picci-), pirecu (pircci-) to squirt, milk (as a cow); piccal, pircecal sytinging: pirccah-kural syringe. Ma. piccuka to squirt, syringe. Ka, picu id.; , squirting. DED 3462. 4216 Ma, pili straw. Go. (M.) pici, (Ko.) picil, pisii paddy straw (Voc. 2217). Pe. piyal straw. Kuwi (P. T.) picu, (F.) pict id.; (S.) picu grass; (Isr.) picu grass, straw. Cf.
Ka. bidu mass, pile, heap, crowd; bidaya a mass, great number. Tu. bidy gather- ing, assembly. ? To. per, in: ir fey id- (if-) to drive buffaloes in a herd. DED(S) 3463.
Tu. bidu dross, alloy of iron. Te. bidu iron filings or dust. DED 3464.
Ta. pitu waste, uncultivated land. Ka. bidu, bir(u) id. Te. bidu id; waste, useless; (Inscr.) bedda-cénu a dry land, a waste uncultivated land. DED 3472.
Te. bida poor, destitute, indigent, penurious, wretched; bidagillu to become poor or weak; bidatanamu, bidarikamu poverty, indigence, destitution, penury. Konda bidur poor, wretched. DEDS 686.

Ta. pir abundant flow, milk flowing from a woman’s breast; piram mother’s milk; pir-itu to stream, flow (as milk), gush out (as blood); pir-enal onom. expr. of streaming. Ma. pira inilk of grated coconuts. Tu. piruni to exude, percolate. DED(S) 3465.

Ka. pit to suck, suck in or up, drink. Te. pir(u)cu, pil(u)cu to suck, suck in or up, drink with a noise, inhale, take in with the breath. Ga. (S.3) pirsap- to inhale (< Te.). Go. (Koya Su.) pirs- to breathe (< Te.). DED(N) 3466.

Ta. pir, piram, pirai sponge gourd; pirkku id., strainer vine, Luffa acutangula. Ma, pira, piram, pirakam, piccakam, picci sponge gourd. Ka. hire, hiri, ire L. acutangula Roxb., Cucumis acutangulus Lin. Tu. piré _L. acutangula, Te. bira, bira kaya snake gourd, Trichosanthes anguina; (B.) céti bira bitter cucumber. Kol, (SR.) bira gourd. Pa. bira L. acutangula. Konda bira, bira-dolu Tricosanthes anguina. [L. acutangula Roxb. = C. acutangulus Wall.}] DED(S) 3467.

Te. piri straw, thatch. Kol (Kin.) piri straw. Nk. (Ch.) piri id. Pa. pir grass, straw. Ga. (S.) pir(u), (P. S.”) pir straw. Go. Ph.) piri, (Tr. W.) piri, (D. Ma. S.) piri straw; (Mu.) pir, piri id., fodder (Voc. 2275); (LuS.) peeree rice-straw; ? (Tr.) ptiral-kafa a kind of thin, poor grass (Voc. 2325). Konda piri hay. Pe. piri straw, thatching grass. Mand. piri straw. Kui piri id. Kuwi (Isr.) piri reeds used to make rope fibre; piri dori rope from reed; (F.) piri dori rope (grass). DED(S) 3468.

Ta. pili peacock’s feather. Ma. pili id. Ka. pili id., peacock’s tail, eye in a peacock’s tail. Kod. pi-li peacock’s tail feather. Tu. pili id.; bila tail. Te. pili rudder, (K. also) pea- cock’s feather. Nk. (Ch.) pika feather, pea- cock’s tail. Ga. (S.2) pince tail of peacock. Kui piseri, plieri tail feather of a peacock; pieli peacock. Malt. pice tail of a peacock; picale peacock in full plume. / Cf. Skt. piccha- peacock’s tail; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8151. DED (S) 3469.

Ta. pili toe-ring of a woman. Ma. pili id. Ka. pilli silver ring worn on the second toe by married women. Tu. pilli, pillé silver ring worn on the toes. Te. pillani, pill@di sort of ring worn by women on the fourth toe. DED 3470. piel (375] pukai

Ko. piel weakly; pi-l(n) a weakling; fem. pi-ly. To. pu-ly good-for-nothing fellow. Tu. pili dry, thin, light. Te. pila thin, lean, slender, stunted, dried, shrunken; boli worthless, pithless. Kui bili very tired, ex- hausted, emaciated. DED(S) 3471.

Ta. piru (piri-) to be torn; tear, rip, split, lacerate; m. rent, tear; piral, pirral tear- ing, rending, slitting, rent, rip. Ma. piru torn, bad, vile. Ko. pi-r- (pi-ry-) to cut (animal’s belly), press way through obstruction. DED 3473.
Ta. pukar tawny colour, brown; brightness, light, colour; por tawny colour. Ma, pukar dun colour. Ka. pogar shine, brightness, lustre, colour, DED (S) 3475,

Ta. pukal (pukalv-, pukanr-) to say, state, sound; 2. word, mode of singing. Ma. pukaluka to speak. ? To. po-@- (only 2nd stem, recorded in song; see TGT VI. 4.23) (barking deer) barks. Ka. pugal, pugil the cuckoo’s note. Pa. pokk- to speak, tell. Ga. (Oll.) pok- to say; (S.) pokk- to say, tell. Kuwi (Mah.) pokh- to cry (of sambar). Cf.
Ta. pukalvu pride, arrogance. Ka. puggu, purgu id. Te. pogaru id. Kuwi (S.) pokh’nai, (Ist.) pok- (-h-) to swagger. DED (S) 3477.

Ta. pukag (-v- -nt-) to praise, extol, applaud; n. praise, eulogy, fame, exploit; pukagcci, pukagvu praise, adulation; pukartal adoration; pukagmai praiseworthiness, fame; pukal fame, renown. Ma. pukaruka to be praised; praise; pukaz, pukagca praise, renown; pukazttuka to praise. Ka. pogar to praisc; n, praise, renown; pogarke, pogarte praising, praise. Ju. pugaruni, pugaluni, pogaruni, pogaluni to praise, eulogize, flatter; pugarigé, pogulu praise, flattery. Te. pogadu, povadu to praisc, applaud, eulogize, flatter; pogadika, pogad(i)ta praise, fame, flattery. Kui ponga (pdngi-) to be sounded abroad, be famed, praised, made known; ». fame; pdpka (< pok-p-; podkt-) to make known, proclaim, preach; 7. proclamation. Malt. pogole renown, praise; pogolare to be praised; pogolatre to praise, extol. Cf. 4233 Ta. pukal- DED(S) 3478.

Ka. pukali, pukuli, pukku, mukali, mukuli, mukli female organ of generation, anus; piku female organ of generation. Tu. pukuli anus. Te. (SAN) ptku female organ of generation. Konda pttku id. DED(S) 3479.

Ta. pokafi, pukifi, piti ear ornament worn by women on the top of the helix. Ka bugadi, bugudi ear ornament of females worn in the upper part of the ear. Tu. bugudi female’s ornament worn in the tip of the ear. Te. bugada, bogada gold pin worn by women in the tip of the ear. /Cf. Mar. bugdi ear ornament. DED 3480.

Ta. puku (pukuv-, pukk-) to reach, attain, enter, commence, go, ride, happen; (-pp-, -tt-; also pukuttu [pukutti-]) to cause to enter, insert; pukaftu (pukaffi-) to insert, introduce, infuse, instil; pukal entering, residence, help, refuge; pukali newcomer, settler; pukavu entering, mounting (as a vehicle); pukufi doorway, estuary; pukuti front door, occurrence, way, income; pukutu (pukuti-) to enter, happen; pukuru (-y-, -nt-) to enter; pukir mouth of a river; pukkil abode, place of refuge; pir (-v-, -nt-) to enter; pitu (puti-) id.; ptlvi (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to enter. Ma. pukuka (pukk-) to enter, begin; pukukka to put into; pukkikka to make to enter or receive; ptikuka to enter, reach a time; pitkikka to make to enter. Ko. uk- (ug- also in the negative; uky-) to enter. To. pux- (puk-) id. Ka. pugu, pogu (pokk-), hugu, hogu (hokk-), ogu id.; pugil entering, door, arrival. Tu. pogguni to enter, penetrate; poggivuni, pogpavuni to cause to enter or penetrate, pierce. DED(S) 3481.
Ir. bugari, bugiriya a large bamboo flute. PalKu. bugiri bamboo flute. AlKu. buguri id. Ko. bugi-r flute. To. puxury Toda flute. Ka. (Badaga) buguri id. ? Cf. 4368 Ta. puri. DED(S) 3482.

Ta. pukai smoke, mist, vapour, steam; (-v-, -nt-) to smoke, emit vapour or steam; (-pp- -tt-) to cause to smoke; pukaiccal- smoke; pukaippu smoking, fumigation; pukai- vu smoking; pukar mist, fog, haze. Ma. puka smoke, vapour; pukayuka to reek, look dim, be darkened by smoke; pukekka to fumigate, preserve or dry by smoke. Ko, peg smoke; pog, po» tobacco. To. pax smoke, tobacco. Ka. poge to smoke; ». smoke, steam, vapour. Kod. poge smoke. Tu. puge, pogé smoke, vapour, fume; pugeyuni to smoke; pugepuni, pogepuni to fumigate. Te. poga, pova smoke, fume; pogayu, povayu to smoke; pogucu, pogacu, (K. also) pogapu to smoke, fumigate, fry, season with spices; pogapu, pogupu frying, seasoning with spices. Kol. pog smoke, tobacco. Nk. pog smoke. Ga. (Oll.) pog, (S.) pogu id.; (S.2) pogasur soot (see 2686 Te. cflru); (S.2) pdgu smoke; pdga tobacco; pogd- (pogud-) to fill with smoke. Go. (M:) pogo, (L.) poga tobacco (Voc. 2372b); (A. SR. 8.) poya, (G. Mu. Ma.) poyo, (M.) pof smoke; (Mu.) poy- to smoke (intr.) (Voe. pukkaliyuni 2398); (ASu.) poyyUs- to season (curry). Konda pogo smoke. Kui (K.) pokari_ id. Kuwi (F.) bhoiyi, (S.) bai, (Su.) bdyi, (sr.) boyf, (P.) biy, (Mah., p.75) po’kari id. DED(S, N) 3483.

Tu. pukkaliyuni to gargle, rinse the mouth. Te. pukkilincu id. Pe. puski ki- to swill out the mouth. Kuwi (F.) gtti prokhali to rinse out the mouth; (Isr.) prok- (-h-) to wipe mouth. ? 7a. pilku (pilki-} to gargle, spit, flow, drip (as dew), exude (as honey from flowers); pilirru (pilirri-) to gargle (¢r.), drizzle, drop, sprinkle, let out (as milk from the udder); pilir (-pp-, -tt-) to gargle; pilirru (pilirri-) to spit out, vomit. DED(S) 3484.

Ka. buggi cheek. Te. pukkili inside of the cheek; pukkédu mouthful; bukkédu a mouthful, as of any liquid; bukka inside or hollow of the mouth, mouthful; bugga cheek, inside of the cheek. Kol. pukli cheek. Nk. pukli id. Pe. bukka id. Ga. (P.) buggal (pl buggasil) id. Go. (M.) buka, (L.) phuka, bhuka, (G. Mu. Ma. Ko.) bukka, (S.) bugga id. (Voc. 2558). Konda buku mouthful; bugli cheek. Kui pdkul round-faced, plump; bukali fat-faced, with cheeks puffed out; ? britkera swollen-faced man or boy; fem. brikeri, Kuwi (F. Isr.) biiga, (S. Su.) bugga cheek. Kur, pok’6 chubby-cheeked, DED(S) 3485.
Te. buggi ashes, dust. Kol (Kin.) buggi ashes. Ga. (S.) buggi earth. DED 3486.

Ta. puikan fool. Ma. puitkan id. DED 3487. 4245 Ka, pucakkane suddenly and with a smali noise, as when spittle is ejected with force, a mouse quickly enters its hole, a lancet is quickly inserted into a boil, a stick is moved up and down in mud, etc. Tu. pucukku the force, as of spitting. DED 3488.

Ka. bus, busu, bussu, bos a sound to imitate the puffing or hissing of a serpent, of a pair of bellows, of the snorting of cattle, etc. Kod. bus ku-f- to hiss. Tu. busu, busu- busu, bussu gasping, panting, hissing. Te. busa hiss of a serpent, hissing, snorting, snoring, a deep ‘breath, a sigh; busabusa noise of the boiling of water; busabusal-adu to hiss; bussu hiss of a snake. Kol. (Pat., p. 143) puskarileng to hiss. DED 3489. 4247 Konda puski brain. Kuwi (Su. P. T. Ist.) biski, ($.) bhijki, bhiski id. DEDS 688.
Te. pucca colocynth, bitter apple, Cucumis colocynthis. Ga. (S.2) pucca kaya id. DEDS 689.

Ka. buda, budu, boda sound in imitation of that produced by a vessel, etc., when immersed into water, or by water issuing from the spout of a vessel, and of that produced by a small rattle-drum; budakkane with the sound of buda; budabudike, budu- budike a small rattle-drum. Tu. budubudu in drops. Te. budabuda with a bubbling noise; [376] pufai budukku, budukkuna sound produced by anything suddenly sinking in water; budu- budukka a small rattling drum. /MBE 1969, pp.294f., no. 32, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDJAL, no. 9278, Skt. budbuda-, budabuda. DED 3490. 4250 7a. pufal, pufalai, pufdl snake- gourd, Trichosanthes anguina. Ma. puttal, pittal id. Ka. pofla, padala, pafala, pafla id.; T. dioeca Roxb. Kod. (Shanmugam) padala kayi snake-gourd. Tu. pafla 7. anguina. Te. pofla 7. colubrina (? anguina]. Ga. (S) budrugi kaya gourd; (S.*) pofla snake-gourd (< Te.). Go. (Ko.) pofla a kind of gourd (Voe. 2383). Konda pofla snake-gourd. Kui pudra a kind of pumpkin. / Cf. Skt. patola-, pafu-, pafuka- T. dioeca; Turner, CDIAL, no. 7698. DED(S) 3491.

Ka. puduku, puduiku, huduku, hudaku, hudiku to search or feel for with the hands, grope in search of, seck. Te. puduku, punuku to pick up, handle, take with the five fingers, give in small quantities, pinch, feel; punikirincu to give in small quantities or insufficiently, hand{e, finger, fect; pudisili handful; pudisilincu to hold in the hollowed palm; (K.) pudikili holding, taking, handful. Kol. pudk- (pudukt-) to touch; (Pat., p. 131) pudkeng to feel by touch. Nk. purk- to touch. Go. (Koya T.) pursk- to feel for. Kuwi (F.) briicali (brit-) to snatch; (Isr.) bru?- (-t-) id., grab; brf- (-t-) to touch. / Cf. Mar. hudakné to search (< mod. Ka.). DED(S, N) 3492,

Ta. putai (-pp-, -tt-) to winnow, thresh, beat, strike, beat (drum), flap (wings); n. blow; pufaippu stroke, sifting. Ko. porc- (pore-) to winnow (rice) with long up-and- down strokes to remove the husks after pestling. To. purf- (purt-) to winnow, flap (a branch of leaves in cleaning house, in wiping off Kurumba witchcraft), Gwaterfall) dashes down, (fever) attacks; pur beating; tomk/ tobk wad (wad8-) to beat drum, publish by drum (< Badaga; see 3082). Ka. pode to strike, beat, throw (as a stone), drive (cattle, cart), beat off, prevent, fell (a tree); n. beat- ing, etc.; (PBh.) podar to tremble; podarpu trembling, quivering. Kod. poda- (podap-, podat-) to flutter quickly to and fro (hen with head cut off, persons struggling on ground, mouth in chewing). Tu. pudepuni, podepuni to winnow, fan; podytonuni to struggle, strive, flutter, flap the wings, bustle, be in a hurry; pudipuni quarrel, fight; boffuni to beat (as a drum), hammer (as metal), knock {as a door); caus, botfavuni. Te. poducu to fight; pofu battle. Ga. (S.) podu fight. Pe. pod- (poft-/port-) to hit (not to miss), (rain) to fall. DED(N) 3493.

Ta. putai (-pp-, -tt-) to swell, dilate, puff up (as from a blow); #. bulkiness, pro- tuberance: pufaippu swelling, protuberance from a blow; pufavi Cpp-, -tt-), pufali Cpp-, -tt-) to be swollen (as from a blow). Kod. putai [377] putty pudi- (pudip-, pudit-) (eg, part of body) swells. Tu. pudké plump, fat; pudke, bodde a stout man. Go. (Tr.) porana to swell (of dough), grow big (of a man, physically and esp. socially); (Ph.) poyana@ to swell; (Driberg) ponitle to swell (of grain) (Voc. 2453). DED

Ta. putai (-pp-, -tt-) to utter a loud noise, roar, rattle; nm. sound, noise (as from a stroke). Kol. pod- (poft-) (dog) barks; i+sa pod- to whistle. Nk. por- to bark. Ga. (P.) porp- (port-) id. DED (S) 3495.

Ta. pufai, putam side, place. Ma puta side. Ka (Hav.) hode id. Tu. pudé border, edge, brink, margin, brim, side, interval, space; podataré the side-bone of the skull. ? Te. peda side, page. DED(S) 3496. 4256 Ta, putaivai, pufavai cloth, garment, sari; puffam, puffakam cloth. Ma. pufa, pufava woman’s cloth of 8 to 10 cubits, an awning. Ko. porv sari. Ka. huttige, hutguge man’s raiment, woman’s cloth; (PBh.) puftige a kind of dress. Kod. podea sari. Te. puttamu cloth. /Cf. Skt. phutgika- a kind of cloth; BIS phujfaka- id. DED (S) 3497.
Te. putta a collection, a number, lot, heap, crowd. Konda pufa (K., texts, p. 43) near mound. Kui pufa heap, collection, pile, stack.

Ta. poti that which is small, a little child; potiyan boy; insignificant person. Ka. Puffa, pufti, puta smallness, shortness, little- ness; small, etc.; a small, short man; huduga boy, child; hudugi girl Tu. pufta small, little, diminutive; (B-K.) puttu small, chubby. Te. poftfi, pofi short, dwarfish; a dwarf; budugu short; buduta a little one, a child; short; budutaédu a little boy; botfe a little girl, a lass; botfédu a little boy, a Ha, bodiga a boy, a child. Pa. pifit little. Ga. (S.?) putti small. Go, (SR.) putti short (Voc. 2290). Kui bofoli short, thickset, stumpy. Kuwi (F.) potila, (Isr.) potila a short man, a dwarf; fem. (F.) poti (pl potisika). Kur. pudda short (not tall), too short; puddna to be too small or too short for. Malt, pufgond a very small Fenny of fluid. ? Br. pafak short, stunted. Cf. Skt. putt to be small (Turner, CDIAL, no. 8256); Pkt. (DNM) phidda- dwarf. DED (S) 3498. Paras Pe, pu a to set fire to, kindle. ui putpa (puft-), purpa (purt-) to roast. DEDS 690. pur
Ma. putfa fox, jackal. Tu, puffé id. DEDS 691.
Ta. puffi measure of capacity (750 Madras pati), land measure (8 to 114 acres), bazaar weight (20 manu = 500 Ib.). Te. putti measure of capacity (20 ta&mu), weight of

Ta. puftil quiver, sheath, basket, winnow. Ma. putfil thick mat serving as receptacle or covering of the body. Ka. pufti, bugfi, butte basket; (K.?) pudike, puduke id., a case. Tu. putti small round basket; pudayi, pudayi, butfi basket. Te. puti flower-basket; pufika, puftika, putike, puffike small basket; puffi circular basket- boat covered with leather; buffa, buffi, buftika basket; boffa large cylindrical basket for storing grain. Ga. (S.3) butqu basket. Go. (Ko.) buful basket with lid (Voc. 2564). Konda buti a small basket. Kui pufi large basket, Kuwi (F.) pitka basket (larger); (Isr.) pufka small basket. /Cf. Mar. bufff basket. DED(S, N) 3500.

Ko. uf- (uc-) to be born, (plant, hair) sprouts (< Badaga); ufe- (ufe-) to create, bear, beget; uf beauty of body (esp. shining complexion), facts of a matter. To. puf- (puty-) to grow (intr.; grass, tree, hair); uf- (ufy-) to be born (in songs; < Badaga); uf creation, birth, physique, beauty. Ka. putfu, huffu, uffu to arise, originate, come into existence, be born; nm. birth, origin, progeny, family; puffisu, hugfisu to cause to be born, create. Kod, putf- (putfi-) to be born, (seed) sprouts. Tu. putfuni to be born, come into existence, originate, spring up, be produced; putfavuni to produce, originate, cause to be born. Te. puftu to be born; produced, arise, come into existence; 7. birth; puftincu to create, generate, produce; puffuka, puffugu, puffuvu birth, creation, origin. Kol. (SR.) putf- to be born. Nk. puft- id. Pa. puff- id. Ga. (S.) puft-tr- id. Go. (Tr.) puffana id., occur, be found, be met with; (Y. G. Mu. M. S. Ko.) puf- to be born, be got, be found; caus. (SR.) pufusana to acquire: (ChD.) pufsutana to earn; (Ph.) pursahtana to get; (W.) pursahtand to meet; (Mu.) putih/ puth- to give birth to (Voc. 2289). Konda puf- to be born, be produced, be got; putu ilu, pudgu ilu birthplace, parents’ house; pudgu masu birth-mark, mole. DED(S) 3501.
