Ta. (lex.) utu oar, boatman’s pole. To. put stirring stick. Ka. puftu wooden ladle or spoon, paddle; huftu, uffu paddle. DED 3502, 503. 4265A Ta. putfi flask, bottle. Ko. poty bottle. To. puty id. Ka buddi glass bottle. Te. buddi, buddiga bottle, flask. Go. (SR.) puegi earthen vessel (Voc. 2566). DED(S)

Ta. puffa, puttai swelled testicle, elephantoid scrotum. Ka. budde rising or swelling, swollen testicle. Te. budda bubble, rupture, testicle; (K.) madda, modda penis. Kol. budde genitals (male or female, esp. penis), (Kin.) testicles. Pa. budda genitals. Go. (M.) moda penis (Voc, 2978). Kuwi (F.) budda testicle (animal); (Isr.) buda testicle, penis. DED(S) 3504. pufki-pitté [378] puy

Ta. pun wound, ulcer, raw sore, scar. Ma. pun sore, ulcer, wound. Ko. pun sore, wound in process of healing. Ka. pun wound, ulcer, sore, boil. Kod. punni sore (not an open wound). Tu. pudi sore, ulcer, abscess; pundely matter from a sore. Te. pundu sore, abscess, ulcer, boil. Kol pun (pl pundl) wound, pussy sore. Vk, pun (pl. punkul) boil, wound. Vk, (Ch.) pun wound. Go, (A. S.) pun boil; (Ko.) pundu wound (Voce. 2300). Konda pundu (pl putku) id. Kui pudu (pl putka) pimple, dry itch. Kuvwi (Isr.) piu scar, mark; pimples, boil; ptgka a- to have pimples or swelling. Malt. punu sore, wound, DED(S) 3506. 4269 Ka, (Hav.) huni borders of a rice- field. Kod. kippuni lower level in a field; meppuni upper level in a field (cf. 1619, 5086). Tu. puni the border of a field.
Ka. punija a kind of insect. Te. piddju, pidadu, pindju, pinidu a tick.
Ta. punai, panai bamboo. Ko. pen bamboo milkpot. To. pin id. Kod. punda bamboo. DED 3507.

Ka. punda refractory, turbulent; a free-booter, rascal, lawless fellow, a licentious man; pundu, pundutana lawless proceedings, brigandage, depravity. Kod. pundi quarrel, pundé quarrelsome man. Tu. punda mis- chievous, quarrelsome, wicked. /Cf. Mar. pid refractory, turbulent. DED 3508.
Ta. puntai pudendum muliebre. Ko. pid id. To. pidy penis. Kuwi (F,) pitki male organ. Cf. 4379 Ta. ptiru. DED 3509.
Ta. putavu, puta, putavam, puta door, gate. Ka, pudi the side of a door. DED 3510.

Ta. putu, putiya new; putitu that which is new or wonderful; first sheaves of a tice crop (offered to Laksmi); putiyar, putiya- var, putiyOr newcomers, guests; putir first sheaves; putinam newness, novelty, wonderful thing; putukku (putukki-) to renovate, adorn; putuppi (-pp- -tt-) to renovate, repair, re- model, modernize; putumai newness, novelty, strangeness, a wonder, plenty, excess, inten- sity, brightness; putuvatu anything new; putuval land newly brought under cultivation; putuvor strangers, inexperienced persons; putai novelty; puttapputiya, puttamputiya brand-new, very recent; puttapputumai brand- new or very recent thing; puttan new person or thing; putt@l novelty; stranger, strange woman; god; punar newness; ? puniru recency of delivery (as of a woman), that which is recently born, newness. Ma. putu new, fresh; putukuka to be renewed; putukkam newness, a wonderful thing; putukkuka to renew, mend; putuma a novelty, wonderful sight, an entertaining story; puttan a fresh, new thing; puttari new rice used in ceremonies. Ko. pud new; ock ritually new after purification. To. pud, puOn new. Ka. posa, posatu, hosa, hosatu, hosadu, hostu that which is new; newness, freshness, beauty, novelty, wonder- fulness; new, etc.; posamba, hosaba a new man. Kod. pudume wonder; puttéri rice harvest festival; (Shanmugam) pudiyé new (masc.). Tt. posa new, novel, fresh, recent, strange, wonderful; posaty that which is new, fresh; newly; posataye, posabe a new man, novice; (Shanmugam) fem.
Ka. buddanige, buddali, buddalike, budli bottle made of skin to hold oil, ghee, etc. Tu. budulé, buddali, buddoli skin bottle. / Cf. Mar. budla a bottle or vessel made of skin, DEDS 694.

Ta. puy (-v-, -nt-) to be pulled out, torn off, wrested, disappear; (-pp-, -tt-) to extract, pull out, uproot; puya (-pp-, -nt-) to extract, pull out, uproot, depart, separate; puyakku departing, separation; ply (-pp-, -tt-) to extract, pull out, uproot; poy (-v-, -t-) to be pulled out, torn off. Te. (K.) puccu to pull out, remove (as a thorn or arrow). Kol. pus- (pust-) to pull, pull out, take off (clothes), (Kin.) bring out. Nk. pus- to pluck, take, remove. Nk. (Ch.} puc(c)- (pust-) to take out something, pull out. Pa. puce- to extract, take off; puyk- to pull out, pluck. Ga. (OlL.) puskir- to put off (clothes); (S.) pucc- to pull out, remove; (P.) pusk- to take out something, pull out; (S.4) puc- to take out. Go. (Tr.) puckana to become loose (of puyil [379] purai a shoe, ring, lid of a box, etc.) (Voc. 2284); (Ko.) puc- to pull off, remove (skin of fruit) (Voc. 2283). Malt. puce to put off (as attire), undress; pucgre to become loose. DED(S) 3513.

Ta. pura (-pp-, -nt-) to keep, preserve, protect, govern, bestow, reverence; purantar kings; purappu kecping, protection; puravu care, protection, gift, boon, tax, land given free of rent by a king; puravalan protector, preserver, defender, king. Ko. por first offer- ings of food to god in pabm ceremony. Ka. pore to nourish, cherish, support, preserve, invigorate; be nourished or fed; 2. nourishing, invigoration, refreshment; pokkula, pokkuli state of having oneself fed by others (e.g. a cuckoo); horakuli person who depends on others for his sustenance. Te. prdcu to nourish, protect, preserve, support; prodi nourishment; prdpu protection, support; propadu to be nourished; prapu support, protection, patronage, refuge. Kol. (SR.) puray- to tame, maintain. Pa. porip- (porit-) to rear, foster. Go. (L.) porpand to nourish, cherish; (Ko.) porp- to bring up, foster (child) (Voc. 2411). DED(S) 3515.
Ta. puracai, purdcai halter or head- stall of an elephant. Ka horaje cord put round the neck of an elephant; hor(a)ji a stout and long rope used in drawing an idol- car. DEDS 697.

Ta. pural (puralv-, puranf-), piral (piralv-, piranf-) to roll over, :tumble over, be upset, slip off, roll (as waves), overflow, be deranged or changed (as times, customs or laws), be overturned (as a state), go back on one’s word, be refuted, die; purali, pirali lying, deceit, insurrection; purattu (puraffi-) to turn a thing over, roll, turn up (as the soil in ploughing), fry (as vegetable curry), nauseate, deceive, pervert, smear, foul with dirt; n. turning over, overturn, prevarication, deceit, treachery, nausea, colic; purafci upsetting, disorder, anarchy; purattan, pirat- tan deceiver, liar; pirattu deceit. Ma. puraluka to roll; purattuka to turn about; puralikka to revolve in the mind; piraluka to wallow, roll, turn, be smeared, overflow; pirattuka to roll about (¢.), distort words, deccive, rub as ointment, soil; pirattu deceit, lewdness; pirattan fraudulent; pirafti confusion, con- sternation; piralea turning about, wallowing. Ko. porn- (pord-), poen- (po-nd-) to roll backwards and forwards (intr. ); porf- (porty-), po-t (po-yf-) id. (tr); pord, porn] act of rolling backwards and forwards; pern- (perd-) to turn upside down (intr.), (person) does opposite of what he promised; perf- (perfy-) to turn upside down (tr.). To. pi-l- (pied-) to tumble over or down; pi+{ (pi+fy-) to push over or down. Ka. poral, purul to roll, welter, wallow; (Hal.) hoddu to roll about. Tu. pureluni, pureyuni, poreluni, poreduni to roll (intr.), turn; purepuni, porepuni, porelpuni to roll (7)

Ka. puri strength, courage, pride; (Hal.) hurupu energy; (Hav.) huruppu id., enthusiasm. 7’e. puri-konu to gain strength or spirits; puri-kolupu to incite, rouse, urge, goad, encourage. DED(N) 3517. .

Ta. puri (-v-, -nt-) to desire; (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to desire; m. desire; purivu desire, love, attachment. Te. pura-pural-adu to be eager or desirous. DED 3518. 4288 Kui buru, burku fine rain. Kuwi (S.) biri biri rinai, bari piyu rinai to misle [ie. mizzle, drizzle]; (T.) bUri piyu, (isr.) buri buri piyu drizzle. DEDS 699. 4289 Kol. (Kin.) purug (odI, purg-) basket. Ga. (S.?) burki a small kind of basket. Kui burka, burki id. (Kamaleswaran). DEN 61.

Ta. purufu, To. pis); tes gun (2? < *[musket] ball; or with 3438 Ta. teri). Ka. tera state of being balled or heaped one upon the other, a mass; teratu to make round, roll up, tuck up, join, unite; teralu to ball itself, coalesce, join, be numerous, be amassed, come together, assemble; terale a round lump; teralcu_ to amass, join; teralke a mass, multi- tude. Tu, tirlu kdduni to be full-grown; tirle a full-grown, strong man. Te. (K.) teralu to abound, increase, swell up. Go. (LuS.) térép bundle. Konda (BB) terb- to coil round (intr.); terp- (-t-) to roll up, coil up (#7.). DED(S, N) 2654. tiri - [282] tir
Ka. burude mud, mire. Tu. burdé id. Te. burada id. Pa. (S.) burda mud. Ga. (P.) burda id. Go. (Ko.) burda id. (Voc. 2576). Konda burda slush, mud, dirt. DED (S) 3520.
To. pit Ilex wightiana. Ka. (Badaga) (Lush.) hurulu id. DED 3521.

Ta. purai (-pp-, -ft-) to be defective, slip into wrong note in singing; n. defect, blemish, falsehood; puraical defect, weakness, discord, flaw; puraittal defective note in singing; puraippu fault, flaw, defect, doubt; purivu error; puraiy-@ru to be suffocated by food passing into the windpipe. Ka. pore, porake error, mistake, fault; the wrong way in drinking or swallowing; porey-@ru choking sensation to arise from a mouthful going the wrong way. Te. pora error, misunderstanding, duplicity, fraud; pora-padu, pora-badu, pora- povu anything to go the wrong way in swallowing. DED(S) 3522.

Ta. purai house, dwelling, small room; pirai shed, factory; puraiyan house, purai [380] bulla cottage, dwelling made of leaves; puraiyul house. Ma. pura house (esp. thatched house), hut, room. Kod. pore thatched roof. Tu. poré, puré roof, ceiling; pura house. DED(S) 3523,

Ta. purai cataract (on the eye); pori (-v-, -nt-) to form asa thin stratum or layer of salt; porukku flake, skin, thin layer that peels off, scale, bark, rind; pokku flake, scale. Ka. pore a fold, layer, stratum; horike, horige the scale or coat of an onion, a thin layer or scale of a stone, lamina, stratum, layer. Te. pora layer, film, membrane, skin, stratum, fold, cataract, cast skin of snake; porafa page, (K. also) layer. Konda poro layer, space between two rocks. Cf. 3981 Ka. pare. DED(S) 3524.
Ta. purai deep ulcer, fistula. Ka. pure, pore boil, ulcer. DED 3525,
Ta. purai tubular hollow, tube, pipe, windpipe. Tu. perevuni to be bored, per- forated; perepini to bore, perforate; burma, burmu a gimlet; berpuri a borer. DEDS 698.
Nk. purni bone. Ga. (Oll.) ptin id.; (P.) indmul piin backbone. ? Ka. huri id., spine. ? Cf. 4339 Te. punuka. DED(S) 3526.

Ta. pul grass, grass family (e.g. bamboo); pillu grass; pul-vay deer. Ma. pul grass, hay, straw. Ko. pul grass, thatching grass. To. pul thatching grass; ul grass (in songs; < Badaga). Ka, pul grass, straw; pulle deer. Kod. pilli grass. Tu. pullu a rush, a kind of grass. Te. pulu, pullu, pillu grass. Go. (SR.) pilp thatch (Voc, 2252); (Koya Su.) pula hay. Kuwi (Isr.) pilka grass, bunch of grass. DED(S, N) 3528.

Ta. pul smallness in quantity, number, or value; pun small; punmai smallness. Ka. (PBh.) pul mean, in: pul-vage a mean or low thought (see 5205). Te. pullu little, small. Malt. pul-ond a little; pulo-qadi a little only, very little, least. DEDS(N) 701.

Ta. pula (-pp-, -nt-) to pout, sulk, be displeased, suffer pain, dislike; pulaval dis- pleasure, dislike; pulavi sulks, displeasure, dislike; pulavu dislike. Malt. pule to be jealous; pulware jealous. DEDS 702.

Ta. pulam arable land, rice field; place, region, quarter; pulan, pulavu arable land, ? pulampan chief or lord of a maritime tract [prob. originally ‘chief of a village in the maritime tract’]. Ma. pulam cornfield; place. Ka. pola a plough-field; place, direction. Tu. pula pasturage. Te. polamu field, place of cultivated land; polamari cultivator; (inscr.) pulambu, pulombu field. Kol. (Br.) polam field. Pa. polub (pl polbul) village. Ge. (Oll.) polub, (S.) polltib id. DED(S) 3529. 4304 Ta, pulampu (pulampi-) to sound, speak foolishly or incoherently, wail, cry out, grieve, utter repeatedly; 7. sound, foolish talk, lament, grief; pulampal sound, chattering, weeping, grieving; pulappam chattering, raving, lamentation; pulavu (pulavi-) to make noise. Ma. pulampuka to sound, bemoan, lament, speak hastily; pulampal lamentation. Ka. palumbu, halubu to lament. Kod. poli- (-v-, -fij-) to bark repeatedly (song-word), Tu. palambuni to lament; pollé slander, tale- bearing. Te. panavu, (K. also) palumu, palavu to lament; ? palumu to prattle; n. prattle; ? palavu, palavincu, palavarincu, paluvarincu to talk in sleep; palavaramu, palavarinta, anes ide talking in sleep (or with 3887 Ta. i). Pa. polla noise, hissing of snake. Go. polo (SR.) story, (G.) word, advice, (S.) paswer, matter; (Mu.) pallo word, speech; (Ma.) pola language, speech (Voc. 2422). DED(S, N) 3530.

Ta. pular (-v-, -nt-) to dawn; putarcci dawning; pularpu, pularvu, pulari dawn; pulavu (pulavi-) to dawn; pur-pull-enal éxpr. indicating break of day; punnam-pulari carly dawn, Ma. pularuka to dawn, light to appear; pular dawn; pularcca daybreak; pularttuka to bring to the light. Kod. pola- (polav-, poland-) to dawn; polat- (polati-) to spend night until dawn; polace, (Mercara dialect) polaca dawn; pola-ka morning. Tu. pullé, pullya, pullyakela, pullayakolé morning, day- break; early in the morning. Malt. pulpulre to. shine through whiteness. Cf. 4551 Ta. poli. DED(S) 3531. 4306 7a. pulavai, pulavai-marutu, ptlatti flowering murdah, Terminalia paniculata. Ma, pulla-‘marutu a timber. Ka. hulive the timber tree 7. p. Te. (DCV) pula-maddi, puligi id. DEDS 703.

Ta. puli, pul tiger. Ma. puli id. Ko. puj id. To. piisy, tily (< Badaga huli) id. Ka. puli id. Tu. pili id. Kor. (M.T.) hili id. Te, puli, pedda puli, bebbuli id.; ciruta puli leopard, cheetah. Kol. (Kin. P. Haig, Hislop) pul, (Kin. SR.) perpul tiger. Nk. pul id. Nk. (Ch.} pul(a) id. Ga. (OIL) berpul, (S.) pullu (pl. pulkul), berbullt (pl. berbulkul), (S.*) pulu id. Go. (tr pulli (pi pulk), (SR.) puli, (Ma.) pul, (W. Ph.) puliyal id. (Voc. 2313). / Cf. Pkt. (DNM) pulli- tiger. DED(S) 3532.

Ta. pulku (pulki-), pullu (pulli-) to embrace, copulate, be attached (to friends); poli (-v-, -nt-) to cover (bull or ram); poli, policcal, polippu, polivu covering (among animals). Ma. putkuka, pulluka to embrace, copulate. DED(S) 3533. 4309 Ka, bulla, bulli male generative organ. Te. bulla, bulli penis (used with refer- ence to a child). / Cf. Mar. buli, bulli a child’s gugans Turner, CDIAL, no. 9292(1). DED(S) 534, pul {381} purai

Ta. pul tawny colour; pullai dull, yellowish colour. Ma. pulla a yellowish colour of cattle. Ko. bul liver-coloured; bul(n) xn. pr. dog or bullock; fem, buly. Te. pula ycllowish; pulla brown, tawny. Ga. (S.3) pula light brown colour (< Te.). DED(S) 3535.
Ka. buwa boiled rice (in children’s language). Te.’ buvva food, rice. DED 3536.

Ta. pugu worm, maggot; Cpp-, -tt-) to breed worms, be worthless (as a worm- eaten thing); puxuppu breeding of worms. Ma. pugu worm, caterpillar, maggot, grub, moth, mite; pugukka to be caten or infected by worms, putrefy, rot. Ko. pus worm, maggot. To. puf worm, intestinal worm; us worm in skin, brain, or body (not intes- tinal) (< Badaga hite; Emeneau, Language, 15.45); pory stink of a corpse (or with 3999 Ta. para). Ka. puru, pura worm, insect in general, snake; pugt to be eaten or infected by worms, get worm-eaten, putrefy; puricil’ rottenness, cariousness; puruku that is eaten by vermin, state of being worm-eaten or decayed, ulcer, sore; purcu ruin, wickedness. Kod, pulu worm; pung- (pungi-) (meat) becomes slightly decomposed, (sore) festers a little. Tu. puri worm, mite, moth, skin parasite; pura, puru snail; puriganty anything eaten or perforated by worms, worm-eaten; purkuni to rot, decay, become putrid; purf- guni, purunguni to ferment, decompose, decay; purnhgely fermented, decayed, mouldy; purfigafy id.; fermentation, decay; puppi, puppu decay, rottenness, Te. pur(u)gu, pur(u)vu, pruvvu worm, any inscct or reptile, snake; p(r)uccu to rot, be putrefied, decay; p(uppi rottenness, decay; (K,) pruhgudu leucoderma. Kol purre worm. Nk. purre id. Nk. (Ch.) pur(r)e worm, insect. Pa. pujut (pl. purtil) worm. Ga. (S.) pudut insect. (Tr.) puri (pl. purk), (W.) puri worm, insect: (A. Ch.) puri @t purk), (Ph.) puri, puri, (Mu.) puri, puruy (pl purk) worm (Voc. 2316); (Tr.) purit

Ta. puruku, punuku civet; civet cat. Ma. puryuku, puru id. Ka. pura, puru, punugu, punagu, punagu, punugu, pula civet. Kod. pu--bekki civet cat. Tu. punungu, punugu, punugu civet. Te. punfigu id. DED 3538.
Ta. puguku arrowhead; purutu feath- ered part of an arrow. Ka. piluku, pilku id. /? Cf. Skt. puttkha- shaft or feathered part of a grows Turner, CDIAL, no, 8247. DED 3539.

Ta. purufku (purunki-) to be steamed, parboiled, stew, be sultry, be hot with anger; purunkal parboiling, anger, sultriness, perspir- ation; purukku (pugukki-) to boil (paddy) before husking, scorch (as the sun); 1. boiling grains, well-boiled food; pusgukkal anything slightly boiled, cooked rice; purukkam heat, sultriness, sweat, grief. Ma. purunfiuka to be boiling, stewed, steamed; parboil; purukkuka to boil (tr.); purukkam boiling, steaming heat; purukku fruits or vegetables boiled, a dish of curry; pugukuka to be hot (by a close room). To. pusk- (pugky-) to boil (potatoes). Ka. purgu to burn; purgi, puggi rice boiled with pulse, salted or sweetened. Kod. pukk- (pukki-) to boil so as to loosen skin or husk. Tu. purguni to be well-boiled (as rice); purkuni to be half-baked; purkadvuni to bake in embers; (B-K.) purkavu to half-bake. Kor, (T.) purga overboiled rice. Te. pulagamu rice boiled with green dal (? < Skt). ? Go, (A.) piri- to boil (intr.); (Tr.) piritana to boil furiously, boil over; (Ph.) piditana to boil over (Voc, 2244). Kur, ptxni to boil (tr.); refl. and pass. pUxrna. Malt. pige to boil; pugpe, puipge boiled grain. ? Br. palhing to be boiling, on the boil, stewed, boil with rage; paléfing to make to boil, boil (meat). (For Kur. Malt., Burrow 1968, .67; for Br., MBE 1980a, p. 312, n. 1). /Cf. Skt. pulaka- a lump of boiled rice; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8295. DED(S, N) 3540.

Ta. puruti dust, pulverised or fine powder, dry earth; pUgti dust; ptgi powder, dust, sacred ashes; [pUti ashes, dust, powder]. Ma. puruti, piri dust, earth put to the roots of trees, pollen, rust; (Tiyya) ptyi sand; {pUti, bhiti ashes]. [Ko. pu-dy id.}. [To. pu-@Oy id.j. Ka. [biida, bddi, bidu ashes]; hoyige, (Hav.) shoyge sand. [Kod. bu-di ashes]. Tu. poyyé-sand; [bidi ashes; budifga, budu ash-coloured]. Te. bidida, [budi] ashes. Kol. (Pat., p. 23) btiddi ash. Nk. burdi id. ? Pa. pormil, pormil rubbish. ? Malt. porsi sweepings. / Cf. Skt. bhuti- ashes. The forms in brackets are probably, but not all necessarily, borrowed from Skt. DED (S, N) 3541. 4317 Ta, purai hole, tube, entrance, gate, forest path, by-path, narrow path; (-pp-, -tt-) to bore, riddle; puzal tube, drain; par hole; -plrai wicket gate, mountain pass, crevice, opening. Ma. pura gap in a wall. Ko. pul ‘crevice, crack, lane between houses: pil way, road, entrance to path through thicket. To. puga [382] burn pil way through thicket; wa-l pil drainage hole in wall (cf. 999); pil hole in wall, esp. for leading off bath-water. Ka. pore path, road. Tx. poraly, (B-K. also) puraly, pulaly fife, flute. Te. punta path or road for cattle. DED(S, N) 3542.

Ma. pura river. Ko, pey id., peyv-e-r (obl. peyyert: id. (eer id., sv. 5159 Ta. yaru). To. parw id.; pa» top the river’s noise of waves beating (for top, see 3069). Ka. pore river. Kod, pole id. DED 3543.
Ta. pul bird; stick used in the game of tipcat. Ma. pul any small bird. Ko. pul, pil small stick used in tipcat. To. pil bird. Te. pultigu id. DED 3544.

Ma. pulayuka to twirl about, wriggle, snake to twine itself round the body of men or beasts; pulayikka to brandish, swing, switch; pulekka to roll oneself, loiter about, revel, swagger, strut; pulappu revelling, overbearing manner. Ka. pole to roll, roll about, move to and fro, wallow, move in a circle; polapu trembling, shaking, rolling, a swing, hammock; polayisu to cause to roll, cause to go round in a circle, brandish. Te. polayu to wander, move about. DED(S) 3545.

Ta. punai raft, float, ship. Tu. puld- vuni to float; pulapavuni to cause to float; pulampuni to set afloat; punaly floating; punaluni, punaluni to float. Kor. (M.) bola id. DEN 63, and from DED(S) 3559.

Ta. puli (-pp-, -tt-) to turn sour, ferment, be leavened; 7. acidity, tartness, tamarind, curry containing tamarind, acid substance; puliccal anything acid, ‘sour, OF leavened; pulippu sourness, acidity, fer- menting, acid. Ma, pulikka to be sour, ferment; puli sourness, acidity, acid, tama- rind; pulippu acidity; puliyan, pila sour. Ka. puly sour; puly-/pulc- (pule-) to be’ sour, (mouth) tastes sour. To. piily tamarind: piily- (pitlc-) to be sour. Ka. puli, pulla, pulu, pulla acidity, sourness; punise, hunise, hunisi tamarind; huli (-t-) to be or turn sour. Kod. puli sour, orange, vinegar; (Shanmugam) pulifije tamarind. Tu. puli, puli acidity, a sour fruit, tamarind, leaven, sour, acid; pulipu acidity; pulipuni, puliyuni to become sour, be leavened; pulippena sour; punike, puniké tamarind; bulé fermenting; bulekali sour toddy. Kor. (O.) pulcu to feel sour in the mouth. Te. puli sour, acid; puliyu to become sour, ferment; pulusu, (K. also) pulupu acidity, sourness, juice of tamarinds, sour, acid, pulupa an acid dish made of various edible roots; pulla, pullana sourness, acidity; pullani sour, acid; (VPK; Telangana dial.) pundi (kira), punfi (kira) sorrel (a sour vegetable). Kol. pulle sour; (SR.) puliy- (teeth) are on edge. Pa. pul, puldi, pulla, pullat sour; pulp- (pult-) to turn sour. Ga. (S.) pulla sour. Go. (G.) pulla sour preparation of vegetables; (Ma.) puila, pulla id., sour; (M.) pula, (S. Ko.) pulla sour (Voc, 2315). Konda pula sour soup, boiled tamarind water. Kuwi

Ta. pulifian, pulinan hunter, moun- taincer; pulluvan dweller in a desert tract. Ma. puliyan a caste of jungle dwellers, fem. pulicci. Ka. (K.?) punaje a hunter woman. {7 Cf. Skt. pulinda- name of a barbarous tribe. DED 3547.

Ta. puluku (puluki-) to tell bare- faced lies; n. obvious lic, barefaced false- hood; pulukan liar; (Shanmugam) fem, puluki; pulukuni, purukuni liar (comm. by N. Kumaraswami Raja). Ko. pulg boasting; pulgn a boasting man; fem. pulgy. DED(N) 3548. 4325 Kol, puls- (pulust-) (hair) becomes grey. Vk. puls- id. Pa. pule- id. Go. (A. Ch. G.) pirs-, (SR.) pidcana, (Tr.). pircana, (Ph.) pircana, (Ma.) pirc- (pir?t-) id. (Voce. 2249); (Koya Su.) pinsk- id. Kur. (Hahn) pundna to become hoary, grey in hair; (Grignard) pondna (pundyas/pondos) id., get mouldy or musty. Mait. ponde id. (Or Kur. Malt. < IA, Turmer, CDIAL, no. 8259; Pfeiffer 1972, p. £70.) DED(S, N) 3549.

Ko. pulc various Oxalis species. To. piile Oxalis corniculata. DEN 62. 4327 Ta.’ pulli mark, dot, speck. Ma. pulli dot, spot, point. Ko, puly dot. Tu. kar-bule, kar-bollé a fowl! having white plumage with black spots. Te. bolli spotted white; white leprosy, leucoderma (or with 5496(a) Ta. vel). DED(S) 3550.
Ka. pulle, (K.? also) pulli a small bit of very dry wood. Kod. pulli fuel. Tu. pullé a split, splint. Te. pulla a small stick or twig. DED 3551.

Ko. bum, brn, burakn, burburn, brbrn suddenly, quickly; pre++, pure++n, purpurn, bre+*, burs+«n, burk, burkn noise of bird flying up suddenly from ground. Ka. bura, bura, buru sound in imitation of that of a rash movement; bura, bura, buru, burr imitation of the sound produced by a bird rising suddenly, or by breaking wind; bura burane with the repeated suddenness or sound of bura; burrane with the sound of burr; pur sound in jmitation of breaking wind, of easing nature; barrane with a whir or sudden and noisy vibration of wings; with a rush or dash, smartly, swiftly. Tu. bury, burané, buru, buruburu, bursry, burrane, burru the noise of a bird’s wings in flying; burky, burku noise of breaking wind. Te. (B.) burru loud and sudden noise such as is made when a bird flies. Go. (SR.) bhurrne suddenly (Voc, 2661). /MBE 1969, p. 295, no. 33, for areal etymology, with reference to Mar. bhurbhur sudden, and several other items in Turner, CDIAL, no. 9535. DED(S) 3552. bira [383] punai
Tu. bira a kiss. Pa, burr- to kiss; (S.) burru a kiss. Go. (Tr. SR. Ph.) burrand, (Ma.) bor- to kiss, nose (of dogs), caress (Voc. 2577); (ASu.) bur- to kiss. DED(S) 3527.
Ta. pirutai false rumour, lie. Ka. burade, burude lie, untruth. Te. buruda lie; (K.) buridi id., deceit; b. kotfu to cheat. DEDS(N) 704.

Ka. burade, burude, burudi gourd- bottle, calabash, dry gourd, head, skull. Tu. burudé bottle made of the shell of a coco- nut, gourd, etc.; skull. Te. puriya, purriya, purre skull; burra empty shell of a long gourd; head, skull; pucce skull; shell of a coconut, etc. Kol (SR.) burrz pumpkin. Nk. burra id., gourd. Nk. (Ch.) putte gourd. Pa, butri bamboo spoon, ladle; burka gourd. 2? Ga. bundurke (S.?) a kind of gourd, (8.3) Cucurbita lagenaria. Go. (Tr.) purka the gourd of which Pardhans make their guitar; (A. Y.) purka gourd; (Ch.) purka, (G.) burrka, (Mu. Ko.) burka, (Ma.) bohka gourd, gourd vessel (Voc, 2307). Konda pure small vessel made of scooped gourd shell; purge drinking vessel made of gourd shell; bura head; (BB) puRka gourd, gourd vessel. Pe. puska gourd. Kuwi ((. Isr.) pugla id. DED (S) 3553.

To. puram, puran outside, exterior, that which is foreign; aspersion, calumny; adj, pura-, puyam-; purakku outside; puraku excluded person or thing; purani slander; outer bark, skin, anything that is outside; puratti that which is outside, foreign, ex- trinsic; purattavan, purattiyan foreigner, stranger; purampu the exterior, outside, that which is separate, other; purampar the out- side; puravu, puravam forest, jungle, forest tract, hilly tract; pura-ppafu (-ppafuv-, Pratt) to set forth, proceed, start, go out; pira other things; pirar outsiders, strangers; piran another man, stranger; fem. piral; piritu other thing; pirantu clsewhere. Ma. puram the outside, west; beyond, more than; adj. pura; purattu out, out of; piran other. Ko. poranj the out- side, place outside; por kay the right shoulder bare (e.g. at ceremonies); por pal teeth that grow irregularly and crooked. To, pir-mun the outside; pir no-r the southwestern area of the Nilgiris that is hilly/jungly; wiroc fé-r nickname, name used in abuse or ridicule (< Badaga). Ka. pora, poragu, porage. the outside, outside; porabiga stranger, alien; horaccu the outside; horatu, hortu that is outside, that is excepted; being left alone, being not that, being not present; except, without; horame that is outside or in the rear; horadu (horaf-/honf-; < *pora-vadu- < *pora-patu; cf. Kod. Tu. Kor. [comm. by Garman]) to go outside or out of, come forth, set out, start, appear (as a_ boil); horadisu to cause to go outside or out of, cau

Ta. pura, pura, puravam, puravu dove, pigeon. Ma. piravu, pravu id. Ka. horasu a kind of pigeon; (PBh.) porasu pigeon. Tu. puda dove, pigeon. Te. burra- pitfe a sort of pigeon. Ga. (P.) purus tite dove. Go. (Tr.) purar, purar the small speckled ground-dove; (Ch.) purral (pl -or), (W. Ph.) purraj dove; (Mu.) purrar (pl. purrask), (Ma.) pugar, porar(i), (L.) pogar pitte pigeon (Voc, 2306); (Tr.) paorish blue rock-dove (Voc. 2067). Konda (BB) poroz dove. Kur, (Hahn) purra id. Malt. pure id. DED(S) 3555.

Ta. purru, purram white anthill. Ma. purr ground thrown up by moles, rats, esp. a white anthill. Kurwb. (LSB 1.12) pitti snake hole. To. wit-xudy high white anthill (kudy- pit); wit-mon id. (mon earth). Ka. puttu, putta, hutta, huttu, utta white anthill. Kod. putti id. Tu. pufica id., snake’s hole. Kor, (T.) hufica, (M.) huntu anthill. Te. putta anthill, snake’s hole. Kol, (Kin.) puffta white anthill. Vk. pufta id. Pa. putkal (ol. putkacil), (NE.) putkal id.; putta, (NE.) putta nest inside anthill. Ga. (Oll.) putkal white anthill. Go. (Tr. Ph.) putti, (many dialects) putti, (Ko.) puttu anthill (Voc. 2298). Konda puRi id.; puR(i) seti winged flies that swarm round the lamp after the rains; puga anthill. Pe. puci anthill. Kui pusi, (K.) pucci white anthill. Kuwi (F.) pci, (Su. P.) pucci, (Isr.) puci anthill. Kur. putta anthill; putbeld white ants’ queen (beld id.). Malt, pute anthill. /Cf. Skt. (pipilika)-puta- anthill; puttika- the white ant or termite; Turner, CD/AL, no. 8264. DED(S, N) 3556.
Ta. purru anything scurvy, scrofulous, or cancerous. Ma, puryu scab, dry pus. ? Go, (Mu.) pitr wound (Voce. 2238). Kuwi (F.) porro scab. DED(S) 3557.

Ta. punam upland fit for dry culti- vation; puna-kkafu shifting cultivation on the hills; pun-cey land fit for dry cultivation only, dry crop; puficai dry land. Ma, punam a jungle, chiefly highland overrun with underwood and capable of irregular culti- vation; puna-kkanfam a hill-tract; pufica dry crop; pufica-kkanfam field under irri- gation, yielding even three harvests. Ka, punaji dust-like dry soil in which a kind of paddy is grown. Tu. pufica-kanda a very good rice-field. Te. punja land cultivated without artificial irrigation, high land. DED(S) 3558.
Ta. punal, punai water, flood, river. Ma. punal, punal water, river. Ka. ponal stream, river. DED 3559.
Te. punuka skull. Kuwi (S.) ponki id. ? Cf. 4299 Nk. purni. DEDS 705.

Ta. punai (-v-, -nt-) to dress, put on, adorn, make ready, wear, make, form: n. punku [384] pu beauty, decoration; punaivu beauty, decor- ation, making. Ma. punayuka to put on, undertake. Cf. 4361 Ta. pin. DED 3560.

Ta. punku, pufku, punkam, ptnti Indian beech, Pongamia glabra; rosewood, Dalbergia lanceolaria. Ma. punhu P. glabra; unnhu Bauhinia vartegata {considered a kind of punfu]. Ka. honge P. glabra Vent.; D. oujeinensis Roxb. [= Ougeinia dalbergioides Benth.]; puligil, pulitgil, huligilu P. glabra; huligali, hulivili, hulige D. oujeinensis. Tu. pungu P. glabra. Mand. bukli mar D. oujeinen- sis. DED(S) 3561.
Ta. punku, punku, (Lush.) punkan (?; spelled pungan) sp. privet, Ligustrum perrottetii. Ma. (Lush.) puftu id. DEN 64.

Ta. punnai, pinnai mast-wood, Calo- phyllum inophyllum; punku Malabar poon, C. tomentosum (also kattu-ppunnai). Ma. punna C. inophyllum; Rettlera tinctoria. Ka. punnike, ponne, honne Terminalia tomentosa W.& A.;honneya mara id.; R. tinctoria Roxb.; sura-honne R. tinctoria; Alexandrian laurel, C. inophyllum Lin. Tu. ponné R. tinctoria; Siri-ponné C. apetalum. Te. ponna C. ino- phyllum; sura-ponna, sura-punnagamu C. longifolium. / Cf. Skt. pumnaga- R. tinctoria; C. inophyllum (Turner, CDIAL, no. 8244); suraparnika- R. tinctoria. {Ochrocarpus longi- folius Benth. & Hook. = C. longifoloum Wall. Mallotus philippinensis Muell. = R. tinctoria Roxb. Hooker does not identify C. apetalum. The remaining species are all different from these.]. DED 3562. 4344 (a) Tu. pinpini to know, understand, comprehend: pinpavuni to make to know,. make known, reveal. Kol. (Kin.) pun- (punt-) to know. Pa. pun- (pund-, putt-) id. Ga. (OIL) pun- (pres.-fut. puy-) id. Go. (A.) pund- (putt-), (Tr. W. Ph. M.) pundana, (Mu.) pun-/pund- (putt-), (Ko.) pun- (putt-) id. (Voce. 2302). Pe. pun- (puc-) id. Mand. pun- (puc-) id. Kui punba (pus-) id., understand, comprehend; n. knowledge, understanding. Kuwi (F.) pinjali, (S.) punnai, (Su.) pun- (pucc-), (Isr.) pun- (puc-) to know; (S.) punbi kinai to inform; (Isr.) punmbi/prumbi ki- to explain; (Isr.) pupmbu knowing; ? (S. Isr.) pusponi, (F.) pUsponi a story. (b) Ta, pulam sense, sensation, knowledge, learning, treatise, the Vedas, clue; pula (-pp-, -nt-) to make k

Ta. pit flower, cock’s comb, richness, flourishing condition, wire edge on tool, cataract in eve, albugo, menstruation; (-pp-, -tt-) to blossom, flower, bloom, flourish, menstruate; produce (as flower), create, give birth to; pippu flowering, blooming, menstru- ation. Ma. pil, ptvu flower, blossom, comb of a cock, menses; pitkka to blossom, bud, expand, menstruate; piikkal flowering; pippu growing, a crop, menses; pUppikka to cause to blossom. Ko. pus flower, wire edge on tool. To, pu-f flower; (pu-t-) to blossom. Ka. pa (put-) flowers to be produced, flower, bloom; n, flower, blossom; pivu flower; puvvu id., albugo; (Gowda) ptmbE bunch (plantain flower). Kod. pus, (Mercara dialect) pu-vi flower, neck feathers of a jungle cock. Tu. pa flower, cataract in eye; pmbé the flower bud of a plantain. Te. pad flower, blossom; ptvu, puvvu id., albugo; ptcu to blossom, flower, bloom; pUta flowering, blossoming, blooming. Kol. puv, (Kin.) pita flower. VK. pitta id. Pa. pti (pl. ptivul) id.; pap- (ptt-) to flower, blossom. Ga, (OIL) pi (pl pal flower; pip- (ptit-) to flower; (S) puvvu flower; puk- (pUt-) to flower, blossom; (S.7) bam pindake bumble bee. Go. (Tr. W. Ph.) pungir, (A. D. G. Ko.) pungar, (Mu.) punar, Ma.) punggag flower (Voc. 2282); (LuS.) poongagai id.; (W.) puyand, pilina, (Tr.) pdiana, (Ph.) puiyana, (A.) poy-, (Mu.) pily-, (Ma. Ko.) puy-, (S.) pily- to flower, bloom, blossom (Voc. 2303); (ASu.) poy- id.; (Tr.) phuki, (W.) phtki wisi bee (*i fly); (Ph.) phiki honey; phiki

Ma. ptkka (tongue) becomes’ furred. Ko. pusv- (puet-) (mouth, tongue) becomes dry with fever. 7u. pU agra = agra a sore mouth, aphthae, the thrush (cf. App. 3 Ta. akkaram). Te. ptcu (tongue) blisters or becomes sore, rough, and furry, be affected with thrush; ptita aphthae, thrush. DED 3565.
Ta. pt spark, as of fire. Kui ptivala id.; (K.) pua embers. Kuwi (P.) ptlya id,; (F.) puiya; (Isr.) puva spark; (Isr.) puyd burning coal. DEDS 706.

Ta. pi, pttvu, ptvam, pUvatti lac tree, Schleichera trijuga; ptvam longan, Nephelium longana, Ma. ptivam a fine timber tree, (Lush.) S. trijuga; (Lush.) mala-pilvana, cem-ptivana N. longana; ? ptivattam a tree that yields a red dye, Rubia munjista. Pa. puy S. trijuga. pitgna [385] pan Ga. (P.) puy marin id. Go. (LuS.) pooshee the koossum tree [ic kusum tree, S. t.]. Konda (BB) piizi maran id. Kui (K.) pahi mranu id. Kur. pusra id. Malt. pusre a tree and its fruit so called. DED(S) 3566. 4349 Kur. ptigng to win in a game or match, Malt. pungare id. DEDS 707.

Te. psa bead; ? ptdiya, pUde bead of gold or silver. Kol, pu-sa necklace. Nk. pUsa bead. Go. (LuS.) pooshangé necklace of black beads worn by women. Konda psa bead. Kuwi (Su.) plsa (pl -ya) id.; (Isr.) pisa id., necklace. DED(S) 3567.
Ta. pical clamour, battle, wailing, lamentation, distress. Ma. plyal fight. Ka. puyyal vociferation, lamentation; puyatcu, puyyalcu to call or cry out, vociferate, lament. DED 3568.

Ta. pitcu (ptci-) to besmear, anoint, tub, daub on, plaster, scrub the floor with cowdung dissolved in water, clean, wash; 1, daubing, smearing; ptccu daubing, smearing, coating, gilding, plastering. Ma. picuka, pusuka to smear, daub, rub, whitewash, plasier, gild; pUcal, pUccu smearing, daub, coating. Ka. pilsu to smear, daub, apply, anoint, plaster; n. act of smearing, etc.; oil for smearing on the body; pusuvike smearing, etc. Tu. pijuni_to smear, rub, daub, apply. Te. piyu id.; pifta smearing. / Cf. Skt. pusta- working clay, modelling; pusta-karman- plastering, painting. DED 3569.

Ta. pticci insect, beetle, worm. Ma. pilcci any insect. Ka. piici, baci worm, insect. ? Cf. 4312 Ta. pugu. DED 3570. 4354 Ma, pitcci a fart. To. pi-x id- Cift-) to break wind silently. Ka. ptisu to bread wind. Tu. ppuni id.; paki a fart; (B-K.) dsu id. Kor, (O.) ptmpu to fart. DED 3571.

Ta. pUccai, plcai, pifiai, pUnai cat. Ma. Pucca id. Ko. pisc id. Kog. pu-fie id. Tu. pice, puccé id. Go. (Ma.) pisal(i) (pl. ptsasku), (M.) pusal, (Pat.) pusal id. (Voc. 2327). Kuwt (D.) pUse id. DED (S) 3572.

Ta. plficu, pUficanam, pUiicalam mould, mildew, cobweb; pUficai cobweb; pUfici dust; pil (--pp-, -tt-) to become mouldy. Ma, pikka id.; pUppu mould. Ka. buju, bije, bisi, biisu, bis¢e mould, mildew, mustiness. Tu. baju, bicu, baci, puhké mould, mildew; (B-K.) bugutu mould formed on the surface of foodstuffs. Te. bifju, bifdu mould, mildew. Pa, busj- to go mouldy; burja mould. Kui buru stpa (stpi-) to become mildewed; n. mildew. Kuwi (F.) bilttr mildew. DED(S, N) 3574.

Ma. pita down of birds, wool, fine hair. Ko. kambu- eyebrow (kam- < kan cye). Tu. (B-K.)} pulle plume, feather. Kol) bu-r (pl. bu-dl) eyelash, eyebrow; (Pat., p. 35) bir fur. Vk. biir (pl. bud]) down, fine feather. Pa. biidul (pl) hair, fine feathers, down. Ga, (P.) burgul eyebrows. Go. (A.) burda, (Y. 8.) bura down, (L.) burda feather (Voc. 2589); (L.) pula feather; (G.) bulo id., down; (Ma.)} bubla, bula small feathers, down (Voc. 2316); (W.) buiya down; (Ph.) buiya hair, feathers: (Tr.) buiyd down, small feathers (Voc. 2584). ‘Konda bulus (pl -ku) pubic hair, feathers, hair (on legs and chest); (BB) burus feathers, down. Pe. bilra (pl. -g) smail feathers, down, wool, pubic hair. Mand. burin pubic hair. Kui bari, biiru hair, fur, feather, wool; (K.) pruma feather. Kuwi (D.) kanu bUru eyebrow; burka (of) down. Malt. (BB) purgu hair on the body. Br. put hair (> Bal. pug a hair; Elfenbein, p.65); pug kanning to pluck (of a fowl, the tobacco crop); purruk long hair se males cut at the shoulders. DED(S, N)

Ta. plffakam, pOftakkam, pO¢tihkam vain pretension, boasting, guile, unsubstantial work; pUffafikam guile, inveigling. Ka. bUfa, bifaka, bufakatana, bifafa trickery, fraudu- lent display, lie. Tu. bufi, bugu, bUtatga, bufkatane empty boast; bOfuni to boast, brag. Te, bUfakamu false appearance, deceit, trick; false, deceitful; bitakidu, biftakidu rogue, cheat. DED 3576. : 4360 Ta, ptitu, ptntu small plant, herb; punfi shrubbery, garden. Tu. purndé a thorny bush; pundé bush, thicket; ptndely a thicket, clump, as of bamboos. DEDS 708.

Ta. poli (-v-, -nt-) to chisel, split (as a stone), dig, make holes, open (as a blister); be perforated, punctured, become dented: (-pp-, -tt-) to bore, perforate, tear into strips (as fibre); . holes made with a chisel, de- pression made by digging; polivu chiselling, hewing, picking (as a millstone); pollu (polli-) to bore, make a hole, hew, chisel: be rent or torn; pollal boring a hole, chisel- ling, hole, rent, fissure, hollow in a tree; pol, pollai hole. Ma, poli split, chip, what is torn (as a palm-leaf); poliyuka coverings or roof to break, skin to be peeled off; polikka to break open, unroof, undo; polla tube, pipe, perforated, empty, bamboo; pulakka to be split, open the mouth; pulappu piece, split; pularuka to split. Ka. potlu hole in a tree, hollow in the soil. Kod. poli- (poliv-, polifij-) to break (of a stick-like thing, tree; intr.); (polip-, polic-) id. (tr.); pola- (polap-, poland-) to open the mouth. Tu. poliyuni to be broken; polipuni to break; (B-K.) pori, poli id. Te. poducu to pierce, prick, stab, thrust, gore, bore, perforate; podupu piercing, thrusting; pd{u a thrust, stab, pain, ache. Pe. polka hole in tree. Kui blongu inba to be pitted, holey, Kuwi (Su.) polyga hole in tree; (F.) porongo hollow; (Isr.) polonga hollow in a tree. Br. polo hollow, empty (or sae Turner, CDIAL, no. 8398). DED(S)

To. pln (pinp-, ptnt-) to put on, wear, be fettered or yoked with, undertake (as a business); become entangled (as a lock of hair), be caught (as birds in a snare}, be yoked, become inseparable; ». ornament: pUnu-nol, pondl sacred thread; pUfei wearing, ornament, union, belief, determination; pufkai belief, determination; punfan husband; pani bull, bullock; puni (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to appear, bring into existence; make a vow; pinippu vow, decision, resolve; pattu (putti-) to lock, hook, unite, yoke, fetter, put on (as Jewels, garlands), entrap; n. locking, harness- Ing, etc.; ptfai cord for fastening a bullock to its yoke. Ma. ptnuka to be closed, be yoked, embrace, put on (as cloth, ornaments); pUnpu girdle, ornament; pinundl, pandl sacred thread; pUttuka to lock, yoke, bend the bow, embrace; pfu closure, lock, yoking. Ko. puer- (pu-ty-) to yoke, prepare smithy by getting ready bellows; pusf- (pu-fy-) to fit (t#.) into sheath (at smithy). To. pusl- (pu-d-) to wear around neck; pu-f- (puefy-) to tie around someone etse’s neck; ? u- {ne vow (in songs); ? pu-f- (pu-t-) to wear (cloak); pu-txuty cloak; puec- (pu-é-) to put (garment) on someone else. Ka. piin (piind-) to fix (as an afrow), begin; admit, assent, consent, promise, vow, challenge, bring forward or introduce: 7. admission; pilnke admission, promise, vow: hopi a vow; pldu to join (tr.), tie to, yoke, fix (as an arrow), prepare, arrange, bring about, create, begin; . established state; punipu to intend, aim at; piin
Ta. pinti town, village, district. Ka. hundi hamlet; htide village fort. Te. (in place names) piidi small village, hamlet. DEDS 709.

Ka. biatu foul, shameless, obscene language; a shameless, obscene man. Te. biltu obscene or foul language; obscene, foul, indecent. DED 3578. . 4364 Kol, pu-nd- (pusnt-) to solve (riddle), win (contest). Go. (Tr.) phund@ a riddle (Voc. 2460); (ASu.) pun- to solve a riddle. ? Te. pundalincu to invent. Cf. 4163 Ta. pitir. DED(S, N) 3579.
Ta. piivai lady, woman. To. pupup small baby. Ka. hitthe child; puppet. Te. pupa an infant; pupil of the eye. For mean- ings, cf. 4107 Ta. pavai. DEDS 695.

Ka. bira, bitraga, bitruga, bUruge, birla silk-cotton tree, Bombax. Tu. biruga, borada mara Eriodendron anfractuosum, Te. biiraga, bUruga, biirugu silk-cotton tree, E, anfractuosum; (Lush.) biraga id.; B. malabaricum. Kol. (Kin.) burg. Seft silk- cotton tree. Pa, biru id. Ga, (S.?) birje id. Konda biirgu maran id. /Cf. Skt. ptirani- B. heptaphyllum; Pkt. bitra- a species of tree. [B. malabaricum DC. = B. heptaphylla Cav. E. anfractuosum DC. = B. pentandrum Lin.] DED(S, N) 3580.

Te. puri peacock’s tail. Kol. (Kin.) ptirage id. Pa. (S.) piril (pl) peacock’s tail feathers. Go, (Ko.) ptiri peacock’s tail; ptir mal peacock (Voce. 2326). Konda puri feather of a peacock. DED(S) 3581.
Ta. piri a wind instrument; ptrikai trumpet. Ma. pUrika id. Te. bird id. ? Cf.

Ta. piil, pila, ptlicam, pulafci black-berried feather-foil, Phyllanthus reticu- latus. Ka. holi (Kitt.) a straggling shrub, (Lush.) P.» Te. puli Crinum pratense; (Lush.) P.r. [C. p. and P. r. are two different species. } DEDS(N) 710.

Ta. ptlvam trijugate-leaved soap-nut, Sapindus trifoliatus; pivanti id.; four-leaved soap-nut, [Temigyrosa canescens. Ma. pivanti S. laurifolius, (S. trifoliatus Linn. = S. lauri- folia Vahl. H. canescens Thwaites = S. tetra- phylla Vahl.}] DED 3583.
Te. piival red colour, red earth smeared on cottages. Ma, pilval red colour.
Ma. plvan cock; male of plants. Ko. bu-jn cock. Ka, pufija, pufiju id. Tu. ptiije id. Kor. (M.) htinji id. Te. punju id. Konda punzu male, esp. of fowls. DED(S) 3586.

Ta. pt quail, jungle fowl; pUrain Indian partridge, jungle fowl; kurum-pUr quail. Ko. mona-mbrl id. Ka. burli, burali, buruli id. Tu. kudumbulu id. Te. pttrédu a three-toed gregarious bird resembling the quail; puridi-pitta quail. Konda (BB) pUr id. DED(S, N) 3587.

To. pilmozm mist in valleys or on hills (cf. mozg, s.v. 4641 Ta. maficu; is To. to be analysed as two words, pilm mozm, with -m...-m ‘and’ ?). Kod. pu-li mist on moun- tains. ? 7a. pUri nagfu name of a district. DEN 65.

Ma. pUguka, punuka to be buried, stick in the mire; pOntuka to sink in the ground; pUttuka to press into, bury; pUttu a grave. Ko. pug-/purv- (purt-) to hide (¢r.). To. pu-®, in: irfu- 6, o-ffu-@ n. pr. places at the end of the road to the world of the dead ‘where the buffaloes, the people sink into the ground’ (cf. 647). Ka. ptix(u) to inwrap, insert, cover, bury; hig(u) to cover, bury; sink into (as a foot into mud), be filled up (as a channel); htm to bury oneself in a mélée; hiinu, (PBh.) pUp- to bury; ? hugi id.; (Hav.) huggu to hide. Kod pu-l- (pusp-, pu-t-) to bury. Te. pidu to be filled or closed up (as a pit); pld(u)cu to bury, fill up (as a pit); pd(u)pu burying, treasure- trove. Kol pur- (purt-) (hole) becomes filled in; purip- (puript-) to fill in (hole). Nk. purp- to bury. Kur. puttnad to set (of the sun only). DED 3588.

Ma. pwl chip, slice, wedge; pUluka to wedge asunder, chip, take the eatable part out of a stone fruit, extract a mango or caco- pulai [387] nut with a knife, slice. To. piel chip of wood, splinter, upright stick at end of ridgepole to keep thatch in place; u+ piece (< Badaga). Ka, (Badaga) hd one part or piece (Emen- eau, Language 15.46). Tu. ptlu chip, small piece of anything, piece of betel-nut. DED
Ta. pulai red silk-cotton; pula red silk-cotton tree. Ma. pula silk-cotton tree, Bombax malabaricum. ? Kui ptvulara a piece of fluff. DED 3590.

Ta. puru, piru anus. Ma. plram, puru buttocks; membrum muliebre. Kod. pu-ri female privates. Tu. pati id. Te. pUda anus. Br. pundt bottom of a receptacle, buttocks, anus. Cf. 4273 Ta. puntai. DED(S) 3591.
Te. ptne casket, box; ponaka large basket. Pa. ptina id. DED (S) 3592.
Ka. pega, pegga simpleton, fool. Tu, pefge, peime id., stupid fellow. Te. pefke an impudent, mischievous person; impudent. DED 3593.
Ka. besada, besta fisherman, who also hires himself as palanquin-bearer. Te. besta fisherman, palanquin-bearer. DED 3595.

Te. peccu rind, peel; (K.) pecce, peccika shell of coconut, etc. Pa. pocid rind, usk, shell (of egg), pod, serpent’s slough. /Cf. Skt. pesi-, pesika- rind of fruit; May- thofer III, p. 762. DED(S) 3596. 4385 Ka, heficu, hajicu, aficu tile. Kod. afici id. Tu. haficu id. Te. pencu, pencika potsherd; penku tile, broken piece of pot, coconut shell, or the like, potsherd, skull. Go, (W.) pinka, pirak broken tile; (Ph.) pinka, pirka id., earthen pan; (Se.) pinka a towa (Voc, 2229). Pe. pina potsherd. Kuwi (8.) pinanga tiles; penkunga id., bricks; (Isr.) pina tile; broken pot; pina gicu (pl. p. giska) potsherd. ? Malt. parka roof. DED(S) 3597.

Ma. pitaruka to burst. Ka petal, peflu sound in imitation of exploding, crack- ing as a rocket, etc.; peftal(u) popgun. Te. petapeta crackling; pefapefal-ddu to crackle; pet(ulu, pedulu, (K. also) petilu to break, crack, burst, split, open in chinks. Kol, (Pat., p. 55) petal thunder. Cf. 4490 Ta. potfu. DED(S) 3598. veftanavu
Ta. pottal baldness, Me. petta id. Ka. baftatale bald head. Te. batta bald. Kol. petfiar id. DED(S) 3599.

Ta. pefti box, chest, basket; pefta- kam chest, box; poffi box. Ma. petti box, trunk; petfakam box, chest. To. pofy box; pe-dy large basket tied around with cloth (or with 4442). Ka. pefti box, chest, trunk: pettige, pettiya id., basket. Kod. potti box. Tu, petfigé box, chest. Te. pette id., safe. Pa. peteya box. Cf. 4442 Ta. pagai. / Cf. Skt. petaka-, petika- box, Pkt. peda-, pedig- id., H. peti id., etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8164. DED(N) 3600.

Ko. pet beating; pet id- to beat. Ka. pettu to beat (as with hand, staff, hammer, etc.); n. striking, a blow, stroke, hurt. Tw. petfu blow, slap. Te. petfu blow, stroke, knock, thump; (K. also) vb. to hit, beat. Kol. (Kin.) pett a blow. /? Cf. Pkt. piff- to beat, H. pitna id., etc.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 8165. DED 3601.

Ka. hetfu to push or put into, insert; petfu to push penis into vagina. Kor. (M.) hetfi to prick. Te. pettu to place, put, lay, keep, deposit, rest on something, give, build (as a wall), plant (as a tree), charge one with, use, employ; “. giving, gift. Konda bef- (-t-) to put (cattle in yard). DED(N) 3602.
Ta. peffu lie, delusive word; peftan jar, deceitful person, Kod. pofti lie. DED(S) 03.

Ta. veftanavu cruelty, hardness, force: veffi (-pp-, -tt-) to be harsh, rough, violent: veffitu that which is harsh, rough or violent; veffimai harshness, roughness, harshness of speech, anger; vetf-enal expr. of harshness, violence, severity; veft-enavu severity, harsh- ness, that which is severe or hard; veffai . hardness as of metals. Ko. vet hard (of gold mixed with alloy); befn one who is strong and big. Ka. pettu pride, domineering, daring; pedasu hardness (as of metal), stiffness, inflexibility, brittleness, harshness (of speech), ‘difficulty, trouble (as of a business); betta, befti, befqu, bette firmness, hardness, excess- ive hardness of metals, harshness, rudeness; betfane with harshness, force, fierceness, or vehemence; dep tit(Ou that which is hard, firm, strong, stiff, harsh, fierce, rough, rude, difficult, etc. Tu. botfana excessively, ex- ceedingly. Te. petuku unpleasant, disagrée- able, rough, stiff, inflexible, discourteous, uncivil; unpleasantness; beffa hardness, diffi- culty; hard, difficult; bettu hardness, stiffness, rigidity, respect, awe: much, excessive; excessively, violently; beffidapu, beffidamu hardness, harshness, severity, violence, fury; hard, harsh, severe, stiff, violent; bettidtidu, beffiduratu a furious or cruel person; bedida trouble, ado, complication; bedidamu severity, harsh language; severe, harsh, much; bediyu to have a difference or misunderstanding, be on bad terms. Kui peda difficult, obstinate, pett-enal [388] penda troublesome,

Ta. peff-enal expr. signifying quick- ness. Ma. pef{-ennu suddenty, unexpectedly. ? Ko. petakn, pete-n (to seize the legs of someone) in supplication so that he cannot move away. Tu. pettugu, pettige immedi- ately, directly. DED 3605.

Ka. petfe, petta, penfe, henfe, hende clod, lump; ? (Hav.) pitte excrement of goat ot sheep (or with 4185). Tu. hengé, ente clod of earth. Te. pedda clod, lump of earth. Go. (S.) beda sod (Voc, 2598). Konda (BB 1972) beta clod. Kuwi (Isr.) beda brick. / Cf. Mar. péd a large turf or clod. DED(S) 3606. 4395 (a) Ta. pen woman, daughter, girl, wife, bride, female of animals or plants; pentu woman, wife; pentir (pl) women, pentan, pentakan, pentakam hermaphrodite, eunuch; pennan effeminate man; penmai being born a woman, feminine grace, woman- liness, modesty; pinai female of animals, doe; pinaval female of the dog, pig, deer, or yak; pinavu, pina id., woman; pennai palmyra palm (cf. pen-panai female palmyra palm). Ma. pen a female, esp. a female child, girl, maidservant, bride; penti girl, woman; pennan effeminate. Ko. pen female; en id. (< Badaga); pe- id. (peemog woman; pe-bi-c female cat); ped wife. To. in woman (in songs; < Badaga). Ka. pen, pennu, penda female. woman: (Shanmugam) pendir (p/) women; pendati, penditi wife; pentana state of being a female, feminine character or behaviour; henniga impotent man, coward; hennuga pimp; hehgasu female. Kod. ponni wile, female; ponna-li woman; ponga.id. (in songs); pommakka women, wives. Tu. ponnu girl, female, maid; ponna female, feminine, bridal; ponjavu, ponjevu a female in general, a grown-up woman; bodedi wife; emmalu woman. Te. penfi the female of any animal or plant; pendili, pendli, (modern) pelli, (substandard) pelli marriag
Ka. bene peg, plug, stopple, cork. Ll. Tu. biina peg, wedge. DEDS 7

Ka. henda vinous liquor or toddy extracted from the wild date tree. Tu. henda toddy, palm-wine. Ga. (OI) pendom rice- beer. Konda pendam kalu id., country liquor. Kuwi (S.) pendomi kadu beer; (Isr.) pendomka (pl.; nd!) rice-wine. DEDS 712.
Ka. pendeya a trinket for the foot. Te. pendemu, pendaramu, pendéramu anklet. / Kitt. seems to posit IA origin. DEDS 713.

Te. pedavi lip. Kol. pedave id.; (Haig) peddel lips. Nk. pedave lip. Nk. (Ch.) peddave id. Ga. (S.2) pedeve, (S.3) pedive id. Go. (Hislop Ma.) pilivi, (L.) pilvi id. (Voe. 2251). Konda bedve id. ? Tu. bimma id. DED(S) 3609.

Ka. bidar, bedar(u) to be agitated, confounded, bewildered, alarmed or fright- ened, take fright, fear; bedar(u) agitation, alarm, fright, fear; scarecrow; bedarane, bedarike fright, alarm; bedarisu to alarm, frighten; bedarisuvike frightening; bedarke agitation, alarm, fright; hedaru to be alarmed; (Hav.) hedaru, (Gowda) hediri, (Bark.) hediri, (Nanj.) edru to fear; (Hal.) hedaru id.; hedarike fear. Tu. bedary, bedariké start, fright, fear, alarm; bedaruni to be alarmed. Te. bedaru, (K. also) beduru to fear, be afraid or alarmed, start, be startled; m. fear, fright, alarm, terror; bedarincu to frighten, alarm, terrify, startle, bully, threaten; bedarimpu frightening, threat- ening, bullying, threat; bedarupotu timid person. Konda bedris- to scare, frighten. Kuwi (F.) pedrali, pidrt-aiyali, (Isr.) pedr- (-it-) to be startled. From DED(N) 4425.
Te. pendala-kada(n), pendalada(n) early, betimes, in time. Konda pendal (odl pendaR-) early in the morning. 4406 Kol. (SR.) bebde tomato. Vk. bhedre id. Go. (SR. Ph.) bebre id. (Voe. 2601). DEDS 716.

Ta. pey (-v-, -t-) to rain, fall (as dew or hail); pour down or into (¢r.), put, place, serve up (as food in a dish), throw out, dis- charge (as urine), shed (as tears), distribute; peyal showering, rain, raindrop, cloud; peyalai rain; puyal, pucal cloud, raining, water; gale, storm, tempest; poci (-v-, -nt-) to ooze, percolate, flow. Ma. peyyuka to pour, rain; peyttu raining; peyyikka to cause to rain. Ko. oy- (oc-) (rain) rains. Ka. poy to pour, cast; m. pouring; puy to pour, cast; podcal pouring water, Kod. poyy- (poyyuv-, pojj-) (rain) rains, (wind) blows. Kor. (O.) pi- to pour. Te. pdyu to pour, cast in metal; (K. also) (sweat, pus) forms; pSta pouring, casting in metal. Kol. (SR.) paiyeng to pour. Nk, (Ch. pi- id. Go. poy- (Ma.) (water) pours, flows, (Ko.) to be spilled, flow (Voe, 2396); (Koya Su.) poy- (water) to be spilled; (ASu.) pocci- to pour out (water). Kuwi (F.) boiyali (b6t-) to overflow (a vessel); (Su. Ist.) bo- (-t-) to be spilled. Kur. po&na (before vowel ponn-, pdyy-; past possa) to fall (of rain); pufdna to pour some water on rice when ready for a meal. Malt. poye (pos-) to rain; puthye to pour. Cf. 4132 Ta. picir, 4199 Go. Pitt, and (for o-forms) 4470 Go. pongana, DED(S, N) 3610, and from DED(S) 3722.

Ta. pey (-v-, -t-) to wear (as jewels, cloths, flowers), put on (as harness), tie, fasten. Ko. peev- (pe-t-) to wear; perc- (pesc-) to put clothes on (someone else). To. piy- (pis-) to wrap up (someone). Te. (K.) péru a necklace of pearls, beads, etc. DEDS(N) 717.

Ta. peyar (-v-, -nt-) to move, depart, turn about, return, grow loose, be detached, be displaced, dislodged; (-pp-, -tt-) to remove, displace, dislodge, dispel, drive away, uproot (as a plant); peyarcci shifting, moving, re- moval, displacement; peyarttu, peyarttum, peyarntu again, in return; peyarppu removal, translation; pér (-v-, -nt-) to become foose, separate, depart, go away, move, retreat, change; (-pp-, -tt-) to change (t7.), remove; pérttu, pérttum, pérntum again, in return. Ma, péruka to come off, be plucked up, cattle to turn in ploughing; péreca collection; pérkka tr. of p&éruka; to copy, transcribe, translate; pérttu again; pérppu copy, trans- lation. Tu. pijiruni to turn, return, abate (as a fever). DED(S) 3611. 4410 Ta, peyar, piyar name; reputation, renown, fame; person; shape, form, sub- stance; peyarkku nominally, for form’s sake; peyaran one who bears a name; (also péran) grandson (as bearing the grandfather’s name), grandfather; peyartti, pértti granddaughter. grandmother; peyariya having a name; pér name; person, individual; living thing; praise, fame. Ma. peyar, piyar name; pér a name, as it were the duplicate of a person or thing; a person, individual. Ko. pe+r name. To. pé-r id.; pé-Sf- (pd-St-) to name (with po-r as object). Ka, pesar(u) name, appel- lation; greatness, fame, celebrity; pesarisu to name, mention, tell. Kod. peda name. Tu, pudaru name, appellation; fame, celebrity, reputation. Bel, (LSB 2.3) hudari name. Kor. (O.) podari, (T.) hudari id
Ta. peru). Ka. (Hav.) padavu a hilltop. Kod. parambi large flat pasture land without aie Tu, padavu plateau, tableland. DED(S)

Ta. piracam pollen, honey, honey- comb, toddy, bee, beetle, wasp; ? peruntén honey secreted by bees; large-sized honey bee (or with 4411 Ta. peru). To. pes ton sp. bee (or with 4411 Ta. peru). Tu, perya a large bee; periya neyi honey. Te. pera honeycomb, beehive; perayiga a kind of honey bee (for Yga, see 533). Kol, (Kin.) pera a kind of bee; pera tatfa hive of such bees; (SR.) perapotte bee. Go. (A.) beral visi sp. bee (Voc. 2603). DED(N) 3614.
Ta. perukilai pink-tinted heart-leaved glory tree, (Lush.) Clerodendrum infortuna- tum, Ma, piraku, peruku id.; peruvila its leaf. For ilai/ila, see 497. DEDS 718.

Ta. peruku (peruki-) to be injured, ruined, snap as a marriage thread. Te. perugu to break or snap, as a (marriage) thread; (K.) pempu to destroy, ruin; n. damage, destruc- tion. Pa, perng- to fall in ruins; perkip- (perkit-) to knock down in ruins. Ku? prenga (prengi-) to be cracked, cloven; prepka (< Aah, sept rene pte hadnt nernien etn perukku (391] prek-p-; prekt-) to crack, cleave; préju (pi, preska) cleavage, crevice, crack; (K.) bre- (boil) bursts. Kuwi (P.*) bre- (-t-) id. DED (S) 3615. 4415 Ta, perukku (perukki-) to sweep; perukkal sweeping; paruvu (paruvi-) to sweep or gather together. Ma. perukkuka to sweep. Ka, porake, parake, baralu, boralu broom. Te. poraka broom, broom-twig, straw. Cf. 1088(a) Ta. kacatu, esp. Ka. kasaparige, etc. DED(S) 3616.
Ta. peruccali bandicoot; (Koll.) perukkan id. Ma. periccari id. Kod. (Shan- mugam) percali id. DED 3617.

Ka. pere skin or slough of a serpent; here to scrape, shave; (Gowda) hermane grater, scraper. Tu. perevuni to be peeled, scraped; perepini to grate, scrape; ereduni to scrape, shave; peradané, peramané grater, scraper; piresuni to peel, scrape, pare off. Te. beradu bark, rind, shell; baradu bark of a tree. Kur, caya-pert, cayi-perper® snake’s old skin. DED(S) 3618.

Go. (Y. Mu.) pereka, (G.) perka, (Ma.) perenka, pen?ka bone; (M.) pereka backbone, rib; (Tr.) panéka bone, hard seed inside a fruit; (Ch.) paneka rib (Voc, 2352); (LuS.) pénka a bone. Konda (BB) peren (pl, perek) id. Pe, prén (pl, preku) id., stone of fruit. Mand. prén (pl. preke) bone. Kui prénu (pl. préka) id., stone or seed of fruit, kernel, heart of tree. Kuwi (1°.) prent bone; (S.) plénu id., stone of fruit; (Su.) prénu, (Isr.) prénu (pl. préka) bone; (T.) pérnu id., stone of fruit. Cf. 4153 Pa. pidka. DED(S) 4419 Ta, perkani (-pp-, -tt-) to be afraid. Ka, pelagu fear, alarm; pelar to tremble, fear; n fear, alarm; pelarisu to cause to fear, frighten; pelpalisu, belpalisu to tremble, fear. Tu, perci shying, starting suddenly; percuni to shy, start aside suddenly. Te. pelukuru to fear, be alarmed. Pa. birk- to be startled. DEDS 719. : 4420 Ka, belava, beluva wild pigeon. Te. bela-guvva, belava ring-dove. DED 3620. 4421 Ta, peruku curdled milk; pirai fer- mented buttermilk used for curdling milk. Ma, piriyuka to coagulate (as milk); piriccal coagulation of milk; pirannuka to ‘curdle. Ko. pep solidified, curdled milk; pev- (pevd-) (ghee) solidifies, (butter) forms in churning; (pevt-) to make (ghee) solidify, make (butter) form; pet- (pety-) (liquid) solidifies. To, pep coagulant agent put in fresh milk: perf- (pert-) (milk) solidifies; peSf- (peSt-) (ghee or milk) solidifies; pesry’ watery buttermilk. Ka. per (pett-) to thicken, congeal, curdle (ghee, curds, oil); heppu cur

Ta. peru (peruv-, perr-) to get, obtain, beget, generate, bear; peruti gain, profit; peruttu (perutti-) to cause to obtain; perri acquisition; perravan father; perrin id., husband; péru acquisition, childbirth; pira Cpp-, -nt-) to be born, be produced; pirantai birth; pirappu birth, origin; piravi birth; pirat crescent moon, Ma, peruka to bear, bring forth, obtain, get; péru birth, bringing forth, what is obtained; pérri midwife; pirakka to spring up, be born; pirappu, piravi birth; pira crescent moon. Ko. perv- (perd-) to be born; perp birth, personal appearance; per new moon. To, perp, peer, peerf act of birth (in songs); per days from the third to the eighth after new moon; pe-rf- (pe-d-) to be born; per- id. (only pero@ ‘he is not born’ In a story phrase; TGT, text 32); ped, in: enwiy fed my younger sibling, ninwir fed your younger sibling, etc. (lit. one born after me, you, etc.). Ka. per (pett-) to get, obtain, beget, bear; pere crescent moon. Kod. per- (peruv-, pett-}) to bear (child). Tu. pedpini, pedduni to bear, bring forth (as a child); pedpavuni to assist a woman in childbirth; pedmedi lying-in woman; perga winnings made in a game; profit, gain; péty, pétu parturition, childbed; (DCV) pedikke birth. Te, pettu to bear (young), lay (eggs); (K.) perayu to obtain. Kol. pet- (pett-) to bear young (said of animal). Vk. pegt- to give birth to. Pa. ped- (pett-) to obtain. ? Go. (Ch.) pirr- to sprout from the ground; pirrana (Tr.) to grow (of young wheat), (Ph.) sprout, DE

Ka. p&ca trouble; péc-adu to be involved in trouble and difficulties. Kod. pe-c-aed- to try hard. Tu, péc-Aduni to try, strive, be in trouble; (B-K.) péc-afa affliction, distress; struggle. Te. péci trouble, difficulty, embarrassment. DED 3628.

Ta. ptcu (péci-) to talk, speak, converse, make noise, roar; tell, say, recite, praise; pécal, péccal talking; péccu speaking, speech, language, praise, talk, report, rumour, word. Ma, pécuka to speak, chatter (as birds); péccu speech, language. Ko, pe+c- (pe-c-) to talk to oneself; perc- (perc-) to give irrelevant answers, talk nonsense, talk with impropriety in presence of holy man. ? To. 68t- (G8ty-) to say, tell (story, lament) (or with 4003 Ta. pagiccu). Ka. per (pérd-) to utter, say, speak, narrate, tell, command; périke saying, telling, rumour; a saying, enigma; pégige saying, etc.; notoriety, fame; péguvike saying, telling. Tu. pérmé fame, glory. Te. p(rélu to praittle, chatter, talk nonsense; prélari a prattler. Kuwi (Isr.) pét- (-it-) to talk sweetly; pelki a- to discuss with another. Kur. pésna to command; péski command, order, precept; perpererna to prate, prattle, talk with a high-pitched voice; (vessel in which some- thing is cooking) to hum, buzz, sing; (Hahn) perxperxna to babble, prattle; parparermna to prate, prattle. Malt. perge to talk, speak, simmer, hiss. DED(S, N) 3629.

Ko. e+c paec unclean sccretions of body (eg. urine, excrement, snot, vomit). To, e-syje+§ 6d- (6d6-) to dislike, hate. Ka. pésu, hésu to feel aversion, have a dislike; n, aversion, disgust; pésike, hésike, ésike, hésige aversion, disgust, nastiness, object that causes disgust. Kod. he+sige disgust at touching dirty things. Tu. pésiyuni, pécuni to loathe, be dispusted; pésigé, p&sigé disgust, loathing, dirt, filth; (B-K.) hésige, ésige filth, dirt. DED 3630.

Ta. peti hermaphrodite; impotence, fear; (-pp-, -tt-) to be afraid; pétan herma- phrodite with male characteristics predominat- ing; peu hermaphrodite; female sex, female of birds and of certain quadrupeds; pétai female of birds, hen. Ma. péfi fear, cowardice; petikka to be afraid, to shy (a horse), fear; péta female of a deer, turtle; a pea-hen, ctc. Ka, hédi, edi, (PBh.) pedi coward; effeminate man, hermaphrodite; hte, hénfe, hyate hen; béda timid man; pdta coward. Kod. po-di fear; po-de ko-li hen. Tu. hédi, héde, tdi coward; timid; podiyuni to be afraid; podiya- vuni, podipavuni to frighten, threaten, terrify; podigé fear, fright. Te. p®édi eunuch, herma- phrodite; péde having no moustache, beard- less man. / Cf. Skt. pofa- hermaphrodite. DED (S) 3631. 4435 Ta, pettu what is unproductive, useless, or kernelless. Ma. pétu what is seed- less, unproductive, shrivelled. DED 3632.

Ta. pén protection; pénu (péni-) to treat tenderly, cherish, foster, protect, regard, - esteem, honour, treat courteously, worship, care for; pénam tenderness, regard, care, nurture; penal nursing; pinai protecting with loving care; pel (petp-, petf-) to protect; petpu protection. Ma. pénuka to foster, take care of; pénam caution, pennuka to take care of, use, take to oneself. Te. pen(u)cu to nourish, nurture, foster, support, rear, fatten, increase, extend; penupu, pempu nourishing, fostering, rearing, increase, cn- largement; pempakamu adoption of a child, rearing; pempudu adopted; tame, domesti- cated. DED(S) 3633. petu [393] pen

Ta. petu bewilderment, folly, ignor- ance, delirium, sorrow, distress; p&tai simple- ton, ignorant ‘person, woman as_ simple- minded; girl between the ages of 5 and 7; poor person; pétaimai folly, ignorance, simplicity; pentu (pénti-) to be bewildered; petumpai girl between the ages of 8 and 11. Ka. p&tu confusion or distraction of mind. Tu. péty, pétu fear. Te, péda poor, timid; poor man, servant; p&édaralu poor woman; pédarikamu poverty; pédarimi id., timidity; (SAN) pédagilu to become thin, poor; pédatanamu poverty. Ga, (S.2) pédafon a poor man. DED(S) 3634.

Ta. pey devil, goblin, fiend; madness (as of a dog), frenzy; wildness (as of veg- etation); péyan demoniac, madman; peytti, péycci, pécci demoness, woman under pos- session of a demon. Ma. p@, p@yi demon (fem. pecci); rage, madness, viciousness; péna ghost, spirit; pé-nayi mad dog. Ko. pe-n, peenm possession of woman by spirit of dead; pe-y demon. To, 6-n the god of the dead. Ka. pé, he madness, rage, vicious- ness; growing wild (as plants), worthlessness; pétu, hede demon; péfkuni, péakuli, hekuli demon; madness, fury (for -kuli, cf. 1918 Ta. kuli); h@ga a mad, foolish man. Tu. peyi demon. Go. (Tr.) pén (I. -k), (Y. D. Mu. S)) pen, (Ph.) pen, ven, (Ma.) p@nu god; (L.) pen (pl, pendku) idol, god; (G.) pénvor priest (Voce, 2364). Pe, pen (pl. -ku) god. Kui pénu (pl. pénga), venu (pl, venga) a god, a spirit. Kuwi (F.) pent, (Su.) penu (pl. pneka), (Isr.) pénu (pl. pénka/pnéka) god; (S.) pénu (pi. pénka) devil; (S.) péne’esi, (Isr.) péne’esi deceased person. ? Malt. peypeyre to feel fervent. or animated. Cf. 5530. Pa. védid. DED(S, N) 3635. 4439 Pe, bé&ron speech, conversation, dis- cussion, language. Mand. béron language; story. Kui b@rori conversation. DEDS 722. 4440 Ka, (Hav.) béru to lift hand to beat. Tu. béruni to be waved, lifted up, as the hand; lift up, as the hand. Cf. 4446 Ta. pirakku. 4441 (a) Ta. pal (palv-, penr-), pel (palv-, pént-) to case oneself. Ka. pel to void excre- ments; m1. excrement. Go, (Tr. W. Ph.) pélana, (Mu.) pél- to defecate; (Mu.) pél, pétkle excr

Ka. beru and 4565 Ta. poru. DED(S, N) 3641, and from DED(S) 3623, 4447 Konda per- (-it-) to chase or pursue (thief, etc.), drive (cattle), expel. Pe. pez- (pést-) to drive, drive away, chase. Mand. pey- to chase. Kui peha (pehi-) to drive away, drive off, repulse, dismiss; (K.) pér- to chase, drive away. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) pér- (-h-) id.; (P.?) pre- (-t-) to chase; (F.) perhali to chase, drive; (S.) perh’nai to scare, impel. DEDS 723. 4448 Ko, pe+r steep slope. To. pd-r (obl. pé-t-) cliff. DED 3642.

Ta. p®rai chest, box, basket; pira round wicker-basket, baling basket. Ma, péta basket of reeds or of bamboo slips. To. pe-dy large basket tied around with cloth (or with 4388). Kod. po-lia basketry box full of edibles, carried by girl of bridegroom’s house to bride’s house, Kuwi (Isr.) pera box. Cf.

Ta. pérai-maram — Carey’s myrtle bloom, Careya arborea. Ma. pér(u), pera id. /Cf. Skt. pilu- id., Turner, CDIAL, no. 8239. DED 3638. _ 4444 To. pa+t each seed section of a Jackfruit. Ka, béle split pulse, the half of a seed of the gufija or Abrus precatorius. Kod. be-le each of the halves of a seed that can be divided. Tu. belé split pulse; bls seed of a jackfruit. Te. bedalu (p/.) split pulse. ? Ko, yer ‘badly ground coarse flour. DED(S)

Ka. ba, béluve bewilderment, in- fatuation, madness; bitana state of being bewildered, etc, Te. bala simpleton; simple, ignorant. DED 3640. 4446 Ta, pipakku (pirakki-) to heap, pile up; perru a pile. Ma, peru a load, esp. bul- lock-load; péruka to load (as oxen), pile up, Ko. pe-rn bullock-load. Ka. péru to lift up and put upon, load, pile up; n. a load, esp. bullock-load. Kod. poer- (po-ti-) to transport by pack-animal. Tu. péruni, péravuni to load, burden; pérafa lading, shipping; béruni to pile up earth; periké a bullock’s load, any burden; periyé a heap, pile; héry a sack of corn. Te. pér(u)cu to pile or load up, arrange in a column or row; pér(u)pu piling or heap- ing up; perikatamu carrying grain on bullocks; perika a large sack or bag, (K.) a bullock which carries loads; (K.) peruku to carry loads. Ga. (S.*) pérsap- to arrange (< Te.). Go. (Ma.) pag to pile up grain in a stack (Voe. 2339). Konda per- (peRt-) to take up, lift up (a load to carry on head or shoulder), heap up (burnt pieces of wood), lay (pot on fire); Ppergis- to cause to lift up (as load onan animal). Pe, pez- (pest-) to pick up, lift. Mand. piy- to pick up. Kuwi (F.) péthali to lift; (S.) perh’nai to lift, raise; (Isr.) per- (-h-) to lift up. Malt. pehe to take up; pehre to take along; pehtre to assist in lifting. Cf.

Ta. pen louse. Ma. pén id. Ko. pe-n head-louse. To. pd-n louse. Ka. pén id; vb. (lice) to increase or grow greatly. Kod. pai pe-ni louse. Tu, pény id. Te. pénu (pl. pélu) id. Kol. pe+n (pl. -kul) id. Nk. pen id. Nk. (Ch,) pen id. Pa, péni (pl. pénul) id. Ga. (OU.) pen (pl. penil) id. Konda (BB) péni (pl. penku) id. Pe. pen (pl. -ku) id. Mand. pen (pl. -ke) id. Kui pé@nu flea, Kuwi (F.) pénti id.; (S.) pénu, (Su. Isr.) penu_ (i. pneka), (P.) penu (el. ptnka) touse. Kur, pén id.; alla-pén flea (alla dog). Malt. pénu louse. DED(S) 3643. -

Ta. pai bag, sack, purse, satchel, bladder, duct; pacumpai a pedlar’s pack carried over the shoulder; acampi, acampai traveller’s bag thrown over the shoulder. Ma. pai, payimpa bag, sack, stomach, womb. Ka. pasube, pasumbe, hasibe, hasube, has- umbe a long bag that has its opening in the midst and is thrown over the shoulder so as to form two divisions. Tu. pasymbé, pasambé sack or bag made of coir; paimbé bag; paiky, paika pouch, scrip made of rushes or palm leaves; payicily bag made of palm leaves. Te. (B.) asimi bag placed on the back of a bullock to carry things. Cf. 4049 Ma. pakku. DED(S) 3644.
Ka. pai upper, external, extra, upon. Te. pai, payi upper or external surface, exterior, top; upper; on, upon, above; in future, hereafter. DED 3645.

Ta. po (-pp-, -tt-) to perforate, puncture, make a hole; poy (-v-, -t-) to be hollowed; , tubularity, hole, hollow or recess in tree; poku (-pp-, -tt-) to make a hole, perforate; pokkanai hole in a tree, stone, or ground, cleft in rock; pokku hollow in a tree, defect, fault, blemish; pokkai little hole, crack, having a part deformed, blemish; potixr (pp-, -tt-) to pierce; potu (-v-, -nt-) to be perforated; (-pp-, -tt-) to bore, pierce; potumpu hole, hollow in a tree, pit, cave; pottu hole, rat-hole, hollow in a tree, rent or puncture, defect; pottal, pottai hole, orifice, defect; pottilam hole in a tree; pOttu, pontar, pontu hole, hollow; pon cave. Ma, pottu hole in the ground, cavity, hollow hand; potu a hole as in worm- eaten wood. Ko. pok-va-yn man whose tecth are all gone (cf. Ta. pokku-vay, pokkai-vay toothless mouth). Ka, bokke any round, small hole made by rats, etc.; hodaru hollow of a tree, hole in the ground; bokka a tooth- less man; (Hav.) bokku toothless. Tu. bonhku hollow, void, empty; (B-K.) pogulu a hole, usually in a mud dam across a watercourse; (B-K.) bokku, bokkubayi mouth without teeth. Te. bokka hole, orifice, aperture, pit; (Telangana dial., K.) pokka hole; botta hole, leak; bonda hole, bore; bokki toothless. Kol. pokka ditch, grave; (Pat., p. 115) pokor hollow; bogga smail hole, perforation, Nk. pokka hole, cave;,bogga hole. Pa. botta id.; potpa, poppa a chisel. Ga. (P.) bonga hole. Go. (Tr. W. Ph.) pohpi, (Ma) popi chisel (Voce. 2432); (D. G. Mu. Ma.)

Ka. pogade, pagade Mimusops elengi. Te, pogada id. DED 3148. 4454 Ma, pokina, pokana, pdna, pina the green imperial pigeon, Carpophaga sylvatica. Kol. (Kin.) podlg id. Pa. ponal id. Go. (Tr.) ponar, (other dialects) ponar id. (Voce. 2445). Kuwi polgu (T.) green pigeon, (Isr.) green dove. Kur. poxa green pigeon. Malt, .poge id. DED 3647. .

Ta. pokuttu bubble; pokku (pokki-), po (-pp-, -tt-) to be blistered; pokkulam a boil, bubble, blister; pokkuli (-pp-, -tt-) to rise in blisters, Ma, pokkula, pokkila blister, vesicle, bubbie; pokkulikka to bubble; pokifu, pola bubble. Ko. pogl blister; pogic- (poglc-) (hand) becomes blistered from heat. To. pig bubble; pig- (pigy-) (hand) gets blistered by friction; ni» pikwily water-blister (for ni-, see 3690). Ka. pugul, bokke, bobbe blister; hokku a boil; hoppala blister occasioned by a burn; hoppalisu to blister. Kod. pokkala a blister. Tu. pokké id., pustule; a sore, ulcer; bokki, bokké an itch, pustule, pimple; ponky a kind of boil or sore. Te. pokku blister; vb. to blister; bugga bubble; bobba blister; ? pota eruption of smallpox. Kol. (Kin.) pokk blister; bugga bubble. Nk. (Ch.) popondel bubbles (or with 4525). Pa. pova (pl. povel) blister. Go. (A.) poppul id. (Voce. 2393); (A.) popota, (Mu.) papel bubble (Voc, 2392). Pe, poka blister. Mand. puka boil. Kuwi (Su.) bugga (pl, -na), (Isr.) btiga bubble. Kur. pokkhna (pukkhya) to get blistered, swell. Malt. poka blister, blain; pogto, in: mugpoglo wart. Cf. 4525 Ka. bobbuli.and 2106 Ta. koppalam. /Cf. Nep. phoko blister, boit (Turner, CDIAL, no. 8391, where it is the only item having these meanings, all the others meaning ‘hollow’ or developments from it). DED(S, N) 3648. 4456 Ta, pukai (-v-, -nt-) to burn as the heart, be chagrined, grieve; n. distress; puk- aivu heart-burning. Tu. buguluni to be grieved. Te. pogulu, pov

Ta. pokkapam beggar’s bag, wallet, cloth used.as a sack; mokkani feed-bag, nose- bag; a kind of bridle for mules, etc. Ma. pokkanam beggar’s bag, wallet. Ka. bokkana pocket in a coat, betel pouch, beggar’s bag, horse’s gram-bag. Tu. bokkana bag, pocket. Te. bokkanamu, bofkanamu pouch, purse; bokkaniya gram-bag, nose-bag: bokkena id.; bucket for drawing water, leathern bag for baling water out of a boat, etc.; (B.) ponka- namu purse, pouch; ? bokkasamu money bag; treasury. Kol (SR.) bokane bucket (Kamaleswaran). /Cf. Skt. (Sebdaratnakara 1478) bhukkana- the food-bag tied round the head of a horse. DED(N) 3650.

Ta. pokkam lie, falsehood, deceit, fault; poccam, poccu lie, falsehood; fault, defect; poccai fault; poccappu badness, evil, wickedness, fault. ? Ma. pokkar low people. Ka. bonku to lie. Tu. bonkuni to deny. Te. poccemu deceit, trick, defect, fault; bonku to lie; n, a lie; false, untrue; bonkincu to deceive, tempt; cause to tell a lie; bonkari a liar; bonkul¥du id.; fem. bonkulidi. Kuwi (S.) bokinai to pretend; (Isr.) pok- (-h-) to tell lies. Cf. 4531 Ta. poy. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) phukka = mithya. DED(S) 3651. 4460 (2) Ta. pokkul, pokil navel; pokkani a large open navel. Ma. pokkil, pokkul navel. Ko. puku- id. To. piku- id.*Ka. pokkug, porkug id. Kod, pokki. id. Tu. puvalu, piivolu id. Bel. (LSB 2.2) hodkulu id. Kor (T.} hokru id. Te. pokkili id; a depression. Kol. bogur navel. Nk. bogur id. Pe. pinel id. Mand. punel id. Kui ptrenji, punenji id. Kuwi (F.) ptleri, (S.) pttleni, (Su. Isr.) puleni id. Br, put id., umbilical cord, , (b) Ma, potu the navel, umbilical rupture. Ka. (Bell.; U-P.U.) budda navel. Te. boddu id. Pa, bod id., navelstring. Ga. (Ol) bodi bori navel. Go. (Ph.) budri, boddi, (Ko.) bodum id.; (A. Ch.) mod, (Mand-)’modd, (G. Ma.) moddi, (Ph.) mud(d), (SR.) madd. (Mu.) maddi id., (Ph. also) navel-string; (Tr.) mud, mad navel-string (Voce. 2977). Konda bodu navel. Kur. (Hahn) butfi id. DED(S, N)

Te. (Savara Dora dial., S. Bhatta- charya) poka mahua flower. Kol (SR.) pokke flower; (Kin.) mahua flower. Nk. pokke mahua flower, DED(S) 3653. 4462 Kur. pokol cocoon, silk, Malt. poklu’ tassur silk; pokl-fowa cocoon (towa anything hollow, as the shell of an cosh DED 3654,

Ko. pog, pogl foam. To. pig foam (on river). Ka. burugu foam, lather, scum. Kol. boskur, (Kin.) Pogcur foam. Pa poyor id. Go. (Ko.) boyul id. (Voc. 2635); (Koya Su.) borju lather. Kur, poxta froth, foam. Malt. potge, potgo id. ? Ta. pavvam id.; water bubble. DEB(S, N) 3655. , pofku
Ka. (Hav.) bo bitch. Tu. bogre, (B-K. boggi bitch. male dog; boggi also) boggé dog;
Te. boggu charcoal, carbon. Kol. (Kin.) bogg charcoal. Pa. bog (pl. boggul), bogum (pl bogmul) id. Ga. (S.1) bogge id. Kuwi (F.) bogtina id. ? Malt. posange soot. Br. pogh charcoal. DED(S) 3656.
Ka. pohgara, hofigara Indian coral tree, Erythrina indica. Tu. pongaré, pohgara id. DED 3657.

Ta. ponku (ponki-) to boil up, bubble up by heat, foam and rage (as the sea), increase, swell (as with joy), shoot up, be elated, burst in anger, be swollen (as a boil or sore), rise, grow high, abound, flourish, be fruitful, cook (¢r.); n. prosperity, fortune; pofikal boiling, bubbling, swelling, violent anger, abundance, splendour, boiled rice seasoned with salt, pepper, cumin seeds and ghee; n. pr. of a festival; height, largeness, fulness, abundance, profusion; ponkam in- crease, abundance, joy, splendour; pokkam abundance, eminence, splendour: pommu (pommi-) to swell, excel in appearance; pommal abundance, thickness, plumpness; (Ramnad dial.; Annamalai, p. 875) bomm- enal to swell. Ma. portuka to boil over, bubble up, spread (as light, noise, report); pofinal boiling, bubbling up, ostentation; ponfikka, pokkuka to raise; pofnaccam display, boasting; pokkam height, boiling over, evaporation. Ko, pog- (pogy-) to boil over; pong- (pongy-) to increase (intr.) magically in number, (water) springs forth magically, anger increases; ponge- (pongc-) to increase (t.) magically in number. To, pig- (pigy-) to bubble up, boil over, (stomach) swells or heaves with exertion. Ka. pofgu to boil over, burst open, expand, open, blossom, swell, be elated, exult, be over- jJoyed; n. boiling over, expanding, etc.; pofigisu to cause to expand, etc.; pofga, man of ebullition or of exalted courage; pongalu, pofgil rice boiled with pulse, salted, or sweetened with sugar; (PBh.) potigam pride; (Ha

Go. (Tr.) pongand to flow; be washed away, drown; (Gr., p. 70) of a river, to over- flow its banks; pohtana to drown a man, cause to be washed away; (M) pofgana to flow; (W.) pongana to float away; pongitana to spill; (Ph.) ponganad to flow; pongsahtana to cause to flow (water, blood, etc.); pon- (G. Mu.) to flow (saliva, etc.), (S.) flow, drop (tears) (Voc. 2374); (ASu.) pon- (pus or blood) to come out of a wound. Konda (BB) pon- to be spilled; pok- to spill; (K.) pok- to pour (water). Pe. boy- (-t-) to be spilled; bok- (-t-) to spill. Mand. buk- to pour. Kui ponga (pongi-) to be spilt, scattered; popka (< pok-p-; pokt-) to spill, scatter. Kuwi (F.) bokhali to spill; (S.) bokh’nai to shed, spill; (Isr.) bok- Ch-) to spill. Cf. 4407 Ta. pey, esp. Kuwi (F.) boiyali, intransitive correspond- ing to bokhali (t7.) above, and other forms with o-vowel. From DED(S, N) 3658.

Ma. pontu a float, raft, buoy, boat; pottfiuka to rise, as out of the water. Tu. ponguni, pufguni to rock, reel, toss (as a boat); pofgely tumbling, rocking, unsteadi- ness; pofgayi canoe; purge, pungele a fickle- minded man. Ga, (OlL) ponor act of floating; ponor er- to swim, float. Go. (A. Ch.) pong- to float (Voc. 2374). Konda pongray 4- to swim. DED(S) 3659.

Ta. ponkotam child’s amulet tree, Putranjiva roxburghii. Ma. potkolam id. DED 3660. 4473 Kur. boygna to run, run away, leave a place for good, keep clear of; bongta’and to make run, carry about or off with great speed, lose (by death or otherwise). Malt; bonge to run, flee; bongtre to cause to run, carry away. DED 3661.

Ta. pocunku (pocunki-) to be united, agree. Ka. posayisu, posavisu to join (¢r.), unite, apply to, attach. Te. posigu to be favourable, suit, be agreeable, suitable, or fit, be congruous, consistent, or compatible, {396} poti be got, happen; friendship to exist, good or friendly terms to exist or prevail; posdgincu to cause to agree; posdgimpu causing to agree; posigudu agreeing, agreement; occurrence; obtaining. DED 3662.
Ta. poccd (-pp-, -nt-), poyccd (-pp-, ? -nt-) to forget; poccappan forgetful person, poccappu forgetfulness. Konda pos- (+t-) to forget. Pe. p&c- (-c-) id. DEDS(N) 727.

Ta. poccu woman’s pubic hair, vulva, anus. Ma. pocca, pocci membrum muliebre. Ko. poj vulva. Ka. pucci, pucce id. Br. pos id. / Cf. Pkt. posa- anus, vulva; posana- anus; phosa- id. Also Mar. pucei vulva (Turner, CDIAL, no. 8248). DED(S, N) 3663.
Ta. poccu quantity of hair. Ka. boceu wool, fine hair, down. Te. boccu hair, down, wool. Nk. bucura a knot of hair. Pa. bocca eyebrow. DED 3664.

Ta. poceai paunch, pot-belly. Ka. bojje belly, paunch; bojju pot-belly. Te. bojja belly, paunch; borra pot-belly. Pa, bokka big intestine, large stomach of ruminants.
