Ta. miti (-pp-, -tt-) to tread on, tread down, trample on, insult, dishonour, rush or dash against, attack as the door of a fort; 7. treading, tread, step in a tank or well, treadle of a weaver’s loom, food trampled and formed into a ball, walking, gait; mitiyal treading; (Koll.) meti to step on. Ma, meti treading on, treading out grain; metikka to tread as loam, trample, thesh; metiyuka to be bruised; (Tiyya) motikkuva to trample. Ka. midi to pound, kill, destroy, rub, grind, triturate; midisu, middisu to cause to pound, have pounded. ? Kod. mey- (meyv-, meyfij-) (paddy) becomes well pounded or cleaned; (meyp-, meyc-) to pound (paddy). Tu. medi- minumu [434] milix puni, medupuni, medpuni to clean by pound- ing as rice; full, mill. Te, medugu to be pounded or ground, be reduced to fine powder or paste; be trained or practised, be accustomed or habituated; meducu to grind, pound, reduce to fine powder or paste, medupu id., train, accustom, habituate; médincu to rub, grind. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) med- Git-) to put down foot; (F.) medali to push; metkali to jostle. DED(S, N) 3982.

Te. minumu (usually pl minumulv) black gram, Phaseolus radiatus. Pa. midi (usually pl midil) id. Ga. (Ol) mindil P. mungo. {2 Skt. ex.) malada- P. radiatus. [P. radiatus is a variant of P. mungo.} DED (N) 3983. 4863 Kol. mind- (mint-) to bury. Nk. mind- to fix stakes in ground. Ga. (S.*) medup- to bury. ? Go. minjana (SR.) to bury, (Tr.) (snake) to bury itself in the earth, (field) to be covered with crop, (M.) to hide; (Ko.) mins- to hide (Voce. 2821); (SR. Tr. W. Ph.) missand, (Mu.) mis-, (Ma. Ko.) miss-, (M.) misina, (Ch.) mistana to bury (Voc. 2849), ? Malt. mande to bury (or with 4836). DED(S, N) 3984. 4864 Kol. (SR.) kanla mindi, (Kin.) kandl mindig (pl) eyelash. Go. (A. Ch. Ma.) mindi, (Tr. W. Ph.) mind? id.; (M.) konda-mindi eyebrow; (Ko.) konda-mindi eyelid, eyelash (Voc. 2831). /Cf. Halbi mendi eyelashes. DEDS(N) 786.

Go. (SR.) minangana to taste sweet, (Tr.) mingand id. (3sg. neut. minugta); (Ph.) mingana (n, not 9, before g), (Ch. Ma. Ko.) ming-, (M.) mingana id.; (G.) migta, (Mu.) mirigt, (Mu.) milin, (L.) mirgul sweet; mirggul burka sweet gourd (Voc. 2829); (Lus.) mingtura sweet; (ASu.) minnil man- (fruit) to be tasty; minntin- (fruit, water, etc.) to be tasty. Mand. mrik- to be sweet. DEDS(N) 788.

Ta. mirunku (migupki-), mugunku (muzunki-), viruntku (viguhki-) to swallow, gulp, devour, consume; vigukku a gulp, swallow; muruntu (mugunti-) to swallow. Ma. mizunnhuka, virutfuka to swallow, devour, gulp down; miruinikka to absorb. Ko. migg- (mingy-) to swallow; ming act of swallowing. Ka. miigu, mufgu to swallow. Kod. mugg- (muggi-) id. Te. m(r)ifigu to swallow, devour, consume. Kol, (Kin.) ming-, (SR) mig-_ to swallow. Nk, migg- id. Nk. (Ci) ming- (minkt-) id. Go. (A.) ming-, (Y.) mig- id. (Voce. 2818). Konda rin- Git), (Guri dial.) _miriq- id., devour. Pe, rugginga, rugginda onom; of swallowing. Kur. (Hahn) munxna to devour. DED(S, N) 3985.
Ta. milaku black pepper, Piper nigrum, Ma. milaku, mulaku pepper. Ko. melg chilli. To. mélx pepper. Ka. menasu, merasu, morasu id. Kod. malu chillies. Tu. munuci, munci pepper, chilli. DED 3986.
Ko. mily rope made of two leather thongs (runs from yoke to head of plough- share). Ka. mili, mini rope made of twisted leather straps. DED 3987.

Ta. milir (-v-, -nt-) to be upset, turned topsy-turvy; Gpp-, -tt-) to roll (¢r.), turn over, upset. ?Kol (Pat., p. 131) midi- yeng to fall over. Pa. milgg- to be overturned, milkip- (milkit-) to overturn (tr.); ? bilj- to fall on the back; bilja lying on the back; biljip- (biljit-) to make to fall on the back. Go, mirnd- (Mu.) to turn round, turn back, (Ma.) to be turned over, inverted (of the eyes); caus. (Ma.) mir?- to turn over, invert; (L. midina, midiyand to overturn (Voc. 2845). Konda mer-, met- to fall over. Kur mlinga (mlingi-) to be turned over, turn on the side; mlipka (< mlik-p-; mlikt-) to turn over (tr.), overturn, turn upside down; 7. overturning; milpa (milt-/mif-) to overturn, change over, turn ovet; mliva (mlit-) to be changed, transformed; #4. transformation. Kuwi (S.) mli- to change; mith’nai, milph’nai to invert; mriphnai id., turn over; (Isr.) moi-/ mri- (-t-) to change into (horse, etc.); myip- (A-) id. (tr.). Kur. mulukhna to turn upside down, overturn, capsize (fr.); mulUkhina to get upturned, capsize (fr.). DED(S, N) 3988.

Ta. milir (-v-, -nt-) to roll (as the eyes), jump, leap. Ka. milir to move to and fro, move about, swing, wave, jump. or fly about, roll; 1, moving to and fro, etc.; milircu to move about (¢r.); milliyi to move to and fro, move about; millisu to move about, fly, float, wave, jump, frisk; mellisu to move round (t7.), cause to revolve, roll (as the eyeball); melle rolling, looking obliquely, squinting; mella id., a squinting man. Tu. malave man with squint eyes. Te. mella squinting, squint; adj, squinting, squint; melaigu to wander, rove about, behave, conduct oneself; caus. melicu; melapu to conduct, lead, manage; (K. also) 7. mannerly behaviour, skill; meliyu to be twisted; meli, melike turn, twist; (K.) melayu, meliyu to wander, behave. Go. (ASu.) melli- to move; melliis- to cause to move. DED(N) 3989. milir [435] micai

Ta. milir (-v-, -nt-) to become famous; n. greatness; mili king, chief, strong man, great man;strength, valour, etc. ? Ma, merukka to shine with fat, thrive, grow stout; meruppu lustre, brilliancy, stoutness; meruppikka to fatten. Ka, milir to prosper, flourish, increase or grow greatly, become powerful. ? Tu. meliyuni to become rich. DED 3990.
Ta. milai wood, forest serving as a defence, thicket, copse, bush, fenced en- closure; guard, watch; mifai bush, small shrub. Ka. mele bush, clump, thicket; midi bush, small shrub. DED 3991.

Ka. milli, minni small wooden or metal spoon; small metal vessel in the form of a cambu, any smali metal vessel used for drinking. Tu. milli small metallic vessel. Te. milli small spoon, teaspoon. DED 3992.

Ta. mirai (-pp-, -tt-) fo oppress, harass; suffer, be afflicted; n. fear, trouble, torment. Mae. mira excitement, fear. Te. meramu to cause pain or mortification; rankle; (K. also) pierce, stab; merumu to pierce, stab; meramera rankling, fear, anxiety; merameram-anu, merameral-ddu to rankle. Raye mergtre to act furiously. DED(S)

Ta. min flash, glitter, lightning; minmini firefly; minnu (minni-) to emit lightning, shine, glitter; minnal lightning; bright coin; minudku (minuitki-) to glitter, shine, appear bright; minukku (minukki-)’ to polish, brighten, beautify, make a display; minukkam, minukkal polish, brightness, excel- lence, showiness, show; min star. Ma. min- nuka to flash, shine; minnal lightning; minni shining; a gem in ear-rings; minnikka to cause to flash or shine; minnu lightning; minukka to be fine, glitter; minukkuka to polish, varnish, make glitter; minukkam_ shining, polish; minunnuka to glitter; minuppu spark- ling; mim star. Ko. mine- (minc-) to flash, glitter, be dazzling, lighten; mi-+n star. To. mic- (mié-) to flash, lighten; mic lightning (in songs); mien star. Ka. mina glittering, sparkling; minaku, minuku to glitter; 7. glitter; minu, mini sparkling, shining; minuku, minugu to shine, glitter; ». lustre, etc.; mificu to shine, be bright, sparkle, glitter, flash, lighten; #. shine, lustre, brightness, glitter, lightning; min star. Kod. minn- (minni-) to lighten, flicker. Tu. minimini twinkling, glistening, dimly shining; meny glitter, sparkle; minuku, menaky, menuku sparkling; min(u)- kuni, mep(y)kuni, minukuni, mejicuni, mifi- cuni to shine, sparkle, glitter; mefici bright- ness, lightning; (B-K.) menkoli, menkori glowworm. Te. minuku to glimmer, sparkle; n, glimmer, glimmering, sparkling; miniigu, mindguru, midiigu, midiiguru spark of fire, firefly; min(u)ku twinkling, twinkle,

To. micy- (mi-d-) to bathe (used of the priest of the ti-, ie. the most sacred grade of dairy). Ka, mi, miyu (mind-, mid-) to take a bath, bathe; cause to bathe, wash, bathe, pour over (the body); 7. bath; miyisu to cause to take a bath, bathe; mina, miha bathing, bath. Tu. mipini to take a bath, wash oneself; mipavuni to make or cause to bathe, bathe, wash, give a bath; miyarpuni to wash a corpse. Pa. mi- (mifi-) to bathe. Ga. (OIL) (nir) muy-, (S.) mi-, miy- id. Go. (G. Mu.) miy-, (Ma. Ko.) mi-, (L.) miyina, mint id.; (Tr.) mihitanad to wash somebody else’s body, esp. infants; (L.) micna to bathe another; (Mu.) mih- id. (Voe. 2855), Mand. mi- Ct-) to bathe. Kui miva (mit-) to lave, bathe or anoint oneself, be anointed or spattered; n, act of bathing; mispa (mist-) to bathe, wash (another); m, act of bathing; musa (musi-) to wash the head. Kuwi (F.) miali to bathe (oneself); mithali to bathe (another); (S.) minai to wash; (Su. Isr.) mi- (-t-) to bathe; Csr.) mit- (-h-) to give bath. Kur. mijn to wash the face of; miijhma to wash one’s face. Malt, mtnje to wash another’s face; munjre to wash one’s own face. DED(S) 3995.

Ta. micai, vicai moustache. Ma. misa, visa moustaches, whiskers; feelers of insects, antennae. Ko, mi-c moustache. To. mi-s id. Ka. mise id. Kod. mi-se id. Tu. misé moustaches, whiskers; feelers of an insect. Te. misamu (pi. misamulu, misalu) moustache, whiskers. Kol mi-sal (pl) moustache. Nk. (Ch.) misak id. Go, (M.) misang, (Y.) misin, (Mu.) misan, mé@chan, (Ma.) misag, (Ko.) misok id. (all pl. forms); (S.) misal (pl. -ku) id. (Voce. 2857). Konda minja [436] muka mésamku (pl.) id. Pe. mésku id. Kui (K.) misamku id. Kur, mici, misi, mecha id. /Cf. Mar. misi (pl. miss) moustache. Cf. also Santali misi, Mundari mici, misi id. (Pfeiffer, p. 183). DED(S) 3996. 4880 Kui minja (minji-) to attack, charge, rush or pounce upon; #, attack; minjora an attacker; fem., neut, minjori. Kuwi (F.) minjitesi he snatched away from; (S.) minj- Cit-) to rob; (Isr.) minj- (Cit-), mih- (t-) to take by force; miski 4- id., tug. DEDS 789.

Ta. mil. (milv-, minf-) to return, disappear, vanish, be cured as of a disease, be rescued, redeemed, liberated, pass beyond; (mitp-, mif{-) to liberate, extricate, release, bring back, recover, remove, cause to dis- appear, redeem, restore, rescue, chew the cud; mila again; mili returning; mifci id., turning; bringing back, causing to return, releasing, redeeming; mitpu releasing, redemp- tion, salvation; miftu (miffi-) to cause to return, save. Ma. miluka to return; bring back; mileca returning, bringing back; viluka to return, get back; avenge; vintu again; vilea return, ransom. Ko. mi-n- (mi-nd-), mi-nd- (miendy-) to escape; mi-nt- (mi-nty-) to make to escape; mi+{- (mi-ty-) to be successful in dispute or quarrel; (?) eat. Cf.

Ta. miru (miri-) to go beyond, exceed, transgress, violate, infringe, domineer, be in excess, remain over, be great, grow lofty as a tree, stout as a person, be haughty; viru (viri-) to be distinguished, pre-eminent, increase; #. excellence, distinction, abundance. Ma, miruka to exceed, transgress; viruka to be grand, dignified, be puffed up, proud. Ko. misr- (miery-) to be extreme in anything, (beauty) is great, refuse to heed. To. mier- (mi-ry-) to become uncontrollable. Ka. miru to go beyond, go beyond the proper limit, transgress, evade, surpass, exceed, put into the shade; pass, elapse (as time); move out of reach, become excessive (as trouble, etc.); be elated, lofty, unrestrained or proud, act overbearingly; miruha going beyond, transgressing, etc. Kod. muer- (mueri-; Mercara dialect mu-ruv,mu-nd-) to disregard, disobey (one’s words), be superior to, outdo. Tu, miry much, exceeding; miruni to trans- gress, violate, exceed; miravuni to cause to surpass or exceed; miriké, mirigé, miruviké transgression, violation. Te. miru to exceed, transgress, excel, surpass, increase, pass, elapse, grow haughty, conceited, impertinent or disobedient. Kol. (Pat., p. 195) videng to overtake. Go, (Tr.) wiraind to pass, surpass, outstrip; (A. Ch.) vir- to surpass; (Mu.) vit- to be more than sufficient, win; (Pat.) widana (d = 7) to cross over (Voc. 3268). Kui mija (miji-) to exceed, excel, surpass, overtake, pass by, precede. DED(S) 3998.

Ta. min fish. Ma. min, Ko, mi-n. To. mien, Ka. min. Kod. mi-ni. Tu. miny. Te. minu. Pa, mini (ol. minul). Ga. (Oll.) min (pl. minil). Go. (Tr. A. W. Ph. Mu. ete.) min (Voe. 2852). Konda (BB) min (pl minga). Pe, min (pl, -ku). Mand. min (pl. -ke). Kui minu (pl. minga). Kiwi (F.) minu (pl. mrika), (S.)} minu (pf minka), (Su. P.) minu (pl. mnika), (Ist.) minu/mninu (pl. mpika). Malt. minu. /Cf. Skt. mina- fish; Turner, CDIAL, no. 101404. DED(S) 3999.

Ta. muka (-pp-, -nt-), mukar (-v-, -nt-), mo (-pp-, -nt-) to smell; moppam smell; nose; mor smell; (Tinn.) modru to smell; mtcu (mUci-) to sniff; mticcu respiration, breath. Ma, mukakka, mukaruka to smell, kiss. Ko. mu-t- (mu-yt-) to smell (¢.); ? mung a kiss. To, mu+t- (mu-ty-) to smell (¢7.); mu-t act of smelling, sniffing; scent (of track). Ka. misu to smell; (Hal.) mttcu, nttcu id. Kod. mues- (mu-si-) id. (t.). Ti. mUsuni to smell, take notice; mUsu smell; mUs&vuni to make smell; miisaliyuni to scent, trace, track, smell; mugalté scent; milri bad smell, stench; modga fragrance. Te. mclcu, mUru-konu, mirkonu to smell. Kol, (Pat., p. 139) mun- stoleng to sniff (i.c. munst-dleng sniffing, to see; cf. 1066). Pa, muf-, (S.) mtifik- to smell, sniff. Go. (fr.) muskana to smell at, e.g. flower; (W.) maskanad, (M.) muskanad to smell; (SR.) muskang to inhale, enjoy per- fume; (G. Mu. Ko.) mUsk- to smell, sniff (Voc. 2932); (ASu.) mUsk- to smell. Konda (BB) mtnz- to sniff, smell. Pe. mtnj- (mtnc-) id. Mand. minj- id. Kui mtnja (mitnji-) id.; n, act of smelling. Kuwi (S.) mtnjinai, (F.T.) miinj-, (Isr.) mnj- (it-) to smell. ? Kur. nusUngna id. DED(S, N) 4000.

Ta. muka (-pp-, -nt-) to draw (as water), bale, measure (as grain or liquid), obtain in full measure, lift, take up; mukavai drawing as water, taking up as grain, anything which is given in large quantities, bucket for drawing water, ladle, heap of paddy on the threshing floor; mukai large earthen vessel; mo (-pp-, -nt-) to take in a vessel (as water). Ko, mus+y- (mu-d-) to scoop up (water) with pot; mueg clay pot in which cows are milked by priests at milk-ceremony. To. mu- the most sacred dairy-vessel; ady mu-+ earthen ‘vessel with various dairy uses, including that of fetching water from the stream. Ka, moge to take water with a vessel out of a pond, tank or stream for one’s use, ladle out (any fluid with a small vessel out of a larger one), scoop, bale; n. state of (water) being sufficient to be taken (out of a pond, etc.); moge, mage, magi small earthen vessel for lading out; (K.?) mogape a vessel for lifting water. Tu. mugé, mugayi small earthen vessel; mogére mukafu [437] mukir fisherman; muggery a Malayalam fisherman; magapuni to draw and turn over; niry m. to draw water (or with 4617 Ta. makifi). / Cf. Mar. mogha a kind of vessel (used on water- wheels, etc.). DED(S) 4001.

Ta. mukatu top, highest part, ridge of a roof, hump of a camel, platform; mofu height, hill, eminence, top of a house, etc.; mucei crown of the head, tuft of hair on the head, crest. Ma, mukatu the head-end of a cloth; mukal top, summit, tidge, roof. Ko. moy]| ridge of roof. To, muxul id. Ka, mogadu, mogalu, magil id. Tu. mugili turret, top as of a temple; modu hill. Te. mogadu ridge of roof; modu raised or high ground; (Inscr.) moru peak. Go. (W. Ph.) mukur comb of cock (Voe, 2864). / Cf. Skt. mukuta-, mauli- crest, diadem; BHS, Pali makuta- id.; Pkt. matila- id; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10144, DED 4002.

Ja. mukam face, mouth; mukappu front, forepart, porch, facade; mukari, muka- nai forepart, front, beginning, headship; mukarimai chieftaincy; mukarai, morai face, chin; mukavu facade, porch: mokkaffai, mokkai face. Ma. mukam face, front, mouth, commencement, chief; mukana forepart; mukappu frontispiece; mukaru face, forepart; mukaruka to go forward; mor face. To, mix open ground in front of village, where people sit and buffaloes are milked. Ka. moga face, mouth; more face. Tu. moga, Muganu the front; moré face, visage. Te. moga the front part, beginning, commencement, mouth of a river; mogakonu to face; mogadala the front part, front; mogamu face, mouth, the front: momu the face, countenance; mora face or head, generally of the lower animals; (B.} mokkatfu features, face, likeness. Kol, mokam face. Nk, mokam id. Pa. mokom id. Go. (G. Ma.) mukam, (M.) mukum id. (Voc. 2861); (A. S. Ko.) mokom id. (Voc, 2972). Konda mokom id.; moro protruding face of animal. Pe. mtm face. Mand. mUmb id. Kui (K.) mrambu id. Kuwi (Su. P. Isr. \.) mitimbu, (S.) mumbi, (Mah.) maka id. DED(S) 4003. 4890 Ta, mukari, mukuli, mucali, mutali fragrant screw-pine, Pandanus odoratissimus. Te. mogali, (Inscr.) movali id. DED 4004.

Ta. muki (-v-, -nt-) to end, terminate, be finished; Cpp-, -tt-) to finish, conclude, achieve, accomplish; mukivu end, termination; muy (-v-, -nt-) to end; muficu (mufici-) id. To. mux- (mux@-) (one’s days) are numbered, finished. Ka. mugi to end, terminate, be finish- ed, accomplished, mugiyuvike ending, ceasing, state of being complete; mugisu, mugasu, mugusu to conclude, finish (t7.). Tu. mugiyuni to end, terminate, cease; mugipuni to finish (tr.); mugité, mugipu_ settlement, decision, contract, bargain; mugitala end, conclusion; final; mugituruni to accomplish, conclude, finish. Te, mugiyu to be finished, completed, ended, terminated, concluded, terminate; mugincu to finish, complete, end, terminate, conclude; mugimpu finishing, completion, end, close, termination, conclusion; mugiyika terminating, ending. Kui mtiga (miigi-) to be completed, terminated, accomplished; 7. completion, accomplishment; mupka (< muk- p-; mukt-) to complete, finish, accomplish; mupa (milt-) id., succeed, be able, have power; n. ability; mugse completely, entirely. Kuwi (S.) ma- (-t-) to -be able, sufficient; Usr., Rayagada dial.) ma?- (-t-) to be able. Kur. munjna to finish off, come to the end of, consume, spend completely; mufjurna to come to an end, be completed, die; (Pfeiffer 1972). DED(S) 4005.

Ta. mukil cloud. Ma, mukil id. To. nis muxulm mist on hills in morning (in song; see 3675). Ka, mugil cloud, series or bank of clouds; the sky. Tw, mugaly, mugaly cloud; mugaliyuni to become cloudy (the sky). Te. mogulu, moyilu cloud. Kol. morgar id. Pa. malgur id. Ga. (Ol. P.) mogul id., sky; (S.) moggul id. Go. (M.) moyol, (Ma.) moyol (pi. moyosk), (L.) muyol cloud (Voc. 2987); (LuS.) moyoolee a cloud; (Koya T.) moyyel id. DED(S) 4006.

Ta. mukir (-v-, -nt-) to appear in the form of a bud; (-pp-, -tt-) to bud, put forth buds, appear, fold or close up as a flower in petals, shut as the eyes; display, cause to appear, bear, bring forth; kai-mmukir (pp-, -tt-) to join the palms of the hands, as in salute, prayer, etc.; mukiram flower-bud about to bloom; mukiri (-pp-, -tt-) to fold or close up as a flower its petals, shut as the eyes; mukai (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to bud: x. opening bud; moO (-pp-, -tt-) to close as a bud; an. flower-bud; mokku flower-bud; bud-like designs on saris; mokkul flower-bud; mokkuli (-pp-, -tt-) to gather up. Ma. mukig a sprout, bud. ? Ko, mu-v- (mu-d-) to salute; kay mu-y- to make an afijali; move- (move-) (priest) prays to god for someone else, moving tight hand up and down. To, koy mux- (mux6-) to salute. Ka. mugi to contract, shut up or close as a flower, close, shut as eyes, mouth, etc., join the two hands with the open palms brought together; mugisu to close (a flower; tr.); mugul to close or shut as the eyelids, bud, sprout; mugul, mugalu bud, opening bud; muguleu to cause to contract, close or shut; shut; moggu, mogge, maggu a bud; (Hav.) mugutu flower-bud. Tu. mugi- yuni to close, contract, shut up; kai m. to salute; muguru sprout, shoot, bud; tender, delicate; muguruni, mukuruni to bud, sprout; muggé, moggé flower-bud, germ; (BRR; Bhattacharya, non-brahmin informant) mukké bud. Kor. (O.) mutke flower-bud. Te, mogudu to close, be closed, contract, be folded: mogidincu to close

Ta. mukig, mokkul, modrai bubble: mokkuli (-pp-, -tt-) to bubble up. Ma. mukki- likka id., ferment. Tu. muguli bubble. DED 4008. 4895 Ka, miikuti, miguti, (Kumt.; U.P.U.) milgute nose-ring; (Tipt.) mukura a nose- ornament. Tu, mikuti, milkudi, miiguti a nose jewel. Te, mukkara a sort of nose- ornament or nose-ring; mungara a kind of nose-ring worn by women. Kol. (Kin.) muk- keram, (SR.) mukkeram nose-ring; (W.) mo+karam_ nose-stud. Nk. (Ch.) mukra nose- ting. Pa. (S.) mungram id. Go. (G.) mukkara, (SR.) mukkera, (A.) mukera id. (Voc, 2866); (LuS.) moongagoi id. Cf. 5024 Ta. mttkku. DED(S) 4009. 4896(a) Ta. mukku (mukki-) to strain as a woman in travail, make great efforts; mukkal, mukku straining as in travail, great effort. Ma, mukkuka to strain, grunt, make an effort as in travail or when easing nature; mukkal, mukkam straining, etc.; mikkuka to press, strain at stool, Ko. muk- (muky-) to strain to deliver child, make a strenuous effort; muk act of straining, great effort. Jo. muk- (muky-) to grunt while defecating when constipated; hesitate. Ka. mukkiri, mukkare to strain, make violent efforts in pain, etc. Tu, mukkuruni to snort, grunt, growl; muk- kuru snorting, grunting, growling; (B-K.) bukku to strain. Te. mukku to strain, exert with a strain or strenuously, moan, groan, grumble, Pe. mitk- (-t-) to lift with effort. _? Br. mukking to stammer. (6) Ma. miiluka to groan, moan, mumble, buzz, assent with a hem; mflal hum, groan, buzzing noise. Ka. mtugu,-muluku to strain w

Ta. mukku (mukki-) to eat in large mouthfuls; mokku (mokki-), mokki-ttin- to eat greedily in large mouthfuls. Ka mukku to eat in a certain manner, put any dry grain, etc., into the mouth with the hollowed hand and gobble; mukkul, mukkule mouthful of water for rinsing the mouth, rinsing the mouth, cleaning the teeth; mukkulisu to rinse the mouth, spit out, abandon, reject. Tu. mukkuni to gobble, swallow, devour; mukka- vuni to over-feed (f.); mukkele voracious man; mukkuli, mukkuli mouthful of any liquid; mukkuliyuni, mukkuliyuni to gargle or rinse the mouth. Te. bokku to eat greedily, stuff the mouth and eat voraciously, gobble. Kui muka (muki-) to cast food into the’ mouth with the hand; mukal mouthful; mukal giva to rinse out the mouth, take a mouthful, gargle. Kuwi (Isr.) buk- (-it-) to gobble, swallow. Cf. 5127 Ma. m@tkuka. DED 4011.

Ta. mukku corner, lane, nook. Ma. mukku corner, narrow lane. Kurub. (LSB 1.11) mgt corner. To, muk edge; musk corner, edge. Tu, mukku corner, narrow lane. Kui mudgu corner. Kur, mttxa edge, brink, margin. DED(N) 4012. 4899 Ka, mukku a certain vessel, pot, pan or cup. Te, miikudu an earthen pan, a plate, covering dish. DED 4013.

Ta. mUhk& mongoose, Viverra ich- neumon. Ka. muhgi, muygisi, munguli, mungili, muigali, munguri. Tu. murgili, munguli, mungilé. Te. mungi, mufgisa. Kol. mungus. Nk, mugsak. Vk, (Ch.) muggus. Ga. (S.2) mungi potu. Go. (Tr.) mungus, (Y.) mungus, (S.) mugsi, (A.) mugus (Voc, 2870); (ASu.) muggtts. Konda mungi, mungi elka. Kuwi (P.) mungi orli. /Cf. Skt. (Hem. Un.) madgusa-, (Bhoj. Un.) maggusa-, Pkt. mam- gusa-, muggasa-, muggusu-, 1]. miigils, mags, Mar. mgtis, miigas, Sgh. mugati; Turner, CDIAL, no. 9702. DED(S, N) 4014.

Pe. muh- (must-) to chew. Kui muhpa (muht-) to munch, chew, grind the teeth. DEDS 793. 4902 Ta, mucar buttermilk, curds; mor buttermilk, curd diluted with water. Ma. mor buttermilk. Ko. mocr id. (in proverbs). To. moer id. (used only at the ti+ dairy). Ka. mosar(u), masaru curds. Kod, moeri buttermilk; katti mo-eri curd (cf. sv. 1148 Ta. kattu). Tu. mosaru curds. Te. (SAN) moru buttermilk. ?.Br. maringing to form into curds, curdle. / Cf. Skt. morata-, morana- sour buttermilk. DED(S) 4015.

Ta. muci (-v-, -nt-) to be torn, be crumpled as a garment, be tired, feel dis- couraged; (-pp-, -tt-) to faint, become tired, be distressed, grow thin, perish; mucippu thinness, emaciation, languor, debility, fatigue, weariness, destruction; mucivu crumpling. Ma, musikka to dirty; musiyuka to grow faint, weary, be worn out, dirty; musiccal wearisomeness; musippikka to tire. Ka. muccuru to become torpid or stupified, lose consciousness, faint away, swoon, be troubled in mind. Te. musincu to be spoiled, become weak or unsound, decay, decline, fail; mucciru to grieve, sorrow: mucciri one who is sorrowful; mucciritanamu perturbation, sorrow, grief. DED(S) 4016.

Te. musi_ musi navvu a smile. Pa, muck- to smile. Go, (Tr.) mus mus kowwana to smile or half-laugh (Voc. 2931); (ASu.) mus mus kavv- to smile. Pe. mus mus in- id. Kui musu/musuk inba to chuckle, grin, smile. Kur, muskarnad, musmusurna to smile. / An areal etymology for which cf. Turner, CDIAL, no, 10227, *muss- (e.g. H. muskana to smile, musfi [439] muccu Nep. musmusdunu id.), and no. 14754 Lahnda Bryonia scabra. (M. scabrella Arn. = B. muskan id. DEN 70. scabrella Linn.] DED 4023. 4905 Ka, musti the vomit nut, Strychnos nux vomtica. Te. musidi, musfi id, Pa, (S.) muti id. DED 4017.
Ta. muciru, muyiru red ant. Ma. miru, niru id. Kui murari, (P.) mujori large red ant, (K.) muci traku a kind of ant. Kuwi (T.) mubiri id. DED(S) 4018.

Pa. (S.) muc a kind of grass. Go. (LuS.) moochee the moochan grass. /?< IA. DEDS 794. 4908 Ka, (Hav.) mujanti a kind of honey (very small bees). Tu. mujanté, mujjinti, mojanfi a small kind of bee; a honeycomb; majanfia small kind of bee; its wax. 4909 Pa, muiijur mucus of nose. Ga, (OIL) mufizur (z = dz), (S.3) mOjur id. Go. (Mu. Ko.) mUter, (Ma.) milter id. (Voe. 2939); (LuS.) mootyagai running discharge from the nose. Konda muRi mucus of nose, Pe, mci id. Mand, mci id. Kui musi, (K.) mci id. Kuwi mtci (Su.) id., (F.) phlegm, (S.) cold (i.e. in the nose), (Isr.) id., mucus. Kur. mussd running from the nose. Malt. muso mot the nose. ? Ko. muku- snot. DED(S) 4019. "4910 Ta. mucu langur, Semnopithecus priamus. Ma. mocca a light-coloured monkey (or with 4626 Ka. mazga). Ka. musu, musuku, musuva a large and black kind of ape; (Hav.) muju black monkey; (Gowda, Dr. Ling., p.98) mucca bdlack-faced monkey. Kod. muccé langur. Tu. mujji, mujju a black monkey. Te. konda-muccu large black-faced monkey, baboon. Kol. muy black-faced monkey; (Haig) mui langur. Nk. muy black- faced monkey. Pa. muy id. Ga. (P.) muy id. Go, (Tr.) mUtnj (pl. musk) langur monkey (female); (W.) mUnjal ape; (M.) munj monkey; (D. Mu.) monjal, (Ma.) m€nji, (S.) munju, (Ko.) mUnj black-faced monkey (Voc. 2937). Kui misu (pl. mtska) sp. monkey or ape. Kuwi (F.) maha (pl, mtska) monkey (hanu- man); (S.) mthu monkey; (Su.) muhu (pl muska), (Isr.) mUhu (pi, mUska) black-faced monkey. Malt. muge baboon. DFD(S, N) 020.

Ta. mucuftai leather-berried bind- weed, Rivea ornata; clove-scented creeper, R. hypocrateriformis; a kind of creeper, Ipomaea candicans; mucuntai leather-berried bindweed, R. ornata. Ka. musute elephant creeper, Argyreia speciosa Sweet. DED 4021.

Ta. mucuntar low, mean people. Ma. (PCV) muSsatan coward. Ka. musundi, misandi a crooked, perverse, rude person, a coward; musunditana crookedness, per- versity, cowardice. Ju, musundi, musudi a shameless woman. DED(S) 4022.

Ta. mucumucukkai bristly bryony creeper, Mukia scabrella, Ma. musumusukka 4914 Te, musuru a constant or continued rain. Pe, muficud dinom/dina rainy season. Ga, (P.) mursund din rainy season. Go. (M.) musur, (Ko.) musur, musur tain (Voc. 2933). Konda musur constant train, cloudiness; mosop cloud(s), being overcast (of the sky). DED(S) 4024. 4915 Ta, muccu (mucci-) to cover; mly (-v- -nt-; mUyi-) to cover, fill, surround closely; a, a cover; moy (-pp-, -tt-) to cover, enclose; ? muyanku (muyanki-) to embrace, copulate; muyakkam embrace, copulation. Ko. muc- (muc-) to cover; mucan_ tight- fitting cover. To. miic- (miié-) to cover; miicxu+s lid, cover. Ka. muccu to close, shut, shut up, cover, conceal; m. shutting; muccaka closing, shutting; muccalu closing, covering, screening; muccala covering, cover, lid; muccike shutting, closing; muccige covering, ceiling of room; muccisu to cause to close; muccuvike shutting, etc.; mucce cover, lid; musuku to cover. Kod, mucc- (mucci-) to close; mucci lid, cover. Tu. muccuni to shut, close, cover, screen, shelter; mucca, muccala, mucci, muccu lid of vessel; muccanfé a close mouthpiece; muccavuni to cause to shut; muccige lid, cover, ceiling. Te. mUyu to cover, cover up, shut, close, conceal, hide, screen; (K. also) be shut, closed, (flower) closes its petals; miita covering, shutting, a cover, lid, shutter; musfigu, musfigudu cover, covering, veil; musfigu-vadu to be covered, veiled; (K.) muccu to close (ingr., tr.); (K.) muncu to cover, e

Ta. mujfici reedy sugar-cane, Sac- charum arundinaceum. Ka. muiiji, muiija, mode, madi, node S. munja, Tu. muiiji id. /2< Skt. mufija- S. sara ot munja, [S.arun- dinaceum Retz. = S. munja Roxb. = S. sara Roxb.}] DED 4026.

Ta. mufifai, munnai firebrand teak, Premna; headache tree; dusty-leaved fire- brand teak; woolly-leaved firebrand teak; charcoal tree. Ma. muififia Premna integrifolia (the wood serves to produce fire by attrition). DED 4027.

Ta. mutatku (mutanki-) to contract, become Jame or maimed, bend, be spoiled; abide, remain, stay, lie down; 2. rheumatism; lane, turning in a street; mufaftkar physical exhaustion as in confinement; mutarikal being bent as a bow; roll of paim-leaf used in letter-writing; mufanki bed-ridden person; elbow or jutting part of a piece of land; mutakku (mutakki-) to bend as knee, arm, etc., wind round, wrap (as one’s person), cause to bend or contract, disable (as one’s limbs), roof in (as a hut), cover, lie down; n, curve, bend, tongue corner of a winding sheet, a kind of ring; mutakkam contraction, lameness, being crippled by paralysis, bend, curve; mufantai lameness, anything bent, amenorrhoea; mutam crippled condition of leg or arm, anything bent, bend; mufalai ball, globe, twist in the fibre as of firewood; muftavan lame, Jame man; (Shanmugam) mutatti lame woman; mufavu (mutavi-) to limp; mutukkar short street, pathway difficult to pass, lane; mountain cavern, place where water presses against a tank and erodes, interstice, interspace, mutukku corner, narrow winding street; munafku (munafki-) to be contracted, withdrawn, bent, be subject to; n, withdrawing, contracting, bending, shaking off drowsiness of laziness by stretching one’s limbs, idleness. Ma, ‘mutam, mufakkam contraction of members as by landwind, lameness; mutakalam lame-footed (said of birds, etc.); mufakka to limp, halt; mufan- tan, mufavan lame; fem. muf{anti, mufavi; mufafiiuka to become lame; mutakku an uneven

Ta. mutalai bad smell as of flesh; mufuku bad odour, stench; mufai_ flesh, stench, offensive odour, smell of sour butter- milk or curds. Ma. mufa dirt as in cloth, salt, sugar, etc.; mufayuka to be soiled, dirty. Ka, ge? mode-nar to smell badly. DED 4029. .

Ta. mufi (-v-, -nt-) to tie, fasten, make into a knot, put on, adorn; (-pp-, -tt-) to fasten, tie, decorate with, put on as flowers; n. knot, tie, tuft or coil of hair, man’s hair tuft, crown of head, top as of mountain, bundle as of paddy seedlings for transplantation, noose; muficcu tie, tuft of hair, small bundle, knot in wood; mutinar those who tie knots; mutippu tying, fastening, tie, knot, bundle. Ma. mufi knot, bundle of rice plants, top-knot, hair of head, head-dress; mufa knot; mutikka, mufiyuka to tie up; mufippu money tied up in a cloth, esp. for, being presented. Ko. mury Knot tied by priest in his front hair, ascetic’s hair-knot; mury ae] Toda conical dairy. To. mury beautifully shaped top (as of tree), top of conical dairy; mudy hair (as cut in paying vow); in: mudy irk- (sce 516). Ka. mudi to bind or tie the hair, fasten or set in the hair of the head, as flowers, etc.; :m. knot or bundle of hair, braid of hair, bundle of grain packed in straw; mudisu to fasten or set in the hair-knot or braids (of others), as flowers; mudipu, mudupu bundle, money- bag. Tu. mudi bundle of rice packed in straw, lump of tamarind packed in leaves, (B-K.) tuft; mudiyuni to be turned into a knot, be knotty; mudipini, mudipuni to dress the head with flowers, make the top-knot tight;"mudi- pavuni to have a person’s head dressed with flowers; mudipu, mudupu, mudupely bundle of money, jewels, etc., devoted to an idol; mudepini, mudepuni to bind. Te. mudi knot, tie, joint in reed, bundle

Ta. muti (-v- -nt-) to end, terminate, be completed, effected, accomplished, be destroyed, perish, die; (-pp-, -tt-) to end (7), terminate, effect, accomplish, destroy; mutipu, mufivu end, finality, compietion, conclusion, decision, death, limit; mufimar those who accomplish things; mufiya unto the end, finally; mufiyal all, whole. Ma. mutiyuka to come to an end; mufikka to finish, spend, destroy; muftippu destruction; mutiyan waster, prodigal; mufivu end, destruction, extravagance. Ko, murc- (mutc-) to finish (work); arymury gey- (gec-) to over- come (someone) in a fight (for ary-, see 77 Ta. atu). Ka, mudi to end, come to an end, become extinct; m end, ruin, destruction; mudipu to end. ? Tu. mudusuni to.eat up, consume. Te. (K.) mifdu, mudu to end Gntr.), terminate, (death) to approach. Go. (Ma.) muri end, termination (Voce. 2922). Kuwi (S.) muth’nai to complete; (Isr.) muf- (-t-) id.; be finished. DED(S) 4031.

Ta. muti (-v-, -nt-) to appear, be possible, capable. Ko. mury- (murce-) to be possible, be able. DED 4032. 4924 Ta, mufi-varai cuscus grass, Andro- pogon muricatus. Ka, mudivala A. schoenan- thus Linn. Tu. mudivala, muddela, mudyala, mudyela A, muricatus. Cf. 5374 Ta. muti- varai. [A. squarrosus Linn. = A. muricatus Retz.] DED 4033.

Ta. mutuku (mufuki-) to hasten, be in haste, move quickly, become urgent, throng and surge, be strong, meet, advance against; n. arapid movement in verse; mufukal quickness, strength; mutukku (mufukki-) to urge, bring pressure on, drive in as a screw, drive, cause to run as a horse, set in motion, bite off hurriedly, plough, induce, urge on, feel urgently as the call of nature, increase in price, hasten; nm. urging, pressing hard, increase (of price), dearness, strength, power, pride, arrogance stiffness of manners, mufuk- kati busy time, critical moment; mutukkam high price, tightness; mufukkan strong man; mufalai strength, greatness. Ma, mufukkuka to urge on, e.g. cattle. Ko. murg- (murgy-) to be screwed up; murk- (murky-) to screw into place, keep (wife) in order; murk showing of strength in contests (wrestling, running, etc.). To. mud(k) kiy- to be persistent. Cf. 4846 Ta. mifal. DED 4034.

Ta. mufuku bracelet made of lac, ting. Ma. mutuku bracelet, woodbind to tie up sugar-cane. DED 4035. 4927 Ta, mutai (-v-, -nt-) to braid, plait, wattle; m. ola basket, umbrella of palm-leaves; mutaical plaiting, braiding, that which is plaited. Tu. mudepini, mudepuni to plait, braid, knit; mudeyuni, mudevuni to be folded, get entangled; mudé screen made of coconut leaves. Te. muduta plait, coil. Cf.

Ta. muttattanam ignorance, stupidity; mutfan dunce; mutfal id., simpleton, stupid fellow; muffaffam stupidity, pertinacity or conceit arising from ignorance. Ma. muffan a stout, obstinate, stupid person; muffal, muftalan obstinate, hopelessly stupid. Ka. muf{aa cowardice, bluntness, stupidity; muftilu coward, stupid man. Tu, muffaly very stupid or indigent man. Cf. 5135 Ta. motu. DED 4038.
Ta. muntu short log, wooden prop. Ma. mu{tam, mutfan trunk, log of wood, mutfi short log or block. Pa. mofti log, beam. Go. (Ko.) mot log of wood (Voc. 2976). DED(S) 4039.

Ta. mutti small earthen pot. Ma. mutti vessel used for drawing toddy. Tu. mutti small kind of earthen pot equal to a pint, by which toddy is sold to customers. DED 4040. 4932 Ta, muftu (muffi-) to dash against, butt, oppose, meet, assault, attack, fight; 7. battering, butting; muttikai jeweller’s small hammer. Ma, muttu knocking, tapping, butting, dunning; mutfuka to dash against, knock, tap, butt, strike as a bell or clock; muttikka to press, harass, make to hit, knock, dash, etc.; mufti hammer. Xo. muf- (muc-) to meet, harm; mifk small hammer. To, -mil- muty (to quarrel) about (for -mil, see 5086): 2? muf- (muty-) to outstrip. Ka, moftu to rap the head (of another) with the knuckles of the fist; 7. a rap with the knuckles of the fist. Kod. mutt- (mufti-) to bump against. Te. moftu to give a blow or stroke with the knuckles, esp. on the head, give a blow or knock on one hard body with another that is smaller; moffu, moftukdya a knock with the knuckles, a blow on a hard body with one that is smaller. Pa. muff- to hammer; mutka blow with fist. Ga, (P.) muta fist. Go. (Mu.) muf, (Ko.) mufiya hammer; (Mu.) mutka a blow (Voc. 2874). Pe. mufla hammer. Mand. mufla id. Kuwi (Su.) mufla id, Kur. mutga’ana to deal a heavy blow with the fist; mufga, mufka clenched hand or fist, hammering with the fist; mufka’ana to hit or hammer at with the fist. /Cf. Skt. muf- to crush, grind, break; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10186. DED(S) 4041.

Ta. muttu (muffi-) to hinder, be hindered, prevented; m, hindrance, obstacle, impediment, difficulty as in passing, prop, support; mufakku (mufakki-) to prevent, hinder; m. hindrance, delay; mutakkam restraint, hindrance; mutukkafi hindrance, objection, thwarting, straits, difficulties; mutafiku (mufaniki-) to be hindered, frus- trated. Ma. muttu impediment, stoppage; muftuka to be hindered, stopped: mutfikka to stop, hinder, block up; mufafhuka to be muffu [442] . munfu hindered, obstructed; mutakkam hindrance, stop, suspension; muftakku prohibition, suspension; mufakkuka to stop, impede, arrest, forbid. Ka. muftu impediment, hin- drance, stoppage; prop, stay. Go. (Tr.) miuirana to be blocked, of a bamboo or gun- barrel: (A.) mur- to be blocked up: muh- to block up:(SR.) muhana to plug; (Ph.) muhtand to block up with earth (Voe. 2895, 2935). Br. mirk stopper, bung; miirk tining to stop up. DED(S) 4042.

Ta. muttu pollution, menses. Ma. muttuka to touch. Ko, muf- (muc-) to touch (corpse at funeral). To. muf- (mufy-) id. Ka, muttu to touch, come in contact with, reach, arrive; be laid out, be expended or used as money; m. touching, touch, contact, defile- ment by contact, menses, lochia; muftayisu to touch, reach; muftal, muftale, muttave, muttil, muflu touch, contact, menses, im- purity from childbirth; muttisu to cause to touch, convey, cause to reach, deliver, ex- pend; muffuvali expenses, outlay: muftuvike, muttuha touching, touch, contact. Tu. muf- {uni to touch, attain, reach, arrive, come to hand, cost; muftu touching, touch, contact; menstruation, ceremonial uncleanliness; muft- favuni to cause to touch, accompany to one’s destination, cause to reach, deliver; mutta proximity, nearness; near; muftadaye a relation, man with whom one can associate; fem. muftadaly; muttuvali, muffoli expense, cost. Te. muftu to touch, be received, ob- tained or paid (as a thing or money), reach; n. touch, contact; menses; menstruous; muttu-konu to touch; muftu-kdlu -touching, contact; muffuta, muftudi a menstruous woman; muftagu, muftavu to be in menses; muttincu to make or cause to touch. Go, (W.) mutis, (Ph.) muftis, mottis, (Pat.) motas (i.e. mofas), (LS7, Chanda Maria, p. 540) mofras near (Voc, 2875). Konda muf- to touch. DED(S) 4043.

Ta. mufttu knee, elbow, knuckle. Ma, muttu knob, joint or knot of cane or bamboo, knee and elbow. To. mut etf bone from elbow to wrist, top joint of front leg of a calf. Tu. mufté knuckles, joints of fingers. Te. céti-muduku elbow; kali- muduku, mudumu knee. Kol. (SR.} mudsi, (Pat., p.19) mds, (P.) murug id.; (Kin.) mutuc id., elbow. Ga. (S.*) mudkul knee-cap, Cf. 4919 Ta. mufatiku. DED 4044.

Ta. muttu dilemma, need; want; muttu-ppatu to be pressed, perplexed, be in difficulty or extremity as one beset. by creditors; muftu-ppafu dilemma, need, want, trouble, distress, evil. Ma. muftu being non- plussed, perplexity, want; muffu-pafu straits, want; tormenting, importunity. ? Ko. mundy state of man unable to pay debt, rent, or contribution. Ka. muftu nonplus, embarrass- ment, want. Tu. muttu-kadi, muftu-gadi critical state, dangerous illness, narrow path, straitened circumstances. Te. muftu- padu to be embarrassed, be in trouble or fix, be distressed; mutfu-pafu trouble, embarrassment, fix, trouble. DED(S) 4045.

Ta. muffu tool, instrument, sundry things. Ko, mutga+rn Kota cconomic associate with Badaga or with Kurumba (he gives tool, etc.); Kota economic associate with Toda (ess formal than kel relationship). Jo. mut things given by Kotas to Todas, including tools. Ka, muffu implement, tool, thing, utensils, furniture, things belonging to a house as beams, etc. Te. muttu instrument, tool. Cf. 3041 Ta. tattumuttu. DED 4046. 4938 Ta, muttu (mufti-) to be deficient; n, shortness, deficiency. Ma, muft-dru_ tiver with a short course; munfu anything short; muntan short, a dwarf, dwarfish; munfatti, munti short woman. Ko, mod short of stature; mon short. Tu, munda small. Kol. (Pat., p. 111) mofti short. Nk. mottik id. Pa. mundi id. Go, (Mu.) munda small; (Ko.) mondo short; (Tr.) mund@ wiring (should be wirinj) thumb (Voc. 2881). Cf. 4661 Ta. mattam. DED(S) 4047.
Ta. muttai cgg, ovum, world as a globe; muntai egg. Ma, mufta, motta id. Ko. mot id. Jo. muty id. Ka. motte id. Kod, mutfe id., testis. Tu. mofte egg. DED 4048.
Ta. muttai-dry cake of cowdung. Ka. midal dry cowdung. Te. mufamu cowdung found dried in woods. DEDS 795.

Ko. mutm (obl, muft-) day when moon is not visible. To. mut new moon; mut no-t day when moon is not visible. DED 4049, 4942 Tu, muddi buttock. Te. muddi id., anus; back, hind part; mUdi anus. Pa. mufus lower back. Ga. (S.2) muddi back, anus (< Te.). Go, (Ma.) mutonji buttock (Voc. 2876). Konda mudi female otgan. Kui modengi, modenji rump of a bird or fowl Kuwi (1.) mUddi, (Ist.) mudi anus. DED(S) 4050.

Pa. munda tank. Ge, (OlL.) munda id. Go. (Mu.) munda id. (Voc. 2882). Konda (BB) munda bund, embankment. Pe, munda tank, bund. Kui munda tank, reservoir. / Cf. Halbi munda tank. DED(S) 4051. 4946 Ka, (Hav.) mundi a kind of yam with very broad leaves. Tu. (B-K.) mundi, mopdi a kind of bulbous root used in preparing curry. 4947 Ta, muntu a short-sized cloth. Ma. muntu the short cloth of Kerala men. Ko. mund waijstcloth. To. mud cloth worn over shoulders by Badaga women (< Badaga mundu). Ka. mundu short cloth used as a turban or as an undergirth cloth. Kod. mundi waistcloth. Tu. mundu small upper mundige [443] mutu garment; munni the folds of a male’s cloth. DED 4052. 4948 Ka, mundige post; (PBh.) mudige a pole (planted in the middle for support). Tu, mindu a pole to support a wooden bridge. Ga. (Oll. P.) munde post, pillar. Go. (SR.) munda, (G. Ma.) munda, (Ko.) munde id. (Voce. 2883); (ASu.) munda a wooden pole in the shape of Bhima; mundét pole. Konda mundam erected poles in the con- struction of cowpen. Pe. munda pillar. Kuwi (Su. Mah. Isr.) munda post, pillar./ Possibly of IA origin; cf. Turner, CDJAL, no. 10191; DBIA, no. 299. DEDS(N) 798. 4949 Kui mundru cucumber. Kuwi (Su. P. J. Mah. Isr.) mundra id. DEDS 797,

Ta. mutal beginning, first as in rank, place, etc.; cause, God as the first cause, one who is first or oldest, best, that which is superior; principal, fund, capital, money yielding interest; place; mutalvar persons beginning with; celestials; mutalvan one who is first, chief, head, god, king, father; mutalvi lady of first rank; mutali head, chief, religious leader; mutalu (mutali-) to commence, begin, come first, have a beginning, have as the origin, begin with; mutanmai priority superi- ority, supremacy; mutir (-v-, -nt-) to precede, excel, surpass; mutirvu excess, plenty. Ma. mutal beginning; blossom or catkin of Arto- carpus, considered as the first fruit (mittu) and superstitiously plucked off; principal or capital, stock in trade, property, money; mutanma greatness; mutali headman; mutal- van a chief; mutiruka to go forward, be beforchand, early; mutirttuka to forestall. To. moOit valuable thing. Ka. modal, madalu state of being first, preceding, in front, prior; state of being chief, the chief thing; beginning, extremity or tip; original, principal or capital, stock in trade; at the beginning, first, etc., for the first time; motta-modalu, moda- modalu the very beginning, at the very first; modaliga a chief, headman. Kod, moda an article belonging to one. Tu. mudely origin,’ cause, capital, principal. Te. modalu begin- ning, commencement, origin, source, principal, capital, the chief or principal thing or person, base, basis, foundation; mofta-modalu the very beginning

Ta. mutal base, foot, bottom or lowest part of anything, stump, lowest part of stem. To. mi@ straw (cf. ‘stubble’ in Tu.). Ka, modal, madalu root, base: moddu block, log of wood. Tu. mudely the bottom, stubble, stump of a tree; madady log, trunk. Te. modalu root; moddu stump, block. Kol, (P.) modal (pl. modasil) tree trunk; (Kin.) modal stump; (Kin) modd trunk; (SR.) modda fog. Nk, modhal trunk of tree, stump. Vk. (Ch.) maddun stem of tree. Pa, model trunk of tree, base; model key wrist. Go, (Mu.) maddol plant, shrub (Voc. 2701); (Mu. Ko.) modol, (S.) modal trunk of tree (Voc. 2981) Konda modol id. Cf. 4950 Ta. mutal. DED(S) 4054.

Ta. mutalai, mutalai, mucali crocodile. Ma, mutala. Ko, mocal. Ka. mosale, masale. Kod. mosale. Tu. mosalé, mudale, mudalé, mUdalé. Te. mosali. Kol. (Kin.) moséli. Pa. moca. Konda (BB) modi, mUdi. Kur, (BB) boca. Malt. boce. /Cf. Skt. (lex.) miacala- crocodile; lex.) musali- house-lizard; alligator; Pkt. muduga- = grahavisesa-. DED(S) 4055.

Ta. mutirai pulse or other legu- minous plant, pigeon-pea, dhal. Ma. mutira horse-gram. Kod. mudire id. ? Kol. (SR.) mudde pulse; (Kamaleswaran). DED(N) 4056. 4954 Ta, mutu old, ancient; mutt-eyil ancient fortress; mutumai antiquity, oldness, old age, proverb, maturity; mutari being old or ancient, that which is ripe; mutiyavan, mutiyan, mutiyan aged man, elder, senior, Brahma; mutiyal elderly woman, woman possessed by a spirit; mutiyo| old woman, goddess; mutiyOr persons of ripe wisdom, mutir (-v-, -nt-) to grow old, have the qualities of age, become mature, grow ripe; mutircci ‘maturity, ripened condition, . great age, excellence in learning or experience; mutirvu maturity, ripened condition, great age; mutuvar elders, old persons, persons of ripe wisdom, men of experience, counsellors; mutuval that which is time-worn; mutuvor elders, men of experience, the great, the elders as king, teacher, mother, father, elder brother, poets; mutai, mutai-ppunam ground long under cultivation; mutaiyal wild jungle, ancient forest; mi (-pp-, -tt-) to become old, be older than another, be senior in age; n. old age; mUvar the Devas as never ageing; mU-tévi the elder sister of Laksmi, goddess of misfortune; mUtt-appan father’s father; mUttavan one who is senior in age, elder brother, superior; mlittar aged persons, elders, husbands, elder brother; mUtta{ aged woman, elder sister, first wife; mUttdn aged person, elderly man, senior, elder brother, Ganega (as the eldest of Siva’s sons), man be

Ta. mutu); mut et- (eyt-) to go round completely. To. mut- (muty-) (child) becomes strong, (fruits) become hard and unfit for use, (potatoes) are hard and good for seed (or with 4954 Ta. mutu). Tu. muttuni to become extinct, be destroyed; mutt&vuni to ruin, destroy. Te. m(r)uggu to ripen, become overtipe (or with 5046 Ta. mtg). DED(S) 4117.

Ta. mutuku coarseness, grossness. Te. mutuka, muduka coarse not fine, thick, rough, rude, indelicate, gross, blunt, vulgar, indecent, foul, obscene; moduka rough, thick, close; modukana roughness, thickness. DED(S) 4059.
Ma. muttati Triacanthus, a fish with three horns. Tu. muttadi id. DED 4060. 4958 Ta, muttalam morning. Ma. muttayam breakfast. Cf. 143 Ta. attalam. DED 4061.

Ta. muttu pearl, tear, castor-bean, oil-seed ‘(as castor, rape etc.), succulent seed of pomegranate, a goldsmith’s weight, seeds or shells, etc., used in games, that which is excellent or praiseworthy; muttam pearl, castor-bean, a superior quality of emerald; muruntu pearl. Ma. muttu pearl kernel (cf. musu oyster). Ko. mut woman’s ear-ring. To, mut pearl. Ka, muttu, mutya id. Kod. muitti id. Tu. muttu id., a drop. Te. mutti- yamu, muttemu, mutyamu pearl. /Cf. Skt. mukta-, mauktika-, Pali Pkt. mutta-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10152, DED 4062.

Ta. muttu, .muttam” kiss; muttu (mutti-), muttam itu to kiss; (Koll.) muce kiss. Ma, muttu, muttam, mutti kiss; muttuka, muttu [445] murafu mottuka to kiss. Ka. muttu, muddu kiss; muddidu to kiss; (Coorg) mudku id.; ? mundu kissing, caressing, fondling. Kod. mutta kiss. Tu. mutta, muttu, mudda, muddu id. Te. muddu id.; muddidu to kiss. Kol. mud kiss; mud i-d- to kiss. Pa, moftf- id. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) muddi- kiss. DED(S) 4063.

Ta. muttu pock of smallpox, pustule; mutti yellow spreading spots on the breasts of women. Ma. mutukkan pimple, blotch, muttacci small red pimples on the body of infants. Tu. mutteri a kind of skin eruption; muddainy, muddany pimples or blotches on the face. Kui mUtenji mole, birthmark, wart, corn, Kuwi (F.) matesi pimple; (isr.) mutesi wart. DED(S) 4064.

Ta. muttai lump, large mass, boiled rice gathered into a ball; mottai ball, round lump. Ka, muddi, mudde a roundish mass, ball, roundish lump; ragi porridge; moddu, maddu lump, mass. Tu. muddé lump, un- shapen mass. Te, mudda lump, mass; morsel, mouthful. Konda muda lump (of tumeric, mud, etc.). / Cf. Mar. mud lump of boiled rice as turned out of a ladle. DED(S) 4065.
Ta. muntiri cashew tree, Anacardium occidentale; vine; muntirikai common grape vine, cashew tree. Ma, muntiri vine, grape. Te. munta-mamidi cashew. DED 4066.
Ta. muntiri, muntirikai the fraction =. Ma. muntiri id. DED 4067. 320°

Ta. muntai a small vessel; montai a small earthen vessel, a small wooden vessel; (Koll.) monne a kind of pot. Ma, monta cruse, goglet; moOnta brass ewer to draw water, water-vessel of kings. Ka. munde a jar-like brass vessel. Te. munta small pitcher. Pa. (p.97) mutta basin. Konda muta small pitcher or pot. Pe, muta metal vessel, Kui miata small brass pot. Kuwi mtnta (F.) water pot of brass, (Isr.) small metal jug, pot, vessel. DED(S) 4068.

Ta. mummaram, mummuram_ im- petuosity, vehemence, fierceness, swiftness; mummari (-pp-, -{t-), mummudi (-pp-, -tt-) to rage, be violent, vehement or impetuous, Te. mummaramu acme, crisis or height of a disease, violence, intensity, vehemence; mum- marincu to grow violent, reach the crisis. DED 4069.

Ta. muyal (muyaly-, muyanr-) to practise, persevere, make continued exertion, take pains, begin, undertake; n. war; muyalvu endeavouring, persevering, exercising, effort; muyarci, muyarri, muyarru effort, exertion, activity, perseverance, diligence, industry, employment, performing religious ceremonies; muyanku (muyamki-) to do, perform. Ka. mogasu to begin to exert oneself, make one- self ready for work, act zealously after or for a contrived plan, exert oneself. Te. mogi exertion, effort, endeavour; mogiyu to attempt, try; (K.) moggu to undertake to do, be prepared for action. DED 4070. 4968 Ta, muyal, mucal hare. Ma, muyal id. Ir. munna, monna id. Ko. molm id. To. miies id. Ka. mola, mala id. Kod. mona id. Tu. muyery, mug(g)ery id.; mola a large hare; (B-K.) méry hare, rabbit. Kol. mi-te hare. Nk, mite id. Pa. mifda id. Ga. (OIL) munde, (S.) munde id. Go. (Tr.) malol (pl. malohk), (A. Ch.) malol, (MuE.) malor (pl. malohk). (Ko.) malor (pl -i), (Ph.) malol, mulol, (MuW.) malol, molol, (W. D. Ma. M.) molof (pl. D. molohk, Ma. molosku), CY.) molor (pl. -k) id. (Voc. 2751). Konda morol id. Pe, mrol id. Kui myadu id. Kuwi (.) m ad, (S.) mrafu, (Isr.) mralu id. Kur. munya id. Malt. munye id. Br. murtl id. DED(S) 4071. 4969 Ta, muraficu (murafici-) to mature, be old, ancient; n. maturing; mUri antiquity, old age. Kol, murtal old woman. Nk, murtal id. Pa. murtal id. Ga, (Oll.)} murtal id. Go. (Tr. Ph.) mtrana to grow old (of a woman), (SR.) murand to ripen (of a boil) (Voc. 2900);

Ta. muraficu (murajfici-) to be full, abundant; mri strength, greatness. Tu. mary fat, stout, corpulent. Te. maru to increase, be full. DED 4073. A971 Ta. muratu roughness, unevenness. ruggedness, knob or knot as in timber, joint of the body, ill-temper, wildness, rudeness obstinacy, largeness, bigness: murafan rude man, obstinate man; muraftati. rough, rude, lawless, reckless behaviour; muran (muranp-, murant-) to: be at variance, be opposed: n. variance, opposition, perversity, spite, hatred. fight, battle, strength, greatness, roughness, stubbornness, fierceness, a flaw in rubies; muranu (murani-) to be at variance; muranar enemies; muranfan cross-tempered, quarrel- some person, obstinate person, impatient person, hasty, impetuous person; muran{u (muranti-) to be obstinate, opposed, cross- grained; n. persistence, obstinacy, opposition, disagreement, unyielding nature; murappu roughness; murampu mound of gravel or stone, mound, elevation, rock, rough hard ground; murarai hard, stout trunk of a tree; murippu hump of an ox; murivu greatness, murutan obstinate person, reckless person, ignorant, foolish person, hunter, rude person. savage; murufu coarseness, roughness, obsti- nacy, obdurateness, cruelty, knot in wood, stump, piece of wood, largeness; mOri hump: muntu (mupti-) to be unruly, refractory. attack vehemently; 1. petulance, obstinacy. vehement attack, stupidity, knot as in a tree, bulging or protuberance (cf. 4988 Ma. muga), joint of the body, hump; moptu refractorin

Ta. mural (muraly- muranr-) to make sound, cry, sing; murala! sounding, confused noise, high pitch; muralvu soft sound as of a lute; muravam noise, reverberation, drum; murarkai, murarci sound, song; murarru (murarri-) to make sound, cry; murali flute, nose-flute; muracam, muracu drum, tabour, war drum; muravyu, murufu drum. Me. mura- luka, muruluka to hum, grunt, growl; murali ute; muracu, murajam a small drum; moram- puka dogs to snarl, men to hawk. Ka, moral to hum, grunt, growl, buzz, howl; more to hum, buzz, sound, murmur, creak, gurgle, whizz, be noisy; cause to sound; 4. humming, etc.; moraha, morahu, morehu humming, etc.; mure to hum, buzz, sound or play a lute, sound as a lute does. Kod. morad- (moraduy-, morat-) to weep; more lamentation. Tu. [446] murayu muresuni, moresuni to rumble, rattle (as thunder); murepini, murevuni, mureluni id., creak (as shoes); muriya cry, weeping, loud - noise; muriyatu lamentation, wailing; muriye- | duni, mureduni, mutiyoduni, moreduni to weep, lament, bewail; mutiyedpini to grumble, murmur; moré cry; muriyd alas!, weeping loudly, cry of lamentation, distress, etc.; morampu a cry, toaring. Te, morayu to sound, resound; morayika, morapamu sound, noise; mrogu, mrdyu, mrdvu to sound (or with
Ta. mural needie-fish, Syngnathus, Ma. mural-min id. / Cf. Skt. murala- a species of freshwater fish; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10213. DED 4077.

Ta. muravu break, broken condition as of the mouth of a pot; muri (-v-, -nt-) to break off, snap off, perish, be ruined, be scattered, go wrong, be defeated, separate, leave, lose one’s position; (-pp-, -tt-} to break off (¢7.), snap off, ruin, defeat; n. piece, bit broken off, scratch, blemish; murippu break- ing, bruising, cracking; muriyal bit, fragment, that which is brittle; murivu breaking, snapping, leaving, separation; murukku (murukki-) to destroy, crush, ruin, kill, break in pieces, dissolve; nn. killing; murufku (murutki-) to perish, be destroyed, break; milri a bit, part. Ma. mUruka to cut, cut up (a hog, etc.), reap. Ko. mury- (murc-) to break (stick-like thing; intr, f.), indent (neck in throwing pot); mury small piece, small crack in iron of tool. To. miiry- (miirs-) to break in two (stick; infr.); (miirc-) id. (t.). Kod. muri- (murip-, muric-) to make a cut. Ju. muri an incision or notch; murku fragment, piece, bit; milruni to mince, cut up (as vegetables). Te, muri, muriya a bit, piece; muri-konu to cut; mutiyu to break; mukka piece, bit, fragment, part; bruyyu to be destroyed, perish; brufigu to die; (K.) bruncu to kill. Kol. (Kin.) murk- to split (wood). (SR.) murk- to break; 7? (SR.) muriy® flesh (< Te. muriya piece [of flesh]). Vk. mur- to break (intr.); murk- id. (¢7.); murnde a quarter (of bread). Pe. mur- to cut (horizontally). muratci [447] Kui mroku inba to snap off, be broken off; mrunga (mrungi-) to be torn; mrunga vipka to break away from a torn p

Ta. muri (-v-, -nt-) to bend, lack in strength, be gentle; 7. curve, bend; muri- muri (-v, -nt-) to curve, bend; murivu con- tracting, fold, laziness; mtri laziness, indol- ence; shaking off Jaziness; mUri nimir (-v-, -nt-) to stretch oneself as from laziness. Afa. murufuka to pluck by twisting (as coconuts); muri numbness, apathy, stiffness; mUruka, mri niviruka to stretch oneself. Ko, muniv- (munivd-) to stretch and groan when one wakes; mu+nj- (mu-nj-) to jerk spasmodically and groan in sleep. To, miiry fury- (furs-) to stretch the body in yawning. Ka. muri to bend, be bent, grow crooked, wind, meander, stretch oneself with windings of the limbs; bend, turn round or twist (as the whiskers); n. state of being bent, curved, etc., a bend, winding course of a river, ring; murike bend- ing, crooking by straining; murige bending, twisting, a twist; muripu to turn round or whirl the fist; murivu bending or twisting, a bend, winding course of a river; surrounding; murucu to turn round, twist; murudisu to pluck up by twisting; muruhu a bend, curve, winding course of a river, a surrounding place, crookedness of mind, a crooked object, a pervert; turn, repetition; (PBh.) murul crooked; (Gowda) murnf{u to become crooked; mor(a)ku to be turning round or be giddy from pride; morahu a bend, winding course of running water. Tu. muri curve, circle, ring, twist, the creases of the hand, windings of a conch, etc; murigé twist, entanglement; murlu stretching of a. limb; murluni to be stretche

Ta. muruku tenderness, tender age, youth, beauty, Skanda; murukan youth, young man, Skanda. Ma. murukan Subrah- manya. ?7To. mo-l pitad, mo-l kwi-dyad young birds, young cocks (a pair of song units); mo-Im (obi. mo-lt-) in: kin mox mo-ltkm even to the children. Tu. murku the young of an animal; murlé little, smalt: tender. Te. murupu beauty, loveliness, grace. Konda (BB) murli (pl. murku) young man. DED(S) 4081.

Ta. muruku ornament worn in the helix of the ear. To. murx, in woman’s name Pitymurx (pity silver, 5496(a)). Ka, mura, muru, murugu, muruyvu, muruhu a wire ring used as an ornament for the nose and the ear. Tu. muru ear-ting; murgi twisted bangle or wristlet made of silver. 8) murugu, muruvu bracelet, bangle. Ga. (S.? ) murug bracelet. Go. (Ko.) mur (pl, muhk) bracelet, bangle (Voe, 2897). Kuwi (Su.) murmu (pl. murka), (F.) mirmt (pl. mrilpka) nose-ring. / Cf. Pkt. muravi- a kind of ornament; H. Mar., etc., murki ear-ring. DED(S, N) 4082.

Ta. muruku fuel; muruAku (murunki-) to simmer [i.e. smoulder]; muruku (muruki-) to exceed the proper limit in heating (or with 5009); murutu firewood. Ma. (Tiyya) mori- yuva to become roasted. Ka. murunfu to cause to burn, Kindle. Tu. muripuni to burn, consume (as firewood for culinary purposes); muripavuni to cause to burn; murlu kindling fire into a blaze; murluni a spark to be kindled into a blaze; murtely dry leaves, etc. used for kindling fire. Pa. murkub sweat. Mait. murye to singe the feathers of a bird preparatory to roasting it; muthre to be scorched; muthrtre to scorch. /Cf. Skt. murmura- an expiring ember; burning chaff, Pali mummura- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10215. DED(N) 4083.

Ta. murukku palas tree, Butea frondosa.. Ma, murikku Erythrina indica (in Lush. usually with some qualifving word); (Lush. also) B. frondosa. Ka, mutta, muttaga, muttala, muttuka, muttuga, muttula, murtaga, mugtuga B. frondosa. Tu. muttaka id. Te. moddugu, (inscr.) mrddugu id. Kol (Kin.) modg id. Ga, (S.2) murtand id. Go. (Tr.) mur mara (A. Y. W. Ph.) mur, (Mu.) murray, (G.) murar, (M.) moghari id. (Voc. 2896); (LuS.) mogaree id.; (Koya Su.) mu{3, mofo id. Kur. murka id. Malt. murko name of a tree. / For similar words in Munda languages, see Pinnow, p.93 (Santali muru’d, etc.). DED(S, N) 4084.

Ta. murufkal Moringa pterygosperma, Indian horse-radish tree. Ma. muritiha Hyper- murudi [448] muzu anthera moringa, Indian horse-radish. Ka. nugge, neg M. pterygosperma, Tu. nurige, nurge id. Te. munaga id. Pa, munga, (8.) muinga id. Go. (Mu.) murunga a kind of tree (Voc. 2903); (ASu.) muigé, munge Indian horse-radish_ tree. Konda muyna maram, (BB) muluga mara drumstick tree fie. M. pterygosperma|. Kur. mungga a shrub, the fruits and leaves of which are eaten as curry. /Cf. Skt. murangi-, muruigi- M. pterygos- perma; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10209. [M. pterygosperma Gaertn. = H, moringa Vahl.} DED(S, N) 4085. 4983 Kol. murudi black. Nk. mulogi id. DED 4086.

Tu. muli thatching grass. Kui mliu a reed-like grass. ? Malt, mali a kind of reed of which arrow shafts are made. ? Br. milinj a wild grass used as fodder. DEDS(N) 800, 4985 Ta, mulai woman’s breast, teat. Ma. mula id., udder. Ko. mel breast; mol id. (in a proverb; < Badaga), pointed end of anvil. To. mig nipple, teat, breast, udder; mil breast (7S, p.878). Ka. mole female breast, dug of an animal. Kod. mole breast. Tu. miré breast, nipple or teat of any animal. DED 4087.

Te. mola waist, middle; ? malugu, malumu small of the back above the loins. Kol. mutke (P.) back, (SR.) waist. Vk. mulke waist; mulkene d@d backbone. Pa, mulka bila, mul biila id. Go. (L.) molki back (Voc. 2995). Konda mola waist. Br. mux waist, loins. DED(S) 4088.

Ta. mullai Arabian jasmine, Jasminum Sambac; some other varieties of jasmine. Ma. mulla J. sarnbac, etc. Ka, molle J. multiflorum or pubescens. Te. molla jasmine. /Cf. Skt. mallika: id.; Turner, CDIAL no. 9913. DED 090.

Ma. muga protuberance, tumour, excrescence; rough, knotty, teeth of a saw, etc.; muyantu knot, protuberance on a tree; murekka to swell as a tumour, rise in a wen; (Kaut.) mugafifu knots, protuberance. Tu, muré, more node, protuberance swelling, bruise; morampé swelling or protuberance on any part of the body; muranda, murapdeé, (B-K.) murampe, molampe swelling, pro- tuberance; (B-K.) murely, murevu the foetus to be formed. DED(S, N) 4091, and from DED 4074.

Ta. mugahku); mrdgudu, mrdta sound. Kol. moray- (morayt-) to produce musical sound (bell, etc.); morp- (moropt-) to play (flute), ring (bell), make to produce musical sound, Nk, moray- to sound (infr.); morap- id. (tr.). Pa, mur- to grow! (tiger), hoot (owl); murip- (murit-) to snore. Go. (Tr.) moritana to guggle in the throat while sleeping; (F-H.) muri-, (W.) muritand, (SR.) mudiyand, (Ma.) mor- to snore (Voc. 3002). Kui muru inba to mutter, grumble, growl. Kuwi (F.) murt- kiali to growl; (Isr.) muru muru 4- to grumble, complain. Kur. murrna to thunder, esp. with repeated peals, utter threats, threaten. Malt. mure to speak, say yes or no, ? Br. marram shout, call, cry, fame (or with 5013). Cf.

Ta. mugam cubit; mugan kat knee, part of the leg from knee to ankle; murah kai elbow, forearm; mugi joint as of the body; mogi joint as of wrist, knee, ankle, etc.; muli joint as of. the body, knot in trees, ankle. Ma, mugam, mulam cubit, projecting joint; murafi kal knee; mugaf kai elbow; muri knuckle, joint. Ko. molm cubit; ? malm id.; mogay, mogi+ elbow; moga+l knee. To. magoy cubit; ? mieg-mu-+ knee (mu- pot); ? mury all the ribs attached to the backbone; mury elf rib. Ka. moga, mona cubit, joint; moya kal knee; moya key elbow. Kod. mola cubit; mola kay elbow. Tu. mora, mura, mola, morafgé, murahgé cubit; morampu knee; molatky, molatkana knee-pan. Te. mora cubit; mo (K. also mrd) ceyyi, mo ceyyi elbow; m6 (K. also mr6) kalu, md kalu knee; mokarincu, mOkarincu, mokaril()u_ to Kneel. Kol. muera, (P.) movka, (Pat., p. 27) modki elbow. Pa. mira cubit. Ga, (P.) ki- mundku elbow. Go. (G.) mori id. (Voe. 2990); (W.) murf{sla knee (Voc. 2910). Konda mirva id.; mupku_ knee-joint. Kui miru, mriu, medu id., mireka a cubit’s length. Kur. maka, mffka knee, elbow; (Hahn) muka elbow; (Hahn) mika, mikul knee. Malt. mike knee; muki cubit. Cf. 5123 Ko. ad mug-, Ka. morgu. DED(S, N) 4093.

Ma. muri vexation; mugippu sadness, anger; mugikka to trouble, vex; musiyuka to grow angry, be in a pet; mugiccal displeasure, disgust, pettishness; musippikka to make dis- pleased. Jo. muf- (mut-) to become angry (e.g. Kurumba who consequently practises sorcery); musp o«x- (ory-) to be pettish, ask in coaxing whine; mu-p xiy- (xis-) to spoil (a child). Ka. mul to be irritated, vexed, annoyed, become angry; muli to grow passionate, angry; muliyisu to cause to become angry; mulisu anger, passion. Te. mudi a quarrel. DED(S) 4094,

Ta. mugu all, entire, whole; large; (-pp-, -tt-) to be whole, entire; muyzukka, muruka wholly, entirely, altogether, com- pletely; murucu entirety, whole; murutta fully developed; mugutu all, whole, entirety; mugutum, muguttum wholly, entirely; mur- untu entireness; mugumai id., completion, perfection; great size; muruvatu(m) all, whole; wholly. Ma. mugu whole, entire; murukka to grow thick, big; mugukku bigness; muruppu completion, size, thickness, thronged state; muyuku [449} mul muguppikka to bring to the highest degree, condense, make bulky; mugutu, muguvan whole, entire; muguval a complete man. Tu. murka full, brimful, exceeding, very much. Konda (BB) murtin completely. Pe. murke altogether. ?Go. (Mu.) mul all persons; mulnahk ali day; (L.) mulu people (Voce. 2928); (LuS.) mooloo army, retinue. DED (S, N) 4095.

Ta. muguku (muguki-) to bathe the entire body by dipping or pouring, sink, be entirely immersed (as in business); mugukku (mugukki-) to immerse (tr.); mugucu (muguci-) to dive, dip, get in, enter; muguttu (murzutti-) to plunge, dip in, drown (tr); muruval an acquatic bird; mt (-pp-, -tt-) to submerge, engulf; migku (mUzki-) to plunge, submerge, sink as a ship; mU€gttu (mUgtti-) id. (tr.); mufku (munki-) to plunge, sink, be im- mersed; mukku (mukki-) to press under water, immerse; mukkuli (-pp-, -tt-) to dive; mufikacci plunging in water; mukkuvar a sect of fishermen. Ma. mugukuka to sink under water, dive, be immersed; mugukikka, mun- hikka to bathe a child, plunge, immerse; mutfiuka to dive, plunge, sink; mukkuka to dip, immerse, plunge; drown (as criminals); dye; mukkuvar a class of fishermen; (Kaut.) mulkkuka to immerse. Ko. mulg- (mulgy-) to be lost to one completely; mulk- (mulky-) to make to suffer great loss; have intercourse with thoroughly; mu-g- (mu-yg-) to sub- merge oneself, dive, drown; mu-+k- (mu-+yk-) to submerge (tr). To. mulx- (mulxy-) to be destroyed; mulk- (mulky-) to destroy; mu-x- (muexy-) to be destroyed, drown; mu+k- (mu-ky-) to destroy; mu-xc- (mu-xé-) (river) drowns, is deep; destroy. Ka, murugu, muragu, mugigu, muz(u)ku, muritku, mur- unku, mugufgu, mupugu, munagu, munigu to go or sink under water, sink, become plunged in a liquid, immerse oneself, dive, dip, set as the sun, be ruined; murugisu, munugisu, etc., to immerse, dip, etc. (¢r.); muzavu, mu

Ta. murai (-v-, -nt-) to enter; (-pp-, -tt-) to pierce, bore; m. large mountain cave, cavern, den; mugaificu cave, hole; mUzai a hollow place; modgai cleft, crevice, subter- ranean water-course, pool. Ma, mulayuka to creep in, retire, cattle to enter the stable; mulekka to gather, shut up as cattle for the night. Ka, mole a small hole (made by crabs, etc.) in the banks of ricefields or tanks, a fissure. Cf. 3714 Ta. nugai. DED 4097. 4995 Ta, mul thorn, brier, thistle, bristle, spine, anything sharp or pointed as a fish- bone, porcupine’s quill, etc., goad, spur, sharp-pointed instrument, sharpness; mulku (mulki-) to enter, pierce; mulli thorny plant, name of various plants; mulari bramble, thorny twig; mulavu, mul_ porcupine; mullam-panri porcupine; muntakam thorn, thorn-bush, fragrant screw-pine, Pandanus odoratissimus. Ma. mul sharp-pointed, thorn, prickle, an iron pin, spur, bones of fish and snakes; mullan thorny, as plants; porcupine; mulli different plants; a fish. Ko, mul thorn; milyp thorny plants. To. mut thorn; mul digging-fork; mul-ody porcupine (< Badaga): miity sp. thorny plant; penis. Ka. mul, mulu, mullu thorn, pointed thing as a prickle. a sting, spur, hand of a clock, tongue of a balance, etc.; vb. to prick, sting: muluhu pricking, stinging, goading, goad; mulla hand of a clock; mullu fork. Kod. mulli thorn; munde screw-pine. Tu, mullu thorn, thorny bush, iron prickle, spur, bone of a fish or muli [450] muyi serpent, spine, fork; mulladi thorny ground; mundey
Ta. mul. /Cf. Mar. mola nail (whence Go. (ASu.) mola iron nail), DED(S, N) 4101. 4999 Ta, mujai core of a boil. Ka. mole id, Te. molaka id. DED 4102.

Ta. muli (-v-, -nt-) to dry, burn, be scorched, perish; mature, curdle; (-pp-, -tt-) to dry, become dried, be scorched; m, dryness, faded condition, that which is dry; mulari firewood, sacrificial fuel. fire, firebrand, fever. Ma, muli scorched or dry, the appearance of skin scalded and burst; muliyuka to be scorched, torn as skin. To, miily- (miits-) to be dry (of leaves, flowers). Cf. 5048 Ta. mut. DED(S) 4099.

Ta. mulai (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to spring, grow as horns, hair, etc., sprout as shoots, germinate as seeds; n, shoot, sprout, seedling; ?mulari lotus. Ma. mula germ, shoot, young plant; mulekka to germinate, shoot, grow up as rice-plants; mulavu, mulep- pu sprouting, germinating. To, mit, mil shoot of plant. Ka. mole to germinate, grow, shoot forth, come out of the ground, sprout, bud, shoot; m. germ, bud, sprout, first shoot out of the ground; molake, molike germ, bud, sprout; molasu germination; a kind of morbid swelling of the guttural glands; (Bark.) munigi, (Hav.) munge sprout. Tu. muliyuni, muleyuni, mulevuni, molipuni, molepuni to germ, shoot, sprout; mulé germ, shoot; molike bud, germ; munhgé shoot, bud, sprout; mtku the germ of corn. Kor. (T.) mUygku tender shoot. Te. molaka, moka germ, sprout shoot; young; molakettu, molacu, mol(u)cu, molatencu to sprout, germinate, shoot, bud, grow; mola- pincu to cause to sprout, grow; molava sprout; mokka young plant, germ, shoot, sprout: mdsu a sprout, shoot. Go. (M.) moriyana to sprout; (Mu.) mari- (seeds) to sprout (Voe. 2992); (ASu.) moyoka plant; (Koya Su.) mod- (plant) to spring up. Pe. moka shoot, sprout. Kui moka a shoot. Kuwi (Su.) mrogla shoot of bamboo; (P.) moko sprout; (S.) mokko herb, plant, tree; (Isr.) moko sprouting of crop; myogla eye, shoot of potato, etc, Kur. mul the minute pedicle (in pulse grains) which is to develop when sprouting; mut- muitna to germinate, either in soil or spontaneously. DED(S, N) 4100

Ta. mulai peg, stake, pivot, pin of a door, etc. Ma, mula peg, stake. Ko. mol peg. Ka, mole pin, nail, spike, wedge, peg, stake, pointed object. Tu. mule, mole nail, peg, wedge. Te. mola pin, nail, spike. Cf.
Ta. mulai piles. Ma. mula external piles, polypus. Ka. mole piles. DED 4103,
Ta. mulai bamboo. Ma. mula id. DED 4104.

Go. (Tr.) murhuttand to cover up; murutand (Ch.) to conceal, (W,) to cover; (W.) murihfana to shut down; (Ph,) murtana, (Mu.) murih-, (Ma.) mur?- to cover (Voe. 2927). Pe. mytig- (mrUkt-) to put on (hat, dancing mask), cover oneself with (e.g. umbrella); mtg- (m@kt-), (Kal) mtin(g)- id., close up, be closed (mouth, lotus); mttk- (-t-) to surround, enclose, close (mouth), obstruct one’s way. Kui mltinga (mlangi-) to be covered; mlipka (< mltik-p-; mlakt-) to cover. Kur. (Hahn) mulga’ana to cover, protect. Cf. 5034 Ta. mtfu. DEDS(N) 802.
Ta. mulka (-v-, -nt-) to sit with arms and legs folded. To, mugulm ir- (18-) (person) squats on heels, (tiger) sits on haunches. DEDS 801.
Ta. mullanki, mula radish, Raphanus sativus. Ma, mullaikiid. Ka. mullaigi, mUlage, millaigi id. Tu. mullengi, mUlengi id. Te. mullangi id. /Cf. Skt. mUlaka- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10250, DED 4105.

Ta. muram winnow; murril, muccil toy winnow. Ma. muram winnow to sift grain. Ko, morm winnow. To, mutn (obi. murt-) id. Ka, mora, mara id.; mucca) small winnowing fan used by children as a toy. Kod. mora winnowing basket. Te. morife small winnowing basket; (B.) muccili small winnowing fan used by children as a toy. DED 4106.

Ta. (DCV) muralai hermit’s tree [i.e. Buchanania latifolia}. Ma. mural, mural id. Ka, morafe, morappi, morale, morave id. Te. morali Sponia orientalis; (Lush.) moralli B. latifolia. [Trema orientalis Blume = S. orientalis Planch. 8. latifolia is different.] DED(S) 4107. §007 Ta. muri (-v-, -nt-) to be spoiled; (-pp-, -tt-) to change the nature of (as milk, medicine); muri (-v-, -nt-) to be spoiled; mukkal stench of things owing to dampness. ? Ma. mugiffu, muyifthu, musufhu an offensive smell. Ka. murikil impurity, foul- ness, uncleanness; muggu to get or have the smell of corruption, become or be musty or mouldy by dampness; 1. an offensive smell, mustiness, fustiness, mouldiness; muggalu mustiness, mouldiness; (Hav.) mfri bad smell, Tu. murguni to mould, become musty; mugga mould, mustiness; musty; mari a bad smell, stench. Te. musiki dirt, filth, impurity; dirty, foul; murugu to become putrid, rotten or rancid; mukku, (K. also) mrukku to spoil, become mouldy or musty, mould; m(nukka spoiled, mouldy, musty; (K.) mugga mould, mustiness; mouldy etc.; (B.) muggu to be musty or mouldy, be damaged, spoiled. Kol. (Kin.) murr dirt. VA. murg a bad smell. Pa, mur, murru dirt; mura, mur(raf rotten. ? Ga, (OU.) muyur dirt. Go. (Kos murk id, muri 1451] (Voc. 2905). Konda muRki dirty. DED(S) 4108.

Ta. muri (-v-, -nt-) to break, give way as a branch, be defeated, discomfited, perish, cease to exist; (-pp-, -tt-) to break (as a stick), cut, discontinue; n. piece, half, broken half of coconut, piece of cloth, deed, written bond, ola receipt, part of village or town, room; muriccal breaking, indigence, want; muyippu antidote, estrangement, breach of friendship; muriyal breaking; murivu id., breach, rupture, fracture, enmity; antidote; murukku (muruk- ki-) to break. Ma. muri fragment, piece of cloth, room, chamber, apartment, parish, hamlet, a note, bond; murikka to break, cut, wound, decide, scttle; muriccal breach; murippikka to cause to cut down; muriyan cutting; muriyuka to break through or in _ pieces, be wounded, be decided, curdle as milk; murivu breach, wound; mUruka to cut up vegetables. Ka, muri to occasion a flaw in a thing by bending it, sever by fracture, break, break off as Icaves, fruits, etc., crush, break down, defeat, rout, destroy, break up, put an end to, do away; break (intr.), become weakened in constitution, lose strength, be impaired; 1. fragment, piece, broken or torn- off particle, state of being broken, broken off, etc.; murige, muruyuvike breaking, etc.; murivu crushing, destruction; murisu to cause to break, cause to crush, get changed as large money into smaller; muruka, muraka man who breaks or ruins, maimed man, imbecile; murukatana breaking, ruining; muguku frag- ment, bit (as of bread); muruva, murava a maimed, imbecile wretch. Tu. mudipuni
Ta. muri (-v-, -nt-) to exceed the proper stage, as in boiling or heating; muruku (muruki-) to exceed the proper limit in heating (or with 4980). Ma. muriyuka to be scorched in frying. DED 4110.

Ta. muri sprout, shoot, tender leaf, leaf. Ma, muri-ppettatu stage of palm tree’s growth beyond orra-kaccatu (i.e. a coco- palm in the 8th-10th year). DEDS 803. 501 1(2) Ta. murukku (murukki-) to be proud, haughty arrogant, disagree, be angry; n. arrogance, impertinence, disagreement, discord, rancour, stiffness of manners, vehemence; murukkafi (-pp-, -tt-) to oppose, disobey (as a command); muruku (muruki-) to be haughty or insolent, bluster, become vehement; murippu pride, harshness; murai (-pp-, -tt-) to become stiff, hard, be stiff- necked, haughty; muraippu stiffness (as of a corpse), haughtiness, impudence; high price. Ma. muygukuka to be coagulated, become stiff. Ko. murk- (murky-) to be on bad terms with, refuse to do what one is told, sulk. To, mur- (mugO-) to become angry, become sad in mind, have dispute with; murk- (murky-) to murumuyru be on bad terms with, be offended. Ka, muruku, murku, mukku pride, arrogance; muruku, muruka, muraka making foppish or coquettish airs. Tu. murtapa, murtima haughtiness, arrogance, pride; folly, foolish- ness. Te, muriyu, (K. also) muriyu to exult, be in great glee, walk affectedly and grace- fully, (K. also) be proud; murikddu a proud or conceited man; muripamu, muripemu graceful affectation in gait, pride, arrogance, haughtiness; pride arising from ‘conscious beauty, coquetry, caressing fondling, coax- ing; muripincu to make oneself entreated or begged for anything, coquet; moraku, mora- kidu an obstinate and foolish man; mora- k

Ta. murukku (murukki-) to twist (as a rope), twirl, spin (as a potter his wheel); n. twisting, turn or thread of screw, ball of thread; muruku (muruki-) to wriggle. twist. Ma, murukuka to be twisted, be tight; muru- kal a twist; muyukku twining, twisting, writhing, a twisted cake; muyukkal writhing, spasms; murukkuka to twist, twine, tighten; muriccu-kuttuka to wreath, plat; muriccu- kutti anything twisted. Ka. muruku to give a different shape (to the face), twist (it) out of its natural shape, distort (it), change the expression; , distortion of the features; murukisu to cause to appear distorted, distort or change (as the face or its features); (PBh.) murigu curve. Kod. mur- (murti-) to tighten (intr); murik- (muriki-) id. (17.); muriki string which binds or tightens, rope round centre of drum by which its heads are tightened. Te. muraxatame turning; muraka- fincu to turn (t.). Kol (Pat., p. 139) murga- dileng to twist (a limb). Kur. murka’an@ to sprain; murkarna to sprain oneself. DED(S) 4112.

Ta. murumuru (-pp-, -tt-), moru- moru (-pp-, -tt-), morumoru (-pp-, -tt-) to murmur, grumble; muraiyifu to complain, express grievance; muraiyifu complaint. Ma. murumurukka to murmur; mura lamentation, wailing; mura ifuka to complain, find fault. Ko. murg- (murgy-) to growl; morv- (mord-) to mumble; moyr complaint; moyr id- to make a complaint. To, mugx- (murxy-) to growl; mury complaint; murk- (murky-) to complain. Kg. more to roar, cry aloud, clamour, bawl, yell; more, morata roaring, muruval [452] mun wailing, etc.; moreyuvike crying aloud, etc.; morey-idu to wail, lament, complain; mord imitative sound of crying aloud. Te. mura- muramanu, muramuraladu to be angry. fret, frown; morugu, moruvu to bark as a dog, bawl; 2, barking of a dog; morugudu barking; mora, morra cry, scream, shriek, howl, wailing, clamour; moralidu to cry out. Kol. (Pat., p. 139) mutkeng to groan. Pa, mokk- to bark. Go. (Tr.) mohcana, (W.) muhcina, (M.)} muhcana, (A. Y. Mu. S.} muhe-, (Ko.) buhs-, (Ma.) bu?c- id. (Voce. 2936); (Ma) murr- to growl (Voc. 2916). Konda mutli- (-t-) to bark. Mand. murg- id. Kui muska (muski-) id.; 7. barking. Kuwi (F.) mokhali, (S.) mukh’nai, (Isr.) muk- Ch-), (Su. Isr.) musk- (-it-) to bark. ? Br. marrim shout, call, cry (or with 4973). Cf. 4973 Ta. mural. / MBE 1969, pp. 295-6, nos. 37, 38, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 10215, which probably on semantic grounds should be divided into two entries, the second being related to (no. 38) Te. moramor
Ta. muruval tooth, smile, happiness; muruyali (-pp-, -tt-) to smile; mUral tooth, smiling. Ma. muruvél tooth. DED 4114.

Ta. murai, muraimai order, arrange- ment, system, regularity, turn by which work is done, time (as once, twice), birth, manners, custom, approved code of conduct, relation- ship by blood or marriage, justice, antiquity, fate, nature. Ma. mura what is binding, law, custom, duty, turn; murama custom. Ko. moyr relationship between kin. To, mir relationship by blood or marriage. Ka, more a turn, time, (K.?) propriety, virtue; relation- ship: (Hav.) mare relationship. Tu. mudé, muddé consanguinity. Go. (Hislop, I, p. 133) mura custom. Konda (BB) mur kinsman, DED(S) 4115. 5016 Ta, murram courtyard of a house, inner yard of a house, esplanade, open space, expanse. Ma. murram, muri, mirram front yard, court before a house. DED(N) 4116.
