Ta. murru (murti-) to become mature, ripen, be fully grown, be advanced in age, abound, increase, become hardened as the core of a tree, be fulfilled (as one’s desire), come to an end, be finished, die; complete (t.), finish, destroy, kill; mn. perfection, completeness, that which is complete, ripe- ness, maturity, end, termination; murrukai completion; murrum wholly, entirely, all; mura entirely, ‘fully; murral maturing, anything that is fully grown or developed, hardness (as of the core of a tree), fruit almost ripe, completing, ending, strength, old age; murri (-pp-, -tt-) to complete, finish; murrimai mature wisdom; murr-avai grand- mother; muruku (muruki-), muruku (muruki-) to ripen. Ma, murru entireness; murruka to grow ripe, entire, perfect; murra wholly, entirely. Ko, mut- (muty-) to become ripe, become mature or advanced in age (or with

Ta. murru (murri-) to surround, besiege, blockade; n. siege; murrukai block- ade, siege, surrounding; distress, want; mugral surrounding, encircling; murrikkai siege. Ma. murruka to be close, crowded. Ko. mut- (muty-) (people) come together in a dense crowd. Ka. muttu to enclose, cover, settle upon {as flies, etc.), environ, encompass, surround, hem or shut in, besiege, drive into straits, attack, swarm or crowd together (as bees), throng; muttalu environing, surround- ing; muttiga, muttige covering, surrounding, siege, blockade. 7. muttuni to surround, besiege, hem in, encompass; mutti, muttigé siege. Te. muftadincu to besiege; muftu id., (K. also) surround, spread over, be crowded; muttadi, mutfadamu siege, surrounding. Kuwi (F. ) mUcali to surround, enclose; (S.) muccinai to besiege; (Isr.) muc- (-it-) to lay siege. Cf.
Ta. mun. /Cf. Mar. muddal principal, capital; primary, fundamental. DED(S, N) 4053.

Ta. muni (-y-, -nt-) to dislike, be angry with; muninan one who dislikes or hates; munivu anger, wrath, dislike, avetsion, fatigue, suffering, endeavour; munai (-v-, -nt-) to fight, be zealous, be deeply engaged, be angry with, dislike; (-pp-, -tt-) to attack, busy oneself with, be overweight; 2. battle, fight, war, battlefield. enemy country. hatred, dislike, aversion, austerity, boldness, audacity; munaivu. aversion, dislike, anger, wrath; munai (-v-, -nt-) to dislike, feel aversion to; n, aversion; mupavu (munavi-) to dislike, feel aversion to, be angry; munaval anger. Ma, muniyuka to abhor (the world), be entranced, look sullen; muni excited. Ka. muni to become angry or wrathful, manifest anger, be displeased; m, that becomes angry, anger; munipu, munisu anger, wrath, passion, rage, enmity; munisugaya a passionate man; muniyisu to cause to become angry, irritate; mone affray, fight, battle; monegara a bold, courageous man. Tu. munipu anger, hatred; muniyuni to be in a passion, hate; munipuni to detest, hate; mufiguduni to be angry. Te. (K.) mun(ujku to be aggrieved, displeased: 1. displeasure, grief; (K.) monayu to attack, meet in battle; mona battle array. Go. (Tr.) mungsiang yétand to be more angry than sulky (Voc, 2871). DED 4120. 5022 Mand. munel urli a species of rat. Kuwi (D.) mulendi ori id. (molova mtsa). DEDS 805. , 5023 Kur. muxa frog. Malt. mige id. / Cf. Skt. mtkaka- id. DED 4421.

Ta. mitkku nose. nostril, beak, nose- shaped part of anything; mtikkan man witha large or prominent nose; mukarai, mukari bottom of the nose. Ma. mitkku nose, nozzle, beak; miikkan long-nosed. Ko. mu-k nose, funnel of bellows; mu-ekn man with long nose: fem. mu-ky. To, mu+k nose (in songs); mu-kuer- (mu-ku-ry-) to meet (of persons, rivers); mu+ku-f{- (mueku-fy-) to cause to meet; ?mu-kur- (mu-kury-) (person, cer- emony) approaches. Ka. miigu, mi nose, forepart, snout, beak, nozzle; miga, mUgi man with a nose. Kod. mu-ki nose. Tu. miku, mifgu, mifku nose, beak; mitike man who snuffles or speaks through the nose; fem. mtki. Te. mukku nose, beak, end, point, tip. Kol. mungad, (Kin.) mukk, (SR.) mukku nose. Vk. mungar id. Nk. (Ch.) munggan id. Pa, muvad- (pi, muvacil) id. Ga. (Oll.) muyan, (S.) muyan, (P.) mungan id. Pe, muygel id. Mand, mungel id. Kui mungeli, (K.) mungi id. Kuwi (F.) mtUngelli, (S.) mungeli, (Isr.) mufgeli, (Su. P.) mungeli (p/. mungelka) id. Kur, mui id. Malt. munyu id. Cf. 4895 Ka. mtikuti. DED(S) 4122.

Te. mliga, miiva, muvva small bell or tinkling ornament; (PK) milga, mlva, muvyva, movva bells fastened on leather strap round bullock’s neck. Kol. mu-yge anklet bell. VA. munge id. Pa, mtva id. Go, muya (Mu.) wooden cowbell, dancing-bell, (Ma.) dancing-bell (Voc. 2893); (LuS.) mooya a small metal bell. Konda muva a small bell or tinkling ornament tied to the legs.” Kuwi (Su.) silik muya dancing-bell; (F.) milya small bell; (Isz.) milya& small bell used in anklets and bracelets. DED(S) 4123.

Ta. mUfkai, miMikaiyan dumb person; miinkar dumb persons. Ko. mu-ygg dumb; mu-ygn dumb man; fem. mu-yng. Ka. mthga dumb, dumb man, Je. mfiga dumb, dumb- ness; dumb person; mtigavadu dumb man; fem. mfigadi; mufiga dumb. Go. (Koya Su.) mika! dumb man. / Possibly adapted from Skt. mitka-, Pali mfiga-, DED(N) 4124.
Ta. munka owl. Ma. muna, mukan id., Bubo orientalis. DED 4125.
Ta. mthkil bamboo, Bambusa arun- dinacea. Ma. miinkil id. Jr. mu+ynga bamboo. Tu. (B-K.) mupgily id. DED 4126.

Ta. miicatai tancidity, anything that has become rancid, anything unclean; mUcu | (mici-) to go bad, be spoiled, rancid; mtcal being spoiled. Ma, mUsé{ta uncleanness. Ka. musare boiled rice, which is considered to be unclean by Madhva brahmans, though they eat it and offer it to idols; the vessels in which anything has been boiled and which want cleansing; (Gowda) musirE dirt on utensils. Tu, musaré anything boiled or dressed for eating; uncleanness; boiled, dressed, unclean, foul. DED 4127.

Ta. miicu (mlci-) to swarm about, gather round; n. swarming, thronging; mtcal swarming, thronging; mtg (-pp-, -tt-) to swarm round, surround; mtgal a lid; mifikai vast horde; moy (-pp-, -tt-) to crowd, press, throng, swarm; spread as an eruption, crowd round, swarm round, cover, enclose; #. press, throng, swarm, crowd, closeness, tightness, battle, war; moyppu crowd, multitude; moci Cy-, -nt-) to swarm. Ka. musuku, musugu to cover, hide, settle upon as flies, close, swarm or crowd together, spread over or about; 7. cover, veil; musur to cover as flies, crows, etc., crowd together, cover, hide; n. cover, veil; musumbu cover, veil; mukaru, mukkuyu, mukkuriku to come or fall upon, inclose, cover, besiege, surround; mogasu to cover, fall upon, attack; moge to cover, fall upon, close with. Te. musaru, musuru, miigu, (K. also) miivu to collect or gather around, settle upon as flies upon any object, infest, surround, crowd, swarm; miika crowd, multitude, host, swarm, army; (K.) mogiyu to overspread, attack, close with. Ga. (S.) ming(i) €- to swarm. Go (Tr.) moiinda, moittind to rush at, close with; (SR.) moyand to kill (Voc. 2986). Cf. 4915 Ta. muccu and

Ta. mjfici face; mUficai pouting, sullen countenance; longish face, longish nose; miUfictru, (STD) mfiijeli muskrat. Ma. mlccu, mucci, mufifii, moi, motti, monta face. Ko. mo-end id.; mucl mouth- susie She SM Sac age Pee Renee eae NN miificu part of man, bird, or animal, meeting place of two people on path. Jo. mu+n (od, mu-t-) face, front; musnér the front, place in front; muen el- (ef-) to like (see 862); mu-net affection, love; ? miituf nose; ? m6OS cheek. Ka, muti, moti face, mouth, snout; musudu, musadi, musuli id., muzzle, nose or nostrils of a horse; (PBh.) musumbu face. Kod. mu+di face. 7 musunfu, mus- undu face, snout, muzzle; moné face, coun- tenance, front of a house. Te. mtti mouth; muffe snout of hog, beak of bird. Kol, mu-ti mouth, beak; (Kin.) mffiti mouth; (Pat., p. 35) mitndur snout. Nk. mtiti mouth, beak. Pa, muydur lip, snout of pig. Ga. (S.7) muti snout of pig. Go, (Tr.) massdr, (M.) mosor, (SR.) mosor, (L.) mosor, mosok, (Mu.) mosor (ob. mosot-), (Ma.) mosog(i) (obl mosoft-) nose (Voc. 2996); (G.) mundur beak; (G.) mundori, (Ma.) mundogi snout (Voc. 2890); (L.) moha face (Voc. 2997). Konda mUnzi nose; (BB) mUtu beak. Pe. mutla snout of pig. Mand. mutli id. Kui munduri snout; mudra id., upper lip. Kuwi muti (I.) beak, (Su. Isr.) id., snout of pig. Kur. mocc& mouth (not said of men unless in joke or abuse). Malt. mudra face. DED (S,N) 4129.

Ta. mificu (mUfici-) to lick. Ma. mificuka id., devour; mffici voracious; penem lingens,; muficuka, mOficuka to suck. Ka. (Hav.) milfiju to suck (used in a deroga- tory sense). Kod. musiij- (mu-fiji-) to suck (penis). DED 4130.

Ta. mittam dark, clouded sky; chilli- ness, cold; motfam sky overcast with clouds; modfam clouded sky. Ma. miifal dark sky; milfam close weather. Ka. m@dda_ cloud, cloudiness, cloudy day, hazy weather. Kod. mo-da cloud. Tu. muda id.; mdda id., obscurity, dullness; modu cloudy. Te. moda- mu_ cloudiness; (SAN) mfda-mancu_ thick mist. Kol. (Pat., p. 55) mtdam fog. Go. (S.) miram mist (Voc. 2943); (Koya Su.) mu¢am cold. Kui mudengi cloud, sky. Cf. 5034 Ta. motu. DED(S, N) 4131. :

Ta. miltu (mifi-) to cover, shroud, veil, hide, screen, obscure, shut in, enclose, close (as the eyes), shut (as the mouth), surround, encompass; miltal covering, cover, lid; mif¢i that which covers, cover, lid, top; mafam place of concealment, secret place; milttam that which is covered; mUffu that which forms a cover, coating, that which is covered; mifakku, muftakku veil, overall, mantle; mukkatu veil of cloth worn to cover one’s head; mr (-pp-, -tt-) to close, swarm around, surround. Ma. mUtuka to be covered, cover; mifikka to cause to cover; mUtal covering; mOfti cover, lid; mUftu cover; mata foetus born with a covering; mugakku, muf- fakku, mukkafu veil. Ka. mUdige a quiver. Kor. (O.) mde a kind of cap. Ga. (S.?) mUrp- (mOrt-) to close; (S.2) mudup- to cover, encircle. Cf. 5002 Go. mur-, 5030 Ta. micu, and $033 Ta. mftam. DED(S) 4132. * [455] mori

Ta. mtu root, cause, origin. Ma. motu the bottom (e.g. of a dish); root, origin. Ka. miidu to arise, originate, be produced, be born, come into existence, become visible or apparent, come about; mida, midal, miidu the direction in which the sun rises, east; mUdi rising of the sun, etc. Tu. mUdu support; the east; maduni to tise, be born; m&davuni to originate, produce, cause to rise, cause to be born. Te. mffdu to happen, occur, befall; m&ducu to cause to happen. Go. (Mu.) my beginning (Voc. 2942). DED(S) 4133.
Ta. migu ewe; mutuku female elk; mutuval bitch. Kod. muedi girl. DEDS 806. -

Ta. mai sack, sack-load, pack, packed bundle as of paddy, large receptacle for grains; mUftai bundle, that which is tied up, bag, wallet, satchel, load carried in a sack, bale, a large measure of capacity; -mOtfu (miffi-) to join, link, stitch, sew together; n. tie, bond, that which is tied, stitch. Ma. mafa load, bale, esp. corn in straw bundles for exportation; heap, as of corn, straw; mOftuka to stitch together, patch, seam; mUffal stitching. Ko. must bag of grain as a_load for an animal. To. mu-ty full bag. Ka, mide straw bundle containing pulse and ragi; pillow, cushion; mUdave pillow; mufe bundle of grain, cloth, etc., pack,- bale: moffe, mafte leather bag; bundle, bale, burden, load. Tu. moffe bundle. Te, mtfa bundle, parcel, package, load carried by a man, bale. Pa. milfa leaf basket for storing grain. Go. mff{a (M.) a pack, (Ko.) bundle (Voe, 2938). Konda mUfa bundle, packet. Kuwi (F.) mtnta bundle (clothes, etc.); (Isr.) mita a bundle of grass. Kur, mr grain bale. Cf. 4921 Ta. mufi. / Cf. BHS mufa-, mita-, mofa-, etc., some sort of basket, bag, or large container; late Skt. mitaka-, mUdaka- id.; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10233, DED(S) 4134.

Ta. mOf{ai, mUfu, motfu-ppucci bed bug. Ma, mifta id. DED 4135. 5039 Kur. mifdna (muddyas) to strike or dash in one’s course against some obstacle, stumble. Malt. munde to gore. DEDS 807. $040 Ta. mUtari (-pp-, -tt-), mutali (-pp-, -tt-) to establish with evidence, confront with proof; mukatalai confrontation; (-pp-, -tt-) to confront, face. Ka. mtdalisu to confront and upbraid, taunt; mttdale confronting and upbraiding, taunt. Te. mitdala, mUdalike proof; mildalincu to remind, prove, ascertain; mildalimpu reminding, proof; mudarakincu, mudalakincu to oppose, resist, remind of something painful or humiliating, ask, question. DED 4136. 5041 Te, mUri buffalo; ox, bullock; (lex.) mUtiri buffalo. Ma. mOri bullock, ox. Ko. musry untamed (of bullocks). Ka. muri bull, ox. Go. (A. Y.) mura, (G.) mure, (Ph.) mara, (Ch.) mura, (L.) murti cow; (Ma.) mure ponda miirccai [456] manu milch cow (see 4500) (Voc. 2898); (LuS.) moore cow; (ASu.) mura id. DED(S, N) 4137.
Ta. mrccai sharpness. Ma. mirk- kuka to sharpen; mUrcca sharpness, edge, keen sharp wit; mUrccikka to become sharper; mfirppu sharpness; mUrppikka to whet. DED 4138.

Ta. mUlam piles, prolapsus ani. Ma. midlam haemorrhoids; posteriors. Ko. muelm (obl. mu-lt-) eversion of bowel during evacu- ation (2). Ka. (Gul.) mul-vyddi piles (Skt. vyadhi- sickness). Te. millamu piles, haemor- thoids. DED(N) 4139.

Ta. mUlai corner, intermediate point of compass. Ma, milla id. Ko. mu-l corner, direction. To, mu-ly id. Ka. mile corner, point of compass. Kod. mu-le corner. Tu. milé id. point of compass, direction. Kor. (T.) mullu corner. Te. mila id., point of compass, direction. Kol. (SR.) mula corner, elbow. Pa. mila corner. Go. mtla (SR.) horizon, (M. Ko.) corner (Voc. 2945). Konda mila corner. ? Kui muju, mudgu id. Kuwi (F. Su. Isr.) mala id. DED(S) 4140.

Te. mila a braid or tress of female’s hair. Konda mUla hair-knot of women. Pe. -mul, in: temul, (Kalahandi) tremul_ hair (see 3279). Kur. mulna@ to tie the hair into a chignon; refl. mulrna. Ma/t. mulure to gather. up one’s hair into a cue; mulutre to gather up another’s hair into a cue. DED(S) 4089.

Ta. mig (-v-, -nt-) to be mature. Ma. murukka to grow ripe, mature. Te. m(r)uggu to ripen, become overripe (or with 5017 Ta. murru). Pa, mur- to ripen after being plucked; murkip- (murkit-) to ripen off (t7.). Go, mirina to ripen (of a boil); murkstand to cram achar berries into a pot, to rot them before extracting the chironji kernels. DED 4141.

Ta. mUgi ladle, vessel for holding water, vessel used in sacrifices; mUgai ladle; mugai spatula, ladle; m¢ku (mofki-) to ladle out (as rice from a pot). Ma. migi a sluice of ricefields. Ka. mgi spout of a water-jar. ? Tu. mUruni to tap toddy; murté act of drawing toddy. DED(S) 4142.

Ta. mil (mulv-, m@nf-) to kindle, catch fire, be kindled, stirred up (as anget); miftci violent temper, rage; mUttu (mifti-) to kindle (as a flame), stimulate (as a quarrel), stir up (as feelings); m. excitement, provoca- tion; mittam fuel, bonfire. Ma. mUftuka to kindle, nourish (a fire), augment (a quarrel); mitfam smoking out the mosquitoes. Te muffincu to kindle, light; mugtagincu id., create quarrels, make mischief. Konda mutis- to kindle, light. Cf. 4996 Ta. muli. DED(S) 4143.

Ta. mili defect of limb, deformity; one who is deformed or has defective limbs, that which has lost a part or piece esp. at the top, that which is defective, person or thing devoid of the usual ornaments, ugly woman; a term of abuse; mili nay dog with clipped ears. Ma. mtili maimed, having lost an ear. Ka. mura state of being deprived of or without, that of being bare, that of being maimed; man without ears, man who has lost any member; man with a mental defect, fool, brute; a word of abuse; migi state of being deprived of, etc.; an earless woman, woman without ornaments in the ears, widow. Cf.

Ka. mile, (Gowda) mullE bone. Kod. muele id. Tu. (Bhattacharya; non- brahmin informant) mttle id. ? Kur. mudda id. Cf. 4528 Te. bomika. DED(S) 4145, ' §051 Ta. mUlai brain, marrow. Ma, mila id. Ko. mi-l marrow. Tu. (Bhattacharya; non-brahmin informant) mle brain. Te. mulaga marrow. Kol (Kin.) mull id. Pa. nulli, nulgum, nuigut id. Go. (Ma.) muge, murgonji id. (Voc. 2944). Konda. murva, (BB) mufi id. Pe. muti brain; prén mifi marrow. Mand. mura brain; mfOfti_ id., marrow; biti marrow, Kui nili bone marrow; kada-mUla brain; (K.) mtila id. Kur (Hahn) murma marrow. &r. mili id., brain, kernel of nut. DED(S) 4146. .

Ta. mtlnru, (Tinn.) milnu three; adj. mu (before doubled consonants), mtv (before vowels); mtinram third; mirrai triple; mivar three persons; mu-ppatu thirty; mu-nntiru 300; miv-ayiram 3,000; mu-mminru by threes; three times three; mummai state of being three; mifgakku three ugakku. Ma.). Tu. mOji_ three; mijané third; muppa thirty; munntdu 300

Tu. megge, megye, meggi a younger brother; megydi, megdi a female’s younger sister. Kor. (M.) megi, (T.) megge younger brother; (M. T.} megdt younger sister. 5055 Kur. (Hahn) menjxna to incubate, shelter as the hen shelters her chickens. Ma mengje id. Cf. 5088 Go. ménj. DED

Ta. metfi, metfu a kind of plain ring worn on the great tow or the next toe; venfaiyam warrior’s anklet. Ka, meftu a kind of foot-ring of which two are put to the second toe and which tinkle when struck together by walking. Te. metfe, mattiya, matte ring worn on any of the toes;mendiyamu, mendemu ring worn on the forefinger. Nk. maffe toe-ring. Pa. matta id. Konda méti bracelet. DED(N) 4 :

Ta. mettu (meffi-) to spurn or push with the foot. Ko. mef- (mec-) to trample on, tread on; met sole of foot, footstep, footprint. To. mét- (m6ty-) to trample on; m6f{ step, tread, wooden-soled sandal. Ka, meffu to put or place down the foot or fect, step, pace, walk, tread or trample on, put the foot on or in, put on (as a slipper or shoe); n, stepping, step of the foot, stop on a stringed instrument; sandal, shoe, step of a stair; meffisu to cause to step; mettige, mefla step, stair. Kod. moti footprint, foot measure, doorsteps. Tu. muttu shoe, sandal: footstep; steps, stairs. Te mef{u step, stair, treading, slipper, stop on a lute; maftu, (K. also) meftu to tread, trample, crush under foot, tread or place the foot upon;n. treading; maffincu to cause to be trodden or trampled, Ga. (S.2) metfu step (< Te). Konda mat- (-t-) to crush under foot, tread on, walk, thresh (grain, as by oxen); caus. mafis-. Kuwi (S.) mettunga steps. Malt. madye to trample, tread. DED(S) 4150.

Ta. meffu mound, heap of earth: métu height, eminence, hillock; mutfu rising ground, high ground, heap. fa. métu rising ground, hillock; matu hillock, raised ground: miffal rising ground, an alluvial bank; (Tiyya) maffa hill. Ka. médgu height, rising ground, hillock; miffu rising or high ground, hill: mitfe state of being high, rising ground, hill, mass, a large number; (Hav.) mutfe heap (as of straw). Tu. mitfé prominent, protruding; mufte heap. Te. metta raised or high ground, hill; (K.) meftu mound; mitta high ground, hillock, mound; high, elevated, raised, projecting; (VPK) métu, méfa, méeti stack of hay; (Inscr.) menfa-cénu dry field (cf. meftu-n@la, mettu-vari). Kol (SR.) metfa hill; (Kin.) mef¢, (Hislop) met moun- tain. Nk. met hill, mountain. Ga. (S.2, LSB 20.3) metta high land. Go. (Tr. W. Ph.) matfa, (Mu.) matta mountain; (M. L.) meta id., hill; (A. D. Ko.) metfa, (Y. Ma. M.) meta hill; (SR.) metta hillock (Voc. 2949). Konda meta id. Kuwi (S.) metta hill; (Isr.) meta sand hill. Cf. 5474 Ta. vitam. DED(S, N)

Ta. mef{tu place where custom is paid, custom-house. Tu. (K. Ramakrishnaiya, Dravidian Cognates, p. 181) motfu place where custom is paid. Te. mettu id., place where toll is levied, toll-gate. DED(S) 4152,

Te. mendu abundance. plenty, much, a good or great. deal; abundant, plentiful, ample, much, great. Go. (Tr.) ménd (obi. mét-, pl méhk) full (used suffixally, e.g. doppd-ménd a leafplate full); (W. Ph) mer full, whole, entire, complete: (Mu.) mend (pl. mehk) id., e.g. gappa mend (pl gappa mehk) basketful; (Mu.) mefay having the total of; (Ma.) nag mendu the whole village; nag mefor all the people of the village (Voc. 2950). DEDS 809. 5061 Mand. med- to wear (shirt). Kui (K.) med- (-it-) to put on, wear (jacket). Kuwi (T.) med- to put on, wear (coat). DEDS 810.

Ta. mitag brain. Ko. medl id. Ka midul, midudu, medul, medadu _ brain, marrow. Te. medadu brain. Kol. mitik (obl mitk-), (Kin.) mitk id. Nk. mitik id. Pa. meda [458] meruku medek id. Ga. (P.) medik id. Go. (A.) medur id.; (Tr. Ph.) maddur, (G.) medur(i) id., marrow; (Mu.) madur, (Ma.) meddog, (S.) meddur, (Ko.) medur brain; (Y.) vedur id. (Voc. 2954). Kur. meddd, (Hahn) meddo, neddo, (Tiga, Bleses) meddo id. Malt. medo id. DED(S) 41583. 5063 Kui meda scar, spot or blotch on skin. Malt. medgo discoloured by bruise; medgre to discolout, blacken. DED 4154.

Tu. med(ujkuni to be in motion. Te. medalu, medulu to move a little, shake slightly, stir; wander, rove; rb. 7. medalika: medalincu, medal(u)cu, (K. also) medapu to move, stir, put in motion; (K.) medamu to stir, move, cause to move, stir (tr.). DED 4155.
Ka. mede heap. Te. (VPK, intro. p. 128) meda id. DEDS 811.

Ta. mettu (metti-) to plaster, pad, pack. Ko. met- (mety-) to plaster, smear; metc- (metc-) to smear. Ka, mettu to coat walls with chunam or mud, plaster, lay on, apply to, press into; mettuvike smearing over, etc. Tu. mettuni to fill up with earth, repait as a dam. Te. mettu to apply, plaster with mud or the like; 7. plastering with mud. Kol. met- (mett-) to plaster with mud. Vk, mett- to plaster, smear. Pa, mett- to smear. Ga, (P.) mett- to rub on, apply (medicine); (S.3) met- to paint thickly. Mand. mit- to rub on, smear, plaster. DED(S, N) 4156.

Ta. mettai bed, cushion, quilt stuffed with cotton, sleeping-place, coat, jacket, hunting accessory carried on the shoulder. Ma. metta mattress, bedding, quilt. Ko. med mattress. Jo. midy id.. bed. Ka. mette bedding, mattress. Kod. mette mattress. Te. metta bed, bedding, cushion, pad. DED 4157.

Ta. mettai, mettai-vitu storicd house, upper story. Ma. metta terrace. Ko. med upstairs room covering whole house. To. midy upper story. Ka. (HavS.) metti the upstairs. Tu. mettige pavement or boards of the floor of an upper story. Te, middiya, midde flat roof, terrace, terraced house, one with a flat roof, house with an upper story. Konda mide, in: méra mide terraced building (see 4796(b)). DED(S) 4158.

Ta. mett-enal expr. signifying (a) being smooth or soft, (b) being gentle, (c) being slow, (d) being dull; mett-enavu mild- ness of disposition, even temper, gentleness, pliancy; metu softness, gentleness, slowness, dullness, bluntness (as of an edge); metumetu (-pp-, -tt-) to be soft to the touch (as fruit); metumetuppu softness, having a soft surface. Ka. mettage, mettige softness; soft, pliant, etc.; softly, slowly; mettane, mettanna, mettine soft, etc.; medu soft. Tu. mettena soft, pliant; medu id., gentle, ripe, yielding. Te. metta, mettani soft, yielding, mild, gentle, meek, good, lenient, mettena softness, mildness, etc.; soft. etc.; mettanan slowly; meduvu soft; metaka, metuka soft, loose, slack. Nk, mette soft. Ga. (S.2) mettan soft, tender. Kuwi (S.) mettanahi soft; mettana anai to relent. DED(S) 4159. 5071 Kol. (Kin.) mendare a kind of bee. Nk. mendhar, mendhare id. Pa. mendir id. DED 4160.

Ta. mentiyam, méti, méntu, venta- yam, vental, ventiyam fenugreek, Trigonella foenum-graecum. Ma, ventayam id. To, méty id. Ka, mente, menteya id. Tu. mette, mentl, mente id. Te. menti pertaining to fenugreek; mentulu fenugreek seeds. Go. (ASu.) métin id. /Cf. Skt. mantha-, methi-, methika-, methini-, vedhani- fenugreek; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10313. DED(N) 4161.

Ta. mey truth, reality, soul, con- sciousness, body (used euphemistically), breast, consonant; (-pp-, -tt-) to be true; mey-kkol to believe to be true; m, acceptance as truth; meyppi (-pp-, -tt-) to prove, sub- stantiate; meyppu verification, proof; meym- mai truth, reality, natural state, existence, signification; meyyan, one who has realized the truth; true, honest, trustworthy, faithful person; brahman; one who speaks the truth; God; son. Ma. mey, mai body, person; true, truth (e.g. mey-vari true religion). Ko. may body; meyn son. To. moy body; ? miy vulva of animals. Ka. may(i), mey (i), mai body; side, part, place. Kod. may body (in songs), Tu. mai body, person; (Shanmugam) mayn id. Te. meyi, mé body, side, manner, metliod, mode; meyi-konu, m®@-konu to consent; meyi-kolupu, mé-kolupu to per- suade; meyi-kdlu, mé-kolu consenting, agree- ing; (K.) mému to agree, concede; memudu consent; mai the body; manner, way; side; mai-konu to consent, agree, mai-marapu ecstasy, trance, (K.) forgetfulness, intoxi- cation; mai-maravu armour. Cf. 4704 Pe. may and 5099 Ta. m&ni. DED(S) 4162.

Ta. meruku smoothness, glitter, lustre, polish; meravanai procession. Ka. merugu shine, lustre; mirugu to glitter, flash, sparkic, shine; miru shining, sparkling, mirugu, mirupu glitter, shine, lustre; mere to shine, gleam, glitter; become manifest, appear, assume an ostentatious appearance; make manifest, display; . shine, lustre; merasu, merisu, merayisu to cause to shine, make manifest, display, exhibit; meravani, meravan(i)ge ostentatious display. Tu. mere display, parade; merepini, mereyuni, mere- meru] . [459] mezuku vuni to shine; swagger, make an ostentatious display; merepavuni to make shine; merpu glitter, lustre; meranigé, meravanigé pro- cession. Te. meracu to glitter; merapincu to cause to shine, show off, show to advan- tage; merapu shining, glitter, lightning flash; meygayu to shine, glitter, gleam, (K. also) be made public, be known, appear; (K.) mera- yincu to make known, disclose; merfigu to shine, glitter, gleam, appear to advantage; n. brilliance, glitter, polish; bright, shining; merumu to glitter; ”. flash of lightning. Kol. merp- (merept-) to lighten; (SR.) mirniid spark. Nk. merp- to flash, lighten. Pa. marp- (mart-) to lighten; med- to flash (tiger’s eye, etc.). Ga. (S.) merc(i) &r- to glitter; (S.2) mers lightning. Konda mers- to glitter, flash (as lightning). Kuwi (S.) mer- to light; mervu light; merh- to lighten; merpu, merhi manne lightning; (F.) mérh- to shine (stars). Kur. merxa sky, heaven; merxanta heavenly Malt. mergu, merge sky, heaven; merg

Ta. merul (merulv-, merunt-) to fear, shy; nm. fear; meruli shy person; miral (miralv-, miran{-), mirag (-v-, -nt-) to be frightened, startled; miurattu (miratfi-) to frighten; fascinate, deceive, drive away. Ma. meruluka to be scared; merul fright; mira- luka to start, be shaken by fear; mirattal frightening. To. mi-d- (mi-d@-) to look fiercely. ? Te. meramera doubt, suspicion, fear, anxiety, guilty conscience. Cf. 5489 Ta. veru. DED 4164.

Pa. merkubi cucumber. Ga. (P.) meykom id. Go. (Tr.) wehkum, (Ch. Ko.) vehkum, (W. Ph.) ahkiim id. (Voc. 3305). ? Ta. mitukkai country cucumber; (Tinn.) miéuku id. ? 7u. (B-K.) bécily a kind of pumpkin. ? Mand. vidge cucumber. DED (S, N) 4165.

Ta. mel (melv- menr-), melku (melki-) to chew, masticate; chide. Ma. melluka to chew, champ. Ko. mek cud. To. metk id., mouthful. Ka. mel(u), meltu, meli to chew, masticate, eat with a mutter- ing sound, mumble, eat; melaku, meluku, melku, malaku, maluku bringing up again for rumination. Te. mekku to eat, gobble, swallow, gormandize. Ga. (S.7) mekkap- to eat like a glutton. Mand. mref- to chew. Kui mrtda (mrédi-) to chew; n. chewing; mérri giva to chew the cud, ruminate. Kuwi (Mah., p. 250) mrer- to chew (prob, for mréd-); (Isr.) mérd- (-it-) to eat, chew (joking expression). DED(S) 4166.

Ta. mel soft, tender; melku (melki-) to become soft, be light; mella, mella softly, slowly, gently; melli woman; mellikkai thin- ness; mellitu, mellicu that which is soft or fine; thinness, slenderness;' mefliyar the weak, the emaciated, the poor; low, mean person; women (as of delicate build); mell- enal, mell-enal expr. signifying being soft, gentle, being dull; meli (-v-, -nt-) to be weak, become lean, thin, suffer, languish, perish, become poor, reduced in circumstances, be softened (as a hard coconut), be lowered in pitch (music); (-pp-, -tt-) to weaken, make lean, thin, cause suffering, destroy, soften (a hard consonant), lower the pitch; meliya- yan weak, powerless man; melivu weakness, feebleness, languor, fatigue, etc.; menmai softness, etc. Ma. mel slender, tender; meli- yuka to grow thin, lean; melivu, meliccal thinness, leanness; melluka, mellikka to be thin, fine; mell@ slowly, gently, softly. Ko. melg- (melgy-) to soften (intr.) by action of water or heat; melk- (melky-) id. (tr). To. mesx- (megxy-) (thing) becomes soaked and softened; mesk- (megky-) to soak (¢r.) so as to soften; mely slowly, stealthily; meli--- very slowly. Ka, mel(u) soft, tender, pliant, mild, gentle, kind, pleasant; slow, etc.; melpu softness, mildness; mella, mellane, melle gently, softly, slowly; mellitu, mellittu that which is soft, mild, etc. Kod. melle lightly, slowly. Tu. mella slow, soft, gentle; slowly, softly, gently; melipuni to knead as dough, tread into a well-mixed mass (as ear

Pa. melk- to lighten; (S.) malk- (light) to flash. Ga. (P.) mel- (lightning) to flash. Pe. malka- to lighten. DEDS 813. 5080 Kur, melkha throat, neck. Malt. melge throat. DED 4168. 5081 Kur. melkhd husband’s brother’s wife, (Hahn) a concubine. Malt. melqo, melqor contemporary wives; melqaler two or more contemporary wives; melqo-oji polygamy. DEDS 814. . $082 Ta. meruku (meguki-) to cleanse floor with cowdung solution, smear as the body with sandal paste, gloss over, varnish; n, cowdung, wax, gum, soft waxy pill, mass; merukku smearing with cowdung water as the floor, cowdung, substance or solution used to smear any surface; merukkam ground or floor prepared by being smeared with cowdung water. Ma, meru, meruku wax; megukuka to anoint, wax, varnish, daub a place with cowdung; merukku anointing, varnish, daubing, polishing. /r. mékku wax. PalKu., Alku. (Zvelebil 1980 on Ir.) mékku id. Ko. mek bee’s-wax; mek- (meky-) to cleanse floor with cowdung water. To. m6gk- (mOsky-) to smear with dung of buffaloes as a ritual purification; mésk wax. Kod, mukk- mar [460] méetti (mukki-) to smear (mud, manure on ground). Te. m(r)égu, mreévu to smear; 7 (K.) médu id., daub, mix together. Kol. me+g- (mes kt-) to purify with cowdung solution. Nk, mégh- (mékt-) to apply. Go. (Tr.) marhuttana to paint cattle for the Pola or the Diwali festival (Voc. 2748), ? macana (Tr.) to plaster mud on a wall or dam, (SR. M.) to smear; (G. Mu. Ma. Y.) mac- to plaster (Voc. 2773); (SR.) mitana to apply (Voc. 2

Ko. mar iron hook with long wooden handle, used to loosen up straw that cattle are treading on threshing floor. Ka, mere- kolu = Ko. mar; ?mere to wander, roam about. ? Tu. mijipuni, mifiluni to be full of worms, affected with worms. Te. (K.) mer- amu to stir, move; cause to move, stir. Go. (Tr.) mirrand to swarm (of insects in the rains) (Voc. 2839); (Tr.) mirstanad to scatter or splash earth or water over anyone, (lice) to swarm on one’s head (Voc. 2836); (ASu,) mir- to be split: caus. mirus-. DED(N) 4170.

Go. (Koya Su.) mi- to open the eyes. Pe. meh- (mest-) id., see. Mand. meh- (meht-) to open eyes. Kui mehpa (meht-) to look, see, observe, perceive, give attention to; 7. look, sight, observation, perception, attention. Kuwi (S.) meh’nai to look, see; (Su.) meh- (mest-) to see; mespu seeing, sight, vision; (Usr.) meh- (mest-) to see. Cf. 5429 Ta. viri. DED(S, N) 4171. 5085 Kur. mehrarma to get damp (from air moisture); mehrta’ana to damp. Malt. mehare to be damp (as grain). ED 4172.

Ta. me excellence; m@kku height, high place, superiority; west; mé-taku to be eminent; mé-takai, mé-takavu excellence, eminence, greatness; méntalai eminence, excellence; mém-patu to rise high, be great; mél that which is over or above, extra; sky, west, head, leadership, superiority, excellence; that which comes after; more, more than; before. previously; métimai excellence; m@luk- ku on the outer side, extremity; melai upper, western, etc.; mé@lor those who are seated high, as on horses; the great, those of superior rank or caste; mérku west; m@nmai greatness, eminence, excellence, dignity, superiority. Ma. mé over; mén what is above, superiority, excellence; ménavan ‘a superior (title of Sidra writers); mé@fkai authority; ménma excellence, superiority, m@l what is above, surface; mé@lan, m@lavan a superior, melé upwards; mérkku westward. Ko. mé- mu-l higher place, up, western side; me* ciem western part of Nilgiris, me- kay, me- ki- upper arm; me-y gan eyes turned up in death; me+kie- (me-kart-) to get up (cf. 1109 Ta. kafa); me-kaye- (me-karc-) to make to get up. To. me-l up, high (in songs); me- tal upper border of waistcloth; me-tin sleeping platform on right side of house; me-Ipa-w upstream; -mil upon, on, over; concerning, about; (one) after/upon (another). Ka. mé that which is above, etc.; mégu (obi. mégap-), mége the upper side, surface, etc.; m@gana upwardly; mém- upper; mén what is above; upwards, further, and, besides; m@l(u), mela, méle that which is above, the

Ka. mé&ke she-goat; mé the bleating of sheep or goats. Te. m&ka, m@ka goat. Kol. mecke id. Nk. m@ke id, Pa, m@va, (S.) meya she-goat. Ga. (Oll.) m&ge, (S.) mage goat. Go, (M) meka, (Ko.) m@ka id. ? Kur. mexnd (mixyas) to call, call after loudly, hail. Malt. mége to bleat. [Te. mgéka (so correct) is of unknown meaning. Br. méth is without etymology; see MBE 1980a.] /Cf. Skt. ex.) meka- goat. DED(S) 4174.
Ka. médi glomerous fig tree, Ficus racemosa; opposite-leaved fig tree, I op- positifolia. Te. médi F. glomerata. Kol. (Kin.) m@ri id. [F. glomerata Roxb. = F. racemosa Wall.] DED 4176.

Ta. marti haughtiness, excellence, chief, head, land granted free of tax to the - headman of a village; mé@ttimai haughtiness, métaravar [461] méni leadership, excellence. Ka, méti loftiness, greatness, excellence, a big man, a chief, a head, head servant. Te. mé@tari, m@fi chief, head, leader, lord; (prob. m@ti < *mél-ti [cf. 5086]; Ka. Ta. < Te.; Burrow 1969, p. 277). DED(N) 4177.

Ta. mé@taravar, m@tavar a class of people who do bamboo work. Ka, méda, médira, madara .man who plaits baskets, mats, etc. of bamboo splits, man of the basket-maker caste. Kod, me+dé man of caste who make baskets and leaf-umbrellas and play drums at ceremonies; fem. me+di, Te, médara, mé@dari the basket-maker caste, a basket-maker; of or pertaining to the basket-maker caste. Kuwi (S.) mé@tri, (Isr.) métre’esi_ matmaker. /Cf. Skt. meda- a particular mixed caste; Turner, CDIAL,° no. 10320. DED(S) 4178. 5093 Ta, m@y (-v-, -nt-) to graze, feed, prey on (as birds or beasts), gnaw (as white ants), roam, lead a profligate life; (-pp-, -tt-) to make to graze, feed; méykki one who tends cattle, shepherd, cowherd; mé@yccal grazing, common pasturage, grazing ground, food, profligacy; méyppan herdsman, shep- herd, grazier; méyppu feeding, pasturing, grazing ground; méyal grazing, pasture, herbage for cattle to eat; m&yavan, meyan resident, dweller; mévu (mévi-) to eat. Ma. méyka to graze, browse; méykka to cause animals to graze or eat, feed, tend; méypikka to cause another to feed cattle; mé@ccal (Kaut.) méccil grazing. Ko. me-y- (me=c-) to graze; me-c- (me-c-) to make to graze. To. miey- (mi+s-) to graze; mi-c- (mi-¢€-) to make to graze. Ka. m&, m@yu (mé@d-), méy (méyd-) to graze, eat grass, browse, feed, eat, méyisu, mé@su to graze (fr,), feed, cause animals to cat; (PBh.) m@ta grazing; mépu, méhu, mévu grazing, pasturage, feed of any kind; (PBh.) mévali grazing land, pasturage. Kod.

Ka. méruve pile, pyramid, high top. Tu. meruvé, meryé an ornamental post near a gurji. Te. m@ruvu pyramid, cone. /? Cf. Skt. meru-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10230. DED(S) 4180. 5095 Kur. m€r, mér thread, twine, thin cord. Malt. méru thread. DED 4181.

Ta. mévu (mévi-) to join, reach, desire, love, learn, study, level, make even (as the ground), manifest, assume, abide. dwell, be attached, be united, be fitted or joined; n. desire; mévi (-pp-, -tf-) to catise to stay; mévinar friends, allies; meval desire; joining, uniting; mévalar, mé@var foes, enemies. Ma. mévuka to be familiar, occupied with, be- accustomed to a place and prefer it: adjust level; mé@val dwelling; mévalar enemies. Konda ménz- Cit-) to be straight or suitable, be harmonious or go with, unite (individual eads into a rope); ménzu unity, - ight- ness. DED(S) 4182. , ity straight $097 Ta. m@ri plough, plough-tail, handle of a plough; mégiyar agriculturalists. Ma. méri, méfifial ploughtail. Ko. me+y handle of plough. Ka. méti, m&ni plough-tail. Te. médi, (K.) m@di hind part or handle of a plough. Konda méri plough handle, plough- tail. Kuwi (F.) méri plough handle; (Isr.) méri id., plough. DED(S) 4183. 5098 Ka, mélamba the black humble bee. Te. milindamu, id. /Cf. Skt. milinda- bee, ar. miind a bee of the ! i BRD aan e large black kind. $099 Ta. mé@ni body, shape, colou beauty; mal body. Ma, ‘meni body, Sone. beauty, excellence; mé@l body. Kod. me-fi body. Te. ménu id.; méni brilliancy, lustre; belonging to the body, bodily, personal. Kol. me-n (pl me-ndI) body. Nk. mén (pl, ménul) id. Nk, (Ch.) mén id. Pa, mén (pl ménul) id. Ga. (S.) m&nu (pl méngil), (P.) mén id. Go. (Tr.) méndur (obi, méndud-), (A. Y. W. M.) méndul, (L.) mendifl, (SR.) mendol id. (Voc. 2963)
Ta. méni, kuppai-meni Acalypha indica. Ma. kuppa-méni id. DED 4186.

Ta. mai collyrium for the eye, ink, ink-paste, black pigment. black, blackness, darkness, spot as of moon, blemish, dark cloud: fault, sin; dirt: (-pp-, -tt-) to become black, be dim; maippu black, blackness. Ma, mai blackness, antimony. To, moy- (moc-) to become dark, become evening; moy ashes (in songs); mo@p darkness before dawn or after sunset, Ka, masi dirt, im- purity, the black of culinary vessels, soot, lamp-black, black colour, blackness, ink, antimony. Kod. masi charcoal. Tu, maji coal, black powder, ink; maiyi a kind of collyrium; maivaripuni to paint ithe eyes with antimony or collyrium; maipe, maippe a black or dark-coloured fowl. Kor. (0.) majji soot. Te. masi blackness, sootishness, soot, charcoal, ink, Nk, (Ch,) mas _soot. Go. (G.) masi id. (Voe, 2760). Kur, mats ink. Cf. 4627 Ta. macanku, 4781 Ta. ma, and 4792 Ta, macu. /Cf. Skt. masi- ink, lamp- black; Turner, CDIAL, no. 9920; ? cf. Skt. mecaka- black, dark blue DED(S, N) 4187.

Ta. mai barrenness, sterility, barren woman, barren buffalo; maimmai barren woman, barren buffalo. Ma. mat barrenness, macci barren woman. Ko. may sparen (of buffalo). To, moy id. Tu. mafje barren, unfruitful; mafjery sterile, barren. DED(S) 4188. - 5103 Tu. maipuni to pour, cast as in a mould; caus. maipavuni. Kui mespa (mest-) to pour in. DED 4190.
Tu. mogapu clasp of a girdle. Te. mogapu clasp of a necklace or the like. DEDS 815.

Ta. mokkattai that which is blunt, mokku bluntness; mokkai id. (as of an iron style). Te. mokka blunt, pointless, not sharp, keen, or pointed, useless; mokkadi, mokka- didu elephant with short tusks. /Cf. Skt. matkuna- elephant without tusks; Turner, CDIAL. no. 9748. DED 4192.

Ka. moggara, mokkala, mohara mass, multitude, host, army; mOharisu to become plentiful or abundant, be formed into a host; form a host, array as troops for battle. Te. modharamu battle-array; moharincu to array (tr.), prepare for battle. DED 4225. 5108 7a. mokkan stout person oF thing; mokkai bulkiness, stoutness. Te. mokkadu a stout, strong man, a determined, resolute man. DED 4193. [462] mottu

Ka. mokka Schroebera swietenoides. Te, mukkidi, (Lush.) mokkamu, mokkaveépa id. Kui (Lush; Khond = Kui) moko id. / Cf. Skt, muska-, mokga- id.; mugkaka- a kind of tree (S. swietenoides according to Lush,). DED 4191.
Ta. mokkai piece of wood, stump. Te. -(B.) mokka stub of wood. Go. (SR.) meka stump (Voc. 2947). DED(S) 4194.
Ta. moccai hyacinth bean, Dolichos ‘lablab. Ma. mocca D. tetraspermus. [D. tetraspermus not _ identified in Hooker.} DED 4195.
Te. mojfici, monni breasts. Tu. mufifia, mufifié the breast (as called by children). DED 4196.
Ka. modavi, modave small pimple on the face. Te. motima pimple. ? Ko. mordiry mole on skin. DED 4197.

Ta. mottu tender flower-bud; round- , ed top of a car; mofti (-pp-, -tt-) to close like a bud, bud, shoot forth buds. Ma. moftu flower-bud, nipple, bud-like, the blunt end, pommel. Kor, miffe (O.) tender bud, (T.) flower-bud. Ga. (S.2) modo bud. Kui modo id., flower-bud. Cf. 4893 Ta. mukig. /Cf. Mar. mod sprout. DED(S) 4198.

Ta. moftai bluntness (as of a knife); imperfection, incompletencss; monnai blunt- ness; monti lame person; mogai hornless or dehorned cattle, anything defective, stump, block; mannai bluntness. Ma. mdya cattle without horns or with horns turned. Ko. mondy headless trunk, body with useless arms, cut length of fuel, worn-out broom stump; ?med duilness (of knife). To. mudy man without use of legs, trunk of body. Ka. monda, mondu, monde blunt, maimed, deficient; mofa, mBfu state of being deprived, short or stumpy, that of being maimed; stump of tree, stubbles, (Hav.) modntu to bend; nv. lameness; monfé lame man; fem. monfi. Tu. mopdu blunt; mGpfy lameness, crookedness; lame, maimed, crooked. Te. mondi maimed, amputated, lopped, im- perfect, blunt; mondiyamu, mondemu head- less body or trunk, limb lopped off, stump, stub; modu blunt; a stump; mofu stump; mrodu padu to be blunted. Ga, (P.) mofo blunt. Konda (BB) moto id. Kur. mufa crop- eared; tiger. Cf. 3786 Ta. nonfu, 4919 Ta. mutaiku, and 5049 Ta. mali. / Cf. Skt. munda- hornless, lopped, blunt, Pkt. matfa- hornless; munta- cripple; Turner. CDIAL, nos. 9723, 10187, 10191. Cf. also OMar. (Master, p. 171) motaka stumpy, short. DED(S, N) 4199.

Ta. mottai bald head, shaven head; moftaiyan baldheaded man; fem. moffaicci; monnan baldheaded person, mopnai bald- ness; mattai id. Ma. moffa bald head. Ka, (K.2) mottane bare. /Cf. Skt. munda- bald, having the head shaved; Turner, CDIAL, no. 10191. DED 4200.
Te. modavu a milch cow. Ga. (P.) mude cow. Konda modo, modva, modvo id, DEDS 816.

Ta. mottu (motti-) to strike, beat; n, stroke, blow; mdtu (mdti-) to hit, smite, mottu [463] mo strike, dash against; n. blow; ? m@ttai ram, goat. Ma. mdtuka to dash against; mota surge, rising of. waves. Ka, modu to strike, beat, smite; 7. swelling occasioned by a blow, a hard boil, venereal tumour; modhu to beat, smite, strike, deal a blow; mdhisu to cause to beat, dash against or into, apply or put to (with force), thrust. Tu. mdda wave. billow, surge; modu stripe, wale. Te. mottu to strike, beat with something thick or heavy, thump, smite, give a blow to;n, a blow or knock with something thick or heavy, thump; m6du to beat, strike smite, knock; #. beating, stroke, knock. DED(S) 4201.

Ta. mottu dullard, idiot; lazy person _ or animal; mottai ignorant woman, Ka. moddu, maddu stupidity, bluntness (as of knife); madadu «dullness, stupidity. Tu. madady slothful, indolent; madadu_ lazy; a lazy woman; madade blockhead, sluggard. Te, moddadu blockhead, stupid man; moddu id.; dull, stupid, blunt, pointless; modduga bluntly, in a dull manner, stupidly. Ga. (S.3) muddun blunt. DED(S) 4202.

Ta. mottam sum, total, aggregate, whole, universality, generality, bulkiness. Ma. mottam whole, total. Ka. motta heap, mass, multitude, flock, collection, code. Te. mottamu total, sum, whole, collection, multitude, number, crowd, flock. Kuwi (S.) mottom{ total DED(S) 4203.

Ta. moy presents given on special occasions as at a wedding. Ko. moy con- tribution paid at a feast (e.g. at opening of new frouse, at joint piercing of ears of all children in village); total of such contribu- tions. Ka, muy(i), muyvu requital, act of returning like for like, return of good for good, an equivalent returned for anything given, done, or suffered, recompense, return of evil for evil, retaliation, punishment; present given to bride and bridegroom at their marriage by their relations, etc., with the prospect of a recompense being made on such an occasion in their own house. Tu. muyi gift of money at a wedding; muyya returning; muyya-paduni to return, give back; (B-K.) mujare allowance, subtraction, payment to be reduced due to adjustment of accounts, Malt. muje to liquidate a debt. DED(S, N) 4206.

Ta. moympu shoulder. Ma. mugippu id. Kurub, (LSB 1.11) mirpu id. Ko. muer shoulder joint. Ka. muy, muy(i)vu, muyibu, mudupu, muduhu upper part of the arm, shoulderblade, shoulder. Tu. mudu shoulder joint. Te. miipu upper part of the back, shoulder; mfparamu hump on the shoulders of an ox. Ga. (P.) muk shoulder. Konda (BB) mipi id. (contamination with nipi forms in 3671). Mand. mupa id. Kui mdpo upper arm. Kuwi (S.) modpa with’nai to shrug (cf. 5424); (Isr.) mdpa outer edge of the shoulder. DED(S, N) 4207.

Ta. mol (molv-, mont-) to take in a vessel, as water. Pe. mruk- (-t-) to ladle out. Mand. myuk- id. Kui (K.) mluk- to draw water by dipping in a pot. Kuwi mnok- (-h-) (Su.) to draw (water from container with small vessel into larger vessel), (Isr.} ladle out; (S.) mrokh’nai to merge. Malt. mulge to dip in, draw water. Cf. 3790 Ta. nol. DED(S) 4209.
Te. moraka defect, defective; morava blunt; morri defect, imperfection, want, deformity; defective, imperfect, deformed. Kur. murr® tail of a beast cut short, tail stump. dock. DED 4210.

Ta. moO (-pp-, -nt-) to undertake. Ka, mor to carry on the head; modpu load, burden, heaviness. Te. modcu, mdyu to bear, carry, support, (K. also) rest on (intr.), lean on; mBpu to place on another as load, lay, rest, support, charge with, (K. also) cause to rest slowly (as a foot on a slippery floor); #. large bundle, burden, load, severity or aggravation (of diseases), (K. also} responsi- bility; mOpuk3du carrier, burden, mdpudala weight, burden, charge, accusation, responsi- bility, obligation; m6ta bearing, carrying, load. Kol. mo+p burden. Ga. (8.2) mopap- (-t-) to keep [? hold] something heavy. Go. (M.) mdta load (Voc, 3000; < Te.). Mand. m4c- to carry on the head. Kuwi (Isr.) mdc- (it-) to be loaded down with heavy load, be burdened with many worries and responsi- mokuka [464] bilities. Cf. 2677 Ta. cuma (? *cuma- > *cmo- > mod-), DED(S) 4211. 5127 Ma, mokuka, montuka to drink, sip; modyikka to give to sip; moval a gulp. Kur. moxna to eat (anything except cooked rice); pass, or reff, moxmda,; moxta’and to cause or allow someone to eat. Malt. mdge to eat (as meat or fruity Ta. moc (PRs -tt-) to eats Antu (manti-) to eat, drink, expe ; enjoy Po 4897 Ta. mukku. DED(S) 4212.
To. muk, (Towfity dialect) mok up, west. Ka. moku upper portion, top, forepart. Konda musku (od1, muskuR-) above, upon, on; muskuRand from above. ? Kuwi (S.) muhe above, upon, on. DED 4213, DEDS 792.

Te. moku a thick or stout rope, cable. Konda (BB) modko rope. DEDS 817. 5130 Kur. modcna to cut up into con- venient pieces any object too large for use, give a cut in or through. Mair. moce to cut (as meat). DED 4214. 5131 Kur, mdjxa, mosga smoke; mojoxn’, mosga’and, mosOgna to create smoke, smoke anything, fumigate; mdjxOma, mosg?rna to smoke, emit fumes. Malt. moge to smoke, emit smoke; mogje to fumigate; mogtre to cause to smoke; mogare charcoal, soot. Br. moth smoke; (MBE 1980a). DED 4215. §132(a) Ta. makifi trial of magical powers between two enchanters in which one hides some treasute from the other and challenges him to discover it by mantras; common play where things are hidden by one player and discovered by another; mofi trial of magical power. Ma, mot? anything placed by a con- juror who tries to prevent its being removed. Ka, modi = Ta. makifi trial of magical powers, etc. Tu. modi anything placed by. a conjuror to try the ability of another, conjuration, delusion, sorcery. Te. modi a sort of magic or jugglery. (hy Ta, makiti a kind of hautboy used by snake-charmer; modfi snake-charmer’s pipe. Te. magidi a kind of musical instrument used by a snake charmer. DED 4216. 5133 Ta, moti arrogance, way, style, air, grandeur, display, military bearing, dignified bearing, exhibition, show; modfamofi os- tentation, pomp. Ma, moti high bearing, stateliness; fashion. Ka. modi a turn, caste, style, fashion (of speech, composition, action); mddamddi a beautiful, elegant fashio
Ta. moti dried knots of the creeper of long pepper; long pepper root. Ka, modi root of the long pepper vine. DED 4218. mren

Ta. motu, moftai stupidity, dultness of intellect, ignorance; monnaiyan dullard, idiot; mdgai stupidity. Ma. mdttu obstinacy, perverse pride; mdya stupidity. ? Ko. med dullness of senses; medn dull man; fem, medy. Ka, mdta stupidity. Tu, mode ignorant man, silly fellow. Te. motu rough, tude, uncivilized, rustic, boorish, dull, stupid, vulgar, low, indecent. Br. mdf foolish. Cf.
Ta. motu bundle of straw. Te. moda bundle of betel leaves. DED 4220. 5137 Kur. modhrna to forget. Malt. méthre id, DED 4221.

Ta. mdttai spathe or unblown flower, as of plantain, fragrant screwpine, etc. Ka. mote the leaf-like envelope over the flowers of the plantain, coconut, and ketdage. Tu. (B-K.) mdte plantain flower-bud. /? Cf. Skt. moca-, moca- plantain. DED(S) 4222.
Ta. moppi widow (used disrespect- . fully). Ka. m@cu to become a widow. Te mopi widow; (K.) moyu to-be widowed (non-elegant). DED 4223.

Ta. modvay chin, beard. To. mo-vy beard. Te, movi lip. DED 4224. §143 Ta. (DCV) movi black plum. Te. (DCV) movi id., (Sahk.) a kind of wild fruit tree, Eugenia alternifolia. DEDS 818. §144 Ka. mohala that which is affixed; the hilt of a sword or dagger. Te. (B.) mdhanamu sword-hilt. DED 4226. - 5145 Pe. mrania kind of rat. Mand, mrane id. DEDS 819.

Ta. ya a tree; (P. L. Samy, Proc. Second International Conference-Seminar of Tamil Studies 2.42) Hardwickia binata. Te. tpe id.; (for B. @pe Shorea robusta, see 896 Ka. epi). S151 Ta. ya, yivai what or which things; yavum, yavaiyum all, whole; yavatu, yatu what; how; yavatum even a little; yavan which man; yaval, yaral which woman; yar, yavar, ar, avar what persons; yarum any or everybody; yavarum each and every person; yavan, yankan, yantai where; yanku id., how; yantu where; when; @tu which, what; why; whence; how; @tum even a little; anything, everything; Evan who (masc. sg.); €vatum everything; &n why, what, how; e- inter- rogative base; pref. before a noun, meaning ‘what, which’ [e before consonant, evv before vowel]; evan, eval, evarkal, etu, ev/ evai which man, which woman, which persons, which thing, which things; adj. enta; evarum, evvarum everyone; evvelam whatever; evv- evar Whatsoever persons; evv-eval whatsoever things; efkan, efikittai, efku, eke, etol, etoli where; etkanum, efkum everywhere; ethan, efmanam, effan®, evan, ennanam how; where; efifiamrum always; ettanai how many, how much; ettupai, evvalavu how much; ettum by all means; entu how; what; eppati, evvatu, evvaru how; e-pporutu, e- ppoxztu, e-ppdtu when; e-pporutum, e- ppotum always; emparum everywhere; emmai _ which birth, what worlds; evan, entiya how, why; errai when; en why; enru, enraikku when, what day; enrum, enraikkum, erraik- kum forever; enna, ennatu, ennai what; ennatum, enum even a little;

Ta. yatu, atu goat, sheep; att-al shepherd. Ma, atu goat, sheep; attukaran shepherd. Ko, a+r (obl a-t-) goat. To, od id. Ka adu id. Kod. a-di id. Tu. édy id. Te. &dika, (B.) @ta ram. Go, (Tr. Ph. W) yeti, (Mu. 8.) @fi she-goat (Voc. 376). Pe. dda goat. Mand. Ude id. Kui oda id. Kuwi (Mah. p. 110) o’da, (D.) oda id. Kur. ara she-goat. Malt. éye id. Br, het id. / Cf. Skt. eda-, edaka-, edi--a kind of sheep: Turner, CDIAL, no. 2512. DED(S) 4229.

Ta. yantu, aptu year, age: adj, atta; atfantu every year; kig-Antai-ccikai arrears of former years (cf. 1619). Ma. anqu (obl. Att-) year; kir-antai last year (cf. 1619). Ko, Orr ae one year; i-r aey two years. To. wier O*f one year; ier o«r two years; koed last year (cf. 1619). Ka, @du year. Kod. a-ndi id. Tu. iyyodu this year; mtlvodu last year. Te. edu, €dadi, (inscr.) endu (gen. enti), andu year; €dudi ceremony performed on the first aniversary of a person’s death; (Inscr.) énfa- badi yearly contribution; Enté{a vearly. Kol. ie end this year; a+ e»d two years before last. Nk. iyer this year. Nk. (Ch.) iyand id. Pa, iyad id. Ga. (OIL) iyand id. Go. (Tr.) yend, (w.) yenr, (M.) iyend, (Mu. Ma.) iyénd, (Pat.) tyode (i.e. iyode) this year (Voce. 179); (Tr.) hénd the year before last (Voc. 3589); (Mu.) hayénd id. (Voc. 3523); (Ko.) nd, (Pat.) yéda year (Voc, 379); (Koya Su.) énd id. Konda &ndad id.; iyond this’ year; (BB) Ondkara, Ofkara rainy season. Pe, iyondin this year; ratihig last year. Mand. Tyut this year; yatihin last year. Kui yandu last year; rondu in a previous year, year before last: varondi next year, year after next; (K.) ondu year. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) fyona this year; (S.) randu, (T.) randu, (Isr.) rindu last year; S Ist.) vdpona next year. DED(S, N)

Ta. yamai, amai turtle tortoise. Ma. ama id. Ko. e-m(b) tortoise. To. (Sak.) a-my id. (< Ta.). Ka. ame, ave, Eve, (Hav.) me tortoise, turtle; tibélu, timbélu a sea or land turtle; (Bell., UP.U.) alive tortoise. Kod. ame id. Tu. éme turtle. Te. tibelu, timétu id., tortoise. Go, (G.) hemul, (Ma. Ko.) émul, (Mu.) samel, hamul, (M.) hemul tortoise (Voc. 3334); (LuS.) emilee id.; (Koya Su.) yamol id. Konda tambel(i) id. Pe. haiman a kind of large tortoise. Kui stmbi tortoise, Kuwi (S. Su. Isr.) tambeli, (P.) hémbi id. DED(S, N) 4232. :

Ta. yar, fix stringed musical instru- | ment; eu (+v-, -nt-) to emit sound; eral musical notes of the yar, the yar, human voice; eruppu (eguppi-) to call forth (as melody from an instrument), raise (as the voice in speaking or singing); eguvu (eguvi-) to produce or call forth sound; etu (-pp-, -tt-) to utter or sing in a loud voice; efuppu (etuppi-) to produce (as harmonious sounds from an instrument). Ma. éxil music. Ko, et- (eyt-) to sing (song), play musical instru- ment. (TPM, p. 227, for Ta. yay : eru.) DEN 72.

Ta. al (alv-, anf-) to rule, reign over, control or manage (as a household), cherish, maintain, keep or maintain in use; (inscr., Velu Pillai, pp. 158, 392) yaf to rule; alag, ali one who rules; @lukai rule, dominion, control; alvar the Deity, as supreme ruler; atci lordship, ownership, government, rule, reign, use, possession; atcai weekday; fal ruling, reigning; anfar owner, master, lord; gods: antavan, anfai master, Jord; anmai con.:olling power, possession. Ma, auka to rule, possess, have; arca day of the week, week. Ko. a+n- (a+d-) to possess, rule, keep, own, To. o-l- (o-d-) to own (buffaloes), tule. Ka, a (abd-) to get, have, possess, govern, tule, manage; like, aike obtaining, ruling, reign, government; alisu to cause to obtain or get, cause to rule; alutana, altana ruling, rule, sovereignty; alma, ama, apba ruler, lord, master; andari rulers, masters. Kod. asl- (acli-) to rule; arce week, day of the week. Tu. Muni to rule, govern, reign; aiké, Aluvike, aivike reign; adalite, adalte administration, management, government. Te. aiu to rule, govern, control, manage; (Inscr.) alu to rule; élika ruler, king, lord, master; aliki government; @lubadi, tbadi rule, govern- ment; (Inscr.) adavandu ruler, master. Cf.
Ta. yali, ali a tion; a mythological lion-faced, animal with elephantine pro- boscis and tusks. Ma. yagi lion; panther; ali a fabulous animal. DEDS 824.

Ta. yaru, aru (ob) yarru-, arru-) river, brook. Ma. aru river. Ko. peyvy-e-r (obl. peyv-e-t-) id. (see 4318 Ma. pura); eet an edge of bank of stream or tank (or with 916 Ta. @ru); er (obi. e-{-), in: te-- Ivieer Pykara river (< te+l pey e+r), kime-r n.pr. of swamp at Kolme>l village. Tu. (B-K.) ary, tary, sary, hary river, stream. Te. &yu (gen. &ti) river. Pa. pered id. (for per- big, see 4411). Ga. (P.) ber beret big river. Go. (Tr.) yér (obL yet-, pl. yehk) water; yeh-gata the water gnat; yeha-kkorr the diving grebe; (W. Ph.) yér (obi. yét-), (A. Y.) &r (Obl. &t-), (S. Ko.) &r, (Mu.) &r (obi. ét-, pl. thk), (Ma.) ar (obl. &t-) water; (W. Ph.) etk thirst (Voc. 391); (Tr) af-yar, (SR.) atta yér boiling water (Voc, 27); (Mu.) berel, berer (pl. berehk), (S.) bered (obl. beret-, pl. berehgu) river; (Ma.) beriag, berey flood, river in flood (Voc. 2602); (LuS.) beriagai a river. Konda ér(u) (obl ER-) water, ERki thirst. Pe. &% water, in: & iba to bathe, otherwise used in the pl. azuy; &s (variant form of éz used in certain combinations, e.g. és orli otter flit. water-rat]); ‘éski thirst. Mand. ey water; ehki thirst. Kui (K.) Gu water; (W.) su id. (compositional form); éski thirst. Kuwi (f.) éyu, (Su. P.) éyu (construed with pl. verb), (5. Isr.) eyu water; (Su. Isr.) éski thirst. DED(S, N) 4233.

Ta. yan, nan (obi en-) I; yanmai egoism; enavan one who is mine; enava mine (neut. pl). Ma. fian (obi. en-) I. Ko. aen (obl en-; e- with some relationship terms). To. on (obl en-). Ka. an (obl. en-), nan (obl nan-). Kod, na-ni, nae (obi. en-, nan-, na+-). Tu, yanu, yénu (obl. ena-, en-); (L. V- R. Aiyar, p. 403; Bright and Ramanujan) yany (non-brahmin), &ny (brahmin). Te. énu, nénu (obl nan-, na-). Kol, a-n (obl. an-). Vk. an (obl, an-). Pa. an (Obl. an-). Ga. (Oll. 8.) an (obl, an-). Go, (Tr.) ana, (emph.) ‘anna, (W. L.) nanna, (M. L.) nand (obi. na-), (SR. Y. Ma.) nana (Voc. 1923). Konda nan(u) (obl na-, acc.-dat. nani). Pe. an/anen (gen. na, acc.-dat. nangen). Mand. an (gen, nd, acc.-dat. nay). Kui anu, (K.) nanu (obl. for both na-). Kuwi (F.) nang, (S. Su. P. Isr.) ninu (ob na-). Kur. &n (obl. eng-). Malt. én (obl eng-). Br. i (Obl kan-), Cf. $154 Ta. yam. DED(S) 4234. §161 Ta. yinai, anai elephant. Ma. na. Ko. aen. To. arn. Ka. ane, (K.* also) yane. Kod. a-ne. Tu, ané. Te. @nfigu, énika, (B.) ranja [469] Eniga, Enige, Enuga. Kol. (SR.) enagi, (Kin.) éngi. Nk. énagi. Pa. Enu (pl. -1). Ga. (Olli. S.) énig. Go. (Tr. Ph.) yéni, (W.) aini, (A.) Enal, (S.) @ni, (M. Ko.) én (Voc. 384). Konda &ngu (n, not q), (BB) ni. DED (S) 4235. R,R,R 5162 Kui ranja a small bough for supporting climbing plants, a large peastick. Kuwi (S.) ranja branch. DEDS 825.
Ta. iravikkai, ravikkai tight-fitting bodice, jacket, corset. Ka. ravake, ravike bodice, quilted jacket, armour. Tu. ravaké bodice. Te. ravika a sort of bodice. Cf. 318 Ta, aruvai. DED 4238.

Ta. irima-muziyan silvery seafish, Triacanthus strigilifer. Te. ramalu (pl.) a kind of fish. DED 4240. 5167 Konda ripa (pl. ripek) grown-up girl. Pe. Jipoli (ol. Jipok) young woman. Mand. jipar (p/. jipahin) grown-up girl. DEDS 835.

Ta. irappai, rappai, reppai eyelid. Ka. reppe, sappe id. Kod. (Shanmugam) reppe id., hair of eyelashes. Tu. reppé eyelid, eyelash; rampé eyelid. Te. reppa, reppa id. Kol. (Br.) kand-repa id. Nk. kandle reppa eyelash. Go. (Ma.) kand reppa eyebrow (Voce. 3047(a)). Konda fepa eyelid; yema eyelash. Kuwi (V.) reppa id. Cf. 528 Ta. irai. DED(S) 4242.

Ka. rembe, rambe twig, small bough, branch. Te. remma, rebba branchlet, twig. ? Konda riva twig. DEDS 829. S171 Ka. relfu a reed used to write with, Saccharum sara. Te. fol, rellu a treed, S. spontaneum, Kuwi (S.) relli tuppa bulrust (see 3322). DED 4244. PP us 5172 Kui reha pleasure, joy, gladness, desire, interest; reha Ava to be joyful, wish. Kuwi (S.) rah- to be glad; raha joy; rahagatti joyful; (Isr.) raha pleasure; raha ki- to make merry. DEDS 830. :
Te. réku petal. Kol. rekka teaf. Kuwi “(Su.) réku, (Isr.) reku leaves of paddy plant. DED(S) 4245.

Te. rompa cold, catarrh. Konda ropa id. Kuwi (f.) rumpa cold (in the nose). 5175 Pe, rohinq day before yesterday. Kui rodsi, rosi day before yesterday, the other day, some days ago. Kuwi (Su.) ro’ni, (F.) ro’ni, (S.) rd’oni, (Isr.) ro?oni, (T.) ro’la day before yesterday. DEDS(N) 831. 5176 Kui rohpa (roht-) to put, place, put by, store. Kuwi (Mah.) ros- to put; (F.) rossali (rdst-) to put in; (S.) roh’- to put; roh’nai, rdospinai to preserve; {Isr.} roh- (-t-) to put into (a tin, cupboard, etc.). DEDS 832. 5177 Go, (Tr.) rds4na to lay flat things one on top of another (Voc. 3080). Konda ros- Ct) to arrange (as stones in construction), pile up, get things packed (for travel); (BB) to fashion, arrange, prepare, make. Pe. rdh- (rdst-) to make, construct, build. Mand. rth- to build (house), make (bread). Kui ronja (Onji-) to be level, smooth, well ordered, properly formed, prosperous; pl action roska (réski-); rdspa (rdst-) to make level or smooth, fashion properly, give form to; n. levelling, fashioning. DEDS 833.

Ta. lapd-lapov-enal expr. of beating the open mouth with the palm of the hand on account of sorrow. Ka. laba, labo, laba laba sound in imitation of that produced by beating oneself on the mouth. Tu. labalaba noise made by crying and beating the mouth; crying, weeping. Te. labalaba beating oneself on the mouth. DED 4249.

Pe. lekor déw. Mang. lekur id. Kui liheri id. Kuwi (S.) leuri id., snow. DEDS 842. 5192 Kol. levni river (informants; ‘in Amraoti they say nevii’), Go. lavni (A.) big river, (Y.) streamlet; (SR.) lavdi stream (Voc. 3099): (ASu.) lavri, lavni stream, streamiet. DED 4250. §193 Ka. tesu goodness, excellence, superi- ority. Tu. lésy an auspicious ceremony; excellent, good; (B-K.) goodness, excellence.

Ta. lotalota (-pp-, -tt-) to be hollow or empty, become useless; lofaloftai anything hollow or empty, anything useless; lofukku emptiness. hollowness. Ma. lofta empty, vapid. Ka. loftte emptiness, hollowness; loddu, laddu pithlessness, weakness. Te. log({)a hollow, dried up, hollowness; lofa- ramu empty, useless, pithless. DED 4251.

Ta. lofa-lotav-enal onom. expr. signi- fying rattling. Ka. lota, loda sound to imitate that of a stone being shaken in an unsound pot, of a cracked bell being rung, of the wooden bell on a cow’s neck. Kod. lot, lotto- noise of wooden cattle-bell. Te. lofa- lota sound of a broken pot when struck. DED 4252.
Ka. lotte cluck with the tongue in driving cattle. Te. lotfa smack or cluck with the tongue; loffa-véyu to click or smack the tongue against the palate. DED 4253.
Ka. lotta a hollow, ravine, pit. Te. lotta dent, depression, small pit or hollow; loddi a valley, Go. (ASu.) loddi id.

Ka. fol, lot, lol tol, lol lol imitation of the sound of a dog’s barking. Te. (K. loltu, lollu to sound, as dog’s barking. DED 4254. 5199 Kui lumba (lumbi-), (K.) dumb- to be extinguished; luppa (lupt-) to extinguish. Kuwi (Su. P., Isr.) dumb- (it-), (I'.) dtimbali, (Mah.) domb- to be extinguished; dup- (Su. -h-, P. Isr. -t-), (F.) duph- to extinguish. DEDS 847.

Ta. vaku (-pp-, -tt-) to separate, divide, apportion, distribute; (-v-, -nt-) to split (intr.); vakuti division, class, category; vakuppu dividing, classifying, section, division, class; vakitu parting in woman’s hair; vakai (-v-, -nt-) to be divided, divide, cut, arrange a subject; nm. division, branch, section, class, caste, manner, method, way, means, nature. quality; vakir (-v-, -nt-) to slice, cut in slips, split, cleave, tear open, divide; n. tearing, scratch, slice; vaci (-v-, -nt-) to split, cut. Ma. vakukka to divide; vakuppu kind, sort, section, paragraph; vaka division, kind, item, means, property, stock; vakayuka to divide, compose; vakaccal distribution, composition of a work; vakayikka to accomplish a business. Ka. bagaru to scratch with the nails or claws (or with 5322 Ta. varantu); bagi to separate, disunite, divide, make pieces, cleave, tear, lacerate, scratch, dig, scrape (bottom of pot with spoon); bage division, portion, part, section, sort, vanity, class, caste, bay-tale parting of the hair. Kod. bavtale id. Tu. bagé kind, manner, way, species, means, resource, income; bakala gash, deep incision; baktalé, bagytalé parting of the hair by vakuli [471] vafa combing, combing the hair into curls, crown of the head, chaplet. Te. vaga manner, mode, means; trick, pretence, dissimulation. Kui vaka variety, sort, kind. Kuwi (S.) waga 4nai to simulate; wageli dnai to pretend, simulate; (F.) bakrali, (Isr.) bark- (-it-), brak- (-h-) to claw (or with 5322 Ta. varantu;
Ta. vakuli a fish. Ka. baggade a kind of fish. / Cf. Mar. vagti id. DEDS 850.

Ta. vakuli, vakuni sound. To. parg ary- to know how to talk (or with 4031 Ta. parai). Ka. bagul(u), bagalu, bogalu, bogulu to bark, cry out; bagalu, bogulu, bollu bark- ing, crying out; baggu, bargu, baggane the crying, cooing, chirping, chattering, or sing- ing of birds; baggisu to cry, coo, chirp, chatter, sing, cry out, vociferate. Tu. bagaluni, baguluni, baguluni to bark, clamour, vocifer- ate, rave, talk irrationally; bagalu raving, wandering in mind, talking irrationally; bagulafa barking, clamouring. Cf. 5337 Ta. vanku. DED(S) 4256. .

Ta. vakai (-v-, -nt-) to consider, weigh; vai (-pp-, -tt-) to consider, meditate upon, suppose; vaittu-kkol to suppose, take for granted. Ka. bage to think, consider, suppose, conjecture, know; #. thought, concern, regard, notion, idea, intention, purpose, the mind; bagye, bagge the very thought, intention, purpose; on account of, with regard to; (PBh.) pul-vage a mean or low thought (see 4301). Tu. bageyuni, bage- vuni to think, conceive, imagine. Te. vagacu to think, ponder; grieve, sorrow; vaga, vagapu (K. also) vagalu grief, sorrow, regret; (K.) vagiyu to pretend sorrow; vagadencu to decide, settle, determine; vagadegu to be finally settled or determined. DED 4257.
Ta. vakka white stork, Ardea nivea; vanka a bird. Te. vakku crane. / Cf. Skt. baka-; Turner, CDIAL, no. 9115. DED 4258.

Ta. vakku (vakki-) to burn, singe, roast; n, being singed or burnt, being roasted. Ma, vakkuka to singe, burn slightly; vatinhuka to be singed. ? Go. (Ma.) varng- to be burnt, charred, scorched; caus, vars- to scorch, burn, brand; (Tr.) warsana to brand, blister; (Ph.) varsind to burn, brand; (SR.) versanad to blister (Voc. 3189). DED(N) 4259. 5§208(a) Ma. vakkal side; vakku brim, edge..Te. vattka direction, side, quarter. (b) Ta. vikku side, direction. Te. vika side, quarter. DEDS 851.

Ta. vakku-nar woolly ordure tree, Sterculia villosa; hemp fibre; (DCV) elephant rope tree [i.e.S. v.]. Ma. vakka a large elephant rope; (DCV) elephant rope tree; vakku hemp for nets, sackcloth, etc. Ka. (DCV) bakka elephant rope tree. Tu. (DCV) bakku id. DEDS 852.

Ta. vanki a kind of armlet; a kind of iron hook or curved instrument. Ka. vanki, onki hook; gold armlet of a curved shape. Tu. oggi, uggi handle, hook; (B-K.) onki, vaniki a bracelet worn on the arms. Te. vanki curved ornament worn by women on the upper arm; ofikiya, oftke hook or peg fixed in a wall. Go. (LuS.) vakonjee an elephant goad. DED(N) 4260.
Ma. vahki a certain dagger. Ka. vatiki a sort of knife or sword; vafkudi, bankudi dagger. Te. vaiki, vafkini id. DED 4261.

Ta. vatku orifice, hole, hollow as in a stone, rat-hole, snake-hole, cave, cavern, hollow in a tree. Ma. vahku hole of rats, snakes, etc., cave. Ju. oftka hole of rats, snakes, etc. DED 4262. 5213 Ta..vacam, vacampu, payampu sweet flag, Acorus calamus. Ma, vayampu id.; orris root. Ka. baji, baje, vace, vaje A. calamus. Tu. bajé id. Te. vaca, vasa id. /Cf. Skt. vacad- a kind of aromatic root (according to some = A. calamus); Turner, CDIAL, no. 11201. DED(S) 4263.

Ta. vaci rain, water. Ka. basi, bose to drip, drop, trickle, ooze, flow; pour off water from boiled rice, etc., by inclining the vessel, strain; basu oozing. Tu. basabasa gushing, flowing in a stream; bassa over- flowing. DED 4264.

Ka. (Hav.) baccu to be tired; baccelu tiredness; (HavS., p. 159) basvali to be fatigued. Tu. baccuni to be wearied, tired, fatigued; bacca4vuni to tire, fatigue; baccely fatigue, weariness, tiredness; baccargely tiredness, fatigue. Pe. vah- (vast-) to be or become tired. Kui vaha (vahi-) to be tired, exhausted, tire, faint; n. weariness, exhaus- tion. Kuwi (F.) vwahali, (S.) wahinai to be tired; (Su. Isr.) vah- (it-) id., become tired; (Isr.) vahu weariness. Cf. 5293 Ta. vagalikkai, DED(S) 4367 and from DED 4355(@).

Ta. vaiici common rattan of south India, Calamus rotang (and some other similar plants). Ma. vafici, vaitfii bamboo, reed. /Cf. Skt. vafijula- C. rotang; Turner, CDIAL, no. 11210. DED 4265. 5217 Ta, vaficiram a sea-fish, bluish, attaining 6 feet in length, Cybium guttalum, seer, bluish, attaining 3 feet in length, C. interruptum. Te. vanjaramu, vanju coal- fish, seer-fish. DED 4266.

Ta. vata northern; vatakku north, north point of the compass; vafantai that which is in the north, north wind; vafavar northerners; vatatu that which is in the north, north; vatuku the region immedi- ately north of the Tamil country, the Telugu country, the Telugu language; vatukar people of the Telugu country, a caste of Telugu immigrants; vafai north wind, cold wind, wind. Ma, vata north; vatakku id., in or towards the north; vafakkan northern; vata wind. Ka. bada, badaga, badagal, badagu vafa [472] bagr the north, in the north. Kod. badaki north. Tu. badakayi the north, northern. Te. vadakfigonda Himalaya. DED 4267.

Ta. vatam cable, large rope, cord, bowstring, strands of a garland, chains of a necklace; vafi rope; vafti (-pp-, -tt-) to tic. Ma. vatam rope, a rope of cowhide (in plough), dancing rope, thick rope for dragging timber, Ka. vata, vatara, vafi string, rope, tie. Te. vati rope, cord. Go, (Mu.) vatiya strong rope made of paddy straw (Voc. 3150). Cf.

Ta. vati (-v-, -nt-) to drip, trickle, (tide) ebbs; (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to flow out, shed (as tears), drain, strain, filter; n. fil- tration, distillation; vatical straining, filter- ing, rice boiled and strained, ebbing; vatippu straining, filtering; vatiyal id., strained or filtered liquid; vativu that which is strained or filtered, outflow, surplus water. Ma. vatiyuka to overflow, ebb, trickle; vati strainer, filter; vativu a current. To. warf- (wart-) (blood) flows (or with 1010 Ta. oguku). Ka. odi to flow in a small, gentle stream, trickle through, ooze. run and cause blots (as ink); odi-gattu to strain off, filter; (PBh.) badapam continuous rain. Tu. odduni to flow, run; odda flowing; odpavuni to drain; oddavuni to turn flowing water into a field. Te. vadiyu, (K. also) odiyu to be strained or filtered, percolate; vaducu, (K. also) vadacu to let fall in drops, pour slowly; vb.n, vadupu; vadi-gattu, vadiyd-gaftu to strain, filter. Kol. vadp- (vadapt-) to pour. Konda vadis- (-t-) to pour down, serve (of liquid food). Cf. 5296 Ta. vari. DED(S) 4269.

Ta. vati (-v-, -nt-) to be diminished, dry up (as water in a river); vatical decreasing. Ma. vatu thin, poor; vatiyuka to dry up. Ko. varv poverty; varvn poor man; barva+ meyn, barvn meyn, varvn meyn son of a pauper (abusive). To. podfin mox son of a pauper (in songs). Ka. bada, badavu poorness, weak- ness, feebleness, thinness, leanness, humble- ness; poor, etc.; badava a poor, weak man; badaka a lean, thin, feeble male; badakalu state of being weak, thin, etc.; badatana thinness, poverty; badaha thinness, leanness, fineness; banagu a wretch, poor person, worthless person. Kod. bada poor; badavé poor man; fem. badavati. Tu. bada poor, indigent, lean, thin; badave, baduve a poor man, pauper; badé thin, lean; badavu hunger. Te. badugu corpse, a weak creature; lean, thin, slender, skinny; vadalu to fade, wither; (K..) vadiyu to dry up, become tean; vatincu to starve. Go. (Tr.) wadéktana, (Ph.) vadek- tana, (A.) & vatk- to be thirsty; (Y.) vatka vas- thirst to be felt; (Hislop) wutkee thirst (Voce. 3158). Cf. 5342 Ta. vatu. DED(S) 4270.

Ta. vati (-v-, -nt-) to become beauti- ful; (-pp-, -tt-) to refine, perfect; 7. form, shape; vafippam elegance, shapeliness, beauty; vativam form, shape, figure, beauty, comeli- ness, complexion, colour; vativu form, shape, body, beauty, fair complexion, brightness, lustre. Ma. vativu form, size, manner, figure, beauty. DED 4271. §224 Ta. vati small cane or stick; vatippu iron rod. Ma. vati stick, staff, club of armed brahmans, shaft, stroke; vatikka to strike; vatippikka to have the measure struck. Ka. badi, bade, bodi, bode to beat, strike, thrash, bang, pound; ”, beating, blow, castration, a short thick stick, cudgel; badike beating; badige stick, staff, cudgel, hammer, mallet; badisu to cause to beat; badukatana beating, etc.; bay badi to prevent one from speaking, silence ‘one. Kog. badi- (badip-, badic-) to hammer, pound; basy badi- to bawl out. Tu. badipuni, badiyunt to strike, beat, thrash; badu stick, cudgel. Te. badita, badiya, bade thick stick, cudgel. Kol, bedta club (< Te.; Kamaleswaran); (Br.) badya walking stick. Pa. bariya stick. Go. (A. Y.) badde, (SR.) baddi, badga, (G. Mu. Ma.) barga, (Ch.) badga, bariya, (W.) wariyd stick (Voc. 2510). Konda badiga big walking stick; (BB) badga stick. Pe. badge stick, staff. Mand. badga stick. Kuwi (Su. Isr.) barga, (Ist.) bariya id.; (F.) burga stick, club; (S.) badga walking stick. {Cf Mar. badga cudgel, stick; badviné to bruise, beat. DED(S, N) 4272.
Ka. vadi, odi heat. Te. vada, vada- gali the hot or land wind, heat. DED 4273.
Te. vadi quickness, rapidity, speed, briskness, valour, violence; vadikadu a quick man, a brave man; vadigala quick, rapid, brisk, brave, spirited. Kol vadi, vadi vadi quickly. DED 4274.

Ta. vatu wart, mole, scar, wale, mouth of an ulcer or wound, fault, defect, injury, calamity; vatimpu mark, scar; vafuku a flaw in a gem. Ma. vatu mark of stripe, scar, weal, wart, mole, freckle. DED(S) 4275. 5228 Kol. (SR.) vade slices. Go. (Mu.) yarka, (M.) varga division, part; (L.) vadga half, portion (Voc. 3203). (Kamaleswaran.) DEN 75.

Ko. barre barrn onom. of defecating with diarrhoea, or of a falling tree. Ka. badabada, va{avata sound to imitate that of gabbling, jabbering, prating. Tu. badabada babbling, garrulity; noise of a drum. Te. (K.) badabada (to talk) abruptly, noisily. Kur vatti 1473] vanta barbayrna to talk loudly, chatter noisily; baranbarangrna, berengbereygma to talk endlessly, talk together noisily. Malt. bar- barre to prattle. / MBE 1969, p. 296, no. 39, for areal etymology, with reference to Turner, CDIAL, no. 9122, *badabada-. DED(S) 4276.

Ta. vatfi basket made of palm-stem fibre, a measure of capacity; vaftikai basket; vattil id., measure of capacity, quiver for arrows, Ma. vatti round basket of grass, straw, leather, or palm-leaves. Kod: batti basket. Tu. batti rattan basket. DED 4277.
Ma. vatti belly; rupture. Ka. batti rupture, hernia; (Coorg) bafti big intestine. Kod. batti stomach (internal organ). Tu. patti tupture. Cf. 3898 Ta. panti. DED(S)

Ka. bata, batfa, battu bareness, voidness. Tu. bat& open, wide. Te. vatti empty, vacant, void, blank, mere, simple, vain, useless, bare, naked, false, untrue, groundless. Pa. batfi bare ground. Cf. 5477 Ta. vefti and 5513 Ta. veru. DED 4355(8). 5234 Ta, vattuvam pouch in which betel leaves, nuts, chunam, tobacco, etc., are kept, medicine pouch, pocket inside a pouch or purse. Ma, vattuvam betel-purse, portemon-
Ta. vattai mountain tamana-oil tree, Hydnocarpus alpina; marote, H. Wightiana. Ma. vatta a common gum tree; (Lush.) #. Wightiana. DED 4280.

Ta. vanafku (vananki-) to bend, yield, be submissive; worship, ‘salute respect- fully; vapakku (vanakki-) to bend (¢r.), make flexible (as the body), make submsisive; vanakkam, vanakku bending, worship, sub- mission; vanar (-v-, -nt-) to bend (éntr.), curt (as the hair); 1, vault; vanai (-v-, -nt-; -pp-, -tt-) to bend (intr.). Ma. vanafinhuka to bend, bow, salute respectfully; vanakkam obeisance, reverence; vanakkuka to bend (¢r.); vagaftihuka to follow suit, yield, bend, ask humbly; vazakkam submission, obedience; talavarak- kam bowing the head. Cf. 5314 Ta. valai. DED 4281. :

Ta. vantal dregs, lees, sediment, silt, mud, mire, slush, earth washed ashore by a river, lake, etc., alluvial soil; vantalam slush; vanfti sediment, dregs, tees. Ma. vantaru dirt, filth; vantikkaran. cleaner of vessels in a temple. Ka. vandu, ondu, bandalu sediment, deposit, lees, dregs, muddy deposit of a flood, river, or tank, muddiness, turbidness. Te. vanda, vandali, vandu muddy deposit of a river, tank or the like, alluvium, alluvial soil. Cf. 4676 Ta. manti. /Cf. Pkt. (DNM) vendha-suri- muddy liquor. DED(S) 4282.

Ta. vanfinam pelican ibis; vapfara- kuruku a kind of heron. Ma. van{arah-kori Indian crane. DEDS 854. 5239 Ta, vantu bee, a kind of bee; (lex.) vatu beetle. Ma. vantu a black bee, wasp, beetle. To. pod a kind of large fly. Ka. bandu honey, pollen of flowers; band-uni the large black bee. DED(S) 4283.

Go. (W.) vandina, (Ph.) vandana to taste (Voc. 3163). Pe. vend- (-t-) id. Kui vanda (vandi-) id.; . act of tasting; pl. action vatka (vatki-). Kuwi (F.) vwandali, (S.) wandinai, (T. Isr.) vand- (-it-) to taste. DED (S) 4284. $241 Ta. vannan, vannattan, vannattin washerman; fem. vanpatti. Ma. vannattan, vannan washerman; fem. vannatti. Cf. 4684 Ta. mannu. DED 4285.

Ta. vatakkam fading, withering, fatigue, lassitude; vatakku (vatakki-) to roast, dry, harass, annoy; vatafikal that which is withered or dried; vatafiku (vatanki-) to wither, fade, grow dry; be discouraged, dispirited, fatigued; vataval that which is. faded or dried. Ma. vatahhuka to wither; vatakkam withering. To. po@x fallen leaves. Tu. badefkuni to dangle, as a weak limb. DED 4286.

Te. vadalu to leave or leave off, abandon, relinquish, loosen, slacken, let loose; become loose or slack, disappear, go away; adj. loose, slack; vadalincu, vadal(u)cu to loosen, slacken, untie, separate; (K.) udalu, udulu to leave off (grip, hold); udalcu to release oneself from another’s hold. Kur. odoxna (odxas) to render less tight, detach, get rid of. DED 4287. $244 Ta. vatayu (vatayi-) to chatter, prate, be talkative, lisp, abuse. Ka. odaru to sound, cry aloud, shout, shrick, howl; caus. odarisu; odaruvike sounding, crying aloud. Tu. badari- tana defamation. Te. vadaru, (K. also) vaduru to prattle, prate, babble, chatter, jabber; vadariibdtu prattler, babbler; (K.) odaru to prattle, prate, abuse. DED 4288.
Ta. vati mire. Ka. badi mud, mire, slush, ooze. DED 4289.
Ta. vati (-v-, -nt-) to dwell, abide, sojourn; sleep; . lair, nest. Ka. (Gowda) badi a deity’s place, small building. Tw. (B-K.) badi roofed shelter (temple) con- structed for a devil.
Pa. vangel, (S.) vatgel calf of leg. ?Ga. (Oll.) vaggul knee. ? Ma. vanpa calf of leg (or with 4686 Ta. mannai). DEDS 856.

Te. vanta rivulet; vranti river, pit. Kol. (SR.) vanta river; (Kin.) vanta nuligh. Nk, vanta river, stream. Cf. 5296 Ta. vari and $356 Ta. var. DED 4291. vanna [474] varaku 5251 Kol. vanna brother's wife. Vk, vanna elder brother’s wife. ? Konda oni elder brother's wife. maternal uncle’s daughter (older ‘than person concerned). 7? Pe oni elder brother’s wife. /? <IA (Pkt. [DNA] yahunni- husband’s elder brother’s wife); Krishnamurti, Language 39.564, DED(S) 4292. :

Ta. vampu_ instability, uselessness, worthlessness, idle talk, gossip, scandal, evil word, falsity, base conduct, indecent lan- guage, deceit, quarrel; vampan worthless person, gossip-monger, mischievous, wanton person, illegitimate son; fem. vampi. Tu. bambé flattery, false praise, prattling; bam- mana slander, backbiting; bamana back- biting; bamanipuni to backbite. Te. vammu destruction, ruin; useless, vain; vammu-vovu to become useless, wasted, be lost. / 2? Cf. Pali vambheti to despise, revile, scorn. DED (S) 4293.

Ta. vampu curved bamboo pole of a palanquin; pampu-mifkil a variety of bamboo. Ma. vampu a bamboo, chiefly as a measure of palm-wine. Ka. bambu, bombu a large, hollow bamboo. Tu. bambu bamboo. / Cf. Skt. lex.) vambha- bamboo; Mar. babti id. DED(S) 4294. $254 Ta. vaya strength, power; Increase, abundance; vayam power, might, victory, conquest; vayavan strong man, valiant man; yayavu strength. Ma, (DCV) vayam strength. Kod. baymbé hero. DEDS 857.
Ta. vayakku (vayakki-) to tame, break in; improve (as land). Ma, vayakkuka to bring into use, clear jungle. Cf. §258 Ta. vayal, and Kol. va+y-, Nk. vay- S.v. 5549 Ta. vai. DED 4295. :

Ta. vayanam, vayanam manner, method, way, circumstance, condition, clear details, particulars, sumptuousness, good agreeable condition, neatness, favourableness, suitability, reason, cause. Ka. vayina @ way, means, contrivance. Tu. vayana manner, circumstance, way, art of doing a thing, device, scheme, expedient, artifice, dodge. Te. vayinamu details, particulars, clearness, plainness, system, method, order; (K., inscr.) vayanam details. /? < Pkt. vayana- that is said, narrated (< Skt. vacana-); Krishnamurti, Language 39.564. DED(S) 4296.

Ta. vayam desire, vayavu, vaya desire, great desire, love, affection, morbid longings of pregnant women; vayavu (vayavi-) to desire.
