Kui vlénda (vl@ndi-), (Gramm.) vlénda (vléndi-) to rise, expand, swell, distend; pl. action vilétka (vletki-); vipa (vlét-) to swell, distend; viépka (< vlék-p-; vltkt-) to cause to expand, stretch, widen out. Kuwi (Su.) yay- (4t-) to be cracked (stone); (Isr.) rdy- (-It-) to crack, (boil) to burst; (F.) raiyali, (S.) lainai to, burst. Kur. belbelrna to be gaping (of a wound), open to the quick, be swollen and suppurating. DED(S) 4459.

Ta. vil (vilv-, vint-) to say, tell, reveal, make known; vilampu (vilampi-) to speak, say, proclaim openly, make public, reveal; n. word, speech; vilamparam advertisement, proclamation, notice, publication; vilappu saying; vilari the sixth note of the gamut; vili (-v-, -nt-) to say, speak; (-pp-, -tt-) id., sum- mon, sing, roar, shout; 7. sound, song, word, speech, shout of excitement, frenzy, or joy, call; vilippu sound, calling, shouting; vilivu warriors’ shout, war-cry; vilar (vilari-) to call. Ma. vilampuka to divulge; vilamparam pro- clamation, publication; vili call, cry, sum- mons; vilikka to call, invite. Kurubh. (LSB 1.12) belki speech. To. pity- (pifc-) to utter long, high shout of joy or triumph; n. ex- pression of joy. Tu. bulpuni, bul(i)puni to ery aloud, shout, exclaim; bulu, bulpu cry, lamentation, noise of any animal or bird, clamour; bulkaduni to cry as from fear or being possessed of a demon; bulkady crying passionately, bawling. Kor. (T.) burpi to cry. Te. vilambaramu notification, publication. Cf. 4195 Ta. piliru. DED(N) 4460.

Ta. vilampu (vilampi-) to serve food; ‘2 vigampu boiled rice. Ma. vilampuka to distribute food, serve out; vilampan one who superintends the distribution, esp. in victual- ling houses; vilampikka, vilappikka to ask for more food. DED 4461.
Ta. vilakam battlefield, surrounding area. Ma. vilikam battlefield, garden. DED 4462.

Ta. vilimpu border, edge, rim, brink, margin, eyelid; vatimpu border, edge (as of a garment), blade (as of a knife), extremity (as of the foot), eaves, edge of a roof. Ma. vilim- pu, vilumpu, velumpu edge, margin, border or hem of a cloth, eyelid; veluti margin. DED 4463.

Ta. vilai (-v-, -nt-) to be produced, be productive, result, mature, ripen (as grain), occur; (-pp-, -tt-) to raise, cause to grow, produce, bring into being; n. growth, ripening, produce, crop, yield; vilaiccal produce, crop, yield, ripening grain, growth, tipening; vilaiyul produce, crop, maturing; vilaivi (-pp-, -tt-) to cause to grow, cultivate; vilaiva growth, ripening, maturity, produce, crop, yield; vilar fertility. Ma. vila vegetation, crop of corn growing; vilayuka to grow, grow ripe, grow richly or to perfection, produce; vilayikka to cause to grow, cultivate; vilaccal produce of corn, etc., corm grown ripe; vilavu id., perfection. Ko. yelv- (veld-) to grow, become fat, (grain) ripens; (velt-) to cause to grow; vilaiy-atu vel crop. To. pel- (pel@-) (food plants) grow, (persons) grow fat; (pelt-) to grow (fr); pel, pet crop. Ka. bele to grow as corn, grass, or trees, increase; bear a crop; m. growing, growth, produce, crop, standing corn, what is to grow, seed, seedlings; belayisu, belasu, beleyisu, belesu to cause to grow, increase, raise (crop), bring to maturity, rear, foster; beleyuvike growing; belasu growth, increase, crop of standing corn. Kod. bole- (bolev-, boland-) to become full-grown (people, animals, fruit); bolat- (bolati-) to grow (¢.; plants); bole crop. Tu. bulé, bulé standing, growing corn, crop; buleti, buleti full-grown, ripe; bulepini, bulepini, bulepuni, buleyuni, bulevuni, bolepuni to grow, ripen, be ripe; bulepuduni, bulepdvuni to make grow, r

Ta. vira (-pp-, -nt-) to be dense, close, be intense, abound, increase, conquer; virappu crowdedness, density, intensity, in- crease, strength, victory; viral . victory, strength; virai (-v-, -nt-) to shiver (as from cold); (-pp-, -tt-) to grow stiff as from cold, become numb, shiver as from cold; viraippu numbness, stiffness, shivering; veri (-pp-, -tt-) to stand stiff, stand on end as hair; mirait Gpp-, -tt-) to become stiff, as a limb. Ma. virakka to become stiff as from cold; viranna inflexible; virakkam repugnance; vira tremor; virekka to shiver, tremble. Ko. verv- (vert-) to become numb from exposure, insensate. To. per- (pert-) to feel cold. Ka. birasa, birasu, birisu, birusu, biru hardness, firmness, rough- ness, rudeness, vehemence, swiftness; biri firmness, etc., tightness, fastening as a door; biru to become stiff; birubu, biruvu, biruhu hardness, firmness, vehemence, etc.; bere to become firm or stiff as from cold, wind, rain, death, etc. Tu. bir-gali tempest; birsy hard, stiff, rough. Te. birusu, birusu hard, stiff; hardness, stiffness; birra tightness; birru id., tension, hardness, stiffness, strict- ness, sternness, inflexibility; tight, hard, stiff, stern, inflexible; (K. also) vb, to become stiff (as hair); virugu, virugu to curdle. Kol. (Pat., p. 115) beriv solid; (p. 135) bedir ereng to be numb. DED(S, N) 4466.

Ta. viraku firewood, fuel, sacrificial fuel; viray fuel, firewood. Ma. viruku fire- wood. Ko. verg id. To. (Metz, Madras Journal of Literature and Science, 17. 141) berkh id. (probably perx). Tu. bejakiré dried leaves used as fuel; birifiji slender piece of firewood. Kor. (O. T.; LSB 12.8) biji firewood. Go. (G.) verrki, (Mu.) vark, (Ma.) vahk, vehki, [494] vi (Ko.) verk fuel, stick of firewood (Voc. 3288). Konda vergu (pl. veRku) firewood, fuel. Pe, vezgu (pl. vesku) id. Mand. viyke (pl) id. Kui veju, vejgu (pl. veska) id. Kuwi (F.) vegitt @L veska), (Su. Isr.) vegu (pl. veska), (P.) vergu (pi. verka) id.; (S.) weggu, weska dry wood. DED(S, N) 4467. $441 Ta. viricu rocket. Kn, birasu, birisu, birusu. whirring, what whirs, a rocket, cracker, Tu. birsy a kind of firework. Te. birusti-grdvi a kind of firework; birusulu a we firework called flower-pots. DED(S)
Te. virugu to be counteracted; virucu to counteract; virupu counteraction, dis- enchantment. Malt. beje to cure by repeating spells. DED 4469.

Ta. virai (-pp-, -tt-) to become be- wildered; viraippu bewilderment; virumi (-pp-, -tt-) to be dazed or bewildered; virumai bewilderment. Ko. verk state of being out of one’s normal mind, esp. because of Kurumba magic. Ka. biru to be astounded; beragu amazement, astonishment, alarm, wandering or confusion of mind. Tu. beragy wonder, amazement; beraguni to be amazed, aston- ished. Te. verdgu, verigu surprise, astonish- ment, amazement, wonder; verdgu-padu, verégu-padu to be surprised or astonished; verdgu-paracu, verigu-paracu to surprise, astonish, amaze. Kui brX confused, con- founded, amazed, bewildered; bra inba to be confused, etc. Cf. 5465 Ko. vekar-- DED 70. ,

Ta. vinai action, deed, work, karma, evil deed; vinaifiar workers, artisans, artificers, agriculturists, smiths; vinainar, vinaivar work- ers; vinaimai nature of deed, property of functioning; vinaiyam action, deed, karma. Ma. vina action, exertion, sin; mina work, evil work. Tu. benpini to labour, work, cultivate; binnana work, labour; benni, benné land under cultivation, agriculture. DED(S) 4473.

Ta. vi (-v-, -nt-) to perish, cease, disappear, die, leave, deviate as from one’s course; (-pp-, -tt-) to destroy; 7. ruin, destruc- tion, death, drooping, languishing, separation, removal; vikku (vikki-) to kill, destroy, ruin; vivu ruin, destruction, death, eradication, vikku [495] vicu removal, end, defect, flaw, interruption, interval; vital death. Ka. bi to end, cease, fail, fall away or off, fade, perish, die; bikal ending, etc.; bigu to go backwards, recede, retreat; bisara end, destruction. Te. vigu to run away, move, step aside, (K. also) end, disappear; vika flight, retreat; visara de- ficiency; visara-padu, visara-povu to be deficient, fail, be fruitless; visaramu useless, vain, fruitless ?Go. (Mu.) vic tomb, grave; (Elwin) wich a dead man; wich bati disposal place for the dead (Voc, 3263). DED(S) 4474,

Ta. vikku (vikki-) to strike. Ma. vikkuka to flog, hammer; vikku blow. DED 4475. 5448(a) Ta. vittku (vitki-) to increase in size, become enlarged, swell, be inflamed and swollen, grow, increase, be copious, excessive, be close, crowded, become tight, be tight; vitkal abundance; vikku (vikki-) to fill, urge, force out; tie up, bind, control, restrain, hinder; 7, greatness, abundance; tying, tight- ness; vikkam enlargement, swelling, inflam- mation, contusion, dropsy, puffing of limbs, abundance, crowd, greatness, pride; bond, tie, trouble, obstacle, covering, packing, tightness; vifiku (vifki-) to be abundant. Ma. vitviuka to swell, grow large, big; vikkam swelling, abscess, dropsy, throbbing of a wound or tumour, being puffed up with pride, anger; virkuka to swell, be inflated, arrogant; caus. virpikka. ? Ko. vic- (vic-) to talk impudently to a superior. Ka. bigu to become large, stout, bulky, or big, swell, behave proudly, be elated, rejoice. Te. vigu to erect, protrude; endeavour, (K. also) be swollen with pride, be vain; vfka valour, courage, enthusiasm, pride; viku courage, valour; pride; vikku to swell with pride, stretch out. Kui bija enormously swollen; bija bija ava to be enormously swollen. Malt. bije to expand, widen. Cf, 5382 Ta. viku. (b) Ta, vimpu boast, swagger, bombast, vaunt, pride, obstinacy; vimpan boaster, braggart, proud person, pertinacious person; vimp-dftfam ostentatious display, bragging. Ma. vimal swelling of face; vimpu bragging, vaunting. DED(S) 447

Ta. vicam the fraction’of jth. Ma. visam ~th gold fanam, or a rice-corn’s weight of gold. Ka. visa, isa the fraction ith, Ath of a hana, etc. Tu. visy, visa 2th, a weight of gold equal to a grain of rice. Te. visamu a sixteenth part. DED 4478.

Ta. vicu (vici-) to throw, fling (as a weapon), cast (as a net), flap (as wings), swing (as the arm), fan, wave, flourish (as a sword), strike, beat, flog, open out, spread, lengthen, stretch, spill, strew, scatter, lay aside, throw off, abandon, drop, blow (as the wind), be spread, diffused (as fragrance, rays, etc.); viccu throw, cast (as of a net), beat, flap (as of wings), blow, stroke, swing- ing, oscillation, length, quickness, rapidity; viccam smell, effluvium; viciru (viciri-) to fan, wave to and fro, brandish, fling, hurt, cast (as a net), whirl round, pour forth, sprinkle, eject, discharge, remove, swing (as the arms in walking); viciri fan; vicukk-enal onom. expr. of quick movement; vicai (-pp-, -tt-) to hasten, cause to move swiftly, swing, leap, hop, burst, split, be forceful; m. haste, speed, impetus, elasticity, spring, force, contrivance as a trap, lever. Ma. vicuka to fan, cast (nets); vicci fan; viccu throwing a net, a backstroke; viyuka to fan, brandish, swing, wield, the wind to blow; vb.n. viyal; viyikka to cause to fan, etc.; viguka to fan, blow, throw (a net), emit (as scent or rays); visikka to get oneself fanned; viséri, (Tiyya) visala, viydla fan; visa spring-trap, snare for birds, lever; visari fan; vigaru storm of rain; vigaruka to fan, flutter with wings. Ko. vi-c- (vi-c-} to exercise violence, throw violently, make a sweeping blow, grind with grinding stones, (wind) blows; vi-c violent blow; vec force, power, speed; ?vicm (ob1 vict-) thunderbolt;

Ta. vitu (viti-) to perish, be des- troyed, die; vittu (vifti-) to destroy, kill, remove. Ka, bid-&gu to perish. Te. viti ravage, plunder; viff-buccu to spoil, ruin, lose: baffle, disappoint; viti-bévu to become useless, vain or unprofitable. DED 4481.

Ta. vitu-kol (kolv-, kont-) to recover, get back. Ma. vituka to be paid or discharged, be complete as a fast, be revenged; discharge; viftuka to discharge what is due, repay. Tu. bittuni to repay, give in return, avenge; butavuni to make pay or retribute, avenge, revenge. Cf. 4883 Ta. mil. DED(S) 4482. 5454 Ta, vin uselessness, unprofitableness, that which is unprofitable, that which is not necessary; vinan useless fellow, idle fellow. Ma. vinan a vain, trifling person, vinayan silly; vipattaram triflingness. DED 4483.

Ko. yviev hollow on each side of backbone (of man or animal). Tu. bimpu tail. Te. vipu the back. Kol. viep backbone, (Pat.. p. 27) lower back. Kui béa part of the back of animals. ? Kur. bisi the two large muscles on each side of the spine; the flesh of the back; (Hahn) bisi the spine. DED(S) 4484. 5456 Ta, virinam a large, double drum. Ma. virinam a kind of tambourine. Ka. virana a double drum used at weddings, etc. Te. viranamu a double,drum. DED(S) 4486. 5457 Ko, vierasny vanm small gold coin put in mouth of dead man and burned with body (panm coin). To. pi+r bonm small, old coins in bag, used at funeral. Kod. birasni pagoda (= 4 rupees). DED 4487. $458 Ta. vir-vir-enal onom. expr. of screaming, shrieking; vir-enal onom. expr. of a sharp, shrill cry; vir-itu to cry out sud- denly, scream. Ma, vir the roar of elephants, the grunt of pigs; vir-ituka to squeak, bellow. Kui vere vere the squeal of a pig. DED 4488. 5459 Kol. vier- (viet-) to sell. Nk. vir- id. Nk. (Ch.) viy- id. Pa. viy- id. Ga, (OIL) vir- id. ? Kui vira (viri-) to be obtainable, be availablé. Kur. bisna to sell; bisrna to scll oneself, be sold. Malt. bise to sell. DED(S) 4489, $460 Ka. bigu creeper. Kurub. (LSB 1,9) bili id. Tu. bitru id. ? Kod. i-le any creeping plant (< *igay). DEN 77. §461 Tea. vilai whistling shrill sound, noise; vil-enal onom., expr. of a shrill, sudden noise or cry. Ko, vilv- (vilt-) to whistle; vilk act of whistling. DED 4490. §462 Ta. viru (viri-) to be distinguished, be eminent, incre

Ko. vier- (viery-) to throw (light things, eg. dust, grain, grass); uer- (u-ry-) to throw, act violently or thoroughly. To. pier: (pi-ry-) to scatter, shed (tears) in profusion. Ka. bir(u) to throw away, fling, throw as an arrow, stone, stick, etc., give away according to one’s inclination or at one’s pleasure, spend or bestow profusely, give liberally or magnanimously, spread; spread (intr.), become extensive; biriga a scattered, disorderly mass. Tu. biruni to scatter, disperse. DED 4492. 5464 Ta, vekul (vekulv-, vekun{-) to be angry, be enraged at, haté, dislike; vekuttu (vekutfi-) to make angry; vekuli anger, ‘wrath, dislike, hate; vekutci wrath. Ma. vekuli excitement; vekulikka to rut. Tu. bigaduni to take offence; bigagy taking offence. Te. vegatu repulsiveness, disgust, dislike. DED(S) 4493 and from DED(S) 4494,

Ko. vekar- (vekat-) to be puzzled, astounded, thunderstruck; caus. vekarkc- (vekarke-). To. pek in- (id-) to be afraid (song phrase), Ka. bekkasa astonishment, amaze- ment; begadu, begar, begag, bigur to be amazed, astonished, fear; m, amazement, fear, alarm; bigurvu that which is formidable or terrific; vigurvisu, vigurbisu to become or be formidable; vigurbane a fearful object. Tu. bekkasy surprise, wonder; bekkasuni to wonder, be surprised; begaduni to be alarmed, bewildered. Te. vegadu astonishment, sur- prise, confusion, embarrassment, anxiety; vegadu-padu to be anxious; beg(g)adu to be vekkasa [497] vetfu afraid, fear; nm. fear, terror; begadu-padu, beggadil@)u to fear, be afraid; begadu-paracu to frighten; beggalamu fear, concern; begga- lincu to be afraid; beggalikamu fear; beggil()u to be afraid, temble. Kol, (Pat., p. 103) bekadyak fearful, cowardly. Cf. 5443 Ta. virai. DED(S) 4494.

Ka. vekkasa roughness, unsmooth- ness, harshitess, severity, unkindness, un- pleasantness, etc. Tu. bekkasy nausea, disgust; bekkasuni to faint, be tired, feel satiated. Te. vekkasamu excess, satiety, nausea, repugnance, disgust, dislike, envy, reproach; excessive, extreme, unbearable; vekkasi an unbearable, frightful or disgusting person; vekkasincu to be unbearable; be envious, envy. DED 4495.

Ka. veggala, veggale, eggala, heggala abundance, greatness, excess; a great man; veggalisu, vekkalisu to become much, increase greatly, become plentiful, go beyond the common boundary (in emotions), be elated or actuated by impulse. Te. veggalamu, veggalapu excess, too much; excessive, ex- treme; veggafidu a great or extraordinary man. DED 4496,

Ka. bisu (becc-), besu, bese to unite firmly, solder; join, be united; bisu soldering; bisuge, besage, besavu, besike, besige, besuge id., state of being soldered or firmly united, close connexion, composition; beccu state of being soldered or united. Tu, besigé soldering gold or other metal. Cf. 5517 Ta. vé. DED 497. 5469 Kui vesa bowstring. Kuwi (Su.) vacca, (T. Isr.) vaca, (F.) wica id. DEDS 875.
Ka. befice small pond. Te. venca lake. DED 4500.

Ta. vitahku beauty, gallantry; vitat- kan person of beauteous form; vetippu splendour. Ma. vetippu cleanness, neatness, elegance. Ka, bedangu; bedagu novelty, beauty, elegance,. grace, fineness, pleasantness, showiness, ostentatiousness; bedagi a showy woman, coquette. Tu. bedafigy, bedagy coquetry, affectation, haughtiness; bedage a charming man; bidugu coquette. Te. bedan- gu, bedigu beauty, handsomeness, fineness, prettiness, grace, elegance, strength; beautiful, elegant, large, great, strong; bedigutanamu beauty, strength; bedigu, bedagaru to be beautiful; ? vihgadamu beautiful. /Cf. Skt. (Bhagavatapurana) vitafika- trim, nice, pretty, handsome. DED 4501.

Ta. veti (pp-, -tt-) to crack, break, split, break open, burst, explode, blossom, shoot forth; n. fissure, crevice, split, ex- plosion, gun, retort; vetippu crevice, fissure, explosion; vitappu, vitampu, vitavu, vifampai fissure, cleft; vitar id., cleft in a mountain, mountain cave, abode of a sage; vifarvu fissure, cleft; vifu (vituy-, vif{-) to be split, broken, cracked; (-pp-, -tt-) to split (tr); vitai (-pp-, -tt-) to separate (or with 5393 Ta. vitu); véttu the report of a gun. Ma. veti explosion, cracking, shot, gun; vetikka to explode, split, crack; vetiyuka to split, open, separate; vitar fissure, cleft; vifaruka to split open, blow; vitarkka to open, unfold, spread; vb.n. vifarcca, vitarppu; vifavu crevice; vita- vikka to split, crack. Ko. veyy gun. Ka. bife, bidi, bidu, bide chink, fissure, crack, crevice. Kod. bodi act of shooting. Tu. bedi gun; bedebedé the sound of a broken vessel. Te. vidugu to expand, blow, blossom; bia crack, cleft, split, chink; bTfagillu, bTtaluvaru, bifa- luvdyu_to crack. Mand. vetpa- to crack (fin- gers). DED(S, N) 4502.

Ta. vitam, vifari, vintu mountain. Ko. vetm (obi, veft-) id.; beg id. (na-«ng bet the Nilgiris [lit. the four mountains]; betd amn the female member of the Rangr trinity in Kolme-l village [/it, the goddess of the mountain]). To. pé¢ mountain (esp. tit pdf id.). Ka. betta, betqu big hill, mountain. Kod. betta id. Tu. boftu high, lofty; dry (as land). Malt. bite a shore. Br. bit, bug mound, hillock; bafagh summit, top. Cf. 5058 Ta. mettu. DED(S) 4503.

Ta. veti (-v-, -nt-), viti (-v-, -nt-) to dawn, break as day; vetiyal, vefivu, vifi, vitiyal, vitivu, vitivai break of day, dawn; vetta clear, plain; vettam light; vettaviti break of day, early dawn; vetta-veli open plain, vefta-veliccam broad daylight. Ma. vetikka the weather to clear up; vefippu cleared off, gone; vetta, vettam light; vef¢ta- velivu clearness. ? Ko, vart vir- (vif-) day breaks. ? To. ko& fiy- (fif-) to dawn; koes féty(k) lamp lit in funeral hut (for ko+s, see 1458). Te. vidi clearness, transparency, brilliancy; clear, transparent. Malt. bidye to shine; bide to shoot forth (as, rays). DED (S) 4504: 5476 Ma, vetiyuka to avoid, loathe; veticcal loathing. Ka. (Hav.) bodi to be bored, tired. Tu. bodi satiety, loathing, disgust, exhaustion, fatigue; bodiyuni to be satiated, cloyed, be tired, done up; bodipavuni to satiate, exhaust, tire. Cf. 5394 Ta. vitai. DED 4505.

Ta. vetti, vettai uselessness, worth- lessness. Ma. vetti a tree of worthless timber; worthless. Ko. vity uselessness, in vain. To. pityk ( pitky) in vain. Cf. 5513 Ta. veru and 5233 Ka. bata. DED 4506. :

Ta. vettu (vetti-) to cut as with sword or axe, cut off, engrave, dig as a well, injure, destroy; ”, cutting, wound, cut, en- graving. Ma. vetfuka to cut with a sword or axe, dig, engrave; vetfu blow, strike, cut, vetfai [498] verin wound, sunstroke, stitch, felling trees, digging, engraving. Ko. vet cut, mark of a cut. Ka. beftu to cause to enter firmly, strike forcibly into, impress, stamp, coin; ”. tool for making impressions. Tu. bettuni to cut, circumcise; bottuni to beat as a drum, hammer as metal, knock as a door; caus. boffavuni; bottele drummer. DED 4507.

Ta. vettai heat, heat of the ground, passion, lust. Ka. vette heat. 7e. vetta heat, warmth, summer; hot; betta heat; hot. ? Kur. birna (bidd-) (sun) to be hot; biyi sun, time. ? Malt, bidye to shine. DED(N) 4508.

Ta. venftu (venfi-) to dry as in the sun, become withered, be exhausted, be in great want, hanker; #. hollowness as of a pipe, a disease which causes hollowness in trees, sugar-cane, sola-pith. Ma. venf-elpu an old bone. Ka. bendu the white and light corky wood of the bendu kasa, used in making hats, etc., cork, pith, a soft, light, useless sub- ‘stance. Tu. bendy, bendu pith; a light and “spongy plant growing in water, Aeschyno- mene aspera; thinness, leanness, lightness; . thin, light, emaciated; bondu pith. Te. bendu A, indica; (Ginscr., p. 51) soft, powerless. / Cf. Mar. bhéd pith. DED(S) 4509.

Te. vendi again, once more, further. ‘Konda (BB) resi va- to return; repsi si- to give back. Kui vyépa (vrét-) to return, come back again, retreat; m. return, retreat; vréppa (vr@pt-) to cause to return, turn back (fr), pay back, return something borrowed or pledged; ». turning something back, repay- ment, return. Kuwi (F.) vendali to go back, return; vethali to give back; (S.) wend- to return; wendinai to recover, revive; wet’nai to reverse; wende back; again; (Su.) venda va- to come back, return; (Isr.) vend- Cit-) to return (intr.); vef- (-h-) to return something; vende ki- to repair; vende veh- to answer, reply. Kur. birdna to turn over (¢.), turn round, give back; ana-birdnd to answer, retort. DEDS 889. $482 Te. vendruka a hair; (pL -lu) hairs, the hair. Pa, vendra (usually pl. vendrel) hair (on head or body). DED 4510. §483 Ka, bedaku to seek, search for, look for; m. search. Te. vedaku, vetaku, venaku, (K. also) veduku, vetuku to search, explore, rummage, examine, scrutinize, seek or search for, look for; vedakulidu, vetakuladu to search; vedakudu searching search. ? Go. (M.) mehkana, (Ko.) mehk-, (Y. Mu.) mahk-, (F-H.) mahak- to search, (Mu. also) grope; (Tr.) mahakkana to grope, grope in a pool for fish; (SR.) mahakkangd to fish (Voc. 2766). Kur. beddni, (biddyas) to seek, search, get (second wife, concubine), find, discover, want, wish, be about to, be liable to. Malt. bede to seek, marry; beduwre to be married. DED 4512.

Ta. vetir open flower, blossom. Ka. bidir(u), bidur to open as the mouth, unfold as wings, stretch out, loosen, untie; bidircu to spread out, unfold, unharness, unyoke, loosen or free oneself. Kod. bier- (bi-ruv-, biend-), (Mercara dial.) buer- (bu-ruv-, bu-nd-) (flowers) open; bi-t- (bi+ti-), (Mer- cara dial.) bu-t- (bu-ti-) to open out (¢r.; mouth of bag, knife). Tu. biduruni to Joosen by itself. Te. (K.) vidur(u)cu, vidrucu to flap wings. Pa. (S.) vidrup- (vidrut-) cock to flap wings when crowing. Kuwi (F.) vidali to flap. Kur. bidigna to lay partly bare the contents by violent action upon the covering, tear open, make a rent, make burst or crack by weighing upon; bidgrna to be laid bare, get exposed to view. Malt. bithge to ex- pand, open (as the lips or eyelids). DED(S) 4513.

Ta. vetir, vetiri, vetiram, vayir bam- boo; véral small bamboo, spiny bamboo. Ko, vedyr bamboo. Ka. bidir(u), bidru, bidaru id. Tu. bedyry id. Te. veduru id. Kol. vedur (pi. vedudl) id. Vk. vedur id. Pa, vedri (pl. vedrul) id. Ga. (Oll.) vedre (pl -gil), (S.) vedir @L - -gil), (S.2) vedirin id. Go. (Tr.) waddur, (M.) vediir, (L.) veduri, (Y. Ko.) vedur, (Mu.) vaddur, (Ma.) veddur id. (Voc. 3282); (LuS.) wedooree id. Konda vedru dubu tall bamboo tree; (Sova dial.) dér(u) tender bamboo; dér dubu bamboo thicket. Pe, dér (pl ° -ku) bamboo; bamboo shoot. Mand, d&r bamboo shoot. Kui (K.) téru (pl. tréka) bamboo; (W.) téru (pl. térka)a shoot, bud (? bamboo shoot). Kuwi déru (pl. dérka) (F. S. Su. P.) bamboo, (Isr.) bamboo shoot. DED(S) 4514.

Ta. veyar (-pp-, -tt-), vér (-pp-, -tt-) to sweat, perspire; n. perspiration, viyar Cpp-, -tt-) to sweat, perspire; 7. perspiration, weariness, exhaustion; veyarvu, veyarvai, veyarppu = viyar n.; vérppu, vérpu, vérvu, vérvai, viyarppu, viyarvai sweat, perspiration. Ma. viyarkka to perspire; viyarppu, ver sweat. Ka. bemar(u), bevaru. to perspire; n. perspiration; bemaruvike perspiting; be- marcu to cause to perspire; bevi to perspire. Kod. bevar- (bevari-) to sweat; bevari sweat. Tu. begaruni, begapuni to perspire; begary, bemary perspiration. Cf. 5519 Ta. vé. DED 4516.
Ka. berafiju to gather together, collect, put together, heap up. Tu. birafijuni to collect, heap up. Te, verdju to take by large handfuls, steal, plunder. DED(S) 4517.

Ta. verin, ven, ven back. Ko. ven id. To. pem waist, hip (or with 4205 Ta. pin). Ka. ben, bennu, bem the back; bennane at the back, after, behind; bembari following; company, association; the rear; bembagisu to follow. Kod. benni the back; bembara place behind. Tv. beri the back; berifkally, berankally backbone; beratgadé late, after- wards; beratgayi finally. Bel (£SB 2.2) bergi back. Te. ven(n)u the back; ven(u)ka, venukala the hinder part, back, rear, past or former time, later time; hinder, rear; vembadi, vembadini with, along with, behind, veru (499] vel after; vembadincu to follow, accompany, pursue; venta(n) behind, after, along with. Kol. ven (pl. vendl) back; venka behind; then (past); vegka mut one by one; vend okkod second, other; vefta with. Nk. venka behind; vetfa with. Konda venka afterwards; venti cither side of the backbone. DED(N) 4518.

Ta. veru fear, dread; veruvu (veruvi-) to be afraid of, be alarmed, frightened or startled; n. fear, fright; verul (verulv-, verunt-; veruli-), viral (viralv-, viranf-) to be startled, perplexed, bewildered, be frightened, shy, be skittish; ». fear, perplexity, that which is fearful; viratqu (virafti-) to frighten, intimi- date; veruli bewilderment, that which causes terror, bugbear, scarecrow; verutfu (verufti-) to terrify, frighten, confuse, stupefy, drive away (as animals); verufci bewilderment, shyness, skittishness, fear; vira (-pp-, -nt-) to fear; virappu fear; veri (-pp-, -tt-) to be frightened; n. fear. Ma. veruluka to be frightened, confused, furious; viraluka to start, be shaken by fear; virali scarecrow; viraffuka to frighten; virattal frightening. Ko. verp- (verd-) to become extremely afraid; ver{-*(verfy-) to cause (cattle) to have ex- treme fear. To. pe+l- (pe-d-) (buffaloes) are frightened ‘and run away; pe-f- (pe-ty-) to frighten (buffaloes) and cause them to run away. Ka. bercu, beccu to be frightened, scared; n. fear, dread; a scarecrow; bercisu, beccisu_ to frighten; beccalisu to be con- fused, become perplexed or bewildered, be deprived of consciousness, be beguiled; beppala amazement, alarm, fear, etc.; beppu wandering of the mind, confusion; bebbarisu to get bewildered from fear, be greatly frighened;* bebbala alarm, confusion; (PBh.) birut(u) having feared; beragu fear. Kod. borad- (boraduv-, borand-} to be thrown into confusion by fear; boraf- (borafi-

Ta. veruku tom-cat, wild cat; toddy cat, Paradoxurus niger. Ma. veruku, viruku, meruku civet cat. To. pesk flying-fox. Ka. berku, bekku cat. Kod. bekki jungle cats of various species. Tu. beru marten, (B-K.) wild cat. Ga, (OIl.) vérig, (S.) verrig (pl. vergil), (S.*) verig (pl. vergil) cat. Go. (A. M.) verkar, CY. S. Ko.) verkay cat; (W. Ph.) varkar wild cat; (Tr.) warkdr mongoose (Voce. 3289); (ASu.) verkar cat. Kur. berxa id. Malt. berge id. / Cf. Nahali berko, (LS/) berkit id. DED(S) 4520. 5491 Go, (Mu.) vereya a kind of basket (Voc, 3286); (Ma.) viyya a kind of small basket (Voc. 3250). Konda virika container, basket. DEDS 876. :

Ta. vel (vely-, venr-) to conquer, overcome, subdue, destroy, remove, excel; verram, verral, verri, ven, venti victory, success; verrimai victoriousness, victory, distinctive greatness. Ma. velluka to over- come, surpass, kill; venni, verri victory. ? Cf.

Ta. vellam jaggery, unrefined cane- sugar, Ma. vellam juice of sugar-cane, molasses, coarse sugar. Ka. bella coarse dark sugar. jaggcry. Kod. bella id. Tu. bella id. Te, bella- mu id. Go. (A. Y.) bheli, (Mu.) bellT (pl), (Ko.) bela jaggery (Voc. 2667); (LuS.) belee goor. / Cf. Mar. bhel, bheli, Or. bheli jaggery. DED(S) 4523.

Ta. v(rjatu to hang, be suspended, droop; v(r)alucu to hang (tr), suspend; vir)élu to hand, be suspended, be pendulous, droop; vrét(u)cu to hang (tr.), suspend. Go. (Ko.) verng- to hang, be suspended; caus. verh-, vehy-; (Ma.) verc-, (M.) verhtana, (Mu.) varih- to hang up; (Tr.) wayéngang, (Ph.) varengana to be hung up; (Tr.) warhuttana to hang (tr.); (Mu.) varem an article on which something is hung, stalk of fruit (Voc. 3293). Kui repa (ret-) to hang up, suspend; raki hung, hooked; dénga (déngi-) to be suspended, Kuwi (Mah.) rec- to hang; (F.) lecali (let-) to hang up, deck oneself with flowers; lepi kiali to deck (another) with flowers; lepi aiyali to be hung; (S.) leh’nai to hang; (Isr.) lej- Cit-) to hang (intr.); le?- ei (tr.). DEDS 877 (in part from DED 5496 (a) Ta. vel, ven white, pure, shining, bright; veli (-pp-, -tt-) to break (as the day), clear, whiten, become bright; 7. purity; veliccam light, lamp, clearness; velitu. that which is white; velippu clearness, brightness; veliru (veliri-) to grow white, become pale; n, whiteness, paleness, light, becoming clear; velu (-pp-, -tt-) to become white or pate, dawn, become clear or manifest; whiten (¢r., as clothes); veluvai becoming white; veluppu, veluveluppu whiteness, pallor, bleaching; veluvelu (-pp-, -tt-) to become white, be- come pale; velu-veluv-enal appearing white, looking pale; vellar, velliyar white men, true, honest men; velli whiteness, silver, silver coin, star, planet Venus, Friday; vellitu that which is
Ta. vilakku (vilakki-) to solder. Ma. vilakkuka id. Tu. bolakuni id., weld. DED 4525.

Ta. veli (-pp-, -tt-) to be open or public, be vacant, empty; n. outside, open space, plain, space, intervening space, gap, room, openness, plainness, publicity; veliccam publicity; velippu outside, open space, en- closed space; veliyar outsiders, strangers. Ma. veli open field; notoriety; outside; velic- cam publicity. Ka. belavdra an outcaste. Te. veli the outside, exterior, excommunication; outside, external; velalu to go or come out, start; velalucu to send out; velarincu, velar(u)- velikilu (501} veru cu to send or drive out, cast out, make public; veliparacu, velipuccu to make public or known; velipettu. to excommunicate; velucu to drive, send out; velupala outside, exterior; outside, external; velupalincu to put outside, disclose, divulge; velu-vadu to come out, go out; velavarincu, velavarucu to send out, bring out; velayu to be known; velladi open place, publicity, openness; vellatakamu making known, publishing, publicity; velliviri known, published. Pa, valip- (valit-) to expel, drive away; (S.) valkip- (valkit-) to chase. ? Ga. (OIL) vef- to flee; (S.) veff- to go out. Konda veli outside. ? Kui venda (vendi-) to go forward, start forth, go forth; pl action vetka (vetki-). Kuwi (S.) velli kinai to excom- municate. DED(S) 4526.

Te. velikilu to fall or lie on the back; velikila on the back, supine. Kol (SR.) velaka lying on one’s back. Go, (Ko.) veringi, in: pal y. with face upwards; (Tr.} phat warénginad to sleep on one’s back (Voc. 3294). Pe, r&q(g)- (fent-) to lie on the back; yék- (-t-) to lay on the back. Mand. rén(g)- to fall backward. Kui Jangari flat on the back. DEDS 878.

Ta. vellam flood, deluge, sea, sea- wave, water. Ma. vellam water. Ka. (K.?) bella flood. Tu. bolla flood, inundation. Te. velli, vellika flow, flood, stream; velluva flood, inundation. ? Br. bél large hill-torrent. DED 4529. 5504 Ta, vellari cucumber, Cucumis sativus; mottled-melon, C. memordica; kakri melon. Ma. vellari cucumbér, C. sativus. Cf. 5496(a) Ta. vel. DED 4530.
Ka. bellavara, bellara snare, net for snaring game, noose. Te. valle, valletradu noosed rope for tying cattle, noose. ? Br. bilum neck-rope, halter. DED 4531.
Ta. vell-atti maidservant, concubine. Ma. vella{ti id., slave girl, midwife. Te. vella- takatte harlot. DED 4532.

Ta. vellalan, vélajan, vellazan man of the Vélala caste; fem, vellalacci, vellaracci; vellanpmai, vellimai cultivation; vélanmai agriculture, husbandry. Ma. vellajar Tamil Sudras; vellayma agriculture; Vellalas. Te. velama name of a caste, man of this caste; (PCV) agriculture; (Inscr.) vélandu a culti- vator; affix to the names of cultivator caste in Tamilnad. Cf. 5545 Ta. vél. DED(S) 4533.

Ta. velli-mafantai moossanda of Ceylon, Mussaenda frondosa. Ma. vellila id. Tu, bollé id. DED 4534, 5509 Ta, vellil, velliyam, vila, vilam, vilari, vilavu, vilitti wood-apple, Feronia elephan- tum. Ma. vila id. Ka. belala, belaval, belavala, bela, balavala, baldla id. Te. velfga id. ? Pa. pulla virngam id. (pulla sour; cf. Te. puli/ pulla veléga). /? Cf. Skt. bilva- Aegle mar- melos, [Feronia and Aegle are closely related genera of one species each.} DED 4535. 5510 Ta, vellai-namai (also Lush.), vellai- nakai button-tree, Anogeissus latifolia. Ma. (Lush,) vella-nava id. Kol. (Kin.) velma id. Go. (M.) vedm2, (Ma.) verma, (A. Y.) erma, (Ch.) arma, (Tr.) arma, (W. Ph.) arma, (Mu.) orma id. (Voc. 360). Cf. 3599 Ta. namai. From DED(S) 2974. :

Ta. veti Cpp-, -tt-) to be drunk, intoxicated, become mad, be frenzied, be furious; n. toddy, drunkenness, intoxication, bewilderment, confusion, perturbation, mad- ness, frenzy, anger, savagery, wildness, devil; veriy-4tu. to dance under possession by Skanda; verippu drunkenness, Ma. veri intoxi- cation, fury; veriyan drunk, furious. Ka. veri madness, intoxication, fury, folly, confusion, fear. Te. verri madness, insanity, lunacy, mental derangement, craziness, frenzy, un- governable rage, extreme folly or foolishness; mad, insane, etc.; verrivadu madman, lunatic; fem. vertidi; verri-nillu toddy, spirituous liquor; vérfdi foolish man; véridincu to make foolish; viridi fool, madman. Konda veri foolish, simple; (BB) veri a- (shaman) to be possessed. Kuwi (Isr.) verila madman. Br. birr wild, not tame, shy; birri wildness. DED (S) 4536.

Ta. veru (verr- before vowels in cpds.) empty, etc.; verumai emptiness, vapid- vehaina [502] vé ness, profitlessness, uselessness, ignorance, poverty, quality of being unmixed or pure; veri (-pp-, -tt-) to become empty as a place devoid of inhabitants, clear away as clouds; n. (also vericcu) emptiness as of a place devoid of inhabitants; verity emptiness, uselessness, futility, stupidity; verippu famine; veriyan empty, destitute person; veruman worthlessness; verumané in vain, without advantage, without doing anything, idly; verra-veritu. absolute worthlessness; veryar poor people, worthless persons; verrimai emptiness, barrenness, bareness; varal empti- ness; varumai poverty, emptiness, helpless loneliness, difficulty; varitu. that which is small or insignificant, worthlessness, defect, poverty, emptiness. Ma, veru asunder, void of, empty, bare; veruté for nothing, uselessly, for mere pleasure, idly, gratis; veruma empti- ness; verran destitute, poor; varu empty. Ko. ver, verd, vetas ver mere, unmixed, vain; yerasc woman temporarily without a child; ver im buffalo temporarily without a calf; yer asl man without wife or children; ver giem naked (lt. mere hands); verma(-)ntamn naked; veruk small piece of meat for broth. To. per empty, mere; periyi it is worthless, per o-f ordinary man (as priest becomes when polluted); per foes pure milk; per fit mere foolishness; per me-n tree without blossoms; perisy in vain, empty-handed, good-for-nothing. Ka. bare, bari, baru, bara, barahu
Ta. verr-ilai betel pepper, Piper betle; betel leaf. Ma. verr-ila betel. Ko. vet! betel leaf (in proverbs). To, petil es id. ? Tu. bacc- iré id. (or with 3821). DED 4539.

Ta. vina (vinay-), vinivu (vinivi-) to ask, investigate, give ear to, listen, hear; vind question. Ma. vinavuka to ask. Ko. vent- (veynt-) id. To. pint- (pinty-) id. Te. vinu (vint-) to hear, listen, obey; vinukali hearing; vinucu, vinipincu to cause to hear; (K.) vin(u)cu to be heard; tell; vin(@)ki, vinikidi hearing, listening, audience, hear- say; vinu the ear; celebrity. Kol vin- (vint-) to hear. Nk. vin- id. Nk. (Ch.) ven- (vend-) id. Pa. ven- (vett-, vend-) id. Ga, (OIL) ven- (vet-, vey-), (S.) ven- (veyy-) id., (S.3) ven- (vent-) id.; venup- to make to hear. Go, (G.) ven- to hear (Voc. 3283). Konda ven- (veR-) id.; venba- (-t-) to ask. Pe. ven- (vec-) to hear; venba- (-t-) to ask, inquire. Mand, ven- (vec-) to hear; venba- to ask. Kui venba (ves-) to hear, listen, ask, question; #. hearing, listen- ing, inquiry. Kuwi (Su.) ven- (vece-) to hear; (F.) venjali (vec-), (Isr.) ven- (vec-) to hear, ask; (F.) vengali to be rumoured; (S.) wennai, wenbinai to hear, question; (Mah.) vepbu, (S.) wenumbu hearing. Kur. menna (menj-) to hear, listen, obey, question, ask; mendna to tell, inform. Malt, mene (menj-) to hear, ask; mendre to be heard, be rumoured. Br. bining (bing-) to hear. Cf. 5514 Go. veh-. DED(S, N) 4472.
